

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 1: There is no absolute path for the people under heaven
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 1: There is no absolute path for the people under heaven

Tick-tock...

Tick-tock...

Lin Jing gradually regained consciousness. He opened his eyes and was met by a damp, cold air.

Where is this?

The air was thick with an indescribable musty smell, mixed with the scent of damp earth. The surrounding walls were covered in moss and dark mold, and the sound of dripping water echoed from somewhere, making the place feel even more cold and eerie.

A prison cell?

After observing the dim environment, Lin Jing reached a judgment that felt absurd in his heart.

He had just passed the immortal examination, preparing to follow the immortals to cultivate and pursue eternal life. How could he end up in a place like this?

A sharp pain surged in his head, and before he could make further assessments, a weak voice came from nearby. Lin Jing then realized he seemed to have a neighbor.

He wanted to struggle, but found he couldn't move; his body was incredibly stiff.

"Don't waste your energy," the voice said again. "We’ve both been bound by a binding spell."

"Who are you?" Lin Jing asked.

"Like you, I've been abducted by an immortal trafficker," the person replied.

"…"

Immortal trafficker?

What is that?

Hearing this, Lin Jing quickly recalled.

He was a traveler from a blue planet.

After arriving here, he discovered that immortals indeed existed.

Now that he was here, Lin Jing quickly found new motivation.

He wanted to cultivate!

His memory flashed back to when he desperately sought an immortal, eventually passing the entrance examination of a cultivation sect called "Qing Niu Dao Guan" due to his astonishing talent and extraordinary physique.

Then everything went dark, and he couldn’t remember anything after that. He ended up locked up in this place.

"Immortal trafficker?" Lin Jing had no words.

Don't tell him that the cultivation sect he worked so hard to find turned out to be a human trafficking den...

Damn it!

The weak voice came again.

"By the way… brother, what is your physique?"

"The fact that the Yin Corpse Sect has set their sights on you means you are definitely a genius in cultivation like me."

"Me? A genius?" Lin Jing didn't know whether to feel happy or distressed.

Hearing what the other said, it was indeed the case; perhaps he truly possessed some talent for cultivation, otherwise, he might not even be worth being sold.

But now, the only advantage he had after crossing over seemed to have become a death warrant.

"Brother, don’t panic. Just because we’ve been abducted here doesn’t mean we’re doomed."

"I'm not panicking. What will happen to us next?" Lin Jing took a deep breath.

He was an optimistic person.

During his illness, with encouragement from family and friends, he developed a positive outlook on life.

With a good attitude, luck wouldn’t be bad.

Look, he didn’t die after all; he had crossed over, right?

He had unwittingly gained a new life.

"...The Yin Corpse Sect is one of the three major demonic sects in the cultivation world, specializing in the illegal trade of human trafficking."

"They capture low-level cultivators or mortals with rare physiques and sell them to higher-level cultivators for possession or as resources for cultivating demonic techniques."

"Normally, our outcome is obvious; we either become the vessels for some cultivator to possess and restart their cultivation or be refined into great medicinal pills."

"However, brother…" the person said, "we’re not without a way out. The reason I’m asking about your situation is that some special physiques might not meet a grim end."

The weak voice continued, "For instance, I am a Yuan Yang physique, which is the best dual cultivation furnace. Even if I die, it would only be from expending all my essence, dying in pleasure. If I’m lucky, and the buyer is a kind female cultivator, I might not die at all... This is the glimmer of hope I mentioned."

Lin Jing fell silent.

No wonder you’re not panicking.

How does it feel like you’re a bit eager?

"So, what about you, brother? Let me analyze your situation."

"Me..."

What is my cultivation physique?

From what he understood, in this world, more than ninety-nine percent of cultivators start out as ordinary bodies, but there are a few lucky ones who are born with various attribute spiritual bodies or special physiques, allowing them to go further on the path of cultivation.

When he joined "Qing Niu Dao Guan," that Daoist Qing Niu seemed to say he had an extraordinary cultivation physique, a Longevity Body!

A short-lived person matched with a Longevity Body; he himself found it surprising.

The other person claimed that after stepping onto the immortal path, his lifespan would be at least ten times that of other cultivators of the same realm!

The benefits of his physique seemed simple, but it was better than an ordinary body. Lin Jing had already decided to survive for a few thousand years in the Daoist temple and then emerge after becoming invincible.

But who knew, he hadn’t been happy for long when...

So, is my Longevity Body really true?

"If I am a Longevity Body, what will the outcome be?" Lin Jing asked.

"Mm? Longevity Body?!" The Yuan Yang physique was stunned, then exclaimed in surprise, his tone became strong and no longer weak.

"You are actually a Longevity Body!"

"Then you are finished."

Lin Jing: ?

"According to legend, although cultivators with a Longevity Body have long lifespans, their flesh and blood are said to be 'immortal' when consumed. I’ve heard that the flesh and blood of a Longevity Body is a great supplement for demon creatures, and it has unimaginable allure for demonic beasts."

"You might… be sold to some demon king by the Yin Corpse Sect, becoming a meal!" The Yuan Yang physique lamented and expressed regret.

Lin Jing: ?

Is it like Tang Seng's meat?!

"Well, that's acceptable." However, in the next moment, Lin Jing let out a sigh of relief.

"I still have a glimmer of hope."

"Why do you say that?" The Yuan Yang physique was taken aback.

"Although I don’t know much about Longevity Bodies, I speculate that the higher the cultivation realm of a Longevity Body cultivator, the better the effect of consumption." Lin Jing also began to analyze.

"In other words, after being sold, I might not be eaten immediately."

"It's possible that the demon king will forcibly increase my realm before consuming me."

"Moreover, it would be too extravagant to eat me all at once. If I were the demon king, I would slowly improve my quality, taking blood and cutting flesh every day for cyclical consumption, maximizing value. I could suggest to the demon king that maybe we could collaborate long-term."

"That’s better than being possessed immediately..."

"Ah?" The Yuan Yang physique exclaimed, greatly shocked.

He wanted to ask, "Are you a normal mortal? What have you been through!"

But ultimately, he didn't ask out loud...

The two fell silent.

Lin Jing looked up at the sky again in silence.

...

After an unknown amount of time.

With a loud bang, the prison cell shook.

Lin Jing and the Yuan Yang physique, who had their eyes closed and were lost in thought, opened their eyes at the same time.

Outside, there was a great commotion, as if someone was engaged in battle.

Hearing the noise, Lin Jing suddenly thought of some keywords and said, "Brother Lu, didn’t you say before that the Yin Corpse Sect engages in illegal activities and is a demonic sect?"

"This means there’s a 'righteous law enforcement organization' in the cultivation world, right?"

"If that’s the case, do we still have another path to survival, which is to be rescued by righteous people before being sold?"

The Yuan Yang physique leaned out, surprised that this mortal was quick to react, and most importantly, his emotions were incredibly stable.

However, he didn’t have the surname Lu.

"Is it?!" Lin Jing remained calm, knowing that there’s no absolute path for the people under heaven.

This commotion… it might be righteous immortals coming to rescue them!

As soon as he finished speaking, the stone wall shattered, and the large prison cell was smashed to pieces. Amid the flying debris, a massive black bear creature, towering two people high, appeared in front of Lin Jing.

This black bear monster had muscles bulging, and its red eyes were fixed on Lin Jing, drooling from its mouth filled with sharp teeth as it spoke in human language:

"I caught a whiff of a delicious scent from far away..."

"What luck! To find a 'Longevity Body' in this little place, and there are only a few weak foundation-building cultivators guarding it."

"It seems I can have a great feast…"

As the bear spoke, Lin Jing’s smile faded. Wait, where did this monster come from?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 2: The Black Bear Monster
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 2: The Black Bear Monster

Hey... is it really necessary?!

Looking at the monster that came in, Lin Jing was speechless.

It wasn’t the righteous immortal who had come to attack the Yin Corpse Sect stronghold, but rather... a monster attracted by his physique?

A black bear that was a foundational cultivator of the demonic sect...

To be honest, if given the choice, he didn’t really want to engage in a long-term partnership with a demon beast.

Even if the other party was reasonable and consented, it would still hurt him.

Just escaping from a wolf’s den to fall into a bear’s mouth, Lin Jing felt very tired.

Sensing the foul smell coming from the black bear's mouth, Lin Jing sorted out his thoughts and was about to introduce the proper way to eat him.

"Great King, please hear me out..." Lin Jing spoke, needing to appeal to emotion and reason.

But the next second, a familiar voice interrupted his thoughts, leaving him in a somewhat complicated mood.

"Elder Black Bear."

The previously encountered Yuan Yang physique had somehow walked out of the collapsed prison cell, brushing off the dust from his body, and respectfully clasped his fists towards the black bear monster. "He’s just a mortal; please don’t scare him. What if he dies?"

"Ah?" The black bear monster stretched out its palm to wipe its drool, its eyes turning to the Yuan Yang physique. "You’ve done a great job this time."

"I’ve already dealt with the demonic sect cultivators in this Yin Corpse Sect stronghold..."

"Next, we’ll leave it to the Demon Extermination Division to handle. However, this guy..." The black bear monster licked its lips, looking at Lin Jing.

"I know." The Yuan Yang physique laughed heartily.

Saying this, he looked kindly at Lin Jing.

At this moment, Lin Jing could only remain silent.

This is...

The Yuan Yang physique smiled at Lin Jing. "Brother, I know you have many questions in your heart, and I’m here to answer them for you."

"I am Ouyang Hao, an inner disciple of the Beast Master Sect. For the sake of the sect’s mission, I deliberately disguised myself as prey to infiltrate the Yin Corpse Sect stronghold and provide intelligence for the sect."

"It was originally a long-term mission to catch bigger fish, but I couldn’t bear to see you fall into the jaws of a monster, so I contacted the sect early to set up a net."

"Now, this Yin Corpse Sect stronghold has been destroyed, so you don’t have to worry about your safety anymore."

Lin Jing looked up.

He looked at the other person.

Then he glanced at the black bear monster, which had somehow pulled out a Daoist robe and draped it over itself.

On the back of the robe, there were three large characters that appeared to read "Beast Master Sect."

An undercover agent?

"Of course, our meeting is fated. You must have entered the Yin Corpse Sect in search of immortality, so I wonder if you would be interested in returning with me to the Beast Master Sect and become a disciple of the Beast Master Sect, cultivating beasts and seeking immortality?" Ouyang Hao invited.

"Beast Master Sect?" Lin Jing knew...

His life was not meant to end here!

"Yes, the Beast Master Sect!" Ouyang Hao seemed to see through Lin Jing’s caution and said solemnly, "You can rest assured that the Beast Master Sect is a legitimate sect, recognized as a righteous path in the Tianyuan Ancient Kingdom."

Lin Jing nodded, but he still felt that the black bear monster’s drool didn’t seem to be fake.

Moreover, "Beast Master Sect" sounded like a place that raised many demon beasts.

Since his Longevity Body was a great supplement to demon beasts, then entering such a sect would surely bring trouble.

Lin Jing expressed his skepticism.

At this time, the Daoist robe-clad black bear monster turned its head and said, "Kid, do you still want to cultivate? If so, joining the Beast Master Sect is your best option."

"Although the Longevity Body has a long lifespan, its cultivation speed is extraordinarily slow, and there are no divine abilities to enhance it, making its combat power quite ordinary. According to normal cultivation methods, even with abundant resources, it would take a long time to successfully establish a foundation."

"This physique gradually becomes extinct; in addition to being hunted by the demon clan, the difficulty of cultivation is also a factor."

"Therefore, even if a Longevity Body is discovered, most sects can’t afford to nurture it due to its overly harsh cultivation conditions!"

"However, the Beast Master Sect is different. The methods practiced there involve cultivators forming contracts with spiritual beasts and demon beasts, primarily assisted by pet beasts in combat. While cultivating the pet beasts, the cultivation results of the pets will also partially reflect back on the cultivators."

"So, for cultivators whose cultivation speed is too slow due to innate talent and physique, choosing the path of beast cultivation in the Beast Master Sect is the best option."

"The Beast Path is more suitable for your Longevity Body than any other cultivation methods, such as law cultivators, sword cultivators, or body cultivators!"

"What do you think?" Elder Black Bear stared at Lin Jing, laying out the pros and cons.

"I see." Lin Jing understood.

No wonder Brother Lu gave up his task to save him first.

Hearing this, the Longevity Body cultivation physique indeed matched well with the cultivation methods of the Beast Master Sect.

Relying on his long lifespan... he could cultivate one pet after another, quietly increasing his cultivation in the background; that didn’t sound too bad.

"Predecessors... I am willing to join the Beast Master Sect." Lin Jing immediately said. For now, he didn’t care if the Beast Master Sect was reliable; he had no other options.

He certainly couldn't continue being an ordinary person.

After experiencing being sold to the Yin Corpse Sect, Lin Jing didn’t want to wander around outside anymore.

The hearts of people are treacherous.

The hearts of beasts are treacherous.

Having a Longevity Body was like being a walking Tang Seng meat; it was too dangerous.

Moreover... his current life was saved by them.

A drop of water of kindness should be rewarded with a fountain of grace; how could he forget a life-saving grace?

He wanted to enter the sect... to seek an opportunity for repayment!

"Good, good, good." Upon receiving Lin Jing’s answer, Ouyang Hao smiled again. "Then from now on, you can call me Brother Ouyang. Let’s return to the sect!"

Finding a special physique for the sect was worth far more than the rewards of this mission.

Ouyang Hao looked at Lin Jing as if he were a brilliant spirit stone, incredibly pleasing to the eye.

"Okay, Brother Ouyang... Oh! By the way!" Lin Jing suddenly remembered something and said, "What about the Daoist temple that tricked me here?"

Recalling that old Daoist riding a cow, Lin Jing felt particularly resentful.

The grudge of being trafficked was one that couldn't be shared; if it weren't for meeting the undercover agent, he would have almost met his end! He remembered this grudge, as well as the Yin Corpse Sect... he wouldn't forget!

Gratitude should be remembered, and so should grudges. Once he became a powerful cultivator, he would ensure that this trafficking organization was wiped out.

"You don’t need to worry about that. The Demon Extermination Division will take care of it." The black bear monster spoke while observing the surroundings. "This place isn’t safe for long. We don’t know if the demon cultivators in this stronghold have passed on your Longevity Body information to higher-level Yin Corpse Sect cultivators. For safety’s sake, let’s return to the sect first."

"My master, Elder Mo Wuyah of the Beast Master Sect, has been waiting for you. He is responsible for identifying special disciples, and it was he who discovered Ouyang Hao and brought him into the sect. Both of you are talented with special physiques, and once you enter the sect, your treatment will be better than that of ordinary disciples!"

Lin Jing nodded, but with his Longevity Body, he felt that danger would not be scarce after stepping into the world of cultivation.

Based on his character, should he raise a monkey to protect himself...?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 3: The Great Wilderness Region, South
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 3 : The Great Wilderness Region, South

Towering mountains, enshrouded in clouds and mist, reached toward the heavens.

This was the domain of the Beast Master Sect.

High among the peaks, nestled within the sea of clouds, Elder Black Bear swept Lin Jing and Ouyang into the sect’s grounds.

One of the main peaks, Lotus Peak, shaped like a blooming lotus flower, appeared as if it had been carefully sculpted by nature itself.

The black wind carried them to the peak, where clouds churned around them. As Lin Jing’s vision cleared, he found himself standing in a stone courtyard.

The stone courtyard appeared simple and unadorned, yet breathing here felt like being in a celestial realm, refreshing the spirit and dispelling worldly cares.

"Back, are we?"

In the courtyard stood an elder in black Daoist robes, his sparse silver hair combed back, revealing a forehead etched with the marks of time.

"Master," the Black Bear spoke.

"Elder Mo," Ouyang respectfully greeted.

Lin Jing followed their example and saluted.

"I shall take my leave, then."

Without waiting for Elder Mo’s response, Ouyang withdrew voluntarily. The Black Bear yawned and transformed into a cloud of black smoke, vanishing into the distance.

Soon, only Lin Jing and Elder Mo remained in the courtyard.

"Child, what’s your name?"

Elder Mo looked at Lin Jing and asked.

"Elder, my name is Lin Jing."

"Why do you pursue immortality?" His gaze deepened as he looked at Lin Jing.

Lin Jing sighed, "Initially, I sought immortality for a longer life, but then I discovered I possessed an Eternal Life Constitution…"

Elder Mo was at a loss for words.

"And now?"

"I dislike fighting and killing. I initially hoped to cultivate in peace and pursue the path to eternal life."

"But I’ve realized the Eternal Life Constitution can’t remain hidden; it will inevitably attract the attention of demonic beasts."

Lin Jing shook his head.

"So, I wish to cultivate formidable strength!"

"Indeed, only with power can one control their fate," Elder Mo said. "However, with power, one must also use it for righteous purposes."

Lin Jing looked solemn.

"I have already decided. To repay the sect’s life-saving grace, once I am strong enough, I’ll use myself as bait to lure various demonic beasts to the Beast Master Sect. This way, every disciple can have several beasts to subdue."

"???" Elder Mo responded, "There’s no need for that; the sect doesn’t lack demonic beasts."

"Moreover, enslaving beasts is an outdated path for our sect."

"I meant that, given the sect saved you from the Yin Corpse Sect, you should help others in need once you gain strength. Seek virtue and avoid falling into darkness in your pursuit of power."

"Enough."

"Let’s move on to official matters," he continued, though he couldn’t shake the feeling that this Eternal Life Constitution seemed unusual, hoping it wouldn’t cause trouble in the future.

"In the Beast Master Sect, disciple ranks are divided into servant, outer, inner, and true disciples."

"You are now an outer disciple of the sect, but since you possess a special constitution, you’ll receive enhanced training privileges."

"The sect will grant you resources one level higher than your current status. As an outer disciple, you’ll receive resources at the level of an inner disciple. When you reach the inner disciple status, you’ll receive the resources of a true disciple!"

"However, while your constitution is unique, advancement still depends on your efforts. You’ll need to complete sect missions and contribute to the sect."

The sect did not easily bestow knowledge, and while the Eternal Life Constitution was rare with great potential, it would take time to assess Lin Jing’s character and resolve.

After Elder Mo finished, Lin Jing nodded.

"Understood, Elder. Thank you."

"Good."

"May you strive in your cultivation and not disappoint the sect's support."

He continued, "Do you know the types of beasts?"

"Types of beasts?" Lin Jing shook his head. "I don’t know…"

"There are four kinds," Elder Mo said, looking up at the sky. "Spiritual, Demonic, Rare, and Celestial!"

"Those that absorb the essence of the sun and moon, or are nurtured by spiritual fruits and plants, developing wisdom and cultivation, are called Spiritual Beasts."

"Those that feed on flesh, or devour souls, are called Demonic Beasts."

"In the Beast Master Sect, we keep both Spiritual and Demonic Beasts."

"Usually, new disciples are provided with a low-level Spiritual Beast as their pet."

"Ouyang should have explained this to you, correct?"

"No, Elder Black Bear’s speed made us dizzy on the way…," Lin Jing hesitated before replying, "Elder Mo, what about the Rare and Celestial types?"

"Have you seen the night sky?"

"In addition to the sun and moon, countless strange stars are scattered across the sky."

"There is a legend that these stars are fallen immortals transformed."

"They sometimes shine brightly, and when their light touches the Tianyuan Continent, it may cause various wild animals and plants to mutate!"

"Whether a Spiritual or Demonic Beast, those altered by the light gain a mysterious ability, a supernatural power that gives them a unique racial talent distinct from ordinary beasts."

"For example, they might master spatial or temporal powers in the Qi Refining stage."

"Or they might regenerate from a single drop of blood, achieving near immortality."

"Some may even enter the Netherworld or escape the Five Elements."

"However, such beings are rare, and these mutated beasts are called Rare Beasts."

"As for Celestial Beasts, with the severed immortal path, it’s unknown if any exist on the Tianyuan Continent today."

"Spiritual… Demonic… Rare… Celestial…" Since joining the Beast Master Sect, Lin Jing naturally felt it was essential to understand these classifications.

Herbivorous Spiritual Beasts seemed relatively less dangerous.

Carnivorous Demonic Beasts… as someone with the Eternal Life Constitution, he’d best avoid those while he was still weak.

As for Rare Beasts, with their rarity, he figured they wouldn’t concern him either.

"Now, let’s choose your pet beast."

"The sect recently recruited a large batch of outer disciples, so the number of available pet beasts is limited."

"But since you’re new to cultivation, you don’t need to contract a particularly powerful pet beast just yet."

"You have ample time to build a foundation. When your cultivation is stronger, you can then contract a more powerful pet beast with a strong bloodline."

"I will follow your guidance," Lin Jing said without complaint.

"Good." Elder Mo continued, "Currently, we have basic Spiritual Beasts of four attributes remaining—Water, Fire, Earth, and Wood."

"These are the Water-attribute Rain Frog, of which there are seven left."

"The Fire-attribute Fire Spirit Finch, with two left."

"The Earth-attribute Horned Ox, with three available."

"And the Wood-attribute Pine Leaf Mouse, with only one remaining."

"I’ll introduce them," Elder Mo explained. "The Beast Master Sect cultivates five kinds of basic pet beasts suitable for Qi Refining cultivators, one for each attribute: Metal, Wood, Water, Fire, and Earth."

"These five pet beasts are not only friendly but also have potential to reach the Foundation Establishment stage. Additionally, they can quickly help disciples gather cultivation resources."

"The Metal-attribute Battle Ape is efficient in mining. Those who choose it can take mining missions in the spirit mine area, earning daily resources and strengthening the Battle Ape. But this batch has already been chosen."

"Of course, the other four types are equally good."

"The Water-attribute Rain Frog is adept at water techniques, and, when nurtured, can summon rain to irrigate the spirit fields."

"The Fire-attribute Fire Spirit Finch is skilled in fire techniques and can assist in the fire kitchens and fire refining rooms."

"The Earth-attribute Horned Ox, with its boundless stamina, helps transport supplies between peaks."

"Lastly, the Wood-attribute Pine Leaf Mouse excels in wood techniques, with a spirit power that accelerates the growth of spiritual plants."

"Among the five, the Battle Ape, Horned Ox, and Fire Spirit Finch are better in combat, while the Rain Frog and Pine Leaf Mouse are more peaceful and offer higher rewards in daily sect missions."

"However, the most crucial factor in choosing a pet is compatibility with yourself."

"For your constitution, which is like a normal one and without any elemental affinity, any of the Spiritual Beasts would suit you without causing conflicts."

As he spoke, two shadows appeared in the clouds… shortly, two beautiful celestial cranes landed before them.

"Greetings, Elder Mo. Where to next?" one of the cranes inquired in a pleasant, feminine voice.

Elder Mo looked to Lin Jing, signaling him to choose his pet type. These cranes would carry them to the mountain where the selected Spiritual Beast resided.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 4: Choosing a Pet Beast
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 4: Choosing a Pet Beast

Lin Jing understood that his constitution, like that of an ordinary person, was without any elemental attributes.

Looking at Elder Mo, he said, "Then Elder Mo, I’ll choose the Pine Leaf Mouse."

"Oh?" Elder Mo asked, "Are you certain?"

Lin Jing nodded. "I am certain."

His reasoning was simple; as an Eternal Life Constitution, he was not suited for fighting and killing in the early stages. Raising a Wood-attribute pet beast that could assist in farming and nurturing spiritual plants seemed a better option for gradually increasing his strength while staying within the sect.

Moreover, even though his constitution had no elemental affinity, Wood techniques were generally related to vitality and life force, making a Wood-attribute spiritual beast evidently more suitable for him.

As for combat-oriented beasts, he could look for those later, once his cultivation improved and he became an inner disciple.

"Elder Mo, is there a problem with this Pine Leaf Mouse?" Lin Jing asked, a bit concerned.

"Those available for disciples to choose from are generally under a year old, nurtured by the sect's resources to reach the third layer of Qi Refining and possess enough intelligence to understand human speech."

"After all, if a beast can’t reach the third layer with this level of training and can’t understand human speech, it simply isn’t suited for cultivation. Let alone assisting human disciples, it would be a burden."

"This one, however… during the last selection, only reached the second layer of Qi Refining and does not meet the standard."

"Normally, it would be released to roam the Pine Forest and would not be given to disciples."

"However, the elder responsible for basic spiritual beast training discovered that this Pine Leaf Mouse only reached the second layer due to some special circumstances."

"What kind of circumstances?" Lin Jing asked, startled.

Elder Mo hesitated before explaining, "This Pine Leaf Mouse has some peculiarities; it treats the resources provided by the sect like treasures, hoarding them away without using a single piece, relying solely on its talent to recently reach the third layer of Qi Refining."

"I don’t understand its reasoning; can hoarding spiritual stones increase their amount?"

"…Perhaps it’s about a sense of security," Lin Jing speculated.

"Given its talent isn’t bad—indeed, it could be considered exceptionally rare—the elder decided not to revoke its eligibility and granted it a second chance to enter the outer sect."

"Anyway, let’s go take a look first. If it doesn’t suit you, we can look for other pet beasts. If you can subdue it, its talent will greatly benefit your cultivation," Elder Mo said.

"Understood, Elder…" Lin Jing responded, intending to see it first.

…………

**Pine Forest Peak.**

The two celestial cranes landed.

Soon, Elder Mo and Lin Jing arrived at the edge of a pine forest.

"This is Pine Leaf Mouse territory," Elder Mo stated.

Within the forest, Lin Jing spotted many mice. The Pine Leaf Mice resembled ordinary squirrels but were enchanting, possessing soft, shiny fur adorned with delicate yellow stripes—very adorable.

What set them apart from ordinary squirrels were their vibrant green eyes, which seemed to reflect the dreamlike colors of leaves dancing in the morning light.

"Chirp…"

The Pine Leaf Mice leaped around, but those who had just awakened their intelligence or hadn’t at all clearly couldn’t speak like Elder Black Bear or the celestial cranes they had just met.

"Most of the Pine Leaf Mice living in this forest are those whose potential doesn’t meet the contract standards."

As they reached their destination, Elder Mo walked ahead with Lin Jing following closely.

"Except for the one I mentioned that’s a bit special."

Under Elder Mo's guidance, Lin Jing quickly spotted the specific Pine Leaf Mouse.

It sat atop a pine tree, eyes closed in meditation.

Upon noticing someone approaching, it opened its eyes.

"Chirp?"

The two locked eyes.

At that moment, several rebellious strands of Elder Mo's hair danced in the wind, giving him an air of carefree wisdom.

"I’ve spoken with Elder Jiang; this is a new disciple of the sect who wishes to select you as his first pet. What do you think?" he asked the Pine Leaf Mouse, hands clasped behind his back.

"Chirp…" The Pine Leaf Mouse hesitated before turning to Lin Jing, its voice soft.

With its emerald green eyes, it scrutinized Lin Jing.

Beside them, Elder Mo added, "You need to introduce yourself. Generally, spiritual beasts will decide whether to follow a disciple based on their situation."

"Choosing a pet beast in the Beast Master Sect is always a mutual decision; to take a beast, the disciple must earn its acknowledgment."

"This process is also a test for the disciple; whether they can win the beast’s recognition based on their capabilities is very important for disciples of the Beast Master Sect."

"Only when both the cultivator and the pet acknowledge each other can they achieve double the results with half the effort in their cultivation."

"Understood," Lin Jing nodded.

He wasn’t one to boast.

"My name is Lin Jing. Due to my special constitution, my cultivation speed is somewhat slow. If you become my pet beast, you may need to put in extra effort."

Lin Jing looked at the Pine Leaf Mouse, giving it a heads-up; since he wanted its assistance in his cultivation, he didn’t hide his constitutional flaw.

After all, the Eternal Life Constitution was known for being extremely slow in cultivation.

He believed that sincerity would be enough to win its favor.

However…

"Chirp!!" The Pine Leaf Mouse was startled.

If its cultivation speed was slow, wouldn’t that mean using up the spiritual stones it had hoarded for so long?

It took a small step back.

Seeing this, Lin Jing fell silent.

As expected, true feelings were hard to keep.

Elder Mo, watching from the side, couldn’t suppress a hint of amusement.

Such a peculiar pet often had a quirky personality and unique perspective; capturing it wouldn’t be easy.

Yet Lin Jing wasn’t just any person.

Faced with a mouse that could hold onto spiritual stones, resolute like an addict, he pondered and said:

"Storage bag."

Since sincerity didn’t seem effective, Lin Jing decided to appeal to its interests.

He spoke, "Once you become my pet beast, I’ll save up spiritual stones to gift you a storage bag that can hold countless items in a separate space."

"When we reach a higher cultivation level, I can even give you ten larger storage rings."

"Don’t you enjoy hoarding resources? Once you have a lot of items, where will you put them?"

For a mouse that loved to hoard, the worst thing would be running out of backpack space. Lin Jing was certain of this.

Pine Leaf Mouse: !!??

"Chirp!?"

In the next moment, the Pine Leaf Mouse's eyes lit up, instantly captivated by Lin Jing’s offer.

"This Eternal Life body is quite clever," Elder Mo remarked, taken aback.

"It has a peculiar habit of hoarding items, and a storage bag…" Given his prior experience as a seasoned hoarder in his past life, Lin Jing knew exactly what this mouse needed.

"It’s just that this Pine Leaf Mouse doesn’t seem very smart."

"But it has only just awakened its intelligence."

Elder Mo shook his head, seeing how the situation unfolded. A storage bag could be quite costly.

Regardless, it appeared that the Pine Leaf Mouse was hooked.

Soon enough.

One piece, two pieces, three pieces… dozens of low-grade spiritual stones were tossed out of a tree hole by the Pine Leaf Mouse while the two of them watched in silence. Eventually, the Pine Leaf Mouse emerged again.

"Chirp chirp chirp!"

Looking at Lin Jing, the Pine Leaf Mouse seemed excited, as if it had recognized him and planned to bring all its belongings along, embarking on a new cultivation journey together.

Lin Jing smiled in return.

"In order for us to buy that storage bag soon, I’ll help you keep these spiritual stones for now so we can plan and use them wisely."

The Pine Leaf Mouse stared at Lin Jing’s earnest gaze, confused but nodded slightly.

It was filled with anticipation for the future.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 5: First Level of Qi Training!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 5: First Level of Qi Training!

Within the Beast Tamer Sect, the outer disciples and miscellaneous disciples were active at the foot of the mountain.

After Lin Jing chose the Pine Leaf Mouse, Elder Mo somehow summoned the two cranes again.

Riding on the cranes, they quickly arrived at the external courtyard at the foot of the mountain, landing in a beautifully scenic courtyard.

In the courtyard, there was a pond and green trees. Lin Jing, carrying a bag of spirit stones, never expected that his first pet would come with funding, while the Pine Leaf Mouse stood on his shoulder, waving its paw to greet the elder crane.

“From now on, you will cultivate here for a while,” Elder Mo said.

“Even the outer disciples of the Beast Tamer Sect have their own separate courtyards.”

“However, the facilities in the outer sect are certainly not as good as those in the inner peaks. Therefore, you will need to work hard in your cultivation to meet the assessment standards for inner disciples, which is to reach the seventh level of Qi Training.”

“As long as either the disciple or the spirit beast reaches the seventh level of Qi Training, they can participate in the inner sect assessment.”

“Thank you, Elder.” Lin Jing cupped his fists in gratitude.

“Chirp, chirp, chirp!” The Pine Leaf Mouse also cupped its fists in thanks, feeling that this old man was rather nice, even giving them a cloth bag for spirit stones.

“Um…” Elder Mo hesitated to speak.

Looking at Lin Jing with the bag of spirit stones, he felt that the little squirrel might be in for a tough time.

The longevity body’s cultivation speed was extremely slow, and to progress faster, a large amount of resources was required. This bag of spirit stones might not last long…

But that wasn’t something he needed to worry about.

“Take this talisman; wear it on you. Ordinary monsters and cultivators won’t be able to discern your physique without specific means,” Elder Mo said, handing Lin Jing a jade talisman before leaving.

Lin Jing received the square jade talisman with great appreciation.

He felt that the Beast Tamer Sect had a very humane quality.

Such a sect was worth fighting for as a disciple.

“Alright, do you have any questions?” Elder Mo asked.

“Elder Mo, I’d like to inquire about Senior Brother Ouyang.” Lin Jing asked, as he was unfamiliar with the Beast Tamer Sect and, besides Elder Mo, only Ouyang Hao, who knew about his background, seemed suitable for him to befriend.

“Ouyang Hao?” Elder Mo sighed. “He is one of the best among the inner disciples.”

“He is at the ninth level of Qi Training, just one step away from foundation establishment, ready to challenge the ranks of true disciples.” Elder Mo continued, “Although I brought him back to the sect, he is highly favored by the Sect Master. After foundation establishment, he may even become the Sect Master’s disciple.”

“Ah?”

Lin Jing cautiously asked, “Is our Sect Master a male or a female?”

Elder Mo: “…”

“The Sect Master is a man, not what you might be thinking. You’ll understand in due time.” Elder Mo did not wish to elaborate further.

Lin Jing, upon hearing this, felt a mix of emotions.

It seemed that Senior Brother Ouyang’s talent was higher than he had imagined.

He actually had the chance to become the Sect Master’s disciple.

Unfortunately, inner disciples typically operated on the peaks, and under normal circumstances, outer disciples were not allowed up the mountain. It seemed he wouldn’t be able to visit often.

Before long, Elder Mo rode off on the crane.

After Elder Mo left, the Pine Leaf Mouse jumped onto the stone table.

Seeing this, Lin Jing smiled slightly, “From now on, we’ll cultivate together. How should I address you?”

“Chirp,” said the Pine Leaf Mouse.

“…” Lin Jing fell silent for a moment before saying, “When will you be able to speak like Elder Black Bear and the cranes?”

The Pine Leaf Mouse shook its head, indicating it didn’t know.

“It seems you still don’t have a name. Let me give you one.” Lin Jing decided to make a grand gesture.

Before he could think further, a crane's cry interrupted his thoughts.

Another crane arrived.

It came from the clouds, holding a burlap sack, and placed the sack on the stone table.

Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse looked at the “crane.”

The crane looked back at them.

“What are you staring at?” the crane said in a rough male voice.

“The items have been delivered; I’m leaving.”

Then, it flew back into the clouds.

“It seems that the Beast Tamer Sect’s transportation and delivery services are all done by cranes,” Lin Jing speculated.

“Ox demons handle land transport, while cranes handle air transport.”

Lin Jing stepped forward and opened the burlap sack, with the Pine Leaf Mouse peering in as well.

Inside the sack, there was a jade slip, a manual, a set of outer disciple clothing, a token, ten spirit stones, and a bag of spirit rice.

The manual was the “Sect Handbook,” introducing some important matters that disciples of the Beast Tamer Sect should pay attention to.

The jade slip contained the cultivation method “Beast Taming Technique.”

As for the spirit stones, the official disciples of the Beast Tamer Sect were divided into three levels: outer sect, inner sect, and true transmission.

Among them, outer disciples received a fixed monthly cultivation resource of one low-grade spirit stone, while inner disciples received ten low-grade spirit stones.

If they wanted more cultivation resources, they could only go out and perform tasks to earn them.

For instance, mining, farming, or cooking…

Lin Jing received special treatment as a disciple, so the number of spirit stones he got was on par with inner disciples, plus an extra bag of spirit rice.

“The Sect Handbook!” He first picked up the handbook and began to read, while the Pine Leaf Mouse climbed back onto his shoulder to join him.

After a while, the most valuable information was related to the “Beast Taming Technique.”

“By activating the jade slip, the cultivation methods of the ‘Beast Taming Technique’ will emerge in your mind.”

“At that point, you can absorb the spiritual energy of heaven and earth through a specific breathing method, integrate it into your body, and transform it into true Qi for nurturing yourself!”

“After reaching the first level of Qi Training, the cultivator’s body will give birth to a drop of special blood that can be mobilized by true Qi, thanks to the nurturing of true Qi.”

“Using blood as a medium and pressing it onto the spirit beast’s forehead, a blood contract can be completed, linking the spirits of both parties.”

“On the path of cultivation, the stages are Qi Training, Foundation Establishment, Golden Core, Nascent Soul, Divine Transformation, Void Profound, Seeking the Way, and Ascension. This ‘Beast Taming Technique’ can be cultivated up to the Foundation Establishment stage.”

“Disciples at the Foundation Establishment stage, after passing the true transmission assessment, can learn many true transmission secret techniques in the sect, crafting life-space magical tools to store their pets within…”

Lin Jing thought, “It seems I need to improve my cultivation as soon as possible.”

“I can’t learn any more advanced techniques right now.”

“Chirp!” The Pine Leaf Mouse nodded, already at the third level of Qi Training, while Lin Jing still looked like a mortal. For Lin Jing, the urgent matter was indeed to elevate his cultivation.

“The spirit stones contain pure spiritual energy. With so many spirit stones, I should be able to reach the first level of Qi Training soon,” Lin Jing said.

The Pine Leaf Mouse nodded again in agreement.

It lifted a spirit stone given to Lin Jing by the sect and held it up, signaling Lin Jing to hurry up and cultivate.

It was also waiting for their mutual contract so that they could work together to earn more spirit stones to buy a storage bag.

The sect had taught that disciples cultivating the Beast Taming Technique, in conjunction with various beast-taming methods, could significantly enhance the capabilities of their spirit beasts.

Lin Jing stopped hesitating and picked up the jade slip.

He closed his eyes and recited the corresponding incantation.

Soon, the jade slip flickered, and the cultivation information surged directly into his mind.

Beast Taming Technique, Qi Training Chapter!

There are a total of nine levels.

At the first level, one can contract with a pet. Some of the pet’s cultivation achievements will feedback to the cultivator.

In addition, the cultivator can also communicate spiritually with the pet.

Once they reach the seventh level, they will be able to master the beast-taming methods to transfer their true Qi to the pet, or allow the pet to transfer its spiritual or demonic power back to themselves, quickly recovering their own true Qi.

The basic techniques recorded in the Qi Training chapter of the Beast Taming Technique include how to use spirit stones and the fundamental skills for enhancing Qi Training cultivation.

To cultivate other beast-taming methods, one must reach specific standards and apply to the sect for learning.

For Lin Jing, having this basic Beast Taming Technique was enough at the moment.

The specific breathing methods that came with the jade slip, along with the techniques for using spirit stones for cultivation, were sufficient for him to step into the door of cultivation.

Taking a deep breath, he grasped the spirit stone. The spirit stone in his hand shimmered slightly, and mysterious whirlwinds surrounded him.

However.

While Lin Jing was cultivating, and the Pine Leaf Mouse was waiting for him to begin, neither seemed to realize that the longevity body’s cultivation was indeed very

slow.

The Pine Leaf Mouse quietly waited for Lin Jing, and after a while, it began to cultivate as well.

Until…

A few days later.

The spirit stones given to Lin Jing by the sect had all been used up.

“I feel like I’m almost there,” he said as he looked at the confused Pine Leaf Mouse.

After a moment of overload, the Pine Leaf Mouse tentatively took its remaining spirit stones and asked Lin Jing if he wanted to use them.

Lin Jing gratefully accepted.

Half a month after entering the sect, Lin Jing became increasingly proficient at transforming spiritual energy.

However, the Pine Leaf Mouse’s accumulated resources were nearly depleted.

There were only about three or four spirit stones left.

Yet Lin Jing still showed no signs of success in Qi Training.

Seeing this, the little squirrel’s eyes turned red as it was pushed to its limits, its paws waving frantically.

This was no longer just a matter of being slow.

It had hoarded spirit stones for so long… and most of the spirit rice had been consumed by Lin Jing.

It regretted ever suggesting to give spirit stones.

If it had thrown them in the pond outside, at least it could have seen the splashes.

The Pine Leaf Mouse was on the verge of tears, but just then, Lin Jing suddenly opened his eyes, exhaling a breath of turbid air. A surge of life force circulated from within, and a drop of golden blood condensed in his body. He had successfully reached the first level of Qi Training!

After half a month, he finally succeeded!

Lin Jing took a deep breath, feeling this sensation… he looked at the Pine Leaf Mouse and couldn’t help but share his joy with it.

But all he saw was the Pine Leaf Mouse looking at him with a face full of despair.

Lin Jing silently gazed at the pile of spirit stone dust on the ground, saying, “It won’t be long before I’ll repay you double!”

The Pine Leaf Mouse didn’t know whether it heard that or not, tremblingly holding a handful of spirit stone dust.

“Chirp…”

Really?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 6: Mutation
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 6: Mutation

Lotus Flower Peak!

Elder Mo sat at a stone table, sipping spirit tea.

A large black bear monster wearing a white robe ran towards him.

“Master, the longevity body that I brought back with that kid Ouyang Hao seems to have successfully reached Qi Training!”

Upon hearing this, Elder Mo nodded.

“Faster than I expected.”

“This longevity body is indeed difficult to cultivate.”

“However, it’s worth it; Qi Training cultivators can easily live past a hundred years, and if he continues like this, living to a thousand years is not surprising.”

Unlike those with cultivation physiques that display their power during Qi Training or Foundation Establishment...

The longevity body’s effects can only be observed over a long period.

“I just don’t know if he can successfully mutate that squirrel…” The black bear monster scratched its head.

“Yes, if successful, he could soar to great heights without waiting for thousands of years…” Elder Mo’s serious expression softened as he nervously said, “I have already explained to him the differences between spirit beasts, demon beasts, rare beasts, and immortal beasts.”

“However, I didn’t tell him that any special cultivation physique that contracts a pet beast has a certain probability of activating the hidden powers in their bloodline, allowing the pet to undergo ‘racial mutation’ and evolve into a rare beast, awakening a unique divine ability.”

“Forming a blood contract with a cultivator of special physique is the second chance for ordinary spirit beasts and demon beasts to mutate into rare beasts.”

“Unfortunately, the greater the expectation, the greater the disappointment. Since I learned this information, up to Ouyang Hao, no one has succeeded with so many special spirit bodies brought back.”

“I just don’t know how this longevity body will turn out.” Elder Mo’s expression grew impatient as he said, “This time… let’s hope for success!”

Saying this, Elder Mo hurried off to worship his ancestors, lighting incense and praying.

“O Ancestors of the Sect above!”

“We’ve already failed six pairs; it’s time for a success.”

The black bear monster sighed.

“Master, I feel like you’ve definitely been tricked by the people at the Heavenly Machine Tower… The information might be false; don’t gamble on it.”

…………

In the Outer Courtyard!

Inside a room, Lin Jing was holding the Pine Leaf Mouse's pitiful little face.

“Chirp!” The Pine Leaf Mouse wriggled out of Lin Jing’s hand, bravely wiping its tears.

It had chosen its Beast Tamer…

So what if the cultivation was slow?

After eating so many spirit stones, even a pig could fly.

It chose to believe in Lin Jing.

With a solid foundation, the master’s physique must be extraordinary.

These spirit stones would definitely earn back double.

The Pine Leaf Mouse closed its eyes, giving itself a mental pep talk as it waited for the contract.

Meanwhile, Lin Jing felt the vast life force within him; he completely ignored the slow cultivation.

There are gains and losses.

Moreover, he believed that this terrifying life force brought more than just the benefit of longevity.

Since his flesh and blood were a great supplement for demon beasts,

Then, from another perspective, as long as he was willing, he could also be a great supplement for his pet beast.

Of course, directly bleeding or cutting his flesh for his pet was too cruel; he was a normal person and couldn’t accept that.

Thus, he had previously considered whether it was possible to use his blood to refine pills to enhance his pet.

By refining pills, he could weaken the connection between the pills and himself while preserving the nourishing effect, so his pet wouldn’t develop negative feelings towards him…

However, this still sounded like a technique of a demonic cultivator, and he wasn’t familiar with alchemy yet, so it was better to wait until he understood more before researching it.

Lin Jing’s gaze flickered; he was still just a small Qi Training cultivator, and such tactics weren’t suitable for him at the moment.

“Let’s form the contract.” Lin Jing smiled.

Thanks to this little gathering mouse, otherwise, even with the treatment of an inner disciple, it would have taken him much longer to succeed in Qi Training…

He would compensate it later.

Lin Jing began to mobilize the special blood according to the methods recorded in the Beast Taming Technique.

He made a cut on the tip of his finger.

Using true Qi, he forced the special blood out of his body.

Soon, a drop of golden blood became faintly visible at the tip of his finger.

As Lin Jing started to bleed, the Pine Leaf Mouse, who had closed its eyes in anticipation of the contract, suddenly opened its eyes.

Its nose twitched slightly.

Its big eyes looked at Lin Jing.

Master, you smell so good.

Before it could savor the scent, Lin Jing’s finger had already tapped its forehead.

“Don’t struggle.”

“You wouldn’t want me to suffer a backlash and have my cultivation decline, right?”

Startled, the Pine Leaf Mouse didn’t struggle, fearing Lin Jing’s realm would drop.

After all, this was all cultivation earned from its spirit stones!

Buzz!

The golden blood merged into the Pine Leaf Mouse’s body.

The two began to form a blood contract.

Just as the Pine Leaf Mouse sensed the connection between it and Lin Jing growing closer…

Suddenly.

It felt as if it had plunged into a dream, its thoughts drifting back to the time when it was still in the pine forest, meditating on a tree while bathing in starlight…

In that moment, the starlight fell from the night sky onto the earth, and some faint light landed on it.

Time flew by, and as dawn broke, the starlight did not completely vanish; it seemed to have hidden within the bloodline of the Pine Leaf Mouse, waiting for a chance to be awakened.

Boom!

With the contract completed, the Pine Leaf Mouse opened its eyes, its spiritual energy surged, and its pupils grew even greener.

“Chirp?” However, it was still in a daze, not knowing what had happened. The teachings from the sect didn’t mention such abnormalities during the contract process.

“Hm?”

Besides the Pine Leaf Mouse, Lin Jing also saw that scene.

What was that?

As the contract completed, Lin Jing was stunned.

“Is there such an anomaly during the contract process?”

He was puzzled.

In the knowledge Lin Jing had learned, the process of contracting an ordinary spirit beast should be quite calm.

Starlight…

Wait, the starry sky?

Suddenly, Lin Jing recalled something.

Only Elder Mo had mentioned something related to the starry sky… the rare beasts!

Rare beasts are born from the light of foreign stars, and compared to ordinary spirit beasts and demon beasts, they awaken an additional divine ability!

But right now, the Pine Leaf Mouse hadn’t bathed in starlight; could it have the potential to mutate even after having bathed before?

So right now…

Could the Pine Leaf Mouse be mutating from an ordinary spirit beast to a rare beast?

Lin Jing looked at the Pine Leaf Mouse.

If that were true, what he wanted to know even more was what new divine ability the Pine Leaf Mouse would awaken.

The Pine Leaf Mouse clan has a natural affinity for wood attribute energy, allowing it to accelerate the growth of plants.

However, this wasn’t a particularly unique talent, as most wood attribute spirit beasts would possess it.

Moreover, the Pine Leaf Mouse was quite weak in terms of offensive abilities; it could only use its spiritual energy to enhance leaves, using them as projectiles, which had limited power, known as the Flying Leaf Technique.

In this situation, the Pine Leaf Mouse clan’s inherent ability to grow stronger was very slim; they could only learn powerful spells later.

However, now, if it awakened a divine ability without learning spells, it might be a chance to change its fate for this ordinary clan.

“Are you feeling unwell?”

“Could it be… a racial mutation? Did you awaken a new divine ability?” Lin Jing asked.

The Pine Leaf Mouse spun around in confusion, then shook its head, appearing very “clear-headed.”

“Chirp…”

This meant that if the cultivation was slow, one must work harder and not daydream about good things; it didn’t feel anything unusual at all.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 7: The Essence of Spellcraft
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 7: The Essence of Spellcraft

“Didn’t feel anything?”

“How could I not feel anything?”

Lin Jing didn’t believe it.

An anomaly had appeared, yet you claim to feel nothing?

“Chirp…” The Pine Leaf Mouse retorted, its tone confident.

It truly felt nothing.

Lin Jing was taken aback, and suddenly understood.

“Maybe the awakening of your supernatural powers is too strong for you to grasp at your current cultivation level.”

The Pine Leaf Mouse: ???

It slightly opened its mouth; it knew its talent was decent, but it didn’t have Lin Jing's optimism.

Having successfully reached the first layer of Qi training, Lin Jing was in a good mood.

However, he wasn’t sure if it was because the Pine Leaf Mouse's realm was too low that he temporarily hadn’t felt the so-called beast taming feedback.

“By the way.” Lin Jing didn’t dwell on the matter any longer.

He suddenly remembered that according to the introduction in the “Sect Manual,” after an outer sect disciple successfully completes Qi training, they have the opportunity to select a basic spell to study from the Outer Sect Scripture Pavilion.

He was curious about what spells were available in the Scripture Pavilion of the Beast Taming Sect.

Since entering the Beast Taming Sect, it was Lin Jing's first time stepping out of the courtyard, accompanied by the Pine Leaf Mouse he had dragged along.

The Pine Leaf Mouse also needed to clear its mind and ease its complicated emotions.

On the way, they encountered several other outer sect disciples dressed in blue robes, but there was no interaction among them.

According to the map on the back of the sect manual, Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse soon arrived at their destination.

In front of the pavilion, a cluster of ancient trees surrounded them, a gentle breeze rustling the leaves, intertwined with the occasional long cry of a crane from the clouds, creating a serene atmosphere that seemed to cleanse the soul.

Nearby, a pavilion had three or five disciples discussing their cultivation, quite peacefully.

“This is the place…”

Just as Lin Jing was about to enter the pavilion, someone walked out first.

“Junior Brother Lin!” A familiar weak voice called out, and Lin Jing looked at the person in surprise.

Dressed in a white robe, the figure seemed somewhat frail, and the eyes revealed deep exhaustion and helplessness… it was none other than Senior Brother Ouyang!

“Senior Brother Ouyang? What are you doing here?” Lin Jing asked, astonished.

“I’m on a sect mission, stationed at the Outer Sect Scripture Pavilion, responsible for organizing spells and assisting the outer sect disciples in selecting spells,” Ouyang Hao smiled.

“What a coincidence! It seems you’ve succeeded in Qi training faster than I expected. Are you planning to select a spell? Do you need my help analyzing it?”

“Senior Brother Ouyang, you’re still so fond of analyzing things; this task suits you well,” Lin Jing remarked.

“No way, I’m low on spirit stones, and buying supplements every day is too costly, so I have to take on more tasks,” Ouyang Hao replied, shaking his head awkwardly.

“Let me take you in.”

As he spoke, he invited Lin Jing into the pavilion.

Once they entered the Scripture Pavilion together, the surrounding outer sect disciples suddenly began to whisper among themselves.

“Who is this outer sect disciple? He seems to be quite familiar with Senior Brother Ouyang! What’s their connection?”

“Senior Brother Ouyang is friendly with everyone, isn’t he?”

“It’s different; Senior Brother Ouyang even went out to greet that person. He’s a standout in the inner sect, a genius rumored to be capable of breaking through to the position of true inheritance! He usually wouldn’t treat just anyone this way, so their relationship must be significant.”

“Is this Senior Brother Ouyang really that impressive?” a newly joined disciple asked curiously.

“Of course! Senior Brother Ouyang has a contract with a fox demon. Fox demons are beasts that absorb human yang energy. Did you see his demeanor? Now you understand, the fox demon must be trained very well…”

…

Inside the Scripture Pavilion.

First floor.

Ouyang Hao introduced, “This floor contains books that can be borrowed directly, such as the history of the continent, the history of ancient countries, and the history of the sect. You can find all of that here. Let’s go to the second floor to select spells.”

He eagerly led Lin Jing forward.

“Those at the first layer of Qi training can choose one basic spell…”

“Actually, the outer sect's Scripture Pavilion doesn’t have any particularly good beast taming spells. Generally, outer sect disciples choose to learn minor spells that can also nourish their pets’ roots, bloodlines, and spiritual power to assist their growth.”

“This is the mainstream choice, most suitable for disciples of the Beast Taming Sect.”

Arriving on the second floor, Senior Brother Ouyang pointed to the most prominent shelf filled with jade slips.

Titles like “Nourishing Spirit Technique” and “Qi Nourishing Technique” were essentially spells that disciples use their true qi to nurture their pets’ spirit and blood, though some can also be used by themselves.

Lin Jing looked at another shelf.

The spells there, like “Fireball Technique,” “Object Control Technique,” “Binding Technique,” “Water Arrow Technique,” and “Quicksand Technique,” seemed much more normal.

“Senior Brother Ouyang, what about these?” Lin Jing asked.

Hearing this, Ouyang Hao followed Lin Jing's gaze and smiled.

“Well, they’re not unselectable, but these spells can only be cultivated by the cultivator themselves, and are of no use to pets. Generally, spiritual beasts won’t be able to learn them.”

“Your Pine Leaf Mouse would find it very difficult to learn the Wood Binding Technique. Even if you learn it, it wouldn’t help it much.”

“Most of the time, they still have to rely on their racial abilities.”

“Valuable spells that spiritual beasts can learn are mostly stored in the inner sect.”

Ouyang Hao glanced at the Pine Leaf Mouse, which puffed up its cheeks in discontent.

“Overall, these ‘normal spells’ are only suitable for human cultivators to learn. However, for us disciples of the Beast Taming Sect, normalcy is actually a huge trap.”

“Most disciples of the Beast Taming Sect are not suitable for cultivating mainstream techniques, so practicing these spells often yields little return for a lot of effort.”

“Since one’s energy is limited, it’s better to invest all that limited energy into nurturing their pets, enhancing their strength, and then waiting for feedback from their pets. This approach is often more efficient than cultivating oneself.”

“If your pet is a fighting ape or horned bull, you can choose auxiliary spells to nourish their physique. If it’s a Pine Leaf Mouse, you can choose auxiliary spells to nourish its spirit.”

“Of course, if Junior Brother Lin is really curious, selecting a ‘normal offensive or defensive spell’ is fine too.”

After all, you live long, and the cost of trial and error is low… Ouyang Hao mumbled in his heart.

“Is that so…” Before Lin Jing could make a choice, the Pine Leaf Mouse waved its paws frantically: “Chirp!!!”

It suddenly remembered that with Lin Jing’s cultivation speed, normal cultivation methods would be agonizingly slow. Diverting attention to learn offensive spells… wouldn’t that be life-threatening?

“Right!” At that moment, Ouyang Hao seemed to think of something and clapped his hands: “Junior Brother Lin, when you select spells, pay attention to ‘spell potential.’”

“Spell potential?”

“Yes, when choosing spells, try to pick ones that can accompany you throughout your life. For instance, the Object Control Technique, although basic, might still be used even by cultivators at the Nascent Soul stage. In contrast, some basic spells can easily be replaced once your realm is higher.”

“Junior Brother, I don’t know if you’ve heard of ‘Essence of Spellcraft’?”

Essence of Spellcraft? Lin Jing looked at him, shaking his head.

Ouyang Hao explained, “When a spell is cultivated to its peak, it can give birth to an essence that possesses consciousness.”

“Our Beast Taming Sect is located in the Great Wilderness of the Tianyuan Continent's Tianyuan Ancient Kingdom. While we are considered a first-rate sect within the Great Wilderness, we are only a third-rate sect in the entire Tianyuan Ancient Kingdom.”

“The leading sect of the Tianyuan Ancient Kingdom, the Spirit Path Sect, primarily cultivates path techniques. It’s said that many cultivators within that sect have comprehended the true essence of spells, reaching the pinnacle of spellcraft, and have been able to let their cultivated spells give birth to true essences.”

“The essence of spellcraft can communicate with the energy of heaven and earth, can attack automatically without relying on a cultivator’s magic power, can even be passed down to future generations to assist them in cultivating the corresponding spells, and can even protect them.”

“It can be said to be a ‘unique pet beast’ born from spells,” Ouyang Hao lamented: “Like the Xuanwu True Spirit cultivated by the Xuanwu Real Person of the Spirit Path Sect, relying on the Xuanwu Shield spell, it can rival ordinary precious beasts!”

“Perhaps in the future, you might also cultivate an essence of spellcraft and become the first in our sect!” Senior Brother Ouyang chuckled: “As long as you are dedicated enough! Focus on one spell, and comprehend it for half a lifetime.”

Lin Jing understood; Senior Brother Ouyang was suggesting that since he would live long, he could rely on time to refine it.

With a lifespan at least ten times longer than that of ordinary cultivators, it would indeed be easier to thoroughly comprehend a single spell.

“You can try it.” The more Ouyang Hao thought about it, the more plausible it seemed: “If your cultivation efficiency is too slow, you can always switch later.”

“Is it really possible for any spell to give birth to an essence?” Lin Jing was skeptical.

“Theoretically, yes,” Ouyang Hao replied earnestly.

Lin Jing pointed to a spell.

“Contraception Technique.”

“What about this one?”

Ouyang Hao glanced at it and was momentarily stunned.

The Contraception Technique is a spell that disciples of the Beast Taming Sect need to cultivate to prevent their pets from misbehaving when they go out.

After all, pregnant pets would deplete their vitality and hinder their cultivation.

“If cultivated to the extreme, can it give birth to a contraception essence?”

“Ah…” Ouyang Hao said, “Uhm, probably?”

“Doesn’t sound reliable,” Lin Jing shook his head.

His gaze swept across the various shelves, wondering about the essence of spellcraft…

“Let’s just focus on nurturing our pets for now!”

With that, Lin Jing walked to one shelf and picked up a basic recovery spell titled “Nourishing Spirit Technique,” which nourishes the spirit with true qi to alleviate fatigue.

The Pine Leaf Mouse’s racial talent allows it to hasten the growth of spiritual plants, a process that consumes a lot of spiritual power and can be exhausting.

This spell could help share some of the burden, speeding up the growth of spiritual plants. This was crucial for Lin Jing's farming plans; he needed to be self-sufficient in resources since he consumed too many!

Additionally, he planned to learn more about cultivation while training. He had just seen many books related to alchemy on the first floor, and this small spell to alleviate fatigue would also help him in his studies.

In summary, this spell could not only assist his pet but would also be suitable for him to accelerate his absorption of alchemical knowledge. He needed to establish a solid foundation for alchemy through blood refinement; he certainly couldn’t be unaware of the uses of some materials.

“What… why are you suddenly so rational?” Senior Brother Ouyang was puzzled. From his impression of this Junior Brother Lin, he didn’t seem like someone who would choose a normal spell. He nearly thought Lin Jing was going to select the Contraception Technique!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 8: Farming
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 8: Farming

“It seems Senior Brother is quite disappointed with our choice of ‘Nourishing Spirit Technique.’”

“It must be because he left with some wrong impression before.”

Leaving the Scripture Pavilion, Lin Jing not only took away the jade slip for the Nourishing Spirit Technique but also several history books, and most importantly, the basic alchemy technique (Plant and Herb Edition)!

Mastering the effects of various spiritual plants is one of the basic skills in alchemy.

“Outer sect disciples who succeed in Qi training can select one basic spell, but to learn a second spell, they must reach the fourth layer of Qi training.”

“However, since I have the privileges of an inner sect disciple, I shouldn’t have to adhere to this rule…”

“Never mind, let’s put aside the spells for now. There is a reason why the sect limits the number of spells disciples can learn; one can’t be greedy and expect to master everything.”

Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse both rubbed their stomachs.

After half a month of hard training.

Not only were their spirit stones nearly gone,

But they had also consumed almost all the spiritual rice from their bag, leaving very little.

Although the outer sect has a canteen that provides various spiritual delicacies infused with spirit energy, it all costs money.

Ordinary food is not provided by the Beast Taming Sect, and disciples need to go to the towns around the mountain gate to buy ingredients or hunt beasts in the nearby mountains themselves.

To speed up their training, cultivators ideally choose food containing spirit energy for their meals. However, the canteen's prices were steep, and what Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse consumed for training was not enough to afford.

From what Lin Jing understood, there was one advantage to being a disciple who chose the Pine Leaf Mouse.

“We should buy some spiritual rice seeds and plant them in the courtyard. With your talents, we should be able to achieve self-sufficiency in spiritual rice, right?”

“Chirp!!!” The Pine Leaf Mouse nodded. Its kind had done this in the pine forest, finding edible plants and speeding up their growth.

They could harvest food quickly without waiting around like other races.

“However, although spiritual rice is delicious and filling, eating it for half a month might be a bit monotonous.”

“After we buy the seeds, let’s head down the mountain to see if there’s cheap beast meat for sale, or we can hunt some wild game in the mountains.” Lin Jing sighed.

Even some free pickled vegetables would be nice.

……

Outer sect, Harvest Pavilion.

Like the Scripture Pavilion, it is also one of the Beast Taming Sect's basic facilities.

It mainly sells agricultural products.

The entire sect operates through a chain.

When Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse entered the store, a weak-looking outer sect sister came out to greet them.

“What do you need, Junior Brother?” she asked slowly.

It seemed she was another worker here.

Before coming to the Harvest Pavilion, Lin Jing had taken the Pine Leaf Mouse to the Task Hall and made a round.

He discovered that there were various sect tasks covering all aspects.

In addition to mining, farming, and cooking… there were even matchmaking tasks, prompting disciples to find partners for their pets.

Then they could cultivate high-quality second-generation spiritual beasts, achieving a cycle of pets within the sect.

However, from what Lin Jing saw, not many people seemed to take on these types of tasks.

There were also tasks like Senior Brother Ouyang’s, which allowed him to work at a certain facility within the sect.

These types of tasks are easy and well-paid, but they are limited in number and set with certain requirements. Without sufficient strength, one couldn’t even hope to compete for them.

Most disciples could only go to the mines to earn meager spirit stones.

As for the sister in front of him, being able to serve as a greeter at the Harvest Pavilion and snag such an easy task indicated that she likely had some skills.

But why did they all look so weak?

Lin Jing pondered.

“Hello, Sister.”

“I’d like to buy some spiritual rice seeds,” Lin Jing said.

The sister glanced at the Pine Leaf Mouse and nodded, “Planning to grow them yourself, right?”

“Yes.”

“Would you like to buy some fertilizer produced by foundational spiritual beasts? Paired with the Pine Leaf Mouse’s spiritual energy to hasten the growth, it could yield a higher harvest of spiritual rice,” she suggested.

It seems that as long as one’s cultivation level is high, everything on them is precious.

But he patted his pocket filled with a few spirit stones and shook his head.

Moments later, he and the Pine Leaf Mouse spent all their spirit stones and returned with a bag of “spiritual rice seeds” and “spiritual bean seeds.”

The former would produce spiritual rice…

While the latter was a spiritual plant similar to peanuts and was one of the Pine Leaf Mouse’s favorite foods.

“Now we’re completely out of money…”

Before long, Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse returned to the courtyard.

Hearing Lin Jing’s self-talk, the Pine Leaf Mouse wanted to cry in pain, realizing that those spirit stones were hoarded since it was born not long ago!

“Don’t worry.”

“Once we successfully start our farming venture, not only can we be self-sufficient, but any surplus spiritual rice and beans can also be sold, and we’ll quickly earn back the spent spirit stones…”

Lin Jing wasn’t sure how quickly that would be, but he comforted himself saying that.

“Chirp?”

Hearing this, the Pine Leaf Mouse quickly wiped its tears and regained hope for the future.

Seeing this, Lin Jing felt deeply moved.

“This child is so optimistic, just like me!”

……

Lin Jing was not familiar with farming, but the Pine Leaf Mouse was an expert.

He handed all the seeds to the Pine Leaf Mouse, which then began to search for open ground.

As the Pine Leaf Mouse buried the seeds and used its large tail to press down on the soil, infusing green spiritual energy to hasten the growth of the seeds, Lin Jing had already picked up three history books he brought from the first floor of the Scripture Pavilion.

**“A Brief History of the Tianyuan Continent”**

**“History of Ancient Countries”**

**“History of the Beast Taming Sect”**

He opened “A Brief History of the Tianyuan Continent.”

“In ancient times, the Tianyuan Continent was one of the lower realms ruled by the celestial force ‘Celestial Palace.’”

“Hundred thousand years ago, due to an unknown turmoil in the celestial realm, the Celestial Palace was drawn into the war, and the ascension passage of the Tianyuan Continent vanished. Since then, the Tianyuan Continent has been unable to connect with the celestial realm, and no cultivator could ascend.”

“At that time, the Celestial Palace’s force on the Tianyuan Continent, ‘Tianyuan Celestial Palace,’ dominated the continent, and all clans were subservient to its rule.”

“Fifty thousand years ago, as the celestial realm could not be contacted for a long time, the Celestial Palace on the Tianyuan Continent gradually weakened with the flow of time. To maintain its ruling position, the Celestial Palace attempted to forcefully breach the realm, overexhausting the resources of the Tianyuan Continent, leading to immense suffering among the beings.”

“At that time, four major sects united to overthrow the absolute rule of the Celestial Palace over the Tianyuan Continent, thus ending the era of the Celestial Palace.”

“Later, the four major sects formed the ‘Celestial Alliance,’ taking over the rule of the Celestial Palace, becoming the new overlord of the Tianyuan Continent, ushering the continent into the era of the Celestial Alliance.”

“History is cyclical. Although the Tianyuan Continent’s cultivation world flourished under the rule of the Celestial Alliance, it ultimately could not escape the fate of the Celestial Palace.”

“Towards the end of the era of the Celestial Alliance, another exceptionally talented cultivator appeared. He not only successfully destroyed the Celestial Alliance but also established the ‘Tianyuan Ancient Kingdom’ and developed the ‘True Dragon Secret Technique,’ binding his cultivation and infinite territory together, allowing the people to prosper and the land to be rich, while he himself became even stronger.”

“He called himself the Ancient Emperor, and with the True Dragon Secret Technique, he nurtured the True Dragon of Fate, becoming the strongest on the Tianyuan Continent.”

“The Tianyuan Ancient Kingdom now occupies the most central, largest, and richest area of the Tianyuan Continent.”

“Apart from the Tianyuan Ancient Kingdom, surrounding regions such as Qingzhou, Wudi City, Shuiyue Xian Island, and the Snow Plains are not under the ancient kingdom’s jurisdiction, yet they have powerful cultivators stationed.”

“And the place I am in now is one of the nine domains of the ancient kingdom, the Great Wilderness Domain… where the spiritual energy is relatively thin, and the highest activity is from Nascent Soul cultivators.”

After reading through, Lin Jing found it truly to be a brief history. They didn’t even record which four sects made up the Celestial Alliance!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 9: Glazed Immortal Eyes
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 9: Glazed Immortal Eyes

In the following days, after Lin Jing learned the Spirit Nurturing Technique, he stopped his cultivation and devoted himself to reading various books to understand the world of immortality.

The main reason was that his cultivation efficiency was too low.

Compared to cultivating by himself, he was waiting for the Spirit Power feedback from the Pine Leaf Mouse after its breakthrough to enhance his own cultivation!

This was the normal way for disciples of the Beast Control Sect to become stronger.

Currently, although the Pine Leaf Mouse was not absorbing Spirit Qi normally, its frequent use of Spirit Power to accelerate the growth of spiritual rice was also a form of training for its Spirit Power.

After several days, the spiritual rice had grown tall and was close to maturing.

One had to admit that the talent of his Pine Leaf Mouse was indeed impressive.

From various books, Lin Jing learned that an ordinary Pine Leaf Mouse at the third level of Qi Refinement, without external assistance, would take about half a month to mature a stalk of spiritual rice.

Although he occasionally used a bit of true qi to relieve the Pine Leaf Mouse's mental fatigue through the Spirit Nurturing Technique, as a mere first level Qi Refinement practitioner, his help was minimal.

Fortunately, the Pine Leaf Mouse had cultivated extremely pure Spirit Power and was patient, never slacking off.

“You're simply a natural-born farming saint.” Lin Jing closed the book and generously praised the Pine Leaf Mouse, which was busy accelerating the growth of the spiritual rice.

“Zhi!”

Although the Pine Leaf Mouse did not understand what a natural-born farming saint was, it knew it was praise and became even more motivated.

With its emerald green eyes, it eagerly looked at a stalk of spiritual rice before it.

Buzz!

Not long after, the spiritual rice finally changed from a milky white to a golden color, emitting a rich rice aroma, which was a sign that it had matured!!

“Did it succeed?”

With the spiritual rice fully matured, Lin Jing immediately rushed to the side of the Pine Leaf Mouse and together they peeled back the rice husk, revealing translucent and plump white spiritual rice inside, looking incredibly tempting!

“In terms of quality, it’s not much different from the bag we received.”

Lin Jing’s heart, which had been hanging in suspense, finally relaxed.

It was stable!

“Zhi Zhi Zhi!” The Pine Leaf Mouse nodded happily.

As expected of it.

Next, Lin Jing and the others immediately separated the remaining rice husks, and in the end, thousands of grains of spiritual rice appeared on the ground, gleaming brightly.

Looking at the pile of spiritual rice, Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse were in an exceptionally good mood. Although this wouldn’t last long, it was a very good start.

The Pine Leaf Mouse looked at the pile of spiritual rice.

Then it looked at Lin Jing.

Its intention was clear.

“Storing food is a good habit.”

“However, we still need to eat these.”

“How about this, you can take 100 grains.”
