As if seeing through the Pine Leaf Mouse’s thoughts, Lin Jing said with a smile.

After all, these were the results of the Pine Leaf Mouse’s hard work. Taking out 100 grains wasn’t a big deal and could satisfy the Pine Leaf Mouse’s hoarding habit.

They wouldn’t starve to death just because they took 100 grains away.

Who knows, if they were ever poor enough to eat dirt, those 100 grains might come in handy.

Let it hoard!

“Zhi……” As Lin Jing finished speaking, the Pine Leaf Mouse was overjoyed and immediately ran over to take 100 grains, setting them aside in a separate pile.

Although 100 grains of spiritual rice was not much,

Looking at this pile of rice that belonged to it, the Pine Leaf Mouse felt completely at ease, as if its energy pores had been opened, and its strength and Spirit Power surged.

However, just then, an unexpected change occurred.

The Pine Leaf Mouse’s emerald green eyes suddenly radiated a light, making its pupils shine like emeralds.

Then, in the panic of the Pine Leaf Mouse and the surprise of Lin Jing,

Two beams of emerald light shot from the Pine Leaf Mouse’s eyes.

They landed on the 100 grains of spiritual rice.

Immediately, something astonishing happened; wrapped in the green light, the 100 grains of spiritual rice vanished in an instant.

As the green light dissipated, what lay on the ground was a single new golden spiritual rice grain…

…………

At the same time.

In the mind of the Pine Leaf Mouse,

A mysterious inheritance message appeared.

It was as if a divine ability hidden in its bloodline had awakened.

Allowing it to understand without a teacher.

**"Glazed Immortal Eyes"**

Even a pile of worthless stones, when seen through the Glazed Immortal Eyes, would be transformed into precious glazed gems.

The Glazed Immortal Eyes could merge a certain quantity of the same type of resources into another precious resource.

Like a celestial technique, turning stone into gold.

…………

“Zhi?”

In the eyes of the Pine Leaf Mouse and Lin Jing, the 100 grains of white spiritual rice transformed into a single golden, mysterious spiritual rice grain.

Such a change shocked both Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse.

“I knew there was something special about the changes on the day of the contract.”

“You have indeed transformed from an ordinary spirit beast into a rare beast.”

“The divine ability of the rare beast is right here!”

Turning the rotten into the miraculous, like turning stone into gold; a method that turns quantitative changes into qualitative changes!

This golden spiritual rice grain looked obviously more valuable than the white spiritual rice!

Lin Jing couldn’t help but exclaim.

But the next moment, he couldn’t hold back.

He saw the Pine Leaf Mouse directly kneeling on the ground, picking up the golden spiritual rice grain, tears streaming down its face.

“Zhi!!!”

It didn’t want one golden spiritual rice grain; it wanted 100 grains of white spiritual rice!!!

Lin Jing twitched his mouth.

This little mouse seemed to have fallen into a deep obsession.

This golden spiritual rice looked much more precious!

Did it really care that much for its 100 grains of white spiritual rice?

“Alright.”

“I'll give you another 200 grains of white spiritual rice in exchange…”

Lin Jing was about to say that he would use 200 grains of ordinary spiritual rice to exchange for that golden spiritual rice grain for research.

Unexpectedly, the Pine Leaf Mouse, filled with sorrow, glared fiercely at the golden spiritual rice grain.

Then it bit down hard, crushing it and swallowing it!

As if to take revenge.

“Whoa, you just ate it like that.” Lin Jing was shocked again.

He didn’t even know its effects.

“A grain of golden spiritual rice formed from 100 grains of ordinary spiritual rice should, in theory, have effects more than 100 times that of ordinary spiritual rice, right?”

“Otherwise, this divine ability would be meaningless, and the so-called rare beast would be a misnomer.”

“It’s not as if it was 100 bowls of rice; it shouldn’t burst its stomach.”

Thinking of this, Lin Jing relaxed a bit.

“If its effects can rival that of a spirit stone, that would be great.”

Lin Jing had initially thought that this golden spiritual rice grain would be like a spirit stone, providing substantial benefits for cultivation.

Unfortunately, the reality was not so.

After consuming a grain of spirit-rich golden spiritual rice, the Pine Leaf Mouse’s eyes actually flashed with a hint of scarlet, and instead of feeling full, a strong sense of hunger spread throughout its heart.

Then, under Lin Jing’s silent gaze, it seemed like a starving ghost, with its stomach growling thunderously, rushing toward the pile of spiritual rice, madly stuffing the freshly produced spiritual rice into its mouth.

Due to the bloodline contract with the Pine Leaf Mouse, Lin Jing could sense its current state.

Hungry. Very hungry.

The effect of that golden spiritual rice was not to satiate hunger, but to induce a feeling of hunger in the consumer!

Driven by this hunger, the Pine Leaf Mouse went into a feeding frenzy, rapidly shoving spiritual rice into its mouth. In the past, it would have immediately been stuffed and unable to quickly digest the nutrition and Spirit Power within.

But this time, its digestive ability seemed to have undergone a terrifying enhancement. The entire pile of spiritual rice was swept away, and not only did it appear completely unaffected, but its own aura also continued to rise, as if… it had instantaneously digested all the nutrition of the spiritual rice and converted it into its own power!

Only after consuming everything did the Pine Leaf Mouse’s gluttonous frenzy gradually come to a halt, returning to a state of clarity…

“Enhanced digestive ability, rapid absorption of nutrients? The effect of the golden spiritual rice… is actually this powerful.” Lin Jing took a deep breath. This was much stronger than merely enhancing the quality of spiritual rice. What exactly was this rare beast’s divine ability…

Was it an inheritance of an immortal, transformed by an alien race due to starlight? Could this be considered a form of immortal inheritance?

“Regardless, as Elder Mo said, rare beasts are indeed incredibly powerful. We won’t be short on money in the future.” He sighed.

However… Lin Jing was happy, but when the Pine Leaf Mouse regained consciousness and saw that all its hard-earned spiritual rice had vanished, it widened its eyes in confusion, including the 200 grains Lin Jing had just given it… all of it was gone.

The plan to hoard food was shattered once again!

“Zhi!!!!” In the courtyard, the Pine Leaf Mouse let out a heart-wrenching cry. What a useless divine ability, it can’t hoard anything at all! Why couldn’t it awaken a spatial

divine ability!!!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 10: Flying Leaves!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 10: Flying Leaves!

A squirrel that loved to hoard food had awakened a divine ability to synthesize resources, and Lin Jing didn’t know what to say.

Wasn't this bullying a child, forcing it to be unable to hoard anything?

Seeing the Pine Leaf Mouse’s heart-wrenching appearance, Lin Jing couldn't help but smile slightly.

The Pine Leaf Mouse immediately stopped crying, its gaze fixed on Lin Jing.

Lin Jing laughed, “I’m happy for you. From today on, you’re no longer an ordinary spirit beast but a rare beast!”

“Zhi!” The Pine Leaf Mouse was still thinking about the spiritual rice.

“Think positively; your spiritual rice has just changed forms and has been stored away, turning into Spirit Power that will be with you forever. Do you feel your Spirit Power has increased significantly?”

Lin Jing’s words startled the Pine Leaf Mouse awake; it finally noticed that it was close to the fourth level of Qi Refinement, filled with abundant physical and spiritual power.

Lin Jing continued to enlighten it; otherwise, with such a divine ability and its current temperament, the Pine Leaf Mouse's mentality might continue to collapse in the future.

It needed to change its bad habit of only valuing “quantity” over “quality.”

At least it should consider both.

“This divine ability is great; you should practice it more.”

“Just think, spiritual rice may be scarce for us now, but once we reach the Foundation Establishment and Core Formation stages, we can have as much as we want.”

“Even with storage bags or storage rings, hoarding spiritual rice would be too wasteful.”

“Not every resource should be hoarded.”

“Now that you have this eye technique, since it can combine a certain number of low-level resources into a higher-level resource, we should only hoard high-level resources in the future.”

“You saw the effects of the golden spiritual rice.”

“If we ever acquire high-level spiritual fruits or high-level pills that are hard to digest, this golden spiritual rice could very well be the key for us to consume high-level resources!”

“Zhi!” The Pine Leaf Mouse found some reason in that.

“Moreover, now that you’ve mutated into a rare beast, you’ll definitely attract the attention of many bad people.”

“This golden spiritual rice… can also be used as a weapon!”

“In that state of hunger earlier, you nearly lost your sanity. If we feed a few grains of golden spiritual rice to an enemy, along with a few hundred doses of laxatives, we might win without a fight!”

The Pine Leaf Mouse stepped back, looking up. Feeding laxative rice to the enemy?

That made sense!

Once the storage bag arrived, it would have to stockpile a few hundred doses of laxatives!

But getting the spiritual rice into the enemy's mouth seemed a bit difficult?

“Zhi!?”

“Uh.” Lin Jing was momentarily taken aback; he had said it casually, but the little squirrel seemed to be pondering the feasibility.

“It’s feasible.” Lin Jing added, “Don’t forget your Pine Leaf Mouse’s specialty, Flying Leaves!”

“As long as you throw the leaves accurately, you can surely throw the spiritual rice accurately too.”

Upon hearing this, the Pine Leaf Mouse contemplated.

That made sense!

It immediately picked up a fallen leaf from the ground, its gaze sharp as it looked to the side, and waved its little hand!

In the next instant, the leaf flew out, transforming into a green light that cut down a branch from the tree in the courtyard!

Smack!

The branch fell to the ground.

In Lin Jing’s past life, various martial arts stories featured techniques like “flying leaves can hurt people,” which were quite impressive. However, in this immortal cultivation world, that technique was merely the basic attack method of the Pine Leaf Mouse.

Seeing that its skill hadn’t dulled, the Pine Leaf Mouse nodded in satisfaction.

Watching this, Lin Jing stroked his chin and said, “I wonder if a pile of ordinary leaves can be combined with your eye technique.”

With an idea in mind, Lin Jing took action immediately.

Using his true qi, he kicked the leaves from a few green trees in the courtyard, causing all the leaves to fall.

A ground covered in fallen leaves left the trees looking bare.

The Pine Leaf Mouse fell silent upon seeing this.

“Zhi Zhi Zhi!”

Leaves like these had been in abundance in the place it once lived, holding little value. Even as weapons for its attacks, it had never hoarded them.

It turned out the Pine Leaf Mouse really didn’t hoard everything!

But now… following Lin Jing’s idea, it swept its Glazed Immortal Eyes over the ground full of fallen leaves.

It was curious whether ordinary leaves could be synthesized.

In the next moment, two beams of emerald light shot from the Pine Leaf Mouse’s eyes.

They landed on thousands of ordinary leaves.

Then, a similar scene unfolded; under the coverage of the emerald light, these thousands of ordinary green leaves disappeared just like the spiritual rice had!

What appeared instead was a single red leaf, reminiscent of a maple leaf.

Although this red leaf emanated a Spirit Qi fluctuation weaker than an ordinary grain of spiritual rice, Lin Jing was pleasantly surprised to see that it had indeed succeeded and immediately called to the Pine Leaf Mouse, “Throw it and see!”

“Zhi Zhi…” The Pine Leaf Mouse scampered to pick up the red leaf, but as soon as it grasped it, its eyes felt a bit sore, possibly due to excessive use of its eye power, making it quite uncomfortable.

Seeing this, Lin Jing quickly stepped forward and placed his hand on the Pine Leaf Mouse’s head, activating the “Spirit Nurturing Technique.” A glow covered the Pine Leaf Mouse’s head, alleviating its mental fatigue.

“Are your eyes okay? I overlooked that you’re only at the third level of Qi Refinement, and using this divine ability continuously must take a toll.” He grew worried.

After nearly a month of companionship, he certainly didn’t want anything to happen to this little squirrel.

“Zhi!” The Pine Leaf Mouse rubbed its eyes and then pushed Lin Jing’s hand away. Although it felt unwell, it was even more eager to understand the power of this red leaf!

With the red leaf in hand, it vaguely sensed a very close connection with it, as if it was being refined by itself!

Whoosh!

The Pine Leaf Mouse aimed at the bare tree and threw the leaf. In the next moment, a flash of red light occurred, and this time, the flying leaf’s speed far surpassed that of the previous green leaves. In the blink of an eye, a thick branch fell, smooth to the touch.

And this was not the end.

The previous green flying leaves lost power and fell shortly after cutting off a branch, but this time, the red flying leaves not only continued flying further but the Pine Leaf Mouse also widened its eyes, discovering something incredible.

The Flying Leaves Technique was used by the Pine Leaf Mouse to launch leaves with its Spirit Power, which would then attack in a straight line.

But this time, with the red flying leaves, the Pine Leaf Mouse realized it could actually control the trajectory of the leaf’s flight at will, even calling it back!

With a thought, the red flying leaf instantly turned and flew back toward them, cutting through a stone block beneath a tree in the process.

Whoosh! Whoosh! Whoosh!

Under the Pine Leaf Mouse’s focused control, the red flying leaf skimmed the ground, spinning in the air as it went.

“Zhi!!!” This scene made the Pine Leaf Mouse’s eyes sparkle!

Lin Jing was also taken aback. Elder Mo had mentioned that the Pine Leaf Mouse tribe, compared to the strong Fighting Apes, Horned Cattle, and Fire Spirit Sparrows that could use fire techniques, were not particularly strong.

The Flying Leaves Technique had limited effectiveness in battle.

But now, the Flying Leaves Technique in the Pine Leaf Mouse’s hands seemed to be something entirely different.

This was simply a manipulation technique! A Leaf Manipulation Technique!

It was as if it had gained a life-bound magical treasure, the Red Leaf…

The more the Pine Leaf Mouse used it, the more enthralled it became. Pointing skyward, the red flying leaf shot into the sky as if it wanted to pierce the heavens, cutting down immortals!

“Who dares throw things carelessly in the sky?!” An angry voice echoed from the clouds, and a crane revealed its head.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 11: The Three Great Demon Sects
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 11: The Three Great Demon Sects

The crane stood tall, with long legs and its body covered in feathers as white as snow. On its head was a red patch, resembling the sun rising in the dawn, giving it an air of nobility.

It swooped down angrily, clutching the red flying leaves. This crane… judging by the accent, Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse were very familiar with it.

It seemed to be the celestial crane that had delivered resources to them when they first arrived at the outer sect, rather than the crane sister that had brought them down from the inner peak.

Referring to it as a celestial crane was somewhat inaccurate; through reading books, Lin Jing had already learned its species name.

Heavenly Feather Crane, a wind-type spirit beast, holds an extraordinary status in the Beast Taming Sect.

Because the sect master… had contracted one Heavenly Feather Crane!

“Zhi…” The Pine Leaf Mouse was extremely nervous upon seeing the leaves it had carelessly thrown hit the crane.

“Senior…” Lin Jing also showed an apologetic expression and said, “I’m very sorry; we were practicing spells.”

After saying that, it dropped a letter and flew back into the clouds, seemingly in a hurry, as if it were afraid of exceeding the time for its next delivery.

Seeing this, Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse breathed a sigh of relief, and Lin Jing curiously opened the letter.

“Zhi?”

After reading the letter, Lin Jing pondered, “Elder Mo said that the small base of the Yin Corpse Sect that kidnapped me has already reported my Longevity Body information but didn’t manage to transfer me. He advised me not to leave the Beast Taming Sect and not to accept sect missions that require leaving.”

“As long as we stay within the sect, we can remain safe.”

“As for him, he is no longer in the sect… but has gone to the Demon Extermination Division to understand the specific situation and see if he can uproot that higher-level demon sect base as well!”

“Zhi!” The Pine Leaf Mouse looked at Lin Jing with sympathy.

Lin Jing shook his head and said, “I really have bad luck. I’ve only just started my cultivation and am already targeted by the demon sect.”

“Don’t look at me with those eyes. Rare beasts are even scarcer than special cultivation physiques!”

“If we get caught, we might end up at the same dinner table.”

Now, Lin Jing was no longer a novice in the cultivation world and had a deeper understanding of the demon sects.

The three great demon sects in the cultivation world—Yin Corpse Sect, Gu God Sect, and Youming Hall—were all terrifying names.

Among them, the Yin Corpse Sect, which refines corpses and sells seedlings, seizes geniuses, and extracts their foundations, is a thorn in the side of every major sect and cultivation family. Which family doesn’t have a genius with a special physique?

Where there’s trade, there’s persecution. Although the failure rate of seizing and rebuilding the body is very high, many seriously injured high-level cultivators still refuse to resign to their fate, and the Yin Corpse Sect plays the role of a middleman.

The Gu God Sect raises gu worms, placing them in numerous mortals, ordinary cultivators, and even those with special physiques to nourish the gu with human bodies and enhance their powers, allowing them to evolve!

As for Youming Hall, it focuses on ghost cultivation, raising spirits and dispatching souls.

Fortunately, under the crackdown of the Demon Extermination Division of the Tiānyuán Ancient Country and various regional sects, the three great demon sects were like rats crossing the street, unable to see the light of day.

Lin Jing sighed. It was a relief that his Longevity Body, while rare, wasn’t particularly precious. Even if it attracted attention, it shouldn’t be the top priority.

“When Elder Mo returns, we’ll tell him about your mutated rare beast. Since I, as a special physique, can obtain inner sect-level resources, then you should also count as a ‘special physique’ now. Perhaps our resource treatment can be upgraded.”

As a new disciple entering the sect, Lin Jing could enjoy such good treatment. The Pine Leaf Mouse, born in the Beast Taming Sect and raised under its training, had parents who were also within the sect. Its connection to the sect was deeper than Lin Jing’s, so he believed the sect wouldn’t skimp on resources for its training.

“Zhi zhi zhi!!” The Pine Leaf Mouse nodded and then looked at the spirit bean seedlings nearby.

It was curious about what would be synthesized from these after planting 100 seeds and then converting them with the Glazed Immortal Eyes.

“You should stop…” Lin Jing interrupted, “We don’t have any rations left. The little bit of spirit rice we managed to plant has already been eaten by you.”

“We should go find something to eat. Otherwise… let alone the consumption for synthesizing new resources, you might starve during the spirit bean ripening process.”

“Ugh, let’s go hunt some game in the mountains and dig up some wild vegetables to fill our stomachs.” Lin Jing sighed.

Among the dozens of peaks in the Beast Taming Sect, a few were specially designated for raising some ferocious beasts—man-eating monsters kept for outer and inner sect disciples to train.

Lin Jing planned to take the Pine Leaf Mouse to one of those mountains that outer sect disciples could enter, to catch a few wild rabbits and fish.

If it had been before, he wouldn’t have dared to take the risk. But now, the Pine Leaf Mouse had mastered the red flying leaves, which could cut through steel like mud, and ordinary mountain beasts and low-level monsters posed no threat to them anymore.

…

After a break, Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse, armed with the red flying leaves, left the courtyard and began their journey toward “Tiger Peak” according to the map.

This peak had man-eating tigers prowling about, but only a few had the demonic energy equivalent to “first layer of Qi training.” The Beast Taming Sect only allowed disciples at the “first layer of Qi training” to bring pets into the area to cultivate their courage.

These disciples were often just beginning their cultivation journey, still transitioning from a mortal mindset. Facing a monster would be daunting; they might even falter at the roar of a common mountain tiger.

However, with at least a third-layer pet beast protecting them, such disciples entering this peak typically wouldn’t encounter any accidents, making it a suitable place for mental training.

The most important part was that all the hunted resources belonged to the disciple.

Before long, Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse arrived at the foot of Tiger Peak. At the entrance, an old man in a coarse robe stood there. Upon seeing Lin Jing, he waved his hand.

“Outer sect disciple? Want to enter Tiger Peak?” Lin Jing approached, and the old man examined Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse.

“Elder, yes.” Lin Jing didn’t recognize the old man but guessed he was the peak guardian, as peaks like Tiger Peak had dedicated personnel.

“You cannot enter now; a spirit plant unexpectedly sprouted inside the mountain and was eaten by a tiger. Now that tiger has gained intelligence and possesses strong demonic power. It’s risky for you to enter.” The old man waved his hand and said, “Come back another day.”

“Elder, what level is that tiger?” Lin Jing asked. “Perhaps we can…”

“First layer of Qi training.”

“Elder, sorry to trouble you.” Lin Jing cupped his fists and turned to leave with the Pine Leaf Mouse.

What a world it was; after all the hard work in cultivation, he had only just reached the first layer of Qi training.

Yet a tiger that ate a spirit plant could reach the ninth layer?

Fortune… truly more important than effort.

Not only was Lin Jing distressed, but the Pine Leaf Mouse on his shoulder was also indignant.

Just as Lin Jing was leaving, a familiar figure approached.

“Junior Lin?” the young man in white called out in surprise.

“Senior Ouyang, what are you doing here?”

“I accepted a sect mission to subdue the ‘tiger demon of Tiger Peak,’” Ouyang Hao said.

Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse were left speechless.

Undercover, scripture pavilion manager, and subduing tiger demons—Ouyang really was a busy person. Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse cupped their fists in admiration, deeply impressed by him.

It’s good to have you in the sect; the next sect master will surely be you, and no one would compete with you.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 12: The Legend of the Red Fox
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 12: The Legend of the Red Fox

“Junior Brother, are you planning to enter Tiger Mountain for training?” Ouyang asked.

“I have this idea, but now that the tiger demon is present, we’ll have to switch to another peak,” Lin Jing replied helplessly.

Walking also consumes a lot of energy.

He needed to ask what identity one needed to ride a celestial crane.

At that moment, Lin Jing realized the benefits of choosing an ox as a pet beast; along the way, he had seen many disciples riding their oxen.

As for him, it was the pet beast that was riding him—how excessive!

Hearing this, Ouyang looked at the peak guardian.

“Elder, I’ve come to subdue the tiger demon.”

Ouyang smiled and looked back at Lin Jing, saying, “Junior Brother, would you like to come with me? Once I subdue the tiger demon, you’ll be able to train normally.”

“Is that allowed?” Lin Jing was surprised.

“Of course! With me here, there won’t be any accidents.” Ouyang turned to the peak guardian again and asked, “Elder, may I bring this junior brother along?”

“Go ahead,” the elder replied, seemingly unfazed by Ouyang’s strength. “You two go; I’ll continue writing my self-reflection.”

He looked quite annoyed.

……

“Ouyang, what’s the elder’s self-reflection about?” Lin Jing asked after they entered Tiger Mountain.

“The tiger demon’s presence was caused by the unexpected birth of a spirit plant, which the elder failed to notice in time, allowing a wild monster to eat it. He definitely has to write a self-reflection letter to repent and face punishment.”

“He must hate that tiger demon now. Unfortunately, he can’t handle it directly. When a monster hasn’t harmed anyone yet, the sect’s first consideration is to subdue it rather than kill it.”

“Subduing a monster is also a technical job. As long as the situation isn’t too severe, it’s given to promising disciples in the sect to try and train themselves.”

“After all, subduing wild monsters is a fundamental skill that our Beast Taming Sect disciples must master.”

“As disciples, we could perhaps choose from the spirit beasts and monsters cultivated in the sect to contract. However, to go further, we need to venture into the vast cultivation world to find and subdue those powerful beings!”

“The reason I wanted to bring you along is to show you how I subdue a wild monster and make it willingly submit,” Ouyang said with concern.

“Now I understand,” Lin Jing said, surprised. “I thought you were here for the tiger whip.”

Ouyang Hao: “If this tiger demon doesn’t submit to my teachings, I can only accept it with a smile.”

Lin Jing and Ouyang chose to walk through the forest, surrounded by the sounds of chirping birds and insects—a peaceful ambiance, though they still had a distance to cover before reaching the tiger demon.

At that moment, Lin Jing suddenly remembered a question he hadn’t yet resolved!

Elder Mo mentioned that when Ouyang becomes a true disciple, he might take the sect master as his teacher!

The reason was not mentioned, which piqued Lin Jing’s curiosity.

He had forgotten to ask about it during their last visit to the scripture pavilion, so this time he intended to seize the opportunity to inquire directly.

“Brother, I heard that the sect master intends to take you as a disciple?”

“You know about this? Has it spread to the outer sect?” Ouyang looked back in surprise.

“Well, not exactly; Elder Mo told me,” Lin Jing replied, clearing his throat.

“Brother, please teach me! How did you achieve that?”

Only true disciples could learn the sect’s genuine secret techniques, which meant becoming a core member of the Beast Taming Sect.

To become a true disciple, one condition was to take a master with Golden Core cultivation or above.

Taking on a master was akin to a second birth, and having a good master would make the path of cultivation smoother.

It was impressive that Ouyang could connect with the sect master.

“This…” Ouyang looked at Lin Jing strangely, then suddenly shook his head. “Junior Brother, my situation may not be suitable for you.”

“What do you mean?” Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse pressed for details.

Ouyang sighed, stopped walking, and after pondering for a moment, said with a hint of regret, “Forget it; it’s not a secret. I’ll tell you, but remember not to spread it.”

“I recall that you took books about the history of the Beast Taming Sect from the scripture pavilion, right?”

“The first sect master of our Beast Taming Sect was a mortal. This type of body had no fate with the path of immortality, but he had a fortunate encounter and obtained the Beast Taming techniques from the Immortal Palace.”

“The techniques from the Immortal Palace focus on enslaving beasts; a cultivator’s intent could determine the life or death of their contracted beast. With these techniques, he enslaved countless powerful monsters and reached the Transformation God stage, causing the Beast Taming Sect to flourish.”

“However, forcibly enslaving pets ultimately has its drawbacks, and he eventually perished due to a backlash from the very beasts he had enslaved.”

“Learning from this lesson, the second sect master began to consider how the relationship between beast tamers and their beasts should truly be. He concluded that the relationship should not only be master and servant but also companions and kin.”

“Thus, he initiated reforms within the sect, encouraging disciples to cultivate alongside young beasts from the Qi training period, strengthening their bond instead of forcibly enslaving wild monsters. Even if they were to subdue wild beasts, it should be through genuine acknowledgment and followership, not coercion.”

“The second sect master laid a solid foundation for the Beast Taming Sect, and this tradition has been passed down to this day. Originally, this was already perfect, but up until now…the fifth-generation sect master, who is the current sect master, has further deepened the emotional bond with their beasts.”

“He has become true kin with his contracted beasts.”

Lin Jing was speechless.

This was information not recorded in the books.

“Which beast is it?” Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse were both shocked.

“It’s the one from the Heavenly Feather Crane clan…though it hasn’t been publicly acknowledged among disciples, it could be referred to as the sect master’s wife,” Ouyang said respectfully.

At this moment, Lin Jing’s mind was still a bit confused.

Wait, wait!

“So, brother, you’re favored by the sect master. Does that mean…” Lin Jing suddenly fell silent.

“Yeah,” Ouyang sighed. “Hong’er was a small fox I rescued during my mortal days. After that, she became very attached to me, and I kept her by my side.”

“Until Elder Mo brought me back to the Beast Taming Sect, and I performed a blood contract with Hong’er, that’s when I realized she had developed that kind of affection for me.”

“Even though she’s a beast, her feelings are genuine. Such sincerity shouldn’t be betrayed, right, Junior Brother?”

Lin Jing looked up at the sky.

Everything had been unraveled.

However, considering this was a world of cultivation, Lin Jing no longer struggled with the differences in species.

It was just that the “Legend of the White Snake” had transformed into “The Legend of the Celestial Crane” and “The Legend of the Red Fox.”

“Best wishes to you both,” Lin Jing said.

“Zhi, zhi…”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 13: Subduing the Tiger Demon
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 13: Subduing the Tiger Demon

“Thank you for your blessings,” Ouyang sighed deeply.

“As long as you understand, I was worried you couldn’t accept it.”

“But this path is bound to be difficult.”

“Even Sect Master Xu cannot see the future.”

Lin Jing asked, “Why do you say that?”

“Humans and beasts are destined to never have a result.” Ouyang shook his head.

“You still want a result?”

“Unless the other party is from the demon clan, not a demon beast!” Ouyang said seriously.

“Yes, the demon clan.”

“During the era of the Immortal Palace, the Tiānyuán continent was home to numerous clans, and both humans and demons were part of it.”

“Any beast that cultivates to a certain level can transform into a human. Over time, humanoid demon beasts became increasingly common, leading to the emergence of the demon clan. They take on a human form while retaining some beast-like traits, thus forming a new race.”

“The most important point is that the demon clan can reproduce with the human clan, giving rise to half-demons.”

“Toward the end of the Immortal Palace era, countless clans joined forces with the four major sects to resist the tyranny of the Immortal Palace. During this period, the sacrifices made by the various clans were immense.”

“The demon clan was hit the hardest, as the Immortal Palace used their spells to rewrite the rules of the Tiānyuán continent, and demon beasts could no longer transform into humans, causing the demon clan to become extinct.”

“If the demon clan still existed, perhaps Hong’er could cultivate to become a member of that clan. What a pity, what a pity.” Ouyang truly felt it was a loss.

Lin Jing was speechless.

This information was also not recorded in the “Brief History.”

“Sect Master Xu has been searching for ways to resurrect the demon clan. If I were to become his disciple, I would likely spend my life seeking a way for demon beasts to evolve into the demon clan.”

Hearing the emotional story of the current sect master and Ouyang, Lin Jing was quite moved.

“You will definitely succeed.”

“Zhi.” The Pine Leaf Mouse nodded as well; it didn’t understand but was eager to cheer them on.

“Alright, let’s hurry up.” Ouyang said, suddenly remembering their main objective.

……

The journey was silent.

After hastening their pace, Ouyang quickly used his “Spirit Eyes” to locate the traces of the tiger demon’s aura.

Spirit Eyes is a technique that inner sect disciples can cultivate, allowing them to observe various special energies with their naked eyes. It works excellently against foes that don’t conceal their tracks.

At this moment, the lucky tiger demon that had consumed the spirit plant was dozing beneath a tree, its body overwhelmed with spiritual energy, needing sleep to digest it.

It was precisely because its current state was relatively stable and it hadn’t descended the mountain that the Beast Taming Sect had time to send disciples for training instead of directly executing it.

“Hong’er.” Ouyang called out, and as they approached the sleeping tiger demon, the red jade pendant he wore flashed with red light, and a red mist enveloped them, revealing a crimson fox spirit beside them.

“A spirit beast spatial artifact?” Lin Jing’s eyes brightened.

“Zhi?” The Pine Leaf Mouse’s eyes widened with excitement.

“Yes.” Ouyang smiled and said, “Because I have the sect master’s favor and am a special disciple, I obtained this life-bound spatial artifact ahead of time…”

“This item is easy to refine and can be done during the Qi training stage, but the materials needed are extremely expensive, far surpassing storage bags and rings that can’t hold living beings. Most Foundation Establishment cultivators find it hard to afford without the support of elders’ spirit stones.”

“Woo~~~” When the fox spirit named Hong’er emerged, it immediately snuggled up to Ouyang without acknowledging Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse, causing Ouyang to shiver slightly.

“So, Brother, how do you plan to subdue this tiger demon?” Lin Jing asked.

“Listen closely; I’ll only teach this once!” Ouyang stroked the fox spirit’s head and then said, “To subdue a wild demon beast, you must first possess strength that exceeds that of the beast!”

“You need to be stronger than the beast before considering anything else. Never attempt to persuade a demon beast stronger than yourself; otherwise, the only outcome is to become its excrement!”

“Secondly, to gain its acknowledgment, it’s not enough just to be stronger. That’s merely the foundation.”

“You must defeat it in the area where it excels and demonstrate commonality with it. Only then will it willingly recognize you as its leader!”

“For example, if you want to subdue a bird demon that excels at flying, you should surpass it in your flying skills.”

“Thus, those lower-intelligence demon beasts will instinctively regard you as ‘one of their kind’ rather than a ‘powerful outsider.’”

“This is important; it’s like you’d be willing to marry a Foundation Establishment fairy, but you wouldn’t want to marry a Foundation Establishment female monkey.”

“If you achieve all this, whether you then threaten or entice, it will be much more effective and will reduce the other party’s hostility.”

Saying this, he began walking toward the tiger demon.

The fox spirit, Hong’er, followed closely behind. Though it was a fox, it showed no fear toward the tiger demon ahead.

“I’ve learned a lot.” Lin Jing nodded as he watched Ouyang’s back.

Essentially, it was about seeing oneself as a demon beast, defeating the opponent in its area of expertise to gain the highest degree of acknowledgment and submission, rather than using a human’s perspective.

This was the crystallization of wisdom from the predecessors of the Beast Taming Sect?

In an instant, Ouyang had already moved ahead.

As they drew closer, the tiger demon, at the ninth layer of Qi training, suddenly opened its eyes.

It slowly rose, its tall and robust body stretching, its huge mouth opening wide, eyes like lanterns gleaming with amber light, and it let out a thunderous roar, shaking the mountains and forests, causing leaves to flutter. The flying creatures and beasts within several kilometers were terrified and trembled incessantly.

Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse were equally shocked but still forced themselves to remain calm, observing how Ouyang planned to subdue the tiger demon.

Next, Ouyang took a tiger skin coat from his storage bag and draped it over himself. Then he crouched down, mimicking a beast's stance, and showed a fiercer expression to the tiger demon: “They say that tigers are the beasts of utmost Yang, radiating overwhelming Yang energy, making them the nemesis of ghosts and spirits. I’ll let you see who has the stronger Yang energy!”

As he spoke, his Yang energy leaked out, mixed with the genuine Qi fluctuations of the ninth layer of Qi training, and he performed the Beast Taming technique: the Subjugation Technique.

“Roar!!!!!!” Ouyang roared fiercely, his voice deep and resonant.

In an instant, the Yang energy and sound waves overwhelmed the true tiger roar, and it was as if a hurricane swept past the tiger demon, causing leaves to fly backward. The tiger demon’s gaze instantly became clear, resembling that of a large cat.

Lin Jing: ???

“Zhi?”

Wait, is this all about wearing a tiger skin coat and roaring at it with your Yang energy?

After this, the tiger demon seemed to have lost its intention to attack, while the fox spirit looked at its “Beast Tamer” with admiration.

This made Lin Jing even more confused about what this fox spirit saw in Ouyang, but from a non-human perspective, perhaps he understood something—after all, they were indeed a well-matched pair.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 14: Elder Yu
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 14: Elder Yu

After Ouyang senior brother's set of “inappropriate actions,” the tiger demon was initially intimidated by him.

As for what came next, it was nothing more than coercion and temptation; he easily subdued this tiger demon at the ninth layer of the Qi Refining stage.

Although it was said to be at the ninth layer of Qi Refining, its cultivation was actually acquired through a fortuitous encounter, and its intelligence and combat power were still immature.

“With the danger removed, you can now confidently venture into training, junior brother.”

After accomplishing all this, Ouyang senior brother earnestly patted Lin Jing's shoulder, holding great hopes for Lin Jing's future.

After all, Lin Jing was someone determined to collaborate long-term with demon kings; he felt that with a bit of guidance, Lin Jing would definitely not be inferior to him, a genius in beast taming.

“Thank you for your guidance, senior brother.” Lin Jing nodded.

Then, Ouyang senior brother laughed heartily, riding away on the tiger demon.

Watching Ouyang senior brother's departing figure, Lin Jing hesitated, saying, “I might not be able to subdue demon beasts like him.”

“Eek…” The Pine Leaf Mouse slightly nodded, agreeing that it was probably best not to learn that, as it seemed unreliable.

Lin Jing looked around, “Now that senior brother Ouyang has taken away the biggest trouble, next… it’s our hunting time. Tonight, I want to eat fish, let’s go catch one first.”

The Pine Leaf Mouse was curious, “What kind of fish?”

“Squirrel fish.”

“???”

…

On the Miao Tiger Peak, where the highest demon beast was only at the first layer of Qi Refining, Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse were the invincible presence.

Unlike those outer sect disciples with less experience, Lin Jing had gone through being sold by an immortal trafficker and had faced threats from Elder Black Bear; his mentality was already very strong and didn’t need to train here.

He was here purely for procurement.

As the sun set, the old man guarding Miao Tiger Peak saw Lin Jing leisurely carrying a麻绳 strung with wild rabbits, wild boars, and wild chickens, leaving with the Pine Leaf Mouse.

The old man didn’t pay attention; many outer sect disciples would choose to hunt when they couldn’t cook.

Before long, they returned to the courtyard, and the Pine Leaf Mouse began to hint to Lin Jing.

The contract was established;

As for food, whether or not it was nutritious, they had hunted some;

Next, shouldn’t they figure out how to get a storage bag?

The Pine Leaf Mouse hadn’t forgotten about its storage bag; it had come down the mountain for this thing.

Earlier, it had utilized red flying leaves, showcasing its skills on Miao Tiger Mountain, effortlessly defeating numerous ferocious beasts.

Compared to ordinary leaves, it felt that the red flying leaves were much more to its liking.

It decided that in the future, it would use the Glazed Immortal Eyes to synthesize several hundred million red flying leaves.

Just the thought of having several hundred million red flying leaves without a place to carry them made the Pine Leaf Mouse feel a sense of melancholy and insecurity.

“I remember.” Lin Jing also realized that with the Pine Leaf Mouse’s current capabilities, it seemed they could start making money; the storage bag was not out of reach.

“The fastest way to make money is to sell golden spirit rice and profit from the price difference.”

“However, considering that using your eye techniques incurs great consumption, plus the fact that your racial mutation hasn’t been reported to Elder Mo, let’s not reveal the Glazed Immortal Eyes for now.”

“Besides, if we want to quickly earn spirit stones, we can only rely on sect missions.”

Although when Elder Mo returned, the Pine Leaf Mouse could apply to him for better treatment as a rare beast, Lin Jing was unwilling to be a slacker like Ouyang senior brother, who was still diligently doing sect missions to earn rewards.

The main point was, if he wanted to advance to an inner sect disciple, there were not only requirements for strength but also requirements for sect contribution points, and to gain contribution points, one had to undertake sect missions; this was unavoidable and would have to be done sooner or later.

He yearned for the inner sect.

Since senior brother Ouyang could obtain a space treasure for his contracted beast at the inner sect disciple stage, he wanted one too.

Lin Jing felt envious; this was a better thing than a storage bag. With this, he wouldn’t have to carry the Pine Leaf Mouse every day.

For some reason, the Pine Leaf Mouse liked to stand on his shoulder; Lin Jing felt this habit was not good.

Although the time was still short, he could already foresee that if this continued, one of his shoulders would be high and the other low.

“Eek!” The Pine Leaf Mouse indicated that it would follow Lin Jing’s lead, and then it… dragged a cloth bag, leaving the courtyard to climb a tree by the roadside to pick leaves.

The leaves in their own courtyard had already been stripped bare, and it could only go outside to pick more if it wanted to continue synthesizing red flying leaves.

As for using spiritual energy to hasten the ripening… wasting spiritual energy in this way was too extravagant, since leaves were everywhere in the mountains.

It was better to save the spiritual energy for ripening spiritual rice and spiritual beans.

That night, outer sect disciples passing by saw a squirrel carrying a large bag of leaves, jumping back and forth.

“How pitiful…” Two female disciples saw the Pine Leaf Mouse collecting broken leaves and said, “Whose beast master is it? They can’t even feed their pet and have to let it find leaves to eat…”

Until the next morning, the Pine Leaf Mouse was still busy collecting leaves, and their courtyard was already piled high with them.

When Lin Jing woke up and planned to check if there were any suitable tasks in the task hall, the Pine Leaf Mouse dragged in another huge burlap sack.

In addition, it had a delicate pink cloth bag in its hands.

Seeing this, Lin Jing rubbed his eyes and said, “Where did you pick that up? I don’t remember us having such a bag.”

“Eek! Eek!” The Pine Leaf Mouse opened the cloth bag, and inside were several pine cones!

It indicated that this was given to it by a beautiful and kind-hearted senior sister passing by, urging it to eat more nutritious food and not to eat leaves anymore.

“It must be that your leaf collecting made the other senior sisters misunderstand something; don’t take other people’s things in the future.” Lin Jing said helplessly.

The Pine Leaf Mouse nodded, then used the Glazed Immortal Eyes to scan the several pine cones.

Nothing happened.

…

Outer sect, task hall.

Since it was still very early, there were not many people inside; apart from one supervising senior brother, only four outer sect disciples were searching for tasks. They seemed to know each other and were discussing whether to deal with snake demons or pig demons.

The supervising senior brother was reading a book, engrossed, and was not as friendly as Ouyang senior brother. Lin Jing browsed the task wall by himself.

After a round of scanning, he found that aside from tasks that required going out of the sect, there were very few tasks suitable for him and the Pine Leaf Mouse.

Going out of the sect was not an option unless he could find a high-level bodyguard to accompany him, preferably above the Golden Core stage.

As for internal sect tasks, like going to the sect’s spiritual fields to help… the cost-performance ratio was too low; relying on such tasks to accumulate enough money to buy a storage bag and earn contribution points for promotion to the inner sect would take ages.

Although he truly did not lack time.

Just as Lin Jing was feeling troubled, the task hall suddenly fell silent as an elder in blue robes entered.

“Greetings, Elder Yu.” The four outer sect disciples noticed first and respectfully spoke, while the supervising senior brother, who was reading a miscellaneous book, suddenly stood up, putting down the book titled “My Sword Spirit Wife.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 15: Dragon Ascension Festival, Mechanized Bamboo Dragon
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 15: Dragon Ascension Festival, Mechanized Bamboo Dragon

“Elder Yu, what brings you here?” The supervising senior brother immediately put down the miscellaneous book titled “My Sword Spirit Wife” with a nervous expression, looking at the elder.

Lin Jing also looked over; if he remembered correctly, Elder Yu was the elder in charge of outer sect affairs, and he was the authority in the outer sect.

“Ziran, what are you reading again?” Elder Yu frowned, extending his hand, and the supervising senior brother's book flew directly into Elder Yu’s hand.

“Elder! This book was created by Daoist Jian Qingfeng from the Heavenly Sword Sect; it contains profound principles of swordsmanship and is definitely not a useless book…” Before the supervising senior brother could finish, Elder Yu coldly snorted and put the book away.

“Reading miscellaneous books during work hours, I’ll fine you half a month’s worth of spirit stones.”

The supervising senior brother wore an expression as if his pet beast had died.

“Eek eek eek…” The Pine Leaf Mouse covered its mouth, stifling a laugh.

At this moment, Elder Yu's gaze turned toward the Pine Leaf Mouse… then to Lin Jing.

“Hmm?”

“You are Lin Jing?” After a glance, Elder Yu suddenly spoke.

“Reporting to Elder Yu, I am indeed Lin Jing.” Lin Jing was not surprised that an outer sect elder recognized him; Elder Mo had brought him back and surely had informed Elder Yu.

“Are you here to accept a mission?” Elder Yu asked.

Lin Jing nodded.

Elder Yu pondered for a moment and said, “It seems you haven’t found a suitable task yet. Do you want to accept a task that rewards spirit stones or a task that rewards sect contribution points?”

Lin Jing was taken aback; what did the outer sect elder mean…

Tailored to him?

“Reporting to the elder, I would like to take both.”

Elder Yu choked slightly and said, “I am here this time to issue a task.”

“With the Dragon Ascension Festival approaching, we need a large quantity of spirit bamboo to create mechanized beasts. Outer sect disciples who have wood-type contracted beasts can take on this task to go to Jin Zhu Yuan to harvest spirit bamboo in exchange for spirit stones and sect contribution points. Only one person can accept this task.”

“I see you have a Pine Leaf Mouse contracted; do you wish to accept this task?”

Huh?

The supervising senior brother and the other four outer sect disciples were dumbfounded, looking at the unfamiliar Lin Jing, speculating about his background.

Elder Yu's actions screamed favoritism and abuse of power!

Who on earth is this person!

“I am willing!” Although Lin Jing still didn’t fully understand what was happening, as an honest person, he couldn’t refuse the elder’s request.

“Ziran, note down the task. For every ten bamboo stalks harvested, there is a reward of one spirit stone, plus one point of sect contribution.” Elder Yu said, “Let this disciple take care of it.”

After saying that, he waved his sleeve and left the task hall.

Once Elder Yu departed, Ziran senior brother immediately looked at Lin Jing with admiration, saying, “Junior brother, congratulations! To be noticed by Elder Yu means your future is limitless.”

Lin Jing returned the gesture, “It’s just luck. But I don’t know, senior brother, what is this task about? Can you explain it to me?”

With no book to read, Ziran senior brother had free time to explain, “You should know about the Dragon Ascension Festival.”

This was one of the traditional festivals of the Tianyuan Ancient Kingdom. It is said that the founder of the Tianyuan Ancient Kingdom, the first ancient emperor, used true dragon secret techniques to transform the central mountain range of the Tianyuan continent. The mountains soared upward, transforming into a divine dragon that took on the responsibility of protecting the Tianyuan Ancient Kingdom.

This giant dragon was also a symbol of the kingdom’s fortune. It fell into a deep slumber in the earth, transforming into the dragon vein of fortune, protecting the ancient kingdom for generations. Whenever disasters that were hard to withstand occurred within the kingdom, it would awaken to suppress the calamities.

Over time, to commemorate the giant dragon formed from the mountains, the day of the dragon's birth became a traditional festival celebrated not only in the cultivation world but also by ordinary people.

Lin Jing didn’t expect that the Beast Taming Sect would also specifically celebrate this festival.

“The Beast Taming Sect celebrates the Dragon Ascension Festival every year, though it’s not a grand affair; we simply hold some small activities.”

“On the festival day, it’s also a great opportunity for disciples from the inner and outer sects to showcase themselves.”

“And in our Beast Taming Sect, during the Dragon Ascension Festival, there is a special event called ‘Dragon Struggles, Beast Fights.’”

“Disciples can send out their contracted beasts to challenge the ‘dragon’!”

“Of course, this dragon is not a true dragon; our Beast Taming Sect does not have a true dragon yet. This dragon refers to a mechanized dragon created using ‘mechanical arts.’”

“When Elder Yu was younger, he traveled the continent and learned the art of creating mechanized beasts from the myriad arts of cultivation!”

“This skill is still quite rare in our Beast Taming Sect.”

“Because of this talent, Elder Yu is responsible for the festival every year, and the task of harvesting spirit bamboo he issued is likely for the creation of the ‘mechanized bamboo dragon.’”

After Ziran senior brother finished speaking, one of the outer sect disciples present in the task hall said excitedly, “A mechanized bamboo dragon! It seems this year’s mechanized dragon will be wood attribute; my contracted beast is a Fire Spirit Sparrow, which has an attribute advantage.”

“This is valuable news; I can start researching how to deal with wood-type beasts now.” Another outer sect disciple said.

“Over the years, defeating the mechanized dragon has garnered substantial rewards—spirit stones, pills, magical treasures, and spells are all included. Thus, both inner and outer sect disciples are eager to try.” Ziran senior brother smiled and said, “However, the mechanized dragon… although it corresponds to the Qi Refining stage, its combat power won’t be overly exaggerated, but since it is crafted by a Golden Core elder, defeating it won’t be that easy.”

“Now I see.” Lin Jing understood and continued to ask, “May I ask, senior brother, is there anything particular about harvesting spirit bamboo?”

Although he had been working hard to learn various knowledge related to spiritual plants, his learning time was still short.

“Jin Zhu Yuan is a gorge located between two peaks on the northern slope of the Beast Taming Sect, where there is a sea of bamboo that produces spirit bamboo. If I’m not mistaken, this spirit bamboo must be harvested with an axe containing wood properties, or it will lose its characteristics.”

“Oh, using wood-type methods should yield the same effect. For instance, the Pine Leaf Mouse’s flying leaves technique; Elder Yu just mentioned that he assigned you this task because you have a Pine Leaf Mouse.” Ziran senior brother revealed an envious expression again.

“This task’s rewards are quite generous; even among tasks available to inner sect disciples, there are probably none as generous as this.”

“However…” He observed Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse.

“Your Pine Leaf Mouse is still at the third layer of Qi Refining, isn’t it? As far as I know, spirit bamboo is incredibly tough, and even if a mid-Qi Refining Pine Leaf Mouse uses the flying leaves technique, it may require several attempts to cut down a single stalk of spirit bamboo, making it quite laborious!”

“It might not be easy for it to use the flying leaves technique to cut the spirit bamboo.”

The supervising senior brother suddenly realized; that’s why Elder Yu was so generous.

He had been wondering if the rewards were too generous. It turned out Elder Yu believed that this junior brother’s strength wasn’t sufficient to harvest too many spirit bamboos, and if the main intent was to help him train his flying leaves technique, that made sense.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 16: The Spirit Bamboo Slayer
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 16: The Spirit Bamboo Slayer

Shortly after, Lín Jìng left the Mission Hall with a freshly issued mission scroll in hand.

With this scroll, he could enter the Golden Pearl Grove and freely harvest Spirit Bamboo. Without it, he would be arrested for “damaging sect resources.”

“That senior brother mentioned that using the usual Flying Leaf Technique to cut Spirit Bamboo is laborious. But I wonder how effective the red flying leaf would be.”

Lín Jìng and the Pine Leaf Mouse dismissed the senior brother’s comment. He was referring to regular spirit beasts, not their precious Pine Leaf Mouse. Since Elder Jade had entrusted them with this important task, they were determined to cut enough Spirit Bamboo, even if it meant going without food or drink.

Lín Jìng and the Pine Leaf Mouse hurried to the Golden Pearl Grove, conscious not to waste any time. After all, every ten stalks less would mean one less Spirit Stone earned.

They showed the mission scroll to the senior brother guarding the grove and entered the sea of bamboo.

Inside, they gazed up at the canopy of leaves, the towering Spirit Bamboo exuding a deep, vibrant green that radiated life. The sight made the Pine Leaf Mouse's mouth water.

“Squeak…”

Before it used the red leaf, Lín Jìng bent down and picked up an ordinary bamboo leaf, handing it to the mouse.

“Let’s do a comparison and see the difference in damage to the Spirit Bamboo when using a regular leaf versus your red flying leaf.”

The Pine Leaf Mouse nodded, accepting the green bamboo leaf. With one leaf in each paw, it narrowed its eyes and focused on the nearest bamboo stalk.

“Squeak!” Its spiritual energy surged, and with a flick of its small hand, the green leaf shot forward. The leaf collided with the bamboo, leaving only a barely noticeable nick before disintegrating.

The Pine Leaf Mouse frowned. This Spirit Bamboo was indeed tough. Cutting it with normal effort would mean a long day with little gain.

It exchanged a look with Lín Jìng and switched to the red flying leaf for its next attempt.

“Squeak!” The Pine Leaf Mouse leaped from Lín Jìng’s shoulder, tossing the red flying leaf. A red flash cut through the bamboo, followed by a cracking sound as the towering stalk fell.

The test wasn’t over. The red leaf, under the Pine Leaf Mouse’s control, continued slicing through bamboo at incredible speed, taking down several stalks in moments. The efficiency left Lín Jìng and the Pine Leaf Mouse thrilled.

This proved that the Pine Leaf Mouse’s control of the red flying leaf outmatched even a mid-stage practitioner of the important stage in cultivation. Its precision and agility were beyond any ordinary Pine Leaf Mouse.

“We’re going to be rich!” Lín Jìng exclaimed.

“Squeak squeak!” The Pine Leaf Mouse shared his excitement. Though controlling the red flying leaf was exhausting, the thought of the harvested Spirit Bamboo filling its storage pouch fueled its determination.

“We’re not leaving. We’ll stay here,” Lín Jìng suggested.

“I heard a stream nearby. If there’s water, there’s fish. I’ll eat grilled fish, and you can munch on bamboo leaves. Let’s push ourselves to the limit!”

The Pine Leaf Mouse blinked, puzzled. Just moments ago, he said it wasn’t a bamboo rat, and now it was eating bamboo leaves! But food was food, and Spirit Bamboo leaves were certainly better than garden tree leaves.

Their eyes shone with anticipation.

A week later!

The senior brother assigned to guard the bamboo grove noticed something amiss as he meditated in the bamboo hut. He recalled, “A junior brother entered the grove days ago for a harvesting mission, but there’s been no sign of them since.”

“They’re quite diligent,” he mused, glancing at the snacks on the table. Deciding to check on this junior who had received a task from Elder Jade, he took the snacks and entered the grove. The further he walked, the more his eyes widened in disbelief. The once dense bamboo forest had been reduced to piles of cut Spirit Bamboo stacked high.

“What happened?” He was stunned. He distinctly remembered only letting one junior and his Pine Leaf Mouse into the grove, but this scene resembled a mass clearing by the Pine Leaf Mouse tribe.

“Junior Brother, where are you?” Wind spell in action, he swiftly moved through the grove and soon found the Pine Leaf Mouse nibbling on a bamboo leaf, with Lín Jìng massaging it.

“Senior Brother, do you need something?” Lín Jìng paused his energy restoration technique, looking at the wide-eyed senior brother.

“Did you two cut all this?” His face showed disbelief as he looked at the exhausted yet bright-eyed Pine Leaf Mouse.

“Yes,” Lín Jìng admitted with a tired expression.

“Well…” The senior brother hesitated. “Did Elder Jade specify how much Spirit Bamboo he needed?”

Lín Jìng shook his head. “Elder Jade didn’t say, but for the Ascending Dragon Festival, the more Spirit Bamboo, the better. That way, they can build more mechanized bamboo dragons and make the festival even livelier.”

“I’d better check with Elder Jade…” The senior brother couldn’t shake his unease as he turned and left, worried that if Lín Jìng stayed any longer, the grove might vanish.

As he departed, Lín Jìng and the Pine Leaf Mouse, sensing urgency, picked up their pace.

…

Outer Sect, Mechanized Workshop!

Although Elder Jade mainly practiced beast taming, he was a mechanical enthusiast, devoted to studying constructs.

Outside the workshop, a plaque read, “Mechanical Beasts Are Beasts Too.”

At that moment, he was sipping tea and conversing with another elder. If Lín Jìng had been present, he would have recognized the elder as none other than Elder Mo, who had been away for days.

“I just returned, and before reaching Lotus Peak, you stopped me. What’s so urgent?” Elder Mo sipped his tea.

Elder Jade chuckled, stroking his beard. “A month ago, you asked me to look after the Longevity Body you found and placed in the outer sect. I’ve been attentive, even assigning him a custom bamboo-harvesting mission. Isn’t it time you thanked me? Ten stalks of Spirit Bamboo per Spirit Stone—it’s like a gift from the heavens for an outer sect disciple!”

“Ha! Do you expect gratitude for such a trivial thing? You’re always scheming. What do you want from my collection?” Elder Mo scoffed.

“Who’s scheming? I’m just conserving resources for mechanical beast research—” Elder Jade’s words were cut off as a crane landed, delivering a letter.

His expression changed after reading it. “Are you sure the body you brought back is a Longevity Body and not a Wood God Physique?”

“Why?” Elder Mo asked, puzzled.

“The guardian disciple reported that the one sent to harvest Spirit Bamboo is about to clear out the entire grove!” Elder Jade’s scalp tingled.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 17: Dual Breakthrough
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 17: Dual Breakthrough

Golden Pearl Grove, within the bamboo forest.

Lín Jìng and the Pine Leaf Mouse would meditate to rest whenever they felt fatigued, absorbing the natural spiritual energy to rejuvenate. Once recovered, they immediately resumed their harvesting efforts.

When the senior brother came to check on them, Lín Jìng felt that their time was running out, as both sensed that a significant challenge was imminent.

Indeed, before long, two streaks of light flashed across the sky and descended into the bamboo forest, landing directly in front of them.

The arrivals were... a blue-robed elder with a stern expression and a black-robed elder with a look of astonishment.

“Elder Jade… and Elder Mo? You’ve returned?”

Lín Jìng and the Pine Leaf Mouse looked on in surprise.

Elder Jade glanced at the piles of Spirit Bamboo, feeling a pang in his teeth.

It wasn’t the Spirit Stones he worried about but the fact that making mechanized bamboo dragons didn’t require such an excessive amount of material!

“Did you two harvest all this?” Elder Mo asked, eyeing the red flying leaf in the Pine Leaf Mouse's grasp and noticing its unique quality with a start.

A special spiritual plant!

Its type was unknown.

For the Pine Leaf Mouse, having such an item was akin to a cultivator wielding a powerful artifact, indeed enhancing its capabilities. But where had Lín Jìng and the Pine Leaf Mouse obtained it?

Moreover, wielding this type of leaf would drain the Pine Leaf Mouse’s energy tremendously, so seeing this amount of harvested bamboo seemed exaggerated—had they been cutting non-stop day and night?

What incredible determination.

Suddenly, Elder Mo's heart leaped as he connected some thoughts.

“Yes, Elder,” Lín Jìng confirmed. “To avoid delaying the Ascending Dragon Festival preparations, we haven’t dared to slack off, sleeping only two hours each day.”

“Hahaha! Good job!” Elder Mo burst into laughter, clapping Elder Jade’s shoulder. “Old Jade, see how diligent the disciple I brought back is? I’d say this amount of Spirit Bamboo should suffice for your bamboo dragons. How about letting him rest now?”

“Make sure you don’t skimp on the mission rewards…” With that, Elder Mo stepped forward, summoned a cloud of mist, and carried Lín Jìng and the Pine Leaf Mouse away, leaving Elder Jade bewildered.

“Damn! That old miser Mo Wuyá must have secretly trained this Longevity Body! Otherwise, how could a third-stage Pine Leaf Mouse achieve this?” Elder Jade muttered. “This much Spirit Bamboo could make a foundation-stage bamboo dragon.”

“This year’s Ascending Dragon Festival… no one’s going to have it easy! I’ll add some intensity!”

…

Outer Sect, private courtyard.

Elder Mo brought Lín Jìng back and kept scrutinizing both him and the Pine Leaf Mouse, asking, “Where did you get that red flying leaf?”

“Elder Mo, this red flying leaf... was created by the Pine Leaf Mouse itself through its innate ability. Shortly after forming our contract, it underwent a mutation! I suspect it evolved toward the rare type you once mentioned,” Lín Jìng explained.

“Rare beast…” Elder Mo’s breath quickened, his face flushed. “So, it’s true.”

“Yes,” Lín Jìng nodded. “We’ve been waiting for your return to ask—is this normal for it?”

Elder Mo laughed heartily, throwing his head back.

“After six failed attempts, we finally succeeded, haha!”

Whoa!

Lín Jìng and the Pine Leaf Mouse instinctively took a step back at the sight of Elder Mo’s manic excitement.

Moments later, Elder Mo’s gaze focused on them again. “What is its innate ability?”

“Glazed Celestial Eyes, capable of converting certain quantities of lower-grade resources into a different type of high-grade resource,” Lín Jìng explained.

“Glazed Celestial Eyes?” Elder Mo echoed. “Then this red flying leaf must be something synthesized by its ability.”

“Yes!”

“Can you demonstrate it for me?” Elder Mo asked the Pine Leaf Mouse.

Hearing this, the Pine Leaf Mouse immediately adopted an exhausted posture, its body swaying as if about to collapse, eyes weary as it looked at Elder Mo.

“Ah, yes, cutting Spirit Bamboo must have exhausted you both.” He produced a jade bottle, pouring out a white pill and tossing it to the Pine Leaf Mouse.

“This is a ‘Replenishing Pill.’ Once consumed, it will restore your state to peak condition.”

“Squeak?!” The Pine Leaf Mouse gleefully accepted it and bit into it. The moment it swallowed, the pill’s energy coursed through its body like a stream, replenishing its vitality. Unexpectedly, the pill not only restored its spiritual energy but also infused it with newfound power, pushing it straight to the fourth stage of cultivation!

Additionally, due to their blood contract, Lín Jìng felt the powerful feedback firsthand. As the Pine Leaf Mouse broke through, his own spiritual energy surged, breaking past the first-stage barrier and elevating him to the second stage of cultivation.

Both had broken through!

Elder Mo was hardly surprised by the Pine Leaf Mouse’s progress. Considering its rigorous “training” in the bamboo grove and its innate talent, he expected nothing less. When it came to rare beasts, no development was too surprising.

Now at the fourth stage, the Pine Leaf Mouse regained its enthusiasm. It initially intended to synthesize another red flying leaf but soon noticed that the spiritual beans they had planted in the yard were almost ready for harvest.

Eager to see the effects of synthesized spiritual beans, it quickly picked and shelled them. Two beans, four beans, six beans… soon, over a hundred beans were collected.

“Spiritual beans, a basic type of spiritual plant, function like spiritual rice. They replenish a small amount of spiritual energy when consumed,” Elder Mo commented.

The Pine Leaf Mouse activated its Glazed Celestial Eyes to work on the beans, unsure of what would happen.

Fortune favored it. The dazzling light from its eyes enveloped the pile of brownish beans, transforming them into a single, fragrant, energy-filled green bean!

“Elder Mo, this is what the Glazed Celestial Eyes can do. But since it’s the Pine Leaf Mouse’s first time synthesizing a spiritual bean, we’re not yet sure of its effects,” Lín Jìng remarked as he inspected the green bean, noting its potent aroma—it seemed like a significant restorative.

It looked brimming with life, reminiscent of an “Immortal Bean” mentioned in legends.

“Hmm.” Elder Mo flicked his sleeve, and a drowsy Black Bear Monster appeared in the courtyard.

“Master, what’s up?” the Black Bear Monster yawned, seemingly just awakened.

“There’s a treat for you over there. Go try it,” Elder Mo pointed to the green bean on the ground.

“Huh? Let me see!” The Black Bear Monster's nose twitched as it picked up the green bean and popped it into its mouth.

In the next moment—

Boom!

The green bean exploded with surprising force. The mutated bean had the properties of a spiritual energy bomb—it wasn’t edible.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 18: Clash of the Prodigies
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 18: Clash of the Prodigies

“Ahhhhh!”

With a loud “Boom!”, the spiritual bean exploded in the Black Bear Monster’s mouth, sending dark smoke streaming from its nose and mouth. Its frustration immediately erupted.

“Ahhh!”

It glared furiously at Elder Mo, Lín Jìng, and the stunned Pine Leaf Mouse.

This…

Lín Jìng and the Pine Leaf Mouse were thoroughly shocked. The enticing spiritual bean had exploded as soon as it was eaten?

Luckily, they hadn’t tried it themselves!

“I had a feeling the spiritual energy fluctuations in that bean were strange. It’s become a ‘malignant plant’!” Elder Mo sighed. While spiritual plants are nourishing, malignant ones are sparse in nutrition and highly dangerous.

“Infuriating! You guessed it was dangerous and still made me eat it?!” the Black Bear Monster roared in anger.

“A mere Pine Leaf Mouse at the important stage in cultivation made that bean, so what are you, a demon beast with a monster core, afraid of? The fact that you didn’t sense anything just shows your lack of vigilance,” Elder Mo chided.

The Black Bear Monster ground its teeth but couldn’t refute, feeling it had been too naive.

“All right, go back to sleep.” Ignoring his pet, Elder Mo withdrew the Black Bear Monster and turned back to Lín Jìng and the Pine Leaf Mouse with interest.

“Changing the nature rather than enhancing the quality—not bad at all. While not a top-tier ability, it’s certainly impressive!”

“Isn’t the Glazed Celestial Eyes considered a top-tier ability?” Lín Jìng thought it was already quite powerful.

“You underestimate rare beasts,” Elder Mo chuckled. “The divine abilities awakened during the mutation from a spirit beast to a rare beast can sometimes originate from immortal beings. Compared to those, the transformative power of Glazed Celestial Eyes is just one of many potent abilities.”

“For example, the ancient imperial clan had a rare beast called the ‘Boundary-Breaking Bird,’ which could help cultivators shatter the limits of their realms. Under its blessing, an exceptional imperial prince reached the unprecedented thirteenth stage of the important cultivation phase and developed a second and third constitution at the foundation establishment stage, possessing three unique cultivation constitutions.”

“And in the Spirit Dao Sect, there’s the ‘Insight Fish,’ a rare beast credited with enabling many of its cultivators to manifest the ‘Spell Spirit Origin’.”

“The Tianji Pavilion, the Lotus Chamber of Commerce…” Elder Mo listed other rare beasts associated with powerful factions, each holding abilities that bolstered their masters.

Of course, rare beasts varied in their capabilities. Some were astonishing, while others awakened bizarre abilities that ended up in ancient records. There was even a rabbit spirit with the “Hundred Fragrances” ability that emitted more than a hundred divine scents, only to be captured and eaten by a high-level Nascent Soul cultivator. It became renowned as the “Hundred Fragrance Rabbit,” the most delectable dish across the ages.

“In short, rare beasts with world-altering abilities can influence even higher-level cultivators early on. Compared to that, your spiritual beans or red flying leaves are still within the realm of the important stage. But even so, it’s impressive. Don’t be too greedy,” Elder Mo advised.

“Understood.” Lín Jìng chuckled and then asked, “Elder Mo, could we sell some of the synthesized resources in the sect to earn Spirit Stones?”

He described the effects of the golden spiritual rice.

Elder Mo listened and shook his head. “I wouldn’t recommend it. The golden spiritual rice may be remarkable compared to regular rice, but alternatives exist.”

“First- and second-grade pills help with digestion. The disciples of the Beast Taming Sect can obtain these through sect contribution points. And while the explosive beans are more deceptive, basic attack talismans could substitute them.”

“The Glazed Celestial Eyes must use significant energy. Rather than selling the resources, it would be better for you to use them. This way, you save on sect exchanges, which indirectly means more Spirit Stones for you.”

“Focusing too much on earning Spirit Stones will affect your dedication to cultivation. Rest assured, from now on, I will enhance your discipleship benefits, so you won’t have to worry too much about resources.”

“Thank you, Elder Mo,” Lín Jìng replied, taking the advice to heart.

“Just concentrate on improving your cultivation,” Elder Mo said. “As a Longevity Body, you don’t need to rush. Take this time to learn as much as you can, read widely—it’ll help you greatly in navigating the cultivation world in the future.”

Lín Jìng nodded, suddenly recalling something. “Elder Mo, didn’t you go to investigate the Yin Corpse Sect? What did you find?”

“No results,” Elder Mo replied. “However, the Yin Corpse Sect is likely too preoccupied to bother you.”

“I heard from a friend at the Demon-Slaying Bureau that the Grand Celestial Gathering is approaching. The prodigies from major sects are going on expeditions, and the Bureau, alongside the top sects, is intensifying its crackdown on the Yin Corpse Sect. They’re more likely to be in hiding.”

“By the way, do you know about the Grand Celestial Gathering?”

“No…” Lín Jìng admitted, shaking his head.

“The Grand Celestial Gathering is a grand event held by the Ancient Celestial Kingdom every sixty years, where sects and clans from the kingdom’s nine domains participate, and even the Ancient Emperor attends.”

“The gathering features combat competitions among the prodigies of the various sects. The most outstanding factions receive the favor of the True Dragon of Fortune, which boosts the sect’s luck.”

“Our sect master is currently absent because he’s leading several top disciples on an expedition in preparation for next year’s Grand Celestial Gathering.”

“While the Beast Taming Sect has always been a supporting player compared to the likes of the Spirit Dao Sect and the Heavenly Sword Gate, we still strive. Without striving, why cultivate?”

Elder Mo continued, “When I was young, I participated in the Grand Celestial Gathering. But my performance was mediocre compared to the true prodigies. I swore that one day, the Beast Taming Sect’s name would echo throughout the kingdom!”

“As I grew older, I realized that with my talent, achieving that goal was difficult. After giving up on forming a Nascent Soul, I stepped back to mentor disciples, hoping to cultivate talents that would fulfill that vision.”

“Are you curious why the Pine Leaf Mouse mutated into a rare beast?”

“When you joined the sect, I didn’t mention this, but any unique cultivation constitution forming a blood contract with a beast has a slight chance of triggering a mutation!”

“Based on this knowledge, I requested ample resources from the sect master to scout out promising candidates with unique cultivation constitutions. However, six disciples with special constitutions, including Ouyang with his Primordial Yang Body, didn’t trigger any mutations in their beasts.”

“But you, with your Longevity Body, and the Pine Leaf Mouse were the first and only success!” Elder Mo laughed heartily. “Now, when the sect master returns, I finally have something to report.”

“This year’s Grand Celestial Gathering might be out of reach, but by the next one, with your rare beast’s help, you will undoubtedly be one of the main contenders, clashing with the prodigies of the celestial sects and enhancing the prestige of our Beast Taming Sect.”

“For now… isn’t the Ascending Dragon Festival coming up? From what I’ve seen, the red flying leaf is extraordinary. Use the festival as a stage to showcase your talents. The sect has its own rules—only by standing out will the sect fully invest in you as the future of the Beast Taming Sect.”

“And don’t bother milking the small rewards from sect missions. If you win top honors at the Ascending Dragon Festival, resources and storage pouches will come to you.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 19: Ingenious Soul Core
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 19: Ingenious Soul Core

Elder Mo's words got Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse excited. They both felt they should do something. Immediately, they swallowed and cooled down.

"Zhi! Zhi!" However, Lin Jing seemed fine, but for the Pine Leaf Mouse, which had grown up in the sect, the Beast Taming Sect was its home. Now, hearing that its home was struggling so badly and that the hope of its whole family rested on it, a sense of responsibility suddenly surged within.

It tugged at Lin Jing’s hair, indicating it wanted to compete with other prodigies from immortal sects.

"Calm down first; that’s something for decades later," Lin Jing said.

"Decades, for you, it’s just a blink of an eye." Elder Mo was in an extremely good mood as he laughed heartily. "You’ve been tired from cutting the spiritual bamboo these past few days, so you can rest well tonight. I’ll go with Elder Yu to claim your task reward. I’ll have Tianyu Crane deliver it to you later!"

"Focus on preparing for the Dragon Ascension Festival. You've been in the sect for a while now, and it’s time you participated in some sect activities."

"Yes!" Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse cupped their fists.

The Dragon Ascension Festival...

Seven days of training had honed their bamboo cutting skills to an impressive level.

If the rewards were rich, they had to participate.

After Elder Mo left, Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse found a package on the stone table in the yard.
