Opening it, they found ten Spirit Stones and a bag of Spirit Rice. It turned out that their one-month period had ended, and after days of hard work in the bamboo forest, new resources had been delivered.

With this new batch of resources, along with the special treatment promised by Elder Mo for the Pine Leaf Mouse, and the Spirit Stones earned from the bamboo cutting task, Lin Jing felt that he wouldn’t have to worry about Spirit Stones for a long time.

"Since Elder Mo said so, we’ll focus on preparing for the Dragon Ascension Festival," Lin Jing said, holding the Spirit Stones.

"Without such good tasks like cutting spiritual bamboo, it will slow down the process of earning money to buy storage bags through other tasks. But since there are rich rewards in the Dragon Ascension Festival, if we perform well, we might even have a chance to get a storage bag directly!"

After Lin Jing spoke, the Pine Leaf Mouse grabbed the Spirit Stones from him and dragged the Spirit Rice away without looking back, as if it had made up its mind.

"Zhi!"

Its eyes were full of fighting spirit.

On the other hand, it didn’t want Lin Jing to touch the Spirit Stones.

Otherwise, it would be a total waste.

It would rather use them for its own cultivation. As for the Beast Tamer, it would just wait for the spiritual energy feedback.

Besides that... it planned to stock up on a few hundred Spirit Energy Bombs in these few days and blow up the Bamboo Dragons!

---

Time passed quickly, and several days later, Lin Jing could hardly be considered idle. There were too many things waiting for him to do.

For example, these days, Lin Jing had been working with the Pine Leaf Mouse on the Glazed Immortal Eyes.

Through their exploration, they discovered that perhaps the synthesis conditions hadn’t been met, or maybe the Pine Leaf Mouse's realm was too low, and the Eye Technique wasn’t perfect.

The Glazed Immortal Eyes... for now, it seemed they could only synthesize resources related to plants and trees.

Lin Jing tried to give the Pine Leaf Mouse some soil, hoping it would synthesize Spiritual Earth, but it failed.

He pointed at the pond, wanting it to synthesize a drop of Spiritual Spring, but it failed again.

He bought over a hundred common cloth bags, trying to have it synthesize a storage bag, but of course, that didn’t succeed either.

Even attempting to synthesize low-grade Spirit Stones to summon medium-grade Spirit Stones was also a failure.

This situation could mean one of two things: either the number of low-grade resources was insufficient to trigger a qualitative change, or the Glazed Immortal Eyes were still at too low a level. For now, the Pine Leaf Mouse could only synthesize plant-related resources. The Pine Leaf Mouse was still at the Qi Refining stage, so it was likely unable to synthesize more complex items.

Additionally, the Glazed Immortal Eyes might not be able to synthesize high-grade items yet.

The day after Elder Mo left, Tianyu Crane brought extra resources for the Pine Leaf Mouse—twenty low-grade Spirit Stones and a bottle of Spirit Recovery Pills.

Lin Jing tried to have the Pine Leaf Mouse synthesize the Spirit Recovery Pills made from plant materials, but there was no response.

After several attempts and realizing that the Glazed Immortal Eyes couldn’t activate resources beyond basic plant materials, they stopped trying. They guessed that secondary synthesis or biological synthesis might not be feasible for now. Thankfully, with the golden Spirit Rice, red Flying Leaves, and Spirit Bean Bombs, they had enough resources for the Dragon Ascension Festival.

"The scent of dragons is growing stronger..."

The Pine Leaf Mouse was practicing in the yard, while Lin Jing continued his usual visits to the Scripture Pavilion to borrow and return books. In just a few days, many of the outer sect’s buildings had been decorated with dragon head lanterns. The Dragon Ascension Festival was just around the corner.

"I wonder how Senior Brother Ouyang is doing. I’m sure he’s preparing for the Dragon Ascension Festival too. He’s about to establish his foundation, and he’ll need more resources."

The Pavilion Guard had changed, and Lin Jing didn’t recognize the new one. They had no interactions, as he only borrowed some free books on the first floor.

Through these few days of reading, Lin Jing learned more about cultivation knowledge. For instance, the true energy cultivated from each cultivation method was different. The Beast Taming Sect’s Beast Tamer Technique produced true energy that not only allowed practitioners to form bloodline contracts with spiritual beasts but also enhanced the roots and bloodlines of the tamed beasts.

In other words, some fire-based techniques might produce true energy that has a burning effect, and some sword techniques might make the true energy as sharp as sword qi!

The different cultivation methods and true energies determined what kind of spells cultivators should practice during the Qi Refining stage, and also affected their battle power at that stage.

When one reaches the ninth level of Qi Refining, they can establish their foundation... This process is especially crucial for ordinary body cultivators. With the right foundation establishment technique, they could cultivate a later-stage spiritual body. Though the later-stage body might not be as powerful as an innate body, it would make future cultivation much easier. The ancient prince Elder Mo mentioned was a case where someone with an innate body also cultivated two additional complementary later-stage bodies.

"The announcement for the Dragon Ascension Festival activities is out!"

"Let’s go, let’s check out the central plaza!"

As Lin Jing was about to return, voices of conversation reached his ears.

Upon hearing this, he changed his direction and walked toward the central plaza.

When he arrived, over a hundred outer sect disciples had already gathered. Although it couldn’t be called a crowd, it was still a bit cramped in front of the bulletin board.

"Senior Brother Ziran was right. For this Dragon Ascension Festival, whether outer or inner sect, the main event will still be the ‘Dragon Battle Beast Fight’!"

"This time, Elder Yu used spiritual bamboo to create mechanical bamboo dragons for the festival!"

The disciples kept talking, full of anticipation. When they saw the rewards, there was an even louder gasp.

---

Dragon Battle Beast Fight (Outer Sect)

Activity Rules: Any outer sect disciple of the Beast Taming Sect can challenge the "Mechanical Bamboo Dragon (Qi Refining Seventh Level)" in the central plaza during the festival. The Mechanical Bamboo Dragon has a Dragon Orb, which comes in eight colors: white, red, orange, yellow, green, blue, indigo, and purple. During battle, the Mechanical Bamboo Dragon will change the color of the orb depending on the damage it takes or its own consumption. The orb starts white, and purple is the highest.

When the orb reaches the red color, the first-level reward will be granted.

The higher the orb color, the more generous and valuable the reward!

Lin Jing looked at it, and the highest-level purple reward was...

Hmm? Not a storage bag?

Uh-oh, the Pine Leaf Mouse is going to be upset!

Moreover, it didn’t look like something that could be sold!

The highest-level purple reward was actually the "Ingenious Soul Core" and "Mechanical Technique (Basic Beast Creation)."

The Ingenious Soul Core is the core material for creating mechanical beasts. Elder Yu... seems like he’s hiding something here!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 20: Dragon Battle Beast Fight
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 20: Dragon Battle Beast Fight

Mechanical Technique

Among the various arts in cultivation, some techniques are easier to learn than others. For example, Beast Taming is relatively easy to get started with.

Even if other cultivators don't practice the Beast Tamer Technique and cannot form a bloodline contract with spiritual beasts, there are other methods, such as pills, magical tools, and poisons, to control demon beasts.

Apart from the Beast Taming Sect, it’s common in other sects for cultivators to keep one or two pets or mounts.

As for the Mechanical Technique, Lin Jing didn’t know much about it, but he had heard that Elder Yu had been obsessed with it for years, yet he still couldn’t create mechanical beasts that could match the combat strength of his contracted spiritual beasts.

"Elder Yu seems to want to pass on his Mechanical Technique legacy. It’s quite a deep passion of his, but within the Beast Taming Sect, I doubt anyone would be willing to learn the Mechanical Technique," Lin Jing said, crossing his arms and continuing to study the task announcement. From a rational point of view, mechanical beasts, lacking intelligence, were much weaker than spiritual beasts that had their own ideas.

The upgrade of a mechanical beast relies entirely on the mechanic. For more complex mechanical beasts, the mechanic has to invest a lot of effort into controlling them during combat. Moreover, repairing a damaged mechanical beast is more difficult. But a spiritual beast... not only can it cultivate on its own, but it can also fight by itself. Even if it gets injured, there are healing pills or herbs available. A mechanical beast, on the other hand, can’t eat healing pills.

Mechanical beasts might have their own benefits, but they didn’t seem relevant to most Beast Taming Sect disciples, since the sect didn’t have any advanced techniques suitable for learning the Mechanical Technique.

Therefore, this reward wasn’t particularly appealing to Lin Jing.

Although, looking at it from a long-term perspective, learning more things wouldn’t hurt for a Longevity Body like his.

"With the Qi Refining Seventh-Level Bamboo Dragon, it should be manageable," Lin Jing thought.

He stepped back from the crowd. Cultivation level is one thing, but combat ability is another. For example, even though most cultivators at the Qi Refining Seventh Level might not be able to beat a Sword Cultivator, when combining mechanisms and dragons, the result wouldn't likely be weak.

However, spiritual beasts were even stronger. Although the Glazed Immortal Eyes couldn't let the Pine Leaf Mouse fight on a much higher level, it provided unimaginable suppression at the Qi Refining stage. For example, the Spirit Bean Bombs synthesized using the Immortal Eyes were very powerful, so much so that they shocked even the Black Bear Monster. Their power was enough to severely injure those in the late Qi Refining stage.

"It’s a shame the rewards aren’t cumulative. Once you reach the final tier, you can’t receive the rewards from the previous ones," Lin Jing mused.

The Sixth-Tier reward, the "Dragon Blood Pill," was also decent. It’s a second-grade pill made from the blood of a Flood Dragon. After taking it, it could enhance a beast’s bloodline with a small chance of granting it dragon veins!

Of course, in terms of value, even without including the Mechanical Technique insights, the "Ingenious Soul Core" would definitely surpass the Dragon Blood Pill. However, Lin Jing thought that if he got the "Ingenious Soul Core" and sold it to buy a storage ring, Elder Yu would likely be furious.

"Only three days left until the Dragon Ascension Festival," Lin Jing thought as he returned to the courtyard, glancing at the Pine Leaf Mouse, who was sitting cross-legged under a barren tree, cultivating.

Behind it, there were piles of red Flying Leaves and Spirit Bean Bombs!

Since discovering the power of the red Flying Leaves, the Pine Leaf Mouse had stopped collecting Spirit Stones. Its cultivation now focused more on a single line of effort.

In the morning, it would drowsily hasten the growth of Spirit Rice and Spirit Beans. Then, it would sit nearby, replenishing its energy with Spirit Stones and Spirit Recovery Pills. Next, it synthesized golden Spirit Rice and devoured it with an expression of resolve. With perfect digestion, it would convert the nutrients from ordinary Spirit Rice into spiritual energy.

After filling its stomach, it would proceed to synthesize more red Flying Leaves and Spirit Bean Bombs, accumulating them.

Thus, the number of red Flying Leaves and Spirit Bean Bombs kept increasing, while the Spirit Stones and other resources became scarcer. But the Pine Leaf Mouse didn't mind.

After all, Spirit Stones wouldn't help in battle, but red leaves would.

The Pine Leaf Mouse wanted to take advantage of the Dragon Ascension Festival to earn more resources, hoping for big returns with little effort.

Thanks to its tenacity, the resources sent by the sect this month, along with task rewards and Spirit Recovery Pills, had mostly been consumed by the Pine Leaf Mouse.

Unlike Lin Jing, who hadn't gained a single Spirit Stone, the Pine Leaf Mouse had made significant progress. Its cultivation had reached close to the fifth level of Qi Refining while synthesizing its trump cards.

Lin Jing, on the other hand, had only eaten some leftover Spirit Rice and made himself some leaf fried rice.

If there was one thing that set Beast Taming Sect disciples apart from cultivators of other sects in terms of beast-raising, it would definitely be that most Beast Taming Sect disciples gave the best resources to their spiritual beasts, while cultivators of other sects would only give their pets or mounts leftover resources.

Lin Jing quietly returned without disturbing the Pine Leaf Mouse, sitting down nearby to read.

After a while, the Pine Leaf Mouse stopped cultivating and approached Lin Jing to learn about the rewards for the "Dragon Battle Beast Fight"...

Upon hearing that the best rewards were for the Mechanical Technique and mechanical materials, it was immediately disheartened.

"Zhi! Zhi Zhi! Zhi Zhi Zhi!"

"Calm down, the Mechanical Technique isn't that bad. After learning it, you don’t necessarily have to make mechanical beasts."

"Have you heard of the 'mechanical gun'?"

"Using that thing to shoot Spirit Bean Bombs would definitely be far more efficient than throwing them with the Flying Leaf Technique, and it would consume much less of your own energy."

With Lin Jing’s comforting words, time passed slowly.

Before they knew it, the day of the Dragon Ascension Festival arrived.

The Dragon Battle Beast Fight event would start at noon on the day of the festival.

In the courtyard, Lin Jing wore his usual outer sect disciple robes, with the Pine Leaf Mouse, now at Qi Refining Fifth Level, sitting on his shoulder. The mouse looked conflicted as it glanced at the bulging pouches of Lin Jing's robe, which contained all its possessions.

The thought of the upcoming battle possibly causing it to lose most of its belongings made the Pine Leaf Mouse want to back out.

"Zhi..."

Lin Jing didn’t care what the Pine Leaf Mouse was thinking. After three days, he returned once more to the central plaza.

The plaza was even more crowded. Though the event hadn’t started yet, people and their spiritual beasts had already gathered, surrounding the square.

There were many outer sect disciples wearing the same attire, varying in age from as young as ten to those who looked like senior disciples—whether due to age or rapid growth.

In the center of the square, a vivid bamboo dragon was floating mid-air, aided by a small wind formation.

Lin Jing looked up. The bamboo dragon was several yards long, with each bamboo scale gleaming with a greenish glow. Its head was exquisitely crafted, with two dragon eyes carefully carved from bamboo and shining brightly. The dragon’s horns were made of tough bamboo joints, sharp and upright, adding an air of majesty.

Its mouth was slightly open, revealing a complex internal mechanism. One could vaguely see the intricate bamboo wheels and thin bamboo threads interwoven to form gears, and... a white Dragon Orb.

"This year, Elder Yu’s mechanical dragon seems much more refined than in previous years," one disciple whispered, admiring the bamboo dragon.

Its body was covered in neatly arranged bamboo scales, all carefully polished. The dragon's spine was lined with bamboo joints, creating a smooth curve, resembling the spine of a true dragon. The entire mechanical structure appeared very fluid.

"Yeah, it doesn't even seem like a Qi Refining beast. It looks more like a Foundation Establishment beast that’s been forcibly downgraded to the Qi Refining stage," another disciple speculated maliciously.

"They’re probably overthinking it. It’s just for the festival. Elder Yu wouldn’t go to the trouble of making something this elaborate to bother us," another disciple said dismissively.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 21: The Top of the Outer Sect
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 21: The Top of the Outer Sect

In the Outer Sect’s Central Plaza, people gathered and murmured in anticipation as a smiling young man stepped forward from behind the Mechanized Bamboo Dragon.

Lín Jìng recognized him instantly—it was the Senior Brother Guardian from the Spirit Bamboo Forest.

The young man took a step forward and announced loudly, "Greetings, everyone! I am Fang Yonghao from the Inner Sect, at the ninth level of the important cultivation stage, here on behalf of Elder Yu to preside over the Beast Fighting Competition of the Ascending Dragon Festival!"

"From this moment onward, any Outer Sect disciple may challenge the Mechanized Bamboo Dragon. Once a challenger is ready, I will activate the Bamboo Dragon, which will autonomously detect and seek out its opponent within a set range to engage in combat."

"Any junior brother interested in taking on the challenge? Today and tomorrow, each person is limited to one attempt per day, and the reward will be given based on the best score achieved."

As Senior Brother Fang finished speaking, the crowd fell silent; no one was eager to step up first, wary of the Bamboo Dragon's unfamiliar attack patterns. Rushing in blindly could only lead to failure.

After about ten seconds of silence, someone finally broke the quiet with a resolute, "I'll go."

Lín Jìng, who had been about to step onto the stage himself, paused, looking over alongside his Pine Leaf Mouse.

This was none other than the Battle Ape, one of the five foundational spirit beasts of the Beast Taming Sect!

Notably, this Battle Ape carried a long-handled weapon wrapped in white cloth on its back, giving it the air of a master fighter.

"It's Senior Brother He!"

"Senior Brother He Yiming—the number one of the Outer Sect!"

The youth’s presence caused a stir, as he was evidently a well-known figure in the Outer Sect.

"Senior Brother He has been ranked first in the Outer Sect’s Beast Fighting Arena for years."

"After him, Senior Brother Ziran and Senior Sister Caixin hold the second position."

"Who would have guessed that Senior Brother He would open up for us by testing out the Bamboo Dragon!"

The disciples murmured amongst themselves. Even a youth leaning against a tree reading My Sword Spirit Bride and a sickly-looking girl surrounded by female disciples turned their attention toward the top disciple of the Outer Sect.

"Greetings, Senior Brother Fang. I am He Yiming, at the sixth level of the important stage, accompanied by my spirit beast, Battle Ape, who is at the seventh level. We are here to challenge the Mechanized Bamboo Dragon!"

Senior Brother Fang smiled in return. "Greetings, Junior Brother He. I have heard of your reputation in the Outer Sect. It seems certain that you’ll be among the next group of disciples to join the Inner Sect."

"Thank you for the compliment, Senior Brother Fang." He Yiming cupped his fists, then moved forward with his Battle Ape, glancing up at the Mechanized Bamboo Dragon.

"In that case, I will activate the Mechanized Bamboo Dragon. Junior Brother, prepare yourself," Senior Brother Fang said, taking out a small bell strung with bamboo cords.

As he shook the bell, a white light flickered in the Bamboo Dragon’s eyes, and the winds around it began to swirl with renewed vigor.

The moment the bell rang, He Yiming's Battle Ape unwrapped its weapon, revealing—surprisingly—a mining pickaxe!

Though it looked rather unassuming, none of the Outer Sect disciples laughed.

"It's a spirit weapon!"

The pickaxe was forged from spiritual ores by a craftsman, making it a genuine spirit weapon!

"It's said that Senior Brother He Yiming once discovered a 'Bright Jade Stone' while on a sect mission in the mines. To reward him, the sect gifted him this spirit weapon pickaxe. So, it seems to be true."

Seeing the impressive pickaxe, several disciples looked on enviously. With this tool, He Yiming's mining efficiency would skyrocket, allowing him to gather resources at double the usual pace. It was no wonder he held the top spot in the Outer Sect!

Lín Jìng and the Pine Leaf Mouse remained silent as they listened to the crowd's chatter, absorbing the unexpected scene.

"Ha!" As soon as the spirit pickaxe was unsheathed, the Battle Ape was seemingly empowered, bursting forth with an intense strength. Enveloped in a spiraling aura of spiritual energy, it leapt up, propelling itself directly to the Bamboo Dragon's head, and brought the pickaxe down in a powerful blow!

Its speed and explosive force were exceptional, comparable to that of a body cultivator at the same level.

With a loud "bang," the pickaxe struck its target, and many Outer Sect disciples let out a collective gasp, impressed by the Battle Ape’s rapid speed, which seemed to outmatch the Bamboo Dragon's response.

Seeing this, He Yiming smiled.

The Bamboo Dragon's mouth opened as the Dragon Pearl within it changed colors rapidly—from white to red, orange, yellow, and then green!

Green? Just green?

Despite the impressive strike, the Dragon Pearl only reached the fourth tier of rewards. Moreover, the Bamboo Dragon barely budged, not even tilting its head.

It was worth noting that the Battle Ape was also at the seventh level, but the Bamboo Dragon’s reaction seemed almost unperturbed.

Moments later, to the Outer Sect disciples’ surprise, the Bamboo Dragon appeared to come alive as it responded to the blow. Its massive tail whipped forward in an instant, striking the Battle Ape squarely and launching it dozens of meters away!

Bang! Bang! The Battle Ape and its pickaxe crashed to the ground, with the former knocked unconscious. Despite its reputation as having "bones of steel," the Battle Ape couldn't withstand even a single blow. The sight left many disciples visibly shaken.

He Yiming himself looked incredulous, unable to accept the outcome.

"Green Dragon Pearl. Junior Brother He, better luck tomorrow," Senior Brother Fang consoled him.

"Senior Brother, is this Bamboo Dragon really only at the seventh level?" He Yiming asked in disbelief.

Senior Brother Fang nodded. "Yes, but this Bamboo Dragon is configured to be at the peak of the seventh level. Consider yourself fortunate; I’ve heard that the Mechanized Bamboo Dragon used for the Inner Sect’s Ascending Dragon Festival even reaches the Foundation Establishment level. Just the thought of it makes me shudder," he said, grateful for his assignment. Otherwise, he would have had to face the Foundation Establishment Bamboo Dragon, a challenge that he could barely imagine.

"Is there no one else?" Several minutes passed as Senior Brother Fang replenished the Bamboo Dragon’s energy with Spirit Stones, but still, no new challengers emerged.

Rather than encouraging confidence, He Yiming’s defeat had sown even deeper seeds of hesitation among the disciples.

"Come on now, you’re already here," Senior Brother Fang said, smiling at the junior disciples' reluctance.

“Is it ready? We’re up,” came another voice from the crowd.

The disciples parted once more, revealing a clear path to a young man with a delicate appearance, accompanied by a Pine Leaf Mouse perched on his shoulder.

Compared to He Yiming, fewer people recognized this youth, though there were still a few familiar faces.

Notably, Senior Brother Ziran from the Mission Hall and Senior Brother Fang Yonghao looked over in mild surprise as they saw it was Lín Jìng.

"Outer Sect disciple Lín Jìng, at the second level, accompanied by my spirit beast Pine Leaf Mouse, at the fifth level. We’re here to challenge the Mechanized Bamboo Dragon!" Lín Jìng declared, moving toward the center of the arena with his Pine Leaf Mouse.

"Who is that?"

"Second level? Fifth level?"

The crowd was taken aback. Although such levels weren't disqualified from participating, especially with rewards available at even the lowest tier, the sheer strength of the Bamboo Dragon had everyone astonished by Lín Jìng’s boldness.

"You..." Senior Brother Fang gave him an odd look. "Are you sure you want to challenge it?"

The instigator… had appeared!

"Nice to see you again, Senior Brother. Thanks for your ‘hospitality’ last time!” Lín Jìng said with a smile.

"Squeak, squeak!" The Pine Leaf Mouse glared at him as well.

Had it not been for his meddling, they might have been able to chop down Spirit Bamboo for another two days.

"Ah, I see," Senior Brother Fang replied, puzzled by the sentiment. "Since that’s the case, I'll activate the Bamboo Dragon again. Junior Brother, please be cautious."

With that, he shook the bamboo bell to start the Mechanized Beast once more.

Most onlookers assumed this unfamiliar Outer Sect disciple was merely going through the motions.

As the Bamboo Dragon whirred to life, Lín Jìng reached into his bag, drawing out a red maple leaf that the Pine Leaf Mouse caught in its tiny paws.

With a flick, the red leaf shot forth like a crimson comet, far faster than the Battle Ape’s earlier explosive move.

"My god, that’s so fast!" Another disciple who trained a Pine Leaf Mouse gasped, astounded by the technique's sheer speed.

"Did he stop training everything else just to practice this flying leaf technique?"

The other disciples exchanged surprised glances.

The leaf struck the Bamboo Dragon, embedding itself like a dagger driven into a dragon’s hide.

“ROAR!!!” Though it felt no pain, the Bamboo Dragon let out a metallic roar, as if agitated by the intrusion. The Dragon Pearl changed colors rapidly, reaching blue

this time, indicating that the damage from the Pine Leaf Mouse’s Flying Leaf technique exceeded that of the Battle Ape’s spirit weapon by at least two ranks!

The unexpected scene defied all expectations, as the Pine Leaf Mouse was not known for combat techniques.

"Blue?!" He Yiming, who was treating the Battle Ape with healing pills nearby, looked up in shock.

"Could it be something special about that leaf?" some guessed, suspecting the leaf to be an unusual item.

"It must be a rare malignant plant worth even more than a spirit pickaxe! And that Pine Leaf Mouse can handle it so well!"

But as impressive as the attack was, the Bamboo Dragon’s reaction was even more intense. With green spirit flames suddenly engulfing its body, it didn’t resemble an ordinary wood-based Mechanized Beast.

As the flames consumed the red maple leaf, the disciples gaped, completely thrown by the Bamboo Dragon’s unexpected fire skill. For disciples who had just entered the Beast Taming Sect, a Bamboo Dragon capable of both wind and fire techniques was truly an insurmountable challenge.

"It opens its mouth after taking damage, exposing the Dragon Pearl."

"Its initial actions are slow and its attack patterns limited, giving challengers a chance to strike first, followed by a counterattack."

"But relying on external attacks to break it from the outside would be challenging."

"The key must be to make it open its mouth and then..."

"Its weak spot must be inside."

"To disable a Mechanized Beast, one would need to dismantle its intricate mechanisms. Elder Yu wouldn’t make the Bamboo Dragon invincible."

While many disciples shuddered at the Bamboo Dragon’s toughness, Lín Jìng was muttering to himself. As they watched in confusion, he took out ten more red leaves from his bag.

The crowd was speechless.

Not only did the Pine Leaf Mouse take five leaves in each paw, but it also launched all ten at once in a dazzling flurry of crimson streaks aimed straight for the Bamboo Dragon’s head.

Disciples with Pine Leaf Mice were baffled, while others marveled at the sheer precision.

Even though the Bamboo Dragon moved, there was no way a direct hit could...

The Bamboo Dragon tilted its head slightly, causing the leaves to veer off target, with most aimed toward its face at best.

But then, in an instant…

"Object Manipulation!" Lín Jìng formed a hand sign, guiding the leaves.

With Lín Jìng’s command, the Pine Leaf Mouse directed the leaves with pinpoint accuracy into the Bamboo Dragon’s open mouth.

Crack! Crack!

The sound of mechanisms breaking echoed as the Dragon Pearl turned purple, then shattered. With the Bamboo Dragon’s wind and fire extinguished, it fell to the ground in pieces.

Thud!

Silence fell over the crowd.

Seeing this, the Pine Leaf Mouse practically squealed with joy.

Was it really that fragile? Now it didn’t need to use its Spiritual Bean bomb!

It ran forward eagerly, ready to retrieve its precious leaves.

"Who is he? What’s his story?" This was the question on every disciple's mind. Lín Jìng, who had been in the Beast Taming Sect for some time, had finally caught the attention of the entire Outer Sect.

"Senior Brother, does this... count as a successful challenge?" Lín Jìng turned to the Senior Brother Guardian.

Senior Brother Fang’s mouth twitched, thinking, "You harvested the bamboo for it, and now you’ve dismantled it, all without a trace of true energy. Do you have a grudge against bamboo? And your Object Manipulation technique—why is there no spiritual fluctuation at all?!"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 22: The Reward
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 22: The Reward

In the Outer Sect’s Mechanized Workshop.

Inside the workshop, Elder Yu looked at the pile of Spirit Bamboo that was still mostly unused and sighed.

“Elder Yu, something urgent!” A celestial crane swooped down in a flurry.

“What’s going on? Why all the fuss?” Elder Yu looked up with a frown at the approaching Tianyu Crane.

“Your Mechanized Bamboo Dragon… someone destroyed it!” the crane reported.

“It was an Outer Sect disciple named Lín Jìng and his Pine Leaf Mouse.”

Hearing this, Elder Yu simply let out a dismissive hum. “Is that something to panic over?”

“It shows that, under my guidance, our Outer Sect disciples are getting stronger by the day!”

The crane blinked, momentarily bewildered. Elder Yu was famously protective of the mechanized beasts he crafted. Now that one had been smashed by an Outer Sect disciple, he was remaining… calm?

Something strange was definitely going on. The Tianyu Crane left with a skeptical look, carrying away the new Bamboo Dragon.

Meanwhile, Elder Yu inwardly let out a sigh of relief. Fortunately, Elder Mo had given him a heads-up about this Longevity Body's unusual talents. Otherwise, knowing his creation had been demolished would’ve been anything but calming.

Though one Bamboo Dragon had been lost, Elder Yu was grateful for the forewarning and had even come up with a plan!

Now aware that the Longevity Body and the mutated Pine Leaf Mouse might be able to destroy a Bamboo Dragon, he could use this knowledge to encourage Lín Jìng to learn the art of mechanized beasts.

Apart from someone with an abundance of time—like one with a Longevity Body—no one else would be willing to study the craft he had honed!

Now that he had a promising student in front of him, Elder Yu decided to test Lín Jìng’s attitude toward mechanized arts.

…

Back in the Central Plaza, the disciples were still in shock after Lín Jìng’s successful challenge, especially given that he had broken the Mechanized Bamboo Dragon.

“No wonder Elder Yu is willing to teach him personally. This guy… definitely wasn’t recruited through normal channels. He must be a prodigy found by some elder, likely with a unique cultivation body!” Senior Brother Ziran from the Mission Hall clenched his novel tightly, finally understanding what was going on.

“I remember now. When I was guarding the Harvest Pavilion, they seemed to have bought some spirit plant seeds,” Senior Sister Caixin, ranked among the top three in the Outer Sect, also recognized Lín Jìng. She could hardly reconcile the image of the penniless junior who couldn’t even afford a Foundation Establishment spirit beast’s droppings with the skilled fighter in front of her.

The one most affected, however, was the current number one in the Outer Sect. Thinking back on all the praise he had received, he felt a slight flush of embarrassment, reflecting on how the Beast Taming Sect truly deserved its top status within the Wild Domain. Even the Outer Sect was filled with hidden talents, and the Beast Fighting Arena… apparently wasn’t even on Lín Jìng’s radar.

Having firmly entered the minds of all Outer Sect disciples, Lín Jìng was now regarded as the new powerhouse among them. Not only was his Pine Leaf Mouse’s Flying Leaf technique remarkably advanced, but he himself, despite being only at the second level, was also proficient in Object Manipulation—a remarkable feat.

“That kind of speed and power… To manipulate the trajectory so smoothly at the second level while using Object Manipulation? My Phoenix-Tailed Hen laying eggs isn’t that smooth!”

“Is this considered a ‘combined technique’?”

The Pine Leaf Mouse, who had returned after gathering its red leaves, wanted to correct the crowd—it had been controlling the red leaves on its own the entire time!

Lín Jìng’s actions were just for show, mimicking the gestures of Object Manipulation purely to add some theatrics, as his role was mostly limited to helping the mouse carry its cumbersome red leaves and Spiritual Bean bombs.

“Congratulations, Junior Brother…” Senior Brother Fang Yonghao was still in awe, though now more at a loss about how to explain this to Elder Yu. As an Inner Sect disciple, this was the first time he had encountered such an unusual Outer Sect disciple… He suspected that even those with rare constitutions, now true disciples, hadn’t shown such prowess at the second level as Lín Jìng had.

“Senior Brother Fang, does this mean we can proceed directly to the reward?” Ultimately, Lín Jìng chose the highest-grade mechanized reward and gave up any thought of holding back. There was no harm in learning more skills; after all, he had already memorized the Compendium of Flora and Fauna stored in the Outer Sect’s Scripture Pavilion over the past few days.

As far as true alchemy or the opportunity to practice it went, he knew he would have to wait until he joined the Inner Sect. For now, he and the Pine Leaf Mouse would have to endure this waiting period, as there was little else to do.

“Probably…” Senior Brother Fang had barely finished speaking when a shadow appeared in the sky. A celestial crane circled above, clutching the massive Mechanized Bamboo Dragon, and called out:

“The Beast Fighting Competition continues. Outer Sect disciple Lín Jìng, proceed to the Mechanized Workshop to claim your event reward.”

After a nod of acknowledgment, Lín Jìng set off with his Pine Leaf Mouse, leaving the crowd staring at his retreating figure, aware that he was destined for great things. Elder Yu’s mechanized arts, though not on par with the sect’s full Beast Taming techniques, were still far superior to the standard beast taming methods taught to Inner and Outer Sect disciples alike. Without access to a higher-level cultivation manual, these basics would be hard to surpass.

After all, it was a skill capable of creating Foundation Establishment-level mechanized beasts!

“A new contender for the true disciple spot is on the rise, and soon the Inner Sect will have another powerhouse. I wonder how the Inner Sect disciples will react when they find out,” Senior Brother Fang muttered, shaking his head as he continued to oversee the event.

Summoned by the Tianyu Crane, Lín Jìng quickly arrived at the Mechanized Workshop.

Inside, Elder Yu spotted Lín Jìng and his Pine Leaf Mouse and exclaimed, his eyebrows dancing with amusement, “You rascal, have you become addicted to tearing apart bamboo? First you ravage the bamboo grove, now you demolish the Bamboo Dragon!”

He glanced at the remaining Spirit Bamboo piled up in the back, drawing Lín Jìng and the Pine Leaf Mouse’s gaze. They both turned a bit red, feeling slightly embarrassed.

“Outer Sect disciple Lín Jìng greets Elder Yu.”

“Squeak!”

“Elder, the Mechanized Bamboo Dragon you crafted had a protective spirit flame, so the Pine Leaf Mouse’s external attacks were hopeless. We could only go for the mouth… who knew…”

“Enough, enough. The fact that you managed to destroy the internal mechanism using only your abilities at this stage speaks to your skills.”

“This Soul Core and my mechanized beast crafting manual are my reward to you,” Elder Yu said, pulling out the pre-prepared prizes and handing them over with an air of generosity.

The Soul Core was white, resembling an intricate puzzle lock, while the manual on mechanized arts was a hefty tome.

“Thank you, Elder Yu,” Lín Jìng said gratefully. But then, Elder Yu added:

“Oh, right. The extra bamboo you harvested isn’t particularly useful to me. Spirit Bamboo is a basic material for mechanized constructs. Take it back with you, and when you’ve grasped the basics of mechanized arts, you can use the Spirit Bamboo to experiment.”

“Of course, mechanized arts are quite difficult. I doubt you’ll master it quickly. Let’s say if you manage to create a functioning mechanized beast, even if it’s not particularly useful, I’ll grant you one wish—within reason!”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 23: Spirit Bamboo!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 23: Spirit Bamboo!

"A single wish… fulfilled!"

At those words, the Pine Leaf Mouse, which had been yawning in boredom, suddenly opened its eyes wide.

It wasn’t interested in mechanized arts or anything fancy, preferring simple Spirit Stones instead. However, upon hearing Elder Yu’s offer, the Pine Leaf Mouse became instantly excited.

Asking for a storage pouch surely wouldn’t be an unreasonable wish, right?

Whether Lín Jìng felt inclined to study mechanized arts was uncertain, but the Pine Leaf Mouse suddenly felt a strong urge to craft a mechanized beast—if only to secure that wish.

Elder Yu’s proposal was a masterstroke.

Even Lín Jìng, normally calm and composed, found himself looking up at Elder Yu, seriously contemplating if his offer… could really be true.

“Elder, can we really wish for anything within reason?” Lín Jìng asked hesitantly.

“Actually, Elder, my Pine Leaf Mouse and I do have a bit of a dilemma, but it’s probably something within your authority to address…”

“What’s the issue?”

Just as the Pine Leaf Mouse was expecting Lín Jìng to ask for a storage pouch, he communicated with it mentally.

Storage pouches are easy to buy, but a wish granted by an Outer Sect elder is a rare opportunity!

The Pine Leaf Mouse was stunned.

Lín Jìng cupped his fists respectfully. “Elder, since joining the sect, both my Pine Leaf Mouse and I have rather large appetites. We’ve never once had a meal that’s truly filled us up, nor have we ever fully experienced the dining hall. If we succeed in creating a mechanized beast, could we… perhaps eat for free in the Outer Sect dining hall?”

The Pine Leaf Mouse: "!!!"

“What??” Elder Yu had thought it was a bigger issue, but he laughed heartily, stroking his beard. “That’s a small matter. Naturally, it’s no problem. There’s always some surplus in the dining hall each day, which would normally go to the spirit and monster beasts kept by the sect. Adding the two of you to the list is hardly an issue.”

Elder Yu felt relieved; thankfully, Outer Sect disciples had limited experience, and their requests were modest.

“Thank you, Elder! When we return, we’ll focus wholeheartedly on studying mechanized arts!”

While the Pine Leaf Mouse still wanted a storage pouch, it couldn’t find any fault with Lín Jìng’s wish.

After all… with their golden spiritual rice, their daily intake was easily ten times that of an average disciple.

If there were somewhere they could eat high-quality food for free every day, the resources and money they’d save over time could buy several storage pouches.

As the Pine Leaf Mouse calculated this, it looked at Elder Yu, feeling satisfied.

“Good.” Elder Yu, still unaware of the full implications, continued, “Just don’t push yourselves too hard. The path of mechanized arts is one best pursued out of genuine interest.”

“Actually, as a beast cultivator, picking up mechanized skills could be quite advantageous.”

“Mechanized beasts are still beasts, and unlike a bonded contract beast that’s like family or a friend, they have no life of their own and can be used for more dangerous tasks—such as testing formations or exploring perilous areas.”

“You’ve been in the sect for a while now; do you have any other interests besides beast cultivation?” Elder Yu inquired, hoping to gradually spark Lín Jìng’s curiosity about mechanized arts.

“Reporting to Elder…” Lín Jìng replied honestly, “I’m actually interested in alchemy. Recently, I’ve been memorizing the effects of different plants.”

“Alchemy…” Elder Yu sighed. Wasn’t alchemy just too conventional? Alchemy, crafting, and the like—how could they compare to the intrigue of mechanized arts?

“Alchemy… isn’t easy,” he added. “The process is tedious. Before even learning alchemical techniques, you have to memorize the properties of tens of thousands of plants.”

“Indeed, remembering all that has taken me quite a while.” Lín Jìng sighed, recalling his previous life’s grind of exams and the countless hours spent studying.

Elder Yu: ?

“You’ve memorized the Complete Herb Compendium from the Outer Sect already?” Elder Yu asked, almost certain he’d misheard.

Lín Jìng had only joined the sect recently, and the sheer volume and complexity of the information were normally tackled gradually by cultivators in the important stage. The jade slips couldn’t simply transfer the knowledge; that would overload a person’s mind.

“Yep.” Lín Jìng nodded. He had indeed noticed an improvement in his memory after crossing into this world, especially since starting cultivation. Combined with the mental fatigue relief of his Spirit Nourishing Technique, memorizing texts wasn’t too challenging.

“What’s the effect of Ye Linglong?” Elder Yu quizzed.

“It harmonizes Qi and relieves pain. It’s one of the core ingredients in pills for treating ailments caused by disrupted true Qi.”

“And Seven-Star Chrysanthemum?”

“It warms the Yang and dispels cold. It’s used in…”

“And what about Spirit Bamboo Sap?” Elder Yu pointed to the pile of Spirit Bamboo.

“It soothes the mind and revitalizes the spirit. It’s a secondary ingredient in the Pure Spirit Pill.”

Elder Yu was speechless. Initially, he had intended to dissuade Lín Jìng from pursuing alchemy, but now he had no words.

Alright, it seemed this clever Longevity Body could probably handle mechanized arts as well. If he could learn, he might as well learn it all!

…

After giving the remaining Spirit Bamboo to Lín Jìng, Elder Yu left them to their own devices.

His own mess, his own responsibility. Lín Jìng and the Pine Leaf Mouse spent a lot of effort hauling all the Spirit Bamboo back to their courtyard.

They could have hired a disciple with an Ox Beast contract to help transport it, but upon considering the cost, they decided against it.

Before long, the Spirit Bamboo was transferred from the Mechanized Workshop to Lín Jìng’s courtyard.

Gazing at the pile of Spirit Bamboo, Lín Jìng rubbed his chin thoughtfully. Now, to earn unlimited dining hall privileges, he’d really have to put effort into mastering mechanized arts.

“Come to think of it…” Lín Jìng looked at the Spirit Bamboo and suddenly remembered something.

“When we were in the bamboo grove, I thought of having you use your Glazed Immortal Eyes on some Spirit Bamboo, but gathering it all had worn you out.”

“Now that we have the chance, would you like to try? It’s still technically flora.”

“Squeak?” The Pine Leaf Mouse jumped off Lín Jìng’s shoulder and stared at him, Not using it for mechanized arts?

“It’s just Spirit Bamboo; we can always buy more.” Lín Jìng shrugged, unconcerned. This resource wasn’t particularly rare.

The Pine Leaf Mouse circled around, thinking it over, and then directed its Glazed Immortal Eyes at the pile of Spirit Bamboo to test its effects…

Suddenly!

A jade-colored light burst from its eyes.

The divine power of the Glazed Immortal Eyes enveloped ten pieces of emerald-green Spirit Bamboo.

It seemed to be working!

In the next instant, under the radiant green glow, the ten stalks of Spirit Bamboo slowly… merged into a single small, blue Spirit Bamboo stalk!

“Squeak…” The Pine Leaf Mouse clutched its eyes, squeaking in pain. The process had drained much more of its eye power than synthesizing Spirit Rice or Spiritual Beans, and it felt as though its eyes had been strained beyond their limits.

Despite the discomfort, the Pine Leaf Mouse urged Lín Jìng to quickly examine the effects of the blue Spirit Bamboo.

Following its suggestion, Lín Jìng cautiously picked up the blue Spirit Bamboo. Remembering his last encounter with the Black Bear Monster, he handled it with care, avoiding too much force.

"Hm?" As soon as he lifted it, a bead of crystalline sap dripped from one end of the Spirit Bamboo.

This brought to mind Elder Yu’s question earlier about Spirit Bamboo Sap.

“Spirit Bamboo Sap? Normally, Spirit Bamboo requires years of exposure to natural rain, sun, and lunar energy, as well as wind, rain, and lightning, before it produces sap while it’s still growing. These harvested stalks wouldn’t typically produce Spirit Bamboo Sap anymore… Could it be that the effect of blue Spirit Bamboo is that it naturally generates Spirit Bamboo Sap?”

Lín Jìng’s mind raced with analysis, but as he pondered, the pressure inside the Spirit Bamboo kept building. Suddenly, with a loud “boom,” a high-pressure jet of liquid burst from the bamboo, blasting a hole in the wall. This wasn’t Spirit Bamboo Sap—it was a pressurized water cannon!

The force from the blast launched Lín Jìng into the air, sending him crashing into the building as his body and hands trembled.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 24: The Concept of a Mechanized Beast
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 24: The Concept of a Mechanized Beast

“What the heck!” Lín Jìng’s face turned black with frustration.

He had assumed the liquid flowing out of the Spirit Bamboo was Spirit Bamboo Sap, but it kept gushing out with such high pressure that even a water-jet technique from a cultivator at the important stage couldn’t compare.

He struggled to control the wildly spraying water.

Elder Yu had created a Mechanized Bamboo Dragon that could “generate fire from wood,” but this “wood generating water” was a bit much.

After sliding down the wall, Lín Jìng looked over at his Pine Leaf Mouse, which seemed equally baffled.

“Which deity in the upper realms gave the Glazed Immortal Eyes this unreliable power?” he muttered, exasperated.

The Pine Leaf Mouse, seeing that Lín Jìng was unharmed, spread its paws in confusion.

Perhaps it would be better to have Elder Black Bear try out any new items in the future. Experimenting themselves was far too risky!

Setting down the blue Spirit Bamboo, Lín Jìng dipped his fingers in the bamboo water and took a sniff.

“It’s quite refreshing, without any sweetness. It doesn’t seem to have the medicinal properties of regular Spirit Bamboo Sap.”

“But it’s also not ordinary water; it feels more like the water of a spiritual spring, enriched by the ambient spirit energy of a rich spiritual environment.”

“If that’s the case, it might not have a significant effect, but it could still be used to water spiritual plants. Maybe it’ll even boost the yield and growth rate of our Spiritual Rice and Spiritual Beans.”

At his words, the Pine Leaf Mouse pointed to the large hole in the wall. Watering?

Wouldn’t this high-pressure flow blast the Spirit Rice out of the ground?

“We can collect it and then water as needed, and I think I understand its function now. Moving forward, it should be manageable,” Lín Jìng replied. “I read about a type of spiritual plant called the Sky Fountain Tree in one of the books.”

“It can gather moisture from the heavens and store it within itself. Once full, it sprays the stored water skyward, creating a natural fountain.”

“There’s an old tale about a village in the Ancient Celestial Kingdom where a child accidentally scraped the bark of a Sky Fountain Tree, leading the village to battle floods for three years!”

“This blue Spirit Bamboo seems to function similarly. Just now, it probably expelled all the water it had accumulated while mutating. So, as long as we don’t let it store water for too long, we should be able to drain it daily.”

The Pine Leaf Mouse looked at Lín Jìng in admiration. How did he know so much?

“Reading more books really isn’t a bad thing,” Lín Jìng said, adjusting imaginary glasses as he glanced at the Pine Leaf Mouse.

Cultivation life was ideal for this; reading so many books without any worry about nearsightedness.

“Oh, right.”

Suddenly, Lín Jìng’s eyes lit up. “I was wondering what kind of mechanized beast to create, and now I have an idea.”

“Squeak?”

“A complicated mechanized beast is beyond our abilities for now.”

“To get into the dining hall sooner, let’s start with a small, auxiliary mechanized beast.”

“We won’t bother with something that just moves around or with combat abilities like the Mechanized Bamboo Dragon. Instead… we’ll make a mechanized turtle that can automatically find its way around and spray water to irrigate the spiritual plants!” Lín Jìng slapped his palm in excitement at the thought.

“With a blue Spirit Bamboo that can gather ambient moisture, we can save a lot of effort.”

A mechanized turtle?

The Pine Leaf Mouse scratched its head with its long tail.

“Squeak?” Why a mechanized turtle and not a mechanized frog?

The Beast Taming Sect’s five foundational spirit beasts included the Rain Frog, a water-type beast known for summoning rain to irrigate spiritual plants. It was more familiar with Rain Frogs than turtles.

“Because I have the Longevity Body,” Lín Jìng explained.

“The turtle has always symbolized longevity. Don’t you think a mechanized turtle fits perfectly with the Longevity Body?”

He took out the manual on mechanized arts that Elder Yu had given him, along with the Soul Core.

The former held the key to crafting the mechanized turtle, and the latter… wouldn’t be needed just yet.

The mechanized turtle he envisioned would essentially be a glorified “watering can,” a simple construct. The Soul Core, however, served as the “heart” of advanced mechanized beasts, designed for more complex creations.

The Soul Core was the link between the mechanized beast and its creator, enabling the beast to act with the awareness and control of its maker during combat, making it more flexible and responsive.

…

After the Ascending Dragon Festival, Lín Jìng’s name spread across the Outer and even Inner Sects.

Though his reputation grew, Lín Jìng and the Pine Leaf Mouse continued their simple, focused routine. They studied what they needed to, stockpiled resources, and kept a low profile, rarely seen by other Outer Sect disciples.

To Lín Jìng, this wasn’t tedious. It wasn’t that he particularly enjoyed it, but his twenty-something years of life experience had taught him the value of patience and endurance.

More than that, the returns of cultivation and the slim chance of transcendence inspired him, making the hard work feel worthwhile.

But things were not so peaceful elsewhere.

In the Outer Sect’s Beast Fighting Arena…

Bang!

The echo of a powerful impact rang out as a Battle Ape, covered in blood with deep claw marks across its chest, collapsed lifelessly to the ground. Beside it lay He Yiming, the former number one of the Outer Sect, badly burned and writhing in pain beside his beast.

Seeing this, many Outer Sect disciples covered their mouths in shock. In ordinary sparring matches at the arena, even among rivals, it was rare for fights to become this brutal.

“My apologies, I got carried away,” said a black-clad youth stroking a sleek, predatory cat. “But Elder Li was right; the sect truly shelters a bunch of delicate flowers. Too weak.”

Many new Outer Sect disciples didn’t recognize him, but there were three other youths beside him, equally cold and indifferent.

It was clear they held no regard for He Yiming, the once-top scorer in the arena.

“Are… these people also from the Beast Taming Sect?” one Outer Sect disciple murmured in disbelief.

Nearby, Senior Brother Ziran, who had rushed over after hearing the commotion, took one look at their distinctive black uniforms adorned with blood-red patterns. His face paled, and he said in a grave tone, “They’re from the Outer Sect’s Demon-Hunting Hall. I can’t believe so many of them made it back alive this time.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 25: The Demon-Hunting Hall
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 25: The Demon-Hunting Hall

“Excuse me, is Junior Brother Lín Jìng here?”

“You’re… Senior Sister from the Harvest Pavilion?”

It had been half a month since the Ascending Dragon Festival, and Lín Jìng was deeply engrossed in his cultivation when an unexpected visitor arrived.

The young woman, dressed in traditional Daoist robes with a delicate, almost frail beauty, had the appearance of a sickly, elegant lady.

“Junior Brother, apologies for the interruption. My name is Qin Caixin, and I’m here with a request,” Senior Sister Caixin said politely.

“Please, go ahead, Senior Sister.” Lín Jìng recognized the name vaguely; she was rumored to be one of the strongest in the Outer Sect. Surprisingly, she was also the same senior sister who had once tried to sell him fertilizer.

“Junior Brother, have you been outside the sect recently? Do you know about Senior Brother He Yiming, who was seriously injured by members of the Demon-Hunting Hall in the Beast Fighting Arena?”

“You mean the senior brother who was the first to challenge the Bamboo Dragon during the Ascending Dragon Festival?” Lín Jìng was unsure. “What exactly is the Demon-Hunting Hall?”

“Thus, he created the Demon-Hunting Hall. Every few years, he recruits a group of Outer Sect disciples, who then don’t train within the sect but are instead thrown into the wild mountains, left to survive by fighting demon beasts, with their lives dependent on fate.”

“Without the comfort of the sect, their cultivation may not be as high, but they are formidable in life-and-death combat. Senior Brother He Yiming was no match for them.”

“And why are you telling me all this, Senior Sister?” Lín Jìng asked. “It’s not as if they’re coming to pick a fight with me… but if they are, could you let them know I agree with Elder Li’s training method? It just might waste a lot of disciples and spirit beasts.”

Senior Sister Caixin gave a wry smile. “It’s not that. You’re right, after all. Death training far surpasses closed-door cultivation in boosting combat power, but the death rate… Well, three years ago, Elder Li took about a dozen disciples, and only four have returned alive.”

“Our Beast Taming Sect isn’t a demonic sect, so there’s no way we’d adopt this kind of training broadly.”

“Actually, the reason I came to see you… is to ask if you’d be willing to sell me a few of those red leaves you used against the Bamboo Dragon.”

“My contract beast is a Tree Bear, and it knows the Flying Leaf technique. We’ve tried various spiritual plant leaves, but none have the destructive power of the red leaves your Pine Leaf Mouse used.”

“With the Inner Sect assessment a few months away, and with the return of the Demon-Hunting Hall members raising its difficulty, I need all the strength I can get.”

Lín Jìng understood, though he replied with regret, “I’m sorry, Senior Sister, but due to certain unique properties, those red leaves can only be used by us.”

He wasn’t lying. The red leaves created by the Pine Leaf Mouse had a special “synchronized mind” effect, allowing it to control them with ease. Under this “mindful control,” the power and speed of the leaves increased significantly, letting it manipulate up to ten red leaves simultaneously at the fifth level of cultivation. Other spirit beasts, however, wouldn’t share this unique connection.

“I see…” Senior Sister Caixin sighed. “Thank you for your time, Junior Brother Lín Jìng.”

She left, and as Lín Jìng closed the courtyard door, he prepared to get back to his reading.

“Heh heh.” To his surprise, a low chuckle came from inside the courtyard.

Standing there was Elder Black Bear, dressed in white Daoist robes, looking immensely pleased with himself.

“I remember that girl. She’s from a small cultivator family, born with a weak constitution that prevents normal training. Her family sent her to the Beast Taming Sect in hopes that she could cultivate here.”

The Elder Black Bear chuckled, “You’ve got a solid Dao heart, kid. Pretty girl makes a request, and you don’t bat an eye! I’ve got faith that you’ll achieve great things.”

“And, considering you have the Longevity Body, a regular Dao companion wouldn’t be the best match for you. You’d be better off finding a lady with a higher cultivation level so she doesn’t… leave you behind too soon.”

Lín Jìng gave Elder Black Bear a helpless look. “Elder, was there a reason for your sudden visit?”

“Your Pine Leaf Mouse has a stockpile of red leaves it can’t use all by itself. If they could be sold, I’d have done it ages ago. They really can only work for it,” Lín Jìng explained.

“Oh, there’s no pressing issue,” Elder Black Bear replied, glancing around the courtyard at the gears and parts scattered about, including a few crooked turtle models. “So, you’ve been studying mechanized arts? Looking good—it’s quite promising.”

“I actually just came to remind you.”

“That girl mentioned the Inner Sect assessment in a few months. Even if you’re still a ways from reaching the seventh level, with the Pine Leaf Mouse’s talents, if it’s provided enough resources, it might reach the seventh level in time, allowing you to take part in the assessment!”

“After joining the Inner Sect, you’ll have to choose a peak to join. Different peaks within the Beast Taming Sect specialize in various skills, like alchemy at Danxia Peak, dragon-blood beasts at Nine Dragons Peak, and water-type beasts at Clear Pool Peak…”

“Each Outer Sect disciple eventually chooses a peak that aligns with their abilities and studies techniques there.”

“Now, Elder Mo has taken a relaxed role at Lotus Peak, while Elder Yu oversees the Outer Sect and doesn’t involve himself with Inner Sect matters. So, you’ll need to think carefully about which peak to join.”

“Once you’ve decided, I can put in a good word for you with the elders there. In this sect, even the Golden Core elders have to show me some respect,” Elder Black Bear assured.

Lín Jìng was most interested in Danxia Peak due to the effort he’d already invested in alchemy.

“But Elder Black Bear, I don’t think you’re just here for this,” Lín Jìng said, sensing more.

“Ha, you’re sharp.” Elder Black Bear chuckled. “Keep this between us, but I’ve always loved the taste of a spirit fruit called Dragonbone Flower. After reaching the Monster Core stage, though, its nutrients no longer have any effect on me, though I still eat a few now and then for the taste.”

“Recently, my mother came all this way to the Beast Taming Sect to visit me and brought a bunch of Dragonbone Flowers from home. It got me wondering if, since that Pine Leaf Mouse of yours can synthesize Spirit Rice and Spiritual Beans, it could perhaps synthesize the Dragonbone Flower?”

“Maybe a synthesized one would not only satisfy my taste buds but offer other benefits, too. Not everything it makes blows up, right?”

“If you’re willing to give it a try, I’ll compensate you. But keep it quiet—don’t let Elder Mo know I’ve been bothering you. If he finds out, I’ll be in trouble!”

Lín Jìng nodded. “But Elder, I’ve never heard of the Dragonbone Flower, and I don’t remember reading about it in the Complete Herb Compendium... Could it be too high-grade for the Pine Leaf Mouse to handle?”

“Not to worry. It’s just a rare low-grade spirit fruit with no particular value beyond its taste,” he replied, taking out a Dragonbone Flower from his storage pouch. The fruit resembled a dragon fruit, and as he took a bite, its red flesh gave the frightening illusion that Elder Black Bear’s mouth was covered in blood.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 26: Dao Robe
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 26: Dao Robe

Isn’t this a Red Heart Dragon Fruit?!

When Lin Jing saw the spiritual fruit that Elder Black Bear took out, he was momentarily stunned.

"Do you want to try one?"

Elder Black Bear handed Lin Jing another fruit.

"Thank you." Lin Jing took the Dragonbone Flower and stored it.

He then glanced around the room and said, "The Pine Leaf Mouse should be sleeping now. I'll go wake it up."

Even the Pine Leaf Mouse, a naturally gifted worker and farmer, gets tired sometimes.

In a short while, Lin Jing pulled the Pine Leaf Mouse out of its sweet dream.

However, when it vaguely heard Lin Jing mention “reward,” it instantly perked up.

Seeing the nodding Elder Black Bear, the Pine Leaf Mouse cautiously asked:

"Zhi!" Could it ask for a storage bag?

"Ah, well…" Elder Black Bear paused, looking a bit awkward.

"Elder Black Bear, with your rank as a Monster Core stage cultivator, you don’t have a storage bag, do you?" Lin Jing couldn't help but comment.

"Nonsense!" Elder Black Bear retorted. "It’s just… I only have one for personal use at the moment."

"You don’t understand, the materials for creating and upgrading a Beast Taming Space Magical Treasure are completely different from those for making storage bags. And the demand for them is even greater."

"So, in the Beast Taming Sect, normal storage gear is in short supply. Every piece is strictly regulated and distributed according to need... In fact, almost all the spatial materials in the Great Wilderness Domain have been bought up by the Beast Taming Sect, making them rare on the market."

"If you really want one, I can’t promise you a good one... it would be noticed by Elder Mo. But if it’s one of those tiny storage bags made from scrap materials, I can try to get one through... 'unofficial' channels."

"Unofficial channels??? Wait, how small is 'tiny'?"

Elder Black Bear held up a finger. "It can store about one cubic meter of resources."

One cubic meter… it really can’t hold much. It’s just slightly more convenient than carrying a bundle, that’s all.

"Oh well." Elder Black Bear sighed. "You can make do with that. When you become true disciples, you’ll get your own space magical treasure. You’re still in the Qi Refining stage, so there’s no point in having too good of a storage item right now."

"Right, if you really want one, you can work hard for the Inner Sect assessment. There's a proper way to get a storage bag then!"

"The Inner Sect assessment is always done with a points system. The better you perform, the more points you earn, and with enough points, you can exchange them for magical items or pills, including larger storage equipment. It’s a benefit for newly promoted inner sect disciples, and the exchange rate is much better than what you get from contributing sect tasks."

"Inner Sect assessment?" Lin Jing looked up slightly.

Sure enough, no matter where you go, they all love using these promotion systems to push people forward.

Elder Black Bear continued: "I think with your talent, you’ll definitely be the top performers in this assessment. Even though the little brats Elder Li Hai brought back are vicious, I doubt they can beat you guys."

Lin Jing glanced at the Pine Leaf Mouse. Its thoughts were already drifting towards the Inner Sect assessment.

It was eager to go.

Soon, Elder Black Bear and Lin Jing reached an agreement. The Pine Leaf Mouse would help synthesize the Dragonbone Flowers, and Elder Black Bear would find a tiny storage bag as a reward.

A storage bag with one cubic meter of space might not be much, but it would definitely be enough to store Red Leaf Fruits and Spiritual Beans for now.

Thinking that it could soon have its own storage bag, the Pine Leaf Mouse was filled with energy. Its green eyes stared at the Dragonbone Flowers on the ground.

Glazed Immortal Eyes!

Then, nothing happened.

The Pine Leaf Mouse blinked in confusion, casting a pleading glance at Lin Jing.

With its current strength, it seemed… it wasn’t quite enough to synthesize the resource.

"Zhi!!!" It rubbed its sore eyes, its gaze broken. Was the storage bag really that far out of reach?

"Hm?" Elder Black Bear scratched his head. "You want to breakthrough a few times? This doesn’t seem reliable."

Rather than wait for the Pine Leaf Mouse to make a breakthrough, he waved his sleeve. "Wait here. I’ll lend you something to help, give it another try!"

With that, he rummaged through his own storage item... and pulled out a white Dao robe, one that looked very similar to the one he was wearing.

"Small, small, small—" he murmured to the Dao robe, and quickly, the robe shrank to a size comparable to the Pine Leaf Mouse.

"Put on my Dao robe. It’s a magical tool that amplifies most types of magical power, including wood element spiritual energy," Elder Black Bear said as he tossed the miniature Dao robe to the Pine Leaf Mouse.

Whoosh!

The Pine Leaf Mouse caught it, slightly opening its mouth in surprise.

"This..." Lin Jing hesitated for a moment. "Since it’s a magical tool, we might as well try it."

"Zhi, zhi, zhi." The Pine Leaf Mouse nodded eagerly, draping the robe over itself.

The next moment!

The Pine Leaf Mouse, now wearing a pristine white Dao robe, suddenly emitted an air of transcendence.

The robe’s hem swayed gently in the wind. It grasped the edges of the robe with its small paws, walking a few steps. Its movements remained nimble, but now had a certain ethereal grace.

Most importantly, the Pine Leaf Mouse felt an overwhelming surge of power throughout its body.

It opened its mouth slightly, finally understanding why Elder Black Bear was so fond of wearing Dao robes.

"Although, with its cultivation level, it can’t fully unleash the robe’s power, it should still provide some improvement," Elder Black Bear said with a grin.

With that, the Pine Leaf Mouse turned its gaze back to the Dragonbone Flowers on the ground… and sure enough, its Eye Technique activated smoothly.

As the Glazed Immortal Eyes activated... roughly a hundred Dragonbone Flowers instantly merged from their bright red color into a single purple Dragonbone Flower!

"Success!" Elder Black Bear cheered joyfully.

"Success!" Lin Jing let out a sigh of relief. It seemed that both weapon forging and tailoring were worth learning when they had time!

The quality of the Dragonbone Flower was clearly higher than that of Spiritual Rice, Spiritual Beans, or Spiritual Bamboo, making it harder to synthesize. But now, the synthesis was complete, and the Pine Leaf Mouse didn’t feel any eye strain. It couldn't help but lovingly stroke the Dao robe on its body.

"Mine!" Unfortunately, before the Pine Leaf Mouse could carefully observe, Elder Black Bear snatched the Dao robe back and chuckled, "You can’t have this. These robes are my lifeline."

The Pine Leaf Mouse puffed out its cheeks in protest.

Lin Jing smiled. The value of the Dao robe was probably higher than that of ordinary storage equipment. This type of amplification item would be better to buy when they became more successful.

"Elder Black Bear, would you like to try it?" Lin Jing asked, curious about the effect of the Purple Heart Dragon Fruit.

"Try? I don’t need to try. I’m not short of one." Elder Black Bear reached into his robe’s sleeve and pulled out an unconscious white rat demon, shaking it awake.

"This one’s a Qi Refining 9th layer rat demon I caught easily. Let it eat the first one for me."

Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse were speechless.

They didn’t even know what to say.

Elder Black Bear really was cautious. Was it really necessary to be so careful over a first-grade spiritual fruit synthesis?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 27: Everything Goes Black
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 27: Everything Goes Black

"Squeak squeak squeak!"

The white mouse demon trembled in fear from the terrifying demonic power of Elder Black Bear.

When the leaf-covered eyes of the Pine Leaf Mouse heard the squeaking sound, it instantly thought of a distant relative and felt sorry for it.

So, it pulled out a long bamboo leaf soaked in blue spiritual bamboo water and used it as a blindfold. If it couldn't see, it wouldn't feel so bad.

Recently, after using its eye technique, the Pine Leaf Mouse had gotten into the habit of cooling its eyes by soaking them in bamboo leaves dipped in spiritual bamboo water. It felt a lot more comfortable that way.

"Eat it!" Elder Black Bear tossed the white mouse demon in front of the purple fire dragon fruit.

"What if it's something good? You might even profit!"

"If you don't die after eating it, I'll send you back to the mountain."

But now, before it could meet a disciple, it was already caught by Elder Black Bear and used as a test subject.

"Ugh!" The white mouse demon, looking desperate, began to nibble on the purple fire dragon fruit. It dared not refuse, but after just two bites, it was surprised to find that it tasted quite good and didn’t seem poisonous at all.

It... might not have to die after all!

The mouse demon ate faster and faster, and soon, it had consumed an entire mutated dragon bone flower.

Elder Black Bear and Lin Jing observed the little white mouse closely.

After finishing, the white mouse demon suddenly felt a surge of heat in its body.

In the next moment...

Boom!

A terrifying aura suddenly erupted from the white mouse demon, soaring into the sky!

Purple demonic energy flowed rapidly out of its body. This solidified demonic power quickly took shape and formed a terrifying dragon skeleton creature.

"Roar!!!"

The skeletal dragon was enormous, its bone scales flashing with a deep, mysterious red light, like a star shining in the dark night. Its dragon head was held high, and its eyes blazed with purple flames, exuding an undeniable aura of authority.

Its claws were sharp like swords, glinting with cold light as they slowly reached out toward Elder Black Bear.

"What the hell!" Elder Black Bear was completely shocked by the transformation of the white mouse demon. His vision darkened, and his fur stood on end as he felt a moment of fear before this terrifying dragon-shaped creature.

The enormous purple dragon eyes stared at Elder Black Bear, who took a step back as if facing an enormous threat. Sweat poured from his brow.

He panted heavily, but suddenly, he snapped back to his senses, cursed, and swatted at the dragon. To his surprise, it was just an illusion, and the supposed pressure was like water without a source, a tree without roots—completely false.

It seemed that the effect of the purple dragon bone flower was to cause the user to hallucinate into becoming a powerful beast, creating an intimidating effect.

"Damn it, what kind of weird effects are you two creating with your syntheses?" Elder Black Bear growled in embarrassment. He was furious at being scared by a little white mouse.

He should have eaten it himself if he'd known.

The shame of losing his dignity in front of a long-lived body and a little squirrel!

With a slap, Elder Black Bear struck the purple bone dragon, and the illusion immediately disappeared, leaving only the small white mouse demon trembling on the ground.

The mouse's mouth was covered in purple-red fruit juice, looking very bloody...

Lin Jing and Pine Leaf Mouse exchanged a glance but said nothing, each for their own reasons—one because of the storage bag, and the other because of the desire for more rewards.

"Was it tasty?" Elder Black Bear hesitated for a moment before asking the white mouse demon.

The mouse demon trembled and nodded, licking the fruit juice from its lips.

"Alright, you can go now." He blew out a dark wind and immediately blew the mouse demon to a nearby mountain peak.

"Looks like there are no side effects." Elder Black Bear shrugged as if nothing had happened. "Based on what just happened, it seems like once it reaches the 7th layer of Qi Refining, the user can start synthesizing the dragon bone flower. But I can't wait. The robe will be borrowed by you for a few days, and in return, you help me synthesize some more dragon bone flowers."

"Elder Black Bear..."

"What is it?"

"Well... could you perhaps give us some dragon bone flower seeds?" Lin Jing asked. "I noticed that the mutated dragon bone flower has some potential, and it could serve as a trump card in the future when traveling through the immortal world."

An illusion that could scare even a Demon Core Beast—this could prove useful!

"Alright." Elder Black Bear grinned. He too was surprised by the powers of the purple dragon bone flower. Had he not seen the mouse demon's transformation with his own eyes, he would have been terrified if he encountered it outside.

Damn illusion!

At this moment, a flying rainbow streaked across the sky.

An elder in blue robes landed in the courtyard—it was Elder Yu.

He looked around with suspicion before his gaze settled on Elder Black Bear, Lin Jing, and the Pine Leaf Mouse.

"I just sensed a terrifying aura, like the appearance of a Demon King from the Nascent Soul stage..."

"Blind Bear, what are you doing here? Was that noise caused by you?"

Elder Black Bear didn’t want to elaborate: "Just pretend it was. I came to visit the sect's outstanding disciples, and now that I’ve seen them, there’s nothing else to do."

He glanced at Lin Jing and the others, his gaze filled with meaning.

It seemed to say, "I'll be back once I find the storage bags." Or perhaps more threateningly, "If you dare leak what just happened, you're finished."

...

Elder Black Bear didn’t stay long and left. Elder Yu turned his attention to the surroundings but didn’t investigate further. His eyes were soon drawn to the mechanical creations in the courtyard.

"Huh?"

The half-finished mechanical turtles didn’t catch his attention, but a peculiar long tube-like mechanism did.

As a master of mechanical engineering, he could tell that of all the creations, this one was the closest to completion.

"You’re working quickly," Elder Yu remarked.

He marveled at Lin Jing’s learning abilities. In such a short amount of time, he had already completed a basic mechanical creation—clearly, he had been studying hard.

"What’s this?" He picked up the bamboo gun Lin Jing had created.

Instinctively, he pressed the trigger of the bamboo gun and aimed it at the sky.

"Elder Yu, don’t!" Lin Jing yelled, and everything went black before his eyes.

"Chirp!!" Pine Leaf Mouse cried out as well—its vision also went black.

In the next moment,

The bamboo gun activated, and the spiritual bean bombs in the magazine were triggered into a state of readiness. They were then enveloped by a surge of water and shot out at high speed!

Splat, splat, splat!

The blue water flowed in bursts, carrying the spiritual bean bombs into the air.

"Water arrows? No, there are no water formation patterns inscribed in the device. It looks like there’s something else inside..." Elder Yu began to comment, but was interrupted.

Boom boom boom boom boom!

The spiritual beans exploded one after another, sending shockwaves of spiritual energy through the air. The explosions resonated across the sky, whipping up winds as violent as a wind-type spell. In an instant, the trees within a certain radius of Lin Jing’s courtyard were uprooted, leaving a mess in their wake.

"..."

As a few leaves slapped against Elder Yu's face, he looked around at the devastation. He felt the bamboo gun burning hot in his hands, and once again, his vision went black.

"Are you planning to use this thing to battle foundation-building demon beasts?"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 28: The Sky is Falling
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 28: The Sky is Falling

Elder Yu flicked the cartridge, and the Pine Leaf Mouse let out a heart-wrenching scream.

Its ammunition!

The one it had hoarded for so long!

Lin Jing also rubbed his forehead, thinking to himself that in the future, things shouldn't be left lying around... a super-miniature storage bag would be nice.

The one fortunate thing was that Elder Yu had triggered the mechanism in the sky. Although its powerful force left the surroundings in disarray, at least... the courtyard was still intact, and the house wasn't blown up.

However, from a different perspective, if the house had been blown up, perhaps they could have upgraded to a better cave residence.

With this thought, Lin Jing couldn't help but sigh.

"What is this thing!" Elder Yu jumped in shock.

"Elder Yu," Lin Jing said after a moment of silence, "why did you press it?"

"Haha," Elder Yu chuckled awkwardly. He hadn't expected this situation.

Who would have thought that you two, new disciples, would come up with something like this with your new spirit weapons?

"You must have noticed that the mechanism bamboo gun is very simple in design. It uses some basic structure and, due to the nature of the materials—spirit bamboo—it accumulates water vapor and fires high-speed water flows, which can cause an explosion. The real power lies in the spirit bean bombs inside the magazine."

Lin Jing continued, "Elder, these mutated spirit bean bombs are all we have left. We were planning to use them for the inner sect assessment..."

"I'll compensate you," Elder Yu quickly understood the situation. The real power lay in those spirit beans. The bamboo gun itself was not particularly complicated; it was just a simple mechanism that combined mutated spirit bamboo and mutated spirit beans!

That said... with these things, if there are enough of those explosion-prone spirit beans, even a mortal could deal a blow to a low-level cultivator.

This must be what Elder Mo meant by... rare beast abilities. Elder Yu thought to himself, realizing the true power of the rare beasts. Even simple materials like spirit bamboo, without needing intricate array patterns, could provide the same effect as water arrow techniques.

"Elder, you are too kind. Those spirit bean bombs are just improved ordinary spirit beans. They aren't worth much. We can make more. We are already very grateful for your guidance in mechanism techniques," Lin Jing said.

The Pine Leaf Mouse was stunned. No compensation?
