"I can't let you suffer. Here's the deal. I see that your mechanical beast is almost ready. Take this ahead of time. The inner sect assessment is coming up, so you should hurry and make improvements," Elder Yu thought for a moment and tossed a token to them.

"This is my elder token. With it, you can dine for free at the outer sect cafeteria. Additionally, with this token, you can enter the inner peak as outer sect disciples."

After finishing, he waved his sleeve and said, "It seems some disciples were drawn by the noise just now. I'll go disperse them. You can continue your cultivation."

"Thank you, Elder Yu." Lin Jing saw off Elder Yu, then tossed the token to the Pine Leaf Mouse. "Just some spirit beans. Elder Yu is a Gold Core Elder; how could he let us suffer? Let's go to the cafeteria and have a good meal!"

The Pine Leaf Mouse wiped away a tear. Humans sure have a lot of tricks up their sleeves. Though, it had to admit, a lot of the spirit beans it had hoarded were now gone!

...

Outer Sect, Cafeteria!

It was said that even the most ordinary food here was made with spirit rice and steamed with spirit spring water.

After receiving the elder's token, Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse quickly tidied up before rushing toward the cafeteria like starving ghosts.

"Lin Jing, Senior Brother?" As Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse sat in a corner of the cafeteria, many outer sect disciples recognized them. One of the labor disciples, who worked to earn spirit stones, approached them eagerly, as if seeing an idol.

"Senior Brother Lin Jing, I don't know what you'd like to eat, but here is today's menu." The labor disciple eagerly pointed to the menu on the wall.

"We have 'In the Sky, Willing to Be the Winged Bird', 'Green Wild Fairy', 'Wealthy Waistband'..."

"Junior Brother, could you speak in terms we can understand?" Lin Jing politely interrupted.

"Ah, yes! We have roasted wood-scented pigeon, stir-fried fresh spirit mushrooms, caramelized cashews..."

"Alright." Lin Jing's heart fluttered. This sounded much better than tree leaf fried rice. "I'll have one of everything on the menu for today."

"Ah?" The labor disciple kindly reminded him, "Senior Brother, these dishes contain a lot of spiritual energy. For example, the 'In the Sky, Willing to Be the Winged Bird' dish uses fragrant spirit wood and fire control techniques to lock in the nutrients of the cloud pigeon... Even a mid-stage Qi Cultivator would be full after eating one."

"With so many dishes, it might be hard to digest and absorb, and they're not cheap either."

"No problem. Do we look normal to you?" Lin Jing smiled faintly. The labor disciple seemed to understand and nodded before running to notify the kitchen.

Soon, the dishes were served. The Pine Leaf Mouse pulled a golden spirit rice grain out of Lin Jing's pocket, then split it in half with one claw, giving half to Lin Jing and keeping the other half for itself. This way, they wouldn't appear too greedy and end up stealing food from others.

"Gulp." They each ate half a golden spirit rice grain, accompanied by a cup of spirit tea.

...

One Hour Later.

Mechanized Workshop.

"Elder Yu, bad news!" A crane flew in again, the scene all too familiar.

"What happened? Why are you in such a hurry?" Elder Yu walked out and looked at the crane.

"Outer sect disciples Lin Jing and Pine Leaf Mouse were in the cafeteria, and they used your elder token to get free food!"

"What's the big deal? The token is for them," Elder Yu replied. "Lin Jing has a special physique, so his demand for food and resources is high. The inner sect assessment is approaching, and I gave them a small favor. What's wrong with that? If anyone has something to say, let them try to defeat the mechanized bamboo dragon!"

The crane replied, "Elder, no outer sect disciple has an opinion. Not many know about this. It's just that the head chef, who’s a bit pampered, and his fire spirit sparrow got exhausted. Lin Jing and the others ate so much that, by the end of the day, there wasn’t a single bit of leftover food. Usually, there’s plenty left."

"I heard... Lin Jing even ate the meat bones..."

"The leftover food was meant for those special spirit beasts that joined the sect just for 'free food and lodging'... Once they see there's no food left, they might complain."

"The worst part is... they ate so much, and with your token, they didn't pay a single spirit stone."

"If they keep eating like this, the cafeteria’s budget will definitely be affected! You'll have to cover the costs yourself, Elder!"

Elder Yu froze when he heard this.

What?

"Oh no, I've been tricked." Elder Yu's face darkened. How could he be so naive? With Lin Jing's longevity body, of course, he wouldn’t let them suffer! The sky is falling.

"Elder, what should we do?" the Tianyu Crane asked.

"What else can we do? Let them eat!" Elder Yu gritted his teeth. "Once they enter the inner sect... they won’t come back to the outer sect cafeteria for free food! I'll hurry up and push for the inner sect advancement assessment to happen as soon as possible!"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 29: Inner Sect Examination!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 29: Inner Sect Examination!

“Ridiculous!”

Lin Jing’s overindulgence at the outer sect cafeteria quickly spread to many ears, including that of Elder Li Hai, the head of the Demon Hunting Hall.

Upon learning that it was indeed Elder Yu who had given the green light, Elder Li Hai commented, “This Lin Jing must have a special physique, but even if that’s true, this preferential treatment is too much.”

“The more talented a disciple is, the more they should be forged through rigorous trials. Coddling them will only waste their potential. Historically, haven’t the greatest cultivators come from ordinary bodies?”

“Master, there’s no choice. How many geniuses with special physiques are willing to join the Beast Taming Sect? Since someone is willing to join, the sect has to treat them well,” said an eagle demon beside him. “Of the special physique disciples Elder Mo brought back, besides this one and Ouyang Hao, all have successfully established their foundations and become direct disciples. If we don’t give them preferential treatment, it will surely dampen their spirits.”

“Preferential treatment is fine,” Elder Li Hai said, “but there must be a limit! The performance of the Demon Hunting Hall disciples in the Great Wasteland trial further solidified my belief that cultivation... character must come first!”

“Take Luoyu for example. His qualifications are average, but through his perseverance, determination, and wit, he managed to instigate two demon beasts at the 9th level of Qi Refining to fight each other, ultimately benefiting as a mid-stage Qi Refining cultivator and obtaining the beast blood for body tempering!”

“No matter what, I can’t let Lin Jing go down this path of complacency! I’m going to ask him to join the Demon Hunting Hall!”

“Can’t defeat me?” Elder Li Hai’s gaze turned cold as he looked at the eagle demon. “Besides the sect leader and the few Yuan Ying ancestors, has anyone of the same level ever made me afraid?”

“You’re not afraid, but I’ll be the one getting hit!” the eagle demon said. “Master, let’s wait until the Inner Sect Examination is over. If that kid doesn’t perform well, you can still have a reason to recruit him later.”

“Fair enough,” Elder Li Hai said, “We’ll wait until after the examination.”

Fortunately, Elder Mo and Elder Yu didn’t know what Elder Li Hai was thinking. Otherwise, they would surely have hammered the old man who was trying to take advantage of the longevity body.

---

In the courtyard,

Lin Jing was lying in a bamboo chair, patting his stomach and leisurely looking at something.

What he was looking at were the notices for the Inner Sect Examination in previous years.

The format for the Inner Sect Advancement Examination hadn’t changed much over the years. The elders were too lazy to come up with new ideas, so they usually made small tweaks without altering the core rules.

“At registration, the cultivation level will be checked. Only Beast Tamers or their spirit beasts who reach the 7th level of Qi Refining or higher can participate in the survival trial!”

“The survival trial will throw qualifying outer sect disciples into the outskirts of the Great Wasteland, where they must survive for three days.”

“During this period, disciples can earn points based on the strength of the demon beasts they hunt. The stronger the demon beast, the more points they earn. Additionally, disciples are encouraged to compete with each other. Eliminating others will yield more points than hunting demon beasts!”

“However, the highest points are awarded for taming demon beasts!”

“If a disciple can tame a wild demon beast in the Great Wasteland, the points they earn will far exceed those for killing demon beasts or eliminating others!” Lin Jing thought of how Senior Brother Ouyang had tamed a tiger demon.

“After three days, the eight disciples with the most points will pass the examination.”

“And that’s not all! The eight successful disciples must then choose an inner peak to undergo a ‘talent assessment’.”

“If they choose Danxia Peak, the elders of Danxia Peak will introduce them to pill refining. If the disciple’s talent in pill refining is not high, Danxia Peak will not accept them and will send them to another peak.”

Lin Jing looked at the exchangeable resources based on the examination points.

He discovered that storage bags were indeed available, with the largest being... a full ten cubic meters!

However, he also noticed that the points required to exchange for these bags were ridiculously high. Even if one killed dozens of demon beasts at the 9th level of Qi Refining, it would be impossible to exchange enough points.

Clearly... they had no intention of letting disciples actually exchange for them!

“I can already predict that the storage bag Elder Hei Xiong sends later won’t be enough for the Pine Leaf Mouse.” With the mouse’s hoarding speed, if the storage bag only contained regular spirit rice and spirit beans, it would quickly be filled.

“Since I promised it a storage bag before contracting it, this opportunity... is the most fitting one.”

“Two months until the... Inner Sect Examination!”

Lin Jing’s eyes glinted.

“The advancement examinations over the years have never seen any accidents. Barring unexpected issues, the examinations will certainly be conducted under the protection of the elders, and the outer regions of the Great Wasteland will likely be cleared for safety. Given this, maybe I can be bolder than usual.”

---

With a new goal in mind, Lin Jing continued to prepare for the Inner Sect Examination with the Pine Leaf Mouse.

Their daily routine should have been focused on cultivation, hoarding resources, and dining at the cafeteria. However, since Elder Yu was concerned that Lin Jing’s frequent indulgence in the cafeteria would have a bad influence, he gave Lin Jing and the wild spirit beasts the same privileges as the sect’s tamed beasts.

Food delivery directly to their door.

They no longer needed to go to the cafeteria themselves. At mealtimes, the cafeteria would deliver large quantities of food to Lin Jing’s place.

Both Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse didn’t expect that the mundane life of cultivation could actually be so pleasant.

Time flew by.

In the blink of an eye, two months had passed, and Lin Jing had become increasingly accustomed to his new life in the Beast Taming Sect. The months of living together had made him and the Pine Leaf Mouse more in sync and reliant on each other. It was unclear whether it was due to the Pine Leaf Mouse’s influence, but Lin Jing had even started thinking about hoarding some resources himself.

Over these two months, the Pine Leaf Mouse’s cultivation reached the 7th level of Qi Refining, while Lin Jing’s cultivation reached the 3rd level, mainly because they enjoyed resources comparable to those of direct disciples.

With such vast resources, most spirit beasts or cultivators with exceptional talent would have reached the peak of Qi Refining by now. But for them, the Pine Leaf Mouse had spent so much time hoarding that, though its cultivation hadn’t risen as much, it had filled the storage bags sent by Elder Hei Xiong to the brim.

Every time it took out a bag, it was as if it had a trump card ready, and the mouse felt it was more important than reaching the 9th level of Qi Refining—it was all about the sense of security.

As for Lin Jing, his progress was hindered by his longevity body. Even with so many resources and the Pine Leaf Mouse’s assistance, he was still some distance away from the 4th level, even though Qi Refining was one of the easiest stages to cultivate in.

Lin Jing estimated that without obtaining the complete Beast Taming technique or contracting more spirit beasts, relying solely on the Pine Leaf Mouse wouldn’t be enough to pull him through.

“Let’s go.”

Today was the day for registration for the Inner Sect Examination. After registering, they would likely head to the examination site.

Lin Jing called to the Pine Leaf Mouse.

“Eeek!” The Pine Leaf Mouse stretched its arms, wearing a robe. It was no longer the one made by Elder Hei Xiong; it was one that Lin Jing had made himself... the storage bag was now conveniently hung on the inside of the robe.

“By the way, don’t forget this.” Lin Jing walked over to the spirit rice and picked up a palm-sized mechanized bamboo turtle watering can, which was spraying water through its cannon-like nozzle.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 30: Unexpected
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 30: Unexpected

"He Yiming, Qi Refining 7th Layer, Pet Beast: Fighting Ape, Qi Refining 7th Layer."

...

"Qin Caixin, Qi Refining 6th Layer, Pet Beast: Tree Bear, Qi Refining 7th Layer."

...

"Ziran, Qi Refining 6th Layer, Pet Beast: Fire Spirit Sparrow, Qi Refining 7th Layer."

...

At the outer sect,

the Inner Sect Examination registration was in full swing!

The cultivation levels of the disciples were generally normal.

That is, until... Lin Jing arrived.

He walked up to the registration table, and Elder Yu, already aware, casually asked, “Name?”

“Lin Jing.”

Elder Yu scanned him with his divine sense and said, “Lin Jing, Qi Refining 3rd Layer, Pet Beast: Pine Leaf Mouse, Qi Refining 7th Layer!”

Upon hearing this, many of the outer sect disciples murmured amongst themselves.

"Wasn’t that Pine Leaf Mouse just at the Qi Refining 4th Layer? How did it get to the 7th so quickly?"

"The key point isn’t that the pet beast is at Qi Refining 7th Layer, but that the Beast Tamer is only at the 3rd Layer?"

It was rare for a Beast Tamer at the early Qi Refining stage to have a pet beast at the late Qi Refining stage in the outer sect.

Of course, no one dared to look down on Lin Jing or the Pine Leaf Mouse, given their notoriety. The dismantled Mechanized Bamboo Dragon was the perfect testament to their capabilities.

Upon hearing the other disciples' discussion, the Pine Leaf Mouse, who was lying with its shoulders and eyes closed in Lin Jing’s robe, shook its head.

If it had focused entirely on cultivation, it might have already reached the Foundation Establishment stage!

The Pine Leaf Mouse had deliberately slowed its cultivation down, following Lin Jing's advice.

For Qi Refining cultivators, Foundation Establishment was the process of solidifying one’s cultivation base and enhancing one’s physical constitution.

Ordinary cultivators could only reach a few opportunities for acquiring the later stages of spiritual bodies.

Cultivators with innate physical constitutions needed to choose appropriate resources during Foundation Establishment to continue strengthening their physiques.

Likewise, spirit beasts and demon beasts followed similar processes.

They needed to optimize and enhance their bloodlines during the Foundation Establishment process, ideally undergoing a bloodline evolution.

The Pine Leaf Mouse, while powerful, did not possess a strong innate bloodline, so its Foundation Establishment process was particularly crucial. The Glazed Immortal Eyes had granted it abilities, but hadn’t strengthened its bloodline.

Without perfect Foundation Establishment conditions, rashly advancing could be detrimental.

Foundation Establishment was about laying a foundation for invincibility!

Time passed slowly, and Lin Jing felt that accumulating resources and breaking through at his own pace was the best approach.

After Lin Jing completed his registration, he stepped aside, and then Senior Brother Ziran walked up to him.

“Lin Jing, your progress is astonishing,” Senior Brother Ziran greeted him with a smile.

“The astonishing one is the Pine Leaf Mouse. I’m the one moving at a turtle’s pace,” Lin Jing replied.

“Senior Brother is being modest. Fortunately, this time there are the Demon Hunting Hall disciples joining us, or this examination would be too easy for you guys,” Senior Brother Ziran said with a smile.

“Is that so?” Lin Jing wasn’t too concerned about the Demon Hunting Hall disciples. As long as they didn’t come looking for trouble, he wouldn’t seek them out either. With so few disciples, the bulk of the points would still rely on demon beasts.

“Of course. The Demon Hunting Hall disciples are all tough characters. Despite knowing the death rate in the Hall’s trials is extremely high, they still decide to join. If you didn’t know better, you’d think they were carrying a blood debt and were desperate for strength to take revenge on some Yuan Ying ancestor. To be honest, I really don’t want to take the exam with them.”

As they were talking about the Demon Hunting Hall, the disciples from the hall arrived and also registered.

Although their cultivation levels weren’t particularly outstanding, the outer sect disciples all knew of their strength.

“I’m the one who wants revenge,” muttered Senior Brother He Yiming, who had been severely injured by a Demon Hunting Hall disciple. After recovering, he had broken through to Qi Refining 7th Layer, which he considered a blessing in disguise.

“Lin Jing seems to be the real headache…” Sister Caixin thought to herself.

She still hadn’t figured out how to counter the red flying leaf technique that could change direction mid-flight.

“Registration is now complete! A total of 20 disciples will participate in this year’s Inner Sect Examination.”

“Next, Elder Li and I will lead you to the outskirts of the Great Wasteland, where we will act as your examiners. Your task is to survive for three days using any means necessary!”

Elder Yu gave a brief explanation of the examination’s contents, confirming that it was largely the same as in previous years. However, there were more points available for competing with fellow disciples, likely a change requested by Elder Li.

“Alright, all disciples participating in the Inner Sect Examination, come forward.” At this moment, a large flying vessel was floating in the sky. Upon closer inspection, it didn’t exactly resemble a typical flying vessel.

It looked more like a demon beast shaped like a fish... no, perhaps it was better described as a mechanical beast—one of Elder Yu’s creations.

Elder Li stood atop the mechanical fish, his expression emotionless as he addressed the disciples below.

---

Before long, the 20 disciples boarded the mechanical fish.

The fish began flying toward the outer edges of the Beast Taming Sect and the Great Wasteland.

Onboard the mechanical beast, the atmosphere between the 20 disciples began to grow tense. Whether familiar or unfamiliar, they were now all competitors, sizing each other up and recalling each other’s signature techniques.

“We will soon reach the Great Wasteland. Before the trial begins, I must remind you once again.”

“The Inner Sect Examination should be approached according to your ability. Don’t do anything beyond your means. That won’t earn you any extra points; it will only cause you to lose points!”

“Besides myself and Elder Li, some elders from the inner peaks may also be observing you secretly with their divine senses. So, perform well, and remember that if you face life-threatening danger, prioritize protecting yourself and your pet beast. The elders will step in to rescue you, but that will result in automatic disqualification. Do you all understand?” Elder Yu said.

“We understand, Elder!” the outer sect disciples said in unison, bowing respectfully.

“This kind of thing shouldn’t be spelled out so clearly. If we protect them too much, how can we test their true abilities?” Elder Li snorted coldly from the side.

“Trials, examinations—they should be full of unexpected situations. That’s when you truly see who has the real skills!”

Many of the disciples thought to themselves that Elder Li was just sitting comfortably, not experiencing the hardships of cultivation.

But perhaps it was Elder Li’s ominous words that were prophetic. Suddenly, there was a deafening boom in the sky, shaking the mechanical fish.

Everyone looked up in alarm, and saw a massive hole in the sky, with brownish light spreading from it.

“What is that?” the disciples and their pet beasts stared at the hole in the sky.

Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse, who had been enjoying the scenery near the edge of the mechanical fish, also looked up, their expressions changing.

Moments after the hole appeared, a thick beam of light shot down. The target wasn’t anywhere else—it was aimed straight at the mechanical fish, or more precisely, at Lin Jing himself!

“What is that?!” Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse tried to move away, but it seemed as though they were locked onto.

“We’re in trouble. It’s coming straight for us.”

Lin Jing’s vision blurred as the Pine Leaf Mouse placed a paw on its storage bag, only to realize that none of the items it had hoarded were useful for this situation.

“Could it be the Yin Corpse Sect trying to capture us? No, this is still within the sect... How can they be so brazen?”

Their consciousness began to blur, and the world around them was consumed by the light.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 31: Bodhi Heaven
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 31: Bodhi Heaven

A massive brown pillar of light descended upon the Mechanical Fish, engulfing Lin Jing in the process.

All the disciples on the Mechanical Fish were stunned. Almost simultaneously with the appearance of the light pillar, Elder Yu and Elder Li made their moves, but despite their Golden Core-level abilities, they were repelled by the light pillar and could not change its trajectory.

“Trouble!” Elder Yu’s heart sank as he immediately assumed the worst—it must be the Yin Corpse Sect coming to abduct people from the Beast Taming Sect.

But as realization dawned, his expression turned incredulous. “All disciples, listen up!” Elder Li raised his voice, issuing orders. “Quickly enter the light pillar! There are opportunities within. Anyone who enters will be exempt from the Inner Sect examination and promoted directly to the Inner Sect! However, entering carries risks, so decide swiftly. If you feel unprepared to compete for opportunities, prioritize assisting the Outer Sect disciple Lin Jing to secure them.”

Elder Li’s explanation clarified the situation. “This is a ‘Cavern Heaven.’

“In ancient times, our Tianyuan Continent was the lower realm of an immortal palace in the Celestial Realm. To nurture talents, the immortal palace created the ‘Ten Great Cavern Heavens’ and the ‘Thirty-Six Minor Cavern Heavens.’ These Cavern Heavens would appear randomly across the continent, absorbing a select number of prodigies with immense fortune—sometimes three to five, sometimes seven to eight. Each contained countless opportunities.

“Though the immortal palace has long since perished, the Cavern Heavens remain.

“In principle, only chosen prodigies may enter. However, it was later discovered that cultivators of the same realm as these prodigies, if present during a Cavern Heaven’s descent, could also accidentally enter and seek their fortune.”

The remaining twelve disciples, who hesitated until they heard more of the explanation, could only watch as the Cavern Heaven fully materialized, barring further entry. Left outside, they could not help but feel immense regret.

Elder Li turned to these disciples. “Don’t be disheartened. Given your capabilities, even if you had entered, it would be challenging to compete with the prodigies within. Missing this opportunity likely saved your lives.”

Among those who had entered, four were from the Demon-Hunting Hall, and the other three included He Yiming, Qin Caixin, and Ziran.

“Cavern Heaven…” Elder Li continued, while Elder Yu sighed beside him.

Elder Yu couldn’t decide if Lin Jing was incredibly lucky or deeply unlucky. To be swept into a Cavern Heaven—it was only the third occurrence since the founding of the Beast Taming Sect.

“This Lin Jing… he was actually chosen by a Cavern Heaven. Incredible,” Elder Li remarked.

“This isn’t the time to discuss this,” Elder Yu sighed heavily.

The next moment, several streaks of light descended, revealing Elder Mo, the Black Bear Monster, and several other Golden Core elders. They silently observed the light pillar.

“Dmn it,” Elder Mo muttered irritably. He had accounted for all sorts of dangers, including a potential raid by the Yin Corpse Sect, which the sect’s guardian divine beast could repel. Within the Beast Taming Sect’s vicinity, Lin Jing was supposed to be extremely safe.

What he hadn’t expected was for Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse to be swept into a Cavern Heaven—a phenomenon beyond anyone’s control.

Within a Cavern Heaven, no one could protect Lin Jing. Not even ascension-level cultivators could influence such a mechanism from the Celestial Realm.

“Master, look at it positively. Cavern Heaven opportunities are limited to competition within the same cultivation realm! Besides Lin Jing, the other geniuses swept in will also only be at the Qi Refining stage.”

“As long as the two of them remain cautious, their lives should be safe. Given their circumstances, it’s more likely the other disciples from the Outer Sect, or prodigies from other sects, will end up in trouble.”

“…Maybe,” Elder Mo replied, still unconvinced.

---

Inside the mysterious space.

A massive stone stele towered over the landscape. Nearby, three ancient trees surrounded the stele in a triangular formation. Their leaves were gold, silver, and green respectively.

Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse were transported before the stone stele, still somewhat disoriented. As they looked around, several other figures appeared one after another.

“Ha ha!” A bald body cultivator draped in animal skins laughed heartily. “Bodhi Heaven! To think I’d stumble upon one of the Thirty-Six Minor Cavern Heavens!”

“A Cavern Heaven? You’re saying this is a Cavern Heaven?” A scholarly man in a scholar’s robe exclaimed, marveling at their surroundings. “I’ve only read about this in books. To think I’d one day enter one myself!”

“Ah, I see. With my talents, it makes sense to be chosen by a Cavern Heaven,” said a young woman in alchemist robes with a soft laugh.

“Junior Brother Lin…” Before long, Senior Brother Ziran and the others managed to exploit a loophole to enter as well. They observed their surroundings cautiously, eyeing the peculiar group of young cultivators who had also entered.

“What are you all doing here?” Lin Jing asked, surprised to see Senior Brother Ziran and Senior Sister Caixin.

From the discussion around him, Lin Jing began to piece together what had happened. He had read a little about Cavern Heavens before.

“Squeak!!” The Pine Leaf Mouse, however, had no clue what was going on. It only knew that something had gone awry with the Inner Sect examination. After all the preparation, was it about to lose the larger storage pouch it had been promised? The Pine Leaf Mouse was heartbroken.

“Everyone!” The body cultivator spoke again. “This is Bodhi Heaven, one of the Thirty-Six Minor Cavern Heavens. Within it are three Bodhi Trees.”

“Legends say that beneath a Bodhi Tree, a cultivator’s comprehension is greatly enhanced, making it the best place to understand techniques, spells, or even create new ones.”

“The founder of the Solar Temple reportedly achieved enlightenment here, establishing the temple and becoming a revered monk.”

“Even the True Dragon Secret Art of the Ancient Celestial Emperor was perfected in this very place.”

“However, there are only three Bodhi Trees, and we are many. Naturally, the chance to meditate beneath one should go to the strong. Any objections?”

“I, for one, claim the golden Bodhi Tree. The other two—figure it out among yourselves.”

No one paid him any attention. The crowd was fixated on the inscriptions on the massive stone stele.

Stele of Creation.

The stele ranked those who created techniques, spells, or abilities within Bodhi Heaven based on their potential. Those whose creations were recorded would receive blessings from Bodhi Heaven.

1. Gu Huang (Ancient Emperor): Created the True Dragon Secret Art during the Nascent Soul stage.

2. Feng Xian (Sealer of Immortals): Created the Sealing Immortal Art during the Golden Core stage.

...

9. Solar Buddha: Created the Sun Sutra during the Nascent Soul stage.

...

The stele displayed 100 names—each a legendary figure in cultivation history.

Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse exchanged glances.

The Pine Leaf Mouse wanted to ask if it could create an ability to store items in its body. Lin Jing shook his head. At the Qi Refining stage, with so little experience, creating techniques was far out of reach. They shouldn’t even dream about it.

It was better to focus on mastering its Red Flying Leaf attack.

“The real problem,” Lin Jing muttered, scanning the group, “is that there are too many people and too few trees.”

The strange body cultivator, the scholarly man, the conceited alchemist, and the silent swordsman—none of them seemed simple. They were clearly on a different level from the Beast Taming Sect disciples.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 32: A Clear Goal
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 32: A Clear Goal

Inside the Bodhi Cavern Heaven, there were now twelve people.

Five of them had been chosen by the Cavern Heaven to enter, while the other seven were fortunate enough to be nearby when the Cavern Heaven descended. They seized the opportunity before the Cavern Heaven fully formed and managed to get in. To some extent, this too could be considered a stroke of fate.

The bald body cultivator, draped in animal skins, noticed no one responded to his earlier words. He clicked his tongue in annoyance and walked directly toward the golden Bodhi Tree.

“The golden Bodhi Tree offers the best enlightenment benefits in the Bodhi Cavern Heaven,” he said. “The silver one comes next, and the green one is the least effective. The fewer people under a Bodhi Tree, the better the results. Don’t you think monopolizing the golden tree is a bit too much?”

A scholarly man in a green robe spoke calmly to the body cultivator. “If I’m not mistaken, you’re a cultivator from the Snow Plains. Only Snow Plains cultivators wear animal skin skirts like that.”

Indeed, the body cultivator was wearing an animal skin skirt, confirming the man’s deduction.

The crowd turned to look at the body cultivator, whose eyes flashed coldly.

The scholar continued, “This young lady, judging by her violet flame-patterned alchemist robe, must be a disciple of the Fire Domain’s Dan Ding Sect.”

“And this young swordsman carries the Demon-Slaying Sword—a standard weapon of the Outer Sect disciples of the Sword Domain’s Heavenly Sword Gate,” the scholar remarked.

The young swordsman remained silent, slowly unsheathing his sword in response.

“As for these eight, each has a spirit beast. To my knowledge, only the disciples of the Beast Taming Sect from the Wasteland Domain are so uniformly equipped with pet beasts.”

Lin Jing and his fellow disciples had already guessed where the scholar was headed.

“As for myself, I hail from the Literary Domain. All of us come from the Nine Domains of the Ancient Celestial Kingdom. Only this Snow Plains cultivator is an outsider,” the scholar concluded, neatly dividing the group into two camps.

“Ladies and gentlemen, the Snow Plains tribes have long coveted the Central Lands and have never been on good terms with the Ancient Celestial Kingdom. As cultivators of the Kingdom, shouldn’t we unite against this common foe?” He cupped his hands in a gesture of respect, rallying the others.

Outside the Ancient Celestial Kingdom lay the harsh Snow Plains, an inhospitable land with extreme conditions.

“Hahaha! I knew you cowards would gang up on me,” the Snow Plains cultivator laughed uproariously. “But I’m not afraid of any of you.”

The Snow Plains cultivator was well aware of the situation. Knowing their differences in allegiance, these people would naturally target him first. Prepared for a fight, he sneered, “I’ve heard Ancient Celestial Kingdom cultivators are as timid as sheep. Let me show you what a real cultivator looks like!”

Standing before the golden Bodhi Tree, he exhaled a frosty breath. His eyes glinted yellow-green like a beast’s, and his muscles bulged as veins protruded across his body. The sound of roaring lions and tigers echoed faintly from him.

“I mean no offense to the Snow Plains, but you are quite brutish,” the alchemist in the violet flame robe commented, shaking her head.

“Spare me the pretense. Don’t tell me none of you want to monopolize the golden Bodhi Tree!” the body cultivator mocked.

“Everyone, let’s take him down together!” the scholar declared. He produced a magical scroll, and its characters fused with his energy, manifesting ghostly soldiers wielding swords and sabers. The spectral army charged at the Snow Plains cultivator.

“I’ll join in!” The alchemist raised her hand, conjuring a purple flame at her fingertips. This wasn’t an ordinary fire—its intense heat radiated a strange, fragrant scent that was both mesmerizing and alarming.

The flame danced in her palm with a will of its own. With a flick of her wrist, it transformed into a fiery cauldron, spinning through the air with a deafening roar.

“Grahhhh!” The Snow Plains cultivator stood his ground. Beast totems shimmered across his skin as he tanked the attacks. The soldiers’ weapons struck his body with the sound of metal clashing against steel before shattering into smoke.

“Take this!” After enduring the scholar’s attack, the cultivator’s blood surged, and his hands glowed red as he swung his arm to shatter the incoming cauldron.

Boom!

The fiery explosion scattered flames in all directions, illuminating his figure as he advanced like an unleashed beast. He reached into his storage pouch and pulled out a massive warhammer, preparing to swing it at the scholar.

“Why aren’t the Heavenly Sword Gate and Beast Taming Sect disciples helping? Are you planning to swoop in later and reap the rewards?” the scholar shouted as he stepped onto a floating cloud of energy to evade direct confrontation.

“Get down here!” The Snow Plains cultivator hurled his warhammer into the air. The scholar dodged it with ease, but the cultivator redirected his rage toward the alchemist.

Seeing this, the alchemist put on an aggrieved expression. “Forget it. You all fight it out. I’m just an alchemist—I’ll settle for the silver Bodhi Tree.”

No one wanted to exhaust themselves too early, lest others take advantage of their weakened state.

The Snow Plains cultivator snorted, clearly seeing through her pretense. His gaze swept over the Heavenly Sword Gate and Beast Taming Sect disciples. “Hey, sword boy, are you planning to fight?”

“And as for you Beast Taming Sect disciples, you all look like trash. I bet you snuck in by sheer luck, didn’t you?”

The air around him was charged with the roars of countless beasts, making him seem like the king of all animals. The oppressive aura left the Beast Taming Sect disciples visibly shaken.

Even their spirit beasts—Senior Brother He Yiming’s Battle Ape, Senior Brother Ziran’s Fire Spirit Sparrow, and the Demon-Hunting Hall disciples’ ferocious beasts—trembled uncontrollably.

“This guy… he must have the Myriad Beast Body!” Senior Brother Ziran realized with a start. “It’s a rare physique that allows him to fuse the bloodlines of countless beasts to strengthen himself. Not only does it make him stronger as the battle progresses, but it also exerts a terrifying suppression on other beasts.”

The difference between those chosen by the Cavern Heaven and those who slipped in was stark. The mere release of his aura had weakened the combat potential of most of their spirit beasts.

“Not good,” thought the Demon-Hunting Hall disciples, who had hoped to vie for the golden Bodhi Tree. Feeling the Snow Plains cultivator’s oppressive power, they decided to back off.

Seeing the Beast Taming Sect disciples falter, the scholar shook his head. “I knew it. There’s no way all of them are capable. This narrows the competition.”

Suddenly, a barrage of water arrows shot toward the Snow Plains cultivator. Though he had easily shrugged off previous attacks, this time his body tensed with a strong sense of danger. Just as he prepared to dodge, a tidal wave of terrifying spiritual energy swept over him.

Boom! Boom! Boom! Boom! Boom!

A series of deafening explosions rocked the area. The shockwaves sent the scholar tumbling from the sky, left the alchemist and swordsman disheveled, and forced the Beast Taming Sect disciples to retreat in disbelief.

At the center of the commotion stood Lin Jing, calmly firing round after round from his Mechanical Bamboo Rifle. Beside him, the Pine Leaf Mouse showed no fear, its determination outweighing any sense of dread. Its eyes gleamed as it stared at the Snow Plains cultivator’s storage pouch.

As the smoke cleared, the Snow Plains cultivator was still standing, clutching his hammer. However, his body was riddled with wounds, his skin torn apart by the explosions, exposing raw flesh and even white bone beneath.

Despite his injuries, the cultivator let out an enraged roar and charged at Lin Jing.

“What a monster,” Lin Jing muttered, retreating a step as he reloaded his rifle with more Spirit Bean Bombs.

Boom! Boom! Boom! Boom!

Another round of explosions left the Snow Plains cultivator dazed and lifeless. Taking the opportunity, the Pine Leaf Mouse darted forward, snatched the storage pouch, and scurried back to Lin Jing’s side.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 33: The Bully Sect
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 33: The Bully Sect

“No wonder he’s a body cultivator; even after taking a full round of shots, he can still move. That gave me a scare.”

Lin Jing shook the water droplets from the barrel of his weapon, ignoring the looks of disbelief on his fellow sect disciples’ faces. He turned to the delighted Pine Leaf Mouse that had returned to him.

“Squeak!!!” The mouse chittered excitedly. This was so much better than the Inner Sect examination!

“You… son… of a…” The Snow Plains cultivator’s lips trembled, but no sound came out. His broken body prevented him from moving, and his consciousness began to fade. He couldn’t comprehend how a Beast Tamer disciple could be this strong.

Thud.

As the Snow Plains cultivator collapsed, severely wounded, the scholar in green robes, the alchemist in violet flame robes, and the young swordsman all turned their gazes toward Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse.

“Qi Refining Level Three? Qi Refining Level Seven?”

They could sense Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse’s cultivation levels. The mouse’s level was passable, but Lin Jing’s was laughably low. And yet, this supposedly weak cultivator had just overpowered a body cultivator from another country. It was absurd.

“Does it matter?” Lin Jing casually lowered the Mechanical Bamboo Rifle.

The other three showed wariness at Lin Jing’s contraption. Mechanized Craftsmen were often underestimated because their cultivation levels were usually low, but their true strength lay in their mechanical creations.

If the outsiders were cautious, Lin Jing’s fellow Beast Taming Sect disciples were outright dumbfounded. Even the Demon-Hunting Hall members exchanged uneasy glances.

Could Lin Jing really be planning to use this contraption to compete with them in the Inner Sect examination? It sounded like a joke…

“Junior Brother, this…” Senior Brother Ziran began awkwardly. “We don’t have any bad blood, right?”

“Of course not,” Lin Jing replied smoothly. “This is just something I prepared to deal with Foundation Establishment-level demon beasts during the examination.”

He then turned to the scholar, the alchemist, and the swordsman, raising his rifle. “I dealt with the Snow Plains cultivator for you. I’d like to use the golden Bodhi Tree. That’s reasonable, isn’t it?”

“I take it you’re the true prodigy chosen by the Cavern Heaven from the Beast Taming Sect,” the scholar remarked, dusting off his robes. “Impressive. I didn’t expect your sect to produce such a talented Mechanized Craftsman.”

“But mechanical creations consume a lot of energy, and lower-level mechanisms don’t last long. After such an intense assault earlier, your contraption is probably running low on power and close to malfunctioning, isn’t it?”

In response, Lin Jing calmly swapped out the bamboo magazine in his rifle and pulled the trigger without a word. Water arrows shot toward the scholar, who immediately paled. Having seen the fate of the Snow Plains cultivator, he dared not take a direct hit.

“Silver Tongue, Sword and Spear!” The scholar exhaled, and his breath materialized into a flurry of blades and swords in mid-air, intercepting the water arrows before they could explode.

Boom! Boom! Boom!

The Mechanical Bamboo Rifle’s attacks had their limitations, allowing the scholar to block them. However, by the time Lin Jing’s magazine was empty, the scholar was visibly parched from repeatedly using his technique. He quickly took out a Replenishing Pill to swallow, only to feel a sharp sting on his neck as a red leaf grazed it, drawing blood.

The scholar glanced at the Pine Leaf Mouse perched on Lin Jing’s shoulder. It was staring at his storage pouch.

Two against one.

“I concede,” the scholar declared, taking a deep breath. “Thank you for your mercy. I am Xu Zhizhi from Shengyun City in the Literary Domain.”

“Lin Jing of the Beast Taming Sect,” Lin Jing replied, reloading his rifle with a fresh magazine.

Xu Zhizhi’s eyelid twitched. This guy had come prepared, as if he’d known in advance he’d enter the Cavern Heaven.

Lin Jing’s gaze shifted to the alchemist and the swordsman.

Though clearly frustrated, Xu Zhizhi chose to approach the silver Bodhi Tree, deciding to cut his losses. As a scholar, combat was not his strength.

“What a waste. You’re just a Beast Tamer. Even with the golden Bodhi Tree, you won’t produce anything worthwhile,” the alchemist muttered.

But before she could settle beneath the silver Bodhi Tree, the Pine Leaf Mouse leaped ahead of her. It pulled a tiny Mechanical Bamboo Rifle from its storage pouch, set it on the ground, and fired a shot next to Xu Zhizhi.

Boom!

The blast stopped Xu Zhizhi in his tracks.

“Sorry,” Lin Jing said. “The golden Bodhi Tree is mine. The silver one belongs to my companion. We don’t plan on sharing.”

“What?!” Xu Zhizhi exploded. “Isn’t that your spirit beast? Why do you need separate trees?!”

“Don’t be greedy,” Lin Jing replied calmly. “If we were greedy, you’d already be dead, and your storage pouch wouldn’t still be with you. Opportunity belongs to those capable of seizing it. If we couldn’t beat you, we’d naturally back off. Fair, friendly, and harmonious competition is ideal.”

The Pine Leaf Mouse squeaked in agreement, eyeing the storage pouch hungrily.

Lin Jing held back against Xu Zhizhi, knowing scholars from the Literary Domain often had ties to the Ancient Celestial Kingdom’s bureaucracy. Making an enemy of him over a pouch wasn’t worth it.

“Are you two really going to let him monopolize two Bodhi Trees?” Xu Zhizhi turned to the alchemist and swordsman for support.

The alchemist frowned. “Let’s team up. This squirrel isn’t cute at all—it’s practically a Mechanized Craftsman itself.”

The silent swordsman finally drew his sword, tacitly agreeing to join forces.

“Ganging up on someone isn’t heroic,” Senior Sister Qin Caixin of the Beast Taming Sect interjected. “Don’t forget, we have seven disciples here.”

“Senior Brothers, we entered the Cavern Heaven by chance. Our strength may not be enough to secure opportunities, but we can’t let outsiders gang up on Junior Brother Lin.”

“She’s right,” Senior Brother Ziran agreed, stroking his Fire Spirit Sparrow. “While we may be rivals in the examination, we are all Beast Taming Sect disciples outside the sect. Elder Li instructed us to support Junior Brother Lin if we couldn’t secure opportunities ourselves.”

“None of us are strong, but we’re all in the Qi Refining stage. These three don’t seem as terrifying as that body cultivator. A sneak attack would still hurt them,” Senior Brother He Yiming added, removing the cloth from his Battle Ape’s weapon.

The Demon-Hunting Hall disciples nodded, locking onto the three outsiders with their spirit beasts. If not all sect disciples could seize opportunities, they’d ensure the greatest chance for one of their own.

With the Beast Taming Sect’s disciples rallying around Lin Jing, a 16-versus-3 standoff emerged. Though the outsiders dismissed most of the sect disciples as weaklings, the numbers still created pressure.

“You’re not the Beast Taming Sect—you’re the Bully Sect!” the alchemist fumed.

“Thank you, Senior Brothers and Sisters,” Lin Jing said sincerely. He couldn’t predict the outsiders’ trump cards, but a strong display of unity was always advantageous. Cavern Heaven opportunities were rare, and he wouldn’t miss this chance to maximize his and the Pine Leaf Mouse’s gains.

The next moment, the turtle, which was the same color as the Mechanical Bamboo Rifle, pointed its cannon-like muzzle at the enemy.

The scholar in the blue robe felt a twinge of annoyance. He had no doubt that this contraption could fire the same devastating attack as before.

“Damn it, is this really a Qi Refining disciple from the Beast Taming Sect?” the scholar muttered, his frustration evident. “It feels like all the books I’ve read might as well have been written by a dog. This man—and this squirrel—are nothing like the Beast Taming Sect disciples I’ve read about. Who has ever heard of a squirrel using mechanical creations?!”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 34: The Sword Cultivator
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 34: The Sword Cultivator

Inside the Cavern Heaven, the camps were now split into two factions.

From the current situation, the Beast Taming Sect clearly held the upper hand, but the other three weren’t willing to give up easily.

The Silent Swordsman, who had yet to make a move, finally spoke up.

"Enough, I have a sword technique," he said. "If any of you can withstand it, I’ll withdraw. No assistance from the other two."

He took a deep breath, seemingly coming to a decision.

"Fine," Lin Jing agreed.

"If I lose, I’ll withdraw," Lin Jing added, showing fairness.

Upon hearing this, the Blue-robed Scholar and the Violet Flame Alchemist were more than happy to sit back and watch the tigers fight.

Their hope? Both Lin Jing and the Silent Swordsman would be left badly injured.

"Control the sword with Qi!"

The Silent Swordsman released his Demon-Slaying Sword, which hovered mid-air as if held by an invisible hand.

With a leap, he landed on the flying sword and soared away.

Through his earlier observations, he had calculated the effective range of that strange mechanical contraption.

His flying sword was exceptionally agile and fast, quickly widening the distance between them.

"Flying on a sword... That’s usually something only Foundation Establishment sword cultivators with robust True Essence can achieve," the Blue-robed Scholar murmured, speculating on the swordsman’s identity.

This individual must be one of the top talents in the Outer Sect of the Heavenly Sword Gate—perhaps even the first among them this year!

"He’s likely trying to gain distance before using his flying sword offensively, but..." The Blue-robed Scholar suddenly paused, confused.

While flying, the Silent Swordsman turned his head, then fell silent.

To his surprise, a squirrel was imitating him—Pine Leaf Mouse was flying on a leaf! The creature stood on a flying leaf while holding a Mechanical Bamboo Rifle, aimed steadily at the swordsman as if to prevent any escape.

"You’re using a sword technique. Why are you distancing yourself?" Lin Jing quipped.

The absurdity of the scene left everyone stunned.

A squirrel, not only capable of flying on a leaf but doing so at nearly the same speed as a sword technique!

"Flying Rainbow Sword!"

Frustrated, the Silent Swordsman gritted his teeth and summoned a cyan flying sword, which rose into the air under his control.

"Sword Shadow Technique!"

Another spell followed, splitting the Flying Rainbow Sword into nine sword shadows, each as vivid as the original, making it impossible to discern which was real.

"Go!"

Under the Silent Swordsman’s command, the sword shadows attacked the Pine Leaf Mouse from all directions like streaks of lightning.

Such an all-encompassing assault would be difficult to block entirely, even with the agility of a Mechanical Bamboo Rifle.

In response, Pine Leaf Mouse summoned ten crimson leaves from its storage pouch. Mirroring the sword shadows, the leaves transformed into ten streaks of red light.

Swish! Swish! Swish!

The swords and leaves clashed mid-air, shattering nine sword shadows. One crimson leaf was destroyed by the real Flying Rainbow Sword, but seizing the moment, Pine Leaf Mouse fired its rifle at the real sword.

Boom!

The Flying Rainbow Sword shattered, causing the Silent Swordsman to stagger.

Pine Leaf Mouse and the Silent Swordsman had their first round of combat mid-air, leaving everyone dumbfounded.

"What the h*ll..."

Could a mere Pine Leaf Mouse pull off such feats?

The Beast Taming Sect disciples were flabbergasted.

Back in the Great Wasteland, a sword cultivator in the Qi Refinement stage capable of releasing seven or eight sword qi attacks would already be considered a master. Yet, while this Heavenly Sword Gate disciple was a small monster, their sect's basic spirit beast seemed even more monstrous.

"Forget those novels like My Sword Spirit Wife from Heavenly Sword Gate—bizarre tales are happening right here," Senior Brother Ziran muttered.

"Since when did battle pets become this strong?" The Blue-robed Scholar and Violet Flame Alchemist, who initially thought Lin Jing’s strength lay solely in his mechanical constructs, now realized this Qi Refinement Level 7 Pine Leaf Mouse was capable of dueling a sword cultivator in mid-air.

No wonder the Pine Leaf Mouse insisted on claiming a tree for itself. Suddenly, it all seemed reasonable, despite how nonsensical it was.

"I’m done." After two rounds, the Silent Swordsman was worn out and chose to surrender.

Although he could still fight a few more rounds, he understood that the Pine Leaf Mouse was just a pet—and its Beast Tamer had yet to step in.

"Once I reach Foundation Establishment and forge a Sword Body, I’ll come to the Beast Taming Sect for a rematch," he declared, unwilling to accept defeat to a mere Qi Refinement Level 7 squirrel, even if his sword technique would only reveal its full power post-Foundation Establishment.

The Pine Leaf Mouse gave a dismissive squeak and turned away, its robe fluttering as if to express disdain. It hated wasting its leaves and ammo.

"No matter what, I’ll claim one Green Bodhi Tree," the Silent Swordsman asserted.

Since everyone present was in the Qi Refinement stage, their goal wasn’t to create divine techniques but to refine their existing spells using the Bodhi Trees.

In this context, the Silent Swordsman wasn’t particularly obsessed with the Golden Bodhi Tree.

"I’m not leaving without a tree either!" the Violet Flame Alchemist grumbled, watching as all three trees were claimed.

She produced a pill, holding it up.

"This pill can temporarily grant me the power of Foundation Establishment," she said, her tone threatening.

The others ignored her.

"Hey, say something!" she snapped, turning to Lin Jing. "Beast Tamer, how about we share the Golden Bodhi Tree?"

"You don’t want me to take this pill, do you?"

Lin Jing, rifle in hand, smiled. "Fellow Daoist, if this pill had no side effects, you’d have already taken it."

"Go ahead, take it. After defeating me, will you even have the state of mind to gain enlightenment?"

"Moreover, how can you be sure this pill will guarantee your victory?"

"You practice fire techniques, but..." Lin Jing glanced at the Mechanical Bamboo Turtle beside him. "This construct has a Spirit Gathering Formation engraved on it. Its water techniques are stronger than my rifle, and it has a self-destruct function—ten times more powerful than the previous explosion. Want to gamble?"

"Do you want to perish with me or this turtle?"

"It has no intelligence, fears neither life nor death, and doesn’t need to stay in a state of enlightenment. You wouldn’t want a fight to the death with it, would you?" Lin Jing finished.

The turtle silently stared at the Violet Flame Alchemist, intensifying the pressure.

Fuming, the alchemist backed down. Who would want to die with a mechanical turtle?

Lin Jing wasn’t lying. For two months, he’d spent little time cultivating and more on upgrading the Mechanical Bamboo Turtle and Rifle. He had even engraved a Spirit Gathering Formation—the most universal formation across the continent—into the turtle, enhancing its water-based attacks.

During combat, activating the Spirit Gathering Formation allowed the turtle to gather water spiritual energy faster, unleashing even more powerful water streams.

As for the self-destruct function? Pure bluff. Even if it self-destructed, the blast wouldn’t be that impactful. Lin Jing was betting she’d believe it.

In the world outside, there’s no need to be honest all the time.

Sure enough, after a moment of silence, the Violet Flame Alchemist withdrew her Fire Spirit Pill and said, "Beast Tamer, let me share the Silver Bodhi Tree with your squirrel. I’ll owe you a favor."

Before Lin Jing could respond, Pine Leaf Mouse raised its rifle and aimed it at her. Furious, she stormed off.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 35: Creating a Unique Technique
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 35: Creating a Unique Technique

Naturally, the Violet Flame Alchemist wasn’t ready to give up entirely. Lin Jing and Pine Leaf Mouse might have been unyielding, but there was still one Green Bodhi Tree left.

Without waiting for the Silent Swordsman’s consent, she plopped herself down beneath the green tree.

Seeing this, the swordsman slightly unsheathed his blade but hesitated when he caught the fury in her eyes. After a moment of consideration, he sighed and sheathed his sword again.

He could tell that provoking this woman further would push her to extreme measures, and nobody would benefit from that.

"Wait, you’ve already divided the trees?" Blue-robed Scholar was baffled. Lin Jing and Pine Leaf Mouse claimed the Golden and Silver Bodhi Trees, while the Silent Swordsman and the alchemist took the green one.

"Hey, what about me?!"

Weren’t they supposed to work together? How did these supposed allies give up so quickly?

Instinctively, he stepped toward the Green Bodhi Tree, but the Silent Swordsman and the alchemist glared at him coldly.

"Fine!" The scholar gritted his teeth. "You’re all bullying a scholar, huh? You think being a Beast Tamer, playing with fire, or swinging a sword makes you better? Remember, it’s us scholars who govern kingdoms!"

"Whatever. I don’t even want the Green Bodhi Tree," he spat. "But I’m not leaving empty-handed."

Everyone turned to him.

"I, Xu Zhizhi, have slain a Snow Plains Cultivator plotting against our empire’s prodigies, securing their opportunities. Not unreasonable, right?" As he spoke, Xu marched up to the lifeless Snow Plains Cultivator and delivered a solid punch to his head before looking at the group.

Lin Jing stayed silent for a moment.

"Fair enough. It’s yours."

The corpse had limited uses:

1. Feeding beasts, which was prohibited by the Beast Taming Sect.

2. Selling it to the Yin Corpse Sect for crafting corpse puppets, but Lin Jing wouldn’t engage with them.

3. Gifting it to a government official, given the long-standing tensions between the Snow Plains Tribe and the Ancient Celestial Kingdom.

"If you keep your distance from this side, I didn’t see anything," the alchemist added.

The Silent Swordsman didn’t object either, granting tacit approval.

Thus, the unfortunate Snow Plains Cultivator's corpse became Xu Zhizhi’s prize. He stored it in his spatial pouch without hesitation.

All this unfolded under the watchful eyes of the Beast Taming Sect disciples, including four from the Demon-Hunting Hall, who couldn’t help but admire Xu’s adaptability and ruthlessness.

With the Bodhi Trees now divided, Senior Brother Ziran stepped forward.

"Junior Brother, we’ll guard the area for you. Focus on your cultivation."

After all, the Blue-robed Scholar, having lost out, might act out of desperation.

"Thank you, Senior Brothers." Lin Jing smiled. "Oh, by the way, there wasn’t much in the Snow Plains Cultivator’s storage pouch, but it did contain some Foundation Establishment beast blood—perfect for body refinement. Consider it my thanks. I hope it aids your cultivation."

Lin Jing couldn’t help but lament the cultivator’s poverty, but he had no attachment to the beast blood and generously offered it as a gesture of goodwill.

"Junior Brother, you’re too kind," Senior Brother He Yiming laughed, his opinion of Lin Jing improving. The other Demon-Hunting Hall disciples nodded in approval as well.

---

The contest within the Cavern Heaven temporarily came to a close.

Aside from the unfortunate Snow Plains Cultivator, each Ancient Celestial Kingdom disciple gained something valuable.

Those who secured spots beneath the Bodhi Trees began their cultivation.

The Silent Swordsman immersed himself in his sword scripture, surrounded by swirling sword qi.

The alchemist, meanwhile, retrieved a massive cauldron and various materials, experimenting with a unique alchemical method.

As for Pine Leaf Mouse, its obsession with creating an internal space persisted. It was utterly frustrated—the storage pouch from the Snow Plains cultivator was less than one cubic meter!

"You need to be more realistic!" Lin Jing groaned, using their Beast Contract to telepathically scold it. "Creating something from nothing? That’s the work of a Nascent Void Stage grandmaster. You’re just a little squirrel in the Qi Refinement Stage!"

"Chirp! Chirp!" Pine Leaf Mouse retorted indignantly.

"You think mastering Leaf Flight is pointless? That flying multiple leaves at once has no value?" Lin Jing sighed, trying to steer its focus. "Listen, aimlessly experimenting won’t work. Let me guide you. Spatial artifacts—whether storage pouches or Beast Taming space treasures—are forged from spatial materials. Why not start by imbuing your crimson leaves with spatial attributes?"

"For instance, make them capable of easily slicing through spatial barriers, formations, or even breaking into others’ storage gear. That’s far more achievable."

"Once you master that, you could use those spatial-infused leaves as materials to craft a spatial artifact. Step by step."

"Ever heard the saying, One flower, one world; one leaf, one Bodhi? A single leaf could become a universe, holding endless treasures."

After delivering his advice, Lin Jing left the squirrel to ponder. Pine Leaf Mouse began testing by repeatedly inserting and removing its crimson leaves from the storage pouch, studying any changes.

Meanwhile, Lin Jing focused on his own dilemmas.

Currently, his arsenal consisted of three techniques: Beast Taming Art, Spirit Nurturing Spell, and Spirit Gathering Formation.

The Spirit Nurturing Spell relieved fatigue, while the Spirit Gathering Formation accelerated spiritual energy accumulation. Both had limited potential and well-explored upgrade paths. As for the Beast Taming Art, its complete version was readily available within the sect.

In short, none of these techniques required intensive contemplation under the Golden Bodhi Tree.

"Qi Refinement’s biggest flaw: limited accumulation," Lin Jing sighed. "Otherwise, I could be like those leaderboard titans, creating my own techniques."

Despite stockpiling Malignant Plants, he lacked sufficient combat techniques suitable for Bodhi-enhanced insights.

"Maybe I should focus on enhancing the offensive power of Beast Taming Qi?"

Unlike the destructive qi of body cultivators or sword cultivators, Beast Taming Qi primarily excelled at nurturing spirit beasts, leaving Beast Tamers comparatively weaker in direct combat.

Even alchemists, who could transform their qi into flames, might outmatch them in certain scenarios.

Determined to change this, Lin Jing retrieved a Purple Fire Dragon Fruit—a spiritual fruit synthesized from a Dragonbone Flower. It had once granted a mere Qi Refinement mouse a terrifying illusion comparable to a Nascent Soul Demon King.

His eyes lit up.

"Of course! Perhaps I can devise a new technique from this," he mused, energized.

"Could I leverage the Golden Bodhi Tree’s enlightenment to dissect the illusionary principles of the Purple Dragonbone Flower and convert it into a versatile illusion art?"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 36: The Illusionary Path
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 36: The Illusionary Path

Creating an illusion-based technique was no small feat, but Lin Jing believed it was possible.

Although still in the Qi Refinement Stage and unfamiliar with illusionary arts, using the Purple Dragonbone Flower as a foundation for innovation wasn’t entirely baseless.

With the added insight from the Golden Bodhi Tree, Lin Jing felt it was worth a shot.

If he succeeded, it would elevate his cultivation. Simply using the tree to refine existing techniques felt like a waste of its immense potential.

After centering himself, he consumed the Purple Dragonbone Flower.

One second… two seconds… three seconds…

Suddenly, the golden glow of the Bodhi Tree enveloped him, and an overwhelming aura began to rise from his body.

Lin Jing focused intently, immersing himself in his inner transformation.

He watched as the nutrients from the Purple Dragonbone Flower fused with his Beast Taming Qi, transforming it into a new, purple-hued spiritual energy.

"Not enough. I need to see more!"

His consciousness delved deeper, revealing the essence of his Beast Taming Qi.

What seemed like an already insubstantial energy was now breaking down into even smaller particles before his eyes. The nutrients from the dragonbone flower disassembled the invisible Qi into these micro-units, then restructured them into a vastly more complex form—a purple Qi.

"This is it!"

Lin Jing’s mind raced as he tried to memorize the intricate structure of this newly formed purple energy.

But the purple Qi was volatile, attempting to escape his control as it coursed through his body. It wasn’t entirely his power yet.

"This energy isn’t inherently stronger than my own Qi. Its true power lies in its complex structure, which enhances its deceptive capabilities in the outside world."

Illusions thrive on deception.

This purple Qi was the perfect foundation for an illusion-based art.

Lin Jing understood what needed to be done.

---

Externally, the purple energy began to leak from Lin Jing’s body. Like before, it manifested as the skeletal form of a fearsome dragon, a transformation seemingly etched into the dragonbone flower’s lineage.

Perhaps the flower’s ancestors—flower spirits—once mingled with dragonkind.

"So, if I can disassemble my Qi and restructure it into this form, I could simulate the effects of consuming the dragonbone flower," Lin Jing theorized.

Once formed, the purple Qi could be shaped into various intimidating forms—whether the current skeletal dragon or others like the Four Seas Dragon Kings, Monkey King, or even Buddha.

These illusions would not only be incredibly lifelike but also project a level of menace far beyond his current cultivation.

"But how do I break down my Qi and reassemble it at will?"

At his current level, Lin Jing lacked the finesse to deconstruct his own Qi.

Fortunately, the Golden Bodhi Tree provided unparalleled support. Through sheer will and practice, Lin Jing’s consciousness visualized his Qi as a malleable force, allowing him to manipulate it with extraordinary precision.

"Without this tree, such precise control would be impossible."

With limited time, Lin Jing decided to drill relentlessly, repeating the process of disassembling and reassembling his Qi thousands—millions—of times. His goal was to engrain the method into muscle memory, much like an instinctive action.

As Lin Jing immersed himself in this process, the external world stirred in shock.

The sight of Lin Jing transforming into a massive skeletal dragon exuding terrifying demonic energy left the surrounding Beast Taming Sect disciples in awe.

"What technique is this?" muttered a Demon-Hunting Hall disciple, his contracted black panther visibly trembling.

"I’ve never seen anything like it in the Outer or Inner Sects," said He Yiming, temporarily setting aside past grievances to watch over Lin Jing.

"This purple dragon’s presence is leagues beyond that of the Myriad Beast Body, and Lin Jing isn’t even targeting anyone specifically," observed Senior Brother Ziran, beads of sweat forming on his forehead.

"Could he be a demon?" Qin Caixin speculated, recalling Lin Jing’s peculiarities.

It wasn’t impossible. To the disciples, the notion of Lin Jing being a hidden dragon or demon in disguise seemed oddly plausible.

"But weren’t demons wiped out? Dragons, the apex of the demon clans... Could he truly be one?"

Even Lin Jing’s fellow sect members were at a loss. Meanwhile, Xu Zhizhi, busy penning a report on his heroic deeds, froze at the sight of the dragon’s flame-like eyes.

"What... is this? A secret art? A special constitution?" Xu muttered, shaken by the overwhelming sense of doom the dragon exuded.

Both the Silent Swordsman and the Violet Flame Alchemist, deeply focused on their own practices, couldn’t help but notice the transformation. They too were stunned.

This wasn’t something they had encountered in their earlier skirmishes with Lin Jing.

"If he had used this form in battle, thirty of us—or even three hundred—would have stood no chance," they concluded.

Had Lin Jing been holding back this whole time? Was this his trump card?

The Beast Taming Sect had long been dismissed as a third-rate sect, its disciples perceived as overly reliant on their spirit beasts. Yet, Lin Jing challenged that stereotype. His Pine Leaf Mouse was already impressive, but now Lin Jing himself seemed unfathomable.

Who was this enigmatic Beast Tamer?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 37: Emerging from the Cavern Heaven
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 37: Emerging from the Cavern Heaven

It is said that the Cavern Heaven obscures one’s perception of time.

Even if one cultivates inside for months or years, the cultivators within have no strong sense of time, feeling as though it has passed in the blink of an eye.

Immersed in this state, everyone delved deeply into their cultivation and gained significant insights.

The Silent Swordsman’s sword-shaped Qi became completely internalized, and the sound of his heartbeat resembled the hum of a sword. Each inch of his skin shimmered with a crystalline sword light. Clearly, his sword manual had advanced, bringing him closer to achieving the Sword Body he often spoke of.

The Violet Flame Alchemist refined her control over flames during her alchemy practice. The violet fire inside her cauldron formed nine spiraling layers, continuously tempering the medicinal materials and purging all impurities from the pills.

Even disciples of the Beast Taming Sect, who didn’t have access to the Bodhi Trees, found their cultivation much more focused and efficient, making time pass swiftly without the usual tedium.

Meanwhile, Lin Jing had returned to his usual state. Relying on the Golden Leaf Bodhi Tree, he had memorized the structure of Purple Qi and was reconstructing it. After thousands of attempts and tens of thousands of repetitions, his ability to form Purple Qi became faster and more refined.

Unknowingly, his cultivation advanced from the early stage of Qi Refining to the mid-stage, reaching the fourth level of Qi Refining!

The others were no longer surprised by Lin Jing’s transformation into a dragon. They continued their cultivation, but the Pine Leaf Mouse suddenly turned to look at him.

“It seems feasible,” Lin Jing murmured to himself. He felt he had perfectly replicated the effects of the Purple Dragonbone Flower, marking his entry into the Illusionary Path.

Unlike ordinary illusion techniques, his current Illusionary Path techniques were far more deceptive, capable of fooling even higher-level opponents.

However, using such advanced illusions consumed significant energy. Even after his breakthrough, Lin Jing found his Qi insufficient to sustain them.

What was more concerning was that casting these illusions under the Golden Bodhi Tree’s amplification made it relatively easy. Once outside, it would likely require considerable practice to use them smoothly.

“This Illusionary Path spell can indeed be shaped into other beast forms. In that case, I’ll name it ‘Thousand Transformations,’” Lin Jing decided as he attempted to drive a massive dragon claw toward the Pine Leaf Mouse, wrapping it into the illusory form.

Inside the illusion, the Pine Leaf Mouse saw Lin Jing transformed into the Black Bear Monster.

However, this Black Bear Monster didn’t wear a Daoist robe like it did but was instead clad in a kasaya robe, exuding an intimidating aura befitting a demon king.

If the Pine Leaf Mouse didn’t know better, it would have clutched its storage pouch and fled.

Lin Jing dispelled the illusionary Qi surrounding his body and looked at the Pine Leaf Mouse.

“How was it?” he asked.

“Chirp!” The Pine Leaf Mouse sighed. “Nothing. Not a single gain. A flower containing a world? A leaf containing a universe? Lies!”

“I told you, progress has to be gradual. Did you really gain nothing at all?” Lin Jing asked helplessly.

The Pine Leaf Mouse had exceptional talent, and with the Silver Bodhi Tree’s enhancement, Lin Jing was confident it had gained something.

Sure enough, the Pine Leaf Mouse took out a red leaf. If anything, this was its gain.

“What’s this?” Lin Jing asked.

“Chirp!” It tossed a new storage pouch to Lin Jing.

Lin Jing caught the storage pouch from the Snow Plains Cultivator.

The next moment, the Pine Leaf Mouse activated a Flying Leaf Technique, sending a red leaf at the storage pouch in Lin Jing’s hand.

When the red leaf touched the pouch, it vanished.

Moments later, the leaf reappeared and flew off. Although Lin Jing hadn’t done anything, a pile of Spirit Stones spilled out of the storage pouch.

It was as if the pouch had been sliced open by the leaf.

The Pine Leaf Mouse’s Flying Leaf Technique had mutated, allowing it to extract items from a storage pouch.

Lin Jing opened his mouth, wanting to say something but ultimately gave up.

“I was just making a casual suggestion, but you actually managed to pull it off.”

A space manipulation spell!

This was undoubtedly more difficult to learn than Illusionary Path techniques.

Yet the Pine Leaf Mouse had comprehended it by repeatedly placing the red leaf in and out of the storage pouch.

If Lin Jing had known the Pine Leaf Mouse was this monstrous, he would have ceded the Golden Bodhi Tree to it.

“Chirp!” However, the Pine Leaf Mouse didn’t think much of the technique.

Its concern was losing its own storage pouch during combat.

Lin Jing ignored it. Regardless of how impractical the Flying Leaf Technique seemed to the Pine Leaf Mouse, it had now become a storage pouch assassin.

“Let’s call this upgraded Flying Leaf Technique ‘Plucking the Flower with Flying Leaves,’” Lin Jing declared.

The Pine Leaf Mouse didn’t object.

For once, the name sounded decent.

Lin Jing had come up with many names in the past, but the Pine Leaf Mouse found them either unpleasant or perfunctory. Eventually, it insisted on being called Pine Leaf Mouse rather than any of Lin Jing’s suggestions.

The Pine Leaf Mouse resolved that once they had enough money, they would travel to the Literary Domain and consult a learned Feng Shui master for a name worthy of immortality.

“Speaking of which, we’ve made decent gains, but neither my ‘Thousand Transformations’ nor your improved ‘Plucking the Flower with Flying Leaves’ made it onto the rankings.” Lin Jing scratched his chin as he dispersed his illusions, gazing at the monument at the center of the three trees.

Rank 100.

Feng Jiutian, Foundation Establishment Stage, created the Wind Emperor Body in the Bodhi Cavern Heaven.

Lin Jing didn’t know who Feng Jiutian was or what the Wind Emperor Body entailed, but possessing two special physiques in the Foundation Establishment Stage was undoubtedly remarkable.

Lin Jing wondered if he could cultivate a second physique beyond the Longevity Body during his Foundation Establishment Stage…

Shaking his head, he lamented not being able to see rankings beyond the top 100. He was curious to know how his and the Pine Leaf Mouse’s techniques ranked among all the cultivators who had entered the Bodhi Cavern Heaven.

No additional rewards this time.

He hoped for another chance to enter the Bodhi Cavern Heaven in the future.

As that thought crossed his mind, the Cavern Heaven rumbled as if it were about to close.

...

Sword Domain

The Heavenly Sword Gate, the top sword cultivation sect in the Ancient Celestial Kingdom, stood in the Sword Domain.

In the outer sect, as a massive pillar of light dissipated, the outer sect elder of the Heavenly Sword Gate, who had been waiting patiently, looked toward the Silent Swordsman, clutching his Demon-Slaying Sword.

“Yuanjing!” The elder heaved a sigh of relief upon seeing the Silent Swordsman emerge, his face lighting up with delight.

“Well done! In the past ten years, you are the fourth disciple of the Sword Gate chosen by the Cavern Heaven! Entering it with a mortal body—just like your senior sister did in her time!”

“Greetings, Elder.” Yuanjing, the Silent Swordsman, bowed slightly, a hint of nervousness in his demeanor.

Elder Jianxu asked, “Yuanjing, which Cavern Heaven did you enter?”

“Reporting to the elder: it was the Bodhi Cavern Heaven.”

“The Bodhi Cavern Heaven? The one for enlightenment? And it allowed a Qi Refining cultivator to enter...” Elder Jianxu was momentarily speechless. The Bodhi Cavern Heaven worked best for those with profound cultivation, who could draw upon their life’s knowledge to create their own techniques or physique.

For a Qi Refining cultivator, who hadn’t even fully mastered their sect’s foundational techniques, it seemed like a waste.

However, entering a Cavern Heaven was a blessing regardless. One shouldn’t refuse a free opportunity.

The elder regarded Yuanjing with a thoughtful expression. Though this disciple didn’t possess an extraordinary physique, his sword cultivation aptitude was exceptional, easily surpassing those of his generation with special physiques. Yuanjing had risen to the top of the outer sect, a testament to his potential. This trip to the Cavern Heaven further solidified his capabilities.

“Did anyone else enter the Bodhi Cavern Heaven with you? Did you manage to seize the Golden Bodhi Tree?” Elder Jianxu finally broached the most pressing question.

This was the question Yuanjing had been dreading.

“Elder, this disciple was unable to secure the Golden Bodhi Tree. I shared the Green Bodhi Tree with another, bringing shame to the sect. I vow to redouble my efforts in cultivation to atone for my failure.”

Elder Jianxu blinked in surprise. Yuanjing, despite not having an extraordinary physique, had extraordinary swordsmanship. Yet he had only managed to claim the Green Bodhi Tree?

“No need for self-blame. There’s always someone better. I see you’ve already cultivated a Sword Heart; you’re not far from forging the Sword Body. Progress matters more than momentary victory.” The elder reassured him. “Which sect’s Qi Refining disciple claimed the Golden and Silver Bodhi Trees?”

“This time, twelve individuals entered the Bodhi Cavern Heaven,” Yuanjing explained. “A scholar from Shengyun City in the Literary Domain, an alchemist from the Fire Domain’s Danxia Sect, eight disciples from the Beast Taming Sect of the Wasteland Domain—seven of whom entered by coincidence—and one Snow Plains Cultivator.”

He paused before continuing, “The Golden Bodhi Tree was claimed by a disciple of the Beast Taming Sect. As for the Silver Bodhi Tree...”

“It was claimed by his contracted beast.”

“What?” Elder Jianxu was stunned. “The Beast Taming Sect? That Beast Taming Sect?”

“You lost to a disciple from the Beast Taming Sect?”

“Elder, I… His contracted beast… I don’t know how to explain it. What he knows, it knows; what it can do, he can do better. It’s beyond explanation.” Yuanjing was at a loss for words.

...

Fire Domain, Danxia Sect

A similar scene played out in the Danxia Sect. The Violet Flame Alchemist, renowned for her alchemical talents, returned as a rising star within the sect. Her achievements during her time in the Cavern Heaven drew even greater attention to her potential.

The name “Tang Seng” of the Beast Taming Sect also began circulating among the elite disciples of major sects.

...

Literary Domain

In the Literary Domain, a blue-robed scholar dragged a bruised and battered Snow Plains Cultivator into the city. Surrounded by peers and onlookers near the light pillar, the scholar wiped the blood from his face and clasped his hands together in a salute.

He appeared as though he wanted to etch the words “I’m here for promotion!” onto his forehead.

...

Wasteland Domain, Beast Taming Sect

In the Beast Taming Sect, numerous elders awaited anxiously outside the Cavern Heaven. When Lin Jing and his group returned unscathed, the elders were overjoyed.

However, their joy was mingled with curiosity. Among the eight disciples and eight contracted beasts, it was clear that Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse were at the center of attention. The others followed behind them like a protective entourage, their gazes filled with awe and reverence.

The elders couldn’t help but wonder—what exactly had happened inside the Cavern Heaven?
