Elder Yu of the Mechanized Workshop was particularly baffled.

Why were several disciples looking at him so intently, their gazes burning with enthusiasm?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 38: Elder, I Want to Learn Mechanisms
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 38: Elder, I Want to Learn Mechanisms

“They’re out!”

The disciples of the Beast Taming Sect returned safely, prompting Elder Black Bear, clad in a Daoist robe, to exclaim loudly.

“They’re all alive! No one died, no beasts were lost, and not a single limb is missing!” he continued with enthusiasm.

“I told you this Lin Jing kid could handle it!”

“It’s good they’re back safely,” Elder Yu and Elder Mo sighed in relief, soon breaking into smiles.

Regardless of whether the disciples gained anything, surviving the Cavern Heaven was already a valuable experience.

“But… why do none of you show any signs of battle?” Elder Li Hai scanned the group with a curious gaze. “Which Cavern Heaven did you enter?”

“Did you give up on fighting for opportunities?”

Elder Li couldn’t help but suspect the disciples had merely gone through the motions. He turned his attention to Lin Jing, the central figure in this Cavern Heaven event.

Faced with Elder Li’s question, Lin Jing took a deep breath, still somewhat dazed. His mind was preoccupied with the intricacies of his “Thousand Transformations” spell, deeply etched into his memory.

“Ahem,” Lin Jing cleared his throat. Without the Golden Bodhi Tree’s augmentation, he felt his mind working slower than usual, an adjustment he hadn’t yet gotten used to.

“Elder Li, we entered the Bodhi Cavern Heaven and did not abandon the pursuit of opportunities,” he replied.

“The Bodhi Cavern Heaven? The one with the three Bodhi Trees?” Elder Li asked, surprised. “Did you manage to claim them?”

“Yes, Elder. With the assistance of my senior brothers and the mechanism techniques taught by Elder Yu, we successfully fended off geniuses from other sects and claimed two Bodhi Trees,” Lin Jing answered, allocating credit modestly.

The Pine Leaf Mouse’s ears twitched, and it raised its eyebrows. Where was its contribution in this story?

“No, no, we don’t deserve the credit,” Senior Brother Ziran interjected from the back. “We barely helped. Junior Brother Lin Jing fought valiantly against all odds and used his mechanism techniques to intimidate the others. At most, we just added some finishing touches.”

“Exactly,” the other disciples chimed in, reluctant to claim any credit for themselves.

At this, the elders exchanged peculiar glances.

Mechanism techniques?

They turned to Elder Yu, who scratched his head sheepishly.

Was Lin Jing’s mastery of mechanisms advanced enough to suppress the prodigies of other sects?

“It seems you didn’t encounter the personal disciples or descendants of those old monsters. Otherwise, those mechanism techniques wouldn’t have been enough…” Elder Yu speculated, his face lighting up. “But this means that, relying on mechanism techniques, our Beast Taming Sect has successfully competed with the geniuses of other sects for significant opportunities in the Cavern Heaven!”

He clapped Elder Black Bear on the shoulder with pride.

“And your mechanism techniques have finally found the right practitioner,” Elder Black Bear remarked with a grin.

“My techniques were always excellent! You’re implying they weren’t?” Elder Yu retorted. “Go back to Elder Mo’s side if you’re going to spout nonsense.”

“I’m just showing concern for Lin Jing. I couldn’t care less about the others,” Elder Black Bear muttered dismissively.

Elder Li, observing their exchange, was intrigued. It seemed Lin Jing had exceptional talent, and Elder Yu’s personal guidance had paid off.

Even the four Demon-Hunting Hall disciples under Elder Li’s command now seemed utterly convinced of Lin Jing’s abilities.

“As I suggested earlier, you all are exempt from the Inner Sect examination. Effective immediately, you are promoted to Inner Sect disciples!” Elder Li declared.

“Thank you, Elder!” The others, both disciples and their beasts, responded with elation.

Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse, however, felt a slight pang of regret, lamenting the missed opportunity to trade examination points for resources.

In the Great Wasteland, it was a stroke of luck for a demon beast to escape with its life intact—a sign of great destiny.

“We’ll discuss the details later. For now, the major Inner Peaks elders are all present. As newly promoted Inner Sect disciples, you may choose a peak and follow the corresponding Peak Master back,” Elder Mo announced.

But the group hesitated.

“Hmm?” Elder Yu frowned at the disciples. “You know which elders oversee which peaks, don’t you? Go ahead and choose!”

Apart from Elder Yu, Elder Mo, and Elder Li, several Golden Core elders were present. They had been drawn by the Cavern Heaven’s opening and were among the sect’s key figures, each responsible for a Beast Peak and its unique teachings.

“You first,” Elder Yu suggested, looking at Senior Brother Ziran, his most familiar Outer Sect disciple, who often ran errands for him.

Senior Brother Ziran hesitated for a moment before responding tearfully, “Elder Yu, I don’t want to join the Inner Sect. I’d prefer to stay in the Outer Sect and continue serving you.”

“What?” Elder Yu was stunned.

He Yiming, another disciple, swallowed nervously and asked earnestly, “Elder Yu, do you have any plans to establish your own peak to teach mechanism techniques?”

“I want to learn.”

Even the four Demon-Hunting Hall disciples gazed at Elder Yu with burning intensity.

Everyone knew that Lin Jing had acquired his mechanism techniques during the Ascending Dragon Festival, and the source of those techniques was none other than Elder Yu!

After witnessing the power of mechanisms in the Cavern Heaven, how could they not be tempted?

“Mechanisms and beast taming don’t conflict at all. Mechanized constructs can be considered spirit tools, no different from equipping beasts with weapons or armor,” they reasoned.

The Pine Leaf Mouse, equipped with a mechanical rifle, had left a deep impression on them. If possible, they all wanted one too.

Elder Yu froze momentarily, bewildered by their sudden enthusiasm.

Elder Li, equally baffled, glanced at the four Demon-Hunting Hall disciples. What exactly had they experienced in the Cavern Heaven? Didn’t I recommend the Law Enforcement Hall on Yunmen Peak for you earlier?

“So… you all want to learn mechanism techniques?” Elder Yu finally understood why their gazes had been so peculiar.

But there was a problem. Lin Jing’s exceptional mechanized creations were not solely a result of mechanism techniques—they relied heavily on rare materials, derived from mystical beast abilities and other peculiar sources.

Other mechanism practitioners couldn’t easily replicate his constructs. Even if they managed to recreate them through other means, the firepower wouldn’t match. And if the firepower did match, the resource cost would skyrocket, making it inefficient.

“Since these disciples are eager to learn mechanisms, why not teach them, Elder Yu?” an elder suggested.

She was an elderly woman with white hair and a serene expression, unconcerned about the disciples’ choices. Her attention was focused entirely on Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse.

“I am the Peak Master of Danxia Peak. Child, your Pine Leaf Mouse is a wood-type beast adept at accelerating the growth of various herbs. Would you consider joining Danxia Peak to learn alchemy? It would make an excellent alchemy assistant.”

“Nonsense! The Pine Leaf Mouse belongs on Cuiwei Peak!” argued a burly man in a straw hat. “On Cuiwei Peak, you’ll be assigned a cave dwelling with premium spiritual fields, perfect for unleashing the Pine Leaf Mouse’s natural talents.”

“Pure spiritual vegetables and fruits are far more nutritious than pills,” he added.

Having emerged from the Cavern Heaven with valuable opportunities, Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse had instantly become hot commodities among the Inner Peaks elders.

Even the other peaks’ elders began racking their brains for persuasive arguments.

The Nine Dragons Peak elder struggled to come up with a valid reason. He couldn’t exactly claim that the Pine Leaf Mouse had potential to evolve into a Dragonblood Squirrel, could he? That would be absurd.

In the end, he figured the best way to entice Lin Jing would be to offer the finest Dragonblood Beast cubs from his peak.

However, Lin Jing already had a preference.

Before he could voice his decision, a voice rang out from beyond the sect.

“Cao Ziwei from Qianlong Academy seeks an audience.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 39: Hidden Dragon Academy
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 39: Hidden Dragon Academy

Hidden Dragon Academy? Cao Ziwei?

The various elders exchanged strange glances.

Looking toward the direction beyond the sect, Elder Mo remarked, "Stop squabbling for now... As expected of the Hidden Dragon Academy, they act swiftly."

Soon, a man dressed like a scholarly teacher appeared atop a Mechanical Fish, smiling at the Beast Taming Sect’s crowd.

“Inferior one, Cao Ziwei of the Hidden Dragon Academy, greets the fellow Daoists of the Beast Taming Sect.”

He continued, “I’ve come here today for this young friend who just emerged from the Cavern Heaven.” His gaze fell on Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse.

“He has met the qualifications to enter the ‘Hidden Dragon Academy,’ so I’ve come personally to invite him.”

“Greetings, Mr. Cao,” the elders responded with courtesy.

Yet the elders didn’t seem particularly opposed to the situation.

“Young friend, do you know of the Hidden Dragon Academy?” the teacher asked Lin Jing.

Lin Jing, slightly confused himself, replied, “I’ve read a little about it in books.”

“The Hidden Dragon Academy is part of the Ancient Celestial Kingdom, a place to train immortal officials...”

“Exactly,” the teacher explained. “Cultivators from the Hidden Dragon Academy have the chance to become immortal officials. Upon passing examinations, they can directly govern a city, forming an alternative system for becoming an immortal official.”

“Of course, the requirements for entering the Hidden Dragon Academy are incredibly stringent. However, since the Cavern Heaven chose you, you clearly meet the criteria.”

“So, are you interested in joining the Hidden Dragon Academy?”

Lin Jing shook his head. “I apologize, but I am a disciple of the Beast Taming Sect.”

“That’s not a conflict,” the teacher responded with a smile. “Your sect master himself once studied at the Hidden Dragon Academy. In fact, he governed a city in the Great Wasteland for ten years.”

“And now, isn’t he still the sect master of the Beast Taming Sect?”

“Being a Beast Taming Sect disciple and a Hidden Dragon Academy student are identities that can coexist. Many prodigies from various sects have studied at the Hidden Dragon Academy.”

Lin Jing began to understand. The academy sought to deepen its ties to the core members and talents of different sects. Even if someone left the academy later, they would remain aligned with its influence—like retired officials with national affiliations!

He glanced at Elder Mo, who said, “Listen to Mr. Cao’s proposal and decide for yourself.”

The teacher smiled politely at Elder Mo before continuing, “Young friend, you may not fully understand the Hidden Dragon Academy yet. Joining it will benefit you greatly.”

“It possesses a deeper foundation than the Beast Taming Sect and offers a path to acquiring ‘immortal official credentials’ while learning the Hidden Dragon Secret Art.”

“Have you heard of the Hidden Dragon Secret Art?”

“It originates from the True Dragon Secret Art created by the Ancient Emperor. It allows immortal officials to harness the power of local dragon veins, enhancing both cultivation and combat ability. This is why they can govern entire cities and suppress evildoers across realms.”

“Moreover, the Hidden Dragon Secret Art has other uses. For instance, within the Beast Taming Sect, it’s the best method on the continent for nurturing dragon-blooded spirit beasts—bar none.”

“Of course, joining the academy isn’t mandatory; it’s entirely up to your will. But as a cultivator of the Ancient Celestial Kingdom, possessing such credentials would undoubtedly smooth your path to greater heights in the future.”

“Do I need to decide now and follow you, Senior?” Lin Jing asked.

“Not at all. This is merely an invitation. Take some time to consider. Your current cultivation of the fourth level of the Qi Refining stage is still a bit low. You can wait until you are about to reach Foundation Establishment to join. The academy has superior methods for establishing foundations and even advancing to the Golden Core stage.”

“Thank you, Senior. I’ll give it serious thought,” Lin Jing replied with cupped hands.

The Pine Leaf Mouse swished its robes and mimicked his gesture.

“If you decide, crush this jade slip, and someone will come for you. If not, simply discard it,” the teacher said, bidding farewell to the elders of the Beast Taming Sect.

As he departed, the elders finally exhaled deeply.

“That man... his cultivation is likely beyond the Nascent Soul stage,” said Elder Li Hai, a bead of cold sweat on his forehead.

“Could he have reached the Divine Transformation stage?” Elder Yu pondered.

Meanwhile, Lin Jing silently held the jade slip, glanced at the elders, and sighed.

What was going on? He didn’t want to leave the sect or become an official.

“Since the academy invited you personally, you should go,” Elder Mo finally said after some hesitation. “It’s a rare opportunity, and Mr. Cao’s words make sense. The academy indeed has a deeper foundation than the Beast Taming Sect.”

“For instance, they likely have multiple versions of Foundation Establishment techniques that grant Innate Special Constitutions, something the Beast Taming Sect lacks.”

“With the sect’s Foundation Establishment method, you’ll be tied to the Great Wasteland your entire life. It may not matter much, but if you aim for ascension, this difference might hold you back.”

“Elder Mo...” Lin Jing wanted to argue that he had a Longevity Body, and the Pine Leaf Mouse was a rare beast, making them too vulnerable outside.

“I understand,” Elder Mo reassured him. “When the sect master returns, I’ll request a suitable Nascent Soul ancestor to accompany and protect you during your time at the academy!”

Besides, the academy might even be safer.

“Until then, focus on improving your cultivation. Ideally, reach the ninth level of Qi Refining!”

Having heard this much from Elder Mo, Lin Jing exchanged a glance with the Pine Leaf Mouse and decided not to argue further. If safety was guaranteed, who wouldn’t want to explore beyond a single place?

“Should I head to Danxia Peak for cultivation?” Lin Jing asked.

“Come on, then!” Elder Shen of Danxia Peak beamed, her smile as bright as a Dragonbone Flower.

“I’ll personally take you to Danxia Peak and help you choose a cave!”

Fearing competition from the other peaks, she quickly pulled Lin Jing along, leaving the other elders shaking their heads in resignation.

Seriously?

Still, they left, leaving only Elder Yu, Elder Li Hai, and a few newly promoted Inner Sect disciples on the Mechanical Fish.

Seeing this, Elder Yu froze. “Wait, are these disciples really being left to me?”

---

Danxia Peak

Elder Shen arranged an excellent cave for Lin Jing. Though it lacked spirit fields, it came with a pill refining room connected to earthfire.

“Thank you, Elder Shen,” Lin Jing said respectfully.

“No need to thank me. Although you’re skilled in mechanisms, pill refining is a whole new field. You must work hard. Here’s the Fire Control Technique, essential for Danxia Peak disciples. Usually, due to limited energy, disciples won’t directly cultivate fire techniques but instead rely on a fire pet to breathe flames while using the technique for assistance.”

“Since you don’t currently have a fire pet, practice with the earthfire for now. Should you wish to raise a fire pet, let me know. I’ll help you choose a few promising beasts. Beast fire is ultimately superior to earthfire for pill refining.”

Elder Shen left after leaving behind the Fire Control Technique jade slip.

Once alone, Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse exchanged glances.

“Forget about that academy for now. Let’s figure out this pill refining first,” Lin Jing said.

“Chirp!” The Pine Leaf Mouse looked at him curiously—was he planning to find a new pet?

“Not for now,” Lin Jing shook his head. “I can barely afford you.”

The Pine Leaf Mouse nodded in agreement.

“Still, earthfire is mundane. Its low effectiveness makes pill refining success rates terrible. Without a flame attribute in the Beast Taming Qi, it’s unsuitable for fire techniques.”

“Like that Violet Flame Alchemist from the Pill Cauldron Sect—her purple flame looks excellent for pill refining. If only we had something similar to assist...”

Contracting her isn’t an option... Suddenly, an idea struck Lin Jing. “To do a good job, one must first sharpen their tools. If spiritual bamboo can spray water, why can’t a spiritual plant spray fire?”

He recalled a plant from the Encyclopedia of Flora and Fauna—a unique Malignant Plant called “Fire Cloud Chili.” Though of a low grade, it enabled creatures that consumed it to spit fire. The drawback? Severe internal burns.

“What if, by merging it with other plants, we could create a spiritual plant capable of producing exotic fire while nourishing the five organs?”

“You could eat the modified Fire Cloud Chili and pretend to be a fire spirit beast. Then we wouldn’t need a new fire pet.”

The Pine Leaf Mouse froze.

It grabbed its storage pouch, seemingly making a firm decision to leave the cave. But before it could escape, Lin Jing caught it.

“Chirp! Chirp! Chirp!”

The Pine Leaf Mouse flailed its claws, protesting vehemently. It would rather Lin Jing find a proper fire pet. Asking a wood-element spirit beast to breathe fire was simply too much!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 40: Life in the Inner Sect
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 40: Life in the Inner Sect

After calming the future “Fire Cloud Mouse,” Lin Jing stepped into the alchemy room inside his cave residence.

The walls of the room were lined with millennium-old cold stones to regulate the temperature, while an old alchemy stove sat in the center. Below the stove, a blazing fire connected to the earth's depths. Apart from that, the room was barren.

“This cave... feels a bit ancient,” Lin Jing muttered.

“Elder Shen said this was the best available cave. Is that true...”

Lin Jing activated the Fire Control Technique jade slip and absorbed its methods into his mind.

With his previous experience creating the Thousand Changes Illusion Technique, he found the basic fire-controlling spell relatively simple to grasp.

He formed a hand seal, and on his first try, successfully used his Qi to draw the earthfire upward. However, the flames nearly scorched the nearby Pine Leaf Mouse, which leapt aside, its fur standing on end.

“Chirp!”

“Sorry about that! It’s my first time; I’m still getting the hang of it,” Lin Jing apologized. “But if you learned fire techniques, you wouldn’t have to fear fire anymore.”

The Pine Leaf Mouse protested—did he think it was that foolish?

“Alright, it won’t happen again. A little practice, and mastering the entry-level Fire Control Technique should be no problem. Let’s take a break and explore outside,” Lin Jing said, trying to console it.

Outside the cave, Lin Jing first headed to the Harvest Pavilion branch on Danxia Peak.

Unlike the outer sect's Harvest Pavilion, which sold basic spiritual crops like Immortal Beans and Spiritual Beans, the offerings here were of a higher tier.

“Fire Cloud Chili? The Beast Taming Sect doesn’t have that.”

Unfortunately, Lin Jing’s desired Fire Cloud Chili was not in stock.

As they left the pavilion, the Pine Leaf Mouse perched on his shoulder looked noticeably relieved—no spicy food today. But its relief was short-lived.

Lin Jing had left Danxia Peak and climbed to the highest peak of the Beast Taming Sect: the Inner Sect Mission Hall.

The missions here were far more advanced than those in the Outer Sect. Most revolved around slaying monsters and demons. Even Senior Brother Ouyang’s undercover mission in the Demon Sect was likely issued here.

“I’d like to post a mission: ‘Search for Fire Cloud Chili Seeds,’” Lin Jing announced as he inserted his request among the sea of high-level missions.

“A new face?”

The one in charge of the Mission Hall wasn’t a disciple but rather a Foundation Establishment bird demon resembling a magpie. It was one of the elders’ spirit pets, tasked with managing the hall.

Legend had it that this magpie would sing a “Good Luck Song” whenever a disciple left for a mission, wishing them safety. Whenever someone failed to return, it was said to weep bitterly.

“Senior, I am Lin Jing, a newly promoted Inner Sect disciple from Danxia Peak,” Lin Jing introduced himself respectfully.

“Hmm, based on the timing, the Inner Sect trials just ended,” the magpie demon nodded. “Posting a mission requires one Spirit Stone. What’s your mission’s reward?”

“Spirit Stones,” Lin Jing replied. While the storage pouch he’d acquired from the Snow Plains Cultivator didn’t contain anything valuable, it did hold basic currency like Spirit Stones. The rest, such as beast blood, had been gifted to his senior brothers and sisters. The remaining Spirit Stones could now fund the Pine Leaf Mouse’s chili indulgence.

After completing the task, Lin Jing left the Mission Hall satisfied. On the way back to his cave, he stopped in front of the Pill Tower on Danxia Peak.

The Pill Tower was essentially Danxia Peak’s exclusive Scripture Pavilion. Unlike the main Scripture Pavilion, which stored various techniques, the Pill Tower housed countless pill recipes for Danxia Peak disciples to study.

“Now that I have the alchemy room and the Fire Control Technique, the best way to learn alchemy is through hands-on practice. Let’s grab a couple of basic pill recipes,” Lin Jing told the sullen Pine Leaf Mouse on his shoulder.

“Chirp...” It wasn’t interested in eating chilies.

“Relax, I won’t force you. Once Elder Black Bear experiments with it and proves it safe, I’ll handle the fire-breathing myself,” Lin Jing assured the mouse, feeling he was too kind to let his pet suffer.

Upon entering the Pill Tower, Lin Jing found its guardian wasn’t human either—it was a Foundation Establishment rabbit demon with red eyes.

“Are you the newly admitted Lin Jing from Danxia Peak?” the rabbit demon asked.

“Senior recognizes me?”

“My Beast Tamer is Elder Shen, and she informed me about you. She mentioned that if you came to the Pill Tower, I should help you select suitable pill recipes for practice. But I didn’t expect you so soon,” the rabbit demon said. “Have you memorized the Encyclopedia of Flora and Fauna? Have you mastered the Fire Control Technique? And you’re already looking for pill recipes?”

Lin Jing nodded. “I’ve completed memorizing the flora encyclopedia and have grasped the basics of the Fire Control Technique.”

“As expected of a genius valued by Elder Shen,” the rabbit demon nodded approvingly. “The first floor contains basic pill recipes suitable for the Qi Refining stage and first-tier pills. The second floor holds more advanced recipes, such as various types of Foundation Establishment Pills, which require contribution points to access.”

“I’ll stick to selecting two from the first floor,” Lin Jing replied.

“Go ahead and choose. I’ll offer my advice afterward,” the rabbit demon said, rolling a piece of tobacco and sitting back leisurely to smoke.

Lin Jing stood silently for a moment. A smoking rabbit demon?

He covered the Pine Leaf Mouse’s curious eyes and started perusing the first floor’s pill recipes.

Replenishing Pill... Lin Jing had consumed plenty of these before. They restored spiritual energy and were universally applicable across all cultivation realms, though their recipes varied by grade.

Beast Strength Pill, which temporarily granted the physical strength of a fierce beast to the user.

Fasting Pill...

Huh? Contraceptive Pill?

Lin Jing paused, spotting something familiar. Wasn’t there a “Contraceptive Technique” as well?

Next to it were other recipes, including Arousal Powder and Hypnotic Pill.

Lin Jing’s mouth twitched. If he didn’t know better, he’d think he’d wandered into the Harmony Sect instead of the Beast Taming Sect.

“Senior, I’ve made my selection,” Lin Jing said after a while, bringing two pill recipes to the rabbit demon.

“The Replenishing Pill... A solid beginner’s choice. Perfect for practice,” the rabbit demon said. Then it frowned. “But why the Hypnotic Pill? What do you need that for?”

“I recommend the Beast Strength Pill instead. Your Pine Leaf Mouse is too fragile, and as a Beast Tamer, so are you. A Beast Strength Pill can grant you the strength of a tiger or elephant during battle!”

“More importantly... Beast Strength Pills sell much better!”

“The Hypnotic Pill induces hallucinations, blurring the line between illusion and reality for a time,” Lin Jing explained. “I’m currently studying Illusionary Path techniques, so I thought ingesting a few myself might provide some inspiration.”

“I see. That makes sense. Choose as you wish,” the rabbit demon conceded.

Returning to the cave with the recipes, Lin Jing flopped onto his bed, leaving the Pine Leaf Mouse to sort through the storage pouches.

Exhausted from his recent efforts and overuse of his mental energy, Lin Jing finally took some time to rest.

In his dream, he saw himself swallowing a Golden Core while the Pine Leaf Mouse cut open the path to ascension with a single leaf. Together, they became the only cultivators in the Ancient Celestial Kingdom to ascend successfully to the Immortal Realm.

But before they could explore the Immortal Realm, they were knocked unconscious and bound, dragged off to mine in some celestial cave...

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 41: Bloodline Evolution
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 41: Bloodline Evolution

Nightmares were just illusions... Over the following days, Lin Jing diligently practiced the Fire Control Technique, specifically tailoring his efforts to master the pill-refining methods for Replenishing Pills and Hypnotic Pills.

As for the Pine Leaf Mouse, it had exhausted its resources during the Cavern Heaven expedition and was eager to replenish its “ammunition.” However, under Lin Jing’s firm persuasion, it reluctantly dedicated most of its time to cultivation.

Lin Jing believed that with no pressing matters at hand, focusing on cultivation was of utmost importance.

One day, a celestial crane landed outside Lin Jing’s cave residence.

“Inner Sect disciple Lin Jing, the mission you posted in the Mission Hall has been completed,” it announced.

Lin Jing, in the middle of his fire-controlling practice, stepped outside, thanked the crane, and hurried to the Mission Hall with the Pine Leaf Mouse in tow.

Inside, apart from the magpie demon managing the hall, only one other person was present, browsing the mission board.

The individual, clad in white, exuded an air of weakness and fatigue even from behind. Lin Jing found the figure strikingly familiar.

“Junior Brother Lin Jing?”

The man turned around, and both instantly recognized each other.

“I heard you joined the Inner Sect. I had planned to visit you when I had time, but I didn’t expect us to meet here,” Senior Brother Ouyang said with a smile.

“Are you here to accept a mission, Senior Brother?” Lin Jing asked.

“Yes,” Ouyang replied. “I just completed three missions and plan to take on two more. I’m competing for this month’s mission king title. Red’er and I are about to enter the Foundation Establishment stage, so we need to save enough resources. I hope to request Elder Shen to refine two high-quality Foundation Establishment Pills for us.”

“What brings you here, Junior Brother?”

“The mission I posted has just been completed,” Lin Jing explained. Suddenly, a thought struck him. “Senior Brother, could it be that you’re the one who completed the ‘Search for Fire Cloud Chili Seeds’ mission?”

“Was that your task?” Ouyang’s expression shifted. “Now I understand why the reward was so stingy—” He quickly corrected himself. “I mean, what a coincidence!”

He pulled out a pouch of spirit stones and added, “Junior Brother, I can’t accept this payment. Let’s treat the chili seeds as a gift to celebrate your promotion to the Inner Sect.”

“I can’t let you do that.” Lin Jing accepted the spirit stones but added, “However, your kind intentions are difficult to refuse. I will humbly accept the seeds as a token of Senior Brother’s goodwill.”

Watching this exchange, the magpie demon remarked dryly, “Stop arguing and take the chili seeds already. Complete the mission and move on.”

In the end, Lin Jing received the chili seeds, while Senior Brother Ouyang accepted a mission to investigate the disappearance of beasts outside the sect.

Before leaving, the two endured a rendition of the magpie’s “Good Luck Song.”

Outside the Mission Hall, Ouyang turned to Lin Jing and asked discreetly, “Junior Brother, I’ve heard rumors of a Cavern Heaven light appearing near the sect, coupled with the mysterious nature of this year’s Inner Sect trials. Tell me—did you encounter a Cavern Heaven during the trials?”

Lin Jing paused, then nodded. “Senior Brother, you’re correct.”

“As I thought!” Ouyang’s face lit with envy. “What was it like inside the Cavern Heaven? I’ve always dreamed of experiencing it.”

“You’ll get your chance someday,” Lin Jing assured him. “With your potential, being chosen by a Cavern Heaven is only a matter of time.”

“That’s true,” Ouyang said confidently. “When that day comes, we’ll revitalize the Beast Taming Sect together as brothers!”

Ouyang’s gaze shifted to the Pine Leaf Mouse perched on Lin Jing’s shoulder, meditating in its Daoist robes. “Since you’re now an Inner Sect disciple and a special one at that, with the experience of entering a Cavern Heaven, you should consider applying for a Life-Bound Beast Space Artifact from the sect. Such artifacts allow your contracted beast to cultivate more efficiently within a dedicated space.”

“With your current status—not yet a True Disciple but equivalent to one—you could get it with approval from a Golden Core Elder.”

The Pine Leaf Mouse opened its eyes. A space artifact?

“Senior Brother makes a valid point,” Lin Jing mused. “I’ve only just joined the Inner Sect and have been preoccupied with alchemy studies these past few days. I’ll speak to Elder Mo about it when I have time.”

If the sect was encouraging him to study at the academy, it only made sense to carry a sect-approved artifact as a representative.

“Learning alchemy, are you? That’s excellent,” Ouyang said. “The sect has various pills that enhance a beast’s bloodline. Once you master alchemy, you can refine them yourself. It’s much more convenient.”

“Unlike me—I’m stuck in Cloud Gate Peak, where all they teach is combat.”

“By the way, you probably know this, but let me remind you anyway,” Ouyang said earnestly. “You’re a Longevity Body, so don’t rush your Foundation Establishment. The same goes for your mouse. I’m sure Elder Mo has mentioned this to you.”

“Most likely, the Pine Leaf Mouse will establish its foundation before you. The Foundation Establishment process is the best opportunity to elevate its bloodline. If its bloodline can evolve, that would be ideal.”

“We’re working hard on missions now to gather enough resources for Red’er to attempt a Bloodline Evolution during her Foundation Establishment.”

“But... as far as I know, the sect doesn’t have any records of a Pine Leaf Mouse advancing to a ‘King-Level Bloodline.’” Ouyang frowned.

“Thank you for the reminder, Senior Brother. We’ll think of something. There’s still plenty of time,” Lin Jing replied.

Before the decline of the demon race, there was no distinction between spiritual and demonic beasts. Back then, all beasts were classified by the demons into several bloodline tiers.

Each tier represented an immense gap, akin to entirely different species.

Beasts without the potential for sentience were classified as Mortal Bloodline, remaining ordinary beasts.

Those with cultivation aptitude were classified as Spirit Bloodline. Within this tier, weaker beasts typically remained in the Qi Refining Stage, while stronger ones could achieve Foundation Establishment. The elites among them might even form Monster Cores and rise to prominence as formidable monsters.

However, Spirit Bloodlines usually reached their limit at the Monster Core Stage unless extraordinary circumstances allowed them to evolve into King Bloodlines. Such an evolution enabled them to transcend into the ranks of Monster Kings, equivalent to Nascent Soul human cultivators.

Lin Jing glanced at the Pine Leaf Mouse. Although it had acquired a rare divine ability, its bloodline remained at the Spirit level, with little difference in constitution compared to other Pine Leaf Mice.

If possible, he hoped to elevate the Pine Leaf Mouse’s bloodline to “King Level” during its Foundation Establishment, granting it the potential of a Monster King. The Glazed Immortal Eyes ability would also become far more powerful.

Unfortunately, the Beast Taming Sect had no precedent for a Pine Leaf Mouse advancing to a King Bloodline. If he wanted to achieve this, Lin Jing would have to explore the mutated spiritual plants derived from the Glazed Immortal Eyes and his Longevity Blood for opportunities.

By integrating these resources through alchemy, he aimed to refine a custom Foundation Establishment Pill tailored specifically for the Pine Leaf Mouse.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 42: The Squirrel Leaps to Tiandu
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 42: The Squirrel Leaps to Tiandu

“Let’s count on you to become the first Pine Leaf Monster King of our sect! Junior Brother, are you heading to see Elder Mo now?”

“If you are, we can go together. I also have something to ask Elder Mo for assistance with.”

“Sure,” Lin Jing agreed, deciding that there was no time like the present to inquire about obtaining a Beast Space Artifact.

The Pine Leaf Mouse, upon hearing this, immediately perked up. It didn’t care about becoming a Monster King, but the allure of a space artifact was irresistible.

“Alright, let’s go. On the way to Lotus Peak, I’ll show you some of the inner sect’s scenic spots,” Ouyang offered.

“Are we walking there, Senior Brother? When can we ride a celestial crane? Do we have to wait until we become True Disciples?” Lin Jing asked casually.

“Riding those cranes isn’t free! You need Spirit Stones. Even errand disciples can ride them if they pay. Without Spirit Stones, even True Disciples can forget about it—the crane clan is under the sect master’s wife’s protection!”

Lin Jing already knew this but wanted to see if Ouyang was the type to splurge on luxuries. He certainly wouldn’t mind hitching a ride if he could avoid the trek.

---

On the suspended bridges shrouded in mist, Ouyang introduced Lin Jing to the sights along the way.

“Over there is the Nine Dragons Waterfall.”

Lin Jing looked toward the roaring cascade. It thundered down the green cliffs like nine white dragons tumbling from the heavens, creating a magnificent spectacle.

“The Nine Dragons Peak is known for its unique Dragonblood Carp. If one manages to leap over the waterfall using only their own strength during the Qi Refining stage, they can succeed in establishing their foundation and transform into a fish-dragon,” Ouyang explained.

“My visit to Elder Mo today is related to the pet beast I hope to contract after my Foundation Establishment...”

---

“See that purple mist rising in the distance? That’s Purple Cloud Spring.”

“What’s it for?” Lin Jing asked, curious, as he hadn’t come across it in any texts.

“It’s a hot spring that many female disciples love to use for bathing. Don’t wander there—it’s a matter of life and death,” Ouyang warned, grinning.

---

“That’s the Hundred Step Cloud Ladder...”

---

“We’re nearing Lotus Peak now. Oh, look—there’s the ancestor of your Pine Leaf Mouse.”

As they passed Cloud Cultivation Peak, Ouyang pointed out a peculiar rock formation.

The Pine Leaf Mouse glared at him, its expression accusing: "You with those thick brows and big eyes—are you insulting squirrels now?"

“Look carefully. He’s not mocking you,” Lin Jing said, turning his gaze toward the peak. On a modest summit stood a strange rock resembling a Pine Leaf Mouse, its tail extended as though attempting to leap across the immense gorge and ascend the towering Tiandu Mountain, one of the Beast Taming Sect’s main peaks.

“That’s truly your ancestor,” Lin Jing confirmed.

The Pine Leaf Mouse tilted its head, confused: "???"

Lin Jing elaborated, “Didn’t they teach you spiritual beast history in the sect? Especially about your own lineage?”

“Not in such detail...” Ouyang interjected from the side.

Lin Jing continued, explaining to the Pine Leaf Mouse, “Long ago, your species wasn’t part of the Beast Taming Sect. The Pine Leaf Mice were an ordinary species, with only a slim chance of awakening intelligence.”

“Facing harsh environments and numerous predators, one Pine Leaf Mouse sought a way out for its tribe. It secretly climbed to the Beast Taming Sect, hoping to seek immortality and protection for its kin.”

“There, it met an immortal on Tiandu Peak. The immortal gave it a challenge: if it could leap from Cloud Cultivation Peak to Tiandu Peak, he would teach it his arts.”

“Your ancestor began practicing daily, attempting to leap to the high peak and earn the immortal’s guidance.”

“But legend says it never succeeded. Eventually, it turned into stone, forever frozen in its jumping posture.”

“Although it failed the trial, the immortal admired its unwavering determination and took the Pine Leaf Mouse tribe under his wing. He elevated their bloodline, laying the foundation for their future status as one of the sect’s five foundational spiritual beasts.”

Lin Jing had read this account in the sect’s records. That so-called “immortal” was likely one of the Beast Taming Sect’s ancestors—not a true immortal, but close enough in the eyes of a Pine Leaf Mouse.

Hearing this tale, the Pine Leaf Mouse was momentarily stunned.

“Junior Brother, you’re impressively well-read,” Ouyang praised. “You must have spent a lot of time in the library during your time in the outer sect.”

“I did. I enjoy reading—it’s a good way to learn about the world,” Lin Jing replied. “Read ten thousand books, and you’ll fear no journey across the land.”

“True, but here’s something you probably didn’t know,” Ouyang teased.

“What’s that?”

“The immortal on Tiandu Peak wasn’t a human—it was one of the sect’s guardian deities, and it’s still here!”

“I mentioned before that starting with the second sect master, the Beast Taming Sect abandoned the old ways of enslaving spirit beasts. Disciples began forming equal partnerships with their beasts.”

“The second sect master loved making friends. That ‘immortal’ was one of his closest companions during his lifetime.”

“When the second sect master passed away, they made a pact—not a Beast Taming contract—to guard the sect for ten thousand years, aiding it in times of crisis. In return, the sect gifted Tiandu Peak, one of its most spiritually abundant mountains, as a place of cultivation.”

“Today, only the current sect master, the fifth, is at the Nascent Soul stage. The other Nascent Soul elders are actually the pets of the third and fourth sect masters. These monster kings have longer lifespans than human cultivators due to their unique races.”

“But the guardian deity on Tiandu Peak is the only one who remains without a Beast Taming contract. It has never intervened in any crisis, spending all its time cultivating in seclusion. Most disciples—True Disciples included—don’t even know of its existence.”

“So, it’s essentially a guest elder,” Lin Jing surmised.

This Beast Taming Sect truly felt like a “demon den.” Nearly all Nascent Soul-level beings were monster kings. It was both impressive and terrifying—he definitely needed to visit the Hidden Dragon Academy.

Wait, if only the sect master was at the Nascent Soul stage, wouldn’t that mean the one protecting him in the academy might be a monster king?!

“Senior Brother Ouyang, you’re quite knowledgeable yourself,” Lin Jing complimented, brushing aside the sect’s oddities for now.

“Not at all. What I wanted to share is that the guardian deity once made a promise to the Pine Leaf Mouse tribe,” Ouyang revealed.

“What kind of promise?”

“If a descendant of the Pine Leaf Mouse can inherit its ancestor’s determination and successfully leap from Cloud Cultivation Peak to Tiandu Peak, the promise still holds—it will teach them its arts.”

“However, over the years, the sect has seen many Foundation Establishment Pine Leaf Mice try and fail. The skies above Tiandu Peak have a strong suppression aura, making a normal leap impossible.”

“Eventually, the promise was dismissed as unrealistic. The sect master suspects the guardian deity was never serious about teaching and simply set an impossible challenge.”

“That said, you can still try—who knows, you might succeed. The difficulty supposedly scales with the cultivator’s level, so anyone can attempt it, regardless of their strength.”

As Ouyang finished, they watched in shock as the Pine Leaf Mouse pulled out a large red leaf from its storage pouch and soared into the sky toward Tiandu Peak using Leaf Flight.

Ouyang was dumbfounded. “Your Pine Leaf Mouse can fly on a leaf?!”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 43: True Disciple!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 43: True Disciple!

Hearing Senior Brother Ouyang’s story, the Pine Leaf Mouse immediately wanted to test the difficulty of leaping to Tiandu Peak.

Under normal circumstances, such a leap was impossible for it. As a spirit beast not known for explosive power, even without the suppressive aura between the peaks, its current capabilities—boosted by a Beast Strength Pill—still wouldn’t suffice.

But jumping wasn’t its only option; it had Leaf Flight!

With a swish, the Pine Leaf Mouse shot into the sky, riding a red leaf.

Ouyang was stunned, his mouth agape.

However, it wasn’t long before the Pine Leaf Mouse felt an overwhelming pressure, as though the gravity around it had increased severalfold. The sheer weight made controlling the leaf feel like trying to maneuver dozens of leaves at once—impossible.

Halfway to Tiandu Peak, the Pine Leaf Mouse lost control and began plummeting.

“Senior Brother Ouyang, catch it!” Lin Jing called out.

“That scared me! For a moment, I thought it was really going to make it. Seriously, is this even fair?”

“It’s a genius,” Lin Jing explained.

“Senior Brother, you don’t sound very encouraging. Are you afraid it might actually succeed?”

The Pine Leaf Mouse dusted itself off and turned its determined gaze toward Tiandu Peak, clearly ready to try again. It felt compelled to accomplish what its ancestor couldn’t.

“What? Of course not! That wasn’t a leap—it was cheating!” Ouyang protested.

“Flying counts as leaping,” Lin Jing countered. “It seems that Nascent Soul ancestor really doesn’t want to pass on their teachings.”

He tugged the Pine Leaf Mouse away. "Forget it; even cheating won’t work if you can’t make it halfway."

The Pine Leaf Mouse’s flight speed rivaled the sword-riding techniques of prodigies from Heavenly Sword Gate, yet it still couldn’t conquer the peak. The challenge was simply too daunting.

“I told you so,” Ouyang said, shaking his head. “Alright, let’s head to Elder Mo.”

---

Lotus Peak, one of the three main peaks of the Beast Taming Sect, mirrored Tiandu Peak as a place for semi-retired elders like Elder Mo. The last main peak served as the sect master’s residence and the hub for conducting sect-wide affairs.

By the time Lin Jing and Ouyang arrived, Elder Mo was already waiting, aware of their visit.

“What brings the three of you here?” Elder Mo asked, eyeing Lin Jing, Ouyang, and the Pine Leaf Mouse. None of them seemed the type to make trouble-free requests.

“Elder Mo, I’ve decided. Once I break through in the Beast Taming Technique and condense my second drop of blood contract, I plan to contract a Heavenfeather Crane, just like the sect master!” Ouyang announced. “And... I want a male one.”

“That’s called a rooster crane,” Elder Mo corrected with a dismissive wave. “Fine. I’ll speak to their clan on your behalf.”

“Thank you, Elder!” Ouyang bowed gratefully. With this, he’d have his Foundation Establishment pet beast ready, along with an easy means of flying for missions.

“And you?” Elder Mo turned to Lin Jing. “You’re not here just to ask for a fire pet to assist with your alchemy, are you?”

“No, Elder Mo,” Lin Jing replied with a slight smile, the Pine Leaf Mouse clutching its Daoist robe.

“We’ve given the matter of the Hidden Dragon Academy a lot of thought and realized it’s a valuable opportunity to hone our skills and learn new techniques to benefit the sect.”

“However, we also recognize that the academy will likely be filled with prodigies from other sects. Competing with them at our current level might be difficult.”

“So, I was wondering... You once mentioned that as an Outer Sect disciple, I would receive Inner Sect-level treatment, and as an Inner Sect disciple, I would receive privileges akin to a True Disciple. Could I request the Life-Bound Beast Space Artifact, which only True Disciples are entitled to, as well as the sect’s True Disciple Beast Taming Techniques?”

Beside him, Ouyang gave a discreet thumbs-up. Lin Jing’s phrasing was masterful—asking for top-tier resources under the guise of preparing to better serve the sect.

Elder Mo pondered this for a moment. “If you’re heading to the Hidden Dragon Academy, you’ll need to prepare thoroughly. Even our sect master, armed with all the sect’s True Disciple techniques, was beaten black and blue when he attended the academy unprepared. Going in without sufficient preparation is out of the question.”

“However, you’re not technically a True Disciple. To grant you a Life-Bound Beast Space Artifact, I’ll need to convene with the elders and discuss it. Wait for my message.”

“As for the True Disciple Beast Taming Techniques, it’s not that I’m unwilling to teach you—it’s just that they all require advancing the Beast Taming Technique to the Foundation Establishment stage first.”

Lin Jing nodded. “Having the Life-Bound Beast Space Artifact will be enough.”

“Let me think... While you can’t learn the techniques yet, you can begin preparing for them.”

Elder Mo continued, “The Beast Taming Sect has three major True Disciple Beast Taming Techniques and ten Core Beast Taming Techniques.”

“The three major techniques are:

1. Fusion Secret Art – Allows a contracted beast to merge with its tamer, transforming into a ‘half-demon’ state for a massive combat boost.”

Ouyang’s eyes gleamed. This would let him and Red’er fight even more closely together.

2. Immortal Rebirth – If a contracted beast dies in battle, as long as the blood contract remains intact, the tamer can resurrect it. However, each resurrection causes a significant loss in cultivation for both the tamer and the beast, severely draining their vitality.

3. Beast-Weaponization – Enables the tamer to transform their contracted beast into living artifacts, such as armor or weapons, to assist in combat.

“Each technique has specific requirements: Fusion demands a strong physique, Immortal Rebirth requires a resilient spirit and soul, and Beast-Weaponization hinges on high levels of spiritual energy and refined Beast Taming Qi. If you have a preference, start training accordingly.”

“As for the ten Core Beast Taming Techniques, they’re available in the Inner Sect’s Scripture Pavilion. Each one enhances pets of a particular element—gold, wood, water, fire, earth, wind, thunder, ice, yin, or yang—temporarily raising their cultivation level.”

“With proper mastery, it’s not difficult to boost your beast by a minor realm during battle.”

“These techniques form the foundation of our sect and were developed by the third sect master, who refined the original Beast Taming Technique to create them.”

After Elder Mo finished, the Pine Leaf Mouse tugged at Lin Jing’s sleeve.

It had no interest in fusing or becoming a weapon. Immortal Rebirth, however, sounded appealing—it wanted more lives.

Lin Jing smirked and communicated mentally, “You’ve gotten picky now, huh? Fine. When we encounter a strong enemy, I’ll run, and you can stall them. I’ll revive you later.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 44: The Black Chili Pepper
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 44: The Black Chili Pepper

The Beast Taming Sect possessed three major inheritance techniques and ten core secret techniques, all of which Lin Jing was already somewhat familiar with. In addition, the sect had dozens of ordinary beast-taming spells, most of them created by the third-generation sect master.

The first generation founded the sect, the second reformed it, and the third created its techniques—each generation of sect masters was incredibly talented.

However, Lin Jing had no intention of cultivating the ten core techniques for now. As someone with the Longevity Body, his cultivation level was still too low to keep up with his beast companion. Even if he learned them, the improvement for the Pine Leaf Mouse would be minimal. Instead of investing his energy there, it would be more worthwhile to study mechanization techniques or alchemy, both of which would better enhance both himself and the Pine Leaf Mouse.

As for the three major inheritance techniques, those were non-negotiable. Whether it was the Fusion Secret Art or Beast-Weaponization, both significantly boosted a cultivator’s strength.

In particular, the Beast-Weaponization technique reminded Lin Jing of a certain mythical figure—Yang Jian summoning the Howling Celestial Dog. In literary terms, it was often described as "summoning" the dog. Similarly, once Lin Jing mastered Beast-Weaponization, he could “summon the Pine Leaf Mouse!”

“Elder Mo, after I return, I will focus on tempering my body, honing my soul, and refining my qi,” Lin Jing said.

“So, you want to learn it all!” Elder Mo remarked. “No matter, you’ve got plenty of time ahead of you, and sooner or later, you’ll have to learn everything.”

“Since you both seem to have nothing else to discuss, you should hurry back and train. I’ve got work to do as well,” Elder Mo continued.

Elder Mo paused before replying, “Has it offended you recently...? Testing it on yourselves could be dangerous, but I’ll have it seek you out later.”

“It’s been through a lot, even if it hasn’t done much good, so remember to leave me a piece of it alive.”

---

After their visit with Elder Mo, Lin Jing and Senior Brother Ouyang parted ways—one returning to his cave, the other leaving the sect to carry out the "investigate missing beasts" mission he had just accepted.

On his way back, Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse passed by the spot known as the Leap of Tiandu. The Pine Leaf Mouse was still fixated on its desire to leap over the clouds.

Completing a feat that even its ancestors had failed to achieve…

If it succeeded, it would earn its own page in the family annals!

“With just your physical strength, it’s impossible unless you awaken a beast king's bloodline. I told you to practice the Flying Leaf Technique, but you didn’t listen. Regretting it now, aren’t you?”

Though the Pine Leaf Mouse’s Crimson Flying Leaf Technique was indeed formidable, it relied heavily on the mystique of the red flying leaves. Its ability to control the leaves depended on the strength of its mental power, which had room for improvement. If the Pine Leaf Mouse had trained regularly, increasing its mental strength, its speed and control over the flying leaves would undoubtedly improve, making such a leap feasible.

“Squeak…” The Pine Leaf Mouse shed its cumbersome robes, signaling its resolve to start practicing immediately.

“Since there’s pressure at that spot, when we return, I’ll use Thousand Transformations Illusions to simulate a beast king’s aura and help you train your mental strength,” Lin Jing said with a smile.

“But before that, let’s head back and mature the Fire Cloud Pepper seeds!”

---

The inner peaks’ Spirit Qi was indeed far richer than that of the outer peaks. Whether in terms of cultivation speed or the growth rate of spiritual plants, the inner peaks were leagues ahead. Lin Jing was particularly relieved that Elder Yu hadn’t cut off his access to the outer sect dining hall despite his promotion to the Inner Sect. Meals continued to be delivered daily by Heavenfeather Cranes, significantly lightening the burden on both Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse.

Time flew by, and the Fire Cloud Pepper, a first-rank Malignant Plant, was successfully matured by the Pine Leaf Mouse. However, the yield was low—each pepper plant produced only twenty fiery red peppers.

“Spiritual plants already turn fierce upon fusion; can you imagine what a malignant plant will become after fusion?” Black Bear Monster arrived as promised, bringing along the same ninth-stage Qi Refining white mouse it had used previously. Upon seeing the vibrant red peppers, the white mouse fainted before even taking a bite.

Opposite him, Lin Jing addressed Elder Black Bear. “As extremes meet, I believe malignant plants could very well transform into spiritual plants.”

“Go ahead, Xiao Song, use your Glazed Immortal Eyes,” Lin Jing instructed the Pine Leaf Mouse. Facing the pile of Fire Cloud Peppers, the mouse took a deep breath, removed its cooling bamboo-leaf blindfold, and activated its Glazed Immortal Eyes!

Most of the fiery red peppers disappeared, replaced by… a pitch-black pepper.

The eerie color made Elder Black Bear cast a skeptical glance at Lin Jing. This is what you call a spiritual plant? It’s blacker than me!

Luckily, it had the foresight to bring the white mouse. Shaking the mouse awake, it pointed to the black pepper. “Little white, see that black pepper? Eat it. If you survive, I’ll reward you with a big opportunity!”

The white mouse, trembling with fear, couldn’t resist the coercion of a Monster Core Stage beast. It reluctantly crawled toward the black pepper, shaking all the way.

“Don’t worry! It’s just a mutated first-rank Malignant Plant. You’re at the ninth stage of Qi Refining—what’s there to fear? Regular peppers only produce mundane flames, weaker than a commoner’s cooking fire. Even if it mutated, it shouldn’t be that strong!” the Black Bear Monster reassured it.

Encouraged, the white mouse mustered up its courage, shut its eyes, and swallowed the black pepper in one gulp.

The cave grew silent.

Lin Jing, the Pine Leaf Mouse, and the Black Bear Monster all stared intently at the white mouse, waiting for its reaction.

The next moment, the white mouse turned to the Black Bear Monster, opened its mouth, and startled it. But nothing came out.

“Ha! A failed mutation?” The Black Bear Monster laughed, relieved. “Looks like even rare beast abilities can fail sometimes.”

Before it could laugh further, its expression shifted dramatically. It felt a scorching heat in its soul. As it quickly inspected itself, it found black flames burning within—flames that didn’t harm the body but ignited the soul, intangible yet terrifying.

“Nether Ghostfire! The magic flame that burns souls, used by Nether Hall demon cultivators!” it cried, hurriedly extinguishing the flames. Fear filled its eyes. “This is the godforsaken Death Pepper!”

“Destroy it! Destroy it all now!” it exclaimed, pulling up the pepper plants and burning the remaining Fire Cloud Peppers. “You two better not mess with this again! This is ghostfire, the core technique of Nether Hall! If the Demon-Slaying Bureau mistakes us for colluding with demon cultivators, we’ll all be in trouble! Our Beast Taming Sect doesn’t exactly have a stellar reputation with them already.”

Watching the Black Bear Monster’s panic, Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse were stunned. Ghostfire?

“Elder Black Bear, can this ghostfire be used for alchemy?” Lin Jing asked curiously.

“Alchemy? Are you planning to use it for the living or the dead?” the Black Bear Monster glared. “I’m begging you, don’t cultivate this thing anymore! Even if we aren’t demon cultivators, getting caught with it will cause a lot of problems. Unless you’re planning to infiltrate the demon sects yourself.”

“You want special flames for alchemy, right? I’ll find you a Beast Flame to use instead. Please, don’t grow this thing again,” it pleaded earnestly. “This isn’t just a malignant plant—it’s a demonic one! Using it would make us look like demon cultivators.”

Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse nodded obediently. If it was that dangerous, they wouldn’t grow it anymore. Absolutely not.

The Black Bear Monster looked at the still-alive white mouse and declared, “Not bad. I hereby appoint you as the personal test subject of Lin Jing, the future holy son of the Beast Taming Sect. Quite the opportunity, isn’t it?”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 45: The Natal Treasure
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 45: The Natal Treasure

A future Holy Son of the Beast Taming Sect with a test subject child under his command—hearing this, the white mouse spirit froze momentarily.

It thought deeply about the proposition.

After two trial experiences, though terrified at first, it realized neither had caused lasting harm to it. The only reason it had been so frightened was the ominous demeanor of this great monster.

Looking back, during the first test, it had transformed into a bone dragon, frightening the great monster instead. In the second, it spat out an invisible black flame that similarly scared the great monster.

Reflecting on these abilities, the white mouse spirit found them incredibly powerful. If becoming a test subject child meant gaining such capabilities, perhaps it wasn’t such a bad deal!

Just as it was about to agree, believing this to be a great opportunity, a sudden pang struck its heart. The black chili pepper hadn’t been spicy when it entered, just bitterly unpleasant. But after a few seconds, an overwhelming heat surged through its body, as though its soul were being roasted by ghostfire. The white mouse spirit convulsed on the spot, its eyes bloodshot, and dark red tears—possibly blood—streamed from its eyes.

“Sssss!” It desperately called for water. Its steps grew unsteady, as if an invisible weight dragged it down, and even its form seemed distorted by the fiery pain.

Seeing this, Elder Black Bear took a cautious step back. “I knew it! Your creations can’t possibly have no side effects on the eater. Look, you’ve got your test subject bawling from the spiciness.”

“But your water flow settings are too limited. Its pressure might be ineffective,” Lin Jing stopped it and instead produced a more advanced mechanical bamboo turtle.

The bamboo turtle crawled forward at a deliberate pace, and a gentle stream of water for nourishing spiritual plants spouted from its nozzle. The white mouse immediately opened its mouth wide to drink.

It took a long while before the white mouse finally recovered. Dazed, it glanced at the Black Bear Monster, signaling that this opportunity might be too overwhelming for it to handle.

Its life was short; it just wanted to go home.

“Spineless!” The Black Bear Monster snorted and blew the white mouse away with a gust of black wind. “Go back and think it over. I’ll give you another chance to decide next time.”

After sending the white mouse off, the Black Bear Monster patted Lin Jing’s shoulder. “Forget about the chili peppers. I’ll find you a suitable beast flame for alchemy.”

With that, it turned and left.

Lin Jing glanced at the Pine Leaf Mouse. “When will your Immortal Eyes come with an identification function?”

The Pine Leaf Mouse said nothing, instead pulling out its red leaves, determined to practice the Flying Leaf Technique and secure its place in the family annals.

“Fine.” Since ghostfire alchemy was prohibited, Lin Jing decided to start with mundane flames. Though the success rate would be low, the worst-case scenario was wasting some basic materials. But seeing the Pine Leaf Mouse's resolve to train, he chose to accompany it instead.

The Thousand Transformations Illusions was equally crucial. To enhance the Pine Leaf Mouse's control over the leaves, they needed to fortify its mental strength. Without delay, Lin Jing transformed into one of the Pine Leaf Mouse's ancestral predators—a towering, ten-meter-tall giant weasel!

Also known as Huang Dashian or Yellow Weasel!

Draped in massive, flowing yellow robes, it emanated an aura that blanketed the skies with overwhelming authority.

“Ah!!!” Upon completion of the transformation, the Pine Leaf Mouse froze momentarily before emitting a sharp, piercing shriek akin to ultrasonic waves. The sound disrupted its brain, leaving it unable to think or react.

Flooded with chaotic thoughts, its bloodline stirred up ancient fears of predators that had been preserved over countless generations. Overwhelmed by this primal terror, the Pine Leaf Mouse instinctively reached into its storage pouch and pulled out a mechanical bamboo rifle, aiming it at Lin Jing.

“What the—what are you doing?!” Lin Jing was startled, quickly using their contract to suppress the Pine Leaf Mouse's actions, fearing it might actually shoot him.

He wasn’t as resilient as those Snow Plains Cultivators; even a single Spiritual Bean could kill him.

Once restrained by the contract, the Pine Leaf Mouse's eyes cleared somewhat. Lin Jing sighed. “I only unleashed a Foundation Establishment level aura, and you’re already scared stiff? Your willpower is weak! If it had been the aura of a beast king targeting you, wouldn’t you have fainted on the spot?”

“Squeak squeak squeak!” The Pine Leaf Mouse unloaded the rifle's magazine, insisting that it hadn’t been scared. Its mouth, at least, remained resolute.

“Regardless, your weapon is confiscated,” Lin Jing declared. “Relying on firepower to face your fears won’t help temper your resolve.”

“Only when you can remain calm and composed, even unarmed, in the face of absolute terror, will you succeed.”

“For the next few days, whether you’re sleeping or training, I’ll occasionally transform into a monster to appear before you.”

Watching Lin Jing take away its storage bag, the Pine Leaf Mouse’s face fell.

---

Over the next few days, Lin Jing focused on practicing the Illusionary Path, frequently transforming into creatures that were ancestral predators of the Pine Leaf Mouse, such as the Hawk of Erlang, Nine-Tailed Demon Fox, and Xiangliu Serpent Demon, to frighten it.

The constant pressure left the Pine Leaf Mouse restless. It couldn’t sleep well, eat comfortably, or relax at all. Even when it reminded itself the illusions weren’t real, it still couldn’t shake its fear.

Unlike previous untargeted illusions, Lin Jing's transformations deliberately exploited creatures imprinted as predators in the Pine Leaf Mouse’s bloodline. Concentrating the illusion's aura directly on it without dissipating the pressure naturally made it impossible for the Pine Leaf Mouse to stay calm.

Yet the training was undeniably effective. After several days, the Pine Leaf Mouse could remain undisturbed under the aura of a Foundation Establishment beast. Thankfully, the Thousand Transformations Illusions had not reached its limits. Monster Core Stage and Beast King Stage auras could still escalate the training.

Finally, just as the Pine Leaf Mouse’s nerves were nearing their breaking point, Elder Mo arrived with good news.

“Come with me to the Treasure Pavilion and retrieve your natal beast space treasure!” Elder Mo had successfully secured a natal space treasure for Lin Jing, even though he wasn’t yet an inheritance disciple.

“Natal treasures can be bonded with a drop of blood, making them impossible for others to steal. They possess some growth potential, improving slightly as the user’s cultivation increases.”

“The beast space treasure not only functions as a storage pouch but also allows living creatures to enter. However, it’s limited to the contracted beasts of the treasure's owner.”

“The space within also offers superior cultivation conditions compared to the inner peaks, further accelerating training!”

Elder Mo guided Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse to the peak of the Treasure Pavilion, which housed most of the sect’s treasures. Unlike the spiritual artifacts and magic tools meant for Qi Refining and Foundation Establishment disciples, treasures of this caliber were typically used by Golden Core cultivators. Only disciples who had become inheritance disciples and taken a Golden Core elder as their master had access to these treasures.

At the pavilion’s top floor, Elder Mo gestured toward seven treasures floating on glowing pedestals. “These are the seven remaining beast space treasures of the sect. Each has a unique appearance. Choose the one you like, and I’ll teach you how to refine it.”

“Thank you, Elder Mo,” Lin Jing expressed his gratitude before examining the treasures with the Pine Leaf Mouse. As Elder Mo had said, the treasures varied in form but seemed to be crafted using similar methods with different materials.

The types included: a cloth pouch, a stone ring, a jade bracelet, a meditation staff, a precious orb, a gourd, and a scroll.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 46: Refinement
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 46: Refinement

Lin Jing scanned the available options: a cloth pouch, a stone ring, a jade bracelet, a treasure orb, a gourd, and a scroll. They all seemed relatively normal—except for the peculiar addition of a meditation staff.

Isn’t this a Buddhist artifact?

What is it doing in the Beast Taming Sect?

“You’re wondering why the sect crafted a spatial treasure into a meditation staff?” Elder Mo asked, noting Lin Jing’s expression.

“Yes,” Lin Jing nodded.

“It’s tied to an old story, one that isn’t recorded officially but passed down through oral tradition.” Elder Mo chuckled, picking up the meditation staff and holding it in his hand.

“During the time of the second sect master, a fearsome demon beast wreaked havoc in the Great Wasteland Domain. This beast was a rare kind, possessing extraordinary abilities that made it nearly impossible to kill—even by those a level or two higher in cultivation.”

“No cultivator in the Great Wasteland could handle it.”

“Sealed, not slain.”

“To this day, the demon remains imprisoned underground in the Great Wasteland, restrained by four Buddhist pagodas connected to dragon veins. Each pagoda is now under the care of one of the four top sects in the region.”

“After the battle, the second sect master became friends with the monk. For a time, many of the Beast Taming Sect’s spatial treasures were crafted as Buddhist artifacts. This meditation staff has been here for quite some time.”

“But over the years, its unusual design has made it unpopular—few inheritance disciples choose it, even if they know the story behind it.”

“So that’s how it is,” Lin Jing mused. “I never thought our Beast Taming Sect would share the responsibility of guarding a demon-suppressing pagoda.”

After all, the sect was more like a monster sect than a traditional one.

“You’re not planning to pick the meditation staff, are you?” Elder Mo asked.

“No,” Lin Jing replied decisively. “It doesn’t match my elegant and free-spirited demeanor.”

Elder Mo was momentarily speechless. He hadn’t expected Lin Jing to be so self-absorbed, but upon closer inspection, he admitted the young man did bear a resemblance to his own youthful handsomeness. Perhaps that explained the confidence.

“Elder, I choose this one,” Lin Jing said, pointing to the red gourd.

The Pine Leaf Mouse was startled. It clearly preferred the cloth pouch, which seemed more convenient to carry. Lin Jing ignored it.

The space treasure was meant to house the Pine Leaf Mouse, so its portability wasn’t Lin Jing’s concern. Besides, all treasures of this level could change size, making them easy to carry in any form.

“Other spatial treasures have distinct signs of post-crafting refinement. This one, however, seems more like an innate creation. If I’m not mistaken, it’s the Suspended Gourd, a rare plant that naturally contains space within,” Lin Jing observed.

“Not bad—you’ve done your homework,” Elder Mo said approvingly. “The other six treasures are crafted from various spatial materials, but this gourd underwent minimal modification, making it the most primitive of the lot.”

“But that’s not the only reason you chose it, is it?”

“Correct. It’s a plant, and the Pine Leaf Mouse has a wood attribute. They should have good synergy,” Lin Jing explained. “Elder, if I manage to acquire more Suspended Gourds in the future, do you think the Pine Leaf Mouse could use its Glazed Immortal Eyes to fuse them?”

Compared to the other six treasures, the gourd’s plant-based properties were the key factor for Lin Jing.

“That’s certainly possible,” Elder Mo confirmed. “However, Suspended Gourds are incredibly rare. This one was a fortunate find by an ancestor. Even if you encounter more, affording them before achieving significant cultivation progress will be nearly impossible.”

“At least it’s something to aim for,” Lin Jing said with a smile. “Elder, I’ll go with the gourd.”

“That meditation staff is intriguing, though. Once I reach the Foundation Establishment stage and contract a second beast, could I apply for another spatial treasure? It wouldn’t do to have multiple beasts sharing one space, right?”

“Get lost!” Elder Mo finally lost his temper. These treasures represented the sect’s heritage, not some common stockpile! “I don’t have the authority to grant you another one. If you want that privilege, aim to become the young sect master. Then you can pick whatever you like—even let each beast have two treasures!”

After choosing the Suspended Gourd as his natal beast space treasure, Lin Jing followed Elder Mo’s instructions to refine it by imbuing it with a drop of his vital blood.

Once refined, Lin Jing found the treasure lived up to its status as a tool for Golden Core cultivators. The internal space was far larger than his former courtyard at the sect’s outer peaks.

Finally, the Pine Leaf Mouse wouldn’t have to struggle with storage limitations. Previously, its two storage pouches could only hold rare synthesis items, while sacks of ordinary spiritual rice and beans had to pile up in the courtyard. Now, those space issues were resolved.

Although the Suspended Gourd was Lin Jing’s natal treasure, it served as the Pine Leaf Mouse’s residence. This arrangement didn’t hinder the mouse from embracing its hoarding tendencies.

“Squeak!” The Pine Leaf Mouse, seeing Lin Jing fiddling with the gourd, eagerly reached out to touch it.

“Answer me if you dare,” Lin Jing teased, aiming the gourd at the bewildered mouse. Before it could react, the mouse was sucked into the space treasure with a swift whoosh.

Though confused at first, the Pine Leaf Mouse quickly began exploring the new space. Taking a deep breath, its eyes sparkled with excitement.

So spacious!

Looking around, it found the gourd’s interior vast and expansive. The boundaries were shrouded in green spiritual mist, perfect for cultivation. Standing amidst the mist, the Pine Leaf Mouse decided it was time to renovate the place into its new den.

“Squeak…” It began counting on its fingers, planning areas for sleeping, storing food, training, more storage, eating, and… even more storage.

“How does it feel?” Elder Mo asked Lin Jing with a smile. “Now that you have a natal beast space treasure, doesn’t it seem like you’re truly a beast-taming cultivator?”

“It certainly does,” Lin Jing replied, rotating the gourd in his hands. It shrank slightly and hung from his waist. The red gourd complemented his white robes, adding to his refined and handsome appearance.

“It suits you,” Elder Mo noted. “When you’re traveling, if a demonic cultivator mistakes you for an ordinary lone wanderer and tries to rob you, imagine their shock when you summon your beast companions. Truly satisfying.”

Elder Mo’s nostalgic grin revealed his past experiences. After all, one of the greatest joys of being a Beast Taming Sect disciple was the ability to gang up on enemies.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 47: The Demon-Suppressing Pagoda
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 47: The Demon-Suppressing Pagoda

After receiving his natal beast space treasure, Lin Jing once again expressed his gratitude to Elder Mo and pledged his undying loyalty to the Beast Taming Sect. “Alive, I am a disciple of the sect; dead, I am its ghost. Even if reincarnated into the animal realm, I will still serve the sect as a beast.” His fervent declaration left Elder Mo momentarily speechless.

“Alright, I understand your devotion,” Elder Mo sighed. “But for someone with the Longevity Body, you’re surprisingly lacking in composure. Let me send you back.”

“Thank you, Elder Mo, but we can return on our own,” Lin Jing replied. He had plans to pass through Cloud Cultivation Peak and test the Pine Leaf Mouse's progress from their recent training.

“It’s no trouble,” Elder Mo said, waving his hand. With the power of a Golden Core cultivator, transporting them was as effortless as the Black Bear Monster blowing away the white mouse earlier.

“In that case, could you drop us off at Cloud Cultivation Peak?” Lin Jing asked.

“Cloud Cultivation Peak?” Elder Mo raised an eyebrow. “What business do you have there… Oh, is this about the Squirrel’s Leap of Tiandu?”

Realizing something, he continued, “I had nearly forgotten about that spot. Do you know the history of the Pine Leaf Mouse clan and their connection to the sect’s guardian deity?”

“Yes,” Lin Jing nodded. “The last time Senior Brother Ouyang and I passed through Cloud Cultivation Peak, the Pine Leaf Mouse attempted the leap but failed. It has since undergone rigorous training, and I wanted to bring it back for another attempt.”

With a wave of his hand, Lin Jing felt a dizzying shift in his vision. The next moment, they were standing at the Squirrel’s Leap of Tiandu, with Elder Mo by their side. “Go ahead,” he gestured, seemingly intent on observing.

Unbothered by the audience, Lin Jing summoned the Pine Leaf Mouse, which was busy building its new den in the Suspended Gourd.

“After all that training, can you earn your place in the family annals? Let’s try again!” Lin Jing encouraged.

“Squeak?” The Pine Leaf Mouse blinked, surveying its surroundings. Realizing it had returned to Cloud Cultivation Peak, its emerald eyes lit up with renewed confidence. After enduring relentless mental training against terrifying illusions, its once fragile willpower had been honed to a state of tranquility.

Feeling invigorated by its growth, the Pine Leaf Mouse launched itself into action. With a swift motion, it summoned its largest red leaf, leaping onto it. A flash of red light followed as it soared skyward. Elder Mo, observing calmly, nodded to himself. He already knew the mouse’s capabilities well.

“A leap is still a leap, I suppose…” he muttered, unlike Senior Brother Ouyang, who had underestimated the mouse's abilities.

Unfortunately, despite reaching slightly higher than before, the Pine Leaf Mouse was again overwhelmed by the formidable pressure in mid-air. Teeth gritted, it tumbled back down, spinning along with the red leaf like a flaming wheel.

“Elder Mo! Catch it!” Lin Jing called out in alarm. Elder Mo casually intercepted the dazed mouse, placing it beside him.

Another failure. The Pine Leaf Mouse, groaning and retching, looked as though it had been struck by lightning—not because it couldn’t achieve the leap, but because it dreaded the continuation of its grueling training.

“Failed again?” Elder Mo shook his head. “Even for a rare beast, this challenge seems insurmountable. What was the second sect master’s old friend thinking? Is it truly possible for a Pine Leaf Mouse to succeed?”

“Perhaps it will, once it advances to Foundation Establishment and evolves a Beast King bloodline,” Lin Jing suggested. “Or, Elder Mo, does the sect have a specialized location for mental strength training?”

Lin Jing had noticed diminishing returns from their illusion-based training. The Pine Leaf Mouse, aware they were only illusions, had begun developing resistance after repeated exposure. Illusion techniques were most effective in their first use.

“A place to temper the mind...” Elder Mo paused before nodding. “There is one—one I mentioned to you before.”

“The Demon-Suppressing Pagoda?” Lin Jing guessed.

“Precisely,” Elder Mo confirmed. “To thank the four sects for their assistance and guardianship of the pagoda, the Buddhist guest elder from the Demon-Slaying Bureau left behind a unique ability.”

“The pagoda consists of multiple levels, each with formations that draw upon the sealed demon’s power to keep it perpetually weakened, unable to cultivate or resist.”

“This demonic power is converted into demonic pressure on specific levels, creating a formidable test for those who enter.”

“But it isn’t the pressure itself that trains the mind. Surviving it activates the Buddhist elder’s gift—a sacred light that bathes the soul, significantly strengthening mental fortitude.”

“This was intended to help the four sects cultivate more demon-slaying cultivators, arming them with the courage and resolve to face demonic threats.”

The prospect intrigued Lin Jing. “Elder Mo, may the Pine Leaf Mouse and I enter?”

“Absolutely not!” Elder Mo's beard twitched. “The demonic pressure in the pagoda is not something a Qi Refining cultivator can endure. I’ve told you before—of the sect’s three major secret techniques, Immortal Rebirth requires extraordinary soul strength. Only disciples who have reached Foundation Establishment and become inheritance disciples are permitted to enter the pagoda to prepare for learning it.”

“If the mouse only faces Foundation Establishment-level pressure, it should manage,” Lin Jing reasoned. “And as for me, I should be fine as well.”

Lin Jing demonstrated by transforming into a towering Buddha-like figure, seated on a lotus throne. The majestic illusion exuded divine serenity, with 42 radiating beams of holy light connecting north and south, illuminating the entire peak.

“Elder Mo!” The Buddha’s eyes opened, and an oppressive aura descended upon Elder Mo, causing a bead of cold sweat to form on his forehead.

“I am a Buddha of the Celestial Realm, ambushed and forced to reincarnate in this world. I am but 50 Spirit Stones away from reopening the ascension path. Help me return, and I shall reward you greatly!” the Buddha’s voice boomed.
