Startled, Elder Mo hesitated, but the Pine Leaf Mouse’s giggling gave it away. He quickly realized, “This is your Illusionary Path technique from the Bodhi Cavern Heaven! Such realism—it rivals the aura of the sect’s ancestors.”

“Elder, try pressuring me,” Lin Jing invited. Elder Mo complied, releasing his Golden Core pressure. Yet Lin Jing’s illusion, reinforced by intricate Purple Qi, rendered the pressure ineffective, as if it fooled not only the observer but the caster as well.

“Unbelievable…” Elder Mo was stunned by the effectiveness of Lin Jing’s illusionary art. “If that’s the case, you two truly could enter the pagoda to strengthen your minds. Ordinary demonic pressure won’t affect you.”

Reverting to his normal form, Lin Jing grinned. “That’s wonderful! By the way, Elder, this demon has been sealed for so long… Are you sure our entry won’t destabilize the seal?”

“Absolutely not.” Elder Mo was firm.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 48: The Demon
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 48: The Demon

At Lin Jing’s request, Elder Mo, observing their ability to withstand pressure with ease, decided to take them to the Demon-Suppressing Pagoda. The Buddhist elder’s blessing was a rare opportunity for disciples of the four sects to strengthen their mental resilience. Gaining access to it during the Qi Refining stage would provide unparalleled benefits for their future Foundation Establishment.

“Come with me. Due to the unique nature of our sect’s techniques and the second sect master’s friendship with the Buddhist elder, Beast Taming Sect disciples can enter the pagoda with their contracted beasts to jointly endure the demonic pressure and receive the blessing of Buddhist light.”

“Use your illusions to shield yourself and protect your beast. Both of you can benefit from this chance!” Elder Mo said, sweeping Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse with him toward the Clear Pool Peak of the sect. On the way, Lin Jing mused about the privileges of connections. While disciples of other sects braved the tower alone, the Beast Taming Sect allowed its members to bring their beasts along—this was excellent!

The Demon-Suppressing Pagoda, despite its name, had a concealed entrance within a serene pool, a precaution to prevent anyone from attempting to release the sealed demon.

“Who goes there?” A deep voice resounded as the surface of the pool rippled.

As Lin Jing had expected, the tower’s guardian was not human but a Beast King, emerging partially from the water. Elder Mo immediately bowed respectfully, “Greetings, Ancestor.”

“You again,” the guardian grumbled.

“Ancestor, I have brought a disciple to train in the Demon-Suppressing Pagoda.”

Lin Jing quickly saluted.

“A Qi Refining brat?” The tortoise shot a disapproving glance at Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse, then turned to Elder Mo. “Are you trying to turn them into fools? If their minds collapse under the demonic pressure, what will you do when they lose their sanity?”

“Ancestor, Lin Jing is one of our sect’s rising stars, even chosen by the Cavern Heaven. I am confident in his ability to handle this challenge,” Elder Mo reassured.

“Fine, do as you wish,” the tortoise muttered, closing its eyes. It had lived long enough to see three sect masters pass and had grown indifferent to most matters. Stationed beside the pagoda, its demeanor was thoroughly "Buddhist."

With the guardian’s consent, Elder Mo turned to Lin Jing. “One thing I forgot to mention: the pagoda doesn’t allow physical entry. Everything here is an astral projection, including your mind, soul, and intent. Physical items and treasures can’t be brought in, and high-level cultivators are barred from entering to prevent sabotage of the seal.”

“Your spells will work, but not your tools or weapons. Is that sufficient?”

Hearing this, the Pine Leaf Mouse looked alarmed at the thought of leaving behind its precious red leaves and explosives. Lin Jing, however, nodded. “It’s enough.”

As long as Thousand Transformations Illusions could be used, he could resist the demonic pressure.

“Once ready, leave a spiritual mark on this stele,” the guardian instructed. “The mark will allow us to monitor your progress. If it flashes, it indicates danger, and I’ll immediately summon your consciousness back to avoid getting lost in the pagoda.”

Lin Jing pressed his hand on the ancient stone stele, which glowed as it etched a blurry image of a small squirrel.

“The pagoda connects the four sects. Should you encounter disciples from other sects, avoid unnecessary conflict,” the guardian added. With that, the pagoda entrance opened within the pool, rippling to reveal the inverted reflection of a black pagoda.

Before Lin Jing could process it, his vision blurred as though he were drowning. When he opened his eyes again, he stood before a massive gate surrounded by black water.

The intricately carved door bore ancient symbols and runes, glowing faintly with a mysterious blue light that exuded an air of profundity and danger.

“This must be the pagoda’s interior,” Lin Jing said, reaching instinctively for his mechanical bamboo rifle, only to find his storage pouch reduced to an ordinary bag. The Suspended Gourd was similarly inaccessible.

The Pine Leaf Mouse sighed. It had already endured training without its tools in the sect, but even here, its storage pouch was sealed—this was getting unbearable. Nudging Lin Jing, it gestured for him to assume the Buddha form for protection.

“The Buddha form requires too many effects and drains too much qi,” Lin Jing replied. “A simpler Buddhist monk will do.”

With that, he used Illusionary Path Qi to craft a familiar image from his memory: a monk clad in a luxurious red-and-yellow kasaya, wearing a golden monk’s hat. One hand gripped a golden staff, and the other held a violet-gold alms bowl. His white eyebrows and beard gave him an austere, solemn presence.

“Amitabha, I am Fa Hai,” Lin Jing declared, prompting the Pine Leaf Mouse to blink in confusion. It couldn’t fathom why Lin Jing’s mind was filled with such peculiar characters.

“Let’s go,” Lin Jing said, holding the staff and beads as he pushed open the pagoda door.

He had considered transforming into Tang Monk, but that figure was famously hapless against demons and would likely falter under the pressure. To master the essence of illusion, one had to first convince oneself.

Fa Hai, the demon-slaying monk, was the perfect choice!

As the door creaked open, a demonic gale swept out, its pressure equivalent to that of a Foundation Establishment demon, almost tangible in its force. It slammed into Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse like a storm.

However, with the protection of illusion qi, Lin Jing remained unaffected. Even the Pine Leaf Mouse, after its rigorous training, resisted the pressure with sheer willpower.

Step by step, they advanced into the pagoda, where the black energy from the walls condensed into the shape of a terrifying serpent skull. Its fangs glistened as it hissed menacingly.

The hissing turned streams of water into countless venomous snakes that slithered up Lin Jing’s body. The Pine Leaf Mouse, perched on his shoulder, grew increasingly uneasy. Knowing the snakes were illusions created by the pressure, it still couldn’t help but feel its scalp tingle, especially without its trusty tools. It began climbing higher—toward Lin Jing’s bald, illusionary monk head.

“Tricks of the trade,” Lin Jing muttered, ignoring the snakes as he pressed forward. In his illusionary field, he had fully assumed the identity of a high monk. The demonic pressure couldn’t affect his mind, let alone create illusions powerful enough to harm him.

Seeing the Pine Leaf Mouse struggling, Lin Jing let out a cold snort. His monk persona unleashed an overwhelming wave of holy force, shattering the demonic energy and dissipating the illusions. The snakes vanished in an instant.

Deeper in the tower, the sealed demon roared. “Damn Buddhist aura! A bald donkey has come! Curse them! Curse them! I want to devour them!”

The demon paused, its thoughts spinning. “Wait… if this monk is so powerful, they might be able to break the seal. I must find a way to make them free me!” It schemed, its mind muddled by years of imprisonment, failing to realize it was being deceived by Lin Jing’s illusion.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 49: The Treasure
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 49: The Treasure

The demonic pressure on the first level of the Demon-Suppressing Pagoda had little effect on Lin Jing, who felt it wasn’t even as disorienting as a Bewilderment Pill. He walked toward the staircase leading to the second level, expecting the promised Buddhist light to descend and strengthen his mental fortitude.

However, despite waiting for a while, nothing happened. Instead, the walls of the floor began to emit stronger waves of demonic energy, causing Lin Jing to frown. The Pine Leaf Mouse, sensing something amiss, poked its head out from Lin Jing’s monk’s hat and nudged him. This wasn’t what Elder Mo had described—where was the fortuitous encounter? Could Lin Jing’s pessimistic prediction about trouble really be coming true?

Impossible…

Lin Jing hesitated and stepped back slightly, considering retreating. Perhaps it was better to cultivate steadily and progress one step at a time.

“Let’s go back,” he said.

The Pine Leaf Mouse nodded in agreement, and Lin Jing resolutely turned to leave, suppressing his curiosity. Unfortunately, just as he began to retreat, the demonic energy condensed into a small snake-like figure—a black eel that appeared so feeble it looked on the verge of death.

“Wait, great monk!” the black eel called out.

Lin Jing stopped mid-step, his face grim as he turned to look at it.

Lin Jing mulled this over and decided to probe. “It seems the seal on this tower may need reinforcing after all.”

“Great monk!” The black eel panicked. “This little demon knows its wrongs. Please give me a chance to repent!”

“The seal on this tower is still very strong! The fact that I could leak out even a sliver of consciousness came at an immense, painful, and irreversible cost. It poses no threat!”

“After being imprisoned here for years, I have genuinely repented. I beg the great monk to undo the Buddhist seal. I am willing to serve you as a demon servant, faithfully following you, and will turn over a new leaf.”

Lin Jing narrowed his eyes at the creature, letting a smug expression slowly spread across his face.

“Bold demon! Still trying to deceive me!” Lin Jing declared, his voice resonating with authority. “This does not demonstrate the sincerity of your repentance.”

The Pine Leaf Mouse, hidden in the hat, felt its heart race. Beast Tamer, what are you doing?

The eel hesitated but then gritted its teeth. “I understand. I will reveal the location of my treasure to prove my loyalty!”

Lin Jing’s interest piqued, though his face remained impassive. “Where is this treasure?”

“It lies at Tianyin Mountain, on the border between the Wasteland Domain and the Empty Domain. However, only I can open the entrance. If the great monk retrieves it, please return and accept me as your servant. I will dedicate everything I obtained from the Cavern Heaven as tribute!”

Before Lin Jing could respond, a golden light suddenly pierced through the walls of the pagoda, illuminating the entire floor. The eel screamed in agony as the Buddhist light condensed into a phantom figure—a handsome, young monk in white robes.

The monk surveyed the snake-like demon with a calm expression before turning to Lin Jing.

“You… you… you!” The eel was struck with terror at the sight of the monk, but the latter merely sighed. “After all these years, Undying Black Serpent, your senses have deteriorated to such an extent?”

“You expended so much effort to leak a fragment of consciousness, only to beg for help from a ‘great monk’ who is actually a Qi Refining cultivator using illusions?”

“If your former subordinates knew, they would laugh themselves to death.”

The monk waved his hand, dispersing the Illusionary Path Qi that surrounded Lin Jing. With the illusion dispelled, the once-mighty high monk was revealed as a youthful Beast Taming Sect disciple. The black eel rolled over in the pool, and the room fell into awkward silence.

“A Beast Taming Sect cultivator…”

“At Qi Refining…”

“ARRRGHHHH!” the eel exploded with rage, its fury uncontainable as it glared at Lin Jing. “Damn you, Beast Taming Sect! Damn you! Why is a beast tamer practicing illusion arts? Curse you!”

“Damn it! Damn it! Just you wait—when I get out, I will tear you limb from limb and obliterate your sect!” the eel howled before being purified by a beam of Buddhist light, leaving Lin Jing standing there, looking completely innocent.

Heaven help me, Lin Jing thought, I’m just an innocent bystander. All I did was talk for a bit—why so much hate?

“He was desperate because of your illusionary monk aura,” the white-robed monk explained. “This seal can only be broken by Buddhist techniques, and your illusion gave him hope. After all, you’re only the second ‘Buddhist cultivator’ to enter this tower in centuries.”

Lin Jing winced. Why, oh why, did I choose to transform into Fa Hai?

“Junior Lin Jing of the Beast Taming Sect greets Senior,” Lin Jing said, bowing deeply.

“No need to be nervous,” the monk replied. “For a Beast Taming Sect disciple to achieve such mastery of illusion arts, the outside world must have changed greatly. My consciousness was sealed here for this very moment.”

“Centuries ago, that serpent was a fearsome demon, fearless of heaven or earth. Yet now, to escape, it grovels before a Qi Refining cultivator. How pitiful.”

Lin Jing had no response. Who wouldn’t go mad after being sealed for thousands of years? Even the Monkey King was only trapped for 500.

“Junior, I have good news and bad news. Which would you like to hear first?” the monk asked, smiling faintly.

Lin Jing tensed, sensing something ominous. “Senior, I’ll take the bad news first.”

“The bad news is that my consciousness is tied to the seal. Unsealing my consciousness slightly weakens the tower’s overall structure, making the demon’s containment less effective.”

“This failsafe was meant to trigger far into the future when the demon could pose a genuine threat. But now, due to your illusion, we’ve all had a bit of a fright.”

Lin Jing’s face darkened. So the pagoda’s seal really is loosening?

“That’s decades away. There’s no need to worry. Simply report this truthfully to your sect. The demon’s abilities are unusual but not particularly powerful—it is easily suppressed, especially with the Demon-Slaying Bureau involved.”

“And the good news?” Lin Jing asked cautiously.

“The treasure the Undying Black Serpent mentioned—it is real. Long ago, it killed many sect prodigies and looted seeds of rare plants from a Cavern Heaven. These treasures may prove useful to your sect.”

“Thank you for the guidance, Senior,” Lin Jing said, relieved that it wasn’t all bad news.

“You entered the pagoda seeking the Buddhist light, did you not?” the monk said, smiling. “I cannot let you leave empty-handed. This light strengthens the soul. You have a karmic connection to Buddhism, so accept this boon as a token of our meeting.”

Without waiting for a response, the monk invoked a radiant shower of Buddhist light, enveloping Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 50: The Guardian of Tiandu
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 50: The Guardian of Tiandu

Under the radiant Buddhist light, Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse felt as though they were being reborn. At first, the sensation was blissful, but gradually, Lin Jing noticed something unusual. While his mental strength was undeniably strengthened, he couldn’t shake an inexplicable feeling that something else had changed.

Even after opening his eyes, he couldn’t identify the source of this strange sensation.

Outside the Demon-Suppressing Pagoda, Elder Mo stared intently at the evolving squirrel image on the stone stele.

“Stop staring!” the guardian ancestor Wang Batian barked. “He’s just a Qi Refining cultivator. Even if he manages to pass the first level, it’ll take him ages to adjust.”

“Ancestor, how about a bet?” Elder Mo grinned like a child with a scheme. “I bet he’ll quickly pass the first, second, third… all the way to the ninth level!”

The tortoise ancestor burst out laughing. “Have you gone mad? A Qi Refining brat withstand Nascent Soul-level demonic pressure? Back in my day, even we Beast Kings cowered under that weight!”

“Do you dare to bet?” Elder Mo asked, his confidence in Lin Jing unwavering after betting on him being a rare beast companion owner.

“Fine! Let’s bet!” The two turned their attention back to the stele and the pagoda’s reflection in the pool. Before they could discuss the stakes, the reflection suddenly trembled violently as if the entire pagoda was on the verge of collapse.

Moments later, Lin Jing’s astral projection returned to his body. Slowly, he stood up.

“Ancestor? Elder? What’s wrong?” Lin Jing asked, confused by their wide-eyed stares.

“What happened inside?” Elder Mo asked, his voice tight. Lin Jing had exited after just the first level, an extremely rare occurrence.

“Elder, I told you the seal on the pagoda is weakening!” Lin Jing said, his expression pained. “We barely entered before encountering a demon trying to escape!”

“What?!” Elder Mo and the tortoise ancestor exclaimed.

“Thankfully, the legendary Buddhist elder left a contingency behind. His sealed consciousness subdued the demon.”

The two visibly relaxed.

“Unfortunately, according to the elder, the seal will only hold for a few more decades before losing its effectiveness.”

Their faces darkened again.

“At least it’s not an immediate crisis,” Elder Mo tried to reassure himself. “The Demon-Slaying Bureau has plenty of experts. If we report this in advance, they’ll have someone ready when the time comes. Besides, after being sealed for so long, the demon must be significantly weakened.”

“Exactly. I’m not fighting that thing,” Wang Batian muttered.

“Additionally, I learned something potentially valuable,” Lin Jing continued. He then relayed the treasure cave information provided by the demon. After a brief exchange of glances, the two elders decided to convene a sect meeting to discuss the matter.

“For now, let’s seal the pagoda. It’s no longer suitable for training,” Elder Mo concluded, fearing Lin Jing might have been shaken by the experience. He promptly escorted Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse back to their quarters.

After Lin Jing left, the tortoise ancestor turned to Elder Mo. “Isn’t it strange that the seal loosened the moment he entered? Could that brat be a cursed Heaven’s Calamity Star?”

“Impossible!” Elder Mo retorted. “He has the Longevity Body, destined to outlive generations of ancestors. He’s just a Qi Refining cultivator—what could he do? The fact that he stayed composed and reported everything clearly shows his remarkable potential.”

“Longevity Body?!” The tortoise ancestor’s eyes widened. “That brat is going to outlive me?!”

Back at Danxia Peak, Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse sat at the entrance of their cave dwelling, taking deep breaths to calm their nerves.

“Let’s avoid places like that in the future,” Lin Jing muttered.

“How do you feel?” he asked the Pine Leaf Mouse about the Buddhist light’s effects. In response, the mouse pulled out one, two, three… until seventy red leaves floated into the air.

Lin Jing gasped at the sight of the dazzling display. “Next time you meet a sword cultivator, you can show off with your own Ten Thousand Blades Return to the Sect.”

Before the Buddhist light, the Pine Leaf Mouse, at Qi Refining seventh level, could only control fourteen leaves. Now, its mental strength was so formidable that it could command seventy with ease.

Even if the Buddhist light had some peculiar side effects, they were willing to overlook them!

Lin Jing himself noticed that constructing Purple Qi for his Illusionary Path techniques had become easier and more efficient.

“But if I had to name something strange…” Lin Jing mused, “the images of Buddhist figures like Fa Hai and Buddha have grown sharper in my mind, as if the ‘Buddhist essence’ has taken root.”

“Could the light have altered something deeper?” he wondered aloud. “I should find an expert to examine me.”

Shaking off the thought, he turned to the Pine Leaf Mouse. “Want to try the Leap of Tiandu again?”

Returning to Cloud Cultivation Peak, Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse, now fortified by the Buddhist light, prepared for another attempt.

This time, the Pine Leaf Mouse ascended effortlessly. Riding a single red leaf, it soared through the sky, pushing through the oppressive pressure with ease. Soon, it reached the summit and gazed down upon Tiandu Peak.

The view was breathtaking: an expansive mountaintop crowned by an ancient divine pine tree. Unlike ordinary pines, this one glowed with a crystalline emerald hue. Its trunk shimmered with spiritual energy, its branches golden-green, radiating an air of majesty and mystery.

The Pine Leaf Mouse froze, its gaze locked on the Tiandu Divine Pine. Then it noticed something alarming—the tree extended a branch toward it. The summit was devoid of other living creatures, making it clear that the guardian deity of Tiandu was not a beast but a sentient plant spirit—a pine tree!

It was over! The Pine Leaf Mouse quickly called for Lin Jing’s help. After all, it had eaten countless pine cones since birth. Who would have thought that the sect’s guardian deity’s natural enemy was actually me?

No wonder no Pine Leaf Mouse had ever succeeded in making this leap. My ancestor died unjustly!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 51: The Demonic Technique!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 51: The Demonic Technique!

At the summit of Tiandu Peak, the Pine Leaf Mouse trembled under the shade of the majestic golden-green Divine Pine. There was nothing more terrifying than realizing your food had developed intelligence—and far more power than you.

Even when faced with Lin Jing’s illusions of eagles and snakes, the mouse hadn’t felt this shaken.

The Pine Leaf Mouse’s paws twitched as it straightened its robe, pressed its palms together, and knelt in a bow. The Beast Tamer was right—cultivation must proceed one step at a time. Rushing ahead only leads to unpredictable disasters...

“Squeak... Pine Leaf Mouse of the Beast Taming Sect pays its respects to the sect’s ancestral deity!”

“I’m coming to help!” Lin Jing yelled as he scrambled up Tiandu Peak. Having received the Pine Leaf Mouse’s plea for help, he ignored the peak’s status as a restricted area for ordinary disciples. Since the mouse had reached the summit through legitimate means, Lin Jing figured there was no harm in following. Thankfully, the mountain’s suppressive aura didn’t seem to target humans.

Reaching the summit quickly, Lin Jing was stunned silent by the sight of the Divine Pine.

If his memory served correctly, before the Celestial Alliance Era, plants could indeed develop intelligence. However, during the fall of the Immortal Palace, the rules of the Celestial Continent were rewritten to eliminate rebellion. This change made it nearly impossible for vegetation to gain sentience. While there were many beasts capable of cultivation, intelligent plant spirits were now rarer than one in ten thousand.

And yet, the guardian deity of Tiandu Peak was a pine tree that had achieved sentience!

“Be at ease,” a neutral voice echoed across the mountain.

“I was once bound by a contract with your sect’s second sect master. I will not harm you.”

The Pine Leaf Mouse exhaled in relief, grateful that its food wouldn’t eat it in return.

“Honored ancestor, we came after hearing the tale of the Squirrel’s Leap of Tiandu,” Lin Jing explained, justifying their intrusion.

“A Pine Leaf Mouse…” The Divine Pine’s unseen gaze settled on the trembling mouse, making it shift uncomfortably.

“After all these years, a Pine Leaf Mouse with the potential of a Beast King has finally appeared. Your ancestor’s efforts have not been in vain.” The Divine Pine seemed reflective. “I will honor my promise to pass down teachings.”

“Hm?” The Divine Pine paused. “You possess a Longevity Body?”

The revelation startled Lin Jing. Despite wearing the pendant Elder Mo had given him to hide his physique, the Divine Pine had seen through it instantly.

“No wonder this Pine Leaf Mouse is so exceptional—it has signed a blood contract with a Longevity Body, altering its fate.”

The Divine Pine turned to the Pine Leaf Mouse. “What technique do you desire?”

The Pine Leaf Mouse froze. Was it really about to be taught?

Lost in thought, it glanced back at Lin Jing before turning back to the Divine Pine with resolve. It wanted... Longevity Arts!

As the Divine Pine had said, Lin Jing possessed a Longevity Body, but the Pine Leaf Mouse was not a long-lived species. Over time, it had grown used to cultivating alongside Lin Jing. To remain by his side, it begged the Divine Pine for a technique to extend its life.

But was such a technique possible?

“That is not difficult. I have a demonic technique that, if modified, can be granted to you,” the Divine Pine said.

The words sent shockwaves through Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse.

Demonic technique?!

“Honored ancestor... our sect is a righteous one,” Lin Jing ventured cautiously.

“The demonic nature applies to me,” the Divine Pine explained calmly. “The technique in question harvests the essence of vegetation to extend life. As a sentient plant, using it against my kind makes it a demonic technique. For humans or beasts, however, it’s just another method.”

Understanding dawned on Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse. At the same time, they gained a deeper understanding of Tiandu Peak’s guardian deity: a plant spirit, and from the perspective of its kind... a demonic cultivator.

No wonder the Divine Pine remained calm in the presence of a Pine Leaf Mouse that had eaten countless pine nuts—it had done far worse.

With a flash of green light, a technique name appeared in the Pine Leaf Mouse’s mind:

The Green Emperor’s Longevity Art!

Absorbed in the wonder of the technique, the Pine Leaf Mouse closed its eyes. The Green Emperor’s Longevity Art allowed the user to absorb spiritual energy and convert it into Green Emperor Qi, which could be used to control plants or drain their life essence to extend lifespan.

However, the art had a cap on the lifespan it could provide at each level. As one advanced, ordinary plants would no longer suffice—only high-grade spiritual plants or even celestial flora would do.

“The technique has been passed on. Do you have any other requests? If not, you should leave,” the Divine Pine said.

Lin Jing quickly bowed. “Honored ancestor, do you know of the Demon-Suppressing Pagoda and the Buddhist light within it?”

“We trained there and gained strength from the light, but it left us with an uneasy feeling.”

The Divine Pine fell silent briefly. “I am familiar with it and its creator.”

“You need not worry; the Buddhist light is harmless.”

“It is a manifestation of his Buddha Core Technique, designed to unlock potential in low-level cultivators. Its primary effect is granting recipients a minor affinity for Buddhist arts.”

“Back then, those monks entered the Ancient Celestial Kingdom intending to spread their teachings. When forbidden from doing so openly, they adopted this indirect method—offering ‘fortuitous opportunities’ to foster ties with Buddhism.”

“After exposure to the Buddhist light, practicing Buddhist techniques or spells will become significantly easier for you. That’s all. The monks hoped some cultivators would eventually switch to their faith.”

Lin Jing nodded in understanding. “So it’s a subtle recruitment tactic.”

“If you dislike Buddhism, I can remove the Buddhist affinity from you,” the Divine Pine offered.

Lin Jing clasped his hands. “Thank you, honored ancestor. Knowing it’s harmless is enough. Personally, I view Buddhism, Daoism, Confucianism, or even demonic arts as tools. I hold no special biases.”

As a modern individual, Lin Jing’s philosophy was simple: whether praying to Buddha or the Dao, as long as it didn’t cost money, he was open to all beliefs. Daoist techniques, Buddhist techniques, or even demonic techniques—if they got the job done, that was all that mattered.

“You’re not concerned about being subtly converted to Buddhism?” the Divine Pine asked.

“Honored ancestor, with my Longevity Body, I could outlast their attempts and ascend as a Buddha myself.”

The Divine Pine was silent.

“You may leave,” it finally said, deciding that this Longevity Body’s irreverence was too aggravating. In its time, it had created demonic techniques to cling to life, while this human could effortlessly transcend as a Buddha or Daoist. Truly infuriating.

Descending from Tiandu Peak, Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse were overjoyed with their gains. However, before they had gone far, they heard a mournful cry from the summit—a magpie’s dirge.

Many figures rushed toward the Mission Hall. Lin Jing stopped a fellow Inner Sect disciple to ask, “Senior Brother, what’s going on?”

“The magpie’s cry signals tragedy. An inner disciple encountered danger during a mission outside the sect. It’s said to be Senior Brother Ouyang Hao, the task fanatic. He’s been brought back, but his life is hanging by a thread!”

Hearing this, Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse’s faces fell.

Senior Brother Ouyang?!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 52: The Protector
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 52: The Protector

In the Inner Sect Mission Hall, Senior Brother Ouyang Hao and his fox spirit lay unconscious on a warm jade bed, their condition critical. Ouyang Hao’s body was covered in frost, his skin pale as snow, and his hair had turned into icy threads that emanated a chilling aura. His fox spirit companion, likewise, was entirely white, its fur frozen stiff as it lay comatose.

No matter how much the Mission Hall elder tried to nourish them with spiritual energy or call out to them, neither showed any response. Nearby, the magpie spirit responsible for issuing the mission was weeping bitterly, blaming itself for failing to bring good fortune to the disciples sent on external tasks.

---

“How is Senior Brother Ouyang?”

Many Inner Sect disciples crowded around, deeply concerned. Seeing his grave condition, they asked the magpie spirit anxiously, “Elder Magpie, what mission was Senior Brother Ouyang on? How could someone of his strength end up like this?”

Lin Jing also arrived at the hall and heard the magpie spirit’s response.

“The Great Wasteland Domain, Snow Moon City,” the magpie spirit began, anger flickering in its tear-filled eyes. “Our sect manages a Beast Valley near the city, where we rear many snow rabbits, a core industry of the sect.

“A few months ago, disciples in the valley reported snow rabbits mysteriously disappearing. Beyond that, many ice beasts in Snow Moon City have also vanished under strange circumstances. To investigate, the sect issued a mission.”

“Stealing our spiritual beasts and harming our disciples—this enmity cannot go unavenged!”

---

Examining Ouyang Hao, Elder Shen from Danxia Peak, an expert in alchemy and medicine, furrowed her brows deeply. Despite her best efforts, including feeding him and the fox spirit red elixirs, there was no improvement.

“This injury is unusual and appears to result from a strange technique. My spiritual energy cannot counteract it. If it weren’t for their abundant Yang energy, they’d already be frozen corpses,” Elder Shen said grimly.

At that moment, Elder Mo arrived, his urgency palpable. Even though Lin Jing had brought back critical intelligence from the Demon-Suppressing Pagoda, Elder Mo had temporarily set that matter aside to address Ouyang Hao’s plight.

“Elder Shen, is there no way to treat this frost injury?” Elder Mo asked, his face dark.

Elder Shen shook her head. “If I had more time, I might devise a solution. But in their current state, time is a luxury we don’t have.”

“Then I’ll go to the ancestor!” Elder Mo said, quickly taking Ouyang Hao and the fox spirit to seek aid from the guardian ancestor Wang Batian at Clear Pool Peak. With most of the sect’s elders accompanying the sect master on external missions, the tortoise ancestor was one of the few left in the sect.

The Inner Sect disciples, now witnessing the dire severity of Ouyang Hao’s injuries, dispersed in fear. The mysterious injury that even a Nascent Soul elder couldn’t cure left them deeply wary of the Snow Moon City investigation mission.

---

“We need to check on them,” Lin Jing said, worry etched on his face. Ouyang Hao was one of his closest friends in the sect, and he couldn’t sit idly by.

The Pine Leaf Mouse nodded, and together they headed to Clear Pool Peak, where the tortoise ancestor was also at a loss.

“This injury appears to be the work of demonic techniques,” Wang Batian said. “It’s similar to the Nether Ghostfire of the Netherworld Hall, which is notoriously difficult to heal with conventional methods. This frost may be a variation.”

Elder Mo, growing increasingly desperate, could only pace. Ouyang Hao was a disciple he had personally mentored and someone the sect master had great hopes for. He refused to see such talent perish.

“Honored ancestor, is there truly no way to save them?” Lin Jing asked, stepping forward.

“His survival is tenuous,” Wang Batian explained. “As a Primordial Yang Body, his Yang energy is counteracting the frost damage, and the fox spirit survives due to its bond with him and its own Yang-based cultivation. But the frost is spreading, and their condition won’t hold for long.”

Determined, Lin Jing approached Ouyang Hao and the fox spirit. Gently picking them up, he turned to Elder Mo. “Elder, please take me to Tiandu Peak. I wish to seek help from the guardian deity there.”

“What?” Elder Mo glanced at Lin Jing in surprise. “You succeeded in meeting that being?”

Lin Jing nodded firmly. Having just received guidance from the Divine Pine, he knew it might be his only hope, even if asking for help so soon was presumptuous.

“Very well!” Elder Mo, desperate for a solution, agreed. “Perhaps the guardian deity can provide aid.”

Even the tortoise ancestor was stunned. “That monster is willing to see people now? Not even successive sect masters could meet it easily…”

---

At Tiandu Peak, Lin Jing carried Ouyang Hao and ascended without resistance. Standing before the Divine Pine, he wasted no time in explaining.

“Honored Divine Pine, this is Ouyang Hao, a disciple of the current sect master. He was injured during a mission outside the sect. His wounds are beyond the abilities of our elders to heal. I beg you, please lend your aid.”

“I made an agreement with your second sect master to assist only in matters of life and death for the Beast Taming Sect. I am not obliged to save individual disciples,” the Divine Pine replied flatly.

“Squeak squeak squeak!” The Pine Leaf Mouse chimed in, pleading earnestly.

The Divine Pine, however, remained unmoved.

“I understand…” Lin Jing said after a moment, his voice resolute. “My most valuable possession is my Longevity Body. I am willing to trade blood from my Longevity Body for your assistance in saving them. Please, grant us this chance.”

The Divine Pine fell silent, then replied, “There is no need for such a sacrifice. Even I cannot fully heal them. This is no ordinary frost injury but the result of a poison technique. Such a poison is extremely rare and cannot be eradicated through cultivation alone. You must locate the poison’s source to treat them.”

Lin Jing frowned, sensing the enormity of the task. However, he couldn’t let Ouyang Hao die.

---

Returning to Cloud Cultivation Peak, Lin Jing descended with Ouyang Hao, meeting a concerned Elder Mo.

“What did the guardian deity say?” Elder Mo asked urgently.

“The Divine Pine cannot cure the poison but identified it as stemming from a rare toxic technique,” Lin Jing explained. “The only way to counter it is to find the poison’s origin.”

“A poison technique?” Elder Mo muttered. “Many in Snow Moon City cultivate ice-based techniques, but poison techniques…”

His face darkened. “Whoever is behind this is undoubtedly the culprit responsible for the disappearance of the snow beasts. Ouyang Hao must have uncovered a lead and was silenced. Curse them!”

Turning to Lin Jing, he saw the young disciple bowing solemnly.

“Elder, I request permission to take over Ouyang Hao’s mission. I will go to Snow Moon City, investigate the case, and find the poison source to save him.”

“Absolutely not!” Elder Mo rejected instantly. “The Enforcement Hall and the sect elders will handle this. You’re just a Qi Refining cultivator—it’s too dangerous. As a Longevity Body, you should focus on cultivating in safety!”

Lin Jing shook his head, his resolve unyielding. “Elder, Ouyang Hao once saved my life, rescuing me from the Yin Corpse Sect. He has helped me countless times in the sect. I cannot sit idly by.”

“I understand your concern for my safety, but some things must be done personally. If I ignored this, I would regret it forever.”

“And what meaning does longevity have if one’s life is filled with regrets?”

The Pine Leaf Mouse crossed its arms, its robe billowing as it stood firmly by Lin Jing’s side.

Elder Mo sighed, recognizing the determination in Lin Jing’s eyes. “You’re as stubborn as a mule. Very well. But I can only allow this because of the Divine Pine’s protection.”

“Protection?” Elder Mo blinked. “The Divine Pine agreed to aid you?”

Lin Jing nodded. “The Divine Pine promised to act as my protector during this mission. With its strength, we should be safe. It also said the poison is highly unusual, and even it would need the source to attempt a cure.”

Elder Mo stared at Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse, both radiating determination, and finally relented. “Very well. Go. Ouyang Hao is fortunate to have a friend like you”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 53: First Time Leaving the Sect!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 53: First Time Leaving the Sect!

The Longevity Body was leaving the sect, and no one could stop him. However, with the Divine Pine accompanying him as his protector, the elders were quite reassured about Lin Jing's safety.

Elder Mo handed over the information the sect had gathered about the mission, passing it to Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse as they packed their things.

"Snow Moon City is already on lockdown. That demonic cultivator should still be in the city," Elder Mo said.

"I suggest not launching a large-scale operation just yet," Lin Jing said as he accepted the report. "If the sect's Law Enforcement Hall and elders all mobilize, it will likely alert the enemy. The demonic cultivator might go into hiding and cease any further actions. It's better if I go alone. The sect just needs to ensure no one leaves Snow Moon City secretly."

"How do you plan to investigate?" Elder Mo asked.

"Several inner sect disciples went to Snow Moon City to investigate the 'beast disappearances,' but only Senior Brother Ouyang fell victim. As you said, he must have discovered something significant. I plan to start with his movements in the city," Lin Jing explained. "But considering how little we know, any clues might already be hard to find. At this point, we can only rely on the Divine Pine's abilities. Given its cultivation realm, it should be able to detect any anomalies immediately."

Lin Jing browsed through the information. Based on reports from Beast Valley in Snow Moon City, Senior Brother Ouyang had only visited three places after his arrival:

1. The City Lord's Manor.

3. Lotus Pavilion.

Senior Brother Ouyang went to the City Lord's Manor to gather more information about the spirit beast disappearances. Apart from the Beast Taming Sect issuing a mission, the City Lord's Manor had also posted a bounty.

Reportedly, the City Lord of Snow Moon City, Tang Qinghan, had personally received Ouyang Hao after learning that he was highly regarded by the Beast Taming Sect Master and might become a true disciple of the sect master.

"City Lord Tang Qinghan: early Golden Core stage, possesses the innate Ice Spirit Body, practices the 'Frost Soul Art,' and has ruled Snow Moon City for sixty years. The Tang family is deeply rooted in Snow Moon City, holding a dominant position but maintaining a stellar reputation and enjoying the love of the populace."

Continuing, Lin Jing read about Beast Valley, a property of the Beast Taming Sect, renowned for raising snow rabbits with exquisite meat. Using unique secret techniques, the sect raised these rabbits to supply the entire Great Wasteland Domain, making Beast Valley one of the sect's primary sources of income. The missing beasts that prompted Ouyang Hao's mission came from reports of snow rabbit disappearances there.

"Valley Master He Xu: late Foundation Establishment stage, third disciple of Elder Jiang, and an expert in nurturing pet beasts."

Lastly, there was Lotus Pavilion, the largest merchant association in the Ancient Celestial Kingdom, with operations extending even beyond to Qingzhou and Martial Emperor City. The Lotus Pavilion branch in Snow Moon City was small. Senior Brother Ouyang only visited the branch to purchase yang-nourishing pills and was warmly received by the branch master, thanks to an introduction from the City Lord.

"Lotus Pavilion Branch Master Hua Qiandao: late Foundation Establishment stage, possesses the innate Fire Spirit Body, practices the 'True Fire Lotus Scripture,' and has been the branch master here for ten years."

The most popular product of the Lotus Chamber of Commerce was medicinal pills.

"These three individuals are the most prominent figures Senior Brother Ouyang encountered in Snow Moon City. Let's rule out the Valley Master... As for the City Lord and Hua Qiandao, given their strength and status, it seems unlikely they'd engage in such petty and disgraceful acts. It appears Senior Brother Ouyang had additional movements we’re unaware of." Lin Jing pondered.

"The intelligence is indeed insufficient," Elder Mo sighed.

"No problem," Lin Jing said with a smile. "I have another plan. I’ll use the 'Thousand Transformations' technique to disguise myself as a snow rabbit, move into Beast Valley, and then transform into various rare ice spirit beasts to wander around. Since the culprit loves ice beasts, I'll bait them by becoming an ice beast myself."

Elder Mo was speechless. "..."

This was your real plan from the start, wasn’t it?!

Once the culprit appeared, the Divine Pine would handle it.

---

Although Lin Jing planned to go alone, the sect made adequate preparations. To ensure his journey was swift, they provided a Heavenfeather Crane for him and the Pine Leaf Mouse. The crane would enable him to reach Snow Moon City within half a day. The Divine Pine, however, would guard him from the shadows.

"This huge... package." The Heavenfeather Crane, a creature Lin Jing was already familiar with, turned out to be the very same crane with a unique accent that delivered resources throughout the sect. It had even reached the early Foundation Establishment stage.

Lin Jing retrieved the Pine Leaf Mouse and seated himself atop the crane. Watching Elder Mo see him off, he nodded.

"Return safely," Elder Mo said. "Elder Shen will look after Ouyang Hao's remains for now."

"Let's go. Please get me to Snow Moon City as quickly as possible," Lin Jing said with a deep breath, gazing at the distant clouds. This was his first time leaving the sect.

"You got it!!!" the crane bellowed as it soared skyward at an incredible speed. Lin Jing closed his eyes and began cultivating as they ascended into the clouds.

The crane flew faster and faster, and Lin Jing began to feel the chill in the air.

The Great Wasteland Domain bordered the Snow Plains, and several cities near the plains were perpetually blanketed in snow. Snow Moon City was one of them.

The recent incidents in Snow Moon City were possibly the work of demonic cultivators or Snow Plains cultivators.

"Damn!" The Heavenfeather Crane suddenly exclaimed, jolting Lin Jing from his meditation.

"What’s wrong, Brother Crane?" Lin Jing asked.

"Someone just zoomed past me at incredible speed!"

"So?" Lin Jing asked curiously.

"Do you think I’d let them outpace me?"

"You read my mind." With that, the crane surged forward at an even greater speed, leaving Lin Jing's lips quivering in the cold. Before long, a black dot appeared ahead—a white colt with dragon horns galloping through the sea of clouds. On its back lay a young man in black robes, sound asleep.

"How can someone sleep at that speed?" Lin Jing muttered incredulously.

As the Heavenfeather Crane closed in, the white colt glanced at it and silently picked up speed. Not to be outdone, the crane matched its pace. The intense turbulence finally woke the black-robed youth, who leaned over the cloud’s edge and vomited.

"Ugh…"

After emptying his stomach, the youth glanced around in confusion before noticing the Heavenfeather Crane and Lin Jing atop it.

"Brother, where are you rushing off to in such a hurry?" he asked.

Lin Jing responded, "Shouldn't I be asking you that?"

The black-robed youth nodded. "Fair point. I am indeed in a hurry. I'm from the Demon-Slaying Bureau. My name is Gao Sheng. I’ve heard rumors of demonic activity in Snow Moon City and came to investigate. If I’m late, more innocent people and cultivators might suffer. So, I can’t waste any time."

Lin Jing smirked. "Since when can someone at the ninth stage of Qi Refinement become a Demon-Slaying Guardian?"

He eyed the youth’s Foundation Establishment steed—a white dragon-horse—and couldn’t help but feel envious. Whose privileged child was this, coming out to "experience life"? Lin Jing’s gaze lingered wistfully on the magnificent "white dragon-horse."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 54: No Clear Truth
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 54: No Clear Truth

As the two conversed, a powerful aura surged ahead, and a silver-armored cultivator stopped them in their tracks.

"Snow Moon City is on lockdown. Entry is forbidden!"

Both the Heavenfeather Crane and the white dragon-horse halted. Gao Sheng took out a token and declared, "Gao Sheng of the Demon-Slaying Bureau, here to exterminate demonic forces in Snow Moon City."

Lin Jing also retrieved his token, adding, "Lin Jing of the Beast Taming Sect, here to investigate the beast disappearances."

The silver-armored cultivator looked at the two—riding Foundation Establishment mounts despite being only at the ninth and fourth levels of Qi Refinement—and was at a loss for words.

Gao Sheng glanced at Lin Jing. You're here for demon hunting too? You think I’m weak... but you’re only at the fourth level!

"Greetings. Your horse is truly magnificent," Lin Jing commented.

"This is a dragon-horse. Don’t get any ideas, you from the Beast Taming Sect," Gao Sheng replied defensively.

Lin Jing recognized the man from portraits: Tang Qinghan, the City Lord of Snow Moon City.

Lin Jing was momentarily stunned. He hadn't expected to receive the same treatment as Senior Brother Ouyang—personally greeted by a Golden Core cultivator!

"Young Master Gao… Why are you here? How is your esteemed father?" the City Lord asked warmly, ignoring Lin Jing entirely.

"City Lord Tang, let’s not confuse matters. I am Gao Sheng of the Demon-Slaying Bureau, not simply the son of the Gao family. I am here to solve this case and eliminate demons," Gao Sheng replied sternly. "This is my first case with the bureau, so I ask for your full cooperation."

"Snow Moon City will fully support the Demon-Slaying Bureau," the City Lord assured him. Only then did he turn to Lin Jing.

Lin Jing looked skyward, while the Heavenfeather Crane chimed in, "Beast Taming Sect! We’re from the Beast Taming Sect!"

"Oh, a disciple of the Beast Taming Sect… It is my dereliction of duty that your Senior Brother Ouyang Hao met his end in my city. I promise to find the culprit within a month and give your sect a proper explanation," the City Lord declared solemnly.

"Thank you, City Lord. We’re here to assist with the investigation," Lin Jing replied. "I am a disciple of Danxia Peak. I’ve studied toxicology under my master for over two decades. The sect's patriarch determined that Senior Brother Ouyang was poisoned, so I’ve been sent to investigate."

"Very well. Please, both of you, follow me into the city," the City Lord said as the snow fog dispersed, revealing towering walls ahead.

---

At the City Lord’s Manor, the City Lord intended to extend his hospitality to the two guests. However, Gao Sheng waved it off.

"Let’s skip the formalities. City Lord, I’ve already gathered enough information about Snow Moon City’s situation and have a preliminary idea of who the culprit is!" Gao Sheng proclaimed.

"What?!" The City Lord was taken aback. Lin Jing, too, was surprised. Could this brother really have figured it out so quickly, even before the Divine Pine sensed anything?

"First, Snow Moon City’s property has been damaged, and a major sect disciple was attacked. These incidents create serious security concerns. Who suffers the most from this? The answer is obvious—City Lord Tang. If these issues worsen, your political career will undoubtedly take a hit," Gao Sheng explained. "Thus, while this may appear to be the work of demons, it’s also possible that one of your enemies is disguising themselves as a demonic force!"

"I conducted a detailed investigation before coming here. Lotus Pavilion’s Branch Master, Hua Qiandao, came to this desolate city only to see his business severely hindered by your policies. His promotion prospects are dim. Just now, Brother Lin of the Beast Taming Sect mentioned that Senior Brother Ouyang was poisoned… and Lotus Pavilion primarily deals in medicinal pills. Only they have sufficient knowledge of poisons!"

Gao Sheng concluded confidently, "City Lord Tang, I urge you to arrest Lotus Pavilion’s Branch Master Hua Qiandao immediately!" He fanned himself with an air of elegance as he spoke.

City Lord Tang’s expression turned odd. "Young Master Gao, while it’s true that I had past disagreements with Hua Qiandao, we’ve since reconciled. Snow Moon City and Lotus Pavilion have established deep cooperation through formal contracts. If I were to fall, so would he. We now share the same fate."

Gao Sheng fell silent, continuing to fan himself. "Well… just pretend I didn’t say anything."

Lin Jing cupped his hands and excused himself. He was beginning to doubt this Demon-Slaying Bureau operative’s usefulness.

"Senior Divine Pine… is there anything unusual about the City Lord’s Manor?" Lin Jing asked once outside, opening his palm to reveal a pine cone.

A voice emanated from the pine cone. "No anomalies detected. The City Lord himself is not the problem. However, I now know what poison afflicted your Senior Brother."

"What is it?" Lin Jing asked, startled.

"Follow my guidance," the Divine Pine instructed.

Lin Jing quickly complied, following the pine’s directions until he reached a snowy grassland. He brushed aside the snow, uncovering several purple plants.

"What do you see?" the Divine Pine asked.

Lin Jing stared at the plants, lost in thought. After rifling through his mental compendium of botanical knowledge, he suddenly noticed red spots forming on his hand. "Split petals, serpentine leaves, causing rashes upon contact… This is Feathered Insect Grass."

"Correct," the Divine Pine affirmed. "Do you know where it thrives?"

"In areas abundant with venomous insects, particularly near the Yunhai Mountain Range. Are you suggesting that my Senior Brother was harmed by venomous insects?"

"Not just any venomous insects. The poison affecting him isn’t something that would stump a Golden Core elder or a Nascent Soul patriarch," the Divine Pine explained. "This poison… it belongs to the Gu God Sect. Only their peculiar Gu insects could produce something this insidious. Your Senior Brother must have been poisoned by a Child Gu. Find the Mother Gu, and the Ice Gu poison can be neutralized."

---

"Brother Lin!" Gao Sheng suddenly appeared, catching up to Lin Jing.

Lin Jing stashed the pine cone and regarded the privileged youth. "Brother Gao, what brings you here?"

"I’ve had a new revelation! So I ran away from the City Lord’s Manor," Gao Sheng replied, looking anxious. "I suspect the culprit may be the Gu God Sect! My father once mentioned that the sect has been increasing its influence in our region by using Gu poison to control people. City Lord Tang might already be their puppet! After all, his cultivation technique and the Ice Gu poison seem to align suspiciously. The mastermind behind everything could be him!"

Lin Jing: ?

"Brother Gao, do you have any evidence?" Lin Jing asked.

"None."

"Then why are you even saying this?!" Lin Jing sighed. "If the City Lord were breeding Gu insects with beasts, given the Tang family’s resources in Snow Moon City, would he need to steal snow rabbits from the Beast Taming Sect?"

"Uh… Brother Lin, you have a point," Gao Sheng muttered, falling deep into thought again.

"Brother Lin, do you think it’s possible…" He suddenly eyed Lin Jing warily, taking a step back.

"Let me guess. You’re about to suggest that the Beast Taming Sect, often dubbed the ‘Beast Demon Sect,’ might have internal collusion with demonic sects, harming itself to eliminate suspicion and cry wolf?"

Gao Sheng was stunned. "How did you know?!"

"Brother Gao, perhaps you should just go home and inherit your family’s legacy!" Lin Jing retorted.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 55: Baiting the Enemy
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 55: Baiting the Enemy

“Oh, you don’t think I can solve this case?” Gao Sheng was visibly upset at Lin Jing’s dismissive attitude.

“If you’re also investigating this matter, let’s see who cracks the case first!” he declared before storming off, leaving Lin Jing bewildered.

“He seems earnest about solving the case, but I have a feeling he’s not cut out for it,” Lin Jing remarked.

The Divine Pine interjected, “He’s a young human fledgling and still needs to grow. However, having a heart devoted to exterminating demons and defending justice is commendable, even if I am technically a demonic cultivator.”

“Senior Divine Pine, could you refrain from your hellish jokes?” Lin Jing replied dryly. Then, focusing back on the case, he said, “So, Senior Brother Ouyang’s poisoning and the disappearance of spirit beasts in Snow Moon City—this isn’t the work of people?”

“It’s likely the work of an unclaimed Gu insect,” Divine Pine confirmed. “The missing ice-elemental spirit beasts were probably devoured by the Gu insect. If a Gu Master were controlling it, there’d be no reason to stir trouble in Snow Moon City. There are plenty of unowned ice beasts on the Snow Plains. Inviting conflict with the Beast Taming Sect and Snow Moon City would be suicide.”

“As for Senior Brother Ouyang, he was probably just unlucky… Maybe his yang energy was so strong that it irritated the ice Gu, causing a reaction. Yang and yin energies clashing, perhaps?”

“Or maybe, since ice Gu are yin-aligned, it tried to balance itself by absorbing his yang energy,” Lin Jing mused.

Lin Jing protested, “I’m at least a bit stronger, right?”

“Either way, if this is an unclaimed Gu insect, it’s much easier to handle. Be it a regular insect or a Gu, they’re not known for high intelligence,” Lin Jing said confidently. “Senior Divine Pine, can you locate it?”

“If I couldn’t, I wouldn’t have accompanied you,” Divine Pine replied. “But your method of finding it might be faster than mine.”

---

Before long, a snow-white rabbit with blue eyes was hopping through the snowy terrain. It was none other than Lin Jing, transformed using the Thousand Transformations technique.

The snow rabbits raised in Beast Valley were generally at the first level of Qi Refinement and lacked sentience. Such rabbits were sufficient to attract the ice Gu, but Lin Jing upped the stakes.

Though he had disguised himself as a Qi Refinement rabbit, this rabbit had a faint wound on its leg, exuding a subtle aura of longevity blood. For beasts, this blood was an irresistible temptation. For Gu insects, it was no different.

“Hop~ hop~” Lin Jing sang softly as he bounced around in the snow.

Through a night of icy winds, Lin Jing’s face turned purple from the cold. He glanced into his Beast Space and saw the Pine Leaf Mouse resting comfortably, polishing its Mechanical Bamboo Rifle, completely at ease.

“Come out,” Lin Jing grumbled, dragging the Pine Leaf Mouse out. Using illusions, he disguised it as another rabbit. “You’re not going to need to fight with Senior Divine Pine around. At least help me play bait.”

The Pine Leaf Mouse emerged reluctantly, its face filled with complaint—it disliked both extreme heat and cold. Thankfully, it wasn’t long before reality spared them further misery.

After Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse played the role of a legendary pair of rabbits for some time, Divine Pine stirred.

“It’s here.”

Snowflakes drifting from the sky suddenly coalesced into white threads, resembling serpentine strands, and began reaching toward Lin Jing’s rabbit form.

Before the threads could touch him, the pine cone Lin Jing carried emitted a gold-green spiritual energy pulse. A golden resin emerged, enveloping and consuming the incoming threads, suspending them midair.

“Senior Divine Pine, how’s that?” Lin Jing asked, staring at the resin trapping the intricate white threads. “Huh? Threads only?”

“We failed,” Divine Pine admitted. “As expected, this is no ordinary Gu insect.”

“It hasn’t been born yet.”

“An unformed entity cannot be captured.”

The Pine Leaf Mouse squeaked in disbelief, suspecting Divine Pine was just making excuses for its failure. An unborn Gu can eat snow rabbits? Ridiculous!

“Do you know how a Gu insect is created?” Divine Pine asked.

“You place several venomous insects in a container and let them fight until only one survives, absorbing all the others’ toxins. The last one standing becomes the Gu,” Lin Jing recited the basic process.

“Correct. Gu insects are not born naturally; they are cultivated by human hands. This is why their forms vary widely. Among the countless Gu insects created by the Gu God Sect, ten are considered extraordinary.”

“These rare Gu insects take the form of venomous creatures but are composed entirely of elemental energy. They are immortal—each time one is destroyed, it doesn’t truly die. After centuries, it can regenerate in a location rich in its elemental affinity.”

“Although they lose all memories during rebirth, akin to reincarnation, their bloodline power remains intact. Each one is a king among Gu insects. If my judgment is correct, an ice-elemental rare Gu is about to be born here,” Divine Pine revealed. “The white threads are its initial organs, absorbing nutrients to transform from formless to physical.”

Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse were awestruck. No wonder the Gu God Sect managed to survive the Great Demon Purge that eradicated many other demonic sects in the Ancient Celestial Kingdom.

“What should we do? Wait for it to be reborn and then capture it?” Lin Jing asked.

“That’s the only option. But for now, you should focus on finding the real culprit behind your Senior Brother’s injury,” Divine Pine advised.

Lin Jing froze, suddenly realizing something was amiss.

“An unborn Gu insect only possesses primal instincts for feeding. Gu poison, however, is a deliberate attack mechanism of a matured Gu.”

“Could there be a Gu practitioner here waiting for the Gu’s birth? Was my Senior Brother indeed harmed by a demonic cultivator?” Lin Jing speculated.

“Not necessarily a Gu God Sect practitioner, but definitely someone knowledgeable about Gu techniques,” Divine Pine replied. “Newborn Gu insects seek a host to parasitize, entering a dormant state to grow stronger. Few cultivators would willingly become such a host due to the immense drawbacks.”

“But for those willing to endure the consequences, a Gu’s power can be harnessed. This mutual dependency gave rise to Gu Masters. While this practice isn’t inherently evil, most Gu Masters resort to using others as hosts, unwilling to part with the Gu’s power.”

“Each type of Gu insect requires a specific parasitic environment. If someone knew a rare Gu would be reborn here, they could prepare their body to suit the ice Gu’s needs, becoming its ideal host.”

“Furthermore, if this person obtained the method to refine a lesser Gu related to the rare Gu, they could first host the lesser Gu to improve compatibility with the ice Gu. The rare Gu’s birthplace would also be the perfect site to refine its lesser counterpart.”

“An ideal host for an ice Gu…” Lin Jing immediately thought of the City Lord’s innate Ice Spirit Body. Could Gao Sheng have been right?

No, something didn’t add up. While the ice Gu and Ice Spirit Body were compatible, why would the City Lord target Senior Brother Ouyang? As a Golden Core cultivator, he could easily defeat him. Furthermore, it was the City Lord’s people who discovered the injured Ouyang and treated him promptly.

“Could the optimal host for an ice Gu actually have fire or yang attributes?” Lin Jing wondered aloud. “If a cultivator with such a physique learned of the rare Gu’s imminent rebirth and saw Senior Brother Ouyang as a threat to their suitability as a host, they might use a lesser Gu to harm him preemptively.”

“Could Gu insects be playing some yin-yang balancing game? Or is it simply that fire-aligned hosts don’t freeze to death too quickly, giving the Gu a stable home?”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 56: I’m Really Not a Demonic Cultivator
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 56: I’m Really Not a Demonic Cultivator

The night was silent and still.

Lin Jing stood outside the closed doors of the Lotus Pavilion in Snow Moon City. Inside resided Hua Qiandao, the Pavilion Master—a cultivator with a Fire Spirit Body.

Thinking back, it was strange for someone cultivating fire-element techniques to come to a freezing city like Snow Moon City for business. It suggested ulterior motives.

Moreover, Senior Brother Ouyang had purchased yang-nourishing pills from this man, meaning they’d had direct contact. Coupled with the current situation, Hua Qiandao was the prime suspect. While it was unclear how he’d learned about the rare Gu or obtained methods for refining its lesser offspring, everyone had their own fortuitous encounters, making him the most likely culprit.

“Senior Divine Pine… any findings?” Lin Jing asked.

The Divine Pine scanned the area with its spiritual sense but didn’t detect Hua Qiandao’s presence. However, something else caught Lin Jing’s attention—a young man riding a white dragon-horse aimlessly through the snow.

“What are you doing here?” Gao Sheng asked, trotting closer.

“I should be asking you the same thing,” Lin Jing retorted.

“Even the Golden Core City Lord isn’t as busy as him!”

Gao Sheng laid out his reasoning, pointing out Hua Qiandao’s peculiar behavior.

“For once… that makes sense,” Lin Jing nodded.

“You agree this time?” Gao Sheng said, surprised.

“Well, if your grandfather is Soul Formation, everything you say must be right,” Lin Jing quipped, astonished by the extent of Gao Sheng’s background. “And frankly, I’m starting to suspect Hua Qiandao myself.”

“Finally, we agree!” Gao Sheng beamed. “Also, I’ve investigated—Hua Qiandao is nowhere to be found tonight. I suspect he realized his scheme was about to be exposed and fled upon hearing that a genius investigator like me had arrived.”

“Fleeing seems unlikely. More likely, his plan has reached its final stage,” Lin Jing said, gazing at the moon. As snow continued to fall, countless thread-like lines began descending from the clouds, resembling a vast spider web weaving itself across the sky.

Gao Sheng looked up and gasped, his mouth slightly agape. “Spiders—so many spiders!”

On the white web spanning the entire city, fist-sized spiders began hatching, each radiating significant demonic energy. The spiders dropped from the web and attacked any living being they encountered. Several lunged at Lin Jing and Gao Sheng without warning.

“What the hell are these things?” Gao Sheng exclaimed, brandishing his fan. With a powerful wave, a terrifying gust obliterated the incoming spiders.

“These are… parasitic Gu,” Lin Jing muttered, pulling out his Mechanical Bamboo Rifle. With a thunderous shot, the spiders attacking him were blasted to pieces.

“Whoa, Brother Lin! What kind of treasure is that?” Gao Sheng’s eyes lit up, captivated by the weapon.

“Shouldn’t you be more concerned about what these things are?” Lin Jing shot back, scanning the area. The number of spiders was staggering—thousands, perhaps tens of thousands, swarmed the streets. This was just what he could see at a glance; the entire city was likely overrun.

“You mentioned parasitic Gu earlier?” Gao Sheng’s expression darkened.

Parasitic Gu were a rank below lesser Gu. If lesser Gu were commanders, parasitic Gu were soldiers. They typically appeared en masse when a Gu King was about to emerge, siphoning nutrients to support the newborn Gu King.

“There really is a Gu King…” Gao Sheng muttered.

At that moment, a beam of icy light streaked across the sky. City Lord Tang Qinghan descended beside Gao Sheng, his face grim. “Young Master Gao, are you all right?”

“I’m fine!” Gao Sheng replied. “City Lord Tang, a Gu King is about to emerge in Snow Moon City!”

The spiders, seemingly sensing that Lin Jing and Gao Sheng were formidable, redirected their attacks elsewhere, scattering through the city.

“There’s likely a Gu God Sect demonic cultivator hiding in the city!” Gao Sheng declared. “Their goal must be to refine the Gu King.”

His expression shifted as he recalled his earlier suspicions—Tang Qinghan was the most probable suspect. He subconsciously retreated behind his dragon-horse.

“Fear not, Young Master Gao. I will protect you,” Tang Qinghan reassured, though his scalp tingled with dread. His political career felt as fragile as the spider silk above. Nevertheless, he prioritized Gao Sheng’s safety, dispatching his guards to contain the chaos in the city.

“It’s the rare Gu preparing to be reborn,” Divine Pine informed Lin Jing through spiritual communication. “Hua Qiandao is likely hiding, waiting for the Gu to emerge so he can absorb its power and escape.”

“Senior Divine Pine, can you locate him?” Lin Jing asked. “He’s only at the Foundation Establishment stage. Even if he absorbs the Gu, he shouldn’t be a match for you.”

“With you here, he won’t be able to absorb it,” Divine Pine said, chuckling.

Lin Jing’s expression turned peculiar as an unsettling thought crossed his mind.

Suddenly, dazzling blue light surged through the spider web above. Directly overhead, a tiny spider, no larger than a fingernail and made entirely of ice crystals, began to form. Though small, its aura radiated extreme cold, freezing even the raging blizzard and making time itself seem to slow.

“Is that…?” Gao Sheng’s pupils shrank. “Could it be the Ice Soul Gu, one of the ten legendary rare Gu of the Gu God Sect? It was thought to have vanished for a thousand years. Why is it reborn here?”

“The Ice Soul Gu?” Tang Qinghan’s expression turned grim. Each of the Gu God Sect’s ten rare Gu was a calamity. If it fell into the hands of a demonic cultivator, it would become an unparalleled threat.

“Where’s the Gu God Sect’s demonic cultivator?!” Tang Qinghan barked, on high alert.

But before anyone could react, the Ice Soul Gu shot downward like a streak of icy light, piercing directly into Lin Jing’s body.

“Damn, I’m going to turn into a demonic cultivator!” Lin Jing inwardly screamed.

“Even if a Fire Spirit Body or Primordial Yang Body were suitable hosts, nothing compares to a Longevity Body,” Divine Pine said, amused. “With your life force and nutrients, the Ice Soul Gu would naturally choose you over any other prepared host.”

“Let it enter your body. It’s the key to resolving the Ice Gu poison affecting your Senior Brother. Don’t worry—I’ll seal it inside you, preventing it from absorbing your energy. This is also an opportunity for you. Didn’t you once say all techniques, even demonic ones, are just tools? Once you’re strong enough, you can refine it. I’ll handle the Demon-Slaying Bureau.”

“I didn’t mean it literally!” Lin Jing snapped as bone-chilling cold erupted from his body. The ice energy rivaled Foundation Establishment levels, transforming his hair to snow-white and his eyes to icy blue. Gao Sheng staggered back in shock.

“So it’s you! You’re the Gu God Sect demonic cultivator! The Gu King merged with you!”

“Demonic cultivator my !” Lin Jing growled through gritted teeth. Moments later, a pine cone-shaped seal appeared on the back of his hand, stabilizing the energy. His hair and eyes reverted to their original state.

Meanwhile, in a hidden cavern elsewhere in the city, Hua Qiandao watched the Ice Soul Gu’s rebirth with joy—until it failed to choose him as its host. His face turned red, and he spat blood before collapsing.

“My Gu! My ten-years-in-the-making Gu! The Gu manual lied! Why didn’t it choose my Fire Spirit Body?!”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 57: The Cold Moon Sword Immortal
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 57: The Cold Moon Sword Immortal

The atmosphere outside the Lotus Pavilion turned awkward. Lin Jing no longer had the energy to explain himself.

Witnesses: Tang Qinghan, the City Lord of Snow Moon City.

Evidence: The Ice Soul Gu.

And now, another "brilliant mind" was accusing him of being a demonic cultivator. He felt utterly exhausted.

Considering Divine Pine’s jokes about being a "demonic cultivator" and its teachings to the Pine Leaf Mouse about "demonic arts," Lin Jing figured his current situation was ironic yet fitting. He had almost become a demonic cultivator himself.

"Detective Gao, stop speculating. I’ll explain everything later. For now, we need to capture the real Gu practitioner behind all this..."

Before Lin Jing could finish, everyone’s attention shifted to the sky.

At some point, a massive bronze ancient sword had appeared, blocking the moonlight as it hovered in the air. Standing atop the sword was a woman dressed in pure white, her cascading hair tied up with a simple jade hairpin.

Without further delay, she reached for the white sword hanging at her waist. The sword hummed faintly, emitting a soft green glow.

Hum!

In the next instant, the surrounding air seemed to freeze. Thousands of sword lights burst forth from her blade, forming a dazzling array of icy swords that illuminated the heavens.

"Go!" she commanded.

The sword light rained down like a meteor shower, piercing the night sky with overwhelming power. Each strike obliterated the spiders on the ground. The Gu insects shrieked and writhed but were ultimately reduced to icy fragments that scattered into the wind.

In mere moments, the cultivators clearing out the Gu insects in Snow Moon City realized the threat had vanished. They gazed upward in awe and disbelief.

Even Gao Sheng was stunned as he stared at the figure on the ancient bronze sword. His expression turned grim. "I’m doomed."

As he spoke, the white-robed sword immortal descended gracefully. Her cold voice rang out, "Gao Sheng, you did not pass the Demon-Slaying Bureau’s assessment, yet you stole a Demon-Slaying token. Do you understand your crime?"

"I’ve been instructed to take you into custody."

"Nooo!" Gao Sheng shouted. His dragon-horned white horse, sensing the game was up, sighed heavily and trudged off into the blizzard.

"Could you be... the Cold Moon Sword Immortal, Feng Ning?" Tang Qinghan asked cautiously.

"City Lord Tang, I will need a detailed report on the cause of this calamity in Snow Moon City," she said coolly. Tang Qinghan nodded repeatedly.

"Thank you, Sword Immortal, for intervening. Without your help, the city would have suffered immense losses in this Gu disaster."

"Sister Feng, wait! Don’t arrest me—arrest him!" Gao Sheng pointed desperately at Lin Jing. "The Ice Soul Gu was reborn and entered his body!"

Lin Jing, feeling increasingly wronged, raised his hand obediently. "I already said it’s a misunderstanding," he muttered.

His eyes turned to Feng Ning, recognizing her instantly. On the Tianji Pavilion’s Celestial Rankings, Feng Ning, the Cold Moon Sword Immortal, ranked third on the Earth List of the Heaven-Earth-Human rankings. She was a peak Golden Core cultivator and the senior disciple of the Heavenly Sword Gate, the top sword sect in the Ancient Celestial Kingdom.

Moreover, she was a member of the Demon-Slaying Bureau and had once single-handedly destroyed a demonic cultivator stronghold, slaying a Nascent Soul-stage demonic cultivator. The blood-soaked battlefield had earned her the moniker Blood Moon Sword Immortal.

She was a true prodigy of the cultivation world, standing at its pinnacle. In comparison, Lin Jing, a minor talent of the Beast Taming Sect, was not even in the same league.

Lin Jing couldn’t fathom why such a legendary figure would come to the barren Great Wasteland Domain. He glanced at Gao Sheng and thought, So that’s why a Qi Refinement-level disciple managed to infiltrate the Demon-Slaying Bureau. He stole an identity and now he’s about to pay for it.

Still, Lin Jing knew that if he didn’t clear his name soon, he might also get arrested.

"I’ll give you a chance to explain," Feng Ning said, her gaze sharp as her sword.

"Thank you, Immortal," Lin Jing said, meeting her eyes with a faint smile.

"I’m not trusting you. I just trust him less," Feng Ning replied, glancing disdainfully at Gao Sheng, whose face fell.

"Am I really that unreliable?" he muttered.

"Wise judgment, Immortal. Allow me to introduce myself—I am Lin Jing, a disciple of the Beast Taming Sect. I came to Snow Moon City to investigate the disappearance of spirit beasts as part of a sect mission," Lin Jing began.

"Upon arrival, I discovered the growth of ‘Feathered Insect Grass’ beneath the snow. As you all know, this poisonous plant thrives in areas teeming with venomous insects."

"My Senior Brother, Ouyang Hao, was previously poisoned while investigating this case, the nature of which stumped even the sect’s experts. This led me to suspect a rare Gu insect might be involved. Only a blend of multiple potent toxins could create such an intractable poison."

"To identify the Gu, I used illusion techniques to bait it out. This revealed that the culprit was indeed one of the Gu God Sect’s ten legendary rare Gu—the Ice Soul Gu."

"When I found it, the Ice Soul Gu hadn’t yet fully formed. Its poison had not developed, and it acted purely on instinct. This led me to deduce that a demonic practitioner had already prepared a lesser Gu and cultivated a compatible physique, hiding in Snow Moon City to absorb the rare Gu upon its rebirth."

"My Senior Brother’s Primordial Yang Body made him a likely host, and I suspect this person targeted him out of fear that the Gu would choose him instead. However, they couldn’t predict that my Longevity Body, which far surpasses even their Fire Spirit Body, would be more appealing to the Gu."

"And so, as you saw, the Gu chose me. I am completely innocent," Lin Jing concluded.

Gao Sheng gaped in astonishment. "So that’s the truth behind the beast disappearances and your Senior Brother’s poisoning?"

"Was it Hua Qiandao?" Tang Qinghan asked, his expression darkening. "But why would a Fire Spirit Body be suitable for the Ice Soul Gu’s parasitism? Wouldn’t my Ice Spirit Body be a better fit?"

"The Ice Soul Gu’s intense cold nearly froze itself to death during its creation. To counteract this, the Gu God Sect imbued it with an innate preference for yang-rich, heat-intensive environments to balance its own nature," Feng Ning explained.

After Lin Jing’s detailed explanation, Feng Ning seemed convinced. Her icy demeanor softened slightly as she studied him with interest.

"Even so, a mere Qi Refinement fourth-level cultivator achieving all this—outmaneuvering a demonic practitioner’s years of planning—defies logic. I’m curious how you attracted the rare Gu and remained unharmed as its host," Feng Ning remarked.

"Immortal, please don’t take such casual interest in people," Lin Jing replied with a wry smile. "As the future Holy Son of the Beast Taming Sect, I must have some hidden talents, no?"

"He’s right, Sister Feng! This guy is no ordinary disciple. A Qi Refinement cultivator with a Foundation Establishment mount and Golden Core-grade treasures? If you told me he secretly has a Nascent Soul guardian, I’d believe it. That’s the kind of setup I should have!" Gao Sheng declared, spinning yet another theory.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 58: Demon-Slaying Bureau
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 58: Demon-Slaying Bureau

Cold Moon Sword Immortal Feng Ning cast a glance at Gao Sheng. For once, his wild guess wasn’t entirely wrong. She did sense a faint, enigmatic presence of someone powerful lurking around Lin Jing. It was likely that someone truly formidable was secretly protecting him.

“The Hua Pavilion Master you mentioned—is it this person?”

From afar, a figure stumbled through the air. City Lord Tang Qinghan immediately recognized him. “Isn’t that the Hua Pavilion Master?”

Hua Qiandao appeared unconscious, his eyes wide open as if in disbelief. He wasn’t flying under his own power; a small yellow bird carried him by the collar, dragging him to the group.

The bird, clearly not a living creature, vanished into Feng Ning’s sword upon completing its task, revealing itself to be a sword spirit.

“That’s him!” Tang Qinghan confirmed, glancing at the unconscious Pavilion Master. “But… what happened to him?”

“Probably fainted from rage,” Gao Sheng said gleefully. “Who wouldn’t? Losing a rare Gu King is probably more infuriating than losing a wife to another man. And with his Fire Spirit Body, he’s even more prone to blowing up!”

Now that the true culprit was captured, Lin Jing suggested, “I believe everything is clear now. How about this: I’ll take the Ice Soul Gu to cure my poisoned Senior Brother, while Sword Immortal Feng and Brother Gao take the Gu practitioner for interrogation. Everyone gets what they want—doesn’t that sound fair?”

“Feng Ning, since the matter is resolved, let Brother Lin go save his Senior Brother. Saving lives comes first,” Gao Sheng urged. “As for the Pavilion Master, let me handle the interrogation! I actually noticed something was off about him the moment I arrived. Didn’t I, City Lord Tang?”

Tang Qinghan wiped his brow nervously. Pure dumb luck. You got the result right, but your reasoning was way off.
