Feng Ning gave Gao Sheng a long, critical look. “Very well. I’ll escort him to the Beast Taming Sect myself.”

“City Lord Tang, Gao Sheng, I’ll leave the Gu practitioner to you for questioning,” she added, summoning the sword spirit back.

The little yellow bird chirped as it reappeared, then spoke in a sharp, clear voice: “If you two won’t interrogate him, I’ll do it myself. I love using torture for questioning—9,999 sword strikes, and not one is fatal!”

---

The colossal bronze sword took to the skies once more, now carrying Feng Ning and Lin Jing.

The two stood side by side against the cold wind, both dressed in white. While the sword was fast, Lin Jing found himself missing the warmth of the Heavenfeather Crane’s back. He glanced at Feng Ning, understanding she was "escorting" him primarily to monitor the Ice Soul Gu residing within him.

“Senior, are you going to reveal yourself now?” Feng Ning suddenly asked, directing her words at Lin Jing.

As she spoke, the golden-green glow of the pine cone in Lin Jing’s pocket intensified. Slowly, a figure materialized—a hazy, humanoid silhouette formed entirely of spiritual energy, with no discernible features.

Feng Ning regarded the apparition with respect. “How should I address you, Senior?”

“Some call me the Demon Pine, others call me the Divine Pine. But those names are from the past. Now, I am his protector. You may call me Guardian Pine,” the figure said in a calm yet teasing tone.

“Senior, are you intending for him to host the Ice Soul Gu?” Feng Ning asked, ignoring the playful name.

“Why not? The Demon-Slaying Bureau deals with rare Gu in a similar way, sealing them in trusted hosts to prevent their misuse by the Gu God Sect,” Guardian Pine replied. “I recall a certain Bureau cultivator, Liu Gongzhuo, who carries the Heavenly Flame Gu within his body.”

“That’s true,” Feng Ning admitted. “But he’s only at Qi Refinement stage. Hosting the Ice Soul Gu might put him in grave danger if the Gu God Sect learns about it.”

“No matter. He possesses the Longevity Body and Beast Taming techniques. If he bonds with a fire-elemental pet beast carrying a demon king’s bloodline, he can counterbalance the Ice Soul Gu’s power. In fact, even without the Gu, the demonic sects wouldn’t leave him alone. He’s already a natural target. Why not leverage their infighting to weaken their forces?”

“You can let him join the Demon-Slaying Bureau,” Guardian Pine suggested. “That way, the Ice Soul Gu remains under your supervision.”

Lin Jing, standing to the side, felt his frustration bubble over. Are these people even human?

“Longevity Body…” Feng Ning murmured, her surprise evident. She now understood why the Ice Soul Gu had chosen Lin Jing over its preordained preferences.

“My constitution should already be recorded with the Bureau,” Lin Jing said with a wry smile.

“I see…” Feng Ning gave him a thoughtful look. After a pause, her lips curled into a faint smile. “Longevity Body, Ice Soul Gu… This senior is right. Would you like to join the Demon-Slaying Bureau? I could use a teammate.”

Lin Jing rolled his eyes. “Sword Immortal, are you planning to use me as bait to lure out demonic cultivators? You’re a Golden Core powerhouse asking a Qi Refinement cultivator to be your teammate—does that make sense?”

Feng Ning cleared her throat, her cool demeanor faltering for a moment. “Of course not. I simply admire your talents. You showed remarkable abilities during the Snow Moon City incident.”

She handed Lin Jing a jade token. “This token is rare. With it, you can contact me anywhere in the Ancient Celestial Kingdom. Being a sect disciple doesn’t conflict with serving as a Demon-Slaying Bureau operative. Think it over.”

Feng Ning clearly believed Lin Jing was destined to clash with the demonic sects. With the Longevity Body and a rare Gu in his possession, he was practically a walking demonic bait.

Lin Jing accepted the token reluctantly. Joining the Bureau sounded like a death sentence. He’d rather enroll in a quiet academy like Hidden Dragon Academy and study in peace.

“I’m not worried about my low cultivation level—I’m worried about yours. If you were at the Dao-Seeking stage, I’d gladly team up with you, even as a mortal,” Lin Jing quipped.

Feng Ning: ???

From his gourd, the Pine Leaf Mouse perked up, sensing familiarity in Lin Jing’s tone. Why does this sound like something he’s always said to me? And now he’s using it on a Sword Immortal?!

---

Meanwhile, Hua Qiandao had been revived and was undergoing brutal interrogation. Under the threat of "9,999 sword strikes," he confessed everything.

As a youth, he’d stumbled upon a cave and inherited the legacy of a deceased Gu Master. The inheritance included information about the Ice Soul Gu’s rebirth, the method to refine its lesser Gu, and techniques to transform oneself into its ideal host.

Hua Qiandao had painstakingly cultivated a Fire Spirit Body and eliminated all potential rivals. But in the end…

“I refuse to accept this!” he wailed.

“Refuse my !” Tang Qinghan roared, removing his shoe and smacking Hua Qiandao across the face repeatedly. “I vouched for you, and this is how you repay me? My career is ruined!”

“City Lord, take it easy. Don’t kill him,” Gao Sheng chimed in, grinning. “This case is a huge success for me. My first mission with the Bureau, and I broke a major case. As expected of me!”

Tang Qinghan remained silent, knowing full well most of the credit belonged to Lin Jing.

“Of course, Brother Lin deserves recognition. He’s talented. If only he joined the Bureau as my teammate… But I can’t shake the feeling he’s eyeing my dragon-horse. Beast Taming Sect disciples—they don’t chase beauties, but rare beasts? They can’t resist.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 59: Offering Myself in Gratitude
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 59: Offering Myself in Gratitude

Above the Great Wasteland Domain, a bronze ancient sword streaked across the sky.

With astonishing speed, it arrived outside the Beast Taming Sect.

Its formidable aura prompted many elders to emerge from their caves, raising their heads in unison.

They gazed towards the clouds, unexpectedly seeing a familiar figure atop the ancient sword.

After a slight bow towards the Sword Immortal of Lingyue, Lin Jing leaped off the sword, determined to make haste in detoxifying Senior Brother Ouyang.

“Pine Leaf Mouse, catch me!”

As Lin Jing descended, unable to fly or land steadily on his own, he summoned the Pine Leaf Mouse. Layers of crimson leaves appeared beneath his feet, cushioning him as he stepped lightly. With hands clasped behind his back and robes billowing, he descended gracefully onto Danxia Peak, each step exuding elegance like lotus blossoms blooming beneath his feet.

“The Holy Son of the Beast Taming Sect?”

Her main mission in the Great Wasteland Domain was to escort the troublesome Gao Sheng back to his sect. This individual, relying on the influence of his maternal grandfather in the Demon-Slaying Bureau, had wasted twenty years in debauchery, only to abruptly seek validation, causing considerable disruption in the process.

“Elder Shen!” Lin Jing arrived at Danxia Peak, stepping into the Veterinary Hall, where Elder Shen was attending to Senior Brother Ouyang.

Due to the prevalence of spirit beasts in the Beast Taming Sect, injuries during disciples' sparring often affected their companion beasts. Thus, the Veterinary Hall was established, serving as a treatment center not only for spirit beasts but also for Beast Tamers themselves. Here, “beast” referred to both companion beasts and Beast Tamers.

Inside the hall, aside from Elder Shen, there were several unfamiliar physicians.

“Lin Jing?” Elder Shen looked at Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse in surprise. “You’ve returned? And just now, that was…”

“A fairy from the Heavenly Sword Gate who brought me back,” Lin Jing explained. “Thankfully, I’ve located the source of Senior Brother Ouyang’s poisoning. I request everyone here to vacate the room so I can concentrate on detoxifying him.”

The physicians were startled. A method to treat this rare poison? And brought back by a mere Qi Refining disciple? After a moment of hesitation, they bowed to Elder Shen and departed.

Once the room was cleared, Lin Jing immediately examined Senior Brother Ouyang and asked, “Senior Shen Song, what should I do next?”

“The poison from the Lesser Gu has now reached a stage where your Beast Taming Qi can neutralize it. As the host of the Ice Soul Gu, not only are you now immune to all poisons, but you can also cure countless rare poisons. I’ll unlock a fraction of the Ice Soul Gu’s seal, and you can use its power to expel the Gu poison,” Shen Song instructed.

“That simple?” Lin Jing nodded. Soon, the pinecone mark on the back of his hand glowed, and he felt a surge of power coursing through him.

Drawing on his experience with the Purple Dragonbone Flower, Lin Jing recognized that this power was reshaping his Beast Taming Qi.

He extended his hand, intending to unleash the refined Qi, but instead, several strands of white silk shot from his fingertips.

Zzzzz…

The silk radiated an intense cold, appearing extraordinarily resilient. Lin Jing discovered he could maneuver it as flexibly as his own limbs.

“…” He froze, his mind racing. Have I become a spider demon?

“Pierce the silk into their bodies,” Shen Song instructed.

Although feeling awkward, Lin Jing complied, threading the silk into Senior Brother Ouyang and the fox spirit Hong’er.

In that instant, Lin Jing sensed the invisible Gu poison entrenched deep within their souls. Surprisingly, he could now effortlessly absorb the toxins and command the Gu poison at will.

Meanwhile, Elder Shen, watching Lin Jing wield the silk to detoxify, stood dumbfounded. What was… this sorcery?

In a short time, Lin Jing successfully extracted the toxins from both patients. Their complexions began to improve, gradually returning to normal.

“Ugh…” Senior Brother Ouyang groaned in pain, but he had yet to regain full consciousness.

“The poison is neutralized. They’ll recover after some rest,” Lin Jing reassured, retracting the silk. But no sooner had he spoken than a wave of dizziness overwhelmed him, and he collapsed.

“Squeak?!”

The Pine Leaf Mouse and Elder Shen panicked. Shen Song reappeared, manifesting through condensed spiritual energy. “Don’t worry,” he explained. “He’s merely overexerted. Using the power of the Gu King at Qi Refining Stage Four is taxing. He just needs rest.”

“Tiandu’s Guardian Deity!” Elder Shen exclaimed, bowing respectfully before realizing what had been said. “Wait… Gu King? What do you mean, using the Gu King’s power?”

The Pine Leaf Mouse gave a subtle nod, as if to say: Yes, we now have our own demon cultivator in the Beast Taming Sect.

Drip… Drip…

Lin Jing stirred groggily, cold air hitting his face.

“This familiar sensation… feels so familiar,” he muttered nonsensically. Thankfully, he wasn’t in a damp prison. Instead, he found himself in an icy realm made of snow and spider silk.

At the heart of this domain lay a spider entirely composed of ice. Majestic and regal, it exuded an aura of winter’s sovereignty, its body bound by towering pine trunks inscribed with golden-green runes forming intricate sealing formations.

“The Ice Soul Gu… is this…” Lin Jing murmured.

“This is your body’s inner world. What you see now is just a sliver of your consciousness,” Shen Song appeared and explained.

“Senior Shen Song…”

“The Ice Soul Gu, often dubbed the embodiment of frost, is a fearsome Gu. While the Lesser Ice Soul Gu’s poison in your senior brother was rare, the Ice Soul Gu’s venom is even deadlier. Most Gu worms possess mind-controlling abilities, and the Ice Soul Gu is unparalleled in this regard,” Shen Song continued.

“Its poison can freeze a cultivator’s Yin Spirit, transforming them into its puppet. Even if the cultivator’s body is obliterated, the venom can preserve their Yin Spirit indefinitely, controlling their soul.”

“In essence, the Ice Soul Gu’s capabilities surpass the corpse cultivation techniques of the Yin Corpse Sect and the soul manipulation techniques of the Nether Palace. Moreover, its extreme cold can instantly kill even those with innate frost physiques.”

“Hiss…” Lin Jing gasped at the chilling cold surrounding him.

“So… am I really going to contract this thing?”

“Don’t worry. It’s still in its infancy and sealed by me. As long as you contract a fire-type beast of significant bloodline power to counterbalance it, you’ll be fine. For now, I’ll teach you the Sacred Wood Seal to strengthen the bindings and allow you to harness some of its power in emergencies.”

“Thank you, Senior Shen Song.”

“Just remember your promise of Longevity Blood. If you renege, you’ll learn what a demon pine truly is,” Shen Song warned.

Lin Jing quickly assured, “I won’t…”

Sometime later, Lin Jing awakened, finding himself in bed. Beside him sat a fox, a squirrel, and a frail figure keeping watch.

Seeing him awake, the figure burst into tears, exclaiming, “Brother! Thank you for saving me! If I’m reincarnated as a woman, I’ll offer myself to you!”

“Why… does this feel like betrayal…” Lin Jing muttered weakly before passing out again.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 60: Choosing a Fire Beast
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 60: Choosing a Fire Beast

When Lin Jing woke again, Elder Yu, Elder Mo, and several others had come to visit.

During his rest, the story of the events in Snow Moon City had spread, thanks to the incessant embellishments of the Pine Leaf Mouse. It depicted itself heroically battling the Parasitic Gu Spider, supposedly at the cost of countless Spiritual Beans, and now it was requesting the sect to replenish its “resources.”

Elder Mo casually approved the request, more focused on the larger issue. No one had anticipated that the mission in Snow Moon City would involve the Lotus Pavilion and the Gu God Cult’s Rare Gu. Even more surprising was how, in just a few days, an innocent Pine Leaf Mouse had grown so cunning.

“But hosting a Gu worm inside your body… Is that really safe? Junior Brother, just don’t end up turning into a demon cultivator for its sake,” Senior Brother Ouyang warned. Gu worms were notoriously difficult to manage, and the temptation to resort to questionable methods to sustain them often led cultivators astray.

“If you can’t handle it, don’t force yourself. Let it drain my strength instead; I’ll bear the burden for you!” Ouyang offered earnestly.

“A Rare Gu like this… it’d be a shame not to keep it,” Elder Mo remarked. “Who says Gu worms can’t be beasts? Why can’t they be tamed? If Senior Shen Song says it’s fine and the Sword Immortal of Lingyue didn’t object, then there’s no issue.”

“Indeed,” Elder Yu chimed in. “Plus, controlling puppets and mechanisms often uses Qi threads. With the Ice Soul Gu’s silk, his mastery of mechanism techniques and control over mechanized beasts will only improve!” His mind was clearly preoccupied with thoughts of mechanism art.

Lin Jing’s lips twitched. Perhaps he could reconsider planting the black chili peppers Elder Black Bear had demanded he destroy. As Shen Song had said, “Debts don’t burden the debtor.” Now that he was a vessel for Gu worms, what harm could a few fiery experiments bring?

“A fire beast would be perfect,” Elder Black Bear suddenly interjected from the doorway. “With one, you’d even have help refining pills!”

His underlying meaning was clear: Lin Jing might not need the Beast Flame he had previously been promised. Elder Black Bear chuckled slyly.

Even the Pine Leaf Mouse perked up. A fire beast would spare it from being forced to eat those dreaded chili peppers.

“Fire beasts…” Elder Yu pondered. “The Fire Spirit Sparrow won’t work. It’s only of Spirit-level bloodline, and not even particularly strong within that rank. It’s fine as a basic companion beast, but to balance the innate Gu King, the Ice Soul Gu? Impossible.”

“But does our Beast Taming Sect even have any fire beasts with Demonic King-level bloodlines?” Ouyang wondered aloud. “Fire beasts with King-level bloodlines are rare throughout the Great Wasteland Domain.”

“We don’t necessarily need a King-level bloodline,” Elder Mo said. “The key is finding a beast with sufficiently powerful bloodline strength to suppress the Gu King.”

“Ordinary beasts won’t do,” Elder Yu added. “We need something from the dragon lineage!”

Dragon lineage! Both Elder Yu and Ouyang nodded in agreement. In ancient times, before the decline of the demonic races, dragons were the pinnacle of the demon hierarchy. Their bloodline was unmatched in power, making them ideal for suppressing Gu worms.

“So, we just need a beast with fire attributes and dragon blood,” Elder Mo concluded. “King-level bloodlines aren’t strictly necessary because… don’t forget, if he studies at the Hidden Dragon Academy, he can learn the Hidden Dragon Secret Art. As Senior Cao said, it’s the pinnacle method for cultivating dragon-blooded beasts.”

“With the Hidden Dragon Secret Art, a fire beast with dragon blood can evolve into one with King-level bloodlines!” Elder Yu exclaimed, finally remembering the potential. “As long as the beast has fire and dragon attributes, the rest can be developed!”

Lin Jing nodded. “I remember the most common dragon-blooded beast on Nine Dragons Peak is the Dragonblood Carp, but that’s a water-type spirit beast.”

The Dragonblood Carp wasn’t one of the sect’s five foundational spirit beasts. Its combat capabilities were minimal during the Qi Refining Stage, requiring aquatic environments to survive. Unlike other beasts like the Rain Frog, it couldn’t function well on land.

Only when a Dragonblood Carp ascended the Nine Dragons Waterfall, transforming into a Fish Dragon, would it become a coveted creature, even among Foundation Establishment disciples. However, such a transformation was exceedingly rare, with no recent successes in the sect’s history.

“Don’t worry,” Elder Mo reassured him. “There are plenty of dragon-blooded beasts on Nine Dragons Peak. That’s the dominance of dragon bloodlines for you.”

“In the ancient demon era, the dragon race thrived. Their powerful bloodline allowed them to propagate freely across species, leading to an abundance of dragon-blooded beasts on the Tianyuan Continent.”

“What other sects might consider rare, we don’t lack in the Beast Taming Sect!”

“Moreover, our Fourth Sect Master contributed greatly to the diversity of our companion beasts. Let me consult Elder Jiang and see what’s available!” Elder Mo promised, leaving swiftly.

The Fourth Sect Master, though not as renowned as the Second Sect Master, who reformed the sect, nor the Third Sect Master, who developed numerous secret techniques, had nonetheless left an indelible mark.

He had encouraged interbreeding among spirit and demon beasts, utilizing pills to enhance offspring bloodlines. His efforts resulted in many extraordinary companion beasts, albeit with some less successful outcomes. Overall, his contributions were deemed invaluable. The thriving dragon-blood beast population on Nine Dragons Peak was a testament to his legacy.

Elder Mo didn’t keep Lin Jing waiting long, soon returning with a list of three dragon-blooded fire beasts:

A mutated member of the crane family with Firefeather Dragon bloodline, its feathers could transform into white dragon flames. Favored by the sect leader’s wife, it was highly sought after by disciples.

A wild fire lion mutated after consuming a dragon-blooded spirit plant. Now tamed, it serves as an outer sect guest elder, awaiting the right master.

A regression of the Battle Ape lineage, its ancestor was the sect’s late demonic king, the Redscale Ape Monkey, companion beast of the Fourth Sect Master. It possesses Fire-scaled Dragon bloodline.

Elder Mo handed the list to Lin Jing with a smile. “Take your pick!”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 61: Betting on a Long Shot
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 61: Betting on a Long Shot

“Thank you, Elder Mo.”

Lin Jing accepted the list and skimmed through it, marveling at the depth of the Beast Taming Sect's resources in rare and exotic beasts. Any one of these three would make an excellent Foundation Establishment companion beast.

The Heavenfeather Crane could serve as a flying mount, offering convenience for travel.

The Dragon Lion, a large and imposing beast, would project a commanding presence, far more majestic than his Pine Leaf Mouse companion.

The Flaming Arm Monkey—Lin Jing’s gaze lingered. Isn’t this the fabled Monkey King?

After reviewing the options several times, Lin Jing found himself torn. He looked up and asked, “Elder Mo, the descriptions are rather brief. Do you have a recommendation?”

Elder Mo cleared his throat. “Ahem, all three are excellent choices, but each has its quirks. Let me explain, and you can decide for yourself.”

“For instance, the Heavenfeather Crane. It has a proud and aloof temperament and dislikes being ridden.”

“The Dragon Lion, on the other hand, didn’t grow up in the sect and has little sense of belonging. It tends to bargain over Spirit Stones whenever asked to do something.”

Lin Jing glanced at the dozing Pine Leaf Mouse beside his bed. With its “asset-backed” loyalty, the Dragon Lion instantly lost its appeal.

“Looks like it has to be Monkey Bro,” Lin Jing mused.

But Elder Mo dealt another blow. “The Flaming Arm Monkey, due to its special status, has been rather pampered and indulged. It’s known to have a penchant for luxury… It may require some strict discipline in the future.”

Lin Jing: ???

“Elder, doesn’t the sect have any normal companion beasts? It seems like all three are high-maintenance.”

The Pine Leaf Mouse, now awake, blinked at Lin Jing, appearing increasingly agreeable in his eyes.

“There are plenty of well-behaved beasts,” Elder Mo chuckled, “but meeting the dual requirements of dragon blood and fire attributes is a tall order.”

“Choosing a companion beast is much like finding a spouse in the mortal world. There’s no such thing as perfection—it’s all about getting along and growing together over time.”

Still unsatisfied, Lin Jing asked, “Are these three really my only options?”

After a moment of hesitation, Elder Mo replied, “Not exactly. These three were chosen based on their innate potential and suitability for you.”

“However, according to Elder Jiang, who oversees the sect’s myriad beasts, there is one more fire-type dragon-blooded beast. It’s just… unusual.”

“Oh?” Lin Jing’s curiosity was piqued.

“It’s a Dragon Carp from Nine Dragons Peak, mutated into a fire attribute carp.”

“Because of this mutation, it’s… not great at swimming. Instead, it prefers to lounge in hot springs like a salted fish.”

“This makes it unlikely to utilize the Nine Dragons Waterfall to achieve the legendary leaping through the dragon gate transformation that most Dragon Carps aspire to.”

Lin Jing sighed, completely losing interest in finding a fire-blooded dragon beast within the sect.

“Elder, considering I’m still far from reaching Foundation Establishment, I think I’ll wait until I study at the Hidden Dragon Academy and explore external options. Perhaps I’ll come across a warlike Stone Monkey or something similar.”

Elder Mo suddenly paused and said, “If you value temperament above all, the Dragon Carp might actually be your best choice.”

“Unlike the other three, this carp hasn’t developed intelligence yet. It’s like a blank slate—perfect for a Beast Tamer to mold.”

“You can guide its growth however you wish.”

“And if you’re concerned about potential, it’s not lacking in that department either. Think about it—nearly all Dragon Carps are water attribute, but this one developed fire attributes. This anomaly suggests its ancestor might have been a dragon with a powerful fire attribute. Otherwise, it couldn’t have overcome generations of water-dominant bloodlines.”

“To put it grandly, its ancestor could’ve been a True Flame Dragon. If you gamble on its bloodline and win, you might end up with a companion beast far superior to the other three.”

Seriously? A gamble? Lin Jing thought, recalling the sect’s tendency to experiment with rare constitutions and mutated beasts. This sect sure loves rolling the dice.

“Well? Care to take a bet?” Elder Mo asked with an enigmatic smile.

Lin Jing smirked. “Actually, there’s one undeniable advantage to choosing the Dragon Carp.”

“What’s that?” Elder Mo asked, startled.

“It’s easier to take care of.”

“Compared to the aloof Heavenfeather Crane, the indulgent Flaming Arm Monkey, and the mercenary Dragon Lion… this carp seems much lower maintenance!”

“Elder Mo, I’m in! Care to place a bet alongside me?” Lin Jing couldn’t resist. The Dragon Carp’s potential and malleability were indeed appealing.

Besides, the idea of nurturing a carp to “leap the dragon gate” and evolve into a legendary beast intrigued him.

Elder Mo’s breathing quickened. “That’s the spirit! You truly are a disciple after my own heart!”

Nearby, the Pine Leaf Mouse silently watched the two. Gambling addicts… though I suppose my Glazed Immortal Eyes were also the result of a gamble.

After Lin Jing made his decision, Elder Mo immediately arranged for the carp to be brought over.

If Lin Jing had chosen the Heavenfeather Crane, Dragon Lion, or Flaming Arm Monkey, he might have had to negotiate for a contract.

But this mutated Flame Dragon Carp was only at Qi Refining Stage Three, with even lower cultivation than Lin Jing. Lacking intelligence, it was entirely at the sect’s disposal.

Later that day, Elder Mo returned with the Dragon Carp. Using his spiritual energy, he levitated a circular glazed fish tank, within which the carp swam lazily.

The Flame Dragon Carp lived up to its name, its fiery red scales dazzling like flames. However, its vacant expression confirmed that it had yet to awaken its intelligence.

“Elder Mo, why does it smell so good?” Lin Jing asked, noticing that the water in the tank was bubbling and steaming.

It seemed the Flame Dragon Carp was… breathing fire in the water.

“Elder Jiang said this creature hasn’t mastered its flame powers yet. It often nearly cooks itself, so the water smells like fish soup,” Elder Mo explained.

Lin Jing: ???

Wait, is it not just unintelligent but outright dumb?

Was this carp destined to evolve into a rare beast like the Hundred Fragrance Rabbit, which famously mutated into the delicacy “boiled fish”?

Elder Mo warned, “Keep an eye on your Pine Leaf Mouse, and don’t even think about tasting it yourself. In our Beast Taming Sect, eating a fellow disciple’s beast is a serious taboo!”

“That kind of behavior belongs to the demonic sects.”

“Don’t worry, Elder. We’re not that desperate,” Lin Jing replied wryly.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 62: The Sect Leader Returns
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 62: The Sect Leader Returns

The arrival of the Flame Dragon Carp brought new warmth to Lin Jing’s residence.

Where there had once been only him and the Pine Leaf Mouse, now there was a third occupant.

Unfortunately, the Mechanical Bamboo Turtle, lacking intelligence, didn’t add much to the lively atmosphere. Still, it didn’t matter—the carp and the turtle shared the same unthinking demeanor, so they got along just fine.

For the next few days, Lin Jing remained indoors, resting in bed. He was utterly exhausted, unsure if it was due to overusing his illusionary techniques or the aftereffects of tapping into the Ice Soul Gu's power. His face had grown noticeably haggard.

Though Lin Jing was frail, the Pine Leaf Mouse was unusually energetic. Inside its Suspended Gourd, it undertook massive construction projects, planting trees and cultivating crops.

The gourd soon teemed with life: spiritual rice, spiritual beans, spiritual bamboo, and even the few chili seeds the mouse had secretly hidden were now flourishing. In just a few days, these spirit plants had fully matured.

Peering into the gourd, Lin Jing saw the Pine Leaf Mouse draped in a Daoist robe, its vibrant emerald qi radiating outward. With a wave of its paws, the wind howled, and golden stalks of spirit rice shot up, growing as if on steroids.

“The Everlasting Emperor's Longevity Art suits it perfectly,” Lin Jing muttered.

For a spirit beast, such a compatible technique was a rare treasure. To the Pine Leaf Mouse, this opportunity was akin to a full-scale bloodline evolution. With its Glazed Immortal Eyes complementing its cultivation, the mouse could become a resource-hoarding powerhouse. Even without a king-level bloodline, given enough time, it might someday challenge king-level beast cubs.

Once Lin Jing recovered, he stepped out of his cave dwelling. Outside, the sky was a mosaic of dazzling colors, the warm sunlight spilling gently over Danxia Peak.

He chose not to disturb the Pine Leaf Mouse, busy with its hoarding spree, and instead focused on finding more suitable food for the Dragon Carp. Feeding it spiritual rice for days had filled the air with the tantalizing aroma of fish and rice porridge. He feared his cave might soon be mistaken for a farmstead.

The Pine Leaf Mouse was content with spiritual rice, but the Dragon Carp needed a diet of small shrimp or insects.

In the end, Lin Jing spent a fortune at Nine Dragons Peak to procure a bag of shrimp raised in spiritual springs, said to be highly nutritious.

“Hurry up and eat~” he called, tossing the shrimp into the pond he had dug in his cave. The fish tank had become too cramped for the carp’s daily activities, and the pond allowed it more freedom to move. Unless traveling, the tank would remain unused.

As Lin Jing fed the carp, he began its "early education."

“Don’t follow the Pine Leaf Mouse’s example. It refuses to even transform into a weapon for me to use as a Beast Tamer.”

“I’ll take good care of you. Once you transform into a dragon, how about I use Beast-Weaponization to turn you into a spear?”

He imagined himself donning silver armor, wielding a spear with the power of a dragon.

But as the fantasy played out, the spear morphed into a sniper rifle. Lin Jing shook his head furiously.

Determined to focus, Lin Jing retreated to his alchemy room to practice.

The Dragon Carp, seemingly incapable of providing Beast Flame anytime soon, left him reliant on Earth Flame for refining pills. While Earth Flame produced lower-quality pills with reduced success rates, it was excellent for honing his fire control techniques.

With ample spirit plants cultivated by the Pine Leaf Mouse, Lin Jing had a steady supply of materials for crafting Replenishing Pills and Misdirection Pills. The sheer volume of practice allowed him to sharpen his skills significantly.

A month passed in the blink of an eye.

During this time, the cultivation levels of Lin Jing, the Pine Leaf Mouse, and the Dragon Carp remained stagnant. However, their foundational strength grew considerably.

The Pine Leaf Mouse hoarded supplies with zeal, while Lin Jing amassed a sizable stockpile of Misdirection Pills through diligent alchemy practice.

By now, his success rate for crafting Replenishing Pills and Misdirection Pills exceeded 90%. This was impressive for someone at Qi Refining Stage Four, using only Earth Flame and basic alchemical tools. With better equipment, he might achieve 100% success.

Lin Jing also dabbled in mechanized techniques and alchemy, expanding his skill set further. Yet, he still had much to accomplish. Most pressing was mastering the Sacred Wood Seal, taught to him by Shen Song.

The seal required wood-element power to function, something Lin Jing lacked. Thus, he and the Pine Leaf Mouse would need to practice together, their energies combining to cast it. This training could no longer be delayed, as it directly affected his ability to harness the Ice Soul Gu's power.

Closing the alchemy room, Lin Jing called out to the Pine Leaf Mouse within the Suspended Gourd, “It’s been a month. Let’s practice the sealing technique together.”

“Once we’ve made progress, you can focus on advancing your cultivation, and I’ll work on mine.”

He produced a bag of shrimp, the next target for their sealing experiments.

Meanwhile, far away, a flock of celestial cranes descended upon the Beast Taming Sect’s protective formation, carrying passengers into the sect.

At the forefront sat a weathered man, flanked by young disciples—men, women, and others of androgynous appearance—all exuding extraordinary auras. Surrounding them were fearsome Demonic Kings, their menacing expressions belying their role as protectors.

“The Sect Leader and the Ancestors have returned!”

Many elders rushed out to greet them, puzzled by the early return of the sect leader and his party, who had gone out to train the sect’s true disciples. Weren’t they supposed to proceed directly to the Grand Celestial Gathering after the training?

Within the grand hall atop the Beast Taming Sect’s peak, the Fifth Sect Leader, Xu Xiaoyao, sat at the head of the assembly.

Seated on either side of him were Golden Core elders. To everyone’s surprise, rarely-seen Nascent Soul-level Demonic King ancestors had also arrived, either in person or via avatars.

Elder Mo, eager to share his discovery of Lin Jing, could barely contain himself. To him, Lin Jing was a prodigy who could ensure the sect’s prosperity for another ten millennia. With such a talent, the current imbalance—where six Demonic Kings held greater power than the sect leader—might finally be addressed.

However, Xu Xiaoyao’s next words left the assembly in shock.

“Everyone, our Beast Taming Sect… may need to disband temporarily. Soon, the sect’s spiritual veins will be sealed.”

“The Sect Regulatory Office has issued a decree. Our sect's demonic fortune has surpassed its human fortune, and the current balance no longer aligns with the nation's destiny. They have ordered us to close the sect for a hundred years. If we cannot rectify the situation within that time… the Beast Taming Sect may cease to exist.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 63: The End of the Beast Sect?
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 63: The End of the Beast Sect?

The sect leader’s announcement stunned the gathered elders. Their minds reeled as if ten thousand demonic beasts were stampeding through. What? The sect is ending?

“D*mn it! In what way is the Beast Taming Sect a demonic sect?!” A red-haired Golden Core Elder roared in frustration. Beside him, the six Demonic King ancestors nodded solemnly in agreement. “Exactly!”

One of the ancestors, a parrot demon king, squawked furiously, “This is outrageous! The Sect Regulatory Office is targeting us just because the Beast Taming Sect’s top fighters happen to be demonic kings!”

“Which of us hasn’t shed blood for the sect? Which of us hasn’t fought for the Ancient Celestial Kingdom? Just because Beast Tamers die young, they treat us as outcasts? This is blatant discrimination!”

“If it’s so unfair, I might as well end it myself! Don’t try to stop me—none of you!”

The room fell silent. When no one moved to stop the parrot demon king, he awkwardly chose not to follow through on his threat.

Another ancestor, a flood dragon, narrowed its eyes. “We do occupy the sect’s finest training caves and claim the most resources, but is that truly wrong? We’ve fought alongside the previous sect leaders for centuries, some of us for over a thousand years. Don’t we deserve such rewards? Should we starve ourselves to prove we’re aligned with human prosperity?”

The Golden Core elders remained silent. The demonic kings’ contributions to the sect were undeniable, as was their claim to its best resources. But the Sect Regulatory Office cared only for Heavenly Fortune. The Ancient Celestial Kingdom, built on the principle of fortune alignment, prioritized human fortune over all else.

“Blame me?!” Xu Xiaoyao shot to his feet. “You’re the ones parading around under the Beast Taming Sect’s banner! The other sects in the Great Wasteland are tired of being bullied by a pack of demonic kings with a legitimate front. Of course, they reported us to the Sect Regulatory Office! Maybe instead of retiring in luxury, you should find a new Beast Tamer to contract with!”

“Fine! But who?” A stag ancestor retorted casually. “There’s not a single other Nascent Soul in the sect besides you. Tell me, who can contract us?”

The grand hall descended into chaos as the ancestors and elders argued. Elder Mo, sitting near the back, trembled in his seat. He kept quiet about Lin Jing, afraid the young prodigy might end up as a snack for these agitated ancestors.

---

“The weather is wonderful today.”

Lin Jing enjoyed climbing to the top of his cave dwelling to bask in the morning glow, breathing in fresh spiritual energy as he cultivated. The light bathed him in a celestial radiance, making him appear like an immortal descended to the mortal world.

“Life feels more hopeful every day. Once I reach Foundation Establishment, I’ll officially be a true disciple.”

“Titles like ‘future holy son’ or ‘junior sect leader’ aren’t for me. Let the younger folks have those. With my longevity, I’ll be here to support the sect for far longer. Besides, becoming sect leader raises the question of when to step down. According to tradition, sect leaders only abdicate when near death. That’s too much of a hassle.”

He closed his eyes and focused on cultivating, his calm heart nurtured by his Longevity Body.

Not long after, a flustered figure approached from the distance.

“Junior Brother! Something terrible has happened!”

Senior Brother Ouyang, his face pale, grabbed Lin Jing by the sleeve as he descended from his dwelling.

Seeing Ouyang’s panic, Lin Jing frowned. “Senior Brother, what’s wrong? Weren’t you attempting to break through to Foundation Establishment? Did something go wrong?”

“Forget that—Sect Leader Xu has returned.”

“Isn’t that good news? With the sect leader here, your chances of a successful breakthrough should improve.”

Ouyang’s expression grew even grimmer. “The sect leader brought news that… the Beast Taming Sect may be disbanded!”

“Wait, what?!” Lin Jing’s composure faltered.

“Senior Brother, you must be joking! I’ve only been here for a year since you invited me to join, and now you’re telling me the sect is disbanding? With all our resources and heritage, how could this happen?”

Ouyang explained the Sect Regulatory Office’s decree: a century-long sect closure for reform. If the Beast Taming Sect failed to meet the required adjustments, it would face permanent dissolution.

The Sect Regulatory Office, an organization on par with the Demon-Slaying Bureau and Hidden Dragon Academy, wielded immense power in overseeing the Ancient Celestial Kingdom’s sects.

“Seal the sect for a century? And if the reform fails, abolish it entirely?” Lin Jing stared blankly at the sky, struggling to process the sudden turn of events.

“Once the spiritual veins are sealed, our peaks will no longer be suitable for cultivation,” Ouyang said, his voice heavy with despair.

“The Sect Regulatory Office demands that human fortune outweigh demonic fortune. How can we achieve that when our sect has eight demonic king ancestors and only one Nascent Soul human? How many Nascent Souls would we need to balance the scales?”

Ouyang sighed deeply. “We can’t exactly ask the ancestors to… sacrifice themselves. Without them, the Beast Taming Sect would fall to a third-rate sect in the Great Wasteland.”

“Did the sect leader mention any plans?” Lin Jing asked after a moment of silence.

“He did.” Ouyang nodded. “Sect Leader Xu intends to divide the sect into four groups and relocate the members.”

“First, the sect has always been on good terms with Hundred Flower Valley. The second, third, and fourth sect leaders were all married to its leaders. A portion of our members will take refuge there.”

“However, we’re too large to move everyone. The second group will join the Demon-Slaying Bureau as part of our alliance.”

“The third group will leave the Ancient Celestial Kingdom entirely, heading to Qingzhou, where the sect leader’s adopted daughter—our senior sister—has already gone to explore new opportunities.”

Lin Jing exhaled deeply. While technically not disbandment, these plans felt akin to dissolution.

He reflected on Hundred Flower Valley, a second-rate sect with only female disciples who raised spiritual butterflies. Meanwhile, Qingzhou, outside the kingdom’s jurisdiction, offered chaos but also greater opportunities for growth.

“Wait… the sect leader has a daughter?” Lin Jing blinked in surprise.

“She’s adopted. A girl he found as a baby years ago.”

“I see.”

“And the fourth group?” Lin Jing asked.

“The ancestors plan to take on true disciples and personally protect them. These disciples will train wherever they deem suitable, guided by their Nascent Soul demonic king masters.”

“Senior Brother, why did you rush over to tell me all this?” Lin Jing asked.

Ouyang hesitated before replying, “I heard the ancestors are already arguing over how to divide the sect’s resources. Everyone wants to bring as much heritage as possible to their new destination.”

“Even the sect leader suggested that emptying our centuries of accumulated resources might be the only way for the Beast Taming Sect to rise again after breaking.”

“I remember you’re heading to the Hidden Dragon Academy, right? With Senior Shen Song as your protector, you may not need to rely on the ancestors. But you should ask him to advocate for you. You’re the sect’s future—claim as much as you can before it’s all gone!”

Lin Jing fell silent. Seriously? They’re already splitting the family inheritance? Can’t we at least try bribery or backdoor negotiations before giving up entirely?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 64: The Sect Disbands Before the Journey to Immortality Is Halfway Complete
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 64: The Sect Disbands Before the Journey to Immortality Is Halfway Complete

At Tiandu Peak, under Senior Brother Ouyang's urging, Lin Jing visited again. Standing before the towering Divine Pine, he took a deep breath and said, "Senior Divine Pine... I apologize for disturbing you again."

"You're here about the sect sealing?" The Divine Pine's glow flickered as if it had seen through everything.

"You already know, Senior?"

"Yes, I overheard their meeting."

"?" Lin Jing fell silent. For the oldest guardian of the sect, eavesdropping seemed oddly unbecoming.

The Divine Pine continued, its voice transmitted directly to his mind: "Back when I made a pact with your Second Sect Master, it was to protect the Beast Taming Sect from annihilation within the limits of my power."

"But this matter exceeds my abilities."

"The only way forward is for the human cultivators of the Beast Taming Sect to find a solution themselves."

"The Beast Taming Sect has many Demon King ancestors. I believe one of them will agree to protect you," the Divine Pine replied. "Don’t ask me."

"But I still have an unfulfilled contract with you, Senior," Lin Jing argued. "My body carries some of your treasures."

"Wouldn’t it be a bit improper to entrust me to someone else’s protection?"

"If they fail to safeguard me, and I perish, it’ll be over for your belongings too!"

"Even the Tianyuan Bank, with its excellent reputation, has occasionally lost cultivators' deposits of Spirit Stones due to mishaps or embezzlement, hasn't it?"

"Shouldn’t one always look after their own property?" Lin Jing reasoned earnestly, hoping to sway the Divine Pine.

The Divine Pine remained silent. This Longevity Body... why was it so clingy?

"Senior!" Lin Jing pleaded. "If not for me, then at least for your disciple, the Pine Leaf Mouse. It's a rare beast and will easily attract covetous eyes!"

"When did I ever accept it as my disciple? Don't go spouting nonsense with that Longevity Body of yours," the Divine Pine grumbled, its bark rippling in protest.

"Not a master and disciple, but closer than master and disciple," Lin Jing insisted. "Every night when it sleeps, it dreams of your teachings. It swears to repay your kindness once it attains power."

"Say what you want; I won’t become the protector of a Longevity Body."

"Just a hundred years!" Lin Jing pleaded. "Senior, you only need to protect me when I'm in danger. I won’t bother you with trivial matters otherwise."

Lin Jing had little rapport with the other Demon King ancestors. If he had to choose a protector, he trusted the Divine Pine far more. He would beg and plead if that’s what it took to convince this guardian to act.

The Divine Pine fell silent for a long time before sighing. "Fine, one hundred years. After a century, regardless of the Beast Taming Sect’s fate, I will consider my agreement with your Second Sect Master fulfilled. After that, I will leave."

Lin Jing froze. He wasn’t sure what level his cultivation would reach in a century.

"Senior Divine Pine, this is Sect Master Xu Xiaoyao requesting an audience," a voice called from outside Tiandu Peak.

"Come up," the Divine Pine replied.

Shortly after, Sect Master Xu arrived at the peak. Dressed in white, his expression was weathered. Upon seeing Lin Jing, he forced a smile and nodded in greeting.

"Greetings, Senior Divine Pine."

"This must be the Longevity Body disciple you favor, Lin Jing?"

"Greetings, Sect Master," Lin Jing said, bowing.

The Divine Pine addressed Xu Xiaoyao, "Fifth-generation Sect Master, what brings you here?"

"I heard you had emerged, Senior, and even saved a disciple. I came to express my gratitude. Additionally, have you heard the Sect Regulatory Office’s announcement?"

"Of course," the Divine Pine said. "I can’t do much about it. For the next century, I will act as this disciple’s protector, shielding him as he grows. Consider it my final contribution to the Beast Taming Sect."

Hearing this, Sect Master Xu Xiaoyao immediately expressed his gratitude. "Thank you, Senior Divine Pine. With your assurance, I am relieved. Lin Jing may not have been with the sect for long, but he has won the favor of several elders. He’s a good child. I entrust him to you."

"At Hidden Dragon Academy, study diligently," he advised Lin Jing. "Your goal should be to become an Immortal Official."

Lin Jing bowed. "I will follow the Sect Master’s advice. I will dedicate myself to studying at the academy and work to restore the Beast Taming Sect."

"I trust you," Xu Xiaoyao said with a smile. He had faith in Lin Jing.

It was a pity, though. As a Longevity Body, Lin Jing's cultivation speed was bound to be slow. Reaching Foundation Establishment within a century might even prove difficult.

"Before you leave, make sure to memorize the sect’s three core secret techniques. Begin learning them after reaching Foundation Establishment."

"Is there anything else you need?" Xu Xiaoyao asked.

Lin Jing seized the opportunity. "I’d like to request a second Beast Taming Space artifact."

"Hm?" Xu Xiaoyao hesitated briefly but didn’t question the reason. Lin Jing’s prepared excuses went unspoken.

"Granted!" the Sect Master agreed without delay.

...

In the end, Lin Jing made only that one request, receiving a Meditation Staff in return. Knowing when to stop, he refrained from asking for more.

Back at his cave dwelling, however, Sect Master Xu still sent a Heavenfeather Crane to deliver 100,000 Spirit Stones.

"Beast Taming Sect..." Lin Jing sighed again.

The Pine Leaf Mouse emerged, quietly standing on his shoulder. It had already learned of the sect's impending sealing.

"Though I haven’t been with the Beast Taming Sect for long, it’s undeniable that now and in the future, we will remain its disciples."

"As the sect treated us, so shall we cherish it."

Holding the Pine Leaf Mouse in his hands, Lin Jing gazed at the glow atop Danxia Peak.

"We’ll stay for a few more days, then use our token to summon academy cultivators to retrieve us," Lin Jing said.

"Squeak!" The Pine Leaf Mouse looked up, asking if they might one day return home.

Lin Jing nodded. "We will."

Their melancholy was interrupted as a pinecone flew over, carrying a voice. "With your current strength, even at Hidden Dragon Academy, you’ll only get beaten up. The Hidden Dragon struggle is about fortune. Without strength, how will you compete?"

"Longevity boy, stop holding back. Use your Longevity Blood to nourish your beasts already. If you’ve raised Gu worms, why hesitate?"

"I’ll take you to the Great Wasteland to seclude yourselves for a year. There’s a Malignant Plant there called Bloodsucking Vine. It absorbs beast blood to grow and evolve. Feed it your blood, then let the Pine Leaf Mouse use the 'Everlasting Emperor's Longevity Art' to absorb its essence. That’ll help it master the technique more quickly!"

"Understood, Senior." Lin Jing readily accepted the suggestion. As long as the Pine Leaf Mouse didn’t directly consume his blood and flesh, he could manage.

The vine would harm him, and the Pine Leaf Mouse would slay it. Wasn’t this an example of a pet protecting its Beast Tamer? It wasn’t demonic—it was the light of righteousness.

He had considered using alchemy to refine blood-based pills but lacked the skill for such an endeavor. Using the Divine Pine’s method to leverage his physique seemed like a practical solution.

"Senior, if I let regular mosquitoes drink my blood, and after they digest it, feed them to the dragon carp in my cave, that shouldn’t make the dragon carp develop bloodthirsty habits, right?" Lin Jing proposed, attempting to refine the process further.

"An excellent idea! It reminds me of my younger days," the Divine Pine approved.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 65: The Westward Journey for Immortal Teachings
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 65: The Westward Journey for Immortal Teachings

The following day, the Beast Taming Sect remained calm as usual. Ordinary disciples were still unaware of the Sect Regulatory Office’s directive.

Having learned of it early, Lin Jing had begun preparing to leave the sect again. Elder Divine Pine intended to take the Pine Leaf Mouse into the Great Wasteland to cultivate the Everlasting Emperor's Longevity Art, so Lin Jing dared not delay.

Before departing, he visited familiar figures such as Elder Black Bear, Elder Mo, and Elder Yu—elders who had always been kind to him. Who knew when he might see them again?

However, upon returning, Lin Jing wore a strange expression. These elders didn’t seem as upset as he had expected.

“They actually seem... happy?”

A pinecone appeared from nowhere and said, “Of course, those Golden Core elders wouldn’t be sad. Ninety percent of the Beast Taming Sect’s resources have always been controlled by the Demon King ancestors.

“Now, due to the Sect Regulatory Office’s directive, those Demon Kings are forced to allocate the sect’s resources to cultivate human cultivators.

“They’ve had no hope of breaking through to Nascent Soul, stuck at the peak of Golden Core for years. But now, with the sect’s transformation, each elder is allocated a significant amount of resources. With enough opportunity, they might seize this chance to advance. Why should they be upset?”

Lin Jing nodded. “True. It’s the Demon King ancestors who should be upset, not the elders.”

“My beloved Tiandu Peak…” Elder Divine Pine sighed. “I was so fond of that peak.”

“But there’s no helping it. Even the top-tier immortal sects must heed the Sect Regulatory Office’s commands. A few Demon Kings are powerless to resist. Now, it’s up to the Beast Taming Sect’s human cultivators to prove their worth.”

Divine Pine glanced longingly at Tiandu Peak.

“Well, since your farewells are done, pack your things. It’s time to set out. A hundred years is fleeting. Treasure the time I’m here with you.”

At Elder Divine Pine’s words, Lin Jing immediately returned to his cave and picked up his brand-new artifact, the Beast Taming Meditation Staff.

“Hidden Dragon Academy is located in the ancient domain west of the Great Wasteland. This journey really is a westward quest for immortal teachings.”

Lin Jing inspected the staff again.

Typical meditation staffs were wooden, exuding a natural and serene aura aligned with the Buddhist ideal of purity and returning to nature.

However, the Beast Taming Sect had no such aesthetic concerns. They used whatever materials were available. This staff was metallic, with a smooth, cool surface adorned with simple cloud patterns.

The staff’s head was composed of two interlocking rings, forming a spherical shape symbolizing perfection and inclusivity.

Lin Jing looked at the dragon carp in his pond, scooped it up, and wedged it into the spherical staff head. The carp fit snugly—an embodiment of the staff’s motto of inclusivity and perfection.

“Alright, fish, spit fire!” he commanded, raising the staff high.

After a month of training, the dragon carp had learned to respond to simple instructions.

Hearing the word “fire,” its dull eyes lit up. Opening its mouth, it unleashed a fiery jet.

“Fire Control Technique!”

With the staff in one hand and a technique gesture in the other, Lin Jing manipulated the flame into a crude fire dragon that coiled in midair.

He nodded in satisfaction.

Unfortunately, the space artifact could only house contracted pets after being refined. The dragon carp couldn’t move in just yet. Thankfully, since it had mutated into a flame carp, its dependence on water had diminished. Now, it treated the water as a hot spring for relaxation. The only downside was the occasional risk of it turning into fish soup.

Besides soaking in the water, Lin Jing noticed it also enjoyed sunbathing, which minimized travel hassles.

“Squeak…” The Pine Leaf Mouse emerged from the gourd, staring at the dragon carp lodged in the staff. It seemed to ponder whether this was a fortunate or unfortunate fate for the fish.

After bidding farewell to the elders, Lin Jing also copied the three core secret techniques of the sect: the Fusion Secret Art, Immortal Rebirth, and Beast-Weaponization. He also acquired the complete Beast Taming Technique.

These techniques would become critical for him to achieve powerful combat strength after Foundation Establishment.

In the Qi Refining stage, Beast Taming cultivators focused on laying a foundation. Their strength depended entirely on their pets. But upon reaching Foundation Establishment, they could learn the core secret techniques, enabling the cultivators themselves to unleash formidable power. Lin Jing eagerly anticipated that day.

...

“The Longevity Body has departed.”

Atop the sect’s peak, Sect Master Xu Xiaoyao stood beside a snowy-white Immortal Crane.

“A Longevity Body joined the sect, and Elder Divine Pine emerged to protect it. Who would’ve thought that so much could change while I was away? I’m curious—has Elder Divine Pine’s cultivation reached the Nascent Soul stage?”

“It’s occupied Tiandu Peak for countless years. Every sect master has found it unfathomable. It’s arguably the Beast Taming Sect’s strongest force. With its protection, the Longevity Body brought back by Elder Mo should thrive,” the crane said.

“If Elder Divine Pine truly is at the Nascent Soul stage, the Beast Taming Sect’s Demonic Fortune would be incredible. Wouldn’t it bear full responsibility?”

“That’s unlikely. Let’s focus on what’s ahead. This is the sect’s greatest transformation in a millennium. Whether it leads to decline or a return to glory depends on this century,” the fifth-generation sect master mused.

In his gaze, Lin Jing, who had left the mountain gate, sat cross-legged on a cluster of red leaves, holding the dragon carp meditation staff as he ventured into the depths of the Great Wasteland.

The Pine Leaf Mouse controlled the red leaves, their glow enhanced by Buddhist radiance and the Everlasting Emperor's Longevity Art. Its mastery over flora allowed it to maneuver a hundred leaves simultaneously. By combining dozens of leaves, it could barely manage to carry a person in flight for a short distance.

“Senior Divine Tree, which way is the Bloodsucking Vine?” Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse, unfamiliar with the terrain, relied on Elder Divine Pine for guidance.

The Divine Pine’s voice echoed in their minds, filling them with anticipation. The Pine Leaf Mouse accelerated, their red glow streaking across the sky like a shooting star.

The Great Wasteland loomed with its towering mountains and boundless terrain. Beyond it lay another region. But the wasteland’s depths were teeming with ferocious beasts, malignant plants, and perilous grounds. Only high-level Golden Core cultivators could dream of traversing it.

After taking breaks to rest, Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse resumed their journey without incident. A few man-eating monsters emerged, but Lin Jing’s subtle use of Illusionary Path sent them fleeing in terror. The Divine Pine didn’t even need to intervene.

Mist enshrouded the mountains as they arrived at a serene lake. Guided by Elder Divine Pine’s voice, they stopped. The area teemed with demons, making it uncharted territory even for Beast Taming Sect disciples.

The lake’s edge bloomed with wildflowers, and verdant vines stretched from the water to the land. The tranquil setting seemed idyllic. Clear lake water attracted small beasts, but as they drank, vines snared them, dragging them into the depths. Moments later, crimson ripples spread across the surface.

“The Bloodsucking Vine,” Divine Pine remarked. “A second-tier malignant plant capable of devouring even Foundation Establishment beasts when fully grown. Sadly, the times have changed. It now relies on instinct alone, unable to develop intelligence, which makes it less dangerous.”

“Do you know how to set up a Spirit Gathering Formation? Train here for a while. There are no powerful demons nearby, and the lake has plenty of mosquitoes that the vine likes. It’s perfect for you.”

Lin Jing nodded. “Everything has spirit, even flora. Except mosquitoes—those pests have none. I’ll cultivate here and see which mosquito dares to drink my blood. Fish, you’ll stand guard.”

“Blub...” The dragon carp’s dull eyes betrayed its struggle to comprehend.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 66: The Demon of the Deep Wasteland
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 66: The Demon of the Deep Wasteland

In the depths of the Great Wasteland, after finding a suitable cultivation spot, Lin Jing began constructing a simple Spirit Gathering Formation using Spirit Stones, establishing a temporary cave dwelling. While the spiritual energy here wasn’t as abundant as within the Beast Taming Sect, Lin Jing had a wealth of 100,000 Spirit Stones. Even if the ambient energy was lacking, this financial advantage allowed him to sustain a reasonable cultivation pace.

With this many Spirit Stones, even the slow cultivation of a Longevity Body could be sped up. After all, what inefficiency couldn’t be solved by throwing money at it?

While Lin Jing set up the formation, the Pine Leaf Mouse eagerly grabbed a Bloodsucking Vine and began channeling its energy to absorb its wood essence. Although Bloodsucking Vines were considered deadly to most beasts, this particular vine wasn’t fully mature. Moreover, the Pine Leaf Mouse, empowered by the Everlasting Emperor's Longevity Art, exerted a natural suppression over such malignant plants.

The modified Everlasting Emperor's Longevity Art had four stages: Entry, Minor Accomplishment, Major Accomplishment, and Perfection. Reaching Minor Accomplishment alone extended lifespan by 500 years—the average lifespan of a Golden Core cultivator.

According to Elder Divine Pine, once the Pine Leaf Mouse achieved Minor Accomplishment, the resulting life-force transformation would enhance its ability to control plants. It would even be able to transform an ordinary mountain ginseng into a century-old spiritual ginseng in moments.

"Finished setting up? If so, start bleeding," Elder Divine Pine urged.

By the lakeside, the Pine Leaf Mouse clung to the vine, its innocent eyes wide open. Green energy swirled around it, draining the vine's vitality.

Lin Jing nodded and, with a small cut to his finger, let a drop of blood fall onto the vine.

The Longevity Blood dripped onto the vine, which immediately began to convulse violently as if awakened by instinct. It absorbed the blood greedily, and within moments, the vine grew thicker and longer, swelling as if imbued with years’ worth of power. It now wriggled like a massive green python.

“That’s... intense.”

Lin Jing took a cautious step back. It was his first time witnessing the effects of his Longevity Blood. This was just an ordinary drop, not even his essence blood, and the amount he had used was negligible.

"Chirp!"

The Pine Leaf Mouse, thrown into the air by the now revitalized vine, refused to let go. Instead, it bit down on the plant and continued siphoning its essence with the Everlasting Emperor's Longevity Art.

As the essence coursed through its body, the Pine Leaf Mouse felt a profound change. The wood energy nourished its entire being, making it feel as if it had shed years off its young one-year-old age, returning to an almost prenatal state.

A green seed seemed to germinate within its body, spreading vitality throughout, refining its organs, and suffusing it with life force.

“No wonder the Longevity Body can awaken such ‘spirituality’ in a Bloodsucking Vine in this era,” Elder Divine Pine remarked, its pinecone form floating nearby. “It’s a shame that it still can’t grant true sentience to plants. Perhaps when your cultivation reaches the Nascent Soul stage, a single drop of blood might create plant spirits.”

“Are you feeling lonely, Senior?” Lin Jing asked, noting the scarcity of sentient plants in this age.

“I am. Back in my homeland, I devoured all the other plants that achieved spirit alongside me. It’s been ages since I’ve had a proper ‘companion.’ I hear there are more plant spirits in Qingzhou. I must visit someday,” the demonic pine muttered.

“...” Lin Jing stopped commenting and focused on observing the Pine Leaf Mouse’s transformation.

As the Pine Leaf Mouse drained the vine dry, Lin Jing offered another drop of blood. The process repeated, with the vine regaining its vitality, becoming even more vigorous than before.

Day after day, this cycle continued. Unknowingly, the Pine Leaf Mouse reached the eighth layer of the Qi Refining stage. Its emerald eyes glowed more brilliantly, and each breath it took seemed to draw reverence from the surrounding plants.

Meanwhile, Lin Jing sat cross-legged, cultivating the Beast Taming Technique while occasionally feeding blood to the plants. The lake was cleared of threats, so he allowed the dragon carp to enter the waters.

The carp, besides feasting on small insects and shrimp, also nibbled on aquatic plants and fallen seeds. Occasionally, it even chewed on the weakened Bloodsucking Vine.

The carp’s favorite delicacy, however, seemed to be the mosquitoes that tried to drink Lin Jing’s blood.

Some mosquitoes, after sipping even a trace of Longevity Blood, began undergoing life-force transformations. The more gifted among them even reached the first layer of Qi Refining, becoming mosquito spirits.

“These mosquitoes have stumbled upon incredible fortune, consuming what even Demon Kings and Emperors would covet,” Elder Divine Pine marveled.

“Too bad their lives are so short.”

Most mosquitoes were swatted away by Lin Jing or burned by the carp’s fiery breath, becoming the fish’s next meal.

“Blub… blub…” The dragon carp bubbled contentedly, claiming credit for protecting its Beast Tamer from these terrifying ‘monsters.’ Over time, it had begun developing intelligence, albeit at a low level, comparable to the Pine Leaf Mouse in its early days.

“As expected of you. From now on, any beast that harms me is yours to deal with,” Lin Jing encouraged the carp, earning a spirited display of swimming from the fish.

Turning to Elder Divine Pine, Lin Jing asked, “Senior, can you discern this carp’s bloodline?”

“Of course. It’s a dragon carp,” Elder Divine Pine said plainly.

Lin Jing fell silent. While true, it wasn’t particularly enlightening.

“Dragonblood creatures have a direct correlation between bloodline strength and physical prowess. If you want to know the potency of its dragon blood, observe its physical potential. Only carp capable of leaping over the Dragon Gate possess the strength to resist waterfalls.”

“Are you suggesting I begin body tempering with it now?”

“Indeed. Before that, however, I have a demonic technique that can test its physical limits.”

Another demonic technique?

“I’m joking,” Elder Divine Pine chuckled. “It’s merely a common-sense method in the cultivation world. For beasts with powerful bloodlines, extreme pain can stimulate their physical potential, pushing them to display extraordinary strength.”

“Beasts with no potential will grow weaker from pain, while those with latent talent will gain strength.”

“Many beasts achieve breakthroughs after life-or-death battles, driven by the pain of combat to unlock their hidden power. Do you understand?”

The dragon carp, though newly intelligent, was not foolish. It dove to the lakebed, avoiding the conversation entirely. Clearly, it recognized the danger.

Lin Jing shook his head. Such methods seemed too cruel. Instead, he pulled out several black peppers, intending to improve the carp’s meals as a reward for its diligent service.

“Senior, care for one?” he offered, holding out a pepper as if passing a cigarette.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 67: Ferocious and Ruthless
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 67: Ferocious and Ruthless

Elder Divine Pine wasted no time, extending a twig from its pinecone form to snatch the black pepper, draining its essence in an instant.

After savoring it, the pinecone remarked, “It has a demonic flavor, with a powerful afterburn. It ignites a fire in me. You’re full of surprises; I knew I didn’t misjudge you all.”

Lin Jing felt wronged but said nothing.

“Wait, were you planning to feed that pepper to the dragon carp? At its current stage, a whole pepper would probably turn it into spicy fish soup tonight.”

“Of course not! I’m not that reckless,” Lin Jing replied, walking to the lakeside. “I’ll slice it into thin strips before feeding it. I’m not irresponsible.”

“Ah, the precision of a demonic cultivator with a monk’s heart. Not bad. It seems my words are sinking in. This pepper’s soul-piercing spiciness might help us assess its physical limits,” Elder Divine Pine observed.

Lin Jing extended his arm, luring a swarm of mosquitoes to himself. He swatted them, sliced some thin pepper strips, and mixed everything together in a dish, creating a fiery mosquito salad.

“It’s colorful, aromatic, and deliciously spicy. I might have a hidden talent for immortal cooking,” he mused before calling the dragon carp.

“The initial bite might sting, but you’ll come to love it. Spicy hot pot is my favorite,” Lin Jing said.

Hearing that it was a dish even its Beast Tamer enjoyed, the carp opened its mouth wide. Lin Jing poured the food in.

At first, the dragon carp froze, savoring the taste.

Then the heat hit. Its body grew feverishly hot, flames of spiritual energy erupting within it. The carp darted frantically around the lake, a streak of fire flowing through the water.

“Blub!”

The carp swam faster and faster, doubling its usual speed. Soon, the delayed spice fully kicked in, its heat scorching even the soul. The carp thrashed wildly, caught between pain and exhilaration. Its reaction was far more intense than the Pine Leaf Mouse’s earlier experience.

Unable to contain its energy, the dragon carp lashed its tail against the water.

Boom!

A massive explosion rocked the lake, sending waves several meters high cascading outward. The carp leaped over ten meters into the air, its fiery red eyes burning with ferocity.

“What power,” Lin Jing marveled, taking a deep breath. “It’s strong enough to challenge a waterfall already.”

“That strike was far stronger than anything you or the Pine Leaf Mouse could muster together,” Elder Divine Pine observed. “Its latent bloodline is impressive. You’re fortunate; the pets you’ve chosen have excellent potential. Raise it well, and it won’t disappoint when it transforms into a dragon.”

As it descended, the dragon carp underwent another change—black flames erupted from its body, blazing fiercely.

Splash!

It plunged back into the water, its swollen, red mouth giving it the appearance of a demonic fish freshly emerged from hell.

“Bloodlines truly matter,” Lin Jing muttered, recalling how the same black pepper had nearly knocked the Pine Leaf Mouse unconscious with pain. Yet for the dragon carp, the spice unlocked tremendous physical potential.

As a fire-aligned beast, it seemed uniquely capable of absorbing the pepper’s energy, temporarily converting it into strength. This unexpected synergy made Lin Jing grateful for Elder Mo’s recommendation of the carp.

“How’s the taste?” Lin Jing asked.

The carp nodded enthusiastically, puffing steam and signaling that it was painful yet utterly satisfying.

...

That day, Lin Jing decided on the carp’s training regimen: following Elder Divine Pine’s advice to stimulate its physical potential through controlled pain. They would start with mild spice and progress to moderate, extreme, and even diabolical levels.

The stronger its physical body became, the higher the quality of its eventual transformation into a Beast-Weaponized Treasure.

The carp’s newfound ability to control ghost flames was a welcome bonus. However, Lin Jing lamented that, between the fire-breathing fish and the bug-storing gourd, only the Pine Leaf Mouse maintained any semblance of a righteous image in their group.

“Senior, how strong do we need to be before we can head to Hidden Dragon Academy?” Lin Jing asked.

Over several days, the Pine Leaf Mouse reached the eighth layer of Qi Refining, Lin Jing advanced to the fifth, and the dragon carp to the fourth. Though everyone had progressed, their collective cultivation still felt like a patchwork mess.

“There does need to be a standard,” Elder Divine Pine said after a pause. “While I initially said not to worry about it, you represent the Beast Taming Sect’s hope. As your guardian, I must set a proper benchmark.”

With that, Elder Divine Pine manipulated the ground. A crack opened, and a wooden humanoid emerged. Standing over two meters tall, it was fully crafted from wood, with no facial features. It exuded a sense of invincible strength.

“This wooden figure maintains the combat strength of a Foundation Establishment cultivator. It knows no spells, but its strength, defense, and speed surpass those of ordinary Foundation Establishment body cultivators. It is an excellent sparring partner.”

“When you can defeat it, you’re ready to attend the academy.”

“The academy’s students are the elites of their sects and clans. To compete with them for dragon fortunes and master the Hidden Dragon Arts, you’ll need to be even more monstrous than they are!”

“Understood!” Lin Jing replied, turning to the wooden figure with determination. He wanted to test it immediately to gauge their gap in power and adjust their training accordingly.

“Shall we try now?” he asked the Pine Leaf Mouse.

The mouse glanced at the wooden figure, then at Lin Jing. They exchanged a look and instantly understood each other’s plan: retreat while shooting!

Both drew their Mechanical Bamboo Rifles and unleashed a barrage of water arrows and spirit beans as they backed away.

Pfft! Pfft! Pfft!

The attacks filled the air, but before they could land, the wooden figure vanished, moving faster than Lin Jing could follow.

By the time he registered its movement, it was already in front of him, its fist creating a gust of wind as it swung toward him.

Reacting quickly, Lin Jing shifted his rifle’s mode and unleashed a torrent of water at close range. The recoil propelled him ten meters back, creating some distance between himself and the figure.

However, the water stream didn’t even slow it down. The figure advanced through the spray as if it weren’t there.

The Pine Leaf Mouse hurled several flying leaves at it, but they shattered on impact, leaving only faint scratches on the wood.

Whoosh!

The figure charged again, becoming a blur. Before Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse could react, they were both kicked into the lake, landing with painful splashes.

“Glug… glug…”

Elder Divine Pine watched and commented, “Keep training. It won’t be easy to defeat, but if you can beat it, you’ll have a firm foothold at the academy.”

“The water’s so hot… but it smells nice,” Lin Jing muttered, spitting out soup.

“Chirp!” the Pine Leaf Mouse squeaked in agreement, equally drenched.

The dragon carp, enjoying the show, swam lazily nearby. But the next moment, the wooden figure stepped onto the lake and delivered a powerful kick, sending the carp flying. It didn’t spare anyone.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 68: As Seasons Change
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 68: As Seasons Change

Over the next two days, Lin Jing and his companions repeatedly challenged the wooden figure, only to fail every time. The Foundation Establishment wooden man seemed to enjoy kicking their butts—literally—often sending them splashing into the lake.

By now, Lin Jing felt his backside had bloomed into a lotus flower. In the future, he mused, perhaps he wouldn’t even need one to sit on to attain enlightenment.

The wooden man also demonstrated exceptional combat experience. When they attempted to fly on leaves, it hurled stones at them with pinpoint accuracy. When Lin Jing summoned the dragon carp and used the Fire Control Technique to exploit its fire weakness, the wooden man countered by shaking water from the lake to douse the flames.

Even the Pine Leaf Mouse’s Everlasting Emperor's Longevity Art—effective against plants—only briefly immobilized it before the wooden man broke free. Ghostfire and illusions were even less effective, as the wooden figure had no soul to burn or mind to deceive.

Had the wooden man been a true enemy, Lin Jing and his team would have perished countless times.

Despite their losses, Lin Jing still saw hope. Based on its reactions during battle, the wooden man indeed feared fire. Otherwise, it would have withstood fire attacks as easily as it did their flying leaves and water arrows.

Lin Jing analyzed their situation:

- Increase the dragon carp’s cultivation level.

- Help the Pine Leaf Mouse advance in the Everlasting Emperor's Longevity Art. While its magic already had some restraining effect, its limited power could only immobilize the wooden man for less than a second. Reaching Minor Accomplishment might allow it to hold the figure for longer.

“Let’s train,” Lin Jing declared.

With an ample supply of Spirit Stones and food, they threw themselves into grueling cultivation, testing their progress against the wooden man daily. Time blurred as they focused solely on growing stronger. Lin Jing did feel some regret that his Glazed Immortal Eyes weren’t advanced enough to fully analyze the mutated Bloodsucking Vine, leaving its potential untapped.

...

Spring passed into autumn, and three months flew by.

The battles with the wooden man had grown increasingly fierce, though Lin Jing’s group still couldn’t achieve victory.

By the lake, the wooden man stood immobilized. Over the past months, the Pine Leaf Mouse had reached the ninth layer of Qi Refining, and its magic had achieved Minor Accomplishment. However, its ability to absorb the Bloodsucking Vine’s essence had diminished significantly, rendering the vine nearly useless.

At this point, the Pine Leaf Mouse no longer favored the Mechanical Bamboo Rifle as a weapon, opting instead for a simple tree branch.

Wearing a flowing robe, it channeled its enhanced magic to temporarily immobilize the wooden man. Then, using its Everlasting Emperor's Longevity Art, it caused the branch to grow rapidly into a thick trunk, binding the wooden man tightly.

Plant Manipulation!

As the wooden man struggled against the bindings, Lin Jing stepped forward with the dragon carp staff, performing a hand seal. Flames erupted from the carp’s mouth, now far more intense thanks to their training.

In the three months, Lin Jing had advanced to the seventh layer of Qi Refining, while the dragon carp had reached the eighth. With their improved cultivation, the carp’s flames had become hotter and more refined. Lin Jing compressed the fire into marble-sized fireballs, directing them to strike the wooden man through the gaps in its bindings.

Boom! Boom! Boom!

The fireballs exploded on impact, leaving visible scorch marks and causing some damage to the wooden man.

However, to Lin Jing’s dismay, the scorched figure broke free of the bindings and launched a counterattack. Despite being charred and having half its head blown off, it remained combat-ready, a relentless opponent unaffected by pain or fatigue.

Exhausted and low on spiritual energy, Lin Jing, the Pine Leaf Mouse, and the dragon carp were forced to retreat yet again.

“I’m done!” Lin Jing groaned. After months of fighting, they had shifted their mindset—less reliant on stockpiled resources and more focused on testing their raw abilities. The real reason, though, was the Pine Leaf Mouse’s reluctance to waste supplies on training.

“Elder Divine Pine, I swear the wooden man’s getting stronger. Are you secretly adjusting its stats?” Lin Jing accused, lying on the ground, panting heavily.

The Pine Leaf Mouse and dragon carp, equally battered, nodded in agreement.

“So, you noticed,” Elder Divine Pine said. “It’s only natural for your opponent to grow stronger with time, just like you. If it didn’t, how would you improve? Besides, its growth is slow compared to yours.”

“Cultivating as a demonic cultivator isn’t just about surpassing your opponents briefly—you must grow faster than they do. Otherwise, the righteous path will crush you,” Elder Divine Pine added unapologetically.

Lin Jing rolled his eyes, realizing something had felt off all along.

“Let me teach you another lesson: as a demonic cultivator, never trust anyone’s words. You’re too young to believe me so easily.”

“Got it,” Lin Jing muttered, too tired to argue about not being a demonic cultivator.

“Give us a few more days. We’re close to beating it,” Lin Jing said.

“Even if we master the Sacred Wood Seal, its preparation time is too long. During a fight, the wooden man wouldn’t give us a chance to use it,” Elder Divine Pine pointed out.

The Sacred Wood Seal, originally meant to seal the Ice Soul Gu, relied on spiritual imprints that imbued wooden constructs with divine properties. Though Lin Jing had strong mental strength and precise control, he lacked the ability to manipulate plants, requiring the Pine Leaf Mouse’s cooperation. Even then, casting the technique in battle was nearly impossible.

“What if we prepare dozens of pre-imbued wooden constructs before the fight?” Lin Jing proposed.
