Elder Divine Pine scoffed. “Didn’t you say you wouldn’t rely on stockpiled resources?”

“Senior, as a demonic cultivator, I’ve learned never to trust anyone’s words—including my own. I changed my mind.”

“Well, I’ve changed my mind too,” Elder Divine Pine retorted. “I’m giving you a new challenge. Never argue with the one setting the rules—only your fists matter in the end.”

Lin Jing: ???

“Fine, fine. The old are always craftier,” Lin Jing grumbled.

Elder Divine Pine continued, “I’ve sensed the emergence of a spatial-attributed spiritual plant in the Great Wasteland. It could benefit the Pine Leaf Mouse greatly. Go claim it.”

“Squeak?” The previously despondent Pine Leaf Mouse perked up, energy surging as it scrambled upright with renewed vigor.

“A spatial plant?” Lin Jing’s interest was piqued.

“Yes, but several Nascent Soul beasts and Golden Core cultivators are also eyeing it. I won’t intervene—you’re on your own. Complete this task, and you can head to the academy.”

“Senior, on second thought, maybe we should just fight the wooden man,” Lin Jing suggested.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 69: The Qualities of a Demonic Cultivator
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 69: The Qualities of a Demonic Cultivator

“The wooden figure is ultimately a fake. It’s time to face a real challenge. A demonic cultivator turns the impossible into the possible,” Elder Divine Pine said, retracting the wooden man.

Left with no target but his own frustration, Lin Jing muttered curses under his breath.

“Having Qi Refining cultivators fight Golden Core monsters isn’t turning the impossible into the possible—it’s sending Longevity Meat Buns to feed the dogs. No one comes back from that!”

The spatial herb might be tempting, but with Nascent Soul-level beasts around, and even Foundation Establishment-level monsters being too strong for them, success seemed out of reach.

“Squeak!” The Pine Leaf Mouse tugged on Lin Jing’s pant leg, its intent clear: Let’s do this!

It had a hopeful gleam in its eye, as if the spatial herb might help fulfill the wish it couldn’t achieve back at the Bodhi Heavens.

“You think it’s that simple?” Lin Jing glared at the mouse.

Using their Thousand Illusions technique to pose as a Nascent Soul demon king and fool the other monsters was a bold idea, but the risk was astronomical. Still, seeing Elder Divine Pine’s unwavering stance, Lin Jing knew there was no room for negotiation.

“Senior, which direction?”

...

After resting and regaining their energy, Lin Jing, the Pine Leaf Mouse, and the dragon carp—disguised as a honeybee—set out again.

Black Wind Valley, the lair of a long-dead demon, lay shrouded in eerie, twisted trees. Their bare, gnarled branches rattled in the night wind like the wails of spirits.

Once abandoned and devoid of life, the valley now pulsed with a sinister demonic aura. Scores of gleaming eyes stared out from the shadows, fixated on a delicate white sprout growing at the valley’s center.

The beasts didn’t recognize the herb but were instinctively drawn to its overwhelming spatial energy and alluring fragrance. Despite their hunger, none dared act rashly.

First, the herb wasn’t fully mature. Eating it prematurely might yield no benefit.

Second, the corpses strewn around the valley—dozens of fallen beasts—warned them of the deadly cost of trying to claim it.

“Let’s retreat. This herb feels like bait,” whispered a human cultivator lurking in the darkness.

“But it’s a spatial herb! Rare and priceless. The Beast Taming Sect buys such treasures at high prices, no matter the type. If we get it, we won’t have to worry about cultivation resources for the rest of our lives!”

“The Beast Taming Sect is gone! Doesn’t that prove this herb is cursed?”

“That’s true... This place is crawling with beasts; we’re not in our element here. Let’s go.”

Unseen in the shadows, Lin Jing and his team observed the valley. Overhead, the oppressive demonic energy mingled with the dark, forested terrain. Lin Jing dared not move recklessly.

“Elder Divine Pine, are there Nascent Soul-level demon kings here?”

“If we’re in danger, you’ll step in, right?” Lin Jing asked nervously.

Elder Divine Pine stayed silent for a long time before responding, “This herb is known as Spatial Shift Grass, a fifth-grade spiritual herb. Its origin and growth process are mysteries. During its maturation, it frequently teleports to new locations, seeking diverse growth conditions.

“If you want it, act quickly. It could vanish at any moment.”

“And don’t worry about its immaturity; use the Everlasting Emperor's Longevity Art to sustain it after harvesting,” it added.

As Lin Jing mulled over this advice, a mole-like beast proficient in earth techniques tried to stealthily claim the herb. But it underestimated the hidden predators.

Boom!

A crimson beam shot from the darkness, piercing the ground. When the dust settled, the mole lay dead, a gaping hole in its chest.

Seeing this, Lin Jing found himself missing the wooden man.

“We’ll wait,” he decided.

Patience was key. There was no guarantee that posing as a Nascent Soul demon king would fool the monsters, especially with so many hidden threats.

As Lin Jing and his companions hunkered down, Elder Divine Pine watched them closely.

“Adaptability to environmental changes, calm vigilance, keen judgment, and the wisdom to act decisively in complex situations... These are the basic qualities of a demonic cultivator. Good luck, Longevity Kid.”

...

One day passed.

Two days.

Three days.

Though Elder Divine Pine had warned that the Spatial Shift Grass might teleport away, Lin Jing waited. He remained hyper-alert, keeping his illusion intact and his presence concealed, avoiding detection by the hidden monsters.

During this time, several more impatient beasts attempted to seize the herb, only to meet grisly ends. Their failures helped Lin Jing map out the positions of several lurking predators.

Then, something unexpected happened.

“That’s her?” Lin Jing murmured in surprise.

A familiar figure entered the valley—a woman in simple white robes, a long sword floating beside her. It was Ling Yue Sword Immortal Feng Ning, whom he had encountered in Snow Moon City.

Feng Ning descended gracefully, her attention clearly focused on the Spatial Shift Grass. But, like the beasts before her, she quickly became a target.

Multiple beams of demonic light shot toward her, but she was no ordinary cultivator. With a flick of her sword, she unleashed arcs of sword energy that annihilated the incoming attacks and struck down the hidden beasts with precision.

“Spatial Shift Grass?” Feng Ning murmured, stepping closer to the herb.

But before she could claim it, more powerful monsters emerged from the shadows, their forms blotting out the sky.

Without hesitation, Feng Ning’s sword spirit activated, unleashing a storm of sword energy that obliterated the charging beasts. In an instant, the valley was littered with their corpses.

As Feng Ning approached the herb, Lin Jing knew he couldn’t wait any longer.

Boom!

A towering pine tree bathed in golden-green light entered the valley, exuding the overwhelming presence of a demon king. Each step it took revitalized the land, turning the autumn decay into vibrant greenery. Dead trees sprouted fresh leaves as if spring had returned.

“A demon king is here!”

The terrifying aura, coupled with Feng Ning’s presence, shattered the resolve of many hidden beasts, forcing them to flee.

“Damn it, who told you to use my form?” Lin Jing grumbled as he stepped forward.

“And why do I look so ugly? The hairstyle is all wrong!” Elder Divine Pine replied.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 70: The Hidden Dragon Emerges (End of Book One)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chapter 70: The Hidden Dragon Emerges (End of Volume One)

Lin Jing wasn’t sure why Ling Yue Sword Immortal Feng Ning had appeared here, but he knew one thing—this was his chance!

Disguised as the Tiandu Divine Pine, he radiated the might of a supreme demon king, sending shockwaves of fear through the hidden beasts as he strode confidently into the valley.

“We meet again. Do you remember me?” Elder Divine Pine’s voice boomed through Lin Jing’s illusion, heavy with authority.

Feng Ning, her hand on her sword, paused before smiling. “So, it’s Senior Divine Pine.”

“Yes, it’s me. Homeless and wandering. Young lady, this Spatial Shift Grass and I are fated. Might you let this old man have it? In return, I’ll give you the demon cores of every hidden beast in this valley.”

Lin Jing mimicked Elder Divine Pine’s tone perfectly. The hidden beasts, already on edge, fled in a panic, not daring to linger.

“These beasts? I could slay them all myself,” Feng Ning replied, unimpressed. Her words, paired with the corpses of slain beasts littering the ground, painted a chilling picture.

Within moments, the once-crowded Black Wind Valley emptied, leaving only Lin Jing’s group and Feng Ning alive.

Seeing through his act, Feng Ning shook her head. “Alright, you can stop pretending. The beasts have already fled.”

Lin Jing dropped the illusion and grinned at Feng Ning. “You recognized me from the start.”

“I visited the Beast Taming Sect before entering the wasteland. Your Sect Master Xu mentioned you had entered the Great Wasteland with Elder Divine Pine,” Feng Ning said. “Why impersonate your elder? Where is the real Divine Pine?”

Lin Jing chuckled. “I was waiting for a chance to claim the Spatial Shift Grass. When I saw you, I thought I’d lend a hand. Using the guise of a demon king, I scared the beasts off to ensure you wouldn’t be overwhelmed.”

“As for Elder Divine Pine… it called this a trial for me and refuses to interfere.”

“By the way, did you come looking for me?” Lin Jing asked.

“Yes. I heard the Beast Taming Sect was sealed. Your Sect Master said he and a group of elders and disciples plan to join the Demon-Slaying Bureau. I thought you might join them—it’s safer to have allies.”

“Apologies, but I’ve decided to attend Hidden Dragon Academy. The Hidden Dragon Arts are invaluable for raising my pets,” Lin Jing declined politely.

“I expected as much.” Feng Ning sighed but didn’t press further. “If you change your mind, my offer stands.”

Looking at the Spatial Shift Grass, she said, “If you want it, it’s yours. It’s not fully mature, and cultivating it to maturity would take significant effort. But with Elder Divine Pine as your protector, I’m sure you’ll manage.”

At that moment, Elder Divine Pine finally appeared, its pinecone avatar hovering in the air.

“Young lady, I sent him to compete with those beasts for the herb as part of his training! A proper Demon-Slaying Guardian must develop exceptional qualities!”

“And now you’ve ruined everything—killing the beasts for him, handing over the herb without effort. He’s supposed to be a Longevity Body, not a freeloader! Look at him grinning—this was meant to be a trial!”

Feng Ning retorted, “Senior, things don’t always go as planned. I came here to invite him and failed. Why should I be the only one disappointed? Now you can share in my frustration.”

Elder Divine Pine fell silent, its plans thoroughly disrupted.

Ignoring the squabbling, Lin Jing turned to Feng Ning. “You visited the Beast Taming Sect. Has its spiritual vein been sealed? Have the members left?”

“Yes,” Feng Ning confirmed. “In fact, the spiritual vein didn’t need sealing. The Sect Regulatory Office originally planned to give the sect a hundred years to reform.

“But the undying demon guarded by the Beast Taming Sect and three other sects showed signs of breaking free. To prevent disaster, the office partnered with the Demon-Slaying Bureau to fortify the pagoda and suppress the demon.”

“...” Lin Jing and his companions were left speechless.

“Well, since my business here is done, I’ll take my leave.” Feng Ning summoned her ancient bronze sword and prepared to depart. Standing atop it, she glanced back at Lin Jing.

“The Beast Taming Sect’s situation ties to national fortunes. The Ancient Celestial Kingdom thrives on these fortunes; no one can change that.

“If you won’t join the Demon-Slaying Bureau and fight alongside me, work hard at the academy. You seem to think I’m too weak, but I’ll reach Nascent Soul soon. Then you won’t have to worry about me protecting you from demons.”

Lin Jing watched her leave, puzzled by her persistence. “Sword Immortal, you’re already a renowned genius. Why are you so fixated on slaying demons?”

“Also, you’ve grown thinner since we last met. Please take care of yourself—your health is the foundation of cultivation.”

Feng Ning smiled faintly, nodded, and flew away without answering.

Once she was gone, Elder Divine Pine quipped, “Are you trying to woo her? I’ve never seen you this concerned about your pets.”

The Pine Leaf Mouse and dragon carp exchanged glances, both equally surprised.

“I just don’t want her thinking about me all the time. I refuse to be bait for demon sects!” Lin Jing explained. “Why work so hard? Life should be easy and enjoyable...”

He walked over to the Spatial Shift Grass and dug it up.

“Senior, have I passed the trial? Can I crush the Hidden Dragon Academy token now?” he asked cheerfully.

“Go ahead,” Elder Divine Pine replied wearily. It then extended its roots into the ground, absorbing the nutrients from the surrounding beast corpses.

“What are you looking at? After all this work, can’t I enjoy a good meal?” it said, glaring at Lin Jing and the others.

“Of course, Senior. Please, enjoy,” Lin Jing replied.

Taking out the academy’s jade token, Lin Jing crushed it, activating the formation inside. A massive spatial vortex appeared, radiating powerful spatial energy.

Moments later, a figure emerged from the vortex—it was the same cultivator who had delivered the token. Stepping out, he froze, taking in the bloody scene around him.

Where was this?

He turned his gaze to Lin Jing, the Pine Leaf Mouse on his shoulder, and the dragon carp lodged in the meditation staff. Recognition dawned on his face.

“This token... I remember giving it to...”

“Lin Jing of the Beast Taming Sect,” Lin Jing said, clasping his hands. “I humbly request your guidance to Hidden Dragon Academy.”

...

Volume One: [The Beast Taming Sect] – End

Next Volume: [The Carp Leaps Over the Dragon Gate].

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 1: The Hidden Dragon Arts
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 1: The Hidden Dragon Arts

In the Ancient Celestial Kingdom, one of its nine regions was the Ancient Domain. Within it lay Hidden Dragon City and the renowned Hidden Dragon Academy, a cradle of Immortal Officials.

Cultivators who graduated from the academy often became influential Immortal Officials, blessed with dragon fortunes, and found their path to immortality smooth and prosperous.

At the heart of the city, in a serene corner of the academy, stood an ancient pavilion. Its foundation was made of green stone, its frame of redwood, with elegant, curving eaves resembling a phoenix poised to take flight. The pavilion exuded an air of sophistication and dignity.

In this pavilion, suffused with celestial light and rippling spatial waves, a white-robed instructor appeared, bringing a young cultivator and his spirit beasts through a teleportation portal.

“Welcome to Hidden Dragon Academy,” the instructor said, stepping out into the open and gazing at the blue sky and white clouds. “I’ve heard that the Beast Taming Sect is currently under suspension. But as a Qi Refining disciple, that’s none of your concern. Now that you’re here, focus on learning.”

The instructor, a cultivator named Cao, had guided Lin Jing from the Great Wasteland to Hidden Dragon Academy. He offered his kind advice, hoping Lin Jing wouldn’t let sect matters weigh him down.

Lin Jing forced a smile. He had thought the same—until he learned the Beast Taming Sect’s suspension was tied to disturbances in the Demon-Suppressing Pagoda!

He and the Pine Leaf Mouse knew all too well why the pagoda’s seal had weakened, though they’d rather not admit it. Lin Jing felt guilty, knowing he couldn’t waste the space artifact and 100,000 Spirit Stones the sect master had entrusted to him.

“Senior Cao, when can I start learning the Hidden Dragon Arts?” Lin Jing asked eagerly.

Despite its unwitting involvement in past events, the dragon carp stood to gain the most from studying here. Carrying this “burden” wouldn’t impede its growth—it might even elevate it.

Cao Ziyu glanced at the dragon carp lodged in the meditation staff and smiled. “You came prepared, eager to train your dragon-blooded beast with the Hidden Dragon Arts?”

“Yes! I’ve heard the academy’s Hidden Dragon Arts are the continent’s best method for nurturing dragon-blooded beasts,” Lin Jing replied.

“Indeed.” Cao nodded, hands clasped behind his back as he looked into the distance. “The academy periodically recruits students. Normally, only those who pass rigorous exams can enter.

“But for you, it’s different. Being chosen by the Cavern Heaven signifies that you possess great fortune. Hidden Dragon Academy extended a personal invitation to you.

“You’re not alone. The other cultivators who entered the Cavern Heaven alongside you were also invited. They responded earlier and have already begun their studies here.”

Lin Jing hadn’t expected that the Literary Domain Scholar, the Alchemist from the Pill Cauldron Sect, and the Sword Cultivator from the Heavenly Sword Gate had all arrived before him. At least he had some familiar faces in the academy, though they likely bore a grudge against him.

Still, Lin Jing focused on the good news: obtaining the Hidden Dragon Arts seemed easy!

“Don’t get too excited,” Cao said, his tone calm. “The exemption from the entrance exams is your privilege as a special recruit. However, obtaining the Hidden Dragon Arts isn’t. Every student who enters the academy gains access to it immediately.”

“Why?”

“Because the Hidden Dragon Arts are the foundation for attending lectures. Only students who’ve begun practicing it are eligible to listen to the academy’s teachings,” Cao explained.

Taking out a stone seal, he showed it to Lin Jing. The seal depicted a coiled young dragon, brimming with divine energy.

“This is the Hidden Dragon Seal. Once imprinted on your body, it grants you access to the Hidden Dragon Arts,” he said.

“Do you know the core of the Hidden Dragon Arts?” Cao asked, looking at Lin Jing, the Pine Leaf Mouse, and the dragon carp.

Without waiting for a reply, he continued, “It’s not spiritual energy—it’s fame and reputation.”

“Squeak???” The Pine Leaf Mouse tilted its head, puzzled.

“In simple terms, the more renowned you are—within the academy, Hidden Dragon City, the Ancient Domain, the Celestial Kingdom, or even the wider cultivation world—the more advanced your Hidden Dragon Arts will be,” Cao said.

“How fascinating!” Lin Jing was amazed. A technique derived from the True Dragon Arts, it seemed perfectly suited to someone with his Longevity Body, favoring special cultivation methods over mundane training.

Of course, fame required making waves, and Lin Jing had always considered himself an honest man.

“So, I need to build some reputation to begin practicing the Hidden Dragon Arts and qualify for lectures?”

“Correct,” Cao said. “Once I imprint the Hidden Dragon Seal on you, it will act as the seed of the Hidden Dragon Arts. When your reputation reaches a certain level, the seal will glow faintly. When the entire seal is illuminated, you’ll officially be initiated into the Hidden Dragon Arts.

“At that point, its various wonders—including the dragon-blooded beast techniques you seek—will manifest in your mind.

“Furthermore, when you first begin, the Hidden Dragon Arts will materialize a Fortune Dragon Hatchling within you, its appearance reflecting your cultivation, reputation, and fortune.

“These hatchlings come in four colors: white, blue, purple, and gold. The closer to gold, the stronger your dragon fortune.

“Instructors often set attendance requirements based on these hatchling colors. For example, Senior Meng, who teaches Confucian, Daoist, and Buddhist studies, only admits students with golden fortune dragons.

“Additionally, access to the academy’s advanced cultivation techniques, such as Foundation Establishment and Core Formation methods, depends on your Hidden Dragon Arts level.

“So, how much you can learn here depends entirely on your mastery of the Hidden Dragon Arts,” Cao concluded with a faint smile.

He added, “In the Great Wasteland, you’ve gained confidence in your strength. But here, gaining reputation is most directly achieved by defeating other sect geniuses. Use them as stepping stones to boost your Hidden Dragon Arts.”

“However, be warned: a single defeat can cause your reputation to plummet, hindering your progress. The path you take to enhance the Hidden Dragon Arts is entirely up to you.”

“Oh, and one more thing...” Cao continued. “The academy doesn’t provide living quarters. While there are cultivation sites available, you’ll need to rent them with Spirit Stones. Would you like to reserve one?”

Lin Jing: ???

What? No free lodging?

Stunned, Lin Jing finally understood why Sect Master Xu had slipped him an extra 100,000 Spirit Stones. Without them, he might not even afford to stay at the academy.

Remembering the Spirit Stones he’d already spent during his three months of hard training, Lin Jing felt his heart ache.

“Senior, as a special recruit, don’t we get any perks? Waiving rent doesn’t seem unreasonable!”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 2: The Heavenly Pride Ranking
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 2: The Heavenly Pride Ranking

Cao Ziyu chuckled and shook his head.

“Then I won’t need it for now,” Lin Jing replied reluctantly.

The cost of a cultivation cave made him hesitant. For now, he decided to explore the academy and its surroundings before making a decision. After all, he had endured three months in the wilderness—how much worse could this be?

Who would have thought that his first day at Hidden Dragon Academy would have him potentially homeless?

“Many students live outside the academy and only come when there are lectures by renowned teachers. That’s not an issue; Hidden Dragon City is quite safe,” Cao Ziyu assured him. “If you manage to achieve a purple or golden fortune dragon aura, perhaps a teacher might take you as a disciple and provide you with a cultivation spot.”

“Now, hold out your hand. I’ll imprint the Hidden Dragon Seal.”

Lin Jing extended his right hand, choosing to avoid his left, which bore the Sacred Wood Seal from the Ice Soul Gu. Having the two seals overlap could cause unforeseen complications.

Pop!

“And with that, your induction is complete. I look forward to the day your name resonates throughout Hidden Dragon Academy,” Cao said, disappearing into thin air without so much as leaving an introductory pamphlet or a student badge.

“Is this academy even legit?” Lin Jing muttered, staring at his hand. The seal seemed to suffice as proof of his identity for now. It looked like he’d have to figure out the rest on his own.

“A place that trains Immortal Officials? Of course, it’s unreliable,” Elder Divine Pine scoffed, floating out of Lin Jing’s pocket. “But that guy, Cao, was quite powerful—Nascent Soul Perfected level, with considerable combat prowess.”

“Senior, who’s stronger, you or him?” Lin Jing asked.

“What do you think? He’s just a Nascent Soul! But the academy is filled with experts, so I’m not exceptional here,” Elder Divine Pine replied smugly. “Now you see why I insisted you build some strength before coming here. The core of the Hidden Dragon Arts is power. Go find an easily handled Foundation Establishment cultivator and beat him up. That’ll kickstart your Hidden Dragon Arts.”

“Fighting Foundation Establishment cultivators as a Qi Refining disciple? That’s something only the most talented sect prodigies can manage. Doing so would make you a sensation!”

“See? My efforts were for your own good,” Elder Divine Pine concluded, clearly pleased with itself.

Lin Jing said nothing, while the Pine Leaf Mouse and dragon carp shuddered at the memory of their grueling battles with the wooden man.

Hopefully, the academy’s Foundation Establishment cultivators would be easier to defeat.

“Senior Cao mentioned the academy doesn’t teach combat techniques. What exactly does it teach? Administrative skills?” Lin Jing asked.

“Before students graduate, they’re sent to remote cities as lords to validate their learning. The academy teaches them how to govern a city!”

“For example, agricultural cultivators might teach you how to farm and improve a city’s agriculture.

“Geomancers might show you how to plan urban layouts with optimal feng shui.

“Diviners could teach you how to read people’s fates and personalities, helping you identify talent.

“In short, the academy covers all sorts of subjects—except combat techniques!”

“Of course, without strength, you won’t be able to learn much. But as the teacher said, you can gain fame in other ways. Crafting an immortal pill, forging an immortal artifact, or taming an immortal beast could also make you famous.”

“Isn’t that even more absurd?” Lin Jing said. “It’s probably easier to slay a Dao Seeking expert!”

“Exactly. That’s why power remains the most reliable path,” Elder Divine Pine replied. “Speaking of which, have you heard of the Heavenly Pride Rankings from Tianji Pavilion? It’s a shortcut to mastering the Hidden Dragon Arts—worth keeping an eye on.”

The Heavenly Pride Rankings, published by Tianji Pavilion, were divided into three lists: Heaven, Earth, and Human.

Only the most exceptional geniuses across the cultivation world made it onto these rankings. The lists weren’t limited to the Ancient Domain but were revered throughout the Celestial Kingdom and beyond.

For academy students, making it onto the rankings was a surefire way to gain massive fame and accelerate their Hidden Dragon Arts.

“Start with the Human Ranking,” Elder Divine Pine suggested. “The Hidden Dragon Arts are crucial. Attend every lecture the academy offers.”

“Don’t overlook these courses just because they don’t teach combat. The true power of the Hidden Dragon Arts comes from official positions. Strong governance requires mastering city management. The academy’s teachings are invaluable for that.”

“I understand. I might not excel in other areas, but I’m a seasoned student,” Lin Jing replied. “Still, with our current strength, getting onto the rankings seems impossible.”

He glanced at the Pine Leaf Mouse and dragon carp—one at the ninth layer of Qi Refining, the other at the eighth.

Lin Jing recalled that even the 100th place on the Human Ranking was a late-stage Foundation Establishment cultivator capable of defeating perfected Foundation Establishment demons.

“There’s no rush! Focus on Foundation Establishment first. The rankings cap at thirty years of bone age, so you’ve got time,” Elder Divine Pine reassured him.

The Human Ranking was for cultivators under thirty years old and below the Core Formation stage.

The Earth Ranking was for those under sixty and between Core Formation and Nascent Soul stages.

The Heaven Ranking was for those under one hundred and at the Nascent Soul stage.

Given that cultivators in these stages typically lived for hundreds or thousands of years, the age restrictions made the rankings truly exceptional.

“For now, find a regular Foundation Establishment cultivator to challenge. Get your Hidden Dragon Arts started!” Elder Divine Pine urged. “I won’t stay with you inside the academy, but I’ll intervene if you’re in danger.”

Leaving behind a protective pinecone, Elder Divine Pine withdrew, leaving Lin Jing standing outside the pavilion with a sigh.

“Let’s go,” Lin Jing said, after some thought, leading the Pine Leaf Mouse and dragon carp to explore.

...

Strolling through Hidden Dragon City, they soon left the academy and followed the aroma of food to a bustling tavern named Immortal’s Delight.

“Sir, why are you carrying your own ingredients? We don’t offer cooking services!” the waiter exclaimed, eyeing the dragon carp in Lin Jing’s meditation staff.

“Blub!!” The dragon carp, furious, puffed out flames in protest.

“Squeak, squeak...” The Pine Leaf Mouse chuckled, secretly agreeing that the dragon carp looked like a fine ingredient—its tender meat, fragrant aroma, and spicy marination made it perfect for hot pot.

“Have some tact. This is my spirit beast,” Lin Jing retorted.

“My apologies, sir. Please come in,” the waiter said hastily.

Finding a seat, Lin Jing perused the menu. But a nearby conversation caught his attention.

“Have you heard? Fang Shilin, a Hidden Dragon Academy student, has set up an arena in the southern part of the city. He’s aiming for a hundred consecutive wins in same-level duels. Do you think he can pull it off? He’s only at the early Foundation Establishment stage.”

“How could he? How much true essence can an early-stage Foundation Establishment cultivator have? And where will he find so many opponents?”

“He’s the Young Valley Master of Everlasting Spring Valley. Their cultivation techniques allow rapid recovery of true essence. He’s probably relying on that to endure a gauntlet of challengers and gain fame. Even if he doesn’t achieve a hundred wins, the publicity alone is worth it. These academy students love making waves.”

Lin Jing exchanged a glance with the Pine Leaf Mouse.

Everlasting Spring Valley? A sect specializing in wood techniques?

Elder Divine Pine had mentioned finding an easy target—and this seemed like fate.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 3: Qi Refining vs. Foundation Establishment
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 3: Qi Refining vs. Foundation Establishment

For Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse, the easiest targets were undoubtedly cultivators specializing in wood-based techniques.

The Everlasting Emperor’s Longevity Art practiced by the Pine Leaf Mouse had a powerful suppressive effect on wood-based creatures and techniques. Thanks to the mouse’s “special” cultivation methods, the technique’s potency had reached incredible depths.

According to Elder Divine Pine, even the entry-level mastery of this art could completely suppress Qi Refining-level wood-element creatures. At the Small Success stage, it would dominate Foundation Establishment-level beings.

Given its nature, it not only absorbed the essence of its kind but also rendered ordinary wood-element spiritual plants, aggressive vegetation, and wood-based spells virtually powerless.

Normally, such a powerful art was impossible for a Qi Refining wood-element beast to master to this degree. But with Lin Jing’s resources—and his Longevity Body—it had become reality.

“Let’s wait a bit longer. Let the situation heat up,” Lin Jing said, showing no rush to head to the southern part of the city. Instead, he leisurely ordered more dishes, keeping a keen eye on the rumors spreading.

The Hidden Dragon Arts weren’t about victory or defeat—they were about recognition and exposure. Even if Lin Jing defeated a Nascent Soul elder in secret, it wouldn’t matter much if no one knew. On the other hand, if a reputable figure vouched for his skills, his Hidden Dragon Arts would likely progress without a single fight.

“Exposing my connection to the Ten Great Gu could probably skyrocket my Hidden Dragon Arts,” Lin Jing mused. “But it would also get me killed just as quickly.”

...

At the southern edge of Hidden Dragon City, near a vibrant maple forest ablaze with autumn hues, a large crowd had gathered.

At the forest’s edge, a green-robed young man reclined on a wooden chair, radiating an air of self-assured dominance. Nearby, a Foundation Establishment cultivator lay collapsed, bound tightly by vine-like tendrils.

“I... concede...” the defeated cultivator gasped, his face pale and his breathing labored. The vines released him, and he fell to the ground, gulping for air.

“Good match!”

“Who else dares to challenge me? Any Foundation Establishment cultivator may step forward. Defeat Fang Shilin, and I’ll pay you 100 Spirit Stones. If you’re stronger than Foundation Establishment, you’re still welcome—Spirit Stones guaranteed!”

Fang Shilin, the Young Valley Master of Everlasting Spring Valley, exuded calm confidence. Despite facing multiple challengers in quick succession, he showed no signs of fatigue.

Using Spirit Stones as bait, he lured competitors of all levels. For him, losing to higher-stage opponents wouldn’t harm his reputation or Hidden Dragon Arts progress. Meanwhile, weaker challengers only added to his winning streak.

In his mind, the truly powerful academy cultivators wouldn’t bother with his meager fame or Spirit Stones. His strategy was nearly foolproof.

Victory after victory, his reputation was building—aiming for the elusive feat of 100 consecutive wins against peers.

Lin Jing arrived with the Pine Leaf Mouse and dragon carp, observing the commotion from the crowd.

“Eleven straight wins! He’s got real skill!”

“Absolutely. He’s taken down eleven Foundation Establishment cultivators, including two mid-stage ones. Fang Shilin is as impressive as they say, with immense true essence reserves and solid fundamentals,” murmured two nearby cultivators.

“That’s just his fancy artifact—a wood-element fan,” scoffed a well-dressed young man. “Without it, his spells would be half as strong.”

“Young man, you’re mistaken,” interjected an old beggar. “Using artifacts is part of cultivation mastery. Many Foundation Establishment cultivators can’t even activate an offensive artifact properly. Fang’s artifact is formidable, but it’s his Everlasting Spring Valley techniques—renowned for their inexhaustible true essence—that allow him to wield it effortlessly. If someone else tried, they’d run out of energy after one use.”

The crowd nodded in agreement. Hidden Dragon City was full of cultivators, where even beggars spouted wisdom.

“Let me have a go!” A burly, black-clad man with twin sabers stepped forward, his booming voice cutting through the chatter.

“And you are?” Fang Shilin asked, unfazed.

“Ma Wushuang, mid-Foundation Establishment, a Lotus Chamber of Commerce bodyguard,” the man declared. “I’d be a fool to pass up those Spirit Stones.”

The crowd buzzed. The Lotus Chamber of Commerce was not only the Celestial Kingdom’s largest merchant guild but also operated a prestigious escort service. Their bodyguards were experienced and skilled, especially in dangerous territories beyond the kingdom.

“Please.” Fang Shilin folded his fan and waited, still seated.

Swish!

Without hesitation, Ma Wushuang crossed his sabers, sending twin arcs of blazing fire roaring toward Fang. The fiery blade energy scorched the fallen leaves, igniting them into a glowing inferno that lit up the battlefield.

Fang Shilin remained composed. With a flick of his fan, two verdant vines burst forth, coiling into dragon-like forms.

Roar!

The vine dragons spiraled through the air, unaffected by the flames or blade energy. Their tenacity was astonishing—they crushed the fiery attacks mid-air and surged toward the stunned Ma Wushuang.

“Hah!” Ma Wushuang roared, slashing at the vines with his sabers. But his weapons barely left a mark. Within seconds, the vines coiled around him, flinging him dozens of meters away.

“Another one bites the dust,” the crowd murmured. Fang’s artifact-enhanced vine technique was unyielding—fireproof, unbreakable, and imbued with overwhelming strength.

“Who’s next?” Fang Shilin asked, brimming with confidence.

“Can a Qi Refining cultivator challenge you?”

The voice was calm yet clear. A young man in white, holding a meditation staff with a dragon carp and a robed squirrel perched on his shoulder, stepped forward.

The crowd fell silent, turning to stare at the peculiar trio.

Fang Shilin raised an eyebrow. “Who are you? Qi Refining? Don’t waste my time. Go home and train!”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 4: The Squirrel Devours the Dragon
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 4: The Squirrel Devours the Dragon

No one expected that after Fang Shilin’s latest victory over a mid-Foundation Establishment cultivator, his next challenger would be a Qi Refining cultivator.

In Hidden Dragon City, even the stray dogs in the streets might be at the seventh or eighth level of Qi Refining. Such a low cultivation stage was hardly impressive.

Fang Shilin frowned. A victory over a Qi Refining cultivator would bring him no glory—people might even accuse him of bullying the weak. And losing? The idea was laughable. How could he possibly lose to someone so far beneath him?

This was a no-win situation, and Fang Shilin felt it wasn’t even worth dignifying with an insult. He was about to dismiss the challenge outright when something caught his eye.

“Wait a moment,” he said, his gaze landing on the Pine Leaf Mouse and dragon carp. “That attire… You’re a disciple of the Beast Taming Sect in the Great Wasteland!”

At his words, the crowd erupted.

“Beast Taming Sect? Isn’t that the sect the Sect Regulatory Office just ordered to suspend operations for having more demonic fortune than human fortune?”

“I heard all their Nascent Soul elders are demon kings, and not a single one is human. Each demon king has three or four human pets. Truly terrifying.”

Lin Jing sighed. The rumors were getting more absurd by the day.

“Yes, I am Lin Jing of the Beast Taming Sect,” he announced calmly. “But the sect isn’t nearly as exaggerated as the rumors suggest. We do have one strong human expert.”

Fang Shilin chuckled at the crowd’s chatter. As someone from the Great Wasteland, he was familiar with the Beast Taming Sect. “So, after the suspension, the disciples fled to the Ancient Domain?”

Lin Jing replied evenly, “The Ancient Domain is a nexus of fortune. As a disciple of the Beast Taming Sect, I’ve come to hone my skills and restore my sect’s reputation. It’s only natural.”

“I see. Well, I sympathize with your situation,” Fang Shilin said, clearly amused. “Fine, I accept your challenge.

“You’re only at Qi Refining. Your two spirit beasts—one contracted and one not—should fight alongside you. That would be more fair.”

Fang Shilin rarely accepted such low-stakes challenges, but seeing Lin Jing’s sect affiliation intrigued him. Interacting with a Beast Taming Sect disciple could amplify the buzz around his public dueling event.

Moreover, if he fought all three opponents at once, he could claim he was giving them a handicap, avoiding accusations of bullying.

Lin Jing gave a polite nod. “Thank you, but this dragon carp is not yet contracted. It wouldn’t be proper to use it in the duel.”

With that, he planted the meditation staff firmly into the ground, leaving the dragon carp as a spectator. He stepped forward with only the Pine Leaf Mouse, facing Fang Shilin directly.

Fang Shilin squinted.

The crowd, too, was perplexed. Lin Jing already had slim odds with all three fighting together. Now he was deliberately limiting himself to two combatants?

“He’s just trying to make a name for himself to boost his sect’s human fortune,” someone speculated.

“Is the Beast Taming Sect really relying on a Qi Refining disciple to rebuild its reputation?”

Most spectators thought Lin Jing’s actions were foolish. A Qi Refining cultivator challenging a Foundation Establishment stage genius from the academy was the height of recklessness.

Unperturbed, Fang Shilin remained seated. With a casual flick of his fan, he summoned a Binding Spell.

The ground behind him split, and dozens of vine-like tendrils emerged, snaking toward Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse like a swarm of green serpents.

The crowd watched with bated breath, expecting Lin Jing and his beast to be overwhelmed. But then…

The vines froze mid-air.

“What’s this?” someone muttered.

The spectators initially assumed Fang Shilin was holding back. But his startled expression quickly dispelled that notion.

“Impossible… My Binding Spell was interrupted?” Fang Shilin’s eyes widened.

Lin Jing took a single step forward, and the immobilized vines shattered into countless green sparks, dissipating harmlessly.

It was only then that the crowd noticed the Pine Leaf Mouse. Clad in its ceremonial robe, it stood on Lin Jing’s shoulder, calmly gesturing as if casting a spell.

“Squeak!” The mouse shook its head, as though unimpressed by the display of strength.

“Damn it!”

Gritting his teeth, Fang Shilin dismissed his fan’s gentler approach. With a more deliberate movement, he unleashed seven massive vine dragons, each as thick as an ancient tree.

These emerald behemoths roared toward Lin Jing with renewed fury.

And then, behind Lin Jing, an extraordinary phenomenon unfolded.

A towering wooden deity materialized, its form cloaked in verdant energy and brimming with the essence of life itself. Draped in emerald robes, it stood majestically, like the ruler of spring. With each step, grass and flowers blossomed anew beneath its feet.

The aura of this divine apparition left Fang Shilin’s mind reeling. His movements slowed, his focus wavered. The vine dragons faltered, splintering apart as they neared Lin Jing.

The phenomenon crystallized into a surreal image:

A massive squirrel, identical to the Pine Leaf Mouse, loomed over the battlefield. It clutched one of the shattered vine dragons in its claws and devoured it whole.

The Squirrel Devours the Dragon—an ethereal manifestation of the Pine Leaf Mouse’s inner power.

Fang Shilin sat frozen in disbelief.

“This… This is Qi Refining?”

The crowd, too, was stunned into silence. They couldn’t fathom how a Qi Refining cultivator had conjured such overwhelming power.

Before Fang Shilin could react further, his wooden chair came alive. Countless vines erupted from it, binding him tightly in place. No matter how much he struggled, he couldn’t free himself.

Lin Jing stopped walking.

“Curious,” he said, his voice calm but loud enough for the crowd to hear. “If I’m this strong, how could the Sect Regulatory Office claim my sect’s human fortune is weak?

“Perhaps their judgment was flawed. Perhaps I need to take down a few more Foundation Establishment cultivators to prove them wrong.”

With that, the towering apparition dissipated, and Lin Jing turned away. Picking up his meditation staff, he walked toward the city, leaving the crowd in stunned silence.

On his right hand, the Hidden Dragon Seal began to glow faintly, a small section illuminating with newfound renown.

…

The battle quickly became the talk of Hidden Dragon City.

“A Qi Refining cultivator at the seventh layer, with a ninth-layer spirit beast, effortlessly crushed a Foundation Establishment genius wielding a powerful artifact?”

“A Pine Leaf Mouse with mastery over wood techniques, completely suppressing Everlasting Spring Valley’s Young Valley Master? And that bizarre phenomenon—what is the Squirrel Devours the Dragon?”

“Is this Lin Jing really just from the Beast Taming Sect, a so-called third-rate sect? Isn’t their ‘human fortune’ supposed to be weak?”

Within the academy, the news spread like wildfire. Fang Shilin’s humiliation drew both ridicule and awe.

Among the academy’s other top students, three figures in particular—the Blue-Robed Scholar, the Violet Flame Alchemist, and the Silent Swordsman—each reacted to Lin Jing’s name with visible unease.

“He’s here. That monstrous disciple from the Beast Taming Sect… Last time, he even gave us a fake name. Does he have to start by beating up a Foundation Establishment cultivator?”

Lin Jing’s reputation soared. Yet he lamented privately: in the chaos, he’d forgotten to collect the 100 Spirit Stones from Fang Shilin.

“Thankfully, we’re classmates. I’ll find a way to get them later.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 5: Supreme Beast Fire
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 5: Supreme Beast Fire

Hidden Dragon City, night as dark as ink.

Lin Jing, holding the Fire Carp Meditation Staff, stepped into an inconspicuous corner and summoned the Pine Leaf Mouse.

After breaking Fang Shilin's winning streak, Lin Jing wandered around Hidden Dragon City, checking the rates at several inns. Ultimately... he opted to let the Pine Leaf Mouse construct a wooden hut to make do for the night.

“Zhi…” With a flurry of woodwork techniques, the hut was completed. The Pine Leaf Mouse sighed, feeling that Lin Jing’s lifestyle while traveling was surprisingly convenient.

It could build a house and grow crops, while the Fire Carp could provide fire for lighting—the division of labor was exceptionally clear.

“Good...bye!” After finishing its task, the Pine Leaf Mouse stammered out a simple phrase before burrowing back into the Suspended Gourd. Although it had never formally studied human speech, it had picked up enough from listening to manage a word or two.

“Take good care of the Void Herb!” Lin Jing instructed as he glanced at the gourd.

Currently, there were two new plants inside: the Bloodsucking Vine and the Void Herb. The former was easy to care for, but the Void Herb was much trickier and required the Pine Leaf Mouse’s careful nurturing with the Everlasting Emperor’s Longevity Art.

“You should rest too,” Lin Jing said to the Fire Carp while seated cross-legged on a bed made of layered leaves.

Not that it made a difference—the Fire Carp was already asleep within the Meditation Staff.

“Not bad. Looks like I’ll be able to master the Hidden Dragon Arts tonight.”

Seated on the bed, Lin Jing looked at the back of his hand, where a design of a young dragon, faintly glowing and seemingly starving, had almost fully lit up—ninety percent of its intricate lines were now bright.

Thanks to the buzz surrounding the Beast Taming Sect’s closure and the hype from a Foundation Establishment cultivator fighting a Qi Refinement opponent, Lin Jing’s previous battle had already become a hot topic across Hidden Dragon City, further amplified by its dazzling illusionary effects.

As someone from an era of information explosions, Lin Jing wouldn’t claim to be an expert in trending tactics, but having witnessed countless examples, he felt his performance in that battle was meticulously crafted.

"If only I’d taken journalism as an elective back in college," he mused.

Buzz!

Just as Lin Jing was staring at the Hidden Dragon design, the last dim section suddenly lit up. The white, faintly blue-tinted young dragon etched on the back of his hand seemed to come alive, shimmering with vitality.

Simultaneously, a flood of information surged into Lin Jing’s mind.

The Hidden Dragon Arts!

Divided into four tiers, like the Everlasting Emperor’s Longevity Art: Initiation, Proficiency, Mastery, and Perfection.

Currently, Lin Jing had reached the Initiation stage.

The progress at this stage was marked by the dragon’s color: starting with white, then progressing to green, blue, and finally gold. Achieving the golden stage would signify Proficiency in the Hidden Dragon Arts!

Upon reaching Initiation, the Hidden Dragon Arts granted three remarkable effects:

By consuming Hidden Dragon Fortune, practitioners could temporarily enhance their comprehension and elevate their techniques to higher levels.

By using Hidden Dragon Fortune, practitioners could help others unlock hidden potential or awaken dormant abilities. This effect was particularly potent for dragon-blooded beasts, not only activating potential but also purifying their dragon blood.

Subtle warnings of imminent danger.

Automatically consuming Hidden Dragon Fortune allows for premonitions of fatal crises mere seconds before they occur!

"These abilities are all quite practical," Lin Jing thought to himself.

With the initiation of the Hidden Dragon Arts, Lin Jing immediately gained insight into its various applications. Whether it was Spell Evolution or Potential Awakening, both were abilities he greatly needed.

However, there was one downside—using the Hidden Dragon Arts consumed accumulated Hidden Dragon Fortune. To continually train with the technique, his fame would have to remain prominent in the outside world.

A fleeting moment of renown wouldn't be enough to sustain the Hidden Dragon Arts for long.

“Defeating Fang Shilin of Everlasting Spring Valley earlier today might give me a small reputation here in Hidden Dragon City and the academy, but this fame will fade quickly as time passes, eventually vanishing altogether.”

“While it’s rare for someone at the Qi Refinement stage to defeat a Foundation Establishment cultivator, it’s not unheard of. And Everlasting Spring Valley isn’t a particularly powerful sect. Beating Fang Shilin isn’t much of an accomplishment.”

“If he were truly formidable, he wouldn’t have resorted to setting up challenges just to gain some recognition. Most likely, he’s at the academy’s bottom tier.”

“I need to defeat even more outstanding talents…”

Lin Jing wasn’t sentimental about his current Hidden Dragon Fortune. He was eager to experience the miraculous effects of the Hidden Dragon Arts right away.

Spell Evolution? He didn’t seem to have any pressing need for that at the moment.

His gaze fell on the sleeping Fire Carp, snoring peacefully. Lin Jing reached out and shook it awake.

“Bloop!” The Fire Carp opened its round eyes, startled, and stared at Lin Jing.

“I’ll grant you a great fortune!”

The dragon pattern on the back of Lin Jing’s hand glimmered as he stroked the carp’s head.

Secret Technique: Potential Awakening!

Generally, 99% of Hidden Dragon Arts practitioners use the technique to enhance their own abilities through Spell Evolution, as personal strength is the foundation of cultivation.

A small number of practitioners might use it to awaken potential in others, usually children or disciples—after all, Hidden Dragon Fortune was hard-earned.

It was exceedingly rare for students at the academy to use the technique to cultivate their spirit beasts. After all, even if a spirit beast grew stronger, its benefits to the practitioner were often indirect and less significant than investing in one’s descendants.

Only someone like Lin Jing, capable of forming a bloodline contract with a dragon-blooded beast in the future, could justify using the Hidden Dragon Arts to cultivate his pet.

“Bloop!” The Fire Carp’s wide eyes gleamed as it suddenly felt its bloodline surging. Flames ignited in its pupils.

Its head felt itchy…

Its mind became hazy, as though it had accidentally consumed one of Lin Jing’s Bewitching Pills.

In its daze, the Fire Carp thought it saw an awe-inspiring divine beast engulfed in flames, striding majestically.

This creature had a deer’s head, dragon’s horns, a bull’s hooves, a horse’s tail, and was covered in fiery scales radiating an imposing aura.

For a brief moment, the Fire Carp felt an intimate connection with the creature. Flames erupted across its body, taking on the form of scales imbued with the might of a dragon. The transformation was nothing short of miraculous!

In the outside world, Lin Jing wasn’t privy to the Fire Carp’s visions. But he was stunned as a terrifying blaze erupted from the carp’s body after its potential was awakened. Its aura surged, nearing the ninth level of Qi Refinement.

No way!

Was the Hidden Dragon Arts really this powerful?

“What is this?!” Lin Jing exclaimed as he observed the carp’s transformation.

“That’s beast fire! Supreme beast fire!” A familiar voice suddenly rang in Lin Jing’s ears.

Shen Song appeared, saying, “I’ve always suspected that this Fire Carp of yours had an extraordinary bloodline. I didn’t expect it to have inherited the ‘Fire Qilin’ bloodline from its ancestors.”

“This carp’s original flame was just ordinary spirit fire, slightly superior to mundane fire. Over three months of potential awakening and the Longevity Blood Baptism have endowed it with astonishing strength and Ghostfire, but its flame bloodline itself had not changed—until now.”

“Though its flame hadn’t changed initially, the foundation it gained was immensely strong. It was already a rare mutated bloodline, and now, with the reinforcement of the Hidden Dragon Arts, you’ve finally helped it take that crucial step forward. You unlocked the shackles in its bloodline memory, transforming its spirit fire into beast fire!”

“This is Dragonflame, specifically Kirin Dragonflame, an absolutely supreme form of beast fire. It’s leagues stronger than spirit fire. If this little guy were to fight that squirrel without external aid, the outcome would be unpredictable now.”

After Shen Song finished speaking, Lin Jing froze in shock. Fire Qilin bloodline?

Legends say that the Yinglong gave rise to the Jianma, the Jianma birthed the Kirin, and the Kirin gave birth to myriad beasts. It made sense that the Kirin carried dragon blood.

Looking at the Fire Carp, which was now squinting contentedly, Lin Jing couldn’t suppress his delight.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 6: Mastering Tools
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 6: Mastering Tools

“What’s with that expression?” Lin Jing asked, noticing the Fire Carp squinting smugly.

“Bloop!”

The Fire Carp clenched its fins confidently, as if declaring that it could now defeat that green-robed ape from earlier in the day.

Next time, it would be its turn!

Squirrel Devours the Dragon? What nonsense. It should be Carp Devours the Dragon instead!

“Dragons have always been proud and arrogant. Its awakened bloodline is indeed showing its true nature,” Shen Song remarked via voice transmission.

“Well…” Lin Jing chuckled. It didn’t bother him.

As long as it had the strength to back it up, pride and arrogance weren’t necessarily bad traits!

With this supreme beast fire, refining pills in the future would undoubtedly be much easier.

Moreover, once the Fire Carp, possessing the Fire Qilin bloodline, evolved into a dragon, it would likely be capable of counterbalancing the power of the Ice Soul Gu.

As for the Ice Soul Gu’s power… Lin Jing would soon be able to control it as well!

“By the way, Senior Shen Song, why are you still here?” Lin Jing asked.

Shen Song replied, “I said I didn’t want to stay in Hidden Dragon Academy, not that I was leaving Hidden Dragon City!”

“So, you also saw us defeat that Foundation Establishment cultivator in style?”

“Defeat a mere wood-element Foundation Establishment cultivator? What’s there to brag about? You wouldn’t have succeeded without the three months of training I put you through with my wooden constructs. Try challenging a fire-element Foundation Establishment cultivator next time.”

“Senior, you’re such a killjoy,” Lin Jing said with a shake of his head.

“Killjoy or not, I don’t care. But if you don’t put out that fire, you’ll be sweeping up ashes in a moment,” Shen Song warned.

The Fire Carp, in its excitement, had accidentally let its beast fire spill out, setting the wooden hut ablaze. Flames roared into the night sky.

“What’s going on? Someone’s setting fires in Hidden Dragon City!”

A patrolling Foundation Establishment night watchman quickly flew over, shouting angrily. After all, they’d been announcing “Dry weather, be cautious with fire!” for weeks. He’d assumed it was routine. Who’d have thought someone would actually dare ignite a blaze at night?

The next day, after proving his identity and paying a fine of five spirit stones, Lin Jing returned to Hidden Dragon Academy.

He had learned from his inquiries the previous day that the academy rarely held classes. Sometimes, months would pass without a single lecture, and whether or not lessons were held depended entirely on the mood of the instructors.

Other than the occasional lecture, students spent most of their time either cultivating in seclusion or finding ways to make waves and garner fame.

This time, Lin Jing entered the academy with a specific goal: to obtain a Foundation Establishment technique from the library!

The Academy Library

At the library entrance, an elderly man stood guard. After briefly inspecting Lin Jing’s Hidden Dragon Seal, he allowed him to pass.

Had this been the Beast Taming Sect, the guard might have been a wise old ape, turtle, or bird instead.

“Found it!”

The library was silent, with Lin Jing as its sole occupant at this time.

Inside, he discovered that the shelves were not stocked with conventional techniques. Instead, they were filled with books on alchemy, artifact crafting, geomancy, medicine, and agriculture.

These were not beginner-level texts; the content was profound. A wandering cultivator lucky enough to obtain even one of these books and fully comprehend it could rise to become a master in the respective field.

However, Lin Jing wasn’t here for these today.

Walking quickly, he reached for a particular book: “Wood Spirit Foundation Establishment Technique.”

One of Lin Jing’s primary objectives in coming to Hidden Dragon Academy was to find a superior Foundation Establishment method!

The Beast Taming Sect had very few Foundation Establishment techniques. Most disciples relied on Foundation Establishment Pills to break through. However, this method often resulted in a flawed Dao foundation, making it extremely difficult to form a Core later. For those with average talent, reaching Foundation Establishment often meant being stuck there for life unless they encountered extraordinary opportunities.

The best method in the Beast Taming Sect was the Demonic Dao Foundation Pill, refined using Monster Cores. This pill was exceedingly difficult to produce, as it required rare and specific Monster Cores. Generally, only true disciples were deemed worthy of the sect’s resources to create such a special pill.

Even so, the Demonic Dao Foundation Pill, while the best available in the Beast Taming Sect, was considered a low-grade technique in the broader Ancient Celestial Kingdom.

Truly superior Foundation Establishment methods could grant cultivators with mortal constitutions an Acquired Spiritual Body after breaking through. Such methods greatly increased the chances of forming a Core and even provided a slim possibility of reaching the Nascent Soul stage!

For example, the Flower Pavilion Master of Snow Moon City had achieved the Acquired Fire Spirit Body through a special Foundation Establishment technique.

Of course, cultivators with Innate Spiritual Bodies or special constitutions didn’t need such complex methods. They could establish their foundation directly and still possess extraordinary potential.

Lin Jing’s visit to Hidden Dragon Academy wasn’t to seek an Acquired Spiritual Body for himself. Ordinarily, without unique circumstances, a cultivator could only possess one body constitution and cultivate one set of techniques. Trying otherwise would disrupt the balance of spiritual energy within the body, leading to Qi deviation.

He already had the Longevity Body. Without a fortuitous encounter, it was nearly impossible for him to cultivate a second constitution.

Though Lin Jing had dreams of becoming like the “Three-Body Cultivator” Ancient Imperial Prince, mastering multiple constitutions, he knew it was an improbable goal. Without immense luck or powerful backing, it wasn’t even worth considering.

The Foundation Establishment method he sought today wasn’t for himself—it was for the Pine Leaf Mouse. At his current level, merely the seventh stage of Qi Refinement, Foundation Establishment was still a distant goal.

Lin Jing read through the details:

Limitations: This method could only be used by mortal-body cultivators.

“Useless,” Lin Jing muttered.

He discovered that the first floor of the library contained numerous Foundation Establishment techniques for forming Acquired Spiritual Bodies. These superior methods, nearly impossible to find in the outside sects, were freely available here.

Yet, even if someone acquired these methods, meeting all the conditions without the help of senior cultivators would be exceptionally difficult.

Lin Jing continued to browse. Most of the techniques he found were either:

Some were outright bizarre, allowing practitioners to change their gender or drastically alter their physical appearance post-breakthrough. Lin Jing wondered who such methods were even designed for.

“Nothing suitable for me, the Pine Leaf Mouse, or the Fire Carp,” Lin Jing concluded.

“With my current level of the Hidden Dragon Arts, I can’t ascend to the second floor yet.”

He sighed. “Never mind. I’ll start with an alchemy book. If there’s nothing on the second or third floor suitable for the Pine Leaf Mouse, I’ll stick to my original plan: create a unique Longevity Foundation Pill using blood refinement, tailored to the Everlasting Emperor’s Longevity Art, to help it establish its foundation!”

Lin Jing grabbed an alchemy book and began studying. This plan would have been challenging in the Beast Taming Sect, but with the Hidden Dragon Arts’ Spell Evolution ability, it was now akin to having a rudimentary Bodhi Cavern Heaven. As long as he continued to gain fame, creating such a specialized pill might not be impossible.

As he flipped through the book, someone else entered the library—a cultivator at the ninth stage of Qi Refinement, dressed in a scholar’s robe.

Lin Jing glanced at him and paused, slightly surprised. The newcomer, upon seeing Lin Jing, immediately scowled.

"Tang Sanzang... no, wait—Beast Taming Sect's Lin Jing!"

Lin Jing recognized the other person as well. "You're the scholar from Bodhi Heaven who couldn't snatch the Bodhi Tree."

"You haven’t reached Foundation Establishment yet?" Lin Jing asked. Though he hadn’t cultivated any appraisal techniques, his strong spiritual sense—comparable to that of a Foundation Establishment cultivator with spiritual perception—made it easy to judge the scholar’s realm, especially since the latter hadn’t concealed his aura.

"What of it?" Xu Zhizhi retorted, glaring at Lin Jing with a sour expression. "So, you really did come to Hidden Dragon Academy. I still remember what happened in the Cavern Heaven. Back then, you relied on your mechanical constructs to dominate. But outside, those things are nothing special."

Lin Jing chuckled lightly. "No need to get so worked up. Didn’t the Snow Plains Cultivator save you in the end? Besides, yesterday, I beat a Foundation Establishment cultivator from the academy—without using any mechanical constructs."

Xu Zhizhi scoffed. "Fang Shilin, the son of Everlasting Spring Valley's Valley Master, is only in the academy thanks to the preferential admission policy for heirs of major forces. Without it, someone of his talent wouldn’t have been accepted at all. Defeating him means nothing!"

He added, "If I used the cultural Dao treasure gifted by my teacher, I could have defeated him across realms as well!"

Sensing Xu Zhizhi’s lack of confidence despite his bold words, Lin Jing smirked. "So, by your logic, us special-recruit disciples are the true elites of the academy?"

Xu Zhizhi hesitated before nodding. "Naturally! What are you planning?"

"So, you, that Violet Flame Alchemist from the Pill Cauldron Sect, and the little swordsman from the Heavenly Sword Gate are already gaining some fame?" Lin Jing mused. "You’re right! My Mechanical Bamboo Rifle may not be as useful as before. Its momentary fame from duels is fleeting. If I want my name to endure, I need an artifact to carry my legacy."

He continued with a gleam in his eye, "I’m planning to mass-produce Mechanical Bamboo Rifles and auction them at the Lotus Pavilion. Imagine a device that doesn’t require cultivation—anyone can pick it up and suppress Qi Refinement cultivators. It’ll be a hit!"

Lin Jing grinned. "Of course, to market it effectively, I’ll tie it to your defeats. Using your losses as advertising material and binding our fame together. Surely there’s no concept of ‘reputation rights’ here, right?"

He clapped his hands as though inspired. "Picture it: someone uses the rifle, and onlookers remember, ‘Oh, this was made by the genius mechanic Lin Jing, who once crushed academy elites like Xu Zhizhi in the Cavern Heaven.’"

Xu Zhizhi: ???

"You conniving scoundrel!!!" Xu Zhizhi’s eyes widened with fury.

"Don’t be upset, Brother Xu. Losing to me is no shame at all. Once I ascend to the Genius Rankings, this will elevate your reputation. People will say, ‘Xu Zhizhi once contended with Lin Jing.’"

"I’m going to kill you!" Xu Zhizhi roared.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 7: The Lotus Auction House
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 7: The Lotus Auction House

"You two, quiet down!"

"Young man, don’t go around talking about fighting and killing all the time, especially since you’re supposed to be a scholar!"

The voice of the elder guarding the library echoed in, calming Xu Zhizhi's irritated heart.

Grinding his teeth, Xu Zhizhi glared at Lin Jing. "Are you really going to do this?"

Lin Jing put down the alchemy book. "What’s wrong with it? Isn’t this what really happened?"

Xu Zhizhi clenched his fist. "Of course, it’s wrong! You’re trying to use our reputation to help you cultivate the Hidden Dragon Arts!"

"It’s extremely wrong!"

"Didn’t you hear what the senior in the library said? The cultivation world isn’t just about fighting and killing, there’s also human relationships and etiquette!"

"You’re speaking without evidence. How can we verify the authenticity of the mechanized creations’ battle records? But if I also make a move to help you gain publicity, then it’s a different story. The victim himself can step forward and speak, affirming the power of these creations, and it will definitely spread widely! In exchange, all you need to do is add a bit about my heroic image of fighting the Snow Plains cultivator in the Cavern Heaven during the publicity!"

Since he couldn’t change Lin Jing’s mind, Xu Zhizhi decided to join in.

"One more opponent is better than one more friend, don’t you think? The more impressive my image becomes, the more it highlights how powerful your mechanical bamboo rifle is!" He tried to persuade Lin Jing with his silver tongue.

So it’s a commercial mutual promotion, huh? Lin Jing fell silent.

"Exactly." The library guardian, a veteran of such matters, nodded slightly. He had done this sort of thing himself in the past.

...

Not long after, Lin Jing left the library.

Though it had been a spur-of-the-moment decision, he felt his idea wasn’t wrong.

Not just in the entire ancient kingdom, but even in the Hidden Dragon City, there were countless battles taking place every day.

No one would be remembered forever; over time, names would fade into obscurity.

But... if a person’s name was tied to a holiday, a popular item, or a unique symbol, it would undoubtedly spread further and last longer than ordinary battle feats.

"The mechanical bamboo rifle has an advantage over typical magic tools in that it doesn’t require true qi to operate. Even with low cultivation, anyone can use it."

"Besides that, in terms of attack range, power, and frequency, it’s one of the best treasures available to Qi Refining stage cultivators. There are very few tools that combine all these advantages."

"Most importantly, it’s easy to make. Without carving formation patterns, I could easily assemble hundreds of them in a day, just following the process, no skill needed."

"The materials used to make the mechanical bamboo rifle aren’t rare either. The raw materials are just ordinary spirit plants, so the cost is very low."

"The core ammunition is just like a regular spirit bean, but it packs a punch comparable to a serious hit from a ninth-stage Qi Refining cultivator. These spirit beans are combined from regular spirit beans, which are so common that even stray dogs in Hidden Dragon City wouldn’t eat them if they fell on the ground. There’s huge profit here."

"The supply of ammunition can be entirely handled by the Pine Leaf Mouse. With its current abilities, making these is already effortless for it. Moreover, the stockpile it’s been accumulating these days is more than enough to sell."

"By tying the mechanical bamboo rifle to my name, every time something happens, people will think of the mechanical bamboo rifle I endorsed. Every time someone uses the rifle, the viewers will also think of me."

"Also, if it sells well, it could solve my resource problems."

The only thing Lin Jing was worried about now was whether the Pine Leaf Mouse could keep up with production if the mechanical bamboo rifle suddenly became popular. Unfortunately, the items couldn’t be regrown once synthesized; otherwise, he could have contracted a few acres of spirit fields and let the rifle and ammunition grow freely.

Lin Jing glanced at the Pine Leaf Mouse, which was still hoarding goods inside the suspended gourd...

Suddenly, an idea sparked in his mind.

If only he had more Pine Leaf Mice that could only synthesize resources.

Doppelganger!

He remembered there was a spell that could create multiple doppelgangers to fight enemies. Since a doppelganger could fight, could it be used to work for him instead?

Surely the Pine Leaf Mouse wouldn't refuse a spell that could accelerate its stockpiling speed.

...

"Senior Shen Song?"

After leaving the academy, Lin Jing secretly called out.

After a while, Shen Song replied, "What is it now?"

"You once created wooden puppets in the Great Wilderness. This method is somewhat similar to creating doppelgangers. Do you know the doppelganger technique? I want to teach it to the Pine Leaf Mouse."

Shen Song said, "Doppelganger technique? Why bother learning that useless spell? Ordinary doppelgangers are practically useless; they waste mana and have low combat power. As for external avatars, your cultivation is too low for that. Think about it after you reach the Nascent Soul stage! Once you complete the Green Emperor's Longevity Art, you should be able to create grass-and-wood doppelgangers. They'll do the job, I suppose!"

Lin Jing asked, "Can those grass-and-wood doppelgangers use the same spells and divine abilities as the main body?"

"Yes, but the effect will definitely be much weaker!"

"Is that all? If not, I’ll go take care of your worthless fish."

Lin Jing chuckled, "It’s nothing. With your words, I’m at ease now."

He turned and headed straight for the Lotus Pavilion.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 8: Promotional Advertisement
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 8: Promotional Advertisement

“Could I give it a try?” Master Shi's interest was piqued as soon as he heard the question.

He loved appraising all sorts of rare and strange new gadgets.

“No problem.” Lin Jing nodded, and Master Shi opened another door behind him, leading to an open outdoor area.

Seeing this, the Qipao-clad female cultivator excused herself.

“You said this mechanical creation has considerable power. Did you make it yourself?”

Holding the mechanical bamboo rifle, Master Shi asked curiously.

After all, even a squirrel holding it would be nearly invincible at the Qi Refining stage, so such a claim seemed overly boastful.

Many auctioneers liked to exaggerate the effectiveness of the items they sold.

“Yes.” Lin Jing nodded.

“In that case, I’d like to see for myself…” Master Shi tossed the mechanical bamboo rifle to Lin Jing and formed a protective True Qi barrier around himself.

“You attack me with it, and I'll personally experience its power!”

“Nowadays, appraisers like me, who are so dedicated to our craft, are rare to find.” Master Shi added with a bit of self-praise.

“Alright.” Lin Jing, holding the bamboo rifle, pressed the trigger.

Given that the opponent was a Foundation Establishment cultivator and had already prepared, it was certain that the mechanical bamboo rifle wouldn’t harm him.

“Water Arrow Technique? But there are no water formation marks carved into it. How did this water arrow come out… Oh, so this blue spiritual bamboo is the key to the effect.”

“And then the beans were swept out by the water flow. What does that mean?”

“You didn’t activate it using True Qi? Looks like it’s a purely mechanical creation.”

Master Shi continued muttering to himself until the first spirit bean exploded.

Boom!

Master Shi was engulfed by the explosive shockwave. His protective True Qi trembled, and he lifted his head slightly, saying, “Such power! So, these beans can explode!”

The second later...

“Wait, how did you shoot all the beans out in an instant?!”

Boom boom boom boom boom!

The spirit beans exploded all at once, sending a powerful surge of spiritual energy through the air. The sound echoed across the sky, blowing Master Shi’s hair about.

The third second, the fourth second...

Lin Jing quickly swapped magazines with one hand, and the attacks kept coming, never stopping. The relentless firepower caused Master Shi to feel the intense drain on his True Qi. He hastily shouted, “No! Stop! Are these beans made of money? Do you expect me to keep going like this?!”

“Are you here to seriously auction things, or are you trying to kill me?! Please, stop! I can tell by your stance—if I don’t die, you won’t stop!”

Lin Jing ceased firing and said, “I just wanted you to see its firing frequency.”

Master Shi glanced around at the debris, his lips twitching slightly. “I get it now! This mechanical device isn’t suitable for combat. It’s more suited for sieging a city.”

“Are you from Divine Machine Manor? How do you plan to price it? Besides the bamboo rifle, will the beans be included as well?” Master Shi pursed his lips and looked at Lin Jing, thinking of Divine Machine Manor, the top force in the Ancient Kingdom's mechanical arts ranking.

This mechanical bamboo rifle was extraordinary. It could fire dozens of powerful bombs in an instant without the need for True Qi activation. For someone at the Qi Refining stage, it was indeed a deadly weapon.

What stood out most was that Lin Jing didn’t seem at all reluctant to expend the individual spirit beans, which were as powerful as second-tier explosion talismans. This was something that made Master Shi uneasy, making him suspect that these were perhaps cheap new materials developed by Divine Machine Manor!

“One mechanical bamboo rifle, plus 500 beans.”

Lin Jing smiled slightly and said, “The starting price doesn’t matter. I can offer Lotus Pavilion a higher commission. I just have one condition.”

"Mm?" Master Shi was puzzled. "What do you mean?"

"This item has an extraordinary background. I hope that Lotus Pavilion can give it extensive publicity before the auction."

"Oh? What's its background?" Master Shi became even more interested. Items with a known background could often be priced several times higher than those without.

"Master Shi, do you know Lin Jing, the genius disciple of Beastmaster Sect?" Lin Jing asked in a low voice.

Master Shi paused for a moment, recalling, "I’ve heard of him. I believe he recently defeated a Foundation Establishment cultivator from the academy in a battle near the southern city, right? I heard some people mention it."

"Do you know that this Lin Jing was selected by the Bodhi Cave Heaven when he was at the third layer of Qi Refining?"

"Really? I didn’t know that! No wonder he's so strong." Master Shi was surprised. "But how does this mechanical creation tie in with a disciple of Beastmaster Sect?"

"Master Shi, do you know that in addition to Lin Jing of Beastmaster Sect, there were also Xu Zhizhi, the proud student of Xu Fushi from Wenyu Tianyun City, Ziling’er, a genius alchemist from the Fire Domain Dan Ding Sect, and Yuan Jing, who was the top disciple of the Outer Sect of Tianjian Sect at that time?"

"Now, Xu Zhizhi, Ziling’er, and Yuan Jing have all entered Qianlong Academy as special students. Their talents are extraordinary, with immense potential."

Master Shi nodded. "I’ve heard about these people. They did indeed enter the Cave Heaven, but I didn't know that Lin Jing of Beastmaster Sect was with them! I’ve also heard that the young hero Yuan Jing has already established his Foundation and refined the Tianjian Body. His strength is astonishing—just after entering Foundation Establishment, he defeated a late-stage Foundation Establishment rogue cultivator with a single sword strike! Ziling’er is quite impressive too. I’ve heard that she has already refined a perfect Foundation Establishment pill on her own, and will likely enter Foundation Establishment soon!"

Lin Jing nodded. "So, Master Shi, are you aware that inside the Bodhi Cave Heaven, among the three Bodhi trees, the best ones—the golden and silver Bodhi—were not claimed by those geniuses, but rather, Lin Jing took them both, one for himself and one for his pet?"

"What?" Master Shi was stunned. "Is that true?"

"To be honest, Master Shi, it was the mechanical bamboo rifle I made that helped Lin Jing and his pet, the Pine Leaf Rat, each wield one rifle to suppress those three geniuses. You could say that Lin Jing of Beastmaster Sect was the biggest winner of this Cave Heaven event! Without my mechanical bamboo rifle, he would never have been able to achieve such results."

"Whether it's a genius in literature, alchemy, or swordsmanship, they all fell before this mechanical bamboo rifle."

Master Shi was left in shock. Could this really be true?

No wonder this young man had said earlier that even a squirrel holding the mechanical bamboo rifle would be invincible at the Qi Refining stage.

"I hope that before Lotus Pavilion auctions the mechanical bamboo rifle, you can heavily promote its battle record! After all, this item helped a third-layer Qi Refining cultivator suppress three exceptionally talented Qi Refining cultivators at the ninth layer without a scratch."

Master Shi swallowed hard. He hadn't expected the mechanical bamboo rifle to be associated with some of the most famous new-generation geniuses in the current Qianlong City. He said, "But, Young Master, you’re speaking without proof. How do I know whether what you’re saying is true?"

"Lotus Pavilion won't use unverified information in the promotional materials for any auction items."

Lin Jing smiled and said, "Hehe, I won’t hide it from you, Master Shi. I am Lin Jing. I’ve already become a student at the academy. If you don’t believe me, I can bring Xu Zhizhi over to vouch for it!"

Master Shi: ???

Damn it, so this kid isn’t here to sincerely auction anything—he’s just using Lotus Pavilion to help advertise and promote the mysterious Qianlong Secret Technique!

...

The news spread slowly through word of mouth.

Whether in past or present lives, news and public opinion have always been controlled by powerful factions.

Lotus Pavilion, as a top-tier force, had substantial influence even at the level of a single city branch.

Lin Jing took advantage of Lotus Pavilion’s power to promote both himself and the mechanical bamboo rifle.

Soon, Lotus Pavilion announced the details of their upcoming auction, including a list of the items to be auctioned.

Invitations were sent out to their VIP clients, and the descriptions of the auction items were detailed.
