When many cultivators saw the mechanical bamboo rifle on the list, they paused for a moment, as Lin Jing's popularity had not yet waned.

"Mechanical bamboo rifle? The weapon used by Lin Jing of Beastmaster Sect in his early years?"

"What? This Lin Jing was actually chosen by the Cave Heaven? No wonder he's so strong—he must have gained some incredible opportunities in the Cave Heaven! His future is limitless!"

"Did the academy geniuses Xu Zhizhi, Ziling'er, and Yuan Jing also enter the same Cave Heaven with Lin Jing? And he managed to seize the best opportunities from the Cave Heaven with his mechanical bamboo rifle, even though he was only at the third layer of Qi Refining? Is that for real?"

"It’s true. Academy genius Xu Zhizhi confirmed it. He said that no matter who holds this bamboo rifle, they’re nearly invincible under Foundation Establishment, and even he couldn’t match Lin Jing back then. It’s all true!"

"I heard that Xu Zhizhi, in order to protect the national fortune and prevent foreign cultivators from taking the opportunities meant for the ancient kingdom’s cultivators, had to kill a terrifying Snowfield Body cultivator first. It took a huge toll on him, which is why he couldn’t seize the opportunity. What a pity. I wonder if Xu Zhizhi in his prime could match this mechanical creation."

"I heard Lin Jing appeared at Xianke Lai for a meal and left a message saying, 'Xu Zhizhi is not bad!' before departing!"

"I'm curious—what kind of mechanical creation could allow a low-ranking disciple from a third-rate sect to snatch opportunities from the hands of geniuses from large sects? How does this Beastmaster Sect cultivator know mechanical techniques?"

"Such a great item! I’m going to buy it for my illegitimate son to protect himself!"

Lotus Pavilion didn’t actively promote the item, but whenever they released the list of auctioned goods, it always garnered the attention of a large number of cultivators.

Some were pleased, some were worried.

When they saw that Lin Jing had publicly revealed his Cave Heaven experience for the sake of his reputation, an alchemist from Dan Ding Sect was sulking in her cave.

Several of her sisters had already asked if she had truly been beaten up by a squirrel.

The biggest winner, however, was undoubtedly Lin Jing.

Even though the mechanical bamboo rifle hadn’t been auctioned yet, just a few days later, the mark of the Qianlong on the back of his hand turned from white to light blue.

His name from yesterday and today had both gained momentum, and he became more widely known.

Of course, the biggest loser was the Pine Leaf Rat.

It had seen Lin Jing take so many rounds of ammunition from the warehouse... and immediately began wailing with a ghostly, sorrowful cry!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 9: The Academy's Attention
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 9: The Academy's Attention

"Let me see yours."

"Let me see yours too."

"Nice color."

"Yours is darker, though."

"Pleasure doing business with you."

Hidden Corner of Hidden Dragon City

Lin Jing and Xu Zhizhi met a few days later.

Upon seeing that Lin Jing had advanced the Hidden Dragon Arts to the second stage, marked by a blue seal, Xu Zhizhi couldn’t hide his envy. "I arrived here months ahead of you, tried all kinds of ways to make a name for myself, and yet, in just a few days, you’ve already caught up!"

After all, part of the sect's troubles was partially his responsibility.

"I know a bit about your sect’s situation," Xu Zhizhi said thoughtfully. "Blessings often come in disguise, but it seems your fortune might just be too strong for the sect to handle."

"Anyway, don’t worry too much! With your luck, you’re bound to revive the sect. Just make sure to seize the opportunities that come your way!"

"Even the heirs of great sects, sons of Nascent Soul cultivators, descendants of Soul Formation experts, or those with innate spiritual bodies don’t get special recruitment from the academy. But for those like us, chosen by a Cavern Heaven, the academy personally extends invitations. Do you know why?"

"Because cultivators who’ve entered a Cavern Heaven once are highly likely to enter it a second or third time! That’s an opportunity from the Celestial Realm, greater than anything else!"

"That’s precisely why I had to kill that Snow Plains Cultivator," Xu Zhizhi admitted. "Severing their fortune strengthened mine. That incident made me famous as well."

"So, Lin, you just need to seize your future opportunities. I’m certain you’ll become the revivalist of the Beast Taming Sect. I, Xu Zhizhi, have never misjudged anyone!"

"I’ll take your kind words, Brother Xu," Lin Jing said sincerely. "You scholars always know how to put things so nicely."

Xu Zhizhi gave a gentle smile. "We’re friends now. Let’s let bygones be bygones. If you ever need help, don’t hesitate to come to me."

"One last piece of advice, Brother Lin: these days, try not to go out much. And if you do, make sure to disguise yourself!"

"Your newfound fame will undoubtedly attract attention from academy students who want to boost their own reputation by challenging you!"

"I understand," Lin Jing replied. "Next, I’m heading to the library to study. What about you, Brother Xu?"

"I need to settle the spirit stone payments for those people I hired to spread my reputation," Xu Zhizhi said with a grin.

Hidden Dragon Academy

In a pitch-black hall, two rows of long stone tables stretched out. On them lay a dozen white stones, neatly arranged.

Suddenly, one stone began to glow. Shortly after, seven or eight other stones lit up, each radiating beams of light that formed vague shadows.

"The recruitment ceremony held once every five years is approaching. Who will serve as the chief examiner this time?" one shadow asked.

"Let’s have Ziwai do it. He recruited four exceptionally talented students for the academy this year. They can assist with the examinations," another shadow replied.

"How capable are these four? I heard a few monstrous talents will be joining the academy. Can those four handle it?"

"This is an excellent opportunity for them to make a name for themselves. If they fail to seize it, that’s their loss."

After parting ways with Xu Zhizhi, Lin Jing headed to the academy library.

There were only a few pages left in the Scripture of Alchemy, and today he planned to memorize them all. Understanding was secondary; committing it to memory came first. As the saying goes, read a book a hundred times, and its meaning will naturally reveal itself .

Inside the library, Lin Jing held the scripture in one hand, committing it to memory, while using his other hand to press his forehead with a Spirit Nurturing Spell to alleviate fatigue.

Whenever his qi ran low, he would pop a Replenishing Pill into his mouth.

"Creating an original pill recipe isn’t about conjuring something out of thin air," he mused aloud.

"Most ‘original recipes’ by alchemists are, at their core, improvements on existing ones. They build upon the work of predecessors, either adding, subtracting, or substituting ingredients, using different flames or techniques to refine a pill with a similar effect."

From Lin Jing’s recent study, developing a new recipe seemed both challenging and simple at the same time.

If an alchemist aimed to create a recipe for a Soaring Pill that could make even a chicken or dog ascend to immortality, that would indeed be a fool’s dream.

This pill is unprecedented on the Tianyuan Continent, its effects extraordinary—something no alchemist from the lower realms could have created. It is undoubtedly an Immortal Pill.

However, if an alchemist only aims to create a basic Healing Pill, the task becomes much simpler.

With tens of thousands of spirit herbs possessing healing properties in the cultivation world, numerous versions of healing pills circulate in the market.

Even inconspicuous clinics in small towns dare to advertise "exclusive secret recipes" or "original formulas." This is because developing and refining low-grade pills truly isn’t difficult.

Some Grade-1 recipes even require only one or two ingredients, where the alchemist merely extracts their nutrients and forms them into pills.

What Lin Jing desired was a Foundation Establishment Pill designed for blood-refining methods, one that was neither exceptionally difficult nor overly simple.

It wasn’t too challenging because Foundation Establishment Pills were Grade-2 pills, and most sects had their own unique formulas tailored to their cultivation techniques. For example, the Demonic Dao Foundation Pill of the Beast Taming Sect had been studied to exhaustion by countless cultivators.

However, it wasn’t a walk in the park either. Lin Jing had only tried refining Grade-1 pills so far and had never attempted a Grade-2 pill. Refining Grade-2 pills at the Qi Refining stage was extraordinarily difficult—a feat typically reserved for prodigies of the alchemical arts.

Rumor had it that Ziling’er from the Pill Cauldron Sect had successfully refined one of the most challenging Grade-2 pills: a special Foundation Establishment Pill—the exact pill Lin Jing sought. Her success catapulted her reputation at Hidden Dragon Academy.

Of course, Ziling’er was a dedicated alchemist who had gained enlightenment in Bodhi Heaven, so her achievement wasn’t surprising. Lin Jing, on the other hand, was still a novice in the alchemical path.

"If I want to develop my own exclusive Foundation Establishment Pill, it obviously won’t be from scratch..."

"Instead, I’ll build on existing recipes, adding my own adjustments, experimenting repeatedly until either I succeed or I fail endlessly."

Lin Jing had already chosen a recipe from one of the alchemy books in the library: the Grade-2 pill, Beast Blood Pill—a body-tempering pill.

Although the alchemy books in the library didn’t detail any blood-refining methods, the Beast Blood Pill’s materials included various types of beast blood, making it a suitable focus for Lin Jing’s research.

In addition to the Beast Blood Pill, Lin Jing planned to study the recipe for the most common ordinary Foundation Establishment Pill used across the continent. Most sect-specific Foundation Establishment Pills were essentially modified versions of this foundational recipe.

"Now that I have the top-tier beast fire from the Dragon Carp, all I need is a high-grade pill furnace, and my preparations will be complete."

Slowly refining, slowly researching—what Lin Jing lacked least was time.

Surprisingly, the Hidden Dragon Arts' comprehension bonus wasn’t even Lin Jing’s greatest asset for developing pill formulas. It was his time.

Currently, Lin Jing could live for over a thousand years, his Pine Leaf Mouse for five to six hundred years. This was just during the Qi Refining stage! Even the Dragon Carp was quite long-lived; ordinary wild carps could survive for over a century, let alone a Qi Refining Stage Nine Dragon Carp with awakened Dragon Veins!

They were all long-lived beings—victory depended on who could outlast the others.

As Lin Jing exited the library, a look of surprise appeared on his face.

He saw a middle-aged man, scholarly in appearance, standing with a smile.

"Not bad! It’s only been a few days, and you’ve already stirred up quite the commotion," Mr. Cao remarked with a grin. "It seems you’ve adapted well to the rules of Hidden Dragon Academy. As expected of someone chosen by a Cavern Heaven. Because of your outstanding performance, the academy has decided to give you a chance to make a name for yourself!"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 10: Innate Divine Physique
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 10: Innate Divine Physique

“Senior, you flatter me…”

“The opportunity for fame you mentioned, what exactly is it?”

Lin Jing was reminded of his previous life when professors would offer him “opportunities,” only to load him up with tedious and menial tasks.

Cao Ziwei chuckled. “In a few months, the academy’s enrollment season will begin again. At that time, numerous talented cultivators from all over will come to Hidden Dragon City, hoping to gain admission into Hidden Dragon Academy.”

“Every year, there’s a special segment during the assessment where academy students act as examiners and engage in combat trials with the candidates.”

“You want me to act as one of the examiners this time?” Lin Jing asked in surprise.

Cao Ziwei nodded. “To be precise, this is the academy’s arrangement. You won’t be the only one; all the students who have recently shown exceptional performance will be examiners.”

“This is actually a great opportunity. These candidates are geniuses from various regions, often accompanied by senior sect members. During the assessment, the academy and sect representatives will focus intently on the examiners. If you perform well, you’ll gain significant attention.”

“I’ll take the examiner role!” Lin Jing agreed. This opportunity, like many things, had its pros and cons—success would depend entirely on his strength.

“Excellent,” Cao Ziwei said. “Prepare yourself in the coming months. Since you’re at the Qi Refining Stage, you’ll only need to face candidates of the same stage. Don’t underestimate them just because you’ve defeated Foundation Establishment cultivators before. You might end up being their stepping stone. In past assessments, it’s not uncommon for senior students to be defeated by candidates.”

“Even though these candidates might not have been specially recruited like you, it doesn’t mean they’re weak,” Cao Ziwei warned. “I’ve heard there are quite a few monsters in the Qi Refining Stage among this year’s candidates.”

“From the royal family of the capital, there’s a prodigy at the ninth stage of Qi Refining with an Innate Divine Physique!”

“From the Divine Talisman Sect of the Dao Domain, there’s a saintess, also at the ninth stage of Qi Refining, with the Talisman Physique. She can substitute her brush with her hand, the heavens and earth her paper, and inscribe talismans in the void.”

“There’s also the grandson of a Nascent Soul-stage elder from the Demon-Slaying Bureau. Though he was said to be unruly in his youth, he reformed and uncovered a demonic plot by the Gu Cult in Snow Moon City just a few months ago. He’s also at the ninth stage of Qi Refining!”

“That Innate Divine Physique cultivator is already renowned in the Ancient Domain at the Qi Refining stage. He’s a well-known genius in the capital. Defeating him in the assessment would mean far more than defeating someone like Fang Shilin,” Cao Ziwei said with a persuasive smile.

At this, Lin Jing wore a strange expression.

The first two indeed sounded impressive.

Cultivation physiques were divided into attribute-based physiques and special physiques. For instance, Fire Spirit and Ice Spirit Physiques were attribute-based and enhanced corresponding techniques and spells. Special physiques like the Primordial Yang Body and Longevity Body were rarer and offered broader benefits.

Tianji Pavilion even compiled a Physique Rankings list, much like the Genius Rankings, cataloging a hundred physiques. Those on the list were often deemed “divine physiques,” with a guaranteed path to forming a Golden Core and a high likelihood of reaching Nascent Soul or even Soul Transformation without major bottlenecks.

The top ten physiques were called “immortal physiques,” said to offer a chance at ascension to immortality.

The Innate Divine Physique was undoubtedly powerful. Anyone with such a physique who cultivated Hidden Dragon techniques was bound to make waves. Compared to someone like Fang Shilin—who had reached Foundation Establishment without even an acquired spiritual body—the gap was immense.

Lin Jing had initially taken this seriously, but when he heard about the Nascent Soul elder’s grandson, he felt something was off.

He wanted to ask, “Is that Demon-Slaying Bureau genius named Gao Sheng?”

Was that guy finally out of confinement? It seemed his family didn’t want him causing more trouble and had sent him to the academy.

“I’ll work hard during this period to prepare myself,” Lin Jing said.

“Senior, I was thinking… perhaps I should rent a cave dwelling,” Lin Jing added. He realized that if he wanted to refine pills, staying in a wooden hut wouldn’t cut it.

“Very well,” Cao Ziwei said with a smile. “Resources are meant to be used. The rent for a cave dwelling is 10,000 spirit stones a year, but the benefits are worth it. Each dwelling is connected to the academy’s spiritual veins. Cultivating there for a year is far more effective than using 10,000 spirit stones on regular cultivation.”

Cao Ziwei’s words made sense, but Lin Jing still felt a pinch in his heart. Thankfully, he’d already decided to start a business.

The auction for the Mechanical Bamboo Rifle was still some time away. Lotus Pavilion always advertised its auction items for at least half a month, giving sellers ample time to prepare spirit stones.

…

The academy’s back mountain was dotted with cave dwellings for students. Lin Jing rented a small hilltop dwelling, ensuring he wouldn’t have to wander the city like a vagabond anymore.

“Time to bring the Dragon Carp back!” Now that Lin Jing had his own place, he decided it was time to reclaim the Dragon Carp. After all, he couldn’t keep troubling Senior Divine Pine to look after it forever.

He quickly left the academy, calling out silently. Not long after, a Dragon Carp was tossed unceremoniously at his feet. The carp was bruised and battered, its body marked with blue and purple patches—clearly, it had taken quite a beating. Fortunately, it seemed to have gained better control over its flames.

“You’re still alive?” Lin Jing picked up the Dragon Carp and asked with concern.

“Blub…” The Dragon Carp responded weakly. Pain? It was used to it by now. From now on, no more randomly setting fires—it wasn’t worth getting beaten by that old pine tree again.

“As long as you’re alive,” Lin Jing said with relief. Then he addressed the ancient pine tree. “Senior Divine Pine, I’ve got a question.”

“Do you think the Pine Leaf Mouse, with its current strength, could defeat a same-level Divine Physique?”

“A Divine Physique?”

Divine Pine chuckled. “You’ve got some serious misconceptions about Divine Physiques, don’t you? Even a demonic beast with the bloodline of a Demon King would only get shredded to pieces by a same-level Divine Physique. You’d better go find a shady spot and cool off.”

“Not a single chance?” Lin Jing asked. His expression suddenly shifted into a classic villainous smirk as he dragged a finger menacingly across his neck. “I want to take him down—no matter the means.”

The demonic pine was amused by the display. Seeing Lin Jing’s "devil-may-care" attitude, it decided to share a dubious strategy. “Let it eat the Void-Skipping Grass! Doesn’t its innate Crimson Leaf have spatial properties?”

Lin Jing was taken aback. “But it’s not ripe yet.”

“That’s exactly why it can eat it,” Divine Pine explained. “A fully mature fifth-tier spatial spirit grass is so packed with nutrients it could make it explode. It would need to be at the Monster Core stage to barely digest it. If it eats it unripe, the nutrient content is much lower.”

“Eating it unripe won’t poison it?” Lin Jing asked. Most spiritual plants contained toxins before maturity as a natural defense mechanism against being consumed prematurely.

“There’s poison, of course,” the pine replied nonchalantly. “That’s why it needs guts. Its spatial affinity might prevent it from dying, and if it survives, its mastery of spatial techniques will improve dramatically. With high-level spatial abilities during the Qi Refining Stage, it might not be much worse than a Divine Physique.”

The pine’s suggestion was clearly a path to ruin, and Lin Jing waved it off. “Forget it. I’ll stick to being a proper righteous cultivator. I haven’t mastered Immortal Rebirth yet—if I die, I can’t resurrect.”

“What’s there to be afraid of?” Divine Pine retorted. “Devil cultivators thrive on turning the impossible into the possible. Besides, that Pine Leaf Mouse of yours has already achieved minor success with the Everlasting Emperor’s Longevity Art, and you have that golden spiritual rice to enhance its ability to digest plants. With my help, it won’t die. The worst-case scenario is losing a single immature Void-Skipping Grass! So what if a peerless genius gets eaten by a beast? I haven’t heard anyone complain yet!”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 11: Flying Leaf Spatial Technique!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 11: Flying Leaf Spatial Technique!

“Genius of the same race, my foot!

How could an unintelligent plant suddenly become a so-called genius?!”

Lin Jing felt the Elder Shen Pine was acting odd—almost as if it was trying to lure him into becoming a demonic cultivator.

It seemed like one of those situations where someone who had already fallen into trouble wanted to drag others down with them.

The Shen Pine scolded in frustration, “I’ve even agreed to help you, yet you don’t seize the opportunity!

“Dawdling like this, once the hundred-year protection period passes, you won’t even form a Core, and my first attempt at safeguarding cultivators will end in utter embarrassment!”

Lin Jing took a deep breath and, after some serious thought, responded, “Alright.

“Since Senior is so insistent, I’ll trouble you to lend a hand!

“That’s more like it.” The Shen Pine, now in its pinecone form, cracked open in satisfaction.

Lin Jing convinced himself.

A mature Fifth-Grade spatial spirit herb, while incredibly rare and potent, would likely take an eternity to fully utilize.

It was better to use it now! Helping the Pine Leaf Mouse gain stronger combat capabilities earlier wasn’t a bad strategy either.

Who knows? If it managed to beat that so-called Divine Physique during the trial, it would gain immense fame.

The Qianlong Mark might even advance to the next stage!

A higher-tier Qianlong Mark could amplify their spell cultivation more rapidly, making their strength soar in the short term.

With greater strength, they’d be better equipped to seize other opportunities, and their power would snowball from there.

On the other hand, if they hoarded resources but perished midway, they’d only benefit others—a common regret in the cultivation world.

The greatest tragedy for cultivators was dying and leaving resources to their enemies.

And if the worst-case scenario was losing a spirit herb they stumbled upon, it didn’t seem too bad after all.

“Thank you, Senior,” Lin Jing sincerely expressed his gratitude for the Shen Pine’s willingness to help.

The Shen Pine lamented, “If only I were more familiar with your human demonic techniques, I’d have already pushed you, with your Longevity Body, into cultivating something like a life-burning or lifespan-sacrificing demonic art. It would’ve been so much faster than the Beast Taming Art!”

“The Beast Taming Art isn’t that bad,” the Green Emperor coughed.

“Not great, either. Sure, it’s a technique for the stable boys in the Celestial Palace, but at least it has some connection to the palace—it’s still better than the native techniques of Tianyuan’s small continent,” the Shen Pine quipped.

Wasting no time, the Pine Leaf Mouse was summoned by the Green Emperor.

When it heard it couldn’t delay eating the Spatial Evasion Grass any longer, it hesitated, showing great reluctance.

After all, it had taken care of that herb for a long time and grown attached to it.

“…” The mouse had hoped to let the herb mature and propagate a whole batch of offspring before eating it. Eating it now would end that dream of a legacy.

“You’ve got some nerve!” The Shen Pine scoffed. “Even with my mastery of the Everlasting Emperor’s Longevity Art, I couldn’t force the herb to reproduce, and you think you can? Dream on, little mouse!”

Hearing this, the Pine Leaf Mouse sighed. Fine, it would eat the herb.

It scurried back into the Suspended Gourd, pulled out the Spatial Evasion Grass—shaped like a white bean sprout—and held it carefully in its paws. Returning to the outside world, its green eyes turned toward Lin Jing and the pinecone, seeking instructions on how to consume it.

The Shen Pine, scanning the herb and the mouse with its spiritual sense, instructed, “Take out a hundred grains of golden spirit rice first. Eat ten, and set the remaining ninety aside.”

The Pine Leaf Mouse froze.

“!!!” It was stunned. Eating ten grains of golden spirit rice was like courting death! It worried it’d go so mad with hunger afterward that it might even try to eat the pinecone in front of it!

“Suppress the effects with the Everlasting Emperor’s Longevity Art. Alternate between eating rice when full and digesting the herb when hungry. Repeat this cycle!”

“Just follow the instructions. If anything goes wrong, I’ll intervene,” the Shen Pine reassured.

The explanation was clear enough for the Pine Leaf Mouse to understand. Resigned, it could only trust that it would be rescued if needed. Its face displayed utter despair.

“What’s the holdup? Not enough spirit rice? Then make more!” the Shen Pine barked impatiently, seeing the mouse hesitate.

“That’s not the issue. The problem is that it doesn’t want to part with so much spirit rice at once,” Lin Jing, understanding his beast better than anyone, explained.

“Eat it! Remember, a flower contains a world, and a leaf holds a universe. Who knows? After digesting the Spatial Evasion Grass, you might unlock an internal leaf-space!” Lin Jing encouraged.

The mouse’s eyes burned with determination, just like the nearby Dragon Carp recovering from injuries.

Grinding its teeth, the Pine Leaf Mouse poured out its entire stockpile of golden spirit rice. The pile exceeded a thousand grains, leaving the Shen Pine flabbergasted.

“Seriously? You’ve hoarded this much spirit rice, and now you’re whining about a hundred grains?!” the Shen Pine exclaimed in disbelief.

The Pine Leaf Mouse didn’t bother to explain. It grabbed a handful of golden spirit rice with grim resolve and shoved it into its mouth.

In an instant, its emerald eyes turned crimson, and its stomach rumbled like thunder, startling the Dragon Carp, which backed away in fear of becoming the mouse’s next meal.

“Let’s tie you up, just in case.” After the mouse ate the rice, it immediately consumed the Spatial Evasion Grass. To prevent it from devouring anything—or anyone—else, the Shen Pine restrained it with branches, leaving only enough freedom to reach the remaining rice.

Seeing this, Lin Jing closed his eyes. Through their Bloodline Contract, he could vaguely sense the mouse’s transformation—a stark contrast to his earlier inability to connect with the Dragon Carp during its bloodline awakening.

Entering a state of perception, Lin Jing’s vision was overwhelmed by a cascade of silvery-white light. The mysterious silver energy seemed intent on breaking down the Pine Leaf Mouse’s body.

Inside, its golden-green True Energy acted as an unyielding barrier, resisting and absorbing the silver energy, gradually integrating it.

Externally, the Pine Leaf Mouse began to glow. The golden-green light interwove with the silver brilliance, exuding an aura of otherworldly mystery. It seemed to hover between existence and non-existence.

As the mouse entered this peculiar state, Lin Jing was drawn into it as well. Time flew by unnoticed, and the Shen Pine observed in mild astonishment.

The Shen Pine turned to the Dragon Carp, the only other being still awake. “This Beast Tamer and his Pine Leaf Mouse have exceptional spatial perception.

“If they ever ascend to the Spirit Transformation stage, this talent could prove invaluable.”

The Dragon Carp, still resentful from its recent beatings, rolled over in silent protest.

Time passed.

On the seventh day, the mysterious silver energy no longer posed a threat to the Pine Leaf Mouse.

By the tenth day, it had fully digested the Spatial Evasion Grass. Although its cultivation level hadn’t significantly changed, its spatial perception had sharpened considerably.

Maintaining this heightened perception, it instinctively took out a red leaf and held it close. Two days later, the Pine Leaf Mouse finally opened its eyes.

“How do you feel?” Lin Jing, who had stayed by its side throughout, also opened his eyes. During these ten days, he felt he’d undergone some changes, though he couldn’t pinpoint exactly what.

The Pine Leaf Mouse, clutching the red leaf, seemed dazed. It had consumed countless grains of spirit rice to suppress the herb’s power and felt external assistance multiple times—it must’ve been the Shen Pine using the Everlasting Emperor’s Longevity Art to help.

“Sigh…” The mouse stared at the red leaf, feeling disappointed. It hadn’t sensed any internal space opening up.

“Why are you crying?” the Shen Pine asked impatiently. “What exactly do you feel?”

“Even though the Spatial Evasion Grass isn’t something human cultivators can directly benefit from, you’re a spatial-element spirit beast. With the Everlasting Emperor’s Longevity Art aiding your digestion, you should’ve gained something, right?” The Shen Pine studied the mouse intently.

Hearing this, the Pine Leaf Mouse hesitated.

“Sigh…”

It gently tossed the red leaf into the air, and in the next instant, its figure vanished.

Reappearing beside the floating red leaf, it grabbed it and stood atop the heavens.

“Spatial teleportation? No, it’s incomplete—a technique reliant on the spatial red leaf as a coordinate.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 12: Plotting the Grand Venture
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 12: Plotting the Grand Venture

“Spatial teleportation technique?!”

Lin Jing, watching from below alongside the Dragon Carp, widened his eyes in amazement. The Pine Leaf Mouse truly disappeared, only to reappear moments later.

“Indeed.”

“Spatial teleportation,” the Shen Pine chuckled. “It aligns perfectly with the properties of the Spatial Evasion Grass. It seems the little mouse digested it well!

“While it’s not a combat-oriented spatial spell, it’s quite impressive. Keep in mind, spatial mastery typically begins at the Void Profound stage.

“At that level, cultivators truly gain control over spatial forces.

“For example, the academy jade slip you carried—likely crafted by the Void Profound stage headmaster of Qianlong Academy—can instantly connect you across domains from the Great Wasteland Domain to the academy for inter-domain teleportation.

“Similarly, the large teleportation arrays in ancient nations were all constructed by Void Profound or higher cultivators.

“Of course, the Void Profound stage’s perfect mastery of spatial forces doesn’t mean lower-level cultivators can’t use them.

“However, unlike the miraculous abilities of Void Profound experts, low-level cultivators usually need external aids to use spatial spells.

“For instance, crafting storage bags and tools, setting up spatial formations, or laying spatial seals requires access to spatial resources.

“Your little mouse falls into this category. Using the spatial red leaf as an anchor, it achieved a form of spatial teleportation.”

The Shen Pine sighed with a hint of pride. “At the very least, it now has an excellent escape mechanism when facing opponents without spatial abilities!”

“...”

By now, the Pine Leaf Mouse had returned to the ground, letting out a small sigh. It still longed for the internal leaf-space Lin Jing had mentioned.

“Thank you, Senior Shen Pine.” Seeing the Pine Leaf Mouse unharmed and even gaining such a significant ability, Lin Jing was thoroughly satisfied.

“No need to thank me. I merely ensured it wasn’t poisoned to death by a Fifth-Grade spirit herb. Its gains are due to its spatial talent—or perhaps its sheer determination.” The Shen Pine chuckled. “But this ability lacks direct offensive power in combat.

“If you want to defeat a Divine Physique of the same rank using this skill, you’ll need to be creative!”

“Understood!” Lin Jing mused. The value of the spatial teleportation technique lay in its unpredictability.

“Wait, it’s already been over ten days. Do you think anyone in Qianlong City is still looking for you? Shouldn’t you leave by now?” The Shen Pine dispelled the formation it had set up, signaling the end of its assistance.

It needed rest—it had just cared for the Dragon Carp and then the Pine Leaf Mouse. It wasn’t Lin Jing’s protector; it felt more like a glorified babysitter!

“Ten days…” Lin Jing froze. “What? That long? Did the Mechanized Bamboo Rifle auction start without me?!”

How could he have missed such an important event? Quickly, he stowed the Pine Leaf Mouse back into the Suspended Gourd, urging it to practice its teleportation technique while he dashed toward the academy’s cave dwelling.

“I rented that place with spirit stones. If no one’s there, it’s a waste!”

After tossing the Dragon Carp at the cave’s entrance to guard it, Lin Jing hurried to the Lotus Pavilion. Just as he stepped into the street, a figure intercepted him.

“Where have you been these past days, Lin Jing?”

Xu Zhizhi had been waiting here for a long time and finally ran into Lin Jing.

“You missed the Mechanized Bamboo Rifle auction! Do you know how intense it was?” he said, looking regretful.

“Brother Xu!” Lin Jing sighed in relief upon seeing him. “So, did everything go smoothly?”

“I was in seclusion, cultivating spells, and lost track of time. Can you fill me in on the details of the auction?”

“Of course.” Xu Zhizhi smirked. “Let’s skip the other items. Your Mechanized Bamboo Rifle? It was a madhouse!”

“Oh?” Lin Jing was surprised. While the rifle was innovative, it was still just a Qi Refinement weapon.

How could it cause such a frenzy?

“At first, a few Qi Refinement cultivators bid for it.

“By the end, guess which two groups were fighting over it?” Xu Zhizhi’s expression was amused.

Lin Jing shook his head, clueless.

“One was a cultivator from the Agricultural Bureau.

“The other? Someone from the Divine Mechanism Manor!”

“Unlike the others, I think they cared more about the materials of your rifle!

“Perhaps they mistook it for a mutated spirit plant and wanted to study it. After all, the Spirit Bean’s power is quite remarkable.”

“And do you know the final price?”

“Three full slabs of low-grade spirit stones!”

“???” Lin Jing was stunned. He couldn’t believe it fetched such a high price.

“Was it the Agricultural Bureau or the Divine Mechanism Manor that won?” he asked instinctively.

“Neither,” Xu Zhizhi replied with a mysterious smile.

Lin Jing: ???

“Then who?”

Xu Zhizhi explained, “Just as the bidding reached its peak, a young man dressed like someone from the Demon-Slaying Bureau raised the price so high that both sides backed down.”

“The Demon-Slaying Bureau?”

“Yes, he introduced himself as Gao Sheng. I looked into it—he’s from a prominent demon-slaying family. They’re quite wealthy. I’m not sure what he plans to do with your rifle. Maybe this is just what they call ‘having money to burn.’ Exciting, isn’t it?

“Lin Jing, you struck gold this time! Don’t forget to treat me to three meals at Xianke Lai! After all, I instructed the bidders to sing your praises during the auction.”

Lin Jing fell silent. He had a bad feeling about this.

When he finally reached the Lotus Auction House to collect his earnings, Gao Sheng was waiting for him.

Accompanying him was a Foundation Establishment cultivator with a wealthy appearance, who stood to the side with a flattering smile.

“Lin Jing, finally! I’ve been waiting for you!” Gao Sheng, dressed in the black uniform of a demon-slaying officer, greeted him with a hearty laugh and brandished the Mechanized Bamboo Rifle.

“Look!”

“Why’d you spend so much on that thing?” Lin Jing muttered, his lips twitching as he eyed the rifle.

“Well, it’s your creation. I couldn’t stand seeing it go cheap! It’s just pocket money, no big deal.” Gao Sheng waved dismissively. “I wasn’t too thrilled when the old man sent me to the academy, but meeting you made it worth it.”

“What do you want?” Lin Jing asked warily.

“To plot a grand venture together! That item is still with you, right?” Gao Sheng said cryptically. “I’ve got some promising opportunities lately. Let’s partner up! The extra spirit stones from the auction will be your commission.”

Lin Jing’s vision darkened. This guy was probably like the Cold Moon Sword Immortal—someone who wanted to exploit him for personal gain.

There was no way he’d team up with this guy. At least with the Cold Moon Sword Immortal, he knew what he was getting into.

“Of course, there’s no such thing as a free lunch. He must know I have the Longevity Body and is eyeing me,” Lin Jing thought. Still, since Gao Sheng had given him so many spirit stones, he hesitated to outright refuse.

“We’ll see…” Lin Jing replied evasively.

He then turned to the wealthy-looking cultivator beside Gao Sheng. “You must be the person in charge of the Lotus Auction House?”

“I’m Zhang Duobao. I didn’t realize Lin Jing and Young Master Gao were such good friends. Here’s a Blue Lotus-level token from our pavilion. With it, you’ll enjoy discounts at any of our establishments.”

“Thank you.” Lin Jing accepted the token. “Can I withdraw my auction earnings now?”

“Of course,” Zhang Duobao said with a smile.

“Also, I’d like to consign more Mechanized Bamboo Rifles for auction,” Lin Jing said.

“Absolutely. How many?” Zhang Duobao asked eagerly.

“Two hundred. But I’d like 150 of them sent to other cities for auction,” Lin Jing replied with a grin.

“Two hundred?!” Zhang Duobao was stunned.

Beside him, Gao Sheng also blinked in disbelief. “Why not keep them? We could take them to the mountains, round up some monkeys, train them to use the rifles, and wipe out small Demonic Dao strongholds. You’re a Beast Tamer, right? Imagine 200 rifles firing simultaneously—unless it’s a Core Formation expert, we’d be unstoppable!”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 13: You’re a Grown-Up Fish Now
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 13: You’re a Grown-Up Fish Now

Lin Jing had no intention of indulging Gao Sheng’s antics. He needed to focus on making money!

Although he shared a desire to eradicate the Yin Corpse Sect—a demonic sect notorious for human trafficking—now wasn’t the time.

He feared that teaming up with Gao Sheng to fight demons would likely end with both of them being captured. Gao Sheng would probably be sent home safely while Lin Jing got left behind as a hostage.

...

Given the overwhelming success of the first Mechanized Bamboo Rifle auction—partially due to the extravagance of a wealthy bidder—Lin Jing decided to sell the rifles exclusively through auctions for now. Once the prices declined, he’d flood the market with them at a lower price.

After leaving the auction house, Gao Sheng continued following him until they stopped in a quiet corner.

“Brother Gao, are you planning to participate in the academy’s assessment in four months?” Lin Jing asked.

“Of course,” Gao Sheng replied. “My family’s forcing me into it, though I really don’t want to go.

He tapped his temple. “Where I come from, all my peers had innate spiritual bodies or unique constitutions. I was the only one with an ordinary body. That killed my interest in cultivation as a child. But when I grew up, I realized that when immortals close one door, they open another. My talent might lie here.”

“Unfortunately, I’ll be serving as a proctor for one of the assessment stages,” Lin Jing said regretfully. “I also need to enter seclusion for intense training, so I can’t join you on your investigations.”

“Really?” Gao Sheng looked disappointed. “But the unsolved cases I’ve secretly unsealed are fascinating!

“One involves the Gu God Sect!

“Another even ties back to the Heavenly Demon Sect, the former top demonic sect that’s been completely destroyed!”

“Well, if you’re unavailable, I suppose we can wait a few months and go together afterward.”

“Brother Gao, you’d better prepare for the assessment,” Lin Jing advised. “If you get eliminated… I can’t bend the rules with the academy overseers watching.”

“Fair enough,” Gao Sheng nodded. “Once I’ve mastered the Hidden Dragon Arts, I’ll invite you to tackle those cases with me. Solving them could make us famous across the ancient nations—it’s a win-win.”

“By the way, do you know who else will serve as proctors?”

“Is it just you, or are there others?”

“There are several others,” Lin Jing replied. “From what I’ve heard, candidates will choose one proctor to challenge and demonstrate their strength.”

Gao Sheng looked at Lin Jing and smirked. “If I didn’t know you, I’d definitely pick you.

“Beast Taming Sect cultivators usually lack personal combat strength. Even with my minimal training, I think I could beat one.”

“But you’re an exception, of course, considering you’re hosting that thing inside you.”

The Dragon Carp rolled its eyes. “How would you even use it during the assessment?”

The Ice Soul Gu was far from reliable; using it now would make the Dragon Carp hesitant to face the Genius Rankings.

“That’s true,” Gao Sheng chuckled. “It’s not like the candidates have any grudges with him.”

“Brother Lin, let me help you out. I’ll find out which proctor is the strongest and let you know!”

“You’re too kind! You’re the epitome of integrity!” Lin Jing clasped his hands in mock reverence. “Why don’t we talk more after the assessment when we’re classmates? Good luck with your preparations, Brother Gao!”

“You passed the character test—I knew you’d be a good teammate!” Gao Sheng laughed, returning the gesture.

“By the way, where’s your horse?”

“You’re still thinking about my horse?” Gao Sheng asked suspiciously.

Lin Jing shrugged. “No, just curious.”

With the Dragon Carp, now blessed with Kirin bloodline potential, Lin Jing had no reason to covet a dragon-horned white horse. The carp’s eventual transformation into a dragon was far more promising.

What he lacked, however, was rare beast blood for crafting Beast Blood Pills.

“Well, I should be off to buy a fish tank for my new pet carp,” Lin Jing said, bidding Gao Sheng farewell.

Thanks to Gao Sheng’s generosity, Lin Jing had made a fortune today. Coupled with the Pine Leaf Mouse mastering an advanced spatial technique, he felt it was time to celebrate.

Since the Dragon Carp couldn’t yet reside in the Beast Taming space, it remained outside. Lin Jing decided to buy it a fireproof fish tank.

...

At the academy’s back mountain, Lin Jing returned to his cave dwelling. Outside, he found the Dragon Carp basking in the sun, staring at the sky.

Smiling, Lin Jing called out, “Look what I’ve got for you—a gift!”

“A fish tank! And it’s fireproof!”

The Dragon Carp, overjoyed to see Lin Jing, was even more excited to hear about the gift.

Although its childhood memories had been hazy, its bloodline awakening gradually brought them back. As a mutated fire carp rejected by its clan, it felt incredibly fortunate to have found a Beast Tamer like Lin Jing.

Until…

The Dragon Carp stared dumbfounded at the cauldron in Lin Jing’s hands, bristling with fury. Who called this a fish tank?!

This was clearly a cauldron for refining pills! Was Lin Jing mocking its ignorance?

Sure, it was fireproof, but that didn’t make it any better!

“This is the Hundred Beast Cauldron, crafted from companion beast ores of various demonic beasts. It’s inscribed with multiple pill-refinement formations that greatly enhance the efficacy of materials with beast origins,” Lin Jing explained with a pained expression. “It cost me 9,999 spirit stones.”

It was the perfect cauldron for his current needs, given that almost all the materials for Beast Blood Pills came from demonic beasts.

The cauldron was highly effective for refining beast-related pills, and Lin Jing figured it could also be used to temper the Dragon Carp.

After all, some mythical beings—like certain apes—had gained their abilities through being refined in cauldrons.

“Blub…” The Dragon Carp glared at Lin Jing, its sharp instincts sensing that the cauldron was meant more for refining it than pills.

“You need this fireproof cauldron for your own good,” Lin Jing said earnestly. “Too much firepower can be a bad thing.

“I’ve been so focused on unlocking your potential that I overlooked whether you could handle the power we’ve awakened.

“From now on, you’ll live in this cauldron. I’ll teach you pill refining to help you master your flames. The goal is for you to refine pills without my help!”

“You’re a grown-up Fire Carp now—it’s time to learn how to control your own flames.”

“You remember how I refined the Replenishing Pills before, right? I’ve prepared 1,000 sets of materials with the Pine Leaf Mouse’s help. Start by refining 1,000 pills for me.”

The Dragon Carp froze. Although the Shen Pine had tried to train it through sparring, Lin Jing’s approach—using pill refinement—seemed more humane, yet somehow even more devious.

“Blub blub!!” The carp protested, asking why Lin Jing wasn’t refining pills himself after studying pill manuals for so long.

“The situation has changed. I won’t have time to delve into pill refining for the next few months. After the academy assessment, I’ll revisit it. For now, I’m focusing on mechanized arts.”

Lin Jing revealed two items: a palm-sized Mechanical Bamboo Turtle and the Ingenious Soul Core, a prize from Elder Yu during the Ascending Dragon Festival.

He planned to spend the coming months upgrading the turtle.

During combat, he couldn’t rely on the Dragon Carp—it wasn’t bound to him through a Bloodline Contract and was technically an independent entity. Using it in a duel would count as fighting two-on-one, which was generally prohibited in formal competitions unless explicitly allowed.

The Pine Leaf Mouse, however, was a different story. Having a Bloodline Contract with Lin Jing, it was treated as an extension of him. Beast Taming Sect cultivators were unique in that their contracted beasts were recognized as part of their individual strength.

The Mechanical Turtle was another option. Mechanized beasts were considered tools rather than living beings, so there were no restrictions on their use.

To ensure he performed well as a proctor, Lin Jing decided to enhance the turtle. With the Ingenious Soul Core, the turtle could be directly controlled by his spiritual consciousness, making it more powerful and agile.

Although Lin Jing’s cultivation was low, his spiritual strength was unusually high due to being tempered by the Demon-Suppressing Pagoda and his experiences across two lifetimes.

With further research into mechanized arts at the academy, he was confident he could upgrade the turtle to display Foundation Establishment-level combat power, even without the Pine Leaf Mouse.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 14: Handing Out Rifles
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 14: Handing Out Rifles

“Have you heard of Wang Xiao of the Wang family? He possesses the Heavenly God Physique, ranked 93rd on the Physique Rankings! The Heavenly God Physique allows one to command the forces of the sun, moon, and stars, summoning storms and lightning as if a celestial being descended to earth—unstoppable!”

“Who in the Ancient Domain hasn’t heard of this Wang family prodigy? It’s almost certain the Wang family will produce another Spirit Transformation expert in a few hundred years.”

“I heard Wang Xiao arrived in Qianlong City yesterday, traveling alone. He was seen roaming the mountains, roasting all kinds of Foundation Establishment demonic beasts!”

Four months later, Qianlong City was bustling with activity in every teahouse and restaurant.

Time flew by, and Lin Jing had now spent four months in Qianlong City. During this time, he focused on training while relying on the auctions of his Mechanized Bamboo Rifles to maintain and spread his reputation.

Fame, however, required time to settle. If he kept stirring things up too often, people might grow desensitized. A name heard too frequently risks becoming mundane.

Initially, his name only circulated within Qianlong City. But as the Lotus Pavilion branches in other cities auctioned the Mechanized Bamboo Rifles and promoted them, Lin Jing began gaining recognition in other parts of the Ancient Domain.

Meanwhile, Lin Jing made steady progress with his Hidden Dragon Arts, advancing faster than most. His relatively low cultivation level meant that even while delving into mechanized arts and pill refinement, his consumption of Hidden Dragon Fortune remained modest.

Four months of secluded cultivation created a delicate balance in the accumulation and expenditure of his Hidden Dragon Fortune. However, Lin Jing noticed his progress with the Hidden Dragon Arts had slowed recently.

Qianlong City, a gathering place for prodigies and major events, brimmed with fresh topics daily. As the academy’s admissions neared, the names of talented individuals from various domains, sects, and clans spread across the city, overshadowing other news.

At the same time, the academy opened its grounds to applicants and their accompanying elders. Admission wasn’t open to just any cultivator—typically, only young disciples personally recommended by Nascent Soul-level cultivators were eligible.

Thus, the applicants were nearly all heirs of influential factions or true disciples of major sects.

“Time to replenish some Hidden Dragon Fortune,” Lin Jing muttered, looking at the fading blue Hidden Dragon Mark on his hand.

The geniuses flocking to the academy would be the stepping stones for his next rise in fame. He had been preparing for this opportunity for months.

Just as he planned his next move, a message rang out through a spatial transmission: “Come to the Tranquil Spirit Court.”

The Tranquil Spirit Court, a serene courtyard within the academy, was surrounded by wisps of immortal mist.

Under the golden morning sunlight, a rough bamboo pavilion stood at the end of the stone path. Inside, an ancient tea set sat on a stone table, with its fragrance wafting through the air.

Lin Jing arrived to find himself among familiar faces: Xu Zhizhi, the silent Young Hero Yuan Jing, and the alchemist Ziling’er. They were all seated around the table.

Upon Lin Jing’s arrival, Xu Zhizhi greeted him with a smile, but Yuan Jing remained expressionless, and Ziling’er pouted, still harboring grievances.

When all four were present, Cao Ziwei stepped forward with a grin. “Tomorrow is the academy’s assessment. You four will serve as proctors, with the first test being a combat evaluation.

“There are over a hundred candidates this year, all at the Qi Refinement stage. Yuan Jing, Ziling’er, you’re both early Foundation Establishment. Xu Zhizhi and Lin Jing... One of you is at Qi Refinement Stage 9, and... Lin Jing, you’re still at Stage 7?”

Cao Ziwei blinked in disbelief. “You’ve spent four months in a premium cave dwelling, and you haven’t even advanced a single stage?”

The others glanced at Lin Jing, who coughed awkwardly. “Soon! I have a feeling I’ll reach Stage 8 within a month. These past months, I’ve admittedly neglected my cultivation. And, as you know, Beast Taming Sect disciples tend to progress slower.”

The truth was that Lin Jing’s Pine Leaf Mouse, already at Stage 9, could no longer provide much feedback to boost his cultivation. Moreover, advancing during the later stages of Qi Refinement was significantly harder.

Cao Ziwei sighed. “No matter. Your pet at Stage 9 is good enough. The academy will provide you all with the best pills for recovery and healing during the tests.”

“The candidates will choose one of you to duel. I’ll grade the results, so be prepared.”

Turning to Yuan Jing and Ziling’er, Cao Ziwei added, “As Foundation Establishment cultivators, don’t let yourselves be bested by lower-stage candidates. The academy won’t mind, but it would hinder your progress in the Hidden Dragon Arts.”

“Yes!” Yuan Jing and Ziling’er replied in unison.

“Good. Be at Flying Dragon Mountain at sunrise tomorrow. I’ll oversee the first assessment.” With that, Cao Ziwei disappeared, leaving the four proctors behind.

The pavilion fell silent.

Just as Yuan Jing was about to leave, Lin Jing spoke up. “Tomorrow’s the big day. I wish you all the best and have prepared some gifts. They might not be necessary for you, but an extra trump card never hurts.”

He then placed three Mechanized Bamboo Rifles on the table.

“Haha, I’ve wanted to try one of these for ages!” Xu Zhizhi exclaimed, picking up a rifle and inspecting it. “The auction prices were too steep, and I didn’t want to ask you directly.

“Against regular candidates, a few shots should suffice to gauge their level! This handles much better than an ink brush—it’s refreshing!”

“You’re not giving gifts—you’re just trying to promote your inventions!” Ziling’er scoffed, though she still picked up a rifle. “It’s not like I really want it, but since you insist, I’ll take it.”

Grinning, she added, “Hey, Lin Jing, can I load it with poison pills or illusion powder and fire them alongside the beans for an explosive effect?”

Xu Zhizhi blinked. “Wait, you can do that?”

“You should test it,” Lin Jing replied, amused.

“I don’t need this. A sword is enough for me.” Yuan Jing hesitated briefly before grabbing a rifle. “Though, for most Qi Refinement cultivators, it would be overkill for me to draw my sword.”

Having been chased by the Pine Leaf Mouse wielding a rifle, Yuan Jing was curious to try it himself.

Lin Jing had anticipated that few could resist the allure of a mechanized weapon.

Watching everyone claim a rifle, Xu Zhizhi chuckled. “Brilliant! Using spells to defeat challengers might be effective, but it’s dull and unlikely to generate much discussion.

“If we all use Mechanized Bamboo Rifles to beat weaker candidates, then switch to spells for stronger ones, it’ll create quite the buzz. Lin Jing, you’re a genius!”

Unbeknownst to them, Cao Ziwei, still nearby, listened from behind a tree and fell deep into thought.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 15: Chaos in the Assessment
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 15: Chaos in the Assessment

Tianyuan New Era, Year 12,345, Second Month, Second Day

Under a clear blue sky, numerous young cultivators ascended Flying Dragon Mountain. They were the candidates for this year’s academy assessment.

Most climbed alone, while a few were accompanied by elders from their families or sects, eager to witness the spectacle.

At the mountain summit, a massive arena had been constructed. Standing at the center was Cao Ziwei, the presiding examiner and academy teacher, flanked by four academy students:

“That’s Cao Ziwei, the academy’s teacher. They say he’s at Nascent Soul perfection and a master of Qi Observation techniques!” an elder whispered to a younger companion.

“Once, during a family gathering, one of our juniors was hosting a talent selection. Cao Ziwei was invited to judge the candidates. With a single glance, he selected an ordinary physique over a special one, claiming the former had ‘wife-prospering fortune.’ The junior followed his advice, and the two innate ordinary physiques ended up producing a child with the Innate Thunder Spirit Physique—the strongest innate physique!”

“After that, many noble families began paying a fortune to consult Cao Ziwei when choosing spouses for their daughters. If you’re lucky enough to catch his eye and he says you bring wife-prospering fortune, you could join any prestigious family without issue!”

“Are those four the academy students serving as proctors this time? Two at early Foundation Establishment, one at Qi Refinement Stage 9, and one at Stage 7?”

Among the candidates, some scrutinized Lin Jing and the others carefully, weighing their options.

“Challenging the Foundation Establishment proctors will be tough to win, but even in defeat, a strong performance could score higher than beating that Qi Refinement Stage 7 proctor,” one young cultivator reasoned aloud.

Hearing this, his elder smacked him and whispered sharply, “What have I always taught you? Information is key! That Qi Refinement Stage 7 proctor is from the Beast Taming Sect. His strength lies in his contracted beasts. Just months ago, he defeated a Foundation Establishment cultivator with his current level of cultivation. His power is extraordinary!”

“Moreover, those other three? They were all defeated by him half a year ago—though back then, they were still in Qi Refinement.”

“Still, given the fame of that Beast Tamer in Qianlong City, beating him could earn you higher marks. Remember, the academy values reputation and fortune!”

“Wha—?” The young cultivator was stunned. “This is so deceptive! The academy’s assessment is too treacherous!”

“Exactly. Challenge the Beast Tamer. Low cultivation, big reputation—who else could better showcase your talents and earn you top marks?”

While most candidates analyzed their options, a few radiated absolute confidence. Among them stood Wang Xiao of the Wang family, possessing the Heavenly God Physique. His silver armor gleamed, and his aura was so imposing that other candidates instinctively avoided him.

With arms crossed, Wang Xiao fixed his gaze on Cao Ziwei, waiting for the announcement. He didn’t spare Lin Jing and the others a single glance.

“It’s time!”

Hovering in midair, Cao Ziwei addressed the crowd. “I am Cao Ziwei, the chief examiner for this year’s academy assessment. Welcome to all candidates!

“Today’s first test will evaluate your personal strength, the foundation of all cultivation.

“Each candidate may choose one of the seven academy proctors behind me to challenge. Scores will be based on your performance.”

Cao Ziwei paused before adding, “Let us proceed without delay. Who will challenge first?”

A sturdy young man stepped forward, leaping onto the arena. “I am Cao Qi, from Thunder Sound Valley in the Dao Domain.”

Thunder Sound Valley was a minor sect known not for thunder techniques, but for body refinement through lightning tempering. Sparks of lightning danced across Cao Qi’s skin as he moved.

“I choose him,” Cao Qi said, pointing to the slender Xu Zhizhi. “I’ve heard much of your reputation!”

Xu Zhizhi ascended the platform gracefully. “You are brave. I assume you know of my exploits in the Cavern Heaven, where I slew a fearsome Snow Plains body cultivator with an innate Myriad Beast Physique.

“Are you challenging me to prove yourself superior to them?”

As Xu Zhizhi shamelessly boasted, Ziling’er and Yuan Jing twitched in exasperation. They both knew the truth: Lin Jing had done all the work, while Xu Zhizhi merely landed the finishing blow.

“Yes!” Cao Qi grinned, eager to prove himself.

“Begin!” Cao Ziwei declared.

Cao Qi’s body crackled with lightning as he vanished, charging at Xu Zhizhi with terrifying speed.

But to everyone’s surprise, Xu Zhizhi had already drawn a Mechanized Bamboo Rifle. His keen eyes locked onto Cao Qi’s movements, and he pulled the trigger.

The rifle unleashed a barrage of spirit bean bullets, covering Cao Qi’s trajectory and the surrounding area.

Amid the thunderous noise and a burst of spirit bean bombs, the dust cleared to reveal Cao Qi lying unconscious on the ground. He barely understood what had happened.

“You’re fast,” Xu Zhizhi remarked, “but I’ve had exceptional vision since childhood. Speed is meaningless to me.

“Your body isn’t as strong as that Snow Plains cultivator’s. To avoid injuring you too severely, I defeated you with this rifle instead.” With a flick of his sleeve, Xu Zhizhi returned to his seat, reloading his rifle.

Cao Ziwei glanced at him in silence before calling, “Next candidate!”

Cao Qi was carried away. Besides his speed, his performance had been unremarkable.

The remaining candidates stared at Xu Zhizhi’s rifle in disbelief.

“I challenge Xu Zhizhi!”

A young man in black robes embroidered with golden tiger patterns stepped forward.

“Gao Sheng of the Demon-Slaying Gao family,” he introduced himself, causing a stir among the crowd.

Xu Zhizhi smiled, ready for another duel, but froze when he saw Gao Sheng pull out a rifle identical to his.

“Haha! I have one too!” Gao Sheng exclaimed, to the shock of everyone present.

Xu Zhizhi’s vision darkened. This is bad.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 16: Firepower is King!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 16: Firepower is King!

“What’s going on?!”

On the arena stage, Gao Sheng and Xu Zhizhi sprinted wildly along the edge of the platform, frantically pulling their triggers. Explosions reverberated continuously across the stage. Although it was supposed to be a duel between two Qi Refinement cultivators, the intensity felt more like a “Sect War.”

The candidates below the stage were utterly dumbfounded.

Cao Ziwei took a deep breath, feeling a headache coming on. He had no choice but to force himself to find highlights in Gao Sheng’s performance amid the gunfire to give him a score.

“That’s a Mechanical Bamboo Rifle! It’s been selling like crazy at the Lotus Auction House for the past few months. It’s crafted using special materials by that Qi Refinement Stage 7 Beast Taming Cultivator over there,” someone explained, drawing a sharp breath.

No wonder it’s been such a hot item—it really packs a terrifying punch!

“You…” Xu Zhizhi cursed furiously on the stage.

Gao Sheng, though seemingly unreliable, had an undeniably prestigious background. With multiple top-tier spells at his disposal, the shockwaves of the explosions barely affected him as golden protective light shimmered around his body, shielding him from harm.

Before long, Xu Zhizhi was forced to admit defeat due to insufficient ammunition.

“Brother Lin, you’ve ruined me! Why did you sell so many guns?!” Xu Zhizhi cried out in frustration.

At the back, Lin Jing wore an innocent expression. Gao Sheng… was stronger than he had imagined. Even without the Mechanical Bamboo Rifle, Xu Zhizhi would likely have lost anyway. Gao Sheng completely crushed him in spell strength and artifact quantity.

“Well fought.” Gao Sheng put away the rifle and laughed heartily.

“Next!” Cao Ziwei said quickly.

The next contestant was a woman in a fiery red dress, her figure both striking and alluring.

“Fire Domain, Burning Heaven Valley, Qian Zhuo. I want to challenge this examiner.”

The woman in red clearly knew Ziling’er quite well. She was well aware that disciples of the Dan Ding Sect weren’t skilled in combat. Their only strength lay in fire control. However, as someone with the Innate Fire Spirit Body, she was highly resistant to flames. Even though this was a cross-tier challenge, she was confident she could perform exceptionally and secure a high score.

Hearing this, Ziling’er’s face remained calm as she walked up. Yet, as the duel began under Cao Ziwei’s announcement, Ziling’er did something that left the crowd stunned—

She, too, pulled out a Mechanical Bamboo Rifle.

“What?! Another one of these?!” The woman in red was taken aback.

Ziling’er had already started firing, unleashing torrents of water into the air. The woman in red reacted quickly, retreating while conjuring flames from her lips, forming a fire wall to block the attack.

Boom! Boom! Boom! Boom!

The fire wall swelled under the impact of the explosions. It threatened to break several times, but the explosive Spirit Beans failed to breach it entirely. Clearly, the woman in red had more skill than the previous combatants.

“This kind of trick won’t work on me. I’m not like that body cultivator from earlier,” the woman in red smirked confidently as she successfully resisted. “Sister, why don’t you use the Dan Ding Sect’s fire-control secret arts instead? Show me your true skills—”

Before she could finish speaking, her face turned purple. She collapsed to one knee and then fell over with a thud.

Ziling’er gave her a calm look. “I mixed Poison Gas Pills into the ammunition. Don’t forget, I’m an alchemist.”

The Poison Gas Pills had been dispersed into powder by the exploding Spirit Bean Bombs, blending into the water mist. When triggered by the opponent’s high-temperature fire technique, it took immediate effect.

Ziling’er retrieved her rifle, using only minimal ammunition and poison to defeat her opponent.

“What in the world is going on?!”

The candidates were completely bewildered. It was surprising enough that the new examiner also used a rifle. But to think she even modified it!

All eyes turned to the creator of the Mechanical Bamboo Rifle, the Beast Taming Sect’s Pei Fang.

Was this even a fair exam anymore? Was every fight destined to be a gunfight now? Did you even qualify to step onto the stage without a rifle?

At that moment, another contestant stepped onto the platform. He was tall and exuded arrogance.

“If you can’t even handle such simple mechanical creations, you might as well go home and cultivate.”

“I am Jiang Chuanhe from Dao Domain’s Ten Thousand Arts City. I want to challenge the creator of the Mechanical Bamboo Rifle, Lin Jing of the Beast Taming Sect—more accurately, I want to challenge his Pine Leaf Mouse, the one that defeated a Foundation Establishment cultivator while still at Qi Refinement!”

It was clear that this contestant had come with ill intentions, aiming to use Lin Jing as a stepping stone to establish his reputation in Hidden Dragon City.

Lin Jing walked out calmly, unbothered by Jiang Chuanhe’s dismissal of the rifle.

With a gentle smile, Lin Jing said, “Please.”

“But if you want to challenge my Pine Leaf Mouse, you’ll first have to deal with this.”

As he spoke, Lin Jing summoned a Mechanical Bamboo Rifle, holding it in one hand.

Jiang Chuanhe raised an eyebrow.

Before he could say anything, Lin Jing pulled out a second rifle, dual-wielding them.

“Do you think having two rifles—” Jiang Chuanhe smirked. While the firepower of two rifles was indeed intimidating, he was far superior to those weaklings from before. He hadn’t even finished speaking when his expression drastically changed.

Lin Jing summoned a towering, two-meter-tall Mechanical Giant Turtle, standing on two legs!

The giant turtle’s entire body was constructed from intricate mechanical components and special Spirit Bamboo, its dark blue form exuding a chilling presence. Most strikingly, it had mounted turret-like cannons on each shoulder and was wielding Mechanical Bamboo Rifles in both hands.

This was Lin Jing’s newly upgraded Mechanical Bamboo Turtle, enhanced over the past few months!

With the two rifles in Lin Jing’s hands and the turtle’s additional weaponry, there were now four rifles and two cannons in play.

“Wait, this turtle, you… you—” Jiang Chuanhe’s face turned pale.

Lin Jing’s eyes glimmered as he connected with the turtle’s Ingenious Soul Core, using his mind to command the mechanical beast.

“Roar!!!” The turtle let out a ferocious roar, almost as if it were alive. Its complex Spirit Gathering Formations activated under Lin Jing’s control, achieving peak efficiency. Streams of terrifying water pressure gathered at the turtle’s gun barrels and cannons, their spiritual radiance dazzling as they launched devastating attacks toward Jiang Chuanhe from multiple directions!

The strikes were nearly instantaneous!

At the same time, Lin Jing grinned and pressed the triggers of his own reinforced rifles, enhanced with Spirit Gathering Formations.

“Profound Annihilation Finger!”

Jiang Chuanhe’s expression changed dramatically. Veins bulged on his arms as he unleashed powerful spirals of True Qi, forming an ethereal giant finger. The energy it emitted seemed far more sophisticated and destructive than that of a mere rifle.

But…

Even without using Spirit Bean Bombs, the high-pressure water cannons controlled by Lin Jing’s turtle managed to counter the Profound Annihilation Finger, the two forces colliding fiercely in mid-air!

When the Spirit Bean Bombs finally exploded, a devastating spiritual energy storm swept through, blasting Jiang Chuanhe off the stage and leaving his vision spinning.

The candidates watching were stunned. Even the prominent prodigies, like the Goddess of the Divine Talisman Sect and the Heavenly God Physique of the Wang Clan, frowned slightly.

“What… what is this?!”

Even Xu Zhizhi, Ziling’er, and Yuan Jing stood agape. Wasn’t this the tiny turtle from the Cavern Heaven? How had it turned into this monstrous war machine?!

“There’s no opponent a single Mechanical Bamboo Rifle can’t handle. If there is, use two. Or four…” Lin Jing said with a smile. “Cultivation is a path where firepower reigns supreme. My friend, don’t underestimate anything.”

Special thanks to Chen Dong for the reward! You’re the best! Orz!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 17: Upholding Justice
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 17: Upholding Justice

Lin Jing had shown everyone the "correct" way to purchase Mechanical Bamboo Rifles—one was merely the starting point; buying ninety or a hundred was the true endgame!

“I told you, we should catch a mountain full of monkeys and equip each monkey monster with two rifles. That would be unstoppable!” Gao Sheng said, sticking to his wild idea.

“What’s wrong with these examiners?!” Some of the candidates were starting to panic. What was supposed to be a test of their mastery of spells and techniques had been turned into a blatant contest of resources!

Each examiner wielded at least one Mechanical Bamboo Rifle, and Lin Jing himself carried five or six. Even the only successful candidate so far had relied on a rifle to win. Were rifles now mandatory to even participate in the exam?

“What are you doing?!”

One elder noticed their brilliant disciple donning layers of equipment—a protective treasure robe underneath, followed by beast armor, and even a combat suit with a shield in hand.

“Uncle, the examiners’ firepower is just too overwhelming! If I’m not prepared, I won’t even last a round on stage!” The candidate looked distressed.

“They’re examiners; you’re a candidate! The academy isn’t testing your wealth; it’s testing your mastery of spells and techniques. The weapons you’re using aren’t even ones you forged yourself, and your application of techniques is crude at best. Doing this will only cost you points! Just stick to the spells taught by the sect and figure out a way!”

“His grandfather is a Nascent Soul Realm Venerable! What are we supposed to do?”

The prodigy, who had cultivated for years and only just left the sect, suddenly realized how ruthless the world of cultivation could be. Doubts about life began to surface in their heart.

At this point, ordinary candidates didn’t even know which examiner to choose anymore. While it was true that the sword cultivator examiner was unlikely to use a rifle, his Foundation Establishment cultivation level was far more intimidating than any Mechanical Bamboo Rifle.

Even Xu Zhizhi, who had lost his match earlier, was not truly weak. Everyone had seen it—he had simply lost to an opponent with far greater firepower and superior artifacts.

“Anyone else?”

“You, come challenge me.”

Suddenly, two voices rang out at the same time.

From the crowd, a man stepped forward. He was clad in a flowing yellow robe embroidered with intricate talisman patterns, faintly glowing with spiritual light.

Hearing the second voice, he turned toward a small, youthful figure in the crowd—Lin Jing of the Beast Taming Sect, who also bore the Heavenly God Physique.

The two had indeed spoken at the same time.

“Let him go first,” Lin Jing said, nodding toward Chen Lingxi, the Holy Maiden of the Divine Talisman Sect, allowing her to take the challenge.

“Thank you.” Chen Lingxi gave a slight smile, then turned her gaze to another examiner.

“That examiner—I want to challenge him.”

Her golden hair flowed like a waterfall, radiating a faint divine light. There was an air of transcendence about her, both mysterious and extraordinary.

“Please,” the examiner, Wang Xiao, accepted the challenge graciously.

“Before we start, may I confirm something first? Sir, are you a Beast Taming Cultivator? Do you still intend to rely on those one-time-use resource items, like Spirit Bean Bombs, for the assessment?” Chen Lingxi asked, pointing out the disposable nature of Lin Jing’s tactics.

“Miss, mechanized beasts are still beasts. These so-called ‘one-time resources’ are integral parts of mechanized beasts, created as tools for combat. Naturally, they are valid for use in battle,” Wang Xiao replied with a faint smile.

“In that case, I suppose I won’t hold back. Since my mortal years, I’ve focused on drawing and crafting talismans. All the talismans I created during my practice sessions have been stored in my bag, and their number has now exceeded 100,000. Surely, using them for this assessment is perfectly valid,” she replied with another smile.

“Chen Lingxi of the Divine Talisman Sect, at your service.”

Her words caused an uproar among the crowd.

“100,000 talismans?!”

“You’ve got to be kidding me!”

Several cultivators couldn’t help but laugh at the absurdity of the situation, almost forgetting that the Holy Maiden of the Divine Talisman Sect was here!

When it came to stockpiling disposable tools, talisman cultivators were undisputed champions in the cultivation world. Almost every talisman cultivator carried a large number of talismans with them for emergencies. Unlike others who had to purchase them at high prices, talisman cultivators could craft their own.

While Lin Jing’s Spirit Bean Bombs were indeed inexpensive, everyone present was confident that Chen Lingxi, with her innate Talisman Physique and the backing of the Divine Talisman Sect, could craft talismans at an even lower cost.

The upcoming duel... was bound to become an extraordinary war of attrition.

“Hahaha, now this is getting interesting!” A cultivator couldn’t help but laugh.

“This is going to be justice served. Let’s see her use Lin Jing’s own tactics against him and teach these ‘firepower-heavy’ examiners a lesson!”

“Right! Let these examiners know that the candidates aren’t pushovers either!”

“Oh no, Lin might have met his match!” Gao Sheng said with an amused expression, ready to enjoy the show.

Lin Jing was momentarily stunned, but he respectfully replied, “Please, go ahead.”

Cao Ziwei, watching with a playful expression, also felt that Lin Jing was in trouble. As he announced the start of the match, an astonishing scene unfolded.

One talisman… two talismans… three talismans… Hundreds of talismans floated into the air behind Chen Lingxi, densely covering the sky.

The ordinary candidates had never seen such a display. Typically, they used talismans one at a time or at most a few together. But Chen Lingxi, with her Talisman Physique, controlled over a thousand talismans at once, instantly demonstrating the terrifying potential of her physique.

“This physique isn’t even ranked among the top hundred divine physiques. How strong would those be?” Many candidates dared not even imagine.

“If the stockpiled talismans are sufficient, couldn’t she rival even divine physiques?”

Even Yuan Jing and Ziling’er, both in the Foundation Establishment stage, wore serious expressions as they witnessed this scene.

Wang Xiao, the Heavenly God Physique, took a deep breath, realizing just how troublesome Chen Lingxi could be.

“This woman is no joke. That examiner is doomed.”

Even Wang Xiao felt a headache at the thought of the 100,000 talismans stored in Chen Lingxi’s bag.

“Lightning Talisman!”

Chen Lingxi’s lips curled into a smile. Though it might seem wasteful, she knew that in Hidden Dragon City, one had to make an impression. If she wanted her name to spread, she had to stand out.

Lin Jing’s reputation as an unconventional examiner had already made waves. To surpass him, she had to be even more outrageous!

Lightning filled the air as countless talismans activated. Thunderclouds gathered, unleashing a deluge of lightning bolts toward Lin Jing. The entire sky above the arena turned into a sea of golden lightning, enveloping everything.

In terms of “firepower,” it appeared even more terrifying than the Mechanical Bamboo Rifles.

Lin Jing had just declared that firepower was king, but now his opponent's firepower was clearly stronger.

“Indeed, never underestimate anyone,” Lin Jing muttered.

Instead of meeting the attack head-on, he chose a different approach. Knowing that the Mechanical Bamboo Turtle alone wouldn’t be enough to defeat Chen Lingxi, he summoned a small Pine Leaf Mouse, which was holding a piece of wood in its paws.

In the next moment, the mouse used the wood to rapidly construct an enormous wooden fortress, encasing Lin Jing and the Mechanical Bamboo Turtle within. The sky-splitting lightning fell upon the fortress but left no trace.

Then, holes appeared across the fortress walls, and from each hole, gun barrels emerged.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 18: Shock! The Truth Is Actually...
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 18: Shock! The Truth Is Actually...

“Huh?”

Chen Lingxi simultaneously activated countless Lightning Talisman attacks, unleashing a thunderous might comparable to a Foundation Establishment lightning cultivator. The crowd of examinees gasped in astonishment. Everyone had assumed Lin Jing was doomed, but unexpectedly, the beast Lin Jing summoned managed to construct a defensive fortress in the blink of an eye.

If it were just that, people might have simply thought that Lin Jing's beast had mastered powerful wood techniques, capable of quickly creating defensive formations. After all, there were precedents of the Pine Leaf Mouse overcoming Foundation Establishment wood cultivators despite its lower level. However, to block such an overwhelming lightning onslaught completely, without even a scratch? That defense was too terrifying!

“Impossible!”

Even Wang Xiao, a cultivator with the Heavenly God Physique, looked perplexed. The power of these lightning strikes—amplified by countless Lightning Talismans—already surpassed the lightning he could summon with his physique. Yet these wooden structures had blocked it with ease?

“It’s a seal!” Some higher-level cultivators among the examinees furrowed their brows, noticing something others missed.

Inside the fortress, divine patterns shimmered faintly. It wasn’t merely the wooden fortress resisting the lightning; the structures were engraved with seals that significantly enhanced their defensive power!

“This squirrel actually sealed itself and its beast tamer inside this strange structure!”

Who says seals are only for trapping enemies?

The Sacred Wood Seal, a powerful sealing technique previously used by Elder Magic Pine to seal the Ice Soul Gu, had now proven its might. The storm of Lightning Talismans left no mark on the fortress. Grinning, Lin Jing ordered the Pine Leaf Mouse to open a few small openings in the structure. From inside, they extended bamboo rifles and began firing wildly.

“This is what modern cultivation duels should look like!”

Boom! Boom! Boom!
