Chen Lingxi, her chest tightening from the constant barrage, elegantly flicked her slender fingers to draw an intricate talisman filled with peculiar energy.

“Divine Light Talisman!”

A radiant silver talisman hovered in midair, transforming into a wall of divine light that blocked Lin Jing and his allies’ attacks.

“Damn it!” Chen Lingxi cursed, frustrated that Lin Jing had managed to defend against her onslaught. She promptly summoned hundreds of Fire Talismans and activated them all at once. A fiery sea descended, yet the Sacred Wood Fortress remained unscathed.

As the fortress’ openings closed and reopened, Lin Jing unleashed another barrage of mechanized techniques, stunning the spectators.

“Cultivator duels can be fought like this?”

Before long, the duel devolved into a full-scale firefight. It was reminiscent of the clash between Gao Sheng and Xu Zhizhi, but this time the intensity was on a whole new level. Many examinees gazing at the spectacle on the stage doubted whether even an ordinary Foundation Establishment cultivator could survive the crossfire.

“Ha!” The Pine Leaf Mouse, Lin Jing, and the Mechanical Bamboo Turtle all held dual guns, firing relentlessly from within the fortress.

The Pine Leaf Mouse glanced at the female cultivator across the field, clearly impressed by her large stockpile of talismans.

“Ha-ha!!” It jeered while shooting, even mockingly suggesting Lin Jing take notes from her about amassing talismans.

Lin Jing shot back, “You already have alchemy to learn, so when do you have time to study talisman crafting? If she annoys you, think about capturing her and making her your personal servant!”

As another wave of attacks came their way, the Pine Leaf Mouse quickly closed a nearby opening. Lin Jing leaned against the wall, taking a deep breath.

“That guy may not be weak, but I prepared well and have the upper hand. Today, they’ll lose to someone of the same level.”

“This approach works, but her defensive talismans are enough to withstand our attacks. If you’re worried about wasting talisman paper, I’m worried about wasting Spirit Beans.”

The Pine Leaf Mouse nodded, clearly distressed. “This isn’t just a duel; it’s a Spirit Stone-burning contest!”

“Use Flying Leaf Strike to knock her storage bag open and scatter her talismans everywhere!” Lin Jing commanded.

“I’ve noticed that while she can control many talismans at once, they’re all of the same type. This means her current ability allows her to manage a large number of identical talismans, but it’s hard for her to activate a wide variety simultaneously.”

“If we disrupt her stockpile, she won’t be able to cast effectively!”

Just as the Pine Leaf Mouse was about to act...

“Forget it!” Lin Jing decided it was too early to reveal the Space Flying Leaf technique. After a moment’s thought, he decided to mimic Ziling’er’s approach and loaded his magazine with a large number of Bewitching Pills, gambling on their effectiveness.

Soon, amidst the firefight, a strange, mind-disturbing fragrance filled the air on the stage.

Chen Lingxi was already expending significant energy controlling a large number of talismans while also having to draw new ones to defend against Spirit Bean Bombs. She paid no attention to guarding herself against the intoxicating mist wafting in the air.

“When is this going to end?”

“This is insane! Even sect battles aren’t this intense!”

The other examinees were in awe. They were all Qi Refining cultivators, yet how could these two be so terrifying?

Eventually, the effects of the Bewitching Mist began to take hold. Chen Lingxi’s vision blurred momentarily, and in that critical moment, she misdrew a defensive talisman, leaving a gap in her defenses.

“Poison!” She realized immediately, but it was too late.

“Apologies,” Lin Jing thought to himself, “but in addition to being a Mechanized Beast Tamer, I’m also an alchemist!”

Boom!

The shockwave from the Spirit Bean Bombs knocked Chen Lingxi off the stage.

The thrilling battle of offense and defense finally came to a close in front of the stunned audience.

“The Divine Talisman Sect’s saintess actually lost…”

As the dust settled, the fortified war bunker on the stage disappeared. Lin Jing, the Mechanical Bamboo Turtle, and the Pine Leaf Mouse standing proudly on the turtle’s head looked regretfully toward Chen Lingxi, who had been blasted off the stage.

“You’re strong,” Lin Jing remarked. “But unfortunately, your combat strategy is neither reproducible nor complete.”

In other words, it wasn’t as effective as mechanized bamboo rifles combined with a defensive structure.

With proper defensive architecture, the setup could even serve as a sect-protecting formation for small villages, capable of resisting beast hordes!

Below the stage, Chen Lingxi, still dazed and frustrated, couldn’t help but cough up blood. Never in her life had she been so humiliated in a duel.

Lin Jing. She’d remember this name.

At that moment, not only the other examinees but also their elders and the chief examiner, Cao Ziwei, sighed audibly. This was the first time anyone had seen a cultivator fight with such an unconventional style.

Most astonishing of all… this guy was a Beast Tamer!?

Lin Jing, smiling after his victory, turned his gaze toward another examinee. “You were planning to take the test too, weren’t you? You can come up now,” he said, addressing Wang Xiao, the Heavenly God Physique cultivator. Lin Jing had deliberately avoided using his spatial technique, saving it as a surprise for this opponent.

Wang Xiao snorted coldly and replied, “Step down. I won’t challenge you. Let the sword cultivator examiner take my test instead.”

Lin Jing: “???”

Wait, I prepared for you for four months! The Pine Leaf Mouse even trained relentlessly on spatial techniques! And now you’re telling me you won’t challenge me?

What was the point of our four months of hard work?

Understood.

Lin Jing wasn’t one to let himself be shortchanged. An idea instantly formed in his mind—a newsworthy story headline:

“Shocking! Beast Taming Sect’s Lin Jing Is So Strong That the Wang Family’s Heavenly Physique Prodigy Refuses to Challenge Him!”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 19: Dragon and Phoenix
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 19: Dragon and Phoenix

It wasn’t that Wang Xiao was afraid of Lin Jing.

Though Lin Jing had made quite a name for himself, Wang Xiao dismissed Beast Tamers as practitioners of minor, unorthodox paths. Beast Taming Sect? He’d never even heard of it.

By contrast, the Heavenly Sword Gate was on a completely different level. As the foremost sword sect in the Ancient Celestial Kingdom, its reputation had long been illustrious.

With his lineage from a prominent cultivation clan and his innate Heavenly God Physique, Wang Xiao didn’t see defeating a Beast Taming Sect Qi Refinement cultivator as particularly honorable—even if Lin Jing had displayed extraordinary ability.

To him, Yuan Jing from the Heavenly Sword Gate was the most worthy opponent.

“Finally, my turn?” Yuan Jing, brimming with excitement after watching several duels, was practically itching to take the stage.

“Go on, then,” he said confidently, not even waiting for Lin Jing to reply before flying onto the stage on his sword, motioning for Lin Jing to step down.

“...” Lin Jing fell silent, exhaling a long, melancholic sigh after a moment.

“Let’s go,” Lin Jing called out to the Pine Leaf Mouse, stepping down from the stage. The Pine Leaf Mouse hesitated for a moment before quickly climbing onto Lin Jing’s shoulder.

In no time, only Yuan Jing, standing aloft on his sword, and Wang Xiao, clad in silver armor, remained on the stage.

“Draw your sword!” Wang Xiao demanded.

Yuan Jing, a sword cultivator who had refined the Acquired Sword Physique, prepared for battle. Just as the crowd anticipated a classic, traditional duel, something entirely unexpected happened.

As the chief examiner, Cao Ziwei, announced the start of the match, Yuan Jing, to everyone’s bewilderment, pulled out a bamboo rifle instead of his sword. With a swish, he ascended into the sky, flying on his sword while aiming the bamboo rifle at Wang Xiao and pulling the trigger!

This scene left the audience completely dumbfounded.

“What kind of joke is this!?”

Wang Xiao, wielder of the Heavenly God Physique, was enraged. Seeing Yuan Jing, an honest-to-goodness sword cultivator, stoop to such “gimmicks” made his blood boil.

Was this supposed to be fun?!

“Wind, rise!” he roared.

In an instant, the skies churned as winds gathered, and divine light shimmered faintly around Wang Xiao’s body, as though he were channeling the power of heaven and earth. The arena was soon engulfed in fierce gusts of wind, with a silver hurricane spiraling forth as he raised his arm.

Boom! Boom! BOOM!

The winds picked up streams of water, igniting the Spirit Beans within.

As countless Spirit Beans exploded beyond the hurricane’s reach, the sky turned dark with looming clouds.

“Lightning, descend!”

At Wang Xiao’s command, the blackened sky unleashed streaks of white lightning, striking directly at Yuan Jing mid-flight.

Unfazed, Yuan Jing calmly encased himself in a protective aura of sword energy. As the lightning descended, it was instantly dispersed by the sword energy surrounding him.

“Starry Slash!”

While summoning wind and lightning, Wang Xiao soared upward, wielding a battle blade forged from meteorite iron. Infused with starlight, he rode the winds to cleave at Yuan Jing.

“Careful! I’m going to draw my sword now!” Yuan Jing declared once again.

Just as Wang Xiao was about to retort with, “Draw your sword, my foot,” the mechanized bamboo rifle in Yuan Jing’s hands fired a barrage of blue streams resembling flying swords!

It turned out Yuan Jing had modified his mechanized bamboo rifle.

The water streams emanated intense sword energy!

“Sword Intent? He’s actually infused Sword Intent into the water streams!”

The water, imbued with the power of Sword Intent and accompanied by explosive Spirit Beans, tore through the hurricane in an instant, forcing Wang Xiao to redirect his slash toward the incoming “sword marbles.”

“Wow!” Ziling’er exclaimed in surprise. “It can be used like that? Looks like the only idiot who couldn’t modify the bamboo rifle was that useless Xu Zhizhi!”

Beside her, Xu Zhizhi: “???”

“How was I supposed to know you all would pull so many tricks!” he fumed, feeling thoroughly humiliated.

Lin Jing, watching from the sidelines, also looked surprised. Yuan Jing’s strength had grown immeasurably after refining his Sword Physique during his Foundation Establishment stage. Compared to their previous encounter in Bodhi Heaven, Yuan Jing was now on an entirely different level. Lin Jing had to admit that even if he and the Pine Leaf Mouse teamed up again, they’d probably lose to him.

“Look! The Heavenly God Physique is at it again—he’s summoned some kind of heavenly crater!”

As the “sword marbles” clashed with Wang Xiao’s Starry Slash, creating a violent explosion, an unexpected phenomenon occurred.

With a deafening boom, the sky suddenly ripped open, revealing a massive crater emitting both red and blue light.

“What kind of divine power is this?”

At first, everyone assumed it was some new ability related to the Heavenly God Physique, but in the next moment, beams of radiant light descended from the crater, one of which struck Wang Xiao directly.

“Wait, what is that?!”

Lin Jing, watching the spectacle from below, felt his heart race as an unsettling realization hit him.

The light from the crater didn’t just envelop Wang Xiao—it also covered Chen Lingxi of the Divine Talisman Sect, who was in the process of consuming pills to recover her True Energy and heal her injuries. Suddenly, she too was bathed in a pillar of light.

Seeing this, most cultivators finally understood what was happening.

“Cavern Heaven!”

“It’s a Cavern Heaven descending!”

“Wang Xiao, the Heavenly God Physique of the Wang Clan, and Chen Lingxi of the Divine Talisman Sect have been chosen by a Cavern Heaven!”

This unexpected development left many cultivators stunned. Without hesitation, they began forcibly kicking their own geniuses toward the descending pillars of light!

“Get in there, now!”

Even Elder Li from the Beast Taming Sect was aware of the Cavern Heaven’s entry loopholes, and naturally, most of the cultivators present knew about it too.

Before a Cavern Heaven fully materialized, cultivators of the same realm as those originally chosen could sneak in through the incomplete formations and try their luck at obtaining the opportunities inside.

“Pine Leaf Mouse, let’s go!!!”

Lin Jing immediately recalled the Pine Leaf Mouse and leaped toward the Cavern Heaven. Since Wang Xiao and Chen Lingxi were both at the ninth stage of Qi Refinement, the Cavern Heaven they triggered theoretically allowed all Qi Refinement cultivators to enter!

Seeing this, Xu Zhizhi and Gao Sheng hesitated for a moment before swiftly following suit.

“Opportunities!”

In just a few moments, over a hundred Qi Refinement-level examinees on-site all took advantage of the Cavern Heaven’s incomplete formation and rushed inside, hitching a ride on Wang Xiao and Chen Lingxi’s fortuitous encounter.

What was supposed to be a standard examination turned into a mass exodus. The examinees disappeared. Two examiners were also gone.

The autumn wind swept through the now-empty stage. Yuan Jing, who was still hovering on his sword, stared blankly at the desolate arena, lost in thought.

“Why did I rush to Foundation Establishment? Why!” Ziling’er, too, stood dumbfounded, staring at the pillar of light with a face full of regret.

If she had waited just one more month to break through to Foundation Establishment, she would’ve been able to enter the Cavern Heaven again!

The fame brought by breaking into Foundation Establishment was nothing compared to the fortune and opportunities a single entry into a Cavern Heaven could provide!

High above, Cao Ziwei stroked his chin, clearly surprised. “The timing of this Cavern Heaven’s descent is rather... convenient. So many prodigies managed to sneak in at once!”

The academy specifically sought out geniuses who had entered Cavern Heavens before because such individuals were more likely to trigger a second Cavern Heaven. If the Cavern Heavens repeatedly appeared within Hidden Dragon Academy’s grounds, more prodigies of the same realm would have opportunities to enter and claim their own fortunes.

However, even within the academy, such a spectacular scene—so many prodigies entering a Cavern Heaven simultaneously—was a rarity.

“Ladies and gentlemen!”

Cao Ziwei turned to address the many sect elders gathered below the stage. “Judging by the color of the Cavern Heaven’s light, it should be the Dragon-Phoenix Cavern Heaven. This is an incredible opportunity for every examinee who entered. I, Cao Ziwei, will guard the Cavern Heaven’s entrance until it ends. Those of you who wish to wait here are welcome to do so. If not, you may return to the academy, and the examination will resume once all the examinees have returned.”

Dragon-Phoenix Cavern Heaven...

As Cao Ziwei finished speaking, the gathered cultivators couldn’t help but grow anxious. Just moments ago, they had hurriedly thrown their juniors into the Cavern Heaven, desperate to avoid missing out on the opportunity.

But now, upon hearing the type of Cavern Heaven it was, unease swept through them.

The Dragon-Phoenix Cavern Heaven was exceptionally unique. While everyone who entered was guaranteed a chance at fortune, it was also unimaginably perilous.

“Oh no! It’s the Dragon-Phoenix Cavern Heaven! Did I just send my child to their death with my own foot?!”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 20: Breaking Limits
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 20: Breaking Limits

“Mr. Cao, may I ask, is the Dragon-Phoenix Cavern Heaven the one where people don’t feel like eating for years after entering?” a bald monk asked, his face filled with horror.

Terrified, he exclaimed, “I saw the other cultivators throwing their juniors into the Cavern Heaven, so in a moment of panic, I kicked my son in too... But I didn’t realize it was that Cavern Heaven!”

Cao Ziwei nodded. “The Dragon-Phoenix Cavern Heaven is indeed one of the ‘Ten Great Cavern Heavens of the Immortal Palace,’ categorized under the thirty-six Lesser Cavern Heavens.”

“This particular Cavern Heaven doesn’t involve competition among those who enter. Instead, they are transported to different spaces, with no interference from others. Everyone who enters has the opportunity to gain fortuitous encounters. However, how much they gain—and whether they can return safely—depends entirely on their own abilities.”

“It’s said that in the Immortal Palace, one of the delicacies served at the immortals’ banquets is called Dragon Liver and Phoenix Marrow , a dish made from pure-blooded dragons and phoenixes. It is extraordinarily nourishing, delicious, and enhances cultivation without any side effects. This Cavern Heaven derives its name from that dish.”

“This, my friend,” Cao Ziwei continued, “is indeed the Cavern Heaven where one might not feel like eating mundane food for years after entering.”

“Within the Cavern Heaven, there are projections of dragon-blooded and phoenix-blooded demonic beasts, whose strength matches that of the cultivator who encounters them. Each one slain significantly enhances the cultivator’s power, as though they’ve tasted the celestial delicacy of Dragon Liver and Phoenix Marrow. The effect is so potent that mundane food becomes unappealing for a long time afterward.”

The bald monk clutched his head and cried out, “It’s over!”

“It’s not that my son isn’t strong!” the monk shouted in despair. “It’s that our sect’s rules have forced him to eat a vegetarian diet since he was a child. Now, if he even tastes a bit of meat, he gets food poisoning! The flavor of Dragon Liver and Phoenix Marrow might outright kill him! He knows nothing about the Dragon-Phoenix Cavern Heaven’s effects and will definitely fall for it!”

The crowd: “???”

This monk... You’ve taken a wife and had a son, yet you still follow your sect’s rules so rigidly that you don’t let your kid eat meat?

“Hunting dragon and phoenix projections grants power... All the entrants are at the ninth stage of Qi Refinement. If they succeed, won’t they quickly reach Foundation Establishment?” an elderly cultivator mused, stroking his beard. “And with the opportunities in this Cavern Heaven, their foundations will likely be far more solid than with conventional methods.”

Cao Ziwei shook his head. “Unfortunately, the Dragon-Phoenix Cavern Heaven’s feedback cannot directly help cultivators break through to the next major stage. However, according to historical records, those at the peak of Qi Refinement who manage to slay the dragon or phoenix projections can refine and purify their True Energy, potentially breaking through the current limits of the realm to reach the tenth stage of Qi Refinement!”

“Regardless of the outcome, the examinees who entered stand to gain tremendous benefits.”

“When True Energy is pure enough, compressing it to form True Essence and stepping into Foundation Establishment becomes significantly easier. Regardless of the chosen method for establishing their foundation, their success rate will increase dramatically.”

“And if they surpass the realm’s natural limits to achieve the tenth stage of Qi Refinement... Well, that is a feat of unimaginable glory. The Immortal Palace established many extreme boundary rules, including limiting Qi Refinement to nine stages. Those who break this limit to cultivate to a tenth stage are acknowledged as prodigies by the Immortal Palace itself. Throughout history, such cultivators have been exceedingly rare.”

Hearing this, many cultivators revealed looks of astonishment. This Cavern Heaven could actually help cultivators surpass the limits of their realm?

Many immediately regretted not being at the Qi Refinement stage themselves—otherwise, they would’ve jumped in with the others!

“What kind of Cavern Heaven is this?”

Meanwhile, Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse, riding the opportunity created by Wang Xiao, had already entered the Cavern Heaven.

“Whatever kind of Cavern Heaven it is, we owe our thanks to Wang Xiao. Sure, he picked a fight with me and gave me trouble, but moving forward, he’s no longer my bad brother—he’s my lucky brother. What do you think?” Lin Jing summoned the Pine Leaf Mouse.

“Squeak!” The Pine Leaf Mouse nodded indifferently. It had its own concerns—it still wanted to face off against the male talisman cultivator from earlier. After all, looting a few more of his talismans for its own storage bag was a tempting idea.

Suddenly, Lin Jing looked around. Thanks to his prior experience with the Bodhi Cavern Heaven, he quickly activated his senses to survey their surroundings. They were enveloped in a layer of mysterious mist, thin as gauze, which made it impossible to discern their exact location.

Just as he was pondering where they might be, a sharp and melodious phoenix cry shattered the silence, echoing through the forest. Moments later, a deep, majestic dragon’s roar responded from the depths of the woods, the two calls resonating with each other.

Hearing the simultaneous cries of dragon and phoenix, Lin Jing immediately exclaimed, “Dragon-Phoenix Cavern Heaven?!”

The countless legends about this Cavern Heaven surfaced in his mind. “Heavens are truly helping me! I’ve been stuck at the seventh stage of Qi Refinement for so long—looks like I’ll finally break through now.”

As soon as he finished speaking, the surrounding air began to tremble, the leaves rustling loudly. A massive creature emerged slowly from the forest. Its body was covered in blue scales, each one shimmering with a cold, gleaming light.

Roar!!!

A flood dragon emerged, its abyss-like eyes gleaming like cold stars. It opened its maw and unleashed a chilling dragon breath.

The Pine Leaf Mouse immediately released nine red leaves, scattering them in different directions. As the dragon breath descended, a wave of spatial energy surrounded Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse, teleporting them instantly into the air to evade the attack. This was the culmination of the Pine Leaf Mouse’s four months of training—the red leaves could now be used for teleportation.

“Don’t rush it just yet,” Lin Jing said, stopping the Pine Leaf Mouse as it prepared its mechanized bamboo rifle. “Look at the protective killing aura on its scales. Attacking it directly from the outside would take forever to break through. Remember the Mechanized Bamboo Dragon?”

With that, he pulled out a blood-filled bottle, inside which soaked several Spirit Bean Bombs.

Lin Jing retrieved one Spirit Bean Bomb from the bottle and threw it down from the air. In the next instant, the ferocious flood dragon, its eyes turning blood-red as if it had been fed, opened its mouth wide to devour the blood-soaked Spirit Bean.

Boom!

The Spirit Bean Bomb exploded within the unsuspecting flood dragon’s mouth, blasting away half of its head.

As the flood dragon collapsed to the ground, Lin Jing watched in awe as it transformed into two streams of radiant light—one flowed into the Pine Leaf Mouse, and the other entered his own body.

Immediately, Lin Jing felt his cultivation surge, his True Energy doubling in strength. In a single leap, his cultivation rose to the eighth stage of Qi Refinement!

This miraculous improvement left Lin Jing’s breath quickened with excitement.

The Pine Leaf Mouse, though it didn’t achieve a breakthrough, also felt its True Energy become significantly more refined—comparable to the results of a prolonged closed-door cultivation session.

It, too, began to breathe heavily, its eyes darting to its storage bag, which held ten warehouses’ worth of spiritual rice, over a thousand Replenishing Pills refined from Dragonblood Carps, and even the dozens of True Energy recovery pills distributed to the examiners earlier that morning by the academy.

“Squeak!!!” The Pine Leaf Mouse looked at its hoarded resources, its eyes glowing red with hesitation, as if it were contemplating a monumental decision.

It trembled as it grabbed Lin Jing’s sleeve, hoping he would talk some sense into it. The mouse feared it might lose control, throw all its resources into bombs, and start a wild rampage against every dragon and phoenix projection it encountered. That wasn’t the real issue—the mouse was more concerned about how much Longevity Blood they’d need to harvest for such a massacre. Lin Jing might literally bleed himself dry!

But Lin Jing’s eyes had already turned red as well. The insane speed of his cultivation growth had completely hooked him.

“Even the Ice Gu parasite in my body seems thrilled—it’s practically wriggling with excitement!” Lin Jing muttered. “Should I undo a portion of its seal and let us split up? We could go all out, hunting separately and killing to our hearts’ content! This should count as one of those ‘special circumstances’ where Senior Magic Pine said I could temporarily unlock the seal and borrow its strength, right?”

The Pine Leaf Mouse froze in shock, staring at Lin Jing like it was watching someone spiral into madness.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 21: Sacred Wood Seal, Unlocked!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 21: Sacred Wood Seal, Unlocked!

When Lin Jing turned his focus inward, he suddenly discovered that the Ice Soul Gu parasitizing his body had also entered the Cavern Heaven with him.

At the time of his initial entry, he hadn’t given it much thought and had overlooked the Ice Soul Gu. Now that he considered it, this realization came as a surprise.

It made sense that the Pine Leaf Mouse, bonded with him via a bloodline contract and cultivated alongside him using the Beast Taming Art passed down from the Immortal Palace, would enter the same Cavern Heaven. However, the Ice Soul Gu had no such contract. Yet, it followed him into the Dragon-Phoenix Cavern Heaven as if it were one of his contracted beasts.

“Does this mean that the Cavern Heaven regards the Ice Soul Gu as part of me?” Lin Jing pondered.

“It’s certainly possible. Mature Gu worms can act independently, but as Senior Shen Song mentioned, newborn Gu worms are much more fragile. They require a host to act as their vessel to safely sleep. Without an appropriate environment for rest, infant Gu worms will perish.”

‘In these specific circumstances, the relationship between the Gu worm and its host is no longer a simple parasitic one. While it doesn’t reach the level of full symbiosis, it does make the Gu worm highly dependent on its host. Perhaps this parasitic connection, combined with partial symbiosis, made the Cavern Heaven perceive it as part of me.’

A faint smile crossed Lin Jing’s lips.

The Pine Leaf Mouse, noticing the gleam in Lin Jing’s eyes and his restless demeanor, gulped nervously.

“Hey!”

Is this even okay? Senior Shen Song had repeatedly warned him not to use the Ice Soul Gu’s power unless it was a life-or-death situation.

“Senior Mo Song also taught me this: as a demonic cultivator, don’t be overly obedient.”

“This Cavern Heaven is a rare opportunity, not to be wasted.”

“After I came out of the Bodhi Cavern Heaven, I read many books about such places. There are rumors that the Dragon-Phoenix Cavern Heaven can help cultivators break through their limit stages. ”

“Do you understand what this means? It means that as long as we hunt enough Dragon-Phoenix creatures, I can not only reach the Ninth Stage of Qi Refinement, saving myself years of arduous cultivation and countless resources, but I might even have a chance to break through to the Tenth Stage or even the Eleventh Stage of Qi Refinement. That’s a dream come true for countless cultivators! Reaching such heights would make Foundation Establishment much easier for us.”

To establish a foundation, one must compress their True Energy, transform it into liquid form, circulate it throughout the body, purify their spiritual veins, shed their mortal body, and give birth to the True Essence Spirit Sea.

True Essence flowing within the Spirit Sea is far more powerful than True Energy. A Foundation Establishment cultivator’s magical power is over ten times stronger than that of a Qi Refinement cultivator. Additionally, their consciousness and perception will transform into "spiritual sense," allowing them to detect movements within a hundred-zhang radius with just a thought.

For most ordinary cultivators, the difficulty of Foundation Establishment lies in the lack of sufficiently pure and dense True Energy to compress it into liquid form. This is why they often rely on external aids like Foundation Establishment Pills to assist them in breaking through.

However, those with powerful techniques or innate spiritual bodies possess extraordinary True Energy, making Foundation Establishment easier and rendering pills unnecessary.

Lin Jing, unfortunately, did not fall into either category. While the Beast Taming Art he cultivated allowed him to contract spirit beasts, it offered no particular advantage in True Energy refinement. Similarly, his Longevity Body only excelled in lifespan, unlike elemental spiritual bodies, which enhance specific types of True Energy.

This meant that when Lin Jing attempted to break through to Foundation Establishment, he would be no different from an ordinary mortal cultivator, with no significant advantages. Worse still, his Longevity Body's slower cultivation speed and True Energy refinement would force him to spend a long time in the Qi Refinement stage.

As such, he would most likely have to rely on a Foundation Establishment Pill and the feedback from his contracted beasts to attempt his breakthrough.

But now, if he could cultivate in the Dragon-Phoenix Cavern Heaven and reach the peak of Qi Refinement—perhaps even the Tenth Stage—breaking through to Foundation Establishment would become as effortless as drinking water. Additionally, the deeper his foundation during Qi Refinement, the greater the enhancements to his body before Foundation Establishment.

Take, for example, two Qi Refinement cultivators: one establishing their foundation at the Fourth Stage and the other at the Tenth. The latter would absorb spiritual energy several times faster than the former.

These were concepts Lin Jing had gleaned from books. Now that he had encountered such a rare opportunity, his eyes gleamed red with excitement, unable to suppress his desire.

This was one of the few chances to accelerate his cultivation speed before Foundation Establishment!

Lin Jing made up his mind. He and the Pine Leaf Mouse rode a flying leaf, soaring through the vast expanse of the Dragon-Phoenix Cavern Heaven in search of their next prey.

Standing atop the leaf controlled by the Pine Leaf Mouse, Lin Jing crossed his hands behind his back, his robes fluttering as his sharp gaze scanned the horizon.

“Senior Shen Song did warn that repeatedly using the Ice Soul Gu’s power would hasten its awakening, making it difficult to suppress even with the Sacred Wood Seal.”

“But as long as I establish my foundation quickly and contract the Flame Dragon Carp, which possesses a Fire Kirin bloodline, even if the Ice Soul Gu fully awakens, it shouldn’t be a major problem.”

“Now… assist me in unlocking a sliver of the Ice Soul Gu’s seal.”

Lin Jing made up his mind. Hearing this, the Pine Leaf Mouse frantically scratched its head. Isn’t this too extreme? That power—it feared it. If Lin Jing used it, wouldn’t he end up frozen solid?

“No problem. If I can’t handle it, I’ll stop,” Lin Jing replied, opening a vial of Longevity Blood. The blood’s aura naturally diffused into the surroundings.

After flying for some distance, Lin Jing discovered that the Dragon-Phoenix Cavern Heaven was indeed as vast as the records described. Actively searching for Dragon-Phoenix projections would waste considerable time. Perhaps this method could attract the projections to attack them instead.

Now that he thought about it, he had been so focused on his breakthroughs earlier that he hadn’t noticed something. Reflecting on it now, the flavor of the Dragon-Phoenix he had slain seemed to awaken his taste buds.

Lin Jing licked his lips. This kind of simple, sensory pleasure was quite satisfying.

“Hey!!!” The Pine Leaf Mouse also swallowed hard. Even as a mostly vegetarian spirit beast, it found itself wanting to taste it again.

However, its gaze shifted uneasily to the Longevity Blood. For some reason, it found that scent even more enticing…

“Sacred Wood Seal, release!” Lin Jing’s gaze turned deep and resolute.

At the same time, within Lin Jing’s inner world...

A colossal ice crystal spider, entirely bound by vines of the Sacred Tree, slumbered within its sealed prison. The golden-green divine patterns of the seal not only suppressed the spider’s conscious activity but also locked away the terrifying power it exuded.

However, as Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse worked together to partially lift the seal, a horrifying wave of white frost energy surged through the cracks in the vines. This cold aura flowed into Lin Jing’s body, merging with the True Energy within him.

In an instant, his previously ordinary Beast Taming Qi transformed into a freezing cold ice-element True Energy!

Boom!

In the outside world, a wave of horrifying frost energy erupted from Lin Jing’s body, shooting straight into the sky. In mere moments, snowflakes began falling over the forested mountains, and as the ice-element True Energy coursed through him, Lin Jing’s skin turned as white as snow, his hair became frosted with pale white, and his pupils transformed into eerie ice-blue, like those of a frost beast.

Suddenly, a green dragon emerged from the valley, soaring toward Lin Jing. But before it could approach, Lin Jing uttered a single word:

“Die.”

The frosty aura surged violently. The dragon-like serpent froze mid-flight with an audible crack crack crack, turning into an ice sculpture. In the next moment, the frozen sculpture shattered into countless shards of ice, scattering in all directions as the creature’s life force vanished completely.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 22: Unprecedented
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 22: Unprecedented

With the power of the Ice Soul Gu, Lin Jing instantly killed a Ninth Stage Qi Refinement Dragon-Blooded Demonic Beast. The creature stood no chance against him.

Dragon-Blooded Beasts were inherently powerful; those with purer bloodlines could even rival Foundation Establishment beasts. However, under the might of the Ice Soul Gu, they were utterly outmatched.

“The top ten legendary Gu worms... An innate Gu King is far beyond the so-called ‘beast kings’ of the demon beasts. It’s more akin to raising dozens, or even hundreds, of poisonous insects with royal bloodlines together in a deathmatch, resulting in the ultimate Poison King.”

Lin Jing had only unleashed a sliver of the Ice Soul Gu’s power, yet he could already feel a bone-chilling cold spread through his body.

“...” The Pine Leaf Mouse, disliking the frigid aura, stayed far away from him.

As the Dragon-Blooded Beast perished, it transformed into two streams of light. The Pine Leaf Mouse, eagerly anticipating a tasty reward, was shocked when both streams of light flew into Lin Jing instead.

One was absorbed by Lin Jing, while the other entered the Sacred Wood Seal, nourishing the slumbering Ice Soul Gu.

For the first time since its birth, the Ice Soul Gu absorbed energy, causing it to grow noticeably larger in size.

“Give me half of the qi-replenishing pills!”

Meanwhile, in other parts of the Cavern Heaven, other examinees were also locked in fierce battles, hunting the Dragon-Phoenix creatures.

However, not everyone had Lin Jing’s overwhelming strength. Most of the examinees, after managing to slay a single beast, sat down gasping for breath, relying on pills to replenish their True Energy.

“D*mn it, why did I only bring a few bottles of pills? Why couldn’t I bring more?”

“Ugh, these magic tools are such a pain, needing True Energy to activate. If only I had brought a Mechanical Bamboo Rifle instead!”

Many examinees regretted not being better prepared for such a rare opportunity.

“Luckily, this young master brought plenty of spirit stones!” Gao Sheng boasted. While he lacked sufficient pills, his storage bag contained not only large quantities of low-grade spirit stones but also middle- and high-grade ones.

With a spirit stone in one hand and a magic tool in the other, Gao Sheng fought Dragon-Phoenix creatures with relative ease.

Another standout was Chen Lingxi from the Divine Talisman Sect. Having used only a fraction of her talismans against Lin Jing earlier, she now had most of them at her disposal for hunting the Dragon-Phoenix creatures. Her efficiency was unmatched.

Still, despite the talent and resources of some examinees, none could match Lin Jing’s terrifying speed. With Longevity Blood as bait and the power of the Gu King unleashed, Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse were in a league of their own.

As they soared across the mountains, Dragon-Phoenix projections fell in droves. Their corpses turned into streams of light, flowing into Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse. Before long, Lin Jing broke through to the Ninth Stage of Qi Refinement, catching up to the Pine Leaf Mouse in cultivation.

“Die!”

Another flying dragon attacked, but with a single wave of Lin Jing’s hand, several snow-white spider threads shot out, instantly dismembering the creature. The dragon’s corpse appeared as though it had been sliced by icy blades, its surface coated in frost, and its blood frozen solid.

Boom!

Elsewhere, the Pine Leaf Mouse, clutching a bottle of Longevity Blood, used its agility to dart across the battlefield. It hurled Spirit Bean Bombs at Dragon-Phoenix creatures, taking them down one by one. If not for the delicious feedback from the Dragon-Phoenix creatures already satisfying its cravings, it might have succumbed to the irresistible allure of the Longevity Blood it held.

One day earlier.

At the base of Flying Dragon Mountain within the Hidden Dragon Academy, a brilliant light burst forth.

As spatial ripples condensed, a seemingly ordinary Nascent Soul cultivator emerged in a hurry.

Before descending, the cultivator looked at the two beams of light within the Cavern Heaven and laughed heartily.

“My son, Wang Xiao, is destined for enlightenment!”

“Congratulations, Daoist Wang,” said Cao Ziwei, who was meditating above the academy, immediately congratulating him.

“Thank you, Mr. Cao. The Hidden Dragon Academy truly lives up to its reputation as a gathering place of great fortune. If Xiao’er successfully reaches the Tenth Stage of Qi Refinement and then breaks through to Foundation Establishment, his Heavenly God Physique may finally achieve minor completion,” said Wang, a cultivator from the Wang family. Having learned of his son’s rare opportunity, he had come personally to await the results.

“Wang Xiao and Chen Lingxi are indeed the most likely candidates to break through to the Tenth Stage of Qi Refinement,” Cao Ziwei nodded. “After all, the Dragon-Phoenix Cavern Heaven only truly chooses a select few. Although other cultivators may enter and share the opportunities, they’re ultimately not the chosen ones. At best, they can condense their True Energy dozens or hundreds of times to make it comparable to True Essence, but that’s it.”

The Tenth Stage of Qi Refinement was a special realm for talents selected by the Immortal Palace. Reaching it was no simple feat and required both innate talent and extraordinary opportunities.

Cao Ziwei thought of Xu Zhizhi and Lin Jing, two other geniuses who had previously entered a Cavern Heaven. While they might achieve some success here, he doubted they had any hope of reaching the Tenth Stage.

Especially Lin Jing, as a Beast Tamer cultivator, any opportunities he obtained would be split with his contracted beasts, significantly reducing his chances.

“To reach the Tenth Stage of Qi Refinement, one needs both strong innate physique and exceptional opportunities. Without a powerful innate body, the mortal body would struggle to endure or contain the massive amount of True Energy.”

Cao Ziwei’s assessment wasn’t far off.

Inside the Dragon-Phoenix Cavern Heaven, Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse fought relentlessly. However, with the opportunities divided into three parts, the benefits to each individual were diminished.

Despite slaying countless Dragon-Phoenix projections, Lin Jing’s cultivation eventually reached the Ninth Stage of Qi Refinement, but the Pine Leaf Mouse remained stuck at the same level, unable to break through.

Although its Qingdi (Everlasting Emperor) True Energy had been refined over a hundred times, its spiritual bloodline had reached its limit. It could no longer expand its True Energy capacity, leaving it to focus solely on improving the quality of its energy.

Lin Jing found himself in a similar situation—stuck at the Great Perfection of Qi Refinement. His True Energy had been refined to an exceptionally pure level, but the quantity of True Energy remained stagnant, unable to progress further.

As Cao Ziwei had speculated, Lin Jing’s Longevity Body and the Pine Leaf Mouse’s bloodline were far less compatible with this opportunity than the Heavenly God Physique or the Innate Talisman Physique. It seemed inevitable that the two of them would stop at the extreme of the Ninth Stage of Qi Refinement.

However, what Cao Ziwei didn’t know was that the Ice Soul Gu’s overwhelming power, combined with the Longevity Body’s natural ability to attract Dragon-Phoenix projections, had allowed Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse to slay an absurd number of Dragon-Phoenix projections. The number they killed wasn’t just three times that of the average cultivator—it wouldn’t be an exaggeration to say it was thirty times as much.

Even though the opportunities were divided into three parts between them, the sheer scale of their efforts resulted in enormous gains.

Though limited by their physique and bloodline, unable to increase their True Energy capacity, they had nonetheless broken through conventional limits. Their True Energy had been refined to a level of unparalleled purity. In this state, just before the Tenth Stage of Qi Refinement, they reached a "half-step Foundation Establishment" state. Without needing to deliberately focus on it, their bodies began to strengthen themselves under the guidance of their impossibly refined True Energy.

The Pine Leaf Mouse’s Glazed Immortal Eyes gleamed, and within its vibrant green pupils, the image of a clover appeared, marking the successful evolution of its ability to the next stage.

On Lin Jing’s side, he also stopped in his tracks, his expression filled with astonishment as he sensed the changes within his body. Though he hadn’t reached Foundation Establishment yet, under the incomparably refined Beast Taming Qi within him, something extraordinary had occurred—he had condensed a second drop of golden blood within his body.

This blood was the Contract Blood of the Beast Taming Art. Normally, a cultivator practicing the Beast Taming Art could condense one drop during the Qi Refinement stage and a second drop upon reaching Foundation Establishment. However, Lin Jing had defied all convention by condensing two drops of Contract Blood while still in the Qi Refinement stage!

This meant that he could now form contracts with two spirit beasts during the Qi Refinement stage, an unprecedented feat in the history of the Beast Taming Sect.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 23: The Beast Taming Physique
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 23: The Beast Taming Physique

Lin Jing ceased flying and descended to the ground, where the surrounding area instantly froze upon his landing.

Extending his hand, he drew out the golden Contract Blood and examined it carefully.

This Contract Blood appeared no different from the first drop he had condensed when forming a contract with the Pine Leaf Mouse.

Running through the Beast Taming Art once more, Lin Jing confirmed that it was still at the ninth stage and had yet to reach perfection. He couldn’t help but marvel at the phenomenon.

"The opportunities within the Cavern Heaven truly are extraordinary. After refining my Beast Taming Qi to a flawless state, it actually triggered a second transformation of my physique, allowing me to condense another drop of Contract Blood!"

"And yet... after this second transformation of my physique, I feel like there have been other changes as well," Lin Jing muttered, his gaze shifting toward the Pine Leaf Mouse.

The Pine Leaf Mouse, on its end, seemed preoccupied with inspecting its own Glazed Immortal Eyes. Just like Lin Jing, it had undergone a half-step Foundation Establishment transformation, with its physique altered by the refinement of True Energy.

“Squeak squeak squeak!!!” The Pine Leaf Mouse finished studying itself and discovered that its Glazed Immortal Eyes had gained an additional function. Although it still couldn’t synthesize resources outside of plant-based materials, nor could it create higher-tier resources, it now displayed countless dense symbols over synthesized items in its field of vision.

Picking up a red leaf, its information immediately surfaced in the Pine Leaf Mouse’s mind:

[Companion Heartleaf: A plant capable of connecting with the minds of specific beings, containing spatial properties that can serve as a medium for space-related abilities.]

The Pine Leaf Mouse then examined an Exploding Spirit Bean:

[Exploding Spirit Bean: A special bean containing violent spiritual energy, capable of creating intense spiritual energy shockwaves.]

“Squeak!” The Pine Leaf Mouse turned to Lin Jing, excitedly signaling that it could now perceive the effects of synthesized items through its newly upgraded Glazed Immortal Eyes.

“Oh?” Lin Jing was astonished. Clearly, the Pine Leaf Mouse’s gains were not insignificant.

While Lin Jing’s physique had been transformed through the flawless refinement of Beast Taming Qi, the Pine Leaf Mouse’s eyes had been upgraded by the Everlasting Emperor’s True Energy.

Although it seemed that the Glazed Immortal Eyes had not gained any new abilities for synthesizing resources, the ability to perceive the effects of synthesized items was a significant improvement.

After leaving the Beast Taming Sect, they had stopped experimenting with synthesized resources due to the lack of test subjects and other distractions. Now, with the advanced Glazed Immortal Eyes, the Pine Leaf Mouse could resume synthesis without needing experimental subjects. Even if the synthesized items were hazardous, it could immediately identify their effects without risking itself.

“It seems this trip was worthwhile after all,” Lin Jing remarked with satisfaction.

“By the way, can you sense any changes in me?” Lin Jing asked, clenching the golden Contract Blood in his hand and retracting it. Apart from forming the second drop of Contract Blood, he vaguely sensed some changes in his physique, but he couldn’t pinpoint them on his own.

The Pine Leaf Mouse hesitated for a moment before looking into Lin Jing’s eyes. In an instant, it felt as if it had plunged into darkness, with only a pair of eyes as vast and oppressive as celestial stars staring back at it.

“...” The Pine Leaf Mouse staggered back, cold sweat running down its furry body as it felt a shudder deep in its bloodline.

“Squeak…” The Pine Leaf Mouse could feel that Lin Jing had become much more terrifying. It was as though he could subdue it instantly. The faint sense of bloodline suppression it experienced made the blood vial it was holding suddenly seem utterly unappealing.

Lin Jing observed this reaction thoughtfully.

Just then, a giant bird engulfed in flames swooped toward the Pine Leaf Mouse. Without using any spells, Lin Jing allowed his Ninth Stage Beast Taming Qi to erupt.

Boom!

Nine whirlpools of True Energy swirled around Lin Jing, and in an instant, even though the Longevity Blood remained as tempting as ever, the fiery bird—possessing phoenix bloodline—froze mid-air. It appeared as if it had sensed an overwhelmingly dreadful aura and dared not move forward.

“It seems my suspicions were correct. After the second transformation of my physique, my ability to suppress beasts has become significantly stronger,” Lin Jing took a deep breath.

“In the past, the Beast Taming Art could only suppress contracted spirit beasts, preventing them from rebelling. But now, I can even suppress uncontracted beasts. If this were a weaker demonic beast instead of this firebird with a strong bloodline, it would probably submit to me with just a single glance.”

Lin Jing extended his palm toward the flaming bird, clenching the air as if grasping an invisible force. Instantly, the firebird sensed a tremendous danger. It turned to flee, but it was too late.

“Flame Control Art!”

Before the bird could fly far, the flames within its body erupted uncontrollably, turning it into a massive fireball.

Boom!

The fiery bird exploded spectacularly.

“Squeak?!” The Pine Leaf Mouse looked at Lin Jing in awe. Just using his Beast Taming Qi and the Flame Control Art, Lin Jing had annihilated a Ninth Stage Qi Refinement demonic beast. The Pine Leaf Mouse’s regard for him grew significantly.

This time, Lin Jing hadn’t even relied on the power of the Ice Soul Gu!

“While this ability may not have the same effect on humans, it’ll definitely give me an advantage when dealing with demonic beasts in the future. It’s said that certain powerful techniques can directly alter a cultivator’s physique, enabling those with ordinary mortal bodies to develop special physiques. For instance, Yuan Jing once had a mortal body, but after reaching Foundation Establishment, he transformed into the Sword Physique. Now, with this second awakening of my Beast Taming Qi, could it have granted me a special physique as well?”

“The physique that suppresses all beasts under the heavens… the Beast Taming Physique!”

Lin Jing fell into deep thought as this bold hypothesis surfaced in his mind. Even if his physique hadn’t achieved the aura required to suppress all beasts yet, the sheer anomaly of condensing two drops of Contract Blood during the Qi Refinement stage was enough to prove how extraordinary his current physique had become.

“Squeak!!!” The Pine Leaf Mouse tossed a Replenishing Pill to Lin Jing. Both of them had been relying on pills to maintain their peak condition. It then chattered, asking if they would be able to immediately contract the Flame Dragon Carp once they returned.

“Hmm,” Lin Jing nodded. “However, even though I’ve managed to cultivate a second physique by chance, we still haven’t reached the Tenth Stage of Qi Refinement. I’m not sure how much longer this Cavern Heaven will remain stable...”

“I’m already nearing my limit...”

He raised a trembling hand, struggling to move his fingers. His entire body felt like his blood had almost stopped circulating and was already freezing solid. If he continued using the power of the Ice Soul Gu, he feared that he would soon lose the ability to move altogether.

This terrifying freezing power had even resisted his desperate attempts to counter it. He had tried eating black peppers capable of scorching one’s soul, yet they had done nothing to dispel the cold.

Still, without the Ice Soul Gu’s power, the efficiency of his and the Pine Leaf Mouse’s hunting would plummet dramatically.

“What do you think...” Lin Jing asked the Pine Leaf Mouse as he narrowed his eyes. “What if I use the second drop of Contract Blood I condensed during the Qi Refinement stage to contract the Ice Soul Gu directly?”

“It’s currently sealed and suppressed, as well as in a state of dormancy. Since it shares my fate—my gains and losses are its gains and losses—it’s unlikely to resist the Beast Taming Contract. If I successfully contract it, I should be able to suppress its power more effectively using the contract and my Beast Taming Physique.”

Previously, Lin Jing wouldn’t have dared to make such a bold move. But now, with the Beast Taming Physique cultivated, and given that Gu worms were classified as beasts, he believed he might not even need to rely on a fire-type spirit beast to suppress the Ice Soul Gu. The Beast Taming Contract alone might be enough to control its power to some extent.

If successful, he would be able to harness the Ice Soul Gu’s power far more effectively.

However, there was one concern. Gu Masters had specialized techniques unique to their craft for controlling Gu worms, while the Beast Taming Art wasn’t designed for contracting Gu. At least, the Beast Taming Sect had no records of such an attempt. What might happen was completely unknown.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 24: Three-Body Cultivator
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 24: Three-Body Cultivator

After Lin Jing decided to preemptively contract the Ice Soul Gu, he wasted no time and began acting immediately, sitting cross-legged on the snowy ground.

“Pine Leaf Mouse, guard me.”

Hearing this, the Pine Leaf Mouse froze, dumbfounded. It hadn’t expected the Ice Soul Gu to take over the Dragon Carp’s contract slot so soon, instantly becoming the second contracted beast.

The poor Dragon Carp not only missed out on the opportunity to benefit from the Cavern Heaven, but now its second contract position was also snatched away!

“...Alright.” The Pine Leaf Mouse manipulated the surrounding vegetation to construct a wooden fortress, shielding Lin Jing within. It then stationed itself atop the fortress to ward off demonic beasts from the Dragon-Phoenix Cavern Heaven, ensuring they wouldn’t interrupt Lin Jing’s attempt to contract the Ice Soul Gu.

Knowing it was futile to dissuade Lin Jing, the Pine Leaf Mouse simply obeyed the order.

Inside the sealed space.

Lin Jing’s consciousness manifested, and he gazed at the slumbering Ice Soul Gu before him. Though still asleep, its cultivation had already reached the Great Perfection of Qi Refinement. A ripple of thought crossed his mind.

“After entering my body, it was further sealed and suppressed, resulting in extremely slow growth. But now, nurtured by the Dragon-Phoenix Cavern Heaven, its bloodline may awaken and break through to Foundation Establishment once we leave this place. At that point, if I haven’t yet contracted the Dragon Carp, it will be hard to maintain balance and restrain it. I must contract it before it awakens.”

The current Beast Taming Art, which had been revised by the Beast Taming Sect, was no longer a master-slave life-and-death contract but rather claimed to establish equality. However, in practice, Beast Tamers still maintained a level of suppression over their contracted beasts. The dynamic was akin to that of “brother and brother” or “father and son” — not absolute equality but lacking the extremity where the Beast Tamer could determine the life and death of their beasts with a single thought.

“Blood, descend.” Lin Jing raised his hand. With a single thought, golden light sparkled in the sealed space’s sky. A massive drop of golden contract blood descended slowly, falling toward the slumbering Ice Soul Gu.

This method was clearly not the traditional contract technique used by today’s Beast Taming Sect. It was more reminiscent of the early practices of the sect, where demonic beasts were subdued, sealed, and contracted by force while they were unable to resist.

The golden contract blood seeped into the Ice Soul Gu through the Sacred Wood Seal’s gaps. Although the Ice Soul Gu didn’t resist, Lin Jing still felt immense pressure during the process. The Ice Soul Gu’s bloodline was too powerful; even with his Beast Taming Physique, the contract wasn’t proceeding smoothly.

Lin Jing’s manifested consciousness began to freeze.

It could be said that as one of the Ten Rare Gus, its power level wasn’t necessarily weaker than the same-tier Heavenly God Physique.

Inside the wooden fortress, as the contract progressed, an intense cold aura surged out from Lin Jing’s body, instantly freezing the entire structure. The Pine Leaf Mouse, seeing this, shivered uncontrollably and immediately used its Flying Leaf Technique to hover in mid-air while continuing to guard.

“Ack… ack…”

While guarding, the Pine Leaf Mouse occasionally glanced nervously back at Lin Jing. Inside the wooden fortress, Lin Jing was enduring immense pain. The reason he wasn’t making any sound was that his entire body had been turned into an ice sculpture. Were it not for the contract link confirming Lin Jing still had consciousness, the Pine Leaf Mouse might have thought he had died during the process.

…

Within the sealed space, during the contract process, the slumbering Ice Soul Gu struggled as if trapped in a nightmare, repeatedly attempting to break free from the seal. Fortunately, the seal was created by the Divine Pine and not by Lin Jing or the Pine Leaf Mouse; otherwise, it might have already been shattered.

“This Ice Soul Gu…”

“It’s forcibly altering your physique!” After some time, Lin Jing noticed something unusual. Perhaps due to the Pine Leaf Mouse’s bloodline being too weak, or perhaps it was the inherent nature of Gu insects, the process of contracting the Ice Soul Gu triggered a reaction similar to that of contracting unique spirit beasts or demonic beasts.

During the bloodline contract stage, while the Ice Soul Gu didn’t resist excessively, the ever-deepening bloodline connection caused it to feed back a force that forcibly reshaped Lin Jing’s physical body!

“This is bad…”

“Wait…” The Ice Soul Gu’s ultimate icy power seemed to have completely fused with Lin Jing’s body. Now, even by borrowing the Ice Soul Gu’s strength, Lin Jing could wield ice-type powers as if they were an innate part of himself.

Most astonishingly, as Lin Jing’s body temperature dropped further, he sensed that his life span was increasing by a unique means. Though he possessed the Longevity Body with an inherently long lifespan, the Ice Soul Gu’s contract process was further extending it.

He… was becoming even more “immortal.”

“Rumor has it that cultivators with ice-related physiques… appear younger than normal cultivators and age more slowly. Am I now in a similar situation?”

Inside the wooden fortress, the ice on Lin Jing’s body gradually sublimated into white frost. The contract blood had perfectly merged with the Ice Soul Gu. However, within the sealed space, the Ice Soul Gu remained in its slumber, still unaware it had been contracted. Its reaction upon fully awakening remained a mystery.

“Still, this newfound power seems to come with its own cost…”

The Outside World

Lin Jing reappeared, hovering in midair as he manipulated his energy to fly. At that moment, the Pine Leaf Mouse was carefully monitoring the four incoming Dragonblood Fish from all directions.

“I’ll handle this,” Lin Jing said, signaling the Pine Leaf Mouse not to intervene.

As soon as he finished speaking, a surge of icy energy erupted from his body. Nine spiraling vortices of spiritual energy expanded outward, and the Dragonblood Fish attacking from all sides were immediately coated in a layer of white frost. Many of them froze and shattered, their broken pieces falling to the ground.

At the same time, Lin Jing felt the same sensation as before: using this ice-attribute power still caused him to suffer from “frostbite.”

“I don’t truly possess a natural ice-attribute physique. Instead, I’ve been influenced by the Ice Soul Gu’s power, resulting in something similar to an acquired Ice Spirit Physique. However, this acquired physique is inherently flawed compared to a natural one. Therefore, when wielding this overwhelmingly dominant ice power—more powerful than even the Ice Spirit Physique—it still causes frostbite, just as it did before.”

Not only did his physical body suffer from frostbite, but even his soul was slightly injured. However, the severity was far less than before—a significant benefit of the contract. As long as he didn’t overuse the power and regularly used top-grade fire-attribute or yang-attribute spiritual medicines and pills to recover, it shouldn’t be a major issue.

“Not bad. I’ll call it the Acquired Ice Soul Physique.” Lin Jing smiled. This transformation had greatly enhanced his strength. Furthermore, the Ice Soul Physique could serve as a disguise, misleading others into mistaking it for a natural Ice Spirit Physique, causing them to underestimate him or misjudge his capabilities.

“What?!” With the Dragonblood Fish dead, the Pine Leaf Mouse looked at Lin Jing as he hovered midair. It noticed that the color of his hair and pupils had returned to normal, indicating that he had gained initial control over the Ice Soul Gu’s power.

“Squeak squeak!!” The Pine Leaf Mouse chirped excitedly, genuinely happy for Lin Jing. It didn’t seem jealous of Lin Jing contracting yet another beast.

The Pine Leaf Mouse often fretted over one particular issue: many of the resources it stockpiled would spoil if not maintained with its wood-attribute true energy. Over time, the resources would begin to rot.

But now, with Lin Jing contracting an ice pet, such worries would be a thing of the past. As long as the storage space for the harvested resources was cold enough, their shelf life would be significantly extended, and the Pine Leaf Mouse wouldn’t have to personally “maintain” them anymore!

From now on, the Ice Gu would handle preservation, the Dragon Carp could refine herbs into pills, and the Pine Leaf Mouse felt like this arrangement was perfect!

“Thank you. I’ve already resealed the Ice Soul Gu. The power I displayed earlier... it has already been converted into my own strength. While it’s not as potent as directly using the Ice Soul Gu’s power, it’s still comparable and more than enough to instantly kill these Dragonblood and Phoenixblood demonic beasts,” Lin Jing said, his body enveloped in cold mist.

“Let’s move on.”

He pondered for a moment, wondering whether these changes to his body meant he now possessed two additional unique physiques. If so, wouldn’t that make him similar to the Ancient Imperial Prince, effectively becoming a “Three-Body Cultivator”?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 25: Qi Refinement Tenth Stage?
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 25: Qi Refinement Tenth Stage?

After enhancing their physiques, both Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse found themselves able to accommodate more true energy, giving them the possibility of advancing to the Qi Refinement Tenth Stage. The two continued hunting the Dragon-Phoenix projections together.

However, to Lin Jing’s surprise, even after resealing the Ice Soul Gu, it still continued to absorb a portion of his energy.

“You two better leave me some!” Lin Jing redoubled his efforts in hunting the Dragon-Phoenix beasts, driven by a bad premonition.

Given how difficult it was for someone with the Longevity Body to break through, he feared that by the time the Cavern Heaven event ended, the Pine Leaf Mouse and Ice Soul Gu might both reach the Tenth Stage of Qi Refinement while he remained stuck at the Ninth Stage!

Boom!

The Pine Leaf Mouse blasted yet another projection attempting to consume the Longevity Blood, and the light particles released from the projection scattered uncontrollably.

Absorbing these light particles, the Pine Leaf Mouse suddenly discovered a surge of spiritual energy within its body!

Buzz!

Whoosh!

Under the control of even more powerful Everlasting Emperor’s True Energy, enhanced by its willpower, the Pine Leaf Mouse’s speed with Flying Leaf Technique increased drastically!

Watching this, Lin Jing felt conflicted. Even as a "Three-Body Cultivator" now, the Pine Leaf Mouse still managed to break through before him. This seemed to confirm that his two new physiques didn’t significantly enhance his cultivation speed.

Fortunately, the Pine Leaf Mouse’s breakthrough produced an abundance of spiritual energy that fed back into Lin Jing through their bloodline contract, bringing him closer to the Tenth Stage.

“Breakthrough!” Lin Jing muttered some time later.

“Too bad it wasn’t me.”

The Ice Soul Gu absorbed nutrients before Lin Jing and reached the Tenth Stage of Qi Refinement first.

Though still asleep, the Ice Soul Gu also fed back a substantial amount of spiritual energy to Lin Jing. Yet, Lin Jing remained just short of breaking through.

Both his contracted beasts had broken through their limits, leaving him the only one still lagging behind. Feeling frustrated, Lin Jing unleashed an even more ferocious wave of icy energy, injuring himself in the process—not because of the Dragon-Phoenix beasts but because he had to release fresh blood to attract more of them!

Finally, after a night of relentless hunting, Lin Jing hovered in midair, his body emanating massive waves of spiritual energy. Ten rings of spiritual energy formed, swirling around him like ten vortices, wrapping him in their embrace. With a deep exhale, Lin Jing released a frosty breath, then retracted all his true energy into his body.

Within him, his true energy had grown vast, like an ocean of clouds. The Qi Sea of the Tenth Stage was ten times greater than that of the Ninth Stage, and every strand of true energy remained flawless. This unparalleled power filled Lin Jing with immense confidence.

After days of tireless effort, Lin Jing had finally crossed into the pinnacle of the Qi Refinement Tenth Stage!

“Let’s keep going!” Lin Jing flew onward without pausing to revel in his breakthrough.

He recalled ancient books he had read in the academy’s library, which claimed that anyone who reached the Tenth Stage of Qi Refinement possessed talent worthy of catching the eye of the ancient Celestial Palaces, qualifying them to become outer disciples of such immortal sects.

The Celestial Palaces, as forces of the upper realms, undoubtedly set the bar extremely high. The thought of it filled Lin Jing with excitement, though he knew he had not yet reached his limits.

Elder Mo had once mentioned that the Ancient Imperial Prince, the son of the Second Ancient Emperor, achieved the absurd feat of reaching the Thirteenth Stage of Qi Refinement using the Boundary-Breaking Bird and the True Dragon Secret Arts.

This proved that the Tenth Stage was far from the peak.

“Qi Refinement Twelfth or Thirteenth Stages may be out of reach for now, but I wonder… could I push to the Eleventh Stage in this Cavern Heaven?”

Boom!

As Lin Jing flew, the Ice Soul Gu hovered behind him at a slow pace. In his storage pouch, various pills to replenish true energy were steadily being consumed, primarily to compensate for the energy lost during travel.

In the Dragon-Phoenix Cavern Heaven, what drained the most energy for the Ice Soul Gu and Pine Leaf Mouse wasn’t fighting but traveling. The cavern was enormous, and even when using Longevity Blood as bait, they frequently had to move to different areas because the Dragon-Phoenix projections were clustered too densely in certain spots.

Hidden Dragon Academy, Flying Dragon Mountain.

As time passed, more and more people gathered here. Many candidates had initially come alone, but upon entering the Cavern Heaven, elders from their sects and families had also arrived to observe their progress.

By now, Flying Dragon Mountain was filled with Nascent Soul cultivators, with Golden Core cultivators everywhere.

At one corner of the mountaintop, a pine cone hidden within a tree was muttering to itself.

“By my calculations, the Cavern Heaven should be ending soon.”

In midair, Cao Ziwei sat cross-legged. His cultivation at the Great Perfection of Nascent Soul placed him among the top five on Flying Dragon Mountain.

Standing up slowly, his Nascent Soul aura radiated outward as he transmitted his voice across a hundred miles:

“The Cavern Heaven is about to close. Anyone who breaks through their limits within the Dragon-Phoenix Cavern Heaven will be awarded a sixty-year use of an academy cave dwelling after passing the assessment.”

A sixty-year cave dwelling usage period… Upon hearing this, cultivators from second- and third-rate sects became instantly envious. Cultivation depended on four key factors: techniques, wealth, companions, and location. The academy’s spirit-veined cave dwellings were unquestionably prime cultivation sites, even superior to those of most ordinary sects.

“They’re coming out!”

With a shout from the crowd, the cultivators watching saw people emerging from the light pillar of the Cavern Heaven. One by one, candidates returned. Regardless of their current state, most wore smiles, clearly satisfied with their gains.

“How was it?” One candidate was immediately pulled aside by their elder for questioning.

“My true energy was refined more than tenfold in the Cavern Heaven! After continuous tempering, its strength now rivals true essence. Even against an early Foundation Establishment cultivator, I might hold my own in a direct confrontation!” the candidate exclaimed excitedly.

“Good, good, good!” The elder was delighted. They hadn’t expected the candidate to break through any limits, so the current gains were already excellent.

In midair, Cao Ziwei stood watching as one candidate after another emerged. He nodded slightly, satisfied with this batch. This cohort of candidates might be the strongest in terms of Qi Refinement power in the past century.

“Ninth Stage, Ninth Stage, Ninth Stage…” His spiritual sense swept over the candidates, but so far, all of them were still at the Ninth Stage of Qi Refinement.

Cao Ziwei sighed. “No matter how solid your foundation in the Ninth Stage of Qi Refinement, time alone will eventually bring you there. But the Tenth Stage? That’s a matter of luck and opportunity, and it seems most candidates lack that bit of fortune.”

His expression shifted suddenly. “Hmm? Finally, someone has reached the Tenth Stage.”

His gaze locked onto a woman in a yellow dress stepping calmly out of the Cavern Heaven—Chen Lingxi from the Divine Talisman Sect.

As she descended from the light column, nine swirling vortices of spiritual energy appeared around her, exuding a transcendent aura.

“Xier!” A beautiful Nascent Soul woman immediately approached her.

“Master… I’m sorry.” Chen Lingxi looked remorseful as she greeted her master, clearly referring to her earlier defeat at Lin Jing’s hands.

“I’ve already heard about it. It doesn’t matter. Now that you’ve reached the Tenth Stage of Qi Refinement, your future prospects will be vastly different.” The Nascent Soul woman smiled warmly, placing a hand on her disciple’s shoulder. Upon hearing that Chen Lingxi had been selected by the Cavern Heaven, she had rushed here without delay.

“Master, now that I’ve reached the Tenth Stage of Qi Refinement, I will not lose to him again.” Chen Lingxi was brimming with confidence, her aura unrestrained. This monumental breakthrough overshadowed her previous loss to Lin Jing, and she felt invigorated as if her progress with the Hidden Dragon Arts would now be unstoppable.

With her arsenal of talismans and her brief appearance on the Physique Rankings for her Talisman Physique, Chen Lingxi believed her gains from the Cavern Heaven far outstripped everyone else’s.

Even those with the Heavenly God Physique couldn’t compare.

As for Lin Jing… in her mind, he had only been at the Seventh Stage of Qi Refinement before entering the Cavern Heaven. Without a special physique, it would have been nearly impossible for him to break through the pinnacle.

The gap between them from now on would be insurmountable. Just thinking about it filled her with renewed vigor, sweeping away the gloom of her earlier defeat.

“Fellow Daoist Xie, congratulations.” The father of Wang Xiao, another notable cultivator, stepped forward and addressed Chen Lingxi’s master with a smile.

Among those present, three stood out as the most powerful: Wang Xiao’s father, the Nascent Soul woman from the Divine Talisman Sect, and Cao Ziwei from the academy. All three had reached the Great Perfection of the Nascent Soul stage. Even among Nascent Soul cultivators, their strength and background commanded respect, and they addressed each other as equals.

Just as the words left his mouth, Wang Xiao emerged from the Cavern Heaven. Dressed in a gleaming suit of silver armor, his aura was overwhelming. Spiritual energy erupted around him, and behind him loomed the faint silhouette of a deity. He, too, had broken into the Tenth Stage of Qi Refinement!

“Haha, congratulations are in order!” Wang Xiao’s father laughed heartily, realizing that his son had also ascended to the pinnacle.

Wang Xiao nodded to his father and then turned his sharp gaze to Yuan Jing and Ziling’er in the distance. Before entering the Cavern Heaven, even with his Heavenly God Physique, the gap in cultivation realms had given him only a 20% chance of defeating Foundation Establishment swordsmen.

But now, after being tempered in the Cavern Heaven, Wang Xiao’s confidence soared. He was 90% certain he could defeat Yuan Jing and make a name for himself across the Ancient Domain!

“You…” Yuan Jing had waited for days, but seeing the transformation in Wang Xiao, he felt immense pressure. Even his Sword Heart wavered slightly under Wang Xiao’s aura.

As the Cavern Heaven closed, most of the candidates had returned, and no fatalities were reported.

“It seems that only those truly chosen by the Dragon-Phoenix Cavern Heaven, like Wang Xiao and Chen Lingxi, were able to break the pinnacle,” Cao Ziwei noted. The other geniuses, who had entered through happenstance, ultimately lacked the same opportunities.

Even latecomers like Gao Sheng and Xu Zhizhi hadn’t reached the Tenth Stage.

At that moment, as all attention was focused on Chen Lingxi and Wang Xiao, Cao Ziwei’s voice rang out in shock.

“You’ve reached the Tenth Stage as well!”

“No… it’s both of you!”

Another Tenth Stage? The crowd followed Cao Ziwei’s astonished gaze and saw a young man in a white robe stepping out of the Cavern Heaven. Perched on his shoulder was a robed Pine Leaf Mouse.

Boom!

An overwhelming explosion of spiritual energy erupted as Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse simultaneously unleashed their Qi Refinement Tenth Stage true energy. Twenty layers of spiritual energy vortices stacked atop one another, surging into the sky and shocking everyone present.

A cultivator… at the Tenth Stage!

A beast… at the Tenth Stage!

This was… the Beast Tamer!

This duo, who had entered the Dragon-Phoenix Cavern Heaven and were thought to have split their opportunities, had both reached the Tenth Stage!

What was even more shocking was that Lin Jing had entered the Cavern Heaven with the lowest cultivation level among all participants—merely at the Seventh Stage of Qi Refinement, with the weakest foundation.

And yet…

When Chen Lingxi and Wang Xiao exchanged glances with Lin Jing, their hearts skipped a beat, as if they had fallen into an icy abyss, overwhelmed by an indescribable pressure.

What was going on?!

“Ke ke ke…” Hidden within an ancient tree, a pine cone suddenly cracked open with laughter. The Divine Pine’s voice rang out in astonishment.

“I don’t know what happened, but this Longevity Brat actually contracted the Ice Soul Gu… and this Ice Soul Gu…”

“…has reached the Eleventh Stage of Qi Refinement! Its cultivation is higher than theirs!”

As the only one able to see through the seals hiding Lin Jing’s internal state, the Divine Pine was utterly shocked.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 26: Illness
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 26: Illness

Until the Cavern Heaven event concluded, neither Lin Jing nor the Pine Leaf Mouse managed to break through to the Eleventh Stage of Qi Refinement. Instead, the Ice Soul Gu reached that level ahead of them both.

This situation demonstrated that the Ice Soul Gu’s innate talent far exceeded that of Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse.

For someone with the Longevity Body, breaking through remained exceptionally difficult. If Lin Jing had a different physique, he might have already reached the Eleventh or even Twelfth Stage by now. However, opportunities still lay ahead.

Having emerged from the Cavern Heaven, Lin Jing speculated that since he had contracted the Ice Soul Gu and the Pine Leaf Mouse, their eventual breakthroughs to Foundation Establishment would provide him with significant spiritual energy feedback. That energy could potentially push him further in his cultivation.

After stepping out, Lin Jing didn’t bother hiding his cultivation level. He openly displayed his and the Pine Leaf Mouse’s realms, seeing this as an opportunity to draw attention to his training in the Hidden Dragon Arts.

“Even his pet beasts… have reached the Tenth Stage of Qi Refinement?” The surrounding cultivators were astonished, completely unprepared for such a development.

Xu Zhizhi and Gao Sheng stared at Lin Jing in shock. How? How did this guy get to the Tenth Stage?!

Meanwhile, Wang Xiao and Chen Lingxi found their once-dominant auras overshadowed by Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse.

“Who is this boy?”

“Could it be…” Only Gao Sheng seemed to piece something together, gaining a faint understanding of the situation.

Facing the astonished and puzzled crowd, Lin Jing stepped forward and addressed Cao Ziwei, “Chief Examiner, I exhausted myself using secret arts within the mystic realm and am temporarily unable to oversee the remainder of the assessment. I need to return to my cave dwelling to recover.”

Cao Ziwei nodded, noticing Lin Jing’s weakened state. Judging by his and the Pine Leaf Mouse’s cultivation levels, it was clear that they must have used some extraordinary methods to hunt down the numerous Dragon-Phoenix projections.

“Go ahead.”

With Cao Ziwei’s approval, Lin Jing cupped his fists to the crowd, then took to the air and flew back to his cave dwelling. Having gained fame as a Beast Tamer who, along with his contracted beast, had both reached the Tenth Stage, there was no need to continue the assessment.

Upon returning to his cave dwelling, Lin Jing immediately leapt into the furnace, startling the drowsy Dragon Carp within.

Seeing Lin Jing return after several days, the Dragon Carp was both happy and confused.

What’s wrong with my Beast Tamer? Why is he diving into my tank? Doesn’t he know it’s hot?!

“This feels much better.” Immersed in the furnace, Lin Jing instantly felt his body warming up. The Ice Soul Gu’s breakthrough to the Eleventh Stage had fed back an overwhelming amount of ice-attribute spiritual energy. While this greatly enhanced Lin Jing’s cultivation, it also made his Ice Soul Physique even colder.

After days of frequently using icy powers, Lin Jing could no longer tolerate the cold. All he wanted now was to burrow into the warmth of the furnace and escape the freezing environment.
