The Dragon Scales Fire, a top-tier beast flame, was truly remarkable. Even though the Dragon Carp’s cultivation level wasn’t high, its flames were already capable of alleviating the burden of Lin Jing’s Ice Soul Physique.

Lin Jing ended up staying in the Hundred Beast Cauldron for an entire week, falling into a deep sleep and slowly digesting his gains.

As for the Dragon Carp, it soon learned from the Pine Leaf Mouse that it had missed the Cavern Heaven event and that the second contract position had been taken by the Ice Soul Gu. The news hit it like a thunderbolt on a sunny day.

“!!!” The Dragon Carp was utterly devastated. I was supposed to be second!

“…Well,” Lin Jing attempted to console it. Actually, the Ice Soul Gu was technically second, and that’s the only reason I brought you back as the third contract in the first place…

Hearing this, the Dragon Carp’s body turned pale, and a ghostly fire surrounded it. Though it hadn’t yet become a Gu, it began harboring hostility toward the Ice Soul Gu. Taking Lin Jing’s suggestion, it started rigorously training its fire control abilities, determined to prove to the Ice Soul Gu that it was superior.

I am the real Number Two.

Watching the Dragon Carp dedicate itself to cultivation, Lin Jing nodded in satisfaction, feeling like he had accomplished something entirely pointless as a Beast Tamer.

A week later, Lin Jing woke up.

“You’re awake?” A cheerful voice greeted him as soon as he opened his eyes.

“Quite the harvest, huh, kid?”

“Senior Divine Pine?” Lin Jing immediately recognized the voice and asked, “Why have you come to the academy?”

“The Cavern Heaven appeared—how could I not pay attention?!” The Divine Pine replied irritably. “Tenth Stage of Qi Refinement, and you even contracted the Ice Soul Gu beforehand. Not bad at all, kid.”

Lin Jing smiled wryly. “It was a matter of necessity, Senior. Opportunities within the Cavern Heaven are rare; I couldn’t afford to let them slip by.”

“Well done!” The Divine Pine praised him. “The news of you and your contracted beasts breaking through to the Tenth Stage alongside Chen Lingxi and Wang Xiao is already spreading like wildfire, making you famous. If people knew you also contracted an Eleventh Stage Ice Soul Gu… haha, their reactions would be priceless!”

“But it’s better this way. No one knowing about your Ice Soul Gu is a good thing. Always keep a few cards up your sleeve. Don’t worry—unless an immoral Nascent Soul cultivator specializing in spiritual sense forcibly breaks through the seal on your body to probe your secrets, no one will find out about it.”

As the Divine Pine finished speaking, Lin Jing glanced at the back of his hand, where the Fortune Dragon’s color had turned to violet-gold. It had reached the pinnacle of its first stage, just shy of transforming into a golden dragon. His secret art was close to perfection.

“Although you didn’t complete the academy assessment or defeat the Heavenly God Physique in front of everyone, the fact that all three of you reached the Tenth Stage of Qi Refinement in the Cavern Heaven is far more significant than any academy test.”

“The Heavenly God Physique cultivator continued the assessment after emerging from the Cavern Heaven. With his Tenth Stage cultivation, he defeated the Foundation Establishment swordsman and gained widespread fame. But your reputation is no less. Not only did you defeat Chen Lingxi, who also reached the Tenth Stage, but both you and your beasts reached the Tenth Stage together—a feat most can’t even dream of.”

“Now, everyone is debating who is stronger— you or Wang Xiao, the Heavenly God Physique cultivator. Let the rumors spread a little longer. There’s no rush to fight him. Wait until his reputation grows even more; defeating him then will bring you even greater renown. For now, your fame is already enough to fuel your cultivation.”

"Thank you, Senior Divine Pine," Lin Jing said, bowing in gratitude. He could tell that Senior Divine Pine had been guarding him during his recovery.

Divine Pine waved him off. “Enough of that! It looks like someone’s here to see you. This time, I won’t interfere!” With that, the Divine Pine disappeared from the academy, leaving Lin Jing to handle the visitor outside his cave dwelling.

Standing there was a young man in black robes.

“Brother Lin!” Gao Sheng called from outside. “I brought some exquisite dishes made from advanced yang-attribute and fire-attribute spiritual beasts, specially prepared by Xianke Lai’s chefs. How are you feeling? May I come in?”

The door to the cave dwelling opened, and Gao Sheng entered, only to see Lin Jing climbing out of the furnace.

“Brother Lin!” Gao Sheng grinned knowingly. “Congratulations on reaching the Tenth Stage of Qi Refinement! You must’ve relied on that thing’s power in the Cavern Heaven, right?”

“Yes,” Lin Jing admitted, nodding. “Without it, I wouldn’t have been able to take down so many Dragon-Phoenix projections.”

“Lucky you,” Gao Sheng said with envy. “Even though I came ‘well-prepared,’ I was still a step away from the Tenth Stage. This garbage mortal physique of mine holds me back. I can’t wait to reach Foundation Establishment and develop an acquired cultivation physique.”

Lin Jing smiled faintly, and Gao Sheng continued. “Oh, by the way, my grand-uncle sent me a message. He asked me to tell you that the doors of the Demon-Slaying Bureau are always open to you. You don’t need to worry about the Ice Soul Gu. Currently, only my grand-uncle, Sister Feng, and I know about it.

“Even the City Lord of Snow Moon has had his memory of the Ice Soul Gu erased by my grand-uncle. The whole Snow Moon incident has been written off as the work of a rogue practitioner from the Gu God Cult.”

Gao Sheng pulled out a white token and handed it to Lin Jing. “This is a special token from the Demon-Slaying Bureau. With this, even if you use the Ice Soul Gu, you’ll be under official protection and won’t be treated as a demonic cultivator. If you ever face pursuit from righteous cultivators, just show this token.”

“Thank you…” Lin Jing accepted the token with genuine gratitude.

“What are you thanking me for?” Gao Sheng laughed. “Once you and your beasts reach Foundation Establishment, we’ll be teaming up to crack cases for the Demon-Slaying Bureau!” From his storage pouch, Gao Sheng pulled out a table full of dishes and wine.

“You’re just worried that if I use the Ice Soul Gu to solve a case, you’ll get dragged into trouble with me,” Lin Jing retorted dryly.

“…Maybe,” Gao Sheng admitted with a sheepish grin.

“Fine.” Lin Jing, looking at the spread of food and the special token, nodded and said, “Wait until you reach Foundation Establishment.”

Though Lin Jing agreed, he figured his own breakthrough to Foundation Establishment would take quite some time. With his lifespan extended yet again, he planned to spend at least ten years calmly studying at the academy before considering his next step.

“Eat and drink!” Lin Jing grabbed a chicken leg and stuffed it into his mouth—only to gag immediately. He spat the bite out, spraying it all over Gao Sheng’s face.

“What is this? It’s so disgusting!” Lin Jing exclaimed.

“Huh?” Gao Sheng wasn’t angry but rather stunned. “That’s impossible! I spent a fortune at Xianke Lai to have their top chef prepare this—it’s guaranteed to suppress the effects of the Ice Soul Gu!”

Gao Sheng picked up a chicken leg and took a bite. “It’s not that bad.”

Lin Jing, reluctantly taking another bite, gagged again and spat it out. It tasted unbearable, and he couldn’t swallow it.

“I must be sick,” Lin Jing said, feeling nauseous. “Cold and heat really don’t mix well…”

“Oh no!” Gao Sheng suddenly looked regretful. “I think I know what’s happening. You’ve been exposed to the taste of Dragon Liver and Phoenix Marrow too many times in the Cavern Heaven, and now your sense of taste has mutated! That flavor has completely warped your palate!”

Gao Sheng sighed. “I grew up eating delicacies, so I’m less affected. But you… well…”

Lin Jing’s vision darkened. The Cavern Heaven has side effects like this too?! Was the Pine Leaf Mouse suffering the same problem?

Although Lin Jing could sustain himself through breatharian techniques now, eating delicious food was one of life’s great pleasures, and he couldn’t imagine giving it up!

“Is Brother Lin here?”

A voice interrupted his thoughts.

“This is Wang Xiao of the Wang family.”

Lin Jing sighed. While he had planned to let Wang Xiao’s reputation grow before challenging him, it seemed Wang Xiao couldn’t let go of the pressure he had felt during their brief encounter. He had come here to issue a challenge.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 27: Suppressing the Divine Physique
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 27: Suppressing the Divine Physique

“Wang Xiao? What’s that guy doing here?”

Inside the cave, Gao Sheng’s expression turned sour upon hearing the shout from outside. At the moment, he was deepening his bond with Lin Jing, and Wang Xiao’s arrival was truly untimely.

“I’ll go take a look,” Lin Jing said, desperate for an excuse to escape the unpalatable food in front of him.

Outside the cave, Wang Xiao saw Lin Jing emerge and immediately opened his mouth arrogantly, his divine eyes flashing: “Lin Jing, three days from now, at the peak of Flying Dragon Mountain—do you dare face me in battle?”

Lin Jing heard this and shook his head. “Go back. I’m not in the mood for this right now.”

“What’s this? Are you scared?” Wang Xiao frowned. Lin Jing’s fame had skyrocketed recently, and if rumors of his defeat spread, it would tarnish his reputation. After all, Lin Jing had even defeated a Foundation Establishment sword cultivator from the Heavenly Sword Gate—if he was afraid, it would be understandable.

“Refusing to fight someone of the same level doesn’t sound great either if the word gets out,” Wang Xiao added, provoking him further.

“You’re really something…” Lin Jing’s patience had reached its limit. “Fine, let me send you on your way.”

“Spiritual Threads?”

Wang Xiao sneered at the sight. This technique, forming threads of Qi, was similar to controlling objects in the air—it wasn’t even a proper spell. It was mainly used by mechanists and puppet masters to manipulate their creations. Lin Jing intended to use such a method against him?

Laughable.

“Thunder…” Wang Xiao was about to counter with his domineering thunder-based techniques when Lin Jing vanished without a trace. His speed was so fast that Wang Xiao didn’t even have time to react.

By the time Wang Xiao realized it, he felt a chill at the back of his neck. Lin Jing was already standing behind him, his palm aimed at the back of Wang Xiao’s neck.

Under Lin Jing’s feet—or rather, all around the area outside the cave—there were scattered red leaves blending perfectly with the surrounding maple leaves. One of the red leaves emanated faint spatial ripples.

Perched on Lin Jing’s shoulder, the Pine Leaf Mouse yawned lazily as the spatial fluctuations subsided. Wang Xiao’s entire body was now covered in frost.

“You… Spatial Arts!” Wang Xiao was utterly shocked. The technique that left him no time to react was undoubtedly a spatial ability. This Beast Tamer actually had mastery over such a powerful art!

Wang Xiao’s eyes widened as he tried to use his unparalleled Heavenly God Qi of the Qi Refinement Tenth Stage to break through the frost. However, the chilling aura from the spiritual threads piercing his body was overwhelming, even freezing his Qi entirely.

What was even more terrifying was that as the threads moved within him, Wang Xiao found himself unable to control his body. Instead, his movements were dictated by Lin Jing’s finger gestures, and his body unwillingly began walking down the mountain.

He couldn’t understand it at all—how could this basic technique used by puppet masters and mechanists control even someone like him, a Divine Physique cultivator?

“Leave already,” Lin Jing said, dismissing him. “I’m not fighting you because I consider how hard it was for you to build your reputation. You should focus on leveraging your fame and advancing your strength with the Hidden Dragon Arts before challenging me again.”

For Lin Jing, even if he and his Pine Leaf Mouse, both at the Qi Refinement Tenth Stage, managed to defeat this Heavenly God Physique, it wouldn’t bring much of a boost to his own reputation. It was better to wait until Wang Xiao had achieved greater renown before defeating him for maximum impact.

In just moments, Lin Jing sent Wang Xiao down the mountain. As the spiritual threads withdrew, Lin Jing smirked to himself. Although the threads were a crude imitation of the Ice Soul Gu’s power, their combination with spatial teleportation had still caught Wang Xiao off guard.

At the base of the mountain, Wang Xiao turned his stiff neck to glance back, deeply shaken. He had always thought Lin Jing’s mechanical creations were limited to crafting weapons from special materials. Who could have imagined that the spiritual thread technique was powerful enough to even control a Divine Physique?

And the frost… Wang Xiao’s mind raced as he recalled the pressure he had felt back when Lin Jing emerged from the Cavern Heaven with the Ice Soul Gu.

“I’ve always thought I didn’t have any special constitution, but now it seems I’ve been hiding a deeply concealed cold attribute physique. Could it be… that I possess an Ice Divine Physique?”

“You will come back…” The Pine Leaf Mouse lazily gave up its grudge match against Wang Xiao, knowing that even with the aid of magical treasures, it couldn’t match Lin Jing, let alone the Ice Soul Gu’s power.

Wang Xiao slowly walked away, planning to enter closed-door cultivation. He resolved to master his techniques and improve his abilities before seeking another challenge.

The scene of Lin Jing suppressing the Divine Physique was observed in its entirety by Gao Sheng, who was inside the cave. He was astonished beyond words. When Lin Jing returned to the cave, Gao Sheng immediately raised a toast.

“I’m impressed,” Gao Sheng said sincerely.

“It’s just the Ice Soul Gu doing the work,” Lin Jing replied modestly.

“But still,” Gao Sheng insisted, “not just anyone can control the Ice Soul Gu. The Demon-Slaying Bureau has obtained it a few times, but even there, only a select few can handle it.”

“Let’s not talk about that,” Lin Jing said with a bitter smile. “Tell me, what should I do about this situation?”

“Are you really unable to eat at all? Perhaps time will smooth things out…” Gao Sheng suggested.

“Or,” Gao Sheng’s eyes suddenly lit up with an idea, “why don’t you indulge a bit and only eat dishes of the highest quality, like Dragon Liver and Phoenix Marrow?”

“Too expensive,” Lin Jing replied decisively.

“Oh, right!” Gao Sheng suddenly remembered something. “Before we left the Cavern Heaven, Mr. Cao mentioned that anyone who breaks through to the extreme within the Cavern Heaven can use the academy’s Cavern Heaven free of charge for a year!”

"I don’t know how you and the Pine Leaf Mouse are being accounted for. If you’re considered separately and assigned two caves, you could rent one out. Collect decades’ worth of rent upfront, and voilà—you’d have the money you need," Gao Sheng suggested.

“Hm?” Lin Jing tilted his head, considering the idea.

...

After Gao Sheng left, Lin Jing sat cross-legged in his cave, focusing inward to assess his current cultivation state. If the Qi Refinement Tenth Stage were divided further into early, middle, and late phases, then the Pine Leaf Mouse would clearly be in the late phase, while Lin Jing himself was currently in the middle phase.

“Next up, I’ll refine my alchemy skills and figure out how to upgrade the bloodline levels of both the Pine Leaf Mouse and the Dragon Carp!” Lin Jing resolved.

The recent journey through the Dragon-Phoenix Cavern Heaven had given him a deeper understanding of how crucial physique and bloodline talents were.

He opened his eyes, deciding to enter another period of closed-door cultivation. The Pine Leaf Mouse had the same idea—it had nearly depleted its carefully accumulated resources inside the Cavern Heaven and wanted to stockpile more for its sense of security.

“Come here.” Lin Jing reached into the depths of his cave and pulled out the Dragon Carp. “You seem like you’re in a bad mood?”

“Blub blub~ (It’s just the Ice Soul Gu’s credit~),” the Dragon Carp’s mouth opened and closed as it made disgruntled sounds.

Lin Jing couldn’t help but laugh. “You’re destined to soar in the nine heavens as a true dragon. Why are you jealous of a mere spider?”

“And to think you’re supposed to be a proud male carp,” he teased.

As he spoke, Lin Jing extended his palm, releasing the aura of Hidden Dragon Fortune.

“Alright. This time, our trip to the Cavern Heaven was largely thanks to the thousand-plus pills you helped me refine. Now, let me enhance your potential again.”

“When the day comes that you transform into a dragon, it will also be the day you ascend to the nine heavens and become invincible.”

The Hidden Dragon Arts were approaching the stage of minor mastery, and Lin Jing wasn’t stingy with his fortune power. Once again, he used it to enhance the Dragon Carp’s abilities.

Seeing how much care the Beast Tamer had for it, the Dragon Carp’s mood lifted, and it regained its cheerfulness.

“Enhancement complete. Next, I’ll need to start refining second-tier pills.”

The Dragon Carp’s smile instantly disappeared. Refining pills again?!

Its entire body already reeked of the scent of medicinal herbs, and no matter what it tried, it couldn’t get rid of it.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 28: Teaching the Way
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 28: Teaching the Way

The news of the Cavern Heaven’s emergence at Hidden Dragon Academy, where three students had advanced to the Qi Refinement Tenth Stage, continued to spread. Lin Jing’s mastery of the Hidden Dragon Arts had reached minor completion, transforming his Fortune Qi into a golden young dragon.

However, while reaching the Qi Refinement Tenth Stage was exceedingly rare, it didn’t compare to the unparalleled accomplishments of figures like the Ancient Imperial Prince. Over the centuries, many geniuses had reached this stage through various fortuitous encounters, so Lin Jing’s achievements didn’t earn him widespread fame across the Ancient Celestial Kingdom as he had hoped.

Most of Lin Jing’s reputation came from discussions among the influential families and powerful cultivators backing the academy’s students. While these discussions focused on the quality of his achievements, his fame was mostly limited to the Ancient Domain. Beyond that, his recognition dropped significantly.

Nowadays, Lin Jing regularly helped the Dragon Carp unlock its potential, enabling it to grow rapidly. With the combination of Dragon Scales Fire and Ghostfire, its combat strength was exceptional, though the mixed use of the two flames was tricky and best avoided.

In terms of physical toughness, Lin Jing had become ferocious. His defensive capabilities now rivaled those of the Snow Plains Body Cultivators he had encountered earlier, making him capable of withstanding a Spirit Bean Bomb head-on.

As for alchemy, thanks to the Dragon Carp’s exceptional flames and its cauldron-like qualities, Lin Jing’s fire control proficiency soared to the level of an alchemy genius. While his success rate in refining Beast Blood Pills was still low, it was enough to qualify him as a second-tier alchemist. These achievements were remarkable for a Dragon Carp widely considered weak and useless before its ascension through the Dragon Gate.

One day, a voice echoed throughout the academy:

“I, Cao Ziwei, will be holding a lecture on Tianyi Mountain in three days. The topic will be Treasure-Hunting Techniques. All who have initiated the Hidden Dragon Arts are welcome to attend.”

This was the first lecture Lin Jing would attend at Hidden Dragon Academy. Unfortunately, the topic wasn’t Cao Ziwei’s famed “Prosperous Wife Techniques,” much to the disappointment of many academy students.

Still, the four words “Treasure-Hunting Techniques” carried undeniable allure. Lin Jing’s curiosity was piqued, and he decided to attend the lecture in three days.

...

Tianyi Mountain was soon shrouded in a massive barrier of radiant light that covered the entire area.

Three days later, a large number of academy students arrived to attend the lecture, including those in the Qi Refinement and Foundation Establishment stages. However, not a single Golden Core cultivator was present.

Each cultivator who entered the barrier disappeared instantly, their presence vanishing as if they were transported to another space.

When Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse arrived, they stepped through the barrier and found themselves inside a room filled with white light. The space was entirely bare, except for a massive whirlpool of spiritual energy suspended in midair, projecting the image of Cao Ziwei sitting cross-legged.

“A familiar teaching method,” Lin Jing murmured as he sat cross-legged on the ground, calming his mind and preparing to listen.

The Pine Leaf Mouse slid off his shoulder, lying flat on the ground and staring at the ceiling, utterly confused as to why Lin Jing had dragged it here.

“Two people listening to the lecture for the price of one—it’s more economical,” Lin Jing explained for the umpteenth time.

Moments later, Cao Ziwei opened his eyes and said, “The time has come. Today’s lecture will cover Treasure-Hunting Techniques.”

“Treasure-Hunting Techniques and Qi Gazing Techniques share many similarities, as both rely on resonating with the world’s energy to perform their respective arts.”

“Furthermore, there are various types of Treasure-Hunting Techniques. Today, I won’t be teaching you how to find rare spiritual plants or minerals—the traditional treasures. Instead, we’ll focus on ancient treasures .”

“Whether during the era of the Immortal Palaces or the era of the Immortal Alliances, the continent has seen many great wars, leaving countless ancient treasures buried across the land. For instance, the Air Domain is widely known as the region with the most ancient relics and secret realms from the Celestial Kingdom era, hiding innumerable ancient treasures and opportunities.”

“But beyond the Air Domain, every city has its own glorious past. What might seem like an ordinary mortal village today could very well have been the closed-door cultivation site of a minor ancient power in the past.”

“Treasure-Hunting Techniques will teach you how to explore, identify, and excavate these treasures. And before you ask, no, there will be no questions allowed after the lecture. You’ll have to figure out any uncertainties yourself afterward.”

A voice suddenly emerged from the void:

“Mr. Cao, treasure hunting sounds fascinating. If one were to find an ancient artifact left by a minor power, it could change one’s fate entirely! But… does the academy also teach practical techniques like city management?”

Cao Ziwei replied, “Any ancient artifact with significant power would have long been discovered and taken. Do you think such treasures would be left for you? The entry-level treasure-hunting techniques I’m teaching require talent and luck. By the time you’ve mastered them, you’ll have at least unearthed some historically valuable items, if not practical treasures.”

“These ancient relics can then be placed in museums or cultural halls within the cities you govern, enhancing their historical and cultural depth. Isn’t that also a form of effective city management?”

The students: “…”

Lin Jing: “…So, basically, a history museum?”

The academy’s students knew full well that the academy’s teachings rarely involved straightforward techniques. But since they were already here, many decided to learn the skill patiently, hoping it might improve their future prospects as officials.

For three consecutive days, the students listened to the lecture in a daze. Compared to other techniques that involved channeling Qi to perform spells, treasure-hunting techniques were incredibly abstract. They required not only gathering spiritual energy into one’s eyes using a unique method but also sensing the energy of the heavens and the earth while feeling the “pulse” of history. Only by doing so could one perceive the faint light of ancient treasures amidst the passage of time.

Cao Ziwei concluded, “For the next ten days, you can practice treasure-hunting techniques here. There’s no need to force results. If you’re not interested, you may leave now. If, after a few days of practice, you find that you lack the talent for it, you’re free to leave then as well.”

“In this place, I have hidden many ancient relics. If you can find them using the Treasure-Hunting Technique, they will be yours to keep.”

With that, the lecture concluded. The students discovered that they had been transported from their small, independent spaces to a massive mountain range. Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse were no exception. This mountain range was even larger than the Beast Taming Sect’s headquarters, containing over a hundred sprawling peaks. Who knew how many ancient treasures were hidden here?

Lin Jing sat cross-legged on a boulder, half-closing his eyes as he tried to enter a meditative state and sense the so-called “pulse of history.” After a long time, he sighed in frustration. He hadn’t sensed anything—not even a flicker—and his eyes were beginning to ache from the strain. Not a single glimmer of ancient treasure light was in sight.

“Looks like I don’t have the talent for this,” he muttered, sighing again. He wasn’t keen on wasting his Hidden Dragon Fortune to cultivate a technique for which he had no aptitude.

“How about you? Did you see any glimmers of ancient treasure light?” he asked the Pine Leaf Mouse, which had been sitting cross-legged on his shoulder. Lin Jing had made sure it participated in the lecture alongside him, though he wasn’t sure how much potential it had for treasure hunting.

“Uh…” The Pine Leaf Mouse remained utterly silent, its mood visibly uneasy. It had a lingering sense that something had gone terribly wrong during its practice of the Treasure-Hunting Technique.

“Hmm?” Lin Jing looked at it curiously.

The Pine Leaf Mouse hesitated before cautiously asking, “This Treasure-Hunting Technique… are you sure spirit beasts can even learn it?”

“Cao Ziwei clearly said that the technique isn’t limited by race—it wasn’t created by humans in the first place,” Lin Jing replied. Hearing this, the Pine Leaf Mouse nodded in acknowledgment.

It rubbed its eyes, which glimmered with a unique brilliance. Something definitely wasn’t right. Everywhere it looked, the mountains were bursting with ancient treasure light. The sheer number of glimmers made its Glazed Immortal Eyes feel like they were about to go blind.

Especially on one far-off mountain peak. Compared to the faint light scattered across other areas, the radiance from that peak was so intense, it was like a blazing star in the night sky. The brilliance was nearly overwhelming.

“Uh…” The Pine Leaf Mouse hesitated before weakly admitting, “I think I saw… maybe a few hundred beams of light.”

Lin Jing fell silent.

“…Are you a hoarding mouse or a treasure-hunting mouse? A few hundred beams of light? Seriously?”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 29: Treasure-Hunting Mouse
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 29: Treasure-Hunting Mouse

“Could it be the blessing of the Glazed Immortal Eyes?”

The treasure-hunting technique taught by Mr. Cao still involved "seeing" with one's eyes.

Thus, it was possible that a treasure-hunting talent was related to some kind of ocular ability.

After the Pine Leaf Mouse mutated into a rare beast, its unique ability was also eye-related. This was Lin Jing's best guess.

"Take me to the nearest spot," he said after some contemplation, grabbing the Pine Leaf Mouse. Before long, the two arrived at the foot of a mountain.

The Pine Leaf Mouse pointed at a broken, ordinary-looking tree, indicating that a beam of ancient treasure light had originated from there.

"Dig," Lin Jing instructed, feeling quite fond of this treasure-hunting activity. After digging for about three or four meters, they unearthed a chamber pot with no spiritual energy fluctuations.

The chamber pot was carved with dragon and phoenix motifs, giving it a mysterious and ancient appearance. It seemed extremely old.

"Is this what Mr. Cao referred to as the ancient artifact placed here?" He began to suspect that the remaining antiques were all of a similar nature.

"Sigh..." The Pine Leaf Mouse gestured toward another direction. They quickly headed there and unearthed a stone stele.

Just as Lin Jing thought they might have found something valuable, the text engraved on the stele left him speechless. It was a tombstone.

[Grave of Immortal Li Tiangang, a cultivator during the Qi Refinement Stage.]

[He perished from severe injuries after slaying a demonic pig that attacked the Li family village.]

Once again, it was a tombstone without any spiritual energy.

“May the deceased rest in peace. My apologies for disturbing you, Senior,” Lin Jing muttered as he buried it back again. No wonder Mr. Cao had so generously said that they were free to take whatever ancient items they found.

Honestly, even if it were gifted daily, Lin Jing wasn’t particularly interested.

"Are all these beams of ancient treasure light the same?"

The Pine Leaf Mouse shrugged, indicating that most of the beams were indeed faint and unremarkable—except for a particularly dazzling one in the distance.

"Let’s leave these ordinary relics to others. Let’s go directly to the one you mentioned," Lin Jing said. With those words, he rose into the air with Qi Manipulation, soaring toward the mountain peak where the most brilliant beam of treasure light shone, guided by the Pine Leaf Mouse.

As he flew, Lin Jing noticed that many other cultivators' talents in treasure hunting were better than his. Many had already mastered the technique.

However, they too were unearthing piles of useless junk, with no real value.

“This stone sword…” Outside a mountain cave, Lin Jing happened to encounter Yuan Jing, who held an ancient stone sword. Just as Yuan Jing imbued it with sword intent, the sword crumbled into dust.

The young swordsman’s face darkened with disappointment at the so-called ancient treasure.

“Lin Jing?” Yuan Jing also noticed him. The two exchanged polite nods before continuing on their own paths. After flying for nearly an hour, Lin Jing finally arrived at the destination.

Here, the ancient treasure light was particularly dazzling—obviously different from the other relics they had encountered. Lin Jing took a deep breath and was about to start digging when a powerful aura suddenly descended.

“Young man, I discovered this first,” said a voice.

The newcomer was a youth with three eyes, clad in an elegant blue-green robe, exuding an aura of sophistication like an immortal descended to the mortal world.

“Late Foundation Establishment!” Lin Jing observed. This person was likely a senior student. Clearly, he had also noticed the unusual brilliance of the treasure light here.

“We arrived first,” Lin Jing replied firmly. Although the treasure hadn’t been unearthed yet, he had no intention of giving it up.

Apart from the two eyes on his face, the youth had a third eye on his forehead, suggesting some kind of special constitution.

“Qi Refinement Stage 10…” The three-eyed youth remarked, “I’ve been in seclusion for so long, and to my surprise, this year’s academy freshmen are quite impressive—not only reaching the tenth stage of Qi Refinement themselves but even their companion beasts are at the same level.”

“This item is ominous. Would you consider giving it to me? With your cultivation, obtaining it may bring more harm than good,” the youth added.

“Senior, I happen to like ominous things,” Lin Jing replied, unmoved.

Seeing that Lin Jing had no intention of yielding, the three-eyed youth shook his head and said, “Very well, then. You can have it. My name is Liu Yu. Consider this a friendly gesture. If you’re interested, you can seek me out after this area closes. The ‘Eye Alliance’ I’ve established is currently recruiting. It’s not a sect but a temporary organization among academy students, where members help each other. Unlike me, others might not be so easygoing. Joining a group will make it easier to cultivate the Hidden Dragon Arts, and the results will be much better.”

With that, he glanced into the distance and flew away.

Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse had expected a fight over the treasure but, seeing Liu Yu leave so easily, they resumed digging without hesitation.

“Liu Yu, the three-eyed cultivator—he’s ranked thirty-first on the Human Ranking of the Genius Rankings.” While digging, Lin Jing recalled the man’s identity. If they had truly fought, unless he unsealed the Ice Soul Gu, there was no way he and the Pine Leaf Mouse could defeat Liu Yu on their own.

“This guy also has an exceptional treasure-hunting talent!”

“But why did he say this item was ominous?”

As they continued digging, they finally unearthed the ancient treasure at this location. It turned out to be an old wooden box. However, the so-called treasure wasn’t the wooden box itself, but the item inside!

At the urging of the Pine Leaf Mouse, Lin Jing opened the wooden box. Inside was a tattered, blood-stained marriage certificate!

The certificate was badly damaged, with most of the text illegible. Only a faintly visible "Double Happiness" character remained. Despite its age, the certificate still emanated spiritual energy—though this energy was extremely cold, with a chilling aura reminiscent of Ghostfire.

“Mr. Cao, are you there?” Lin Jing, holding the wooden box, fell into deep thought and called for Mr. Cao.

“Hahaha…” Moments later, a projection of Mr. Cao materialized in the air. He glanced at Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse and said, “I didn’t expect you two to display such talent in treasure hunting.”

“I thought the one with the greatest talent would be that three-eyed Liu Yu. Yet, it turns out to be you two. If you were both at the Qi Refinement stage, I doubt even he could have discovered this wooden box.”

Mr. Cao studied Lin Jing and then looked at the Pine Leaf Mouse. Puzzled, he asked, “Could it be that this little one has the bloodline of the Treasure-Hunting Mouse? After all, the treasure-hunting technique itself was originally created by the ancient demonic Treasure-Hunting Mouse Clan and later taught to their followers.”

“Eh?” The Pine Leaf Mouse pointed at itself in confusion. “Me?”

Lin Jing nodded but thought it was more likely due to the Glazed Immortal Eyes. He didn’t want to delve too deeply into the matter and instead lifted the wooden box to ask Mr. Cao, “Mr. Cao, what is this? Why is it so different from the other ancient items? Is this something you placed here as well?”

Mr. Cao chuckled. “Indeed, I placed three spiritually-infused ancient treasures here for destined students to find. This marriage certificate is one of them.”

“This certificate was something I stumbled upon by chance. Its origin is unknown, but I can roughly infer its backstory… In ancient times, wars were frequent. This certificate exudes a chilling and resentful energy, likely because a woman’s fiancé died in battle before they could be married. The lingering grief and resentment condensed into this object.”

Lin Jing fell silent. No wonder Liu Yu had called it ominous—it truly was. With his powerful ocular abilities, Liu Yu must have sensed it.

“Mr. Cao… what’s the purpose of this marriage certificate?” Lin Jing asked.

Mr. Cao looked up at the sky and replied, “It doesn’t have much use. I just tossed some useless ancient items from my storage bag here to help you all practice treasure-hunting techniques. If you don’t like it, feel free to discard it.”

“However, if you insist on finding a use for it, the energy within this certificate is extremely cold and filled with resentment. For ordinary people, possessing it would bring misfortune and illness. But for cultivators, it can be used to temper one’s resistance to such energy! In the future, if you encounter demonic cultivators from the Yin Corpse Sect or the Nether Hall, this could help you better handle their attacks.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 30: The Flow of Time
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 30: The Flow of Time

Lin Jing nodded. The chilling spiritual energy from the marriage certificate was indeed potent. Had he not reached the tenth stage of Qi Refinement and possessed an ice-attribute physique, merely touching the item would have caused him harm.

He carefully stored the marriage certificate in his storage bag. While the item wasn’t highly useful, it did contain spiritual energy. If he simply discarded it, this lesson would yield no gains at all.

After storing the marriage certificate, Lin Jing turned to the Pine Leaf Mouse. Although it had talent, its treasure-hunting technique was still at a beginner’s level. It had only located one of the three spiritually infused ancient treasures that Mr. Cao had placed in the area. The other two remained undiscovered.

However, realizing that the highest-quality ancient treasure was merely an ominous marriage certificate, Lin Jing no longer held high expectations for the other two items.

“Mr. Cao, I recall you once said that anyone who achieves an extreme breakthrough in the Dragon-Phoenix Cavern Heaven would gain sixty years of access to an academy cave dwelling. Both the Pine Leaf Mouse and I broke through to extremes within the cavern. Does that mean we receive 120 years of access, or two cave dwellings for sixty years each?” Lin Jing asked, remembering a crucial matter as he addressed Mr. Cao.

Hearing this, Mr. Cao chuckled. “You’re a sly one, always looking for a win-win. By logic, you and this squirrel spirit should be considered a single entity…”

Lin Jing feigned a pained expression. “Sir, my sect is in decline, and my family is impoverished…”

“The cave dwelling on the adjacent mountain peak is currently unoccupied. I’ll grant you access to it as well,” Mr. Cao said with a wave of his hand. “Use it wisely.”

In the blink of an eye, a few days passed. Whether the other two spiritually infused treasures had been found by others or the Pine Leaf Mouse’s treasure-hunting skill failed to locate them, Lin Jing ended the course with the ancient marriage certificate as his sole harvest.

Over these days, Lin Jing had also worked hard to cultivate the treasure-hunting technique. Unfortunately, his talent in this area was lacking. After several days of effort, he could only find three or five pieces of junk. The gap between him and the Pine Leaf Mouse’s innate ability was vast, leading Lin Jing to ultimately give up on mastering the technique.

However, his greatest gain was undoubtedly the additional cave dwelling. After considering it, Lin Jing decided not to rent it out. Instead, he planned to transform it into a spiritual field, expanding its cultivation area to grow medicinal ingredients for pill refining and for the Pine Leaf Mouse to use.

As for his current cave dwelling, he decided to remodel it into a pill refinement chamber, which he would share with the Flame Dragon Carp.

By dividing their activities into separate spaces, they could avoid competing for spiritual energy, allowing for greater efficiency.

“The Hidden Dragon Arts have finally reached minor mastery…” Returning to his cave dwelling, Lin Jing glanced at the back of his hand, where a golden infant dragon had fully formed. This symbolized his ability to freely access all levels of the academy’s Scripture Pavilion, enabling him to learn and explore much more knowledge.

“Next, I’ll need to face the challenge of Foundation Establishment.”

“However, I have plenty of time. Staying in the Qi Refinement stage for three or five more years won’t affect me in the slightest.”

“A skyscraper is built from the ground up. I should use this time advantage to learn as much as possible and prepare for a more perfect Foundation Establishment.” Lin Jing’s gaze was firm. He pushed aside his impatience and calmed his heart as much as possible.

Excessive impatience was incompatible with his Longevity Body, which would be detrimental to his cultivation.

Time flowed on. In the blink of an eye, three years passed.

During these three years, Lin Jing remained reclusive. Besides continuing to sell Mechanical Bamboo Rifles to various cities in the Ancient Domain, he dedicated himself to refining pills and attending a few academy courses.

Meanwhile, his peers had all advanced to the Foundation Establishment stage. Xu Zhizhi, Gao Sheng, Chen Lingxi, and Wang Xiao had all achieved breakthroughs, leaving Lin Jing as the only one still lingering in the Qi Refinement stage. Many were puzzled as to why this genius, who had also reached the tenth stage of Qi Refinement, had yet to advance.

During this time, Wang Xiao repeatedly challenged Lin Jing, only to be turned away by the Flame Dragon Carp. Eventually, before Wang Xiao’s own Foundation Establishment, he gave up on his attempts entirely.

As for Gao Sheng, he invited Lin Jing to join him on some case investigations after noticing that even the Flame Dragon Carp was preparing for Foundation Establishment.

Inside the cave dwelling, surrounded by Danxia mists and soaring dragon flames, the Flame Dragon Carp opened its eyes. From the Hundred Beast Cauldron, a fiery pine leaf floated out, followed by the carp spitting out a red pill.

“It’s finally a success,” Lin Jing murmured.

Few people knew that over the past eight years, the Flame Dragon Carp had devoted much of its time to studying pill refinement. It even used its accumulated Hidden Dragon Fortune to simulate and improve pill refinement techniques while cultivating spiritual plants with the Pine Leaf Mouse.

Although the carp and the mouse couldn’t yet be called eighth-grade alchemists, they had reached a point where they could occasionally produce eighth-grade pills. However, this wasn’t their smallest gain over the eight years.

Their greatest achievement was the creation of entirely new pills by integrating the spiritual plants cultivated by the Pine Leaf Mouse, modifying and innovating pill recipes using fortune simulation and experimentation.

Currently, Lin Jing held in his hand a Rising Dragon Pill, refined from dragon-attribute beast fire, Purple Dragonbone Flower, and Dragon Blood as its core ingredients. It was a third-grade pill.

“According to pharmacological calculations, this pill should be able to grant any demonic beast a high-purity dragon bloodline, transforming them into a Dragon-Blooded Demonic Beast! If the beast already has dragon blood, this pill can significantly enhance the quality of its bloodline. However, to verify its effects and ensure it’s non-toxic, we’ll need to test it on a white mouse first.”

Lin Jing glanced at the exhausted Flame Dragon Carp. He couldn’t possibly have it test the pill.

The third-grade pill was extremely potent, and if something went wrong, the consequences could be dire. The current results of the Rising Dragon Pill were still based on theoretical calculations.

“Who would’ve thought that even after synthesizing spiritual plants without the need for white mice, pills refined from these plants would still require testing on white mice,” Lin Jing muttered.

He stepped out of his cave dwelling, having been cooped up for three months without sunlight to refine this single Rising Dragon Pill. Now that it was finally complete, he couldn’t wait to verify its effects.

“Found it!” Lin Jing laughed after a short walk, catching sight of a small green caterpillar nibbling on a leaf. Thanks to prolonged exposure to spiritual energy, this little insect had even reached the first stage of Qi Refinement.

“You’ll do. Old Master here will grant you a stroke of fortune,” he said, adopting an elder’s tone.

“If you survive, I’ll make you my official pill-testing assistant.”

Grabbing the small caterpillar in one hand, Lin Jing stuffed the Rising Dragon Pill into its mouth despite the creature’s terrified gaze. Worried it might struggle to digest the pill, Lin Jing also shoved a second, golden pill into its mouth.

The second pill, crafted from golden spiritual rice, resolved the issue of losing rationality after consuming golden rice. Instead, it greatly enhanced digestion. Lin Jing had named it the Devouring Pill.

“Chirp—!” After swallowing the Devouring Pill and Rising Dragon Pill, the caterpillar’s eyes rolled back, and it nearly passed out. However, a miraculous transformation soon unfolded.

“Roar!!”

The once-insignificant caterpillar began to emit an intense dragon’s might. Dragon-shaped shadows swirled around its body as its cultivation skyrocketed: Qi Refinement Stage 2, Stage 3, Stage 4... it continued rising until it reached the ninth stage of Qi Refinement, where it finally stabilized.

At the same time, its body glowed with brilliant white light. Under this radiance, the caterpillar’s form expanded, its body sprouting green scales. Although it remained roughly the size of a human forearm and retained its caterpillar-like shape, its aura had become extraordinary, far surpassing other demonic beasts of the same level!

“Whew!!” The caterpillar, now with awakened intelligence, cast a panicked yet ferocious gaze at Lin Jing. Without hesitation, it opened its mouth and unleashed a blast of green dragon breath before turning to flee.

Boom!

The dragon breath struck Lin Jing directly. He didn’t dodge and instead emerged from the smoke, his clothes slightly disheveled but his eyes burning with excitement.

“The power of this dragon breath... it’s already stronger than a Spirit Bean Bomb,” Lin Jing said with a chuckle. “And this was just a caterpillar mutation with no prior training. If a powerful demonic beast consumed the pill, the effects would likely be even greater, and their strength wouldn’t fall short of the projections in the Dragon-Phoenix Cavern Heaven.”

“However… it probably won’t be effective for the Flame Dragon Carp. Its bloodline strength has already been enhanced too much through the Hidden Dragon Arts.”

Still, that didn’t matter. What truly excited Lin Jing was that the Rising Dragon Pill was the precursor to his envisioned Longevity Foundation Pill. The overlapping alchemy techniques involved meant that if he could refine the Rising Dragon Pill, he was likely on the verge of being able to refine his specialized Foundation Pill as well.

Afterward, Lin Jing looked at his own hand. The golden fortune dragon on the back of his hand, a product of the Hidden Dragon Arts, hadn’t revived.

“It’s been eight years. It seems its revival has been forgotten.”

He glanced at the Pine Leaf Mouse, thinking aloud, “In the Beast Taming Sect, they have similar concoctions for enhancing spirit plants into medicinal treasures. I wonder how the market for this would look.”

“Let’s first test it within the academy,” he decided.

“I’ll let the students see the strength of a Dragon-Blooded Carp cultivated through the Hidden Dragon Arts. Then, I’ll promote the Rising Dragon Pill to them…”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 31: Three Years
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 31: Three Years

The little green caterpillar scurried off, but Lin Jing paid it no mind—it was heading straight toward the Pine Leaf Mouse’s cave dwelling.

“Let’s find him first,” Lin Jing thought to himself as his body floated into the air, flying toward a nearby mountain peak. Before long, he landed in front of a cave dwelling and called out loudly, “Brother Xu, are you home?”

Boom.

The stone door of the cave opened, and a young man in a scholar’s robe stepped out, smiling. “What a rare visitor! Brother Lin, it’s been three years since you’ve left your cave, hasn’t it?”

“I haven’t missed a single lecture by Mr. Zhu, Mr. Ji, or Mr. Lü,” Lin Jing replied with a smile.

After Mr. Cao’s lecture three years ago, these three instructors had also each held their own classes:

Both Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse had diligently attended these classes.

Many speculated that Lin Jing was aiming to cultivate an exceptionally powerful acquired physique. Even with his solid foundation, success wasn’t guaranteed, which could explain his hesitation.

“These past three years, I’ve been cultivating a new companion beast—a Dragon-Blooded Beast!” Lin Jing began.

“It has already reached the ninth stage of Qi Refinement but hasn’t formed a contract yet. I’d like to have it spar with the academy’s geniuses. Brother Xu, I assume you have contact information for this year’s candidates, correct? Could you notify them and see who’s interested in a match?”

“Huh?”

Xu Zhizhi was stunned. He asked incredulously, “You’ve spent the past three years cultivating a new companion beast???”

Lin Jing nodded.

“So you’ve been raising a second companion beast… but using the Hidden Dragon Arts for this? That’s far too extravagant! I’ve only ever used my Hidden Dragon Fortune to push the boundaries of my spell techniques,” Xu Zhizhi remarked, still in disbelief.

“Brother Xu, just help me pass the word. I’ll make it worth your while,” Lin Jing said with a smile.

“And what would this reward be? I have no use for Spirit Bean Bombs anymore,” Xu Zhizhi said. Now that he had reached Foundation Establishment and had grown tired of experimenting with Mechanical Bamboo Rifles, his interest in such creations had waned.

“You’ll find out when the time comes,” Lin Jing replied cryptically. He intended to prioritize selling his limited supply of Rising Dragon Pills to trusted acquaintances like Xu Zhizhi.

Xu Zhizhi didn’t disappoint. While Lin Jing hadn’t mingled much with senior students over his eight years at the academy, Xu Zhizhi, possessing the Innate Administrative Physique, had personally evaluated and built connections with most of the current freshmen during their entrance assessments.

For someone aspiring to govern, building a network of relationships within the academy was just as important as cultivating.

Eight years ago, during the infamous case involving Gan Qian, these new students had already been exposed to the brilliance of Lin Jing. Nearly 40% of those Qi Refinement Stage 4 freshmen had now advanced to Foundation Establishment, but they hadn’t forgotten Lin Jing’s astounding achievements—such as crafting Mechanical Bamboo Rifles or leaving the Dragon-Phoenix Cavern Heaven with unmatched mastery of the tenth stage of Qi Refinement.

And now, to learn that Lin Jing had spent three years in seclusion to cultivate a new companion beast stirred their curiosity.

“Three years... He’s had the patience to spend three whole years raising a new beast?”

“He used the potential of the Hidden Dragon Arts to cultivate a Dragon-Blooded Companion Beast? He’s really willing to invest!”

The students were intrigued. They decided to attend, eager to witness the strength of this companion beast. After all, no student in their cohort had ever used their Hidden Dragon Fortune to raise a beast or mount before. Hidden Dragon Fortune was so rare that most people couldn’t get enough of it for themselves. They were curious—just how far could it push the potential of a companion beast?

One month later, at the south gate of Hidden Dragon Academy, Lin Jing had already set up an arena, waiting for the students invited by Xu Zhizhi to arrive.

On the stage, Lin Jing sat cross-legged with his eyes closed, resting quietly.

In front of Lin Jing was a flopping red carp.

“A Dragon Carp?”

“His newly cultivated companion beast is actually a Dragon Carp?”

The students who had gathered at the scene one after another froze in place when they saw the Qi Refinement Stage 9 Dragon Carp on the arena platform.

A carp spirit... a Dragon Carp… It was a creature that many cultivators were very familiar with. After all, the tale of the Carp Leaping Over the Dragon Gate was a legendary story that most cultivators had grown up hearing. However, because they were so familiar with it, they also knew the truth: a carp spirit, before it transforms into a dragon, is far weaker in battle compared to most Dragon-Blooded Beasts.

All the students present had been to the Dragon-Phoenix Cavern Heaven, where Qi Refinement Stage 9 Dragon-Blooded Beasts were anything but weak. There, you’d encounter Flood Dragons, massive serpents, or giant crocodiles. Other than those, the Dragon-Blooded beasts often included fierce lions and tigers, all of which were incredibly powerful.

Yet there had been no Dragon Carps in the cavern heaven, which was telling. It indicated that even celestial beings in the immortal palace didn’t consider the Dragon Carp to be a combat-worthy Dragon-Blooded Beast.

“Maybe after it successfully transforms into a dragon, its strength would improve more dramatically than other Dragon-Blooded Beasts. But for now, its combat abilities shouldn’t be too strong. Besides, this isn’t even a water-attribute environment.”

“Wait, hold on. Something’s not right—why is this Dragon Carp fire-attribute?” someone suddenly exclaimed.

Most of the cultivators present were already at the Foundation Establishment Stage and had developed their spiritual senses. Observing the Dragon Carp with spiritual senses, they discovered that its spiritual energy contained faint traces of firepower—it was actually a fire-attribute beast.

The crowd was filled with confusion, curiosity, and astonishment.

As more people arrived, Lin Jing stood on the platform, wearing a white alchemist’s robe. While a few, like Ziling’er, another alchemist, noticed this detail, most of the audience had their attention fixed on the Dragon Carp, paying little heed to Lin Jing’s outfit.

“What is he planning?” Ziling’er, as an alchemist herself, couldn’t help but wonder why Lin Jing was dressed in alchemist attire at this moment.

“Everyone,” Lin Jing began, scanning the crowd and spotting many familiar faces.

“This is a companion beast I cultivated using the Hidden Dragon Arts, my exclusive pills, and my unique methods: the Flame Dragon Carp. It has not yet formed a contract with me. However, I’d like to test its capabilities first. Would any of the junior brothers present be willing to spar with it? Regardless of whether you’re at the Qi Refinement Stage or the Foundation Establishment Stage, you are welcome to step forward.”

“Hm?”

Suddenly, Lin Jing turned to look at a silver-clad figure walking toward the platform from the distance.

“Is that Wang Xiao, possessor of the Heavenly God Physique?” Lin Jing asked with a smirk. “Over the past three years, you’ve challenged me multiple times, but I couldn’t respond as I was in seclusion. Brother Wang, how about trying your luck now?”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 32: Dragon King
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 32: Dragon King

A silver figure approached from the distance—it was Wang Xiao. His expression was grim as he said, "You want me to fight this fish of yours? I’ve already reached Foundation Establishment. Even if the two of you attack together, it’d feel like bullying the weak. Did you spend three years in seclusion just to raise this fish?"

As Wang Xiao's words landed, the crowd erupted in an uproar. Wang Xiao was undoubtedly the most prominent of the new disciples. Since stepping into the Foundation Establishment stage, he had defeated many senior disciples at the late Foundation Establishment stage. His minor mastery of the Heavenly God Physique made him overwhelmingly powerful.

Previously, there were debates about whether Wang Xiao or Lin Jing was stronger. However, after Wang Xiao advanced to Foundation Establishment and Lin Jing went into seclusion, the discussions stopped.

Now, the crowd finally understood that Wang Xiao had repeatedly issued challenges, yet Lin Jing had ignored him under the pretense of being busy. Wang Xiao himself was furious. Ever since he lost to Lin Jing during their first encounter, he had trained relentlessly. After mastering various spells and techniques, he issued several challenges, only to be brushed off by Lin Jing. Eventually, due to a fortuitous opportunity, Wang Xiao was forced to break through to Foundation Establishment.

“Just suppress your cultivation to the Tenth Stage of Qi Refinement and fight it. Problem solved,” Lin Jing said casually.

The Heavenly God Physique user raised an eyebrow—this was indeed a feasible solution. Suppressing his cultivation to the Tenth Stage of Qi Refinement to battle Lin Jing at the same level would also address his lingering regret.

“Fine. Then you and this Dragon Carp can come at me together.” Taking a few steps forward, Wang Xiao ascended into the air, landing on the arena. He sealed his spiritual sense and locked away the majority of his true essence within his Spirit Lake. Channeling his energy, he reconfigured his body to its peak Qi Refinement Tenth Stage state.

Boom!

This promised to be a spectacle.

Many disciples quickly sent messages to inform friends who weren’t present at the scene. Amid the surging anticipation, Wang Xiao's erupting true energy manifested a celestial phantom—a shadow of a god. Its aura condensed into tangible silver light and shot directly at the Dragon Carp.

“The might of a god!”

The beam of light shone like divine radiance descending upon the mortal realm. Dust and stones within the arena floated in its wake. Outside the stage, some of the spectators trembled uncontrollably merely from witnessing the divine light, their bodies paralyzed.

“Is this really just the Tenth Stage of Qi Refinement?” Many had never faced Wang Xiao at this level before and were awestruck by the overwhelming dominance of the Heavenly God Physique. Resistance seemed impossible.

At this moment, they finally understood why Yuan Jing, a Foundation Establishment Sword Cultivator, had lost back then.

The Heavenly God Physique—it was invincible!

“This power cannot be withstood by anyone at Qi Refinement Nine or even the average Foundation Establishment cultivator. Lin Jing himself is only at the Tenth Stage of Qi Refinement. Can he protect his beast from this power?”

The spectators’ gazes flickered. However, Lin Jing, the Beast Tamer, showed no intention of helping his pet. He merely smiled faintly and said, “Stop testing it. Intimidation alone won’t scare it.”

Feed the god, then carve the meat!

“Roar—!”

The Dragon Carp glanced at the divine light. It opened its mouth slightly, and a terrifying dragon roar erupted. Its bloodline boiled, and an equally potent aura of bloodline suppression spread throughout the arena. The dragon’s roar was like the king of beasts’ furious bellow, full of unparalleled dominance. The soundwave surged forward, shattering the divine light with a bang —and the roar continued unabated!

Wang Xiao’s expression shifted. He crossed his arms defensively and was blasted several meters backward, only stopping at the edge of the arena.

“Hm?” Seeing his Heavenly God Physique’s might being suppressed, Wang Xiao looked confused. In the next instant, the Dragon Carp entered a battle-ready state. It floated into the air, fueled by its vast reservoir of demonic energy, glaring at Wang Xiao with savage intent.

Boom!

Suddenly, the Dragon Carp lunged forward, its massive tail sweeping toward Lin Jing.

Crash!

Lin Jing raised his Starry Blade to block the tail strike. The blade trembled violently as an intense wave of spiritual power rippled from the collision point, transforming into a gale that swept toward the onlookers. Lin Jing stared into the Dragon Carp’s eyes, his expression darkening.

In a split second, the Dragon Carp unleashed a burst of physical strength that even exceeded the power of the Heavenly God Physique. The sheer force sent Lin Jing flying toward the center of the arena.

This scene left the spectators utterly stunned. Even with Wang Xiao suppressing his cultivation, he was still a Heavenly God Physique user. How could he be overpowered by a Dragon Carp at the Ninth Stage of Qi Refinement?

Unless…

Finally, Wang Xiao realized something.

“Demonic King bloodline… This creature possesses a royal bloodline!”

His declaration ignited waves of shock among the crowd.

“This Dragon Carp… possesses the bloodline of a Demonic King?!”

The bloodline of a Demonic King refers to the inheritance of a Nascent Soul-level Demonic King. While a Demonic King can have countless offspring, only those who inherit the entirety of its bloodline power can be called bearers of the Demonic King bloodline. Producing such a descendant severely depletes the life force of the Demonic King itself, often rendering it infertile afterward. As a result, demonic beasts with the bloodline of a Demonic King are akin to human cultivators with rare special constitutions—they possess immense strength and, barring unexpected death, are highly likely to achieve the Nascent Soul stage.

No one had anticipated that Lin Jing's seemingly ordinary little carp would possess the bloodline of a Demonic King. Lin Jing, however, remained calm. The Dragon Carp’s bloodline had undergone atavism, making it incredibly powerful. Additionally, with the enhancement of "Fortune Dragon" energy, its bloodline quality had been elevated to the king level, even before it reached Foundation Establishment, surpassing Lin Jing's Pine Leaf Mouse in terms of potential.

What’s more, unlike regular king-level bloodlines, the Dragon Carp is a dragon-blooded demonic beast, and the dragon race is the monarch of all demonic beasts. Its bloodline is unimaginably powerful.

"Not bad. Take this!" Lin Jing said with a faint smile. Around the Dragon Carp, an infinite surge of Dragon Scales Fire erupted. Within the flames, dragon-shaped true energy swirled and coiled. As this supreme beast flame emerged, it instantly formed a Heaven-Earth Furnace , connecting heaven and earth. Layers upon layers of fiery waves rotated endlessly, trapping Wang Xiao within. His expression darkened even further.

The manifestation of this furnace drew an astonished cry from Ziling’er. “Top-tier beast flames… and a Heaven-Earth Furnace! This is a fire-controlling technique that only a third-grade alchemist could master!”

Xu Zhizhi, Yuan Jing, Gao Kai, and others stared at Wang Xiao, who was trapped inside the blazing furnace. They were utterly dumbfounded. No one could have imagined that this Dragon Carp, which had not even leaped over the Dragon Gate yet, could be so ferocious!

“Heh…” Only a hidden observer, disguised as a pine cone, sneered coldly, “A bunch of naive righteous cultivators. Faced with a demonic fish, they’ve gone dumb with shock. To catch up in power to that Ice Gu, this carp has spent day and night refining itself like a pill using ghostfire and feasting on Longevity Blood Mosquitoes every single meal!”

If anyone thought this Dragon Carp was just an ordinary young king-level beast, they would be gravely mistaken!

Boom! The Heavenly God Physique was still as formidable as ever. After being trapped for a moment, Wang Xiao managed to slash through the furnace. However, his once radiant silver armor was now blackened. Although his body was unharmed, his dominance and oppressive aura remained intact as he stepped forward, glowing faintly in the dim light.

“Interesting. Over the past three years, you’ve raised yet another monster… Seems I underestimated you!” Wang Xiao laughed heartily. Instead of weakening, his power surged even further. Divine radiance shone brighter, and arcs of lightning crackled across his astral energy. Just as he was about to launch a counterattack—

Lin Jing interjected, “You’re wondering how I managed to raise such an exceptionally powerful Dragon Carp from something so ordinary? You’re right—it’s not solely due to the Hidden Dragon Arts . But when paired with this Rising Dragon Pill that I refined, the results are entirely different.”

“A demonic beast with a powerful bloodline, when combined with this pill, has the potential to reach the level of a Dragon King. With this pill, you could also find a demonic beast with a strong bloodline—not necessarily dragon-blooded—and quickly raise a young Dragon King to serve as your mount!” As Lin Jing spoke, he extended his hand, revealing a crimson pill.

Wang Xiao: ?

“I didn’t even ask!” he barked, gripping his battle blade tightly.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 33: The Gifted Pill
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 33: The Gifted Pill

"Who asked you?!"

The moment Lin Jing brought out the pill, the new disciples immediately understood his true intentions for coming out of seclusion.

It seemed that training his new pet was just an excuse—his real purpose was to introduce a new product and earn some fame!

“Didn’t ask, huh?” Lin Jing blinked, retracting the pill with a smile. “Looks like I misheard.”

“But, Fellow Daoist Wang, won’t you buy one? You’ve seen its power for yourself!”

Wang Xiao let out a cold snort, raising his Starry Blade . A deafening thunderclap boomed as heavenly lightning descended, channeling through the blade and striking his Heavenly God Physique. His entire body radiated with lightning and wind energy, shrouded in violent currents.

“Wind Thunder Flash!” Wang Xiao’s figure vanished in an instant.

The Dragon Carp’s eyes darted around nervously, and in the next moment, a loud cracking sound came as its scales shattered. Despite holding the advantage moments ago, the Dragon Carp was now smashed into the distance, leaving a deep trench across the arena.

“The Dragon Carp you raised is indeed stronger than an ordinary beast with king-level bloodlines, but that’s all there is to it. Nothing more. Earlier, it was merely because I hadn’t fully adjusted to suppressing my cultivation…”

He spoke calmly, but before he could finish, his brow furrowed. To his surprise, the Dragon Carp, having just been struck down, leaped back up, lively and vigorous as if nothing had happened.

“Even the innate Heavenly God Physique at the Tenth Stage of Qi Refinement has to admit that a dragon-blooded beast raised with the Rising Dragon Pill surpasses ordinary Demonic King progeny!” Lin Jing remarked.

“I DID NOT SAY THAT!!!” Wang Xiao gritted his teeth, gripping his battle blade even tighter, utterly infuriated. He was clearly mocking the Dragon Carp, not praising it!

Once again, Wang Xiao’s figure disappeared. Though the Dragon Carp had managed to overpower him in “aura” and “strength” earlier, in terms of speed , Wang Xiao was on an entirely different level.

Pop! Pop! Pop!

The Dragon Carp spat out several fireballs toward Wang Xiao, but he easily dodged them all. In the blink of an eye, Wang Xiao reappeared behind the Dragon Carp, slamming it dozens of meters away once more.

“Now do you see?” Wang Xiao took deliberate steps toward the Dragon Carp. Just as he was about to continue speaking, Lin Jing cut in again.

“Everyone, don’t be fooled by the fact that the Dragon Carp is currently being suppressed. I suspect that this state of stimulating the body with heavenly lightning is something Fellow Daoist Wang can’t maintain for long. Besides—”

“SHUT UP.”

Wang Xiao was beginning to regret accepting the challenge. Earlier, when the Dragon Carp had the upper hand, Lin Jing wouldn’t stop rambling. Now that Wang Xiao had turned the tide, Lin Jing was still talking nonstop.

Irritated, Wang Xiao decided to change tactics. Instead of targeting the Dragon Carp, he leaped forward, shattering the arena as he charged straight at Lin Jing!

“Break—”

But just as Wang Xiao focused entirely on Lin Jing, he failed to notice the astonishing transformation taking place on the Dragon Carp.

The Dragon Scales Fire on its body began to compress wildly, its temperature skyrocketing. The flames turned violent, coalescing into the shape of a massive flaming dragon head, which shot toward Wang Xiao with a deafening roar.

Bang!

Wang Xiao, with his full attention on Lin Jing, let his guard down for just a moment. That moment was enough. The flaming dragon head slammed into his abdomen, caving it inward. His eyes rolled back slightly, saliva spurted from his mouth, and he was sent flying more than ten meters before managing to steady himself. Clutching his abdomen, his expression was grim.

Lin Jing looked at him with an odd expression. “Demonic beasts with dragon bloodlines possess incredibly strong physical bodies. Even an innate Heavenly God Physique attacking at full power can’t suppress them quickly.”

Despite its shattered scales and fresh wounds, the Dragon Carp still flailed about energetically. The most shocking part was its terrifying self-healing ability—its injuries were closing at an exaggerated speed.

“Pfft.” Yao Ai coughed up a mouthful of blood.

Meanwhile, Lin Jing, struck by Wang Xiao, was also sent flying, and for a moment, the scene fell into silence.

That last exchange had caused immense destruction, far surpassing the Dragon Carp’s earlier attacks, and had clearly dealt a significant blow to Wang Xiao.

The impact was enough to break Wang Xiao’s self-imposed suppression. He coughed up another mouthful of blood as his cultivation returned to the Foundation Establishment stage, stabilizing him.

“Flame compression... that’s yet another fire-controlling technique only a third-grade alchemist could master!”

To the untrained eye, it was a spectacle, but to someone knowledgeable like Ziling’er, the truth was far more impressive. She pondered aloud, “Typically, demonic beasts are crude when it comes to controlling spiritual energy. They rely entirely on their bloodline strength, and their techniques are wild and unrefined. How is this Dragon Carp so different?”

Ziling’er wasn’t aware of this, but the Dragon Carp had no choice in the matter. Normally, reasonable people know that demonic beasts are naturally bad at controlling power, so they simply avoid pushing them too hard, playing to their strengths instead to save time.

But Lin Jing? He didn’t follow normal conventions. He forced the Dragon Carp to practice alchemy with him.

Is fire control progressing too slowly? No problem. With their long lifespan, they could afford to train patiently over time. Gradually, as the Dragon Carp kept practicing, it realized it wasn’t as difficult as it first seemed.

“ Again!!! ”

Wang Xiao glanced at the arena, then extended his hand to recall his weapon. His anger burned furiously as he realized that Lin Jing had disrupted his focus. He was too eager to defeat Lin Jing, which had caused him to make repeated mistakes.

Just as Wang Xiao was preparing to suppress his cultivation once more and continue the fight, Lin Jing shook his head, signaling that it was time to wrap things up.

“Fellow Daoist Wang, this is a Rising Dragon Pill . It can grant ordinary demonic beasts a powerful dragon bloodline. I raised my dragon-blooded pet using this pill and the Hidden Dragon Arts. Let’s call this match a draw, and thank you for your guidance. Please accept this pill as a token of my gratitude.” Lin Jing casually tossed a pill to Wang Xiao.

“What kind of joke is this?!” Wang Xiao caught the pill, clearly unwilling to let the fight end here.

However, as he thought about the battle, a realization dawned on him: he had yet to face Lin Jing directly. If a mere Dragon Carp had already pushed him to such extremes, how difficult would it be if Lin Jing himself joined the fight? His expression darkened.

This Dragon-blooded Carp… why was it so strange? He had fought juvenile Demonic Kings before, yet none had been as troublesome as this carp.

"If Lin Jing truly fought alongside it and used all of its abilities, including his secrets, I might still win against the carp, but I wouldn’t stand a chance against them both. How could there be such a frustrating Beast Tamer technique?!"

Wang Xiao glanced at the Dragon Carp, whose expression oscillated between ferocity and blankness, then let out a deep breath. Clenching the pill in his hand, he turned and left, knowing that continuing the fight would only make things worse for himself.

"I need to raise my own demonic pet!"

What Wang Xiao didn’t realize was that while he believed he could defeat the Dragon Carp, the beast had only used the surface level of its strength. Its more sinister abilities, such as Ghostfire and other demonic techniques, had yet to come into play.

After Wang Xiao left, Lin Jing understood that his goal had been achieved.

The story of Lin Jing from the Beast Taming Sect raising a dragon-blooded demonic beast using the Hidden Dragon Arts and a special pill to fight the Heavenly God Physique to a draw would undoubtedly spread like wildfire.

With a slight smile, Lin Jing turned to face the silent crowd of geniuses below the arena. “Are there any other Fellow Daoists who would like to exchange pointers?”

“If, like Fellow Daoist Wang, you suppress your cultivation to the same level and either draw with or defeat my pet, you’ll receive a Rising Dragon Pill as a reward.”

The crowd remained silent. A draw? Competing against a carp that could stand on equal ground with the Heavenly God Physique at the same level? Who here could claim they had the ability to fight it evenly?

Suddenly, someone in the audience broke the silence. “This Rising Dragon Pill of yours—what’s the price? If I bought a few, could I really raise a dragon pet as powerful as your carp?”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 34: The Dragon Group of the Academy
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 34: The Dragon Group of the Academy

The one who suddenly spoke up was none other than the curious Gao Sheng.

"I may not practice the techniques of the Beast Taming Sect and cannot sign a bloodline contract with a dragon pet, but the methods of beast taming are not limited to bloodline contracts. Just imagine how exciting it would be to own a dragon pet capable of rivaling a Heavenly God Physique!"

In the crowd, Xu Zhizhi looked toward Gao Sheng. Could this guy be a plant Lin Jing hired?

To be honest, he even suspected that Wang Xiao had been bribed. Until now, he still couldn't fully accept what he was seeing. He glanced at the dumb-looking Dragon Carp on the stage. How could this Dragon Carp possibly rival the Heavenly God Physique? If this had been a battle between cultivators of the same level, wouldn't this fish have instantly defeated him?

Could it really achieve such a feat just with the Hidden Dragon Arts and this single pill?

Lin Jing looked at Gao Sheng and smiled. "As long as it’s trained properly, even if it doesn’t compare to my Dragon Carp, it would still be extremely powerful. As for the price…"

Achieving the same level as the Flame Dragon Carp was impossible. After all, no one else had Longevity Blood to feed their pet beasts. However, Lin Jing had tested the product many times and only dared to sell it after gaining full confidence.

"We’re all classmates here. If anyone wants to train a dragon pet, I definitely won’t hold back on guidance."

"Moreover, I plan to establish a student organization where interested peers can explore the methods of training dragon pets together. Anyone who purchases a Rising Dragon Pill can join this organization. I, Lin Jing, will personally guide you on how to nurture a dragon pet throughout the process. What do you think? Quite a good deal, isn’t it?"

A student organization ?!

The crowd was stunned.

Xu Zhizhi took a deep breath. He realized Lin Jing's ambitions were far from simple.

In this academy, student organizations were essentially factions of the Ancient Kingdom. Typically, only the most talented from prominent families could establish such organizations. Talented individuals from smaller forces who joined them would essentially be pledging allegiance to these factions, making it easier to navigate the cultivation world in the future.

Lin Jing, a freshman from a third-rate sect, actually dared to entertain the idea of founding a student organization!

Who would possibly want to follow him?!

"Are you serious? I’m joining!" someone exclaimed the next moment—it was Gao Sheng again.

"What the h***?! Someone’s actually joining?!" Xu Zhizhi was dumbfounded. Gao Sheng, a descendant of a Nascent Soul cultivator, was ignoring the elite offspring of prominent families and instead choosing to associate with Lin Jing. What was he thinking?!

"Student organization? What’s it called?" Gao Sheng asked, genuinely interested.

He happened to own a Foundation Establishment Dragon Horse, but it lacked a royal bloodline. Even with his status, his family wasn’t willing to provide him with a royal beast cub as a mount, let alone a dragon-blooded royal beast cub.

The allure of Lin Jing's Dragon Carp was undeniable. Just imagining his Dragon Horse being able to rival cultivators with Heavenly God Physiques was enough to make him salivate.

He’d come to the academy under family pressure and had no interest in broadening his network. Pursuing what interested him was far more appealing.

"Dragon Palace," Lin Jing replied. "The organization I plan to establish will be called Dragon Palace."

"Besides Brother Gao, are there any other classmates interested?"

As Lin Jing’s words fell, the crowd erupted into discussion.

Many freshmen looked at him with intrigue, unsure of what he was up to.

"Dragon Palace? Could you sell me a Rising Dragon Pill now? I want to try it out first," a voice said.

It was Ziling’er, the seventh-ranked Dragon Carp cultivator of the Pill Cauldron Sect. She was clearly intrigued by the Rising Dragon Pill.

"Not yet," Yuan Jing remarked as he eyed the dragon pet. Although his ideal companion was a "Sword Spirit," he couldn’t deny being tempted by a young dragon capable of rivaling a Heavenly God Physique.

"What about you, Brother Xu?" Lin Jing smiled at Xu Zhizhi, seeing that some of his former companions from their time in the Cavern Heaven were already showing interest.

Xu Zhizhi, however, hesitated.

On one hand, he was indeed envious of dragon pets. Everyone had seen the battle earlier. On the other hand, he felt that following Lin Jing wouldn’t help him build connections useful for his future in the cultivation world.

He was still deliberating between joining student organizations established by scions of noble families.

"Count me in! But make sure to give me a discount on the pill!" Xu Zhizhi exclaimed, his eyes red with frustration. The Kirin scion from the Wang family in the capital had already been overshadowed by Lin Jing—what was the point of currying favor with noble descendants anymore?!

He only hoped Lin Jing wasn’t just a flash-in-the-pan genius. Whether this decision was right or wrong, he didn’t know, but he gave up the chance to cling to a noble family’s coattails and boarded Lin Jing’s wild ride .

The battle between the Dragon Carp and Wang Xiao had already achieved its promotional goal. Now, every freshman present wanted to witness the Rising Dragon Pill’s effects firsthand.
