"I’m short on Spirit Stones. Can I trade this pill for a Rising Dragon Pill instead?" Ziling’er tossed a vial toward Lin Jing.

"This is a third-grade pill—the Titan Pill. It allows lower-level cultivators to simulate a 'Nascent Soul Avatar' once and experience the power of the Nascent Soul. Among third-grade pills, it’s one of the most valuable."

"Naturally," Lin Jing said, accepting the Titan Pill. Third-grade pills were typically used by Golden Core cultivators, and the Titan Pill was quite valuable.

In truth, the cost of a Rising Dragon Pill wasn’t particularly high among third-grade pills. The only expensive component was the dragon blood from a Monster Core-stage dragon-blooded beast. The other ingredients were all synthesized by Pine Leaf Mice. Once the Dragon Carp reached the Monster Core stage, Lin Jing wouldn’t even need to purchase dragon blood anymore.

Lin Jing handed a Rising Dragon Pill to Ziling’er.

"Fairy Zi, do you have a pet beast?" he asked.

"I have a Foundation Establishment pill beast I use for testing pills. It’s currently at my cave abode," Ziling’er replied. "Fellow Daoists, you’re welcome to come with me."

With that, she eagerly took the Rising Dragon Pill, condensed her True Essence, and flew toward a nearby mountain. The crowd immediately followed her.

Before long, everyone arrived at Ziling’er’s academy cave abode. She performed a hand seal, and the stone door to her cave opened. A white Foundation Establishment rabbit demon slowly emerged.

"Master, what’s the matter… so many people. I’m scared," the rabbit demon said in a soft, trembling voice, gazing nervously at the dozens of cultivators.

"I have a pill here. Give it a try," Ziling’er said, tossing the Rising Dragon Pill to the rabbit demon.

"That’s… a pill rabbit!" someone exclaimed, recognizing the rabbit demon’s species. It was a unique type of beast with a bloodline that could mitigate the toxicity of various pills. Pill Rabbits were specially bred by the Pill Cauldron Sect. Their combat abilities were weak, but they were ideal companions for alchemists.

"Fairy Zi, while a Pill Rabbit is a perfect test subject for pills, its bloodline strength is low. It might not fully display the Rising Dragon Pill’s effects," Lin Jing remarked. "The stronger the bloodline before consuming the pill, the purer the transformation into dragon blood."

"It doesn’t matter," Ziling’er replied.

Under her gaze, the Pill Rabbit consumed the Rising Dragon Pill.

Thump thump!

Its heartbeat grew incredibly loud. The red in its eyes began to cloud, and a terrifying dragon aura began to emanate from its body, causing even the Foundation Establishment geniuses around it to feel a faint sense of fear.

Dragon aura was far more overwhelming than the pressure of a cultivator of the same level!

With a hum, spiritual energy surged as the Pill Rabbit broke through to the mid-Foundation Establishment stage. Its body underwent a dramatic transformation: its fur shed, its frame expanded significantly, and its forelimbs became unusually thick and muscular, resembling coiled vines wrapped around its bones. Its hind legs were even more astonishing, with muscles bulging like mountains.

The muscles on its back and waist were tightly knit and fluid, outlining a majestic dragon-like shape. When it shifted slightly, the movement of its back muscles resembled the undulations of a divine dragon. Even its abdomen, though covered by short fur, displayed eight distinct abdominal muscles.

Its long ears transformed into a pair of crimson dragon horns.

Seeing this, Ziling’er took a deep breath and said, "Attack me to test your strength."

In the next instant, the Dragon Rabbit lunged at Ziling’er, kicking her with immense force. Her protective True Essence shattered, and she was sent flying into the mountain, spitting blood.

The crowd was dumbfounded.

Was this the same timid Pill Rabbit from before? And why did that kick feel like it carried some pent-up resentment, rather than merely testing its strength?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 35: Myriad Beast Circle
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 35: Myriad Beast Circle

“You wretched thing!!!”

Ziling’er flew out of the mountain crevice, wiping the blood from the corner of her mouth, glaring angrily at the Dan Rabbit.

“I asked you to attack me to test your strength, not to kill me!”

“Master, I didn’t mean to…” The muscular rabbit hid behind a rock, looking innocent and scared of Ziling’er’s retaliation.

Ziling’er was furious. She quickly descended in front of her cave and scolded the Dan Rabbit, “Go inside and reflect on your actions! Don’t come out unless I allow it!”

The Dan Rabbit pouted and hopped straight into the cave.

The others watched Ziling’er’s disheveled appearance, suppressing their laughter but unable to hide their amusement.

It seemed this Dan Rabbit wasn’t all that submissive toward Ziling’er.

Lin Jing chuckled and explained, “Dan Rabbits are indeed very docile, but now it has become a Dragon-Blooded Dan Rabbit. Dragons are inherently proud and self-assured. Considering how you’ve likely pushed it hard during your experiments, it’s not surprising that now, with its newfound strength, it wants to let off some steam.”

“I can see that this Dan Rabbit is still quite loyal to you, Miss Ziling. If you treat it better in the future, it’ll naturally become even more obedient.”

“I’ll join the Dragon Palace,” Ziling’er finally said with a pout. She was clearly captivated by the effects of the Rising Dragon Pill—or perhaps by the Beast Taming profession itself.

Whenever a cultivation technique or profession demonstrated immense power, it inevitably garnered admiration. It was human nature. History had no shortage of examples where powerful cultivators ignited new cultivation trends.

As an alchemist, Ziling’er’s own combat strength was relatively weak. Having a strong pet beast wouldn’t be such a bad thing.

“I recall that the Dan Pill Manual from the Dan Ding Sect mentions a type of pill that can make demonic beasts docile and tame, even purifying their wild nature,” Ziling’er mused to herself, deciding to leverage her alchemy skills to better control the Dragon-Blooded Dan Rabbit.

As for how the Dragon Palace handled pet beasts, Lin Jing didn’t care. These geniuses weren’t disciples of the Beast Taming Sect, so he didn’t plan to impose his sect’s rules on them.

“Welcome aboard,” Lin Jing said, clapping his hands. “Miss Ziling, I’m sure you’ll be glad you made this choice.”

Ziling’er gave Lin Jing a curious look, still puzzled about the origin of the Rising Dragon Pill. Was it his creation? That didn’t seem likely. Was it a secret formula from the Beast Taming Sect?

Even if it was, the fact that Lin Jing and the Dragon Carp could refine it was absurd. Refining a third-tier pill at the Qi Refinement Stage was already challenging. If she, a prodigy of the Dan Ding Sect, could do it, it would be expected. But Lin Jing? He wasn’t just a Beast Tamer; he was also proficient in Mechanized Arts and alchemy?!

“The effects are indeed remarkable,” Ziling’er admitted, finally recognizing the Rising Dragon Pill. Hearing this, Yuan Jing felt reassured.

“Although raising a Dragon Beast is costly, it’s a worthwhile investment for the future,” Yuan Jing thought to himself.

In the Heavenly Sword Gate, there were two ways to cultivate a Sword Spirit. The first was for exceptionally talented swordsmen to nurture their swords with their hearts, allowing the sword to develop its own spirit—a rarity. The second method was to seal a beast’s soul within the sword and use a special secret art to transform it into a Sword Spirit. However, this method was also fraught with challenges.

If a genius sword cultivator were to raise a pet beast, their ultimate goal would undoubtedly be to merge the beast entirely with their sword.

“I’ll join as well,” Yuan Jing said, deciding that using a Dragon Beast as his Sword Spirit would not disappoint.

“You’re joining too? Good. Take some time to gather Spirit Stones and go buy a demonic beast!”

At this point, many of the new students began to waver. Most of them came from well-off backgrounds and could afford third-tier pills.

“Once you’re ready, come find me at my cave,” Lin Jing said with a welcoming smile, not turning anyone away.

He was genuinely curious to see how the academy would evolve if he slowly turned it into a “Beast Taming Sect.”

Soon, whether they planned to join the “Dragon Palace” or not, all the new students dispersed—except for Gao Sheng and Xu Zhizhi.

…

When Lin Jing returned to his cave, Gao Sheng and Xu Zhizhi were still tailing him closely.

Gao Sheng grinned and asked, “How many Rising Dragon Pills do you have left? If my Dragon Horse takes one, what effect will it have? Could it possibly achieve a King-Tier Bloodline?”

Lin Jing rolled his eyes. “You’re dreaming. While the Rising Dragon Pill can enhance dragon blood purity, achieving King-Tier Bloodline isn’t that easy. Your Dragon Horse’s bloodline isn’t bad. If it consumes multiple Rising Dragon Pills and you invest a significant amount of Hidden Dragon Fortune to cultivate it, then it might be possible.”

“I can buy the Rising Dragon Pills, but Hidden Fortune is hard to come by…” Gao Sheng sighed bitterly.

“Ahem, Brother Gao, I’ve got a few plans to make a name for myself, but I’m short on startup funds. Would you be interested in investing?” Xu Zhizhi said ingratiatingly after cursing “rich bastards” under his breath.

“Let’s hear it,” Gao Sheng replied.

“You two can discuss this later,” Lin Jing interrupted, tossing two Rising Dragon Pills to them. “Consider these a gift. However, refining the Rising Dragon Pills isn’t easy, and the cost is high. If you want more, you’ll have to trade for something of equal value like Ziling’er.”

The Dragon Blood used to refine the pills came from Lin Jing’s own pocket, purchased from the Lotus Pavilion.

“Brother Xu, help me spread the word about today’s events. Consider this pill a thank-you gift.”

“Of course!!!” Xu Zhizhi’s eyes lit up as he accepted the pill. “Don’t worry! I’ll make sure everyone in the Ancient Domain knows that even a Heavenly God Physique is no match for your Dragon Pets. I’ll ensure the entire Ancient Domain becomes curious about your Rising Dragon Pills!”

He pocketed the pill, already planning to buy a Lynx Demon for cultivation. The fur of Lynx Demons was essential for making brushes, and Xu Zhizhi was curious about the effects of using Dragon-Blooded Lynx fur for creating brushes. How much would it enhance literary techniques?

“I won’t take advantage of you either,” Gao Sheng said, taking out a circular artifact and handing it to Lin Jing.

“This is the Myriad Beast Circle, a treasure refined by the Myriad Beast Sect in Qingzhou. It’s similar to the Beast Taming Sect’s artifacts and can also store living creatures and capture demonic beasts. However, any beast subdued by the Myriad Beast Circle will have its intelligence erased, turning it into a puppet that only follows orders.”

Beyond the Ancient Kingdom, in Qingzhou, there was the Myriad Beast Sect—a faction similar to the early Beast Taming Sect.

But unlike the Beast Taming Sect, the Myriad Beast Sect had always enslaved demonic beasts. They erased the intelligence of the beasts they captured instead of nurturing them. Once a beast died in battle, they simply replaced it with another, endlessly cycling through them.

The sect earned its name from this approach.

If the Beast Taming Sect disciples cultivated elite pet beasts, then the Myriad Beast Sect cultivators employed a swarm strategy—subjugating vast numbers of low-level demonic beasts, erasing their intelligence, and unleashing beast tides to overwhelm enemies.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 36: Longevity Foundation Pill
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 36: Longevity Foundation Pill

"What a treasure!!!"

When Xu Zhizhi saw the Myriad Beast Circle, his eyes lit up again. Gao Sheng truly seemed to be a walking treasure trove!

Honestly, Xu Zhizhi had been conflicted about how to handle a dragon beast if he raised one.

Without practicing the techniques of the Beast Taming Sect, they couldn’t form bloodline contracts with their pets. It would be difficult to ensure their obedience.

Xu Zhizhi didn’t want a scenario where he raised a pet only for it to disobey him.

But with a treasure like this, all those concerns would vanish. He could directly erase the beast's consciousness and subdue it with the Myriad Beast Circle.

"Brother Gao, did you buy this treasure?" Xu Zhizhi asked.

Gao Sheng nodded. "It’s available at the Lotus Pavilion, but the stock is extremely limited. Even for the Lotus Pavilion, trading with Qingzhou is no easy task. The journey is far, and there are plenty of demon kings and bandits along the way who intercept shipments."

This was a Golden Core-grade magical artifact, far more valuable than a third-grade pill.

"It’s all the same. Just prioritize giving me your newly refined Rising Dragon Pills, and we’re even," Gao Sheng said casually.

Lin Jing fell silent. Its value wasn’t the main issue. Accepting it would be like regressing to outdated methods of the past. Erasing the intelligence of demonic beasts for control had been banned after the Beast Taming Sect abolished such techniques.

"Don’t worry, Brother Lin," Gao Sheng said, seemingly reading Lin Jing's hesitation. "On the path of cultivation, you’re bound to encounter some ignorant demonic beasts. This Myriad Beast Circle will be perfect for dealing with them!"

"I actually bought this intending to use it during this year’s Academy Autumn Hunt. But thinking it through, a treasure like this would just be wasted if kept idle. It’s better to put it to use with the Pine Leaf Mouse instead."

Lin Jing sighed. "Thanks a lot."

Even though the rules of the Beast Taming Sect forbade the use of such methods, Gao Sheng's reasoning wasn’t entirely wrong.

"Indeed, the Autumn Hunt is almost upon us," Xu Zhizhi interjected. "It’ll be a good opportunity to enter the hunting grounds and see if there are any rare and extraordinary beasts to subdue."

"Is Zou Qi going to participate?"

"The Autumn Hunt? Let’s see," Gao Sheng replied indifferently.

The Academy’s Autumn Hunt occurred once every seven years. It was organized by the academy, which would send its students into a specific area to hunt demonic beasts. It was also a chance for students to make a name for themselves.

The process of hunting ferocious beasts was inherently a process of gaining fame.

In essence, the Autumn Hunt wasn’t much different from the trial set up by Master Cao back then. Or rather, the treasure-hunting trial designed by Master Cao was a simulation of the Academy’s Autumn Hunt.

The hunting grounds for the Autumn Hunt wouldn’t just include ferocious beasts and savage animals. The academy would also release rare spiritual beasts from the Ancient Kingdom for the students to compete over.

Beasts from the Snow Plains, the Cloud Sea Mountains, Qingzhou, the deep seas, and the Desert Wastelands would all be present.

These rare spiritual beasts were exceptional, whether used as mounts, alchemy and artifact-refining materials, or for cultivation. Even for Golden Core cultivators, these beasts were walking treasures.

"It’ll definitely be worth a visit. After all, with the academy’s supervisors overseeing things, there won’t be any danger," Gao Sheng commented.

"That’s true. But is the Pine Leaf Mouse really going to participate at its Qi Refinement stage?" Zou Qi asked. "The Autumn Hunt is only a few months away. It should at least break through to the Foundation Establishment stage first, right?"

"Even if it’s strong enough to fight above its level and challenge Foundation Establishment cultivators, the participants won’t just be new students at the early Foundation Establishment stage. There will be mid- and late-Foundation Establishment cultivators, as well as senior students at the peak of Foundation Establishment."

"It’s going to be difficult to snatch treasures from us," Zou Qi remarked with a smile.

Lin Jing shook his head. "You really want to compete with us…?"

Not long after, the three went their separate ways.

Lin Jing returned to his cave abode and took out a pill recipe.

The Longevity Foundation Pill!

It was based on the following pill recipes: the Foundation Establishment Pill, the Treant Pill, the Beast Blood Pill, and the Longevity Pill.

"Foundation Establishment, huh? I’m still not entirely confident…"

"For now, I’ve only prepared a Foundation Establishment recipe for the Pine Leaf Mouse."

"I’ll wait until the success rate for refining Rising Dragon Pills exceeds sixty percent before attempting to refine the Longevity Foundation Pill."

"Besides, I’m still missing a critical ingredient for refining the Longevity Foundation Pill…"

Deep in thought, Lin Jing carefully stored the pill recipe, exited his cave, and left the academy. Soon after, he called out to Senior Pine.

"Senior Pine, are you there?"

"It’s been three years, and you finally remember this old man." The transformed Pine Spirit appeared as a pine cone, gazing at Lin Jing.

"Senior, this is the Foundation Establishment pill recipe I’ve been developing over the past three years, using the fortune of the Hidden Dragon Arts to improve my alchemy skills. What do you think?" Lin Jing handed the Longevity Foundation Pill recipe to Senior Pine.

"Hmm?" Senior Pine’s attention was caught when he noticed the inclusion of the Treant Pill. "The academy actually has such a pill recipe in its collection?!"

The Treant Pill was a recipe that added plant-based organs to the body. The original creator had intended it as a way to cultivate a wood-based physique but failed, resulting in the Treant Pill. Its side effects transformed arms into tree branches. Despite the failure, acquiring a Treant Physique provided enhanced abilities to manipulate plants. Later attempts saw transformations like hair turning into straw and the heart becoming a fruit…

"Indeed, the Treant Pill can be used not only by humans but also by wood-attribute demonic beasts. Through fortune-based calculations, it has the highest compatibility with the Foundation Establishment Pill," Lin Jing explained.

Senior Pine pondered aloud. "Longevity Foundation Pill… With this pill, a Foundation Establishment cultivator could gain plant-based organs and an unparalleled lifespan…"

Lin Jing nodded. "The lifespan of plants far exceeds that of humans or beasts. Even ordinary trees in the mortal world can easily live for a thousand years."

"Thus, through calculations, the Treant Pill has a very high compatibility with longevity-related attributes."

"The key lies in the compatibility between the Foundation Establishment cultivator and a specific plant! The Pine Leaf Mouse has its natal Crimson Leaf, but that leaf is ultimately an external object. If it could merge perfectly with the mouse’s body and evolve alongside it, the results would be extraordinary!"

Senior Pine’s gaze burned as he studied the recipe, though he couldn’t shake the feeling that something was amiss.

After reading it several times, Senior Pine commented, "Something’s wrong. This Longevity Foundation Pill involves integrating two plants into the Pine Leaf Mouse’s body!"

"The Crimson Leaf is just one of them!"

"What’s the other?"

Lin Jing fell into deep thought.

"The Crimson Leaf is a rootless plant. It will need something to provide nutrients—a root—to complete the cycle for the Longevity Foundation Pill."

"My initial idea was to use the Bloodsucking Vine, which could link to Longevity Blood."

"But the quality of the Bloodsucking Vine is too low and isn’t the ideal choice."

…

"Now that I think of it, back in the Great Wasteland, you consumed demonic beast blood. This suggests that, Senior, you possess the ability to convert blood into nutrients."

"Moreover, you and the Pine Leaf Mouse both practice the Everlasting Emperor’s Longevity Art, meaning your physique is highly compatible with the Pine Leaf Mouse."

The Pine Spirit erupted in anger. "You little brat! Are you scheming to use me for your pill refining?!"

"Are you planning to refine me into a pill ?! Even demonic cultivators must respect their elders!"

"Disciple… I just need a single pine cone…"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 37: Killing
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 37: Killing

"Disciple... I just need one pine cone..."

"A pine cone?!"

"You dare ask for a pine cone just to refine a Foundation Establishment Pill? What’s next? When you refine a Nascent Soul Pill, will you ask for my tree heart?"

Although pine cones were something it had in abundance, Senior Pine was outraged by Lin Jing’s audacity in targeting its body.

"Unbelievable! I haven’t even asked for your Longevity Blood, yet you’re already scheming for my fruits!"

"Senior..."

"Heh." Senior Pine chuckled coldly. "Getting a pine cone isn’t impossible. You just need to do one thing."

"Oh? Please tell me, Senior!" Lin Jing's eyes brightened. As long as there was room for negotiation, everything else could be worked out!

"You're still at the Qi Refinement stage, yet you’ve been in closed-door cultivation for three years. And what’s worse, your cultivation hasn’t improved one bit! I can’t even imagine how long you’ll isolate yourself when you reach the Foundation Establishment stage!"

"I foresee that the next hundred years will pass in the blink of an eye. Before I leave, I need to ensure you can stand on your own. Only then will I feel at ease and consider myself worthy of repaying the Second Sect Master’s life-saving grace. So… I want you to kill and temper yourself!"

"Killing your own kind is entirely different from hunting demonic beasts. You must kill at least one hundred cultivators with your own hands, and then I will grant you a pine cone to assist in your Foundation Establishment!"

Senior Pine’s demand left Lin Jing in stunned silence.

Kill one hundred people…

"Oh, and it has to be cultivators! You must kill them with your own hands; you can’t let your pet beasts do the job," Senior Pine added.

"Killing people…"

The conditions for obtaining the pine cone made Lin Jing's head spin.

In all his previous encounters, no matter what kind of duel or conflict, Lin Jing had never harbored killing intent toward other humans. Even when facing foreign Snow Plains cultivators, he merely incapacitated them, leaving Xu Zhizhi to deliver the final blow.

Now, Senior Pine wanted him to personally kill one hundred people. This made Lin Jing feel conflicted. Yet, he couldn’t deny his strong desire for the pine cone.

"The Beast Taming Sect’s Mission Hall often has tasks to eliminate mountain bandits. If they’re the type to plunder and murder, or if they’re demonic cultivators like those from the Yin Corpse Sect who engage in human trafficking, killing them shouldn’t weigh on my conscience."

"But the Ancient Domain is said to be the most well-governed of the Nine Domains of the Ancient Kingdom. Under the shadow of the imperial capital, where would I even find mountain bandits or demonic cultivators to eliminate?"

"Unless I take up Gao Sheng’s suggestion and join a cross-domain task force to eradicate a demonic cultivator base?" Lin Jing murmured to himself as he wandered through the academy.

Suddenly, an idea struck him, and his eyes lit up.

Lin Jing rose into the air using his spiritual energy and flew to Gao Sheng’s cave abode. When he arrived, he saw Gao Sheng feeding a Rising Dragon Pill to a Dragon Horse.

"Eat it! Come on, eat!" Gao Sheng coaxed as he struggled to get the reluctant Dragon Horse to consume the pill.

"Huh?" Gao Sheng noticed Lin Jing’s sudden arrival. He quickly tidied his clothes, wiped the drool off his hands, and greeted him enthusiastically, "Brother Lin? What brings you here? We just parted ways, and now you’ve come chasing after me again."

He grew wary.

"Wait, don’t tell me you’re still eyeing my Dragon Horse!"

Lin Jing remained silent. While the bloodline of Gao Sheng’s Dragon Horn Horse wasn’t bad and had the potential to form a Monster Core, Lin Jing no longer cared for it since he now had the Dragon Carp with Kirin bloodline.

"Brother Gao, have you ever killed anyone?" Lin Jing asked bluntly.

Gao Sheng was momentarily taken aback. Then he recalled, "Of course. When I was six, my father, determined to make me an excellent demon hunter, had me execute a Qi Refinement criminal on death row."

"Perfect!" Lin Jing took a deep breath and continued, "Brother Gao, do you have any connections that could get me a job at a mortal courthouse or the Demon-Slaying Bureau as an executioner for heinous criminals?"

"Huh?" Gao Sheng was dumbfounded. "Are you crazy?!"

"Why would you want to take on such dirty and exhausting work?"

He grew suspicious and began analyzing. "No, something’s off. Did you stumble upon some demonic technique that uses murderous energy as its core?"

Lin Jing rolled his eyes. "How could that be? I have the Ice Soul Gu. Why would I even bother with such low-level demonic techniques?"

"True," Gao Sheng agreed. "Demonic cultivators who focus on murderous energy or resentment typically come from third-rate sects."

"And those third-rate demonic sects that could be completely wiped out by the Ancient Kingdom can’t compare to the grand path of Gu cultivation."

"At least the founder of the Gu God Sect was at the Dao Seeking stage. If not for the severed immortal path, they might have ascended."

"It has nothing to do with demonic techniques," Lin Jing clarified. "It’s just that an elder of mine thinks my cultivation path has been too smooth and wants me to toughen up by killing."

Gao Sheng frowned. "Killing? That’s not the same as hunting demonic beasts. It’s easy to become entangled with murderous energy and resentment, which can disrupt your mental state and hinder your cultivation."

"Still… if it’s only a shallow attempt and you use special pills afterward to cleanse the murderous energy, it should be fine."

After some thought, he said, "Let me think about it. Give me a few days, and I’ll get back to you."

"Thank you." Lin Jing trusted Gao Sheng’s reliability. Having connections truly made a difference when facing challenges. Senior Pine’s seemingly impossible task was already halfway resolved.

At that moment, the Dragon Horse suddenly spoke up.

"That pill you gave me earlier—do you have more? I thought you were trying to poison me, but it turned out to be a treasure! My bloodline purity has improved significantly!"

"I told you it was a treasure!" Gao Sheng shot the Dragon Horse a resentful glance. "Do you think I can’t tell the difference between treasure and poison?"

"Exactly. I was worried you couldn’t," the Dragon Horse retorted.

"Heh…" Lin Jing chuckled from the side. "Brother Gao, to show my gratitude, you can have an unlimited supply of Rising Dragon Pills in the future. As long as you provide the main ingredient—‘dragon blood’—I’ll prepare the other materials and refine them for you free of charge."

"Seriously?!" Gao Sheng was overjoyed. "Will the Dragon Horse’s blood do?"

The Dragon Horse: ?

"It’s a bit weak… I need blood from a Monster Core-stage creature."

Gao Sheng sighed in disappointment but quickly regained his enthusiasm. "No problem, I’ll take care of it. Just relax and wait. I’ll have it sorted within seven days!"

With Gao Sheng’s assistance, Lin Jing returned to his cave abode and resumed refining Rising Dragon Pills with the Dragon Carp. He focused on improving his alchemical skills to lay the groundwork for crafting the Longevity Foundation Pill.

Several days later.

Thanks to Xu Zhizhi’s flair for spreading gossip, most of Hidden Dragon City had heard about the battle between Wang Xiao, the possessor of the Heavenly God Physique, and Lin Jing’s Dragon Carp.

In a teahouse, several cultivators sat sipping tea and listening to music while chatting.

"Have you heard? The Wang family’s Kirin child, who was expected to make it onto the Genius Rankings, actually lost to a Qi Refinement ninth-stage Dragon Carp!"

"How is that possible? Are you drunk? Isn’t the Heavenly God Physique already at the Foundation Establishment stage?"

"The battle was fought with suppressed cultivation levels! That Dragon Carp may not have high cultivation, but its bloodline is terrifying—it’s at the ‘royal’ level. And it’s not just any ordinary demon king bloodline; it’s pure royal dragon blood. Even a Heavenly God Physique can’t withstand it!"

"A royal dragon cub?! Is it a pet raised by one of the academy’s instructors?"

"No, no! Don’t you remember Lin Jing from the Beast Taming Sect? This Dragon Carp was cultivated by him over three years using special pills and the Hidden Dragon Arts. It’s said that he poured all the fortune accumulated from the Hidden Dragon Arts into raising the Dragon Carp, planning to contract it after reaching Foundation Establishment."

"I’ve also heard that since the Beast Taming Sect was sealed, Lin Jing plans to establish a new Dragon Taming Sect and is recruiting within the academy, proclaiming himself as the sect master."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 38: Executioner Lin Jing
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 38: Executioner Lin Jing

Within the academy, news of Lin Jing's intention to establish the "Dragon Palace" organization was spreading rapidly.

Outside a cave residence, a cultivator with a third eye glanced at the intelligence scroll in his hand. After a brief chuckle, he burned it to ashes.

"These new students are getting more arrogant by the day. I remember personally inviting this Lin Jing to join the 'Eye Alliance.' Not only did he decline, but now he’s even starting his own organization."

"Rising Dragon Pill? A Qi Refinement cultivator from some third-rate sect, playing at forming cliques and alliances? A year or two? No, perhaps just a few months before this so-called Dragon Palace falls apart."

A frail young man dressed as a scholar stepped forward from behind him and said, "The founders of the academy's major organizations are all geniuses in the Foundation Establishment stage like you, ranked on the Genius Rankings. Even if his talent is decent, Lin Jing is moving too hastily. Without enough strength or reputation, he’s bound to run into trouble. For someone like him, aligning himself with a powerful faction would be the best choice."

"Perhaps it’s because his sect has been sealed off, and he’s desperate to rally support from heirs of other sects. If the Beast Taming Sect gains more allies among the human race, their Heavenly Fortune might improve," Liu Yu interjected. "However, if the Rising Dragon Pill is as miraculous as the rumors suggest, he won’t be able to protect it with his strength and background. Even I am tempted by it."

"Although the idea of cultivating a dragon-blooded demonic pet with the Hidden Dragon Art and a single pill seems far-fetched, there are always those who don’t care about practicality. They’ll definitely try to seize the pill recipe."

...

The marketing of the Rising Dragon Pill was far more successful than expected. The overwhelming interest was even affecting Lin Jing’s pill refining sessions.

Over the past few days, many new students had placed orders for the pill, along with some older students who hadn’t made much progress.

This was all thanks to Xu Zhizhi's efforts in spreading the word.

"Even Wang Xiao, after acquiring a Flame Dragon Carp, took the Rising Dragon Pill. He seems to be planning to raise a dragon pet!"

"A Heavenly God Physique is already an unstoppable path; having multiple dragon pets just adds more avenues of strength!"

"If even the invincible physiques acknowledge it, what are you waiting for? In the path of cultivation, if you lag behind, you’ll always be behind!"

Recently, Wang Xiao had seemingly left Hidden Dragon City. While Xu Zhizhi hadn’t spread false rumors, the growing buzz led to exaggerated claims, including one that Wang Xiao had been so scared by a Flame Dragon Carp that he wet himself. Despite this, Wang Xiao hadn’t appeared to refute the claims, leaving Xu Zhizhi to speculate that he had likely returned to the Wang family to search for a demonic beast with stronger bloodlines.

"Is Lin Jing in?"

Another visitor had arrived.

"And you are?" Lin Jing stepped out of his cave residence, looking at the black-robed young man before him. The man radiated an oppressive aura, and Lin Jing faintly sensed that his cultivation was likely at the Great Perfection of the Foundation Establishment stage.

"My name is Chu Ren. Are you willing to sell the recipe for the Rising Dragon Pill?" the black-robed youth asked directly.

"I only sell the pills, prioritizing those planning to join the Dragon Palace. The recipe is not for sale," Lin Jing replied, shaking his head.

The black-robed youth pressed on, "Name your price. You won’t be disappointed."

"The Rising Dragon Pill requires unique materials exclusive to the Beast Taming Sect. Even if I sold the recipe, you wouldn’t be able to refine it yourself," Lin Jing explained.

Chu Ren’s brows furrowed. "Are you sure there’s no room for negotiation? Don’t you know who I am?"

"The Chu family of the Ancient Domain, ranked 98th on the Genius Rankings, Chu Ren, at the Great Perfection of Foundation Establishment. So what? My brother said it’s not for sale, and that’s final!"

At that moment, another black-clothed figure landed outside the cave residence. The newcomer glared at Chu Ren with a warning expression.

"It’s him." Lin Jing recognized the man and smirked slightly.

"Think it over." Chu Ren shot Lin Jing a final glance before leaving.

After Chu Ren had gone, the newcomer, Gao Sheng, sneered. "Lin Jing, do you know him?"

Gao Sheng snorted. "Of course. He was a hedonistic scion like me in the past, but his innate talent is much better, possessing a rare innate physique. He probably sees the Rising Dragon Pill as an opportunity and thinks your small sect, the Beast Taming Sect, is easy to bully since you have no strong backers. He likely planned to force you to sell."

"But don’t worry. With me here, he won’t succeed. Besides, in the academy, he wouldn’t dare act recklessly."

Lin Jing shook his head. "People like that really exist, huh? It’s fine. I was mentally prepared when I started selling Mechanical Bamboo Rifles. But it seems the Rising Dragon Pill is a much greater temptation."

"That’s for sure," Gao Sheng agreed. "The Chu family raises a Dragon-Blooded Demonic King, which frequently spawns dragon offspring. Chu Ren likely sees the Rising Dragon Pill as a way to cultivate a king-level bloodline. Compared to the Mechanical Bamboo Rifles, this pill is far more valuable."

"In any case, don’t worry about him for now. By the way, I’ve taken care of that favor you asked me for," Gao Sheng said with a grin.

"Really?" Lin Jing’s eyes lit up.

"Yes. Actually, there’s an immortal prison here in Hidden Dragon City where demonic cultivators are detained under the control of the Demon-Slaying Bureau. I pulled some strings to get you a position as an executioner there."

"An immortal prison in Hidden Dragon City?" Lin Jing was slightly surprised. "With so many powerful figures here, there are still demonic cultivators around?"

Gao Sheng explained, "No, these demonic cultivators were captured from various parts of the Ancient Celestial Kingdom. The kingdom is founded on Heavenly Fortune, and these demonic cultivators harm that fortune. But executing them also enhances the kingdom’s luck. Where they’re executed is important, too. Hidden Dragon City, as a hub of fortune, provides great benefits to the academy’s talents when executions are carried out here."

"I see," Lin Jing nodded. "When do I start?"

...

The immortal prison controlled by the Demon-Slaying Bureau was located deep underground.

Gao Sheng led Lin Jing through multiple checkpoints until they reached a heavy prison gate. Outside the gate, a middle-aged Core Formation cultivator with a murderous aura awaited them.

"Young Master Gao, is this Lin Jing you mentioned?" The Demon-Slaying Guard glared coldly at Lin Jing.

"Yes... Uncle Zhang, I’ll leave my friend in your care," Gao Sheng replied with a smile.

"It’s a small matter." The guard named Zhang turned to Lin Jing and said, "Lin Jing, I see you’re only at the Qi Refinement stage. This prison is saturated with killing intent, and the prisoners here are all at least Foundation Establishment cultivators, many with countless lives on their hands. Before carrying out executions, shouldn’t you first learn a calming and mind-clearing technique?"

"Otherwise, I’m afraid this place will start to affect you over time. Back when I started, I couldn’t go a day without killing someone," he added grimly.

"I’ve already told him," Gao Sheng said seriously. "Lin Jing, it’s best to cultivate something like the Calming Heart Technique. There’s a collection of techniques here, and Uncle Zhang will help you choose one. These are mandatory for working in this environment."

"That’s right. I currently use the Seven Kills Divine Sword technique to process my killing intent," Zhang added. "But since you’re not planning to stay here long, just pick something easy to learn."

"Thank you." Lin Jing nodded. Although he had prepared pills beforehand, he understood the importance of additional precautions.

Suddenly, a thought occurred to Lin Jing, and he asked, "By the way, do you have any Buddhist techniques here?"

He remembered that a Demon-Slaying Bureau expert had once performed a blessing on him, and cultivating Buddhist techniques might be more effective for him. Since his cultivation aptitude wasn’t particularly high, it would be best to choose something simple to learn.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 39: Buddhist Techniques
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 39: Buddhist Techniques

"Buddhist techniques?"

Hearing Lin Jing's request, Zhang Hu couldn't help but be puzzled.

In the Ancient Celestial Kingdom, although Buddhist followers were scattered across the Nine Domains, their influence was shallow, and their overall power was weaker than even second-tier cultivation sects.

Why would Lin Jing want to cultivate Buddhist techniques? What’s even worth learning in them?

"There are some…" Zhang Hu mused for a moment. Although Buddhism wasn’t mainstream in the Ancient Kingdom, Lin Jing was actually asking the right person.

This was because almost every Buddhist expert who entered the Ancient Kingdom was roped into working for the Demon-Slaying Bureau as guest elders. Naturally, the Demon-Slaying Bureau had amassed a large collection of Buddhist techniques.

"Why would you even want to cultivate that stuff? Aren’t you worried about going bald?" Zhang Hu asked, perplexed. Beside him, Gao Sheng also looked equally puzzled.

"You go bald cultivating Buddhist techniques? Isn’t that because monks shave their heads?" Lin Jing was startled, hearing this for the first time.

Lin Jing didn’t bother hiding anything. "A Buddhist expert once unlocked my potential, so cultivating Buddhist techniques benefits me more and is easier to learn."

"Ah, I see!" Gao Sheng laughed. "You’re so skilled at beast taming; you don’t need to worry about being led astray by Buddhism. Go ahead and cultivate it."

He suddenly paused to think—Lin Jing was sort of half a Gu cultivator. Did that make him a dual cultivator of Buddhism and demonic techniques?

"Cultivating Buddhist techniques is fine, but I recall there are only three such techniques recorded here, far fewer than other methods. Not sure if you’ll like any of them," Zhang Hu replied. "They are the Diamond Fist , the Death Burial Mantra , and the Buddha-Demon Pearl ."

"I’ll explain them to you."

"The Diamond Fist is a martial technique with demon-subduing effects. Once you master it, you’ll be immune to all kinds of evil influences. After performing executions, practicing a set of Diamond Fist techniques can effectively dispel negative effects."

"It’s a bold, powerful technique, especially suited for body cultivators."

"The Death Burial Mantra is a chant recited before killing. It can partially protect you from the negative effects of taking lives, such as murderous energy, resentment, and killing aura, preventing them from clinging to you."

"Reciting the mantra requires some mental energy, though."

"As for the Buddha-Demon Pearl … it’s a sealing technique." Zhang Hu thought for a moment before continuing, "It’s the most effective and high-potential technique among the three."

"Oh?" Lin Jing showed interest. "Senior Zhang, what can you tell me about the Buddha-Demon Pearl ?"

Zhang Hu explained, "The Buddha-Demon Pearl involves condensing a Buddha Pearl within your body. This pearl can seal negative energies like killing aura and demonic energy inside it without affecting you.

"With enough mastery, the pearl can even seal living demons or magical creatures, far surpassing the cultivator’s own strength in terms of sealing power."

"There was once a Buddhist prodigy who suppressed a calamity-bringing Demon God by sacrificing himself and saying, 'If I don’t enter hell, who will?'"

"Many Buddhist disciples cultivate the Buddha-Demon Pearl . However, it has a major drawback: if the sealed negative energy within the pearl isn’t purified in time, the Buddha Pearl can turn into a Demon Pearl, causing the cultivator to fall into demonic ways!"

"There are many tales of demonic, evil, or corrupted Buddhas in Buddhist history. Most of them arose during the cultivation of the Buddha-Demon Pearl when the practitioner was overwhelmed by its power."

"Wait," Lin Jing asked, "does the Buddha Pearl have any additional functions beyond sealing demonic power?"

Zhang Hu nodded. "Not much else, really. The more demonic power it seals, the less it radiates Buddhist qualities. Even when it does display its Buddhist essence, at best it makes you look like a virtuous monk. Not really practical in combat."

"Displaying Buddhist essence might cause wandering ghosts and spirits to avoid you. For mortal monks, that could be useful, but for us cultivators? If we encounter such beings, we just exterminate them!"

"Probably only those pearls from top-tier Buddhist experts who used their lives to seal demons would have divine effects," Zhang Hu added.

"I see," Lin Jing smiled. "That’s great, then! I actually like techniques that prove I’m a good person."

"I’ll cultivate the Buddha-Demon Pearl ."

"As expected," Zhang Hu sighed. "Among the three techniques, this one is indeed the most advanced, so it’s a reasonable choice. But it’s also the most dangerous. While it can help you subdue demonic power, don’t forget to purify the energies sealed inside regularly."

"Don’t worry," Gao Sheng interjected. "With me keeping an eye on him, there won’t be any problems."

Zhang Hu hesitated, glancing at Gao Sheng, finding him somewhat unreliable. However, looking at Lin Jing, who appeared upright and capable, he felt a bit reassured. Lin Jing’s name was familiar to him, and though Zhang Hu rarely entered the city, he had heard some things about him.

...

Soon after, Zhang Hu led Lin Jing and Gao Sheng to a room where they waited while he retrieved the cultivation method for the Buddha-Demon Pearl .

Before long, Zhang Hu returned with a tightly closed Buddhist scripture. It appeared sealed, with no visible pages.

"Lin Jing, the Buddhist techniques left in the Demon-Slaying Bureau are quite unique. They all have seals, and only those deemed ‘fated’ with Buddhism can open them."

"But honestly, this is as pointless as putting pants on over your underwear. I’ve never met anyone who couldn’t open a Buddhist scripture."

"So, everyone is ‘fated with Buddhism,’ huh?" Gao Sheng scoffed. "These Buddhist missionaries sure know how to sell themselves."

Zhang Hu chuckled. "Indeed, while everyone can technically open these scriptures, actually cultivating Buddhist techniques requires talent. Here in the Demon-Slaying Bureau’s immortal prison, almost no one chooses these techniques because they’re much harder to master compared to other exorcism methods. Even those who do select them often take forever to get started."

"But since a Buddhist expert once recognized your potential, you should have no problem cultivating this technique."

He handed the scripture containing the Buddha-Demon Pearl technique to Lin Jing.

Taking it in his hands, Lin Jing immediately opened the scripture without encountering any resistance. As he did, Buddhist light radiated from the text, and the scripture’s glowing words imprinted themselves directly into his mind.

Much like opening a jade slip of cultivation methods, a wealth of information flooded Lin Jing’s consciousness.

Buddha-Demon Pearl!

A single Buddha Pearl, sealing ten thousand demons.

Lin Jing immediately sat cross-legged and began circulating his true energy. To his surprise, the method required merit to form the pearl. Those who hadn’t performed good deeds would struggle to condense one.

"Use the Heart Mantra to guide the power of merit, condensing energy into the pearl," Lin Jing read.

He couldn’t even remember what good deeds he’d done, but as the Heart Mantra activated, traces of merit within his body were drawn out. Images replayed in his mind—donating to disaster relief, giving up his seat to a pregnant woman, rescuing an injured bird that had fallen outside his window. All these small, forgotten acts of kindness had transformed into strands of merit, now merging with his true energy.

Following the method detailed in the scripture, Lin Jing began constructing the pearl. It wasn’t just a simple sphere. Its intricate structure resembled a miniature pearl-shaped immortal prison—deep, complex, and incredibly sturdy.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 40: Dual Cultivation of Buddhism and Demonic Arts
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 40: Dual Cultivation of Buddhism and Demonic Arts

Outside the chamber:

Zhang Hu and Gao Sheng observed Lin Jing, who had been meditating for just a few minutes. To their astonishment, a faint golden glow began to radiate from Lin Jing’s body, making him appear sacred, as if shrouded in Buddhist light.

“Buddhist essence!” Zhang Hu exclaimed in shock. “It hasn’t even been the time it takes to burn one stick of incense, and he’s already condensed a Buddha Pearl? This kind of Buddhist cultivation talent is outrageous. I wonder which Buddhist expert performed the blessing for Lin Jing.”

“Already cultivated it?” Gao Sheng was stunned. After some thought, he said, “I might know who it was—Master Konglin. Thousands of years ago, he joined the Demon-Slaying Bureau for a time. He once sealed an immortal beast in the Great Wasteland but left the Ancient Kingdom long ago to return to the Western Deserts. There hasn’t been news of him for ages. Who knows if he’s still alive?”

“If he is alive, his cultivation level must have reached an incredible stage,” Gao Sheng speculated.

Nascent Soul cultivators have a lifespan of a thousand years, Soul Transformation cultivators two thousand, the Void Refinement stage five thousand, and the Dao Inquiry stage ten thousand. Gao Sheng calculated that if Master Konglin’s final cultivation was at the Soul Transformation stage, he would likely already be dead. If he had advanced to the Void Refinement stage, he might still be alive.

Thinking of this, Gao Sheng couldn’t help but envy Lin Jing. This guy had a Longevity Body; his lifespan as a Nascent Soul cultivator would rival the strongest beings on the continent. It was truly something to be jealous of.

As the two conversed via voice transmission, Lin Jing opened his eyes. Golden light flickered in his pupils.

“Congratulations, congratulations!” Gao Sheng quickly smiled, though he scratched his head while looking at Lin Jing, who was still glowing. “But isn’t your Buddhist talent a bit too absurd? It feels like you didn’t do anything, yet your Buddhist essence is still increasing.”

Lin Jing remained silent, sensing the situation in his body. Didn’t do anything?

The power leaking from the Ice Soul Gu sealed within him, which had always required his Ice Soul Physique to neutralize, was now automatically being absorbed and sealed by the Buddha Pearl.

The Ice Soul Gu was created through countless venomous insects refined with Gu techniques, often involving cruel and sinister methods. It was natural for the Buddha Pearl to classify its energy as demonic power.

This meant that the Buddha Pearl was sealing the Ice Soul Gu’s energy within him, passively generating immense Buddhist essence and merit. Lin Jing realized that he didn’t have to do anything, yet his aura exuded divine righteousness.

“It’s your imagination,” Lin Jing said flatly. Gao Sheng liked deducing things, so let him piece it together. Gao Sheng already knew about the Ice Soul Gu sealed within Lin Jing, so it shouldn’t be hard for him to figure out that Lin Jing was essentially suppressing a demon within himself.

The Buddha-Demon Pearl truly was a perfect fit for Lin Jing.

“Really?” Gao Sheng rubbed his chin thoughtfully. At that moment, Zhang Hu interrupted, “Since you’ve already succeeded in cultivating it, would you like to test the Buddha Pearl’s abilities by executing a death row prisoner?”

“Please arrange it, Senior Zhang,” Lin Jing replied with a bow. He was eager to execute a hundred prisoners right now so he could claim the pine cones from Senior Pine.

Execution was still killing, and Senior Pine couldn’t deny that, could he?

Deeper into the prison:

Zhang Hu led Lin Jing deeper into the execution chamber. Guards stationed along the way maintained their stoic expressions, though they stood at attention in deference to Zhang Hu. With Zhang Hu’s status, their path was unimpeded.

“The prisoner in Execution Room Fourteen is scheduled for today,” Zhang Hu said.

“From which sect? Yin Corpse Sect? Gu God Sect? Or the Netherworld Hall?” Gao Sheng asked curiously.

Lin Jing shook his head. “It’s rare to capture a demonic cultivator from one of the Eight Great Demonic Sects. Most are from smaller ones.”

“This particular prisoner was once an elder at the Great Perfection of the Foundation Establishment stage in the Heavenly River Sect . Due to his potential to form a Golden Core, he sought out the ‘Blood Pill Technique’ from the remnants of the Blood God Sect. This technique required the blood essence of a hundred Foundation Establishment cultivators, a thousand Qi Refinement cultivators, and ten thousand mortals to refine pills.”

“He targeted rogue cultivators, wandering between cities and secretly murdering them. By the time he was captured, his hands were stained with countless lives. His crimes are unforgivable.”

“He deserves to die,” Lin Jing said coldly, furrowing his brows. “Senior Zhang, should I just go in directly?”

He glanced at the stone prison gate, which bore the pattern of a divine tiger.

“That demonic cultivator has been drained of spiritual power and is restrained on the execution platform. You may use any method to kill him,” Zhang Hu replied. “Afterward, throw his corpse into the incinerator behind the chamber.”

Lin Jing nodded and pushed open the heavy prison gate. Inside the dimly lit execution chamber, a red-haired elder was chained to the execution platform.

As Lin Jing entered, the elder slowly opened his eyes, lifeless yet filled with malice.

Boom!

A surge of killing intent exploded towards Lin Jing. Despite containing no spiritual energy, it overwhelmed him with visions of blood and slaughter.

“To think they sent a mere Qi Refinement brat to execute me…” the elder muttered weakly. Before he could finish his sentence, his head was severed, two white strands of energy wrapping around the stump of his neck.

The severed head fell to the ground, its lifeless eyes wide open.

Lin Jing looked back at the head. This was his first time killing someone, yet he felt no discomfort.

The next moment, he sensed a faint wisp of resentment clinging to him. This invisible, intangible force wouldn’t harm a cultivator in small amounts, but if it accumulated, it could eventually create inner demons and disrupt one’s mental state during critical moments.

“I’m just doing my job as an executioner. Why blame me?” Lin Jing muttered. He sealed the resentment into the Buddha Pearl, then used a strand of true energy to toss the elder’s corpse into the incinerator behind the execution chamber.

After neatly finishing the task, Lin Jing didn’t linger and left the chamber.

“How do you feel?” Zhang Hu asked as Lin Jing emerged.

“Pretty good, no discomfort,” Lin Jing replied with a smile. For some reason, killing this person left him feeling unexpectedly satisfied.

“That’s good,” Zhang Hu said. “If that’s the case, how about I arrange one death row prisoner for you every seven days?”

“Once every seven days? Senior Zhang, can we make it seven prisoners a day?” Lin Jing asked, frowning. At this rate, it would take him two years to meet Senior Pine’s requirements!

“Seven a day? What do you think this is, a pig slaughterhouse?” Zhang Hu snapped.

Lin Jing sighed. He was already 27 years old.

Although this was still very young for someone with a Longevity Body, the Genius Rankings for the Human List only accepted cultivators under 30. This meant he had just three years to help the Pine Leaf Mouse reach Foundation Establishment, evolve its bloodline, and attempt to challenge the Genius Rankings.

If he missed this chance, he would lose out on the massive recognition the Genius Rankings brought across the continent. This would severely limit the progress of his Hidden Dragon Arts, and his Flame Dragon Carp wouldn’t be able to achieve a perfect transformation.

After negotiating, they settled on one execution per day. This way, Lin Jing could complete Senior Pine’s task in about three months.

Outside the prison:

As Lin Jing exited the immortal prison, Senior Pine, who had been secretly observing, snorted.

“This sly brat… Fine, let it slide. Burning? He learned that. Killing? He’s tried it. But as a demonic cultivator, he still hasn’t learned how to rob. Next time he wants my help, I’ll make him go loot something! Always handing out pills to others during duels without gaining anything. No wonder he’s poor. Wasting the spatial gift of that squirrel!”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 41: Prison Break?
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 41: Prison Break?

In the following days, Lin Jing executed a demonic cultivator every day. During the remaining time, he focused on managing the production of the Rising Dragon Pills refined by the Flame Dragon Carp and recruiting new members.

The Pine Leaf Mouse cultivated fields, the Flame Dragon Carp refined pills, and Lin Jing handled pre-sales. Before long, the orders were overflowing, but due to the Flame Dragon Carp's limited production capacity, Lin Jing prioritized selling to those willing to join the Dragon Palace.

Meanwhile, in the capital city of the Ancient Celestial Kingdom, Tianyuan Immortal City, lay the residence of the Wang family, a prominent family of cultivators. As an old and established cultivation clan, the Wang family’s ancestor had been a great cultivator in the Void Profound Stage, famous across the kingdom. Though that ancestor had long passed, a Peak Nascent Soul ancestor still presided over the family.

Wang Xiao, the most talented junior of the current generation, was highly valued by the family.

“Xiao’er, why have you returned?” Wang Xiao’s father asked when he saw him returning home one day.

Wang Xiao paused before replying, “I’ve come to retrieve a Battle Tiger.”

The Battle Tigers, known for their combat prowess and extraordinary beast bloodline, were the mounts of Wang family disciples. Some were even capable of momentarily standing against King-tier Beasts. When Wang Xiao had left for the Hidden Dragon Academy, his father had asked if he needed a mount, but Wang Xiao had declined.

Now, his request for a Battle Tiger left his father puzzled.

“Investigate what happened in the Hidden Dragon Academy,” the family head commanded.

The Shadow Guard soon returned. “Master, word has spread in Hidden Dragon City about a duel involving the Divine Son…”

“Xiao’er lost?” The Wang family head showed little surprise. He had sent Wang Xiao to the academy precisely to temper him.

Though the Heavenly God Physique was strong, it ranked only around 90th among the Physique Rankings. For the Wang family, it was decent, but far from the strongest within the academy, let alone the cultivation world.

The academy housed numerous divine physiques, some of which had once been listed in the Physique Rankings, like the Talisman Physique, which later fell off the list. The differences between such physiques were not as significant as imagined; their strength depended largely on the wielder.

Having anticipated setbacks for Wang Xiao, his father was mentally prepared.

“He didn’t lose. It was a draw,” the Shadow Guard replied.

“A draw? With whom? For Xiao’er to return home and seek a War Beast to strengthen himself? He once dismissed King Bloodline Demonic Beasts as insignificant.”

The Shadow Guard hesitated before saying, “The Divine Son… drew with Lin Jing of the Beast Taming Sect.”

“Lin Jing? The one at Qi Refinement Stage 10 with both himself and his pets?” The Wang family head was stunned. “He broke through to Foundation Establishment already? The Beast Taming Sect must have undergone a revival, producing a genius with great fortune. I’ve met this Lin Jing before—quite remarkable. If Xiao’er fought him to a draw, it’s plausible.”

“Xiao’er must have fought against multiple enemies and felt frustrated, so he sought a mount to assist him,” the family head concluded, shaking his head.

“Master, that’s not the case,” the Shadow Guard interjected. “Rumors say the Divine Son… drew with a newly trained Qi Refinement Stage Flame Dragon Carp of Lin Jing’s. Lin Jing’s contracted Pine Leaf Mouse didn’t even fight, nor did Lin Jing himself, who hasn’t broken through to Foundation Establishment. The Divine Son suppressed his cultivation level for the duel, witnessed by many.”

The Wang family head: ???

“My son lost to a carp? HAHAHAHA! Impossible! Absolutely impossible! Investigate further and report back!” Wang Xiao’s father waved his hand, laughing uproariously.

After taking a Battle Tiger with King-level bloodline, Wang Xiao disappeared. His whereabouts remained unknown, but under the rising influence of his name, Lin Jing’s Dragon Palace continued to grow.

In front of Lin Jing’s cave residence, Xu Zhizhi, dressed like a strategist, reported, “In the past three months, the Dragon Palace has recruited 24 members, including 20 new Foundation Establishment disciples at the early stage and four seniors at the mid-stage.”

“All 24 have now obtained Dragon-Blooded Pets and are training them rigorously, preparing to participate in the Autumn Hunt.”

“Not bad.” Lin Jing gazed at the morning sun and instructed, “If they perform well in the Autumn Hunt, more people will pre-order the Rising Dragon Pills. Tell them to focus not only on training their pets but also on improving their own strength. Pet and owner must grow together for optimal combat power.”

He handed Xu Zhizhi a manual. “Distribute this Dragon-Blooded Pet Training Guide to them and let them know that outstanding performance in the Autumn Hunt will earn them free Rising Dragon Pills as rewards.”

In the past three months, Lin Jing had sold Rising Dragon Pills to every genius who joined the Dragon Palace. A few impoverished ones couldn’t afford them, as acquiring pills, buying pets, and preparing to control them required a large amount of Spirit Stones.

Out of goodwill, Lin Jing offered his test beasts, such as the Dragon-Scaled Caterpillar, at low prices. For non-members, Lin Jing withheld sales, planning to sell only after the Autumn Hunt.

The Rising Dragon Pill business was immensely profitable. Each sale yielded significant profits, and the pets’ training required a continuous supply of pills. Lin Jing envisioned a flourishing future for the Dragon Palace.

Currently, the four deacons of the Dragon Palace were Gao Sheng, Xu Zhizhi, Yuan Jing, and Ziling’er.

Initially, Gao Sheng wanted to be a strategist but was persuaded otherwise by Lin Jing. Xu Zhizhi managed publicity and strategic planning, discussing affairs with Lin Jing. Yuan Jing and Gao Sheng served as core combat strength, while Ziling’er was being groomed to handle the pill refinement for Rising Dragon Pills, ensuring the recipe wouldn’t spread since the core ingredients were with the Pine Leaf Mouse.

However, Lin Jing regretted not being able to recruit Wang Xiao and Chen Lingxi, two of the academy’s strongest freshmen. Chen Lingxi had already joined another organization.

After sending off Xu Zhizhi, Lin Jing left the academy and headed for the Demon-Slaying Bureau’s underground immortal prison.

Today marked the hundredth day of his executions. With one more kill, he could obtain a Pine Cone.

Over the past three months, the Flame Dragon Carp had rapidly improved its pill-refining techniques, nearing mastery of Foundation Establishment Pills. Once Lin Jing obtained the Pine Cone, they could begin attempting the Longevity Foundation Pill.

In the execution chamber, Lin Jing used Diamond Fist to crush the head of the demonic cultivator for the day.

Diamond Fist was a Buddhist technique Lin Jing had recently cultivated. He discovered that the Buddhist Pearl, which suppressed the Ice Soul Gu, emitted a Buddhist aura that enhanced his ordinary punches and kicks. Through experimenting with Diamond Fist, Lin Jing found that strong Buddhist energy amplified the technique’s power, surpassing conventional spells.

Thus, his recent executions often involved the Diamond Fist, integrating punches, palms, and finger techniques to enhance both his strength and physique.

The technique not only subdued demons but also tempered the body.

“Thank you, Senior Zhang, for looking after me these past few months,” Lin Jing said, bidding farewell to Zhang Hu, a Demon-Slaying Guard.

“No need for thanks,” Zhang Hu laughed. “I’ll escort you out.”

“To be honest, I wish I could keep you here. You’re the best executioner I’ve seen in this place. Most Foundation Establishment cultivators would lose their sanity after three months of high-intensity work here, but you look unaffected.”

Lin Jing smiled, taking it as a compliment.

As Zhang Hu accompanied Lin Jing aboveground, they were about to part ways when Lin Jing’s expression suddenly changed. His body was flooded with warnings.

The sky shifted.

A massive void vortex appeared above, and a gigantic claw emerged from within. Zhang Hu’s face turned pale with shock.

“Someone’s breaking into the prison?!” he exclaimed.

Lin Jing froze, feeling a life-threatening danger. “Why would someone attack this prison? The strongest here are only Golden Core demons… This is aimed at me!”

In that moment, Lin Jing’s mind raced to identify who might be coming for him.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 42: The Southern Mountain Demon Emperor
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 42: The Southern Mountain Demon Emperor

On the Tianyuan Continent, one of the ten most perilous regions is the Mist Forest. Spanning half of the Ancient Celestial Kingdom's borders, this colossal forest is an unavoidable passage for anyone traveling from the kingdom to Qingzhou.

There had been attempts by great cultivators in the Void Profound Stage to construct a spatial teleportation channel between the Ancient Celestial Kingdom and Qingzhou, but the peculiar mists pervading the forest were even capable of penetrating space, often causing people to get lost in the void.

Thus, traversing the Mist Forest on foot was the only option, a path fraught with immense danger.

If the Ancient Celestial Kingdom is the land where human destiny converges, the Mist Forest serves as a paradise for demonic beasts.

To date, only the Lotus Pavilion is rumored to have a relatively stable method of crossing the Mist Forest. Many cultivators from the kingdom rely on accompanying Lotus Pavilion caravans when traveling to Qingzhou.

Deep in the Mist Forest, a colossal dragon resembling a mountain and covered in Malignant Plants suddenly opened its sun-and-moon-like eyes. It fixed its gaze on a human cultivator draped entirely in white corpse cloth, whose face was obscured.

“Protector Yuan Buyi of the Yin Corpse Sect greets the Southern Mountain Demon Emperor,” said the corpse-cloaked cultivator as he bowed.

“You of the Yin Corpse Sect... Are you planning to set your sights on my body?” The massive dragon’s voice rumbled, deep and menacing.

“I’ve come today only to propose a deal. Your Majesty, are you interested in living another lifetime?”

The Southern Mountain Demon Emperor’s massive dragon eyes narrowed as it stared intently at the protector. “The Longevity Body of a human? After so long, another one with this physique has emerged?”

“Speak. What are your terms?” The dragon’s heavy breathing betrayed its excitement. Its days were numbered, and all means of extending its life had failed. But the Longevity Body… that might be different.

“The body of one of your molted dragon forms,” the protector replied.

The demon emperor snorted coldly, its tone dripping with disdain. So, it still boiled down to coveting its body!

“The information on the Longevity Body…”

“The Yin Corpse Sect guarantees to provide it exclusively to Your Majesty. Our sect's reputation, as you are well aware, is built upon never violating contracts. This has been the foundation of our standing for tens of thousands of years and our cooperation with all races.”

The demon emperor growled, “Speak then. Where is this Longevity Body? This deal, I will accept.”

The protector replied, “The Longevity Body originally belonged to a disciple of the now-dissolved Beast Taming Sect in the kingdom’s Great Wasteland Domain. The individual is currently in Hidden Dragon City within the Ancient Domain.”

“Hidden Dragon City?” The demon emperor let out another cold snort. “The city is guarded by Void Profound cultivators. Are you asking me to seek death?”

The protector chuckled. “Everyone knows that during its prime, Your Majesty’s cultivation was at the Half-Step Void Profound Stage, only a step away from the Refining Void Stage. Although your current realm has fallen, your spatial comprehension remains intact.”

“Allow me to offer Your Majesty a bonus piece of intelligence: the three deans of the Hidden Dragon Academy have been absent from Hidden Dragon City for the past two years, and they won’t be returning for a long time.”

“Currently, the academy is only guarded by some ordinary Nascent Soul cultivators. These individuals merely safeguard the academy and are not protectors of the Longevity Body.”

“Moreover, this Longevity Body is merely a small sect disciple, not the heir of a major family. The level of protection it enjoys will naturally be low. Those guarding the academy wouldn’t expend energy constantly monitoring his situation. As long as Your Majesty avoids the academy and acts within the city, they won’t react immediately.”

“With your skills, snatching the Longevity Body and escaping instantly should be within your grasp. Of course, there will be repercussions, but compared to extending your life into a seventh cycle, isn’t it worth it?”

“Of course, Your Majesty could also wait until the Longevity Body leaves Hidden Dragon City—if you still have the time and patience…”

Not long after, the protector left the Mist Forest and returned to a dark underground altar.

“Elder, the information about the Longevity Body has been passed to the Southern Mountain Demon Emperor,” he reported to a purple-haired elder lurking in the shadows.

“That demon emperor is nearing its end and surely won’t miss this opportunity. Using the Longevity Body as bait will undoubtedly cause chaos in Hidden Dragon City,” the elder remarked with satisfaction.

“This way, those three old monsters should return to Hidden Dragon City to deal with the situation.”

“A genius being taken by a demonic beast within the city… That would indeed be a major scandal for the academy,” the protector said.

“Exactly. Though our branch in the Great Wasteland Domain failed to capture the Longevity Body, passing the information upward was enough to make it useful. The only question is whether the demon emperor can successfully abduct the Longevity Body…”

“During its prime, the Southern Mountain Demon Emperor was at the Half-Step Void Profound Stage. Though its cultivation has now dropped to the Nascent Soul early stage due to age, its spatial comprehension remains formidable. Escaping Hidden Dragon City unscathed should still be possible,” the elder explained.

“After all, the city’s top cultivators are already being preoccupied by our sect’s forces. Since these kingdom experts have set their sights on undermining our ten-thousand-year plan, it’s only fitting that we create some trouble for them in return.”

Ancient Domain, Hidden Dragon City

The Southern Mountain Demon Emperor, once resigned to its fate and preparing to use its final strength to produce a descendant with its Demon Emperor Bloodline, was now rekindled with hope.

It stealthily entered the Ancient Celestial Kingdom, moving through the void toward Hidden Dragon City. Thanks to its profound spatial comprehension, cultivated at its peak, it avoided detection.

Upon confirming that no Void Profound cultivators were present in the city, the demon emperor was thrilled. However, to be cautious, it refrained from entering the city with its true body, fearing any hidden contingency plans left by a Void Profound expert.

“If there’s no Void Profound guardian, only ordinary Nascent Soul cultivators, I don’t need my true body. A Half-Divine Avatar will suffice to seize the Longevity Body,” the demon emperor decided.

Through its failing lifespan, the Longevity Body appeared to the demon emperor as a dazzling flame of vitality.

“Found it!”

The Southern Mountain Demon Emperor quickly locked onto Lin Jing, who had just left the Demon-Slaying Bureau’s immortal prison. Lin Jing’s aura was so enticing that it drove the demon emperor to madness.

Delicious!

From its hidden position in the void, the demon emperor struck. A massive vortex appeared in the sky, releasing demonic energy as a gigantic dragon claw reached out toward Lin Jing.

However, just as the attack began, the demon emperor sensed a terrifying pressure approaching.

“Who dares snatch what belongs to this old man?!”

The demon emperor was stunned. How had the academy’s Nascent Soul cultivators reacted so quickly? And more importantly, this person’s spatial comprehension was astonishing!

Before the demon emperor could react, its hundred-meter-long Half-Divine Avatar and extended dragon claw were entangled by towering tree branches, which appeared seemingly out of nowhere and tightly restrained it.

“This power…” The demon emperor’s expression grew grim.

“A Tree Spirit at the Nascent Soul Stage?! Why is there a tree deity here?!”

Among Nascent Soul cultivators, tree spirits were known to be immensely powerful. Their cultivation was against the natural order, and those who succeeded were incredibly formidable. In the current era, they even surpassed dragons of equivalent rank.

“The damned Yin Corpse Sect! Didn’t they claim the strongest protector around the Longevity Body was at most a late Nascent Soul demonic beast? Where did this tree deity come from?!”

Meanwhile, Lin Jing and Zhang Hu, overwhelmed by the immense pressure, stared as a massive dragon claw extended from the void, only to be pulled back by enormous tree roots.

“Damn fool! You’ve exposed my cultivation! Now I won’t have a moment’s peace again!” Shen Song, the divine pine, muttered angrily.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 43: The Nascent Soul Divine Pine
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 43: The Nascent Soul Divine Pine

“What is going on here?!”

Zhang Hu stared at the sky in shock, feeling a terror he had never experienced before. As a late-stage Golden Core cultivator, he had interacted with many Nascent Soul-level experts, but the sheer power radiating from that massive dragon claw and the binding tree roots far exceeded Nascent Soul capabilities.

“…Could it be two Nascent Soul Transformation experts battling?” he muttered.

The Southern Mountain Demon Emperor had come for Lin Jing, whose senses were even sharper. The moment the Hidden Dragon Technique warned of danger, Lin Jing’s heart raced. However, his expression quickly turned strange as he realized what was happening.

“Shen Song…”

He had also heard the Southern Mountain Demon Emperor’s words. A Wood Spirit at the Nascent Soul Transformation Stage—that could only mean the Divine Pine that had been protecting him all along!

“Senior Shen Song is at the Nascent Soul Transformation Stage?!”

Lin Jing had always assumed Shen Song’s cultivation level was at the Peak Nascent Soul Stage, as reaching the Nascent Soul Transformation Stage was exceedingly difficult. Even in the vast Great Wasteland, with its hundreds of cultivation sects, not a single one had produced a Nascent Soul Transformation expert.

Even the Beast Taming Sect likely had no idea that Shen Song had already broken through to the Nascent Soul Transformation Stage. The old Divine Pine had concealed its strength for possibly centuries or even millennia.

Lin Jing couldn’t help but marvel at Shen Song’s ability to remain hidden. To suppress such strength and avoid revealing oneself as a Nascent Soul Transformation expert must have required incredible patience!

Despite the attack, Lin Jing’s initial panic quickly turned to calm. With Shen Song taking action, his heart settled completely.

“Who dares cause trouble in the Demon-Slaying Bureau?!”

Moments later, a Nascent Soul cultivator emerged from the bureau’s underground immortal prison. This cultivator, who was at the Peak Nascent Soul Stage, was already formidable. However, upon sensing the powerful primordial spirits of both the Southern Mountain Demon Emperor and the Divine Pine, his expression immediately changed.

As the Yin Corpse Sect had predicted, the academy’s true experts had not responded immediately. However, Shen Song’s intervention bought precious time for the academy’s powerhouses to react.

Boom! Boom! Boom! Boom!

Four overwhelming Nascent Soul Transformation auras erupted, their divine radiance illuminating the sky. Along with their immense power came the energy of over a dozen Peak Nascent Soul cultivators, their combined presence awe-inspiring. In an instant, they descended upon the immortal prison of the Demon-Slaying Bureau.

“Southern Mountain Demon Emperor!”

The four Nascent Soul Transformation experts had all heard of the demon emperor’s notorious name.

“Don’t look at me,” a booming voice echoed from the void. “I am merely the guardian Divine Pine of academy student Lin Jing. This is only one of the demon emperor’s avatars. Go after its true body!”

The four experts scanned the area, their divine eyes retracing the events as if time itself had rewound. In an instant, they understood the situation.

Though they were unsure why a mere disciple from the Beast Taming Sect had a Nascent Soul Transformation-level Wood Spirit as his guardian, the priority now was clear: they needed to eliminate the demon emperor’s true body!

“Thank you for your intervention,” the four experts said in unison, bowing slightly before activating their divine bodies, which radiated with divine light. Four avatars spread across Hidden Dragon City, forming a city-protecting barrier, while their true forms pursued the Southern Mountain Demon Emperor beyond the city.

The demon emperor was a wanted criminal within the kingdom, and now it had dared to intrude and attempt to abduct a genius cultivator. Letting it escape would bring disgrace to the city and the academy.

“Lin Jing, are you unharmed?”

Cao Ziwei, an academy instructor, landed beside Lin Jing, his expression filled with concern and astonishment. He was well aware of Shen Song’s existence. When he had escorted Lin Jing from the Great Wasteland to the academy, he had already sensed the presence of a tree spirit as Lin Jing’s guardian.

However, back then, he had only perceived Shen Song as a Nascent Soul-level spirit. A Nascent Soul-level tree spirit and a Wood Spirit at the Nascent Soul Transformation Stage were worlds apart.

“I’m fine, thanks to Senior Shen Song’s assistance,” Lin Jing replied, breathing a sigh of relief.

Moments later, the Southern Mountain Demon Emperor’s Half-Divine Avatar was obliterated by Shen Song. Its dying screams reverberated through the void, shaking the entire Hidden Dragon City and leaving countless cultivators stunned.

From the void, a massive golden-green pine cone emerged, radiating divine brilliance. Upon seeing it, the assembled Peak Nascent Soul cultivators immediately bowed respectfully.

“This demon emperor came for him,” Shen Song said coldly, his voice filled with annoyance. “I also suspect this was a distraction tactic. Where are the academy’s top experts? Why are they absent from the city?”

Before anyone could respond, Shen Song enveloped Lin Jing in his branches and vanished, saying, “I’ll hide him away for now. Once you deal with the demon emperor, we’ll come out again!”

In the blink of an eye, Lin Jing found himself in an unfamiliar location. Surrounded by radiant mist, he saw the towering figure of Shen Song standing tall amid the haze. Before the Divine Pine lay the massive corpse of a dragon.

“Senior Shen Song?” Lin Jing asked, his voice filled with confusion.

“This demon emperor came for your Longevity Body,” Shen Song replied bluntly. “From the state of its avatar, its lifespan is nearly exhausted.”

Lin Jing nodded. “I thought as much. It must be because of my Longevity Body. Still, I can’t believe it dared to come to Hidden Dragon City…”

“It almost succeeded,” Shen Song snorted. “With the academy’s top experts absent, the demon emperor saw an opportunity to exploit the situation. Its spatial comprehension is indeed remarkable. If I hadn’t had some fortuitous encounters in my youth, allowing me to gain insights into the Dao of Space, it wouldn’t have been easy to deal with it.”

Lin Jing paused before asking, “Senior, are you truly at the Nascent Soul Transformation Stage? Did you break through back when you were with the Beast Taming Sect?”

Shen Song’s voice turned exasperated. “Are you trying to blame me for the sect’s downfall? Claiming that the Beast Taming Sect’s demonic fortune outweighed its human fortune because of me?”

“Absolute nonsense! I never even joined the Beast Taming Sect! I merely rested on its territory for thousands of years and protected it out of convenience. Blame your sect’s numerous unaligned demonic kings, not me!”
