“Of course not, Senior!” Lin Jing quickly waved his hands, a smile spreading across his face. Learning that Shen Song had reached the Nascent Soul Transformation Stage only filled him with joy. “But, Senior, if you’re so powerful, why have you hidden your cultivation all this time?”

“You think I want to hide?” Shen Song snapped. “To humans, I may not be as enticing as you are to demonic beasts, but I’m still a treasure tree!”

“Do you know the shortcut most cultivators take to break through from Peak Nascent Soul to the Nascent Soul Transformation Stage?”

Lin Jing shook his head.

“After reaching Peak Nascent Soul, the key to advancing is mastering the natural forces of heaven and earth. Only by comprehending these forces and fusing them into one’s primordial spirit can one achieve transformation.

“But comprehending natural forces is incredibly difficult. Out of a hundred Peak Nascent Soul cultivators, not even one may succeed. Only those with special physiques, like that Heavenly God Physique brat you fought, have an easier time.”

“Some idiot discovered a shortcut: use supreme-grade vegetation nurtured by heaven and earth to craft a body highly attuned to natural forces. Then, a Peak Nascent Soul cultivator’s primordial spirit could occupy this body, fusing with it to gain insights and achieve transformation!”

“Over the years, most Nascent Soul breakthroughs have used this method. That’s why you saw those four old geezers earlier—they all have two bodies!”

“I, however, naturally comprehended the forces of heaven and earth, making me an ideal material for crafting such a vessel. If I wandered around freely, I wouldn’t even know how I died!”

Lin Jing’s mind buzzed. Using vegetation and Nascent Soul cultivators to craft new bodies? He’d heard of such things before. Nezha, was it?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 44: Before Foundation Establishment
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 44: Before Foundation Establishment

“I deeply apologize for exposing your cultivation, Senior…”

Having learned why Shen Song wished to remain hidden, Lin Jing felt an overwhelming sense of guilt.

“No matter,” Shen Song replied calmly. “I already considered the possibility of being exposed when I agreed to protect you for a hundred years.”

“If someone dares to try and use me as a material to craft a Nascent Soul Transformation avatar, let them see if they even have the ability to pull it off. I didn’t grow this tall by eating meat!”

Taking advantage of the moment, Lin Jing finally voiced a question that had been lingering in his mind. Shen Song had always referred to himself as a Wood Spirit Demonic Cultivator and possessed such an incredible cultivation level. What kind of connection did he have with the Second Sect Master of the Beast Taming Sect? Why had he remained by the sect’s side for so many years and even agreed to protect Lin Jing?

“Why are you so curious about everything?” Shen Song snorted. “Knowing too much isn’t good for you. Just focus on cultivating and raising your strength.”

“You’ve been in seclusion for three years with no improvement in cultivation. Honestly, I’m doubtful whether you’ll even reach the Golden Core Stage within the hundred-year time limit,” he added skeptically.

Lin Jing immediately shut his mouth, not daring to continue the conversation.

“After all, a demon emperor nearing the end of its lifespan may seem weak, but it’s precisely because it’s on its last legs that it fights like it has nothing to lose—dragging everyone down with it!”

“The Southern Mountain Demon Emperor is dead?” Lin Jing was stunned. He hadn’t expected the academy’s powerhouses to act so quickly.

“They likely had some external assistance,” Shen Song speculated. “Unfortunately, I couldn’t personally chase after it while protecting you. A demon emperor’s body is a treasure trove—especially one as rare as a dragon demon emperor.”

“Still, even its Half-Divine Avatar wasn’t bad. No wonder those two Demon-Slaying Bureau folks wanted to use you as bait—it’s truly satisfying!” Shen Song chuckled darkly.

Demonic cultivation stages:

The bloodline of a demon emperor was far superior to that of a demon king. And a dragon demon emperor like the Southern Mountain Demon Emperor, infamous for its savagery, was even more extraordinary.

Back in Hidden Dragon City, the large-scale mobilization of academy powerhouses and the activation of the city’s barrier formation had left many cultivators stunned. Such an event hadn’t occurred in a long time, leading to widespread speculation about what had happened.

Upon returning to the academy, Lin Jing was immediately summoned to a council chamber. At the head of the room sat an elderly man with a goatee. He appeared entirely ordinary, like a mortal without any distinct aura.

Seated on either side of the hall were the four Nascent Soul Transformation experts Lin Jing had seen earlier. As for the elderly man at the head of the table...

He was one of the three vice deans of Hidden Dragon Academy, and Lin Jing surmised that he was likely the one who had slain the Southern Mountain Demon Emperor.

“So, you are Lin Jing?” The goateed elder scrutinized Lin Jing and nodded slightly. “A Longevity Body, huh? It’s been a long time since one of your physique has appeared. No wonder the Southern Mountain Demon Emperor went mad trying to seize you.”

“You’ve done a decent job keeping your abilities hidden. However, the Yin Corpse Sect already possesses information on your Longevity Body. From the memories we extracted from the demon emperor, it was they who leaked the intelligence.”

“Even though the demon emperor is dead, your Longevity Body’s existence will likely be sold to others by the Yin Corpse Sect. Your life as a cultivator won’t be easy from here on out.”

Lin Jing forced a bitter smile.

“Still, you should thank the junior cultivators who helped slay the Southern Mountain Demon Emperor. As for what comes next, we’ll take things one step at a time.”

“You needn’t worry too much,” the vice dean said with a faint smile. “Your guardian is quite extraordinary. To break through as a Wood Spirit to the Nascent Soul Transformation Stage in such a hostile era is no small feat. With it protecting you, your safety shouldn’t be significantly compromised.”

“Additionally, this incident, where the Southern Mountain Demon Emperor exploited the academy’s lapse in security, was the academy’s fault. Going forward, we will strengthen Hidden Dragon City’s defenses. As long as you remain in the city, such incidents shouldn’t happen again.”

After comforting Lin Jing, the academy powerhouses dismissed him.

Once Lin Jing left, the atmosphere in the council chamber turned somber.

One of the female Nascent Soul Transformation experts looked at the vice dean and said, “Vice Dean, this is clearly an act of retaliation by the Yin Corpse Sect.”

“I know,” the goateed elder replied grimly. “The Yin Corpse Sect’s goal of refining a Celestial Corpse cannot be allowed to succeed.”

“If not for the recent demon-cleansing campaigns, we wouldn’t have discovered that they possess the corpse of a celestial beast. If they successfully refine it into a corpse puppet, the consequences will be catastrophic.”

“Currently, most of the kingdom’s powerhouses are focused on eradicating the Yin Corpse Sect. The fact that they resorted to luring a demon emperor to Hidden Dragon City shows that their situation is dire.”

“As for this Longevity Body… There’s no need for us to give it excessive attention,” the vice dean said with a shake of his head.

While a Longevity Body in the Qi Refinement Stage was relatively easy to protect, its allure was limited unless a demon emperor was nearing the end of its lifespan. But as the Longevity Body grew stronger, it would attract far more dangerous threats, including demon sovereigns.

Without a Dao-Seeking powerhouse as its guardian, Lin Jing’s cultivation journey would inevitably be fraught with peril.

Moreover, cultivating a Longevity Body was notoriously slow.

Aside from smaller sects like the Beast Taming Sect, which lacked broader experience, most top-tier experts were unwilling to invest resources into nurturing one. The risks of it being consumed were simply too high.

Even the vice dean had no intention of devoting himself to Lin Jing’s protection. It was too difficult and exhausting. Success would ultimately depend on Lin Jing’s own fortune.

Back in his cave residence, Lin Jing suddenly remembered that he had forgotten to ask Shen Song for a pine cone.

The earlier commotion had halted the Flame Dragon Carp’s pill refining and the Pine Leaf Mouse’s farming. Both of them had been anxiously watching from the hilltop. Upon seeing Lin Jing return safely, they sighed in relief and hugged each other.

“Come on, let’s go make pills!” Lin Jing declared cheerfully, concealing the troubles from earlier. He dragged the two of them along and called for Shen Song, eager to begin refining the Longevity Foundation Pill.

With Shen Song’s Nascent Soul Transformation cultivation, its pine cone would surely be extraordinary. Incorporating it into the pill would produce unimaginable results—especially for the Pine Leaf Mouse’s Foundation Establishment!

“So, you want a pine cone?” Shen Song appeared, manifesting as a pine cone, and sighed. “Fine, fine. It’s about time for you to break through to Foundation Establishment. I’ve been waiting long enough.”

“Senior, please grant us the pine cone and stand guard while we refine the pill,” Lin Jing requested.

“No rush.” Shen Song planted a small sapling near Lin Jing’s cave. The sapling emitted a faint red glow, exuding a strange aura. “This sapling contains my essence. It will grow a few Third-Grade Pine Cones. The higher-grade ones are beyond your ability to refine, but these will suffice.”

“It also functions like a Bloodsucking Vine. From now on, you’ll need to water it with your blood,” Shen Song added.

“Understood!” Lin Jing replied decisively, glancing at the Pine Leaf Mouse. If it could achieve a perfect Foundation Establishment, everything would be worth it.

“Senior, with this, will the Pine Leaf Mouse’s bloodline surpass King-tier and reach Emperor-tier?” Lin Jing asked hopefully.

“Impossible,” Shen Song replied bluntly. “It might surpass the Flame Dragon Carp’s bloodline and approach the current level of the Ice Soul Gu, but only the Ice Soul Gu has the potential to evolve to Emperor-tier during Foundation Establishment. The other two simply lack the innate talent.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 45: Refining
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 45: Refining

"Even among King-tier bloodlines, there are varying levels of quality."

"Take the few Demon Kings of your Beast Taming Sect, for example. If they were to produce offspring with inherited bloodlines, at best, those offspring would have an ordinary Demon Lord's bloodline. Their bloodlines are too diluted. When faced with geniuses raised by major factions, like those with the Heavenly God Physique or the Talisman Physique, they’d only end up being suppressed."

"One step above that would be the offspring of more powerful Demon Kings, such as the descendants of dragons and phoenixes."

"As for your Dragon Carp, although it is not an inheritor of the Dragon Clan's Demon King bloodline, its atavistic mutation grants it extraordinary potential. Coupled with the nourishment of your Longevity Blood and the fortune accumulated through your mastery of the Hidden Dragon Arts, most of which has been dedicated to nurturing it, its bloodline potential now ranks in the second tier of King-tier bloodlines."

"As for the pinnacle of King-tier bloodlines, those belong to primordial creatures capable of devouring dragons and phoenixes. Even if their bloodlines have thinned over eons, reducing them to the King-tier, they can still suppress dragons and phoenixes of the same tier, making them supreme Demon Kings."

"The Ice Soul Gu refined by the Gu God Sect falls into this category. Although its bloodline is currently at the King-tier due to its recent birth, given time, its bloodline will naturally evolve. This is why most people cannot control the ten rare Gu."

"This is why I said it has the greatest potential to achieve Emperor-tier bloodline status. With its current foundation at the peak of the Eleventh Stage of Qi Refinement, it could evolve into a Gu Emperor upon reaching Foundation Establishment. Even without such a strong foundation, its evolution to the Emperor-tier is only a matter of time."

Lin Jing listened carefully and glanced at the Pine Leaf Mouse and Dragon Carp. As expected, a Demon Emperor bloodline was exceedingly rare.

"Enough thinking. Time to plant trees and refine pills!" Activating the sealing formation of his cave dwelling, he signaled to others not to disturb him during his seclusion. The barrier would also prevent any spirit beasts within the academy from being attracted by the Longevity Blood he was using to water the saplings.

While Lin Jing secluded himself for pill refinement, cultivators from various factions began investigating the cause of the changes in Hidden Dragon City. However, the academy wanted to keep the fact that a Demon Emperor had nearly succeeded in assassinating one of its geniuses under wraps, forcing the matter to be downplayed.

Still, some individuals managed to uncover the truth.

"Brother Lin, you're incredible." Gao Sheng stood outside the closed mountain-protecting formation, having already heard the story from Zhang Hu.

What shocked him most wasn't that Lin Jing had been chased to Hidden Dragon City by a Demon Emperor but that Lin Jing had a Nascent Soul protector!

Even Gao Sheng's own Nascent Soul-level grandfather had refused to act as his guardian. His family hadn’t even assigned him a Golden Core protector, only giving him a few life-saving treasures and a pile of Spirit Stones!

Compared to Lin Jing, Gao Sheng suddenly felt his own treatment wasn’t even worth mentioning.

Then again, as a self-admitted wastrel, he hardly needed a guardian since no one would bother targeting him.

"Faced with such a major event, Brother Lin immediately went into seclusion. He must be feeling a lot of pressure. Mortals do have their advantages," Gao Sheng muttered as he shook his head and left after finding that Lin Jing wasn’t available.

To him, Lin Jing must have panicked and was rushing to improve his strength. By contrast, Gao Sheng realized that his lack of talent was something of a blessing—no one coveted his life as they would those with special physiques like the Longevity Body.

Gao Sheng mounted a majestic Dragon Horse and set off to find Xu Zhizhi. With Lin Jing in seclusion, Gao Sheng felt it was his responsibility to maintain order in the Dragon Palace.

As the Dragon Horse soared through the air, Gao Sheng speculated aloud, "Brother Lin's protector slew a Dragon Clan Demon Emperor. That Dragon Blood should definitely be used to refine some top-tier Rising Dragon Pills. When Brother Lin emerges, I’m sure it’ll be time to distribute pills. Whether he can advance his bloodline to the Demon King tier depends on your performance during this time." Gao Sheng patted the white horse. "Off we go. Get to work!"

"Master is wise!" The Dragon Horse was stunned. While it was actually information provided by the Demon-Slaying Bureau, for Gao Sheng to connect the dots with his pig-brain was a miracle. The Dragon Horse couldn’t help but feel pleased.

Back at Lin Jing’s cave, he continued watering the saplings daily with his Longevity Blood. The saplings thrived and didn’t take long to grow under the nourishment.

Thanks to the perfected Longevity Art and Senior Pine’s assistance, the saplings bore fruit much sooner than expected. Within days, nine golden-green pinecones were produced, delighting Lin Jing.

Standing before the sapling, Lin Jing cradled the divine fruits and asked, "Senior Pine, what effects do the seeds within these pinecones have?"

"What do you expect? Longevity and vitality, that’s it," Senior Pine replied dismissively.

"That’s all I need. Let’s try refining pills!" Lin Jing nodded. Despite Senior Pine’s terse response, the fruits were precisely what he needed to enhance the Pine Leaf Mouse’s longevity and vitality.

He collected the pinecones and, noticing the now barren sapling, summoned the Pine Leaf Mouse.

"With nine pinecones, we have nine chances to refine pills. Surely we’ll succeed at least once. The tree is no longer useful; you can absorb its energy with the Longevity Art," Lin Jing said, stroking the Pine Leaf Mouse.

The Pine Leaf Mouse's bright eyes sparkled as it eagerly eyed the sapling.

But before it could act, Senior Pine intervened, reclaiming the sapling.

"Your mastery of the Longevity Art has reached a minor level. This sapling holds no value for you; its nutrients are all in the pinecones."

"Ah!" The Pine Leaf Mouse protested.

While they argued, Lin Jing entered the cave and examined one of the pinecones. Preparing to refine pills, he first called for the Dragon Carp.

The Dragon Carp leaped from the Hundred Beast Cauldron at Lin Jing’s arrival, its gaze sharp with anticipation.

It was finally time.

The Dragon Carp had waited for this day, knowing that after the Pine Leaf Mouse’s Longevity Foundation Pill was completed, Lin Jing would use it, alongside a Rising Dragon Pill, as the basis for developing the Transforming Dragon Foundation Pill.

In other words, the Pine Leaf Mouse was merely its test subject. When it underwent its Foundation Establishment to transform into a dragon, it would surpass all.

As a proud member of the Dragon Clan, the Dragon Carp refused to remain the weakest among Lin Jing’s companions.

"With all the materials ready, let’s begin."

Lin Jing’s eyes gleamed as he stared at the Hundred Beast Cauldron. Taking a deep breath, he retrieved the nine materials required to refine the Longevity Foundation Pill and prepared them one by one.

With a wave of his arm, a ripple of spiritual energy flashed around his storage bag.

“Begin refinement. I’ll control the flame’s temperature,” Lin Jing said. With that, the Hundred Beast Cauldron was enveloped in flames. Dragon flames surged as the Dragon Carp exhaled fire, and Lin Jing carefully added the Spirit Ginseng to begin extracting its essence using the fire refinement technique.

The Spirit Ginseng... the Parasitic Grass... the life-bound Red Leaf... Lin Jing’s hand formed seals as he concentrated, merging his spirit with the flames. He meticulously extracted the essence of each material, removing impurities and residues.

Even when faced with the most challenging material, the Divine Pine Pinecone, there were no mishaps. All the materials’ essences were flawlessly refined and arranged in separate containers.

Wiping sweat from his forehead, Lin Jing noted that essence extraction was the simplest step in pill refinement. He had already used the fortune accumulated through the Hidden Dragon Arts to enhance his alchemy skills and the Dragon Carp’s potential. If he had failed at this stage, there would be no point in continuing.

“Now for the blending and ratio adjustments!” Lin Jing’s gaze fell on the prepared essences.

This step required mixing the refined powders or liquid essences in precise proportions according to the pill recipe. Since this was a newly created formula, Lin Jing relied on the experiences of past alchemists for a general guideline. Swiftly, he combined the nine components into a fist-sized medicinal paste.

The most critical step followed—Lin Jing threw the paste into the cauldron and began the final fusion process. Using high heat, he needed to ensure the materials blended thoroughly and took shape as a pill.

“Fire Dragon Spits the Pearl!”

Within the cauldron, the leaping flames transformed into a dragon, devouring the materials. The fiery dragon’s head compressed and fused the materials with lifelike movements. This technique, Fire Dragon Spits the Pearl, was among the most suitable methods for dragon-based beast flames, learned from the pill manuals in the Scripture Pavilion.

It was a composite third-grade fire control method, combining multiple lower-level techniques into a versatile approach capable of handling various situations.

Outside the cave, the Pine Leaf Mouse and Senior Pine had been arguing for an entire day without resolution. Suddenly, a loud "BOOM" echoed from within the cave, drawing their attention.

“Heh,” Senior Pine chuckled. “It seems the first attempt failed and the cauldron exploded.”

Lin Jing and the Dragon Carp emerged from the cave, their bodies covered in scorch marks.

“There was an issue with the blending ratio during refinement,” Lin Jing admitted, distressed over the wasted materials but resigned to the outcome.

For a self-created pill formula, success on the first try was nearly impossible. Unfazed, Lin Jing prepared to try again.

Over the following days, Lin Jing and the Dragon Carp repeatedly attempted the refinement. Explosions frequently echoed from the cave as they failed and made improvements, refining their techniques with each iteration.

The Pine Leaf Mouse stood anxiously outside the cave. Watching Lin Jing and the Dragon Carp emerge injured time and again, it couldn’t help but think about simply attempting a normal Foundation Establishment breakthrough. Given its current state, achieving Foundation Establishment directly wouldn’t be difficult.

But it also understood that Lin Jing was aiming for perfection. He wanted to use the Longevity Foundation Pill to elevate its bloodline to new heights. For this goal, they had labored for over three years. Success was now within reach, and giving up was not an option.

The Pine Leaf Mouse prayed that Lin Jing and the Dragon Carp would succeed soon and that the pill would help it achieve a transformative breakthrough.

A month passed. Dragon roars occasionally echoed from the cave, though they were often followed by explosions. Meanwhile, the Pine Leaf Mouse tried to trade its strange mutant spirit plants with Senior Pine in exchange for treasures.

Unfortunately, most of the Pine Leaf Mouse’s one-grade mutant plants were only useful during the Qi Refinement stage, making them almost entirely worthless to both Senior Pine and itself.

“You’ve hoarded all this junk and still expect treasures from me? Dream on,” Senior Pine scoffed.

Suddenly, a rich medicinal aroma wafted from the cave.

“The seventh attempt... is it finally successful?”

“Hahaha...” Lin Jing’s laughter echoed from the cave, a mix of relief and lingering bitterness. Senior Pine couldn’t help but remark, “This boy does have some talent.”

“Senior Pine, the pill is complete!” Lin Jing’s voice rang out.

“Please evaluate the results, Senior!”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 46: The Pine Leaf Mouse's Foundation Establishment
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 46: The Pine Leaf Mouse's Foundation Establishment

After countless trials, Lin Jing and the Dragon Carp finally succeeded in refining the Longevity Foundation Pill! However, due to its complex recipe and the rarity of its ingredients, not to mention its tailored design, finding a test subject for the pill was impossible. The pill was uniquely crafted for the Pine Leaf Mouse—where else could one find another Pine Leaf Mouse with a life-bound Red Leaf?

Thus, only Senior Pine, a Nascent Soul-level entity of the plant world, could evaluate the pill's quality. Not by consuming it, of course, but by examining it with its profound cultivation and understanding of botanical properties.

"Let me see."

Lin Jing emerged from his cave, his face radiant with satisfaction. Behind him, the Dragon Carp lay motionless inside the cauldron, completely exhausted. Lin Jing himself was drained but determined to witness the Pine Leaf Mouse's evolution.

The Longevity Foundation Pill, the size of a sugar pellet, was jet-black in color. Senior Pine examined it, its silence weighing heavily on the group.

“Minimal toxicity, almost negligible.”

“The scent alone could extend a mortal’s lifespan by a hundred years.”

“Perfectly balanced medicinal properties—seamless, natural.”

“Appearance doesn’t matter,” Lin Jing said with a laugh. “The pill’s color is determined by its ingredients. The complex materials must’ve caused this shade. As long as the pill works, that’s all that matters, right, Pine Leaf Mouse?”

Lin Jing turned expectantly to the Pine Leaf Mouse. After nearly five years of cultivation, it was finally time for Foundation Establishment. For a Beast Tamer, the advancement of their spirit beast was as significant as their own breakthrough.

The Pine Leaf Mouse hesitated, staring at the ominous-looking pill. However, it nodded resolutely, unwilling to let Lin Jing’s months of effort go to waste.

“Senior Pine will safeguard you; there’s no need to worry,” Lin Jing reassured it, tossing the pill into its paws.

The Pine Leaf Mouse caught the Longevity Foundation Pill, its tiny hands trembling slightly. Although it trusted Lin Jing and the Dragon Carp, the black color of the pill was unsettling. With a gulp, it swallowed its fears.

“Squeak!” The Pine Leaf Mouse let out a determined cry. Its preparation was perfect—it had been resting and storing energy for this moment. With a deep breath, it tossed off its little robe, which fluttered onto a tree branch outside the cave, and swallowed the pill in one swift motion.

The world seemed to pause. The wind ceased, and time itself felt frozen. Even Senior Pine watched silently as the Pine Leaf Mouse sat cross-legged, eyes closed in meditation.

“It will succeed,” Lin Jing whispered, though his clenched fists betrayed his nervousness.

With a resounding boom, the Longevity Foundation Pill’s immense medicinal power activated. The Pine Leaf Mouse’s body began to transform as the pill compressed its Everlasting Emperor’s True Energy. Waves of qi burst forth, forming spiraling vortices that surged into the heavens. Leaves and grass trembled across the mountain.

The tyrannical true energy compressed into a radiant green meteor, coalescing within the Pine Leaf Mouse’s body. Its inner world seemed to be reborn—a vast expanse of spiritual mist filled the air, and raindrops formed from the mist, merging into glowing white lakes.

These spirit rains nurtured the nascent world, integrating with every corner, initiating the crucial transformation of Qi into True Essence. It was the hallmark of Foundation Establishment, where True Qi condensed into True Essence within the spiritual sea.

Through their bloodline contract, Lin Jing’s consciousness projected into the Pine Leaf Mouse’s inner world. He witnessed the birth of a spiritual sea, its waters rising from one foot to a hundred, then a thousand feet, expanding into an unfathomable depth. It was as if Lin Jing had entered an entirely new realm, his senses absorbed and overwhelmed.

“Foundation Establishment involves the liquefaction of true qi into true essence, forming a spiritual sea. The body undergoes a complete transformation, and spiritual awareness evolves into divine sense, allowing one to perceive their surroundings with a thought.”

“The depth of the spiritual sea corresponds to the range of divine sense,” Senior Pine explained. “For most at the early Foundation Establishment stage, divine sense extends about a hundred feet. Exceptions occur based on bloodline, talent, and cultivation method. Some far exceed this range.”

Lin Jing's awareness sank deeper into the Pine Leaf Mouse’s spiritual sea—1,000 feet, 1,500 feet, 2,000 feet. It seemed endless.

Outside, the Pine Leaf Mouse’s divine sense expanded rapidly, unconsciously spreading to cover the entire mountain.

“At this rate, its strength rivals the mid-Foundation Establishment stage and even approaches the late stage!”

“And this... is only the result of a normal breakthrough for a Qi Refining Tenth Stage Pine Leaf Mouse cultivating the Everlasting Emperor’s Longevity Art,” Senior Pine remarked.

Meanwhile, the Longevity Foundation Pill began to take effect.

Golden-green light enveloped the Pine Leaf Mouse as its inner spiritual rain transformed into gold-green botanical essence. The entire spiritual sea changed color under this miraculous rain, triggering another round of bodily purification.

Boom!

With a blinding explosion of light, an immense aura burst forth from the Pine Leaf Mouse, signaling a leap in its bloodline. Its transformation was accompanied by a terrifying pressure that jolted the Dragon Carp awake.

The Dragon Carp darted outside the cave, perching atop a nearby peak to witness the Pine Leaf Mouse’s evolution. Its bloodline trembled as it watched, knowing instinctively that its companion’s life essence was undergoing a metamorphosis far beyond its understanding.

The Pine Leaf Mouse’s form was obscured by radiant spiritual light. Even Senior Pine could only vaguely sense that the Pine Leaf Mouse’s bloodline mutation was elevating its life essence to an entirely new level, the direction of which remained unfathomable.

Though its final form was still uncertain, one thing was clear—its divine sense had reached a level of near monstrosity.

The Pine Leaf Mouse’s divine sense expanded: 200 meters… 300 meters… eventually reaching 1,000 meters—a range typically reserved for those at the pinnacle of Foundation Establishment. But the mouse’s limits were nowhere near this. Its divine sense continued to grow, surpassing 1,100 meters, then 1,500 meters, and finally exploding outward to an incredible 2,000 meters.

Even Senior Pine was left awestruck. Such an extraordinary divine sense was far beyond the scope of ordinary Foundation Establishment cultivators. Only those Golden Core aspirants who artificially simulated a core—a "pseudo-core cultivator"—might achieve such strength.

"With divine sense this powerful, it will hold a massive advantage in combat," Senior Pine mused. "This must be a result of its frequent use of rare beast abilities and its reliance on spiritual control, which has built an extraordinarily strong foundation."

At last, the Pine Leaf Mouse’s divine sense stopped expanding at the 2,000-meter mark. Simultaneously, the radiant light of its bloodline evolution began to fade. Lin Jing’s consciousness returned to his own body, and he stared in disbelief as the Pine Leaf Mouse slowly rose and opened its eyes.

Its clover-like pupils spun wildly, emitting an aura of terrifying power. This was no ordinary Foundation Establishment beast. Yet what left Lin Jing even more stunned was its appearance.

While the Pine Leaf Mouse retained its squirrel-like form, with its soft, glossy brown fur, it now looked more like a fusion of animal and plant life. Its triangular ears had transformed into ruby-red leaves, and its bushy tail now resembled a large maple leaf. The name "Maple Leaf Mouse" seemed far more fitting now.

The once fragile creature now exuded immense strength. Its body radiated power, and its tail, with leaf-like blades, was sharper than any weapon. Lin Jing couldn’t help but feel that even Yuan Jing’s Demon-Slaying Sword, imbued with sword intent, was less fearsome than the mouse’s newly transformed body.

"The life-bound Red Leaf has truly become part of its form," Lin Jing marveled. "And this strength... It has just reached Foundation Establishment but could already challenge the peak of this realm. This is the mark of an Unmatched Demon King Bloodline!"

"My Longevity Blood, infused into the Divine Pine Pinecone, has also granted it unparalleled vitality. Its lifespan now..."

Senior Pine suddenly froze, its eyes widening. The Pine Leaf Mouse’s evolution wasn’t finished yet. As the light in its Glazed Immortal Eyes rotated, a tree-like substance began to grow across its body. This substance radiated immense life force, forming the image of a divine tree around the mouse.

BOOM!

In an instant, the Pine Leaf Mouse vanished, replaced by a towering colossus over thirty meters tall, its entire form composed of wood. The Squirrel Colossus maintained the mouse’s basic appearance, but its body was now like a mountain, bristling with branches laden with countless pinecones. It was as if the mouse itself had become a divine tree spanning heaven and earth.

"This is impossible!" Senior Pine exclaimed, aghast. "It didn’t just integrate its Red Leaf—it's also fully assimilated my pinecones into its being!"

Senior Pine struggled to comprehend. As a plant-Nascent Soul cultivator, it had expected the Red Leaf’s integration, but its own power being refined and absorbed by the mouse was beyond belief. According to Lin Jing’s theories, the pinecones were only meant to provide energy for Foundation Establishment.

"Is this the power of its eyes?"

As the Glazed Immortal Eyes glowed brighter, the pinecones on the colossus began to vanish, converging into the mouse’s forehead. A golden pinecone emerged, resembling a third eye embedded in its brow, exuding an extraordinary and divine aura.

BOOM!

The colossus emitted a wave of terrifying energy. The vegetation across the mountain began to wither, their life force siphoned into the giant form. Even Senior Pine felt its vitality threatened by this devouring force.

The gathered life essence temporarily elevated the Pine Leaf Mouse’s bloodline, producing an overwhelming pressure that caused the Dragon Carp—barely awakened—to faint once more.

"This... this is the True Form of a Demon King!" Senior Pine shouted, finally understanding the phenomenon before it.

"What is a Demon King’s True Form?" Lin Jing asked, stunned by the transformation. He could sense the Pine Leaf Mouse’s unconscious state as it adapted to its bloodline’s evolution, but this new form was entirely unexpected.

Senior Pine took a deep breath and explained: "For human cultivators, once they reach the Nascent Soul stage, they can manifest a Nascent Soul Avatar to enhance the power of their techniques. A Demon King’s True Form is a similar ability. However, unlike the Nascent Soul Avatar, which any Nascent Soul cultivator can achieve, the True Form is an innate racial gift. Only the most exalted Demon Kings possess it. It’s a rare transformation even more coveted than the so-called divine physiques of your human race."

"In this state, the bloodline enhancement is immense. A Demon King in its True Form could force even Half-Divines to retreat. The bloodline strength in this state rivals that of Emperor-tier bloodlines!"

"Even more remarkable is that this racial gift evolves with cultivation, potentially advancing to a Demon Emperor’s True Form or even a Demon Sovereign’s True Form."

Senior Pine murmured, "But a Demon King’s True Form is typically innate. I’ve never heard of it being acquired postnatally. Could this be because it’s a rare beast? Regardless, this single Pine Leaf Mouse, at the early Foundation Establishment stage, could now dominate most so-called geniuses on the Genius Rankings."

"With its unmatched Demon King Bloodline and the Demon King’s True Form, even a Demon Emperor Bloodline would struggle against it!"

At that moment, completely absorbed in the Pine Leaf Mouse’s transformation, Lin Jing failed to notice that his own cultivation had quietly advanced to the Eleventh Stage of Qi Refinement. The spiritual energy feedback from the Pine Leaf Mouse’s evolution had even pushed him close to the Twelfth Stage—a feat yet to be influenced by the still-unbroken Ice Soul Gu in his body!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 47: Venturing Deeper Down the Path of Demon Cultivation
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 47: Venturing Deeper Down the Path of Demon Cultivation

The Autumn Hunt.

One of the few events held at Hidden Dragon Academy beyond regular classes. Scheduled three to four years after the entry of new students, the event was fast approaching for the current cohort.

Talents eager to participate in the Autumn Hunt had begun procuring pills, artifacts, and various aids. The event had no rules or restrictions—participants could wield any power they could control. Pills, treasures, puppets, mechanisms, and spirit beasts were all permissible.

However, as the hunt was primarily a stage for fame and lacked rankings or extravagant rewards, no one relied on external forces such as Nascent Soul-level Demon Kings to gain an advantage. Such a display would be ridiculed, as it was obvious to both the instructors and the world that the power didn’t belong to the participant.

Nevertheless, using one’s personal spirit beasts or mounts was not only accepted but encouraged, as beast taming was a respected cultivation path.

This year, the Dragon Palace faction decided to participate with their spirit beasts in tow.

“Is Lin Jing still in seclusion? Is he preparing for Foundation Establishment?” Xu Zhizhi, the temporary leader of the Dragon Palace, stood outside Lin Jing’s mountain residence, gazing at the still-active protective formation.

Although Lin Jing’s participation wasn’t essential for the Autumn Hunt, his presence carried symbolic weight. The Dragon Palace’s first group venture without their leader would lack its intended impact.

Moments later, Lin Jing emerged from the mountain, followed by the Pine Leaf Mouse perched on his shoulder.

"Brother Lin… was that your spirit beast?" Xu Zhizhi exhaled deeply. Despite the mouse’s unchanged appearance, she was certain that the terrifying aura had emanated from it.

The Pine Leaf Mouse had advanced.

Even before its breakthrough, its aura had been oppressive enough to make Xu Zhizhi, an experienced cultivator, feel like prey caught in a hunter's gaze. Now, its bloodline had grown even stronger.

“It’s been a long time, but yes, I’ll participate,” Lin Jing confirmed with a smile. He glanced at the Pine Leaf Mouse on his shoulder, its newfound strength reassuring him. With its current capabilities, only those in the top ten of the Genius Rankings could pose a significant threat.

“Excellent!” Xu Zhizhi beamed. “Your participation will bolster the Dragon Palace’s collective strength, allowing us to secure more resources during the hunt.”

After Xu Zhizhi left, Lin Jing turned to the Pine Leaf Mouse. “You don’t look as excited as I expected. What’s wrong?”

The Pine Leaf Mouse scratched its head, clearly conflicted. While it was thrilled by its newfound power, it couldn’t shake the strange feeling of kinship it now felt toward Senior Pine, almost as if it were looking at a parent.

“The Longevity Foundation Pill contained Senior Pine’s bloodline power. It’s natural for you to feel this way,” Lin Jing explained. “Senior Pine has been like a parental figure to us. When its time comes, you should care for it in its old age.”

The Pine Leaf Mouse clenched its tiny fists and nodded solemnly. “Squeak!” Even though Senior Pine had refused to let it absorb the sapling, the mouse resolved to repay the elder’s kindness, especially for the pinecones.

“Bah! Who’s taking care of who is yet to be decided!” Senior Pine’s voice rang out from the mountain. “I hope you lot are still alive when we meet again in a hundred years.”

Afterward, Lin Jing returned to his mountain to test the Pine Leaf Mouse’s improved abilities.

Before its breakthrough, the mouse could only synthesize first-tier spiritual and malignant plants. Now, as a Foundation Establishment beast, it should be capable of creating second-tier plants.

Lin Jing pulled out a batch of Bloodsucking Vines from his Suspended Gourd. These had been cultivated by the Pine Leaf Mouse over the past few years.

The mouse leaped down from Lin Jing’s shoulder, its confidence evident. Its Glazed Immortal Eyes activated, the clover-like symbol spinning wildly as golden-green light bathed the vines.

The Bloodsucking Vines withered under the light, condensing into a small bundle of blood-red tendrils.

“Success!” Lin Jing exclaimed.

Through its Glazed Immortal Eyes, the Pine Leaf Mouse communicated the properties of the new plant to Lin Jing:

[A blood-infused vine that can absorb the blood of beasts with a rank no higher than its own, condensing the blood into a corresponding Blood Beast.]

The Pine Leaf Mouse: !?

Lin Jing: ?!

They both stared at the tendrils in stunned silence.

“This… this is basically demonic cultivation,” Lin Jing muttered, bewildered.

The Pine Leaf Mouse tilted its head, as if to say, "You wanted second-tier plants, didn’t you?"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 48: The Autumn Hunt Begins!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 48: The Autumn Hunt Begins!

Lin Jing silently retrieved a vial of beast blood from his storage pouch and poured it onto the Blood Devil Vine.

This beast blood came from a Foundation Establishment Flood Dragon. As the vine absorbed the dragon’s blood, it began to transform. Moments later, faint bloody shadows appeared around it, and the vine disappeared, replaced by a massive Blood Flood Dragon, soaring into the sky.

“Roar!!!”

The dragon’s power was extraordinary, emanating an oppressive aura close to the mid-stage of Foundation Establishment, despite the beast blood being far from pure. Its ferocious crimson eyes locked onto Lin Jing, and it lashed out with savage blood claws!

Seeing this, Lin Jing raised his arm. Buddha's light radiated from his body as his hand moved to grab the dragon. A colossal golden Buddha Palm manifested in midair and seized the Blood Dragon firmly.

Diamond Palm.

Crack.

The Blood Dragon shattered under the palm’s might, disintegrating entirely. The vine, now drained of power, fell lifelessly to the ground, its ferocity gone.

“After the vine absorbs blood and transforms into a Blood Demon Beast, you can’t control it?” Lin Jing asked the Pine Leaf Mouse.

“Squeak!” The Pine Leaf Mouse squeaked, suggesting it might be controllable.

“ Might ? You just watched it attack me!” Lin Jing retorted irritably. “This thing’s too dumb. It doesn’t seem to have much potential on the market. Plus, it’s too similar to blood demon techniques. At best, it might pair well with that Ghostfire Pepper.”

From the void came the mischievous laughter of the Pinecone, clearly amused by the vine’s antics.

Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse resumed their experiments, testing whether Glazed Immortal Eyes could further synthesize materials post-Foundation Establishment. However, every attempt ended in failure.

They also tried synthesizing non-plant resources, which similarly failed.

“Maybe it really is tied to the Pine Leaf Mouse’s attributes…” Lin Jing speculated.

The Pine Leaf Mouse currently possessed wood-based attributes. If it could cultivate metal, water, fire, and earth attributes in addition to wood, would it then be able to synthesize items of corresponding properties?

“There’s no rush. Let’s wait and see if it can master the Five Elemental Forces in the future. Using wood as the foundation, the five elements could give rise to each other, culminating in a Five-Element Monster Core, which might evolve the Glazed Immortal Eyes even further.”

The Glazed Immortal Eyes were the key to the Pine Leaf Mouse’s strength.

After planning out the Pine Leaf Mouse’s cultivation path, Lin Jing suddenly thought of something.

“What about the Dragon Carp? Still not awake?”

“It must be exhausted. It can rest at home during the Autumn Hunt; it’s unlikely we’ll miss any major opportunities.”

Two days later.

The Autumn Hunt of Hidden Dragon Academy officially began.

“All students participating in the Autumn Hunt, proceed to the Transmission Mountain to prepare for teleportation to the Lin Jing Mountain Range!”

A commanding voice echoed across the academy. Moments later, streaks of light flew out from various peaks. Young cultivators, some riding demonic beasts, others flying on swords or clouds, descended toward Transmission Mountain.

One of the academy’s front mountains, Transmission Mountain, was now enveloped in a vast light barrier. Junior cultivators had constructed a teleportation array, preparing to send the academy’s prodigies to the hunt’s location.

“The Cangmang Mountain Range… Where is that?”

Soon, over two hundred Foundation Establishment talents from various forces across the Nine Domains gathered near the array. Most knew little about this so-called Cangmang Mountain Range.

“It’s a perilous area on the border of the Ancient Domain, teeming with fierce beasts. However, for us, it’s not overly dangerous—there are hardly any great demons there. It seems the location was chosen purely for the event.”

“I wonder what rare beasts the academy brought from beyond the Ancient Celestial Kingdom this time! Those exotic creatures are the real prizes!”

The participants buzzed with excitement. Rare beasts from foreign lands were more valuable than local ones, their materials fetching prices ten or even a hundred times higher.

Even though many of these prodigies came from affluent backgrounds, no one objected to gaining more resources. The rare materials from such beasts could significantly aid their cultivation.

Lin Jing, clad in white with a gourd hanging from his waist, descended quietly among the crowd. Among the two hundred-plus participants, most were strangers to him. However, several older students exuded powerful auras, and a few even seemed threatening.

“Ninety-eighth on the Genius Rankings, sixty-sixth, thirty-first…”

Lin Jing recognized a few renowned prodigies. He also spotted Liu Yu, whom he had briefly met before, and Chu Ren of the Chu family, who had previously sought to purchase the Rising Dragon Pill formula.

Both glanced at Lin Jing, their expressions calm and unreadable.

However, the most striking figure in the crowd was a silver-armored youth accompanied by a Battle Tiger. Both exuded overwhelming power, causing even some seasoned students to look wary.

“No doubt about it—that’s a Battle Tiger with Demon King Bloodline! And its forehead bears dragon scales—could it also have dragon blood? The Wang Family’s Heavenly God Physique… Could it be…”

Indeed, the silver-armored youth was Wang Xiao, who had vanished after his confrontation with Lin Jing’s Dragon Carp months ago. Now he had returned with a king-level battle pet!

Wang Xiao gave Lin Jing a taunting glance, leaving Lin Jing speechless.

This guy… had raised a king-blooded battle beast. Lin Jing doubted it was solely the Rising Dragon Pill’s doing; the Battle Tiger’s bloodline was likely already near king-level and had been enhanced with dragon blood before being nurtured by Wang Xiao’s fortune.

“With a Heavenly God Physique and a king-blooded battle beast, even with Wang Xiao’s current cultivation, senior students might avoid crossing him,” Lin Jing mused, his expression calm. He didn’t consider Wang Xiao a threat.

“Palace Master.”

Moments later, Xu Zhizhi approached Lin Jing with the Dragon Palace group. The Autumn Hunt had no strict rules, so students could form teams for hunting. Those with organized groups held a clear advantage when vying for rare spirit beasts.

In public, Xu Zhizhi addressed Lin Jing respectfully as “Palace Master,” instantly elevating Lin Jing’s status among the students.

Lin Jing acknowledged Xu Zhizhi, Gao Sheng, Ziling’er, and Yuan Jing with a slight nod.

This scene amused many senior Foundation Establishment students. A group of Foundation Establishment cultivators calling a Qi Refinement cultivator “Palace Master” seemed absurdly comical.

“Once the hunt starts, let’s give them some ‘special care,’” the seniors whispered among themselves.

As the group gathered, a woman clad in a magnificent imperial palace gown appeared in the sky, radiating authority.

Upon seeing her, the students saluted immediately.

Lin Jing recognized her—one of the Nascent Soul Realm guardians of the academy.

“The Autumn Hunt begins now. Enter the teleportation array,” she announced softly, her voice graceful yet commanding.

“The Cangmang Mountain Range will be monitored by academy instructors and Tianji Pavilion cultivators. Seize this opportunity.”

The mention of Tianji Pavilion caught everyone’s attention. Known for its ability to divine secrets, the pavilion produced the Genius Rankings and Physique Rankings. It was one of the few non-official forces capable of disseminating information across the entire Ancient Kingdom instantaneously, with widespread credibility.

Hearing this, Lin Jing stepped into the glowing barrier.

Meanwhile, in a distant region of the Celestial Domain, a young girl gazing at a star chart noticed a sudden celestial shift.

“Master, it’s the sign of the Dragon Devourer! In the Ancient Domain, a wild prodigy is about to defeat a Genius Rankings cultivator! Should I observe further?” she exclaimed.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 49: Tianji Pavilion
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 49: Tianji Pavilion

“Further observation? With your current cultivation, attempting to spy on such a prodigy will only bring backlash,” the old man said coldly.

“The Ancient Domain… That’s where Hidden Dragon Academy is hosting its Autumn Trials. After every trial, the Genius Rankings shift—it’s normal. Your senior brother Huihua has already gone to the Ancient Domain in person.”

A white-haired, white-bearded elder emerged from the depths of the pavilion, his gaze fixed on the celestial patterns on the star chart. He paused, visibly surprised.

“A true ‘wild genius,’ with almost no ties to sect fortune… Could they be the disciple of a powerful rogue cultivator?”

Strangely, the aura of their fortune was tinged with black. Could they be an infiltrator from a demonic sect? Should we inform Hidden Dragon Academy?”

“Master, I’ll notify them!” the young girl said eagerly.

“Disciples of the Tianji Pavilion must endure fifty years of astrology and thirty years of divination training—a total of eighty years of painstaking cultivation—before they can venture into the world. You’re far from ready,” the elder replied firmly, his tone leaving no room for argument.

The Cangmang Mountain Range stretched endlessly into the horizon. In the sky above, many powerful figures hovered, their divine senses enveloping the terrain below. Among them were cultivators from both the academy and Tianji Pavilion.

Tian Huihua, a disciple of Tianji Pavilion, was the pavilion’s sole representative at the Autumn Trials. The true disciples of the pavilion were few; its secret arts required exceptional talent, and the core members of Tianji Pavilion had never exceeded ten. Most others associated with the pavilion were guest cultivators who had benefited from its insights.

Though Huihua was only at the Nascent Soul Great Perfection stage, the academy’s instructors treated him with great respect. After all, as a disciple of the pavilion, he could easily summon a Nascent Soul guest cultivator from Tianji Pavilion to intervene when necessary.

“I foresee that a prodigious rogue cultivator, unaffiliated with any sect, will experience a dramatic shift in fortune and ascend the Genius Rankings,” Huihua said, his gaze fixed on the mountain range below, a faint smile on his lips.

“An unaffiliated genius?” The academy instructors were delighted. A new addition to the Genius Rankings was always cause for celebration. The fact that this individual was a rogue cultivator made it even more thrilling.

Such prodigies, unbound by sect ties, were especially valued by the academy’s teachers. Unlike those from established sects, rogue cultivators could be more easily recruited into the academy, free from the complications of sect politics.

“Let’s see who it is,” mused Cao Ziwei and the other instructors, wracking their brains to recall which rogue talents might qualify. But no name came to mind.

Though talented, rogue cultivators often lacked the resources and inheritance to compete with sect-trained peers.

As streaks of light signaled the teleportation arrivals in the Cangmang Mountain Range, everyone turned their attention to one particular figure.

This individual had the best luck among the hundreds of transported participants, landing directly beside a mid-Foundation Establishment demonic beast.

“Yang E’peng of the Everlasting Spring Valley, early Foundation Establishment,” an instructor identified him.

“A top scorer in the lower division’s entrance exams. Facing a mid-Foundation beast… this might be a problem.”

A man in a green robe, Fang Shilin, watched with keen interest.

“Just a serpent demon,” Yang E’peng said calmly. In the blink of an eye, a red shadow darted from his sleeve, piercing the serpent’s head. The beast collapsed, slain in an instant.

“Impressive,” remarked a Dragonblood Woodpecker Demon, flying over. Fang Shilin tossed it a spirit fruit as a reward, smiling faintly.

“I’m Fang Shilin, from the Dragon Palace.”

Fang Shilin was the first student Lin Jing had defeated upon arriving at Hidden Dragon Academy. Far from resenting Lin Jing, Fang Shilin had grown frustrated with those who mocked him for losing to a freshman.

What was so funny about struggling with the Hidden Dragon Arts?

Determined to prove himself, Fang Shilin joined the Dragon Palace, aiming to master beast taming and silence his detractors.

“No wonder the Dragonblood Woodpecker Demon is so strong—it was raised following the Palace Master’s methods,” Fang Shilin said, filled with admiration for Lin Jing. Despite being defeated by Lin Jing, he deeply respected him as a prodigious genius.

Elsewhere, Lin Jing was surveying the landscape after his teleportation, soaring above the forest to scout for rare resources.

“Bold, isn’t he? Flying openly in the Cangmang Mountains with such low cultivation, unafraid of provoking a swarm of beasts,” someone remarked.

“Still, this is the prodigy who reaped the greatest rewards from the Dragon-Phoenix Cavern Heaven. Both he and his beast companion achieved the peak of Qi Refinement, correct?”

“That’s true, but this mountain range is far more dangerous than the cavern projection. The beasts are much more scattered here.”

“Look ahead—there’s one of the fierce beasts we captured from the Cloud Sea Mountains,” an instructor said.

This beast had cornered Qin Yiran, ranked ninety-second on the Genius Rankings.

The Cloud Sea Mountains, located south of the Ancient Celestial Kingdom, were perpetually shrouded in mist that blocked divine senses. Only a few ancient tribes practicing sorcery resided there, as did the elusive Gu God Sect, whose hidden presence had long eluded eradication.

“A Cloud Sea Mosquito Beast?” Huihua of Tianji Pavilion gazed at the instructor’s mentioned beast with newfound interest.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 50: Cloud Sea Mosquito Beast
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 50: Cloud Sea Mosquito Beast

These mosquito beasts had bodies that gleamed with a metallic sheen, their forms covered by tightly arranged, tiny metallic scales. In the sunlight, they reflected a cold, icy light. Most striking were their enormous wings, resembling two sharp, blood-red scythes. Their heads were unnaturally large, with compound eyes that glowed a ghostly blue and needle-like, highly developed mouthparts.

Despite each mosquito only possessing Qi Refinement cultivation, the woman under their attack seemed to face a life-threatening ordeal. Dressed in a white dress, she wielded a bamboo flute. The sound waves she created transformed into lethal invisible sword energy, scattering in all directions, leaving the surrounding flora in disarray. However, the mosquito beasts’ blood energy coalesced into a shadowy barrier. The sonic sword waves merely clanged against their forms without causing any harm.

“What kind of monsters are these? Even with my late-stage Foundation Establishment cultivation, I can’t pierce the defenses of a group of Qi Refinement beasts!” The woman’s face was filled with disbelief. Still, she continued to play the bamboo flute, holding off the relentless assault.

“Cloud Sea Mosquito Beasts—one of the Ten Terrors of the Cloud Sea Mountains and a key challenge in this Autumn Trial,” a scholarly voice explained.

“The Cloud Sea Mosquito Beast’s bloodline is peculiar. They can never reach Foundation Establishment but reproduce at an astonishing rate. In the Cloud Sea Mountains, they always move in massive swarms, with a typical group numbering at least ten thousand.”

“Even this alone wouldn’t warrant their status as one of the Ten Terrors. These beasts drink the blood of their own kind, forming a tight, almost military-like connection. When they act as a collective, their strength multiplies exponentially. Facing a swarm of these beasts is every cultivator’s nightmare. Even a Nascent Soul cultivator would hesitate to confront a million of them.”

“To keep this trial manageable for the talents, we captured only a hundred or so. Even so, it seems they’re already overwhelming for a genius ranked on the Genius Rankings.”

Within moments, over a hundred Qi Refinement mosquito beasts had pushed a talented disciple ranked on the Genius Rankings, Ouyang Chengcheng, into a dire situation. Forced to act, he threw out a magical artifact. The artifact formed a rectangular barrier, enveloping him in protection.

The beasts’ sharp proboscises relentlessly struck the barrier. Inside, the disciple of the Tianyin Pavilion sat in agitation, staring at the densely packed swarm outside. He suddenly realized he had underestimated this trial.

Despite this, he didn’t give up. He continued playing his flute, attempting to disturb the mosquitos’ minds and make them attack each other. However, their predatory instincts overpowered such tactics, leaving them unaffected. Out of desperation, he changed his tune, trying to lure other beasts into the fray to distract the swarm.

Instead of other beasts arriving, a white-robed youth appeared below the barrier. He looked up, observing the scene calmly. His arrival shifted the mosquitos’ focus entirely; they immediately abandoned their current target and surged toward the newcomer.

Ouyang Chengcheng was stunned to see the youth smile as the deadly swarm approached him.

“Poor Dragon Carp, it missed out on this delicious feast. These mosquitos are its favorite treat!”

“If I only bring back dead ones, it’ll sulk. Guess it’s time for this toy to finally prove its worth.”

Above the clouds, one of the observing teachers, Mr. Cao, squinted, clearly doubting that Lin Jing could handle the swarm. Even a Foundation Establishment cultivator would struggle with such a challenge. However, in the next moment, the gathered teachers and the Tianji Pavilion's Tianhuihua all displayed expressions of astonishment.

Boom!

Lin Jing raised his hand slightly. A surge of true energy erupted like an icy cyclone, his hair flying wildly as a chilling aura enveloped his body. He looked like a demon god descending into the mortal world. The mosquito swarm, previously united and bloodthirsty, suddenly halted mid-attack, seemingly sensing an overwhelming presence. They scattered like a defeated army, fleeing in all directions.

"Too late," Lin Jing murmured. He had already recognized the swarm and their threat level. Testing the suppressive power of his Beast Taming Physique , augmented by the Ice Soul Gu’s Ice Soul Physique , he found it far more potent than anticipated. It disrupted the mosquitos’ bloodline connection, breaking their formation instantly.

With a casual wave of his hand, lines of condensed icy true energy descended from above, forming a massive birdcage that trapped the fleeing mosquitos. Frost emanated from the energy, freezing the swarm one by one. Lin Jing then summoned his Myriad Beast Circle , tossing it above the cage. The circle expanded rapidly, releasing white ripples that captured the frozen mosquitos into its confines.

Ensuring their preservation with ice and the spatial properties of the circle, Lin Jing maintained their vitality as fresh food for the Dragon Carp. After completing this, he descended slowly to the ground, meeting the shocked gaze of the Tianyin Pavilion disciple trapped inside the barrier.

“Genius Rankings, number 92, Qin Yiran?”

“And you are…?” Qin Yiran’s voice trembled with uncertainty. She had no idea who Lin Jing was but was awed by his effortless handling of the terrifying swarm.

“Creator of the Dragon Palace, Lin Jing.”

Lin Jing cast her a glance before walking away. “You know what to do. I don’t need repayment for saving you. Just make sure everyone knows how I dealt with the Cloud Sea Mosquito Beasts.”

Qin Yiran: “???”

She stood frozen in confusion as Lin Jing disappeared into the distance.

“Did he say… Lin Jing of the Beast Taming Sect?”

“How is that possible? He’s at the Eleventh Stage of Qi Refinement!”

Even among the top ranks of the Genius Rankings, most were at the pinnacle of the Tenth Stage, with very few reaching the Eleventh—a rare extreme. Her disbelief mirrored that of the teachers who had painstakingly captured the swarm.

"Qi Refinement Eleventh Stage? When did Lin Jing achieve this level? What fortuitous encounter could have propelled him so far?" one teacher murmured. Lin Jing’s recent concealment methods, taught by Senior Pine, had hidden his progress until now. His full strength only became evident with this display.

“It’s not just the Eleventh Stage. These mosquitos lost their bloodline connection instantly. His abilities as a Beast Taming cultivator are extraordinary,” Tianhuihua remarked.

“Oh no, that’s the Myriad Beast Circle. He’s captured the entire swarm! These mosquitos are harmless to him but deadly to other students. Have we just handed Lin Jing the means to dominate the Cangmang Mountain Range?”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 51: The Blood Pool
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 51: The Blood Pool

“Could it be that this wild genius capable of defeating a ranked talent from the Genius Rankings is none other than him?” Tianji Pavilion’s Tianhuihua mused quietly as he observed Lin Jing, whose mastery over beasts had subdued the mosquito swarm. After a brief pause, he continued, “Though he’s still in the Qi Refinement stage, his Beast Taming methods, coupled with the aid of the mosquito swarm, give him enough strength to rival the talents on the Genius Rankings.”

“Plus... considering the current state of the sealed Beast Taming Sect, it’s plausible to regard him as an unaligned wild genius.”

The academy instructors exchanged uneasy glances. It wasn’t impossible, and they were momentarily reminded of the Beast Taming Sect’s sealed status.

“Horizons vast and skies so wide, mosquito beasts gather by the mountainside~” Lin Jing cheerfully hummed a tune as he soared back into the clouds, his mood lifted after subduing the swarm. Participating in the academy’s Autumn Trial had already proven rewarding. The effort the instructors had invested in setting up the trials within the Cangmang Mountain Range was evident.

Lin Jing was particularly pleased with the Cloud Sea Mosquito Beasts—arguably the most famous among mosquito-type demonic beasts. According to Records of Ancient Monsters and Anomalies , they were bred in antiquity by the monstrous Frog Emperor to strengthen his clan. Using unimaginable techniques, he created a species of mosquitos capable of rapid reproduction by consuming blood, even from beings far more powerful than themselves.

Unlike ordinary pests, these mosquitos could devour high-grade blood without their bodies bursting, a far cry from other creations like the trashy Blood Devil Vine synthesized by the Pine Leaf Mouse. While consuming stronger blood didn’t increase the mosquito’s power, it enhanced two unique traits: reproductive ability and nutritional value.

The Frog Emperor’s Swallowing Frog clan utilized these mosquitos as a food source to swiftly grow out of their vulnerable juvenile stage and into a formidable force. However, after offending the Celestial Palace and being annihilated, the mosquitos were left masterless. They proliferated wildly in the Cloud Sea Mountains, mutating over time into their current fearsome forms, earning their place among the Ten Terrors of the Cloud Sea.

Although now categorized as one of the Ten Terrors rather than food, Lin Jing, well-read as he was, immediately recognized their original identity.

“Though Records of Ancient Monsters says these mosquitos are no longer suitable as food, they must still be better than regular ones, right?”

“If I were to rear these Cloud Sea Mosquito Beasts on Longevity Blood, they could become a steady source of nutrition for the Dragon Carp, akin to the ancient Swallowing Frog clan.”

The records also highlighted two main reasons the current mosquitos weren’t ideal for consumption: their diminished ability to convert blood into nutrients and their evolved metallic bodies, which were harder to digest. Still, Lin Jing reasoned that with the Dragon Carp’s fire attribute and its ability to refine metals, it should be able to manage.

Pleased with the prospect, Lin Jing knew that capturing these beasts in their native habitat would’ve been nearly impossible, even if he were at the Foundation Establishment stage. As he flew, he paused to land and retrieved his Myriad Beast Circle . Without the suppressive power of his Beast Taming Physique , the swarm reestablished its bloodline connection, instantly melting the frost encasing them. Though their intelligence had been nearly wiped clean by the circle, their savage instincts remained, and they began ramming against its boundaries.

“This bloodline connection is truly terrifying. A mere swarm of Qi Refinement mosquitos can suppress a Genius Ranking talent. No wonder they’re one of the Ten Terrors... Without my suppression, putting the Dragon Carp with them might result in it being eaten instead!” Lin Jing mused. “Even the Myriad Beast Circle from Qingzhou’s Myriad Beast Sect struggles to contain their ferocity. I’ll need to take this step by step to tame and refine them.”

He began channeling his Beast Taming Qi into the circle, gradually suppressing the swarm’s violent nature. Meanwhile, the academy instructors shifted their attention away from him, their expressions mixed.

“What a waste,” one instructor lamented. “The mountains contain an opportunity even for these mosquitos, yet they met their nemesis before reaching it.”

“Well, perhaps it’s for the best. If the swarm had reached that opportunity, the students would’ve suffered immensely.”

The Cloud Sea Mosquito Beast trial was one of five challenges prepared by the instructors for the Autumn Trial, requiring the collaboration of many students to overcome. They hadn’t anticipated that a Beast Taming cultivator like Lin Jing would so thoroughly neutralize this challenge. Now, their focus turned toward another trial site: a pool of crimson liquid.

It wasn’t a spiritual pool but a Blood Pool. The monkey demon tribe of the Cangmang Mountains had occupied the area and was soaking in the pool. As they bathed, their physiques and cultivation levels soared, and their bloodlines began evolving toward dragon blood.

“This is...” Tianhuihua paused, feeling an inexplicable sense of pressure from the pool, even as a Nascent Soul cultivator. “Could it be...”

He suddenly recalled something.

“Is this the blood of the Southern Mountain Demon Emperor who invaded Hidden Dragon Academy recently?”

Though few knew of the Demon Emperor’s assault, Tianji Pavilion had pieced together some details. The academy, reluctant to reveal the event, had not publicly disclosed the information.

“Yes,” confirmed an instructor. “The academy extracted some of the Demon Emperor’s blood, diluted it, and infused it with medicinal ingredients to create the Dragon Transformation Blood Pool . Any demonic beast that bathes in it will see its demonic power surge and has a high chance of evolving into a Dragon-Blooded Demonic Beast. Human cultivators can also use it as a perfect body-refinement bath to enhance their physiques.”

The instructors discussed how the Blood Pool would attract fierce competition among students once discovered, leading to a chaotic struggle. The beasts soaking in the pool were the first hurdle, with the pool itself serving as the prize.

Observing a group of individuals approaching the pool, the instructors’ expressions turned strange. Most of these individuals shared a common trait: each had a dragon-blooded pet beast by their side.

“These people...”

“They’re all members of the Dragon Palace founded by Lin Jing. Everyone has a dragon-blooded demonic beast!”

“This Blood Pool is a massive opportunity for them, enabling their dragon-blooded pets to advance further.”

“Why is it always Lin Jing?” Tianhuihua muttered, falling silent. Lin Jing, a mere newcomer, had somehow become more prominent than the top talents on the Genius Rankings. At every turn, his name seemed to surface.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 52: The Demon Core Stage!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 52: The Demon Core Stage!

“This blood...!”

A Dragon Palace member arrived near the Blood Pool—a woman riding a muscular giant rabbit. It was none other than Ziling’er, the Dragon Palace’s exclusive alchemist. Her rabbit, long accustomed to testing experimental medicines, was highly sensitive to unusual auras. Shortly after arriving in the Cangmang Mountain Range, it had sniffed out this location.

Looking at the blood-filled pool occupied by numerous beasts, Ziling’er’s expression shifted.

“A Dragon Blood Medicinal Bath!” she exclaimed. If her Dragon-Blooded Medicinal Rabbit could soak in this pool, it could easily break through from mid-Foundation Establishment to late-Foundation Establishment. Its bloodline purity would also see a significant improvement.

“But these beasts...” In the pool were dozens of Foundation Establishment monkey demons, led by a peak-stage Foundation Establishment Monkey King. Ziling’er doubted her ability to drive them away, nor did she think they would tolerate her sharing the bath. She could already sense their unspoken warnings.

“Ziling’er, you’re here too,” a familiar voice called out. She turned to see Yuan Jing riding a massive mantis beast. Her face lit up with joy.

Yuan Jing’s mount, the Giant Saber Mantis, was a wind-elemental demonic beast, renowned for its sword-like techniques. After purchasing a Rising Dragon Pill, Yuan Jing had captured and nurtured this beast himself, even teaching it swordsmanship.

“This is the Dragon Blood Medicinal Pool. It greatly benefits both Dragon-Blooded beasts and human cultivators. We should find other Dragon Palace members to drive the monkeys away together!” Ziling’er said, now grateful for her association with the Dragon Palace. Alone, she had no hope of claiming the pool.

Ziling’er nodded in agreement. They retreated a short distance to remain on guard. Sure enough, before long, more Dragon Palace talents began to arrive, drawn by the pool’s presence. After Ziling’er’s explanation, everyone quickly understood the pool’s significance.

“There are enough of us now; let’s drive out the monkeys!” Ziling’er proposed, seeing over a dozen Foundation Establishment cultivators and Dragon-Blooded beasts gathered.

“Drive them out? What a waste!” countered Xu Zhizhi, the Dragon Palace’s strategist. “If we can subdue these monkey demons, we’d all gain another Dragon-Blooded pet. Look at their evolved bloodlines—we’d save on Rising Dragon Pills! Even if we can’t afford to raise a second beast, we could sell them to new Dragon Palace recruits. The academy’s next enrollment year is just around the corner.”

“That’s true!” added Gao Sheng, the group’s financier, perched atop his horse. “Dozens of monkey demons—whether Dragon-Blooded or not—would still be incredibly useful armed with Mechanized Bamboo Rifles!”

The Dragon Palace members now not only sought to claim the pool but also to capture the monkeys for their own benefit. The Autumn Trial was, after all, an event focused on subduing and taming beasts. Simply seizing the pool wasn’t enough.

As the group prepared for action, another figure arrived—but not from the Dragon Palace.

“Roar!”

Wang Xiao, clad in silver armor, descended from the clouds atop his Dragon-Blooded Battle Tiger. Dust billowed as they landed heavily. Though not a Dragon Palace member, Wang Xiao had also sensed the pool’s importance thanks to his beast.

“Lin Jing...!” Yuan Jing’s sword floated upward in response to the arrival.

Lin Jing had just finished spreading his spiritual sense, instantly grasping the situation. His expression remained calm as he said, “You’re here for Ziling’er.” Turning to the Dragon Palace members, he asked, “Is Ziling’er here?”

“If I were present earlier, they might not have kept that Dragon Blood,” Wang Xiao remarked. He had long decided not to restrain his cultivation for fairness’ sake. Compromising for Lin Jing’s slower progression was unnecessary.

Wang Xiao, now at mid-Foundation Establishment, had resolved to defeat Lin Jing, compelling him to catch up rather than holding himself back.

“Wang Xiao, shall we cooperate?” Xu Zhizhi quickly suggested. “The pool is large enough for dozens to bathe together. Let’s work together to drive out the monkey demons and fend off external threats.”

Wang Xiao ignored Xu Zhizhi’s proposal, his focus shifting to the bald pet beast on Xu’s shoulder. “Is that supposed to be a Dragon-Blooded beast? Where’s its fur?”

Before Xu Zhizhi could respond, a voice echoed from above.

“A group of newcomers should stop dreaming of claiming such treasures. Leave now while you can.”

The newcomer, clad in a black robe, possessed a peak Foundation Establishment cultivation. Riding atop a Dragon-Blooded Flood Dragon, he exuded a chilling aura. His beast’s bloodline wasn’t at the king level, but it was close.

“Gu Domain’s Chu Clan—Rank 98 on the Genius Rankings, Chu Ren,” someone whispered, recognizing him.

The Chu Clan was renowned for their Nascent Soul ancestor, who raised a Dragon-Blooded Demonic King. The beast had produced countless offspring, some of which now served the clan. Gao Sheng stiffened, having forgotten about Chu Ren’s potential involvement.

Chu Ren gazed coldly at the Dragon Palace members. Since Lin Jing hadn’t sold the Rising Dragon Pill formula to him, he saw no reason to be courteous.

“What if I don’t?” Wang Xiao’s defiant voice broke the tension.

Chu Ren sneered. “So, the Wang Family’s Kirin Scion is playing house with the so-called Dragon Palace?”

“The Chu Clan’s reputation for decadence precedes you,” Wang Xiao retorted, drawing his saber. If Lin Jing wasn’t here, he would warm up by using this “bottom-feeder” of the Genius Rankings to sharpen his blade.

“Wang Xiao, you’re amazing!” Gao Sheng cheered, stoking the flames. “Take him down, and you’ll make the Genius Rankings at mid-Foundation Establishment! Such dominance!”

Chu Ren drew his magical artifact. The air grew tense, the battle imminent, when a sudden explosion rocked the area.

Boom!

A terrifying wave of energy surged from the Dragon Blood Pool, its demonic power shooting into the skies and drawing the attention of beasts across the Cangmang Mountains. The Monkey King within the pool had condensed a Demon Core, breaking through to the Demon Core Stage.

“A Demon Core-stage beast?” Lin Jing, having just finished taming the mosquito swarm, opened his eyes and looked toward the commotion.

“This Autumn Trial is terrifying...” he murmured.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 53: This Pool... Is Now Dragon Palace’s!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 53: This Pool... Is Now Dragon Palace’s!

The Monkey King successfully reached the Demon Core stage, causing the gathered cultivators’ faces to darken. Xu Zhizhi, in particular, scratched his head in frustration.

If it had only been the Foundation Establishment monkeys earlier, he was confident he could lead the Dragon Palace team to drive them off. However, facing a demon of this level was a completely different story. This cunning creature was proving far trickier than Chu Ren. Even Chu Ren, despite his skills, wouldn’t stand a chance against the Monkey King. Fighting a Demon Core cultivator with a Foundation Establishment cultivation level was the prerogative of only the top few on the Genius Rankings—definitely not Chu Ren’s domain.

As the Monkey King completed its breakthrough, it stepped out of the Dragon Transformation Blood Pool. Earlier, the other monkeys had refrained from acting recklessly during the enemy invasion, focusing solely on protecting their king’s advancement.

"Let’s see how you handle this," Chu Ren chuckled, turning his Flood Dragon mount around to leave. He had no intention of confronting the Monkey King directly.

“D*** it! That guy is all about infighting! He dares to fight us for the blood pool but runs away from the Demon Core Monkey King!” Gao Sheng froze for a moment before hurling curses.

Wang Xiao also furrowed his brows. He hadn’t expected Chu Ren to flee so decisively, though the danger of the situation was undeniable. One misstep could lead to complete annihilation.

After all, the Monkey King wasn’t fighting alone—it had dozens of Foundation Establishment monkey demons under its command.

“ROAAARR!!!”

“‘Within the Spirit Trap Formation, even immortals cannot escape. Hidden truths lie within the Diagram of Nine Armies!’”

“Everyone, do you know the Spirit Trap Formation?! Didn’t Mr. Lü from the Academy teach us how to enter it?”

At that moment, a yellow-clad young man from Dragon Palace stepped forward. Xu Zhizhi’s eyes lit up when he recognized him.

“Lin Jing, from the Divine Formation Sect,” Xu murmured. Lin Jing was a newcomer to Dragon Palace, hailing from the renowned Divine Formation Sect and proficient in the Dao of Formations. Among the new recruits, he had the fewest spirit beasts—a dragon-snake, a bird demon, a turtle demon, and a tiger demon, even fewer than Tang Cong’s pets.

Xu Zhizhi analyzed the situation and realized Lin Jing might be planning to use his beasts as the formation’s core to execute the Divine Formation Sect’s famous Seven Saints Slaughter Formation.

“Opportunities are fleeting; missing them would be a pity,” Xu Zhizhi declared. “You, arrange the formation! Everyone, enter it and fight together!”

The usually low-profile Lin Jing now shone as he swiftly prepared the formation. His confidence lifted the spirits of the Dragon Palace members.

"Among those who can enter Dragon Palace, none are mediocre. This situation can be resolved!" Xu assured everyone. “Let Senior Brother Zhou command!”

The Divine Formation Sect prodigy became the focal point. Even with their combined talents, defeating a low-level Demon Core creature and subduing dozens of Foundation Establishment monkeys was a tall order. Yet, Xu Zhizhi believed in their collective potential.

Lin Jing, using a flag as the formation’s core, quickly set up the Spirit Trap Formation. The Monkey King, sensing imminent danger, lunged at Tang Cong, but the formation was already operational.
