“Impressive,” commented a scholar observing from the clouds above. “That Tang Cong executed the Spirit Trap Formation so swiftly, even managing to lure everyone into it without confusion.”

This autumn trial was the perfect stage for Hidden Dragon Academy students to showcase their skills. Although Dragon Palace was Chu Ren’s creation, who would have guessed one of its special recruits would lead the charge, trapping and slaying a Demon Core monster alongside the others?

Under the formation’s might, the newly ascended Demon Core monkey was crushed by the combined efforts of the geniuses before it could even retaliate.

“Take down the remaining monkeys too! Tie them all up!” Xu Zhizhi shouted. The Dragon Palace team, energized by the formation’s power, quickly routed the monkeys, leaving the area in chaos.

When the battle ended, Zhou Lang, who had acted as the formation’s focal point, looked pale, and the others were visibly drained. The Spirit Trap Formation had allowed a group of newly advanced Foundation Establishment cultivators to defeat a Demon Core monster and subdue dozens of Foundation Establishment monkeys, but the cost was immense.

The group hurriedly consumed pills to restore their energy. Despite the exhaustion, their faces beamed with satisfaction—it had been worth it.

Beyond the victory, the Dragon Transformation Blood Pool stood before them, a prize of immeasurable value.

“Senior Brother Zhou, you deserve the most credit for this battle. I’ll personally report your merit to the Palace Master,” Xu Zhizhi praised, marveling at the hidden talents within the Academy.

“Already seen it.”

As Xu prepared to distribute accolades, Lin Jing arrived, observing the scene with mild surprise. Even he had underestimated the newcomers. They had achieved what even a Pine Leaf Mouse’s intervention might have struggled to accomplish.

This was the strength of teamwork.

“You’ve finally made it,” Xu greeted Lin Jing with relief.

“The Rising Dragon Pill is good, but this Dragon Transformation Blood Pool might be even better,” Zhou Lang mused.

Lin Jing inspected the pool and immediately recognized its significance. “This pool contains the blood of the Southern Mountain Demon Emperor. Its scent is identical to that of the blood left behind by its Half-Divine Avatar after being slain by Senior Pine.”

The Academy had audaciously made the Demon Emperor’s blood a part of this autumn trial...

“This pool’s effects are exceptional. You should all soak in it with your spirit beasts,” Lin Jing suggested.

“Yes! Hurry up and use it!” Xu urged. “That Monkey King’s breakthrough will likely attract more challengers—especially Chu Ren. He’ll be back soon to claim this opportunity.”

“No need to worry,” Lin Jing said, watching everyone recover their strength. “Since Dragon Palace won this territory, it belongs to us. Enjoy the pool—I’ll stand guard. Anyone who wants to take it will have to go through me first.”

“Just you, at Qi Refinement stage?”

Before Lin Jing could finish speaking, clouds churned in the sky. As expected, Chu Ren returned, riding his Flood Dragon. Noticing the subdued monkeys and the heavily drained team, he smirked. Lin Jing’s bold words only added to his amusement.

But Lin Jing responded calmly, unleashing his Myriad Beast Circle, releasing over a hundred mosquito beasts. Chu Ren initially dismissed the threat, but in seconds, the swarm engulfed him. Amid flashes of magical energy, silence fell.

Moments later, a desiccated Flood Dragon and Chu Ren’s unconscious body plummeted from the sky.

The Dragon Palace had triumphed again. The blood pool was theirs.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 54: Solo Pet Adventures
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 54: Solo Pet Adventures

“What kind of monster is this?!”

The terrifying swarm of mosquitoes summoned by Lin Jing had subdued a top-ranked Genius Rankings cultivator, leaving the Dragon Palace team utterly stunned. Even Wang Xiao, who had been on the verge of challenging Lin Jing, swallowed his words and stared at the mosquito swarm in disbelief.

“That’s... one of the Ten Terrors of the Cloud Sea, the Cloud Sea Mosquito Beast!!!”

“How could there be so many of them here?!” Xu Zhizhi exclaimed in shock.

“Isn’t that the Myriad Beast Circle I gave him?!” Gao Sheng recognized the artifact Lin Jing had used to unleash the swarm.

Lin Jing chuckled, “I happened to tame these fierce beasts along the way, thanks to Brother Gao’s artifact. Otherwise, it wouldn’t have been so easy. Now, this mosquito swarm will join us in soaking in the blood pool!”

He glanced at the Demon Emperor’s Blood Pool—it would not go to waste. For him, the Pine Leaf Mouse, and the Dragon Carp, the pool was of limited use. The bloodline of the Dragon Carp was already highly pure, and the blood here had been diluted extensively to accommodate Foundation Establishment cultivators. Moreover, the Dragon Carp wasn’t even present at the moment.

However, Lin Jing knew the mosquito swarm would benefit immensely from this opportunity. Who knew how far their population could expand after soaking in the blood pool?

“Thanks to the Palace Master for standing guard.”

“Congratulations in advance on making the Genius Rankings!”

The Dragon Palace members quickly followed, entering the pool alongside their spirit beasts. Xu Zhizhi’s flattery only deepened the team’s awe for Lin Jing. His feats today were far more shocking than his earlier battle against the restrained Wang Xiao.

Even Zhou Lang, who had been the center of attention moments before, silently entered the pool, feeling humbled. The group couldn’t fathom how Lin Jing had managed to tame such terrifying beasts in the short time since their arrival in the Cangmang Mountain Range.

Even the Academy instructors watching from above were puzzled.

“Managing to control the Cloud Sea Mosquito Beasts so quickly...”

“The Myriad Beast Circle from Qingzhou’s Myriad Beast Sect is powerful, but the low-grade versions sold to the public hardly refine the beasts’ instincts…”

“Lin Jing must have other Beast Taming methods. Combining Spirit Dao Sect secrets with Myriad Beast Sect artifacts—truly a nemesis for demonic beasts.”

Chu Ren had been caught off guard, his spiritual sea overwhelmed by the metallic buzzing of the mosquito swarm. A spectator murmured, “Brother Tian, it seems that the wild genius who defeated a Genius Rankings cultivator is Lin Jing. He should make the list soon, don’t you think?”

Tian Huihua nodded. “Naturally. It’s rare for someone at the Qi Refinement stage to enter the rankings, and it hasn’t happened in a long time.”

Despite Lin Jing’s dazzling performance, his reputation was still confined to the Ancient Domain. Without the backing of a top sect, even a record-breaking Qi Refinement cultivator would struggle to become well-known beyond their domain.

The last Qi Refinement cultivator to gain such fame was the Ancient Imperial Prince, who reached the thirteenth stage of Qi Refinement. Perhaps even Lin Jing’s sectmates in the Beast Taming Sect were unaware of his accomplishments.

Tian Huihua predicted, “When the new Genius Rankings are released, his name will surely appear. But where he’ll rank is hard to say.”

As Lin Jing sat cross-legged above the blood pool, Dragon Palace members soaked contentedly below. Fang Shilin of the Everlasting Spring Valley arrived, looking bewildered. Seeing the unconscious Chu Ren tossed aside, he accepted Lin Jing’s polite invitation to the pool.

However, this privilege was exclusive to Dragon Palace members. Any outsiders attempting to approach were immediately warned off. Although some veteran students were initially furious, the sight of the defeated Chu Ren and the eerie mosquito swarm quelled their anger.

“Brother Wang, why not join us? You’ve been watching me for quite some time now. Even if you’re not part of Dragon Palace, you’re one of us in my heart! Xu Zhizhi told me about your heroic support for Dragon Palace earlier,” Lin Jing said to Wang Xiao.

Wang Xiao remained silent. “I have the Heavenly God Physique, and my companion beast has a king-tier bloodline. This resource isn’t particularly beneficial for us.”

Though he stayed to observe, Wang Xiao had no intention of challenging Lin Jing. He wanted to further assess Lin Jing’s abilities. The mosquito swarm was simply too formidable.

As Wang Xiao watched, another formidable figure arrived—a man with three natural pupils and a commanding presence, accompanied by a group of followers.

“The creator of the Eye Alliance, Liu Yu.”

“Ranked thirty-first on the Genius Rankings.”

Unlike Chu Ren, who relied largely on his family background, Liu Yu was a true genius. Wang Xiao observed him carefully.

“Cloud Sea Mosquito Beasts…” Liu Yu’s gaze lingered on the subdued Chu Ren, the blood pool, the Dragon Palace members, and Lin Jing.

Lin Jing, calmly toying with the Myriad Beast Circle, met Liu Yu’s gaze with interest. “Finally, a big fish has arrived,” he thought. He wondered whether the combination of the Pine Leaf Mouse and the mosquito swarm could defeat Liu Yu.

“Brother Liu, long time no see. Is the Eye Alliance interested in this Dragon Blood Medicinal Pool?” Lin Jing asked. “However, since Dragon Palace defeated the demon here, you’ll need to pass me first if you want a share.”

Liu Yu smiled faintly. “I was wondering what kind of treasure created such a commotion. Turns out it’s just a pool of blood.”

Sensing no strong desire to fight, Liu Yu turned to leave but paused to remark, “I underestimated you. Taming these mosquitoes is impressive. But guarding this pool seems shortsighted. To the east is a mountain peak with a fierce plant transplanted from the Misty Forest. It seems to be protecting some treasure. Plenty of experts are trying to unravel its secrets—you should take a look.”

Lin Jing remained unmoved. Having gained the mosquito swarm and the blood pool, he was already satisfied. Besides, leaving to pursue another opportunity could lead to the mosquito swarm losing control. Its ferocity was still largely unrefined, and only Lin Jing’s presence kept it in check.

He summoned the Pine Leaf Mouse.

“Pine Leaf Mouse, go and investigate. If there’s treasure, bring it back. With Brother Liu’s generous invitation, we shouldn’t decline.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 55: The Daoist Squirrel
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 55: The Daoist Squirrel

Though Lin Jing couldn’t leave the blood pool area, it didn’t mean he was without options to seek other opportunities. He could simply send a pet instead. With the strength of the Pine Leaf Mouse, Lin Jing wasn’t worried about it encountering trouble.

Deploying the mouse wouldn’t affect his situation either. With his Qi Refinement Eleventh Stage cultivation, enhanced by the Ice Soul Gu, plus the mosquito swarm and Dragon Palace’s members, few could pose a threat to him.

Lin Jing patted the gourd hanging from his waist, releasing a beam of red light. A squirrel spirit riding on a red leaf soared out, instantly capturing everyone’s attention.

“What is that…?”

The crowd gawked at the Pine Leaf Mouse, whose appearance had undergone a dramatic transformation. Most striking was its wide, maple leaf-shaped tail, clearly not made of flesh but of plant material. Its crimson leaf-like ears further underscored its unnaturalness.

“Is that still the same squirrel?”

The Pine Leaf Mouse, unfazed by the stares, retrieved a pristine white daoist robe from its storage pouch and draped it over itself. The robe’s elegance replaced the mouse’s earlier unsettling aura with an air of immortal refinement.

However, Liu Yu was stunned to realize Lin Jing intended to send this pet alone to vie for a treasure against a group of geniuses. While the mouse’s appearance suggested a significant bloodline enhancement, sending it solo against numerous experts seemed laughable.

Is Lin Jing being overly naïve? Liu Yu thought.

The Pine Leaf Mouse, calm and composed, flew off on its red leaf, brushing past Liu Yu’s group in a flash.

“Doesn’t he fear his pet will be killed?” Liu Yu muttered, puzzled. Nevertheless, he and the others moved on, leaving the Dragon Blood Pool behind to pursue another hunt elsewhere.

Back at the pool, Wang Xiao struggled to calm his King-Bloodline Battle Tiger, which growled uneasily. Of all the beasts present, only the tiger seemed to sense the Pine Leaf Mouse’s terrifying bloodline. Lesser beasts, oblivious to the mouse’s power, barely reacted.

“What is he hiding…?” Wang Xiao’s unease deepened. He had initially thought Lin Jing’s taming of the mosquito swarm was sheer luck. Now, he was convinced otherwise. Despite having nurtured a powerful battle pet of his own, Lin Jing’s enigmatic Pine Leaf Mouse remained beyond comprehension.

“You’re really sending just that squirrel? Not worried?” Wang Xiao asked from outside the blood pool.

“No need,” Lin Jing replied, closing his eyes and resuming meditation.

Meanwhile, the instructors observing from above marveled at the Pine Leaf Mouse.

“Could it have consumed a Treant Pill to alter its bloodline?” one instructor speculated.

“The mouse seems to be a Foundation Establishment spirit beast with a peculiar bloodline. It’s clearly hiding its true strength, much like Tang Yun does,” another added.

The Pine Leaf Mouse’s power concealment stemmed from advanced use of the Sacred Wood Seal, a technique originating from the Divine Pine. By masking cultivation and bloodline strength, it created an illusion of mediocrity. Only a high-level spiritual probe could break the seal, which would be quite impolite in this situation.

The Pine Leaf Mouse’s destination was a peculiar mountain peak shrouded in relentless rain, though the surrounding terrain remained normal. The rain, colorless and seemingly ordinary, originated from a towering plant within the mountain—a verdant, majestic tree with silver-veined leaves and delicate flowers. This tree absorbed moisture from the air and released it as rain.

“Impossible.”

Attempts by various geniuses to pierce the rain barrier failed spectacularly. Protective True Energy, enchanted treasures, and elemental escape techniques were all rendered useless. Even flying vehicles lost their power halfway through and crashed to the ground.

“This is likely a Fourth-Rank Malignant Plant, the Rainfall Bodhisattva, a dangerous species from the Misty Forest,” Chen Lingxi of the Divine Talisman Sect remarked. Her talismans were particularly vulnerable to the rain, leaving her powerless to advance.

The Rainfall Bodhisattva’s rain corroded magical energy, even affecting Core Formation cultivators. Under normal circumstances, the geniuses gathered here wouldn’t challenge the plant. Yet, the radiant treasure light emanating from within the mountain was too tempting to ignore.

Suddenly, a flash of red streaked by—it was the Pine Leaf Mouse, flying nimbly on its red leaf. The geniuses froze, stunned by the sight of the small creature gazing intently at the treasure light within the rain barrier.

“What is that…?”

Some, like Chen Lingxi, recognized traces of the mouse’s evolved bloodline.

“Could it be a rare spirit beast imported by the Academy?” others speculated.

“No way! Who’s ever seen a wild spirit beast wearing a daoist robe?!”

Before further speculation could unfold, the Pine Leaf Mouse moved. Fixing its gaze on the rain barrier, it created a narrow, rain-free path and darted through, leaving the stunned crowd behind.

“It said no one could pass through alone… but it’s not human,” Tang Yun, the 66th-ranked genius, remarked. He was a white-haired cultivator with the Innate Cloud Mist Physique, seated atop a cloud holding a thunderous spear.

“The daoist squirrel spirit clearly has a way to counter this malignant plant!”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 56: The Furious Pine Leaf Mouse
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 56: The Furious Pine Leaf Mouse

“!!!”

The Pine Leaf Mouse darted through the curtain of rain, heading straight for the giant leaf of the Rainfall Bodhisattva. Beneath the colossal leaf sat a treasure chest radiating brilliance.

Overjoyed, the mouse couldn’t believe how easy it had been to obtain this treasure. Breaking through the rain seemed far less challenging than it had imagined. Merely disrupting the malignant plant’s defenses from a distance was enough to carve a pathway. The Everlasting Emperor's Longevity Art , taught by Senior Divine Pine, truly deserved its reputation as the bane of all vegetation!

With trembling paws, the Pine Leaf Mouse opened the treasure chest, only to freeze in confusion. Inside was a piece of grass paper, its surface glowing faintly, inscribed with elegant writing:

"The path of immortality is fraught with peril. The treasures you risk life and limb to obtain may not be what they seem—they could very well be traps."

The next instant, the treasure chest exploded. While the blast wasn’t particularly destructive, it was profoundly insulting.

The Pine Leaf Mouse stood there stunned, holding the ashes of the paper, a wisp of black smoke escaping its mouth. Its small heart had been utterly shattered.

“Hahahahaha...” Above the clouds, a group of gentlemen laughed uproariously at the sight of someone falling for the trap.

“I believe it was Brother Cao…”

Cough, cough, Cao Ziwei glanced downward, a faint smile on his face. “Trials are meant to teach students about the dangers of the cultivation world. However, my original intention was for them to work together to cross the Rainfall Bodhisattva and share the reward. Who would’ve thought this squirrel would act alone…”

“It seems this wood-attribute spirit beast can somewhat influence the malignant plants’ behavior,” one observed.

“Well, that works too. Lin Jing hasn’t earned much treasure from this trial, so let my spirit beast suffer the loss in my place,” Cao Ziwei remarked with a grin.

The Pine Leaf Mouse, thoroughly disheartened, climbed back up the Rainfall Bodhisattva and hurled the remains of the paper away. The gentlemanly observers winced.

As the rain dissipated, the mouse sighed, preparing to leave. But with the rain’s retreat came waves of divine sense, all locking onto it.

“Big squirrel, what treasure did you manage to obtain? Show it to us,” a young man in resplendent attire said with a smile.

“Indeed, we’ve all been waiting for the rain to end to collect the treasure. Your behavior lacks courtesy,” another chimed in.

Although the others remained silent, their actions spoke volumes. Tianjiao-ranked talents, including Liu Yu, the cultivator with the triple-eyed physique, and Wang Xiao, who possessed the Heavenly God Physique, focused intently on the Pine Leaf Mouse. They seemed convinced it had acquired a valuable item.

The Pine Leaf Mouse hesitated, its small mouth opening slightly. It wanted to explain that there was no treasure at all, but seeing the surrounding geniuses’ postures, it understood no one would believe the truth.

Among those watching was even Tang Yun, ranked sixty-sixth on the Genius Rankings . He held a Thundercloud Battle Halberd, his expression one of growing interest. Although the Autumn Trial was unlikely to yield an artifact capable of changing one’s fate, the curiosity surrounding the treasure’s nature meant no one would simply let this go.

“Attack!”

Suddenly, someone made the first move. The youth in ornate robes tossed a small net toward the Pine Leaf Mouse.

The artifact, Beast-Confining Net , was crafted from dragon sinew and possessed suppressive power over beasts with bloodline abilities!

“Ah!”

The Pine Leaf Mouse was furious and aggrieved. It had already been duped—now these people dared to attack it?!

“If anyone tries to rob you, don’t hold back. Take their treasures instead!”

Lin Jing’s words echoed in the Pine Leaf Mouse’s mind. Its eyes sparkled as it stared at the Beast-Confining Net. If they could rob it… why couldn’t it do the same to them?

Understanding this, the Pine Leaf Mouse’s gaze burned with intensity. Ignoring the descending net, it glanced at the crowd. In the next moment, its tail flicked gently, emitting a red glow that sliced the net apart.

The young man who had thrown the net froze, blood spurting from his mouth. The Beast-Confining Net had been bound to him through refinement, but with the bond severed, he suffered backlash.

The Pine Leaf Mouse grabbed the net and stored it in its pouch after completely breaking its connection to the youth. Its sharp gaze then shifted to the well-dressed attacker. With a flick of its tail, a red leaf shot toward the young man like a flying sword!

The youth’s expression changed dramatically. He attempted to retreat, but the red leaf moved faster than he could have imagined, streaking past him like a crimson flash.

Confused by the lack of pain, he quickly patted himself down, realizing something was amiss.

When he turned, he saw the Pine Leaf Mouse now standing on the same red leaf, clutching his storage pouch with glee.

“Spatial arts?” Liu Yu’s triple eyes flickered with surprise. He had detected faint spatial ripples.

“Indeed! That’s how it attacked me before!” Wang Xiao confirmed bitterly.

“Damn you! Give me back my storage pouch!” the youth shouted angrily, his eyes red with rage.

The Pine Leaf Mouse ignored him, its shimmering eyes locking onto another target: Chen Lingxi of the Divine Talisman Sect. Her storage pouch was overflowing with talismans—an irresistible temptation.

“What are you doing?!” Chen Lingxi exclaimed, hastily protecting her pouch.

Before she could react, a red leaf vanished before her eyes, only to reappear at a distance.

Moments later, a spatial fissure opened around her, spilling stacks of talismans to the ground. Chen Lingxi was momentarily dazed, and in that instant, the Pine Leaf Mouse darted in and disappeared with the talismans.

“Dead squirrel! Give them back!” Chen Lingxi roared, tears of fury and frustration welling in her eyes.

With red leaves raining down like a storm, the surrounding geniuses felt a cold chill run down their spines.

“This is too much!”

Finally, Tang Yun leapt into action. Wielding his Thundercloud Halberd, he descended upon the Pine Leaf Mouse, a storm of lightning trailing in his wake.

Boom!

Before he could strike, a massive wooden arm, radiant with Buddhist light, materialized beside the Pine Leaf Mouse. With a mighty fist, it unleashed the Diamond Subduing Demon Fist .

Tang Yun was blasted hundreds of meters away, embedding into a mountainside with a resounding crash.

The battlefield fell into stunned silence.

“Why did it just use Buddhist techniques?!”

Above, the academy gentlemen froze mid-laughter, their faces pale with shock.

“When you bully the honest, expect retribution,” a pinecone hovering in the sky muttered. "We’re kind by nature, but the world is unjust!”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 57: The True Successor of the Divine Pine
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 57: The True Successor of the Divine Pine

“What the h*ll is this squirrel…?” A burly gentleman among the academy’s teachers shot to his feet in shock.

The newly enlightened Pine Leaf Mouse was far too terrifying.

With the Rainfall Bodhisattva uprooted and the rain ceasing, a deluge of red leaves filled the sky. These crimson leaves were far more menacing than the prior rain. Even cultivators skilled enough to avoid being touched by a single drop of rain couldn’t defend themselves against the power of the falling leaves.

Each leaf carried ripples of spatial energy, and as the waves spread, treasures spilled from the cultivators’ storage pouches like cascading rain. Spirit stones, magical artifacts, pills, and demonic beast materials littered the ground.

Though reclaiming the fallen resources was technically possible, the Pine Leaf Mouse’s incredible speed left everyone in the dust.

It darted through spatial nodes like a streak of red light, and each time it passed, the scattered treasures were swept up in a blink. The geniuses present felt their hearts bleeding at the sight.

The most horrifying aspect wasn’t even its strange spatial technique but its overwhelming strength.

Had the Pine Leaf Mouse relied solely on its spatial abilities, the geniuses would have cornered and subdued it despite its speed. However, the moment it had knocked Tang Yun—ranked sixty-sixth on the Genius Rankings —flying with a single punch, everything changed.

If even Tang Yun couldn’t withstand a single strike, challenging the Pine Leaf Mouse seemed suicidal.

“This squirrel has been hiding its strength,” someone muttered in astonishment.

When the teachers probed it again, they were shocked to discover that, despite being in the early Foundation Establishment stage, the Pine Leaf Mouse’s mana rivaled that of pseudo-Core Formation cultivators.

For a mere early-stage Foundation Establishment beast to possess such mana was unheard of. If it underwent two more elemental baptisms, its strength would be unimaginable.

Upon breaking into Foundation Establishment, both cultivators and beasts experience three baptisms: the first from Qi Refinement to Foundation Establishment, the second when advancing to mid-Foundation, and the third when reaching late-Foundation. Each baptism significantly enhances one’s mana reserves.

Having mana equivalent to a pseudo-Core Formation cultivator after just the first baptism suggested only one possibility—the Pine Leaf Mouse’s bloodline was close to that of a royal demonic beast descended from a Demon Emperor!

“This squirrel isn’t just a product of Lin Jing’s training. It’s likely that the Nascent Soul-transformed Divine Pine behind Lin Jing isn’t protecting him but this squirrel!”

Some teachers noted that the Pine Leaf Mouse’s organs were becoming plant-like, a sign of its potential evolution into a fully wood-based lifeform.

“This Pine Leaf Mouse… is the true successor of the Divine Pine!”

“D*mn it! What’s wrong with this squirrel?!”

Though the Pine Leaf Mouse’s Diamond Subduing Demon Fist hadn’t knocked Tang Yun unconscious, it had severely injured him. Protected by the mists surrounding his body, he used a secret healing art to quickly recover, but his internal organs remained shaken.

Meanwhile, the Pine Leaf Mouse had shifted its attention to two new targets—the ripest sheep in the flock.

Wang Xiao, the bearer of the Heavenly God Physique.

Liu Yu, ranked eighty-first on the Genius Rankings and wielder of the Eight-Eyed Innate Physique.

Only these two had managed to withstand its flying leaf techniques without losing resources.

The Pine Leaf Mouse’s gaze first fell on Wang Xiao’s hand, where a silver ring gleamed. Next, it shifted to Liu Yu, who wore a jade bracelet radiating brilliance—both items far superior to ordinary storage pouches.

“Why did I even come here…” Wang Xiao sighed inwardly. His Battle Tiger’s fur bristled with unease.

Fortunately for Wang Xiao, the Pine Leaf Mouse’s gaze passed over him, as Lin Jing intended to recruit him into the Dragon Palace.

As for Liu Yu…

“It wants to rob Liu Yu!”

The teachers were stunned. Liu Yu was one of the academy’s top talents, ranking within the top three. Stronger geniuses had already graduated, leaving Liu Yu as a de facto leader.

“Your courage knows no bounds…” Liu Yu began, but before he could finish, the Pine Leaf Mouse vanished, reappearing before him via a red-leaf teleportation.

Its tail’s crimson maple leaf, sharper than the others, was like a spatial blade. As it materialized, treasures began pouring out of Liu Yu’s vicinity.

“Extinguish!”

Liu Yu’s third eye opened, emitting golden divine light. This Heavenly Eye targeted the soul and spirit, an unparalleled weapon in the Foundation Establishment stage where few cultivated soul defenses.

But just as Liu Yu prepared to obliterate the Pine Leaf Mouse’s consciousness, an incredible sight unfolded. The mouse calmly met his gaze, revealing a third eye of its own—formed from a pinecone!

“You…” Liu Yu’s heart sank.

The moment their gazes locked, he was plunged into a shadowy forest. Before him stood a towering pine tree, its branches seemingly connecting to the heavens, exuding an aura that overwhelmed his spirit.

When he regained consciousness, his third eye was bleeding, and the Pine Leaf Mouse was gone, along with all his resources.

“Liu Yu’s Heavenly Eye failed?!”

The onlookers were dumbfounded. Not only had Liu Yu failed to stop the mouse, but he’d also lost his treasures in the process.

“The third eye…” one teacher murmured. “That pinecone eye proves it—this squirrel is the Divine Pine’s successor. Far more terrifying than Lin Jing ever was.”

Out of mana, the Pine Leaf Mouse paused its rampage. The Demonic King Form it had attempted to activate consumed too much energy, leaving it powerless.

Calming itself, it began consuming pills while flying toward Lin Jing, planning a strategic retreat. It needed Lin Jing’s protection to recover its strength.

Meanwhile, Liu Yu clenched his fists, a mix of anger and fear in his heart.

“Boss, it’s out of mana!” members of Liu Yu’s Eye Alliance whispered.

“Chase it?”

“No.”

Though the Pine Leaf Mouse was currently powerless, Liu Yu refrained. Its earlier display of a spiritual shadow had shaken him to the core, its might exceeding even his clan’s Nascent Soul Ancestor.

The geniuses, however, erupted in anger and prepared to pursue the fleeing mouse.

“Get our storage bags back!”

“That d*mn thing took the sword my partner gave me!”

“That’s nothing—it stole my partner!”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 58: The Demonic Sky Sect
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 58: The Demonic Sky Sect

Dragon Transformation Blood Pool

With Lin Jing and the mosquito beasts holding the area, no one dared contend with the Dragon Palace for control of the pool. Over time, the region became exclusive to the Dragon Palace disciples, with no outsiders daring to intrude.

Soaking in the blood of a Demon Emperor, the disciples experienced remarkable growth, with most nearing a breakthrough to the mid-Foundation Establishment stage. Their dragon-blooded pet beasts reaped even greater rewards, with nearly all early-stage Foundation Establishment beasts advancing to mid-stage.

The most alarming transformation, however, was in the mosquito swarm. Egg after egg was laid into the blood pool, leaving the Dragon Palace disciples horrified yet reluctant to intervene.

By absorbing the Dragon Blood’s nutrients, the swarm rapidly multiplied, with hundreds of eggs descending into the pool in moments. Though the eggs hadn’t yet hatched, the swelling swarm was becoming unstoppable.

“This can’t continue,” Lin Jing muttered, realizing the situation was spiraling out of control.

While he could barely suppress the current swarm of several hundred mosquitoes with his Myriad Beast Circle and Beast Taming Physique , containing over a thousand would be impossible without upgrading the circle’s quality to mid-grade. The current artifact was only low-grade, already nearing its limit.

“We need to feed a batch of them to the Flame Dragon Carp soon,” he sighed.

“Hey! Hey! Hey!”

Its high-pitched squeaks echoed as it approached, causing every Dragon Palace member to glance up—and freeze in shock.

The vine was bundled with countless storage pouches and magical artifacts that had been refined and couldn’t fit into spatial storage.

“What the—?! Did it soak in too much blood and fry its brain?” Xu Zhizhi exclaimed. “Didn’t Lin Jing send it to investigate the malignant plants on the eastern mountain? Why is it hauling back a mountain of loot?”

The Pine Leaf Mouse landed and met Lin Jing’s gaze. In that moment, he understood everything. Lin Jing sighed, a mix of amusement and exasperation flashing across his face.

Without hesitation, he transferred 90% of his true energy into the mouse, replenishing its depleted reserves. With its mana partially restored, the Pine Leaf Mouse eagerly gulped down pills, its vitality returning. It squeaked with excitement, ready to rush back into the fray.

“Don’t overdo it,” Lin Jing warned. “Drive them off, but don’t cause too much trouble. Our Beast Taming Sect already has a bad reputation—we should leave a good impression.”

Meanwhile, the geniuses who had been chasing the Pine Leaf Mouse intended to reclaim their stolen treasures, thinking it had run out of energy. But as they pursued, the mouse suddenly regained its strength, brimming with ferocity.

“Run!!!”

The mouse’s fearsome glare sent the geniuses scrambling in terror, more afraid of losing additional resources than their pride.

Above the clouds, the academy teachers watched the bizarre scene unfold. A single squirrel spirit was now chasing geniuses from various factions up and down the mountain.

“This year’s Autumn Hunt has truly broadened my horizons,” Tianji Pavilion’s Tian Huihua remarked. “It seems no one among the remaining students can handle Lin Jing’s tricks. That Liu fellow has even given up the pursuit. I wonder what he sensed in that pinecone…”

Cao Ziwei nodded in agreement. However, thinking about the Nascent Soul-transformed Divine Pine backing Lin Jing, he felt less surprised. Even among super-geniuses from Nascent Soul factions, it was rare to have a Nascent Soul expert personally shielding and mentoring them.

“But his bone age indicates he’ll soon pass thirty,” Tian Huihua noted. “If Lin Jing advances to Foundation Establishment, he could aim for the top five on the Genius Rankings . It would be a shame if he ages out before securing a higher spot.”

The teachers murmured in agreement. “Once the Autumn Hunt ends, perhaps we can offer him opportunities to shine. A chance to train with Hidden Dragon techniques would suit someone of his caliber.”

Back at the Dragon Blood Pool, the Pine Leaf Mouse returned with another haul of resources, leaving the Dragon Palace disciples speechless.

“Lin, this… this is…” Xu Zhizhi stammered, his faith in the Pine Leaf Mouse wavering.

“It’s fine,” Lin Jing reassured him, understanding Xu Zhizhi’s concerns. “The weak have no diplomacy. If we appear strong, our position will improve.”

“That’s true, but…” Xu Zhizhi hesitated.

“And most of this will be returned,” Lin Jing added. “Refined artifacts are too troublesome to reforge, and we’ll return items of personal significance. We’ll only keep spirit stones, pills, and talismans.”

As he spoke, his gaze fell on a scroll clutched by the Pine Leaf Mouse. Unrolling it revealed a painting of a stunning, unclothed immortal maiden. Lin Jing shook his head. “This must be deeply important to whoever owned it. Return it.”

Relieved, Xu Zhizhi sighed. “At least you have some sense of decency.”

“Have them redeem their treasures with spirit stones,” Lin Jing said with a sly smile.

Xu Zhizhi: “???”

“They have to pay? You might as well ask them to kill me outright!”

Lin Jing chuckled, leaving Xu Zhizhi to handle the logistics.

Moments later, Gao Sheng emerged from the blood pool, grumbling about its lack of excitement compared to his family’s medicinal baths. He approached Lin Jing, speaking in hushed tones.

“Lin, your pet is at Foundation Establishment now. Doesn’t that count as you reaching it too? Once the Autumn Hunt ends, how about teaming up to solve a case?”

Lin Jing rolled his eyes. “What case?”

Gao Sheng’s tone turned conspiratorial. “Remember what I mentioned about the now-extinct Demonic Sky Sect?”

“This case involves uncovering the true identity of the Demonic Sky Sect’s leader. There are no traces of him in the Tianyuan Continent, almost as if he came from another celestial realm entirely!”

“If we can prove he’s an otherworldly demon, my Nascent Soul patriarch might even get a promotion!”

“The ruins of the Demonic Sky Sect are located in the Air Domain!”

“Go soak in the blood pool,” Lin Jing muttered, cold sweat running down his back. He had expected nothing but trouble from Gao Sheng’s schemes.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 59: Extreme Cold
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 59: Extreme Cold

"That Gao Sheng kid sure has a big appetite. Investigating whether the Sect Leader of the Demonic Sky Sect is an Outer Realm demon or a cultivator from beyond this world? This isn't about checking out the leader; it's about eyeing his head."

Lin Jing thought for a moment. Whether the Demonic Sky Sect leader was from the Tianyuan Continent or not, he couldn't confirm. But one thing was certain—he wasn't someone to mess with.

"In the end, don’t get yourself thrown in while investigating," Lin Jing muttered. Then, brushing Gao Sheng off with a vague response, he said, "We'll discuss this later."

“You’re not scared, are you?” Gao Sheng nudged Lin Jing with his elbow, speaking confidently via voice transmission. “With my wisdom and luck…”

Gao Sheng's confidence in his investigative skills grew day by day, especially after cultivating the Acquired Spirit Luck Physique using his family's secret technique during the Foundation Establishment stage. The physique, as its name suggests, amplified his fortune.

"Get lost!" Lin Jing kicked Gao Sheng back into the Dragon Transformation Blood Pool.

“Think about it again! If it doesn’t work, we can switch cases!” Gao Sheng persisted.

Just then, the Divine Pine sent a message: "After the Autumn Hunt ends, you should head to the Air Domain."

With twitching lips, Lin Jing replied, "Senior Divine Pine, have you been possessed by Gao Sheng? The leader of the Demonic Sky Sect is likely a powerhouse in the Dao Inquiry stage. His secrets aren’t something small cultivators like us in Qi Refinement or Foundation Establishment stages can uncover."

“If it were a case like the one in Snow Moon City, where you could use Longevity Blood as bait for fishing, I might consider it.”

"Relax," the Divine Pine responded. "I’m talking about something else entirely. You should start preparing to cultivate the Ice Soul Gu.”

"After the Pine Leaf Mouse reaches Foundation Establishment, the next step is for the Ice Soul Gu to advance. With its natural bloodline and aptitude, you don’t need special treasures to enhance its lineage. That said, the Ice Soul Gu is the embodiment of ice and snow. For a perfect Foundation Establishment, you’ll need an environment rich in frost energy.”

“The best options are the Snow Plains in the Great Wasteland or the Air Domain. Fortunately, I know of a freezing region in the Air Domain suitable for the Ice Soul Gu’s Foundation Establishment. When the time comes, I’ll accompany you.”

Lin Jing nodded. “I see.”

Then, testing the waters, he asked, “Senior Divine Pine, is the leader of the Demonic Sky Sect really from beyond this world?”

After a moment of contemplation, the Divine Pine replied, “I’m aware of the relic site Gao Sheng mentioned. In that relic, a powerful Ancient Kingdom cultivator established a unique spatial-temporal formation. It allows lower-level cultivators to dream of the past and explore the Demonic Sky Sect.”

“That crafty Nascent Soul brat probably wants to investigate it that way. Considering the effort the Ancient Kingdom put into creating this formation, it’s possible that the sect leader isn’t a native cultivator of Tianyuan.”

“Legend has it that beneath the Celestial Realm lies countless worlds of cultivation. Tianyuan Continent is just one of them. Cultivators from these worlds share a common goal: ascending to the Celestial Realm to achieve immortality.”

“Due to special circumstances, Tianyuan’s ascension pathway was severed. If the leader of the Demonic Sky Sect truly came from another cultivation world, it might mean Tianyuan’s cultivators can travel to those worlds to ascend!”

“Perhaps the Ancient Kingdom destroyed the sect primarily to uncover this cross-realm method.”

“Of course, this is mere speculation. The Demonic Sky Sect fell long before I gained sentience. The matter is far too complex, full of hidden dangers. Even if you uncover everything, it won’t matter to a small Qi Refinement cultivator like you. Let Gao Sheng play his own games.”

Lin Jing agreed to let Gao Sheng handle his own mess, as he had no interest in ascension.

The Autumn Hunt lasted a month. During this time, the elders of the Dragon Palace didn’t leave the blood pool, instead choosing to cultivate diligently. Though the pool’s effects waned over time, they dared not leave, fearing retaliation.

After all, the Pine Leaf Mouse had stolen plenty of treasures, leaving many glaring resentfully at Lin Jing and the mouse. While they couldn’t do anything to Lin Jing, they could take it out on the Dragon Palace members. Thus, they opted to stay put and make the most of the remaining energy.

Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse also stayed, working tirelessly. Lin Jing harvested thousands of Mosquito Eggs, hatching larvae and testing the limits of the Myriad Beast Circle. Meanwhile, the Pine Leaf Mouse used its spoils—lifeforce absorbed from the Fourth-Rank Malignant Plant, Rainfall Bodhisattva—to cultivate the Everlasting Emperor's Longevity Art.

When the hunt concluded, beams of light descended from the heavens, transporting participants back to Hidden Dragon Academy.

At the Initial Point near the teleportation mountain, figures reappeared. Many looked haggard, a stark contrast to the Dragon Palace members, who radiated energy. They had reaped numerous fortunes with little hardship.

Previously dismissive senior students now looked at Lin Jing with complex expressions, their resentment palpable. Most had their treasures stolen but lacked the strength to reclaim them.

Lin Jing sensed their hostile gazes and chuckled, saying, “Don’t look at me like that. My pet beast was just playing a little prank. Since we’re all fellow students, I’ll return your treasures.”

“Really? Is my companion’s portrait still intact?” a buzz-cut cultivator asked excitedly.

A disciple of the Divine Vision Sect, he practiced visualization techniques, allowing him to vividly recreate images in his mind. Lin Jing’s silence seemed to confirm the portrait’s owner.

“Of course,” Lin Jing replied with a wave, softening many expressions.

However, some talents turned away coldly, uninterested in reconciliation.

"Xu Zhizhi, I’ll leave this to you,” Lin Jing said, patting Xu Zhizhi’s shoulder before transforming into a streak of light and flying off. After a month away, he wondered how his Dragon Carp was faring.

“Wait, redemption? For our own treasures?!” The crowd exploded after Lin Jing’s departure. Xu Zhizhi could only sweat nervously.

“I’ll join Dragon Palace if it means getting my treasures back!” someone shouted. “I’ve lost everything—my spiritual tools and stones! I avoided the beasts, but I can’t afford to redeem anything now!”

The academy’s resident teachers, who hadn’t joined the Autumn Hunt, were baffled by the uproar. Meanwhile, those who returned from the Cangmang Mountains remained calm, knowing Lin Jing’s name would soon spread across the Ancient Kingdom, with the Genius Rankings update solidifying his fame.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 60: Return to the Great Wasteland
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 60: Return to the Great Wasteland

At Hidden Dragon Academy, a streak of light descended onto a mountain peak. Inside the mountain's cavernous abode, intense heat pervaded the air, and a scarlet-scaled Dragon Carp Spirit was busy refining pills in a fiery cauldron.

Having overexerted itself creating a Foundation Establishment Pill for the Pine Leaf Mouse, the Dragon Carp had fallen into a deep slumber, awakening only to find a jade slip left by Lin Jing:

"I’ve gone to participate in the academy’s Autumn Hunt with the Pine Leaf Mouse. I’ve left enough food for you in the cavern."

The Dragon Carp replayed the message repeatedly, waiting silently for its master. Nearly a month passed before Lin Jing finally returned.

“You’ve recovered well! I thought you’d be asleep for a whole month,” Lin Jing remarked with a slight smile, noticing the Dragon Carp's vigor.

“Blub blub blub!” The Dragon Carp leapt from the cauldron, flapping its fins in protest.

Lin Jing chuckled. “It was just some backwater mountains—nothing exciting.”

The Dragon Carp, still bitter over being left out of the Dragon-Phoenix Cavern Heaven adventure, eyed him suspiciously. “Nothing exciting…?”

The Dragon Carp froze. "What?!"

“It wouldn’t have done you much good anyway,” Lin Jing added casually. The Dragon Carp sighed in relief, its trembling fins calming down.

“Relax. I made sure to think of you this time. After all, you worked yourself to exhaustion to make the Foundation Establishment Pill for the Pine Leaf Mouse. So, I brought back something delicious—premium Mosquito Beasts from the Cangmang Mountains as a treat.”

“Those mosquitoes have been gorging themselves on the Dragon Blood Pool. By the principle of equivalent exchange, eating them is practically like you enjoying the blood pool too!” Lin Jing explained with a grin.

The Dragon Carp’s eyes sparkled with anticipation, eagerly awaiting its reward.

When Lin Jing released the Mosquito Beasts, the cavern was soon filled with their loud buzzing. Over a hundred Mosquito Beasts emerged, each the size of Lin Jing himself, their plump bodies practically radiating vitality. The Dragon Carp’s eyes lit up as it dove toward the nearest one, mouth open wide.

But before it could bite, the Mosquito Beast's oppressive aura surged. With a sudden burst of force, it slammed the Dragon Carp back into the ground, creating a crater.

The Dragon Carp winced in pain, staring at the Mosquito Beast in disbelief. Each mosquito was now shrouded in an overwhelming Blood Shadow, and its King-tier Bloodline was faintly threatened.

Not ready to give up, the Dragon Carp targeted another. But even before it could approach, the Mosquito Beasts' collective pressure forced it to retreat, stumbling backward.

For a moment, the Dragon Carp doubted its reality. How had it fallen so far that even "prepared food" was out of its league?

Lin Jing’s expression turned serious. “What’s wrong with you? Did you slack off on your cultivation this past month? How can you lose to food?”

The Dragon Carp wanted to cry but had no tears. Missing one Autumn Hunt had cost it so much! Its mind raced—was the Dragon Blood Pool truly such a useless opportunity?

It suddenly panicked. Would Lin Jing now prefer to contract a Mosquito Beast for Foundation Establishment instead of it?

Gripping Lin Jing’s pant leg with its teeth, the Dragon Carp pleaded with watery eyes. It couldn’t endure another event without being included.

“Alright, next time for sure,” Lin Jing relented with a smile. “In fact, in a few days, we’ll be heading out again. I’ll definitely bring you along.”

The Dragon Carp’s eyes brightened. “Where to?”

“To a frozen wasteland. I need to help the Ice Soul Gu achieve Foundation Establishment.”

The Dragon Carp froze. "What?!"

Before Lin Jing could elaborate, a powerful presence descended outside the cavern. “Never mind, I’ll explain the mosquitoes later,” Lin Jing said. “Don’t question your worth as a fish.”

Stepping out of the cavern, Lin Jing found Cao Ziwei, one of the academy’s teachers, waiting for him.

“Your performance during the Autumn Hunt was truly impressive,” Cao Ziwei said with a smile.

“You flatter me, Sir Cao,” Lin Jing replied. “What brings you here?”

“I come with a message. During the hunt, a strong cultivator from Tianji Pavilion was observing. He was impressed by you and asked me to pass this on: two years from now, the pavilion will host a Genius Battle in the Heaven Domain. It will be an opportunity to reshuffle the rankings of the Genius Rankings.”

He added, “You’re twenty-seven now, right? While this Autumn Hunt will likely put you on the Genius Rankings, the list only includes those under thirty. Your time on it will be short-lived. The pavilion hopes you’ll achieve Foundation Establishment by then and participate in the battle. With your potential, you could rank among the top talents.”

Lin Jing raised an eyebrow. “Tianji Pavilion is hosting such an event?”

“Indeed. The pavilion relies on selling intelligence to sustain itself, and the Genius Rankings are a major revenue stream. However, the rankings have been stagnant for years, impacting their business. This Genius Battle is their way of shaking things up,” Cao Ziwei explained with a laugh.

Lin Jing nodded. “Thank you for the message, Sir Cao. Coincidentally, I was planning to leave the academy to seek a feng shui treasure land for Foundation Establishment. I’ll keep the battle in mind.”

Hearing this, Cao Ziwei grew concerned. However, recalling Lin Jing’s powerful protector, he decided not to press further. “As long as you travel discreetly, it should be fine.”

As Lin Jing prepared for his journey to the Great Wasteland Domain, he contemplated a side trip to visit the Beast Taming Sect’s remnants in Hundred Flower Valley. Though the sect no longer existed, many of its members had relocated there. It had been four years, and he was curious to see how his old acquaintances were faring.

To cross domains, Lin Jing could use Hidden Dragon City’s interdomain teleportation array, but the cost was exorbitant. Even with his spoils from the Autumn Hunt, he refused to waste his resources. Fortunately, with the Nascent Soul-level Divine Pine as his protector, traversing the regions wouldn’t be an issue.

Meanwhile, in the Heaven Domain’s Tianji Pavilion, Tian Huihua reported the Autumn Hunt’s events to her master.

Her master dismissed her concerns about the Genius Rankings. “Never mind that for now. Focus on finding your junior sister. She slipped away recently.”

Tian Huihua frowned. “Eighty years of cultivation can feel stifling. It’s only natural for her to want some freedom.”

“She left after divining something about that Lin Jing,” her master said. “She hasn’t eaten or drunk anything since, just giggling to herself. Keep an eye on her. If there’s an opportunity, seize it—but don’t let Lin Jing deceive her. That man is trouble.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 61: Hundred Flower Valley
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 61: Hundred Flower Valley

Compared to other domains, the Great Wasteland Domain was undoubtedly barren, yet it earned the nickname Haven for Wanderer Cultivators. This land teemed with countless beasts and ferocious monsters. The sheer variety and number of these creatures surpassed any other domain in the Nine Domains. According to the Ancient Kingdom, the Great Wasteland likely housed the highest concentration of Demon Kings deep within its expanse.

Many rogue cultivators made a living by hunting beasts, exchanging the materials harvested from them for spirit stones. They would then rent abodes atop spiritual veins in immortal cities to cultivate, living lives constantly on the edge.

Above the Great Wasteland’s dense clouds, Lin Jing sat cross-legged atop a flying giant pinecone, gazing down at the rogue cultivators battling beasts below. He sighed softly.

“We’re almost in the Great Wasteland. The journey was faster than I imagined,” he remarked. “After the Beast Taming Sect was sealed, I wonder if its disciples have fallen to lives like those rogue cultivators.”

“Of course it was fast!” the pinecone grumbled irritably. “I’m moving at full speed! You wouldn’t spare enough spirit stones for a teleportation array.”

Lin Jing retorted, “The spirit stones we ‘acquired’ wouldn’t even cover a single use of the one-way array. Even if they could, the Pine Leaf Mouse would never allow it. You know how possessive it is.”

He continued, “Besides, with your help, Senior Divine Pine, this was the most practical way back. Rest assured, I’ll fulfill my promise to you.”

“Hah!” the Divine Pine sneered. “Don’t pretend you’re altruistic. I know you’re using me to demand the Immortal Demon Serpent’s treasure. This isn’t about reconnecting with old friends in the Beast Taming Sect; you’re after those riches!”

He speculated they might have. With the sect’s spiritual veins gone, elders and disciples alike would have faced major cultivation issues. Unearthing treasure from the serpent’s hoard could have alleviated some of these challenges.

Though Lin Jing was the one who discovered the treasure’s location, as a lowly Qi Refinement cultivator, he had no illusions about securing any core items. At most, he’d be rewarded with a magical artifact. However, with the Divine Pine—a Nascent Soul-level protector—making the demand, the sect’s surviving elders wouldn’t dare refuse.

“Senior Pine, that’s absurd,” Lin Jing replied with an air of righteousness. “I’m just thinking about the sect’s well-being.”

The Divine Pine snorted. “And how many days have we been traveling now?”

“It should be around the New Year,” Lin Jing estimated. “January 1st is when Tianji Pavilion publishes the latest Genius Rankings. Let’s stop by a nearby immortal city to buy a copy.”

“Hmph. So now you’re willing to spend?” the Divine Pine scoffed.

Lin Jing chuckled. “No need. The Beast Taming Sect always subscribed to the World Report, so Hundred Flower Valley likely does too. We’ll read it there.”

Hundred Flower Valley was a second-tier sect in the Great Wasteland. While it didn’t have the same rich heritage as the Beast Taming Sect, it boasted a Nascent Soul elder and a Nascent Soul butterfly, providing its foundation. The sect’s specialty lay in its Butterfly Taming Arts, and it had maintained close ties with the Beast Taming Sect over generations.

Unlike the Beast Taming Sect, which relied on long-lived demon kings for its legacy, the Hundred Flower Valley had no such entities. However, this difference seemed advantageous in hindsight, as the sect had avoided the fate of being sealed.

Flying onward, Lin Jing and his companions soon spotted the valley’s mountain range in the distance. Even from afar, the scenery was breathtaking. The air grew fresher as they approached, and fields of wildflowers stretched endlessly below. Vibrant patches of blossoms painted the landscape, ranging from tiny scattered flowers to sprawling seas of color.

At the mountain gate, two female disciples appeared, blocking Lin Jing’s path. Dressed in light, flowing robes embroidered with intricate butterfly patterns, their delicate features only added to the valley’s charm. Each held a sword, upon which perched a butterfly—one blue, the other yellow—radiating spiritual energy.

“Who goes there?” they called.

Lin Jing stepped forward and bowed politely. “Greetings, Senior Sisters. I am Lin Jing, an Inner Sect Disciple of the Beast Taming Sect. After the sect’s closure, I went abroad to study. I have returned to the Great Wasteland and seek to reconnect with my sect.”

The disciples exchanged glances before the one with the blue butterfly stepped forward. “Do you have proof of identity? I will notify the elders.”

Lin Jing tossed her his Beast Taming Sect disciple token and thanked her. She nodded and entered the valley gates, while the other disciple, with the yellow butterfly, stayed behind and observed him curiously.

“He must have been an important member of the Beast Taming Sect,” she speculated aloud. “Yet I don’t recall hearing of him.”

“Oh? What makes you think so?” Lin Jing asked with a smile.

She gestured to his lack of a Beast Taming Space Artifact. “Such artifacts are reserved for the sect’s true disciples. But your Qi Refinement stage cultivation seems low for that rank. I only recall an Elder Feng accepting a disciple by that name.”

Lin Jing chuckled, “I do have a companion beast.”

At that moment, a red dot appeared in the sky, gradually descending. The disciple soon realized it was an unusual red Dragon Carp.

“Roar!” With a loud cry, the Dragon Carp hovered beside Lin Jing, flaunting its formidable aura.

The carp radiated an air of arrogance, clearly relishing its dominance. For a moment, it feared Lin Jing might have left it behind with the Divine Pine. Thankfully, it had made it here—just barely!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 62: The Genius Rankings?
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 62: The Genius Rankings?

Before long, two streaks of light shot out from Beast Taming Peak in the Hundred Flower Valley. Both exuded the aura of the Golden Core Stage and quickly landed outside the valley gates, their expressions filled with delight.

"Lin Jing, how come you're back?"

The newcomers were Elder Yu, known for his passion for Mechanized Arts, and Elder Mo, the one who had brought Lin Jing into the sect.

"You weren’t expelled, were you?" Elder Mo stepped forward, his excitement fading into a worried frown.

"How could that be?" Lin Jing’s lips twitched.

"Good, as long as that’s not the case." Elder Yu nodded in relief.

"Elder Yu? Elder Mo?" A female disciple guarding the valley gate clearly recognized the two Beast Taming Sect elders. When she saw that Lin Jing, an Outer Sect disciple, had managed to summon two Golden Core elders to personally welcome him, she was stunned.

Even a core disciple wouldn’t receive this kind of treatment. She realized she had underestimated Lin Jing's status in the Beast Taming Sect. Could he be… the sect master’s illegitimate son?

After aligning with the Hundred Flower Valley, the Beast Taming Sect was given a mountain peak rich in spiritual veins—previously used for silkworm farming—as their base.

"You're too kind, Elder…" Before the disciple could finish, everyone’s attention was drawn upward. A pigeon-like spirit beast carrying a storage pouch swooped down outside the valley gates.

"A Communication Pigeon!"

Recognizing the bird, the female disciple hurried forward. In the next moment, the gray pigeon retrieved a bundle of newspapers from the pouch, handed it to her, and flew off.

"What a coincidence! Is that the new issue of the World Report ?" Lin Jing asked, surprised.

"Hmm? I don’t see why not." The disciple reluctantly handed a copy to Lin Jing.

Lin Jing stored the newspaper and followed the elders back to Beast Taming Peak.

Shortly after they left, a blue-butterfly-clad disciple returned from delivering a message, panting. "Who is this Lin Jing? Just hearing his name made Elder Yu rush to notify the other elders to greet him."

The yellow-butterfly disciple shook her head. "No idea. But he snagged one of our newspapers! So annoying! I didn’t dare refuse with the elders there—a single copy costs several Spirit Stones. Beast Taming Sect disciples really know how to take advantage!"

"Huh?" The blue-butterfly disciple glanced at the box of newspapers. "Forget it. Take the rest inside to distribute."

She casually picked up a copy, flipping through the major events that seemed distant and unrelated to her life. But as she turned a page, her pupils shrank.

"I think I know why those two elders greeted him personally."

"But how could it be...?"

"Lin Jing, tell us, why did you suddenly return?"

The Ancient Domain lay at the heart of the Ancient Kingdom, while the Great Wasteland was at its borders. Even a Golden Core cultivator would take years to traverse the distance between them. The elders couldn’t believe Lin Jing had come back simply because he missed the sect.

In a small hall, Lin Jing looked at the two elders and smiled. "A descendant of Divine Pine is taking me to a promising cultivation site. Passing through the Ancient Domain, I thought I’d stop by."

"Speaking of which, Elder Mo, where’s Elder Black Bear? I wanted to see him." Lin Jing was fond of Elder Black Bear. The Academy’s test subjects for medicine trials couldn’t compare to his resilience.

"He went back home to visit his Nascent Soul mother," Elder Mo said casually.

"Oh..." Lin Jing fell silent. So, Elder Black Bear’s mother was also a Nascent Soul demon king? And a wild one at that. The network of demon kings behind the Beast Taming Sect might be deeper than he had imagined.

"Are things going well at the academy?" The elders asked briefly, not expecting much progress in just a few years. With Lin Jing’s Longevity Body, breakthroughs often took decades or even centuries.

Lin Jing remained at the Qi Refinement Stage, as expected. Yet with rare beasts and Divine Pine’s guidance, Elder Mo wasn’t too worried about him being bullied.

The topic soon shifted to the sect’s current situation.

"The sect master, Elder Li Hai, and Ouyang Hao have taken some disciples to Qingzhou," Elder Yu explained.

"Most ordinary disciples and I have temporarily stationed ourselves in Hundred Flower Valley."

"As for the demon king ancestors, they initially took the core disciples to train in the Air Domain but recently returned to the Great Wasteland and are staying here in the valley," Elder Mo added.

"Incidentally, their Air Domain training was related to the intelligence you brought back from the Demon-Suppressing Pagoda."

"The treasure of the Immortal Demon Serpent?" Lin Jing hadn’t expected Elder Mo to bring it up before he even asked.

"Exactly. The Immortal Demon Serpent’s treasure stirred fierce competition among the Great Wasteland sects. Since the Beast Taming Sect obtained the location first, they naturally had to investigate."

"Did they succeed? Was the intel genuine?" Lin Jing inquired.

"It was," Elder Mo replied. "The treasure’s entrance was sealed by a nearly unbreakable formation, requiring the caster to unlock it personally."

"However, the so-called unbreakable formation, prevalent thousands of years ago, was deciphered 1,200 years ago by the Qingzhou Thief Sect and is now widely known in the cultivation world."

Elder Mo laughed. "The demon king ancestors couldn’t believe how easy it was to obtain the treasure. If not for the Demon-Slaying Bureau guarding the pagoda, they might’ve returned to mock the Immortal Demon."

Lin Jing was speechless. The Immortal Demon Serpent had claimed that only it could unlock the treasure, a claim Lin Jing had doubted. Still, he hadn’t expected the treasure to require so little effort.

Then again, the serpent had been sealed for millennia, mistaking Lin Jing for a Buddhist master. It hadn’t considered that formations deemed unbreakable back then might now be outdated...

"What was in the treasure?" Lin Jing asked.

"We don’t know," Elder Mo replied. "The demon king ancestors took the treasure, but even the elders are unaware of its contents. All we know is it contained rare spiritual plant seeds, which require Hundred Flower Valley’s spiritual veins to grow. They even negotiated with the valley master over this."

Lin Jing nodded and grinned. "Shouldn’t those seeds include a share for the disciples?"

Elder Mo chuckled. "Since you provided the intelligence, in theory, yes. But it depends on what the demon king ancestors decide."

"Currently, the sect’s strategy is to prioritize all resources for enhancing our sect’s fortune. The focus is on nurturing core disciples to the point where at least one can secure a spot on the Genius Rankings and elevate the sect’s status."

"The Genius Rankings? You mean this?" Lin Jing handed Elder Mo the newspaper he had taken earlier. Both elders scanned it with their spiritual senses, stunned.

Genius Rankings, Rank 87: Lin Jing

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 63: Rising Fame
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 63: Rising Fame

Elder Mo and Elder Yu froze upon seeing a certain name in the latest Genius Rankings .

Rank 32: Lin Jing

This had to be a mistake.

No, wait… this ranking had been personally delivered moments ago by the Tianji Pigeon. Could it actually be true? But how was this possible?

Wasn’t the Longevity Body notoriously difficult to cultivate? How could Lin Jing have broken through to the extreme limit of Qi Refinement and reached the eleventh stage?

Moreover, he had even achieved this alongside his spirit beasts!

"Are you truly a Longevity Body?" Elder Yu couldn’t help doubting.

The Tianji Pavilion 's entry on Lin Jing gave no mention of any special constitution, leaving it unclear whether the pavilion had concealed this information or was simply unaware of it. If the world found out that Lin Jing possessed such a difficult-to-cultivate Longevity Body, his ranking would undoubtedly become even more impressive.

The two elders were stunned. For a sect like the Beast Taming Sect, which was considered third-rate within the Ancient Kingdom, the Genius Rankings were a distant dream.

Even in the Great Wasteland, only a handful of cultivators occasionally made it onto the rankings, usually in the lower tiers. Lin Jing’s debut—landing within the top fifty at that—was an unprecedented feat for someone at the Qi Refinement stage, not just in the Great Wasteland but even across the entire Ancient Kingdom.

How had he achieved this?

Was it due to the Pine Leaf Mouse? That couldn’t be it. The Pine Leaf Mouse’s synthesis abilities weren’t suited for such rapid advancement, and its species wasn’t combat-oriented.

The Ice Soul Gu? Unlikely. Lin Jing’s current cultivation was insufficient to control such a Gu. Besides, revealing the Ice Soul Gu just to make the Genius Rankings would be a foolish move, and the rankings’ description suggested it wasn’t involved.

The Dragon Carp? Laughable. The elders glanced at the floating Qi Refinement Carp by Lin Jing’s side and shook their heads.

"Could it be the Ten Terrors of the Cloud Sea?"

The two elders could only assume Lin Jing had tamed some powerful new beasts. As Beast Taming Sect elders, they naturally knew what the Ten Terrors were—ten of the most terrifying species in the Cloud Sea Mountain Range. Any one of them could easily crush the spirit beasts raised by the sect.

Even the demon kings of the Cloud Sea would avoid crossing paths with these species.

If Lin Jing had reached the extreme limit of Qi Refinement and had the aid of a Terror from the Cloud Sea, it would explain how he had defeated others on the Genius Rankings.

"I got lucky," Lin Jing said honestly. "At the academy, I was accidentally drawn into a cave heaven inheritance alongside two contemporaries, which gave me the chance to break through."

"As for the Ten Terrors, they were released by the academy’s teachers during a trial. I was fortunate to capture one."

The two elders fell silent for a moment before Elder Mo burst into laughter.

What they had thought to be a slow-growing Longevity Body, needing centuries to reach its potential, had instead shown growth so rapid it rivaled demonic cultivators who used life-shortening techniques.

"The Immortal Demon Serpent’s treasure should include a share for you," Elder Mo said, clutching the rankings list. "I’ll go speak with the demon king ancestors."

Meanwhile, in the Hundred Flower Valley, a yellow-butterfly disciple hurried to report to the valley’s Guardian Elder. The latest issue of the World Report also concerned the valley.

Many disciples had been unhappy about the Beast Taming Sect taking over part of their resources. After all, no one liked sharing the sect’s spiritual energy. However, the two sects had been long-time allies, with the Beast Taming Sect helping the valley on multiple occasions. Their mutual support was well-known throughout the Great Wasteland.

This arrangement was both an act of kindness and an investment in the Beast Taming Sect, a former giant that still held latent potential.

"Master, look at this—the latest Genius Rankings. Rank 32," the disciple said breathlessly.

"What has you in such a rush?" the elderly woman frowned, taking the document. Moments later, her expression shifted to one of disbelief.

"Lin Jing? A disciple of the Beast Taming Sect? Rank 32? Qi Refinement Stage 11? How is this possible?"

Her worldview was shaken. The information seemed so far-fetched that she wondered if the Tianji Pavilion had made an error.

The yellow-butterfly disciple stood quietly, sensing her master’s shock. Until now, the Beast Taming Sect’s disciples, while somewhat stronger overall than those of the Hundred Flower Valley, were still within a comprehensible range.

But this Lin Jing, who had suddenly emerged, was beyond comprehension.

"Return to the gate. I must see the valley master immediately," the elder said, transforming into a butterfly and vanishing at incredible speed.

Her mind was in turmoil. If the rankings were accurate, the valley master’s decision to support the Beast Taming Sect had been brilliant.

Anyone on the Genius Rankings was destined for Nascent Soul, and not just any Nascent Soul, but one with the potential for perfection. Such a cultivator could eventually become a top-tier powerhouse in the Great Wasteland.

Lin Jing’s foundation, achieving Qi Refinement Stage 11, was so extraordinary that he might one day ascend to the Nascent Soul Transformation stage. Even if he failed, his immense power could allow him to influence the forces of heaven and earth, qualifying him as a half-divine existence.

The Beast Taming Sect had hidden a prodigy far surpassing its core disciples—no, even its current sect master.

With Lin Jing, even if the sect didn’t fully recover within a century, his eventual rise would ensure its restoration. Upon reaching Nascent Soul Transformation, the Beast Taming Sect would not just be a leading sect in the Great Wasteland but would stand among the Ancient Kingdom’s finest.

Across the Great Wasteland, the new World Report left many cultivators in disbelief.

"Beast Taming Sect? Lin Jing?"

"Never heard of him!"

"Not surprising. According to the report, he’s been studying at Hidden Dragon Academy in the Ancient Domain. That’s a world away from the Great Wasteland."

"How lucky is the Beast Taming Sect? Just before they were sealed by the Sect Regulatory Office, a super-genius emerged!"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 64: The Seven Spiritual Seeds
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 64: The Seven Spiritual Seeds

"Valley Master…"

"It seems the Beast Taming Sect’s fortunes have not yet ended," the Hundred Flower Valley Master remarked, tossing aside the World Report . "In that case, let’s allocate another mountain peak to them."

"Is that all, Valley Master?" The Guardian Elder fixed her gaze on the Valley Master, her eyes earnest. "This young man’s talent is a once-in-a-millennium phenomenon in the Great Wasteland. Moreover, he’s a disciple of the Beast Taming Sect, a sect that has been our ally for generations..."

"Lady Fang, there are no outsiders here. Speak your mind," the Valley Master replied, looking at the elder woman.

The elder regarded the Valley Master, who was seated amidst a sea of flowers, surrounded by butterflies, with a look of satisfaction. As a woman, she could fully appreciate the Valley Master’s extraordinary charm.

Time had been kind to her, tracing only faint and graceful lines at the corners of her eyes, adding a layer of elegance and wisdom. Her hair, as smooth and dark as midnight silk, shimmered faintly under the light. It was tied into a simple yet elegant bun with a butterfly-shaped hairpin, while a few stray strands swayed gently in the breeze.

Wrapped in a plain dress that highlighted her graceful figure, her serene and delicate face exuded both beauty and authority.

The Guardian Elder began cautiously, "I’ve watched you rise step by step to become the Valley Master. Our valley has long intermarried with the Beast Taming Sect. Unfortunately, their current sect master, Xu Xiaoyao, broke this tradition with his… unusual preferences, favoring beasts over people."

"Indeed. Your talent surpasses even Xu Xiaoyao’s. The Beast Taming Sect only trails us due to its reliance on demon kings. If their sect master were someone of your caliber—"

Before she could finish, the Valley Master’s eyes flashed coldly. A faint ripple of power surged, and the Guardian Elder was instantly flung into the distance, leaving a trail of blood as she hit the ground.

"Still as fiery as ever, I see…" The Guardian Elder struggled to her feet, muttering, "I’m already seventy-one; what does it matter if I get scolded again?"

Meanwhile, Elder Mo, on his way to visit one of the sect's demon king ancestors, paused mid-step as a shadow flew past him.

"Was that Elder Fang… being thrown out?" he muttered. "Who in the valley could overpower her? She’s in the early Golden Core stage..."

Shrugging, he continued on his way. The Beast Taming Sect had more pressing matters, particularly regarding the true disciples.

Currently, the sect's true disciples were enduring rigorous training under the demon king ancestors. Once respected figures in the Great Wasteland, these disciples were now subjected to brutal trials. Even their teachers, though sympathetic, dared not object.

Elder Mo’s first visit was to Wang Batian, the lowest-ranking of the seven demon king ancestors. Known for his stern methods, Wang Batian was a turtle demon famous for his resilience.

When Elder Mo reached the cave, he heard cries of pain. Inside a formation, a young disciple clad in armor and wielding a turtle-shell shield was enduring attacks from all directions—beams of light equivalent to the strikes of Foundation Establishment cultivators.

"A Sword Light Formation," Elder Mo murmured, shaking his head. The sect master must have undergone similar training under the Divine Pine to achieve his current strength.

"What's your purpose here?" Wang Batian, in his turtle form, basked atop a mountain under the sun’s warm rays.

"The sect master has returned," Elder Mo said. "I came to request a share of the Immortal Demon Serpent’s treasure."

"Hmm?" Wang Batian frowned. "Even though he provided the intelligence, the sect’s resources are scarce. You understand that the Longevity Body is an uncertain investment at best."

Elder Mo sighed. The Longevity Body, while valuable, required immense time and resources to cultivate. Without abundant resources, even the most promising constitutions could be liabilities.

"I heard a genius who trained at the Hidden Dragon Academy has returned," Wang Batian said. "What does he want now? Spirit seeds?"

"Yes," Elder Mo replied.

"Tch, another competitor for the seeds," grumbled the disciple within the formation as he deflected the light beams.

"Silence!" Elder Mo snapped. This true disciple, despite his defensive prowess as a Turtle Body, was one of the less successful outcomes of the sect’s experiments.

Wang Batian explained, "The spiritual seeds are too rare to distribute freely. Once matured, they will yield peerless spiritual plants, capable of aiding even Golden Core cultivators in their pursuit of Nascent Soul."

Elder Mo nodded. "I understand. However, Lin Jing’s circumstances are unique. See for yourself."

He handed over the Genius Rankings . Wang Batian scanned the document, and the mountain grew quiet.

"Where is he?"

Even the ancient Wang Batian was shaken by what he read. A disciple of the Beast Taming Sect, with a Longevity Body, ranking 32nd on the Genius Rankings at the Qi Refinement Stage?

"If this is true, then he is indeed deserving of a spiritual seed. Among the seven seeds, there is one particularly suited for him."

The seeds retrieved from the Immortal Demon Serpent’s treasure were all extraordinary:

"Unfortunately, even with the Hundred Flower Valley’s spiritual veins, these seeds will take decades to mature," Wang Batian sighed, recalling the treasure’s incredible value.

Elder Mo nodded in understanding but remained hopeful. With Lin Jing’s rise, the sect’s future seemed brighter than ever.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 65: The Demon King's Youthful Self
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 65: The Demon King's Youthful Self

Among cultivators, there exists a significant group that views immortality not as a blessing but a curse. Many ancient monsters, having lived far too long, often “go mad” in their twilight years. This “madness” isn’t about losing one’s mind but about losing one’s humanity, growing increasingly indifferent and evolving into something other than human.

The personalities of high-level cultivators, solidified over millennia, often become extreme and eccentric. While some cultivators manage to retain their original nature despite their long lifespans, such individuals remain a rare minority.

Cloud-Hidden Orchid.

This sixth-grade spiritual plant, once consumed, can keep a cultivator’s mind calm even through centuries of secluded meditation. It shields against inner demons, the erosion of time, and negative emotions, with effects lasting a lifetime.

Grand Ancestor Wang Batian mulled over the situation. This item seemed tailor-made for Lin Jing, whose Longevity Body might otherwise lead him to lose his humanity. It would ensure Lin Jing wouldn’t degrade into a shadow of his former self due to his longevity.

“Since you think it’s suitable, perhaps we should consult the other Grand Ancestors as well?” Elder Mo suggested.

Nearby, a Turtle Body True Inheritor crawled out of the Sword Light Formation, his armor and shield transforming back into a Mechanized Armadillo and Mechanical Turtle, respectively. He had clearly employed the Beast-Weaponization Secret Art, turning his pet beasts into magical artifacts. Confused, he stared at Elder Mo, unsure what he had shown Wang Batian to make him change his mind so abruptly.

“He won’t need to compete with you anymore.”

This… is the Genius Rankings?

Indeed, the Grand Ancestors had stated that if a sect disciple could enter the Genius Rankings, it would greatly enhance the sect’s fortune!

“That’s impossible!”

The True Inheritor’s breathing stalled as he spotted Lin Jing’s name on the list.

“Even if all of you True Inheritors worked together, you might not match him alone,” Elder Mo revealed a harsh truth, leaving the Turtle Body True Inheritor struggling to accept it.

Meanwhile, Wang Batian had already transmitted messages to the other Demon King Grand Ancestors.

Previously, not even the Sect Master had placed significant hopes on Lin Jing achieving great progress within a century. Resources were prioritized for the True Inheritors instead. Everyone knew how difficult it was for someone with the Longevity Body to progress. But seeing the new Genius Rankings, Wang Batian realized they had made a grave mistake.

Just as exceptional mortals occasionally appear amidst the multitudes, Lin Jing might very well be a prodigy among those with the Longevity Body!

“Come to me at once.”

At that moment, the various Demon King Grand Ancestors, each engaged in grueling training with their disciples, received the transmission from Wang Batian, the most senior among them.

Moments later, one figure after another descended onto the mountaintop. Among them were a stag-shaped Demon King, an elephant-shaped Demon King, and a bird-shaped Demon King, their demon auras surging skyward.

While these mighty beings gathered, none of them noticed a shadow sneaking into a forbidden area within the Hundred Flower Valley.
