“A few minor formations set up by those Demon Kings? Laughable! Nothing can stop me!”

The Divine Pine, in its Pinecone Avatar, slipped into the Beast Taming Sect’s Spiritual Seed Nursery.

“Moonlit Dao Lotus, Violet Heaven Ginseng, Four-Sun Divine Lingzhi, Dawn Dew Flower, Four-Life Exquisite Grass, Eternal Evergreen Vine, Cloud-Hidden Orchid… Such rare treasures! I underestimated this Dead Demon Serpent’s hoard. But it makes sense. These spiritual seeds are precious enough to make it risk everything to seize them.”

The Divine Pine initially held no interest in the Dead Demon Serpent’s treasure. But seeing the variety of spiritual plants, its thoughts shifted. Even for itself, obtaining such plants in a short time would be nearly impossible.

“I could use the Violet Heaven Ginseng as the core and the others as supplements to forge a Thunder Pine Avatar. Cultivating as grass and wood, defying the natural order, will invite tribulation sooner than any human cultivator. Training with lightning techniques would even benefit the heavens.”

“It seems the heavens must pay a price for this ride, haha!”

The Divine Pine Avatar vanished in a flash, reappearing moments later above the Demon King Grand Ancestors.

By now, the Demon King Grand Ancestors had all reviewed the new Genius Rankings, astonished at Lin Jing’s performance.

Though they were demons, their hearts had long grown human after guarding the sect alongside its human cultivators for over a millennium. The Grand Ancestors genuinely wished for the Beast Taming Sect’s legacy to endure.

If Lin Jing had merely been an ordinary Longevity Body, the Grand Ancestors might have compensated him in other ways while focusing resources on others. But with Lin Jing demonstrating unparalleled potential, their hesitation grew.

“He deserves a share,” declared the Elephant Ancestor. “Even if he doesn’t reach Nascent Soul within a century, his talent proves he’s worth more than such constraints!”

“Indeed. I’d argue one share isn’t enough. He should receive all seven spiritual seeds for full cultivation support! Even his Protector should receive additional resources!”

A voice interrupted their agreement, unfamiliar yet commanding.

“Who goes there?!”

The Grand Ancestors looked up, spotting a cracked pinecone gazing down at them.

For a moment, they felt transported into another realm, surrounded by towering grass and wood, staring up at an immense Divine Pine stretching toward the heavens.

“The power of heaven and earth… A Nascent Soul Transformation of Grass and Wood!”

The Grand Ancestors were shaken. A Nascent Soul Transformation cultivator had appeared among them unexpectedly.

“It’s you! You’ve achieved Nascent Soul!”

The Giant Turtle, once a pet beast of the Second Sect Master, stared in disbelief at the Divine Pine.

“Heavenly Pine Ancestor of Tiandu Peak?!” Elder Mo exclaimed, stunned as he witnessed the Divine Pine’s full power for the first time.

“You’re already in the late Nascent Soul stage, Little Turtle. Isn’t it natural that I’ve reached Nascent Soul as well?” The Divine Pine chuckled, glancing at Wang Batian with an air of indulgence, as if regarding a mere child.

At this moment, all present were deeply shocked. None had expected the Divine Pine Guardian Deity of Tiandu Peak to be a Nascent Soul cultivator!

And they recalled… this Divine Pine had accompanied Lin Jing to the academy as his Protector.

Suddenly, everything made sense. Lin Jing’s monstrous growth was no longer a mystery—he had been guided by a Nascent Soul Transformation cultivator!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 66: Bestowal of Title
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 66: Bestowal of Title

Indeed, without the Divine Pine teaching Lin Jing the Everlasting Emperor's Longevity Art through the Pine Leaf Mouse, and taking him to train in the depths of the Great Wasteland, Lin Jing might still be selling Mechanical Bamboo Rifles, progressing at a snail’s pace!

Not to mention the terrifying existence of the Ice Soul Gu, which was key to his greatest fortune in the Dragon-Phoenix Cavern Heaven, enabling him to break through extreme boundaries. It was only thanks to the Divine Pine that the gu was safely housed within him.

In many ways, Lin Jing’s rapid growth, which defied the typical challenges of the Longevity Body, could be attributed to the Divine Pine’s unconventional, almost devilish, methods of tutelage.

"Do you all agree with me?"

The Divine Pine’s voice was calm but commanding.

"I’ve seen the spiritual seeds planted in the Hundred Flower Valley. Given the quality of the spiritual vein, they’ll take at least sixty years to mature. Planting them in another sect’s domain is risky and wasteful.

"Leave them to me. With my cultivation of the Grass and Wood Dao, I can accelerate the growth of all seven seeds instantly.

"Using them entirely to nurture Lin Jing, the Beast Taming Sect’s prodigy, will benefit the sect far more."

"Senior is correct," Wang Batian finally spoke after a long pause. "Entrusting the seeds to you will maximize their value as quickly as possible."

The Divine Pine let out a satisfied chuckle. "Since you’ve agreed, I’ll take these seeds. Also, keep my Nascent Soul Transformation a secret—it’s no benefit to your sect. After all, I’m no longer your Guardian Deity."

With that, the Divine Pine’s figure vanished, leaving the Demon King Grand Ancestors standing on the mountaintop, breathing heavily.

"Do you think the Heavenly Pine Ancestor will truly use all the seeds for Lin Jing…?"

"..."

"I’ll take four; you can have three."

"???"

Elsewhere, Lin Jing had been discussing Mechanized Arts with Elder Yu when the Divine Pine abruptly called him away.

Outside, the Divine Pine handed Lin Jing three spiritual seeds, leaving the latter baffled.

“The Elder Mo and the Demon King Ancestors agreed to give us all seven seeds. I’ll take four, and you get these three.”

The seeds Lin Jing received were for the Nine-Sun Divine Lingzhi, Nine-Life Exquisite Grass, and Cloud-Hidden Orchid.

“What are these? Wait, they’re all for us? This… seems too good to be true,” Lin Jing asked, incredulous.

“The Nine-Sun Divine Lingzhi is for your Dragon Carp. When it reaches the Demon Core Stage, feeding it this should help it leap from early Core Formation to Core Formation Perfection, saving years of cultivation.”

The Dragon Carp, hovering nearby, widened its eyes and began to breathe heavily. It knew it was lucky to follow Lin Jing—such fortunes were beyond belief! The Core Formation Stage would soon be within its grasp, its reign just on the horizon. It glanced at the Divine Pine, its former resentment over past beatings now replaced with admiration.

“The Nine-Life Exquisite Grass, once matured, grants unparalleled regenerative ability during the Qi Refinement Stage. For ordinary bodies, it could even regrow someone from mere bones. However, with your Longevity Body, the effect may weaken. Still, you’ll no longer need pills to replenish blood or heal injuries. Your strong physique, bolstered by Longevity Blood, will recover any energy loss quickly.”

“The Cloud-Hidden Orchid is simply for consumption. It will ensure you don’t lose yourself on the path of longevity.”

Lin Jing took a deep breath, feeling overwhelmed. Yet, curiosity lingered—what were the four seeds the Divine Pine had claimed?

The Nine-Sun Divine Lingzhi was powerful but irrelevant for at least a decade. The Nine-Life Exquisite Grass, while impressive, gave Lin Jing an ominous feeling. With his regenerative capabilities, he worried about being targeted by a powerful beast. As for the Cloud-Hidden Orchid, it seemed far removed from his current concerns, as he hadn’t yet experienced the passage of time.

“You’re wondering if I kept all the good stuff for myself, aren’t you?”

The Divine Pine scoffed at Lin Jing’s questioning gaze. “One plant increases lifespan—useless to you. Another enhances insight—you already have the Hidden Dragon Arts, so you don’t lack it. A third aids breakthroughs—you have all the time in the world.

"I plan to use these three as supplements, with a fourth spiritual plant containing heavenly lightning power as the core, to forge a Thunder Pine Avatar. That way, I won’t risk being struck dead by tribulation lightning."

"While you’re far from facing tribulations, remember to prepare. Cultivate lightning techniques or contract a lightning beast. Ignorance of lightning power will only leave you vulnerable."

Lin Jing nodded earnestly.

Legends say tribulation lightning is a celestial punishment designed to test lower-realm cultivators. In essence, it is divine lightning. Human cultivators typically face tribulations only upon reaching the Vacant Profound Stage, but those practicing special techniques or belonging to unique races often attract it earlier.

For example, the Grass and Wood Clan, forbidden by the Immortal Court to gain sentience, often faces tribulations prematurely.

Lin Jing recalled Senior Brother Ouyang once expressing concerns that even if a method to revive the demon race was discovered, it might lead to even greater calamities—like being targeted by tribulations.

Tribulation lightning is far deadlier than ordinary lightning. Most high-level cultivators die not in battle or from old age but during their breakthroughs, unable to withstand the trial.

While cultivating lightning techniques or raising lightning beasts might not guarantee survival, it at least provides a layer of defense, strengthening resistance against lightning.

Dazed, Lin Jing returned to the hall. Elder Mo also reappeared, equally stunned.

Elder Yu noticed their changed expressions and couldn’t help but ask, “Lin Jing, when do you plan to leave?”

“There’s no rush,” Lin Jing replied. Though he couldn’t stay indefinitely, he figured another ten days or so wouldn’t hurt.

“That’s perfect!” Elder Mo took a deep breath. “The Demon King Ancestors plan to officially name you the Holy Son of the Beast Taming Sect. The ceremony will be held in a month, inviting sects from across the Great Wasteland to reestablish the sect’s prestige.”

Lin Jing: ???

“But the Sect Master isn’t here!”

“The Demon King Ancestors believe the Sect Master won’t object. He has no children, so there’s no one to challenge your position as heir. The only potential successor, his adopted daughter, practices a different technique altogether, disqualifying her from inheriting the position of Sect Master.” Elder Mo gave Lin Jing a pointed look.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 67: The Stirring of the Wasteland
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 67: The Stirring of the Wasteland

Since Lin Jing had joined the Beast Taming Sect only recently and hadn’t traveled the Great Wasteland as a disciple of the sect, no one had even heard of him prior to the announcement of the Genius Rankings. This was why many suspected Tianji Pavilion had made a mistake when his name appeared on the list.

Now, with Lin Jing's potential shining brightly, the sect’s Demon Kings and Ancestors could not allow him to remain an unknown “Inner Sect Disciple.” It would tarnish the sect’s reputation, making it seem like they did not value talent.

After all, the Inner Sect disciple rank was far from the core of the sect. Even the status of a core disciple couldn’t fully reflect his importance. Only by naming Lin Jing the Holy Son of the Beast Taming Sect and a candidate for the next sect master could the sect officially and irrevocably bind him to their future.

When a sect has a successor with the potential to reach the Nascent Soul Transformation stage, its fortune would undoubtedly flourish. Such a proclamation also served as a signal:

"Boosting the sect's destiny isn't just about improving the strength of its disciples. Recruiting exceptional talents or cultivators with extraordinary potential can also achieve this.”

If a few human Nascent Soul cultivators joined the Beast Taming Sect, the balance between human and demon fortunes within the sect would significantly stabilize. However, such a scenario had always been nearly impossible. After all, Nascent Soul cultivators, even rogue ones, were powerful figures in their own right. Most preferred their freedom or, if they sought to join a sect, would opt for super sects rather than a struggling one like the Beast Taming Sect, which lacked spiritual veins and was under lockdown.

But if these rogue cultivators could see the sect's potential, things might change.

A sect that could potentially produce a Nascent Soul Transformation cultivator would be a rare gem in the Great Wasteland. By becoming the Holy Son, Lin Jing would signal that the Beast Taming Sect was on the rise.

Thus, Elder Mo explained, “You see now why the Ancestors wish for you to become the Holy Son.”

Lin Jing nodded. “I understand all that… I just have no interest in the position of sect master.”

Senior Brother Ouyang, for instance, seemed particularly keen on that role.

“Fine, Holy Son it is,” Lin Jing relented. “Being the Holy Son doesn’t mean I have to become the sect master, after all.” Considering the role the sect played in his past and the treasures he had taken from the Immortal Demon, he felt obligated to accept the title.

However, one thing worried him. By openly showing himself, wouldn’t he attract too much attention? He wasn’t sure if the Demon Kings and elders knew he had already been assassinated by a Demon Emperor in Hidden Dragon City. If they knew, would they still dare to hold this inauguration ceremony?

He sought counsel from Senior Pine. “Elder?”

“Don’t worry. With me here, even if you flaunt yourself, those hunting you won’t dare act recklessly,” the Thunder Pine replied nonchalantly.

Lin Jing wasn’t sure how reassuring that was, but it would have to do.

The Genius Rankings announcement hadn’t reached everyone yet, with only those cultivators who regularly purchased the World Report learning of it immediately. Among the Beast Taming Sect disciples, few were aware, as they typically saved their Spirit Stones to focus on cultivation.

Disciples like Wang Laozu, Qin Caixin, and Senior Brother Ziran—who had accidentally entered the Cavern Heaven with Lin Jing—remained in closed-door cultivation, improving their strength.

Without frequent sect events, disciple interactions had dwindled. But today, confusion spread when the sect suddenly issued a summons.

At a plaza on Beast Taming Peak, a large number of disciples gathered, all looking puzzled.

“Even the core disciples are here?” He Yiming noticed with surprise. Even the elusive true disciples had shown up.

“What’s going on?” Ordinary disciples weren’t the only ones baffled; many core disciples were equally unsure. Only the core disciple with the Turtle Body Physique gazed skyward and murmured, “Something big is about to happen.”

His cryptic words drew even more confusion. “We’re already in this state—what ‘big’ could possibly happen now?”

Before anyone could ponder further, elders began to arrive at the plaza. Nearly every member of the Beast Taming Sect based in Hundred Flower Valley was present, deepening the ominous atmosphere.

Finally, Elder Mo and Elder Yu appeared in the sky, addressing the crowd. Elder Mo spoke first, “Today, the new Genius Rankings have been released.”

“Our sect has a disciple on the list!” he announced.

“What?!” Shock rippled through the crowd. Even the elders looked surprised.

“Which core disciple made it?” Elder Li Hai of Nine Dragons Peak speculated. “Could it be Zhang Jia?” He glanced at the Turtle Body disciple, as that disciple had been personally mentored by Grand Ancestor Wang Batian.

“No, no, no!” The disciple hurriedly waved off the suggestion. He didn’t dare claim credit.

Elder Mo let the tension build before revealing, “Rank 32 on the Genius Rankings. Cultivation: Qi Refinement Stage 11. Listed for entering the Immortal Palace Cavern Heaven twice, breaking the Extreme Stage of Qi Refinement alongside his spirit beast, subduing the Ten Terrors of the Cloud Sea, and defeating ranks 98 and 66. The listed individual is Lin Jing, a former Inner Sect disciple of the Beast Taming Sect!”

The crowd fell silent in disbelief. He Yiming, Qin Caixin, and Senior Brother Ziran, who had once been Outer Sect disciples with Lin Jing, looked astounded. Yet, as the news sank in, they realized Lin Jing was the only one capable of such feats.

“Lin Jing… The guy who won first place during the Outer Sect Ascending Dragon Festival?” Some disciples were still trying to recall who he was.

Not giving them time to process, Elder Mo continued, “By the decree of the sect’s Ancestors, Lin Jing is hereby named the Holy Son of the Beast Taming Sect. A month from now, at the site of the sect’s original mountain gate, we shall hold the Holy Son Inauguration Ceremony!”

“Next, each of you will report to Elder Yu to receive invitations. You are to deliver these invitations to designated sects, families, and rogue cultivators’ Immortal Cities as swiftly as possible!”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 68: Master of Hundred Flower Valley
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 68: Master of Hundred Flower Valley

Disciples of the Beast Taming Sect scattered in all directions, and while the Great Wasteland stirred because of Lin Jing, he had already retreated into the best cave dwelling on Beast Taming Peak to focus on cultivation.

"The Genius Rankings are beginning to show their worth," Lin Jing murmured as he glanced at the back of his hand. A golden aura dragon flickered faintly, as if on the verge of awakening.

"Unfortunately, without holding an official position, the Hidden Dragon Arts can never fully mature."

Currently, his Hidden Dragon Arts had only reached a minor level of mastery. No matter how much fame he accumulated, it could only replenish the consumed Hidden Dragon Fortune, but not push the technique further.

To truly evolve the Hidden Dragon Arts, he needed to merge the Hidden Dragon Fortune with an official position's fortune. At that point, a new ability would emerge: the power of dragon fortune, which could temporarily elevate his cultivation.

It was said that the first Ancient Emperor relied on this ability to channel the national fortune of an entire kingdom, becoming the strongest individual on the continent—someone whose cultivation was closest to immortality.

"Senior Pine, are you there?" Lin Jing called out.

"What is it?"

"I want to help the Flame Dragon Carp reach the tenth stage of Qi Refinement. With it by my side, the cold might be more bearable."

"By harnessing the potential of Hidden Dragon Fortune, feeding the carp with Longevity Blood for nourishment, and relying on its natural talents, achieving the tenth stage of Qi Refinement shouldn’t be impossible. But it's still not a guarantee," Lin Jing speculated.

"Hmm?"

"If we could borrow some diluted dragon blood from the Southern Mountain Demon Emperor's semi-divine avatar... there’d be no question about it achieving the tenth stage."

The Flame Dragon Carp silently nodded, though its frustration was evident.

"No need," Senior Pine cut in. "You have the Ice Soul Gu within your body; you won't freeze to death. A tenth-stage Flame Dragon Carp is far inferior to an eleventh-stage Ice Gu. Its warmth is barely one percent of what the Ice Soul Gu can disperse."

Lin Jing fell silent.

The Flame Dragon Carp glared angrily—was it really worse than a spider? It couldn’t believe how much it disliked Senior Pine.

"Fine, let’s give it a try," Lin Jing said, pulling the indignant Flame Dragon Carp deeper into the cave dwelling.

In the depths of the cave, Lin Jing watched as the Flame Dragon Carp floated in midair. He knew it had been longing to eat the Cloud Sea Mosquito Beast for a while now.

With the carp's current cultivation, even with its strong bloodline, it was impossible to contend against a swarm of mosquito beasts. Fortunately, he only needed to summon one mosquito beast at a time.

"Go."

Lin Jing activated the Myriad Beast Circle. Inside the barren land of the circle, a swarm of mosquito beasts buzzed aimlessly. Suddenly, a drop of Longevity Blood fell, releasing an enticing aroma that sent the mosquito beasts into a frenzy. They became savage, abandoning their cohesion and attacking one another in a mad rush toward the blood.

"Fools!"

Moments later, a fat mosquito beast at the fourth stage of Qi Refinement managed to swallow the drop of Longevity Blood. Its body began to emit an ominous red mist, and its aura grew terrifyingly stronger.

Boom!

In an instant, it showed signs of mutation, radiating a special aura that made the other mosquito beasts retreat in fear. This mosquito beast seemed poised to become a "Mosquito King."

"Huh. Can Longevity Blood cause such a transformation in demonic beasts?" Lin Jing observed. "The fear it inspires among the other mosquitoes feels similar to the Beast Taming Physique."

Before Lin Jing could determine whether the transformation was due to the Longevity Blood or his own blood's beast-taming traits, the Mosquito King began to display an alarming ability: commanding the swarm.

Previously, the Cloud Sea Mosquito Beasts moved in groups but lacked leadership. Now, with the Mosquito King, their cohesion increased, and they began a new assault on the Myriad Beast Circle's barriers.

Realizing the danger, Lin Jing quickly pulled the Mosquito King out of the circle to prevent it from competing with him for control over the swarm.

At that moment, the Flame Dragon Carp, which had been waiting eagerly, opened its mouth wide—wider than its body—and swallowed the Mosquito King whole before it could react. Flames ignited within the carp, refining the beast like a pill.

"One at a time seems manageable," Lin Jing nodded. "In that case, we’ll start hatching the dormant mosquito eggs soon."

As he patted the contented carp on the head, Lin Jing noticed its blissful expression and considered bestowing more Hidden Dragon Fortune upon it. Suddenly, his face darkened.

"You seem to be enjoying yourself," he muttered.

The Flame Dragon Carp froze, puzzled by its master's sudden foul mood.

With a sigh, Lin Jing thought about how, ever since leaving the Dragon-Phoenix Cavern Heaven, he hadn’t been able to enjoy food properly. The lingering effects of eating Dragon Liver and Phoenix Marrow had ruined his appetite for anything else.

Looking at the carp’s satisfaction, he felt a sudden urge to indulge his own taste buds.

"I heard that Hundred Flower Valley produces a unique nectar called the Hundred Flower Immortal Honey. Its flavor is said to be unparalleled, one of the top ten delicacies in the Great Wasteland. However, it never leaves the valley and is reserved only for the valley master."

"Maybe that could awaken my dulled palate," Lin Jing mused. He decided to give it a try.

Unable to resist, he flew out of the cave, leaving the Flame Dragon Carp behind, confused and frustrated at being left alone again.

Lin Jing intended to visit the Master of Hundred Flower Valley. Even though he was only at the Qi Refinement Stage, as the Holy Son of the Beast Taming Sect, he had every right to pay his respects as a junior.

Before he could request an introduction from the sect’s grand elder, an elderly woman flew toward Beast Taming Peak and happened to cross paths with him.

"Greetings, Senior," Lin Jing bowed respectfully.

"You..." The elder, Elder Fang of Hundred Flower Valley, scrutinized him. "I’ve never seen you in the valley before. Are you Lin Jing?"

"Yes, Senior. I am Lin Jing," he replied.

"So it’s you." Elder Fang, aware of the recent events in the Beast Taming Sect, had been on her way to inquire further. She hadn’t expected to run into the central figure of the commotion.

"I am Fang, the Grand Elder of Hundred Flower Valley. If you need anything in the valley, just come to me," she said warmly. Although strict with her disciples, she was notably kind to this rising genius.

"The Grand Elder?" Lin Jing was surprised but quickly asked, "Senior, I’ve heard that Hundred Flower Valley has a rare delicacy, the Hundred Flower Immortal Honey..."

"You want the Hundred Flower Immortal Honey?!" Elder Fang exclaimed. The honey was a top-tier treasure, reserved for the valley master. Historically, only the sect master of the Beast Taming Sect—when married to the valley master—had ever been granted a taste.

Elder Fang’s expression turned thoughtful. "I knew it! My instincts were right. You’re young and don’t want to take unnecessary detours. Very good, I support you!"

Lin Jing was stunned. "Huh?"

Why couldn’t he understand what the Grand Elder was talking about?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 69: The Myriad Beast Sect
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 69: The Myriad Beast Sect

"Grand Elder, could there be some misunderstanding?"

Lin Jing’s expression was flat. He had only wanted a taste of the Hundred Flower Immortal Honey, but somehow the conversation had veered into whether he was avoiding detours in life.

Could it be that this honey carried some special implications?

"Misunderstanding?"

Elder Fang’s wrinkled face broke into a faint smile. "The Hundred Flower Immortal Honey is a sacred treasure of Hundred Flower Valley, produced in minuscule amounts. Only the Valley Masters of each generation are allowed to consume it.

"Beyond the Valley Master, only the Valley Master’s spouse, on the day of their grand wedding, has ever had the privilege of tasting it. Don’t tell me you didn’t know."

Lin Jing fell silent.

He suddenly recalled hearing about this.

But the Valley Master’s age... wasn’t she older than his great-grandmother?

"Senior, I merely wish to taste the honey. I harbor no improper intentions toward the Valley Master," Lin Jing quickly clarified.

Elder Fang eyed Lin Jing suspiciously.

Was he too embarrassed to admit the truth, or did he genuinely just want to satisfy his palate? The latter seemed unlikely—Lin Jing didn’t look like a glutton.

"Are you sure?"

"I’ll pass," Lin Jing replied, deciding that Elder Fang’s personality was too unpredictable. He made an excuse and promptly left.

Better to watch the Flame Dragon Carp eat than risk tarnishing his reputation.

If he actually ate that honey, he’d never live it down.

A Few Days Later

East of the Great Wasteland, in the Misty Forest.

In the soft light of dawn, a procession of carriages emerged from the forest’s edge. The carriages, along with the horses pulling them, appeared to be crafted entirely from ink strokes, exuding a sense of profound elegance.

Seated atop the leading carriage was a coachman dressed in black-and-white robes. His face was obscured by a wide-brimmed straw hat, and his entire being radiated an imposing aura.

Inside the carriage behind him were three individuals—an elderly man and two younger disciples, all dressed in matching robes.

"Finally, we’ve arrived in the Ancient Kingdom. Thank you for guiding us!" The gray-haired elder cupped his hands in gratitude toward the coachman.

"No need to thank me," the coachman replied. "Your Myriad Beast Sect is an important partner of our Lotus Pavilion. Escorting you here was no trouble at all.

"However," he added with a note of caution, "I must remind you—once you enter the Ancient Kingdom, mind your behavior. This is no longer Qingzhou."

With those words, the coachman and his ink-formed carriage vanished, leaving the three members of the Myriad Beast Sect behind.

The gray-haired elder turned to the two younger disciples—a man and a woman.

"Qingyu, Whitebird, let’s go."

"Yes, Deputy Sect Master," the two replied. Transforming into streaks of light, they flew toward the nearest Immortal City.

"The price we paid to travel from Qingzhou to the Ancient Kingdom wasn’t small," the Nascent Soul elder began. "With the Lotus Pavilion’s guarantee, we’re free to act in the Great Wasteland. I brought you two here to broaden your horizons. Remember, do not violate the Ancient Kingdom’s laws."

The disciples nodded solemnly.

Before they could land and find a place to rest near the city, the elder suddenly halted in midair, extending his hand.

A massive hand of true essence materialized, snatching a cultivator riding a giant eagle from the ground below. The rider, along with his mount, was lifted into the sky, his face pale with terror.

"Spare me, Senior! I don’t know how I’ve offended you!" the rider begged, standing atop the captured eagle. His fear grew as he gazed at the Nascent Soul elder’s imposing projection in the sky.

"Are you a disciple of the Beast Taming Sect?" the elder asked.

The eagle-rider hesitated, then quickly answered, "Yes, Senior. I am an Inner Sect disciple of the Beast Taming Sect. May I ask what you need from me?"

"Just as I thought," the elder remarked with a smile. "I sensed your Beast Taming Qi and guessed as much. To think we’d run into someone from the sect we were looking for so soon after arriving."

The disciple’s expression turned to dismay. Why had he, of all people, been singled out by this mysterious elder?

"Senior, I am currently on an official mission, delivering invitations..." the disciple said nervously.

"Invitations?"

"Yes, Senior. I am here to deliver invitations for the upcoming Holy Son Inauguration Ceremony of the Beast Taming Sect. If you’re interested, you’re welcome to attend."

"Holy Son Inauguration Ceremony?" The Myriad Beast Sect trio exchanged puzzled glances.

The elder’s gaze sharpened. "Didn’t your sect just get sealed by the Ancient Kingdom? Yet you still have the leisure to hold an inauguration ceremony?"

"Lin Shixiong’s unparalleled talent earned him a spot on the Genius Rankings. This was a unanimous decision by several of our demonic ancestors," the disciple explained cautiously, offering the invitation with both hands.

The elder studied the invitation for a moment before speaking. "Very well. Return and tell your sect’s elders that the Myriad Beast Sect of Qingzhou will attend the ceremony to offer our congratulations."

Qingzhou? The disciple’s heart sank.

He knew Qingzhou was a region outside the Ancient Kingdom, a place without laws where massacres and exterminations were common. Hearing that his captor hailed from Qingzhou only deepened his unease.

"Thank you, Senior. I’ll take my leave." Without hesitation, the disciple fled, abandoning his task of delivering invitations and heading straight for Hundred Flower Valley.

The Nascent Soul elder handed the invitation to his two disciples.

"Eleventh stage of Qi Refinement?" Qingyu and Whitebird exclaimed after reading it.

The invitation not only provided details about Lin Jing’s placement on the Genius Rankings but also extended an open challenge to Core Formation cultivators from the Great Wasteland to spar with him during the ceremony.

The Myriad Beast Sect disciples were stunned. Lin Jing’s sect seemed confident in his abilities—not just boasting about his Genius Ranking placement but declaring him the strongest of his generation in the Great Wasteland.

The Nascent Soul elder frowned. "This complicates our mission. The Beast Taming Sect’s dissolution was supposed to be our opportunity to absorb their members. But with a talent like this, it won’t be easy to convince their elders to abandon their sect."

Turning to his disciples, he asked, "Now that you’ve seen the information on this Holy Son of the Beast Taming Sect, do you have the confidence to defeat him?"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 70: The Third Extreme Beast
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 70: The Third Extreme Beast

The Myriad Beast Sect disciple Qingyu studied the description on the Genius Rankings and remarked, "Deputy Sect Master, reaching the peak of Qi Refinement and being able to fight at the pinnacle of Foundation Establishment is monstrous, no doubt. But at the end of the day, Qi Refinement is still just Qi Refinement.

"Sure, his contracted beasts might have reached the Foundation Establishment stage, but..." She paused and then continued with confidence. "Alone, I might not be his match. But if the challenge allows participants below Core Formation and imposes no rules, as stated in this invitation, then with my perfected Foundation Establishment cultivation and the beast army nurtured in my Myriad Beast Circle, he’s no match for me."

"The Myriad Beast Sect has always prided itself on one disciple being equivalent to an army. Even a pseudo-core cultivator would hesitate to face a Foundation Establishment disciple leading a beast tide. No matter how strong he is, it’s impossible for him to take on a Core Formation cultivator while still in Qi Refinement."

"But," Qingyu added, "our combat style may not be easily accepted here in the Ancient Kingdom. They might consider it 'unfair.'"

The other disciple, Whitebird, nodded in agreement. "I’ve studied the Beast Taming Sect’s combat techniques. Compared to the Myriad Beast Sect’s war tactics, they’re much less threatening."

The Nascent Soul elder nodded thoughtfully. "You two are among the Myriad Beast Sect’s top five true disciples. Naturally, I trust your strength. But this Beast Taming Sect truly is an enigma."

"How could a sect with a Nascent Soul early-stage master as its leader, supported by a few demonic Nascent Soul elders, produce such a little monster?"

"Our sect leader is at the Nascent Soul Transformation stage, yet the young master he nurtured never broke through the peak of Qi Refinement."

"In any case, persuading the Beast Taming Sect amicably seems unlikely now.

"The only way to sway their strong members is to convince them that the Myriad Beast Sect offers a better future than this so-called Holy Son.

"Better yet, we could make their Holy Son aspire to join us."

The elder’s eyes gleamed. Defeating Lin Jing with Myriad Beast Sect techniques and offering him the promise of becoming a disciple under their Nascent Soul Transformation leader in Qingzhou might make him reconsider his loyalties.

"In the coming days, I’ll train you two further," the elder added.

The Deputy Sect Master, a perfected Nascent Soul cultivator, looked down on the Beast Taming Sect. Since its second-generation master, the sect had followed a path of sentimentality and bonds with beasts, which he believed fundamentally clashed with the principles of true beast mastery.

He was confident that once Lin Jing witnessed the superior methods of the Myriad Beast Sect, he would understand that it was far more suited to him than the Beast Taming Sect.

A few days later, a disciple returning from an encounter with the Myriad Beast Sect reported the incident to Elder Yu in Hundred Flower Valley.

Elder Yu was astonished and immediately informed the various demonic ancestors.

"The Myriad Beast Sect? What are those folks doing in the Great Wasteland?"

"Trying to poach members, probably! I’ve had people approach me recently, hoping I’d join their sects as a guest elder or a guardian beast after leaving the Beast Taming Sect. Fools! They underestimate my loyalty. I’ve spent more time in this sect than they’ve spent eating meals!"

The demonic ancestors speculated that the Myriad Beast Sect’s presence boded ill, especially with the Holy Son Inauguration Ceremony approaching.

"Inform the Holy Son about this. He needs to be prepared," one ancestor instructed, though it seemed more like a directive for Senior Pine than Lin Jing.

Elder Yu rushed to Lin Jing’s cave dwelling but found it enveloped in a dense protective formation.

"Hmm?" Elder Yu was impressed. Despite returning only briefly, Lin Jing was already hard at work cultivating. Such dedication, combined with his Longevity Physique, was the reason for his rapid progress.

"But why is it so hot?" Elder Yu murmured, sensing the oppressive heat radiating even through the formation. "Could it be that Flame Dragon Carp?"

Deciding to wait outside, Elder Yu guessed that the dragon carp might be attempting Foundation Establishment.

Inside, however, Lin Jing wasn’t helping the dragon carp break through Foundation Establishment but pushing it toward the peak of Qi Refinement. After ten days of effort, the carp was finally touching the threshold of the so-called "Extreme Stage of Qi Refinement."

Two days later, the formation around Lin Jing’s cave dissipated. When he emerged with the dragon carp, Elder Yu approached immediately.

Expecting a “transformed” dragon carp, Elder Yu was puzzled to find it looking the same as before.

"Wasn’t it breaking through?" he asked.

Lin Jing nodded. "It was, but not to Foundation Establishment."

Elder Yu fell silent. Another Extreme Stage Qi Refinement beast?

An uncontracted dragon carp, yet Lin Jing had nurtured it to the Extreme Stage of Qi Refinement?

Looking at the carp, Elder Yu felt a pang of worry. If certain powerful individuals discovered such a rare creature, it might be treated like a precious treasure and seized.

"Keep a close eye on it," Elder Yu advised. "Especially now... the Myriad Beast Sect is here in Qingzhou."

Lin Jing frowned upon hearing this.

He was well aware of the Myriad Beast Sect’s capabilities, having used their Myriad Beast Circle before. The sect’s reputation for overwhelming force made them formidable.

"The ancestors believe the Holy Son Inauguration Ceremony won’t be peaceful. Stay on guard," Elder Yu warned.

"I will, Elder Yu," Lin Jing replied. "I’ve been preparing carefully. I certainly won’t let myself become someone else’s stepping stone during the ceremony."

Lin Jing’s confidence was evident. Despite his current spot on the Genius Rankings, he understood that the list was age-restricted. Many stronger cultivators over thirty were not listed but remained a significant threat.

And now, with the sudden appearance of the Myriad Beast Sect, things were even more complicated.

"If there’s nothing else, I’ll return to my seclusion," Lin Jing said with a respectful bow.

"I still need to prepare a mechanized suit as a precaution. Elder Yu, I hope you’ll evaluate it when it’s complete."

"A... what?" Elder Yu asked, baffled.

"A mechanized power armor," Lin Jing replied nonchalantly.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 71: Demonic Beast Cultivation Method
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 71: Demonic Beast Cultivation Method

After the Pine Leaf Mouse’s Glazed Immortal Eyes evolved, Lin Jing began designing a new type of mechanized creation.

During the past few days, as he fed the Flame Dragon Carp with the Cloud Sea Mosquito Beasts, inspiration suddenly struck him. The primary material for the next generation of mechanized weaponry would undoubtedly come from the peculiar plants synthesized by the Pine Leaf Mouse. Since reaching the Foundation Establishment stage, the Pine Leaf Mouse had indeed created a variety of fresh and interesting combinations.

After extensive observation, Lin Jing discovered that the most promising synthesis was the Blood Devil Vine, created from the combination of the Bloodsucking Vine and other materials. The Blood Devil Vine could absorb the blood of demonic beasts no higher in rank than itself and condense a corresponding Blood Beast from that blood.

If the Blood Devil Vine absorbed blood from ordinary demonic beasts, the resulting Blood Beast would have lackluster combat power—barely making an impact during the Foundation Establishment stage. It paled in comparison to the suppressive effect of the Spirit Bamboo and Spiritual Beans combination during the Qi Refinement stage.

But what if it absorbed the blood of the Cloud Sea Mosquito Beasts?

The Blood Devil Vine could then manifest Blood Mosquito Beasts. Mosquito beasts as a species are known for growing stronger in greater numbers. By leveraging a large number of Blood Devil Vines and the blood of mosquito beasts, a terrifying chain reaction could be created. Lin Jing dubbed this phenomenon Mosquito Fusion.

In this way, he could command not only a conventional mosquito beast army but also a Blood Mosquito Beast army. Unlike the conventional mosquito beasts, which were notoriously difficult to control, the Pine Leaf Mouse had exceptional control over Blood Beasts. Whether for combat or other purposes, manipulating them would be far more manageable.

Currently, Lin Jing envisioned using the Blood Devil Vine as the mechanized core, with Blood Mosquito Beasts as the driving force, combining them into a massive armored construct. He planned to engrave formation patterns onto the construct to further enhance its collective strength, creating a colossal mechanized monstrosity powered by a Mosquito Fusion Reactor.

While Lin Jing was constructing the Blood Mosquito Titan, the date of the Holy Son Inauguration Ceremony for the Beast Taming Sect drew closer.

Suddenly, the tranquil Hundred Flower Valley was disrupted by an unexpected visitor.

"Deputy Sect Master Miao Ling of the Myriad Beast Sect requests an audience with the Beast Taming Sect’s esteemed Demonic Kings!"

The speaker was clad in a gray robe, with gray hair and a powerful presence. Without needing an announcement from the disciples of the Hundred Flower Valley, both the Valley Master and the Demonic Kings of the Beast Taming Sect treated his arrival as a significant event.

“Nascent Soul Great Perfection!”

The cultivation level of the Myriad Beast Sect’s Deputy Sect Master was far beyond that of any Nascent Soul Demonic King in the Beast Taming Sect, making him an intimidating force. Like the Beast Taming Sect, the Myriad Beast Sect also specialized in beast taming techniques, so his capabilities were hard to gauge.

Faced with such a formidable figure, the Demonic Kings dared not be negligent and revealed their full might. Six mighty Demonic King Ancestors appeared, each emanating overwhelming pressure.

Among them were:

Although the visitor's strength was undeniable, the Demonic Kings remained unafraid. After all, they were in the Ancient Celestial Kingdom, and the Myriad Beast Sect wouldn’t dare act recklessly here.

“What business does the Deputy Sect Master of the Myriad Beast Sect have?” the Parrot King asked. “If you’re here to offer congratulations, you’re a bit early!”

“There's no need to be so tense,” Miao Ling replied with a chuckle. “I simply thought it best to pay my respects to you all before the ceremony. Shall we continue this conversation inside?”

The Demonic Kings exchanged glances. Within the Hundred Flower Valley, the Protective Sect Formation was active and could potentially suppress even a Nascent Soul Great Perfection cultivator. This indicated that Miao Ling bore no immediate ill intent.

"Very well. Please, come in."

With that, several figures flickered and reappeared within the guest hall atop the Beast Taming Peak. Surrounded by the six Demonic Kings, Miao Ling wasted no time and stated his intentions.

Upon hearing him out, the Boar King snorted angrily. "Do you take us for fools? We know exactly what your Myriad Beast Sect stands for! You think we’d give up our freedom to become your slaves?”

The other Demonic Kings remained silent, their expressions unreadable. The Boar King’s bluntness was useful for pressing Miao Ling to reveal his true intentions. Though none of them were inclined to join the Myriad Beast Sect, they were curious about the conditions he would offer.

Miao Ling, noting the composed demeanor of the majority, smirked and nodded slightly.

“It seems you’ve been in the Ancient Celestial Kingdom for too long to understand the changes in the Myriad Beast Sect,” he began. “It’s true that our core techniques involve controlling demonic beasts, but what does that have to do with you?”

“Does the Beast Taming Sect not rear demonic beasts and spirit beasts for its disciples to hunt as trials or use as sustenance? And do the human-centric sects not exploit their disciples like livestock?”

“If you join us, you’ll be treated as Elders. To demonstrate our sincerity, we’re willing to swear an Oath to Heaven, guaranteeing your freedom and safety.”

Still, the Demonic Kings were unmoved. While there were ways to ensure the Myriad Beast Sect wouldn’t harm them, this alone was not compelling enough.

“Furthermore,” Miao Ling added, “if you join us, we’ll grant you access to the Myriad Beast Codex, an unparalleled cultivation method. If I’m not mistaken, all of you have relied on your bloodline alone for cultivation, haven’t you?”

“The Myriad Beast Codex, refined by our Sect Master, is a groundbreaking technique that can be cultivated by both humans and demonic beasts. It allows you to learn various secret arts, significantly boosting your power. With it, you might even have a chance to ascend to the level of a Demonic Emperor.”

“Surely you understand how rare such a technique is. Most demonic beasts, no matter how mighty their lineage, lack suitable cultivation methods and can only rely on their bloodlines.”

“Before I came to this Great Wasteland, two of our sect’s Demonic King Elders had already begun cultivating the Myriad Beast Codex. And now, both are on the path to becoming Demonic Emperors.”

At the mention of the cultivation method for demonic beasts, a flicker of intrigue crossed the faces of the Demonic Kings. Now they understood the Myriad Beast Sect’s ace in the hole.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 72: The Mysterious Young Woman
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 72: The Mysterious Young Woman

Previously, the six Demonic King Ancestors of the Beast Taming Sect had little interest in the Myriad Beast Sect. However, upon hearing that the Myriad Beast Codex could be cultivated by demonic beasts, their expressions grew solemn.

Techniques suitable for demonic beasts were extremely rare, let alone cultivation methods. At least within the Ancient Celestial Kingdom, none of them had ever heard of a sect whose core method allowed beasts to cultivate.

The Myriad Beast Codex was known to the Demonic Kings as a technique designed to work in tandem with the Myriad Beast Circle, the signature treasure of the Myriad Beast Sect. Without the codex, controlling powerful and ferocious demonic beasts often led to chaos and rebellion. But when combined with the codex, it was possible to suppress and manipulate even entire armies of demonic beasts.

Moreover, the codex enabled users to employ these armies for extraordinary combat strategies, such as synchronized attacks—or even mass self-detonation.

Another unique aspect of the Myriad Beast Codex was its ability to double the nutritional absorption when consuming beasts as sustenance, enhancing cultivation. Though this property wasn’t particularly remarkable compared to the Beast Taming Art, which provided similar benefits via feedback from pet beasts’ growth, the key difference lay in the fact that the Myriad Beast Codex could be cultivated by the beasts themselves.

This aspect alone would significantly enhance the cultivation and combat abilities of the Demonic Kings. To them, the value of a cultivation method specifically designed for beasts far surpassed any treasure left by an Immortal Demon.

Seeing their silence, the Deputy Sect Master of the Myriad Beast Sect smiled faintly, sensing that he had already achieved part of his goal. Rising slowly, he said, “Please take a few days to consider. After the Holy Son Inauguration Ceremony, you can decide.”

“On the day of the ceremony, I will have disciples of the Myriad Beast Sect challenge your sect’s Holy Son to demonstrate the power of the Myriad Beast Codex. After all, as kings among beasts, you should be cultivating techniques suited for dominion over your kind!”

After some time, Grand Ancestor Wang Batian broke the silence. “You don’t seriously intend to join the Myriad Beast Sect, do you? Their offer sounds good, but when has a pie ever fallen from the sky for free?”

“If it weren’t for the Longevity Body, and if the Myriad Beast Sect were based in the Ancient Celestial Kingdom, I might’ve been tempted,” the Boar King admitted. “But I’ve no interest in heading to Qingzhou.”

“But you’re still itching to get your hands on the codex, aren’t you?” asked the Flood Dragon King.

The Boar King nodded begrudgingly.

“Too bad they wouldn’t be foolish enough to carry an improved version of the codex with them,” the Boar King growled in frustration. “Otherwise, I’d consider just stealing it!”

The other Demonic Kings shot him exasperated looks. Given the Deputy Sect Master’s Nascent Soul Great Perfection cultivation and the unknown number of demonic beasts within his Myriad Beast Circle, even a combined effort wouldn’t guarantee victory.

“Don’t be greedy,” the Stag King advised. “The codex is impressive, but it’s not ours to claim. Focusing on developing the Beast Taming Sect is the true path forward.”

“Exactly,” the Elephant King agreed. “Once the Holy Son’s Longevity Body fully matures, just a bit of his blood will surpass anything that codex offers!”

Indeed, the Myriad Beast Sect had underestimated the Beast Taming Sect’s trump card—a prodigy with a Longevity Body. For demonic beasts, the allure of Longevity Blood was unparalleled.

Time passed swiftly. Lin Jing had now been back in the Great Wasteland for a month, and the Beast Taming Sect’s preparations for the Holy Son Inauguration Ceremony were complete.

On this day, most disciples returned to the Beast Taming Sect’s original mountain gate. While the spiritual veins of the old site had been sealed, the location remained sacred to the sect, making it the perfect venue for such a momentous occasion.

Under the cloudless blue sky, cultivators from sects across the Great Wasteland arrived with invitation letters, flying towards the Beast Taming Sect from all directions. The sheer turnout concealed the fact that the sect had been in decline after its Sect Closure. This was the prestige a rising prodigy brought to a sect.

If the Beast Taming Sect had chosen to inaugurate a Holy Son who wasn’t listed on the Genius Rankings, few sects would have attended, let alone brought generous gifts.

“Sect Master of the Immortal Mist Sect, presenting a bottle of fourth-grade Rising Mist Pills as a gift!”

“Grand Elder of the Wilderness Body Sect, presenting a jade treasure as a gift!”

“Violet Cloud Master from Tianyun City, presenting…”

Elder Yu, smiling warmly, welcomed representatives from various factions and tallied their gifts. The Demonic King Ancestors had indeed shown their cunning, using Lin Jing’s Genius Ranking debut as leverage for this grand occasion, ensuring a bounty of treasures flowed in.

Meanwhile, Lin Jing had emerged from seclusion and returned from the Hundred Flower Valley to the original mountain gate. As he listened to the announcements of the gifts, he silently committed the names of the sects to memory.

His sharp mind, trained from years of study, had memorized the hundreds of cultivation factions within the Great Wasteland. When the time came, it would be easy to identify those who hadn’t sent gifts.

“Congratulations, congratulations…”

“Congratulations to your sect for producing such a peerless genius…”

The Beast Taming Sect buzzed with excitement as cultivators exchanged greetings. Suddenly, a hush fell over the crowd.

“The Deputy Sect Master of the Myriad Beast Sect has arrived, bearing the Myriad Beast Circle as a gift…”

Laughter echoed across the venue as the Deputy Sect Master ascended the mountain with two disciples. His overwhelming cultivation made him the most powerful presence at the event, drawing attention. After all, the appearance of a Qingzhou-based sect here was nothing short of unusual.

Hidden among the crowd, Lin Jing observed the Myriad Beast Sect’s envoy. However, before they even fully revealed themselves, Lin Jing sensed a presence watching him from the shadows. Turning quickly, he spotted a young woman in a white dress.

He was startled. “Who is she?” he wondered. Lin Jing couldn’t recall any faction bringing such a figure.

Locking eyes with her, he spoke slowly, “Who are you? Did you sneak in here without presenting a gift?”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 73: A Dire Omen
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 73: A Dire Omen

"Finally noticed me, have you?"

The mysterious girl in a white dress smiled faintly. As Lin Jing’s gaze landed on her, she leaped lightly to stand before him. A delicate fragrance wafted over, causing Lin Jing to frown. Suddenly, the girl grabbed his wrist.

Smack!

Lin Jing flung her hand off and immediately followed up with a Diamond Fist, but she casually neutralized his attack.

"Who exactly are you, and what is your purpose?"

The term Longevity Body flickered across her lips, making Lin Jing's expression change slightly. How did this woman know about his physique?

"Relax, I’m Bai Yuran, the 27th-generation disciple of the Tianji Pavilion. I mean no harm." She smiled as she clapped her hands. "I just wanted to confirm something. Seems like I was right."

"A Tianji Pavilion disciple?"

Still, it wasn’t completely unexpected. The Tianji Pavilion, known for their insight into all worldly matters, deducing his Longevity Body wasn't too far-fetched.

"What do you want from me?" Lin Jing asked. Tianji Pavilion disciples rarely roamed the cultivation world and were almost never involved in worldly affairs. Why would one suddenly show up at the Holy Son Inauguration Ceremony of the Beast Taming Sect?

Bai Yuran looked at him with an air of melancholy. "You, my dear young man, carry the Longevity Body. Your fate is bound to be turbulent, and in the future, countless beings aiming to become sovereigns will target you."

"What’s your angle?" Lin Jing shook his head. "This has nothing to do with your Tianji Pavilion, does it?"

"The Southern Mountain Demon Emperor, who suddenly appeared in Hidden Dragon City, was likely after you, wasn’t he?" Bai Yuran twirled a finger and smiled. "You were lucky to escape that calamity, but what about next time? Your luck won’t always hold out, will it?"

"What if," she continued, "someone could predict your future, letting you know months or even years in advance when crises will strike? Even with the Longevity Body, that would drastically increase your odds of survival, wouldn’t it?"

"Are you saying that person is you?" Lin Jing asked.

"Exactly!" Bai Yuran puffed out her chest proudly. "As the most outstanding disciple of the 27th generation of Tianji Pavilion, I want to collaborate with you, predicting the crises you’re destined to face. What do you think?"

Lin Jing’s face was unreadable. He thought to himself, When someone offers help for no reason, they’re either a thief or a schemer.

"Speak plainly," he demanded.

"I won’t lie to you," Bai Yuran replied. "Using Tianji Pavilion’s secret techniques to predict the future often comes at the cost of our own lifespan."

"Because of this, we don’t casually perform major divinations. However, since Tianji Pavilion exists in the cultivation world, it’s unavoidable that many powerful figures seek us out for predictions. Thus, within our secret techniques, there’s a little trick."

"If the person being divined cooperates, they can shoulder more than 99% of the lifespan loss for the divination, instead of the user."

"Do you understand now?"

Lin Jing nodded slowly. "So, during a prediction, you risk minor backlash from the heavens and lose some lifespan. But you found a loophole: make the target bear the brunt of the cost instead. Since I have the Longevity Body, losing a bit of lifespan means little to me. And in return, you gain cultivation insights from using the secret technique."

"Exactly!" Bai Yuran smiled. "Compared to the crises you might face, losing a tiny bit of lifespan is a small price to pay, right?"

Lin Jing remained silent, thinking. To her, I’m basically a walking cultivation resource. He sighed internally. Bai Yuran seemed convinced this was a win-win, but Lin Jing couldn’t bring himself to trust her just yet.

"Well, aren’t I just irresistible," he muttered bitterly. Without giving her a direct answer, he said, "Your identity is enough for now. The inauguration is about to begin, and I have a role to play."

"Think it over, little brother. I’ll even give you a free reading—there’s a major calamity in your near future!" Bai Yuran called out as Lin Jing left.

The Beast Taming Sect’s Central Plaza was abuzz. Several powerful demon kings and ancestors hovered above, their monstrous auras filling the air. As the elders summoned Lin Jing, he made his appearance.

"Is that him, Lin Jing?"

The Genius Rankings didn’t include portraits, so this was the first time many cultivators were seeing Lin Jing. He wore snow-white robes, his long black hair tied up with a simple wooden hairpin, swaying gently in the breeze. His unrestrained demeanor made him seem both carefree and strikingly handsome.

"This kid is terrifyingly strong."

Though Lin Jing concealed his cultivation level, the deep, glacial look in his eyes made even seasoned experts feel uneasy. The two disciples of the Myriad Beast Sect paid close attention to him but couldn’t discern much else.

The ceremony officially began. Lin Jing ascended the Hundred Step Cloud Ladder at a measured pace. Following behind him were the elders of the Beast Taming Sect, each carrying their beast-taming artifacts, their expressions solemn.

At the summit of the ladder, Lin Jing stopped. The Second Sect Master’s pet beast, Grand Ancestor Wang Batian, appeared in a humanoid form, holding an ancient tortoiseshell in his hands. His gaze fell on Lin Jing.

This was the sect’s Beast Treasure, a shell Wang Batian had shed in the past. Only when a Holy Son obtained the token of at least one demon king or ancestor would they be recognized as the sect’s true heir. Wang Batian raised the tortoiseshell and announced in a booming voice:

"Today, I formally appoint Lin Jing as the Sixth Holy Son of the Beast Taming Sect. May he carry the torch forward and bring glory to our sect!"

Lin Jing accepted the tortoiseshell. "I, Lin Jing, will live up to the expectations of my predecessors. I shall tame the Dao, protect the sect, and spread the light of our way across the heavens and realms!"

"Ha ha ha, excellent!" Several demon king ancestors laughed heartily.

"Such a grand occasion calls for some entertainment! Our newly appointed Holy Son, would any Great Wasteland geniuses dare challenge him?"

Lin Jing flicked his sleeve and took a seat at a floating cloud table, lifting a teacup.

"Challenges are welcome, but first, you’ll have to get through the core disciples. Not just anyone is worthy of facing me."

As per tradition, the sect’s core disciples would deal with the weaker challengers, preserving Lin Jing’s energy.

Meanwhile, Lin Jing idly toyed with the tortoiseshell Wang Batian had given him.

This thing is useless. I’d have preferred a Mechanical Bamboo Turtle.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 74: The Might of the Holy Son
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 74: The Might of the Holy Son

On the day of the grand ceremony, numerous geniuses from the Great Wasteland arrived, most of them aiming to challenge Lin Jing. Such an event was a golden opportunity to make a name for oneself. Others, knowing their limits, simply came to witness the majesty of a Genius Rankings contender.

Unfortunately for many challengers, they couldn’t even defeat the core disciples of the Beast Taming Sect.

“Ha! Didn’t eat enough? Make sure to grab a good meal later!” Zhang Jia, a Turtle Body Physique core disciple, clad in heavy armor and wielding a shield, didn’t bother dodging the luminous attacks of the senior disciple from the Immortal Mist Sect. He laughed, claiming it only tickled.

"Too slow! Taste my blade!" Another core disciple, a Wind Spirit Physique cultivator, combined with his hawk-shaped beast to transform into a bird-man hybrid. His incredible speed left a body cultivator from the Wilderness Body Sect flustered, unable to catch him while repeatedly struck by sword-like feathers.

...

"Careful, my dear!" A genius from another sect, now entwined by a massive green snake spirit, wore a dazed expression, much to the anger of his elders. His opponent was a core disciple of the Beast Taming Sect, who possessed a Bewitching Physique. Once a courtesan, her unique physique aligned with the traits of a rare beast, and Elder Mo had redeemed her. Now a core disciple, her rise was nothing short of inspirational.

The Beast Taming Sect, already a first-rate power in the Great Wasteland, naturally had exceptional core disciples. Their strength ranked among the top of the younger generation. Furthermore, they had been rigorously trained by the demon king ancestors, making ordinary geniuses no match for them, let alone worthy of challenging Lin Jing.

"Excellent," one of the demon king ancestors remarked, satisfied with the display.

"I’ll go first," said Whitebird, the female disciple of the Myriad Beast Sect.

"Be careful," replied Qingyu, her male companion.

After receiving approval from the deputy sect master, Whitebird took flight. Her agile form resembled a true bird as she landed gracefully on the dueling peak, drawing the attention of many. Even the demon king ancestors of the Beast Taming Sect turned serious, aware that the Myriad Beast Sect had finally entered the fray.

"Whitebird of the Myriad Beast Sect in Qingzhou requests a duel," she announced.

The appearance of the Myriad Beast Sect prompted murmurs among the Great Wasteland sects.

"A Qingzhou sect? Are they here with ill intentions?"

"No, they brought a congratulatory gift. They’re probably not here to cause trouble."

"But aren’t their beast-taming philosophies completely different from the Beast Taming Sect? Why would they be connected?"

Meanwhile, Lin Jing remained unfazed, sipping tea in silence. A burly, bearded young man leaped onto the stage.

"The Myriad Beast Sect? I’ll take this one."

This was Duan Wenchang, a Foundation Establishment Great Perfection cultivator and one of the Beast Taming Sect’s core disciples. Though not blessed with a special physique, he had an innate affinity for beasts, being able to understand their language naturally. This made him a natural fit for the beast-taming path.

He quickly transformed into a lion-man hybrid, wielding a tiger-tooth blade. Using both the Fusion Secret Art and the Beast Weaponization Art, he fully armed himself.

"He’s your opponent," he declared.

"Let’s see if he’s enough," Whitebird replied.

As the battle began, the demon king ancestors and other spectators were astonished when Whitebird unleashed her beast army—a torrential swarm of white seagulls.

One, two... eight... a thousand!

A thousand white seagulls filled the sky, their sheer number creating a hurricane-like force that overwhelmed Duan Wenchang’s blade attacks and sent him flying.

Whitebird, now standing among her bird army, took out a jade flute. As she played, the flock's formation shifted seamlessly.

"A thousand bird demons... including over ten at the Foundation Establishment stage!"

The Great Wasteland cultivators were stunned. They couldn’t fathom how someone could raise so many demon birds.

Even the disciples of the Beast Taming Sect were incredulous. After all, they struggled to raise just one or two pet beasts due to resource constraints.

"To control such a massive bird demon army..." The demon king ancestors took deep breaths, suspecting that this army was funded by the Myriad Beast Sect, not Whitebird herself.

"What now?" Bai Yuran, watching from the clouds, grew anxious. She thought, As a genius of the Ancient Kingdom’s Genius Rankings, Lin Jing can’t lose to Qingzhou cultivators!

Yet Lin Jing remained calm, sipping tea as he observed the swirling seagulls. "Impressive beast army. The wind techniques are integrated well, but unfortunately, it lacks true unity."

Compared to mosquito beasts, it’s far inferior.

Whitebird frowned at his critique. "Holy Son, it’s your turn to fight."

Lin Jing chuckled. "Defeating Senior Brother Duan shows you have some skill. As a guest, let me give you some advice. Competing in beast-taming against me is like showing off a minor craft before a master."

"Don’t pin your hopes on these birds to defeat me."

【Show this Holy Son of the Beast Taming Sect what we’re capable of!】 The deputy sect master of the Myriad Beast Sect sipped his tea, confident in Whitebird’s abilities.

Lin Jing finally descended onto the battlefield. Whitebird raised her hand, commanding the seagulls to unleash a whirlwind.

"Is he bluffing?" she wondered, but then something felt off.

The seagulls trembled as if under an oppressive bloodline suppression.

"What’s happening?"

Whitebird hadn’t realized the severity of the situation. As Lin Jing’s Beast Taming Qi surged, the bird army’s formation collapsed. The seagulls scattered, attacking indiscriminately as if provoked by a primal fear.

"This isn’t good," muttered the deputy sect master, locking onto Lin Jing’s Beast Taming Qi.

"Could mastering the Beast Taming Art at an extreme level grant suppressive effects on beasts?"

The demon king ancestors exchanged glances, their expressions suggesting, If the Myriad Beast Sect’s techniques are only this good, they’re not worthy of our allegiance.

Though Whitebird regained control over the seagulls, Lin Jing smirked. From one hand, he produced a Beast Taming Gourd; from the other, a Myriad Beast Circle.

"So, would you prefer to face the beasts in the gourd or the fiends in the circle?" he asked, his tone mocking yet calm.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 75: Whose Holy Son?
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 75: Whose Holy Son?

Lin Jing held the Beast Taming Artifact of the Beast Taming Sect in his left hand and the Beast Taming Artifact of Myriad Beast Sect in his right. As soon as he took them out, the entire arena fell silent.

"This is..." The three disciples from Myriad Beast Sect were completely stunned.

Though they had indeed gifted a Myriad Beast Circle to the Beast Taming Sect as a congratulatory gift for the Holy Son Inauguration Ceremony, it had only just been given. How could Lin Jing already be using it?

Moreover, the Myriad Beast Sect had given this gift with ulterior motives.

The Beast Taming Sect’s artifact could only store contracted beasts, meaning it had a limit on the number of beasts it could hold. However, its internal environment was exceptional, perfect for nurturing beasts and cultivating various spiritual plants.

On the other hand, the Myriad Beast Sect’s artifact could store an unlimited number of demonic beasts, but only after erasing their intelligence. Its internal environment was rather barren.

These two artifacts represented the fundamentally different philosophies of beast taming between the Beast Taming Sect and the Myriad Beast Sect.

From the Myriad Beast Sect’s perspective, gifting a Myriad Beast Circle was a calculated move. They hoped that the Holy Son of the Beast Taming Sect would be unable to resist its temptation. The moment he started using it, it would mark a significant step toward corrupting his beast-taming philosophy.

This Holy Son of the Beast Taming Sect wasn’t playing by the usual rules at all!

Lin Jing’s move left Bai Niao completely off guard and made the elders of the Beast Taming Sect break into a cold sweat.

"Holy Son, what are you doing… That thing is forbidden!"

Countless cultivators turned their eyes toward the Demon King Ancestors of the Beast Taming Sect.

Was the Beast Taming Sect planning to change its beast-taming philosophy?

Bai Niao’s eyes turned cold. “Without practicing the Myriad Beast Sect’s techniques, you won’t be able to unleash the full power of the Myriad Beast Circle. I advise you not to embarrass yourself.”

“I’ll admit that’s true.” Lin Jing nodded. “But you’re overlooking one thing—so long as the beasts inside the Myriad Beast Circle are strong enough, even if the tamer does nothing, they can still sweep through everything in their path.”

“The Myriad Beast Sect is far from the Cloud Sea Mountains, so you may not have heard of the Ten Terrors of the Cloud Sea.”

“They are ten terrifying races that thrive in the Cloud Sea Mountains, creatures that can never coexist peacefully with other species. Against such monsters, even I must admit—your Myriad Beast Circle is the best tool for the job.”

With that, Lin Jing casually tossed out his Myriad Beast Circle.

In an instant, over a hundred vicious Mosquito Beasts swarmed out of the circle.

The moment these Mosquito Beasts spotted the countless seabirds in the sky, they reacted as if they had seen a feast. Their savage nature was on full display as they charged forth, their bodies shimmering with a blood-red metallic sheen.

"This is..."

The moment the Mosquito Beasts appeared, the Deputy Sect Master of Myriad Beast Sect’s pupils contracted sharply.

Unlike the Ancient Celestial Kingdom, which was located in the heart of the continent, Qingzhou was far from the Cloud Sea Mountains. Two formidable natural barriers, the Misty Forest and the Ancient Celestial Kingdom, separated them.

Thus, Qingzhou cultivators had never truly experienced the terror of the Ten Terrors of the Cloud Sea.

But as soon as the Deputy Sect Master saw this swarm of Mosquito Beasts, he instantly grasped their horrifying nature.

Despite being outnumbered ten to one by Bai Niao’s Bird Demons, the Mosquito Beasts tore through the sky as if it were empty. They streaked through the aerial storm like flashes of blood-red lightning, each pass leaving another bird demon plummeting to the ground, covered in its own blood.

The cultivators from the Great Wasteland gasped sharply.

Although the Genius Rankings had listed Lin Jing as having tamed the Ten Terrors of the Cloud Sea, even the cultivators from the Great Wasteland had been skeptical.

Most assumed he had only tamed one of them.

The Ten Terrors of the Cloud Sea represented ten terrifying species. Taming even one was already incredible. But taming an entire swarm of them? That was an entirely different level!

So now, which one was truly the Beast Taming Circle—the artifact, or Lin Jing himself?

"Why…?"

Seeing the swarm of Mosquito Beasts closing in, Bai Niao’s pupils shrank.

She blew into her jade flute, commanding her birds to self-detonate in an attempt to block the incoming swarm.

But to her horror, the explosions had no effect on the Mosquito Beasts.

Watching as the swarm was about to engulf her, Bai Niao finally abandoned her pride. Her movements slowed, and she turned to look at Myriad Beast Sect’s higher-ups in disbelief.

At last, she understood—this whole battle had been a cruel joke at her expense.

From the very beginning, Lin Jing had never taken her seriously.

"You admit defeat?"

"Is admitting defeat all I can do?"

The Myriad Beast Sect had hoped their beasts could feast on Lin Jing’s swarm.

But Bai Niao had surrendered so quickly that Lin Jing lost interest.

With a single thought, the Mosquito Beasts immediately halted in midair and then, in perfect unison, turned and flew back to him.

At this moment, the Deputy Sect Master of Myriad Beast Sect felt like he had tried to steal a chicken but ended up losing the rice instead.

His own disciple had lost to a Qingzhou cultivator!

"Well… at least this proves that the Holy Son of the Beast Taming Sect isn’t blindly stubborn. Once he experiences the thrill of commanding a vast beast horde, it will be difficult for him to abandon it completely."

"But no matter what, we cannot afford to lose today. Fortunately, we have taught Qingyu how to control a mid-tier Myriad Beast Circle..."

"Qingyu, your turn." The Deputy Sect Master said expressionlessly.

"You want to challenge me too?"

Lin Jing looked at the male disciple who had stepped forward from the Myriad Beast Sect.

"If you’re stronger than her, then come at me."

"Among the Five True Disciples of Myriad Beast Sect, I rank second, while my junior sister ranks fourth. Naturally, I’m stronger than her."

Qingyu glanced at his defeated junior sister before leaping onto the Duel Peak. He turned to Lin Jing and said, "Lin Holy Son, that Myriad Beast Circle treating you well?"

"I’ll admit that the Mosquito Beasts I tamed with it are terrifying, but do you really think this guarantees you victory?"

With that, he summoned his own Myriad Beast Circle.

However, instead of releasing any beasts, his artifact radiated a terrifying suction force.

Lin Jing’s entire Mosquito Beast swarm began to show signs of losing control—being forcibly pulled toward Qingyu’s Myriad Beast Circle!

"This is... a mid-tier artifact!?"

The powerful cultivators narrowed their eyes.

Most lower-level cultivators only had low-tier artifacts, while mid-tier artifacts were typically wielded by Nascent Soul cultivators.

Yet, this Myriad Beast Sect disciple could actually wield a mid-tier artifact!

Under the suppression of Qingyu’s artifact, Lin Jing’s Mosquito Beasts showed signs of slipping from his control.

Realizing this, Lin Jing swiftly recalled them into his Myriad Beast Circle, refusing to let them be stolen.

Seeing this, Qingyu smiled at Lin Jing.

"Whether a beast is unclaimed or contracted, under my Myriad Beast Circle, all can be refined."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 76: I Am the True Path!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 76: I Am the True Path!

“The Holy Son reacts quickly. If you had been slower, your swarm of mosquitoes would already be mine.”

“What’s your next move? Even if you summon your contract beast, it won’t change anything. Under my Myriad Beast Circle , it’ll still be suppressed.”

“If your luck is bad enough, I might even seize your beast entirely.”

Lin Jing summoned the Ten Terrors of the Cloud Sea , crushing the previous disciple of the Myriad Beast Sect effortlessly. The cultivators from the Great Wasteland had thought Lin Jing to be almost invincible—until this next Myriad Beast Sect disciple appeared, wielding such power that even mid-tier magical tools were at their command.

Both sides displayed techniques to counter beasts, but now it seemed the methods of the Myriad Beast Sect disciple were far more terrifying.

Sweat beaded on the foreheads of several Beast Taming Sect disciples as they watched, fearing for Lin Jing. If his contract beast were absorbed and refined by the Myriad Beast Circle , it would be catastrophic.

“Heh.”

It was clear to all that Lin Jing was in a dire situation, but to everyone’s surprise, he still managed to smile—a smile that, no matter how one looked at it, wasn’t the least bit friendly.

“I’ve already said it: even summoning your contract beast is useless,” Qinyu declared, holding the Myriad Beast Circle . “Using the Myriad Beast Codex to activate this mid-tier treasure, I can instantly suppress any beast below the Demon Core Stage. Even Demon Core Stage creatures would struggle to resist.”

“Who said the contents of this gourd are my contract beast?” Lin Jing’s expression remained indifferent. “I’m curious, though. Can your Myriad Beast Circle suppress anything ?”

Faced with his opponent’s mid-tier magical tool, Lin Jing doubted that his Pine Leaf Mouse could handle it directly, so he avoided a head-on clash.

With a single thought, the sky suddenly darkened. As everyone looked up, they saw an unending mass of vines emerge from Lin Jing’s spatial artifact, blotting out the sun!

The countless vines hung overhead like a massive storm cloud. Some among the crowd who recognized the item gasped. Though its color was off, the shape unmistakably resembled the infamous Bloodsucking Vine , a malignant plant.

“Beast Taming of plants?”

Qinyu dismissed the idea that Lin Jing would use a mere Bloodsucking Vine , a low-grade malignant plant, against him. The Myriad Beast Circle couldn’t suppress plants or tree spirits, but relying on something as weak as a Bloodsucking Vine seemed foolish.

However, in the next moment, Qinyu’s pupils constricted. After summoning the Bloodsucking Vine , Lin Jing summoned a mosquito beast—and promptly crushed it with his bare hand!

Boom!

The mosquito beast burst into a spray of blood. The blood drops defied gravity, surging upward and into the vines, which absorbed every drop!

Qinyu shuddered, sensing something ominous.

The elders and demon kings of the Beast Taming Sect twitched at the sight. They had grudgingly accepted Lin Jing’s earlier use of the Myriad Beast Circle to control a fierce mosquito beast, but sacrificing living creatures for power? This was practically as ruthless as the Myriad Beast Sect disciple who used exploding birds in battle earlier.

The Beast Taming Sect’s demon kings exchanged grim looks, understanding full well that Lin Jing had been forced to resort to such measures by this Myriad Beast Sect disciple.

Even Qinyu began to regret his actions. Mosquito beasts were bloodsucking creatures by nature, but to see one reduced to being drained by something else—it was deeply unsettling.

And then, horror materialized. Under the gaze of the shocked crowd, the Bloodsucking Vine began to spawn swarms of blood-red mosquito beasts, their numbers multiplying until they filled the sky.

One mosquito beast had been sacrificed to summon hundreds, if not thousands, of blood mosquitoes.

This was the Blood Mosquito Fusion !

“You’ve got to be kidding me…” Qinyu’s face paled as he activated the Myriad Beast Circle . Yet, just as Lin Jing had said, these blood mosquitoes weren’t living creatures—they were mere constructs of blood and had no consciousness to suppress.

The oppressive blood energy filled the air, shaking the disciples of the Myriad Beast Sect to their cores. Even summoning his own army of beasts seemed futile, for it was clear that nothing could rival the sheer terror of this blood-red swarm.

As desperation crept in, Qinyu glanced toward the Deputy Sect Master for help.

“Who dares to use demonic arts here?!”

Before Qinyu could act, a voice rang out, interrupting the duel. It was like heavenly music to Qinyu’s ears.

Several figures descended onto the peak, clad in black robes emblazoned with the insignia of the Demon-Slaying Bureau . These cultivators, tasked with overseeing the Demon-Suppressing Pagoda after the Beast Taming Sect’s closure, had been invited to the inauguration ceremony. Although they hadn’t initially attended due to their duties, the overwhelming blood energy had drawn them here.

However, what they found wasn’t a demonic cultivator but rather the Beast Taming Sect’s Holy Son…

Blood mosquitoes filled the sky, and even the elders of the Beast Taming Sect looked nervous. The demon kings grimaced, for even they couldn’t vouch for Lin Jing’s alignment after this display.

“Take him away! Take him away!” The Myriad Beast Sect disciple prayed fervently that the Demon-Slaying Bureau would remove Lin Jing from the battlefield. This way, he wouldn’t have to continue the fight.
