Fortunately for Lin Jing, he had prepared for this scenario. With a calm glance at the black-robed cultivators, he produced a token.

Gao Sheng had assured him that with this token, even wielding an Ice Soul Gu would not compromise his identity.

“We’re on the same side.”

Upon seeing the token, the Demon-Slaying Bureau cultivators froze. Their expressions shifted, and they immediately cupped their fists. “Apologies, my lord. We shall take our leave.”

Qinyu: ???

The token bore the insignia of the prestigious Gao Clan, one of the four great demon-slaying families. Even the Demon-Slaying Bureau couldn’t question Lin Jing’s actions.

“See? Now you’re out of options,” Lin Jing said, turning back to Qinyu with a smile.

“Bluffing!” Qinyu retorted, his face pale. He refused to believe that a single mosquito beast could multiply into a swarm of this scale while maintaining its power.

Desperate, Qinyu summoned his beast army, commanding them to attack Lin Jing.

Qinyu’s judgment wasn’t entirely wrong. Lin Jing’s experiments had shown that the blood mosquito swarm wasn’t as powerful as an equal number of living mosquito beasts. However, their sheer numbers and bloodline connection made them terrifying nonetheless.

And this was only the beginning.

Though Lin Jing had a semi-finished blood energy formation —intended to power a massive mechanized creation—he had yet to deploy it. If he had, the blood mosquitoes would have been used as energy to drive an even more terrifying contraption.

Watching Qinyu’s beast army fall before the endless blood mosquitoes, the Deputy Sect Master of the Myriad Beast Sect glared at his disciple in frustration.

“Fool!”

If the disciple had realized that both the vines and blood mosquitoes were being controlled by the Pine Leaf Mouse hidden in Lin Jing’s sleeve, the situation might have been salvaged. But for a lower-stage cultivator, grasping such critical details in the heat of battle was nearly impossible.

With this victory, Lin Jing not only shattered the disciple’s morale but also ensured that the Myriad Beast Sect would think twice before targeting him again.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 77: A Divination Delivered
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 77: A Divination Delivered

After defeating two disciples of the Myriad Beast Sect, Lin Jing returned to his seat at the table, earning loud applause from the Beast Taming Sect’s elders and disciples alike.

The elders eagerly touted the victory to outsiders, proclaiming, “The Myriad Beast Sect is a beast-taming sect from Qingzhou with a Nascent Soul cultivator overseeing it. However, it’s clear our Holy Son surpasses them all.”

Despite the boastful words, cultivators from other sects in the Great Wasteland could only offer polite smiles. Lin Jing’s combat style was so unconventional for a Holy Son of the Beast Taming Sect that it seemed more in line with the Myriad Beast Sect’s approach. A few outspoken individuals even voiced this thought but were quickly rebuked by Beast Taming Sect disciples.

“That just shows how profound our Holy Son’s mastery of the Beast Taming Dao is! He can easily defeat his opponent in their area of expertise. We can only wonder if anyone today will be able to force him to reveal the true secret techniques of our sect.”

The elders had talked big, but the truth was that Lin Jing had yet to reach the Foundation Establishment stage. Without a breakthrough in his cultivation, he didn’t qualify to learn the Beast Taming Sect’s three core secret arts. No matter how much they boasted, there was no way for those techniques to emerge today.

With the display of the blood mosquito technique, fewer challengers dared to step forward. No one liked the thought of being drained by a swarm of mosquitoes, so most contestants took the stage only briefly, sparred lightly with Lin Jing, and quickly stepped down.

“This guy…”

Among the crowd, Bai Yuran, a disciple of Tianji Pavilion, observed Lin Jing, who was sipping coconut juice while munching on a spiritual fruit. When Lin Jing furrowed his brow and set the fruit down, an intense aura of oppression suddenly emanated from him.

“To show my sincerity, I’ll use a treasure to divine his fate in greater detail,” Bai Yuran decided.

Many shared Bai Yuran’s sentiment. It wasn’t just Lin Jing’s strength that intrigued them—it was his ability to perform techniques resembling blood-path demonic arts under the nose of the Demon-Slaying Bureau without repercussion. This signaled that someone highly influential within the bureau favored Lin Jing.

This unspoken endorsement carried more weight than Lin Jing’s place on the Genius Rankings, revealing a political reality that many sects couldn’t ignore.

The Hundred Flower Valley, a key ally of the Beast Taming Sect, had also sent important figures to the ceremony. The valley’s master, her face veiled like an elegant lily, sat gracefully at the event. Beside her, the Great Elder of the valley hesitated before making a decision and spoke through a sound transmission.

“Valley Master, there’s something I haven’t yet shared. This Holy Son of the Beast Taming Sect once expressed interest in our Hundred Flower Immortal Honey .”

The Valley Master’s gaze sharpened.

“This is no fabrication,” the Great Elder continued. “But reflecting on it today, I believe the Holy Son may simply want to savor the honey for its flavor rather than for any ulterior motives.” She gestured toward Lin Jing.

From the start of the ceremony, Lin Jing had been sampling all kinds of delicacies. Each time he tasted something, he would frown as if dissatisfied.

Seeing Lin Jing’s apparent pickiness, the Great Elder began to believe that he genuinely wanted the honey for its taste. After all, during the marriage of the previous Beast Taming Sect Master and the former Hundred Flower Valley Master, a single drop of Immortal Honey added to spiritual spring water had filled the Beast Taming Sect with its fragrance. Lin Jing must have read about such events and become curious.

However, the truth was that the Hundred Flower Immortal Honey was not just a rare delicacy. It was essential for practicing certain secret techniques of the Hundred Flower Valley, some of which could only be cultivated after consuming it—such as their dual-cultivation arts.

This was why only the valley master’s Dao companion, the Beast Taming Sect’s former master, had ever shared the honey.

“What do you suggest?” the Valley Master asked.

“The honey yield this year is excellent,” the Great Elder replied. “If you’re willing to gift a portion to the Holy Son, it would undoubtedly strengthen our alliance with the Beast Taming Sect. His talent and potential are undeniable...”

The Valley Master fell silent for a moment before responding.

“Using the Immortal Honey for his mere appetite would indeed be wasteful. Let’s present him with an entry-level secret technique that requires it. If he’s serious about cultivation, he will accept both together.”

The Great Elder hesitated but nodded. “Understood.”

“Hey, Holy Son! Holy Son!”

Lin Jing sighed, putting down the delicacies he had been sampling. Turning to the Tianji Pavilion disciple seated on a distant peak, he asked, “You’re still hesitating?”

“No, I’ve decided to give you a divination!” Bai Yuran declared.

“If you’re just going to tell me that I’ll face some vague disaster in the near future, don’t bother,” Lin Jing replied.

“This is no ordinary reading,” Bai Yuran said. “Our disciples of Tianji Pavilion possess a unique life-bound treasure that can shield us from backlash during a divination. Though it damages the treasure, I’m willing to use it to uncover the truth about your upcoming calamity.”

Lin Jing raised a hand, inviting Bai Yuran over with a wave of spiritual energy. Handing him a spirit fruit, he said, “I appreciate your effort. I can tell you’re a good person.”

Rolling his eyes, Bai Yuran took the fruit and sat beside Lin Jing, producing a mirror.

“Here?” Lin Jing asked.

“Here.” Bai Yuran nodded and closed his eyes, beginning the divination.

Time passed, and Bai Yuran suddenly opened his eyes, staring at the mirror in his hand.

Within the mirror, a black coffin’s shadow appeared, floating in an eerie void. Inside the coffin was a miniature figure of a black-robed elder, controlling the coffin as it chased something unseen.

Crack.

A fissure formed on the mirror. Bai Yuran’s face paled as he whispered, “Soon, you’ll encounter this person. Their cultivation is vast, at the Void Mystical Stage . However, their body has been destroyed, and they’re seeking to possess yours!”

“A coffin as a manifestation of the Void Mystical Stage… This must be a cultivator of the Yin Corpse Sect! Fortunately, their state is poor, and they didn’t detect my divination!”

“Possess me?”

“Are they out of their mind?” Lin Jing muttered, momentarily stunned. It was baffling. Targeting someone with a Longevity Body for possession was undoubtedly a terrible choice.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 78: Rushing to Rebirth
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 78: Rushing to Rebirth

Soul Seizing.

Cultivators who reach the Nascent Soul Stage possess the ability to seize another’s body. However, to protect the prodigies of the lower realms, the Celestial Palace in the Immortal Realm imposes strict regulations on this practice. Soul seizing is fraught with risks and constrained by the world’s laws, making it highly prone to failure. A failed attempt often results in the Nascent Soul dissipating entirely.

Under extreme circumstances, Lin Jing could understand why some cultivators would resort to soul seizing. After all, anyone desperate enough to consider such an act was likely at death’s door, with nothing left to lose.

But what baffled Lin Jing was the target: why on earth would someone try to seize his Longevity Body?

The Longevity Body cultivated at a painfully slow pace and, though prized by demons and monsters for its essence, offered little practical advantage. Even in the rare case of a successful soul seizure, the aftermath would be far worse than selecting a different special physique. If the Longevity Body were truly such a blessing, history would have seen cultivators with it reach the Nascent Soul Transformation Stage—but none ever had.

“Are you sure about this reading? You look younger than I am,” Lin Jing remarked skeptically.

“Don’t underestimate me. Despite my age, I’ve passed my master’s rigorous tests and earned the right to walk the cultivation world as a Tianji Pavilion disciple,” Bai Yuran replied confidently.

“Strange. If this really is a Void Mystical cultivator from the Yin Corpse Sect, they shouldn’t lack suitable bodies. Why would they choose such a poor strategy?” Lin Jing frowned, pondering the situation as he silently called out to Senior Pine.

After a brief silence, Senior Pine’s voice echoed in Lin Jing’s mind. “Void Mystical Stage? And they’ve fallen to the point where they must seize a new body to survive, with no other options?”

“Seems that way,” Lin Jing replied.

“Trash,” Senior Pine scoffed.

“So… does this mean you have a way to deal with them?” Lin Jing asked, his spirits lifting.

“No, I just wanted to insult them. With cultivation that high, how could they end up in such a sorry state? I could’ve done better with that power,” Senior Pine muttered.

Lin Jing: “???”

“Are you planning to return to the Hidden Dragon Academy?” Bai Yuran asked, breaking Lin Jing’s reverie.

She believed the academy was the safest place for him now. Returning there might help him avoid the looming danger, whereas wandering the outside world would only invite more trouble. The ostentatious inauguration ceremony had already drawn too much attention.

“Don’t listen to the little girl!” Senior Pine interjected eagerly. “If it were a fully-powered Void Mystical cultivator, even ten of me wouldn’t dare face them. But someone so desperate they need to seize a body to survive? Hah! Hiding would be a waste!”

“Think about it. Void Mystical Stage cultivators are treasure troves! If we could pull off another ambush like the one with the Southern Mountain Demon Emperor, imagine the spoils we’d collect!”

Lin Jing blinked in surprise. “Senior, how confident are you?”

“You won’t be in danger. I’ll set up a formation leveraging natural terrain. Once they fall into it, there’ll be no escape for their spatial primordial spirit!”

“Besides,” Senior Pine continued, “aren’t you curious why this Yin Corpse Sect cultivator skipped over countless other bodies and targeted you? Or what turmoil within the Yin Corpse Sect caused someone of such high rank to end up in this pitiful state?”

Lin Jing hesitated but ultimately chose to trust Senior Pine, his guardian.

“I’m not going back to the Hidden Dragon Academy,” Lin Jing said, turning to Bai Yuran. “Even a clay figurine has its limits. First, they sold information about my physique to the Southern Mountain Demon Emperor. Now their cultivators want to seize my body. I’ve had enough!”

Bai Yuran: “???”

“You’re just a Qi Refinement cultivator! What can you even do?” she exclaimed.

Lin Jing didn’t reply, only hoping that Senior Pine’s plan would work. Deep down, he wished for greater strength so he could one day obliterate the Yin Corpse Sect himself.

Half a day later, the Holy Son Inauguration Ceremony concluded. Not wanting to entangle the Beast Taming Sect further in his affairs, Lin Jing announced his plans to travel to the Air Domain and challenge a renowned ruin, leaving behind a clear trail of his whereabouts.

Although he was eager to leave the Great Wasteland immediately, Lin Jing couldn’t resist selecting a few gifts from the ceremony’s offerings. Among them was the Myriad Beast Circle from the Myriad Beast Sect—a valuable treasure that Lin Jing insisted on keeping to prevent its misuse or the propagation of more mosquito beasts.

The Beast Taming Sect’s leaders, aware of the dangers associated with the Longevity Body, fully understood Lin Jing’s decision to leave. However, to other sects, it was unusual for a newly inaugurated Holy Son to leave his sect so soon. Traditionally, this was a time to build alliances and establish connections with other sects.

“Holy Son!”

The Great Elder of the Hundred Flower Valley, worried that Lin Jing might bypass their valley altogether, personally sought him out.

“Senior?” Lin Jing asked.

“Ah, the Holy Son still remembers me.” The Great Elder chuckled. “Previously, you expressed interest in our Hundred Flower Immortal Honey . After consulting with the Valley Master, we decided it would be inappropriate to present it as a mere ceremonial gift. Instead, we’ve chosen a more suitable occasion to gift it to you.”

“Additionally, the Hundred Flower Valley will also grant you a matching secret technique!”

Though Lin Jing’s desire to satisfy his palate had waned in the face of impending danger, the mention of a secret technique piqued his interest.

“There are four secret techniques in our valley that require the Immortal Honey for cultivation,” the Great Elder explained. “Usually, only the Valley Master or their successors are eligible to learn them, as the honey’s low yield limits its use. However, the valley is willing to grant one of these techniques to the Holy Son.”

“Please elaborate,” Lin Jing requested.

“The first is the Art of Shedding , a feigned death technique that conceals one’s vital energy. When perfected, it can even hide you from tribulations. However, the technique is incomplete, as our inheritance of it was an accidental find.”

“The second is the Art of Ascension , a flight technique far superior in speed to normal methods. When performed by a Nascent Soul cultivator, even most Nascent Soul Transformation experts would struggle to catch up without special means.”

“The last is the Art of the Heavenly Cocoon , a healing technique that creates a cocoon-like structure using condensed spiritual threads. It allows the user to heal from any injury, no matter how severe—even damage to one’s core or soul. With this, a Golden Core cultivator could repeatedly use core-burning techniques without fear of their core shattering.”

Lin Jing’s eyes lit up as he asked, “Senior, the Art of Shedding can conceal one’s vital energy?”

This technique seemed tailor-made for someone with the Longevity Body. While Lin Jing had methods to hide his physique, his boundless vitality would become increasingly difficult to conceal as his cultivation grew. This technique could be a perfect solution.

“Yes, indeed. If you choose it, our Valley Master will personally instruct you,” the Great Elder offered.

“That won’t be necessary, Senior. I’ll accompany you to retrieve it. Afterward, I’ll be leaving the Great Wasteland,” Lin Jing replied.

The Great Elder was stunned. “Holy Son, are you in such a rush to… reincarnate? A Nascent Soul cultivator offering personal guidance, and you refuse?!”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 79: The World Beneath the Frozen Pool
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 79: The World Beneath the Frozen Pool

The Air Domain, once a principal battleground during the Celestial Palace and Celestial Alliance era, is littered with the remains of countless Void Mystical cultivators. Ruins and secret realms abound in this region, and many unknown cultivators have risen to prominence after gaining fortuitous encounters here. Most could trace their meteoric rise back to treasures found in the Air Domain.

Bordering the Snow Plains like the Great Wasteland, the Air Domain is often a sanctuary for rogue Snow Plains cultivators seeking resources. Unlike the more regulated Great Wasteland, the Air Domain is not just a treasure trove for the Ancient Celestial Kingdom but also a haven for outsiders.

After retrieving the Hundred Flower Immortal Honey and the Art of Shedding from the Hundred Flower Valley, Lin Jing, accompanied by Senior Pine, set off for the Air Domain in search of an extreme cold region suitable for using the Ice Soul Gu to establish his Foundation.

Back in the Great Wasteland, two individuals were particularly upset after Lin Jing’s departure: the Deputy Sect Master of the Myriad Beast Sect and Bai Yuran of Tianji Pavilion.

“You useless fools…”

Outside the Beast Taming Sect’s mountain gate, the Deputy Sect Master of the Myriad Beast Sect glared at his two disciples, barely restraining the urge to slap them. However, he also knew it wasn’t entirely their fault. The Beast Taming Sect’s Holy Son was simply too powerful.

“Please, Deputy Sect Master, punish us!” the two disciples said, their faces pale with shame.

“Punish you?” The Deputy Sect Master gave them a cold look. “After the inauguration ceremony, the demon kings of the Beast Taming Sect no longer take our sect seriously. If punishing you would restore our reputation among the Ancient Celestial Kingdom’s cultivators, your deaths would be worth it. But…”

“The Ten Terrors of the Cloud Sea … If we can bring some of those beasts back to Qingzhou, it would significantly bolster our sect’s foundations. You two will accompany me to the Cloud Sea Mountains to capture some mosquito beasts and atone for your failure.”

The disciples’ eyes lit up. Having experienced the terror of the mosquito beast firsthand, they knew its potential. Lin Jing’s control methods had been crude at best, yet even so, the mosquito beast had overwhelmed them. If they could build a mosquito beast army and combine it with their sect’s techniques, their battle strength would increase dramatically.

“Yes, Deputy Sect Master!”

Meanwhile, Bai Yuran, after some hesitation, decided to head for the Air Domain. Lin Jing wasn’t foolish; knowing a Void Mystical cultivator might target him and still leaving a trail meant he had some confidence. However, before she could follow, someone blocked her path.

“Senior Brother Tian Huihua…”

Bai Yuran froze in shock as the man appeared before her.

“Enough games. Time to return to Tianji Pavilion,” Tian Huihua said calmly.

“W-when did you find me?” Bai Yuran stammered.

“I’ve been following you since you left for the Great Wasteland.” Tian Huihua stared at her impassively.

Bai Yuran took a step back, forcing a smile. “I just gave Lin Jing a divination. I wanted to…”

“No,” Tian Huihua interrupted, shaking his head. “Discovering the Beast Taming Sect’s Holy Son’s secret was your chance encounter, and our master allowed you to pursue it. But now that your divination has tied this matter to the Yin Corpse Sect, you cannot involve yourself further.”

“Why not…?”

“This escapade has earned you some credit,” Tian Huihua replied. “The Yin Corpse Sect possesses the corpse of a celestial beast. They’ve long sought to refine it into a puppet. However, their efforts have been repeatedly thwarted by the Ancient Celestial Kingdom’s interference. One of their ancestors even lost his body in the process.”

“Is he the one targeting Lin Jing?” Bai Yuran asked, startled.

“Most likely,” Tian Huihua replied.

“Why would he want to seize Lin Jing’s body? That seems… unusual.”

“Our master suspects the Yin Corpse Sect lost a crucial resource needed for refining the celestial corpse. Without it, they can’t continue their project. Your divination suggests they may be seeking a substitute.”

Tian Huihua explained, “Refining and controlling a celestial corpse requires not only immense death energy but also a delicate balance with life energy. By targeting Lin Jing’s Longevity Body, they aim to integrate its vitality into the process, turning their failure into an opportunity. However, this is only speculation.”

“The Demon-Slaying Bureau has been informed and should already have experts protecting Lin Jing. His choice to head for the Air Domain rather than return to the academy aligns with their plans. Letting the Yin Corpse Sect ancestor think there’s hope for possession is the best way to draw him out. The bureau likely wants to capture him alive.”

“Moreover, Lin Jing appears to have a powerful protector by his side. You needn’t worry about him.”

Though Bai Yuran nodded, her curiosity remained piqued.

“Is this the place, Senior?”

Days later, in the Air Domain, Lin Jing stood atop a snowy peak, gazing at the pristine white expanse below. Beneath the snowline, hardy plants like snow lotuses dotted the landscape. Despite the chill, the area seemed no different from other snowy mountains.

“It’s luck that brought us here,” Senior Pine said after surveying the mountain. “For thousands of years, this place seems to have gone unnoticed. For cultivators practicing ice techniques, this would be an ideal spot for a sect, a family, or a cave abode.”

“Most extreme cold regions are already occupied. Finding a wild one like this is rare. Don’t be fooled by appearances, though. Beneath this mountain lies a frozen pool, and that pool connects to a cavern—a true extreme cold region!”

“Long ago, an Iceheart Snow Lotus grew here, gaining sentience from the cold energy.”

Lin Jing was intrigued. “Senior, are you saying that Iceheart Snow Lotus is…?”

“Dead,” Senior Pine replied curtly. “And before you ask, it wasn’t me. A group of cultivators refined it into avatars to reach the Nascent Soul Transformation Stage. I happened to witness it and avenged the lotus.”

“What happened to its remains?”

“Don’t ask.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 80: The Awakening of the Ice Soul Gu
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 80: The Awakening of the Ice Soul Gu

In an instant, Lin Jing reached the frozen pool that the Divine Pine had mentioned.

The pool's surface was calm and mirror-like, with a thin layer of frosty mist rising gently from its depths. This mist hovered over the water, adding an ethereal charm to the scene.

Holding the Flame Dragon Carp, Lin Jing plunged into the icy pool. The deeper he went, the colder it became. However, the dropping temperature didn’t bother him. Not only did the warmth of the Flame Dragon Carp shield him, but his Ice Soul Physique also gave him exceptional resistance to the cold.

Bang—

Time seemed to blur. At some point, Lin Jing felt as though he had fallen from a heavenly pool. Water splashed violently as he landed heavily on the ground. The surrounding temperature underwent a drastic shift, growing so cold that even his Ice Soul Physique began to feel the chill.

Looking up quickly, Lin Jing saw that the pool's bottom had turned into a celestial pool above him. He now stood in a massive icy cavern. The walls were piled with frosty stones, and the biting cold seemed to pierce his very soul. As he ventured further, he felt the Ice Soul Gu, which was sealed by the Divine Tree, begin to stir faintly.

“Blub!!!”

The Flame Dragon Carp, which had been indifferent to the cold until now, began to shiver. Lin Jing promptly tossed it back into the frigid pool above, saying, “Stay in the water and train until you’re no longer afraid of the cold.”

The Flame Dragon Carp shot Lin Jing a look that seemed to accuse him of ignoring the attributes that came with its lineage.

With the carp taken care of, Lin Jing exhaled a puff of frosty breath and surveyed the area. “Anyone below the Golden Core stage and without a special physique would freeze to death here.”

“Freezing to death would be the least of their worries. Even higher-level cultivators could suffer permanent damage if they lingered too long. Now, hurry and break the Ice Soul Gu’s seal. Let it absorb this extreme cold!”

A pinecone suddenly materialized and urged him on. Lin Jing nodded. “I’ll count on you to protect me, Senior. As for the formation you mentioned for dealing with the Yin Corpse Sect cultivators…”

“Don’t worry. If anyone from that sect finds their way here, I’ll ensure they don’t leave alive,” the Divine Pine replied confidently.

Reassured, Lin Jing closed his eyes and entered the sealed world in his mind. There, he saw the Ice Soul Gu tightly bound by the branches of a massive tree.

The Ice Soul Gu was a giant spider made entirely of ice and snow. As Lin Jing gazed at it, he couldn’t help but sigh.

“You’ve been sealed for so long… Though, to be fair, the Dragon-Phoenix Cavern Heaven hasn’t treated you poorly. You even broke through to the eleventh stage of Qi Refinement.”

“Let’s hope that when you reach Foundation Establishment, you’ll help me ascend to the twelfth stage. Thirteenth stage would be ideal!”

Crack.

With Lin Jing’s actions, the branches binding the Ice Soul Gu began to loosen. Its full form gradually emerged, and an influx of cold air surged from the outside world into Lin Jing’s body, which the Ice Soul Gu passively absorbed.

At the same time, the Ice Soul Gu emitted a steady stream of cold air. The two types of chill were distinctly different: one was the pure, natural cold of heaven and earth, while the other was a toxic cold converted by the Ice Soul Gu.

The latter was far more damaging to Lin Jing’s body. Fortunately, a golden Buddhist bead within him absorbed most of the sinister energy.

As the Ice Soul Gu freely absorbed the extreme chill, its cultivation was no longer restricted. Reaching Foundation Establishment was now only a matter of time. Meanwhile, Lin Jing decided not to remain idle. He retrieved a jar of Hundred Flower Immortal Honey gifted by the Hundred Flower Valley and a one-time-use jade slip of a secret art. Seizing this opportunity, he began practicing the Veil of Dissolution, aiming to further reduce his presence.

“Smells divine.”

The golden floral scent of the Hundred Flower Immortal Honey, one of the top ten spiritual treasures of the Little Wasteland, wafted through the air as Lin Jing opened the jar. Even the Ice Soul Gu, a creature that had long been indifferent to earthly delights, couldn’t help but twitch in response.

“That smells divine.”

A voice rang out, startling Lin Jing. It wasn’t the Divine Pine, nor was it the Pine Leaf Mouse or the Flame Dragon Carp. His expression darkened as he probed his surroundings cautiously.

Outside his body, in the snow-laden expanse, the Ice Soul Gu had awoken. The enormous, crystalline spider had shed its slumber.

Lin Jing was momentarily shocked. Wasn’t it supposed to remain dormant until after Foundation Establishment? Why had it awakened immediately after the seal was broken?

“Container, you smell divine,” the Ice Soul Gu said again.

“Turns out it was talking about you,” Lin Jing muttered, a vein throbbing on his forehead.

The Ice Soul Gu’s sudden awakening was completely unexpected. Furthermore, Lin Jing remembered that the top ten Rare Gu usually lost their memories upon reincarnation. This meant the Ice Soul Gu should be like a newborn. Yet, it already knew that Lin Jing was its host and was capable of telepathic communication. Its intelligence far surpassed that of the Pine Leaf Mouse or the Flame Dragon Carp.

“Let me have a bite,” the Ice Soul Gu said, beginning to crawl inside Lin Jing’s body. Breaking through its icy confines, it attempted to latch onto him like a bloodsucking beast. Alarmed, Lin Jing immediately restrained it with the power of their contract.

The Ice Soul Gu froze in place, perplexed. “Why are you stopping me?”

“You’re trying to eat my flesh and blood. Do you even need to ask?”

“But you’re using my power without offering anything in return. Isn’t that unfair?”

Lin Jing was stunned. Was this Ice Soul Gu… trying to reason with him?

“No means no. Stop eyeing my body.”

“Fine, then release this strange restraint. I’ll leave.”

Lin Jing remained silent.

“I said I’d leave, but you still refuse?”

“If you want me to work for you, we should cooperate fairly. Otherwise, you’re just exploiting me,” the Ice Soul Gu argued.

Lin Jing felt a chill. “You just awakened, didn’t you? This doesn’t sound like something a baby spider would say…”

If the Ice Soul Gu was this intelligent from birth, then the legendary bloodlines of the top ten Rare Gu were indeed extraordinary.

In comparison, the Flame Dragon Carp, with its Qilin lineage, seemed downright foolish at first.

“I don’t want to argue, but I sense strange restrictions on me. You must have tampered with me while I was asleep. I can’t attack you directly, so I have no choice but to talk things out,” the Ice Soul Gu admitted.

“If you don’t respond, I’ll just end myself. I can reincarnate, but without me, you’ll lose your chance to grow stronger.”

“Go ahead.” Lin Jing picked his ear nonchalantly.

The Ice Soul Gu fell silent. Lin Jing chuckled and added, “Don’t try to scare adults with childish threats. While I won’t let you feed on my flesh, I can offer you something else.”

The Ice Soul Gu’s mood seemed to lift. “I knew that fish in the pool was meant for me. I misjudged you. That container of mine really smells divine.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 81: Reasoning
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 81: Reasoning

“Container, your strength is still too weak. Directly absorbing your nutrients would hinder your cultivation and ultimately affect me as well,” the Ice Soul Gu stated with calm logic. “It’s better to consume external resources, like the extreme cold here or that fish, rather than depleting you directly.”

Lin Jing didn’t even need to analyze the situation; the Ice Soul Gu had already outlined the pros and cons with flawless reasoning.

“You make a good point, but that fish is your companion, not your food,” Lin Jing replied, his expression dark.

“What I intend to feed you is this.” Lin Jing retrieved the Myriad Beast Circle and pulled out a Mosquito Beast that had absorbed Longevity Blood.

The Ice Soul Gu sensed the Mosquito Beast and fell silent for a moment, seemingly unimpressed.

“I prefer food with ice attributes. This mosquito doesn’t suit my tastes,” it finally said.

Lin Jing clenched his fist. “The Flame Dragon Carp is fire-attributed. Why did you find it appealing?”

“Eating too much cold food isn’t good for my system. Occasionally, I crave something warm. My organs told me so,” the Ice Soul Gu muttered.

Reasonable, my *ss!

Annoyed, he threw the Mosquito Beast into the cold pool. The Flame Dragon Carp immediately swam over and swallowed it. Unlike the Ice Soul Gu, it could refine the Mosquito Beast’s metallic essence with its Flame Refinement ability, facing no digestion issues.

“You should learn from it—at least it’s not picky about food.”

The Ice Soul Gu scoffed disdainfully, clearly unimpressed by the Flame Dragon Carp.

Sensing the gu’s emotions, Lin Jing suddenly smirked. “It looks like you’re underestimating it. How about a little wager?”

“A wager?” the Ice Soul Gu tilted its head in curiosity.

“This Flame Dragon Carp is your key partner. It can assist you in growing stronger. Let’s settle this: the two of you will fight. If it wins, you’ll acknowledge its strength and stop treating it with contempt. You’ll also obediently train and provide it with your support.”

“And if it loses?” the Ice Soul Gu asked.

“If it loses, it’ll stop interfering and follow your lead. All decisions will be yours to make,” Lin Jing proposed.

The Flame Dragon Carp froze, stunned by this sudden challenge. Fight the Ice Soul Gu? Was this a joke?

The carp was confident it could crush Lin Jing in an instant, let alone the spider. “It’s doomed to lose,” it declared smugly.

“Let’s begin, then,” the Ice Soul Gu said, releasing the Flame Dragon Carp.

In the next moment, the cold cavern was filled with a terrifying chill as the snow-white spider fully emerged. Its icy blue eyes locked onto the Flame Dragon Carp in the pool below, where the fish appeared dumbfounded by its rival's overwhelming aura.

Boom!

The Ice Soul Gu unleashed its strength. Its eleventh-stage Qi Refinement cultivation, combined with the bloodline of a Gu King, released a frigid wave that even Lin Jing found life-threatening.

The Flame Dragon Carp trembled under the Ice Soul Gu’s predatory gaze.

“It woke up, and it thinks it can replace you,” Lin Jing calmly explained to the carp, which began to shake with fury.

Without waiting for further provocation, the Ice Soul Gu pointed a sharp, crystalline leg at the carp, releasing a torrent of bone-chilling cold that surged toward it. Though the carp was at the tenth stage of Qi Refinement, its bloodline and cultivation couldn’t withstand the Ice Soul Gu’s superior power.

But just as the spider felt certain of its victory, it noticed something strange about the carp’s trembling—was it fear, or… rage?

“ Suck it!!! ”

The Flame Dragon Carp roared, producing several jade bottles seemingly out of nowhere.

Grade-Three Pills!

The carp devoured them one by one:

Despite its low cultivation and bloodline, the carp’s resourcefulness turned the tide. As it swallowed the pills, its aura skyrocketed. Fiery dragon-shaped flames coiled around its body, elevating it to the peak of Foundation Establishment for a brief period.

One pill to swallow, and the heavens bow to my will!

With a ferocious glare, the Flame Dragon Carp erupted with fiery might. Under the Ice Soul Gu’s stunned gaze, it transformed into a blazing dragon, its searing flames vaporizing the frigid cold in an instant.

Moments later, the carp emerged victorious, leaving the Ice Soul Gu crippled and sprawled at Lin Jing’s feet. “Weakling,” the carp snorted before retreating to a quiet corner to endure the side effects of its pills.

Lin Jing shook his head at the battered spider. “You might be powerful, but you’ve learned an important lesson today. The cultivation world is merciless. Even a seemingly foolish and weak Flame Dragon Carp can pull out unexpected tricks and crush you.”

“From now on, listen to me. I’ll ensure you avoid unnecessary suffering.”

The Ice Soul Gu didn’t argue. Though its wounds healed rapidly in the icy cavern, its pride had been thoroughly shattered.

“You still look unconvinced,” Lin Jing observed. “Fine, I’ll give you one more chance.”

He gestured to the Suspended Gourd hanging at his side. “Inside this gourd is another companion of yours. It’s excellent at cultivating spiritual plants. If you can defeat it, I’ll let it grow Ice Spirit Plants here to provide you with food. The other terms remain the same. What do you think?”

The Ice Soul Gu raised its head, surprised by Lin Jing’s generosity. “You’re actually giving me another chance?”

“I won’t lose this time,” it said confidently.

Without hesitation, Lin Jing sent it into the Suspended Gourd.

It never came out again.

Later, while Lin Jing savored a few bites of Hundred Flower Immortal Honey and practiced the Art of Shedding, he glanced at the Beast Taming Space and discovered the Ice Soul Gu bound once again. The Pine Leaf Mouse had used wooden strips to restrain it and was directing it to create icy storage boxes for its hoard of spiritual fruits.

“Was it worth it?” Lin Jing sighed, shaking his head.

Seeing that the Ice Soul Gu hadn’t triggered any unique divine abilities through his Longevity Body, Lin Jing knew it would struggle to rise above its current limitations, no matter how extraordinary its bloodline might be.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 82: Thunder Pine
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 82: Thunder Pine

Despite its impressive strength, the Ice Soul Gu was no match for the Flame Dragon Carp and the Pine Leaf Mouse at this stage.

The Flame Dragon Carp, while lacking the bloodline strength of the Ice Soul Gu, had always been part of the team as a counter to it. Thanks to its innate talent in alchemy, it had stockpiled a variety of pills specifically designed to suppress the Ice Soul Gu.

As for the Pine Leaf Mouse, its bloodline had evolved to rival that of the Ice Soul Gu. Its cultivation was far superior, making a direct confrontation a one-sided slaughter.

That said, the Ice Soul Gu wasn’t weak. As an artificial creation, it could only unleash its full potential when paired with a skilled Gu Master. Among Lin Jing’s three contracted beasts, only the Ice Soul Gu’s power could perfectly merge with Lin Jing’s True Qi, creating a synergy that exceeded the sum of its parts.

Additionally, at the Qi Refinement stage, a Gu’s abilities were far from fully developed. Once the Ice Soul Gu reached Foundation Establishment and its Gu King bloodline fully awakened, its power would skyrocket. However, the other two beasts were also on the verge of breakthroughs.

The Flame Dragon Carp had yet to reach Foundation Establishment. Once it transformed into a dragon upon breaking through, its growth wouldn’t lag far behind the Ice Soul Gu.

Meanwhile, the Pine Leaf Mouse had an arsenal of resources: the Everlasting Emperor’s Longevity Art, the Divine Tree Seal, and warehouses full of Divine Tree fragments. Not to mention its Blood Mosquito Army and the Demon King True Form Talent, both of which offered incredible growth potential.

The Ice Soul Gu wasn’t weak—it was simply surrounded by freakishly strong companions.

Settling down in the icy cavern, Lin Jing began practicing the Art of Shedding. He also sent a telepathic message to the Pine Leaf Mouse:

“Don’t overdo it. Let the Ice Soul Gu return soon to focus on its main task—Foundation Establishment.”

Lin Jing was sure that after being bossed around by the Pine Leaf Mouse for a while, the Ice Soul Gu would obediently come back to absorb the extreme cold energy and prepare for its breakthrough. Its bloodline-born pride had likely been worn down to nearly nothing.

Meanwhile, the Divine Pine guarding the frozen pool above sensed the Ice Soul Gu’s awakening.

“Good thing I’ve been cultivating this Longevity Body well. If it were any other container, the Ice Soul Gu might have already taken over,” it mused.

“Now, all that’s left is to wait for that old monster from the Yin Corpse Sect. I wonder if he’ll manage to find this place… and if he dares to show up.”

The days passed. Lin Jing and his companions trained in the icy depths for more than half a month, while the Divine Pine stood vigil above.

Finally, the long-anticipated disturbance occurred. The Divine Pine, now appearing as a floating pinecone, snapped to attention as dark clouds gathered in the sky.

Under the blackened heavens, a coffin floated ominously. Standing atop its spectral image was a diminutive figure in a black robe, staring down at the frozen pool with a lifeless gaze.

“It seems the Longevity Body is here,” the figure muttered.

“Those fools failed to bring it back to headquarters. It always falls to me to clean up their mess.”

The black-robed figure’s gaze shifted, landing on the Divine Pine. “A sentient tree acting as a protector? No wonder the target dared to leave the academy.”

“Scram!” A wave of spiritual pressure emanated from the figure’s soul, attempting to force the Divine Pine to withdraw.

Despite having lost his physical body, this ancestor of the Yin Corpse Sect possessed Void Mystical Stage cultivation. Compared to the Divine Pine’s Nascent Soul Transformation Stage, he held an overwhelming advantage.

“I don’t want to waste my soul energy. Leave within three breaths, and I’ll spare your life,” the ancestor threatened.

The Divine Pine chuckled, its voice laden with mockery. “Yin Corpse brat, are you talking to me? Your cultivation may be higher, but I’m much older than you. Didn’t anyone teach you to respect your elders in the demonic path? If anyone should leave, it’s you! I haven’t even decided whether to consume this Longevity Body yet—you think you’re entitled to it?”

“You court death!” the ancestor snarled, stunned that a mere Nascent Soul cultivator would dare provoke him.

“Then I’ll refine you along with the Longevity Body!” With a flick of his arm, the coffin’s shadow released a surge of deadly energy.

However, just as the attack began, a loud crackling sound echoed. The ancestor’s eyes narrowed in shock.

From beside the pinecone, a massive pine tree had appeared. Though its cultivation was also at the Nascent Soul Transformation Stage, this tree radiated golden-green light interwoven with arcs of lightning.

Golden lightning snaked across its trunk, spreading a massive vortex of clouds above. Thunder rolled ominously within.

“Void Mystical brat, you’ve experienced tribulations, haven’t you?” the Divine Pine asked, laughing sinisterly. “For us spirit trees, attracting tribulations is far too easy. My Thunder Pine avatar just triggered one. I hope you enjoy it!”

The ancestor’s expression turned grim. Lightning tribulations were deadly to Yin Corpses. In his current weakened state, getting caught in one would leave him gravely injured, if not outright destroyed.

“I’ll remember this!” he hissed, preparing to flee.

But before he could, countless Thunder Pines sprouted across the snowy mountain, forming a vast Wood-Sealing Formation that trapped him in place.

“You’ll have to sacrifice something to catch the wolf. Losing this avatar is a small price to pay for eliminating a Void Mystical Yin Spirit. Besides, learning from the tribulation’s impact on my avatar will benefit me,” the Divine Pine muttered.

The ancestor struggled, breaking through the formation in mere moments. But those moments were enough. The connection to the Thunder Pine avatar drew the tribulation’s wrath upon him.

Both the Thunder Pine and the Yin Corpse Sect ancestor were engulfed in a torrent of divine lightning.

As the Divine Pine’s avatar crumbled under the assault, it showed no sadness, only curiosity. “I wonder if that old corpse brought enough treasures with him…”

Boom!

While the ancestor endured the relentless tribulation, the Divine Pine concealed its true body and transmitted a message to the void.

“Hey, you in the shadows. You’re from the Demon-Slaying Bureau, right? Hands off—this one’s mine.”

“I’ve already sacrificed an avatar and risked attracting the tribulation myself. Don’t expect me to do your dirty work for free!”

From the void, a voice responded, tinged with amusement. “You’re a bold little tree. Do you even know who you’re talking to?”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 83: The Heavenly Demon Physique
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 83: The Heavenly Demon Physique

The voice sounded incredibly youthful, more like that of a child than an elder. When it reached Thunder Pine's ears, the ancient tree spirit froze in surprise.

“A Child Immortal?”

During the tribulation that trapped the Ancestor of the Yin Corpse Sect, a boy clad in yellow robes, seemingly no older than ten, appeared out of the void. Thunder Pine had been focused on the tribulation and the Void Mystical Yin Spirit, but when the child emerged, it couldn't help but be amazed.

“I expected the Demon-Slaying Bureau to send someone, but I didn’t expect such a significant figure.”

“A significant figure? Compared to you—a grass and wood spirit that has cultivated to this level—I’m the one who should be in awe,” the boy said with a playful grin. “I heard that thousands of years ago, there was a faction in the Misty Forest entirely made up of grass and wood spirits. But a traitor arose within their ranks, slaughtering the entire tribe and fleeing to the Ancient Kingdom.”

“That traitor, the Malignant Plant who wiped out their kind, was you, wasn’t it?”

Thunder Pine chuckled. “As expected of one of the bureau's leaders, you’ve dug up my past so thoroughly.”

“But don’t worry, I’m no threat. Back then, I was young and foolish, thinking I’d found a family. Who would have guessed I’d stumbled into a faction of Malignant Plants? For self-preservation, I had no choice but to destroy them.”

“You certainly have been quiet,” the boy replied. “But one order from the Sect Regulatory Office, and here you are, unleashed.” He added, “You can stay in the Ancient Kingdom if you wish, but the Yin Corpse Sect cultivator? The Demon-Slaying Bureau will handle that.”

With a resounding boom, the Void Mystical Yin Spirit, who had just survived its tribulation but was severely weakened, was absorbed into the Celestial Prison summoned by the boy, along with its Void Mystical manifestation, the Black Coffin.

Thunder Pine's massive form appeared, revealing a face on its trunk as black as ink. Its gaze toward the boy was anything but friendly.

“Relax,” the boy said with a chuckle. “Using the tribulation to weaken it was the most efficient method. If I had directly intervened, I’d have had to deal with its countless strange tricks.”

“As a token of the Bureau’s gratitude, how about I adopt you as my foster son?”

“Go f*** yourself!” Thunder Pine barked irritably. “I’m no younger than you, kid! I’ve heard of the Demon-Slaying Bureau’s leader who cultivates the Child Immortal technique, remaining forever in a child’s form and, as a result, childless. You love adopting people as foster sons, don’t you?”

“Well, I’m not interested in being one of them!”

The boy sighed regretfully. “What a pity. I’ve adopted humans and beasts as foster children but never a grass and wood spirit.”

“It’s fine. That guy’s not of much use to you anyway. Most of his treasures were destroyed in his battle with the Ancient Kingdom’s experts. All that’s left is his memory, which you’d struggle to extract. Handing him over to the Bureau ensures his knowledge is put to maximum use.”

“Enough nonsense!” Thunder Pine snapped. “If I’m not satisfied, even if you’re Void Mystical, I won’t let this slide.”

“I’m curious what other cards you’re hiding. You’re not bluffing—I can sense that. But I can also tell you don’t want to play them,” the boy mused. Then, suddenly, he called toward the Frozen Pool, “Come on out, kid!”

Ripples spread across the pool’s surface, followed by a massive surge of water. A white-haired, blue-eyed youth emerged, his aura terrifying. Seventeen layers of freezing whirlpools surrounded him, and snow began falling from the sky.

“Qi Refinement Stage 17, peak level,” the boy said, observing Lin Jing.

“This Ice Soul Gu, which parasitized within me, has finally reached Foundation Establishment.”

“With Qi Refinement Stage 12 cultivation and the Ice Soul Gu of the Gu King’s bloodline, even an average Core Formation cultivator wouldn’t be able to defeat him.”

“Remarkable talent,” the boy said, his gaze admiring. “Only one level below the Ancient Imperial Prince with his Boundary-Breaking Beast’s assistance. Is this still the Longevity Body I thought I understood? It seems the Beast Taming Art and the Longevity Body are an extraordinary match.”

Pulled from the depths of the Frozen Pool by a powerful force, Lin Jing looked at the boy and Thunder Pine. “Senior, what’s going on here?” he demanded.

“That boy… is he…”

“Look closely. He’s alive, not a Yin Spirit,” Thunder Pine said. “This guy is one of the old monsters of the Demon-Slaying Bureau.”

“You’ve heard of the four great Demon-Slaying Families, right? Your detective friend Gao Sheng’s great-grandfather is an ancestor of one of them.”

“And this old monster before you? He’s the Nascent Soul Transformation Ancestor of those families, and they all call him foster father.”

“What!?” Lin Jing’s eyes widened as he glanced at the yellow-robed boy.

“The Yin Corpse Sect cultivator has been dealt with, but he’s taken the Yin Spirit,” Thunder Pine said coldly.

The boy laughed. “I may not have anything to offer you directly, but since you’re acting as his protector, compensating him instead should be fine, right?”

He preferred to reward Lin Jing, a human, rather than further empowering a Malignant Plant like Thunder Pine.

Thunder Pine was about to retort when the boy produced a jade slip.

“This contains the method for cultivating a physique from the continent’s former number-one demonic sect, the Heavenly Demon Sect.”

“The Heavenly Demon Physique can temporarily boost cultivation in exchange for lifespan. According to the Bureau’s records, during the sect’s downfall, a Nascent Soul demonic cultivator burned a thousand years of his lifespan, temporarily attaining Void Mystical strength. He maintained this power for ten years before falling back to Nascent Soul.”

“Though he lost a millennium of life, he survived a certain death scenario. During those ten years, he used his immense power to plunder resources beyond what his former strength could achieve.”

“Whether it was a loss or a gain depends on perspective. But for someone with a Longevity Body, cultivating the Heavenly Demon Physique is a surefire win. Burning a thousand years is hardly a concern.”

“So this technique really exists!” Thunder Pine exclaimed, delighted. It had long sought a demonic art to allow Lin Jing to trade lifespan for power and hadn’t expected to find it in the Bureau’s collection.

Then again, it made sense. The Bureau had dismantled countless demonic sects during the Ancient Kingdom’s founding wars.

“Not bad,” Thunder Pine said with a grin. “Though I didn’t expect the Bureau to offer a demonic technique as compensation.”

The boy gestured toward Lin Jing. “He’s one of only two Longevity Body cultivators alive. Losing him young would be a shame. This technique will give him a trump card to preserve his life, easing your burden as his protector.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 84: Joining the Demon-Slaying Bureau
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 84: Joining the Demon-Slaying Bureau

The Heavenly Demon Physique.

Hearing about this physique, Lin Jing was visibly surprised. Its abilities, while certainly not to be used casually, were akin to possessing a forbidden weapon that could intimidate enemies merely by existing.

Even with his current Qi Refinement cultivation, he could theoretically sacrifice a thousand years of lifespan. At that point, suppressing even ordinary Nascent Soul cultivators or Demon Kings wouldn’t be out of reach. This was just at his Qi Refinement stage—if he reached the Core Formation or Nascent Soul stages and sacrificed even more lifespan, those one or two realms above him would think twice before attempting to take him on.

The most critical point was that the cultivation gained from the Heavenly Demon Physique wasn’t fleeting. A Nascent Soul cultivator could burn a millennium of lifespan to temporarily achieve Void Mystical combat strength and maintain it for a full ten years.

During that time, one could accomplish feats impossible at lower cultivation, amassing enough resources to offset the cost of the lost years. The Heavenly Demon Physique, as a second physique for someone with the Longevity Body, was practically a perfect match.

“Senior, besides me, are there really other Longevity Body cultivators alive?” Lin Jing asked, recalling the boy's earlier comment that he was one of two.

“There is,” the boy replied. “A thousand years ago, a Longevity Body emerged in Qingzhou. However, before they could mature, they were captured and caged by the Sky Lion Clan.”

“That clan sacrifices Longevity Blood and Flesh every decade during their ancestral ceremonies, just like the Ancient Celestial Palace used Dragon Liver and Phoenix Marrow for celebratory feasts.

“After being caged for a thousand years, one can only imagine the state of her mind.”

Lin Jing fell silent. It was shocking to learn another Longevity Body lived, but her circumstances were far worse than his. Fortunately, he had been rescued by the Beast Taming Sect. Otherwise, if the Yin Corpse Sect had sold him to a demonic beast, his fate might have been the same.

The Sky Lion Clan was well-known—a terrifying demonic beast tribe in Qingzhou. They had a Void Mystical Demon Ancestor and their own pocket dimension as a territory.

“No one ever tried to save her?” Lin Jing asked.

Realizing the foolishness of his question, he regretted it immediately. The Tianyuan Ancient Kingdom had no obligation to rescue her, especially since she was from Qingzhou and not a cultivator of the kingdom. Even if she were, saving her from the Sky Lion Clan could cost numerous Nascent Soul and Void Mystical lives with no guarantee of success. Rescuing her would undoubtedly be a losing venture.

The boy held up the jade slip for the Heavenly Demon Physique. “This is the fate of a Longevity Body. It’s almost unchangeable. You should count yourself lucky to be in the Ancient Kingdom, where no powerful demonic beast tribes exist.”

“Understood. What’s the other prerequisite for cultivating the Heavenly Demon Physique?” Lin Jing asked.

“You have to join the Demon-Slaying Bureau,” the boy said.

“It’s not as if the Bureau will constantly assign you tasks or restrict your freedom. However, the Bureau must know your location at all times.”

Thunder Pine spoke up, “Agree to it. At least it will provide you with an additional layer of protection. For someone like you, allowing the Bureau to track your movements is a small price to pay for some measure of security.”

After a moment’s thought, Lin Jing nodded. He had little choice. The Heavenly Demon Physique was too important to pass up.

The boy tossed the jade slip and a token to Lin Jing. “This token is custom-made. Once refined, it will not only identify you as a Demon-Slaying Guard but also act as a miniature Bureau branch.

“When guards on missions discover traces of demonic cultivators or sects, they immediately report to a Bureau branch through a secret technique. Your token will work similarly, allowing you to receive intelligence on demonic sects and cultivators from all over.”

“Moreover, this token allows me to track your location. If you encounter a threat Thunder Pine can’t handle and don’t want to die without help, keep it safe.”

“One last thing. The Heavenly Demon Physique is incredibly difficult to cultivate. Of all the Bureau’s confiscated techniques, only two people have succeeded with it…”

Lin Jing put the token away and said, “Thank you, Senior.”

Thunder Pine muttered, “If I can’t handle something, the Bureau probably can’t either.”

Ignoring the comment, the boy left with the sealed Yin Corpse Sect Void Mystical Yin Spirit in tow.

“Senior Thunder Pine… Couldn’t you give him some face?”

“What did I say that wasn’t true?” Thunder Pine glared at Lin Jing. “If you were the one caged by the Sky Lion Clan, do you think the Bureau would rescue you?”

“Still, this token isn’t useless. If the situation is something the Bureau can handle, like this Void Mystical Yin Spirit, they’ll probably come to your aid. You have some value as a Longevity Body. But don’t think they’ll save you if the cost outweighs the benefits.

“So, why are you just standing there? Start cultivating the Heavenly Demon Physique! If you want to survive, you must rely on yourself. If you encounter a situation I can’t handle, don’t count on me—I’ll be the first to run.”

Lin Jing silently nodded. He looked at the jade slip for the Heavenly Demon Physique. Thunder Pine’s cherished Void Mystical Yin Spirit had been taken, and the compensation fell into his hands. Thunder Pine had accepted it.

For this alone, Lin Jing swore that if Thunder Pine were ever captured and refined, he would avenge him and outlast his enemies.

Later

Lin Jing lay on the snowy mountain.

“What’s wrong?” Thunder Pine asked.

“I can’t learn it,” Lin Jing admitted. “The Heavenly Demon Physique technique is too difficult. Even with the help of Hidden Dragon Fortune, I haven’t made any progress.”

“At this rate, I’ll exhaust the Hidden Dragon Fortune without mastering it.”

After a moment of silence, Thunder Pine said, “You just finished practicing the Molting Technique from Hundred Flower Valley. Take a break. The Ice Soul Gu has reached Foundation Establishment. Our goal here is achieved. Return to the academy for now.”

“Understood,” Lin Jing replied.

Just then, the Demon-Slaying Bureau token he carried received a message:

“A Yin Corpse Sect stronghold has been discovered. It contains two captives with special physiques, guarded by a Core Formation demonic cultivator!”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 85: The Twins
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 85: The Twins

“What’s wrong?” Thunder Pine asked when it noticed Lin Jing staring intently at his token.

“Nothing much. A Demon-Slaying Guard discovered a Yin Corpse Sect stronghold, and I received the intel,” Lin Jing replied.

“Are you planning to raid that stronghold?” Thunder Pine guessed his intention.

“Yes,” Lin Jing said, clenching the token tightly. “The report says two individuals with special physiques are imprisoned there.”

“I recall that’s how the Beast Taming Sect rescued you from a Yin Corpse Sect stronghold back then,” Thunder Pine said.

“Indeed. The nearest Demon-Slaying Bureau branch should have also received the intel. If luck is on their side, those two will be saved. However, this is a rare opportunity for me to take revenge against the Yin Corpse Sect with my own hands. It’s just a small stronghold guarded by a Core Formation demonic cultivator, but even an empire’s downfall can begin with an ant nest.”

“Fine. Consider it a test of your abilities. Go ahead,” Thunder Pine said approvingly. “But I won’t intervene unless a Nascent Soul shows up.”

“Understood.” Lin Jing activated the token, which pointed him toward the stronghold.

Lin Jing soared into the sky, transforming into a streak of blue light as he flew toward his target.

In a secluded valley near Qianxun City…

A black-robed elder stood before the lifeless body of a Foundation Establishment Demon-Slaying Guard, a gaping hole in the guard’s chest. The elder casually waved a hand, and the body disintegrated into ash.

Behind him were two gray-robed Yin Corpse Sect cultivators escorting a young boy and girl, both bound by a spell. Tears welled in the captives’ eyes as they saw the fallen Demon-Slaying Guard.

“Master, it seems the Demon-Slaying Bureau has discovered us. Should we evacuate immediately?” one of the gray-robed cultivators asked.

“No need,” the elder said. “I’ve already arranged the transaction with the buyer here. Changing the location now would be too troublesome.”

“Complete the trade quickly, and then we’ll leave. Your efforts in capturing these two have been commendable. I’ll make sure you’re rewarded after the deal,” the elder promised.

“Yes, Master!” Though they felt staying was risky, the two gray-robed cultivators dared not disobey.

The young captives, a boy and a girl with similar features, were Qi Refinement cultivators. Despair filled their eyes as they anticipated their grim fate. The captors, meanwhile, marveled at their good fortune—snagging a pair of Twin Physiques so effortlessly.

The Twin Physique manifests only in twins. Individually, it has no effect, but when the twins fight together, their minds synchronize, allowing them to share techniques and amplify their strength exponentially. This made them formidable in battle.

Suddenly, a beam of white light pierced through the heads of the two gray-robed cultivators, killing them instantly. A Core Formation Demon-Slaying Guard, clad in crimson armor and exuding righteousness, appeared in the sky.

Seeing him, the elder cursed their luck for not insisting on relocating the trade. The young captives’ despair briefly gave way to hope.

But that hope quickly turned into horror.

“You arrived quickly,” the Yin Corpse Sect elder said coldly to the Demon-Slaying Guard. “Next time, keep your subordinates in line.”

“That one was new and foolish. He’s paid the price with his life, hasn’t he?” the guard replied. “The usual deal: I’ll destroy this stronghold, and you take the twins.”

“Your spoils are already inside the stronghold,” the elder said indifferently.

The twins froze in fear, their hearts sinking into a frozen abyss. They never imagined that the Demon-Slaying Bureau’s guards—dedicated to eradicating evil—would collaborate with their captors.

Struggling futilely against their bonds, they realized they were mere goods in this transaction.

Unbothered by their thoughts, the Demon-Slaying Guard remained focused on his cooperation with the Yin Corpse Sect. Over the years, he had used his position to secretly feed them information in exchange for rewards. This arrangement had allowed him to rise from Foundation Establishment to Core Formation, his path far smoother than the perilous battles he once endured.

As the trade neared completion, a strange laugh echoed from the sky.

Thunder Pine, observing the scene from above with Lin Jing, couldn’t help but laugh. “Well, this is interesting. I wonder what you think of all this?”

Lin Jing’s expression remained cold. “What’s there to think about? This is common enough.”

“Except now there are two Core Formation enemies instead of one.”

“At least they’re only early-stage Core Formation,” Lin Jing said calmly.

“Who’s there?”

Neither of the Core Formation cultivators had sensed Lin Jing’s presence due to his layered concealment techniques. It was only when Thunder Pine’s laugh drew their attention that they noticed him.

“A Qi Refinement cultivator?”

Both were puzzled by the sudden appearance of such a low-level intruder. However, with their deal exposed, they shared a single thought: eliminate him.

“Die!” the Demon-Slaying Guard roared, his eyes shooting twin beams of white light— the same technique that had killed the gray-robed cultivators earlier.

Lin Jing raised his hand, and a surge of icy energy spread from his arm, freezing the beams mid-air. Snowflakes began to drift down, and the twins looked up at Lin Jing with a mix of awe and disbelief.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 86: Demon Extermination
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 86: Demon Extermination

With a swift motion, Lin Jing absorbed the power of the Ice Soul Gu. His true energy merged seamlessly with the Gu's chilling force.

His jet-black hair and dark eyes transformed into snow-white locks and icy blue irises. The stark white hair and piercing blue eyes gave Lin Jing a cold, detached aura.

Due to the overwhelming strength of the Ice Soul Gu, empowered by the Gu King's bloodline, Lin Jing's body once again underwent unavoidable changes. Yet, this incredible frost power was worth any price.

“He’s no ordinary Qi Refinement cultivator! This is the power of Gu insects—he’s a demonic cultivator from the Gu God Sect!”

Before the Demon-Slaying Guard could react, the Yin Corpse Sect Leader shouted in shock, staring at the white spider pattern that had surfaced on Lin Jing’s neck.

“The Gu God Sect? Why would someone from the Gu God Sect appear here?”

The Crimson Armored Demon-Slaying Guard was visibly stunned. Unlike the Yin Corpse Sect, the Gu God Sect usually operated near the Cloud Sea Mountains. In his many years in the Air Domain, this was his first encounter with a demonic cultivator from that sect.

A demonic cultivator from the Gu God Sect? The revelation sent shockwaves through the two Twin Physiques, their emotions fluctuating wildly.

“Let’s attack together!”

The Yin Corpse Sect Leader issued the command. Five formation diagrams materialized around him, summoning five corpses, each with a distinct hue—gold, green, blue, red, and earthy yellow. These were the legendary Five-Element Corpse Buddhas.

“These corpses were crafted from cultivators with gold, wood, water, fire, and earth spiritual physiques, refined into Corpse Puppets. And through the Corpse Core Technique, they’ve been elevated to the false core stage. Impressive, but... too bad they encountered this kid.”

As the Divine Pine remarked, the Five-Element Corpses soared into the air, encircling Lin Jing. The Crimson Armored Guard also unleashed nine righteous sword artifacts, brimming with energy that suppressed evil.

“Demon-Purging Sword Formation!”

Hovering behind him, the sword formation gathered power. When the nine sword artifacts shot forward, the five corpses formed a Five-Element Formation under the Yin Corpse Sect Leader's control, trapping Lin Jing within.

“My Five-Element Formation is so potent that even a mid-stage Core Formation cultivator wouldn’t escape easily!” The Yin Corpse Sect Leader sneered.

Lin Jing indeed felt the immense pressure. His true energy was suppressed, unable to flow freely.

As the nine swords closed in, the five corpses unexpectedly moved. They leapt to shield Lin Jing, intercepting the incoming swords!

Boom!

The five corpses were sent flying by the impact.

“What are you doing?!” The Demon-Slaying Guard glared at the Yin Corpse Sect Leader, who spat out blood in shock. “Fool! Look carefully!”

“What...?”

A spiritual scan revealed nearly transparent spider threads emanating from Lin Jing’s fingers, linking the five corpses. The threads severed the Leader’s connection to them, granting Lin Jing full control.

“This Gu insect... it’s extraordinary! It instantly usurped my refined corpses!”

“This can’t be real. How could such a Gu insect exist?”

The Yin Corpse Sect Leader’s panic deepened. To his horror, he realized the Gu toxin had infiltrated him through his severed connection with the corpses, bypassing physical contact entirely.

“This Gu toxin... even my spiritual energy is freezing. I’ve lost control of my own body!”

The Ice Soul Gu's frost power easily overpowered the leader’s defenses. Its toxic force could subjugate even Nascent Soul cultivators if its strength continued to grow.

“Old man, if you take care of those two, I’ll let you spar with this squirrel and the carp again. How about that?”

With the Ice Soul Gu’s current might, enhanced by his fusion with Core Formation power, Lin Jing casually dismissed his Qi Refinement adversaries.

“No need,” Lin Jing replied calmly. Frost energy erupted from his body in a storm that engulfed the two Core Formation cultivators. The Yin Corpse Sect Leader shouted, his core cracking slightly under the immense cold. His body, riddled with corpse spots, barely dodged the onslaught.

Meanwhile, the Crimson Armored Guard, seemingly unfazed by the Ice Soul Gu's terror, invoked a protective white true energy shield and redirected his swords toward Lin Jing.

To his dismay, as the swords neared Lin Jing, they froze mid-air, unable to advance. The frost emanating from Lin Jing surpassed even the Ice Spirit Physique, rivaling the mythical Ice God Physique.

“Who are you?!”

The Guard roared as the frost invaded his protective energy, freezing him completely. His Core Formation cracked under the strain, but he managed to activate his trump card: the Core Formation Divine Ability. Two radiant swords burst from his eyes, shattering the frost around him as they hurtled toward Lin Jing.

The Divine Pine murmured, “Core Formation Divine Abilities are the ultimate ace of Core Formation cultivators. They damage the core with each use, so they’re rarely deployed unless absolutely necessary. To use it now means...”

The frost rapidly tried to freeze the radiant swords, but their sheer power shattered the ice, streaking toward Lin Jing’s head.

“Fight each other to the death!”

Seeing this, the Yin Corpse Sect Leader abandoned the fight, hoping to escape with the Twin Physiques. However, as he reached them, a single red leaf descended, rippling with spatial energy and drawing everyone’s attention.

In the next moment, Lin Jing vanished, reappearing in front of the twins and the Yin Corpse Sect Leader, holding the red leaf between two fingers. A squirrel perched on his shoulder.

“!!”

The squirrel’s eyes glowed red as thousands of blood mosquitoes materialized, swarming the Yin Corpse Sect Leader and the Demon-Slaying Guard, their pupils contracting in terror.

“He evaded the Divine Ability! That’s spatial sorcery!”

“And blood arts! Who is this guy?”

“Damn it, that looks like... the Cloud Sea Mosquito Beast!”

To protect the twins, Lin Jing summoned the Pine Leaf Mouse to join the fray.

“Don’t be afraid. I’m a Demon-Slaying Guard, here to save you.”

Lin Jing faced the two Core Formation cultivators, blood mosquitoes swarming around him. The twins nodded nervously, their fear deepening.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 87: Battling Core Formation
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 87: Battling Core Formation

The swarm of blood mosquitoes launched relentless suicidal attacks, leaving the two Core Formation cultivators, one a leader from the Yin Corpse Sect and the other a branch head of the Demon-Slaying Bureau, in utter disarray. Neither had ever encountered such an eerie blood-path technique that mimicked the characteristics of the Cloud Sea Mosquito Beast.

Despite their Core Formation cultivation, the sheer number of blood beasts, attacking without regard for life, left them unnerved. After eliminating hundreds, the relentless assault made them realize this was a battle they couldn’t afford to prolong.

Initially, seeing Lin Jing as a mere Qi Refinement cultivator, the pair had struck with confidence. However, they soon discovered this was no ordinary Qi Refinement practitioner. Not only did he harbor a powerful Gu insect, but he also had a Foundation Establishment-level pet beast proficient in spatial arts!

Realizing that Lin Jing wasn’t easily subdued, the two began to negotiate.

“Fellow Daoist, we have no grudge with you. Extermination will bring you no benefit. If you force us to the brink, we’ll shatter our cores and unleash our divine abilities. Neither of us will walk away alive!”

Surrounded by the blood mosquito army, the two Core Formation cultivators exuded a menacing aura as the power of their cores surged.

“If your goal is the Twin Physiques, you can have them. But if you push us further, no one will come out unscathed.”

Standing protectively before the twins, Lin Jing regarded the trapped cultivators above. He didn’t think they were bluffing.

Core Formation divine abilities were a double-edged sword. Using them inflicted wear on the core, which could take decades of secluded cultivation and rare healing treasures to repair. Shattering the core entirely would drop a Core Formation cultivator to the Foundation Establishment stage, a state from which most never recovered.

The two cultivators’ threat to unleash their most destructive divine abilities, even at the cost of shattering their cores, was a clear message: Lin Jing should back down to avoid mutual destruction.

“Full-powered divine abilities, huh...” Lin Jing’s lips curled into a faint smile as he weighed the threat.

For Core Formation cultivators, divine abilities were their ultimate trump card, rarely used in battles unless the disparity in power was immense. That two Core Formation cultivators were threatening a Qi Refinement-stage opponent with such measures was almost unheard of.

Lin Jing responded with actions instead of words, making it clear he was unafraid. The Pine Leaf Mouse continued directing the blood mosquito swarm, while Lin Jing raised his right hand, launching spider silk toward the pair.

“Watch out! Those threads are laced with terrifying Gu poison. It almost controlled me. Even using my divine ability, I could barely suppress it. Don’t let it touch you!” the Yin Corpse Sect leader shouted, wary that the Crimson Armored Guard might become Lin Jing’s puppet and turn against him.

“Let me tell you something,” Lin Jing said, his fingers deftly manipulating the white threads. “Even if you suppress the Gu poison now, it’s only temporary. Without me, there’s no cure. The poison will spread until it freezes your soul. Do you still want me gone?”

The Yin Corpse Sect leader’s expression faltered as Lin Jing’s words struck home. This Gu insect was unlike anything he had seen. It had not only usurped control of his refined corpses but also infiltrated him through their spiritual connection.

“Since you insist on courting death, don’t blame fate,” he snarled.

The sect leader stopped evading and reached for the incoming spider silk. As his body exhibited more corpse-like features, he activated a secret art: Corpse Transformation . This technique temporarily converted his physique into that of a corpse puppet, enhancing his resistance to negative effects and mitigating the Gu poison’s influence.

Even so, the transformation pushed his body to its limits, cracks appearing across his form as his corpse-like state deepened. Seizing the spider silk, he planned to capture Lin Jing and force a cure for the poison.

However, before he could act, a chilling gust swept through the battlefield. A spatial ripple appeared, and Lin Jing, along with the Pine Leaf Mouse and the Twin Physiques, vanished.

The sect leader froze, scanning the area with his spiritual sense, only to find no trace of Lin Jing or the twins. The swarm of blood mosquitoes, now without the Pine Leaf Mouse’s guidance, continued their frenzied attacks, driven solely by instinct.

High above, concealed by an illusion, Lin Jing observed the scene below.

“You’re pretty quick on your feet, kid,” remarked the Divine Pine, watching the scattered red leaves. It was clear that Lin Jing’s strategy was to avoid the divine abilities entirely. Though powerful, a divine ability was useless if it couldn’t hit its target.

The two Core Formation cultivators had issued hollow threats. Without a guaranteed strike, activating their divine abilities would be pointless.

Furthermore, Lin Jing had cleverly used the Gu poison as a counterthreat, forcing the Yin Corpse Sect leader into a corner. To survive, the sect leader now had no choice but to capture Lin Jing alive.

The sect leader’s Core Formation divine ability, when unleashed, had amplified his strength twofold. While Lin Jing couldn’t match him in direct combat, his spatial arts and illusionary tactics left the leader struggling.

“Drawing out just one of their divine abilities is enough,” Lin Jing murmured as he watched below.

Enraged by Lin Jing’s disappearance, the Yin Corpse Sect leader annihilated countless blood mosquitoes with a sweep of his corpse banner. His gaze shifted to the Crimson Armored Guard, and with a roar, his Five-Element Corpses surrounded the guard. In a flash, his claw pierced the guard’s chest.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 88: Followers
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 88: Followers

“What are you doing?!”

The Crimson Armored Demon-Slaying Guard’s pupils shrank as he was suddenly attacked by the Yin Corpse Sect cultivator.

“Wondering why I didn’t chase that brat?” The sect leader sneered. “Having activated my Heavenly Corpse Transformation to its full potential, I can’t allow you to walk away with the spoils.”

“After this battle, my cultivation will likely drop to the Foundation Establishment stage. But if I can secure a Core Formation corpse puppet, it won’t be a complete loss. With my talent, advancing to the Nascent Soul stage was never an option anyway.”

“A Core Formation corpse puppet will allow me to condense a high-quality Corpse Core and attain a false core with little hindrance.”

The Yin Corpse Sect leader's tone was cold. “That brat may have escaped, but now that I’ve regained control of the Five-Element Corpses, I’ll deal with you first.”

“No need to worry. My Heavenly Corpse Transformation will last long enough to finish you off. Afterward, I’ll find him and kill him too. I’ll even preserve your reputation.”

Having gained a surge of power, the first thing the sect leader did was launch a sneak attack on the Crimson Armored Guard. Observing this, the Divine Pine commented disdainfully, “At least the Yin Corpse Sect brat is acting sensibly. That Demon-Slaying Guard, relaxing his guard around a demonic cultivator—what an idiot.”

“It was a possibility I accounted for,” Lin Jing replied calmly. “That’s why I returned control of the Five-Element Corpses to him—to enhance his strength. Yin Corpse Sect cultivators are different. Even if they drop to the Foundation Establishment stage, they can form false cores and turn enemies into corpse puppets to bolster their power.”

“With such an increase in strength, it was almost certain he’d target the Demon-Slaying Guard first. Ideally, the guard would have reacted and realized the sect leader would betray him, leading to mutual destruction. Unfortunately, the guard didn’t catch on and thought I was still the shared enemy.”

“Still, this situation is easier to handle. Once his divine ability runs out, I can finish him off. And even if I leave now, he’s already infected with the Ice Soul Gu’s poison. He won’t live long.”

Lin Jing’s eyes flashed. The fate of the sect leader was sealed, much like what had happened to Senior Brother Ouyang years ago. The Ice Soul Gu’s poison was incurable.

The Divine Pine and Lin Jing’s telepathic exchange went unnoticed by the Twin Physiques, who remained paralyzed with fear. Although they had escaped the Yin Corpse Sect, they now found themselves in the hands of what appeared to be a demonic cultivator from the Gu God Sect.

“Senior, since he’s doomed anyway, why not finish him off now?” the girl stammered nervously.

“Why don’t you do it yourself?” Lin Jing asked. “Haven’t you killed anyone yet?”
