Killing the Crimson Armored Guard would likely alert the Demon-Slaying Bureau. Lin Jing suspected this guard wasn’t the only one colluding with demonic sects. Perhaps his time would be better spent elsewhere.

“Then the spoils of war are mine,” the Divine Pine sighed, lamenting that these early-stage Core Formation cultivators weren’t challenging enough for Lin Jing.

Lin Jing was about to respond when, suddenly, a pine tree branch pierced the chest of the Yin Corpse Sect leader below. Blood streaked across his eyes as he realized he had been outplayed from the start.

With both Core Formation cultivators dead, Lin Jing retrieved a jade token. Though he had several Demon-Slaying Bureau tokens, only this one could establish contact.

The token had been given to him by Cold Moon Sword Immortal Feng Ning, a disciple of the Void Mystical Sword Ancestor from the Heavenly Sword Gate. While Feng Ning herself was only at the Core Formation stage, her status meant even top cultivators showed her respect.

Rather than act alone, Lin Jing decided to report the internal corruption to someone reliable.

Activating the token, a familiar voice echoed in his mind.

“Lin Jing? You’ve finally activated this jade token. Have you changed your mind about joining the Demon-Slaying Bureau?”

The voice belonged to Feng Ning.

“No, Senior Feng,” Lin Jing replied. “I’ve already joined under the invitation of Senior Tongzi Xian.”

“Tongzi Xian personally invited you?” Feng Ning’s surprise was evident.

Lin Jing briefly explained the circumstances of his joining and the incident involving the collusion between the local Demon-Slaying Bureau and demonic sects. Feng Ning’s tone turned cold.

“Well done. This isn’t just a problem with one bureau branch; it’s likely a widespread issue across the Air Domain. I’ll contact my associates in the Bureau to investigate. Handling this alone might be too much for you.”

Lin Jing nodded.

“But to think you could take on two Core Formation cultivators as a Qi Refinement practitioner... If word spreads, it will shock the Ancient Celestial Kingdom.”

“It was the Ice Soul Gu’s power,” Lin Jing said. “Surely you don’t think I could manage that with just my Qi Refinement cultivation.”

“True, I know the strength of Beast Taming cultivators depends on their beasts. Still, many won’t care about those details. They’ll only see ‘Qi Refinement defeats Core Formation’ and the fame that comes with it.”

Feng Ning chuckled. “Let them talk. Fame can be useful. And thanks to exaggerated stories like these, hidden techniques like the Hidden Dragon Arts remain relevant.”

Lin Jing agreed. Clever manipulation of rumors often kept hidden techniques thriving.

Ending the conversation, Lin Jing resolved to remain cautious around government-affiliated cultivators to avoid betrayal.

Turning to the Twin Physiques, he finally addressed them.

“Introduce yourselves. How did the Yin Corpse Sect capture you, and what makes your physique special?”

The young man stayed silent, paralyzed by fear, but the girl mustered the courage to reply.

“We’re from the Jiang family of Tianshui City, and we possess the Twin Physique. We were captured during a trip outside.”

“Twin Physique?” Lin Jing murmured.

The Divine Pine interjected, “That’s a rare physique with many uses. Kid, why not do a good deed and grant them an opportunity? Take them in as human companions. After all, a Holy Son can’t only have animal partners, right?”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 89: The Sub-Gu
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 89: The Sub-Gu

"Human pets! Yes, I want to take human pets!"

As soon as Divine Pine said this, Ice Soul Gu was the first to agree.

After reaching Foundation Establishment, Ice Soul Gu had gained confidence that it could defeat the Pine Leaf Mouse and Dragonblood Carp.

But after witnessing how Pine Leaf Mouse controlled a swarm of Blood Mosquitoes, Ice Soul Gu suddenly realized it was lacking something.

The Dragonblood Carp had pills that could greatly boost its combat power.

The Pine Leaf Mouse had hoarded plenty of battle supplies and had many trump cards.

Only Ice Soul Gu, freshly born, had nothing.

It believed this was the key reason it had lost to the Pine Leaf Mouse and Dragonblood Carp before.

Lin Jing indeed had the ability to take "human pets."

This ability was embodied in the Ice Soul Gu itself.

As the emperor of all Gu, Ice Soul Gu could not only use Gu poison to directly control other beings, but it could also "nurture" Sub-Gu.

A Sub-Gu was essentially a miniature version of Ice Soul Gu, completely controlled by the mother Gu and possessing a weaker version of its abilities.

Ice Soul Gu could implant a Sub-Gu into another person, granting them power while also controlling them.

In truth, Ice Soul Gu had only just awakened and had not yet fully utilized its abilities, which was why it had been bullied by Pine Leaf Mouse and Dragonblood Carp in turn.

"Even a Sub-Gu of the Ice Soul Gu contains power that most cultivators cannot bear. At the very least, they would need to have an Ice Spirit Body or a Fire Spirit Body."

"But even with an Ice Spirit Body or a Fire Spirit Body, their abilities would still be too one-dimensional. An Ice Spirit Body can indeed maximize the power of the Ice Soul Sub-Gu, but an extreme cold force isn't necessarily a good thing. Ice upon ice—if the body can't handle it, it will only become a burden and could even freeze itself to death."

"For example, look at you now. After merging with the power of Ice Soul Gu, your body and appearance have changed drastically. This is a sign that your body is struggling to adapt. If you use it too frequently, your body will eventually collapse."

"By comparison, someone with a Fire Spirit Body would make a better vessel for the Ice Soul Gu. They may not be able to fully unleash its power, but at least their body would remain stable."

"Therefore, to perfectly wield the Ice Soul Gu, the ideal host would possess both Ice and Fire attributes. That way, they could fully integrate with the Ice Soul Gu’s power while also having the means to suppress it."

"But possessing both Ice and Fire attributes is incredibly rare. Just like you—you’ll have to form a contract with the Dragonblood Carp in the future to gain fire-related abilities."

"However, Twin Physique cultivators are an exception. Since the two share a mind and power, they could potentially become cultivators who simultaneously wield both Ice and Fire abilities. Most cultivators can only practice a single technique, but Twin Physique cultivators, thanks to their shared power, can cultivate two techniques at once."

"You can have one of them train in a powerful Ice-based technique, and the other in a powerful Fire-based technique. Then, you implant the Ice Soul Sub-Gu into them. That way, they will become your sharpest blades—the rare few who can fully unleash the power of the Ice Soul Sub-Gu!"

"Of course, this is just one way to utilize the Twin Physique," Divine Pine chuckled. "As the vessels nurture the Sub-Gu to greater strength, the mother Gu, much like Beast Taming Sect cultivators, will receive power feedback from the Sub-Gu and grow even stronger!"

"Not a bad method to cultivate the Ice Soul Gu!"

"I see..." Lin Jing hesitated for a moment, then shook his head.

Even if Twin Physique cultivators were the ideal vessels for nurturing Ice Soul Sub-Gu, wasn't this essentially forcing them onto the demonic path of Gu cultivation?

That would be wrong.

Unless... they agreed willingly.

Seeing that Zhu Rui had remained silent for a long time, the Twin Physique siblings were starting to panic.

Then, Ice Soul Gu spoke again: "Tianshui City, the Jiang Clan."

"What level of cultivation family is that?"

"A Golden Core early-stage ancestor exists within the Jiang Clan," the twin sister replied.

Lin Jing nodded. "No wonder you two were targeted by the Yin Corpse Sect. A single Golden Core ancestor likely isn’t enough to protect you."

"You two have two choices. One, I send you back to your family."

"Or two, you follow me, and I will give you power."

The twins exchanged alarmed glances. Their minds were connected, so they didn't even need to speak or look at each other to understand each other's thoughts.

"He... he says he’ll send us back?"

"Don't believe him! We've seen too much. There's no way he’d let us go back safely. He’ll definitely kill us to silence us!"

"He’s testing us—to see if we’ll obey. His goal must be the same as the Yin Corpse Sect. He wants our bodies!"

...

"Senior, please don’t kill us! We’re willing to serve at your side..." The twins pleaded desperately.

"...Who said anything about killing you?" Lin Jing said flatly. "Think carefully. If I send you home, at most, I’ll make you forget a few things."

"But following me isn't necessarily a good thing either. However, if you obey, it won’t take long for you to become the strongest cultivators in your clan."

"Senior... May we ask what we would have to do if we followed you?" The twin brother swallowed nervously.

Lin Jing chuckled. "Nothing much. You’d just serve as vessels to raise two Gu. Don’t worry—with your physiques, as long as you cooperate, these Gu won’t harm you. In fact, they will become your greatest partners."

The twins' vision darkened.

Gu vessels?!

"...We will follow you, Senior. We won’t return home," the twin sister spoke nervously.

Compared to falling into the hands of the Yin Corpse Sect, they found the idea of being in the Gu God Sect slightly more acceptable.

If the Yin Corpse Sect captured them, they would either be possessed or turned into corpse puppets.

But the Gu God Sect... at worst, they would just have to deal with poisonous insects.

"Are you certain?" Lin Jing asked.

"We are. We wish to follow you, Senior," the twins confirmed after a brief exchange of thoughts.

"Good. In that case, let’s first go to your clan. You should at least check in and reassure them before we leave the Air Domain," Lin Jing said casually.

"...We can actually go home and check in?" The twins were stunned.

"I told you—I’m a good person."

The twins nodded frantically.

"By the way, what techniques have you been practicing? Do they have elemental attributes?" Lin Jing asked.

He looked at the twins—they were both at the seventh stage of Qi Refinement and around fifteen or sixteen years old. Their cultivation wasn't too high.

"Senior, we practice our family’s ‘Nine-Turn Pure Essence Technique.’ It is a non-elemental technique that focuses on fast cultivation speed."

"Hmm." Lin Jing nodded. "For now, stop cultivating. Prepare to abandon your current technique and start anew. The ‘Nine-Turn Pure Essence Technique’ is neither good nor bad, but it won’t suit your future path."

"I will find new techniques for you to replace it."

But as he said this, Lin Jing fell into deep thought.

Where could he find powerful Ice and Fire cultivation techniques?

It seemed he’d have to go searching.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 90: Target 6
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 90: Target 6

Tianshui City, Jiang Clan—one of the city's three major cultivation families.

At present, the Jiang Clan was in chaos. The family’s greatest hope, the twin siblings, had been missing for half a month.

"I told you long ago—we should’ve found a powerful master for Qi'er and Lin'er and sent them to a great sect for training! But no, you insisted on keeping them in the clan! Now look—an accident has happened!"

In the Jiang Clan’s main hall, a middle-aged man was berating a crying woman.

“How was I supposed to know this would happen? I just wanted them to stay home longer to develop a sense of belonging to the family!”

“If we sent them to a sect so young, they’d take another master as their closest kin. By then, would they even recognize the Jiang Clan? Would they still remember their parents? They’d only see their sect and their master, not their own father and family.”

“Besides, you agreed to this at the time. Now it’s suddenly all my fault?”

“There’s no point arguing now. I just hope they ran off to play and lost track of time. But if they’ve been targeted by the Yin Corpse Sect… The family has been searching for so long. Where could they have gone?”

"Patriarch! Patriarch! Young Master and Young Miss have returned!"

Just as the patriarch and his wife were overcome with anxiety, a guard’s urgent shout came from outside. The two of them rushed out in an instant.

At the same time, Jiang Qi and Jiang Lin stood trembling at the gates of their family estate under Lin Jing’s escort.

They had never expected to return home after being captured by the Yin Corpse Sect.

"Could he really be a good person…?"

"Watch what you say. With his strength, he could wipe out the entire Jiang Clan effortlessly. Do not say anything unnecessary."

"For the safety of our family, we must keep quiet."

"Qi'er! Lin'er!!!"

Just as the twins were struggling with their thoughts, their parents arrived in a flash. Their mother and father anxiously examined them.

Both were at the late Foundation Establishment stage, which wasn’t exactly weak. Lin Jing withdrew his gaze.

"...Mother..." The twins' eyes welled with tears.

At this moment, the Jiang couple finally turned their attention to Lin Jing. But before they could speak, Lin Jing said:

"They were captured by the Yin Corpse Sect. By chance, I rescued them. I brought them back today so they could reassure you of their safety. After this, I will be taking them with me to train outside."

"What?!"

Upon hearing that their children had indeed been taken by the Yin Corpse Sect, the couple was shocked.

However, Lin Jing looked like a mere Qi Refinement stage young cultivator—how could they fully believe his words?

Jiang Lin quickly spoke up, "Father, Mother, what Senior said is true! He saved my brother and me. We’ve already agreed to follow him for training, so please don’t worry. Senior is incredibly powerful—not even Golden Core cultivators are his match!"

"Gol—"

The Jiang Patriarch was about to say something when Lin Jing suddenly furrowed his brows and looked in a certain direction.

At the same time, deep within the Jiang estate, an elder meditating in seclusion suddenly found his hair covered in frost.

His eyes snapped open in shock.

"Qi Refinement stage? Impossible! I merely used my divine sense to probe him, yet it felt like I had plunged into an abyss of extreme cold—my consciousness was nearly frozen! This person’s strength is definitely no weaker than mine!"

Buzz!

The elder instantly appeared outside.

Seeing Lin Jing, he immediately cupped his fists and said, "Greetings, Fellow Daoist. Many thanks for rescuing my Jiang Clan’s twins. May I ask for your esteemed name?"

Jiang Lin looked at the elder and pondered for a moment.

Since he had no intention of stealing away another family’s disciples and he wasn’t some human trafficker, he figured he might as well leave a name. That way, they would be less worried.

He took out a jade token from the Demon-Slaying Bureau and said, "Demon-Slaying Bureau, Zhu Qing. Besides this… I have another identity."

"Jiang Lin?"

The Jiang Patriarch suddenly froze, then looked at Jiang Lin more carefully.

Her cultivation, her age—could it be...?

With disbelief in his eyes, he asked, "Could it be that you are Rank 87 on the Genius Rankings—Jiang Lin of the Beast Taming Sect?!"

"Oh? He knows you?"

Lin Jing and Jiang Qi both turned toward the patriarch.

"I do! I do! She is at the eleventh stage of Qi Refinement and, along with her contracted beast, has broken through the extreme limits of Qi Refinement! Who wouldn’t recognize her in the cultivation world?"

The Jiang Clan Master was astounded.

Originally, he had doubted that Jiang Lin had truly been saved from the Yin Corpse Sect.

But now that he knew Lin Jing’s identity, he immediately understood—Jiang Lin definitely had the ability to do so.

"In that case, I wish to take Jiang Qi and Jiang Lin with me. Clan Master Jiang, what do you think?"

"This is their fortune!" The Jiang Patriarch immediately agreed.

"I have long wished to find a great sect for them to join. For them to catch Young Master Lin’s attention is a great blessing!"

"Indeed."

Now that they knew Lin Jing’s identity, even the Jiang Clan’s elder had no objections.

Lin Jing gave him a terrifying feeling.

If just one Qi Refinement cultivator could give him such pressure, there must be even stronger protectors behind him.

Following such a genius could only bring benefits to Jiang Clan’s descendants.

The twin siblings were a bit stunned.

They hadn’t expected their parents and ancestor to send them away so quickly.

Lin Jing smiled faintly.

"Since that’s decided, I won’t stay long. I’m in a hurry to leave. Now that the Jiang Clan knows they’re safe, I’ll take them with me."

The Jiang Clan members were surprised by Lin Jing’s urgency.

The Jiang elder gestured to the patriarch, who immediately understood and handed over a storage bag.

"We have yet to properly thank you for saving Jiang Qi and Jiang Lin. Please accept this token of gratitude."

"No need."

Lin Jing grabbed the storage bag and tossed it to Jiang Lin.

"Consider it their training resources."

With that, he glanced at the twin siblings.

These twin vessels were the best gift he could receive...

With two excellent vessels for nurturing his Sub-Gu, Lin Jing was truly ready to return to the academy.

He had been away for so long—his luxurious cave dwelling had been left empty. Who knew how many Spirit Stones had been wasted?

Now, he needed to enter seclusion at the academy and consolidate the gains from this journey.

He still remembered what Mr. Cao had said—two years from now, Tianji Pavilion would invite all the top geniuses to compete in the Genius Rankings Battle .

The rankings would be reshuffled.

To ensure that the Dragonblood Carp received enough Hidden Dragon Fortune to undergo a perfect transformation, Lin Jing had to seize this opportunity and take first place on the Genius Rankings!

After leaving the Jiang Clan, Lin Jing knocked out the twins and prepared to return to the Ancient Domain.

Before departing, he retrieved the latest Genius Rankings and took another look.

Lin Jing gazed at the list.

If he wanted to claim the top spot, these three would be his biggest obstacles.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 91: Advancement
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 91: Advancement

When the Jiang Family twins woke up from their coma, Lin Jing and the others had already arrived at the Ancient Domain.

As soon as they regained consciousness, the twins felt sore all over, as if their bones were about to fall apart.

The Thunder Pine’s flying speed was too fast, and the twins’ cultivation was too low to withstand the pressure, resulting in them being overwhelmed. Knocking them out directly had actually spared them from some of the pain—after all, Senior Divine Pine wasn’t about to concern itself with the comfort of some random passengers.

“Senior, where are we?” The fact that they were still alive after waking up was already a relief for the twins.

“The Ancient Domain, Hidden Dragon City,” Lin Jing replied, gazing at the vast immortal city ahead.

“We’ve actually arrived at the Ancient Domain?” Jiang Lin was utterly astonished.

Just like how many people in Lin Jing’s past life yearned to visit the capital, cultivators from other regions were naturally curious about the central domain of the Ancient Kingdom.

“Hidden Dragon City… could it be the legendary city where the Academy is located?” Jiang Qi asked excitedly.

“Looks like you know quite a bit. That’s right, I’m a student of this Academy,” Lin Jing said. “For the next few years—maybe even ten or several decades—you two will be cultivating here.”

“If you do well in your cultivation, I might even arrange for you to enter the Academy as well!”

Gaining admission to Hidden Dragon Academy only required a recommendation from a Nascent Soul cultivator and passing an assessment. With Lin Jing’s current status, securing a recommendation was no trouble at all.

“We… we could enter Hidden Dragon Academy too?”

At that moment, the Jiang twins suddenly started to believe that Lin Jing might not be a bad person after all.

After all, how could someone with ties to such a sacred place as Hidden Dragon Academy possibly be a demonic cultivator?

Even though Lin Jing practiced blood-path demonic arts, raised Gu worms, and likely intended to use them as hosts for his parasites, perhaps there was some misunderstanding in all of this!

The twins exchanged a glance, feeling that their situation might not be as dire as they had initially thought.

“Here, take these.”

In the next instant, Lin Jing’s hands surged with icy energy, forming two tiny spiders, each no larger than a fingernail.

Swoosh!

Before the Jiang twins could react, the little spiders shot into their mouths and slid down their throats into their bodies.

“Ugh!”

The twins immediately clutched their throats, bending over and retching violently.

“These are the Gu worms that will soon reside inside you. They’re currently in a dormant state and won’t cause you any harm for now,” Lin Jing explained calmly.

“For now, find a place to settle down in Hidden Dragon City. Once I find suitable cultivation techniques for you, I’ll come back.”

“When that time comes, after cultivating the new techniques, you’ll be able to harness the power of the Gu worms. Your strength will undergo a complete transformation.”

Lin Jing’s expression remained composed. These two Ice Soul Lesser Gu were carefully nurtured by the Ice Soul Gu over a long period. They had the potential to ascend to the Gu King bloodline. As long as the twins used them well, they had a real chance of reaching the Nascent Soul stage in the future.

After speaking, he transformed into a streak of light and disappeared, leaving the Jiang twins drenched in cold sweat.

At this point, whether Lin Jing was a good person or not no longer mattered—now that the Gu worms were inside them, they had no choice but to obey.

Hidden Dragon Academy

Before Qing Emperor returned, Lin Jing immediately retreated to his own cave dwelling.

Returning to his alchemy site and his personal vegetable garden, both the Dragonblood Carp and Pine Leaf Mouse felt an overwhelming sense of nostalgia.

By now, the Dragonblood Carp had fully grasped the advantages of mastering a skill. If it hadn’t learned alchemy, there was no way it could have defeated the Ice Soul Gu, which was an entire minor realm above it.

Now that the Ice Soul Gu had reached the Foundation Establishment stage, the Dragonblood Carp was eager to establish its own Foundation and complete its dragon transformation. It also wanted to refine even stronger pills—ones it could use against the Ice Soul Gu in the future.

“Senior, could I trouble you to help mature the Nine-Sun Divine Lingzhi, the Nine-Life Exquisite Grass, and the Cloud-Hidden Orchid?” Lin Jing asked within his cave.

These three supreme-grade spiritual plant seeds were obtained from the Beast Taming Sect.

Among the seven spiritual seeds, the Divine Pine had taken four, while Lin Jing kept three. The Divine Pine had already matured its four seeds and refined them into Avatars to deal with the Void Mystical Ancestor of the Yin Corpse Sect.

Lin Jing’s three seeds, however, remained in their undeveloped form. While the Pine Leaf Mouse could mature spiritual plants, its current Foundation Establishment stage cultivation meant it would take an incredibly long time to do so.

“I carried you on my back, protected you, exhausted my strength maturing four spiritual seeds, and even used them to forge Avatars to fight your enemies. Do you take me for a workhorse?” the Divine Pine grumbled.

Lin Jing forced a smile. He knew Senior Divine Pine had helped him immensely this time. Originally, those four supreme-grade spiritual seeds were meant to be refined into top-tier Avatars, but instead, they had been sacrificed in battle.

And in the end… it was Lin Jing who reaped the rewards from the Demon-Slaying Bureau.

“Then please rest, Senior. These three seeds aren’t urgent—I’ll have the Pine Leaf Mouse handle them,” Lin Jing decided. He resolved not to rely too much on the Divine Pine from now on.

“That’s the right mindset,” the Divine Pine remarked. “Maturing spiritual plants is also a form of training. Let it handle it.”

“When it was still at the Qi Refinement stage, it had already reached minor success in the Everlasting Emperor’s Longevity Art, allowing it to surpass ordinary Foundation Establishment cultivators in wood-element mastery. Now that it has reached Foundation Establishment, a minor success in the Longevity Art is no longer sufficient. You should find a way to help it achieve major success.”

“With major success, combined with its bloodline, it will possess the wood-element mastery of a Core Formation spirit creature. Whether in combat or in maturing spiritual plants, it will be invaluable.”

“I understand,” Lin Jing nodded. Improving the Everlasting Emperor’s Longevity Art was actually quite simple—it just required absorbing the life essence of plants.

However, with its current cultivation, ordinary plants—even first or second-rank spiritual plants—were no longer sufficient to help the Pine Leaf Mouse progress.

Previously, they had used a combination of the second-rank Malignant Plant, the Bloodsucking Vine, along with Longevity Blood to rapidly cultivate a large number of high-quality Malignant Plants, which allowed the Pine Leaf Mouse’s Longevity Art to reach minor success.

Now, if they wanted to push it to major success, relying on the Bloodsucking Vine alone would no longer be enough.

They needed a vast quantity of higher-grade spiritual plants and Malignant Plants—quantity first, then quality!

Lin Jing sneaked a glance at Senior Divine Pine. Of course, the fastest method would be to have the Pine Leaf Mouse absorb Divine Pine itself… but he wouldn’t dare suggest that.

“Two years… I wonder if that’s enough to push the Pine Leaf Mouse’s Longevity Art to major success.”

“The top contenders on the Genius Rankings are all capable of slaying Core Formation cultivators—I can’t afford to be careless. I must ensure the Longevity Art reaches major success!”

“It’s also time for the ‘Dragon Palace Organization’ to give back.”

Instead of outright purchasing large quantities of high-grade spiritual plants, Lin Jing had a better method—his own Dragon Palace Organization!

The members of the Dragon Palace Organization came from various cultivation sects and families, each with their own supply of spiritual resources. Having them procure the plants would save him a tremendous amount of effort.

In return, they needed a large supply of Rising Dragon Pills to nurture their dragon pets. Lin Jing could simply use those pills as a form of trade.

With this monopolized barter system, he could acquire what he needed at a much better rate than buying everything himself.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 92: Decision
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 92: Decision

That very day, Lin Jing sent a message to Xu Zhizhi, summoning the Dragon Palace’s strategist.

“You’re finally back,” Xu Zhizhi remarked. “You missed a class!”

“No big deal,” Lin Jing replied. The gains from this trip were far more valuable than attending a couple of Academy lectures.

News from the Great Wasteland didn’t spread easily to the Ancient Domain, so Xu Zhizhi simply assumed Lin Jing had gone back to visit his hometown. He had no idea that Lin Jing had become the Holy Son of the Beast Taming Sect, secured numerous resources, and, more importantly, ventured out with the sole purpose of helping the Ice Soul Gu reach Foundation Establishment.

After this trip, Lin Jing’s strength had reached an entirely new level. Before the Ice Soul Gu had established its Foundation, he and the Pine Leaf Mouse were already formidable opponents for anyone below the Core Formation stage. But now, even an ordinary Core Formation cultivator wouldn’t be too difficult to handle.

“Nothing major happened while I was away, right?” Lin Jing asked. It would be absurd if the Dragon Palace collapsed the moment he left, following in the footsteps of the Beast Taming Sect’s downfall.

“No issues at all. Ever since you ranked on the Genius Rankings, more and more people have been joining the Dragon Palace, and no one dares to cause us trouble,” Xu Zhizhi said with a grin, fanning himself. “The freshman admissions are coming up soon—I’m planning to make a grand entrance!”

“Good.” Lin Jing nodded and continued, “Xu, I need you to handle something for me. I require a large amount of high-grade wood-element materials—third rank or higher, with strong life energy. Announce to the Dragon Palace members that they can exchange these materials for Rising Dragon Pills.”

“As for the exchange rates, we’ll determine them based on the quality of the spiritual plants and cultivation techniques,” Lin Jing concluded.

The Rising Dragon Pills—created through years of synthesis and mutation of spirit plants by Lin Jing, the Dragonblood Carp, and the Pine Leaf Mouse—had become his most valuable trading currency.

Xu Zhizhi pondered for a moment before replying, “Getting the spiritual plants won’t be a problem, but high-level cultivation techniques… Cultivation techniques capable of supporting cultivation to the Nascent Soul stage are important inheritances for sects. I doubt many students here have the authority to trade them.”

Lin Jing nodded in understanding. A Nascent Soul-level cultivation technique was indeed quite valuable. Even the Everlasting Emperor’s Longevity Art that Divine Pine had imparted to the Pine Leaf Mouse only reached the Nascent Soul level when cultivated to perfection.

Of course, that wasn’t due to a lack of potential in the Everlasting Emperor’s Longevity Art—it was simply a technique designed specifically for plant-type creatures, not for beasts. The version Divine Pine had given to the Pine Leaf Mouse was an adapted version, allowing it to cultivate but at a cost—the technique’s upper limit was significantly lowered.

“Still, it’s worth a try. Some sects have deep foundations and may have accumulated various techniques beyond their own lineage,” Lin Jing said with a smirk. “I’m sure some people would rather put their ‘useless’ techniques to good use. After all, a Nascent Soul-level technique alone doesn’t guarantee a Nascent Soul breakthrough—but a king-tier bloodline dragon pet has a very high chance of evolving into a powerful Demonic General.”

Soon after, Xu Zhizhi left to distribute the sect-wide mission on Lin Jing’s behalf.

With that settled, Lin Jing set the Dragonblood Carp to work refining Rising Dragon Pills, while the Pine Leaf Mouse focused on gathering special ingredients for the pill refinement process.

For the Dragonblood Carp, the next two years wouldn’t involve any combat training—just pure alchemy.

The more pills it refined, the more spirit plants the Pine Leaf Mouse could obtain, which would, in turn, allow it to advance the Everlasting Emperor’s Longevity Art to greater mastery.

If the Pine Leaf Mouse could achieve this breakthrough, Lin Jing would have a much greater chance of securing first place on the Genius Rankings. That, in turn, would grant him enough Hidden Dragon Fortune to help the Dragonblood Carp perfect its dragon transformation.

If the Dragonblood Carp wanted to catch up to the emperor-tier bloodline of the Ice Soul Gu, then using a vast amount of Hidden Dragon Fortune to trigger its evolution was absolutely crucial.

In the end, everything the Dragonblood Carp was working for… was for its own benefit.

After Lin Jing explained this, the Dragonblood Carp could only lower its head and grind away at refining pills.

As for the Genius Rankings Battle, Lin Jing had no intention of letting the Dragonblood Carp participate.

Even though it had reached the extreme stage of Qi Refinement, it still wasn’t comparable to the true geniuses of the rankings as long as it hadn’t fully transformed into a dragon.

Thus, the true main forces of the Genius Rankings Battle would be Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse.

And if things got too dangerous…

If even Lin Jing himself struggled to handle an opponent, then there was no way he could afford to expose the Ice Soul Gu!

Of course, while revealing the Ice Soul Gu might put Lin Jing in a precarious situation, looking at it from another perspective—the Ice Soul Gu was one of the legendary Ten Rare Gu. Publicly displaying it would bring significant fame to Lin Jing.

Aside from helping the Pine Leaf Mouse evolve, there weren’t many areas where Lin Jing himself actually needed Hidden Dragon Fortune. As someone with the Longevity Body, his cultivation speed was already absurdly fast—he didn’t need to rely on fortune to improve his comprehension.

However, the newly acquired Heavenly Demon Physique technique was an exception.

This technique was incredibly difficult to cultivate. Many experts estimated that fully mastering it at the Nascent Soul stage would require a massive amount of fortune energy.

That meant securing first place on the Genius Rankings was non-negotiable.

Considering all this… Lin Jing found himself somewhat willing to expose the Ice Soul Gu, depending on the circumstances.

Of course, the reason he wasn’t too concerned about potential risks was simple—after the Genius Rankings Battle, he would retreat into seclusion in Hidden Dragon City for decades, making full use of his cave dwelling’s duration.

As long as he stayed within the Hidden Dragon Academy, his safety would be almost guaranteed.

“Besides advancing the Pine Leaf Mouse’s Everlasting Emperor’s Longevity Art, my next priority will be breeding more mosquito beasts.”

Lin Jing took out the Myriad Beast Circle gifted by the Myriad Beast Sect. With two Myriad Beast Circles in his possession now, he could incubate and control even more mosquito beasts.

These mosquito beasts weren’t just food for the Dragonblood Carp—they would also serve as the sacrificial energy sources for his mosquito-powered mecha.

In the coming weeks, Lin Jing planned to further refine his mosquito-powered mechanical constructs, strengthening his arsenal of combat techniques.

While controlling the mosquito swarm directly was already powerful, their individual strength was too dispersed. A more focused method of harnessing their power would allow him to create a flawless combat system.

“However… all of this is just external power.”

“The most important thing is… my own cultivation.”

“I’m currently at the peak of Qi Refinement Twelfth Stage.”

“I’m just one step away from Qi Refinement Thirteenth Stage, a realm comparable to the Ancient Kingdom’s strongest bloodline geniuses.”

“But this final step is proving incredibly difficult.”

“At this point, unless I obtain immortal-grade resources, I won’t be able to advance further in the Qi Refinement stage.”

“I can’t progress through ordinary cultivation anymore.”

“And relying on pet feedback is no longer an option.”

“Since I’m still at Qi Refinement, my contracted beasts—the Pine Leaf Mouse and the Ice Soul Gu—are limited to early Foundation Establishment due to the constraints of the Beast Taming Contract. Unless I break through first, they won’t be able to advance further.”

“So… should I wait for an opportunity to push for Qi Refinement Thirteenth Stage?

Or should I break through to Foundation Establishment immediately?”

“If I break through, I can finally learn the Beast Taming Sect’s true inheritance techniques—my strength will skyrocket, and the Pine Leaf Mouse and Ice Soul Gu will also have a high chance of advancing to mid or late Foundation Establishment. Winning first place on the Genius Rankings would be nearly guaranteed.”

“But if I remain in the Qi Refinement stage, my power will be limited… yet there’s a major advantage.”

“If I manage to take first place while still in Qi Refinement, my fame will skyrocket, granting me even greater fortune.”

Lin Jing fell into deep thought.

Should he challenge the Genius Rankings as a Qi Refinement cultivator, proving himself against all odds?

Or should he establish his Foundation now—and crush his opponents effortlessly?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 93: The Demonic Cultivator Squad
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 93: The Demonic Cultivator Squad

A few days later.

Xu Zhizhi found Lin Jing and brought good news.

"I found the techniques!" he announced excitedly.

"So fast?" Lin Jing stepped out to greet him, a bit surprised. He hadn’t expected it to be this easy. After all, Nascent Soul-level cultivation techniques were often the highlight of auctions in top-tier immortal cities.

"Of course! You can count on me!" Xu Zhizhi sat down at the stone table outside Lin Jing’s cave.

Lin Jing chuckled and immediately poured him a cup of tea.

"This tea… why is it sweet? And cold?" Xu Zhizhi took a sip, momentarily stunned.

"Just tell me if it tastes good."

Lin Jing grinned. "My pet beasts and I brewed it ourselves. We call it 'Iced Red Tea'."

"Alright, let’s talk about the techniques."

Xu Zhizhi wiped his mouth and began,

"I found two techniques, both from the same creator. They are indeed Nascent Soul-level techniques, but they have stricter cultivation requirements compared to standard ones."

"Have you heard of Ice-Fire Island ?"

Lin Jing shook his head. "Never heard of it. A cultivation faction?"

"Exactly. The reason it's so obscure is because it no longer exists," Xu Zhizhi explained. "Thousands of years ago, two Nascent Soul-stage Demon Emperors battled—one with a fire bloodline, the other with an ice bloodline. Their clash triggered terrifying natural forces, transforming an island into a unique ice-and-fire landscape."

"This became known as Ice-Fire Island. Later, a group of cultivators specializing in fire and ice techniques settled there, using its natural energy for cultivation. Over time, they formed a faction called Ice-Fire Island ."

"However, as the island’s natural energy gradually faded, the faction lost its cultivation grounds and eventually disappeared."

"In our Dragon Palace, there’s someone whose ancestor was a Nascent Soul cultivator from Ice-Fire Island. Because of that, he has preserved cultivation techniques for both ice and fire attributes. However, these techniques were specifically developed based on Ice-Fire Island’s unique environment. After reaching the Golden Core stage, further cultivation becomes highly dependent on an extreme heat-and-cold environment. Without such conditions, these techniques are no different from ordinary Golden Core-level techniques."

"Since Ice-Fire Island’s legacy is gone, he’s willing to trade these techniques for a Rising Dragon Pill . He also said that although the techniques have limitations, he can include a few matching minor techniques as a bonus."

"I see. A technique restricted by cultivation grounds..." Lin Jing pondered for a moment. It wasn’t a huge issue. By the time the twins reached the Golden Core stage, it would already be decades later. No point worrying about it now.

As for what to do when they reached Golden Core?

With their cultivation and the power of the Ice Soul Sub-Gu , they could either steal higher-grade techniques or seize territories rich in extreme ice and fire energy.

Solutions always outnumbered problems.

When the time came, they would just have to rely on their own wisdom!

"This will do," Lin Jing nodded. In the short term, only extinct factions like Ice-Fire Island would be willing to trade high-level techniques. Disciples from established sects wouldn't have the authority to do so.

He also didn’t want to waste too much time searching.

The Jiang twins were at the perfect age for cultivation—wasting years just to find techniques would be a shame.

Besides, even if they found better techniques later, they could always undergo another transition. While switching techniques would cost time and effort, as long as their foundation remained solid, it wouldn’t be a big issue.

"Is there anything else?" Lin Jing asked.

Xu Zhizhi handed him a list of spirit plants.

In Hidden Dragon City

The Jiang twins had already settled in an inn.

Compared to the Air Domain, Hidden Dragon City was a well-known hub for cultivators. From Lin Jing’s arrival to his rise on the Genius Rankings , his name was widely discussed.

After hearing about Lin Jing’s journey, the twins became even more confused.

Because in all the rumors about him, there was no mention of blood magic or Gu techniques .

"Senior Lin… hides his true nature so well," Jiang Qi muttered.

"Don’t think too much about it. No matter what, Senior Lin saved us. Otherwise, we’d already be corpses," Jiang Lin said, looking at her brother.

"Helping Senior Lin raise Gu insects… isn’t a big deal. I don’t feel any malice from him." She continued, "Besides, didn’t Grandmother always teach us to repay kindness?"

Jiang Qi nodded in agreement.

But just as he was about to speak—

Crash!

The wooden door was kicked open!

The twins tensed, sensing a powerful aura.

"Demon-Slaying Bureau business!"

A richly dressed young man stood arrogantly in the doorway.

"Two Qi Refinement Gu Masters dare to set foot in Hidden Dragon City? Even though your Gu insects are well hidden, you were unlucky to run into me—the bane of all demons!"

Gao Sheng held up a Demon-Seeking Compass .

"Surrender immediately!"

The twins’ expressions changed.

Just then, a red leaf drifted in from the window.

Everyone froze.

A spatial ripple flickered—

Lin Jing’s figure materialized.

The sight of Lin Jing made the twins breathe a sigh of relief.

But Gao Sheng’s face twisted in shock.

"Why are you here?" Lin Jing asked, unimpressed.

Gao Sheng held up his compass. "I’m hunting demons! They—"

The Demon-Seeking Compass was an extremely rare magical treasure crafted by the Demon-Slaying Bureau.

It could detect Gu insects, corpse puppets, and undead creatures—something even most Golden Core-stage demon slayers couldn't obtain.

"They’re with me. The Gu in their bodies were placed there by me. You’ve got the wrong people," Lin Jing said, surprised.

He was certain that the Sub-Gu was well-hidden within the twins.

Yet, Gao Sheng had still managed to detect them?

"Ugh, seriously?" Gao Sheng looked disappointed. He had thought he was about to crack a big case in Hidden Dragon City.

"But since we’re all here, let’s make use of them. Have them assist you on your next mission."

"...Huh?" Gao Sheng blinked in confusion.

Lin Jing tossed two jade slips to the twins.

"Senior…?" The Jiang twins were still processing everything.

" Divine Fire Transformation and Royal Extreme Ice Whale Technique ," Lin Jing said.

"The first is a fire-attribute technique, the second is an ice-attribute technique. These were created by the cultivators of the now-extinct Ice-Fire Island, based on the battle traces left by two Nascent Soul-stage Demon Emperors—the Nine-Tailed Divine Fire Fox and the Ice Whale Emperor ."

"Once you cultivate these techniques and combine them with your Twin Physique, you’ll be able to fully control the Gu within you."

"Nascent Soul Demon Emperors?!"

The Jiang twins stared in awe at the jade slips.

"Ice and fire techniques… Twin Physique… Gu insects… So that’s why you posted a request in Dragon Palace! It was for them! I see now—Gu vessels!"

Gao Sheng might not have been a detective, but as someone from a prestigious background, he quickly pieced it all together.

"Jiang Qi, Jiang Lin—this is Gao Sheng, a genius from the Demon-Slaying Bureau’s prestigious Gao Clan."

"Once you’ve switched to these new techniques, you’ll join the Bureau under Gao Sheng. You’ll be his teammates and subordinates, solving cases together. With his endorsement, you’ll be able to use Gu techniques freely."

"Wait, hold on—" Gao Sheng was stunned.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 94: The Starry Cosmos Turtle
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 94: The Starry Cosmos Turtle

The Jiang twins never expected that Lin Jing would have them join the Demon-Slaying Bureau as Gu Masters!

And Gao Sheng never expected that instead of teaming up with him, Lin Jing would toss him two Gu cultivator rookies.

He wasn’t a babysitter!

"Brother Gao, their situation is rather special," Lin Jing transmitted his voice directly to Gao Sheng and briefly explained what had happened in the Air Domain .

Hearing this, Gao Sheng’s face darkened, and rage flared in his heart.

Although he had been a playboy in his youth, he took great pride in being from a family of demon slayers and considered the Demon-Slaying Bureau his home.

Now that he knew the Bureau had such corrupt elements within it, he was furious.

Forcing a smile, he looked at the Jiang twins and said, "I never imagined you two went through such an ordeal. On behalf of the Demon-Slaying Bureau , I apologize. The fact that such scum exist within the Bureau is a disgrace to my grandfather’s leadership. When I return, I will make sure to have a word with him!"

Lin Jing nearly choked on his own breath.

"Anyway, don’t worry! I promise you, most of us in the Bureau are dedicated to eradicating evil. From now on, follow my lead! Whether it's corrupt officials within the Bureau or demonic sects outside, we will cleanse them all together!"

Gao Sheng put his arms around the twins, as if afraid they’d misunderstand the Bureau.

Lin Jing smirked slightly.

Though Gao Sheng was unreliable in many ways, his background ensured that the Jiang twins would be well taken care of.

Being by his side would provide both protection and legitimacy for their identities as Gu cultivators.

And knowing Gao Sheng’s enthusiastic nature, he certainly wouldn’t mistreat them.

"We’ll do as you say, Senior," the twins responded, still unsure of the situation but completely trusting Lin Jing’s arrangements.

After handing them the cultivation techniques, Lin Jing and Gao Sheng left.

Once they were away, Gao Sheng returned to his "serious" demeanor and complained,

"Brother Lin, you tricked me! You said you were just visiting the Great Wasteland ."

"Not to mention running into the Nascent Soul Elder of the Yin Corpse Sect …."

"And I’ve never even met my grandfather’s sworn brother, but you actually met him!"

"Oh, right, what rank of Demon-Slayer did he give you?"

Lin Jing took out his Demon-Slaying Bureau medallion.

Six demonic heads were engraved on it.

It was an eerie design—almost resembling a demonic sect’s relic rather than an official Bureau token.

"Sixth Rank."

Gao Sheng’s eyes went green with envy.

A Sixth-Rank Demon-Slayer !

Most Demon-Slaying Bureau branch leaders at the Golden Core Stage were only at the Seventh Rank.

With this medallion, Lin Jing could directly command certain lower-ranked squads in the Bureau.

Gao Sheng also had similar authority, but only because of his grandfather—he hadn’t actually earned it himself.

"It’s a pity that the Bureau’s ranking system isn’t connected to the Imperial Cultivation Official System . Otherwise, you could use this rank to train in the Hidden Dragon Secret Arts ," Lin Jing remarked, putting the medallion away.

"Even so, it’s already impressive!"

"Next time you visit your family, make sure to take me along. From now on, the Beast Taming Sect is my home too!"

"Damn, missing out on meeting the Nascent Soul Demon Cultivator still stings!" Gao Sheng sighed.

Lin Jing rolled his eyes. "You wanted to meet an old monster from the Yin Corpse Sect ?"

"If it weren’t for that Tianji Pavilion disciple warning me, and if Divine Pine’s descendants hadn’t prepared for the encounter, we’d all be dead."

"Speaking of which, Bai Yuran is pretty impressive. Did she go back to Tianji Pavilion ?"

"If she wants to follow you, just let her. A squad with a Tianji Pavilion disciple, you, and me? We’d be demon-hunting legends !" Gao Sheng practically drooled at the thought.

Lin Jing remained silent for a moment before saying, "We’ll see. The Genius Rankings Battle is in two years—we’ll probably meet again then."

"In that case, I’ll join you!" Gao Sheng declared.

"Do as you like. But the twins are your responsibility now. Once they reach Foundation Establishment, their combat strength—enhanced by the Ice Soul Sub-Gu —will be formidable. You won’t find better squadmates elsewhere."

"Of course, no problem! But taking two Gu cultivators into a demon-hunting squad still feels a bit off ."

"What’s so strange about it? Honestly, the Beast Taming Sect was unjustly suppressed," Lin Jing said, his tone indifferent.

"Think about it— Demonic Fortune outweighing Human Fortune is such a big deal? Demon-Slaying Bureau high-rankers have all practiced demonic arts , haven’t they?"

Over the years, the Bureau had destroyed countless demonic sects and confiscated countless dark cultivation techniques.

Some of these, like the Heavenly Demon Physique , were undoubtedly studied and cultivated by Bureau leaders.

As long as the techniques didn’t harm the empire’s fate or break fundamental laws, there was no reason to destroy them outright.

Gao Sheng coughed awkwardly.

"That’s why power is such a great thing! Why do you think my father named me Gao Sheng ?" ( "Soaring High" )

"And why do you think my father chased after my mother for 520 years, married into her family, and even changed his surname?"

With the twins settled, Lin Jing focused on his closed-door training.

He had decided to participate in the upcoming Genius Rankings Battle while still at the Qi Refinement Stage.

If, after the battle, he still hadn’t found an opportunity to break into the Thirteenth Level of Qi Refinement, he would simply establish his Foundation at the Twelfth Level instead.

During this time, although he couldn’t train in the Beast Taming Sect’s heritage techniques, he focused on perfecting:

As for the Heavenly Demon Physique , he would continue cultivating it, but he wasn’t confident about mastering it within two years.

As the seasons passed, Dragon Palace thrived.

Thanks to its influence, a steady flow of rare spiritual plants was being sent to Pine Leaf Mouse .

At the same time, Rising Dragon Pills were constantly being distributed among Dragon Palace geniuses.

Pine Leaf Mouse’s Everlasting Emperor's Longevity Art improved rapidly.

Meanwhile, Dragon Palace members who were raising dragon pets also advanced at an astonishing rate.

Some were even willing to sacrifice their Hidden Dragon Fortune and pour resources into their dragon pets—resulting in bloodline enhancements that neared King-tier.

At some point, Dragon Palace had become a major power within Hidden Dragon Academy .

With Xu Zhizhi’s efforts, many new students eagerly joined the faction, all wanting their own dragon pets.

Lin Jing, the faction’s founder, hadn’t even needed to show up in person.

The bigger Dragon Palace grew, the more geniuses it attracted.

And the more resources it acquired, the faster Lin Jing advanced.

Menial tasks?

He just delegated them to Dragon Palace members through "sect missions."

Finally.

As Lin Jing approached his thirtieth birthday, Tianji Pavilion sent word.

He had received an invitation.

These invitations weren’t just sent to geniuses already ranked on the Genius Rankings .

They were also sent to those whom Tianji Pavilion deemed worthy —talents who had never sought fame but possessed immense potential.

Some of these cultivators had been in seclusion for years or even decades.

Many weren’t interested in reputation.

But even so, Tianji Pavilion had prepared massive incentives to lure them out.

They were determined to make the next Genius Rankings Battle a spectacle.

"First place on the Genius Rankings will receive a blessing from Tianji Pavilion’s sacred beast—the Starry Cosmos Turtle?"

"The Starry Cosmos Turtle… a legendary beast with awakened Cosmos Dao abilities… one of Tianji Pavilion’s strongest hidden assets!"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 95: Guidance
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 95: Guidance

“As expected, not rushing into Foundation Establishment was the right choice.”

Lin Jing held the invitation in his hand. Although he wasn’t sure what effect the blessing of the Starry Cosmos Turtle had, as a rare beast that inherited the divine abilities of immortals and cultivated the Dao of Time, it was definitely not an ordinary opportunity.

Judging by its divine abilities, the Starry Cosmos Turtle was undoubtedly even rarer than the Pine Leaf Mouse.

In fact, since it was considered one of Tianji Pavilion’s strongest hidden assets, this Starry Cosmos Turtle had to be one of the most absurdly powerful rare beasts in the entire Tianyuan Continent.

Perhaps casting a blessing was an easy task for the turtle, but for the cultivator receiving the blessing, it was an extraordinary fortune.

“A blessing related to the Dao of Thunder, huh…”

“It doesn’t seem to have much to do with my Extreme Stage of Qi Refinement…”

“But since Tianji Pavilion didn’t explicitly state what the blessing does, it might vary from person to person. I’ll just hold onto some expectations for now.”

But now, things were different. That person had to fiercely defend their position, or else they’d get pushed down in the new rankings and miss out on the rare beast’s blessing!

“Unfortunately, after two years, despite the Pine Leaf Mouse constantly consuming high-grade herbal essence, the Everlasting Emperor's Longevity Art still hasn’t reached perfection.”

Lin Jing felt somewhat regretful about this.

A cultivation technique that extends lifespan was, after all, not so easy to cultivate...

Only Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse knew just how many herbal resources had been consumed during this time.

“It seems that relying solely on the spirit plants provided by the sects and families backing the Dragon Palace geniuses is still not enough…”

“I should take this opportunity to open up markets beyond the Dragon Palace.”

He put away the invitation and changed into a blue-white robe, tying up his long hair.

“This time, I’m heading to the Mystic Domain, where Tianji Pavilion is located. Are you staying behind to guard the place?” Lin Jing looked toward the depths of the cave mansion, where the Dragonblood Carp was refining pills.

Bang!

The Dragonblood Carp flipped over the pill furnace and leapt up!

It was not staying behind!

Lin Jing rubbed his forehead and sighed. “Fine… then I’ll have Senior Pine watch over you again.”

The Dragonblood Carp’s mouth twitched.

Senior Pine, huh… oh well.

As long as it wasn’t left behind, it didn’t matter who it followed.

Besides, its relationship with Senior Pine wasn’t bad these days.

All three of Lin Jing’s contracted beasts maintained good relationships with Senior Pine.

The Pine Leaf Mouse, as Senior Pine’s successor, naturally had an inseparable bond.

The Ice Soul Gu, constantly seeking new human pets, shared Senior Pine’s philosophy.

Unfortunately, finding a Twin Physique cultivator—someone perfectly suited to becoming an Ice Soul Lesser Gu vessel—was extremely difficult.

As for the Dragonblood Carp, it was no longer the reckless creature that used to set everything on fire and get beaten up every day.

To curry favor with Senior Pine, the Dragonblood Carp had even developed a "Demonic Smoke Pill" by refining Ghostfire with tobacco. While it had no nutritional value, its unique demonic aura suited Senior Pine’s tastes perfectly, making it a proper tribute.

“You’re still at the Qi Refinement Stage?”

At the Dragon Palace Assembly Hall, Wang Xiao, clad in battle armor, stood tall.

After the Autumn Hunt, despite Xu Zhizhi’s repeated invitations, he had ultimately chosen to join Dragon Palace, becoming its second strongest member.

Now, seeing that Lin Jing was still in the Qi Refinement Stage after five or six years, Wang Xiao fell silent.

He had already reached the peak of Foundation Establishment!

At this rate, he suspected that by the time he reached Core Formation, Lin Jing would still be in the Qi Refinement Stage!

“I have a mortal physique—unlike you guys with innate divine physiques.” Lin Jing stepped into the hall and asked, “Are you the only one who arrived?”

“Mortal physique? Heh… I don’t know why you keep hiding your special physique, but you are definitely not a mortal.” Wang Xiao snorted.

Just then, the other Dragon Palace members entered the hall one after another.

Yuan Jing, Ziling'er, Xu Zhizhi, Gao Sheng, Zhou Lang from the Divine Formation Sect, and Song Youqing, a descendant of Ice-Fire Island…

They, too, had received invitations from Tianji Pavilion.

Although only Lin Jing and Wang Xiao had officially made it onto the Genius Rankings, that didn’t mean the others lacked the strength to enter now.

The veteran members of Dragon Palace, such as Xu Zhizhi, had only grown stronger with age, hoping to make a spectacular entrance in this year’s Genius Rankings Battle.

After all, they had been chosen by Cavern Heaven—once their cultivation advanced, it was only a matter of time before they broke into the rankings.

For example, Zhou Lang, the formation prodigy who led Dragon Palace to defeat the Demonic Core Stage Monkey King during the Autumn Hunt, and Song Youqing, who had exchanged many Ascending Dragon Pills with Lin Jing for Nascent Soul techniques, had both grown formidable alongside their Dragon Pets.

Since Tianji Pavilion could observe the fortune of all the world’s geniuses, being invited meant they were acknowledged as powerful cultivators.

“The Academy is fully cooperating with Tianji Pavilion for this Genius Rankings Battle. A spirit-powered flying ship has been prepared for students heading to the Mystic Domain, and it will take about a month to travel from Hidden Dragon City to the competition venue.”

Lin Jing scanned the gathered group and continued, “I gathered you all here for one reason…”

“I hope that in the Genius Rankings Battle, you all give your contracted beasts as much time in the spotlight as possible. Against weaker opponents, don’t fight personally—let your beasts handle it.”

“Why?” Wang Xiao asked.

Xu Zhizhi glanced at him and smirked. “Isn’t it obvious?”

Lin Jing waved a hand. “It’s simple. You all know what kind of state the Beast Taming Sect is in right now.”

“Demonic Fortune outweighs Human Fortune!”

“There are only two ways to change this.”

“One is for a human cultivator to reach the Nascent Soul Stage.”

“The other is for the sect to be brimming with talented human cultivators, whose potential outweighs the declining demonic fortune.”

“This Genius Rankings Battle is a perfect opportunity, and it will attract immense attention.”

“Now that I have become the Holy Son of the Beast Taming Sect, I naturally want to do something for the sect.”

“If we can showcase the power of Beast Taming in this battle, even though the sect is currently in seclusion, many cultivation families will still send their talented disciples here. Even rogue cultivators tired of a wandering life might choose to join the sect as guest elders for a chance at a better future.”

This was exactly what the Demon Kings had urged him to do before he left—spread the sect’s influence so that more people would join.

None of the Dragon Palace members objected to Lin Jing’s request. It wasn’t a big deal.

“Too weak an opponent isn’t worth my effort anyway,” Wang Xiao said indifferently. A year ago, he had already reached the top fifty of the Genius Rankings at late Foundation Establishment.

Now, at peak Foundation Establishment, he was aiming for top ten.

“Then I’ll leave it to you all,” Lin Jing chuckled.

After all, he had stockpiled plenty of Ascending Dragon Pills…

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 96: A Descent into Madness
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 96: A Descent into Madness

The time had come for the academy’s students to depart for the Mystic Domain.

A massive spirit-powered flying ship set sail from the academy.

Shortly after takeoff, the Dragon Palace members arrived with their Dragon Pets to visit Lin Jing.

Yuan Jing’s Giant Saber Mantis, Ziling'er’s Pill Rabbit, Gao Sheng’s Dragon Horse, Wang Xiao’s Battle Tiger, Xu Zhizhi’s Bald Lynx, Zhou Lang’s counterfeit Four Sacred Beasts, and the descendant of Ice-Fire Island’s Flood Dragon…

Among them, Wang Xiao’s Battle Tiger, Gao Sheng’s Dragon Horse, and the Flood Dragon had already reached King-tier Bloodlines.

The first two had naturally strong bloodlines due to their wealthy backgrounds.

As for the Flood Dragon, it was a case of a severed lineage desperately seeking a new path. After trading two cultivation manuals and numerous high-grade spirit herbs with Lin Jing for a ton of Ascending Dragon Pills, it had chosen to cultivate Beast Taming Arts to refine its strength.

Of course, the others weren’t weak either.

However, breaking through this bottleneck would require more resources than all their previous efforts combined.

Now, everyone was here out of curiosity—curious about the mysterious pill Lin Jing had mentioned for unlocking their beasts’ latent bloodline potential.

“Ascending Dragon Pills enhance the bloodline quality of dragon-blooded beasts,” Lin Jing explained.

“However, improving their bloodline doesn’t necessarily mean they can fully unleash 100% of their inherited power.”

“This pill was refined alongside the Ascending Dragon Pills, specifically to help high-bloodline beasts excavate their latent bloodline power, significantly boosting their strength. But due to the extreme burden it places on the body, I’ve never given it to you until now.”

Lin Jing pulled out a black pill bottle.

The moment he opened it, everyone felt nothing unusual—except Ziling'er and her Pill Rabbit, whose pupils instantly contracted as they caught a terrifying scent.

“This is a Demon Pill!!” Ziling'er gasped.

“Demon Pill?” The descendant of Ice-Fire Island frowned. “What’s that? Never heard of it.”

Ziling'er’s face turned serious.

“Demon Pills… are forbidden pills that, upon consumption, have an extremely high chance of killing the user, causing severe illness, or inducing addiction. Even the weakest Demon Pill can overload a high-level cultivator’s body. Normally, only demonic cultivators who constantly walk the edge of life and death dare to use such extreme pills.”

The crowd turned to Lin Jing.

What the hell was this guy trying to do?!

Lin Jing smiled.

“It’s really not as bad as it sounds. Both the ingredients and the final pill itself have been consumed by my own beasts for a long time. At this point, they’re immune to it—it doesn’t even have much of an effect anymore.”

Saying this, he casually tossed the Pain Pill, a concoction refined from Black Death Pepper and the blood of violent spirit beasts, to the side.

A moment later, the Dragonblood Carp leaped forward, gulping down several black pills in one bite.

Under Ziling'er and her Pill Rabbit’s horrified gazes, the Dragonblood Carp shuddered for a brief moment—

Then let out a satisfied burp.

Ziling'er was dumbfounded.

“What kind of joke is this?! Even a Foundation Establishment-stage beast would die from the pain of that pill!!”

Hearing this, the Dragon Pets of the Dragon Palace instinctively took a step forward.

But after seeing that Wang Xiao’s Battle Tiger was fine, they hesitated and stepped back.

Lin Jing chuckled.

“This pill is called Pain Pill. The moment you take it, you experience an agonizing pain that reaches the soul. However, if you can endure the pain, your bloodline potential will be forcibly excavated and activated.

“In theory, this pill was made to complement the Ascending Dragon Pill.”

Gao Sheng swallowed hard.

“…You weren’t lying… but that Dragonblood Carp is not a normal case.”

Zhou Lang, using his Heavenly God Physique, sensed the ominous energy radiating from the Demon Pill, took a deep breath, and turned toward Wang Xiao, who looked completely unfazed.

Suddenly, everything clicked.

This madman had been eating Demon Pills all along to unlock his bloodline potential!!!

“I suddenly feel… unwell. Boss, maybe we should skip the Genius Rankings Battle…” Gao Sheng’s Dragon Horse took another step back.

The others might not know better, but it did—Lin Jing was a real demonic cultivator, so those Demon Pills were definitely not fake.

Was winning the Genius Rankings Battle really worth risking their lives?!

Nope. No way. The Dragon Horse wanted out.

“Shut up and take it! If you’re too afraid to eat a mere Demon Pill, how will you ever slay demons in the future?” Gao Sheng dragged it back. After all, he wasn’t the one eating it.

Meanwhile, under Ziling'er’s panicked expression, the other Dragonblood Beasts also began trembling.

“You don’t need to worry,” Lin Jing reassured them. “I’m not sending you to your deaths.”

“Endure the greatest suffering, and you shall become the greatest beast.”

“If the pain gets too unbearable, I’ll immediately apply an ice compress to numb it—don’t worry.”

Lin Jing raised a hand, releasing a frightening cold aura.

Even Wang Xiao, who had an Innate Ice Physique, shuddered.

This…

This was definitely not a normal ice attribute physique.

Ziling'er hesitated.

“Actually… with this level of ice numbing, it could effectively counteract the pain caused by the pill…”

Beside her, the Pill Rabbit stared at its master with wide eyes.

Wait. You’re actually considering eating this?

As a pill refining expert, didn’t she understand how terrifying Demon Pills were?!

It looked at Lin Jing, horrified.

It had been a test subject for years, but even it had never eaten a Demon Pill before!

“Well? What’s your decision?” Lin Jing smiled.

“These pills are free—a token of appreciation for your cooperation.”

Free?

The Dragon Palace members exchanged glances.

If it wouldn’t kill their pets, then why not take the chance to power up?

“…Looks like we’re all in.”

Lin Jing grinned and turned to Zhou Lang.

“Brother Zhou, could you set up a soundproofing formation outside my room?”

“Soundproofing? No problem.”

Zhou Lang nodded—he had the weakest beast bloodlines in the group, so he couldn’t afford to miss out on this opportunity.

“Oh, by the way…” Lin Jing suddenly added, “Humans can take the Pain Pill too. The effect isn’t as dramatic as with beasts, but… it still works.”

He pulled out several more bottles.

“I recommend taking them together—experiencing the same pain will strengthen your bond with your Dragon Pets.”

He chuckled.

The Dragon Palace members froze.

Their Dragon Pets slowly turned to look at them.

Their expressions darkened.

One Month Later

Mystic Domain, Tianji City

After enduring a month of soul-burning agony, the academy’s students finally arrived in Tianji City.

Under Lin Jing’s persuasion, they had ultimately chosen to share the pain with their Dragon Pets.

By now, the Dragon Palace members had developed complete pain resistance.

No matter how badly they got injured in battle, they would feel nothing.

Even their souls had gone numb.

“You have some seriously twisted tastes.”

“You barely took any yourself, yet you made them eat so much.”

As they landed in Tianji City, Senior Pine couldn’t hold back any longer.

Lin Jing just laughed.

“I didn’t need to take them—I had my Dragonblood Carp eat them for me. Thanks to our Beast Taming Secret Art, all the pain gets transferred to it instead.”

“Besides… daily bloodletting also hurts, you know.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 97: The Power of the Future
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 97: The Power of the Future

“Tianji Pavilion has arranged accommodations for all of you. There’s still time before the Genius Rankings Battle begins, so make sure to get some proper rest.”

Mr. Cao didn’t press for details.

The demonic aura surrounding the group was slowly dissipating, and frankly, it was best not to pry into other people’s affairs.

Upon reaching Tianji City, Lin Jing left the exhausted students behind and headed straight for Tianji Pavilion.

With the upcoming Genius Rankings Battle, Tianji Pavilion was bustling with visitors. Cultivators from all over crowded the entrance, seeking divine guidance, while dozens of guards maintained strict security over the pavilion.

“Please! Just let one of the Pavilion Masters read my fortune! My Dao companion has been missing for three years—I need to know where she is!”

A disheveled Foundation Establishment cultivator pleaded desperately at the gates.

“Move along,” a guard barked, shoving him away without hesitation.

“Your wife ran off with another man. Give it up—Tianji Pavilion doesn’t deal with petty issues like this.”

“Your wife ran off! Not mine!”

The desperate cultivator and the nosy passerby immediately started brawling, only to be arrested by the city guards moments later.

Lin Jing shook his head.

The vast majority of visitors to Tianji Pavilion came seeking divination, including Nascent Soul and even Soul Transformation cultivators.

As Lin Jing approached the pavilion’s entrance, a guard stepped forward to block his path.

“I am Lin Jing of the Beast Taming Sect,” he stated. “I am a close friend of Bai Yuran, a 27th-generation disciple of Tianji Pavilion. She invited me here.”

The guard studied Lin Jing for a moment, then seemed to communicate telepathically with someone inside the pavilion.

After a brief pause, he nodded.

“You may enter.”

Lin Jing cupped his fists.

“Thank you.”

Inside, Tianji Pavilion resembled a vast starry sky, with murals of constellations covering its walls.

“Holy Son, it’s been a while.”

A voice, soft yet ethereal.

Lin Jing looked up.

A white-robed maiden, as if descending from the cosmos itself, appeared before him.

Bai Yuran smiled.

“You’re here to accept my proposal, aren’t you?”

Lin Jing met her gaze seriously.

“Actually, I came to thank you for that divination.”

“Without it, I might have—”

“Oh, cut the act.”

Bai Yuran rolled her eyes.

“You obviously came because you need something from me again.”

Lin Jing: “…”

Bai Yuran smirked.

“Let me guess. You want to know what the Starry Cosmos Turtle’s blessing actually does. That’s why you’re here.”

Lin Jing was genuinely surprised.

“As expected from a Tianji Pavilion disciple!” he said in admiration.

“You’re right—I do want to know. But I really did come to thank you as well.”

“…And I brought a gift.”

Bai Yuran chuckled.

“Come with me. This isn’t the place to talk.”

Tianji Pavilion – Cloudwatching Pavilion

A serene balcony high above the clouds.

Lin Jing and Bai Yuran sat facing each other.

Bai Yuran spoke first.

“The Starry Cosmos Turtle’s blessing isn’t just one blessing—you can actually choose from several.”

“As long as it’s related to the Dao of Space-Time, it can bestow almost any type of blessing.”

“It’s also quite adept at blessings beyond that.”

“As I expected.”

Lin Jing nodded.

“It matches what I predicted.”

“You came straight to me the moment you arrived in Tianji City, asking about the final reward for the Genius Rankings Battle—which means you must be determined to win first place.”

“If you do win, you could ask the Starry Cosmos Turtle to shroud you from fate itself.”

“If it does that, even someone at the Soul Transformation Stage would be unable to track you down unless you deliberately revealed yourself.”

“For a creature like the Starry Cosmos Turtle, that’s practically effortless.”

“That’s true.”

Lin Jing agreed.

Still, his greatest concern remained: his Longevity Body.

His life force was far too overwhelming, making him as conspicuous as a blazing sun in the dead of night.

Even if he cultivated a technique to mask his presence, he wasn’t sure if it would be strong enough once his cultivation advanced further.

At that point, he wouldn’t even need to be tracked—his very existence would be impossible to hide.

Bai Yuran grinned knowingly.

“…Aside from that.”

She smirked.

“You haven’t broken through to Foundation Establishment yet.”

“I’m guessing you’re trying to reach multiple levels of Extreme Stage in Qi Refinement, aren’t you?”

“The deeper your Qi Refinement foundation, the stronger your Foundation Establishment breakthrough.”

“And when cultivators like you—who possess special physiques—break through, those physiques will experience major enhancements.”

Lin Jing nodded.

This was similar to beasts reaching Foundation Establishment, which was the optimal moment for bloodline evolution.

For human cultivators, Foundation Establishment was one of the most crucial breakthroughs in their cultivation journey.

Even Nascent Soul or Soul Transformation wouldn’t bring as drastic an improvement to their physique as Foundation Establishment.

Cultivators classified physique development into three stages: Minor Achievement, Major Achievement, and Perfection.

His Longevity Body, Beast Taming Physique, and Ice Soul Physique—none of them had even reached Minor Achievement yet.

Improving these physiques required vast resources—for example, strengthening the Ice Soul Physique demanded a large amount of ice-attribute spiritual materials.

However, Foundation Establishment provided a one-time boost to all physiques.

If he could accumulate enough foundation before breaking through, he could save years' worth of resources, allowing him to focus entirely on cultivating techniques and spells instead.

Bai Yuran leaned forward.

“I have a solution.”

Lin Jing raised an eyebrow.

“What is it?”

“I already told you—the Starry Cosmos Turtle can bestow any blessing related to Space-Time Dao.”

“One of them is the ability to borrow power from the future.”

“The Power of the Future?!”

Bai Yuran nodded.

“For example—when you reach Core Formation, your Golden Core will automatically develop a divine ability based on your true energy.”

“That’s an ability you would normally only acquire in the future.”

Lin Jing understood immediately.

“You’re saying that the Starry Cosmos Turtle can let me access that divine ability early—even while I’m still in Qi Refinement or Foundation Establishment?”

“Exactly.”

“Of course, since you don’t have a Golden Core yet, you wouldn’t have the mana needed to use the ability.”

“But if you can find a way to generate that power, you could unleash a Core Formation divine ability while still in Foundation Establishment.”

“This is what it means to borrow power from the future.”

“But there’s one catch,” Bai Yuran warned.

“This only lets you access your predetermined Core Formation divine ability early—it does not give you an extra divine ability later.”

“So when you actually reach Core Formation, you won’t gain a new ability.”

Lin Jing processed this carefully.

“Got it. You’re saying that if I cultivate the Beast Taming Art, I’ll normally gain one drop of Contract Blood upon reaching Foundation Establishment.”

“But by using the Starry Cosmos Turtle’s blessing, I could obtain that drop early, while I’m still in Qi Refinement.”

“This means I could contract another beast right now and use its breakthrough feedback to push myself even further in Extreme Stage Qi Refinement!”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 98: Dragon Palace, Lin Jing!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 98: Dragon Palace, Lin Jing!

Bai Yuran giggled. "That's right! But I heard from Senior Brother that many hidden geniuses have been invited to this Genius Rankings Battle. Getting first place won’t be easy."

Lin Jing replied calmly, "According to this year's rules, isn't the final ranking decided by Tianji Pavilion rather than the results of the duels?"

This time, there were no rules—except for the restriction against killing opponents. Participants could use any means available, showcasing their mastery of all forms of cultivation techniques. However, according to Tianji Pavilion, the duel results were only for reference. The final rankings depended on each contestant’s overall performance, as judged by Tianji Pavilion.

For instance, if a genius relied too much on external assistance, even if they won their battles, their ranking might not be high. If two contestants used the same tier of magic treasures, but one had personally refined theirs while the other received theirs from an elder, the rankings would naturally favor the former in a tie.

"That may be true, but the world isn’t blind. Tianji Pavilion will arrange things fairly," Bai Yuran said with a smirk. "Since I've shared so much insider information, don’t you think you should show some appreciation?"

Lin Jing looked out the window. "I agree. But now isn’t the time. When the time comes, I’ll use my own lifespan to request a divination from you."

Bai Yuran pouted. "So, just an empty promise, huh?"

After leaving Tianji Pavilion, Lin Jing went into seclusion to focus on cultivation.

This was because only the first-place winner would receive the Rare Beast’s Blessing , causing many to hesitate.

The participants could generally be divided into two groups:

Those in the middle of the rankings, who had no hope of reaching first place but were secure in their current positions, mostly chose not to attend.

For Tianji Pavilion, the real goal was to shake up the top ten rankings. The number of participants didn’t matter—what mattered was drawing the world's attention.

Time passed quickly, and soon, the day of the Genius Rankings Battle arrived.

Tianji Pavilion had prepared a colossal arena. Surrounding it were numerous floating stone platforms serving as spectator seats.

A total of 100,000 seats were available, and Tianji Pavilion had sold every last one. From Qi Refinement disciples to Nascent Soul cultivators, most of the Mystic Domain’s cultivators had their eyes on this event. Many elders had even brought their disciples to witness the clash of geniuses firsthand.

"Looks like you all have recovered well," Lin Jing remarked with a smile.

Standing on one of the floating platforms, he looked at the members of Dragon Palace .

Xu Zhizhi’s mouth twitched. "Honestly, my original goal for this battle was just to make it onto the Genius Rankings."

"But after what we endured last month, if I don’t at least break into the top seventy, I’ll feel like all that suffering was for nothing!"

"Don’t worry, you’ll make it. You were personally chosen by Cavern Heaven , after all," Zhong Xin said, laughing. "If you face a tough opponent, just use your best techniques. If they’re too strong, let your beast deal with it."

"That’s true. Winning that way will boost our ranking significantly. But as long as we show our strength in earlier matches, it won’t affect our climb on the rankings."

Even Wang Xiao, standing nearby, nodded. After the grueling month they had endured, he was convinced that very few people outside their group could even defeat his Battle Tiger !

The Genius Rankings Battle was overseen by Nascent Soul cultivator Tian Huihua , a Tianji Pavilion elder.

There were 128 participants in total.
