Floating in the air, Tian Huihua announced, "When I call your name, step onto the arena. A match begins when one side either admits defeat or loses the ability to fight."

"First match: Gao Sheng of the Gao Family, a cultivator family."

"Versus Zhong Xin of the Zhong Family, a military cultivator family."

"Huh?!"

Upon hearing his name, Gao Sheng was stunned. "Me? First match? Am I really that eye-catching?"

"Maybe it’s your Spiritual Fortune Physique at work," Lin Jing said, arms crossed, as he looked at the armored woman stepping onto the stage.

"Damn it, why couldn't I be in an odd-numbered match? Then I might've gotten a free pass to the next round."

The Dragon Palace members stared at him like he was an idiot.

"The rankings aren't entirely based on wins or losses. Performance matters more. Whether you get a free pass or not doesn’t really change anything," someone pointed out.

"Alright, fine. But my opponent… Foundation Establishment Perfection ? That’s strong. I doubt my Dragon Horse can handle it alone."

"No problem. Fight together."

Hearing Lin Jing’s words, Gao Sheng leapt onto the arena.

The Zhong Family was a renowned military family, whose cultivators had served in the army for generations. Though they had no Nascent Soul cultivators, it was said that when fighting in formation, the Zhong Family army could even take down Nascent Soul opponents.

As Gao Sheng and Zhong Xin stepped onto the stage, the audience erupted in discussion.

"The Zhong Family’s True Qi Horse Manifestation technique is terrifying for its cavalry tactics and relentless charge. Zhong Xin might not be on the Genius Rankings, but that’s probably because she’s been training in the military. Now that she’s here, she’s definitely aiming to make a name for herself!"

"On the other hand, the Gao Family excels at the Great Enchantment Technique , which enhances magic treasures with exorcism power, making them natural counters to demonic cultivators!"

Meanwhile, on the stage—

"Zhong Xin, of the Zhong Family," the armored woman announced. As she spoke, she summoned a phantom warhorse made of true qi, mounted it, and drew her spear. Her powerful aura locked onto Gao Sheng, whose cultivation was far lower than hers.

The Gao Family’s enchantment techniques could enhance magic treasures, but against an opponent like Zhong Xin, Gao Sheng himself was not much of a threat.

However, he didn't introduce himself as a Gao Family member.

"Dragon Palace, Gao Sheng , from Hidden Dragon Academy !"

With that, he took out a small jade bottle, shook it, and suddenly—a white Dragon-Horned Horse appeared, growing from miniature size to its full, majestic form.

This was the Beast-Sealing Technique , a beast-taming spell that allowed cultivators to shrink and store their beasts conveniently. The bottle wasn’t a spatial treasure, just an ordinary jade container, but it was more than enough to hold the shrunken Dragon Horse .

The horse's four hooves were clad in metal horseshoes forged from rare alloys, and it was covered in battle armor, looking awe-inspiring.

" True Qi Horse Manifestation ? It won’t stand a chance against my exorcism-enhanced Dragon Horse ! Let’s see how the Zhong Family’s cavalry measures up!"

Gao Sheng quickly enchanted the Dragon Horse’s armor with the Great Enchantment Technique , strengthening it.

"Charge!"

With a roar, the Dragon Horse ’s eyes glowed red, its powerful bloodline energy erupting as it galloped forward.

Zhong Xin blinked. "What the hell?"

Then, her expression changed—she had just realized that this Dragon Horse carried a King-tier Dragon Bloodline !

Moreover, it was fully equipped with rare metal armor and further enhanced by Gao Sheng’s enchantment!

Zhong Xin wasn't the only one confused.

Even Gao Sheng’s father, watching in secret, had a dark expression.

"Damn it! Who taught you to use Great Enchantment Technique like this?!"

He had sent his son to Hidden Dragon Academy hoping he’d learn something useful, but never expected this!

Yet, as bizarre as it seemed, the Dragon Horse ’s charge was unstoppable, smashing through Zhong Xin’s defenses and sending her flying!

"Next time, just get a real horse," Gao Sheng said, stroking his victorious mount.

The entire arena fell silent.

And then, murmurs spread—

"Dragon Palace? What the hell is that?"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 99: Blood Kill Tower
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 99: Blood Kill Tower

"Lin Jing, huh?"

At the Genius Rankings Battle, thanks to the relentless promotions by the Dragon Palace’s External PR Agents (hired by Xu Zhizhi), even the top-ranked geniuses were now familiar with Lin Jing’s name.

Xu Zhizhi had spent a fortune on this—though he didn’t pay for extra publicity, he had given each promoter a highly valuable entry ticket to the battle.

"Interesting. I hadn’t noticed him before, but now that I’ve taken a closer look, this Holy Son of the Beast Taming Sect really isn’t simple. Should I quickly refine a treasure specifically to counter beast tamers?"

Among the audience, an orange-haired man grinned. He was Yan Xiangbei, the third-ranked genius on the Human List , from Burning Immortal Valley of the Fire Domain.

Although Lin Jing was already ranked on the Genius Rankings, he was only in the low twenties. That wasn’t enough to draw serious attention from the top contenders.

But after the rankings were updated again, Lin Jing's position was now 25th place.

Lin Jing, disciple of the Beast Taming Sect from the Great Wasteland Domain, Qi Refinement 11th Stage.

Even though Tianji Pavilion had deliberately concealed details about Lin Jing’s Longevity Body and Blood Path Techniques to protect him, his record alone was already quite shocking.

"Qi Refinement 11th Stage… making the rankings at Qi Refinement level..."

On a floating stone platform, a young man in a black-and-white Daoist robe spoke slowly.

Because Lin Jing had not publicly demonstrated his breakthrough to Qi Refinement 12th Stage, the rankings were still slightly outdated.

"The Beast Taming Sect’s techniques rely on pet beasts providing cultivation feedback. That might be how he reached Qi Refinement 11th Stage."

"Normally, a cultivator at Qi Refinement 10th Stage already has combat power equivalent to early Foundation Establishment. Qi Refinement 11th Stage would be on par with mid-Foundation Establishment."

"This Lin Jing has reached Qi Refinement 11th Stage himself, plus he has a Foundation Establishment pet beast. Ranking in the twenties is indeed impressive. But I wonder if he has already reached Foundation Establishment?"

"If he has, then with the foundation of Qi Refinement 11th Stage, his power would be terrifying. And if he’s contracted a second pet beast by now, then he really needs to be taken seriously."

The one speaking was Chen Zhongdao, the top-ranked genius on the Human List.

"That said… my Spell Spirit Origin is quite similar to a pet beast in function. I wonder if Lin Jing can force me to reveal its full power?"

Among the top ten on the Genius Rankings, every single one of them could fight Golden Core cultivators despite being at Foundation Establishment.

Now that they had analyzed Lin Jing’s information, even the best-ranked geniuses acknowledged Dragon Palace’s Lin Jing as a formidable opponent.

After all, breaking into the rankings at Qi Refinement alone was impressive enough. If he had already reached Foundation Establishment, he would be an absolute nightmare to deal with.

However, what they didn’t know was… Lin Jing was still at Qi Refinement Stage.

Moreover, now that he had discovered a method to reach Qi Refinement 13th Stage, he had no plans to advance to Foundation Establishment anytime soon.

The next few matches proceeded relatively normally. Most geniuses fought using their sect or family techniques—nobody pulled a stunt like Gao Sheng.

Until…

"Match Six: Cao Yi from Black Wind Mountain."

"Versus: Zi Linger from Dan Ding Sect."

Black Wind Mountain was a sect located in the dangerous borderlands. Its disciples specialized in wind techniques and relied on unique environments to cultivate. Because of this, they rarely left their territory.

Cao Yi was ranked somewhere in the 90s on the Genius Rankings. He had entered this battle mainly to protect his spot from dropping out of the top 100.

He descended onto the arena, wrapped in black wind.

Upon hearing that his opponent was an alchemist—and that she wasn’t even ranked—he immediately assumed this would be an easy win.

However…

When he saw Zi Linger step onto the stage, holding a Pill Rabbit, he fell into deep thought.

"Wait… you’re not part of Dragon Palace too, are you?" he asked cautiously.

He had heard the name Dragon Palace so many times from the audience that his ears were practically bleeding.

"Why is everyone obsessed with that stupid student organization? Have they never seen the world before?"

Zi Linger responded indifferently, "Now that you mention it… yes, I did join."

Cao Yi: "…"

"Don’t worry," Zi Linger said, smiling. "I’ll go easy on you."

The Pill Rabbit in her arms twitched, glancing at Cao Yi’s beast—a Dragonblood Rabbit.

"Wait… you have a King-tier bloodline beast too?" Cao Yi muttered.

Silently, he took out a handful of pills and shoved them into his beast’s mouth.

The Pill Rabbit had been maintaining a gentle, docile appearance using its Mimicry Pill.

But the moment it swallowed the pills, it transformed—its muscles bulged, its long ears became dragon horns, and a faint red glow filled its eyes.

Standing on its hind legs, it was actually taller than Cao Yi himself.

Zi Linger’s Pill Rabbit immediately sensed a dangerous presence. It quickly summoned two white wind formations, unleashing twin wind dragons toward the opponent.

Unfortunately for her, Cao Yi’s Dragonblood Rabbit, after consuming a ridiculous amount of power-enhancing pills, had reached a level of brute force that completely defied common sense.

"ROAR!!!"

With a single leap, it shredded through the wind dragons with its claws.

It then lunged at the Pill Rabbit, which quickly raised a wind barrier for defense.

But the Dragonblood Rabbit simply punched through the barrier, sending the Pill Rabbit and its owner flying out of the arena.

"ROAR!!!"

The victorious Dragonblood Rabbit then beat its chest in triumph—only to fracture its own ribs.

Unfazed, it calmly pulled out a Bone Mending Pill, swallowed it, and continued acting like nothing had happened.

The crowd was speechless.

"What the hell is this? What kind of monster is that?!"

Even the alchemists in the audience were dumbfounded.

"Wait a second… isn’t Dan Ding Sect supposed to be famous for fire control techniques and alchemy flames? Since when did they start feeding experimental pills to their beasts and letting the beasts do the fighting?!"

This was the second shocking match featuring a Dragon Palace member.

First, Gao Sheng.

Now, Zi Linger.

One thing became clear—the combat style of Dragon Palace members was completely unorthodox!

It was as if Lin Jing, the Holy Son of the Beast Taming Sect , was corrupting geniuses from other sects, dragging them all into the Beast Taming Path!

Yet… no one could deny that both Gao Sheng’s and Zi Linger’s Dragonblood Beasts were terrifyingly strong.

A thought began creeping into the minds of many cultivators—

"Damn… I wish I had a beast like that."

Finally, Lin Jing’s match was announced.

"Match Forty-Two: Lin Jing, Beast Taming Sect."

"Versus: Qi Yuan, Blood Kill Tower."

The audience erupted with excitement—finally, Dragon Palace’s leader was about to fight!

But then someone frowned.

"Wait… Blood Kill Tower? Isn’t that an assassin organization from Qingzhou? How are they even allowed in the Genius Rankings Battle?!"

Lin Jing glanced at his opponent and chuckled.

"So, you’re here to advertise your sect too?"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 100: Fighting Fire with Fire
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 100: Fighting Fire with Fire

"So arrogant."

"But if it's Blood Kill Tower… they might actually have the strength to back it up."

"My sect's secret technique requires the Monster Core of a beast that resides in the Misty Forest. Every time we try to obtain one, we suffer heavy casualties. I wonder how much Blood Kill Tower would charge for the job..."

Though few knew Qi Yuan, the reputation of Blood Kill Tower had spread even to the Ancient Celestial Kingdom, proving its fearsome renown.

A transformed Blood Kill Tower facing off against the Holy Son of the Beast Taming Sect?

Many spectators were highly invested in this match.

Lin Jing, however, simply shook his head at Qi Yuan’s words.

"You’re not lucky. Instead, you should consider whether Blood Kill Tower has somehow offended Tianji Pavilion, leading you to face me so early."

"Tianji Pavilion’s first-round matchups are randomly selected. Who you face is part of your luck—and luck is a form of strength," Tian Huihua interjected, casting Lin Jing a glance. "The second round will group contestants based on their first-round performance, turning into a multi-combatant melee. This ensures that true talent isn’t buried due to an unlucky first-round matchup."

"In other words, this round is partly a test of fortune. If you encounter a strong opponent too soon and get eliminated without showing your true strength, you’ll just have to accept it as bad luck."

"In the second round, multiple participants will be placed in the same arena for a battle royale. Only those who outlast others in chaotic battles will advance to the third round!"

"Then let’s see whose luck runs out first."

Qi Yuan’s eyes narrowed. "Summon your pet beast."

He had already sensed that Lin Jing was still at Qi Refinement Stage—he hadn't broken through to Foundation Establishment. However, as a Beast Tamer, Lin Jing’s real strength lay in his pet beasts. The rankings had already made it clear that he had at least one Foundation Establishment beast.

The audience noticed this too.

Why was Lin Jing still in Qi Refinement?

Since he had already reached the extreme limit of Qi Refinement, he could have advanced to Foundation Establishment before the tournament, making himself far stronger.

Why enter the Genius Rankings Battle while holding back his breakthrough?

Yet Lin Jing remained unfazed.

" I don’t need a pet beast to deal with you. "

Qi Yuan’s expression darkened.

At that moment, he vanished.

The entire arena went silent.

Even Golden Core cultivators in the audience couldn't track him.

" Spatial Concealment Technique! "

"This Blood Kill Tower genius has mastered spatial arts at Foundation Establishment Stage?! That’s insane!"

"Is it because of a special space-related physique, or is his comprehension just that terrifying?"

"No wonder Blood Kill Tower’s reputation spread across Qingzhou… Who can defend against space techniques?"

The top-ranked geniuses in the Ancient Celestial Kingdom frowned at this development.

Spatial abilities were universally troublesome.

"Why hasn’t Lin Jing summoned his pet beast yet?"

"Against an opponent who can disappear, having an extra set of eyes is crucial!"

"Is he really planning to fight a peak Foundation Establishment assassin while still in Qi Refinement?"

Yan Xiangbei, the third-ranked genius from Burning Immortal Valley , scoffed. "Even if he's at Qi Refinement 11th Stage, that’s impossible."

Among the audience, some students from Hidden Dragon Academy flinched, recalling a certain pine leaf mouse that had once stolen their storage bags.

" Space techniques, huh? That’s nothing new. "

Sure enough—

Lin Jing didn’t even turn his head.

Without speaking a word, he raised a single hand, summoning a storm of red flying leaves around him.

Flying Leaf Technique!

SHING!

A crimson streak cut through the air.

The spectators had just been scanning for Qi Yuan’s whereabouts—

And suddenly, blood sprayed into the air.

Qi Yuan reappeared, his arm slashed open. He stared at Lin Jing in disbelief.

" He actually detected my position...?! "

Cai Sidao murmured, "Let him show his full power."

For Lin Jing, entering this battle wasn’t just about victory—it was about prestige.

A weak opponent wouldn’t help him make a name for himself.

So, if his opponents weren’t strong enough—

He’d simply restrain his own strength, forcing them to fight at their full potential.

That’s why, as a Beast Tamer, he refused to summon his pet.

He was deliberately making things harder for himself—to get people talking.

Clad in white robes, Lin Jing stood with his hands behind his back, his aura ethereal and unshaken.

He didn’t even spare Qi Yuan a second glance.

" Full power…? "

" Then don’t die. I don’t want to be disqualified. "

A murderous glint flashed in Qi Yuan’s blood-red eyes.

His spatial technique had been countered—but he had far more up his sleeve.

Blood-red energy erupted from his body, his floating crimson hair whipping in the wind.

The sheer killing intent radiating from him formed a scarlet storm, swirling with the souls of slain beasts and humans alike.

" Blood Shura Armor! "

The storm compressed, condensing into a blood-red suit of armor around his body.

He brandished his short sword and charged at Lin Jing, the intensity of his murderous aura multiplying tenfold.

Even Golden Core cultivators in the audience visibly paled.

" That’s a secret technique that strengthens killing intent! "

"Qi Yuan’s cultivation level hasn’t increased, but his killing aura has undergone a qualitative transformation. Even high-level Foundation Establishment cultivators might be paralyzed by fear!"

"Such demonic arts…"

Yet—

The moment the killing intent surged toward Lin Jing—

A golden radiance blossomed around him.

Instead of resisting the attack—

He absorbed the killing aura.

Lin Jing’s calm voice echoed through the battlefield:

"I, Lin Jing, despise demonic cultivators like you the most."

"This killing aura of yours… what difference is there between it and demonic techniques?"

As he spoke—

A divine Buddhist glow erupted from his body.

Qi Yuan staggered, his expression shifting in horror.

"Buddhist Arts?!"

A Beast Tamer... wielding Buddhist Techniques?!

And that wasn’t all.

The audience collectively froze as they finally sensed Lin Jing’s cultivation.

"Wait… Qi Refinement 12th Stage?!"

Qi Yuan’s eyes widened in shock.

Lin Jing’s spiritual aura had the power of late Foundation Establishment!

And his Buddhist Light was utterly devouring Qi Yuan’s killing aura!

In truth, Lin Jing had long been studying Buddhism to refine his own power.

His Buddha-Demon Pearl and Diamond Fist were natural counters to Qi Yuan’s killing arts.

Raising his hand—

Lin Jing summoned eighteen golden palm imprints into the sky.

One by one, they rained down.

Qi Yuan’s sword slashed through several, but ultimately—

He was blasted dozens of meters back, his Blood Shura Armor shattered, and blood spewing from his mouth.

Standing tall, Lin Jing pressed his palms together.

"May you walk the righteous path and cease your killings."

The crowd was stunned.

Even Gao Sheng and Bai Yuran, who knew about Lin Jing’s demonic arts, were utterly speechless.

The most shocking thing wasn’t just that Lin Jing used Buddhist Techniques—

It was that he had reached Qi Refinement 12th Stage.

This was an unprecedented feat, save for the Ancient Imperial Princes, who had the Boundary-Breaking Bird to push them to Qi Refinement 13th Stage.

Now, the top-ranked geniuses finally understood.

They had underestimated Lin Jing.

And some among the audience even began murmuring:

"A sect with such a Holy Son… how could the Beast Taming Sect be called heretical?"

Meanwhile—

Gao Sheng just stood there, completely dumbfounded.

"Wait… what if he starts using his demonic techniques next? What will you guys think then?!"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 101: Chaos Battle
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 101: Chaos Battle

The young genius from Blood Kill Tower in Qingzhou had mastered the Spatial Concealment Technique, and his killing aura could shake even Core Formation cultivators. Such strength was extraordinary, even among the ancient kingdom’s prodigies.

At first, many believed Lin Jing would struggle in this fight. But to their shock, this Holy Son of the Beast Taming Sect, a man with a beast’s visage but a Buddha’s heart, transformed into a Buddhist prodigy, displaying profound mastery of Buddhist techniques.

The Buddhist sect mainly operated in the far western deserts beyond the ancient kingdom. It boasted countless powerful figures, rivaling even the entirety of Qingzhou. If Lin Jing were truly a Buddhist prodigy, then suppressing a Blood Kill Tower cultivator would indeed be no challenge.

Coupled with his outrageous cultivation at the twelfth stage of Qi Refinement, some even began speculating:

"Has Lin Jing abandoned the Beast Taming Sect and switched to cultivating Buddhism because of the sect's closure?"

"The Buddhist sect has always wanted to spread its teachings in the ancient kingdom. High monks often bestow fortunes upon fated individuals. Could Lin Jing have been chosen by an esteemed Void Mystical Stage Buddhist master? Otherwise, how else do you explain his overwhelming Buddhist aura? I’ve seen monks who have formed their Buddhist Core, but none had such a powerful Buddhist presence!"

"If a Buddhist master helped him, reaching the twelfth stage of Qi Refinement wouldn’t be impossible. After all, Buddhism excels at unlocking potential. No wonder he founded Dragon Palace and shared his Beast Taming insights so freely—he's no longer focusing on that path. Buddhism is now his true weapon!"

As the crowd speculated, Lin Jing had already left the arena.

"To some extent, these people aren’t entirely wrong," Tian Huihua thought. Thousands of years ago, a Buddhist monk had aided the Great Wasteland in subduing demons. That monk had since reached the Void Mystical Stage. If Lin Jing had received his blessing within the Beast Taming Sect’s Demon-Suppressing Pagoda, then indeed, he had some ties to a Void Mystical Stage monk.

However, while Lin Jing had been blessed by a Buddhist master, giving him exceptional talent in Buddhist arts, his true power came from the combination of the Buddha-Demon Pearl and the Ice Soul Gu. By using himself as a vessel to nurture demons and leveraging the Ice Soul Gu, he refined his Buddhist abilities to an extreme.

Upon returning, Gao Sheng looked at Lin Jing enviously.

"In just two years, you've cultivated Diamond Palm to this level—eighteen strikes in an instant, each strong enough to suppress a perfected Foundation Establishment demon. Even my Grand Enchantment Technique isn’t that terrifying."

Lin Jing smirked, "Then how about I teach you Diamond Palm, and you teach me the Grand Enchantment Technique?"

Gao Sheng: "???"

"You think I need you to teach me Diamond Palm? I'm not lacking in techniques!"

"But if you want to learn the Grand Enchantment Technique… you could always marry into the Gao family."

"I'm good, thanks." Lin Jing flatly declined.

For the past two years, he had been training in Buddha-Demon Pearl and Diamond Palm, constantly strengthening his body. The intense physical cultivation had significantly enhanced his physique, making his body as tough as that of a Foundation Establishment body cultivator.

This was crucial for Lin Jing. While most physiques could endure some injuries in battle, his Longevity Body was a different case. If he bled or lost a limb in combat, it wouldn’t just be a minor setback—it could be a death sentence. His blood would likely attract even more enemies, turning the battle into a disaster.

Thus, Lin Jing’s philosophy was clear: build his defense as strong as possible. Body cultivation could not be neglected.

Once he perfected Diamond Palm, he planned to start training in Buddhist Golden Body, which offered even greater defense!

"Forget Buddhism for a second," Wang Xiao interjected, his expression shifting as he studied Lin Jing. "I want to know about the twelfth stage of Qi Refinement..."

He couldn’t comprehend it. He had reached the tenth stage of Qi Refinement before Foundation Establishment, which had already allowed his Heavenly God Physique to enter its early stage.

From his perspective, Lin Jing must have a unique physique. Otherwise, reaching the twelfth stage of Qi Refinement before Foundation Establishment was simply unfathomable. He even suspected that Lin Jing might fully mature his physique before reaching the Nascent Soul Stage!

Lin Jing chuckled, seeing the confusion on the faces of his Dragon Palace members.

"Just assume I was blessed by a powerful Buddhist figure."

Now that he thought about it, many spectators indeed believed he had switched to Buddhism. It made sense—his Diamond Palm was too extraordinary. It was natural for people to misinterpret.

"Alright, let's focus on the matches."

The rest of the first round of the Genius Rankings Battle went smoothly. Unfortunately, spectators were disappointed because the top ten ranked geniuses didn’t go all out. Most matches were one-sided dominations.

Not long after, the first round concluded. Standing in the sky, Tian Huihua announced:

"Of the eighty-seven defeated contestants, all are eliminated. Tianji Pavilion will assess their performances and determine their new rankings on the updated Genius Rankings."

"In seven days, the second round will commence. The remaining sixty-four participants will be divided into eight groups, with eight people in each group."

"In each group, all eight contestants will enter the stage simultaneously for a chaotic battle. The last person standing will advance to the third round."

"Tianji Pavilion will post the groupings across Tianji City tomorrow morning. You have seven days to gather intelligence on your opponents. This is your preparation time—use it wisely."

Tian Huihua smiled. He was eager to see how these geniuses would perform in the upcoming chaotic battles.

Some well-known prodigies might even face group assaults. While unfair, that was exactly what Tianji Pavilion wanted to witness. After all, in real combat, absolute fairness didn’t exist. Surviving an unfair battle required true skill.

"A chaotic battle in the second round, huh?"

The audience buzzed with excitement. An eight-person free-for-all meant survival depended on overall ability.

"That guy from Blood Kill Tower is so unlucky. If he hadn’t been eliminated early, his Spatial Concealment Assassination Technique would have made him perfect for this round."

"Well, luck is part of strength. He showed what he needed to. Even though he was eliminated, his ranking on the Genius Rankings shouldn’t be too low. His performance was just... embarrassing. What did he last? Seven or eight hits under the Beast Taming Sect’s Holy Son’s Diamond Palm? Hahaha!"

While the first round had some excitement, the audience clearly anticipated the second round far more. The grouping by Tianji Pavilion promised thrilling chaos.

For the participants, however, the pressure was immense.

"Let’s go."

Lin Jing turned to his Dragon Palace members. "The strongest won’t necessarily advance, and the weakest won’t necessarily be eliminated. When the battle starts, just have your beasts draw fire."

"With our month of special training, they don’t feel pain anyway."

Dragon Palace members: ...

Tianji Pavilion clearly wanted to see if any geniuses could outwit stronger opponents and survive through cunning. Sometimes, strategy was more valuable than sheer cultivation.

" Sigh... does this mean I have to start working? " Xu Zhizhi sighed.

Lin Jing nodded.

"Yep. Come with me later—the organization has a mission for you."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 102: The Weapon Master Physique
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 102: The Weapon Master Physique

“So many?!”

Back at their residence, Xu Zhizhi took the storage pouch that Lin Jing tossed to him. Sweeping it with his spiritual sense, he was instantly stunned.

As far as he remembered, Lin Jing had been exchanging Rising Dragon Pills for high-grade spiritual plants at Dragon Palace for the past two years. By all accounts, he should have spent a fortune on those trades—so how did he still have so many pills left?

“Wait… don’t tell me your alchemy dragon carp hasn’t slept for two years?”

Lin Jing replied matter-of-factly, “With Awakening Pills, who needs sleep?”

Xu Zhizhi froze. “You’re saying it never slept? Even Ziling’er’s pill rabbit doesn’t have it that bad!”

“Just kidding,” Lin Jing chuckled. “It’s mostly because practice makes perfect. Our success rate in refining Rising Dragon Pills is ridiculously high now.”

And besides… they never lacked materials.

“Work with any auction house you want,” Lin Jing instructed. “I want all of these Rising Dragon Pills sold within seven days. Then, take the spirit stones we earn and buy as many third-grade and above spiritual plants in Tianji City as possible.”

“That’s easy enough.” Xu Zhizhi didn’t mind helping Lin Jing—mostly because Lin Jing paid him. Every time, he’d give Rising Dragon Pills as compensation, unlike his stingy master, who worked him to the bone for free.

“…But if I handle this, I won’t have time to prepare for the Genius Rankings Battle,” Xu Zhizhi hinted, eyeing Lin Jing expectantly. This time, one measly pill wouldn’t cut it.

“Not a big deal,” Lin Jing replied. “You weren’t going to qualify anyway.”

Xu Zhizhi: ???

“Lin, my brother, even if you’re speaking the truth, do you have to be so blunt?!”

“Just you wait—thirty years on the east bank, thirty years on the west bank! Thirty years from now, you’ll see me in a whole new light!” Xu Zhizhi declared indignantly.

Lin Jing chuckled. “That phrase was practically made for you. Given your potential, once you become a city lord and gain an official position, combining your Hidden Dragon Arts with political fortune, you’ll be unstoppable. The Earth Rankings will definitely have your name in the future.”

Xu Zhizhi laughed. “I’ve been studying the classics of sages for years, all to secure a high-ranking government post someday. Hopefully, I can rise swiftly through the ranks!”

The third stage of the Hidden Dragon Arts directly tied strength to one’s official position—the higher the rank, the stronger the cultivator. Lin Jing had no doubt that the Earth Rankings would be Xu Zhizhi’s domain one day. After all, climbing the political ladder without a background took time, and even with talent, patience was required to reach the top.

Soon, rumors spread throughout Tianji City—Dragon Palace members relied entirely on Rising Dragon Pills to train their beasts.

Even more shocking, a massive number of Rising Dragon Pills were now appearing in Tianji City’s auctions.

Lin Jing, however, paid no attention to any of this.

His focus was on the second-round matchups for the Genius Rankings Battle, just announced by Tianji Pavilion.

If it were one-on-one, he wouldn’t be worried at all. After all, for him, “one-on-one” was actually a gang fight—he had too many allies.

But an eight-person free-for-all?

Given how much attention he’d drawn, he was almost certain that he’d become the prime target for everyone in the match.

He had to be cautious.

“First group…”

“Second group…”

“…You’re in the eighth group,” Long Dan murmured after checking the list. “Apart from you… the ‘weakest’ in your group is…”

She hesitated.

“…Lin Huixin, ranked eighth on the Genius Rankings.”

“…What?”

Lin Jing frowned.

“Why am I getting matched up with this guy right away?”

“Well, at least now you won’t be the one getting ganged up on,” Long Dan sighed. “It’ll probably be him instead. But honestly… he’s a tough opponent. I didn’t expect to face him this early.”

Lin Jing had a feeling Tianji Pavilion had done this on purpose.

After all, as a Beast Tamer, he relied heavily on external forces—his pets, formations, and talismans. So now, they paired him against someone who specialized in external forces.

Yan Xiangbei, a disciple of Burning Immortal Valley in the Fire Domain, was a legendary artifact refiner with the Innate Weapon Master Physique.

This Innate Weapon Master Physique was a God Physique, ranked 87th on the Physique Rankings, even higher than Wang Xiao’s Heavenly God Physique.

The Heavenly God Physique allowed Qi Refinement cultivators to control wind, rain, lightning, and even draw upon starlight—already an absurd ability. When breaking through to Nascent Soul, those with this physique had a 100% success rate due to their deep connection with the laws of nature.

Yet even that was ranked below the Innate Weapon Master Physique.

This physique granted its bearer the ability to refine and control magic artifacts far beyond their cultivation level while using minimal energy to activate them.

That one ability alone…

Was enough to make every single past bearer of this physique surpass Heavenly God Physique users.

According to rumors, Yan Xiangbei, despite only being at Foundation Establishment Perfection, had already refined a high-grade magical artifact—something no ordinary Foundation Establishment cultivator should be able to use.

Even Core Formation cultivators couldn’t fully activate a high-grade artifact—their spiritual energy would be instantly drained, leaving them exhausted or even dead. And worse, they wouldn’t even be able to control the artifact’s power properly.

But Yan Xiangbei, thanks to his Innate Weapon Master Physique, could do the impossible.

Because of this, even Core Formation cultivators avoided fighting him head-on.

Meanwhile, geniuses across Tianji City were preparing for their battles.

Including Yan Xiangbei.

Inside a cave dwelling, the orange-haired youth silently examined the matchup list in his hand.

“The most noteworthy opponent… is Lin Jing from the Beast Taming Sect.”

“But… not enough of a threat to make me use my high-grade artifact.”

Although his physique allowed him to use high-grade artifacts at a low energy cost, it wasn’t without limits—his Foundation Establishment realm was still too low. Even a single activation drained him heavily.

That’s why, in normal battles, he only used mid-grade and low-grade artifacts. Thanks to his physique, a low-grade artifact in his hands could perform like a mid-grade one.

As for his high-grade artifact…

That was reserved for Chen Zhongdao, the number one genius, and Mo Qianyi, the second-ranked genius. Against those two, he’d need to go all out.

“Should I refine a weapon specifically to counter beast tamers?”

“I’ve studied the Myriad Beast Circle from the Myriad Beast Sect before. Since Lin Jing defeated a Myriad Beast Sect disciple, that means the circle alone isn’t enough to suppress his pets.

“So I’ll modify it. I’ll remove the ability to tame beasts and strengthen its suppression effects instead.”

“Additionally, since he might have powerful Buddhist techniques, I need a way to break his Buddhist aura. The simplest method? Desire Manipulation.

“Good thing I have a Harmony Sect artifact from Qingzhou. I’ll enhance it further.”

“…But something still feels missing.”

At that moment, a transmission token buzzed.

Yan Xiangbei read the latest intelligence and chuckled.

“As expected. According to my academy contacts, Lin Jing’s pet actually has spatial abilities.

“No wonder he was able to counter that assassin from Blood Kill Tower.

“In that case… I’ll prepare one more artifact to disrupt space.”

Seven days wasn’t much time, but he was ready.

“Let’s begin refining.”

Yan Xiangbei smirked. With so much preparation, he couldn’t see any way he would lose.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 103: Gathering of Geniuses
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 103: Gathering of Geniuses

“We’re rich, we’re rich…”

A few days later, after the first batch of Rising Dragon Pills was sold, Xu Zhizhi immediately went to find Lin Jing, only to discover that he was in seclusion.

“He's really diligent. Well, it makes sense—his next match is against Yan Xiangbei. That’s definitely tough!” Xu Zhizhi sighed, unsure if Lin Jing would be able to win.

He felt that unless Lin Jing broke through to Foundation Establishment before the second round, he probably wouldn’t stand a chance. No wonder Lin Jing had urged him to sell the pills quickly!

Once the Dragon Palace contestants suffered a collective defeat, the pills would surely drop in price.

At this moment, inside the inn room, Lin Jing was actively preparing for battle.

The first thing he and Pine Leaf Mouse needed to do was check the Mosquito-Powered Mech, just to avoid any mechanical failures at a critical moment.

His opponent had a high-grade magic artifact—a terrifying force. Lin Jing didn’t want to engage in a direct confrontation.

As for Pine Leaf Mouse, even though it possessed the True Form of a Demon King, it still wouldn’t be a match for a high-grade magic artifact unless the Everlasting Emperor’s Longevity Art was fully mastered.

In summary, Lin Jing didn’t want to overburden himself, the Ice Soul Gu, or Pine Leaf Mouse, risking severe injuries that could affect future battles.

So… the Mosquito-Powered Mech had to take the stage. Even if it got destroyed in combat, Lin Jing wouldn’t feel bad about it—he could always build another one.

Most of its materials were synthetic creations from Pine Leaf Mouse, similar to the pills he sold. There was no need to worry about expensive repair costs.

Seven days passed in a flash.

During this time, the spectators were thoroughly entertained. The eight battle platforms sparked endless debates among cultivators, expanding their horizons. Many even purchased rare pills leaked from certain contestants, making the trip well worth it.

On the main stage of the Genius Rankings Battle, Tian Huihua announced the lineup for the first battle royale match.

“Chen Zhong of the Spirit Dao Sect, Xiao Chen of the Purple Heaven Sword Sect, Changsun Sheng of the Tianluo Gate…”

As the eight names were read aloud, seven of them looked absolutely miserable. Ever since the matchups were revealed a few days ago, they hadn’t had a single good night’s sleep.

“You all should be thanking me. Thanks to my Fortune Physique, your luck improved, and you managed to avoid Chen Zhong,” Gao Sheng joked as he laughed with the contestants from Hidden Dragon Academy and Dragon Palace.

This Chen Zhong was currently ranked first on the Genius Rankings. Encountering him was a nightmare.

“I heard that achieving a Spell Spirit Origin requires completely mastering a spell. This Chen Zhong is only at Foundation Establishment, yet he’s accomplished something that even many Nascent Soul and Soul Formation cultivators fail to do…”

Yuan Jing of the Heavenly Sword Gate had originally thought his swordsmanship comprehension was extraordinary. He had even grasped Sword Intent, making him one of the top talents. But now, he realized that Chen Zhong was on another level entirely.

“He possesses the Innate Dao Physique, ranked nineteenth on the Physique Rankings. It grants an enormous boost to spell cultivation. Plus, the Spirit Dao Sect supposedly owns a rare Insight Fish…” Ziling'er sighed.

If this man had been born in the Era of Immortal Palaces, he might have had a high chance of ascending.

The first battle royale featured two top-tier geniuses:

Although Xiao Chen didn’t possess an Innate Divine Physique, he had cultivated the Innate Sword Physique and later awakened the Acquired Thunder Spirit Physique, meaning he possessed dual physiques—a rarity in itself.

As the battle began, the other seven contestants immediately targeted Chen Zhong without even needing to discuss it beforehand.

“Gravity Art.”

Chen Zhong simply closed his eyes slightly.

A terrifying gravity field erupted around him, centered on his position, forcefully repelling and suppressing every opponent!

It was a spell technique—Gravity Art.

In an instant, space warped, as if the heavens were collapsing. Every magic artifact and spell was instantly crushed into nothingness.

The seven attackers were all slammed to the ground, their bodies glued to the platform, their bones audibly fracturing. Blood leaked from the corners of their mouths.

“Spirit Dao Sect, Chen Zhong—victory.”

Tian Huihua glanced at Chen Zhong, whose Dao Eyes were now open, before announcing the result.

Chen Zhong calmly flew back to his seat.

This scene left the remaining contestants breathless.

After all, Chen Zhong hadn't even summoned his Spell Spirit Origin yet!

Simply using his own mana to cast a basic Gravity Art had been enough to completely overwhelm Xiao Chen, a dual-physique genius ranked eighth, leaving him utterly unable to stand.

“So this is the Innate Dao Physique. Even though I’m from the same Sword Domain as Xiao Chen, I wouldn’t stand a chance against him. But even he couldn't last a single move against Chen Zhong…” Yuan Jing was speechless.

“小元子, don’t stress too much. Your Heavenly Sword Gate still has Cold Moon Sword Immortal Feng Ning, who’s ranked first on the Earth Rankings. You don’t have to carry the sect’s honor alone. Besides, you’re only twenty-something, the youngest among us.”

Gao Sheng patted his shoulder.

Indeed, Heavenly Sword Gate was the top sword sect of the Ancient Kingdom. The sect’s strongest genius wasn’t Yuan Jing, but Cold Moon Sword Immortal Feng Ning, who had automatically risen to first place on the Earth Rankings after the previous leader surpassed sixty years of age.

The most anticipated contestant of the second battle royale was Mo Qianyi of Shenji Manor.

A female mechanist, she dressed plainly, her yellow cloth robe making her look like a commoner. In stark contrast, the other female cultivators were ornately dressed and impeccably groomed.

This group also included the genius ranked tenth.

However, Mo Qianyi quickly proved why the top three were on an entirely different level.

Even without deploying her famous mechanized formation, she effortlessly crushed her opponents using only a single Celestial Shot Chariot.

Through her Exquisite Physique, she connected directly to the Soul Core of her mechanical creations. The Celestial Shot Chariot fired forty-nine arrows, each carrying magic artifact-level power, imbued with attributes like gold, wood, water, fire, earth, wind, thunder, yin, yang, and poison.

The arrows moved according to her will, shattering every opponent’s defense before automatically returning to the chariot after impact, whether damaged or intact.

After a light pat on her Celestial Shot Chariot, Mo Qianyi vanished from the platform and returned to her seat.

In the process, an heir of Ice Extinction Island and a King-Blooded Flood Dragon were both eliminated, making them the first casualties from Dragon Palace.

“What a pity,” Lin Jing sighed.

Against true geniuses, even King-Blooded Dragon Pets were insignificant.

Fortunately, he had urged Dragon Palace members to showcase their pets early. Now that the Rising Dragon Pills were sold out, there was no way he’d allow refunds.

“An Innate Exquisite Physique is ridiculously powerful. This basically lets her control dozens of magic artifacts at once. Weapon Master Physiques focus on quality, while the Exquisite Physique is about quantity. And thanks to her mechanized creations, her energy consumption is minimal…”

Xu Zhizhi turned to Lin Jing. “Are you ready for Yan Xiangbei?”

Lin Jing nodded as the next match was announced.

“Yan Xiangbei of Burning Immortal Valley, Lu Tong of the Divine Movement Sect, Lin Jing of the Beast Taming Sect…”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 104: Mosquito-Powered Mech
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 104: Mosquito-Powered Mech

On the battle platform, the eight geniuses arrived one after another.

After witnessing the previous two battle royales, they all understood one thing—Yan Xiangbei was undoubtedly the most formidable opponent in their group.

Anyone ranked among the top three on the Genius Rankings was no pushover. There was a high probability that Yan Xiangbei could single-handedly defeat all seven of them.

"What are you looking at? Just come at me all at once."

Yan Xiangbei spoke indifferently as he noticed the wary gazes of the other contestants.

If Chen Zhong and Mo Qianyi could defeat seven opponents alone, then he could do the same.

However, what made Yan Xiangbei feel a bit annoyed was that despite preparing so many magic artifacts specifically to counter Lin Jing, this guy still refused to break through!

Among these seven opponents, the only one he had originally taken seriously was Lin Jing—because he had expected Lin Jing to reach Foundation Establishment before the match.

The moment the match started, Lu Tong of the Divine Movement Sect shouted.

As someone ranked within the top ten, he had no qualms about ganging up on Yan Xiangbei.

After giving the command, he vanished into the sky, executing his sect’s famed movement technique.

"Damn it! He told us to attack, then ran away himself!"

One of the contestants cursed upon seeing Lu Tong disappear.

Disciples of the Divine Movement Sect were known for their incredible speed and mastery of escape techniques.

"Who said I ran? Don’t be ridiculous."

A voice echoed from above.

"Our Divine Movement Sect doesn’t rely only on evasion."

“Heaven-Treading Step!”

A massive foot, dark as blackened steel, suddenly descended from the sky, aiming directly at Yan Xiangbei.

A terrifying black aura swirled around it, causing cracks to appear on the arena floor beneath Yan Xiangbei.

"What kind of spell is this?"

Spectators gasped as they watched the giant foot descend.

"I know! It’s a battle between an Innate Weapon Master Physique and an Acquired Foot Qi Physique!"

A young cultivator blurted out excitedly, only to have an elder beside him quickly cover his mouth.

One did not casually mock geniuses ranked among the top ten!

"How foolish."

Despite the overwhelming pressure of the descending foot, Yan Xiangbei’s battle armor gleamed with brilliant light, shielding him completely.

Without a hint of concern, he casually summoned a flying sword—a simple low-grade magic artifact.

For any genius on this stage, possessing a low-grade magic artifact was basic.

Even though low-grade artifacts were generally reserved for Core Formation cultivators, the top-tier prodigies present could wield them effortlessly.

However, Yan Xiangbei was different.

In his hands, a mere low-grade flying sword surged with power, instantly rivaling the might of a mid-grade artifact.

As the sword streaked through the air, it pierced straight through the foot’s center, causing the massive construct to shatter into a storm of black clouds.

"Useless, absolutely useless."

Lu Tong felt frustrated.

He had failed to restrain Yan Xiangbei, yet the other contestants were just standing there, doing nothing!

"Fine, I’ll take care of it myself."

Yan Xiangbei suddenly split into four identical copies.

While he couldn’t control a large number of artifacts as easily as those with an Exquisite Physique, he had mastered a secret technique to compensate.

"Weapon-Master Avatars."

Each of the four copies wielded one or two low-grade magic artifacts, but despite their supposed quality, all of them radiated the power of mid-grade artifacts.

The sky lit up as a storm of magic artifacts surged forward.

One of the flying swords even ignited with a strange blue fire, increasing its speed and destructive power exponentially.

"Flame-Sacrificing Technique!"

By burning and sacrificing artifacts, one could temporarily boost their power—at the cost of permanently destroying them.

With a flash of the flaming sword, a figure hidden among the clouds groaned in pain and plummeted from the sky.

Even with his superior speed, Lu Tong couldn’t evade the attack in time.

Meanwhile, the other contestants struggled against Yan Xiangbei’s relentless assault.

One by one, they fell.

“As expected, he’s unbelievably strong.”

Spectators were thrilled, but soon, they noticed something strange.

One person was still standing.

Lin Jing.

Yan Xiangbei’s mental attack artifact, a delicate jade sculpture of a celestial maiden, had been aimed at Lin Jing.

However, even after defeating six others, the jade sculpture still failed to affect him!

Instead, as a small squirrel appeared on Lin Jing’s shoulder, a massive wooden box materialized out of thin air, sealing the jade sculpture inside.

"What?!"

Yan Xiangbei’s connection to his artifact was suddenly severed, and he turned toward Lin Jing in disbelief.

“Impossible… This artifact was crafted by the Harmony Sect of Qingzhou. Even Core Formation female cultivators would struggle to resist its effects. How did you…?”

Even the spectators were stunned.

The top three prodigies had been expected to dominate effortlessly, yet the one resisting the longest wasn’t another top-tier genius—it was a mere Qi Refinement cultivator!

“Look! Lin Jing truly possesses incredible Buddhist discipline! Even a Harmony Sect artifact couldn’t shake him!”

A Buddhist disciple in the audience marveled.

But then… Lin Jing casually revealed the real reason behind his resistance.

“It’s simple—heat expands, cold contracts.”

Lin Jing held up a frost-covered finger, his voice calm.

“They say warmth ignites desire, while cold preserves clarity. And the Ice Soul Gu… is extremely cold.”

"What nonsense are you talking about?!"

Yan Xiangbei scoffed.

There was no way that simple ice techniques could counter a high-grade mental attack artifact.

All four of his avatars turned toward Lin Jing, their hands summoning even more artifacts.

A tiger-shaped seal infused with Demon Emperor blood, capable of suppressing beast bloodlines.

A bell that could distort space, restricting teleportation techniques.

“Looks like you prepared quite a few counters for me.”

Lin Jing sighed. Honestly, he hated being taken too seriously.

"Of course I did."

Yan Xiangbei raised four weapons—an axe, spear, blade, and cauldron—then ignited them with sacrificial fire.

To deal with Lin Jing’s strange abilities, he willingly sacrificed four artifacts!

The audience was shocked.

Even Gao Sheng, known for his wealth, was speechless.

"Damn it! This is just burning money at this point!"

BOOM!!!

In the next instant, a massive, humanoid mechanized creation appeared behind Lin Jing, shaking the ground.

Standing dozens of meters tall, its sleek, dark-red exterior blended modern technological aesthetics with ancient mechanical artistry.

The audience froze.

What the hell was that?!

Not a Nascent Soul Avatar.

Not a puppet.

And then Lin Jing stepped inside the mech, sealing himself inside its cockpit.

“This is a special mechanized creation—the Mosquito-Powered Mech!

“You have artifacts to counter beasts and space techniques… but do you have anything to counter a mech?!"

Yan Xiangbei's face darkened.

The audience was in chaos.

And Mo Qianyi of Shenji Manor, an expert in mechanized constructs, was deep in thought.

"Wait… is this thing even an artifact…?!"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 105: Unscathed
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 105: Unscathed

A Beast Tamer who knew Buddhist techniques and mechanized arts, yet had never actually been seen taming a beast!

This was the thought running through the minds of every cultivator present.

However, Lin Jing strongly disagreed with that sentiment.

Strictly speaking, he wasn’t actually the pilot of the Mosquito-Powered Mech.

He had merely assembled the machine using mechanized techniques, but he couldn’t control it himself.

The real pilot was Pine Leaf Mouse.

So, in essence, he was still taming a beast—it was just that his beast was piloting the mech!

Of course, some refused to acknowledge that this mech was a mechanized construct at all.

For example, Mo Qianyi’s Celestial Shot Chariot contained a Soul Core, which she linked to her Exquisite Heart, allowing her to directly manipulate its transformations.

This was somewhat similar to how a cultivator refined and activated a magic artifact, but instead of channeling Qi, a mechanist controlled it with their spirit.

But this massive mech had no Soul Core—so to Yan Xiangbei, it couldn’t possibly be a mechanized construct.

Lin Jing, however, disagreed.

After all, Pine Leaf Mouse was controlling the mech through its spirit, much like how it controlled Red Flying Leaves.

Its connection to the mech’s wooden components was entirely spiritual.

If controlling a machine with one's spirit was the defining characteristic of mechanized constructs…

Then why wasn’t this mech considered one?!

"Take this!"

Zzzzzzzzzt!!!

Up until now, the Mosquito-Powered Mech had only passively defended and counterattacked.

But now, as its power surged to full capacity, a massive blood-colored longsword materialized in its hand.

A weapon forged entirely from blood essence!

The thirty-meter-long blood blade sliced through the air, slashing toward Yan Xiangbei!

Faced with this terrifying attack, Yan Xiangbei reacted instantly, summoning a mid-grade magic artifact—a miniature gate-shaped talisman.

Fueled by his Divine Physique, the tiny gate expanded, forming three layers of phantom barriers in front of him.

BOOM! BOOM! BOOM!

The blood sword struck, tearing through the first barrier… then the second… and finally shattered the third, sending Yan Xiangbei flying backward.

As he crashed onto the platform, he spat out a mouthful of blood.

"One attack… it took three layers of my defenses to finally stop it?!"

"This level of blood essence power surpasses ordinary Core Formation body cultivators. And the worst part… its blood energy isn’t depleting at all!"

Clutching his chest, Yan Xiangbei retrieved his gate-shaped talisman and decided to switch to offense.

With a flick of his wrist, he summoned a mid-grade flying sword.

"Go!"

Yan Xiangbei formed a hand seal, and the flying sword shot toward the mech’s chest.

But then—

The Mosquito-Powered Mech’s mouth suddenly opened.

A violent gust of blood energy surged forth, filling the air with a nauseating crimson mist.

The flying sword’s momentum slowed immediately, then was blown back, forcing Yan Xiangbei to recall it.

"What the hell is this thing?!"

Even with his Divine Physique, he still couldn’t enhance his mid-grade artifacts to match the sheer power of the mech!

The pressure was immense.

The audience had fallen into complete silence, their gazes fixed on the tiny creature piloting the massive mech.

"How dare you!!"

Yan Xiangbei’s eyes narrowed as his instincts screamed danger.

At that moment, to everyone’s shock—

The Mosquito-Powered Mech… self-destructed.

Because what kind of mech wouldn’t have a self-destruct system?!

BOOOOOM!!!

A monstrous crimson explosion engulfed the entire battle platform.

By the time the smoke cleared, Lin Jing and Pine Leaf Mouse were already hovering high above the battlefield, gazing down at the massive, fiery explosion below.

A single red leaf had carried them into the sky at the perfect moment.

The audience was stunned.

Even the high-altitude wind currents were twisting under the sheer force of the explosion.

"Well… I’d say that counts as defeating a powerful opponent without taking any damage."

High above the battlefield, Lin Jing’s hair flowed in the wind as he calmly observed the aftermath.

Pine Leaf Mouse wiped the sweat from its forehead, looking exhausted.

Yep, totally unscathed.

Except… the mech died a very tragic death.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 106: Shenji Manor
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 106: Shenji Manor

No one had expected that the moment Yan Xiangbei activated his high-grade magical treasure, Lin Jing would decisively choose to self-destruct his mech rather than continue fighting head-on.

In the arena, white flames spread in all directions, debris scattered everywhere. Yan Xiangbei stood there, covered in blood, looking utterly miserable. His aura was weak, and his mana was completely drained.

Activating a high-grade magical treasure had already required his full effort, yet instead of using it for offense, he was forced into defense.

He stared at the now-obliterated mech, which had been blown to nothing—not even dust remained. A smile crept onto his face, but it was a self-deprecating smile. He had lost… to a Beast Tamer. And that Beast Tamer had defeated him… using a mechanized construct.

No… no, that wasn't right. His opponent's pet beast’s spatial movement had been the true key to his defeat!

But so what? In the end, he still lost.

"You win." Standing on the stage, Yan Xiangbei withdrew his Extinction Treasure Lantern and looked at Lin Jing, who was slowly descending. Before losing consciousness, his gaze drifted toward the audience.

The cultivators from Burning Immortal Valley who had accompanied him were all in disbelief, unable to accept what had happened.

This was something they couldn’t even have imagined before.

"Thanks for the match." Lin Jing looked at his opponent but didn’t have time to dwell on it before an announcement rang out:

"The winner of Group Three—Lin Jing!"

As these words echoed through the arena, the audience was still reeling from the aftermath of the powerful explosion, the blinding light still lingering in their eyes.

"He really won?"

"Is it over? The third-ranked prodigy lost?"

"Was that an upset?!"

"Incredible. Lin Jing planned this perfectly! The moment Yan Xiangbei activated his high-grade magical treasure, he was left with no energy to use a space-restricting treasure. At that moment, Lin Jing only needed to abandon his construct, using its final detonation to drain Yan Xiangbei's mana—securing victory!"

"This Lin Jing… he’s ruthless. That mech was incredibly powerful. If it were me, I definitely would’ve wanted to fight head-to-head with the high-grade magical treasure. But he… just gave it up like that."

"But he won, so it was all worth it. After this fight, he’s practically guaranteed a spot in the top five of the Genius Rankings, maybe even the top three!"

The cultivators fell into silence, as if witnessing the rise of a new, supreme prodigy. The most terrifying part? Lin Jing stood there on the arena completely unscathed.

Many of the other prodigies were secretly pleased with this outcome.

With his high-grade magical treasure, Yan Xiangbei had been a massive threat. But now that he was eliminated, he wouldn't make it to the third round. And Lin Jing? He had lost his powerful mech in the battle, meaning his combat strength had taken a major hit.

The audience believed there was no way Lin Jing could have another mech of that caliber.

Even if he could rebuild it, it wouldn’t be something he could accomplish in a short time. Crafting another would require immense mental effort, and the rare materials needed were undoubtedly in short supply.

With Yan Xiangbei gone, Lin Jing would soon become famous across the Ancient Kingdom. But for some prodigies, defeating a weakened Lin Jing in the next round was an incredibly tempting opportunity.

After becoming the Group Three victor, Lin Jing returned to his seat to rest. Even though he hadn’t done much personally—his Ice Soul Gu had helped him resist the enchantment treasure, and his Pine Leaf Mouse had piloted the mech to dodge explosions—he still felt mentally exhausted.

"That guy…" The Dragon Palace cultivators stared at Qingdi in silence.

They wanted to ask—since when had Dragon Palace been renamed "Mechanized Dragon Palace"?

The matches continued.

Among the eight final winners, four were Academy graduates, further proving the Academy’s immense influence.

"The third round will follow a challenge-style format."

Tianji Pavilion would randomly select one contestant to serve as the Arena Master, forcing them to accept challenges from other prodigies. Losers would be eliminated, and the final standing Arena Master would be the overall winner of the tournament.

"Heaven's Regret Flower" announced that the third round would take place in seven days.

Unlike the chaotic eight-person free-for-all, this round placed greater emphasis on both luck and endurance.

Whoever became the first Arena Master would be in serious trouble. They could face seven consecutive battles, and even the strongest prodigy might not last to the end.

However, in the end, the Starry Cosmos Turtle's Blessing would not be given to the winner of the tournament, but to the contestant ranked first on the updated Genius Rankings, determined by Tianji Pavilion based on overall performance.

"Seven days… I should be able to convert all my Spirit Stones from the auctioned Rising Dragon Pills into high-grade spiritual plants by then."

"If my Pine Leaf Mouse is lucky, its Everlasting Emperor's Longevity Art will reach completion in time!"

"If it fully matures, the Pine Leaf Mouse will gain the plant manipulation abilities of a Core Formation-stage Wood Spirit, and combined with its Demon King True Form and Spatial Techniques, it might surpass my mosquito-powered mech..."

From the current tournament battles, Lin Jing felt that the strongest opponent was likely the Innate Dao Physique prodigy. He wasn’t sure if Pine Leaf Mouse alone would be enough to counter him.

As he was heading back to prepare for the third round, a group suddenly intercepted him.

At the front stood the second-ranked prodigy, Mo Qianyi, along with a group of Shenji Manor cultivators.

Behind her were several Core Formation and Nascent Soul cultivators, though Lin Jing didn’t sense any hostility.

"Miss Mo, is there something you need?" Lin Jing asked. Seeing her dressed in simple robes, calling her 'Fairy Mo' felt a bit strange, so he opted for a more casual address.

He had already guessed why they were here.

"Saint Lin," Mo Qianyi said, "Shenji Manor is interested in the design blueprint of your construct. Of course, we wouldn’t take it for free. Name your price—anything within Shenji Manor's ability, we can provide."

She paused, then added with a slight smile:

"As a Beast Tamer, perhaps you'd be interested in our techniques to grant intelligence to mechanized beasts, turning them into living constructs? If you’re interested, we can discuss further."

Meanwhile, beneath Divine Pine, a Dragon Carp suddenly sneezed.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 107: The Mechanical Turtle
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 107: The Mechanical Turtle

"Mechanical life?"

"You mean… a mechanized beast can develop sentience?" Lin Jing asked.

The plain-robed Mo Qianyi smiled. "If magical treasures can, why not mechanized beasts?"

"Fair point, but I assume the methods differ," Lin Jing responded.

He didn’t reject Shenji Manor's offer outright.

The mosquito-powered mech had strict requirements for use, but high-level cultivators from Shenji Manor might find a way to overcome them.

Its true power came from its energy source—the combination of the Blood Devil Vine, a mutated demonic plant, and the Cloud Sea Mosquito Beast’s blood, which created a terrifying fission reaction that fueled the entire construct.

The Cloud Sea Mosquito Beast wouldn’t be difficult for Shenji Manor to obtain, considering their Void Mystical Ancestor guarded their legacy.

However, there was one major issue—once the Blood Devil Vine absorbed blood and transformed into Blood Mosquitoes, only Lin Jing’s Pine Leaf Mouse could control them!

Without the Pine Leaf Mouse, the Blood Mosquitoes would attack indiscriminately—even Lin Jing himself had been targeted before. Getting them to cooperate willingly was no easy task.

But looking at it another way—if someone found a solution to this issue, the mosquito-powered mech could become a widely usable war machine.

Though Lin Jing hadn’t found such a solution yet…

Moreover, since his Pine Leaf Mouse held absolute control over the Blood Devil Vine, he didn’t have to worry about others using it against him.

A Deal Worth Making

From Lin Jing’s perspective, this trade was worth considering.

After all… besides the mosquito-powered mech, he had another, more traditional mechanized beast in his storage space.

"Saint Lin, you’ve heard of Ingenious Soul Cores, right?" Mo Qianyi asked. "Most high-level mechanized constructs require a Soul Core for better control."

"Yes."

"The Soul Core is the key to granting sentience to mechanized constructs. We have a method in Shenji Manor to evolve Soul Cores into full-fledged consciousness!"

Lin Jing raised an eyebrow. "Then why haven’t I heard of sentient mechanized constructs being used by Shenji Manor?"

Mo Qianyi chuckled. "You’re from the Beast Taming Sect, so you should understand—consciousness is a double-edged sword. While it allows mechanized constructs to act autonomously, it also makes them harder to control. Most mechanists prefer directly controlling their constructs rather than trusting their intelligence."

"If I’m not mistaken, the Beast Taming Sect has had cases of pet beast rebellion, right? Many mechanists in Shenji Manor fear similar risks, so they prefer constructs without willpower."

Lin Jing nodded.

"The method to create sentient mechanized beasts was developed by one of our ancestors," Mo Qianyi continued. "But very few mechanists actually use it. Currently, only a handful of guardian constructs left behind by our ancestors possess true consciousness."

"If you’re willing to trade the design blueprints of your mech, Shenji Manor will give you a mechanized beast blueprint capable of reaching the Nascent Soul stage—and even help it develop sentience."

Mo Qianyi’s lips curved into a knowing smile.

"Tell me, Saint Lin—wouldn’t you like to know if the Beast Taming Art of your sect could form a contract with a mechanized beast that has true self-awareness?"

The Negotiation

Lin Jing smirked.

"Interesting." He narrowed his eyes slightly. "But you’ll be disappointed. Even if I give you my blueprint, you won’t be able to replicate it. There’s a key material in the construct that only I possess—it’s the core of its power, defense, and sustained combat ability."

"Of course," he added casually. "I could provide you with some of that material. But the method to control it is unique to me. I can’t pass it on. You’d have to figure out your own method."

Mo Qianyi turned to the Shenji Manor elders beside her, silently communicating with them.

In truth, she hadn’t made up her mind yet—her proposal regarding mechanized beast intelligence had not been pre-approved by the elders.

As someone who had trained in mechanized arts since childhood, Mo Qianyi was deeply fascinated by Lin Jing’s new type of construct. Her love for mechanized arts made her eager to study it.

The elders of Shenji Manor exchanged glances and nodded.

If the material was from the Tianyuan Continent, they refused to believe they couldn’t find a way to control it.

Shenji Manor was the second most advanced force in material studies across the continent.

If they had the material, they were confident they could replicate it or create an alternative!

Mo Qianyi turned back to Lin Jing.

"Name your conditions," she said.

Lin Jing smiled. "Just as you suggested—I want a blueprint for a Nascent Soul-stage mechanized beast. And the secret method to grant it sentience."

Mo Qianyi shook her head. "The blueprint? Yes. The secret method? No. We won’t hand it over directly. But once you build the mechanized beast, we can help awaken its consciousness for you. Alternatively, we can give you a special construct that will allow you to gradually upgrade the Soul Core over time."

Lin Jing wasn’t surprised. He had expected them to refuse.

He had purposely asked for something he knew they wouldn’t agree to, so he could bargain for something else instead.

"In that case," Lin Jing said, "I have one more condition."

"If Shenji Manor figures out how to control the special material I mentioned, you have to share that method with me."

"Agreed."

"Then we have a deal."

Mo Qianyi didn’t bother haggling any further.

"Shenji Manor has over a hundred different Nascent Soul-level mechanized beasts to choose from," she said. "What kind do you want?"

Lin Jing stroked his chin, then extended his hand.

Light shimmered.

A two-meter-tall mechanized turtle appeared, standing on its hind legs. It wielded a heavy cannon on each shoulder and gripped a Mechanical Bamboo Rifle in its hands.

"This is…" The Shenji Manor elders scrutinized the construct.

"A mechanized turtle?"

So Lin Jing really was a mechanist!

It was no wonder he had agreed to the trade—he already owned a mechanized beast!

"Do you have any blueprints for turtle-type mechanized beasts? I want to upgrade this one," Lin Jing said.

He was a sentimental person.

Though this Mechanical Bamboo Turtle had long outlived its combat usefulness, reduced to helping the Pine Leaf Mouse water spiritual plants, it was one of his earliest constructs.

It had been his first—his second, if he counted his early failed experiments.

It was, in a way, the elder predecessor of his mosquito-powered mech, carrying the hard work and dedication of his early cultivation years.

Mo Qianyi studied it for a moment. "Yes, we have two Nascent Soul-grade turtle-type mechanized beasts."

"One is the Wave Turtle, a water-element construct. It specializes in water escape techniques, assists cultivators in underwater combat, and can manipulate tidal waves large enough to flood a city."

"The other is the Mountain Turtle, an earth-element construct. It has monstrous defensive capabilities, can self-repair by consuming minerals, and serves as a fortress-type construct, shielding its master from harm."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 108: The Completion of a Divine Art
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 108: The Completion of a Divine Art

"A turtle-type mechanized beast… it's dual-element, water and wood, right? In that case, the Wave Dragon Turtle would be a better choice. After all, you already have a defensive construct—your mech," Mo Qianyi suggested.

Lin Jing, however, shook his head. "Besides these two, is there anything else? Something more unique?"

Neither of the options appealed to him.

The Shenji Manor elders twitched their beards in exasperation—this kid had quite the appetite! Even a Nascent Soul-grade mechanized beast wasn’t good enough for him?

What did he want, a Nascent Soul Transformation-level construct?

That wasn’t even up for debate. Nascent Soul Transformation-level mechanized beasts were Shenji Manor's foundation, and no way would they trade one away.

Mo Qianyi hesitated before speaking. "There is one… a Core Formation-level turtle-type mechanized beast. I don’t know if you’d be interested. It has spatial attributes."

"Your other pet beast cultivates spatial techniques, right? You should understand how powerful that is. A spatial attribute construct is as valuable as a Nascent Soul-grade beast."

Spatial abilities…

Lin Jing's interest was piqued. "What can this Spatial Turtle do?"

He wasn't concerned about its lack of combat capabilities—he had plenty of firepower already.

Mo Qianyi explained, "It's called the Longevity Treasure Turtle, and its ability is treasure-hunting."

"This mechanized turtle possesses an innate ability to sense treasures. Additionally, like a storage artifact, it contains a vast internal space—even larger than any known storage treasure."

"It doesn’t need a mechanist to control it. The Longevity Treasure Turtle can roam freely in search of treasures, collecting anything it finds into its internal space, and then returning to its master."

"Whether it's a single Spirit Stone, a discarded magical artifact, or a rare spiritual herb, it will gather anything valuable it detects."

"Due to its spatial nature, it possesses a trait similar to that Blood Kill Tower prodigy—the Void Concealment Physique. Even Nascent Soul cultivators would struggle to detect it."

"Furthermore… because of its spatial abilities, it can enter small pocket dimensions, hidden realms, and ancient ruins to retrieve high-level magical treasures."

Lin Jing's eyes flickered. "Truly a treasure-hunting turtle… but why is it called the Longevity Treasure Turtle?"

Mo Qianyi sighed. "Because it's best at finding longevity-extending treasures."

"The mechanist who created it was an ancestor of Shenji Manor whose lifespan was nearing its end. He deeply understood the importance of longevity, so he built the Longevity Treasure Turtle. It was designed to only return once it had found a longevity-enhancing treasure."

She paused before adding, "But such treasures are incredibly difficult to find. Even after creating more of these Longevity Treasure Turtles, one of them spent twenty years searching before finally bringing back a single spiritual ginseng that extended life by a hundred years."

Mo Qianyi smiled wryly. "The only good thing about mechanized beasts, however, is that they don’t age. Unlike normal demonic beasts, as long as they are maintained and repaired, they can search for eternity."

"That’s true," Lin Jing agreed.

A mechanized beast’s lifespan depended entirely on the durability of its materials. While normal materials would deteriorate over time, regular maintenance and part replacements could extend a construct’s lifespan indefinitely.

Mo Qianyi looked at him. "So, have you decided?"

Lin Jing smiled.

A mechanized turtle that could find longevity treasures… what an ironic match for him.

Though he didn’t need longevity-enhancing treasures himself, as someone with the Longevity Body, he would inevitably watch his friends grow old while he remained young.

Using such treasures as gifts during festivals… wasn’t a bad idea.

But more than that…

What truly mattered was giving his first mechanized beast sentience—letting the turtle that had been by his side open its eyes and see the world.

"I’ll take the Longevity Treasure Turtle blueprint," Lin Jing decided.

Though it would be harder to craft and upgrade, the main difficulty lay in acquiring spatial materials.

Coincidentally, Pine Leaf Mouse might be able to help with that.

After all, the original mechanized turtle had been made using materials provided by Pine Leaf Mouse.

If the new version was also built using Pine Leaf Mouse’s resources, then it would truly be a complete cycle.

Besides… Pine Leaf Mouse seemed to have a natural talent for treasure hunting as well.

Would the Longevity Treasure Turtle inherit that ability after gaining sentience?

"Then it’s decided," Mo Qianyi said.

"We’ll make preparations and complete the trade after the Genius Tournament ends. No issues?"

Lin Jing nodded.

"For the transaction, shall we use Lotus Pavilion’s Contract Scrolls?"

"Agreed."

With the deal finalized, Lin Jing and Mo Qianyi separated to prepare for the next round of the Genius Tournament.

The Completion of the Everlasting Emperor's Longevity Art

The deal with Shenji Manor hadn’t slowed Lin Jing down.

Under his instructions, Xu Zhizhi continuously delivered high-grade spiritual plants over the next few days, rapidly consuming the Spirit Stones he had earned from auctioning the Rising Dragon Pills.

Inside his Beast Taming Space, Pine Leaf Mouse, now clad in Daoist robes, radiated an overwhelming aura.

It grabbed a high-grade spiritual plant, instantly draining it into a withered husk before tossing it aside.

Who could have imagined a wood-element spirit beast cultivating this brutally?

This was the Everlasting Emperor’s Longevity Art—a technique that absorbed life force to extend one’s own.

For five days, Pine Leaf Mouse absorbed and refined spiritual plants without pause.

Finally, its body began to change.

Seated cross-legged, its life force surged like a flowing spring, reshaping its form and granting it new life.

Ordinary squirrels only lived ten years at most, and even a cultivated squirrel spirit wouldn’t live longer than a human cultivator of the same rank.

But now, Pine Leaf Mouse had defied that fate.

As a Foundation Establishment-stage Pine Leaf Mouse, it now had a thousand-year lifespan—nearly matching Lin Jing himself.

Even as a Longevity Body cultivator, Lin Jing’s Qi Refinement-stage lifespan was barely over a thousand years.

However, due to his Ice Soul Gu parasite, his life force drained at an exceptionally slow rate.

Lin Jing estimated that he would live far longer than the standard for his physique—possibly over two thousand years.

And now…

Pine Leaf Mouse had finally achieved full mastery of the Everlasting Emperor’s Longevity Art—extending its lifespan by another thousand years.

It now had two thousand years to live.

For a mere squirrel, this lifespan potential had already surpassed countless Demon Kings in the Beast Taming Sect, many of whom had survived for millennia only because they were long-lived species like turtles, elephants, parrots, and dragons.

But Pine Leaf Mouse…

With just a single cultivation breakthrough, its lifespan had skyrocketed to the same level as ancient demonic beasts.

"Congratulations," Lin Jing said sincerely, sensing Pine Leaf Mouse's breakthrough from his inn.

At least now, he didn’t have to worry about it dying of old age too soon.

He had always feared that, as someone with the Longevity Body, he would outlive all of his contracted pet beasts.

But now…

Perhaps he and his pets could find a way to ascend together—becoming immortals and living as long as the heavens themselves.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 109: Finally, Beast Taming
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 109: Finally, Beast Taming

The wooden lot with Lin Jing’s name fell out, leaving the entire audience momentarily stunned. If this was truly a random draw, then whoever became the first arena master wouldn’t have mattered. But after the Tianji Pavilion cultivator explicitly stated that the first arena master was highly likely to be someone with great fortune and the ultimate champion of the Genius Rankings Battle, this result suddenly seemed rather uncanny.

"How could it be him? In just seven days, it’s almost impossible to repair a destroyed mechanized construct. Could he really have a second or even third one? If that’s the case, that would be truly terrifying."

"Even so, it doesn’t mean he’s destined to be first. I don’t believe any mechanist can outmaneuver the geniuses of Shenji Manor, nor do I believe that any mechanized construct could surpass the legendary Innate Dao Physique or the Spell Spirit Origin! One’s own strength is the foundation of everything."

"Let’s not jump to conclusions too soon. Master Tian Huihua also said it’s only ‘possible’ that he has a chance. These things are unpredictable— even true divinations aren’t always 100% accurate, let alone a mere lot draw."

The spectators discussed fervently, while the contestants also appeared puzzled.

Especially those who had been eliminated by Lin Jing—they hadn’t yet left Tianji City.
