One of the geniuses from the Assassins’ Tower, upon seeing that Lin Jing was now a strong contender for the championship, suddenly felt his earlier frustration of being eliminated dissipate. First, Lin Jing had defeated the third-ranked genius, and now he was likely to win it all. In that case, losing to him didn’t seem so disgraceful after all. At least this way, he might not be punished by the Assassins’ Tower— after all, his opponent had simply been too terrifying.

Yan Xiangbei also furrowed his brows, eager to know whether Lin Jing really had more of those so-called mechs hidden up his sleeve.

As confusion spread among the crowd, Lin Jing let out a sigh, feeling somewhat troubled.

Although he was determined to claim first place— there was no way he would give up the Precious Beast’s Blessing— the thought of enduring a grueling gauntlet of challenges left him feeling exhausted already. After all, he had to stay completely focused… to avoid getting injured.

Seven consecutive battles. To accomplish that while remaining unscathed would require an immense amount of effort.

"If I display overwhelming strength from the start, maybe some geniuses will give up challenging me. Would that make things easier?" Lin Jing pondered.

Then, under the silent gazes of the Dragon Palace disciples, his figure flickered and instantly appeared on the arena platform.

Whoosh!

As Lin Jing materialized, seven pairs of sharp eyes locked onto him.

"Then I’ll take on the role of the first arena master," Lin Jing declared.

He looked toward Master Tian Huihua, who smiled back at him. While Tian Huihua didn’t know what trump cards Lin Jing was hiding, he was certain that Lin Jing indeed carried the fortune of a champion. No wonder his little junior sister had taken a liking to him— inviting Lin Jing to participate in the Genius Rankings Battle had clearly been the right decision.

"So, he wasn’t bluffing after all. Good thing I spent so much time introducing him to the Starry Cosmos Turtle’s blessing," another disciple from Tianji Pavilion remarked casually, sitting among the spectators. Seeing Lin Jing chosen as the arena master, he smirked slightly, curious to see what other tricks this Longevity Body cultivator had in store.

"Who will challenge him?"

Tian Huihua scanned the crowd.

Chen Zhong and Mo Qianyi didn’t react immediately, especially Mo Qianyi— having just struck a deal with Lin Jing, challenging him first would seem too ungrateful.

"I still don’t believe he can win first place. I’ll wait for him to be eliminated and then challenge the next arena master," Mo Qianyi shook his head.

"I’ll challenge him!"

The first to respond was the fourth-ranked genius, Zhao Qingquan!

He laughed heartily. Others might worry that becoming the second arena master meant facing relentless challenges and excessive energy depletion, but he wasn’t afraid!

As an Innate Spring Source Physique cultivator, his mana recovery was astonishingly fast— his true essence flowed endlessly. He never had to worry about exhaustion. Moreover, having mastered a secret healing technique, he could recover from injuries at all times, making him a well-rounded powerhouse.

Although he knew he could defeat Lin Jing, the current spotlight on Lin Jing intrigued him. Zhao Qingquan wanted to experience Lin Jing’s strength firsthand.

"City Lord of Qingquan City, Zhao Qingquan. Junior, please enlighten me!"

Zhao Qingquan, also a student of the academy, though not yet graduated, stepped onto the arena and bowed to Lin Jing.

"Please," Lin Jing responded calmly, looking at his senior.

Seeing Zhao Qingquan step up, the other geniuses watched intently. Among the four prominent competitors, aside from Chen Zhong, who wasn’t confident about defeating He Zimei, even Mo Qianyi— ranked seventh— had confidence in defeating Zhao Qingquan.

Ranking was one thing, but every genius developed at a rapid pace. Missing a single moment of enlightenment or an additional technique could easily change the rankings.

What made Zhao Qingquan particularly formidable was that he was a city lord who had achieved multiple feats of merit. This year, his city had even been renamed Qingquan City in his honor. Furthermore, in all his ranked battles, he had never once revealed his eighth-stage Hidden Dragon Secret Technique, which further strengthened his advantage.

At this point, no one knew Zhao Qingquan’s true limits.

"Indestructible Immortal Gang!"

As soon as the battle began, a fierce azure wind surged, forming a protective barrier around Zhao Qingquan. Standing amidst the wind, his hair danced, and he gazed at Lin Jing with an expression of composure.

"The Indestructible Immortal Gang is one of my Zhao family’s top-tier defensive techniques. Our ancestor once used this technique to withstand an attack from a Grand Dao cultivator. I wonder how it compares to your mechanized constructs’ defenses?"

"Indestructible Immortal Gang?!"

Many in the audience were shocked by the spell Zhao Qingquan had just unveiled. This was indeed one of the Zhao family’s ultimate defensive spells, rumored to be strong enough to withstand the attacks of immortals if cultivated to the highest level. However, due to its extreme difficulty and mana consumption, even within the Zhao family, very few had ever successfully learned it. Without the mana reserves of a Core Formation cultivator, one couldn’t even sustain the Indestructible Immortal Gang for a few seconds.

"So that’s it—he’s mastered a new spell!"

Yan Xiangbei took a deep breath, sensing the strength of the Immortal Gang. Its defense already surpassed the protective artifacts he himself had. If he were to attack Zhao Qingquan, without using a top-tier magic treasure, he likely wouldn’t be able to break through it.

The most terrifying part was that Zhao Qingquan seemed capable of maintaining this defense indefinitely!

In such a scenario, the only way to defeat him would be to strike with enough force to break through the Immortal Gang and do so faster than his true essence could replenish. That was an incredibly difficult feat.

"This remains to be seen."

Facing Zhao Qingquan’s question, Lin Jing simply shook his head.

"My mech has already been destroyed, so I won’t be able to compare it with Brother Zhao’s technique," he said.

At those words, Zhao Qingquan was visibly surprised. Without that terrifying mech, how did Lin Jing plan to claim first place?

Lin Jing smiled slightly, seeing the doubt in his opponent’s eyes.

"Since we’ve reached the final round, I suppose it’s time to reveal my true strength," he said.

"Though my cultivation is still low, and I have yet to master the Beast Taming Sect’s three core secret arts, I have devoted myself to nurturing my pet beasts and have achieved considerable success in that regard."

"Pine Leaf Mouse."

"Go and test Brother Zhao’s Indestructible Immortal Gang. Let’s see if it’s as formidable as the legends say."

As Lin Jing spoke, the robe-clad squirrel, previously seen only assisting with spatial movement techniques, slowly appeared on his shoulder.

"Wait… he’s finally going to use beast taming in battle? But this squirrel… doesn’t seem nearly as impressive as that mech!"

The audience was momentarily dumbfounded.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 110: The Squirrel Was His Trump Card All Along!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 110: The Squirrel Was His Trump Card All Along!

The Pine Leaf Mouse was simply too small. For most demonic beasts, the stronger their bloodline, the larger their body.

Moreover, its cultivation level was too low— only at the Early Foundation Establishment Stage.

Everyone's understanding of this squirrel was limited to its mysterious spatial abilities. Without those, Lin Jing would’ve struggled to defeat Yan Xiangbei.

Though its space manipulation had been the key to turning the tides, the real factor that had forced Yan Xiangbei into a desperate situation had been Lin Jing’s bizarre mechanized construct.

Now, without that terrifying firepower and defense, Lin Jing’s strength had, in the eyes of the crowd, dropped significantly. Was he planning to break through the Indestructible Immortal Gang with a mere Diamond Fist? Impossible.

"That squirrel spirit’s appearance is rather peculiar," some cultivators noted. Unlike ordinary squirrel spirits, this one had ears and a tail that resembled plant structures.

"It’s certainly unusual. Then again, if it weren’t special, no ordinary squirrel spirit could possibly learn spatial techniques."

There were too many strange demonic beasts in the world, so the cultivators didn't dwell on the Pine Leaf Mouse’s plant-like characteristics. Among the geniuses, Mo Qianyi murmured, "Is Lin Jing planning to use that squirrel spirit to break through the Indestructible Immortal Gang?"

Unfortunately, the pressure during battle had been too overwhelming for Yan Xiangbei to notice the red leaf in time. If he had, he might have changed the outcome.

Now, everyone was waiting to see how Lin Jing planned to use a mere Foundation Establishment squirrel spirit to break through an Indestructible Immortal Gang supported by the Spring Source Physique.

And that wasn't even all Zhao Qingquan had.

On the arena platform, Zhao Qingquan waved a single hand. Outside the protective Immortal Gang, a colossal sword formed purely from true essence emerged in the sky, radiating the ferocious power of the White Tiger. The sword's presence was overwhelming, and as it descended, many in the audience gasped in recognition.

It was one of the Zhao Family’s top-tier offensive techniques— the White Tiger Divine Sword!

Under Zhao Qingquan’s control, the massive sword plummeted toward Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse.

The pressure it exerted was immense, far more terrifying than even the murderous aura of the Assassins’ Tower genius.

Most assumed that Lin Jing would use spatial techniques to evade the attack.

But then— something completely unexpected happened.

The Pine Leaf Mouse, rather than retreating, leapt from Lin Jing’s shoulder and landed on the arena floor, standing firm against the overwhelming pressure.

Then— its body began to expand at an unimaginable speed!

As it grew, a mysterious wooden material spread across its body. This material continued to grow and pulse with an exaggerated vitality, as though a divine tree was sprouting from within the Pine Leaf Mouse!

BOOM!

In an instant, its size increased to over a hundred meters tall, its massive form towering over the arena.

The Pine Leaf Mouse had transformed into a colossal squirrel, its entire body seemingly composed of ancient, living wood!

The Squirrel Colossus still resembled its pre-evolution form, but now, its body was covered in branches, and those branches bore countless pinecones, making it look like a divine tree merged with a demonic beast.

It was no longer a flesh-and-blood creature, but a being of plant life!

Its entire lifeform had changed!

"What the—?!"

The transformation was so rapid that the audience barely had time to react. They could only gape as, in its giant wooden hands, the Pine Leaf Mouse caught the White Tiger Divine Sword— and then, with a crisp snap, broke it in half.

Zhao Qingquan's pupils contracted sharply.

That White Tiger Divine Sword was a technique capable of challenging Core Formation cultivators— and the squirrel had just shattered it with ease!

And then, an even more terrifying scene unfolded.

A third eye opened on the Pine Leaf Mouse’s forehead— its Pinecone Third Eye!

From that eye, pure life force condensed into a beam of radiant white light, which blasted straight toward Zhao Qingquan inside the Indestructible Immortal Gang.

BOOM!

A deafening explosion shook the arena, sending a massive cloud of smoke and debris into the air.

As the smoke cleared, everyone saw that several layers of the Indestructible Immortal Gang had been destroyed.

Within what remained of the Immortal Gang, Zhao Qingquan stood, his expression frozen in shock.

That attack… had breached his defenses?!

And that squirrel… was still only at the Foundation Establishment Stage?!

Zhao Qingquan took a deep breath, forcefully stabilizing his defenses.

But then—

Lin Jing, standing atop the Squirrel Colossus, smiled slightly.

"Not bad. As expected of the Innate Spring Source Physique," Lin Jing said. "But… can you withstand this next attack?"

Golden Buddhist light surged around Lin Jing’s body.

The audience gasped as, from the Squirrel Colossus’s back, a hundred enormous wooden arms sprouted, each radiating a divine golden glow.

Some of the hands clenched into fists.

Others spread into open palms.

Each radiated a sacred power comparable to a Core Formation cultivator!

Under the golden light, the Squirrel Colossus looked like a divine Buddhist statue!

But Lin Jing was not the only one with a Buddhist affinity— the monks of the Demon-Suppressing Pagoda had granted both him and his Pine Leaf Mouse a Buddhist talent.

Now, by activating the seventh layer of the Beast Taming Art, Lin Jing was able to transfer his Buddhist power to his Pine Leaf Mouse— allowing it to merge this energy with its overwhelming wood-element control to create a fusion technique.

Hundred-Armed Demon Buddha Statue!

A combined ultimate move— executed by Lin Jing, the Beast Tamer, and his Pine Leaf Mouse!

Zhao Qingquan's jaw slackened.

An ominous feeling welled up inside him.

He instantly activated his Hidden Dragon Secret Technique, temporarily boosting his cultivation from Perfected Foundation Establishment to Early Core Formation.

But—

Too late.

A hundred golden Diamond Fist palms rained down upon the Indestructible Immortal Gang.

One strike. Two strikes. Three. A hundred. A thousand!

Under the relentless barrage, the Indestructible Immortal Gang began to shatter and reform at an alarming pace.

The battle had turned into a terrifying contest of offense and defense, causing the watching geniuses to instinctively hold their breath.

The Pine Leaf Mouse’s attack speed was simply too fast.

Having mastered the Everlasting Emperor's Longevity Art, it possessed the power of a Core Formation-level plant spirit.

But unlike a Core Formation wood cultivator, a plant spirit did not manipulate external plants— its own body was the plant!

Thus, the Hundred-Armed Buddha Statue was as easy for it to control as a natural extension of its limbs.

After an uncountable number of blows—

The Pine Leaf Mouse finally ceased its assault.

The arena lay in ruins.

Zhao Qingquan’s Indestructible Immortal Gang had been completely destroyed.

He lay at the bottom of a massive crater, surrounded by rubble, his pupils blank and unfocused, his mouth frozen in disbelief.

Zhao Qingquan had lost.

Even Chen Zhong took a deep breath, his gaze snapping toward Lin Jing atop the Squirrel Colossus.

"This… is a Demon King True Form?! At this level, its bloodline rivals a Royal Dragon Cub!"

The audience fell silent.

Then—

The arena erupted with shouts of astonishment!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 111: The First Clash
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 111: The First Clash

The transformation of the Pine Leaf Mouse deeply shocked the other prodigies. After mastering the Everlasting Emperor’s Longevity Art, the true form of the Demon King Pine Leaf Mouse was even more ferocious than the Mosquito-Powered Mech from before. Beneath its massive hundred-meter body, a hundred colossal arms sprouted, each fist and palm radiating power far beyond the Foundation Establishment stage.

Even with the aid of the Hidden Dragon Arts, Zhao Qingquan had temporarily boosted his cultivation to Early Core Formation, yet he was still ruthlessly crushed by this gigantic colossus. The spectators suspected that this behemoth’s strength was already formidable enough to challenge the Earth Rankings of the Genius List.

"This... actually makes me feel like I don’t want to face it..." A Mid Core Formation elder took a deep breath. Unlike Zhao Qingquan, who only had Core Formation-level mana but lacked the corresponding divine abilities, this elder could fully utilize Core Formation Divine Abilities. Yet even he felt an instinctive dread toward this colossus.

Many spectators gasped in disbelief. No wonder Tianji Pavilion had declared that Lin Jing had the fortune to win the championship. Now, with the Demon King Pine Leaf Mouse's true form displayed before them, they speculated that even Chen Zhong, the top prodigy, would struggle against it.

"The true form of a demonic beast is a symbol of its innate potential, just like Heavenly Physiques among human cultivators—no, even rarer than that! It’s said that it might rival the boost granted by a Nascent Soul Dharma Form. Right now, it’s like a Foundation Establishment cultivator manifesting a Nascent Soul Dharma Form, whereas Zhao Qingquan is merely a Foundation Establishment cultivator who temporarily gained Core Formation-level strength but hasn’t acquired any Core Formation Divine Abilities yet. How could he possibly be a match?"

"But that’s not even the most terrifying part. A Foundation Establishment early-stage beast should, in theory, struggle to control such a powerful true form and would at most be able to launch crude attacks. However, what’s truly shocking is how skilled it is at controlling its true form! Those hundred wooden arms— even a Core Formation wood-element cultivator wouldn’t be able to wield them so effortlessly!"

"When unparalleled strength is paired with absolute control, that is when a creature becomes truly terrifying..."

The cultivators present were all stunned by the sheer power and precision of the Pine Leaf Mouse. Even some Nascent Soul cultivators found themselves taken aback—it was almost unimaginable that such devastating strength was being unleashed by a mere Foundation Establishment-stage demonic beast.

"This guy..." A disciple from the Myriad Venom Sect, who had recently returned from the Cloud Sea Mountains, furrowed his brows. Looking at the golden colossus, he doubted whether his poison arts could even erode its Buddha-like resilience. The odds seemed slim, and the wooden-like body of the Pine Leaf Mouse didn’t seem susceptible to poison either.

As for Lin Jing, the Beast Tamer... The disciple glanced at him again and felt that even if he could break past the colossus, it would be nearly impossible to land a direct attack on Lin Jing himself—especially since that squirrel also wielded spatial techniques.

"I can’t challenge him for now..."

The strength of a Five-Element Physique prodigy lay in their mastery over five different elemental arts, allowing them to combine them into even stronger divine abilities. However, after witnessing the synergy between the Pine Leaf Mouse’s wood-element and Buddhist-element techniques, this prodigy suddenly realized that his own combination spells were... mediocre by comparison. He’d probably be annihilated instantly under the barrage of those thousand fists.

Unless he could fully master his Five-Element Physique, allowing his five-element techniques to cycle in harmony, he stood no chance— but that would take a long time.

For the moment, all the major prodigies hesitated to challenge Lin Jing’s terrifying demonic pet. They didn’t see a path to victory. If the difference in strength was small, they wouldn’t mind taking a shot. But after seeing how Lin Jing effortlessly crushed Zhao Qingquan, they had to admit—the gap was simply too vast.

Even Mo Qianyi, ranked second among the prodigies, hesitated slightly. Now it made sense why Lin Jing had so casually traded away his mech designs—because to him, the mechs weren’t even that important.

This squirrel was the real monster, stronger than thousands of troops combined!

Mo Qianyi had the urge to test her strength against it, but her gaze first shifted toward Chen Zhong, the top prodigy. At this moment, the first-ranked prodigy had Dao patterns flowing in his eyes, filled with unexpected excitement and battle intent.

"The world truly has countless geniuses. Before today, I never imagined that a Beast Tamer could reach such a level. Fellow Daoist Lin, allow me to challenge you next!"

Chen Zhong's body seemed to defy gravity as he floated into the arena, rising to the same hundred-meter height as Lin Jing, maintaining his gaze at an equal level—without touching the ground.

The top prodigy, Chen Zhong, had stepped into the arena!

The audience's hearts pounded. This was it! This was what they had been waiting for!

"A battle between the foremost prodigies..."

"It’s finally happening!"

Leaf Mouse, standing on the Pine Leaf Mouse’s shoulder, smiled. "I hope no one else challenges me before I get to crush Lin Jing."

He knew Lin Jing’s limits—the question was whether he needed to reveal even more trump cards to handle this.

"Go!"

Lin Jing flicked his wrist. In an instant, a horizontal gravity wave transformed into a shockwave, surging toward the towering Pine Leaf Mouse. Though the Demon King True Form had greatly amplified its strength, it had also slightly reduced its agility.

Faced with Lin Jing’s attack, the Pine Leaf Mouse could only respond with Diamond Palm Fists. But when its Buddhist fists clashed with Lin Jing’s gravity wave, the sheer force shredded the gravity field into a blur.

"Incredible! That squirrel is insane—it completely ignored Lin Jing’s gravity technique!"

"Wait... could it be... the legendary Innate Dao Physique is finally being tested?"

Suddenly, realizing that gravity magic was ineffective, Lin Jing’s palm began to glow with a star-shaped, ethereal entity.

The entity was entirely golden, traced with white patterns, and the moment Lin Jing summoned it, a thunderous boom erupted. It was as if the sky was collapsing—the Pine Leaf Mouse’s punches froze in mid-air.

Its massive body suddenly stopped moving, as though locked in place.

"D-d-d-dazed!"

At that moment, even its hundred colossal arms began to crack under the overwhelming gravity, breaking off from its body one by one.

"That’s... a Spell Spirit Origin?"

Everyone turned to look at the golden spiritual entity in Chen Zhong’s hand.

Chen Zhong had finally unleashed his Spell Spirit Origin, and its sheer power left the audience in shock.

And this was merely a basic gravity spell’s Spell Spirit Origin—yet it had completely immobilized the terrifying Demon King True Form that had shaken the entire arena moments ago.

With its arms shattered and its body paralyzed, the Pine Leaf Mouse no longer held its invincible aura.

"So, this is all it amounts to?" Chen Zhong shook his head. "It seems... my Spell Spirit Origin is still superior. But I must admit, making me use it—that alone is worthy of pride."

"Mm?"

Just as he spoke, Chen Zhong suddenly froze—as if sensing something unexpected.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 112: No Hope in Sight
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 112: No Hope in Sight

"I was wrong… The First Prodigy truly lives up to his name. I actually entertained the thought that Lin Jing might defeat him… but now that I think about it, how could that even be possible? One is the prodigy of the Spirit Dao Sect, the greatest sect of the Ancient Celestial Kingdom, while the other is merely the Holy Son of the Beast Taming Sect, a third-rate sect..."

"Raising a pet beast to this level is already a miraculous stroke of fortune for the Beast Taming Sect, but in the end, the gap in foundation between the two sects is simply too vast!"

With the Spell Spirit Origin unleashed, Chen Zhong had completely immobilized the Pine Leaf Mouse, leaving it struggling in place. Under the immense gravity, its arms crumbled one by one, until only its main body remained, barely holding on due to its incredible physique.

For a human cultivator, such gravity would be outright lethal.

Chen Zhong turned his gaze toward Lin Jing, and the spectators followed.

Yet Lin Jing remained seated atop the Pine Leaf Mouse’s shoulder, seemingly unaffected!

Around him, shimmering flakes—like falling snow—drifted in the air, creating a mesmerizing aura that made him appear mysterious and untouchable.

"Hmm?"

The gravity field had even crushed the Pine Leaf Mouse, yet Lin Jing himself was completely unaffected?

"It’s happening again... That strange ice energy! What exactly is it?" Wang Xiao from Dragon Palace muttered.

"Wait a second… That power can ignore Chen Zhong’s gravity technique?!" Yan Xiangbei was stunned. Previously, Lin Jing had used this very ice power to bypass a Harmony Sect treasure, but he hadn’t given it much thought. Now, however, it seemed far more peculiar.

"No matter how much one refines this technique, Fellow Daoist Chen, you're not actually manipulating true gravity—you’re merely simulating it with true energy. Since it's only true energy and not the force of Heaven and Earth itself, as long as I find the right method, I can negate it."

Lin Jing sat calmly upon the Pine Leaf Mouse, speaking as if explaining something mundane.

His body continuously emitted frost energy, freezing the true energy forming the gravity field around him.

"Pine Leaf Mouse!"

At Lin Jing’s command, the Pine Leaf Mouse—despite its initial struggle—gradually began moving again. Though slow, it stretched out its arms and reached toward Chen Zhong.

The arena fell into a dead silence.

But then, with a mere snort, Chen Zhong compressed the gravity field, causing the giant arms to collapse again, fractures spreading like cracks in ice.

Yet what shocked Chen Zhong next was that, in the blink of an eye, the Pine Leaf Mouse’s true form regenerated completely—its immense life force allowing it to heal at astonishing speed. Again, it raised its arms toward Chen Zhong.

"Your Demon King True Form must be exhausting… how much longer can you last?"

Chen Zhong positioned his planetary Spell Spirit Origin high in the sky, crushing the Pine Leaf Mouse’s arms once more.

With just one ability, he had completely suppressed the Pine Leaf Mouse.

But despite this, his gaze remained fixed on Lin Jing, frowning deeply.

"Indeed… I underestimated the Spell Spirit Origin."

"I thought my Pine Leaf Mouse could withstand at least some of it, but now I see… its cultivation is still too low. If it had reached Great Perfection of Foundation Establishment, it might have had a chance."

Lin Jing shook his head. Under this terrifying gravity, the Pine Leaf Mouse was burning through its energy at an alarming rate. Soon, it would lose the ability to maintain its Demon King True Form, making resistance against the Spell Spirit Origin’s gravity even harder.

"So, you must have other trump cards, right?" Chen Zhong stared at the snowflakes drifting around Lin Jing, speaking calmly.

A mere ice technique couldn’t possibly counter his gravity spell so easily.

"Well, well…"

Meanwhile, in the Dragon Palace faction, someone muttered hesitantly, "Could it be… is he actually going to use that?"

Wang Xiao and the others turned their attention toward Ice Soul, instantly understanding what was being implied.

"I was hoping not to use it. But it seems… he’s still too weak after all."

Ice Soul sighed.

The conversation between them confused the audience—what were they talking about?

"Whatever it is, it doesn’t matter. At this level, there are almost no techniques that can defeat a Spell Spirit Origin!"

The elders and disciples of the Spirit Dao Sect were brimming with confidence. Lin Jing was impressive, but Spell Spirit Origins weren’t something that could be easily overcome.

And yet, at that moment, they suddenly felt a bone-chilling cold creeping over them.

"...I have no choice, then."

Lin Jing stood up from the Pine Leaf Mouse’s shoulder.

In that instant, the temperature of the entire arena plummeted.

Though it wasn’t enough to harm the audience, it felt like winter itself had descended upon them.

Lin Jing slowly descended from the Pine Leaf Mouse, and in that moment, he activated the power of the Ice Soul Gu.

His true energy merged with the Ice Soul Gu’s power, and in an instant—

His black hair turned white.

His dark eyes became an icy blue.

Snow-white strands danced in the frozen wind, and what truly stunned the spectators was that, at that moment—

The entire gravity field was instantly frozen solid.

Above the arena, a massive, towering phantom began to take shape—

A colossal ice spider, stretching high into the clouds, with countless frosted legs piercing the edges of the stage.

Its entire spectral form, forged from pure cold, had completely frozen the gravity field—and for the first time, Chen Zhong’s soul trembled in fear.

"I suppose, as long as I freeze the entire gravity field, the match is decided, isn't it?"

Lin Jing’s snow-white hair fluttered in the frigid air as he spoke calmly.

And just as he finished speaking—

The freezing had already taken effect.

The Pine Leaf Mouse instantly regain full mobility.

"You—!"

Chen Zhong’s eyes widened in shock, staring at Lin Jing as if he were an impossible existence.

"Gu Insect Fusion… You’re housing a Gu in your body?! That power level… This is one of the Ten Rare Gu—the Ice Soul Gu!"

Chen Zhong couldn’t comprehend it. How could Lin Jing possess something like this?

As Chen Zhong’s shout echoed, the entire arena fell into chaos.

"Ten Rare Gu?!"

"The Ice Soul Gu?! But every Rare Gu carries the bloodline of the Gu King! Once they mature, they rival Nascent Soul cultivators!"

The most terrifying thing about Gu techniques was that a Gu Master could fuse with their Gu, unleashing power beyond comprehension. A Nascent Soul-level Gu, when fused, could even wield Void Mystical power!

It was said that, at the height of the Gu God Sect, all Ten Rare Gu were in their possession. Each one granted its user Void Mystical combat prowess.

And yet, now, one of them was in the hands of a mere Qi Refinement cultivator?!

And he could perfectly control it?!

If that were true...

Then—

After a thousand frozen punches, Chen Zhong was finally knocked backward, barely saved by his Spell Spirit Origin, which formed a protective repulsion field around him.

Despite avoiding fatal injury, his expression was hollow.

"I've lost."

Even if he didn’t know how Lin Jing got the Ice Soul Gu, he could no longer see a way to win.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 113: The Third Contract
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 113: The Third Contract

Just as Lin Jing had anticipated, displaying overwhelming strength would make the other prodigies abandon even the thought of resisting.

Under his gaze, even Mo Qianyi hesitated, uncertain of how she could possibly defeat him.

Fighting a mechanized cultivator was notoriously difficult because one had to break through their many layers of mechanized defenses to strike them directly. However, for Lin Jing, who possessed the Ice Soul Gu, this was hardly an issue.

High-level mechanized cultivators formed a direct connection between their mind and their mechanical creations, linking them through a soul core. Yet, the toxic energy of the Ice Soul Gu could travel through this very connection, delivering its poison to the cultivator.

In other words, their greatest strength became their fatal weakness!

"Looks like the Starry Cosmos Turtle’s blessing is out of reach," Mo Qianyi sighed.

In truth, when she first saw Chen Zhong’s Spell Spirit Origin, she had already started doubting her ability to defeat him.

Yet, in the blink of an eye, someone even stronger than Chen Zhong had emerged!

If the second-ranked prodigy had already recognized the insurmountable difference in strength, how could the others not see it?

The remaining prodigies quickly came to terms with reality—challenging Lin Jing now would only humiliate themselves. Winning would be impressive, but if they lost, they’d be ridiculed for lacking self-awareness.

Not everyone had the courage to challenge someone far stronger than themselves. Such individuals were rare.

“No one else?” Tian Huihua chuckled, watching the silent prodigies.

With the top prodigy replaced, and someone contracting one of the Ten Rare Gu, this Genius Rankings Battle had proven well worth hosting.

"If there are no more challengers, then the final champion of this Genius Rankings Battle is—Lin Jing of the Beast Taming Sect! Three days from now, the Tianji Pavilion will release an updated Genius Rankings, based on the performances in this battle!"

“Wait, no way… Are you telling me the final champion is actually…?”

“Is there even any doubt? Didn’t you see his strength?”

A nearby cultivator took a deep breath and looked at Lin Jing, who floated above the icy battlefield, his presence almost ethereal.

“That guy… contracted one of the Ten Rare Gu!”

"If you’ve studied history, then you’d know just how much trouble the Ten Rare Gu caused the Ancient Celestial Kingdom in the past. Countless powerful cultivators perished trying to eliminate the ten Gu Masters who controlled them."

"The Gu God Sect… is not weaker than the Spirit Dao Sect, the number one sect of the Ancient Celestial Kingdom. In fact, it might even be stronger. The Ten Rare Gu were originally their lost relics. Now that one of them has resurfaced… this guy is in danger. The Holy Son of the Beast Taming Sect will have a rough road ahead."

"The Gu God Sect will never allow something like this to remain outside their control. The moment a Rare Gu is born, they will stop at nothing to retrieve it."

As the audience whispered among themselves, they could already envision the storm Lin Jing was about to face.

The Ten Rare Gu were even rarer than Spell Spirit Origins, but they were also an incredibly dangerous burden.

Lin Jing remained expressionless, hovering above the battlefield, sensing the cautious gazes of the prodigies and the murmurs of the audience.

Of course, he understood their concerns.

But the moment he obtained the Ice Soul Gu, its exposure had been inevitable. Since it couldn’t be hidden forever, he might as well use it to cement his dominance.

At that very moment, Lin Jing felt the power of the Hidden Dragon Seal surging, its fortune energy growing at a terrifying rate—and this was before the official rankings were even updated!

His blue eyes flickered toward Tian Huihua, as if to say:

"You revealed this much about me just to boost your Tianji Pavilion’s newspaper sales… so I assume I’ll be taking first place in the rankings, right?"

Though it was eight versus one, he was a Beast Tamer. It was perfectly fair to use all of his contracted beasts!

"What a terrifying prodigy… Longevity Body, Ice Soul Gu—can he even survive long enough to reach the Nascent Soul Stage?"

Tian Huihua met Lin Jing’s piercing gaze, feeling a strange sense of curiosity.

Meanwhile, within the Academy’s faction, Mr. Cao, who had been watching the entire battle, sighed deeply.

“No wonder Tian Huihua went to such great lengths to ensure Ice Soul participated in the Genius Rankings Battle…”

As someone proficient in fortune-reading, Mr. Cao had always known Lin Jing was exceptional. However, even his insight fell short compared to the Tianji Pavilion’s foresight.

He never would have expected that the talent he discovered in the Great Wasteland would climb to first place on the Genius Rankings.

Countless Academy prodigies had achieved this before, and even Mr. Cao himself had once been ranked first in his youth.

But back then, the competition was nowhere near as fierce as it was now.

"The Ice Soul Gu… so that’s his fortuitous encounter."

For someone not born into a great sect, such achievements were impossible without a stroke of fortune.

Mr. Cao privately sent a transmission to the other Academy elders:

"In the coming days, protect Lin Jing. I will try to persuade him to return to Hidden Dragon City via teleportation array. Now that the Ice Soul Gu has been exposed, his return journey will be anything but peaceful."

The other elders silently nodded.

At the very least, the Spirit Dao Sect disciple didn’t take first place this time.

The Academy elders had always disliked the Spirit Dao Sect’s unwillingness to join their ranks and cultivate using Hidden Dragon fortune energy. Seeing one of their own surpass the Spirit Dao Sect prodigy was deeply satisfying.

With the Genius Rankings Battle concluded, Lin Jing returned to his inn and immediately sought out Senior Pine to request a Dragon Carp to warm his body.

"You really know how to put on a show," Senior Pine chuckled. "You went beyond your limits with the Ice Soul Gu, just to scare those prodigies, didn’t you?"

Normally, Lin Jing only partially merged with the Ice Soul Gu, fusing its cold energy with his true energy. Even then, it was painful.

But this time, Senior Pine had seen him completely merge—body, mind, and soul—with the Ice Soul Gu’s power.

As a result, even his soul had suffered frost damage, not just his physical body.

“I had no choice,” Lin Jing sighed. “Didn’t you see how powerful that Spell Spirit Origin was? Even after inheriting your bloodline and mastering your divine techniques, my Pine Leaf Mouse still couldn’t completely counter it.”

“If I didn’t fully merge with the Ice Soul Gu, I wouldn’t have been able to crush that guy.”

At Lin Jing’s words, the Pine Leaf Mouse, who was snacking on sunflower seeds, spat out a shell in protest.

"Don’t blame me! You’re the one with a low cultivation level! If you had reached Great Perfection of Foundation Establishment, we wouldn’t have struggled so much—you could’ve fought a hundred rounds on your own!"

Senior Pine laughed heartily at the Pine Leaf Mouse’s response.

"Well, at least now you have no choice but to rely on the Ice Soul Gu. Now that it’s been exposed, the Gu God Sect’s cultivators will surely emerge soon… Heh heh heh."

Lin Jing rolled his eyes.

It didn’t matter—he had already decided to seclude himself in the Academy for at least sixty years.

He wouldn’t leave unless the Academy collapsed—which, realistically, wasn’t happening anytime soon.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 114: Renowned Across the Ancient Kingdom
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 114: Renowned Across the Ancient Kingdom

"Congratulations."

The visitor was indeed Mo Qianyi. Upon seeing Lin Jing, she immediately offered her congratulations, though her expression remained neutral.

"I should thank you for not continuing to challenge me; otherwise, I might not have been the last one standing," Lin Jing said, inviting her inside.

"There’s no need to say that. Even if all six remaining contestants had challenged you in succession and one of them eventually wore you down, it wouldn’t change the fact that your overall combat strength was the highest among all participants."

"Besides, based on what you showed, I don't believe you hadn't anticipated that scenario. Would you really have been worn down and defeated?"

Lin Jing simply smiled without answering. Who knew? But he did have one last trump card—one he saw no need to use. Even if he had exhausted himself and collapsed, it wouldn't have mattered.

After all, what he wanted was to be ranked first on the Genius Rankings, not necessarily to be the final victor of the Genius Battle.

"Judging by your expression, did I hit the mark?" Mo Qianyi smirked. A Beast Tamer proficient in Mechanized Arts, capable of dual-cultivating Buddhist and Demonic techniques—who knew how many more hidden cards he had?

"And my item, have you prepared it?"

Mo Qianyi took out a contract scroll and said, "This is a contract from Lotus Pavilion. Let’s sign it first to guarantee the transaction."

—

Shortly after, Tianji Pavilion released the latest Genius Rankings.

This particular ranking was especially anticipated due to the Genius Battle. Not all cultivators could personally visit Tianji City to watch the event. Most cultivators in their respective regions simply awaited the rankings to be announced.

—

Wasteland Domain, Hundred Flower Valley, Beast Taming Peak

"Say, your Holy Son should have participated in the Genius Battle that Tianji Pavilion broadcasted, right? I wonder if Holy Son Lin made it into the top twenty?"

A petite, adorable Hundred Flower Valley female disciple chattered away as she strolled alongside a Beast Taming Sect disciple, her mouth never stopping.

Ever since Lin Jing had displayed his formidable strength during his Holy Son Inauguration Ceremony, the relationship between the two sects had grown much closer. Hundred Flower Valley disciples no longer saw Beast Taming Sect as beggars and even treated them with genuine friendliness.

This left Beast Taming Sect disciples ecstatic, shouting praises for their Holy Son.

"He should be fine, right? Junior Brother Lin is extraordinarily talented. I think he might even make the top ten." Senior Brother Ziran of Beast Taming Sect chuckled, scratching his head.

"Really? Senior Brother Ziran, I heard you once entered a Cavern Heaven with Holy Son Lin. Is that true?" The adorable girl leaned in, her large eyes filled with curiosity.

Under her gaze, Senior Brother Ziran gulped.

Just then, a streak of rainbow light flashed across the sky above Beast Taming Peak.

"Eh? Senior Sister Teng??"

Inside the rainbow light was a Hundred Flower Valley disciple wearing robes embroidered with a yellow butterfly pattern. Upon hearing her junior sister call out, she stopped briefly.

"Senior Sister, aren’t you supposed to be guarding the mountain gate? Why are you here at Beast Taming Peak?"

The petite girl asked curiously.

Taking a deep breath, the Yellow Butterfly Disciple held a stack of newspapers and said, "The latest World Report is out. I came to deliver a copy to the elders of Beast Taming Sect."

"The Genius Battle has ended? Senior Sister, what’s Holy Son Lin’s rank?"

Both the petite disciple and Senior Brother Ziran eagerly awaited the answer.

"Lin Holy Son… I…" The Yellow Butterfly Disciple still looked utterly dumbfounded, unable to express herself properly. Instead, she simply tossed them a newspaper.

"They can read it themselves... I’m leaving."

"I hope their hearts can withstand the shock…"

When Senior Brother Ziran and the male disciple from Hundred Flower Valley grabbed the paper and read it, their pupils shrank.

"F-First place???"

Moments later—

Hundred Flower Valley was in an uproar.

No matter what people were doing, they were all startled by the deafening commotion.

At Beast Taming Peak, there was an eruption of wild howls and bestial roars, so much so that slower disciples thought all of Beast Taming Sect’s contracted beasts had gone into heat simultaneously.

"OOOOHHH!!!"

The Demonic Beast Ancestors of Beast Taming Sect revealed their true forms, roaring in delight.

"I told you he could do it!"

"This Holy Son was the right choice!"

"Genius Rankings, First Place! Even though it's only the Human List, anyone who’s reached first place on this ranking—unless they die prematurely or exhaust their lifespan—has always reached the Nascent Soul Stage!"

"With the fortune he brings alone, Beast Taming Sect should be able to rise again. The potential of a first-place Genius Ranking talent should be more than enough to surpass us old folks, right?"

—

Genius Rankings - First Place: Lin Jing

Cultivation: Qi Refinement Stage 12

Age: 29

Affiliation: Beast Taming Sect

Profile: First place in the Genius Battle. Contracts two beasts—one is a Squirrel Spirit possessing the True Form of a Demon King and powerful Buddhist Arts, the other is one of the Ten Rare Gu of the Gu God Sect—the Ice Soul Gu. Defeated the previous Genius Rankings leader, Innate Dao Physique Chen Zhong, proving himself unrivaled beneath Core Formation!

—

"Too bad it’s just fortune. The Longevity Body makes it hard to convert that potential into actual strength…"

One of the Demonic Beast Ancestors sighed in sorrow. Every previous first-place Genius Ranking holder had stably reached Nascent Soul, but with a Longevity Body, things were much less certain.

Especially now that he also carried the negative effects of one of the Ten Rare Gu.

"Send him a letter. Tell him to stay put in the academy and not roam around. The sect can manage fine without him for now."

Grand Elder Wang Batian shared the same thoughts as Lin Jing himself—he should stay in the academy and develop for a hundred years first.

—

Meanwhile—

Deep inside Hundred Flower Valley, the Valley Master read the newspaper under Elder Fang’s watchful gaze.

Hearing the Beast Taming Peak’s wild commotion, Elder Fang sighed in regret.

"I told you, I told you, Valley Master, you missed out on a heaven-sent opportunity."

"But at least we established goodwill by giving him a bottle of Hundred Flower Honey and a secret technique."

"First place… huh?" The Valley Master’s gaze flickered.

In all these years of close ties between Hundred Flower Valley and Beast Taming Sect, when had the latter ever produced such a monstrous genius?

"But… Ice Soul Gu? Is Beast Taming Sect serious about this?"

She frowned. Being knowledgeable in insect cultivation, she was keenly aware of just how dangerous the Ten Rare Gu were.

—

Tianji City

With Lin Jing’s first-place ranking, Tianji Pavilion sent him an invitation.

"Come with me. Master asked me to bring you to receive Senior Starry Cosmos Turtle’s blessing. You must be satisfied now, right?"

"I thought you were still at Qi Refinement Eleven, trying to break through to Qi Refinement Twelve, but you’re actually challenging Qi Refinement Thirteen now?"

Bai Yuran clicked her tongue.

"It’s thanks to the Ice Soul Gu, right? With this, you should unlock your third contract slot. Have you already found a pet beast strong enough to help you break through?"

Lin Jing smiled at Bai Yuran.

"Of course I have. In fact, I entered the Genius Battle just for its evolution. Let’s hope everything goes smoothly."

This was the day he had waited for—the day his Dragon Carp would leap over the Dragon Gate.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 115: The Starry Cosmos Blessing
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 115: The Starry Cosmos Blessing

Under Bai Yuran’s guidance, Lin Jing arrived at Tianji Pavilion for the second time.

Inside the grand hall, Tian Huihua was already waiting. He had the air of a mystic fraudster, smiling as though lost in thought.

"Senior Brother." Bai Yuran greeted him with a slight bow before asking, "I’ve brought him. Where is Master?"

"Senior Tian." Lin Jing also bowed respectfully.

Tian Huihua smirked, "Master calculated that he and the Longevity Body are incompatible. It’s best if they don’t meet, so I’ll be the one taking him to see Senior Starry Cosmos Turtle."

"Huh?" Bai Yuran was momentarily stunned. "The Longevity Body clashes with Master?"

"Doesn’t seem like it. More likely, Master just doesn’t want to see Senior Starry Cosmos Turtle. After all, his heart isn’t too strong."

Bai Yuran nodded in understanding.

"Has Bai Junior Sister already explained the blessing process to you?" Tian Huihua asked, turning to Lin Jing.

"She explained it long ago. He wants to borrow the Power of the Future." Bai Yuran added, "With Senior Starry Cosmos Turtle’s abilities, that should be no problem, right?"

"We’ll find out once we ask."

"But before we go, Beast Taming Sect kid, no matter what you hear next, don’t take it to heart."

Tian Huihua waved his sleeve, and a surge of power enveloped them—Spatial Shift.

Before Lin Jing could even process what was happening, they had already arrived at the top floor of Tianji Pavilion.

—

The room was vast and empty, with only a single stone table in the center.

Atop the table sat a small black turtle, gazing up at the starry sky.

Its shell was embedded with countless colorful gems, resembling stars in a miniature night sky.

"Senior."

Upon seeing the turtle, Tian Huihua immediately covered his ears and said, "I’ve brought him. He’s the first-place winner of the Genius Battle."

"Longevity Body... what a shame you didn’t inherit any talent. Otherwise, you’d be far more suited to lead Tianji Pavilion than those old men."

Starry Cosmos Turtle slowly turned its head toward Lin Jing, making him pause in silence.

Lin Jing coughed lightly and said, "Greetings, Senior. I wish to borrow a Foundation Establishment contract power while still in Qi Refinement Stage, essentially forming a drop of Contract Blood ahead of time. Is this possible?"

"Easy."

Starry Cosmos Turtle replied nonchalantly, "A trivial matter. When it comes to Time and Space Dao, not even that Ancient Emperor brat can compare to me."

Lin Jing fell silent again before stepping forward and extending his hand.

The small turtle floated into the air and gently landed on Lin Jing’s palm.

In the next instant, a mysterious transformation occurred within Lin Jing’s body.

To his amazement, a third drop of golden Contract Blood condensed instantly inside him, yet his cultivation remained at Qi Refinement Stage.

It was as if the Contract Blood had appeared out of thin air—a truly mystical process.

"It’s done. Remember, this blood is something you were destined to condense in the future. You’ve simply borrowed it early. Once you reach Foundation Establishment, you won’t be able to acquire this drop again."

"Understood, Senior!"

With the blessing complete, Starry Cosmos Turtle turned back to the sky and muttered, "Now, let me continue my nap. I was dreaming that the Ancient Kingdom’s fortune is about to be depleted. I need to finish the dream to see what happens next."

Tian Huihua unplugged his ears and waved his hand. "All done? Then we’ll take our leave, Senior. Rest well."

—

Shortly after, Lin Jing reunited with Bai Yuran and the others.

"Senior Brother! Dragon Carp—did it succeed?"

"It worked," Tian Huihua confirmed. He then turned toward Dragon Carp and smirked.

"Bai Yuran’s younger self had quite a sharp tongue. I assume you heard everything she was saying?"

"Just some trivial gossip." Dragon Carp replied casually.

"Good, that’s a relief," Tian Huihua sighed in relief.

"Well, as long as it worked." Bai Yuran didn’t dwell on the mystery and instead turned to Lin Jing excitedly.

"So, Holy Son, what’s your next move?"

"As far as I know, you joined the Demon-Slaying Bureau, right? How about—"

"Hold on."

Lin Jing suddenly interrupted, rubbing his chin thoughtfully.

"From what I’ve heard, Tianji Pavilion disciples undergo 50 years of astrology training and 30 years of fate-reading, for a total of 80 years of cultivation before they’re allowed to travel. Bai Yuran, you’ve only trained for 20 years, correct?"

"That means… you still have 60 years of closed-door cultivation ahead. And I’m also planning to retreat in the academy for a full cycle of 60 years. So let’s talk about cooperation… in six decades!"

Lin Jing smiled.

Bai Yuran froze on the spot.

"Are you kidding me?!"

She nearly coughed up blood.

The only reason she had sought out Lin Jing was to avoid 80 years of closed-door training by using his Longevity Body for faster cultivation.

And now, he was planning to shut himself away for 60 years too?!

Lin Jing hurriedly explained, "The further one progresses in cultivation, the harder it becomes. Bai Yuran, you can steadily advance with your current training. Wouldn’t it be wiser to seek my help only when you hit a bottleneck?"

"Besides, I’m still in Qi Refinement, and I plan to remain secluded in the academy. What dangers could possibly find me?"

"He’s right," Tian Huihua agreed.

"But… but…" Bai Yuran pointed at herself. "Sixty years later, I’ll be an old woman! I’m not a Longevity Body like you!"

"Nonsense," Tian Huihua chuckled.

"In 60 years, you’ll probably have reached Nascent Soul Stage. With a lifespan of over a thousand years, you’ll still be a child by cultivation standards."

Bai Yuran rolled her eyes. Since Lin Jing wasn’t interested in cooperating early, she had no choice.

"Fine. Once I finish my closed-door training, I’ll come find you in the academy. You’d better still be around by then!"

"Hehe, I promise." Lin Jing nodded.

"In that case, farewell for now. The academy elders have been secretly watching over you the whole time. They won’t feel at ease until you safely return."

Tian Huihua smiled.

Lin Jing nodded once more. He was just as eager to return to the academy’s safety.

But before that…

—

Leaving Tianji Pavilion, Lin Jing quickly returned to his inn and pulled Dragon Carp aside.

He condensed the Contract Blood and presented it to the beast.

Upon seeing it, Dragon Carp’s breath quickened.

"So fragrant!"

"Calm down," Lin Jing reassured. "I already told you—you are my third contract beast. No one else can take your place."

"But aren’t you still missing materials for a Mechanized Turtle?"

At that moment, Divine Pine suddenly materialized and muttered, "A Mechanized Turtle won’t keep you warm in the winter, though."

"That’s irrelevant."

Lin Jing pressed the Contract Blood onto Dragon Carp’s forehead.

In the next moment, a wave of warmth surged through his body.

Although his cultivation remained unchanged, the side effects of using the Ice Soul Gu—cold limbs—instantly disappeared.

"Not bad."

Just as Lin Jing was marveling at the effectiveness of contracting a fire-attribute beast, Dragon Carp suddenly froze, its eyes flashing as if recalling something.

Its thoughts drifted back to the waterfall in Beast Taming Sect, where it had once meditated beneath the pool.

An infinite starlight illuminated the water’s surface, cascading down onto its body.

BOOM!

A sudden change erupted within Dragon Carp’s body.

Not only had its bond with Lin Jing deepened…

Something else was happening.

"Wait… what’s going on?"

Even Divine Pine widened its eyes in shock.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 116: An Unexpected Gain
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 116: An Unexpected Gain

"Rare Beast?"

"A mutation?!"

Lin Jing, still in the midst of his contract with Dragon Carp, suddenly opened his eyes, a look of astonishment flashing across his face.

He could clearly sense the changes happening to Dragon Carp.

It was the exact same phenomenon that had occurred when he contracted Pine Leaf Mouse, causing it to mutate into a Rare Beast.

"Wait…"

Lin Jing's expression froze.

Was Dragon Carp also mutating into a Rare Beast just because of the contract?!

Seeing this, Lin Jing took a deep breath.

Before this contract, he hadn’t given much thought to Rare Beast mutations.

Though Elder Mo had mentioned that contracting a beast while possessing a special physique could increase the probability of mutation, Lin Jing had always assumed that Pine Leaf Mouse’s mutation was just a lucky accident.

After all, when he contracted Ice Soul Gu, nothing happened—no mutation, no change.

That had led Lin Jing to completely set aside the idea of Rare Beast mutations this time.

His only focus had been to contract Dragon Carp and help it undergo its Dragon Transformation.

But now, this unexpected change had given Lin Jing a huge surprise—one that made him reconsider his own circumstances.

"Could it be that… I have some kind of physique that naturally triggers beast mutations?"

"The reason Ice Soul Gu didn’t mutate into a Rare Beast might be because Gu worms have a unique life form that prevents them from awakening Rare Beast abilities… Or maybe it was just had bad luck."

Ice Soul Gu: ???

"But if creatures like Pine Leaf Mouse and Dragon Carp, which have normal life cycles, contract with me and have a high probability of mutating into Rare Beasts…"

"Then why am I even calling myself Longevity Body? I should be calling myself Rare Beast Body instead!"

Lin Jing’s breathing quickened.

With Pine Leaf Mouse, he already understood the power of Rare Beasts.

Now, he only wanted to know—what kind of Rare Beast ability would Dragon Carp awaken?

Would it be a support ability? A combat ability? Or something entirely new?

—

A Moment Later…

The contract was completed, and Dragon Carp's eyes gleamed in a daze.

A massive wave of inherited knowledge surged into its bloodline.

Ancient memories surfaced in Dragon Carp’s mind as if awakening a latent ancestral ability.

It instantly understood everything.

“BO!!!”

Dragon Carp's dazed gaze turned sharp and fierce.

Rare Beast Ability: [Flame Emperor]

Effect: Allows the fusion of multiple types of fire, drastically enhancing their power.

—

"An offensive ability, huh…?" Lin Jing murmured.

—

Inside the sealed chamber, Dragon Carp activated its top-tier beast fire—Dragon Scales Fire.

Dragon Scales Fire took the form of overlapping scales, its flames coiling like a soaring dragon, radiating immense heat.

Apart from that, Dragon Carp also possessed Ghostfire, a demonic flame specialized in burning souls.

Ghostfire was black, chaotic in form, resembling twisting spirits. Its eerie nature made it highly unsettling.

These two flames had completely different properties:

With the Flame Emperor ability, Dragon Carp’s two unique flames began to merge.

Within moments, a black dragon-like flame roared into existence.

The Black Dragon Flame coiled around Dragon Carp, dark mist rising from its body as its power surged dramatically.

"Interesting."

Divine Pine, who had been observing from the side, murmured in fascination.

"In terms of quality, this flame is already comparable to that Extinction Wildfire."

"The one inside Yan Xiangbei’s high-grade magic treasure?" Lin Jing asked.

During his battle against Yan Xiangbei from Burning Immortal Valley, the opponent had used a top-tier foreign fire fueled by his high-grade magic treasure, possessing terrifying destructive force.

"Yeah, that one was strong. If a Nascent Soul cultivator refined it, it could be used as a deadly trump card—it’s definitely top-tier."

"But in terms of potential, this Black Dragon Flame isn’t inferior. Once Dragon Carp completes its Dragon Transformation and reaches Foundation Establishment, its power might even surpass the Extinction Wildfire used in the tournament."

"And this isn't even the full potential of its ability, is it?" Divine Pine asked.

Lin Jing nodded.

Exactly.

Right now, Dragon Carp could only control two different flames.

But what if it could control three, four, or even more than ten different types of fire?

The flames it fused could become even more terrifying.

It might even reach the level of Celestial Fire, a flame strong enough to resist Tribulation Lightning!

Unlike Pine Leaf Mouse’s Rare Beast ability, which focused on material synthesis, Dragon Carp’s ability was pure destruction.

No—not just destruction.

A powerful flame could be an invaluable tool for alchemy and artifact refinement!

A grin slowly formed on Lin Jing’s lips.

The next step—gather more unique flames for Dragon Carp to refine!

—

“Bo…”

At the same time, Dragon Carp—now surrounded by Black Dragon Flames—turned its sharp gaze toward Pine Leaf Mouse and Ice Soul Gu.

It wanted a fight.

A spar, a duel, a battle of abilities.

Seeing this, Divine Pine fell silent for a moment.

Then, it finally spoke.

"You… You brat… what are you even?"

"This Longevity Body of yours… is it truly just that?"

"Two Rare Beasts as contracted pets… Your luck is absolutely ridiculous."

Divine Pine suddenly thought of a possibility and turned toward Lin Jing with a serious expression.

"Are you… a reincarnated immortal?"

Lin Jing laughed.

"Impossible."

"At most, I’m the reincarnation of an unlucky guy. Whatever I lacked in my past life, I’m making up for in this one."

"Scammer," Divine Pine muttered, still unable to understand.

—

Back at Tianji City - Interdomain Teleportation Array

Holding his meditation staff, a gourd hanging from his waist, and dressed in white robes, Lin Jing couldn’t stop smiling.

Mr. Cao, standing beside him, shook his head.

"You look way too pleased."

"Then again, reaching first place on the Genius Rankings—yeah, I suppose it’s something to be happy about."

"Of course," Lin Jing laughed.

"Here, the Hidden Dragon Fortune is constantly accumulating. In just one day, I’ve gained as much fortune as I used to in an entire year!"

Unfortunately, once he turned 30, he’d be removed from the rankings.

And as for the Earth List, which allowed geniuses under 60 to compete…

With opponents like Cold Moon Sword Immortal Feng Ning, who could already slay Nascent Soul cultivators at Core Formation Perfection, Lin Jing wasn’t eager to join that battlefield just yet.

He’d retreat for ten years first—until all of his beasts reached Perfected Foundation Establishment.

But his happiest gain from this trip?

Aside from Divine Pine, he couldn’t tell anyone just yet.

"Time to head back to the academy," Mr. Cao nodded.

Lin Jing agreed.

And so, he chose the Interdomain Teleportation Array.

It was expensive, but also extremely safe.

Moreover, the process itself was a great opportunity to comprehend spatial laws.

Or so they had been told.

—

But the moment Lin Jing and Pine Leaf Mouse arrived back at Hidden Dragon City…

"That was way too fast. I didn't feel anything at all."

They had been scammed by the Ancient Kingdom’s marketing tactics.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 117: Leaping Over the Dragon Gate
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Upon returning to the academy, Lin Jing felt an immense sense of ease. He had spent more time cultivating here than in the Beast Taming Sect—it wouldn’t be an exaggeration to call it his second sect. However, one small regret remained: in the Beast Taming Sect, he was the Holy Son, but in the academy, no such title existed.

At this moment, with everything prepared, Lin Jing didn’t want to think about anything else. He only wished to complete his Foundation Establishment as soon as possible. After years of cultivation, the breakthrough to a major realm was right before him. In the cultivation world, it was commonly believed that one only truly stepped into the path of immortality upon reaching Foundation Establishment. After all, only at that stage did one’s lifespan significantly surpass that of mortals. Though Lin Jing didn't strictly follow this rule, he had long yearned for Foundation Establishment.

No choice—after all, the three great Core Beast Taming Techniques of the Beast Taming Sect could only be cultivated at Foundation Establishment. Moreover, most spells and secret arts became much easier to learn with higher cultivation. His storage bag was filled with techniques, all waiting for him to master.

“Reaching the first rank on the Genius Rankings with just Qi Refinement and the Ten Rare Gu… The Hidden Dragon Fortune I’ve accumulated over the past few days is a hundred times greater than before—and it's still growing. Among all past first-place holders, few could have had more fame than I do now. I must make good use of the Hidden Dragon Arts—exposing the existence of my Ice Soul Gu must not be in vain!”

Landing on the mountain peak of his cave abode, Lin Jing had a Red Leaf Squirrel perched on his right shoulder, while a fiery Dragonblood Carp hovered beside him in mid-air.

The Pine Leaf Mouse was dozing off. It found the interdomain teleportation array too boring—such a waste of Spirit Stones, only to arrive in an instant without even having the chance to comprehend anything.

“Bo~”

The Dragonblood Carp didn’t care about spatial principles. It only wanted one thing—to evolve into a dragon. Twisting its body, it looked at Lin Jing eagerly.

“Not just you—I want this too.”

“I’m helping you break through now. Not waiting another second.”

After setting up the formation and barriers, Lin Jing immediately flew to the mountain’s summit. With a grand wave of his sleeve, an immense surge of Hidden Dragon Fortune gushed forth like a fountain. The tangible, dragon-shaped fortune cascaded down like a waterfall from the peak to the ground.

“Fortune turns into a waterfall.”

“If not now, then when?” Lin Jing declared, channeling fortune energy for the Dragonblood Carp to absorb.

Never before had the Dragonblood Carp witnessed such a colossal amount of fortune energy. Its pupils gleamed with light.

“Once I transform, I will be the strongest…” It struggled to speak human language, a remarkable improvement for a creature that used to forget things so easily.

“Power…”

As its draconic bloodline grew stronger, the Dragonblood Carp’s obsession with power intensified to near madness. This noble bloodline no longer wished to remain a mere spectator—it longed to compete with the prodigies of all races.

Crack!

The Dragonblood Carp surged upward like a fish climbing a waterfall, diving into the fortune cascade. While absorbing the Hidden Dragon Fortune to refine its bloodline, it also compressed its flaming draconic True Qi, advancing toward Foundation Establishment!

“Strongest?”

Lin Jing’s left shoulder suddenly turned to ice, and a crystalline spider crawled out—it was the Ice Soul Gu. It scuttled over to the Pine Leaf Mouse and asked:

“What do you think?”

“Do you believe that after this fish reaches Foundation Establishment, it’ll be stronger or weaker than us?”

The Pine Leaf Mouse glanced at the Ice Soul Gu. Does it even matter?

The stronger the Dragonblood Carp became, the better its alchemy skills. The better its alchemy skills, the faster it could refine pills. For the Pine Leaf Mouse, which had stockpiled crates of pills, a stronger Dragonblood Carp only meant more benefits.

They were all on the same production line—who was stronger or weaker didn’t matter. As long as the Pine Leaf Mouse and the Ten Rare Gu handled the fighting, it wouldn’t have to consume its stockpile of resources at all.

But seeing how eager the Ice Soul Gu was, the Pine Leaf Mouse decided to analyze the situation.

“Let’s see…”

Both it and the Ice Soul Gu were rare beasts—the Pine Leaf Mouse possessed the True Form of a Demon King, while the Ice Soul Gu was one of the Ten Rare Gu. Neither had an emperor-tier bloodline, but…

The Pine Leaf Mouse was wood-element, the Ice Soul Gu was ice-element, and the Dragonblood Carp was fire-element. Fire countered both of them.

Not to mention, the Pine Leaf Mouse had awakened an offensive fire-element divine ability—an inherited technique from an immortal’s legacy. The Ten Rare Gu might have been created by cultivators of the upper realm, but how could it compare to an immortal's inheritance?

Moreover, the Pine Leaf Mouse wasn’t skilled in combat, and the Ice Soul Gu could only reach its full strength when fused with a host. If the Dragonblood Carp’s bloodline reached emperor-tier after its evolution…

“What? I’ll be the weakest one again?” The Ice Soul Gu was in disbelief.

After the Pine Leaf Mouse’s analysis, the Ice Soul Gu realized that even if it collected more human pets, its disadvantage was obvious.

“Gu can devour one another… Boss, can’t you capture the other nine Rare Gu for me to devour? If I absorb my ‘siblings,’ I might become a Gu God…” The Ice Soul Gu suggested to Lin Jing.

“Go back to sleep. Don’t come out unless necessary.”

Lin Jing shoved the Ice Soul Gu back. What do you mean, capture the other nine Rare Gu for you to devour?

The remaining Rare Gu were either in the hands of Nascent Soul or Void Mystical Stage cultivators from the Gu God Sect or held by the powerful experts of the Demon-Slaying Bureau. Where was he supposed to get them?

“Damn, this Ice Soul Gu is turning into another Old Demon Pine… One big demon, one little demon.”

Seriously thinking of devouring the other Rare Gu…

Lin Jing dismissed the unrealistic idea from his mind and focused entirely on the Dragonblood Carp’s leap over the dragon gate.

“Heh heh.”

At that moment, a voice echoed from the void.

“Since you’ve refined so many pills for me, old man here will lend you a hand.”

“This is the blood of a Nascent Soul transformation of the Southern Mountain Demon Emperor. It counts as emperor-tier blood. I’ve refined it into a Foundation Establishment Elixir—use it to enhance your bloodline now!”

As the words fell, a surge of blood flowed into the fortune waterfall, dyeing it crimson. The merging of two extraordinary forces—a tangible and an intangible aid—brought the Dragonblood Carp immense benefits.

“ROAR!!!”

The Dragonblood Carp trembled with gratitude.

With a resounding dragon roar, it absorbed both the emperor-tier blood-refined elixir and Lin Jing’s Hidden Dragon Fortune at an incredible speed. Its True Qi compressed further, and a brilliant red light enveloped its entire body. Around it, clouds of fire materialized!

“ROOOOAR!!”

Dragon roars echoed endlessly. Amidst the vast sea of flames, a colossal, fiery-red divine dragon—dozens of meters long—spiraled into the sky. Its entire form seemed to be forged from the purest flames, radiating both brilliance and mystery!

“Did it succeed in transforming?” Lin Jing took a deep breath, his eyes filled with joy.

No—calling it a Fire Qilin Dragon was more accurate!

Its scales, like meticulously carved rubies, radiated both scorching heat and immense power. Tracing up its body to its dragon head, its eyes burned like twin miniature suns—deep and luminous, exuding an ancient awe-inspiring presence.

Its bloodline had ascended to an unfathomable level, surpassing even Lin Jing’s Beast Taming Physique!

Under Lin Jing’s watchful gaze, the Fire Qilin Dragon spread its wings—vast and boundless like flaming feathers slicing through the sky. It left behind dazzling trails of crimson fire, illuminating the heavens.

Seeing this, the Pine Leaf Mouse blinked, a bead of sweat rolling down its forehead, its body heating up.

“Wait.”

Inside Lin Jing, the Ice Soul Gu felt an even stronger reaction—it could feel Lin Jing’s blood boiling like magma. The Fire Qilin Dragon’s flames had finally completely suppressed the Ice Soul Gu’s frost energy.

“Emperor-tier bloodline… This is unexpected. But… its ancestral qilin must have been incredibly powerful…”

Even the ancient Divine Pine muttered in astonishment.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 118: A Decade of Foundation Establishment
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 118: A Decade of Foundation Establishment

When Lin Jing reached the thirteenth stage of Qi Refinement, he barely had time to feel joy before his entire body felt as if it was about to explode—his body swelling as if it couldn't contain his energy.

“Proceed with Foundation Establishment.”

Sensing Lin Jing’s condition, Senior Pine immediately spoke up.

“It seems your body simply cannot withstand the power of Qi Refinement Stage Thirteen. Quickly, compress your True Qi and begin Foundation Establishment.”

“You are not one of those special physiques capable of carrying massive amounts of True Qi. Although the Ice Soul Gu has strengthened your body to some extent, bearing the power of Qi Refinement Stage Twelve was already your limit.”

“By pushing your limits with Thunder Dao techniques, you forced your breakthrough to the thirteenth stage, but you won’t be able to remain at this level for long. Still, your goal has been achieved—begin your Foundation Establishment now!”

“Alright.”

Without hesitation, Lin Jing sat down, not even sparing a moment to congratulate the Dragonblood Carp on its evolution. He had to begin his own breakthrough immediately. Reaching Foundation Establishment from the thirteenth stage of Qi Refinement required no pills, no rare treasures—his unparalleled foundation alone was the greatest aid he could ask for.

Seated cross-legged, Lin Jing resembled the center of a black hole, his surroundings whirling into a storm of thirteen layers of Qi Refinement energy. Immense spiritual energy from heaven and earth surged into his body, causing his expression to contort in pain.

“You three.”

Senior Pine, ever the worrier, turned to the Pine Leaf Mouse, the newly evolved Fire Qilin Dragon, and the Ice Soul Gu.

“Protect him. Use the power of your contracts to transfer your True Essence to him and help him compress his True Qi. Once he completes Foundation Establishment, you will also be able to break through.”

“You’ve all absorbed far more resources than your current cultivation stages allow. Once the contract’s suppression lifts, your breakthroughs will be even more dramatic!”

The Pine Leaf Mouse had absorbed an immense amount of high-grade wood-elemental life essence...

The Ice Soul Gu had absorbed an entire region of extreme cold energy...

The Fire Qilin Dragon had devoured vast amounts of Hidden Dragon Fortune and the blood of a Dragon Emperor...

Their current early Foundation Establishment stage was far from their true potential.

As Lin Jing began his breakthrough, their cultivation realms would undergo a second explosive surge.

“ROAR!!!”

The Fire Qilin Dragon, now wielding Dragon Emperor blood, effortlessly controlled its body size, shrinking to the thickness of an arm before settling beside Lin Jing.

The Ice Soul Gu, after throwing a glance at the Fire Qilin Dragon, silently emerged to guard Lin Jing, refraining from disturbing his breakthrough.

The Pine Leaf Mouse, too, sat down beside Lin Jing.

The three beasts encircled him, entering their own closed-door meditation, refining the vast reserves of power they had accumulated.

“This kid is too greedy.”

Observing Lin Jing’s condition, Senior Pine muttered to himself.

“Qi Refinement Stage Thirteen… He’s clearly struggling. If he had advanced to Foundation Establishment from Stage Twelve, it would’ve been effortless. But now, coupled with the burden of his Longevity Body, it will take him years of seclusion to stabilize his Foundation Establishment.”

“However, if he succeeds, the benefits will be unimaginable. I wonder how much his three physiques will improve…”

“In any case, this old man has worked hard for too long. Time for a good nap…”

Hidden Dragon City, Hidden Dragon Academy.

It had been ten years since the Holy Son of the Beast Taming Sect and academy student Wei Fang claimed victory in the Genius Rankings Battle, securing first place on the list.

Since that battle, Lin Jing had vanished into seclusion. It was as if he had evaporated from the world.

Many speculated that he was hiding in the academy to evade the Gu God Sect’s demonic cultivators.

They were half-right.

Even within the academy, Lin Jing had not shown his face once.

For ten whole years, not a single student or teacher had seen him.

The barrier outside his cave abode, coupled with Senior Pine’s vigilant protection, prevented all visitors from approaching.

“He’s in seclusion, attempting Foundation Establishment.”

That was the only response Senior Pine gave to anyone who came looking.

Lin Jing’s old friends were baffled.

“People have gone into seclusion for ten years to break through to Core Formation, even Nascent Soul… but Qi Refinement to Foundation Establishment? What kind of Foundation Establishment is he doing?”

At the Academy’s Dragon Palace stronghold.

The now Core Formation realm Xu Zhizhi had long since graduated and become the lord of a city in the Ancient Domain. Still, he occasionally returned to the academy to check on the Dragon Palace’s development and maintain connections.

Lin Jing’s hurried departure into seclusion had left behind very few Rising Dragon Pills, and he hadn’t had time to pass on the pill’s refining method.

As a result, despite Lin Jing’s legendary first-place ranking in the Genius Rankings, the Dragon Palace had stopped mass-recruiting.

No longer an organization that nurtured young cultivators by helping them raise powerful dragon pets, the Dragon Palace had instead become an elite faction—a gathering place for prodigies who wished to forge connections with Lin Jing.

The dragon pets had become little more than a status symbol.

“Oh, it’s you two.”

Xu Zhizhi glanced at the siblings before him.

“Mid Foundation Establishment… Both of you have reached this stage, yet Lin Jing still hasn’t completed his breakthrough? Seriously?”

“Perhaps Senior Lin is cultivating some kind of profound divine technique. How could we compare?” The Jiang Family Twin Physique siblings smiled bitterly.

They were the very same twins Lin Jing had rescued from the Air Domain ten years ago, the ones who had nurtured the Ice Soul Lesser Gu.

After following Gao Sheng for a decade, they had reached mid Foundation Establishment and had even entered Hidden Dragon Academy, taking charge of the Dragon Palace in Lin Jing’s absence.

Ever since they learned the truth about Lin Jing’s contract with the Ice Soul Gu, their devotion to him had only deepened.

But…

He had been in seclusion for far too long.

“Hopefully, that’s the case. Ten years… The first-place ranking on the Genius Rankings has changed three or four times…” Xu Zhizhi sighed, looking toward Lin Jing’s sealed cave abode.

For Nascent Soul cultivators and beyond, ten years was nothing.

But for the Genius Rankings, which focused on cultivators under thirty at Foundation Establishment…

Lin Jing was already a legend of the past.

Many new first-place holders had sought out this legendary figure to challenge him—only to find that they couldn’t even see him.

Lin Jing had no idea he had been cultivating for ten years.

In his meditative state, time had lost all meaning.

He had successfully reached Foundation Establishment some time ago. Now, he was stabilizing his realm, allowing his surging True Essence to settle.

Compressing his True Qi had been far more difficult than expected.

However, with each successive compression, he could feel himself improving beyond imagination.

His Longevity Body was no longer merely beginning to take shape—it was now approaching perfection.

His lifespan had skyrocketed to five thousand years, rivalling even Void Mystical cultivators.

This was the terrifying effect of Foundation Establishment at Qi Refinement Stage Thirteen on special physiques.

His Ice Soul Physique and Beast Taming Physique had also fully matured, granting him frost power rivaling Core Formation cultivators and the ability to suppress Monster Core-stage beasts without his contract beasts' help.

However…

As expected, Foundation Establishment did not grant him another Contract Blood.

It seemed he would have to reach Core Formation before contracting another pet beast.

Lin Jing opened his eyes.

“Mid Foundation Establishment… As expected.”

Beside him, his three pet beasts remained in deep cultivation.

Pine Leaf Mouse - Late Foundation Establishment.

Ice Soul Gu - Late Foundation Establishment.

Fire Qilin Dragon - Late Foundation Establishment.

“Ten years?”

“Well… Might as well stabilize my realm a bit longer.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 119: Emerging from Seclusion
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 119: Emerging from Seclusion

Though Lin Jing had vanished from the world for ten years, his legend never faded.

A decade ago, he had exhausted all his Hidden Dragon Fortune to aid the Dragonblood Carp’s evolution. But over the years, that fortune had accumulated once more, now brimming with power.
