This time, however, it had nothing to do with the Fire Qilin Dragon. Having already awakened its Rare Beast Divine Ability and achieved Emperor-tier bloodline, the Hidden Dragon Fortune could no longer provide significant benefits to it. Instead, Lin Jing planned to use it for his own cultivation.

His first priority—Beast Weaponization Technique.

This secret art allowed contract beasts to transform into various weapons and equipment.

The more compatible the pet and the weapon form, the greater the power they could unleash.

It was a technique that Foundation Establishment disciples of the Beast Taming Sect rarely bought artifacts for—not because they couldn’t afford them (though many were indeed broke from raising their beasts), but because this secret art turned their pet beasts into their strongest weapons.

Lin Jing had wanted to learn this for a long time.

As a Longevity Body cultivator, his own defenses had to be near-invincible—but he also needed a top-tier defensive artifact to further enhance his survivability.

Thus, he planned to master this technique and turn one of his contract beasts into armor to absorb damage in battle.

And for that…

Who better than a dragon?

Even without fully mastering the technique, Lin Jing was certain that the Fire Qilin Dragon, with its Dragon Emperor bloodline, would have defensive power surpassing most mid-grade artifacts if transformed into armor.

Moreover, this wouldn’t be just armor—it would be battle armor. Clad in the Flame Qilin Armor, and combined with his Flame Control Art, Lin Jing could wield the beast’s fused flames both offensively and defensively.

And if he fused it with the Ice Soul Gu’s power, he wouldn’t even have to worry about the backlash from frost energy anymore.

The mere thought of donning the Flame Qilin Armor filled Lin Jing with anticipation.

Wasting no time, he opened the Beast Weaponization manual and began cultivating.

Within a few days, using his fortune-converted comprehension, Lin Jing fully grasped the technique.

Compared to Heavenly Demon Physique, this technique was far easier to cultivate—after all, the three core secret arts of the Beast Taming Sect were interwoven with the Beast Taming Art, making them much more accessible.

Seeing that his three pet beasts were still consolidating their breakthroughs, Lin Jing continued his seclusion, diving into the Heavenly Demon Physique.

However, after half a month, despite burning ten years’ worth of accumulated fortune, he hadn’t made a single breakthrough.

“No wonder only two people in the Demon-Slaying Bureau have ever mastered this in thousands of years.”

“This is probably the hardest technique I’ve ever tried cultivating…”

Lin Jing sighed.

“If only I could borrow the Golden Bodhi Tree again. Compared to that, the fortune’s comprehension boost is still too weak.”

Opening his eyes, Lin Jing sensed little progress—but during this time, his three pet beasts had finally stabilized their cultivation.

With their overwhelming foundations, the power surge from their continuous breakthroughs had been enormous. It had taken them a long time just to fully control it.

“If they weren’t protecting me for the past decade, they probably would’ve reached Core Formation by now…”

Lin Jing mused.

By now, the geniuses from the last Genius Rankings Battle had likely all reached Core Formation. He wondered if, in his current state, he could still compete with them.

The Ice Soul Gu was the first to awaken—its innate talent made it exceptionally quick at mastering its newfound strength.

The Pine Leaf Mouse opened its eyes next. With its Everlasting Emperor’s Longevity Art, its control over its power was exceptional.

The Fire Qilin Dragon, despite its years of refining pills and honing its control, was the last to stabilize. Having evolved the latest, it needed more time to adapt to its drastically enhanced strength.

“How’s everyone feeling?”

The Ice Soul Gu asked.

Immediately, all eight of its sub-Gu stirred restlessly within.

“Fight! Fight! Fight!”

The Fire Qilin Dragon soared into the air, its fiery gaze locking onto the Ice Soul Gu and Pine Leaf Mouse. Clearly, it was very satisfied with its newfound power.

The Pine Leaf Mouse, however, wasn’t interested.

After a quick glance at the spiritual plants it had cultivated… its expression turned despondent.

“So many of them have withered… Ten years without care…”

Heartbroken, it hurriedly burrowed back into its gourd, hoping to revive some of them.

The Ice Soul Gu didn’t acknowledge the overexcited “stupid fish” either. Instead, it focused on sensing its sub-Gu.

“I can feel it—two of my sub-Gu have reached mid-Foundation Establishment. If only they were late-stage as well… If I could reclaim them now, I’d reach the perfect Foundation Establishment!”

The Ice Soul Gu muttered to itself, eager to advance first among them.

The progression from late-stage Foundation Establishment to perfection wasn’t a dramatic leap—it was a gradual refinement of True Essence.

Once a cultivator reached their limit, they could begin compressing their True Essence and attempt Core Formation.

Success meant forming a Golden Core, gaining Core Formation divine abilities, and stepping into the ranks of the true powerhouses.

Failure, however…

At best, it resulted in severe injuries.

At worst—death.

For this reason, many weaker or less confident cultivators chose to condense a False Core instead. Though inferior to true Core Formation, False Core cultivators were still far stronger than those at Foundation Establishment, creating a clear gap in status.

However, for the Ice Soul Gu, reaching perfect Foundation Establishment meant it could attempt Core Formation anytime—and its chances of success were absolute.

Since it was unlikely to defeat a Rare Beast with an immortal’s inheritance at its current level, it prioritized advancing its realm as quickly as possible.

“Is that so?”

Hearing this, Lin Jing murmured,

“The Jiang Twins have already raised the Ice Soul Lesser Gu to mid-Foundation Establishment… That’s the same realm as me now.”

“Let’s go check on them.”

With that, Lin Jing retracted the Ice Soul Gu into his body.

The Fire Qilin Dragon froze.

Wait, what? You’re not going to fight me?

“ROOOAR!!!”

It blinked, momentarily stunned, before realization dawned—Lin Jing simply didn’t dare fight it anymore.

“Fine. You’re the strongest now. If you want to fight, I’ll find you a proper opponent outside.”

Lin Jing pulled out his Meditation Staff, withdrew the Fire Qilin Dragon, then shrank the staff into a pendant, stringing it on a thread of spider silk around his neck.

“Senior Xu, are you leaving?”

At the Dragon Palace Assembly Hall, the Jiang Twins—now the leaders of Dragon Palace—bid farewell to Xu Zhizhi.

“My city’s still a mess. I need to return.” Xu Zhizhi sighed.

“But as always—the moment Lin Jing emerges from seclusion, let me know.”

The Jiang Twins nodded, but just as they were about to respond, their expressions changed abruptly.

They had felt a reaction from their sub-Gu.

“Xu Zhizhi, long time no see. Looks like you finally reached Core Formation—Golden Core early-stage. Impressive.”

A familiar voice echoed from outside the Assembly Hall.

Xu Zhizhi took a deep breath before exclaiming—

“Damn it, you’re finally out!”

“Do you have ANY idea how much of a mess I’ve had to clean up because you didn’t leave behind enough Rising Dragon Pills before you vanished?!”

“…Wait. Ten years of seclusion… And you’re STILL only at mid-Foundation Establishment?!”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 120: The Former Legend
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 120: The Former Legend

"Sorry about that, it was a sudden situation." Lin Jing chuckled and said, "As for my cultivation level... it's hard to explain."

He then turned to look at the Jiang siblings. Jiang Qi had grown from a boy into a handsome young man, dressed in a striking red feathered robe, exuding an extraordinary aura. Jiang Lin had also matured, her blue dress complementing her icy temperament, making her look like a celestial maiden in the snow.

Seeing Lin Jing, the two were overwhelmed with excitement and immediately bowed in unison. "Senior."

"But mid Foundation Establishment is too low. You're at the same level as them!" Xu Zhizhi was at a loss for words. Shouldn't he at least be at perfected Foundation Establishment by now?!

"My talent isn't great, can't be helped." Lin Jing smiled.

Because he had gained extra lifespan through his breakthroughs, he didn't feel particularly troubled. Once his Heavenly Demon Physique was fully cultivated... everything would change.

"The number one on the Genius Rankings is calling himself untalented..." Xu Zhizhi was speechless.

"Relatively speaking... Xu, I've been in seclusion for ten years. Did anything major happen?" Lin Jing asked.

"I know about that." Now that he was out of seclusion and planned to stay in the academy as an "eternal student," he certainly wouldn't let Dragon Palace dissolve.

"Another thing you might be interested in is the Beast Taming Sect." Xu Zhizhi’s gaze flickered as he continued, "Five years ago, the Ancient Celestial Kingdom and the Snow Plains went to war again."

"The Great Wasteland, being close to the Snow Plains, may eventually turn into a battlefield!"

"A war..." Lin Jing muttered. "Another one, huh? I wonder how big it will get this time."

Conflicts between the Ancient Celestial Kingdom and the Snow Plains were nothing new.

The kingdom wasn't just after the fertile lands of the Snow Plains—it wanted to conquer the entire region.

This was closely tied to the Second Ancient Emperor.

The First Ancient Emperor had destroyed the Immortal Alliance, founded the Ancient Celestial Kingdom, and laid its foundation, earning boundless glory.

The Second Ancient Emperor, chosen as the successor when the First Emperor neared his end, had always sought to surpass his predecessor in achievements and power.

Rumors said he wanted to unite the entire Tianyuan Continent under his rule, believing that expanding the kingdom’s borders would enhance his True Dragon Secret Art, allowing him to surpass the First Ancient Emperor.

And the Snow Plains had become his first target after ascending the throne.

However, even conquering the Snow Plains alone was an immense challenge.

The Snow Plains tribes revered four totem guardian beasts, each comparable to Dao-Seeking Stage cultivators. Moreover, they thrived in the harsh and frigid environment, making the Snow Plains Cultivators exceptionally powerful.

The Ancient Celestial Kingdom justified the war by claiming that the Snow Plains Cultivators were being deceived and controlled by these beasts and that the kingdom was acting to "liberate" them from their rule.

Because of this, the Ancient Celestial Kingdom and the Snow Plains had remained in conflict.

"The Beast Taming Sect is under sect closure, so it shouldn't be forced by the Sect Regulatory Office to send disciples to the battlefield. Unless the Snow Plains breach the Great Wasteland’s defenses, we probably don’t need to worry about it."

At this level of war, Lin Jing felt it was beyond him to concern himself—he would just leave it to fate.

"There aren't many other major events. Oh, right, the Genius Rankings— since you dropped off the list, a few new number ones have come and gone. The current first place is also a student at the academy. He’s challenged you at Dragon Palace multiple times, but you were in seclusion. The Jiang twins probably know more." Xu Zhizhi shrugged and looked at them.

"The Genius Rankings, huh?" Lin Jing turned to the Jiang twins. "Are you two on the rankings yet?"

"And... aside from Demon-Slaying Bureau missions, you two mostly rely on the Ice Soul Gu, right? But to truly reach the rankings, you’d need to use it at full power, and you don’t seem confident in doing that." Jiang Qi commented.

"Who’s the current Genius Rankings' number one?" Lin Jing asked.

"A newer student at the academy, Zhao Qingyue from the Cultivation Zhao Clan," Jiang Qi replied. "She’s related to Zhao Qingquan, the Spring Source Physique cultivator you defeated in the Genius Rankings Battle. If we’re talking lineage, she’s technically his younger cousin. But her talent far surpasses Zhao Qingquan’s—she has the Supreme Yin Physique, a Divine Physique that grants her the phenomenon of Bright Moon Divine Radiance."

"She’s probably looking to avenge her cousin. Since you’re both in the academy, she’s challenged you repeatedly."

"Senior, this Zhao Qingyue is strong. Her current cultivation is perfected Foundation Establishment, and her Divine Physique Phenomenon, Bright Moon Divine Radiance, is even stronger than a Core Formation Divine Ability!" Jiang Lin added. "Because of this phenomenon, she might be even stronger than Chen Zhong, who had the Innate Dao Physique, when he was at Foundation Establishment!"

"The Cultivation Zhao Clan... No wonder they’re the second strongest cultivation family in the Ancient Celestial Kingdom, right after the royal family. They really do have a lot of freakishly talented members." Lin Jing mused. Then, he chuckled.

"If she wants a challenge, let’s give her one. One of you go call her over. Tell her Lin Jing will be waiting for her outside Dragon Palace. I’d like to test out some new techniques myself..."

Xu Zhizhi immediately perked up and laughed. "Oh, this is gonna be fun. The current Genius Rankings' first place versus the former first place from ten years ago? Feels a little like bullying, but since she’s confident, I’ll go let her know."

"Go on, then~" Lin Jing waved him off, then turned to the Jiang siblings. "While we wait, I’ll give you two some pointers on using your Gu techniques."

Zhao Qingyue—known as the Bright Moon Celestial Maiden. Many in the cultivation world believed she would be the next Cold Moon Sword Immortal.

She had always been diligent, cultivating tirelessly from a young age. Upon her debut, she swiftly took first place on the Genius Rankings, becoming famous across the Ancient Celestial Kingdom.

However, she carried an obsession.

"You’re strong, but I still feel like you’re not quite at the level of Lin Jing, who defeated my cousin back then."

And the reason?

Her cousin Zhao Qingquan had a terrible habit of running his mouth and making himself incredibly annoying.

"Lin Jing came out of seclusion?"

Zhao Qingyue, dressed in flowing white robes, exuded an ethereal and pure radiance as she stepped forward. Her graceful posture resembled willow catkins drifting in the moonlight, swaying gently in the wind. Her face was as flawless and luminous as a full moon. Even Xu Zhizhi nearly found himself lost in her presence.

"Bright Moon Celestial Maiden, you should be thanking me. The moment the Palace Master emerged from seclusion, I told him about you first." Xu Zhizhi smirked. "Are you really sure you want to challenge him? He’s ridiculously strong."

"Take me to him." Zhao Qingyue had been waiting for years. Who would have thought Lin Jing would remain in seclusion for so long?

Even if Lin Jing had already broken through to Core Formation, she still wanted a battle.

But when she arrived outside Dragon Palace and saw Lin Jing—at mid Foundation Establishment—she froze, deep in thought. She hadn’t expected that after ten years, his cultivation would still be so low.

Seeing his level, Zhao Qingyue deduced that his contract beasts must also still be at Foundation Establishment, given how Beast Taming Sect techniques worked. Since his own level was so low, his beasts couldn't have reached Core Formation either.

"Why is his cultivation so slow? Fine if he’s slow, but even his beasts? Did he just sleep for ten years?" Zhao Qingyue was baffled.

Lin Jing’s beasts were equally innocent. Helping him break through had taken them over nine years—honestly, not much different from sleeping for a decade.

"This is the Bright Moon Celestial Maiden, huh? Truly a proud daughter of heaven." Lin Jing stopped his conversation with the Jiang twins and looked at his challenger.

"Your cultivation..."

"No need to worry about that. Since you’re eager to fight, let’s begin." Lin Jing said. "You’re the younger one, so you can make the first move."

"...You're only ten years older than me. Stop acting ancient." Zhao Qingyue was speechless—especially since his cultivation was lower than hers.

"This celestial maiden respects the elderly and cherishes the young. You go first—let me see those so-called Ten Rare Gu that once defeated an Innate Dao Physique cultivator." Zhao Qingyue’s expression remained calm, but she had long been eager to test out the "demonic cultivation techniques" she had heard so much about.

"Alright..." Lin Jing couldn't exactly refuse the kind gesture of such a 'respectful' junior. Besides, he wasn’t entirely proficient in his new techniques yet—going first worked just fine.

He planned to experiment with Beast Weaponization first. If she wanted to see the Ten Rare Gu, she'd have to get past his weaponized beast first.

"Be careful, Celestial Maiden... this technique might be quite powerful." Lin Jing’s voice was calm.

The next moment, a ripple of fire-tinged spatial energy appeared before his chest.

Inside his Beast Taming Artifact, the Fire Qilin Dragon suddenly felt an unfamiliar, overwhelming beast-taming force surge through its body. This strange sensation made it feel as though it was being reshaped and reforged!

Wait... he wasn’t summoning it to fight?

He was... turning it into a weapon?!

"Armor is too complicated... let's start with a simpler weapon first."

Lin Jing reached into his Beast Taming Space with his right hand. The next instant, accompanied by an earth-shaking burst of fire, a long spear materialized—a spear with a flame-shaped spearhead, its shaft adorned with intricate carvings of a fire dragon’s head.

As this transformation completed, countless fire threads flickered around Lin Jing's body, coalescing into a phantom fire dragon that wrapped itself around the spear. The temperature in the area soared dramatically.

"Not ice, but fire???" Zhao Qingyue was momentarily stunned, as were the Jiang twins.

Only Xu Zhizhi immediately understood—it was Lin Jing’s contract beast, the Flame Dragon Carp!

BOOM!

Though it wasn’t frost power, Zhao Qingyue didn’t dare underestimate this fire. The Fire Qilin Dragon, a beast with Demon Emperor bloodline at late Foundation Establishment, had been transformed into a weapon. When combined with Lin Jing, a monstrous Qi Refinement Thirteenth Stage Foundation Establishment Beast Tamer, the resulting power sent an instant warning through Zhao Qingyue’s mind.

She had originally thought…

This battle is going to be difficult.

But the next moment, her pupils shrank as her thoughts completely shifted—

Lin Jing... doesn’t even know how to use a spear?!

He simply swung it in a wide arc, and mid-swing, a black flame erupted from within the spear!

Everyone present turned pale.

"Ghostfire?!"

Everyone knew Lin Jing practiced demonic cultivation techniques, but since the Demon-Slaying Bureau vouched for him, it wasn’t a huge issue.

However, no one knew that he had also mastered the Ghost Annihilation Art of the Netherworld Hall.

Even more terrifying—

The Ghostfire fused seamlessly with the fire dragon flames on the spear’s exterior, merging into an overwhelming black inferno. It took the shape of a black fire dragon, clinging to the spear, instantly doubling its power!

Zhao Qingyue’s body tensed instinctively in warning.

Rare Beast Ability—Flame Emperor!

Lin Jing hurled the spear directly at Zhao Qingyue.

Her expression changed drastically as she instantly unleashed her strongest Divine Physique Phenomenon—a radiant full moon appeared behind her, casting down boundless divine light.

However, the moonlight, which was said to be as powerful as a Core Formation Divine Ability, was instantly devoured by the black flames!

BOOM!

Lin Jing’s attack shattered her Divine Physique Phenomenon, obliterated her protective True Essence, and sent her hurtling backward. A searing impact struck her abdomen, leaving a charred crater on her robes.

"Ugh!" She coughed up several mouthfuls of blood before managing to stabilize herself midair. Clutching her abdomen, she stared at Lin Jing, who was also floating and had already retrieved the spear back into his hands.

A wave of utter disbelief surged through her.

It wasn’t just her—Xu Zhizhi and the Jiang twins were completely dumbfounded as well.

They knew Lin Jing was strong… but they hadn’t expected this.

Even Lin Jing himself was a little surprised.

As expected, the secret arts of the Beast Taming Sect still suit me best...

"The Beast Taming Sect’s Secret Art—Beast Weaponization… That contract beast…" Zhao Qingyue’s mind was in turmoil.

She had expected victory.

She had expected defeat.

But she had never expected—

She couldn’t even withstand one hit?!

Was this man a monster?

Was that beast... a newly contracted one?!

He didn’t even use his first or second contract beast, and I already lost?!

The gap between me and the number one from ten years ago... is this vast?

"...A little carp that hadn’t even been contracted before. But it has since leaped over the Dragon Gate and become my third contract beast."

Lin Jing gazed at the younger prodigy with a calm expression, his grip firm around the spear.

"Celestial Maiden, would you like to continue?"

He was quite satisfied with the power of Beast Weaponization.

If weapons were this effective...

Then turning a contract beast into armor would be just as formidable.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 121: Unable to Learn
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 121: Unable to Learn

Zhao Qingyue left.

She now understood the gap between her and Lin Jing.

Perhaps ten years ago, it was uncertain whether the past Lin Jing and the current number one on the Genius Rankings were stronger or weaker.

But now, Lin Jing had reached Foundation Establishment, contracted two Rare Beasts and a Rare Gu, and in a battle against opponents of the same level—he was nearly unmatched.

"I’ve never seen Celestial Maiden Bright Moon look so miserable before..." Jiang Lin murmured as she watched Zhao Qingyue’s retreating figure in a daze.

"Senior is just too strong..." Jiang Qi swallowed hard, his respect for Lin Jing deepening even further.

Beside them, Xu Zhizhi was feeling quite pleased. He was now certain—Lin Jing was the most promising powerhouse he had ever befriended.

When he eventually became Heavenly Minister, Lin Jing would be his greatest backing!

The Innate Dao Physique and the Supreme Yin Physique were both ranked among the top twenty physiques in history, yet Lin Jing had defeated both. Perhaps only the top ten Celestial Physiques, known as "destined to ascend," might be capable of challenging Lin Jing.

But such supreme talents were extremely rare. The last time one appeared was five thousand years ago.

Xu Zhizhi sighed. Even within Lin Jing’s lifetime, it was uncertain whether he would ever encounter a Celestial Physique prodigy.

Then again, perhaps that was for the best. Whenever a Celestial Physique emerged, it wasn’t just the prodigies of that era who were overshadowed—even ancient monsters from past generations couldn’t escape their shadow.

Not meeting one wasn’t necessarily a bad thing.

"Have the students from my cohort all graduated?" Lin Jing asked.

"No, Gao Sheng still hasn’t graduated," Xu Zhizhi replied. "That guy says that once he graduates, his family will force him into arrangements, so he’s been deliberately staying on as an academy student, just wandering around."

"Alright, forget about him then." Lin Jing shrugged.

"So, what’s your next move?" Xu Zhizhi was curious. "Are you applying for graduation? With your strength, you could easily become a city lord in the Ancient Domain. You wouldn’t be sent to some remote area—the path of promotion would be wide open for you!"

"I don’t plan on it for now."

Although an official position would allow his Hidden Dragon Secret Art to advance further, Lin Jing felt that the current stage of the technique was sufficient.

The third level of the Hidden Dragon Secret Art, which enhanced cultivation speed, wasn’t something he urgently needed. More than anything, completing his Heavenly Demon Physique was the priority.

The Immortal Child had once said that a Nascent Soul-stage cultivator with a Heavenly Demon Physique could burn a thousand years of lifespan to gain ten years of Void Mystical-level power.

This kind of return was absolutely insane.

And for Lin Jing, who already had a five-thousand-year lifespan at Foundation Establishment, sacrificing a thousand years was nothing.

If he could master the Heavenly Demon Physique, even if his base cultivation was slightly lower and his breakthroughs were slower than normal cultivators, his life-burning power-ups would completely outclass the Hidden Dragon Secret Art.

But right now, his biggest problem was—

He couldn’t learn it.

"I'm going back into seclusion," Lin Jing declared.

He planned to retreat for a few more years until he had completely grasped the Heavenly Demon Physique.

"Again?!?" Xu Zhizhi almost spat blood. You just came out!

"Before I do, I’ll make sure there are enough Rising Dragon Pills available." Lin Jing grinned.

"Fine, whatever." Xu Zhizhi sighed. Lin Jing could do whatever he wanted—geniuses had their own way of cultivating.

The Fire Qilin Dragon had just reached its seventh task before undergoing Foundation Establishment Dragon Transformation—continuing alchemy training.

At its current strength, Lin Jing suspected that the Rising Dragon Pills it refined would be of even greater quality.

And for the Pine Leaf Mouse, which had already reached late Foundation Establishment and had perfected the Longevity Technique, gathering alchemy materials had become much easier.

As for Lin Jing himself—

He devoted everything to his Heavenly Demon Physique cultivation.

Lin Jing had burned through most of his ten years' worth of Hidden Dragon Fortune.

But unfortunately, when it came to the Heavenly Demon Physique—

He still barely understood it.

"Pathetic."

At this point, even Senior Divine Pine had woken up. Having observed Lin Jing’s training from the start, the tree spirit now found it incredibly boring.

It was more eager than Lin Jing himself to see what kind of power the Longevity Body would unleash when paired with the Heavenly Demon Physique. Surely, it wouldn’t be inferior to a Celestial Physique, right?

"Kid, if you’re getting nowhere, switch things up," Divine Pine finally spoke, seeing that Lin Jing’s eyes were bloodshot, on the verge of going insane from frustration.

"This shouldn't be so difficult…"

"The process is simple—you just reshape the heart with True Essence, cultivate a Life-Flame that burns lifespan, and use the heart as a cauldron, refining your lifeforce into a cultivation-enhancing pill..."

"I’ve successfully reshaped my heart according to the technique, but why can’t I cultivate the Life-Flame?"

Lin Jing focused inward, staring at his now pitch-black heart—the core of the Heavenly Demon Physique. Did he modify something incorrectly?

"Talent is talent," Divine Pine scoffed. "That Thousand Transformations Technique of yours—on paper, it’s simple. All it does is restructure spiritual energy. But think about it—aside from using the Golden Bodhi Tree to master it, has anyone else ever succeeded?"

Lin Jing sighed. He decided to take the tree’s advice and train other techniques for now to clear his mind.

He sat cross-legged, his life aura weakening until he appeared completely lifeless, as though he had succumbed to death in meditation.

"I’ll practice Shedding Technique and Thousand Transformations for now."

For him, these two techniques were equally important at the moment.

With five thousand years of lifespan, his life energy had become overwhelmingly radiant—a beacon to anyone with spiritual perception. Fortunately, he had learned the Shedding Technique from Hundred Flower Valley, allowing him to suppress his lifeforce aura.

But even that wasn’t enough.

His mastery of the Shedding Technique wasn’t deep enough to fully suppress a five-thousand-year lifespan. However, if he combined it with the illusionary effects of Thousand Transformations, the two techniques together would allow him to fake his own death with near-perfect deception.

"Senior Divine Pine, in my current state, at what level could someone still detect me?"

"At Qi Refinement, your Thousand Transformations could deceive even Nascent Souls. Now that your True Qi has compressed into True Essence, your Illusionary Path arts are even stronger. You could probably fool a Nascent Soul cultivator now."

"Unless a Nascent Soul expert who specializes in spiritual detection makes a full-force probe, almost no one below the Void Mystical Stage could sense you."

"Hmm… If you had used your Hidden Dragon Fortune to master these two techniques instead of struggling with the Heavenly Demon Physique, you might have even been able to fool Nascent Souls completely..."

"Then I’ll keep training Shedding Technique and Thousand Transformations for now. As for the Heavenly Demon Physique…" Lin Jing hesitated. "It’s supposed to be trainable without external aid, but…"

"Senior, what if I find a flame that also burns lifespan, let my Fire Qilin Dragon refine it, and then study it from there? With its Flame Emperor Beast Physique, it can easily refine and manipulate exotic flames, and I can observe its properties in detail."

"Wait—forget that for now. Something’s happened."

Just as Lin Jing was waiting for Divine Pine’s response, the void suddenly trembled—

And Divine Pine’s shocked voice echoed in his mind.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 2: Chapter 122: Heaven and Earth Reversed (End of Book Two)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 2 Chapter 122: Heaven and Earth Reversed (End of Volume Two)

Divine Pine let out a startled cry, but before Lin Jing could fully grasp what was happening, an unprecedented surge of Void Mystical energy erupted within the academy.

A suffocating pressure filled the air as chaotic spatial energy spread across the sky, turning the once-clear heavens above Hidden Dragon Academy into a vast, murky gray void.

In an instant, dozens of towering Nascent Soul manifestations, Heaven and Earth True Souls, and even Void Mystical phenomena appeared. The sheer chaos of it all sent every student within the academy rushing outside, eyes filled with alarm as they looked up in disbelief.

"Who dares invade the academy?!"

Powerful academy elders scanned their surroundings.

At the center of the academy, the three Void Mystical Deans floated above their respective Void Mystical treasures, their immense spatial powers rippling outward as they tried to break through the spatial distortion trapping the academy.

All three deans were Void Mystical cultivators, and Lin Jing even recognized the goateed Vice Dean among them from a previous encounter.

At this moment, however, all three wore grim expressions—their shock was not because of an invader…

Something like this should have been impossible—unless…

A Dao-Seeking cultivator had intervened.

But Dao-Seeking cultivators were extremely rare, and even if one did make a move, why would they target a single academy?

Though Hidden Dragon Academy was important, it was not an Ancient Celestial Kingdom core stronghold.

"It’s not a Dao-Seeking cultivator… it’s the Celestial Palace Compass."

Suddenly, the white-haired female dean detected the source of the spatial anomaly.

In the void, a projection began to take form.

A shadowy figure stood atop a white celestial compass, his features obscured by thick, swirling death energy.

But the three deans immediately recognized the signature of his power—

"Yin Corpse Sect."

A cultivator from the Yin Corpse Sect had arrived, wielding a supreme spatial artifact.

"That’s right," the shadowy figure said, his voice dripping with malice. "I trust you’ve been well."

"I should thank the three of you for repeatedly interfering with my Yin Corpse Sect’s plans. Today, I have come to return the favor!"

"This Celestial Palace Compass… surely you recognize it?"

"Nine Infant Demon Lord, you seek death!"

The Grand Dean roared in fury, shattering the projection with a single strike.

But it was too late—

The Celestial Palace Compass had already activated.

A terrifying, formless spatial force swept across the academy, and before anyone could resist—

Students began vanishing into thin air.

Even the Nascent Soul-ranked professors were powerless—one after another, they were forcibly teleported away.

"We’re screwed! This is a relic from the Celestial Palace ruins! It was supposed to be in the hands of the Gu God Sect! How the hell did the Yin Corpse Sect get it?!"

As Divine Pine desperately tried to resist the teleportation force, it flooded Lin Jing’s mind with crucial information.

"Senior Divine Pine…!"

"It looks like the Gu God Sect and the Yin Corpse Sect have joined forces," Divine Pine growled.

"This Celestial Palace Compass is an ancient teleportation artifact—it has no offensive power, but its teleportation is completely random and uncontrollable.

"Even Void Mystical cultivators, who specialize in spatial arts, can’t break its teleportation force in time—and once you’re caught, no one can intercept the teleportation before it completes."

"Everything covered by the compass’s power is randomly scattered to different locations. At first glance, this might seem like a harmless gimmick, but in the right circumstances, it’s an unstoppable method of removing targets.

"And right now, you are the primary target!"

"Hidden Dragon City has Void Mystical protectors and is near Ancient Celestial City. Even if the three great demonic sects attacked with full force, they couldn’t capture you without risking destruction.

"But the Gu God Sect wants your Ice Soul Gu, and the Yin Corpse Sect wants your Longevity Body—and you’ve been hiding inside the academy all this time."

"If they can’t capture you in the city, then they’ll just forcefully teleport you away instead!"

"Sure, the destination is random—but once you’re out of the academy’s protection, the odds of them tracking you down are infinitely higher.

"Not to mention—this chaos also scatters the academy’s students all over the continent, disrupting the academy’s influence across multiple sects as an added act of revenge!"

"Listen up, kid! You have some resistance to the teleportation force, but not for long. Take this!"

Divine Pine hurled a leaf into Lin Jing’s storage bag.

"This will help you conceal your fate to some degree. It won’t make you impossible to track, but it’ll take much longer for anyone to pinpoint your location.

"Now pray to fate that you don’t end up in some demonic sect or a beast’s nest!!!"

Before Lin Jing could even respond—

He vanished.

The Celestial Palace Compass’s power had completed its work.

Under Divine Pine’s watchful gaze, the artifact’s spatial energy tore through the academy.

Even Lin Jing’s contract beasts weren’t spared.

Because only beasts directly bound to Lin Jing’s contract were considered one entity with him, his secondary beasts, stored within his Myriad Beast Circle, were forcefully ripped away—

Each one randomly scattered to an unknown destination.

Lin Jing felt like he was bleeding internally as he was forcibly teleported away.

Before disappearing, he glared at the last location of the Yin Corpse Sect’s cultivators.

"Damn Yin Corpse Sect."

"They disturb my seclusion… they steal my beasts… this grudge will not be forgotten."

Divine Pine also cursed aloud—

"Damn it! A Longevity Body? Of all things?!"

Moments later—

Even Divine Pine failed to resist the teleportation force.

It, too, vanished from the academy, its destination unknown.

That day—

A shocking event rocked the Ancient Celestial Kingdom.

The Yin Corpse Sect, wielding an ancient celestial artifact, had launched a devastating attack on Hidden Dragon Academy.

Hundreds of elite prodigies, professors, and scholars—

Forcibly teleported to unknown lands.

Some were lucky, still landing within the Nine Domains of the Ancient Celestial Kingdom.

But over eighty percent were completely lost—their whereabouts unknown.

The Ancient Celestial Kingdom, though vast, was only one part of the Tianyuan Continent.

Beyond it lay the Misty Forest, the Cloud Sea Mountains, Qingzhou, Martial Emperor City, the Snow Plains, the Western Desert, and even the Boundless Sea beyond the continent…

After a random teleportation, the chances of remaining within the kingdom were low.

Enraged, the Ancient Emperor wanted to retaliate—

But with the ongoing war against the Snow Plains, he couldn’t spare the forces to hunt down the missing prodigies or strike back at the demonic sects.

Meanwhile—

With the academy prodigies scattered, the Gu God Sect began hunting for the Ice Soul Gu.

The Yin Corpse Sect, armed with body-tracking artifacts, started searching for special physiques among the lost students.

With the kingdom at war, the demonic sects resumed their disruptions.

Hidden Dragon Academy shut down.

Their new mission—recover their lost prodigies.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 1: Qingzhou
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 1: Qingzhou

Crack!

A turbulent stream suddenly froze over as a surge of frost erupted into the air.

Shatter!

From within the frozen surface, a fist punched through the ice.

“Where the hell did I get teleported to?”

The ice split apart, and a young man with short black hair climbed out.

With a single sweep of his divine sense, he immediately grasped everything within a few kilometers.

This young man was none other than Lin Jing, who had been teleported to an unknown location by the Celestial Palace Teleportation Compass!

Since this was a random teleportation, if what Senior Pine said was true, and he ended up in the den of some high-level demonic beasts, then his life would be as good as over.

On the Tianyuan Continent, the domain of demonic beasts was hardly any smaller than that of humans…

The Misty Forest, the Cloud Sea Mountains, and the Western Desert Snow Plains—each was ruled by a Beast Emperor at the equivalent of the Dao-Seeking Stage, overseeing a vast empire of monsters.

Fortunately, a quick scan of his divine sense confirmed that he wasn’t so unlucky. He had merely landed in an uncharted forest.

There were no significant threats within a few kilometers—not even a single Foundation Establishment-level beast.

“A forest…”

Just knowing that he was in a forest wasn’t enough for Lin Jing to determine his exact location.

As he pondered his situation, three contracted beasts—Pine Leaf Mouse, Fire Qilin Dragon, and Ice Soul Gu—emerged one after another, reacting to the sudden teleportation.

“Hey! Hey!!”

“Roar!!!”

"Good thing you three are linked to me through our contract, treating us as one entity…”

With his contracted beasts by his side, Lin Jing felt slightly reassured.

Senior Pine had also been teleported to an unknown location and could no longer protect him. If he had been separated from his beasts as well, his remaining combat power would have left him feeling utterly defenseless.

“This damn compass.”

Lin Jing checked his Myriad Beast Circle once more and confirmed that his mosquito beast swarm had indeed been stripped away. He cursed under his breath.

Without them, his Mosquito-Powered Mech was temporarily useless.

Thankfully, mosquito beasts weren’t exactly rare. As long as he could return to Hidden Dragon Academy, he could easily commission an instructor to help him capture a few from the Cloud Sea Mountains. Once he had a breeding pair, they’d multiply quickly.

Still, given the current unknown circumstances, even if he had the mech, he wouldn’t dare use it—it would make him an instant target for the Demonic Sect.

“What now?”

Lin Jing recalled Senior Pine’s warning—this teleportation was likely orchestrated by the Yin Corpse Sect and the Gu God Sect.

Although the Yin Corpse Sect’s demonic cultivators claimed they were retaliating against the academy’s interference in their grand scheme, no one on Lin Jing’s side believed that was the whole truth.

Chances were, they were after his Longevity Body.

“You think the Gu God Sect has a way to track you down?” Lin Jing asked Ice Soul Gu.

“No idea… Maybe I should fake my death and go into hibernation?” Ice Soul Gu whimpered, clearly terrified.

For all its usual arrogance, it wasn’t willing to risk being captured by the Gu God Sect.

Better to cry inside the body of a Longevity Body cultivator than to laugh in the hands of a Gu Sect parasite master.

Just like birds choose the best tree to nest in, Gu creatures do the same.

Ice Soul Gu firmly believed that Lin Jing’s potential would surpass the entire Gu God Sect. The sect only had one Gu God at the Dao-Seeking Stage, after all.

If Lin Jing grew strong enough, not only could he devour the Nine Rare Gu, but he might even surpass the Gu God himself one day.

“Then go ahead and fake your death. Keep your aura hidden at all costs.”

"Our next major goal… is to get back to the Ancient Celestial Kingdom as soon as possible.”

Lin Jing opened his palm, revealing a golden leaf that Senior Pine had given him before the teleportation.

Senior Pine had claimed that this leaf could help mask his presence from fate-divining techniques, making it harder for powerful cultivators to track him down.

But just how effective it was… Lin Jing wasn’t entirely sure.

“Well, it’s gotta be useful to some extent. Senior Pine managed to break through to the Nascent Soul Stage unnoticed and hide in the Beast Taming Sect for so many years without being discovered—he definitely has some tricks up his sleeve.”

“With this leaf, combined with my Shedding Art and Thousand Transformations Technique, I just need to lay low and avoid exposure. At least in the short term, the two Demonic Sects shouldn’t be able to track me… If they do, well, that’s just my bad luck.”

Lin Jing took a deep breath and recalled Pine Leaf Mouse and Fire Qilin Dragon into his Myriad Beast Circle.

For now, he needed to minimize the use of his Beast Taming Abilities to avoid drawing attention.

His body slowly floated upward. While extending his divine sense to scout ahead, he transformed into a streak of rainbow light and flew toward the outer edges of the forest.

During this time, his appearance and body shape gradually changed…

Returning to the Ancient Celestial Kingdom was the priority, but first, he needed to figure out where he was.

If he was already within one of the kingdom’s regions, that would make things much easier.

After flying for a while, though he hadn’t yet left the forest, Lin Jing finally spotted a living person.

A mid-stage Qi Refinement teenager, no older than fifteen or sixteen, was wandering alone through the forest, seemingly searching for something.

Lin Jing sighed in relief.

In situations like this, the worst thing would be not encountering anyone at all.

As long as there were people around, it meant civilization wasn’t too far away—at least he hadn’t ended up in some forsaken death trap like the Misty Forest.

Whoosh!

A sudden gust of wind roared through the air.

Descending from the clouds, Lin Jing landed directly in front of the startled youth.

The moment he saw Lin Jing’s disguised ordinary-looking form, the Qi Refinement teenager felt an overwhelming pressure.

Foundation Establishment!

Without hesitation, he dropped to his knees, bowing his head.

"Junior greets Senior! I didn’t know you were cultivating here—I have disturbed your meditation. I deserve to die for my offense!"

"I swear this is all I have—please, have mercy!"

He hastily took out a few Spirit Stones as an offering.

Lin Jing scoffed internally.

If only the Yin Corpse Sect had been this respectful.

He had spent a decade training quietly in the academy without stepping foot outside, yet he still got dragged into this mess.

Damn it all.

A silken thread shot out and pierced the youth’s body.

Instantly, the boy’s eyes glazed over as Ice Soul Gu took control of his mind.

“Where is this place?” Lin Jing asked.

In a monotone voice, the youth replied, “Senior, this is Senluo Path, one of the Sixteen Paths of Qingzhou, just outside Linlang City, within the Evergreen Forest.”

Qingzhou!

Lin Jing looked up at the sky.

So he had ended up in Qingzhou…

Not exactly a demonic beast stronghold, but returning to the Ancient Celestial Kingdom from here wouldn’t be easy either.

Since the Misty Forest’s fog disrupted divine sense and spatial travel, no teleportation arrays existed between Qingzhou and the Ancient Celestial Kingdom.

To return, one had to physically traverse the Misty Forest.

And that was demonic beast territory—full of terrifying creatures who called themselves emperors and ancestors.

Only Lotus Pavilion had a relatively safe trade route through the forest, supposedly thanks to some kind of deal with a powerful beast.

But under current circumstances, with the Yin Corpse Sect and Gu God Sect on high alert, even disguising himself and traveling with Lotus Pavilion’s trade caravan wouldn’t be entirely safe.

And to make things worse, Qingzhou was actually the Yin Corpse Sect’s main base of operations.

In this situation, returning safely to the kingdom seemed nearly impossible.

“…Didn’t Sect Master Xu Xiaoyao and Senior Brother Ouyang Hao come to Qingzhou with Lotus Pavilion to make a name for themselves?”

“…But given the current situation, meeting up with them might not be the best idea. Staying hidden and waiting for Senior Pine to find me seems like the safer option.”

Lin Jing sighed.

If only he had already cultivated the Heavenly Demon Physique.

With at least four thousand years of lifespan to burn, he wouldn’t have to be so damn cautious all the time.

If he had reached Core Formation, with a lifespan nearing ten thousand years, combined with the Heavenly Demon Physique, he’d feel far more secure.

But alas, he hadn’t.

Gazing at the controlled youth, Lin Jing asked, “Who are you, and what are you doing alone in this forest?”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 2: Thousand Illusions Mountain
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 2: Thousand Illusions Mountain

Under Lin Jing’s control, the Qi Refinement teenager spilled everything.

His name was Lin Yun, a member of the Lin Clan from Linlang City.

Though he lacked any special physique, he had an exceptional mastery over True Energy, allowing him to execute techniques with extraordinary finesse.

For the past few months, Lin Yun had been experiencing a strange recurring dream—one that hinted at a great opportunity hidden within the Evergreen Forest.

At first, he dismissed it as a mere dream. But after having it more than ten times, he started to suspect that a high-level cultivator was using dreams to bestow him with a fated chance.

So, he came to the forest to seek it out.

“What an idiot…” Lin Jing shook his head, retracting the spider silk before instructing Ice Soul Gu to erase the boy’s recent memories. Then, he turned and left.

“What opportunity? As if it’s real…”

“They lure a so-called 'genius' like him out of his clan, deceive him into venturing deep into the wilderness, and then— bam —kidnap him. …No one understands being abducted better than I do.”

Having personally experienced the Yin Corpse Sect’s attempt to kidnap and sell him, Lin Jing had zero faith in these so-called fated opportunities—they were just elaborate traps.

Yet, as he continued flying forward, he suddenly halted in midair.

His gaze locked onto a cave entrance, concealed beneath a dense canopy of trees.

At first glance, the mountain appeared utterly unremarkable, but Lin Jing, whose mastery of Illusion Arts was second to none, could easily perceive the illusion formation concealing the cave.

He hesitated for a moment before flicking out a strand of spider silk, dragging a manual out from within the cave.

The grandiose name of the technique momentarily stunned Lin Jing.

Wait… was there actually an opportunity here?

But as soon as he flipped open the manual and read its contents, he fell silent.

Yeah. No thanks.

“To cultivate this technique, one must first sever their roots. Only by severing your roots can you slay immortals! I, the Rootless Immortal, spent two hundred years perfecting this divine technique during my Core Formation stage. Alas, my lifespan is running out, and I can no longer see this divine art reach its full potential… I hereby leave it in this place, awaiting a fated successor. If you have found this, it means your talent is as extraordinary as mine. Remember my name—Rootless Immortal! May you master this Immortal-Slaying Art and, with great determination, defy the heavens and sever the Dao!”

Lin Jing flipped through a few more pages before confirming—this was a completely useless technique.

Without hesitation, he tossed it back into the cave.

If that Qi Refinement kid had found this so-called opportunity, he likely wouldn’t have cultivated it anyway.

Shortly after, Lin Jing arrived in Linlang City.

Qingzhou was divided into sixteen regions, similar to the Nine Domains of the Ancient Celestial Kingdom. Each region was home to numerous cultivation sects and cities.

However, unlike the Ancient Celestial Kingdom, Qingzhou was far more chaotic. Demonic cultivators ran rampant, which was precisely why the Yin Corpse Sect had established its main base here.

Beyond that, sects like the Harmony Sect, which specialized in flesh trade, and assassination organizations flourished here far more than in the Ancient Celestial Kingdom.

Of course, while there was no imperial government, cultivators who slaughtered mortals indiscriminately or used them for cultivation experiments were still hunted down and exterminated by rival sects.

Especially if the mortals in question resided within a sect’s territory.

After all, cultivators don’t just appear out of thin air.

Cultivation sects needed a large mortal population to produce talented disciples. Whether they wanted these disciples to inherit the sect’s Dao or simply use them as cheap labor, they needed people.

Thus, when demonic cultivators massacred mortals for their own cultivation, orthodox sects saw it as a direct attack on their foundation.

Lin Jing gathered more information upon arriving in Linlang City.

He learned that Thousand Illusions Mountain was located in Senluo Path, one of Qingzhou’s Sixteen Paths.

The weakest faction in Senluo Path was called Senluo Hall, which had no Void Mystical Stage cultivators overseeing it.

However, Senluo Path was also home to three first-rate factions, each with Nascent Soul-level powerhouses:

“…Right, I almost forgot—Senluo Path is where the Myriad Beast Sect of Qingzhou is located.”

“What a coincidence.”

Lin Jing wasn’t sure whether the Myriad Beast Sect’s Deputy Sect Master and his two disciples had returned from the Ancient Celestial Kingdom yet.

Those three knew him to some extent.

But as long as he avoided using techniques that could expose his identity, he should be fine.

Originally, Lin Jing had planned to head for Tongtian Path, the strongest path in Qingzhou, ruled by the most powerful human faction—Tongtian Tower.

Tongtian Tower was a pill-refining faction.

Qingzhou had always been renowned for its herbs and alchemy, far surpassing the Ancient Celestial Kingdom in pill refinement techniques.

And the most prestigious alchemy faction in Qingzhou was none other than Tongtian Tower.

Rumors claimed that Tongtian Tower housed a sentient Celestial Pill.

Just a trace of its aura could grant a cultivator a massive surge in battle power.

The Tower Master was said to be so powerful that even among Dao-Seeking cultivators, he ranked among the strongest—entirely thanks to this Celestial Pill Spirit.

One of the reasons why the Ancient Celestial Kingdom had never annexed Qingzhou was because of this very Celestial Pill Spirit.

Even if the Ancient Emperor could harness the power of national destiny, the Celestial Pill Spirit wasn’t to be trifled with.

At the very least, Tongtian Tower wouldn’t be subjugated as easily as the Ancient Emperor had subdued other sects in the past.

Lin Jing had originally intended to go to Tongtian Path because of its thriving alchemy scene.

Where there was alchemy, there were bound to be many exotic flames—powerful flames used for refining pills.

If he could find a flame that burned lifespan, he could use it to cultivate the Heavenly Demon Physique.

But now, he had changed his mind.

Even if he found such a flame, obtaining it with his current strength and wealth would be nearly impossible.

Moreover, Tongtian Path was crawling with high-level cultivators.

With his current Illusion Dao abilities, he wouldn’t be able to hide his Longevity Body for long.

His enemies weren’t only the Yin Corpse Sect.

There was another Longevity Body cultivator in this world—one who was currently imprisoned within a powerful demonic beast clan in Qingzhou.

Instead of rushing to Tongtian Path, Lin Jing decided it would be safer to first improve his Illusion Arts in Senluo Path.

Thousand Illusions Mountain was a sect specializing in illusions, led by a Nascent Soul Ancestor who was in permanent closed-door cultivation.

Day-to-day sect affairs were managed by the Sect Master, who was half-step Nascent Soul, bordering on Semi-Divine Realm.

The sect possessed not only the Thousand Illusions Codex, but also two powerful secret techniques:

These two techniques made Thousand Illusions Mountain’s combat style unique among illusion sects.

Their disciples manifested illusions of powerful beasts, using their aura and ferocity to inflict mental and spiritual damage on enemies.

Lin Jing smirked.

“Illusion beasts still count as beasts, right?”

“And if they’re beasts… I can tame them.”

“…That works.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 3: Entering the Sect
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 3: Entering the Sect

A few days later, Lin Jing arrived at the Thousand Illusions Mountain Sect in Senluo Path.

On the way here, he had already done thorough research on the sect.

Unlike in the Ancient Celestial Kingdom, where sects only accepted disciples once every few years, most sects in Qingzhou recruited new members at all times.

Thousand Illusions Mountain was no exception.

They had an entire mountain peak dedicated to entry examinations, where potential disciples were tested.

The test was divided into two categories:

Lin Jing didn’t bother concealing his mid-stage Foundation Establishment cultivation.

However, regardless of whether one was a mortal or a rogue cultivator, the most important part of the examination was the Illusory Heart Formation.

This was a powerful illusion formation set up by a high-ranking sect elder.

Anyone who entered would have their true intentions exposed—if they sought to join Thousand Illusions Mountain for impure reasons, they would lose their sanity and reveal their true self.

“An illusion formation, huh…”

Lin Jing wasn’t particularly worried.

It wasn’t because of his own mastery of the Illusion Dao, but because of the Ice Soul Gu within him.

With Ice Soul Gu’s ability to freeze his mind, illusions had little to no effect on him.

“You wish to join Thousand Illusions Mountain?”

At the foot of Ascending Immortal Peak, a fat elder in a purple Daoist robe stroked his beard as he scrutinized Lin Jing.

“You’ve already reached mid-stage Foundation Establishment at such a young age… quite the talent. Why do you wish to join Thousand Illusions Mountain?”

Lin Jing replied respectfully, “Senior, I am an orphan. Since childhood, I followed my master in cultivation. However, not long ago, my master passed away. Before he died, he told me that I had a talent for the Illusion Dao and advised me to join Thousand Illusions Mountain to become a powerful cultivator.”

“A rogue cultivator, huh? What was your master’s name? And yours?” the Core Formation elder asked.

“My master went by the name ‘Daoist Huang Song’. As for me… my name is Huang Yu.”

“Never heard of him.” The elder scoffed and waved his hand dismissively. “There are far too many nameless rogue cultivators.”

“But with your level of talent, as long as you pass the Illusory Heart Formation, joining Thousand Illusions Mountain will be no problem.”

“However…”

The elder let out a dramatic sigh.

“A genius like you? We have plenty. And since you’re joining midway, without someone to guide you, even with your talent in Illusion Dao, becoming a core disciple won’t be easy.”

Hearing this, Lin Jing clenched his jaw—but inside, he knew exactly what this meant.

With a bleeding heart, he took out a pouch of mid-grade Spirit Stones and handed it over.

“Senior, this is just a small token of my respect. I hope you can… guide me a little.”

The elder weighed the Spirit Stones in his hand and chuckled.

“You rogue cultivators always do this kind of thing.”

“Once you officially join Thousand Illusions Mountain, you must never continue such habits! But…”

“Since you are so eager to pursue the Dao, I’ll make an exception this time.”

With a satisfied grin, the elder pocketed the Spirit Stones and continued,

“You can skip the other tests. Just walk through the Illusory Heart Formation. If you pass, I’ll immediately arrange for you to become an Inner Mountain Disciple.”

“And… I’ll also grant you one free entry into the Divine Beast Hall.”

The elder grinned as he gestured toward a stone pathway leading down the mountain.

“Go on. It’s only a hundred meters.”

Using the alias ‘Xin Feng’, Lin Jing nodded in acknowledgment.

At least this elder isn’t completely corrupt—he takes bribes, but he does his job well.

Lin Jing—Jing Lin—reversed, it becomes ‘Yu Huang’… symbolizing my ambition.

Emperor of Beast Taming.

Lin Jing passed through effortlessly.

From his divine sense, this Illusory Heart Formation only had an effect on cultivators below the Nascent Soul Stage.

His own mastery of Illusion Dao was already high enough to render it useless.

Of course, Lin Jing’s Illusion Dao talent wasn’t normal.

He had comprehended it beneath the Golden Bodhi Tree—a level far beyond what ordinary cultivators could achieve.

If Nascent Soul-level rogue cultivators wished to join Thousand Illusions Mountain, they likely wouldn’t even need to walk through the formation. The Sect Master himself would personally negotiate with them.

“Well done.”

The fat elder in purple robes appeared before Lin Jing in a flash, grinning.

“Huang Yu, was it? Wait here—I’ll summon a disciple to bring you inside.”

As he spoke, he took out a jade token and infused it with spiritual energy.

Before long, a streak of rainbow light shot out from Thousand Illusions Mountain toward them.

During this time, Lin Jing received a technique manual from the elder.

The elder smirked and advised,

“I don’t know what cultivation technique you’re practicing now, but you should consider switching while you’re still at a lower realm.”

“To master Illusion Dao, having a proper Illusion Dao technique is essential.”

“If you cast illusions using ordinary energy, you’ll only achieve sixty percent of their full potential. But with Thousand Illusions True Energy, their effectiveness will double!”

“I understand, Senior!” Lin Jing responded sincerely.

But deep down, he had no intention of switching techniques.

He had no plans to pursue Illusion Dao for the long run—he was simply using it as a temporary cover in Qingzhou.

For someone like him, who found cultivation incredibly difficult, the Beast Taming Art suited him far better.

Besides, while the Thousand Illusions Codex was decent, it couldn’t compare to his self-created technique—Thousand Transformations Technique.

His Thousand Transformations Technique could already deceive Nascent Soul cultivators while still in the Qi Refinement Stage.

Of course, its current flaw was that it only created deception.

Unlike Thousand Illusions True Energy, which could cause direct spiritual damage, his illusions had no real offensive power.

That was precisely why Lin Jing had infiltrated Thousand Illusions Mountain—to fill in this gap and further refine his Illusion Dao techniques.

“Second Uncle, you called for me?”

The fat disciple who arrived was a mid-stage Foundation Establishment cultivator.

His round body and shifty eyes made him seem far from trustworthy.

The elder glared at him.

“How many times have I told you?! Call me Elder You while in the sect!”

“Ah! Ahem—Elder You! What are your orders?”

“This is Huang Yu. He just passed the examination and joined Thousand Illusions Mountain as a rogue cultivator genius.”

“He’s qualified to enter the Divine Beast Hall. Take my command token and hand it over to Elder Qin, the guardian of the hall.”

“Yes, Second Uncle!” The fat disciple grinned.

Then, he turned to Lin Jing, flashing a wide smile.

“Junior Brother Huang! Since you entered the sect through my Second Uncle, I’ll be looking after you from now on.”

“Come, let me take you to the Divine Beast Hall!”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 4: The Portrait
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 4: The Portrait

“Senior Brother You, I’ll be relying on your care in Thousand Illusions Mountain from now on.” Lin Jing handed over a small pouch of Spirit Stones, hoping to build a good relationship.

After introductions, he learned that the chubby young man was named You Wanli and was from the same cultivation family as Deacon You, who had just overseen the disciple recruitment.

“No problem at all! Junior Brother Huang, you’re too polite.” Senior Brother You was delighted and said, “Honestly, there’s nothing too special to worry about in Thousand Illusions Mountain.”

“Just remember—whenever you go out, if you see disciples from Myriad Beast Sect or Flower Sect, beat them to death and rob them blind!”

“Our Thousand Illusions Mountain has a deep-seated grudge against those two sects! They’ve stolen several of our Spiritual Veins!”

“As for Myriad Beast Sect, they’re beast tamers—brutal and savage! There’s not a single good person among them. Tell me, what kind of decent person tames beasts? Demonic beasts are living creatures too! Unlike us in Thousand Illusions Mountain, who merely use illusions to simulate demonic beasts in battle.”

“And Flower Sect? Even worse! On the surface, they’re a sect that manipulates plants and flowers, but in reality, they encourage their disciples to hide their identities, marry into small sects and cultivation families, drain them dry, scam them out of betrothal gifts, seize family fortunes—even use secret techniques to siphon their Dao partners’ cultivation!”

“Many disciples of our Thousand Illusions Mountain have fallen for their tricks! Ah, the downfall of men—desire!”

“Good!” You Wanli nodded. “Since you just joined Thousand Illusions Mountain, the sect won’t assign you any tasks for the first three years. You’ll have time to switch your cultivation techniques and train in our sect’s arts.”

“Use these three years to strengthen yourself.”

“Besides waiting for sect missions, you can also join one of the sect’s specialized halls. For example, Illusionary Assassination Hall, which specializes in raiding other sects and seizing resources on behalf of Thousand Illusions Mountain.”

“The disciples of Illusionary Assassination Hall enjoy top-tier benefits, but their mortality rate is also sky-high. If you’re not strong enough, it’s best to think twice before joining.”

“Another good option is Demon-Illusion Hall. Our sect has a powerful artifact—the Demon-Illusion Mirror—which records the power of demonic beasts and preserves their images. These can then be used by sect disciples to hone their illusion techniques.”

“However, disciples of Demon-Illusion Hall must infiltrate battlefields of Demon Kings and above, use illusion techniques to conceal themselves, and record their fights. If your mastery of illusions isn’t good enough, you’ll likely be discovered by these terrifying creatures and die on the battlefield…”

“There’s more…”

As the two flew forward, You Wanli continued introducing the various halls of Thousand Illusions Mountain. Lin Jing was intrigued—this sect was truly unique, with many of its branches focused on the art of illusionary beasts.

Their next destination?

The Divine Beast Hall.

“The outermost layer of the Divine Beast Hall holds 300 statues of powerful Demon Kings, carefully selected by our sect. The middle layer contains 36 statues of different Demon Emperor species, while the innermost layer features three primordial ancestral beasts from legendary bloodlines, whose power rivals Void Mystical Stage cultivators.”

“When you enter, you’ll only be allowed to observe the Demon King statues in the outer layer. As for the Demon Emperors and the Primordial Beasts? Forget about simulating them—just looking at them might be enough to shatter a Foundation Establishment cultivator’s soul.”

“Since Second Elder sent you directly to the Divine Beast Hall, you must have some foundation in illusions, right? If you can manifest just 10% of a Demon King’s essence in your illusion within seven days, then this trip will have been worthwhile. Whether it’s the Illusionary Assassination Hall or Demon-Illusion Hall, they’ll be fighting to recruit you.”

“Remember, don’t get greedy! Choose a demonic beast that resonates with you the most and study it thoroughly!” You Wanli reminded.

Lin Jing nodded.

Manifest 10% of a Demon King’s presence?

Hah…

Back in the Qi Refinement Stage, he had already used his Thousand Transformations Technique to perfectly replicate the full presence of a Nascent Soul-level Demon King!

Since that’s the case…

Let’s see if I can imprint all 800 Demon King images from the Divine Beast Hall into my memory in just one day.

Inside a vast palace, an elder of Thousand Illusions Mountain received an official decree. With a flick of his sleeve, the small door to the Divine Beast Hall opened.

“He only has one day.” The elder stated.

You Wanli turned to Lin Jing and said, “Junior Brother Huang, good luck.”

“Many thanks.” Lin Jing cupped his fists and stepped into the Divine Beast Hall.

The hall was enormous. Statues of over a hundred demonic beasts lined both sides of the chamber, while another hundred were depicted in paintings on the walls.

Lin Jing’s gaze landed on the closest bear demon statue.

As he stared at it, an illusion surged in his mind—

A massive Bear King, cloaked in dark golden lightning, raised its enormous paw to swat at him!

The bear’s palm expanded, blotting out the sky, then slammed down—

BANG!

Lin Jing’s consciousness was forcefully pulled back to reality.

Without sufficient mastery of illusion techniques or cultivation, just looking at these statues could cause a mental backlash.

“All the demonic beasts recorded here have at least late-stage Nascent Soul cultivation. Their bloodlines are absurdly powerful. If they didn’t meet these conditions, the elite cultivators of Thousand Illusions Mountain wouldn’t even bother preserving their images.”

Lin Jing scanned the hall.

Even if he perfectly simulated all these Demon King images, it wouldn’t be of much use to him.

After all, his Thousand Transformations Technique could already partially manifest the presence of Senior Thunder Pine, a Nascent Soul Divine Pine. Even if it was just an illusion, it was far more powerful than these Demon Kings.

He glanced toward the deeper layers of the hall.

If he could enter further and observe the Demon Emperor and Primordial Beast statues, that would be far more beneficial.

But with his current status as a mere disciple, there was no chance of stepping past that closed door.

“The technique used to perfectly imprint a demonic beast’s presence into paintings and statues is worth studying. If I can master it, I could create illusions in specific locations to trap or deceive enemies.”

Lin Jing found something worth learning and began to study.

Seven days later.

In Thousand Illusions Mountain—

After escorting Lin Jing into the Divine Beast Hall, You Wanli had returned to his cave abode. His only task left was to retrieve Lin Jing once he was done and send him back.

“Seven days have passed… I wonder how much Junior Brother Huang has gained from this experience?”

Just as You Wanli was about to set out—

A powerful sect-wide decree echoed across Thousand Illusions Mountain.

“All disciples, hear this! If you encounter the man from the painting, report it to the sect immediately. A reward of a magical artifact will be granted.”

“His name is Lin Jing. Foundation Establishment cultivation. A Beast Taming Sect disciple from the Ancient Kingdom. Skilled in Beast Taming Arts, Buddhist techniques, and mechanized constructs. He possesses a pet squirrel, and his body harbors one of Gu God Sect’s Ten Rare Gu—Ice Soul Gu…”

You Wanli looked up.

A massive scroll floated above Thousand Illusions Mountain, displaying a portrait of a young man dressed in white, with a gourd at his waist, an extraordinary aura, and striking features.

Behind him was a faint shadow—his blue-eyed form infused with the Ice Soul Gu.

At that very moment, similar portraits were appearing in multiple sects…

“Hmm?”

Just as Lin Jing stepped out of the Divine Beast Hall, he looked up at the sky—

Where his own wanted portrait was floating.

He fell into deep thought.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 5: The Prodigy
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 5: The Prodigy

“Junior Brother Huang, you’re out!”

Outside the Divine Beast Hall, a streak of light descended. You Wanli saw Lin Jing staring at the massive portrait in the sky and muttered, “Who is this guy? What kind of trouble did he stir up for Thousand Illusions Mountain to go this far in searching for him?”

“If only I could track him down... a magical artifact as a reward…” You Wanli sighed in envy.

“Senior Brother You, it’s best not to dwell on it,” Lin Jing advised. “That man, just like those Myriad Beast Sect cultivators, is a Beast Tamer. He’s certainly no good. And his connection to the Gu God Sect is murky at best—probably a ruthless character. It’s best not to get entangled in his fate.”

Looking at the wanted notice, Lin Jing felt a chill run down his spine.

Was this issued by the Yin Corpse Sect, or was it a missing person notice from the Ancient Kingdom?

It shouldn't be from the Ancient Kingdom, right?

After all, the academy had lost so many geniuses, there was no reason for them to search only for him.

Besides, Qingzhou was the stronghold of the Yin Corpse Sect. Here, strength ruled, and demonic cultivators thrived far more freely than in the Ancient Kingdom.

Even if the Yin Corpse Sect was despised, there was no denying its dominance—these minor sects with only Nascent Soul cultivators standing guard were nothing to them.

Even the Demon-Slaying Bureau in the Ancient Kingdom was rumored to have secret dealings with demonic sects. In Qingzhou, it was even more common for righteous sects to have connections with the Yin Corpse Sect.

If the Yin Corpse Sect wanted Thousand Illusions Mountain to put out a bounty, would they dare refuse?

Now, Lin Jing’s only concern was:

Did the Yin Corpse Sect already know he was in Qingzhou?

Or were they casting a wide net, posting the bounty in multiple places—Cloud Sea Mountains, and other regions as well?

Lin Jing figured that if the Yin Corpse Sect had the means to use that kind of random teleportation treasure, they must have ways to track someone down as well.

The question was—

How precise was their tracking?

Finding a general location was one level of divination. Pinpointing an exact spot was another level entirely.

Fortunately, Lin Jing had the special leaf from Senior Thunder Pine, which helped conceal his presence.

But relying on a single leaf to completely fool two major demonic sects was unrealistic.

The Yin Corpse Sect and the Gu God Sect might have already figured out that he was in Qingzhou—or perhaps even specifically in Senluo Path.

Still—

One thing was certain:

The Yin Corpse Sect had hit a wall in their search.

If they already knew where he was, they wouldn’t need to mobilize other sects to help find him.

Changing his name, altering his appearance, switching to illusion techniques, and hiding his Beast Taming skills—all of these had been the right decisions.

As long as this wanted status remained active, Lin Jing absolutely couldn’t reveal his Pine Leaf Mouse or Ice Soul Gu.

Thankfully, back in the Ancient Kingdom, he hadn’t showcased his illusion abilities much, so they weren’t listed on the wanted notice.

This gave him a window of opportunity.

Now, his alchemy skills would also be crucial for blending in while traveling through Qingzhou.

As Lin Jing was lost in thought, a voice suddenly rang out.

“This kid speaks sense.”

The voice came from deep within Thousand Illusions Mountain.

“This scroll was given to our sect by Senluo Hall. The man in the portrait has nothing to do with us. No matter what he has done or who he is, it’s best not to meddle.”

“The Ten Rare Gu… who knows? Just getting close to him might already turn you into one of his puppets.”

“Additionally, I heard that ten years from now, this man won’t just be the number one Core Formation genius of the Ancient Kingdom—but he’ll be able to slay Core Formation cultivators while still in Qi Refinement!”

The voice came from Elder Qin, who had been meditating beneath the Divine Beast Hall. He looked toward You Wanli.

“Elder Qin,” You Wanli bowed respectfully, his face filled with shock. “This guy is that powerful? Compared to him, even Myriad Beast Sect’s core disciples seem like weaklings.”

“Well, the Ancient Kingdom is the center of the cultivation world, after all…”

“Speaking of which,” Elder Qin turned to You Wanli, “One day has passed. You remember his name was Huang Yu, right? Go see how much he gained from the Divine Beast Hall.”

“Let’s find out if he’s truly gifted in illusions—or if he just bribed his way in through Deacon You.”

You Wanli glanced at Lin Jing reassuringly, signaling that he shouldn’t worry.

After all, Elder Qin had accepted gifts from the You family as well.

Even if Lin Jing’s performance was poor, he wouldn’t be punished too harshly.

Lin Jing cupped his fists slightly.

This Nascent Soul early-stage elder was already as strong as the Sect Master of the Beast Taming Sect.

However, Lin Jing wasn’t too concerned.

With his current illusion mastery, fooling anyone below the Nascent Soul Transformation Stage was effortless.

But—

Since Thousand Illusions Mountain was a sect that specialized in illusions, he couldn’t be careless.

There was a chance that some Nascent Soul elders might be able to see through his disguise.

So, he kept his illusion techniques fully activated, pushing his abilities to their absolute limits.

Normally, maintaining an illusion required a certain amount of True Energy.

But to perfect his illusion to an extreme degree, he was now consuming ten times the usual amount—an enormous drain on his resources.

Even with his strong foundation, maintaining such an intense illusion for an entire day would completely drain his energy.

Fortunately, his Pine Leaf Mouse, Fire Qilin Dragon, and Ice Soul Gu were constantly supplying him with True Energy, acting as his personal energy batteries.

At this rate, Lin Jing even felt that just maintaining this state for a few years could dramatically improve his illusion mastery—even without actively training.

Too bad his three spirit beasts had to remain hidden.

For now, they could only serve as power sources.

“Disciple has gained significant insights!”

With that, Lin Jing activated his True Energy.

A strong gust of wind swept across the ground, and a towering demonic monkey illusion emerged!

It was massive and menacing, with a ferocious expression.

Its body was covered in reddish-brown fur, and its head, chest, arms, legs, and tail burned with everlasting white flames, making it look like a creature born from a pale inferno.

“ROAR!!!”

The monkey demon let out a deafening roar.

The moment You Wanli laid eyes on it, he was briefly stunned.

Even Elder Qin showed a rare look of surprise.

“This… White Abyss Monkey King…”

“One of the 300 Demon Kings in the outer layer. Its cultivation is half-step Nascent Soul Transformation, and its tyrannical white flames make it the most difficult Demon King to replicate.”

“You managed to capture two-tenths of its essence in just seven days?”

“Genius! This is pure genius!”

“Deacon You has finally done something right—bringing a true illusion prodigy into our sect!”

“If you switch to our sect’s techniques, you might even become a true disciple in the future!”

Elder Qin was deeply impressed.

Lin Jing, on the other hand, put on an excited expression—but deep down, he remained calm and calculating.

He had to show enough talent to be valued—

But not so much that it became excessive.

If he truly wanted to, he could manifest all 300 Demon Kings perfectly, one by one.

But for now—

That level of skill was best kept hidden.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 6: The Outsider
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 6: The Outsider

"Thank you for the elder's praise." Lin Jing kept up his act, unexpectedly enjoying the feeling.

His mastery of illusion techniques was impressive... It really seemed perfect for infiltrating other sects and stealing their spells.

"It's your own talent." Seeing a gifted disciple put Elder Qin in a good mood.

Truthfully, Lin Jing's excellence had little to do with him, but after guarding the Divine Beast Hall for years and witnessing too many dim-witted disciples, Elder Qin felt utterly drained.

He had cultivated to the Nascent Soul stage, so his own talent had once been outstanding. That’s why he simply couldn’t comprehend—why was the basic illusion of transforming into a demonic beast so difficult for so many people? Was it really that hard?

He was just sick of stupidity. So seeing a disciple who could skillfully apply the fundamentals of illusionary transformation put him at ease.

"The White Abyss Monkey King, huh..." Standing nearby, You Fangli was also astonished by Lin Jing’s talent—it far surpassed his own back then.

At this rate, in the future at Thousand Illusions Mountain, who would be taking care of whom was now up for debate.

"When your illusion can fully display the presence of a Demon King, you’ll qualify to compete for the rank of Core Disciple."

"If you make it to Core Disciple status, you’ll gain the right to observe the 'Demon Emperor's Statue' in the deeper chambers of the Divine Beast Hall!"

"You have great talent. This old man looks forward to the day you return to the Divine Beast Hall." Elder Qin chuckled.

Lin Jing’s face flushed, seemingly thrilled at the praise and recognition...
