Not long after, You Wanli escorted Lin Jing back to the inner mountain, to a cave residence designated for him.

The spiritual energy conditions were decent, but compared to his previous academy cave residence, it was far inferior... which reminded Lin Jing of the remaining decades of usage rights he still had there.

"Junior Brother Huang, for now, you should cultivate here," You Wanli advised.

"With the talent you’ve shown, it won’t be long before someone invites you to join one of the sect halls. When that happens, just join whichever hall reaches out to you first—don’t refuse, don’t delay." He patted Lin Jing’s shoulder in encouragement.

"Thank you for your guidance, Senior Brother You."

You Wanli laughed heartily. "No need to thank me. Did Second Elder give you your Inner Sect disciple token along with your cultivation technique? With that token, you can enter the Inner Sect's Scripture Pavilion and select three high-grade illusion techniques to practice for free."

"Don’t forget to claim them..."

After You Wanli left, Lin Jing dropped his polite smile.

"Illusion techniques, huh..."

He didn’t linger in his cave residence and immediately made his way to Thousand Illusions Mountain's Inner Sect Scripture Pavilion.

He had come to Thousand Illusions Mountain for these very things—there was no reason to waste time.

As an Inner Sect disciple, he had unrestricted access to basic illusion techniques. However, most basic illusions involved transforming into people or scenery, and with Lin Jing’s current mastery of illusion arts, he had no need for them.

Only high-grade illusion techniques could be of use to him now.

After careful selection, he chose three techniques: Soul Infusion Art, Spirit-Piercing Method, and Layered Illusion Technique.

Each of these techniques compensated for gaps in his illusion arts from different angles.

The Soul Infusion Art was the very technique that allowed the Divine Beast Hall’s countless demonic beast statues and murals to exude lifelike presence!

It allowed an illusionist to bind illusions to a vessel and keep them anchored to a location. This meant he could create powerful illusions to deter enemies from entering the sect—or even use illusionary imprints to store and transmit information.

The Spirit-Piercing Method was what he truly lacked. Most illusion techniques lacked offensive power and could only deceive or disorient opponents. To cause real damage, he had to rely on his target being timid or lacking mental fortitude.

But with this method, he could infuse his illusions with his own spiritual power. When an enemy encountered a terrifying illusion, in the moment their mind wavered, he could strike directly at their spirit, dealing immense damage.

This technique could even be combined with the Soul Infusion Art , allowing statues and murals imbued with illusions to carry a hidden spiritual attack.

Finally, the Layered Illusion Technique was the most critical—it was the key to strengthening his illusions.

Thousand Illusions Mountain had a famous defensive formation called the Thousand Illusions Array , where intruders would be trapped in layers upon layers of illusions. Even after breaking one, another would take its place—first the second, then the third... up to a thousand layers.

This kind of endless deception could drive people insane.

The Layered Illusion Technique operated on a similar principle, allowing an illusionist to create double-layered illusions .

Currently, Lin Jing used only a single layer of illusion to disguise himself. If someone broke through it with spiritual sense, they would see his true form.

But if he mastered the Layered Illusion Technique , he could seamlessly apply a second illusion over the first.

Even if an enemy dispelled the initial disguise, they might still not see his real face—only another false identity he had prepared beforehand. This would drastically improve his safety.

With the three techniques in hand, Lin Jing hurried back to his cave residence, preparing to enter seclusion for a while...

"Seems like the Yin Corpse Sect isn’t completely useless after all."

Upon returning, Lin Jing noticed something interesting—his Hidden Dragon Fortune , which had been nearly depleted due to cultivating the Heavenly Demon Physique, was surging again.

After thinking for a moment, he realized—damn it, wasn’t the Yin Corpse Sect spreading bounty notices to hunt him down?

In a way, they were boosting his reputation!

Just like earlier—Elder Qin had showered him with praise.

"My fortune in illusion cultivation is here."

Meanwhile...

Amidst the endless chaos of the void, a pinecone drifted aimlessly, its situation utterly miserable.

"This damn Celestial Palace Compass was supposed to randomly teleport me somewhere in Tianyuan Continent—not into a sealed void ruin! Where the hell am I?! I need to get back!!!"

"Does this place even count as part of Tianyuan Continent?"

"And why the hell is this void barrier so damn hard to break through..."

Looking at the ancient war-torn ruins surrounding him, Divine Pine struggled but couldn’t pierce through the void barrier. He had no idea where the Celestial Palace Compass had thrown him.

"This is the World Boundary Wall. The place you're in is the boundary fissure of the Tianyuan Realm," a voice suddenly echoed.

A withered remnant soul emerged from the void ruins, staring directly at Divine Pine.

"You are not a native of this realm, which is why you can't pass through easily. In fact, your arrival here may be due to the realm rejecting your existence—hence your spatial teleportation failed."

Then the soul hesitated before throwing out a series of rapid questions:

"Wait a minute... You're not a native, yet your entire being is drenched in the aura of this world. Are you a tree that smuggled itself into the Tianyuan Realm and reincarnated to blend in?

"Or were you beaten so badly by a powerhouse of this world that you had no choice but to reincarnate as a pine tree to hide?

"Wait—crossing realms illegally? Are you from the Demonic Sky Sect? That sect that loves invading cultivation worlds?"

A string of accusations poured out.

"...F***, I’m just a tree." Divine Pine stared at the remnant soul.

"Who the hell are you? Me, Demonic Sky Sect, an outsider?!"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 7: The Illusionary Assassination Hall
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 7: The Illusionary Assassination Hall

"Of course, it doesn't really matter what your identity is. Pine Tree, my friend, do you want to return to the Tianyuan Realm?"

"Though tracing back to your origins, you're not a native of this realm, you've already absorbed its aura. As long as your cultivation reaches the Void Mystical Stage and you gain mastery over stronger spatial forces, you'll have a chance to cross the World Boundary and return to Guanyuan Realm."

"However, trying to ascend from this chaotic void ruin to the Void Mystical Stage is no easy task. But I… I've wandered here for who knows how many years. I understand this place well. If you have my help, you might just reach the Void Mystical Stage in a short time!"

Divine Pine sneered. "I don’t care what my identity is, but shouldn’t you introduce yourself first? What's your purpose?"

The remnant soul sighed. "Just a pitiful soul who failed to ascend in another cultivation realm. I managed to preserve a sliver of my soul, but I got lost in the void, unable to find my way home, nor enter any other cultivation world…"

"So, I need to attach myself to you and enter the Tianyuan Realm together. With your identity as a reincarnated Demonic Sky Sect cultivator, you should be able to smuggle me in as well."

"I need you to help me reincarnate into this cultivation world, just like you did, becoming half a native of the Tianyuan Realm. This is my only path to survival, and I hope you understand—I’m simply trying to stay alive."

"I can swear an oath to the Heavenly Dao: after you help me, whatever you choose to do in the Tianyuan Realm—whether ruling this world or reopening the path of ascension—I will assist you."

"You’re a demon beast! Not human!"

After a brief silence, Divine Pine suddenly realized the true nature of the remnant soul—it was actually the transformed soul of a demon beast.

"Interesting. It’s been how many years since a demon beast last appeared in the Tianyuan Realm? The Sect Master and his disciple from the Beast Taming Sect have probably been longing for the secret art of demonization for ages..."

"Alright, I agree. Now tell me, how do I reach the Void Mystical Stage in this godforsaken place?"

"Within ten years—do I have a chance?"

Divine Pine asked. If it had to rely on its own cultivation, it would take at least a few thousand years to reach the Void Mystical Stage. By then, the opportunity would be long gone.

By that time, the Longevity Body would have been claimed by someone else—not from old age, but simply because someone would have harvested it already.

Divine Pine had been nurturing the Longevity Body for so long precisely for the Longevity Blood that Lin Jing had promised him upon reaching perfection.

He could not tolerate returning to the Tianyuan Continent only to find out that Lin Jing had already been devoured by someone else.

As for whether this so-called failed ascender's remnant soul would cause trouble in the Tianyuan Continent… Divine Pine couldn't care less.

A Few Days Later

Perhaps due to the Golden Bodhi Tree unlocking his talent, or maybe due to his solid foundation in illusion arts, Lin Jing easily mastered the three illusion techniques he had claimed.

There wasn’t the slightest difficulty.

Cultivating illusion techniques felt even easier than practicing Buddhist teachings.

In comparison, cultivating the Heavenly Demon Physique was like gnawing on a piece of rock-hard bone.

"Just mastering them isn’t enough. Next, I need to refine these three techniques even further..."

"When the time is right, I'll simulate the aura of True Illusion Essence and display even greater strength and talent to compete for the position of Core Disciple at Thousand Illusions Mountain."

Sitting cross-legged in his cave, Lin Jing’s eyes flickered with determination.

These three illusion techniques were never his true goal in coming to Thousand Illusions Mountain.

His real goal was one of the Core Disciple secret techniques—the Inverse Void Art!

It contained the principles of real and illusionary transformation, a secret technique capable of temporarily turning illusions into reality!

However, only Core Disciples could access this secret art. As an Inner Sect disciple, he had no chance of learning it yet.

Moreover, it was said that Inverse Void Art could only be cultivated in a special Illusory Paradise within Thousand Illusions Mountain—a place just like the Divine Beast Hall, serving as the sect's hidden foundation. Only Core Disciples could enter.

As for the other Core Disciple secret technique, Beast Intent Manifestation , which allowed illusionists to perfectly replicate the presence of powerful demon beasts, Lin Jing didn’t need it as much.

"Is Junior Brother Huang here?"

Just as You Wanli predicted, within a few days, someone from the sect sought him out.

The visitor was Zheng, a steward of the Illusionary Assassination Hall.

His purpose, of course, was to recruit Lin Jing into the Illusionary Assassination Hall.

To avoid unnecessary trouble, Lin Jing accepted the offer.

Originally, he had wanted to join the Demon-Illusion Hall.

That hall’s disciples were basically "battlefield reporters"—their tasks mainly involved secretly recording footage of powerful demon beasts, granting them much more freedom.

But the Illusionary Assassination Hall… it was practically a battlefield unit, constantly fighting against disciples from the Myriad Beast Sect, Flower Sect, and other sects over resources and territories. It was far too high-profile for his liking.

"Our Illusionary Assassination Hall needs talent like yours. For now, just focus on your cultivation and take your time switching to our hall’s techniques. We won’t assign you any missions in the short term."

"However, if you want to rise to the rank of Core Disciple, you’ll need more than just personal strength—contributions are a must. And our Illusionary Assassination Hall is the fastest place to earn those contributions!"

"Once you’ve switched techniques, come find me." Steward Zheng chuckled, patting Lin Jing on the shoulder.

He had high hopes for Lin Jing’s potential.

After all, even Elder Qin had acknowledged his talent.

"Understood, Steward Zheng."

Lin Jing had no other choice. However, being part of the Illusionary Assassination Hall did bring him some advantages—it meant fewer disturbances.

Clearly, the hall leaders had already discussed among themselves before deciding to recruit him…

This was exactly why Senior Brother You had advised him to join whichever hall reached out first.

Now, with no more distractions, Lin Jing could focus on his cultivation at Thousand Illusions Mountain.

The sect’s core area was hidden within a massive illusionary formation, offering a degree of fate concealment, making it an excellent hiding place.

This was precisely why Lin Jing had chosen Thousand Illusions Mountain as his temporary refuge...

Time flew by—one year passed in the blink of an eye.

Despite constantly feeling on edge, nothing unexpected happened.

For an entire year, Lin Jing had poured all his energy into cultivating illusion arts.

Soul Infusion Art, Spirit-Piercing Method, and Layered Illusion Technique—he had mastered them all to perfection.

His ability to simulate illusions had improved drastically.

When he first arrived at Thousand Illusions Mountain, he could only maintain his own disguise while manifesting one powerful demon beast illusion.

But now, after a year of rigorous training, he could simultaneously conjure illusions of ten Demon Kings!

With these illusions, paired with Spirit-Piercing attacks, even Core Formation cultivators who failed to see through his deception could suffer severe mental trauma.

However, this method was still unstable.

Until he mastered "Inverse Void Art", he couldn’t truly fight Core Formation cultivators head-on.

"It’s time to consider advancing to Core Disciple."

Wearing the golden disciple robes of Thousand Illusions Mountain, Lin Jing stepped out of his cave and flew toward the Illusionary Assassination Hall.

With his improved illusionary mastery, his disguise was stronger than ever.

Since that was the case…

It was time to take the next step.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 8: The Pledge of Allegiance
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 8: The Pledge of Allegiance

"You successfully switched to our cultivation method in just one year?"

At the Illusionary Assassination Hall, Steward Zheng was genuinely surprised to see Lin Jing again.

Elder Qin hadn’t been exaggerating—this disciple was indeed talented!

It seemed he had recruited a remarkable new member for the Illusionary Assassination Hall.

Seeing the True Illusion Essence radiating from Lin Jing, Steward Zheng smiled. "Not only did you switch over flawlessly, but your cultivation didn’t drop at all—you’re still at the mid-stage of Foundation Establishment. Not bad, not bad."

"So, are you here to take on a mission?"

"You still have three years to consolidate your strength—there’s no rush to start missions..."

"Steward, I want to accumulate contributions as quickly as possible and become a Core Disciple." Lin Jing chuckled, scratching his head sheepishly.

Steward Zheng strode forward and, just like a year ago, patted Lin Jing on the shoulder.

"Since you're so eager, I'll give you a task that’s not too dangerous to get you started!"

"If you complete it successfully, I’ll arrange for you to join our elite squad, where you’ll compete against cultivators from other sects for resources and opportunities. That’s not just fighting for our sect—it’s fighting for yourself. The rewards in this process are immense."

As he spoke, he pulled out two portraits.

Both depicted a sweet, innocent-looking female cultivator. While their appearances differed, their overall aura felt strikingly similar.

"Pretty, isn’t she?" Steward Zheng asked.

"Very pretty." Lin Jing answered honestly.

"Hmm??" Zheng raised an eyebrow.

He ignored the comment and continued, "Her name is Yanran, a disciple of the Flower Sect. Five years ago, she disguised herself as a rogue cultivator and 'accidentally' encountered an Inner Sect disciple of Thousand Illusions Mountain. Through meticulous planning, she made him fall deeply in love with her."

"They got married, and on their wedding day, he gifted her a magical artifact as a symbol of his love."

"However, after the marriage, she spent months setting up a secret formation to absorb half of his cultivation. Then, using her status, she robbed his family of vast wealth and disappeared without a trace."

"A disciple of the Flower Sect scheming for our sect’s artifacts and cultivation? That deserves death!"

"Of course, that Inner Sect disciple was an idiot to be deceived so easily."

"Despicable acts like these deserve punishment!" Lin Jing added in agreement. "I knew at first glance she wasn’t a good woman."

"You better mean that." Zheng gave him a knowing look. "You’re still young, not even twenty yet. When you walk the cultivation world, be careful."

"Women are nothing special. You practice illusion arts—once you master the Inverse Void Art, you can manifest any kind of Dao companion you desire. Our sect’s secret techniques don’t just transform into demon beasts!"

Lin Jing was momentarily stunned. That sounded wrong, but somehow… made sense.

"Recently, our disciples have tracked Yanran down. She has taken on a new identity, disguising herself and marrying into the Lin Clan of Linlang City."

"The Lin Clan has no ties to our sect. Even if they discovered the truth, they wouldn’t dare offend the Flower Sect, a Nascent Soul-level sect."

"Your mission is not to bring her back alive—if capturing her proves troublesome, just kill her and bring back her head."

Steward Zheng glanced at the file. "Since you’re already at mid-stage Foundation Establishment, this shouldn’t be a problem, right?"

Lin Jing nodded—it was no problem.

What he was really interested in was how many contribution points this mission was worth.

This was his pledge of allegiance.

Whether capturing or killing Yanran, he would be making an irreversible enemy of the Flower Sect. Only by taking this step would Thousand Illusions Mountain fully trust and invest in him.

Linlang City, Lin Clan.

Lin Jing was familiar with this place.

When he first arrived in Qingzhou, he had encountered a Lin Clan disciple—he hadn’t expected his first mission to involve them.

The Flower Sect disciple, Yanran, had reinvented herself as a young female cultivator named Yan Xin and married into the Lin Clan, specifically to a low-talent Lin Clan heir.

Although this Lin Clan heir lacked talent, he was incredibly diligent, earning the favor of the Lin Clan patriarch, who showered him with endless resources and pills, boosting his cultivation to mid-stage Foundation Establishment.

According to Thousand Illusions Mountain’s investigation, Yanran specifically targeted him for these reasons—planning to drain his cultivation and seize the vast supply of pills.

However...

When Lin Jing arrived at Linlang City, things took a bizarre turn.

Hiding in the Lin Clan manor, Lin Jing overheard a furious family argument.

"You just married such an outstanding wife, and now you… You unfilial brat!"

"How could you cultivate that ‘Immortal-Slaying Art’ you found?! It has crippled your ability to bear children!"

The elders were furious.

"You don’t understand. Xin'er is indeed a wonderful woman, but ever since I married her, my body has been deteriorating, and even my cultivation has started regressing!"

"I can’t accept this! I worked too hard to reach this level, only to lose it all?!"

"Meanwhile, my brother Lin Yun cultivated the ‘Immortal-Slaying Art’ and his cultivation skyrocketed—he’s already at early Foundation Establishment! After seeing that… I just couldn’t ignore it!"

"Men must prioritize their cultivation!"

"Prioritize, my ass! Your wife coughed up blood and ran away because of your actions! Instead of going after her, you sit here making excuses?!"

Lin Jing fell silent.

…What the hell?

So someone from the Lin Clan really did cultivate this technique?!

Ironically, this twist completely ruined Yanran’s plan—she couldn’t drain his cultivation because his own choices crippled him first.

This Lin Tian had unshakable willpower. Despite his humble background, he had unintentionally resisted a carefully orchestrated Flower Sect scheme.

But the real question—where had Yanran run off to?

This mission was his first task—his pledge of allegiance to Thousand Illusions Mountain. He could not afford to fail.

Lin Jing pulled out a small creature the size of his arm—a Flame Qilin Dragon.

"The target shouldn’t have gone far. Can you pick up any unusual scents?"

The Flame Qilin Dragon let out a dissatisfied growl.

Finally, some fresh air!

It had been confined for so long, it thought it was finally being set free—only to be used as a bloodhound.

How was it supposed to track scents like a damn dog?

"You're telling me that a Fourth-Grade Alchemist, someone who can identify even the subtlest differences between pill ingredients blindfolded, has a weaker nose than a dog?" Lin Jing asked flatly. "Hurry up and help."

"Grr…"

The Flame Qilin Dragon let out a reluctant snort, sniffed the air—

—and actually detected a lingering medicinal fragrance that didn’t belong to the Lin Clan's pills.

It carried the subtle scent of "Mimicry Flower" extract, a rare shapeshifting ingredient.

Lin Jing’s eyes gleamed.

"Found you."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 9: The Demon Ancestor's Ruins
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 9: The Demon Ancestor's Ruins

Outside Linlang City, in the Evergreen Forest.

"Damn that Lin Tian!"

Yanran, a disciple of the Flower Sect, furiously kicked a rock and punched a tree, looking like an aggrieved little wife. Her delicate face twisted with anger, contorted into a fierce expression.

The hem of her crimson dress fluttered as she landed atop a mountain peak, muttering to herself, "I can't just leave like this."

The Flower Sect’s secret technique for siphoning half a cultivator’s cultivation required time to set up... She had been so close to completing it and achieving her goal. But who could have expected that young master of the Lin family to sever the connection at the last moment, causing the technique to fail?

Against a cultivator who practiced the Immortal-Slaying Art , she had no confidence in attempting the secret technique again.

The effort she had already invested wasn’t the main issue—what truly mattered was that this failure would be a stain on her reputation, a laughingstock among her fellow sect sisters.

Wiping a leaf between her fingers, Yanran calmed her expression and spoke to herself, "I just have to make myself look like the victim. From the Lin family's perspective, this would be entirely Lin Tian's fault. As his properly wedded wife, I am the aggrieved party. I can use this as leverage to demand a huge compensation from the Lin family before leaving—otherwise, I'd be at a loss."

Rustle...

Just as Yanran was plotting how to further exploit the Lin family's wealth, the surrounding forest suddenly filled with rustling sounds. Heavy breathing, deep and menacing, accompanied the noise, making her frown.

"Demonic beasts? But isn't the Evergreen Forest supposed to be free of strong demonic beasts?" She expanded her spiritual sense outward to investigate. Through her divine perception, she scanned the area and immediately saw the source of the disturbance.

And then—she screamed in panic.

"A Demon Core Stage beast?! How is there a Demon Core Stage beast in this place?!"

Yanran’s pupils shrank instantly, her instincts screaming danger. Within her spiritual sense's reach, a colossal tiger covered in jet-black fur prowled among the trees. Sunlight filtered through the thick canopy, casting dappled patterns upon its body…

Under the probing of her divine sense, the tiger demon suddenly lifted its head. Its piercing gaze cut through the layers of mist in the forest, locking onto its prey with terrifying precision.

Run!

That was the only thought left in Yanran’s mind. Her heartbeat pounded wildly, but before she could even retract her divine sense, the black tiger let out a thunderous roar.

"ROARRRR!!!"

The entire forest trembled with the beast’s deafening cry. The sheer force of the roar made Yanran’s consciousness blur, her body going weak and limp.

"I'm done for," she thought despairingly. Never had she imagined that she would encounter a Demon Core Stage beast—and worse, that she wouldn't even have the chance to flee.

As the tiger demon approached her, she mustered what little strength she had left and screamed, "What kind of demonic beast are you?! I am a disciple of the Flower Sect! If you eat me, the Flower Sect will never let you off!"

"The Flower Sect has many Nascent Soul elders, and even a Soul Transformation Sect Master—"

"Enough with your 'Flower Sect' this and 'Flower Sect' that," an unexpected voice interrupted.

From behind the tiger demon, a figure emerged—Lin Jing.

He patted the massive tiger's side, and in an instant, the beast’s form dissolved into nothingness.

"So this is what it’s like using an illusionary beast in battle. Didn’t expect it to be so easy," Lin Jing mused, rubbing his chin as he observed the stunned female cultivator before him.

"Him..."

The Flower Sect disciple stared at Lin Jing in a daze before murmuring, "Thousand Illusions Mountain?"

Lin Jing wove strands of spiritual energy together, binding the severely weakened Yanran. In the process, he noticed something surprising—according to the intelligence from Thousand Illusions Mountain, Yanran was supposed to be at Early Foundation Establishment, yet she had already broken through to Mid Foundation Establishment!

Not that it made much difference—she was still too weak. Lin Jing had no trouble crushing her mental defenses.

Realizing her attacker was not actually a demonic beast but a fellow human, Yanran sighed in relief. Though she was still unable to resist, she quickly adopted a pitiful expression.

"Senior Brother, what are you doing?" she whimpered.

"This must be some misunderstanding…"

Even while bound, Yanran arched her chest slightly, accentuating her curvaceous figure. Like a seductive viper, she slowly slumped to the ground, raising her tear-streaked face to look at Lin Jing with a pitiful, glistening gaze.

BAM.

Lin Jing knocked her out with a single punch.

"You're using this to test Thousand Illusions Mountain disciples?" Lin Jing muttered, utterly unimpressed. He had no interest in falling for a seductress’s tricks.

A few days later.

Lin Jing personally escorted the Flower Sect cultivator—who had plotted against the disciples of Thousand Illusions Mountain—back to the sect.

When Deacon Zheng received the news, he was overjoyed.

"Good! This is even better than I expected—swift, clean, and perfect! You managed to capture her even after she reached Mid Foundation Establishment? I knew I didn’t misjudge you!"

With such a valuable trophy, Lin Jing immediately earned Deacon Zheng’s high regard.

After ordering subordinates to throw the Flower Sect enchantress into the Thousand Illusions Mountain dungeon, Deacon Zheng turned back to Lin Jing, smiling.

"I remember you have quite the talent with illusionary beasts, especially that White Abyss Monkey King you manifested?"

"You must have some special connection to monkeys—otherwise, why is it that out of all the demonic beasts, you always bond with monkey-type ones?"

Deacon Zheng’s tone was teasing, but Lin Jing felt a bit embarrassed. He thought back to his childhood days spent watching Journey to the West .

"Deacon Zheng is correct… I’ve always been fascinated by monkeys. Sometimes, I even ignore beautiful women in favor of them," Lin Jing admitted sheepishly.

"Good, then consider this your fortune."

"Most disciples of the Illusionary Assassination Hall start with basic missions—territory disputes, guarding spiritual mines, and the like. These missions are dangerous, though the sect doesn’t treat these disciples unfairly. However, they offer limited personal growth."

"But you—I've decided to assign you a far more valuable opportunity."

"Three months from now, the Demon Ancestor’s Ruins will open. Disciples from the Myriad Beast Sect, the Flower Sect, and Thousand Illusions Mountain will all enter. This ruin was left behind by a Void Mystical Stage Demon Ancestor. It contains both opportunities and dangers, and conflict with disciples from other sects will be inevitable."

"With your abilities, sending you on ordinary missions would be a waste. If you can distinguish yourself in the Demon Ancestor’s Ruins , earning enough merits to qualify for the Core Disciple trials should be within your reach!"

" The Demon Ancestor’s Ruins…? " Lin Jing's expression shifted.

"Indeed. A being worthy of the title Demon Ancestor possesses blood so powerful that a single drop can birth an entire race of its kind… The owner of this ruin was a Primordial Relic Beast , one with the bloodline of the Zhuyan . It had no kin of its own, so as its lifespan neared its end, it created this ruin."

"Those who perform well inside may inherit its legacy—and become one of its kind."

"This Demon Ancestor sought to ensure its bloodline, techniques, and martial arts would be passed down after death."

"And… it doesn't discriminate between species."

"No matter what race you belong to, as long as it deems you worthy, it can make you one of its own."

"However, transforming high-level beings requires immense energy. That’s why only those below the Core Formation Stage can enter."

"For this reason, even the Senluo Hall has chosen not to compete with us for this opportunity," Deacon Zheng concluded with a smile.

Senluo Hall —an elite faction led by a Void Mystical cultivator, with only Nascent Soul and Soul Transformation members. No weaklings among them.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 10: The Observation Team
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 10: The Observation Team

Over the next few days, Lin Jing learned more details about the Demon Ancestor’s Ruins from Deacon Zheng.

"Among the three sects, a small number of disciples have already obtained a trace of the Zhuyan bloodline. However, the majority of our disciples aren’t here for that—not everyone wants beast blood in their veins."

"The disciples of the Myriad Beast Sect aim to grant their pet beasts even the slightest trace of Zhuyan’s lineage, allowing them to cultivate powerful demonic techniques, spells, and combat arts."

"As for Thousand Illusions Mountain , we seek to study the Demon Ancestor’s divine presence , hoping that one day, we can perfectly reconstruct the Zhuyan through illusions. As an ancient relic beast, its strength surpasses even the three Demon Ancestors imprinted in the deepest halls of the Divine Beast Palace."

"When the time comes, your only goal will be to observe and analyze the Zhuyan’s form, spells, and martial arts."

"Since you've already mastered simulating the White Abyss Monkey King , you might gain some unexpected insights in the ruins. It may not be the same species, but at the end of the day, they’re both primates…"

"And what about the Flower Sect ?" Lin Jing asked.

"They’re just here to sabotage us," Deacon Zheng sneered. "A bunch of wenches. When you enter the Demon Ancestor’s Ruins , make sure to deal with them the same way you did today—mercilessly take them down!"

Lin Jing remained silent.

Truthfully, he wasn’t particularly eager to enter the Demon Ancestor’s Ruins , but refusing wasn’t really an option.

This seemed like the fastest path to becoming a Core Disciple , and if all the competitors were Foundation Establishment cultivators, his expertise in illusionary arts would still allow him to compete.

"I won’t disappoint you, Deacon Zheng," Lin Jing said, clasping his hands in salute.

A few days later, the name Huang Yu spread across Thousand Illusions Mountain . Many disciples heard that an illusionary arts genius had emerged in the Inner Sect , joining the Illusionary Assassination Hall and even capturing a Mid Foundation Establishment Flower Sect enchantress alive.

Killing a cultivator of the same rank? Many could do that.

Capturing one alive? That was far more difficult. Usually, only someone with a much higher cultivation level could pull that off.

But according to the Flower Sect disciple’s confession, this Huang Yu had perfectly simulated a great demon, suppressing her in an instant. This further cemented his reputation as a true prodigy.

Meanwhile, Lin Jing himself had entered seclusion once more.

Three Months Later.

Thousand Illusions Mountain gathered 100 elite Foundation Establishment disciples. Their cultivation levels ranged from Early Foundation Establishment to Perfected Foundation Establishment . Among them, 95 were Inner Sect disciples, while five were Core Disciples —all key figures for this expedition into the Demon Ancestor’s Ruins .

The ruins had been discovered by three sects ten years ago. The initial exploration yielded some gains, but none of the sects had obtained the grand opportunity that could reshape their power structure.

Thus, after much discussion, the three sects agreed to send 100 disciples every seven years to explore it again. This would be the eighth expedition…

"Depart!"

From Thousand Illusions Mountain’s airship, a Half-Step Nascent Soul Sect Master led the team, accompanied by several Nascent Soul Elders .

Below, the 100 disciples were divided into seven squads, each led by a Perfected Foundation Establishment Core Disciple acting as captain.

Among them was a female Core Disciple known as Senior Sister Ning .

"Now, let me explain our squad’s mission," she announced.

"Senior Sister Ning, just say the word—we will follow your every command!" a Late Foundation Establishment youth eagerly proclaimed.

Senior Sister Ning nodded and continued, "Based on the last two expeditions, every group of entrants will begin in the same Heavenly Hall ."

"In this grand hall, a drop of Zhuyan’s blood will materialize. Shortly after, it will take form as a Junior Zhuyan at Early Foundation Establishment cultivation."

"Only by defeating this Junior Zhuyan can we pass the first trial."

"Although its cultivation is only Early Foundation Establishment , its actual combat strength is overwhelming. It takes the combined efforts of over a hundred cultivators to bring it down."

"Once slain, the ruins will distribute traces of Zhuyan’s blood to all participants based on their contribution. When absorbed, this blood enhances the physical body. If one continues to accumulate and refine enough of it, they will develop a Zhuyan Bloodline ."

"Those who inherit this bloodline will gain exceptional physical strength, becoming similar to those with rare physiques like the Heavenly Dragon Physique , Mad Bull Physique , and Beast King Physique . They will no longer be considered pure-blooded humans."

"This can be considered a fortuitous opportunity—but it’s not suitable for Thousand Illusions Mountain disciples."

"Our mission is not to obtain Zhuyan’s blood ! There is no need to participate in the battle."

"Instead, we are here to observe the Junior Zhuyan in combat. Because it is merely a projection, Demon-Illusion Mirrors won’t work. We must rely entirely on our own perception and memory."

"During this process, some Flower Sect disciples may abandon the battle against the Junior Zhuyan to disrupt our observations. Pay them no mind—other squads will handle them. Our duty is to focus entirely on the battle."

"As long as we can recreate even one percent of the Junior Zhuyan’s divine essence, the sect will grant us immense rewards."

"If, however, another sect's disciple breaks through our defenses, only then should we stop observing and eliminate the intruder."

Upon hearing this, everyone nodded in agreement.

"As for the second trial," Senior Sister Ning continued, "after passing the first, we will enter another Heavenly Hall . This time, another Junior Zhuyan will appear, but unlike the first, this one will wield a weapon and utilize martial arts ."

"In the first trial, the Junior Zhuyan relies solely on brute force. But in the second trial, it will be far more difficult and cunning."

"However, upon defeating it, aside from the Zhuyan’s blood , the weapon it wields will be granted to whoever lands the finishing blow."

"That being said, these weapons are not particularly valuable—they are merely mid-tier magical artifacts."

"Thus, our duty remains the same: observation . We must commit its martial techniques and battle essence to memory through illusionary arts!"

"As for the third trial…"

Lin Jing listened intently, gradually gaining a clear understanding of his role in this expedition.

His mission was simple: observe the battles between the other disciples and the Junior Zhuyan , committing its combat patterns and divine presence to memory.

Only in the most critical moments would he be allowed to take action—either against rival sect disciples or against the Junior Zhuyan itself.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 11: The Gathering of the Three Sects
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 11: The Gathering of the Three Sects

Qingzhou, Senluo Path.

Deep in the mountains, hidden within a transparent barrier, the air was thick with the scent of ancient vegetation and damp earth. A faint, almost imperceptible aura of primordial power lingered, a wild and profound energy unique to the Zhuyan .

Whoosh!

As the appointed date arrived, disciples from the Myriad Beast Sect , the Flower Sect , and Thousand Illusions Mountain gathered at the entrance to the Zhuyan’s Inheritance Grounds , a place monopolized by the three sects.

As Thousand Illusions Mountain’s airship approached, an impatient voice rang out from outside the barrier.

"Phantom Lord Ming, your Thousand Illusions Mountain sure took its time. We’ve all been waiting on you to unlock the barrier."

"Heh, isn't that Old Miao? I heard you took the Myriad Beast Sect’s Core Disciples on a trip to the Ancient Celestial Kingdom to annex the Beast Taming Sect —only to get beaten back. Is that true?"

Standing at the prow of the airship, Thousand Illusions Mountain’s Sect Master, Phantom Lord Ming , looked toward Myriad Beast Sect’s Vice Sect Master, Miao Ling , with a smirk.

"Hmph, that Lin Jing from the Beast Taming Sect possesses a rare Gu ability. It’s no surprise our disciples were at a disadvantage. But for him to use such power... that sect is bound to pay the price."

Miao Ling , a Great Perfection Nascent Soul cultivator specializing in beast taming, was the very same elder who had attempted to recruit Lin Jing into the Myriad Beast Sect .

However, after what he had witnessed in the Ancient Celestial Kingdom , his interest in Lin Jing had waned.

Why?

Because at the border town of the Cloud Sea Mountains , they had encountered a Nascent Soul Demonic Cultivator from the Gu God Sect !

Fortunately, the demonic cultivator had no interest in them—only in information regarding the Ten Rare Gu and Lin Jing himself...

After that incident, Miao Ling and his team quickly abandoned any thoughts of involving themselves in Lin Jing’s affairs, returning to Qingzhou before they could get caught in the storm.

Their caution proved correct.

Soon after, Thousand Illusions Mountain —which had hidden itself within an academy—was attacked. Now, wanted notices for Thousand Illusions Mountain’s fugitives had even spread into Qingzhou .

Was the Gu God Sect behind it?

Miao Ling wouldn't be surprised.

"Wait, Myriad Beast Sect’s Core Disciples fought against Qu Jun ?"

Hearing the exchange between Phantom Lord Ming and Miao Ling , disciples from Thousand Illusions Mountain and the Flower Sect perked up in interest.

By now, Qu Jun’s name had become infamous due to mysterious wanted notices circulating across multiple regions. Even in Qingzhou , many had heard of this monstrous cultivator.

"Have you heard? Thousand Illusions Mountain isn’t the only sect issuing wanted orders for Qu Jun . Many sects across different domains are hunting him down. It’s insane that he hasn’t been caught yet!"

"Maybe the Ancient Celestial Kingdom’s powerhouses have already taken him down," Senior Sister Ning interjected. "But let’s not concern ourselves with outsiders. Focus on staying calm—distraction is the greatest weakness for illusion cultivators."

As the two Nascent Soul elders exchanged a few more barbed words, the Flower Sect’s Sect Master impatiently urged them forward.

Eventually, the three sects worked together to dissolve the barrier.

Though the three sects often engaged in both open and covert conflicts , they upheld one unwritten rule: Nascent Soul and stronger cultivators would refrain from interfering directly.

Disputes over resources, treasures, and inheritances were left to the disciples.

After all, as long as a sect’s Nascent Soul cultivators remained, its survival was assured. Disciples? No matter how many died, new ones could always be recruited.

"Unlock the boundary!"

With a soft hum, the barrier shattered like scattered fireflies, revealing the landscape within the Demon Ancestor’s Ruins .

At the center stood a massive white stone pillar , its surface etched with the marks of time. Strange, natural rune-like patterns coiled around its base.

" Thousand Illusions Mountain disciples, enter the ruins!"

Hovering in midair, Phantom Lord Ming issued his command.

In an instant, a hundred Thousand Illusions Mountain disciples transformed into streaks of illusory light , surging toward the stone pillar.

" Myriad Beast Sect disciples, go!"

" Flower Sect disciples, go!"

Under the commands of their respective sect leaders, the other two sects’ disciples also rushed toward the pillar.

This white stone pillar was a Void Mystical Treasure of the Demon Ancestor Lin Jing , containing an entire independent realm . Those whose cultivation met the requirements would be drawn into the ruins upon stepping within its radius.

Lin Jing followed Senior Sister Ning and the other Thousand Illusions Mountain disciples toward the pillar.

As waves of spatial energy rippled, countless cultivators seemed to sink into the depths of a vast ocean, vanishing into another world.

Inside the Demon Ancestor’s Ruins

A dimensional shift later, figures began appearing one by one in a vast, dimly lit hall.

Lin Jing blended into the crowd of Thousand Illusions Mountain disciples, his presence unremarkable. Nobody knew that he was the very Ancient Celestial Kingdom’s prodigy listed in wanted notices.

"Is that... Lin Jing’s blood?"

Among the crowd, Lin Jing’s gaze locked onto a single drop of floating red blood at the center of the grand hall. A surge of extraordinary power radiated from it.

As the three sects’ disciples fully entered the ruins, the blood drop began to change—gradually taking the form of a small ape, just over a meter tall .

"So tiny."

For many first-time explorers, the Zhuyan’s diminutive form was underwhelming. After all, the stronger a demonic beast, the larger it tended to be.

"Don’t underestimate it!"

A veteran of previous expeditions immediately shouted.

The Zhuyan appeared mostly white, with crimson markings resembling burning flames. Its eyes gleamed with an intelligence far beyond that of a mere construct.

"RAAAH!!!"

The Junior Zhuyan scanned the entire hall, baring its fangs.

Before it could even move—

BOOM!

Thousands of spells rained down upon it, engulfing the hall in a maelstrom of energy.

Though only 300 cultivators had entered the ruins, the Myriad Beast Sect disciples—each commanding multiple demonic beasts—made it feel as though an entire army was present.

Even Lin Jing casually cast a simple Fireball Technique .

Though his squad’s mission was to observe the Zhuyan , there was one requirement: Only those who had successfully inflicted damage upon it could leave the ruins.

Thus, those who had never entered the ruins before had to contribute at least a minor wound to the Junior Zhuyan to ensure they received a trace of Zhuyan’s blood .

"Second Squad, sit down and concentrate!"

Amidst the chaos, Senior Sister Ning gave the order.

Alongside Lin Jing, the ten Thousand Illusions Mountain disciples under her command immediately focused their divine senses, studying the battle unfolding before them.

And what they saw was shocking.

Despite being only Early Foundation Establishment , the Junior Zhuyan endured thousands of attacks like an unbreakable fortress . Fire, lightning, metal-elemental strikes—none could even scorch a single strand of its white fur.

The sheer defensive power of the creature stunned Lin Jing.

It was stronger than his own Fire Qilin Dragon…

Even though this Junior Zhuyan was merely a construct formed from a drop of blood, its bloodline rank was unquestionably that of a Demon Ancestor .

Lin Jing’s mind raced.

"This creature hasn’t shown any of the overwhelming might typical of primordial relic beasts… just terrifying physical strength. If I want to recreate its essence as an illusionary beast, where do I even begin?"

Illusions typically relied on aura —an overwhelming presence to intimidate opponents.

Yet, the Zhuyan displayed no aura, no oppressive force .

It simply rushed into the horde of beasts —silent, unyielding, and ruthless.

Limbs flew. Flesh was torn.

Its every movement was a brutal, yet elegant masterpiece of slaughter .

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 12: The War Beast
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 12: The War Beast

"Flower Sect disciples, do not let the Thousand Illusions Mountain disciples complete their comprehension!"

At this moment, most Myriad Beast Sect disciples were focused on besieging the Junior Zhuyan .

Meanwhile, the Thousand Illusions Mountain disciples were observing its combat techniques, attempting to comprehend and replicate its form and divine essence.

As for the Flower Sect disciples—whose primary cultivation focused on wood-based spells and who had the least to gain from the Zhuyan Ruins —their objective was clear: sabotage the disciples of Thousand Illusions Mountain and Myriad Beast Sect .

"Heehee, I found a serpent gall!"

"Haha, I snatched a tiger whip!"

Like scavengers, the Flower Sect disciples gleefully looted the remains of demonic beasts that the Junior Zhuyan had torn apart. Their internal organs, pelts, and blood became resources for the Flower Sect .

"Disgusting," one of the Myriad Beast Sect beast tamers muttered. But as long as the Flower Sect disciples didn’t interfere with their assault on the Junior Zhuyan , they were willing to tolerate their presence.

If the Thousand Illusions Mountain disciples truly succeeded in recreating a perfect Zhuyan Illusion Beast , their sect’s strength would skyrocket—leaving the Flower Sect at a disadvantage.

Thus, one Flower Sect cultivator after another controlled their wooden spells, launching attacks on Lin Jing and the other disciples in a state of deep comprehension.

"Courting death!"

Fortunately, the Thousand Illusions Mountain disciples had divided into five squads, one of which was assigned to defend Lin Jing’s group from such interference.

Many Thousand Illusions Mountain disciples materialized illusionary beasts, charging toward the Flower Sect disciples, engaging them in fierce battle.

For a time, the First Trial Hall of the Zhuyan Ruins turned into utter carnage.

Demonic beast corpses from the Myriad Beast Sect littered the floor, while Thousand Illusions Mountain and Flower Sect disciples fought each other viciously.

In just moments, several cultivators had already perished, their remains looted as war trophies by the enemy sects.

Amidst the chaos, Lin Jing silently observed the battlefield, his heart growing heavy.

Back in the Ancient Celestial Kingdom , even when sects clashed, the Sect Regulatory Office ensured that large-scale massacres like this never occurred.

"Right now, my task is…"

Lin Jing steadied his gaze on the Junior Zhuyan . Simply replicating its form was easy—every Thousand Illusions Mountain disciple could do it.

The real challenge was capturing its divine essence .

Without it, no matter how perfect the illusion looked, it was nothing more than a paper tiger .

Only an illusion infused with the Zhuyan’s divine essence could instill true fear in demonic beasts and high-bloodline cultivators, revealing the full might of this primordial relic beast .

But achieving that…

That was akin to reaching for the heavens .

Even after two previous expeditions, not a single Thousand Illusions Mountain disciple had succeeded.

After half a day of battle, the first Junior Zhuyan finally collapsed .

Despite its overwhelming power, it still had limits. Under the relentless assault of formations, magical artifacts, and sheer numbers , it eventually succumbed.

However, the Myriad Beast Sect had paid a heavy price.

The floor was littered with thousands of beast corpses . Several elite disciples had perished as well.

As for Thousand Illusions Mountain and Flower Sect disciples, their casualties were even greater—though not by the Zhuyan’s hands, but by each other’s.

Yet, despite their heavy losses, the Myriad Beast Sect had gained the least reward . The surviving beast tamers and their pets had only obtained weak traces of the Zhuyan’s blood .

Lin Jing, having contributed only a single Fireball Technique , received only a minuscule fraction of Zhuyan’s blood —far too little to alter his bloodline or strengthen his physique.

"The bloodline itself is useless to me. As long as I have the ability to leave the ruins, that’s all that matters."

As the blood-scented air thickened, the echoes of battle still rang in Lin Jing’s ears.

"Did anyone gain any insights?"

With the first trial completed, the three sects tacitly halted their conflict.

Even the bloodthirstiest disciples were pulled back by their own sect members.

Senior Sister Ning turned to Lin Jing’s group and asked.

One by one, the disciples shook their heads.

"It’s fine," she reassured them. "There are still many more trials ahead."

As she spoke, the world around them shifted .

In an instant, everyone was transported into the Second Trial Hall .

Soon, another Junior Zhuyan emerged.

This time, it held a stone staff in its hands.

Yet, just like before, it exuded no aura of intimidation . It simply looked like an ordinary monkey as it charged toward the cultivators.

But after the first trial…

The Myriad Beast Sect disciples no longer carried the same excitement they had upon entering the ruins.

Instead, their eyes flickered with hesitation and fear .

If not for the Vice Sect Master watching from the outside, some of them might have already tried to retreat.

As before, Lin Jing sat at the back, quietly observing the Junior Zhuyan wielding the stone staff, slaughtering its way through the battlefield.

With each death, the atmosphere shifted .

With each clash, the cultivators' expressions darkened .

And then, it finally clicked.

"The Zhuyan is said to be an omen of war and chaos…"

"This is its inheritance ground, yet the ruins place no restrictions on Thousand Illusions Mountain and Flower Sect disciples killing each other."

"This aligns perfectly with the legends—throughout ancient times, every appearance of a Zhuyan led to war and devastation. That is why their entire species was nearly wiped out."

Lin Jing took a deep breath, standing amid what felt like a battlefield straight out of history .

He stared at the Junior Zhuyan , watching it fight tirelessly , watching the Myriad Beast Sect disciples' spirits waver , their willpower breaking down.

And he understood .

"What if the reason the Zhuyan suppresses its aura so well… is because it loves war?"

"What if it doesn’t want enemies to be intimidated and flee? What if it craves the thrill of battle—to break its enemies with pure strength, to make them collapse in despair?"

The more Lin Jing observed, the more he realized:

The Zhuyan did not seek to intimidate .

It sought to shatter the spirits of its enemies .

"The Zhuyan race was exterminated because they repeatedly plunged the world into chaos. Their mere presence incited fear, leading to their eventual downfall."

"Even now, within its own ruins, its mere existence is triggering conflict. The sheer number of casualties is already pushing many cultivators to their psychological limits."

Lin Jing’s eyes gleamed with insight.

"To perfectly replicate the Zhuyan’s divine essence… I need to create a grand, brutal, and merciless battlefield."

"A battlefield where tens of thousands die—where mortals, cultivators, even celestial beings perish."

"Because war is the Zhuyan’s very essence."

The realization sent a chill down his spine.

But instead of fear—he felt excitement .

If he could create an illusionary battlefield of such scale…

Then with Thousand Illusions Mountain’s Inverse Void Art , he wouldn’t just be crafting an illusion.

He would be summoning a true War Beast .

"Now, I just need more battles… more deaths… more raw material for my illusion."

"I just need time."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 13: The Zhuyan Phantom
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 13: The Zhuyan Phantom

Having formed a rough plan, Lin Jing began his attempts.

His divine sense, apart from observing the little Zhuyan that turned killing into an art, also swept across the blood-soaked grand hall.

"The form of the Zhuyan… This is the first layer of illusion."

It didn’t take long before his consciousness simulated the birth of a small Zhuyan.

"The brutal war scene… This is the second layer of illusion."

"What I need to do is use the Layered Illusion Technique to merge the second layer into the first, transforming it into the spirit of the Zhuyan."

After a year of seclusion in Thousand Illusions Mountain, Lin Jing had thoroughly mastered the Layered Illusion Technique.

To craft a Zhuyan with true spirit, his first thought was naturally this technique.

"The reason ancient cultivators feared the Zhuyan was its relentless thirst for war!"

"A madman who constantly seeks battle—anyone would fear such a being, fear that they might die in its wars."

"The essence of dominance is to instill fear in one’s enemies."

"And the root of Zhuyan’s terror lies in its insane battle lust and unwavering combat will."

"Yes, battle intent! The essence of the Zhuyan Phantom must be filled with battle intent!"

"In my past life, there was the concept of war trauma. In this cultivation world, war too would leave inner demons in the minds of cultivators. If I can perfectly merge the War Catastrophe Illusion with the Zhuyan Phantom, it should become the core of manifesting a Zhuyan with true spirit!"

"Using battle intent as the trigger to make enemies wary of fighting the little Zhuyan—this is the essence of the Zhuyan Phantom!"

However…

Even though he had completed the technique, Lin Jing still felt something was missing.

Soon, a small Zhuyan with battle intent burning in its eyes was born. Its gaze surged with an overwhelming battle aura, sharp enough to pierce the soul, making anyone who looked into its eyes feel as if their mind was breaking…

It was as if they were personally experiencing a brutal slaughter.

This suggestion alone allowed opponents to foresee their own tragic fate in battle, crushing their fighting spirit.

Examining the little Zhuyan he had created through layered illusions, Lin Jing knew he had done his best. He was confident that even though this little Zhuyan only appeared to be at the early Foundation Establishment stage, it could still terrify an entire group of Foundation Establishment cultivators.

But Lin Jing felt that this Zhuyan was still imperfect.

Even though conjuring this little Zhuyan consumed less energy than manifesting a Core Formation-level demon beast and was more practical—making it a solid illusion technique—

This little Zhuyan was merely a replication of the phantom he had observed in the ruins.

It was not a true Demon Ancestor Zhuyan.

Perhaps, at this level, he was already far ahead of the other disciples of Thousand Illusions Mountain…

But… did Lin Jing lack this kind of power?

Not only did he not lack it, but Thousand Illusions Mountain likely didn’t lack it either.

After all, there was no real technical difficulty here. Maybe it would be challenging for Foundation Establishment or even Core Formation illusionists, but for Nascent Soul masters, replicating it would be effortless.

What the true experts of Thousand Illusions Mountain wanted was definitely not a little Zhuyan forcefully infused with battle spirit using the Layered Illusion Technique…

They wanted the actual war dominance of a Demon Ancestor-level Zhuyan!

"But how can a group of Foundation Establishment illusionists comprehend the war spirit of a Demon Ancestor? Are the experts of Thousand Illusions Mountain just gambling on luck?"

Lin Jing shook his head, but then—

Suddenly, something clicked in his mind.

"Most demon beast blood contains the beast’s innate might..."

"The Zhuyan blood that fused into our bodies earlier was meant to strengthen our physiques and alter our bloodline, so it didn’t reveal any of its beastly aura."

"But just because it didn’t reveal it doesn’t mean it’s not there."

"No matter how docile a beast pretends to be, there will always be a moment when it bares its fangs. And forcing it to reveal its true nature is simple—just make it feel threatened."

"If I make the Zhuyan blood within me feel threatened, it might unleash its true ferocity, revealing the original might of the Zhuyan. Then, I could personally experience the true dominance of a Demon Ancestor Zhuyan!"

"Beast Taming Physique!"

"My Beast Taming Physique can suppress all beast blood. Zhuyan blood is still beast blood. If I use my physique to suppress the Zhuyan bloodline, it will instinctively resist. As a Demon Ancestor’s blood, it will never allow itself to be subdued."

"But this method is undoubtedly extremely risky. I can’t attempt it recklessly..."

"I must have a powerful expert guarding me before even testing it, no matter how diluted the Zhuyan blood within me is..."

"For now, the little Zhuyan I conjured through the Layered Illusion Technique should be enough to gain the recognition of Thousand Illusions Mountain, securing my promotion to Core Disciple and allowing me to study the Inverse Void Art in the blessed land…"

"After all, this little Zhuyan consumes little mana yet has remarkable power. Aside from its high learning difficulty, it has almost no drawbacks. Though it’s useless against high-level cultivators, if lower-level cultivators manage to master it, its practicality far surpasses those Demon King illusions in the Divine Beast Hall…"

"Since my original goal has been achieved, the next step is to gather more Zhuyan blood to prepare for experiencing the true dominance of a Demon Ancestor Zhuyan…"

Lin Jing opened his eyes.

The surroundings were still shrouded in a dense blood mist. At some point, the disciples of the three sects had already passed the second trial.

At this moment, they had all stepped into the third trial hall.

Once again, it was a small Zhuyan at the early Foundation Establishment stage.

However, compared to the second trial’s weapon-wielding Zhuyan, this one was even more ferocious!

Three heads, six arms!

Unlike the first trial’s unarmed combatant and the second trial’s weapon-user, this third trial’s Zhuyan had now begun casting spells!

In its three-headed, six-armed form, the little Zhuyan wielded six different weapons, continuing its ruthless slaughter.

Yet, despite its immense combat prowess, its presence was almost nonexistent.

Its aura did not manifest into something tangible, making it feel lifeless—so much so that even the disciples of Thousand Illusions Mountain found it hard to grasp its true might.

Because it hid its own presence so thoroughly, even the three-sect cultivators battling it struggled to connect this little monkey to the war god that had slaughtered countless demons and cultivators.

Though they felt apprehension, dread, and fear, it had not yet shattered their mental limits.

For now, the sect mission, relic rewards—these still held greater priority.

"Take it down!!!"

"If we kill it, we might be able to inherit its three-headed, six-armed technique!"

Faced with this third trial’s Zhuyan, the three-sect cultivators grew even more frenzied.

None of them paid any attention to the second team of Thousand Illusions Mountain disciples, who were still comprehending the Zhuyan’s battle spirit.

After all, in the last two attempts, the three sects had only reached the third trial but had never defeated this three-headed, six-armed little Zhuyan.

This time, they had come fully prepared—they were determined to slay it and discover what treasures the third trial would yield.

"Three heads, six arms, huh…"

Lin Jing slowly stood up.

His movement instantly caught the attention of Senior Sister Ning.

"Junior Brother Huang, what are you doing? Sit down and observe! Leave the fighting to the senior brothers who specialize in offense!"

"Senior Sister," Lin Jing looked at her and said, "If we rely solely on them, I fear we won’t be able to land the final blow on this three-headed, six-armed Zhuyan before the Myriad Beast Sect and Flower Sect disciples do..."

"I’ve already grasped how to manifest a Zhuyan with true battle spirit. Let me assist the senior brothers..."

"What did you just say?" Senior Sister Ning was stunned.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 14: Three Heads, Six Arms
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 14: Three Heads, Six Arms

Lin Jing didn’t say much, nor did he wait for Senior Sister Ning’s instructions.

In an instant, a small, white-furred, red-footed monkey appeared beside him, its aura surging with battle intent.

This action made the other disciples of the Second Team of Thousand Illusions Mountain snap their eyes open in shock!

Many of them had attempted to conjure a Zhuyan during their illusion comprehension training, but all they managed to create were empty shells—mere shapes without spirit. They were completely unconvincing and utterly useless in combat.

However, what Lin Jing had manifested was completely different.

The brilliant blue eyes of his little Zhuyan seemed to contain an unparalleled will for battle.

Its entire body radiated a boiling war intent, mixed with a savage aura. A terrifying, primal beastly presence surged from it—wild yet profound.

This sudden emergence of a true prehistoric beast’s aura sent a terrifying chill down the spines of the three-sect cultivators who were locked in battle.

In an instant, every one of them turned their gazes toward Lin Jing’s Zhuyan Phantom.

The moment their eyes met the phantom—their pupils shrank, their minds went blank.

“Another one!?”

“Wait a minute—something’s wrong with this one!!”

The Zhuyan within the trial ruins had always concealed its presence. No matter how many it had killed—cultivators or beasts alike—its aura was suppressed.

Even though the three-sect cultivators feared it, that fear never reached an extreme.

After all, those who had made it this far were resilient elite disciples from their respective sects.

But now—

With the appearance of this Zhuyan Phantom exuding the full might of a beastly predator, a sensation they had never felt before crashed into their minds.

The memories of their fellow disciples’ deaths—of their pet beasts being slaughtered—surged back, as if replaying in front of their eyes.

The threat of death they had experienced countless times before had never been as vivid as it was right now.

The battles they had fought… flashed through their minds again and again, as if forcing them to relive the moment of their own impending demise.

"No… no…!"

Just locking eyes with the little Zhuyan Phantom for a single moment was enough to make them hallucinate their own future deaths at its hands.

Their minds broke.

Their final mental defenses collapsed.

As the Zhuyan Phantom slowly stepped toward the center of the battlefield—

Within seconds, over a hundred disciples from the Myriad Beast Sect and Flower Sect completely lost their nerve.

In a blind panic, they abandoned the trial ruins entirely, choosing to flee immediately!

Meanwhile, the Thousand Illusions Mountain disciples, whom Lin Jing had not targeted, witnessed a scene of absolute horror—

Just a few steps from the Zhuyan Phantom had sent countless Foundation Establishment cultivators into utter madness.

Some screamed hysterically as if they had just seen a nightmare given form.

Some turned and ran without hesitation.

Some froze in place, bodies stiff with terror.

Some collapsed onto the ground, trembling uncontrollably—some even losing control of their bodily functions out of sheer terror.

Even though the Zhuyan’s battle aura wasn’t directed at the Thousand Illusions Mountain disciples, they still felt chills running down their spines.

"A pity… it's still just an illusion. It can only break enemies' spirits..."

"But in this case, because the three previous trials had already left such a deep impression on them, the Zhuyan’s might became even more effective..."

"Inverse Void Art… if I can learn it someday, then simply turning illusions into reality… would be terrifying beyond imagination."

Lin Jing followed behind the Zhuyan Phantom, looking at the crumbling willpower of the two-sect disciples.

Just as expected.

And at that moment—

The three-headed, six-armed Zhuyan that had been battling the three-sect cultivators turned its gaze toward Lin Jing.

"ROAR!!!"

Already heavily injured by the three-sect disciples, the trial’s little Zhuyan charged at Lin Jing.

At the same time—

The Zhuyan Phantom Lin Jing had manifested surged in power!

Its battle intent exploded, reaching the Great Perfection of Foundation Establishment.

Its entire body was now covered in a brilliant azure spiritual aura, and it swung its fist toward the charging Zhuyan.

The disciples of Thousand Illusions Mountain watched from afar, their expressions intense.

"That… is an illusion…?"

"Wait—what did he just do?!"

What happened next shocked even the sect leaders.

"The highest form of illusion arts—infusing massive amounts of spiritual energy into an illusion, not to attack, but to turn it into a mental entity."

"Though it’s not as complete as the Inverse Void Art’s true materialization, its power is still incredible… Who is this disciple!? He’s not just manifesting a Zhuyan Phantom—he’s applying illusion techniques at a level we’ve never seen!"

Many of the Thousand Illusions Mountain disciples didn’t recognize Lin Jing.

But a few did know about the newcomer who had joined the sect less than two years ago.

Regardless of who he was—his name was now known among the elite disciples of Thousand Illusions Mountain.

Meanwhile, in the outside world—

A mass exodus of Myriad Beast Sect and Flower Sect disciples fled from the trial ruins, their minds completely shattered.

Their spirits were broken, their eyes filled with panic, hypersensitivity, and lingering fear.

The sect leaders of Myriad Beast Sect and Flower Sect immediately realized something had gone horribly wrong.

"What happened inside!?" the Flower Sect Master demanded.

She turned to one of her disciples for answers—

But the disciple was already too far gone.

He backed away in sheer horror, trembling violently, as if the sect master herself was the terrifying Zhuyan Phantom.

"D-don’t come near me…! I quit! I quit…!"

The Flower Sect Master frowned.

She waved her hand, sending a soft green light to envelop the terrified disciples, helping them regain some of their composure.

Then, she asked again.

"Lü Lian, what happened inside the ruins?"

A Core Disciple at Great Perfection of Foundation Establishment hesitated.

Her mind had gone blank, but as the memories returned, her expression changed dramatically.

She immediately turned to the sect master and reported:

"In the third trial, we were attacking the three-headed, six-armed Zhuyan… But then, another Zhuyan appeared!"

"That one… that Zhuyan… was different. It felt real. Like an ancient Zhuyan had been reborn. We were completely overwhelmed by its presence!"

Then—

Her eyes widened in shock as she realized something.

"No… No, wait! Sect Master! Reporting to Sect Master!"

"That wasn’t another Zhuyan. It was an illusion created by a Thousand Illusions Mountain disciple!"

"They succeeded in creating a Zhuyan Phantom with true battle presence! Just its aura alone nearly drove us insane!"

Her words left the Myriad Beast Sect’s Vice Sect Master in disbelief.

What?

A Thousand Illusions Mountain disciple actually managed to create a Zhuyan Phantom with true presence?

Impossible!

Even the Sect Master of Thousand Illusions Mountain, Huan Gongming, was stunned.

Seeing the two-sect disciples flee in terror had already made him feel something was off.

Now, hearing the Flower Sect disciple’s explanation—he could hardly believe his own ears.

But after a moment—

He suddenly burst into laughter.

"Who!? Who is this disciple!?"

"If they have no master, I shall take them as my direct disciple!"

The Nascent Soul Elders of Thousand Illusions Mountain were equally thrilled.

After all—

This was a Zhuyan.

Even reproducing a fraction of its presence was an astonishing feat.

Meanwhile—

Having effortlessly shattered the spirits of over a hundred Foundation Establishment cultivators, Lin Jing finally moved onto his real task.

He imbued his Zhuyan Phantom with a mental entity, granting it some actual combat ability.

Then—

He delivered the final blow to the trial’s third-stage Zhuyan.

With that—

The three-headed, six-armed Zhuyan vanished.

Lin Jing received a drop of diluted Zhuyan blood, and in his mind—

A new technique appeared.

Three Heads, Six Arms!

—A technique that could be cultivated by both humans and beasts alike!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 15: How Could the Sect Not Prosper?
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 15: How Could the Sect Not Prosper?

"Junior Brother Huang Yu… You—"

Inside the ruins, Senior Sister Ning Yao, the leader of Thousand Illusions Mountain’s Second Team, stared blankly at Lin Jing, who stood beside the Zhuyan Phantom. She spoke in a hushed tone, still in shock.

So strong…

What’s going on with this Huang Yu?!

At that moment, Lin Jing finally regained his composure. He let out a small smile, cupped his hands, and addressed his "fellow senior brothers and sisters."

"Thank you all for your support and protection."

What a group of good people!

Without them, comprehending the Zhuyan Phantom and slaying the third-trial Zhuyan would have taken him much more effort!

"Did you get a reward for killing the third-trial Zhuyan? Is it the Three Heads, Six Arms technique?"

Several Thousand Illusions Mountain Core Disciples immediately surrounded him with questions.

Although Lin Jing had some reputation within the Inner Sect, to these Core Disciples, he was still an unfamiliar face.

Except for Senior Sister Ning, who had thoroughly reviewed his background, the other team leaders had no idea who he was.

They were still in disbelief—Where had this guy even come from?!

But before Lin Jing could respond, space distorted around them—

The ruins had shifted into the fourth level.

Compared to the third level, this fourth-level Zhuyan had undergone another transformation.

This time—

It was no longer at the early Foundation Establishment.

It had reached the peak of Foundation Establishment!

Its body was massive!

Immediately, tension spread through the group.

Senior Sister Ning took a deep breath and made her decision:

"This is far enough. We’re leaving! Don’t get greedy!"

"Junior Brother Huang, let’s go!"

"This thing is beyond what we can handle!"

"Understood!"

Lin Jing had no desire to continue the challenge either.

With their current manpower, unless he revealed all three of his contracted beasts, they stood no chance against this fourth-level Zhuyan.

Moreover—

If he inflicted too much damage on the Zhuyan, it might alter his bloodline into a Zhuyan variant!

The Beast Taming Sect was already leaning more towards demonic fate than human destiny. Lin Jing had no real desire for the Zhuyan Bloodline.

Perhaps the later trials had even greater inheritances—

But he had no intention of standing out too much.

He chose to quit while ahead.

Whoosh.

Lin Jing exited the ruins.

The other Thousand Illusions Mountain disciples took one last look at the Peak Foundation Establishment Zhuyan, swallowed hard, and decided that their expedition was already a success.

Under the command of the various Core Disciple leaders, they all withdrew from the ruins.

As the Thousand Illusions Mountain disciples emerged, Huan Gongming, the sect master of Thousand Illusions Mountain, immediately moved to protect them all.

Hovering in the air, he gazed at them and demanded:

"Which disciple comprehended the Zhuyan’s true presence?"

His eyes swept across the group, burning with intensity.

One by one, all Thousand Illusions Mountain disciples turned to look at Lin Jing.

"Him?"

Not just them—

Even the Myriad Beast Sect and Flower Sect cultivators shifted their focus onto Lin Jing.

And—

None of them recognized him.

He wasn’t one of the renowned prodigies of Thousand Illusions Mountain!

Lin Jing cupped his fists and responded:

"Reporting to the Sect Master—this disciple, Huang Yu, successfully manifested the Zhuyan Phantom with the protection of my fellow senior brothers. I was also fortunate enough to slay the third-trial Zhuyan and obtain an inheritance technique."

Immediately, a Nascent Soul elder from Thousand Illusions Mountain’s Illusionary Assassination Hall stepped forward.

"Sect Master, this disciple's name is Huang Yu. He was recruited a year ago by Elder You and is currently a disciple of my Illusionary Assassination Hall."

"Excellent, excellent, excellent!"

Huan Gongming spoke three consecutive praises, his excitement barely contained.

Then—

His Nascent Soul manifestation erupted outward.

He raised his gigantic spectral palm and glared coldly at the Vice Sect Master of Myriad Beast Sect and the Sect Master of Flower Sect.

"The three sects agreed to explore the ruins together. Regardless of which sect gains more, no one should harbor resentment. Are you two sore losers?"

His tone was sharp—

The moment he sensed killing intent from the two Nascent Soul Perfection cultivators, his aura turned deadly.

It was clear—if they couldn’t give him a satisfactory answer, he was ready to fight.

The other Nascent Soul elders of Thousand Illusions Mountain immediately released their own spiritual manifestations, creating a tense standoff between the three sects.

"Heh… of course not."

The Vice Sect Master of Myriad Beast Sect and the Sect Master of Flower Sect quickly suppressed their killing intent and forced a smile.

However—

When their eyes landed back on Lin Jing, their thoughts were clear.

This boy cannot be allowed to live.

A disciple who had comprehended the Zhuyan Phantom…

In the future, he would surely become a great enemy to the Myriad Beast Sect and Flower Sect!

They absolutely could not allow Thousand Illusions Mountain to raise this disciple to the Nascent Soul level!

"Hmph."

Huan Gongming coldly snorted.

"It seems this expedition is over. The three sects have suffered heavy losses—we should return to our sects and recuperate."

They had entered the ruins with three hundred Foundation Establishment disciples.

Now—only one hundred seventy remained.

The casualties among the Myriad Beast Sect’s pet beasts were even more devastating.

All three sects had suffered severe losses.

However—

The survivors had undergone tremendous trials and gained valuable experiences.

The chances of these disciples reaching Core Formation had increased significantly.

Between a Foundation Establishment cultivator and a Core Formation expert, every sect valued the higher realms more.

Huan Gongming dispersed his manifestation, turned to the disciples, and commanded:

"Thousand Illusions Mountain disciples—board the flying ship. We return to the sect."

"Huang Yu of the Illusionary Assassination Hall—come with me and report your findings."

"From this day forward, you are a Core Disciple of Thousand Illusions Mountain."

Under Huan Gongming’s orders, Lin Jing followed him into a private chamber.

Inside—

Lin Jing explained his thought process in constructing the Zhuyan Phantom and even manifested it again.

Huan Gongming nodded approvingly.

"To master the Layered Illusion Technique to such a level in only one year after joining…"

"Using the form of war as the spirit, seamlessly merging it into an illusion—this level of mastery even surpasses most Core Formation illusionists."

"It truly feels like a real ancient Zhuyan cub! Compared to this, the other disciples’ illusions were complete garbage!"

"Your control over spiritual energy is terrifyingly precise! And what’s even more impressive is that you completed this illusion technique in such a short time… A genius. Without a doubt, you are a genius!"

However—

Huan Gongming could tell that Lin Jing had not fully comprehended the true presence of a Demon Ancestor Zhuyan.

Instead, he had used a clever technique to create an illusion that mimicked its presence.

Still—for a mere Foundation Establishment disciple, this was already a monstrous feat.

"Sect Master, I am willing to leave my Zhuyan Phantom behind using the Soul Infusion Art so my fellow disciples can study it."

"Additionally, I will contribute the Three Heads, Six Arms inheritance technique to the sect."

Huan Gongming stroked his beard and sighed.

"If all my disciples were like you, how could Thousand Illusions Mountain not prosper?"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 16: Hoping to Escape
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 16: Hoping to Escape

Huan Gongming had high hopes for Lin Jing.

"After returning to the sect, leave behind the Zhuyan Phantom and immediately enter the Thousand Illusions Paradise to learn the Inverse Void Art. I also want to see what kind of power a Zhuyan Phantom, transformed from illusion to reality, will possess… As for the Three Heads and Six Arms Technique, there's no rush."
