The Three Heads and Six Arms Technique was well-known across various ancient texts, practiced by both humans and demons alike.

Its core was to grant the user three heads and six arms, allowing for the perfect control of multiple weapons and magical artifacts, significantly boosting combat prowess.

However, the disciples of Thousand Illusions Mountain did not rely on weapons or magical artifacts, nor was the sect's foundation built upon them. Moreover, this technique was said to be incredibly difficult to master, so Huan Gongming didn’t have high expectations for it.

Compared to the Three Heads and Six Arms, what he truly looked forward to was the Zhuyan Phantom—after all, phantom beasts were the cornerstone of Thousand Illusions Mountain!

Although Lin Jing’s Zhuyan manifestation was incomplete, it was a solid start. Using this as a foundation, with years of exploration in the Demon Ancestor’s Ruins, they would eventually create a Zhuyan Phantom with a complete divine presence!

"Understood." Lin Jing sighed inwardly.

After his journey to the Demon Ancestor’s Ruins, Lin Jing successfully obtained the status of a core disciple.

Ascending to core disciple status meant he could immediately begin learning the Inverse Void Art, which made him happy. However, the sect master of Thousand Illusions Mountain intended to wait for the Nascent Soul Patriarch to emerge from seclusion and introduce him as the patriarch's disciple. That part, Lin Jing was not too thrilled about.

"I'm not the hope of the sect."

"I'm here to steal techniques."

What worried him now was that while his illusion techniques could deceive even perfected Nascent Soul illusion cultivators, he had no guarantee they would fool a Nascent Soul cultivator who had spent a thousand years refining the Illusionary Path.

After all, this wasn’t just any Nascent Soul cultivator—it was one who specialized in illusions.

If his deception worked, everything would be fine. But if his identity was exposed, he would be in deep trouble.

Thousand Illusions Mountain was still searching for him—not out of their own will, but under pressure from others. Lin Jing didn’t dare gamble on what would happen if their patriarch saw through him…

After all, no one would want to keep a fugitive from the Yin Corpse Sect, a walking disaster, right next to them.

"As soon as I master the Inverse Void Art, I need to run. I can't wait for Thousand Illusions Mountain’s Nascent Soul Patriarch to leave seclusion."

Upon returning to Thousand Illusions Mountain, Lin Jing immediately used the Soul Infusion Art to imbue the Zhuyan Phantom’s divine presence into the sect.

Then, wasting no time, he took the Inverse Void Art cultivation manual and entered the sect’s training paradise.

This was a naturally occurring illusionary realm.

Both real and false, both solid and ethereal.

Cultivating illusions here provided tremendous benefits. It was said that the Inverse Void Art could only be successfully cultivated within this place.

Meanwhile, as Lin Jing entered the Thousand Illusions Paradise, his second identity—Huang Yu—was making waves in Senluo Path.

Inside the Demon Ancestor’s Ruins, the fact that he had "perfectly" materialized the Zhuyan Phantom, an ancient relic beast, spread like wildfire. Every sect, big and small, within Senluo Path had heard of the genius disciple from Thousand Illusions Mountain.

Even the Senluo Hall, a faction composed entirely of weak cultivators below the Nascent Soul stage, had heard of the illusionary prodigy named Gongming.

Many believed that Thousand Illusions Mountain was destined to become a core force within Luosha Sect in the future.

Of course, the place where Gongming’s name was mentioned the least was Luosha Sect itself.

Meanwhile, Thousand Illusions Mountain gained a Zhuyan Stone Statue.

Anyone who locked eyes with it felt as if they were staring at an incredibly terrifying primordial beast, sending chills down their spine.

Despite the phantom beast within the statue only possessing mid-stage Foundation Establishment cultivation, it gave off a presence more frightening than a Nascent Soul Demon King.

This statue became a new study object for the core disciples of Thousand Illusions Mountain. Yet the more they observed it, the more shocked they were—none could understand how someone at mid-stage Foundation Establishment had achieved this.

And without a solid foundation in Thousand Transformations, fully replicating the Zhuyan Phantom was no easy task.

..........

Three months later.

The sect master, Huan Gongming, stood outside Thousand Illusions Paradise, frowning in confusion.

Logically speaking, since Lin Jing had comprehended the divine presence of the Zhuyan Phantom in such a short time within the Demon Ancestor’s Ruins, there was no reason why it should take him this long to learn the Inverse Void Art in the paradise.

The longest any core disciple had ever taken to learn it was two months.

Could it be that transforming the Zhuyan Phantom from illusion to reality was more difficult than expected?

With the intent to offer guidance to "Huang Yu," Huan Gongming entered the Thousand Illusions Paradise—only to find it completely empty.

“Hmm?”

“Where is he?”

The sect’s hope had already escaped.

Two kings cannot coexist. Lin Jing didn’t dare gamble on what would happen if he met the Thousand Illusions Mountain patriarch, so he chose the safest option—vanishing.

His goal was accomplished. That was enough!

.............

Meanwhile, on the road from Senluo Path to Tongtian Path, Lin Jing used the Layered Illusion Technique to overlay another identity on top of Huang Yu.

Now, he appeared as a Foundation Establishment realm alchemist, wearing the robes of a Firecloud Pill Sect disciple.

He wasn’t sure if Thousand Illusions Mountain would brand him a traitor, but either way, compared to Yin Corpse Sect, Thousand Illusions Mountain was far weaker.

At most, their strongest cultivator was a Nascent Soul, and it was unlikely that the Nascent Soul Patriarch would personally hunt down a disciple who left without explanation.

If they sent a Nascent Soul cultivator after him, it was even less likely they’d find him.

Even if Lin Jing openly flaunted Huang Yu’s identity, leaving traces and using Thousand Illusions Mountain’s techniques everywhere, there was no way Thousand Illusions Mountain could track him.

So being labeled a traitor of Thousand Illusions Mountain? He could live with that. It was certainly better than being a Longevity Body cultivator infested with rare Gu.

Now, disguised as an alchemist, even if someone powerful saw through his first identity, they would only discover Huang Yu—his true self, Lin Jing, was buried even deeper.

While it might not fool absolute powerhouses, any extra layer of security was worth the effort.

"Waiter, bring me a pot of Spirit Brew."

Stopping in a small town outside Senluo Path, Lin Jing tossed down some Spirit Stones and listened in on the tavern’s chatter.

In Qingzhou, there was no Tianji Pavilion’s World Report, so tavern gossip was often the best source of information.

He was searching for news about Exotic Flames—his top priority was finding a flame that burned lifespan to enhance his Fire Qilin Dragon.

As he listened, he overheard his own name—Huang Yu, Lin Jing—come up repeatedly.

Then, someone exclaimed,

"Did you hear? Lin Jing, the fugitive every sect is after—his sect, the Beast Taming Sect, has people in Qingzhou too!"

"Half a year ago, Luosha Sect tried to capture some Beast Taming Sect cultivators to interrogate them about Lin Jing, but guess what… they got wiped out. And they had a Nascent Soul cultivator!"

"What? Isn't Beast Taming Sect just a Nascent Soul-level sect from the Ancient Celestial Kingdom? How did they destroy Luosha Sect?"

"Because the Demon Court’s Holy Maiden is Beast Taming Sect’s sect master's daughter—Xu Xiaoyao! Luosha Sect was obliterated by the Demon Court! Can you believe it? The daughter of some small sect master from the Ancient Celestial Kingdom is actually one of the Demon Court’s Holy Maidens!"

Lin Jing nearly blacked out.

He had avoided the Beast Taming Sect in Qingzhou to keep them out of trouble…

And now he found out they had already allied with a power stronger than Yin Corpse Sect?!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 17: The Demon Court
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 17: The Demon Court

"Greetings, fellow Daoists."

"I am but a wandering alchemist. By chance, I overheard your conversation just now and found it quite interesting. Would you mind if I joined in?"

"Since fate has brought us together, allow me to cover the cost of this meal."

As Lin Jing listened in, the group suddenly changed the topic, discussing a widowed female cultivator who had lost her husband. Feeling speechless, he stepped forward, hoping to steer the conversation back on track.

He truly knew little about the Beast Taming Sect’s movements in Qingzhou, the sect master's adopted daughter, or this so-called Demon Court.

The three cultivators at the table were all at Foundation Establishment and looked like rogue cultivators based on their attire. They turned to look at Lin Jing.

"You're treating?" The bearded man who had been speaking earlier was momentarily stunned before bursting into laughter.

"You said it yourself—fate has brought us together. No need for you to pay, Daoist friend. Sit down. Ask whatever you want. Are you interested in that widow too?"

"In that case, I’ll gladly accept," Lin Jing coughed lightly before sitting down.

"However, that’s not what I wanted to ask. It’s about your previous discussion. I overheard you mention that the Beast Taming Sect’s sect master has a daughter with half-demon blood, and I’m quite curious… Does that mean she has half-demon race bloodline or just half-demonic beast bloodline?"

Although both were referred to as half-demons, the distinction was vast!

Before the Immortal Palace was destroyed, demonic beasts could transform into humans and were collectively known as the demon race.

There were no reproductive barriers between demons and humans, and their offspring were known as half-demons.

Half-demons inherited the best traits of both races, making them incredibly powerful.

These half-demons had half demon race bloodline.

However, after the ancient Immortal Palace sealed off the demon bloodline, the demon race completely vanished.

As a result, half-demons with demon race blood also disappeared. Over time, demons and half-demons struggled to reproduce. Even when they did, their offspring were merely pure-blooded humans, with no trace of demonic ancestry.

With the demon race extinct, the definition of half-demon changed over time.

Now, the term referred to humans who had undergone a transformation—where their human blood was partially replaced with beast blood through special methods.

However, this type of half-demon was completely different from those with true demon race bloodline.

For example, the Zhuyan Ruins Lin Jing had previously explored had methods to alter bloodlines, but even if successful, it only granted demonic beast traits, not true demon bloodline.

Thus, over the years, the term half-demon had come to have two distinct meanings.

Lin Jing was aware that Sect Master Xu Xiaoyao had a daughter—an adopted one—but he didn’t know much about her. He only knew that she had left for Qingzhou a long time ago.

At the time, he had wondered whether sending a young girl to Qingzhou alone was too dangerous.

But now, it seemed that the sect master’s daughter had some connection to the Demon Court.

And if this Beast Taming Sect senior sister only possessed half-demonic beast bloodline, Lin Jing doubted that the Demon Court would value her so highly.

After all, while humans with beast bloodline were rare, they weren’t unheard of.

Many cultivators had beast-like traits simply because their bodies contained traces of demonic beast bloodline.

Yet, since the Beast Taming Sect’s senior sister had become one of the Demon Court’s Holy Maidens, Lin Jing was inclined to believe that she wasn’t just an ordinary half-demon.

It was likely that she possessed half-demon race bloodline.

Only then would the Demon Court, which sought to revive the demon race, take interest in her.

But that raised another question—hadn’t all demon race bloodlines disappeared?

The bearded cultivator took a sip of his wine and said,

"Of course, she has half demon race bloodline!"

"Relying on beast bloodline alone—even if it's demon ancestor bloodline—would never be enough to become a Demon Court Holy Maiden."

"That place is one of the strongest demonic beast forces, with a Demon Emperor overseeing it…"

"I heard that all three Holy Maidens of the Demon Court have half demon race bloodline!"

"You’re wondering how that’s possible, right? Didn’t the ancient Immortal Palace seal off demon bloodlines, making it impossible for new demons to be born?"

"Exactly." Lin Jing nodded.

"I’ve gossiped about this with others before," the bearded cultivator continued.

"Those three Holy Maidens were only recognized by the Demon Court in recent decades—they weren’t part of some ancient tradition. So, is it possible that over time, the world’s rules, rewritten by the Immortal Palace, are starting to weaken?"

"After all, the Immortal Palace has been gone for so long. Who’s to say their alterations to world laws were truly permanent?"

"Maybe full-blooded demons still can’t be born, but half-demons—those with half demon race bloodline—are starting to reappear. And this Beast Taming Sect’s sect master’s daughter? She had ancestral demon bloodline within her, and as the world’s rules loosened, her bloodline mutated, triggering a reversion to her ancestral form!"

"Reversion to an ancestral form… That does make sense," Lin Jing muttered.

It was possible that the sect master’s adopted daughter had awakened her half-demon bloodline at some point, catching the Demon Court’s attention and prompting them to bring her to Qingzhou for cultivation.

That could explain why she had left for Qingzhou at such a young age.

Perhaps, when she was born, she already exhibited signs of her half-demon heritage, leading her parents to abandon her, allowing the sect master to take her in.

Regardless of the details, it was clear that the Beast Taming Sect’s senior sister was thriving in Qingzhou. No wonder the sect master had brought a group over to establish themselves there.

For a brief moment, Lin Jing considered visiting the Beast Taming Sect’s Qingzhou branch to see the sect master.

But he quickly dismissed the idea.

The sect master's adopted daughter had likely gained her status due to her half-demon race bloodline, making her politically valuable within the Demon Court.

However, she wasn’t the only Holy Maiden—there were two others like her.

While she might have the authority to wipe out a Nascent Soul-level sect, she likely didn’t hold absolute power.

For someone like Lin Jing, who possessed a Longevity Body, approaching the Demon Court was too dangerous.

The court was filled with powerful demonic beasts, and there was no guarantee she could protect him.

If she had been a Holy Maiden of a human faction, even one of the Three Great Demonic Sects, like the Netherworld Hall, he would have considered seeking refuge.

But the Demon Court’s Holy Maidens?

Too risky.

Not just the beasts—even she might want to eat him.

The Beast Taming Sect’s downfall was no accident.

"…Sigh." Lin Jing let out a long sigh.

The world was vast, yet without strength, nowhere truly felt safe.

Still, keeping the Beast Taming Sect’s Qingzhou branch as a backup plan wasn’t a bad idea.

They were probably looking for him, too.

..........

In the end, Lin Jing set out for Tongtian Path, one of the Sixteen Paths of Qingzhou.

His goal was simple—form his core before anything else.

A month later, he left Senluo Path and entered Qingming Path. Unlike Senluo Path, which lay on Qingzhou’s outskirts, Qingming Path was rich in spiritual herbs and renowned for alchemy.

Lin Jing had intended to head straight to Tongtian Path, but along the way, he discovered something interesting in Qingming Path—prompting him to pause his journey.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 18: The Wandering Alchemist
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 18: The Wandering Alchemist

"A shop, huh…"

Lin Jing set his sights on Qingming Commerce Guild's trading district.

In the chaotic land of Qingzhou, renting a storefront here would provide a rare place of order.

With a Void Mystical-stage cultivator overseeing the Qingming Commerce Guild, no one dared cause trouble in its trading district.

Even if Lin Jing, a Foundation Establishment cultivator, displayed treasures tempting enough for Nascent Soul cultivators, they wouldn’t dare break the rules maintained by the Commerce Guild.

And as a business organization, the Commerce Guild valued its reputation above all else—they likely wouldn’t covet his low-grade treasures either.

Of course, none of this was absolute.

But as long as Lin Jing carefully balanced the value of the items he revealed, this place would serve as an excellent temporary base.

"I can collect some and have Pine Leaf Mouse fuse them in the Beast Taming Space, see if anything useful emerges to strengthen my foundation."

"Then, using those fused herbs, I can develop some novel, specialized low-grade pills."

"With these pills, I’ll establish a unique alchemy market."

"And when the time comes, the key currency for purchasing these pills won’t be Spirit Stones—it’ll be Exotic Flame intelligence! Or, even better, the Exotic Flames themselves."

Lin Jing felt that searching for flames alone to strengthen his Fire Qilin Dragon and refine his Heavenly Demon Physique was too inefficient.

Recalling his experience in establishing the Dragon Palace at the academy, an idea struck him—

Why not use alchemy to have others gather the flames for him?

And in Qingzhou, the Qingming Commerce Guild’s trading district would be the perfect stage for this plan.

"Besides, with my current illusion techniques, I can even manifest the aura of a Nascent Soul cultivator if necessary."

"At the right moment, I can create the illusion of having a Nascent Soul master, deterring petty troublemakers."

"With this dual-layered protection, I’ll have plenty of time to steadily increase my strength."

Having made up his mind, Lin Jing followed the map he had "acquired" from Thousand Illusions Mountain and set out for Tianqing City, the second-largest city in Qingming Path.

The largest city, Qingming City, was ruled by a Void Mystical-stage cultivator—too risky to visit.

But Tianqing City was only overseen by a Nascent Soul cultivator, making it relatively safe. He didn’t have to worry about his illusions being casually exposed.

After all, not every Nascent Soul cultivator was as skilled in illusions as Thousand Illusions Mountain’s patriarch.

..........

Tianqing City – Qingming Commerce Guild Trading District

A young alchemist, dressed in Firecloud Pill robes, arrived at the shop rental office.

Inside, a bald Golden Core elder sat behind a counter, casually toying with a mechanical lock. Without looking up, he spoke lazily:

"Renting a shop?"

"Yes. I am a wandering alchemist looking to rent a place to refine pills, rest, and sell my creations."

Lin Jing met his gaze directly, showing no signs of submission despite being a mere Foundation Establishment cultivator facing a Golden Core expert.

"Mid-stage Foundation Establishment… What grade of alchemist are you?"

"Eighth-grade."

Hearing this, the elder paused briefly before breaking into a smile.

"Not bad—so young, yet already a third-grade alchemist. Impressive. Who is your master? Do you have alchemy certification?"

"My master is Fire Dragon Pill Sovereign, a rogue cultivator. No official certification…" Lin Jing replied, then extended his hand.

"But… is this enough?"

A miniature flame furnace appeared at his fingertip, a hundred layers of fire waves swirling around it, dazzling in its brilliance.

"Third-tier fire control technique…" The elder nodded in approval.

"That’s enough."

A third-grade alchemist could refine third-grade pills.

Generally speaking,

First-grade pills were for Qi Refinement cultivators.

Second-grade pills were for Foundation Establishment cultivators.

Third-grade pills were for Golden Core cultivators.

A Foundation Establishment cultivator who was also a third-grade alchemist was far rarer than ordinary Foundation Establishment cultivators. Even some Golden Core cultivators who couldn’t refine pills would treat them as equals.

Thus, by revealing his third-grade alchemist status, Lin Jing had no need to act subservient to a Golden Core cultivator—this was exactly how a true alchemist of his caliber would behave.

More importantly, he had called his master a "Pill Sovereign."

Usually, only Nascent Soul cultivators or fifth-grade alchemists could use the title "Sovereign."

As a result, the elder immediately became much friendlier—regardless of whether Lin Jing was lying, it cost him nothing to treat him with respect.

Of course, Lin Jing could refine third-grade pills.

But if he combined Fire Qilin Dragon’s fused Exotic Flames, he could actually refine fourth-grade pills as well.

However, he deliberately underreported his abilities—

A Foundation Establishment cultivator refining fourth-grade pills would be far too shocking.

Moreover, he hadn’t planned on selling fourth-grade specialty pills anyway.

"How long do you plan to rent?" the elder asked.

"The minimum rental period is one month."

"I’ll take six months."

The Qingming Commerce Guild trading district had an immense flow of customers. While renting a store here was expensive, as long as his products were high quality, he wouldn’t lose money.

"Let me check… Ah, there’s one available alchemy shop—though the location isn’t ideal."

Lin Jing nodded.

"Location doesn’t matter. Good pills attract customers, even in the deepest alley. As long as the price is fair, I’ll take it."

"Excellent!" The elder smiled.

"I’ll draft the rental contract immediately!"

..........

Lin Jing smirked—he had complete confidence in his pills.

The Rising Dragon Pill was proof enough.

However, he couldn’t sell it here, as it had already been auctioned in the Ancient Celestial Kingdom, making it too recognizable.

Similarly, common market pills were widely available—unless he lowered his prices drastically, customers would just buy from major alchemy stores.

Thus, to make his shop stand out, he needed to develop unique pills.

Of course, he already had several exclusive pills that had never been publicly revealed.

Unlike the Rising Dragon Pill, these hadn’t been auctioned before.

For example… Pain Pill.

But that was a demonic pill, which meant its market was too niche. He dismissed the idea.

Instead, the most promising candidate was likely Gluttonous Pill.

He and his group had used this pill frequently, refining it from Golden Spirit Rice.

Golden Spirit Rice was the first special spiritual ingredient ever synthesized by Pine Leaf Mouse’s Glazed Immortal Eyes, known for its digestive-enhancing properties.

When refined with other ingredients, it eliminated side effects and became far more effective.

Normally, a Foundation Establishment cultivator consuming a fourth-grade spirit herb would need years of closed-door meditation to fully absorb its power.

But with Gluttonous Pill, all they had to do was consume a large dose beforehand, and in just two days, they could fully absorb the spirit herb’s energy.

Even more outrageously—

Even a Qi Refinement cultivator could use Gluttonous Pill to safely consume high-grade pills.

Lin Jing had personally tested this when he force-fed a Qi Refinement first-layer insect a third-grade pill—

Normally, it would have exploded from excess energy.

But thanks to Gluttonous Pill, it perfectly absorbed the pill’s effects and even underwent a bloodline evolution.

For young elites or lucky cultivators struggling to absorb high-tier resources, this pill was priceless.

If Lin Jing weren’t a Longevity Body cultivator, he might have reached Nascent Soul already using this pill.

Before long…

A new shop opened in Tianqing City’s trading district—

"Nameless Vessel Pavilion."

While its shelves displayed common first, second, and third-grade pills priced in Spirit Stones, the shop’s centerpiece was:

Gluttonous Pill.

Effect: Allows low-level cultivators to safely consume high-grade pills with no side effects.

Price: Not sold for Spirit Stones!

Only exchangeable for Exotic Flame intelligence or actual Exotic Flames.

"No matter the grade of the flame, as long as my Fire Qilin Dragon has Flame Emperor Divine Ability, it can absorb and convert it into power."

"In Qingzhou, it’s the only beast I can openly train—priority is getting it to Golden Core stage!"

As Lin Jing sat in the back of the shop, observing his Pine Leaf Mouse planting seeds and Fire Qilin Dragon refining flames, he plotted his next moves…

Just then, his first customer stepped through the door.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 19: Sacred Fire Lotus
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 19: Sacred Fire Lotus

Tianqing Commercial Street.

A blue-robed youth at the early Foundation Establishment stage stood at a corner, staring at the newly opened pill pavilion with a sign that read “Nameless,” his face filled with doubt.

“Father, you mean here?”

“This new pill pavilion is in such a remote spot with little foot traffic. It doesn’t seem like a place that would have anything good.”

“What do you know? In my prime, your old man was a fifth-grade alchemist and a Nascent Soul powerhouse. Even though I’m now just a lingering remnant soul, my past experience tells me this pavilion must have some very special pills.”

“If my guess is right, this pill… will be of great use to you. But you’ll have to go in and see for yourself.”

The blue-robed youth stood at the street corner. No one was around him—he was conversing with the remnant soul within his body.

“I understand.”

Once inside, he found the shop’s setup to be quite simple, and the owner didn’t look like an experienced alchemist at all. Instead, he was just a young man lounging on a rocking chair, casually flipping through a book.

His cultivation wasn’t much higher than the youth’s.

That young man was Lin Jing.

Lin Jing glanced at his first customer with a flicker of surprise but quickly returned to his book, showing no intention of greeting him.

What he was reading wasn’t some miscellaneous novel, but a book titled Records of Qingzhou, detailing various aspects of the region. Back in the Ancient Kingdom, he had read many books, but most of them were about that land. Now that he was in a new place, he naturally needed to catch up on local knowledge—power distributions, forbidden zones, rare plants, and spirit beasts…

“Go there.”

Following the remnant soul’s instructions, the blue-robed youth walked directly to where the Gluttonous Pill was stored.

Seeing the description of the golden pill, his pupils contracted, and the remnant soul inside him stirred.

“As expected! It’s a pill that aids in absorbing spiritual treasures!”

“How strange—I’ve never seen a pill like this before. Its grade isn’t high, yet its efficacy seems extraordinary… Among digestion-aiding pills, the highest quality available on the market is the third-grade Transformation Essence Pill, but this golden pill feels even superior to that!”

“The most crucial point is that the Transformation Essence Pill requires at least a perfected Foundation Establishment cultivation to consume without issues. But this pill… its medicinal power is incredibly mild. Even Qi Refinement cultivators could take it without any burden…”

“What a waste. Such a precious pill, yet this pavilion just leaves it out so casually without proper preservation. Its medicinal properties are probably leaking away… But if they weren’t, I wouldn’t have discovered this Gluttonous Pill.”

The blue-robed youth’s expression shifted.

“Father, can this thing… help me digest the inheritance you left behind?”

“Mm. One alone won’t be enough, but if you acquire a large number of them, you’ll be able to quickly absorb the treasures I left you instead of waiting until you reach Core Formation.”

The remnant soul continued, “If you do, you’ll definitely have a chance to compete for that spot!”

“But this Gluttonous Pill… it’s not for sale. It can only be exchanged for an Exotic Flame.”

The youth hesitated.

They didn’t have anything like that.

“In my treasure hoard, there is a Sacred Fire Lotus. If you consume it, there’s a chance you might refine an Emerging Lotus Heart Flame… However, the Sacred Fire Lotus is a fourth-rank spiritual item, and due to its extreme rarity, it’s worth even more than an ordinary fifth-rank spiritual item! I’m afraid that if you use such a treasure for this transaction, you’ll invite trouble.”

As father and son conversed, Lin Jing, still reclining in his chair, suddenly closed his book and said,

“Sacred Fire Lotus… Emerging Lotus Heart Flame… but that’s just a regular flame with no healing properties, right?”

Lin Jing’s simple inquiry made the blue-robed youth freeze in place, his face stiffening as he looked up in shock.

At the same time, the remnant soul within him abruptly stopped their telepathic conversation, staring in disbelief at the young shop owner, who was merely at the mid Foundation Establishment stage.

Both of them were stunned—how could someone at Lin Jing’s level possibly hear their soul-to-soul communication?

“No need to be alarmed. I mean no harm. I just have a sharp perception when it comes to souls—I wasn’t intentionally eavesdropping.” Lin Jing chuckled.

With his Contract Blood ability and the Ice Soul Gu, souls like the one hiding in the youth’s body couldn’t escape his detection. If he wanted to, he could even extract and freeze it with ease.

“Young friend, your talent is remarkable—you actually noticed my existence.”

After a moment, the remnant soul inside the youth no longer hid, slowly manifesting into a spectral form. “Even most Nascent Soul cultivators wouldn’t detect me, yet today, a mere Foundation Establishment youth managed to.”

“I not only know you’re hiding inside him,” Lin Jing said calmly, “I also know why you ended up in this state.”

“If I’m not mistaken, it was the Netherworld Hall, wasn’t it? I can see traces of Netherworld Ghostfire burns on your soul…”

“You were severely injured by a demonic cultivator from the Netherworld Hall, but because of your special soul-related physique, you barely survived… Your soul cultivation is exceptionally high, which is why you felt safe hiding inside him.”

Lin Jing continued, “And after becoming like this, it seems you had no other trusted people to look after your child. Though you left him plenty of treasures, his current cultivation isn’t enough to fully absorb them. That’s why you set your sights on the Gluttonous Pill. Did I get anything wrong?”

The remnant soul was stunned, then asked in shock, “May I ask how to address you…?”

“That’s not important. I’m just a nameless wandering alchemist. I have no interest in your feud with the Netherworld Hall. I’m only here fulfilling my master’s orders and training for a while.”

“If you’re interested in the Gluttonous Pill, feel free to trade that Sacred Fire Lotus for it. I won’t leak your affairs.” Lin Jing said casually.

The remnant soul let out a bitter laugh. “Then I must thank you, young friend. Your deductions are correct. If we exchange the Sacred Fire Lotus for the Gluttonous Pill, how many pills would you be willing to offer?”

Lin Jing rubbed his chin and said, “First, bring me the Sacred Fire Lotus. I’ll need to examine its quality.”

The blue-robed youth cupped his hands slightly and then, together with the remnant soul, left the pill pavilion.

Outside, the youth wiped cold sweat from his forehead. “Father, could he be from the Netherworld Hall? Only someone from there would be so knowledgeable about souls and Netherworld Ghostfire…”

The remnant soul sighed, “No, he’s likely just an alchemist interested in soul-related flames. Otherwise, he wouldn’t have made such a trade offer. Your father possesses the Soul-Extinguishing Physique. If he trained under a powerful alchemist, it wouldn’t be impossible for him to recognize it.”

“No matter what, this is someone we can’t afford to provoke.”

“For now, we can only trust that he means no harm.”

“When we get the Gluttonous Pill, you must immediately absorb the treasures I left you. Use those high-tier items to unlock your potential and enter Tongtian Tower. Your family is not a place for you to stay long-term. Hah… your so-called ‘relatives’… if you remain, they will strip you clean sooner or later.”

“Let’s go. I’ll guide you to retrieve the treasure. Be careful, let me possess you—we must not let anyone know I left behind more wealth.”

“An auspicious start…”

After the father and son left, Lin Jing muttered to himself.

The Emerging Lotus Heart Flame—a healing-type Exotic Flame.

For his future Fire Qilin Dragon, which would serve as his armor and take hits for him, it would be extremely useful!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 20: The Longevity Body’s Intimidation
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 20: The Longevity Body’s Intimidation

After seeing off his first distinguished guests, Lin Jing continued lounging and reading.

One person, three pets—except for the Ice Soul Gu, all were exceptionally busy.

The Ice Soul Gu had no choice but to seal itself in ice and enter a dormant state, feigning death to avoid being traced by the Gu God Sect’s special techniques. After all, it originally belonged to the Gu God Sect, and it was best to be cautious.

Meanwhile, the Fire Qilin Dragon was inside the Meditation Staff pendant, which had been converted into a pill-refining chamber, diligently producing pills. Most of the Nameless Pill Pavilion’s stock, including the Gluttonous Pill, currently depended on its efforts.

As for the Pine Leaf Mouse, it was the busiest of them all.

Inside its Suspended Gourd, it was cultivating three supreme spiritual seeds—Nine-Sun Divine Lingzhi, Nine-Life Exquisite Grass, and Cloud-Hidden Orchid!

Each of them was a treasure ranked even higher than the Sacred Fire Lotus from earlier.

At present, none of them had matured. Even though the Pine Leaf Mouse had mastered the Everlasting Emperor’s Longevity Art, it had yet to fully ripen a single plant.

Currently, the Pine Leaf Mouse was prioritizing the cultivation of the Nine-Sun Divine Lingzhi.

This divine lingzhi was a fifth-rank fire-attribute treasure. According to Senior Pine, once the Fire Qilin Dragon reached Core Formation, consuming it would be enough to propel it from early Core Formation to Core Formation Perfection, granting it the potential to break through to Nascent Soul.

Lin Jing’s plan was simple:

While ripening the Nine-Sun Divine Lingzhi, he would gather multiple Exotic Flames to help the Fire Qilin Dragon condense a formidable Flame Monster Core upon reaching Core Formation.

At that point, the Fire Qilin Dragon would be contractually bound at early Monster Core stage. But that was fine—by then, the Nine-Sun Divine Lingzhi likely wouldn’t have matured yet.

During that time, Lin Jing would focus on his own Core Formation breakthrough.

Once the Nine-Sun Divine Lingzhi was fully matured, the Fire Qilin Dragon could consume it, instantly advancing from early Monster Core to Nascent Soul stage. With its Demon Emperor Bloodline, there would be no bottleneck in this breakthrough.

Ultimately, Lin Jing, as a Core Formation cultivator, would wield Nascent Soul-level power by combining Fusion Secret Art and Beast Weaponization techniques with the Fire Qilin Dragon!

Paired with the beast’s brutal divine abilities, even Nascent Soul cultivators would struggle to oppose him.

And if Lin Jing successfully cultivated the Heavenly Demon Physique as well, then with his extended lifespan, he would gain the ability to challenge top-tier experts by burning his own life force!

After all, at that point, he would likely have nearly ten thousand years of lifespan to burn…

He would already be unmatched below Nascent Soul, and by combining that with Lifespan-Burning Demon Arts, anyone who dared to covet his Longevity Body would pay a steep price.

His Longevity Body would become both a trap and a weapon—if someone attempted to capture him, not only would they deplete his longevity, but they’d also face his devastating counterattacks.

Losing the bulk of his longevity wouldn’t be a concern. If they hesitated to make a move, fearing his retaliation, then his plan had already succeeded.

Apart from ripening the Nine-Sun Divine Lingzhi, the Pine Leaf Mouse had another crucial task: finding its own path to Core Formation!

Lin Jing’s own Core Formation would still depend on the boost from his contract beasts.

Additionally, for the Glazed Immortal Eyes to evolve and gain the ability to synthesize non-plant resources, the Pine Leaf Mouse’s Core Formation needed to be unique.

It had to regress from an ordinary Leaf Mouse into a Seven-Element Origin Mouse!

And the key wasn’t the Treant Pill!

The Treant Pill, one of the reference formulas used to develop the Longevity Foundation Pill, was a medicine that allowed a body to integrate plant organs.

These plant organs were the key.

Just like how the Sacred Fire Lotus and Nine-Sun Divine Lingzhi were both plants and fire-attributed at the same time, Lin Jing needed to find four suitable plant-based organs containing metal-wood, water-wood, fire-wood, and earth-wood properties and fuse them into the Pine Leaf Mouse’s body.

Once these five elements formed a balanced cycle around wood, the Pine Leaf Mouse would have the potential to condense a Five-Element Monster Core, granting itself full mastery over five attributes.

Lin Jing didn’t know if this would trigger an evolution in the Glazed Immortal Eyes, but the Five Elements Dao was overwhelmingly powerful, so it would undoubtedly benefit the Pine Leaf Mouse’s growth.

However, selecting these plant-based organs was no easy task.

Since he needed to refine Treant Pills using them, they had to exist in large quantities to allow for multiple trial-and-error experiments.

Thus, high-tier spiritual plants like the Sacred Fire Lotus and Nine-Sun Divine Lingzhi had to be ruled out.

They were precious, but Lin Jing and his group weren’t yet capable of modifying such advanced plants into Treant Pills.

On the other hand, ordinary first- to third-rank plants weren’t strong enough.

That was where the Pine Leaf Mouse’s own synthesis ability came in.

For example, the Firecloud Pepper—originally just a first-rank Malignant Plant, but after synthesis, it produced Netherworld Ghostfire, making it more rare and powerful than even high-tier spiritual plants!

Thus, Lin Jing needed to search Qingzhou for low-rank plants with fire, water, metal, and earth attributes, allowing the Pine Leaf Mouse to synthesize them and determine which would best serve as organ materials for its Treant Pill transformation.

For the metal-wood attribute, Lin Jing had already found a candidate—the Reflective Diamondwood, synthesized from the Mosquito-Powered Mech’s outer shell!

This Diamondwood could reflect most of the damage it received back to its attacker, making it an ideal choice for the Pine Leaf Mouse’s transformed skin in its Demon King True Form.

If no better metal-wood option was found, Diamondwood would be his first choice.

As for fire, water, and earth-based plants, he hadn’t found any suitable ones back at the academy. Now that he was in Qingzhou, he had resumed his search.

Qingzhou was rich in unique spiritual plants, offering far more choices than the Ancient Kingdom.

“Fellow Daoist, the 100 Explosive Fruits and 100 Sun Herbs you ordered have arrived.”

A cultivator from a nearby spirit plant shop approached. Rather than a true customer, he was a shopkeeper from a neighboring spiritual herb store.

Before opening the pill pavilion, Lin Jing had placed an order with them for two second-rank fire-attribute plants, spending a significant number of Spirit Stones.

Both were Qingzhou specialties.

The Explosive Fruit was a Malignant Plant, similar in effect to Spirit Bean Bombs—when subjected to a strong impact, it detonated like a fireball, capable of killing Foundation Establishment cultivators.

When Lin Jing had initially ordered 100 Explosive Fruits, the shopkeeper had hesitated for a long time, wondering what Lin Jing planned to do with them.

As for the Sun Herb, it was a spirit herb that enhanced fire-attribute spiritual energy. Consuming it while cultivating fire-elemental techniques under the sun significantly boosted efficiency.

Lin Jing wanted to see how these two plants would change when synthesized with the Glazed Immortal Eyes—would they, like the Firecloud Pepper, generate a new Exotic Flame?

If so, the Fire Qilin Dragon would gain yet another upgrade.

“The goods have arrived. Let’s begin synthesis.”

Inside the gourd, the Pine Leaf Mouse paused its ripening process, removed its straw hat, and looked up, its gaze sharp and intense.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 21: The False Core
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 21: The False Core

“I wonder if this will yield anything good…”

Lin Jing sighed as he placed a hundred Explosive Fruits and a hundred Sun Herbs into the Suspended Gourd.

The Pine Leaf Mouse was full of confidence.

“We’ve already synthesized so much junk—something useful has to come out eventually, right?”

“If nothing good comes out this time, we’ll just use the Blood Devil Vine as the ‘water-wood’ attribute organ material to simplify things. It does have a bit of that demonic cultivator flavor, but there’s no denying its effectiveness.”

With this decision, both Reflective Diamondwood and Blood Devil Vine were set as the metal-wood and water-wood attribute materials!

Since he already had enough of these materials stockpiled, there was no need to buy, plant, or synthesize anything new.

That meant only the fire-wood and earth-wood attributes remained. Once those were settled, the Five-Element Organ Transformation would be complete.

“Alright!!!”

The Pine Leaf Mouse didn’t care either way. It eagerly scurried to the edge of the Sun Herb pile, eyes burning with excitement.

The Sun Herb was orange-red, resembling clover, radiating a gentle warmth akin to sunlight.

Under the Glazed Immortal Eyes’ gaze, the hundred Sun Herbs quickly vanished. A moment later, an intense light shone, and a single mutated Sun Herb appeared—its deep red leaves flickered with tiny glowing spots.

“…”

The Pine Leaf Mouse scratched its head, staring at the speckled Sun Herb, lost in thought.

Lin Jing, sharing its vision, also fell into contemplation.

[Mutated Sun Herb: A plant attuned to the power of the sun. Upon consumption, the user’s body will temporarily reach temperatures comparable to the surface of the sun…]

“A Malignant Plant. This has to be a Malignant Plant. How did a perfectly fine fire-attribute cultivation herb mutate into something like this?!”

Lin Jing’s mouth twitched.

A body reaching temperatures near the sun’s surface… even with protective energy, low-level cultivators would just evaporate on the spot!

“Ugh~”

The Pine Leaf Mouse shrugged. What could it do?

“This won’t work—it can’t be used as one of your organs. Your core element is wood, and your ability to endure high temperatures is weak. You won’t be able to convert that heat into an attack.”

Lin Jing shook his head and turned to the Explosive Fruits.

Since these were already Malignant Plants, he had a good idea of what they might turn into.

Moments later, the ordinary apple-like Explosive Fruits underwent a transformation—not in color, but in shape. They were now covered in spikes, resembling red durians.

[Mutated Explosive Fruit: Has lost its explosive ability and is now edible…]

Seeing this, Lin Jing’s eyes lit up. But the next moment, he fell silent.

[Upon consumption, the user gains a one-time enhanced self-destruction ability. They can choose to detonate any organ… or their entire body. The explosion carries extreme fire energy.]

“Oh, wonderful. Another Malignant Plant turned Demonic Plant. Instead of a fruit that explodes on its own, now we have a fruit that helps the user self-destruct.”

Lin Jing looked up at the sky.

“Ugh~”

The Pine Leaf Mouse shrugged again. What could it do?

Wasn’t this always the case?

Rubbing its eyes, it popped a few vision-enhancing spiritual fruits into its mouth.

It was exhausted.

These two synthesis attempts had both failed. Qingzhou’s plants weren’t any better than the ones from the Ancient Kingdom.

Or were they?

“…Actually, this self-destruct fruit… might work as an organ.”

Lin Jing suddenly changed his evaluation.

The Pine Leaf Mouse: “???”

The mouse took a step back, pointing at its own body. Did it look like something that could self-destruct?

Lin Jing glanced at it.

“This isn’t for your real body. Your Demon King True Form is a wooden squirrel colossus covered in branches and pinecones. Aren’t those pinecones technically part of your body?”

“When the time comes, just blow those up.”

“Besides, you’ve already mastered the Everlasting Emperor’s Longevity Art. According to Senior Pine, you should be able to condense wooden clones now. Even if your main body can’t explode, your clones should be able to, right?”

The Pine Leaf Mouse’s mouth hung open.

Wasn’t this a little too ruthless?!

Lin Jing sighed.

“There’s no choice. If we aren’t ruthless to ourselves, the demons and beasts out there won’t go easy on us. If we don’t want to be swallowed whole, we have to be even crueler.”

The Pine Leaf Mouse furiously scratched its head. Self-destruct organs? Was that even practical?!

Lin Jing picked up the speckled Sun Herb and the Self-Destruct Fruit.

“Next, we’ll mass-produce the Self-Destruct Fruit and integrate it into the Treant Pill to give your pinecones the Flame Detonation attribute.”

“The speckled Sun Herb won’t go to waste either. It’ll be the core material for a new pill formula.”

“The Gluttonous Pill alone isn’t enough to dominate the market. If consuming this synthesized Sun Herb grants a body temperature close to the sun’s surface, even Core Formation cultivators won’t be able to withstand it… But if someone can control this power, it won’t just be a Malignant Plant for suicide—it will become a mystical artifact that grants them a high-temperature spell!”

Lin Jing began pondering.

Could it be used for False Core Alchemy?

“…It might work.”

“If I can refine this into a False Core! With the Fire Qilin Dragon assisting me, it could be possible!”

“For those who have no hope of forming a real Core, or those who have fallen from Core Formation back to Foundation Establishment and can’t form a second Core, they often turn to alchemy or secret techniques to create a False Core in place of a real one.”

“Although a False Core lacks the growth potential of a true Core and cannot reach the Nascent Soul stage, some False Cores contain incredible power. When activated, they can unleash abilities on par with Core Formation divine techniques.”

“The Core Formation stage is all about refining and perfecting Core divine abilities. Using them wears down the Core, requiring long periods of seclusion to restore it—or costly pills to repair it… Only after reaching Nascent Soul can a cultivator freely use their divine techniques without worry.”

“That’s why powerful False Core techniques aren’t only sought after by Foundation Establishment cultivators who can’t reach Core Formation.”

“Even those who have already formed a real Core wouldn’t mind refining an additional False Core for extra security.”

“In battle, they could rely on the False Core, preserving their real one until they reached Nascent Soul.”

“After all, unlike a real Core, a False Core doesn’t fear damage. If it gets destroyed, they can just refine another one.”

“If I can seal the high-temperature energy within a False Core, forming a Solar False Core… Its temperature would be close to the sun’s surface. In the Core Formation stage, it would be an absolute trump card. Few would be able to withstand its power.”

Lin Jing picked up the speckled Sun Herb.

“This is a perfect opportunity to research alchemical pills that assist in Core Formation.”

That was the real priority…

If nothing unexpected happened, his own breakthrough to Core Formation would require the aid of pills.

His three contract beasts might not be enough to push him forward.

The further he advanced, the harder it became for a Longevity Body to cultivate.

He would need to refine a pill to assist in his Core Formation.

This False Core Alchemy—right now, it was the perfect training ground.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 22: Tongtian Tower
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 22: Tongtian Tower

The next day.

Lin Jing used Hidden Dragon Fortune to deduce the pill formulas for the "Self-Destruct Treant Pill" and the "Solar False Core."

This was a common method for him.

Just like the Rising Dragon Pill, Gluttonous Pill, and Pain Pill, they were all created this way.

First, he would use Hidden Dragon Fortune, combining it with his knowledge of alchemy to deduce possible formulas.

Once he came up with a few suitable ones, he would throw the formulas and ingredients to the Fire Qilin Dragon for testing to see if they could actually be refined.

If the pills could be successfully made, he would then test them on lab rats. If the effects matched his deductions, then a new pill was officially created...

…

Inside the Nameless Pill Pavilion, Lin Jing lay on a wooden chair, seemingly asleep, but in reality, he was deducing pill formulas in his mind.

The blue-clothed youth who had taken an interest in the Dining Pill the previous day had returned, calling out for Lin Jing.

He was panting heavily, his face tense, sweat dripping.

"Did you bring the Sacred Fire Lotus?" Lin Jing opened his eyes. This youth… he was probably too well-protected in his daily life, appearing a bit naive.

Luckily, his father was still maintaining himself in a remnant soul state. Otherwise, this boy’s path to growth would be incredibly difficult.

"I brought it, Senior."

He carefully took out a stone box, revealing a platinum-colored lotus that looked like it was made of flames. Lin Jing glanced at it and said, "Good quality, well-preserved. It seems your father is quite an accomplished alchemist and knows how to store spiritual herbs perfectly."

"You flatter me. Compared to your master, I am nothing. If I’m not mistaken, this Gluttonous Pill must be an original creation of your master. In Qingzhou, in terms of effectiveness, your master has likely reached the pinnacle—no one can surpass him."

The remnant soul within the blue-clothed youth slowly spoke, fully confident in its judgment. This Gluttonous Pill had an unimaginable effect in helping lower-level cultivators absorb high-level resources.

Lin Jing said, "Alright, how many Dining Pills do you want in exchange for this Sacred Fire Lotus?"

The remnant soul pondered for a moment, then said, "Whatever amount you are willing to give."

"The ingredients for this Dining Pill may not exceed the second rank, but the refining method is extremely refined, at least the work of a third-grade alchemist. Combined with its astonishing effects and rare properties, its value could easily rival an ordinary fourth-grade pill. Name your price. Supply and demand determine worth, and we trust your judgment."

Lin Jing chuckled. It seemed they were willing to take a slight loss in order to establish a good relationship with him.

"There are ten bottles here, totaling one hundred Dining Pills. Take them. I heard you have a large inheritance to absorb. If it’s not enough, you can use Spirit Stones to buy more from me later—consider it a benefit." Lin Jing said.

"Many thanks, Master." The remnant soul laughed heartily, while the blue-clothed youth quickly added, "Xia Yan thanks Master."

After completing the transaction, Lin Jing sent them off and casually tossed the Sacred Fire Lotus into his meditation staff pendant.

The Sacred Fire Lotus was a fourth-rank fire-attribute spiritual plant, but due to the presence of exotic flames, its value rivaled that of a fifth-rank plant. With a Foundation Establishment cultivation, it would normally be difficult to absorb such a treasure. Even if the Sacred Fire Lotus was gentle in nature and the cultivator shut themselves in for decades to refine it, they would still struggle to completely absorb its power.

However, the Fire Qilin Dragon was an exception. With its emperor-tier fire-type bloodline, it could refine the Sacred Fire Lotus in no time, even without relying on the Gluttonous Pill. And if it did take the Dining Pill, the process would be even faster.

Before long, after consuming a small amount of Dining Pills, the Fire Qilin Dragon completely absorbed the power of the Sacred Fire Lotus. Its body radiated layers of white lotus-shaped flames as its cultivation surpassed both the Pine Leaf Mouse and the Ice Soul Gu, reaching Small Perfection of the Foundation Establishment stage—just a step away from Core Formation!!!

"Roar!!!"

Inside the meditation staff, the once small Dragon Carp let out a sky-shaking roar.

"No rush. With your current state, you should be able to refine a few more exotic flames. When your body can no longer withstand more, then you can break through to Core Formation." Lin Jing remained relaxed on his wooden chair, planning everything out as he awaited the arrival of the next "fire delivery boy."

…

Tianqing City, Summer Clan of a Cultivation Family.

The youth, Xia Yan, secretly entered a hidden underground chamber, concealed by a special formation.

Holding a hundred Gluttonous Pills, he walked down the steps and whispered, "Father, is this enough?"

"Definitely not."

"But if we focus on absorbing the most important things, it will barely be enough."

The remnant soul materialized in the underground chamber. In the next moment, five objects radiating treasure light floated around it.

"You have an Acquired Fire Spirit Body and are currently in Early Foundation Establishment. At your current level, there is no way you can pass the trial of Tongtian Tower. Whether you enter through alchemy or strength, you have no chance."

"If you fail the trial, staying in the Summer Clan means you’ll never reach Nascent Soul in this lifetime."

"I had originally paved the way for you, but unfortunately…"

"The Sacred Fire Lotus was meant for you. If it had produced 'Lotus Heart Flame,' it would have been invaluable for both your alchemy and cultivation. But under the current circumstances, that’s no longer our priority. The Gluttonous Pill is what you need the most right now."

"This is a fourth-grade Fire Spirit Pill I refined. It will allow your Acquired Fire Spirit Body to reach perfection and greatly enhance your power. Normally, it should only be consumed at the Core Formation stage, but with the Gluttonous Pill, you can take it directly. While the effect may not be fully optimized, it will still significantly boost your Extinguishing Spirit Physique."

"This is a fourth-rank spiritual plant, the 'Tornado Flower.' Absorbing it will perfect your cultivation of the 'Tornado Technique'..."

"This is…"

The remnant soul introduced each treasure one by one as the youth consumed the Gluttonous Pills, rapidly absorbing and refining them. His cultivation soared from Early Foundation Establishment toward greater heights. The remnant soul watched with satisfaction.

"The pill’s effect is indeed remarkable…"

"At this rate, in less than half a month, he will completely refine these five treasures… reaching Perfection of Foundation Establishment… and achieving near-perfect control over all his powers. Just in time for the Tongtian Tower trial."

…

For the next several days, Lin Jing continued deducing pill formulas and waiting for buyers.

However, whether due to bad luck or other reasons, while a few more customers came seeking the Gluttonous Pill, none could offer exotic flames or relevant information that satisfied Lin Jing.

Aside from the first deal, most traders at best could offer "low-grade beast flames" for exchange. These flames were far inferior to Dragon Scales Fire—completely useless. Even if Lin Jing used the Flame Emperor’s ability to merge them, they wouldn’t amount to much and would only waste his divine abilities.

Just like that, half a month passed. Lin Jing hadn’t made another major trade for the Gluttonous Pill, only selling common practice pills. Special cases like the father and son of the Summer Clan were rare. Plus, due to limited stock, Lin Jing hadn’t actively advertised and was relying solely on the Dining Pill’s fragrance to attract knowledgeable buyers—making business even slower.

"The commercial street seems especially quiet today…"

During his break, Lin Jing stepped outside for fresh air and overheard passersby talking.

"Let’s go, no point hanging around here. The Tongtian Tower is recruiting disciples in Tianqing City. Wonder how many genius seedlings will be chosen this year…"

"I heard that the major clans like the Summer Clan, Zhu Clan, and Fan Clan have sent a lot of their juniors. Let’s go watch."

"Tongtian Tower…" Lin Jing murmured.

It was rumored that the Tongtian Tower frequently recruited disciples from top-tier immortal cities. However, it had too many high-level cultivators, including many at the Void Mystical Stage and even Dao-Seeking Stage. It wasn’t an entity he should get too involved with.

However, the recruiters in Tianqing City were unlikely to be too powerful—probably at most Nascent Soul.

"The Summer Clan… That boy, Xia Yan, is probably from the Summer Clan. The 'quota' his father mentioned—is it a Tongtian Tower disciple quota?"

"If so, he might end up promoting my Gluttonous Pill during the trial…

"I wonder if any Tongtian Tower cultivators will take notice. As the top alchemy force, they must have information on higher-level exotic flames. There's some risk, but if it's just ordinary Tongtian Tower disciples, it might be worth engaging with them."

Lin Jing returned to his house and lay back down.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 23: Unparalleled Talent
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 23: Unparalleled Talent

The news that cultivators from Tongtian Tower were arriving in Tianqing City had long been known to the major families. For them, the recruitment of disciples by Tongtian Tower was an opportunity no faction could ignore.

Tongtian Tower represented the strongest alchemy heritage in the cultivation world, a place where the Grand Dao of Immortal Pills existed—an absolute holy land for countless alchemists. However, most cultivation family disciples were unable to pass the alchemy examinations to enter Tongtian Tower.

This force had extremely strict requirements for passing down alchemy knowledge. Unless someone possessed unparalleled talent in pill refinement, it was nearly impossible to become a core member of Tongtian Tower.

The disciples being recruited this time were more akin to alchemy attendants and test subjects!

They were selecting young cultivators with great potential to become outer disciples of Tongtian Tower—serving as bodyguards and test subjects for core alchemists, growing together and forming a mutually beneficial bond!

Nearly every core alchemist in Tongtian Tower had several powerful cultivators assigned to protect them. In exchange, these alchemists would distribute a portion of their refined pills to these protectors…

Anyone who became a core alchemist of Tongtian Tower was an absolute genius in alchemy. Following them as an attendant meant that achieving Nascent Soul was almost certain, and at the very least, they would reach the Golden Core stage.

Thus, even becoming an alchemy attendant was something that countless cultivation family disciples eagerly pursued.

Tianqing City, Central Plaza

The major cultivation families had already set up the venue, led by Ling Quan Zun-zhe, the local head of the Qingming Commerce Guild in this city, to welcome the esteemed alchemists from Tongtian Tower.

As the flying ship from Tongtian Tower arrived, Ling Quan Zun-zhe laughed heartily, "So it’s Alchemist Jie Geng! I’ve long heard that Alchemist Jie Geng has advanced to the fifth rank in alchemy. Congratulations, congratulations..."

"It’s been a while, Senior Ling Quan."

A figure stepped out from the flying ship—a young female alchemist dressed in a simple white robe. Though she was only at the late Golden Core stage, even a Nascent Soul expert treated her with great respect.

The Nascent Soul cultivators from various major families were also stunned, immediately offering their greetings.

Although this female cultivator was only at the Golden Core stage, she was a core disciple of Tongtian Tower. She had advanced to a fifth-rank alchemist before the age of thirty, meaning she was already capable of refining fifth-grade pills!

Fifth-grade pills were valuable even to Nascent Soul cultivators. A young Golden Core cultivator capable of refining them had limitless potential—reaching the sixth rank in alchemy was not out of the question in the future.

Even if one disregarded her own talent, just the fact that she had the backing of Tongtian Tower and a powerful master meant that everyone present had to show due respect.

"I’m only here to recruit alchemy attendants for Tongtian Tower. There’s no need for such grand gestures." Jie Geng spoke softly.

"How could we not? Alchemist Jie Geng, please—Tianqing City’s most outstanding cultivation family disciples have all gathered here today. Let’s see who has the fortune to join Tongtian Tower."

Ling Quan Zun-zhe looked toward the crowd, where over a hundred young cultivators, all above Foundation Establishment, were eagerly waiting, their gazes burning with anticipation for Alchemist Jie Geng.

Even if they were not qualified to follow this particular alchemist, every single alchemist from Tongtian Tower would at least become a sixth-rank alchemist in the future. No matter who they followed, their cultivation resources would be secured—this was an opportunity for a meteoric rise that few would want to miss.

"Alright." Jie Geng nodded, her figure gracefully descending onto a central arena.

Ling Quan Zun-zhe glanced at the flying ship and sensed several overwhelming auras—Nascent Soul Great Perfection cultivators. He couldn’t help but sigh in admiration. With these experts, combined with the power of alchemy and formations, even an ordinary Soul Formation cultivator would struggle against them.

"This is my trial beast. It has reached Perfection of the Foundation Establishment stage. Anyone in the Foundation Establishment stage who can defeat it will be accepted as an outer disciple of Tongtian Tower…."

As she spoke, Jie Geng waved her hand lightly.

On the arena, a massive three-headed serpent covered in ink-black scales roared into existence, emanating a chaotic aura.

Though the serpent’s body was a uniform dark color, each of its heads was a different shade, seemingly infused with eight distinct elemental forces.

Seeing this beast, many younger cultivators from cultivation families took a deep breath, feeling the powerful bloodline emanating from it!

"…Even among king-tier bloodlines, this is considered potent!"

"That’s the trial beast? If we have to defeat that… this year’s requirement to become an alchemy attendant for Tongtian Tower is lower than before!"

Despite the murmurs, many cultivation family disciples showed hesitation at the sight of the three-headed serpent. But under the firm orders of their family elders, they gritted their teeth and stepped onto the arena one by one—only to be utterly crushed.

The serpent, wielding eight elemental forces—lightning, fire, ice, and more—overwhelmed every challenger with ease.

Pfft!

Yet another Foundation Establishment cultivator was sent flying off the stage.

Jie Geng sighed.

"Is this the best they have?"

After seeing over twenty young cultivators fail, the alchemist shook her head. The reason she had brought this three-headed serpent was to research False Core techniques—she wanted the serpent to condense three monster cores.

Yet even in its current state, it wasn’t at full power, and still, these challengers couldn’t defeat it.

These people were completely unqualified to become alchemy attendants for Tongtian Tower.

Ling Quan Zun-zhe also shook his head, disappointed. It seemed that Tongtian Tower would leave Tianqing City empty-handed this time. He hadn’t heard of any remarkable geniuses appearing in the city over the past decade.

"I’ll do it!"

Just as fear of the three-headed serpent spread among the young cultivators, someone suddenly stepped forward from the crowd!

A red-eyed youth pushed through the throng and strode straight onto the arena!

"Isn’t that Xia Yan…?"

"When did this kid reach Perfection of Foundation Establishment?!"

Among the Summer Clan’s cultivators, several people were momentarily stunned. They clearly remembered that not long ago, Xia Yan had only been at Early Foundation Establishment!

And yet, that wasn’t the most shocking part. What truly left them dumbfounded was the power Xia Yan displayed as he stepped onto the stage!

Blazing fire-attribute true essence surged from both his hands, illuminating his entire body. His near-perfect mastery of fire spirit power finally caught Jie Geng’s interest.

For most geniuses, cultivating a special physique to Minor Completion in Foundation Establishment was already impressive.

But this boy—his physique was nearly at Great Completion!

If Full Completion meant fully unleashing the physique’s potential at 100%, Minor Completion was at 30%, and Great Completion was at 70%. The gap between these levels was massive, akin to a complete difference in class!

"Not only has his Fire Spirit Body nearly reached Great Completion, but his control over fire is also…"

Xia Yan raised his hands, summoning ten fiery tornadoes that intertwined with the power of storms.

As the three-headed serpent let out an electric roar, the two forces clashed fiercely on the stage.

Watching this, Jie Geng asked, "This boy possesses unparalleled talent. At under twenty years old, he already has such strength. He should at least qualify to become an alchemy attendant. He may even have the potential to become an alchemist of Tongtian Tower.

"Senior Ling Quan, do you know this youth?"

Ling Quan Zun-zhe, standing beside Jie Geng, wore an equally bewildered expression.

"I do… His name is Xia Yan, from the Summer Clan."

"His father was the number one alchemist in Tianqing City—Soulfire Pill Sovereign Xia Jie. But a year ago, Xia Jie was reportedly assassinated by demonic cultivators from Netherworld Hall.

"I saw Xia Yan at Xia Jie’s funeral. Back then, he was only at Early Foundation Establishment.

"For him to reach this level in less than a year… even if his father left him a substantial inheritance, this growth rate is simply absurd!"

Ling Quan Zun-zhe felt a deep sense of regret. If he had known Xia Yan had such talent, he would have recruited him into the Qingming Commerce Guild long ago—he wouldn’t have left this opportunity for Tongtian Tower!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 24: The Alchemy Gathering
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 24: The Alchemy Gathering

"Impossible! Absolutely impossible!!!"

After a fierce battle, the young Xia Yan successfully defeated the three-headed serpent, leaving many who knew him in utter disbelief.

"That little bastard… how could he…" The current head of the Xia family, Xia Yan's uncle, turned red-eyed at the sight of his nephew’s sudden surge in power.

"That bastard Xia Jie must have hidden some secret inheritance for this brat! How could a mere Foundation Establishment beginner skyrocket to this level in such a short time? Just what kind of treasure did he get his hands on? And how much of it is left?! I held back before out of family ties, but this little bastard is nothing but a common mortal, a waste who only acquired a Fire Spirit Body through external means. He has no right to such treasures—they should belong to my son instead!"

He wanted to rush forward and demand answers, but as Alchemist Jie Geng stepped out and smiled at Xia Yan, his heart sank. It was too late.

"You didn’t state your name earlier."

"Xia Yan. Alchemist Jie Geng, have I passed the test?" Xia Yan looked at her.

"Mm, you’ve passed. Not only have you reached Perfection of Foundation Establishment before the age of twenty, but you also possess such battle power—you are fully qualified to become a disciple of Tongtian Tower."

"Once the assessment is over, I will tell you more about Tongtian Tower." With a wave of Jie Geng’s hand, Xia Yan was sent onto the flying ship. Meanwhile, under the effects of medicinal power, the severely burned three-headed serpent was healed and stood up once more...

…

By the end of the day, only Xia Yan had passed the recruitment test for Tongtian Tower. The news caused an uproar throughout Tianqing City.

Xia Yan was well-known in Tianqing City—not because of his talent, but because he was the son of the city's top alchemist. Though he was born with an ordinary physique, having a Pill Sovereign father had made him something of a privileged second-generation cultivator.

Unfortunately, his father had died too soon.

…

"Congratulations on being the only one from this city to pass the Tongtian Tower test. I heard that not long ago, you were only at the early stage of Foundation Establishment and weren’t particularly outstanding in strength. How did your power suddenly increase so drastically? If it’s inconvenient to answer, you may disregard the question."

On the flying ship, Jie Geng slowly approached and looked at Xia Yan, who was sitting cross-legged in meditation.

"Master Jie Geng, it’s because my father left me many pills and spiritual medicines that enhanced my strength…"

"But from what I heard, as of a month ago, you were still at the early stage of Foundation Establishment. I can’t recall any pill or spiritual treasure that could allow your physique to reach near completion in such a short time…"

Xia Yan took a deep breath and said, "Under normal circumstances, it would indeed be impossible. But I used a pill called the 'Dining Pill' to enhance my digestion ability, allowing me to rapidly absorb all the treasures my father left behind."

"Alchemist Jie Geng, this is the last Dining Pill I have left. I’d like to gift it to you." Xia Yan took out the final pill and handed it over.

"This pill…" Jie Geng took it and was momentarily stunned before showing a look of surprise.

"This pill… could it be that your father refined it? Xia Jie, the Pill Sovereign, he…"

Jie Geng had never expected that in a remote city in the outskirts of Qingzhou, there would be such a talented alchemist.

If this pill existed in large quantities, it could indeed allow even those with poor talent and low cultivation to rapidly grow stronger through the consumption of high-level treasures.

Though such a method was wasteful, extravagant, and not cost-effective, it could undeniably bring about miraculous transformations.

"That damned Netherworld Hall…!"

A flash of anger flickered in Jie Geng’s eyes—she clearly felt it was a waste for such a skilled alchemist to die at the hands of demonic cultivators.

"Yes, yes, yes," Xia Yan hurriedly added, "but actually… I didn’t get this from my father. I bought it from Shop #44 in the commercial district of Tianqing City."

Jie Geng: "?"

"You bought it from the commercial district?" She looked confused.

"Yes, from a wandering alchemist named Lin Jing at the Nameless Pill Pavilion."

Xia Yan quickly explained everything about the Nameless Pill Pavilion. Then, after a brief moment of thought, he took out a storage pouch and said, "Master Jie Geng, this is my father’s remaining inheritance. It contains many high-grade spiritual items. Please accept them as well."

Jie Geng, still deep in thought about the mysterious Nameless Pill Pavilion, chuckled at his offer and said, "What would I need your father’s inheritance for?"

Xia Yan gritted his teeth. "I… I want to follow Master Jie Geng, even if it’s just as a test subject for your pills."

His heart was filled with sorrow. The day he left seclusion, he found that his father—who had only been surviving as a remnant soul—had completely dissipated, leaving behind only a final letter.

The letter instructed that if Xia Yan passed the Tongtian Tower test, he should offer up all remaining possessions to Tongtian Tower’s alchemists in exchange for a promising future and their protection.

If he failed the test, he should instead give everything to the wandering alchemist at the Nameless Pill Pavilion and follow him, rather than stay with the Xia family—because there were few good people in the family.

His father believed that the mysterious wandering alchemist possessed shocking alchemy skills and was backed by someone formidable. Staying by his side would be safe and full of potential.

Now that Xia Yan had passed the test, he naturally chose to follow his father’s wishes…

…

Shortly After—Nameless Pill Pavilion

Lying on his wooden chair, deducing pill formulas, Lin Jing suddenly opened his eyes.

"They’ve come?"

With a single thought, he saw a female alchemist at the Golden Core stage stepping into his shop, her gaze fixed on the Dining Pill placed in the center of the room.

"Trading it… for exotic flames or exotic flame intelligence…"

"Interesting," Jie Geng said, looking at the lazy Lin Jing, who was only at the mid-Foundation Establishment stage. "Boss, is the formula for this Dining Pill something that can be traded?"

Lin Jing stood up, stretched lazily, and replied, "Sure."

"But only in exchange for a specific type of exotic flame."

"What kind of flame?" Jie Geng asked.

"An exotic flame with the property of burning lifespan," Lin Jing said.

"Candle Age Fire?" Jie Geng suggested.

"Candle Age Fire…?" Lin Jing’s eyes flashed. "Does this flame truly have the property of burning lifespan?"

He had searched through numerous books and even inquired with others, yet no one could tell him of an exotic flame that specifically burned lifespan.

Most had simply responded with—Any flame could do that. Just burn your enemy to death, and their lifespan is over.

While that logic wasn’t entirely wrong, it wasn’t what Lin Jing was looking for.

"Candle Age Fire is an exotic flame wielded by a divine beast with the bloodline of a Candle Dragon. It has the power to burn away time itself and can also consume lifespan." Jie Geng explained. "But unfortunately, I don’t have it. Looks like I can’t trade for your pill formula. Besides, the value of your Dining Pill formula is far lower than that of the Candle Age Fire."

Lin Jing chuckled. "Information works too. If you know how to obtain Candle Age Fire, I can provide you with a large supply of Gluttonous Pills. You’re an alchemist, aren’t you? If you had enough pills to research, you could probably deduce the formula yourself."

Jie Geng smiled. "That works too."

"Tongtian Tower has a special secret realm—the Heavenly Fire Realm. It contains over a thousand exotic flames, including the Candle Age Fire."

Lin Jing fell silent. "Do you think I have any chance of obtaining it?"

"The realm isn’t completely off-limits." Jie Geng’s voice was calm.

"Do you know about the Pill Assembly? It’s a grand alchemy event hosted by Tongtian Tower every ten years."

"During this event, all alchemists in Qingzhou may participate—regardless of race. Even alchemy-proficient demon beasts can join. It’s a pure alchemy competition where nothing matters except skill."

"And the first-place winner of the Pill Assembly is granted a special transaction with Tongtian Tower."

"A transaction?"

"The Pill Assembly doesn’t test basic alchemy skills—it tests alchemy achievements."

"Only newly invented pills are allowed. The extent to which these pills revolutionize the cultivation world determines the winner."

"And the first-place winner may trade their pill formula with Tongtian Tower… in exchange for entry into the Heavenly Fire Realm to claim one exotic flame of their choice."

Jie Geng looked at the mysterious wandering alchemist.

Someone who could create such a miraculous pill… was far too talented to remain hidden in this remote town.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 25: The Path to the Crown
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 25: The Path to the Crown

Finally obtaining information on a flame that could burn lifespan, Lin Jing felt his spirits lifted—it seemed that mastering the Heavenly Demon Physique was just around the corner.

The first-place prize in the Pill Assembly was not determined by the pill’s grade but rather by its impact…

It seemed to be encouraging alchemists to pursue innovation, bringing transformative change to the cultivation world.

This competition format was incredibly favorable to Lin Jing. Thanks to his collection of "special plants," he had plenty of top-tier pill formulas at his disposal…

For instance, the Dining Pill—without a doubt, it could bring significant changes to the cultivation world…

However, Lin Jing had a major problem—all of his pills relied on the unique ability of his Glazed Immortal Eyes, making them nearly impossible for others to replicate.

Would Tongtian Tower be willing to trade a top-tier exotic flame for a pill that was difficult to reproduce? That was a huge concern.

"No, it’s not an issue," Lin Jing reassured himself. "My previous deal with Shenji Manor and now this transaction with the female alchemist prove it."

Jie Geng noticed Lin Jing deep in thought and asked, "So, are you satisfied with this information?"

"Yes."

Lin Jing raised his hand slightly, and several bottles of Gluttonous Pills floated toward Jie Geng.

"If I have time, I’ll come take a look at this Pill Assembly."

Jie Geng smiled. "The next Pill Assembly is in a few years. Here, take this participation token."

She tossed him a jade token before leaving the Nameless Pill Pavilion, having achieved her goal.

After Jie Geng left, Lin Jing sat back in his wooden chair and pulled out some books detailing the history of the Pill Assembly…

The last champion was a sixth-rank alchemist from Tongtian Tower.

Their winning pill was the Heavenly Human Pill, a sixth-rank pill that guaranteed a Nascent Soul cultivator would fully comprehend celestial energy and ascend to the Soul Formation stage.

The champion before that was a demon beast alchemist from the Toad Clan.

They refined the Tadpole Pill, which guaranteed that a child would inherit one parent’s cultivation physique.
