The one before that was the Ginseng King, a sentient plant.

It refined the Lightning-Avoiding Pill, which made the user almost completely immune to lightning-based techniques and natural tribulation lightning—especially useful for surviving heavenly tribulations.

After reading, Lin Jing fell into deep thought.

As expected, this was a true alchemy competition with no racial discrimination.

For three consecutive assemblies, the champions had all been from different races.

Compared to the Ancient Kingdom, Qingzhou was far more open-minded.

Then again, it made sense—although Tongtian Tower was human-led, it had demon beast disciples as well. Allowing non-humans to compete was logical.

Lin Jing also realized the difficulty of the competition.

Whether it was the Rising Dragon Pill, Gluttonous Pill, Pain Pill, or even the Solar False Core he had yet to perfect—none of them could completely surpass those winning pills.

"For centuries, Nascent Soul cultivators relied on creating avatars to comprehend celestial energy and ascend to Soul Formation. The Heavenly Human Pill wasn’t the first pill to assist in this breakthrough… but it was the first to have a 100% success rate."

"The Tadpole Pill… ignoring the ridiculous name, its effect was astounding. Cultivation physiques aren’t always hereditary. Take Xia Yan, for example—he has an Acquired Fire Spirit Body instead of inheriting his father’s Innate Fire Soul Body, making his talent much lower. For factions that emphasize bloodline inheritance, especially those with Innate Divine Physiques, this pill would be invaluable."

"The Lightning-Avoiding Pill… heavenly tribulations are one of the few truly uncontrollable disasters. Weak cultivators can’t possibly withstand them, so this pill would drastically increase the survival rate of weaker cultivators."

These pills all had game-changing effects, though their refining difficulty was surely just as high…

Regardless, Lin Jing knew that his current pill formulas were still lacking compared to these.

Even the Solar False Core, which he had just begun to conceptualize, wouldn’t be enough.

False Core techniques already existed in the cultivation world. While they were rare, adding one more wouldn’t shake the system. In terms of impact, it probably wouldn’t even surpass the Dining Pill.

However, the Dining Pill had its own issue—its low grade.

At most, it could help with the digestion of fourth- or fifth-rank spiritual materials. Once a cultivator’s realm advanced too high, it became ineffective.

It couldn’t help high-level cultivators digest treasures beyond their limits—it only worked for lower-level cultivators.

Even though the Pill Assembly claimed to judge only a pill’s impact, Lin Jing didn’t fully believe that.

Looking at past champions, almost all winning pills benefited powerful cultivators.

Pills that benefited low-level cultivators were acknowledged but rarely took first place.

And the Dining Pill was a pill that benefited low-level cultivators.

Moreover, it was not cost-effective.

Even with digestion-enhancing pills, low-level cultivators still couldn’t fully absorb high-grade resources—there was always waste.

No faction could afford to train an entire generation of young cultivators using sheer wasteful consumption.

High-level resources were better spent directly on already-powerful cultivators, making them even stronger.

Xia Yan’s case was an exception, not the rule.

"In the end, power still belongs to the strongest cultivators. If I want to win the next Pill Assembly, I need to create a pill that truly appeals to those above the Nascent Soul stage."

Lin Jing sighed.

To achieve that, he would need to refine at least a fourth-rank pill.

…

Fire Qilin Dragon was right—it really couldn’t be avoided.

After all, Lin Jing could only refine third-rank pills at present.

But the Fire Qilin Dragon, thanks to its Rare Beast Physique and exotic flame fusion, could already refine fourth-rank pills.

Once it reached Core Formation, refining fourth-rank pills would be effortless.

Fortunately, back when he was in Thousand Illusions Mountain, Lin Jing had mastered the Beast Taming Sect’s Fusion Secret Art—Immortal Rebirth.

Without a background, sending the Fire Qilin Dragon to compete alone in the Pill Assembly would be dangerous.

But with Immortal Rebirth, it could escape most dangers like a cicada shedding its shell.

"Roar!!!"

Inside the meditation staff, the Fire Qilin Dragon assured Lin Jing that it would be fine.

"How could I be at ease?" Lin Jing scoffed. "You’ve been following me since you developed intelligence—you haven’t experienced real hardships. Even if you’re now an emperor-tier Fire Annihilation Dragon, in my eyes… you’re still just a little carp."

Fire Qilin Dragon: !!!

"I’ve decided. Before you go to the Pill Assembly, I’m going to release you on your own. You need to gain real experience and toughen up."

"Ever heard of The Adventures of Little Carp?"

Fire Qilin Dragon: !!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 26: The Little Carp’s Adventure
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 26: The Little Carp’s Adventure

The next day, the Tongtian Tower flying ship departed from Tianqing City. Many were curious about the transformation of Xia Yan, the only son of Pill Sovereign Xia Jie. Among them, the one most aware of the truth was Ling Quan Zun-zhe of the Qingming Commerce Guild.

"This Xia Yan… It seems he underwent his transformation through this Gluttonous Pill. Alchemist Jie Geng should have also interacted with this wandering alchemist… Do you know about the Gluttonous Pill that this wandering alchemist is selling?"

He looked at the bald old man kneeling before him, drenched in sweat.

"R-Respectable One, I-I do know… but I am not an alchemist, so I didn’t realize this Gluttonous Pill had such miraculous effects. If I had known, I would have reported it to the guild right away..."

"I'm not blaming you." Ling Quan Zun-zhe spoke slowly. "You just said that he calls his master a Fifth-Tier Alchemist known as Fire Dragon Pill Sovereign?"

"If that's the case, then this pill likely wasn’t created by him but by his master. A Third-Tier Alchemist at the Foundation Establishment stage is impressive, but they wouldn’t be able to develop such a pill yet."

"Interesting… Fire Dragon Pill Sovereign, huh? I’ve never heard of this name before. Could it be that he isn't a human alchemist but from the demonic beast race?" Ling Quan Zun-zhe pondered.

While the demonic beast race was generally not skilled in alchemy or artifact refinement, there were always exceptions. Some demonic beasts even surpassed humans in certain cultivation arts—like certain monkey demons excelling in spirit brewing…

He truly didn’t know much about alchemists among the demonic beasts.

"Let's go take a look."

…

"Amateur!"

Over at the alchemist’s side, after Jie Geng and Xia Yan, another distinguished guest arrived at the Beauty Hair Pill Pavilion.

Though this individual hadn’t revealed their cultivation level, the alchemist’s innate sensitivity allowed them to sense the powerful presence emanating from the visitor’s soul… It was unmistakable—this was no ordinary elder. At the very least, he was a Nascent Soul cultivator!

Dressed in an opulent blue robe, the elderly man stepped into the pill pavilion. The alchemist immediately rose and quickly guessed his identity.

"Junior greets the esteemed Ling Quan of the Qingming Commerce Guild." They respectfully bowed.

‘It’s easy to recognize you, but I’m not here for formalities. I’m merely interested in this Gluttonous Pill you’re selling. Did you create it?" Ling Quan Zun-zhe approached the Gluttonous Pill, giving it a careful look, deeply intrigued.

The alchemist shook their head. "Respectable One, while I did refine this pill, the formula and refining method were actually created by my master, Fire Dragon Pill Sovereign, who passed it down to me."

"This is the exotic flame Crystal Flame. Tell me, how many of your Gluttonous Pills can I exchange for it?" Ling Quan Zun-zhe stretched out his hand, producing a transparent container with a floating blue flame inside.

"Crystal Flame?!" The alchemist licked their lips—this was a high-level exotic flame!

Flames were categorized by quality from highest to lowest: Mortal Extinguishing Flame, Special Spiritual Flames, Low-Tier Exotic Flames, and Top-Tier Exotic Flames.

Mortal Extinguishing Flames referred to mundane flames that lacked spiritual energy, while Special Spiritual Flames were unique flames generated by cultivators using True Qi or True Essence—such as the commonly used Fireball Technique, which was composed of Special Spiritual Flames.

Starting from Low-Tier Exotic Flames, things became significantly rarer. These required cultivation techniques or secret arts to acquire, or they could be naturally formed in special locations.

Many alchemy sects or fire-based sects had cultivation techniques specifically designed to refine Low-Tier Exotic Flames. For example, the purple pill flame used by Xia Yan and Ziling'er from the Pill Cauldron Sect was one such flame.

Such flames could be used all the way up to the Nascent Soul stage without issue, making them a staple in the cultivation world due to their stability and effectiveness.

The Emerging Lotus Heart Flame obtained by Long Li through the Sacred Fire Lotus was also a flame of this tier.

As for Top-Tier Exotic Flames, they were even rarer and nearly impossible to obtain. The Dragon Scales Fire of the Fire Qilin Dragon was an example—it was a Top-Tier Beast Flame and a variant of Top-Tier Exotic Flames, derived from its bloodline inheritance.

However, unlike naturally occurring Top-Tier Exotic Flames, beast flames didn’t start at full power. Instead, their strength grew with the beast’s bloodline.

When a Fire Qilin used its flame at the King-level bloodline, it was vastly different from when it used it at the Emperor-level bloodline. Even if the energy consumption was the same, the latter’s power was exponentially greater.

The same applied to the Netherworld Ghostfire. Though categorized as a Top-Tier Exotic Flame, it had to absorb and burn souls to increase its power. The more souls it consumed, the stronger it became.

For this reason, the demonic cultivators of the Netherworld Hall had cruelly sacrificed countless souls in pursuit of amplifying their Netherworld Ghostfire.

Since Lin Jing and Long Li were righteous cultivators, their Netherworld Ghostfire had yet to reach its full potential.

Most of the time, they just used it to roast mosquito souls.

Compared to human souls—considered the best fuel—these mindless mosquito spirits barely provided any benefits.

The Crystal Flame, on the other hand, was a high-tier exotic flame, and Lin Jing was definitely tempted. If he recalled correctly, the Crystal Flame had the unique effect of countering water-based techniques!

Under normal circumstances, with similar cultivation levels, a water-element cultivator had a natural advantage over a fire-element cultivator in battle.

However, the Crystal Flame was an anomaly. It not only resisted water techniques but could also envelop its wielder, allowing them to move freely underwater as if they were an aquatic creature—like a personal Water Evasion Technique.

"This is great." Lin Jing thought to himself. His little carp had never properly swum in water since birth. He even started to worry whether it could truly dive into the sea when it eventually transformed into a dragon…

If it refined the Crystal Flame, that problem should be solved.

However…

"Senior, at the moment, I may not have enough Gluttonous Pills to exchange for this Crystal Flame. If Senior doesn’t mind, could I have some time to prepare more?"

Though Lin Jing wanted to make the trade immediately, he had already depleted his stock of Gluttonous Pills after encountering the distinguished guests Xia Yan and Jie Geng.

"No problem." Ling Quan Zun-zhe casually tossed the Crystal Flame to Lin Jing and turned to leave.

"Once you’ve refined more, deliver the pills to Qingming Pavilion. I’ll be waiting for you there."

…

After Ling Quan Zun-zhe left, Lin Jing let out a long breath, his tense nerves finally relaxing. Thankfully, the elder hadn’t seen through his disguise…

He casually tossed the Crystal Flame into his Meditation Staff. The Fire Qilin Dragon immediately refined it and even let out a satisfied belch. Seeing this, Lin Jing sensed that if it absorbed one more high-level exotic flame, it would likely reach the peak of the Foundation Establishment stage.

"Come out. Refining two exotic flames in succession—if you rely solely on alchemy, you won’t be able to stabilize your power. That will hinder your upcoming Core Formation breakthrough."

"From now on, you’ll train your strength through combat. We’re going to refine a False Core soon, and we’re short on one key ingredient—Monster Cores. Monster Cores are expensive, so we’ll just hunt them ourselves."

"I’m putting you in charge of this mission. Outside Tianqing City, in the Sunset Mountains, there’s a popular hunting ground for rogue cultivators. Prioritize fire-element monsters. If you run into danger, activate this Fire Dragon Pearl—it contains my imprinted Nascent Soul peak-level Fire Dragon Phantom Power…"

Thus began the little carp’s grand adventure. Compared to alchemy, it seemed far more interested in hunting for Monster Cores.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 27: Meeting an Old Friend in a Distant Land
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 27: Meeting an Old Friend in a Distant Land

The Sunset Mountains, a gathering place for demonic beasts within the Qingming Path, were home to countless demons at the Monster Core stage…

Although the cultivation families of Tianqing City possessed enough power to wipe out the entire Sunset Mountain range, such a short-sighted approach would be detrimental to long-term development.

By allowing the Sunset Mountains to remain, rogue cultivators seeking resources would continue to hunt demonic beasts, and in turn, many rogue cultivators would fall prey to them. This cycle of hunting and survival would foster growth on both sides, allowing talented rogue cultivators and powerful demonic beasts to undergo transformations, potentially reaching the Nascent Soul stage.

At that point, whether recruiting a promising rogue cultivator or taming a Demon King as a mount, both would be far more valuable than a handful of Monster Cores. Furthermore, the demonic beasts would continue to reproduce, ensuring that the Sunset Mountains remained a sustainable hunting ground for thousands of years to come…

In this rogue cultivator’s paradise, a group of four Core Formation cultivators engaged in conversation, discussing the strange occurrences that had recently taken place in the Sunset Mountains.

"A Royal Dragon Cub?!"

"That’s right. A few days ago, the Emei Monkey Clan was wiped out. All three of their Monster Core-stage great demons had their cores extracted. The battle alarmed many rogue cultivators in the mountains, and someone spotted a Foundation Establishment Fire Dragon flying away from the Emei Monkey Clan’s former dwelling…"

"The Emei Monkeys are naturally violent. For a Foundation Establishment Fire Dragon to destroy them, there’s only one explanation—its bloodline is extremely high-grade, allowing it to battle across realms! Calling it a Royal Dragon Cub might even be an understatement. At the very least, it’s a Demon Emperor’s Cub!" The thick-browed man leading the group spoke.

"Big chest, no brains! That Fire Dragon annihilated the entire Emei Monkey Clan, and you think this makeshift rogue cultivator team of ours has a chance? We’d be nothing but an extra snack!" scoffed a short old man.

"Old man, if you can’t say anything useful, just stay quiet! Who said we’d be doing this alone? I refuse to believe other rogue cultivator teams aren’t interested in that Demon Emperor’s Cub…"

"Shut up! Do you really think this Fire Dragon is like the common demonic beasts of the Sunset Mountains, just waiting to be hunted? You just admitted it—it’s a Demon Emperor’s Cub! Meaning its parents are most likely powerful Demon Emperors of the Dragon Clan! Even if every rogue cultivator worked together to hunt it down, once its parents find out, forget rogue cultivators—even the entire cultivation families of Tianqing City couldn’t bear the consequences!"

The weak-looking Core Formation youth among them finally spoke. "I didn’t join your group to throw my life away. Let’s focus on our actual objective…"

"Our goal is the Morning Sun Flower."

The Morning Sun Flower was a rare spiritual plant guarded by Fire Bees, known for nourishing Yang Qi and carrying a hefty price tag.

"You look so weak; you do need some nourishment." The woman glanced at the frail young man, curling her lips but ultimately agreeing with his reasoning.

Indeed, a beast with such a noble bloodline was unlikely to be a native of the Sunset Mountains. It was far more likely to be the pampered prince of some powerful demonic beast faction out for a leisurely adventure.

Even major human factions wouldn’t dare provoke such a being without first verifying its origins.

Moving cautiously, the four weaved their way through the Sunset Mountains and finally arrived at the Fire Bee Clan’s habitat. However, upon arrival, they fell into silence.

Had they arrived too late? Or too early?

"ROAR!!!"

A Fire Dragon, dozens of meters long, was being swarmed by tens of thousands of Fire Bees. Among them, two were at the Monster Core stage—their Queen and King. Yet, despite the endless fire needles pelting its dragon scales, not a single mark was left.

Even when the Queen, a great demon, unleashed a divine ability from her Monster Core, managing to pierce through the Fire Dragon’s scales, the dragon’s body simply erupted in white, dragon-shaped flames, rapidly healing its wounds.

"This…"

The four Core Formation cultivators’ pupils shrank. Just moments ago, they had been discussing this Fire Dragon—and now, it was standing right in front of them!

To make matters worse, after swallowing both the Monster Core-stage Queen and King in just two bites, scattering the remaining bees in a panic, the Fire Dragon slowly turned its gaze towards them…

"ROAR!!!"

As the Fire Dragon let out a deafening roar, the seven Core Formation cultivators felt as though they were facing their greatest adversary.

"Scatter in seven directions!" The short old man barely finished speaking before the seven of them burst into streaks of light, abandoning their formation and their original target—the Morning Sun Flower.

"Damn it!" The sickly-looking young man cursed. But his frustration had only just begun—because while the dragon had let the others go, it was fixated on him, relentlessly chasing after him!

"F***!" The young man’s already pale face turned even whiter.

"Could it be a female dragon? Is it sensing your Primordial Yang Body? You were supposed to be hiding it well!"

The Fire Dragon’s speed was terrifying. Even though it was only at the peak of the Foundation Establishment stage, it was chasing down a Core Formation early-stage cultivator effortlessly. In mere moments, it had already cut off the young man’s escape route. His entire body tensed as he hovered in midair, cautiously observing the dragon.

"Red!" At his command, a fox demon, also at the Core Formation stage, slowly emerged onto his shoulder, baring its teeth at the Fire Dragon.

At the same time, the young man spoke. "Honored one, we have no quarrel with you. We simply stumbled upon your battle with the Fire Bees by accident. There’s no need to slaughter us all, is there…?"

"Roar…" The Fire Qilin Dragon stared at the young man, hesitating for a moment.

Truthfully, among the four rogue cultivators earlier, it had no intention of chasing anyone down.

But this young man… looked far too familiar.

It remembered—back when it was still a mere fish, this young man often visited its Beast Tamer to chat. What was his name again? Senior Brother Ouyang…

And now, seeing him use beast-taming techniques to summon a fox demon, the Fire Qilin Dragon was absolutely certain!

This was Lin Jing’s legendary human-fox romance partner—Ouyang!!!

【If you encounter an emergency while hunting outside, you can use the Bloodline Contract to call me with all your strength. The distance between the Sunset Mountains and Tianqing City should still allow us to communicate…】

Right now—this was undoubtedly an emergency. The Fire Qilin Dragon immediately reached out to Lin Jing through the Bloodline Contract.

Meanwhile, Ouyang Hao, still facing down the Imperial-Blood Fire Dragon that refused to yield, felt cold sweat trickle down his forehead.

He never imagined there would come a day when a mere Foundation Establishment-stage demonic beast would have him frozen in place with fear.

"Are you truly unwilling to let me pass?" Ouyang Hao pulled out a jade token, preparing to crush it—when suddenly, his pupils shrank in shock.

Because at that moment, the Fire Dragon spoke to him through voice transmission—uttering a sentence that left him utterly stunned.

"Senior Brother Ouyang, long time no see. I never expected to run into you here. This Fire Dragon is my pet, so there's no need to be afraid…"

O-Ouyang… Senior Brother?

Ouyang Hao stared blankly at the Fire Dragon.

His mind raced back to a certain Beast Tamer’s cave residence… and the tiny, boiled Fire Carp that used to glare at him in jealousy.

His mouth opened slightly, and he transmitted a message back:

"Junior Brother Lin Jing… is that you?! Is it really you?!"

Ouyang Hao was completely dumbfounded.

The Qingzhou branch of the Beast Taming Sect had spent two years searching every corner of Qingzhou for Lin Jing without finding a single trace.

Yet somehow—while casually passing through Tianqing City, gathering some resources to nourish his Yang Qi, he just happened to run into Lin Jing?!

"F***! Wait—hold on! Why is your Dragon Carp so damn powerful?! My Red drinks my Yang Qi every day and even got a gift from Senior Sister Tian, and it’s still only at the King-tier bloodline!"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 28: The Plan to Transform Demons
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 28: The Plan to Transform Demons

Tianqing City, Commercial Street, Nameless Pill Pavilion.

After encountering the Fire Qilin Dragon, Ouyang Hao was invited here.

Walking through the entrance, he looked at the unfamiliar young man who had been waiting for him and probed, “A sliver of hope?”

“A kind-hearted female cultivator.” Lin Jing replied.

“The code is correct, it’s you!” Ouyang Hao’s eyes welled up with tears.

Lin Jing smiled slightly. “Senior Brother, please.”

Ouyang Hao took a deep breath. “So you really are in Qingzhou. I heard the academy was ambushed by the Yin Household Sect, and the students scattered everywhere. Not long after that, your wanted notice appeared in Qingzhou—it was the work of the Yin Household Sect. Because of that, most people in the sect assumed you had fled here.”

“We’ve been searching for you all over, and some unexpected things happened along the way. I never thought I’d run into you here!”

“You already know?” Ouyang Hao exhaled sharply. “That’s right. This sect, to claim the Yin Household Sect’s bounty, captured Beast Taming Sect cultivators and tortured them for information about you. But thankfully, our Senior Sister mixed her way into becoming the Holy Maiden of the Demon Court. She ordered her protector to take action and wiped out the Luosha Sect!”

“After that battle, no one dared to make a move against the Beast Taming Sect anymore. Have you noticed? Even the number of cultivators hunting you has decreased!”

Lin Jing pondered. He hadn’t noticed at all—thanks to his illusion techniques, he had yet to encounter anyone searching for him.

“Junior Brother, come back with me. At least with Senior Sister around, you’ll be safer.” Ouyang Hao suggested.

“No rush.” Lin Jing shook his head.

“How much do you know about the Demon Court? If they discover my physique, do you think falling into the hands of the Yin Household Sect would be any different from falling into the hands of the Demon Court?”

Ouyang Hao froze. “This... that shouldn’t happen, right? Senior Sister holds a pretty high position in the Demon Court, and she’s a good person...”

“Better not take the gamble.” Lin Jing interjected. “Besides, I still have other things to do.”

“What about you, Senior Brother Ouyang? What brings you near Tianqing City?”

“The Demon Court’s base and the Beast Taming Sect’s foothold shouldn’t be anywhere near Qingming Path, should they?”

Ouyang Hao sighed. “One reason is to find you. Right now, most of the Beast Taming Sect’s cultivators have scattered to search for any trace of you.”

“The second reason is that I’m handling something for Senior Sister.”

“Handling something for Senior Sister?” Lin Jing stroked his chin. “What kind of task?”

“Recruiting... alchemic retainers...” Ouyang Hao glanced at the room’s furnishings, his expression turning strange.

“Recruiting alchemic retainers?” Lin Jing was surprised. “For what?”

Ouyang Hao explained, “Since you know about the Demon Court, you should also know its purpose—to restore the demon race to the world.”

“Within the Demon Court, three Holy Maidens with half-demon bloodlines are the primary executors of this goal.”

“They have access to vast Demon Court resources to implement their plans, all aimed at reviving the demon race.”

Each of the three Holy Maidens has a different approach to reviving the demon race. The First Holy Maiden believes in reproduction. Although half-demons cannot reproduce with demonic beasts, they still have a small chance of producing demon-blooded offspring with humans...

“She hopes that by birthing more and more children with demonic bloodlines, she can unravel the mysteries of the demon race’s bloodline.”

“That’s why the Demon Court’s Demon Kings and Demon Emperors have captured many talented human geniuses for her—to breed demon-blooded offspring.” Ouyang Hao shuddered. “Thankfully, Senior Sister is protecting me, or I’d have been harvested too.”

“The Second Holy Maiden, on the other hand, is much more reasonable. She’s a genius in her own right and advocates reviving the demon race through innovative cultivation techniques. Over the years, she has created more than ten cultivation methods that allow demonic beasts to cultivate.”

“Although she has yet to develop a technique that enables demonic beasts to take human form, her supporters and followers are the most numerous among the three Holy Maidens.”

“As for our Senior Sister, she is the Third Holy Maiden of the Demon Court.”

Lin Jing, understanding now, asked, “So Senior Sister’s plan is to revive the demon race through alchemy?”

Ouyang Hao nodded. “It’s said that in ancient times, there were only two ways for a demonic beast to take human form and become a member of the demon race.”

“One was through cultivation—changing their bloodline to take on human form.”

“The other was consuming a third-tier spiritual plant called Shapeshifting Grass, also known as Humanizing Grass or Demonizing Grass.”

“However, due to changes in the world’s rules, demonic beasts can no longer cultivate to take human form, and Shapeshifting Grass has lost its original effect. When eaten, it has no reaction at all.”

“So, Senior Sister’s strategy is this—if Shapeshifting Grass can no longer directly help demonic beasts transform, then what if it’s refined into a pill? If turned into a Shapeshifting Pill, could its effects be reactivated?”

“To this end, she gathered many alchemists, provided her own half-demon blood, and an immense amount of Shapeshifting Grass for research into the Shapeshifting Pill. Unfortunately, the Demon Court, being primarily a demonic beast faction, has too few alchemists. The ones they do have aren’t skilled enough to develop the pill, and so far, there’s been little progress.”

“But finding alchemists of the caliber needed—those with both talent and potential, like the ones in Tongtian Tower—is nearly impossible. That’s why the Beast Taming Sect has scattered everywhere. Aside from searching for you, their other goal is to find young alchemists with talent and potential, bring them back to the Demon Court, and have them join the research into the Shapeshifting Pill...”

“As human cultivators, Beast Taming Sect disciples can move through human cities far more easily than demonic beasts.”

After Ouyang Hao finished, Lin Jing nodded. “No wonder...”

“Senior Brother, you still want Hong’er to take human form, don’t you?” He asked the person who had once saved him from the hands of the Demonic Sect.

“Of course!” Ouyang Hao said, puzzled. “Why are you asking this?”

After some thought, Lin Jing said, “Let me help you. I happen to have some skill in alchemy, and since Senior Sister is recruiting alchemists, well, I’m one.”

“But I won’t be going to the Demon Court with you.”

“Can you secretly bring me Senior Sister’s half-demon blood and some Shapeshifting Grass?”

“Huh??” Ouyang Hao was stunned.

“You?”

Lin Jing rolled his eyes. “If you’ve done a thorough investigation on me, you should know that back in the Ancient Kingdom, I refined top-tier pills like the Rising Dragon Pill.”

“Just tell them you found a genius alchemist willing to research the Shapeshifting Pill, but he refuses to go to the Demon Court. As long as you don’t expose my identity to the Demon Court’s higher-ups, it should be fine, right? With your status as the sect leader’s disciple and Senior Sister being the Demon Court’s Holy Maiden, this should be a small matter for you.”

“That makes sense, but just saying it isn’t enough proof, I...”

Lin Jing interrupted, “It won’t just be words—I’ll give you a few pills to bring back, proof of my alchemic skill.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 29: A Secret Collaboration
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 29: A Secret Collaboration

Shapeshifting Pill!

The moment Lin Jing heard about this pill’s concept, he felt an urge to give it a try.

He recalled that Shapeshifting Grass had nearly disappeared from the continent. Now, it seemed that it wasn’t extinct but monopolized by the Demon Court, where vast quantities still existed.

The Demon Court’s alchemists might be unable to refine the Shapeshifting Pill, but that didn’t mean he couldn’t.

Of course, Lin Jing wasn’t completely certain, but it was worth a gamble.

After all, Shapeshifting Grass was a third-tier spiritual plant. As long as the Pine Leaf Mouse advanced to the Monster Core Stage, its Glazed Immortal Eyes would gain the ability to synthesize third-tier plants!

At that point, as a third-tier spiritual plant, Shapeshifting Grass would naturally become something the Glazed Immortal Eyes could synthesize!

Since Glazed Immortal Eyes was an inheritance from an immortal, even if the rules of the Tianyuan Continent had been altered by the Immortal Palace, there was a chance it could bypass these restrictions and restore Shapeshifting Grass’s original effects.

And if he could truly modify raw materials with the Glazed Immortal Eyes to refine a Shapeshifting Pill...

“If it really works, the Shapeshifting Pill could change the entire structure of the continent. But even if I succeed, it would be impossible to present it at an alchemy conference.”

“The demon race—once the only race that rivaled humans in ancient times. If they were to be revived, the impact would be enormous. Even though Tongtian Tower has demon beast disciples, they would never allow the demon race to reemerge.”

“But still, this Shapeshifting Pill is something I must attempt.”

“Not only can I help Senior Brother Ouyang fulfill his wish, but more importantly...”

“If I master the method to refine the Shapeshifting Pill, it means tying my fate to the Demon Court.”

“At that point, the Demon Court will be the last faction that would want me dead. If I ever find myself in danger, the Ancient Kingdom might not lift a finger to help me, but the Demon Court would risk everything to save me.”

“With that in mind, there’s nothing more to consider. The power dynamics of the continent are beyond a mere Foundation Establishment cultivator like me. My own survival is the most important thing! This Shapeshifting Pill is a lifesaving pill—one that could compel the entire Demon Court to fight on my behalf when necessary.”

At this moment, Lin Jing felt as if he had discovered another way, beyond his Heavenly Demon Physique, to counter the schemes of the Demonic Sect.

Leverage.

By using the Shapeshifting Pill, he could bind the Demon Court’s interests to his own.

But, this was only viable if his theory actually worked.

“What kind of pill?”

Hearing that Lin Jing intended to prove his alchemy skills, Ouyang Hao had already scanned the entire Nameless Pill Pavilion. Most of the pills here were common, except for one special pill—the Gluttonous Pill.

“This one?”

“This is one of them, but not the most important one.” Lin Jing said, “There’s another pill I haven’t finished refining yet, but given a few more days, it should be ready.”

“That pill is perfect for you. It might even significantly boost your strength, making your return more convincing.”

“What kind of pill?” Ouyang Hao’s curiosity was piqued. “A pill that nourishes Yang energy?”

Lin Jing smirked. “Much stronger than that.”

“A pill that makes your entire body burn like the surface of the sun—Yang enough for you?”

Ouyang Hao: ???

“It’s a False Core Technique—a technique that creates a pseudo-core filled with the heat of the sun. You possess the Primordial Yang Body, meaning your inner Yang fire is already incredibly strong. If you combine it with this Solar False Core Technique, your strength will skyrocket.”

“False Core?!” Ouyang Hao’s eyes lit up with excitement. “Ever since I awakened my Golden Core Divine Ability, I’ve been reluctant to use it. Pills that restore a shattered Golden Core are too expensive—I can’t afford them at all. What’s the power level of this False Core?”

Lin Jing shrugged. “Not sure. We’ll find out after I refine it. But it definitely won’t be weak. By the way, what is your Golden Core Divine Ability? Can you share?”

Ouyang Hao waved his hand. “You saved my life. What’s there to hide? My Golden Core Divine Ability is Yang Qi Dragon—I can condense an extremely powerful Yang-element dragon using my Yang energy and Golden Core mana.”

“When I saw that Fire Dragon of yours earlier, I almost unleashed my Golden Core Divine Ability against it.”

“But now that I think about it, even if I shattered my Golden Core in the process, I might not have won...”

“As for Hong’er’s Monster Core Divine Ability, it’s Nine Tails—each additional tail enhances her mana in all aspects. But the more tails she grows, the greater the burden on her Golden Core.”

Lin Jing nodded. “I see. That suits your situation well. I wonder what my Divine Ability will be...”

Ouyang Hao grinned. “It’ll probably be something similar. Actually, this Yang Qi Dragon isn’t just for combat—it can also be fed to others to nourish their Yang energy. What if your Divine Ability turns out to be a Longevity Turtle?”

Golden Core Divine Abilities were determined by a cultivator’s techniques, physique, and spells.

Sometimes they were closely tied to cultivation methods.

Other times, they were directly linked to physique.

Ouyang Hao cultivated the Beast Taming Art, possessed the Primordial Yang Body, and awakened Yang Qi Dragon, which fit him perfectly.

This Yang Qi Dragon wasn’t just a manifestation of his Primordial Yang Body—it could also be used as a pet beast.

If Lin Jing’s was similarly linked to his Longevity Body...

Lin Jing shook his head, hoping that wouldn’t be the case.

The two continued chatting about the events that had transpired since the Beast Taming Sect arrived in Qingzhou. At the same time, Lin Jing summoned the Fire Qilin Dragon, which had successfully hunted down enough fire-element Monster Cores to attempt refining the Solar False Core.

A few days later.

Following Lin Jing’s carefully derived pill formulas, the Fire Qilin Dragon finally refined two Solar False Cores.

“It’s done.”

Leaving the pill chamber, Lin Jing went to find Ouyang Hao, who was temporarily staying in Tianqing City, intending to test the Solar False Core’s effects together.

“This is it?”

Ouyang Hao caught the bottle Lin Jing tossed him and examined the Solar False Cores inside. “How do I take it?”

“Just consume it normally.” Lin Jing replied. “But first, we should test it on a trial subject. If it doesn’t have any side effects, then you can take it. Since this is a newly developed pill, there shouldn’t be any major issues in theory, but it’s always best to be safe.”

Ouyang Hao: ...Why does this sound so unreliable?

Alchemists usually have dedicated trial subjects, whether humans or spirit beasts. But this guy was just planning to find one on the spot—how was that professional at all?

The two of them turned into streaks of light and flew toward the Sunset Mountains to find a suitable lab rat.

After flying for a while, they stopped and spotted a wild boar demon at the Great Perfection of Foundation Establishment gnawing on a bone below them.

Either the boar had absolutely no intelligence or it had no idea how it even reached this cultivation level—despite sensing the arrival of two cultivators, it remained completely indifferent, continuing to chew on its bone.

Seeing this, Ouyang Hao unleashed the full pressure of his Golden Core Realm, glaring at the boar.

“You’re still eating? I’m here to grant you an opportunity for enlightenment!”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 30: High-Temperature Technique
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 30: High-Temperature Technique

“Snort, snort...”

The Wild Boar Demon was fully focused on gnawing at its bone, but the moment Ouyang Hao released his imposing presence, it immediately went on high alert, guarding its food like a cornered beast.

“!!!”

In the next instant, invisible threads bound its entire body, causing it to lose balance and crash onto the ground, rolling around in struggle.

Spiritual Thread Binding!

Lin Jing moved his left hand gracefully, and with a flick of his right hand, he shot a dark red pill into the Wild Boar Demon’s mouth.

A few more Gluttonous Pills followed right after.

Condensing a False Core was significantly easier than forming a Golden Core, but it still wasn't something that could be accomplished in a short time.

“Don’t struggle, focus on absorbing the medicine.”

As the pills entered its body, Lin Jing spoke calmly.

The Wild Boar Demon’s pupils shrank instantly, its eyes filling with fear—this was a fear a hundred times greater than what it had felt under the might of a Golden Core cultivator.

Under the pressure of Lin Jing’s Beast Taming Physique, the Wild Boar Demon obediently lay still, focusing on absorbing the medicinal power. However, Lin Jing did not release the spiritual threads binding it.

Next, he would use these spiritual threads to sense the changes happening within the Wild Boar Demon.

“How long will it take?” Ouyang Hao asked anxiously.

Lin Jing, who was currently performing Threaded Pulse Diagnosis, replied, “Judging by the current absorption rate, about an hour.”

“Alright.” Ouyang Hao nodded heavily, observing the changes in the Wild Boar Demon. After all, he would be taking the Solar False Core Pill soon as well.

In the blink of an eye, an hour passed.

The originally earth-brown, earth-element Wild Boar Demon had turned completely red, resembling a roasted pig.

However, despite the high temperatures from the Mutated Sun Herb, it suffered no adverse effects. Flames flickered inside its body, and within, a fiery red False Core floated like a newly formed organ.

Under the effects of this False Core, the Wild Boar Demon seemed to have gained incredible heat resistance...

It snorted softly, its body temperature rising even higher. As time passed, the air around it began to distort, nearby plants withered and caught fire, and an astonishing heatwave radiated from its body, making Ouyang Hao’s eyes light up.

Buzz!

The Wild Boar Demon’s temperature kept climbing. Even the ground and rocks within its vicinity started to melt, flowing like lava—yet this seemed to be just the beginning.

“Don’t let the energy leak out naturally. Try to control the heat using the False Core forming inside you.”

Following Lin Jing’s instructions, the Wild Boar Demon hesitated briefly before slowly opening its mouth.

Then—

Whoosh!

A scorching breath blasted toward a nearby mountain.

With a roar, the entire mountain began melting like an erupting volcano. The sheer destructive power left Ouyang Hao stunned.

“Its current cultivation level is somewhere between Foundation Establishment and Golden Core, right? But just from that one Boar’s Breath, it already surpasses most Golden Core cultivators’ techniques!”

“Yeah.”

Lin Jing exhaled in relief.

This Wild Boar Demon wasn’t originally a fire-element creature and had only been at the peak of Foundation Establishment before taking the False Core Pill.

If a fire-element cultivator or someone with a fire-attribute physique were to take the Solar False Core Pill, their power would be even greater.

It looked like they had succeeded.

“Senior Brother, it’s your turn.” Lin Jing turned to Ouyang Hao.

“Ah...”

Ouyang Hao still looked uneasy, but after witnessing the power of the Solar False Core Pill, he ultimately decided to go for it.

He opened his mouth, and Lin Jing casually tossed the prepared False Core Pill in.

To save time, Lin Jing also gave him a few Gluttonous Pills.

Thump.

Thump.

As soon as he swallowed the pills, Ouyang Hao’s heartbeat sped up dramatically, loud enough for both Lin Jing and the Wild Boar Demon to hear.

His body quickly turned red, absorbing the pill’s power at a much faster rate than the Wild Boar Demon had.

“Hot! Hot! Hot! Hot! Hot!”

He shouted repeatedly, and within him, next to his Golden Core, a red False Core gradually took shape, orbiting his Golden Core at high speed.

As the False Core formed, his condition stabilized somewhat, but his mind remained hazy, as if he were being burned into a stupor.

This phenomenon had also occurred with the Wild Boar Demon earlier, so neither of them were particularly alarmed.

As time passed, the False Core solidified inside Ouyang Hao.

Unlike the clumsy Wild Boar Demon, as a Golden Core cultivator, Ouyang Hao quickly displayed far superior control over the False Core technique.

"High-Temperature Technique!"

He slowly raised his hand.

A swirling, turbulent heat sphere materialized in his palm, its chaotic spirals resembling a storm.

Seeing this, Ouyang Hao pulled out a treasured armor and pressed the heat sphere against it—

With a single tap, the armor instantly melted.

Then, with a casual flick of his wrist, he unleashed a continuous wave of heat. Everywhere his hand passed, the ground split open, leaving behind deep fissures filled with flowing lava.

“Strong...”

Ouyang Hao was deeply shaken.

A single pill had granted him such a terrifying High-Temperature Technique—he now had absolute faith in Lin Jing’s alchemical skills.

Clap, clap, clap.

“Congratulations, Senior Brother, on mastering the High-Temperature False Core Technique.”

Lin Jing smiled and clapped in acknowledgment.

“Don’t congratulate me just yet—I think my Primordial Yang Body’s power can merge with this high-temperature energy to form a combination technique!”

Ouyang Hao’s words caught Lin Jing by surprise.

With a low whoosh, Ouyang Hao’s Yang Energy fused with the High-Temperature Energy, creating a radiant orange flame in his palm.

This flame burned with intense Yang Qi, and Lin Jing instantly recognized it.

“Yangflame! A fire infused with pure Yang attributes, the natural bane of all yin-based and cold energies.”

Lin Jing was astonished.

“So this High-Temperature Technique triggered a mutation in your Primordial Yang Body, resulting in the birth of Yangflame?”

Ouyang Hao burst into laughter.

“Looks like it!”

“Junior Brother, would this be of any use to you? I recall your Alchemy Pavilion has been gathering Exotic Flames, hasn’t it?”

Lin Jing shook his head with a smile.

“No, the essence of Yangflame comes from your own body. It’s not an independent flame seed that others can refine.”

“However, you could leave behind some Sub-Flames for me.”

Rare beasts couldn’t be judged by conventional logic.

Since Ouyang Hao had formed Yangflame through the Solar False Core Pill, there was a chance that the Fire Qilin Dragon could replicate the process and condense its own Yangflame.

If that worked, then all the Exotic Flames it needed for its Golden Core formation would be complete.

“Alright!!!”

Ouyang Hao was now thrilled.

Forget about competing with the core disciples of the Beast Taming Sect—he felt that even the sect elders wouldn’t be able to defeat him now!

He couldn’t wait to return to the Beast Taming Sect and show off his newfound strength.

“Oh, by the way, Junior Brother, you’re not keeping this boar, are you?”

After celebrating, Ouyang Hao turned his gaze toward the Wild Boar Demon, which had also successfully condensed a False Core.

The moment the Wild Boar Demon felt his gaze, it took a step back.

“If you don’t want it, I’ll take it back with me. With some proper training, it could become a powerful Golden Core-level combat beast...”

“Do as you please.” Lin Jing shrugged.

—

Over a month later.

Beyond the thirteen regions of Qingzhou, in a non-human territory—

A young man, riding atop a Wild Boar Demon propelled by jets of searing heat, landed gracefully in front of a grand palace.

“Ouyang Hao, disciple of the Beast Taming Sect Master, requests an audience with the Holy Maiden regarding an urgent matter.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 31: Xu Qingyao
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 31: Xu Qingyao

This vast palace, spanning hundreds of miles deep in the mountains, was the dwelling of the Third Holy Maiden of the Demon Court, Xu Qingyao.

At the massive entrance, two giant armored ape demons stood guard, each wielding heavy weapons. Their cultivation had reached the level of Demon King.

“Yang Qi brat!”

Upon seeing Ouyang Hao, one of the armored giant apes snorted, “Go on in.”

“Thank you, seniors.” Ouyang Hao bowed respectfully, still filled with awe.

No wonder she’s the eldest senior sister.

Even a Demon King—who held the status of an ancestral elder in the Beast Taming Sect—was merely a gatekeeper here!

As Ouyang Hao rode his heat-propelled Wild Boar Demon inside, the two giant apes exchanged glances, muttering to themselves.

It was nearly impossible for most people to even get an audience with the Holy Maiden of the Demon Court. Yet, she had granted these Beast Taming Sect cultivators the privilege of visiting her freely.

“By the way, wasn’t that Yang Qi brat always accompanied by a fox demon?”

“When did he start riding a pig?”

—

“Ouyang Hao?”

The fragrance of aromatic herbs drifted through the air long before Ouyang Hao even stepped into the palace.

The moment he entered, he wasn’t surprised to find his unruly eldest senior sister seated cross-legged in the center of the grand hall—hotpot in hand.

Inside the bubbling cauldron were precious blood-replenishing medicinal herbs, along with the blood and organs of various demon beasts.

“Want some?” Xu Qingyao asked casually.

“I’ll pass. I’m not short on blood.” Ouyang Hao replied awkwardly.

Due to his Half-Demon Blood being frequently used for alchemy, Senior Sister Guan was constantly low on blood.

“True, you lack Yang Qi, not blood. After all, you’ve got a fox demon back home.”

Xu Qingyao smirked, glancing at the Wild Boar Demon by the door.

“So, that pig’s not for me to eat? It’s your own food?”

“No, no, Senior Sister, let me explain…” Ouyang Hao forced a bitter smile.

—

Xu Qingyao, adopted daughter of the Beast Taming Sect Master, eldest senior sister, Third Holy Maiden of the Demon Court...

As Ouyang Hao had once told Lin Jing, although she had left the Beast Taming Sect before even reaching adulthood, she still cared deeply for it.

In fact, she had agreed to become the Demon Court’s Holy Maiden precisely because it gave her a better chance to find a way for demon beasts to take human form.

Her motivation?

Her adoptive father was human, her adoptive mother was a crane.

She wanted them to truly be together.

As for Ouyang Hao, who had a special relationship with a fox demon, Xu Qingyao took a particular interest in him.

At one point, she had even suggested that their father groom Ouyang Hao as the next Sect Master.

But then, word came from the Ancient Celestial Kingdom—some Demon King elders had taken advantage of the Sect Master’s absence to appoint a Holy Son of their own.

Of course, Ouyang Hao held no hostility toward this Holy Son. The two had even saved each other’s lives before, so who became Sect Master didn’t really matter.

“I found an incredibly skilled alchemist.”

“He’s willing to join the research on the Transformation Pill!”

“Which rank is he?” Xu Qingyao set down her chopsticks and asked.

“He can currently refine Fourth-Tier Pills.”

“That’s a bit low…”

Xu Qingyao frowned in thought.

Although Transformation Grass was only a Third-Rank Herb, the Transformation Pill was far beyond what a Third-Tier Alchemist could refine.

Even her own blood required at least a Fourth-Tier Alchemist to process.

The alchemist Ouyang Hao found had barely met the minimum standard.

“You came rushing back in such a hurry—he must have something exceptional about him, right?” Xu Qingyao asked.

“Yes! He gave me two pills to bring back—both of which he personally refined.”

“One is the Gluttonous Pill, and the other is the Solar False Core Pill, which helps cultivators and demon beasts condense False Cores!”

Ouyang Hao quickly retrieved the pills.

As a Half-Demon Holy Maiden, Xu Qingyao was deeply invested in using alchemy to achieve demon transformation.

Naturally, she was an accomplished alchemist herself.

Her eyes gleamed as she studied the Gluttonous Pill and Solar False Core Pill floating before her.

“You and that pig both took the Solar False Core Pill, right?”

“Yes.”

“Then use your False Core Technique to attack me.”

“Attack with full force—no need to hold back.”

“Understood!”

Ouyang Hao had no hesitation.

His eldest senior sister had long reached the Nascent Soul Stage.

No matter how powerful the Solar False Core Technique was, there was no way a Golden Core cultivator could harm a Nascent Soul cultivator.

Qi Refinement, Foundation Establishment, and Golden Core were all part of the same realm.

Nascent Soul, however, was a complete transformation.

Not only could Nascent Soul cultivators freely unleash Golden Core Divine Abilities, but they also possessed Nascent Soul Manifestations and Soul Possession techniques—true immortal techniques.

—

Moments later, Ouyang Hao—engulfed in searing Yangflame—was pinned to the ground by a massive beastly palm.

Above him, the demonic woman attached to that palm gazed at him with an intriguing glint in her eyes.

Lifting her hand, she observed the smoking ground beneath him and spoke softly.

“Alright. I acknowledge this alchemist’s abilities.”

“This False Core Technique is enough for you to fight Golden Core cultivators—even late-stage Golden Core cultivators—without relying on Beast Taming abilities.”

“The only downside is that using the technique gradually wears down the False Core. But for temporary use, it’s a top-tier battle art.”

“Not just the Solar False Core Pill, but this Gluttonous Pill as well—both are groundbreaking. Anyone capable of creating such pills is more than qualified to join the research on the Transformation Pill.”

Xu Qingyao nodded.

“So, why didn’t you bring this alchemist with you? Did he have some conditions?”

Ouyang Hao crawled out from under the beastly palm, looking miserable.

“He… doesn’t want to join the Demon Court.”

“He doesn’t want to be exposed to too many of the Demon Court’s powerful figures.”

“He’s willing to assist from his own territory—but only if we deliver your demon blood and the Transformation Grass to him and leave him alone.”

Xu Qingyao: ???

“…Does he think I’m a damn fool?”

“Senior Sister, don’t get mad!” Ouyang Hao quickly tried to smooth things over.

He recalled Lin Jing’s specific request: Don’t reveal his identity to any Demon Court powerhouses.

However, if Ouyang Hao personally trusted Xu Qingyao, then telling her was fine.

After all, Lin Jing knew asking for someone’s blood outright was extremely rude.

Many Gu Masters, sorcerers, and dark cultivators could cast curses from thousands of miles away using nothing but a drop of blood.

Thus, cultivators were extremely cautious about their own body materials.

For this reason, all alchemists studying the Transformation Pill were closely monitored by Xu Qingyao and the Demon Court.

The Demon Court couldn’t afford for their Half-Demon Holy Maiden to be assassinated.

“He’s a Longevity Body cultivator.”

“He’s worried that if he enters the Demon Court, you won’t be able to protect him.”

Ouyang Hao sent a private transmission.

“Longevity Body...?”

Xu Qingyao’s expression shifted instantly.

She looked at Ouyang Hao, realization dawning upon her.

This alchemist…

He was the Holy Son of the Beast Taming Sect from the Ancient Celestial Kingdom—the Longevity Body Lin Jing—the man who had saved Ouyang Hao’s life!

“So you finally found him…”

A smile slowly formed on Xu Qingyao’s lips.

“Fine. No problem.”

“If he’s one of us, then I’ll let him hold onto my blood for now.”

“But… I’ll deliver it myself.”

—

At that moment, Ouyang Hao had no idea that shortly after he left Tianqing City, Lin Jing had encountered an unexpected crisis…

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 32: The Choice of Heavenly Treasures
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 32: The Choice of Heavenly Treasures

Using illusion techniques to stay hidden in Tianqing City, and relying on alchemy to gradually amass the resources needed for cultivation, Lin Jing felt that maintaining his current state—without constantly fleeing—was rather peaceful.

However, things never go as planned.

Lin Jing had seemingly forgotten something—something that hadn’t troubled him in a long time.

Something powerful enough to tear him away from his stable life, throwing him into chaos once again.

—

Crowds bustled through the busy streets.

Suddenly, the sky trembled, and a massive light pillar descended from above, crashing heavily into the corner of the market district—

Right at the Nameless Pill Pavilion.

A powerful tremor spread across the city.

—

The Nascent Soul powerhouse, Venerable Ling Quan, abruptly stepped out of his cultivation chamber.

“That direction… the Nameless Pill Pavilion?”

“That young wandering alchemist… just got sucked into a secret realm?”

A flicker of realization flashed across his eyes—

Treasure Vault Secret Realm!

His expression changed dramatically.

“Damn it! That brat still owes me Gluttonous Pills! I was planning to collaborate with his master long-term—he better not die on me!”

—

“For the first time in nearly twenty years, I’ve been pulled into another Cavern Heaven…”

Compared to the chaos outside, Lin Jing himself was feeling a bit dazed.

After sending off Ouyang Hao, he had simply returned to refining pills.

One moment, he was focused on his work.

The next, he was dragged into a Cavern Heaven.

It had happened too suddenly, but what concerned him more was—

What kind of secret realm was this? What were its rules?

Before long, in a brilliantly illuminated grand hall, six figures appeared, standing around a stone tablet in the center.

Engraved beneath the tablet were the words—

“Treasure Vault Secret Realm”

And below it, the rules of the realm were inscribed:

The Vault of Heaven Bestows Supreme Treasures.

Rule 1: Upon entering the Treasure Vault Secret Realm, each participant will receive a tailored artifact from the treasure vault, chosen specifically to match their abilities.

Rule 7: Before seeing the artifact’s details, participants cannot choose to accept or reject it.

Rule 8:

If a participant accepts, they must battle all others who made the same choice.

Only one survivor will obtain the final reward.

Rule 9:

If a participant rejects, they will be isolated in a side chamber, becoming a spectator.

They will return to the outside world once the realm closes, with no risk to their life.

“Are you kidding me? Why does it have to be this secret realm?!”

Among the six, a young man dressed as a boatman clutched his head in frustration.

This was a do-or-die realm—you either killed everyone else for the treasure or walked away empty-handed.

The risk was too high.

The six contestants carefully examined one another.

Lin Jing glanced around—

The boatman youth seemed to specialize in water magic, likely from a coastal region.

A gold-haired youth, dressed in luxurious robes, carried the aura of a lightning cultivator—his background was likely extraordinary.

One of the two women wore a green dress and had a gentle temperament—probably a wood-element cultivator.

The other woman had fiery energy, but lacked alchemy traces—she was more likely an artifact refiner than a fire cultivator.

The last young man—Lin Jing recognized him.

Their eyes met, both equally shocked to see each other here.

—

Everyone understood the brutality of the Treasure Vault Secret Realm.

If they chose to compete, they would have to kill every other contender—or risk being killed themselves.

“Forget it…”

The boatman youth muttered to himself.

He could tell that none of the other five were easy opponents.

However—

The moment the artifacts were revealed—

His breathing changed.

His rationality shattered.

Even Lin Jing felt the urge to fight.

As the ten-second countdown began, a phantom hourglass appeared before Lin Jing—visible only to him.

Information flowed into his mind, and his eyes widened slightly.

[Holder may enter the Hourglass Space for cultivation.]

[Internal time flow: 10:1 (10 years inside = 1 year outside).]

[Unless proficient in both time and space laws, external forces cannot interfere.]

Lin Jing’s breath hitched.

—

A Treasure Designed for a Longevity Body

This artifact had drawbacks—

After ten years inside, the user must spend one year outside before re-entering.

Aging and lifespan within the hourglass corresponded to its internal time flow.

For most cultivators, this was a huge issue.

But for someone with a Longevity Body?

It didn’t matter.

Longevity cultivators struggled with one major problem—

How to convert their vast lifespan into cultivation progress.

This Hourglass of Time directly solved that problem.

More importantly, the hourglass space was an ideal hiding place—even Nascent Soul powerhouses skilled in spatial arts would struggle to break in.

“A treasure perfectly tailored for me.”

Lin Jing’s heart raced.

But—

To obtain it, I’d have to kill every other contender.

Lin Jing glanced around.

The others’ greedy gazes showed that their treasures were likely just as valuable as his.

“If I want this Heavenly Treasure, I must follow the realm’s rules and kill them all.”

“The factions backing these geniuses won’t let this slide.”

“If they’re from outside Qingzhou, it’s fine.”

“But if they’re from Qingzhou, things will get complicated.”

“My fake identity will be exposed again…”

“But if I give up, I’ll lose this space-time artifact forever.”

“I forfeit.”

Just as Lin Jing was still weighing his options, one of the six—a Foundation Establishment Perfection cultivator—chose to withdraw.

He was dressed in the alchemy attendant robes of Tongtian Tower.

It was none other than—

Xia Yan.

Back when Xia Yan bought Gluttonous Pills, it had changed his fate, allowing him to pass Tongtian Tower’s trials.

Now, he gazed at Lin Jing with complicated emotions.

“A single pill from that alchemist changed my destiny.”

“He saw through my father’s secrets at a glance.”

“He’s definitely not just a mid-stage Foundation Establishment cultivator.”

“Even based on gratitude alone, I’m not qualified to fight him.”

Thus—

Even though Xia Yan’s treasure was exceptional, he reluctantly stepped away.

—

Ten seconds left.

Seven people remained.

The artifact refiner youth also chose to withdraw.

Now, only five contenders remained.

—

A gold-haired youth smirked.

“Finally, the real competition starts.”

“Let’s see which of you is brave enough to fight me.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 33: Displaying Their Divine Abilities
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 33: Displaying Their Divine Abilities

“That alchemist?”

The female artifact refiner turned to look at Lin Jing, momentarily stunned.

There was nothing remarkable about him.

If she had to point out something, it would be—

His cultivation level was the lowest among them.

“Judging by your attire, you must be a Dan Attendant of Tongtian Tower, right?”

“Don’t tell me that this alchemist is an official Tongtian Tower alchemist…”

“He’s not.” Xia Yan shook his head.

The female artifact refiner exhaled.

“Even if he were a Tongtian Tower alchemist, he wouldn’t stand a chance against that blond guy. These alchemists… if their personal combat abilities were outstanding, there wouldn’t be a need to train Dan Attendants like you.”

“I’m from Martial Emperor City, so I know exactly who that blond guy is.”

“He’s Lei Zhen, from one of the Eight Great Martial Clans—the Lei Clan. He possesses a Dual Innate Lightning-Fire Physique, meaning he was born with two different physique abilities. Among the prodigies of Martial Emperor City, he is firmly in the top tier.”

—

Xia Yan’s heart trembled.

Martial Emperor City.

Unlike Ancient Celestial Kingdom and Qingzhou, Martial Emperor City was an independent city-state.

If Qingzhou was the holy land of alchemy, then Martial Emperor City was the sacred land of artifact refinement—representing the highest level of artifact craftsmanship in the world.

Within Martial Emperor City, eight cultivation clans specialized in martial arts, blending combat techniques, weapon techniques, and immortal techniques into a single discipline.

Each clan was protected by a Void Mystical Stage cultivator, who wielded a pseudo-immortal artifact.

As for the ruler of Martial Emperor City—

He commanded the continent’s only true Immortal Artifact.

A single person, a single weapon, and even Ancient Celestial Kingdom had failed to annex Martial Emperor City.

—

Currently, every non-Ancient Celestial Kingdom faction had at least one powerhouse guarding it.

If this gold-haired youth was truly a prodigy of the Lei Clan, then even a core alchemist of Tongtian Tower would be useless against him.

“...Tch. Figures.”

The artifact refiner sighed.

She was also from one of the Eight Clans, but unfortunately, as a mere branch family descendant, she couldn’t compare to a true Lei Clan genius.

She had thought this secret realm would be her breakthrough opportunity.

Instead, she had run into this monster.

—

“But I still believe… that alchemist will win.”

Xia Yan said slowly.

Years ago, at his father’s funeral, even Venerable Ling Quan, a Nascent Soul expert, had failed to see through his father’s residual soul possession technique.

But this wandering alchemist had seen through it instantly—not only identifying it but pinpointing the exact cause of his father’s transformation into a remnant soul.

That kind of perception…

Made Xia Yan certain—

This alchemist… was a monster.

—

The decision period was far too short.

From the moment they saw their Heavenly Treasures, only ten seconds had passed—

Not enough time for everyone to carefully weigh the risks.

—

Xia Yan, knowing Lin Jing’s unfathomable depth, had immediately forfeited.

The artifact refiner, recognizing the gold-haired youth, had also given up without hesitation.

However, the remaining three contenders—

Hadn’t realized the full implications of the battle.

Weren’t 100% confident they could win.

But simply couldn’t bear to give up on their Heavenly Treasures.

By the time they fully processed the situation—

They had already become competitors.

And they hadn’t even had time to analyze their opponents.

—

The boatman youth took a deep breath.

He had already come this far—

There was no turning back now.

If he wanted the final prize, he had to win at all costs.

—

“Too bad. It would have been easier if you three had also forfeited.”

The gold-haired Lei Zhen glanced at the remaining three opponents, lightning flickering in his eyes.

—

“Keep dreaming.”

The green-dressed girl scoffed.

Despite appearing slightly nervous, her aura didn’t falter at all.

Among the four combatants, Lin Jing was the least conspicuous.

He was only at the mid-stage Foundation Establishment, while the others were all at the peak of Foundation Establishment.

But his expression remained indifferent—

As if he were already looking at three dead people.

There was no personal grudge between them.

But in a life-and-death struggle, there was no right or wrong—

Only who survived.

Either way—

Tianqing City was no longer a place he could stay.

—

Since none of them were willing to attack first,

Each combatant instead focused on strengthening themselves—

Ensuring that they wouldn’t fall behind when the real fight began.

—

Green-Dressed Girl:

A plant-based sigil appeared on her forehead.

Her hair floated, her cultivation surged.

She was using a technique to temporarily condense a False Core, granting her Golden Core-level power.

She wasn’t worried about interference—she had a backup plan.

And the others weren’t trying to stop her, as they were busy preparing their own techniques.

—

Boatman Youth:

Water spirit energy surged into his body.

His skin turned from pale to light green, gradually darkening.

His expression twisted with exhilaration—as if he was activating his bloodline power.

—

Lei Zhen (Gold-Haired Youth):

He raised his palm.

A golden lightning sphere and a red flame sphere fused together.

The violent energy continued to grow, expanding from fist-sized to head-sized.

If given enough time, its power would only become more terrifying.

—

Outside the battlefield, the artifact refiner girl watched the unfolding battle.

“That green-dressed girl’s magical power is already nearing the Golden Core Stage…”

“And that boatman guy… he’s not pure human. He has sea clan blood. His raw physical strength is comparable to a Golden Core Body Cultivator.”

Xia Yan’s expression darkened.

“That girl is using Tai Xuan Scripture—one of Qingzhou’s strongest cultivation techniques.”

“It includes False Core Techniques, Phantom Nascent Soul Techniques, and Temporary Manifestation Techniques—allowing cultivators to fight at half a realm higher than their actual stage.”

“She’s a core disciple of the Tai Xuan Sect.”

—

“As for the boatman, he’s from the Sea Clan—one of the strongest ancient war clans under the Dragon Palace.”

“Although they can’t fully unleash their power outside the ocean, this secret realm contains extremely rich water spirit energy—allowing him to awaken his bloodline abilities.”

—

“But all their efforts are useless.”

“The Thunder-Fire Sphere that Lei Zhen is creating—”

“It has already killed actual Golden Core cultivators.”

“Its only weakness is that it takes too long to form. But if they let him finish it, at least one of them is guaranteed to die.”

—

As the battle reached a stalemate, Xia Yan and the artifact refiner noticed something odd—

While the other three were powering up, Lin Jing…

Was casually eating pills.

One bottle contained Qi-restoring Replenishing Pills.

The other? Gluttonous Pills.

While the other three were still young geniuses, Lin Jing…

Was an old monster in disguise.

And they were extremely unlucky to have met him today.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 34: Fear in the Face of Death
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 34: Fear in the Face of Death

Time passed, and the aura of the green-dressed girl was the first to stabilize, becoming indistinguishable from that of a Core Formation cultivator. Her special cultivation technique, which inherently contained the False Core method, had allowed her to temporarily break through under these dire circumstances.

As the technique completed its cycle, her expression grew more confident. However, when she carefully sensed the state of her opponents, she took a deep breath, a hint of regret flashing across her face.

"If I had known, I wouldn't have been so greedy for that supreme treasure…" She pressed her lips together.

The boatman youth, having activated his Sea Clan bloodline, had transformed into a green-skinned monster resembling a merman. His aura was terrifying, exuding a bloodline pressure akin to that of an unrivaled Demon King. His transformation was still ongoing, fueled by the unexpectedly rich quality of the water-attribute spiritual energy within this secret realm.

"Only the Water Moon Immortal Island and the ancient Dragon Palace could compare to the water spiritual energy in this place."

As expected of an Immortal’s secret realm.

Muttering to himself, he reveled in the moment, his monstrous nature laid bare. For an instant, the green-dressed girl found him even more difficult to deal with.

Meanwhile, Lei Zhen’s Thunder-Fire Sphere was even more terrifying—it had already reached a meter in diameter and was still growing as he held it high above his head. Its power continued to build, and upon seeing it, the green-dressed girl dared not take it lightly. She was deeply concerned that she might become its first target.

Sweeping her gaze across the battlefield, she noticed that Lin Jing, who was currently taking a Replenishing Pill, appeared to be the weakest. Not only was his cultivation lower, but he also lacked any particularly powerful attacks. He seemed far from his peak state, with his True Energy greatly depleted—otherwise, he wouldn't be in such a hurry to restore it.

As a result, the seemingly most harmless person became the first target of attack.

"Take care of the weakest first..."

"He’s an alchemist, which means he must have a large supply of pills in his storage bag. If I eliminate him and take his pills, I’ll be in a much better position to face the other two!"

"If I engage the alchemist, both of us will suffer losses and deplete our strength. The golden-haired youth, not wanting the Sea Clan youth to gain an advantage, will most likely refrain from joining our fight, leading to a three-way battle where the Sea Clan youth remains unscathed. In that case, the golden-haired youth will probably direct his spell at him instead…"

"This is the best path for me to survive until the end."

A sharp glint flashed in the green-dressed girl’s eyes. With a flick of her slender hand, countless resilient vines shot out, winding toward Lin Jing. Yet, even as she attacked, most of her attention remained on the Sea Clan youth and the golden-haired youth…

However, in that very instant of distraction—

Shua!

A streak of crimson sword light flashed by.

Boom!

A deafening roar echoed in her mind. The world seemed to spin wildly.

"W-What’s happening…" she murmured in disbelief.

She saw her own body.

She saw the vines still suspended in midair.

And she saw her own head—severed from her neck—falling to the ground.

Terror filled her eyes.

The scorch marks around her wound, the rapid loss of life—it was too much for her to accept.

"I was supposed to—"

She wanted to scream.

She had gained the power of a Core Formation cultivator!

But before she could even finish her thought, her consciousness completely faded.

She died with her eyes open.

This scene left the Sea Clan youth, Xia Yan, the female artifact refiner, and Lei Zhen utterly stunned. None of them had been able to react until the flaming sword returned to Lin Jing’s side.

The sword was composed of nothing but basic spiritual flames. And yet, this seemingly simple blade of spiritual energy had instantly decapitated a False Core cultivator.

Everyone found it utterly inconceivable.

After all, Lin Jing’s cultivation was only at mid Foundation Establishment. With such a vast difference in realms, how could he possibly have achieved this?

Even though the green-dressed girl’s protective True Energy was wood-attribute and naturally countered by fire, this was far too exaggerated.

"That guy... is dangerous!"

Lei Zhen quickly realized the situation and prepared to hurl the Thunder-Fire Sphere at Lin Jing instead of using it to intimidate others. He had seen through the alchemist’s complexity.

Though Lin Jing was only at mid Foundation Establishment, he had broken through from the eighteenth level of Qi Refinement, possessing an exceptionally deep foundation. His True Energy was extraordinarily dense—comparable to that of a Core Formation cultivator. Even if he relied on the power of his contracted beasts, his strength was still unimaginably fearsome.

The spiritual flames formed from his True Energy, combined with even basic fire-control techniques, allowed him to dominate opponents far stronger than himself.

Sensing the threat Lin Jing posed, Lei Zhen abandoned his previous target and considered him the primary enemy.

"Break—!"

Unfortunately, before he could finish his command, Lin Jing uttered a single word.

Lei Zhen’s mind nearly collapsed.

The flaming sword instantly scattered into sparks, condensing in Lin Jing’s raised palm. In the blink of an eye, an enormous flaming handprint materialized out of thin air.

The fire-formed palm was massive beyond belief. Even more terrifying was the fact that it was no longer composed of ordinary spiritual flames—it was transforming into an exotic fire Lei Zhen could not comprehend.

A pure white flame.

Its five fingers resembled five divine dragons, opening and closing like a blooming lotus of fire.

Then, with a single motion, it swatted the Thunder-Fire Sphere out of existence!

A deafening explosion resounded through the great hall.

Yet the flaming hand remained completely intact.

The white flames continued to surge, sweeping forth with violent spiritual energy undeterred.

Lei Zhen’s pupils constricted.

He hastily retrieved a mid-grade magical treasure-grade battle armor and roared as he ignited his full strength.

But no matter how loudly he shouted, his cries were swiftly drowned out by the merciless advance of the flaming palm.

As it descended, his entire body was engulfed in fire.

The commotion gradually faded…

This so-called genius of Martial Emperor City, known far and wide, died with eyes filled with boundless terror, unable to comprehend who—what—he had just faced.

"AAAAAHHHHHHH!!!"

The Sea Clan youth was utterly petrified.

Whether it was the green-dressed girl’s technique or Lei Zhen’s Thunder-Fire Sphere, he had been wary but not terrified.

Yet Lin Jing’s effortless display of power completely shattered his courage.

As Lin Jing and Lei Zhen clashed, the Sea Clan youth’s instincts overpowered his thoughts.

Without waiting for his mind to catch up, his body moved—water enveloping his arm, forming a trident as he lunged at Lin Jing, hoping for a sneak attack.

However…

Lin Jing simply turned his back to him, not uttering a single word.

In an instant, the Sea Clan youth’s heartbeat accelerated, his blood vessels constricted.

His impure Sea Clan bloodline became his death sentence.

A wave of unbearable terror filled his eyes as his heart suddenly compressed—like an invisible hand had crushed it.

In that moment, as blood seeped from his seven orifices, he had a realization—

The alchemist standing before him… was like the natural ruler of sea beasts.

There was no resistance.

Regret flooded his fading consciousness.

"The Immortal Path is merciless… Don’t cultivate in your next life."

Having dealt with three so-called geniuses, Lin Jing casually waved his hand, drawing their storage items toward him.

His gaze slowly shifted beyond the great hall.

There, Xia Yan and the female artifact refiner—who had not participated in the battle for the divine treasure—were left watching in horror.

Their faces paled.

Their hearts trembled as though they had just died once themselves.

"M-Monster…"

At that moment, the female artifact refiner and Xia Yan shared a single thought.

Even Xia Yan, who had expected Lin Jing to be formidable, was left speechless by the overwhelming display of power.

For the first time, he was grateful for the choice he had made.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 35: The Hourglass of Time
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 35: The Hourglass of Time

In the silent secret realm, as the battle for the supreme treasure came to an end, Xia Yan and the female artifact refiner, who had given up on the fight, were instantly teleported out…

Lin Jing, however, was slightly delayed. A treasure shaped like an hourglass slowly materialized beside him.

"I’ll call you the Hourglass of Time." Placing the hourglass into his storage bag, Lin Jing took a deep breath, feeling a headache coming on.

He had obtained the treasure… but now he had to bear the consequences of taking it.

After retrieving the treasure, some time later, Lin Jing was also transported out of the secret realm.

———

West of the Ancient Kingdom, Martial Emperor City.

One of the Eight Great Clans, the Feng Clan.

"Who would have thought that Feng Ya, a mere branch family girl, would be chosen by the secret realm? Why did no one notice her potential before?"

"What does it matter? That was the Heavenly Treasure Secret Realm. We just received urgent news that Lei Zhen from the Lei Clan was also pulled in at the same time. According to ancient records about this secret realm, if this branch girl was decisive, she might have survived. But if she tried to fight for the secret realm’s fortune, she would likely perish."

"Regardless, being chosen by the secret realm proves her fate is extraordinary. As long as she returns alive, she is worth the clan’s full support! Even if she no longer wishes to pursue artifact refinement, she can do as she pleases!" one elder snorted coldly.

The other elders fell silent.

The Feng Clan had strict rules and traditions. The core Sword Dao inheritance was reserved only for the direct lineage, while the branch families were required to practice artifact forging and sword crafting to support the main family.

Although this rule had long caused discontent among the branch family members, they were powerless against the strength of the direct line.

Until one incident—
