A branch family father, dissatisfied that his daughter, despite her remarkable talent in swordsmanship, was still forced to learn artifact refinement instead of the Feng Clan’s sword techniques, chose to leave the family.

If that had been all, it wouldn’t have shaken the Feng Clan.

What truly shocked the clan was that after the branch family girl was taken to the Ancient Kingdom by her parents, her swordsmanship talent shone even brighter. She was eventually accepted as a personal disciple by the Void Mystical Sword Cultivator of the Ancient Kingdom’s top sword sect—Heavenly Sword Gate. She was recognized as having the potential to reach the Void Mystical Stage.

And true to expectations, she became the most famous Core Formation prodigy of the Ancient Kingdom.

Only then did the Feng Clan realize they had lost an extraordinary talent.

To win her back, they even changed their ancestral rules, allowing talented branch family members to cultivate the core inheritance.

That branch family girl was none other than Cold Moon Sword Immortal, the senior disciple of Heavenly Sword Gate.

Now, another gifted branch girl had appeared. Though her potential might not match that of Cold Moon Sword Immortal, the Feng Clan was determined not to let another genius slip through their fingers.

Buzz!

Suddenly, the secret realm vanished.

Feng Ya stepped out, still in shock, completely unaware that she was surrounded by high-ranking clan elders.

"She’s out!" The elders sighed in relief. They had feared she would perish within the secret realm.

"Feng Ya!" an elder called out. She snapped back to reality and looked toward them.

"It seems you gave up on the battle for the Heavenly Treasure… A wise decision. With your current strength, you were no match for Lei Zhen. But from now on, the clan will fully support your growth. In the end, did Lei Zhen obtain the treasure?" one elder asked.

"Lin Jing?" Feng Ya froze.

Just as she was about to answer—

Boom!

Suddenly, the skies over the Feng Clan rumbled with thunder. Furious lightning crackled as a terrifying presence descended.

A golden-haired woman at the Nascent Soul stage appeared above the branch family’s territory, her gaze sweeping across the elders before locking onto Feng Ya, who had just returned from the secret realm.

"Lei Qianren! What are you doing here?!" The Feng Clan elders glared at the newcomer, their auras flaring as they questioned her.

"My son is dead! He died in the secret realm!" Lei Qianren’s face was twisted with rage.

Her words sent shockwaves through the Feng Clan elders.

Lei Qianren—she was Lei Zhen’s father!

They knew Lei Zhen’s talent and strength well. He had multiple mid-grade magical treasures at his disposal.

And yet… he had died in the Heavenly Treasure Secret Realm?

"It seems your Feng Clan’s child was wise enough to back out and survive… Tell me, do you know who killed my son?" Her expression gradually became eerily calm, but the chilling undertone made everyone shudder.

The Feng Clan elders remained silent.

"… My condolences," one finally said.

"Feng Ya! Speak quickly about what happened in the secret realm!" The Feng Clan elders immediately prompted her to explain, not wanting to provoke this bereaved Nascent Soul powerhouse any further. They even struggled to hold back their amusement—Serves him right! Good riddance!

"I-It was an alchemist from Qingzhou…"

"I don’t know much about him, only that his strength was terrifying. He specialized in fire techniques, and his magic took the form of white dragon-shaped beast flames. He was only at mid Foundation Establishment, yet in an instant… he killed three Foundation Establishment peak cultivators, all of whom had power equivalent to Core Formation…"

"Qingzhou… an alchemist!! Draw his portrait!" Lei Qianren growled, tossing out a blank scroll.

At that moment, the Feng Clan elders were even more shocked.

A mid Foundation Establishment alchemist capable of instantly killing Lei Zhen?

Was there such a terrifying alchemist in existence?

———

Meanwhile, in Qingzhou.

At Tai Xuan Sect, an elderly Nascent Soul cultivator wept before the lifeless, decapitated body of his granddaughter.

"I don’t care who you are… I will make you pay!!!"

"The murderer… is in Qingzhou as well? Good, very good!"

———

Aboard a flying vessel bound for Tongtian Path.

Xia Yan was surrounded by powerful figures, including Alchemist Jie Geng, as they questioned him about the events in the secret realm.

When they learned that the Nameless Alchemist had also entered the secret realm—and had displayed power strong enough to suppress several ordinary Core Formation cultivators—Jie Geng’s expression turned to shock.

"Return to Tianqing City immediately," she ordered.

———

Elsewhere, outside Tianqing City.

Lin Jing had returned.

Outside the Nameless Pill Pavilion, the entire commercial district was cleared out, leaving only Ling Quan Zun-zhe, who smiled at Lin Jing.

"Good, you’re still alive. So, did you give up on the Heavenly Treasure, or did you claim victory in the end?"

"Seeing how your clothes are spotless, I’d guess you backed out. Wise choice—it would’ve been too difficult at your level."

"I was worried you’d die, leaving my debts unpaid," he chuckled.

"Now, tell me about the secret realm. I’m quite curious about what happened in there."

Lin Jing laughed.

Just as he was about to make something up—

Ling Quan Zun-zhe suddenly pulled out a compass. A shadowy figure appeared on its surface.

"Master, Tai Xuan Sect’s Elder Bai Xuan’s granddaughter died in the secret realm. He claims the killer is in the Qingming Path region. Her storage bag contained a soul blood locator!

"Bai Xuan has commissioned the Qingming Commerce Guild to detain anyone exiting the secret realm. Tai Xuan Sect promises a great reward."

Ling Quan Zun-zhe’s face darkened, his smile vanishing as he turned to Lin Jing.

"…?"

Lin Jing blinked, then sighed.

He reached into his storage bag and pulled out a drop of soul blood.

With a casual flick of his fingers—he crushed it.

"Senior Ling Quan," Lin Jing smiled, "this has nothing to do with you. And you wouldn’t want your debtor getting killed, would you?"

"Let’s just pretend you never saw me…"

Even now, Lin Jing remained calm.

Though he hadn’t expected that green-dressed girl to have a soul blood locator, the situation wasn’t too bad.

After all—

Ling Quan Zun-zhe couldn’t see through his illusions.

And if things went south…

He could always hide inside the Hourglass of Time and slip away unseen.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 36: The Most Dangerous Place
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 36: The Most Dangerous Place

Ling Quan Zun-zhe’s face darkened as he stared at Lin Jing. "So, little guy, you didn’t withdraw from the battle for the Heavenly Treasure. Instead, you slaughtered your competitors and claimed the final prize?"

Lin Jing sighed helplessly. "It was self-defense, according to the rules of the secret realm."

"Senior Ling Quan, what do you think about what I just said…?" He was convinced that, in this world, debtors held the real power.

As long as a debt remained unpaid, the person who feared your death the most was your creditor.

And right now, he was the debtor.

Ling Quan Zun-zhe was the creditor.

Ling Quan Zun-zhe shook his head and said, "The secret realm descended upon the commercial district of Tianqing City. The Qingming Commerce Guild even sealed off the area. Any casual cultivator in the city can easily guess that you had dealings with them…"

"If I let you go, it would be the same as slapping Bai Xuan Zun-zhe in the face. He’d hold a grudge!"

"I need more payment!" He looked at Lin Jing. "Letting you escape secretly isn’t a problem, but your pill debt is still unpaid, and this will bring me some trouble. It’s only fair to ask for some compensation, right?"

"I won’t ask for much. Just promise to repay your debt within a year. For each year you delay, the amount of owed pills will double!"

"The hell kind of interest rate is that?" Lin Jing twitched.

"What do you say?" Ling Quan Zun-zhe stared at him. Compared to offending Tai Xuan Sect, he valued the Gluttonous Pill’s market potential even more.

In reality, letting Lin Jing go wasn’t a big deal.

But more than Tai Xuan Sect’s retaliation, he was worried about something else—

That Lin Jing would run off without paying his debt.

The debt for a single high-grade exotic fire wasn’t the real issue. The real problem was losing the exclusive selling rights for the Gluttonous Pill!

That was what really pained him as a businessman.

In his eyes, as long as he could secure the future supply of Gluttonous Pills, it was worth even offending Bai Xuan Zun-zhe.

After all, offending Bai Xuan Zun-zhe meant forging a stronger connection with Lin Jing.

A genius who had fought his way out of the Heavenly Treasure Secret Realm—regardless of his own background—must have an Alchemist Sovereign supporting him.

Between a common Nascent Soul cultivator and an Alchemist Sovereign, it was obvious who could bring in greater profits.

His condition for Lin Jing’s escape was merely a friendly gesture.

Moreover, he didn’t force Lin Jing into a formal contract but instead asked for a verbal promise, another sign of goodwill.

It was his way of saying—Remember, there’s an old, lonely merchant in Tianqing City waiting for you.

Lin Jing pondered briefly, then nodded. "Fine."

As long as he repaid it within a year, that was manageable.

For now, he truly didn’t have many Gluttonous Pills on hand.

After finalizing the deal, Ling Quan Zun-zhe soared into the sky. "Tai Xuan Dao is quite far from Qingming Dao. There are no teleportation arrays in Qingzhou. Even with a Nascent Soul cultivator’s full-speed travel, it will take some time to get here. Make good use of your time and leave this region as soon as possible."

Once Ling Quan Zun-zhe was gone, Lin Jing sighed in relief and returned to the Nameless Pill Pavilion to pack his belongings.

"Leaving? That’s not happening."

Immediately, he conjured an illusion—a perfect replica of himself.

In plain sight, the illusion soared into the sky and left Tianqing City under everyone’s watchful eyes.

Meanwhile, his real body never left.

Instead, he slipped into the Hourglass of Time and vanished from reality.

He had arranged to meet Senior Brother Ouyang here in the commercial district. If he left now, how would they meet?

Since he had to return eventually, why not just stay hidden?

"The most dangerous place is often the safest. As long as enough people witness my illusion leaving Tianqing City, even when Tai Xuan Sect’s cultivators arrive, they’ll be misled!"

Hidden within the alternate space of the Hourglass of Time, Lin Jing could patiently wait for Senior Brother Ouyang’s arrival.

———

Inside the Hourglass of Time—

A vast, sand-filled void.

Upon entering, Lin Jing felt no major difference from the outside world. If anything, the spiritual energy was even denser—far surpassing the Beast Taming Space of the Pine Leaf Mouse and the Dragon Carp, as well as the academy’s secret realm.

Regarding the flow of time—as the master of the Hourglass of Time, he could observe the outside world.

Everything outside moved painfully slow, forming a stark contrast with the rapid timeflow inside.

"This thing is incredible..." Lin Jing murmured, feeling his security greatly enhanced.

Unless an Void Mystical cultivator specializing in time-based techniques came looking for him, no one would ever find this place.

"More time for me."

"I’ll wait here until my meeting with Senior Brother Ouyang. Might as well use the time wisely."

"Beast Taming techniques, Illusion techniques... Never mind that, I should try this one instead. Can’t let it go to waste."

Sitting cross-legged in the sands of time, Lin Jing retrieved the Three Heads, Six Arms Technique he had obtained from the Demon Ancestor’s Ruins and began cultivating.

Meanwhile, he summoned the Pine Leaf Mouse, instructing it to plant trees and transform this barren wasteland into something slightly more pleasant.

———

At the same time—

A short while later—

A massive bird descended from the boundless skies above Tianqing City.

Riding atop it were Ouyang Hao and Xu Qingyao, who had come with him.

Because Xu Qingyao had accompanied him, they traveled aboard a Nascent Soul Demon Emperor, arriving far earlier than Ouyang Hao had expected—well before the agreed-upon time.

"The hell? What happened? Where’s the store? Where’s he???"

By now, the commercial district had reopened.

However, standing in front of the Nameless Pill Pavilion, Ouyang Hao fell into deep thought.

Because Lin Jing’s pill pavilion was now sealed shut.

Its walls were plastered with Tai Xuan Sect’s wanted posters.

And the wanted fugitive?

A Foundation Establishment alchemist, with an illusory face matching Lin Jing’s disguise.

"Hm?" Xu Qingyao raised an eyebrow at Ouyang Hao, clearly expecting an explanation.

Ouyang Hao quickly turned to a nearby shop and asked, "Fellow Daoist, what happened to the Nameless Pill Pavilion? Their contraceptive pills were great—I wanted to buy a few more."

"Why is the owner wanted? Did his pills kill someone?"

The False Core old man at the shop was momentarily stunned. Then he chuckled. "Fellow Daoist, you’re unaware? Not long ago, the owner of Nameless Pill Pavilion got caught up in a secret realm. Inside, he killed a Core Disciple of Tai Xuan Sect. Now, he’s being hunted by Tai Xuan Sect. As for the alchemist himself? He ran off as soon as he could!"

"Why does he keep entering secret realms? I haven’t even gone into one yet," Ouyang Hao muttered, running back to report to Xu Qingyao.

"What a troublemaker." Xu Qingyao sighed.

A wanted fugitive under his real identity.

Now forced into exile under a fake one.

How was this Beast Taming Sect’s Holy Son always getting himself into these disasters?

"What now?" Ouyang Hao was equally speechless.

Xu Qingyao smirked. "What else? We don’t know where he’s hiding, so let’s beat the crap out of Tai Xuan Sect’s Nascent Soul cultivator and force them to revoke the wanted notice."

"That way, the little rascal will feel safe enough to return."

"This was a fair fight in the secret realm—if they weren’t strong enough and died, what’s there to seek revenge for?!"

"They made me waste my time coming here. I’m pissed."

With that, she rolled up her sleeves and shot off like a meteor toward the Nascent Soul presences in the city.

"Wait—Senior Sister, wait!!"

Ouyang Hao was stunned.

Someone was definitely about to suffer.

Ever since joining the Demon Court, Senior Sister’s temper had grown worse and worse…

She was becoming a beast herself.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 37: A Grand Background
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 37: A Grand Background

Tianqing City.

Qingming Pavilion.

On the pavilion’s upper floor, Bai Xuan Zun-zhe of Tai Xuan Sect faced off against Ling Quan Zun-zhe of Qingming Commerce Guild.

“Ling Quan, you still dare claim that you didn’t deliberately let that alchemist go? I captured over a hundred cultivators who were in and around the commercial district at the time. Every single one of them testified that as soon as the Cavern Heaven appeared, your Qingming Commerce Guild immediately sealed off the area! Some even saw you personally outside the entrance!”

The speaker, an elder with long white whiskers draped in a white Daoist robe, pressed forward with his questioning. Across from him, Ling Quan Zun-zhe responded indifferently,

“I put up your sect’s wanted notice the moment you issued it. What more do you expect from me?”

“That alchemist’s master is called Fire Dragon Pill Sovereign. You should understand the weight of that title.”

“I acknowledge that the life of a Tai Xuan Sect core disciple is important. But do you expect me to make an enemy of a Pill Sovereign because of it?”

“In this case, no matter how you argue, you’re in the wrong. Are you really going to risk offending a Pill Sovereign just to avenge your descendant?”

“Fire Dragon Pill Sovereign?” Bai Xuan Zun-zhe sneered in disdain. “Never heard of him. He’s not even a fifth-tier alchemist from Tongtian Tower. Our Tai Xuan Sect has eight Zun-zhe, all fifth-tier alchemists, all at Nascent Soul Transformation. What kind of nobody is this Fire Dragon Pill Sovereign?”

He understood that anyone capable of killing his grandson must be the disciple of a powerful figure. However, Tai Xuan Sect was a first-class force in Qingzhou, backed by sheer numbers. So what if this so-called Fire Dragon Pill Sovereign was the master of that alchemist?

At most, he was a rogue cultivator, likely at Nascent Soul Transformation, with nothing but fifth-tier alchemy skills. Even if he decided not to demand the disciple’s life, he had to extract some form of compensation.

“All of my descendants are marked with soul-blood for precisely this reason—to track down their killers and demand reparation. And Luo’er was one of my most talented granddaughters, lucky enough to enter the Cavern Heaven.” Bai Xuan Zun-zhe grew increasingly resolute.

He had to make this Fire Dragon Pill Sovereign and his disciple pay a heavy price.

“Ling Quan, how are you so sure that my granddaughter attacked first?” Bai Xuan Zun-zhe pressed on.

“Because before you arrived, Ji Geng, the alchemist from Tongtian Tower, was already in Tianqing City. One of her Foundation Establishment alchemy attendants also entered the Cavern Heaven but didn’t fight for its treasures. Instead, he observed everything firsthand.”

“Where is he now?” Bai Xuan Zun-zhe’s face darkened.

“He left once he learned that the alchemist had escaped from Tianqing City.”

“Bai Xuan, I advise you to reconsider. That young alchemist is not only strong but also monstrously talented in alchemy. His master is bound to be extraordinary—certainly not as simple as you assume. Dropping this matter might actually be in your best interest. The rules of the Cavern Heaven are clear: your descendant insisted on fighting, so you can’t blame others for the outcome.”

“Hmph! My grandson’s death is a blood feud! Even if his master were an immortal king, I wouldn’t let this go!!!”

“Oh? Is that so?”

A voice suddenly drifted into the ears of both Nascent Soul cultivators, startling them. They hadn’t even noticed someone approaching.

A woman clad in a robe of green and white feathers stepped onto the pavilion, landing lightly on the railing. She looked down at the two powerful cultivators with an air of dominance.

“Where did this mere Nascent Soul brat come from?” Bai Xuan Zun-zhe snapped. “Ling Quan, since when does your Qingming Pavilion let just anyone waltz in?”

Then, as if realizing something, his expression shifted.

“Wait… you know that alchemist? Don’t tell me—you’re Fire Dragon Pill Sovereign?” Bai Xuan Zun-zhe turned to the feather-robed woman, raising his hand to suppress her.

But before he could act, an overwhelming pressure locked onto him.

And onto that alchemist’s master.

Cold sweat streamed down their backs. Their bodies broke into goosebumps. For a brief moment, their minds went blank.

Even when facing the Void Mystical Ancestor of their own sect, they had never felt such an oppressive aura of impending death.

Just before Bai Xuan Zun-zhe’s vision went completely white, his eyes flickered to the token in the feather-robed woman’s hand. He swallowed hard.

The terrifying beast insignia on that token nearly made him drop to his knees.

A hoarse whisper escaped his throat.

“Demon Emperor’s Might… the Holy Son’s Token…”

“She… she’s the Holy Son of the Demon Court!”

“Fire Dragon Pill Sovereign… Fire Dragon… That alchemist’s master isn’t human! He’s a fire dragon! A member of the Demon Court!!”

In that instant, everything became clear to Bai Xuan Zun-zhe.

Ling Quan Zun-zhe, meanwhile, confirmed his suspicions. He had assumed Fire Dragon Pill Sovereign was merely a “wild dragon.” But who could have expected he hailed from the Demon Court itself?!

The Demon Court’s Demon Emperor ranked among the strongest of all demon emperors.

It was a top-tier demon faction, rivaling even Tongtian Tower—far beyond Tai Xuan Sect’s reach.

And the three Holy Maidens of the Demon Court?

They were infamous.

Especially the woman before them—Bai Xuan Zun-zhe finally recalled her identity.

The Third Holy Maiden of the Demon Court.

Xu Qingyao.

A monster with peacock demon blood, nearly invincible among Nascent Soul cultivators, always accompanied by several Nascent Soul Demon Emperors as her protectors.

Not long ago, a Nascent Soul sect had provoked this demoness… and was promptly burned to the ground.

He had kicked an iron plate.

For a long moment, Bai Xuan Zun-zhe was speechless.

“Holy Maiden…” His knees nearly buckled. He wanted to say something, but Xu Qingyao, seeing that he wasn’t truly seeking vengeance but simply acting out of arrogance, lost interest in fighting.

“Forget it. Since your grandson is dead, but none of mine are, I won’t make things difficult for you. Withdraw the wanted notice and issue a public apology.”

“…Thank you, Holy Maiden.” Bai Xuan Zun-zhe exhaled in relief and bowed slightly.

That same day—

Tai Xuan Sect revoked the wanted notice for the nameless alchemist. Bai Xuan Zun-zhe publicly apologized, claiming he had acted out of momentary rage. He admitted that his grandson had died due to the Cavern Heaven’s rules and should not have blamed others. As for any damage done to the Demon Court’s Fire Dragon Pill Sovereign and his disciple’s reputation, he was willing to offer compensation.

Tianqing City was in an uproar.

The entire cultivation world felt as if they had witnessed a celestial scandal.

The mysterious alchemist who had entered the Cavern Heaven—his master was from the Demon Court!

A human taking a demon beast as a master was rare, but not unheard of.

But for that master to be from the Demon Court?

No wonder Bai Xuan Zun-zhe had backed down so quickly.

That was one of the top-tier demon factions!

At the Nameless Pill Pavilion—

“Ouyang, this Fire Dragon Pill Sovereign was clearly something Junior Brother made up,” Wu Ouyang questioned. “Why go along with it?”

Xu Qingyao shot him a glance.

“That guy wants to roam around refining pills. But with his troublemaking skills? No chance.” She smirked. “This fake identity is perfect. I’ll just have him stick by my side instead.”

“Don’t worry—I have ways to make him agree.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 38: A Newcomer to the Demon Court
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 38: A Newcomer to the Demon Court

The events in Tianqing City continued to ferment, spreading across Qingming Path and even reaching the surrounding regions.

Xu Qingyao and Ouyang Hao were certain that as soon as Lin Jing heard the news, he would immediately understand—Senior Sister had already taken care of Tai Xuan Sect’s problem. Hurry back to the agreed meeting place!

However, despite their long wait, Lin Jing never showed up!

“This guy… don’t tell me he got captured by the Demonic Sect while on the run?” Xu Qingyao speculated.

“No way…” Ouyang Hao frowned. “Junior Brother’s illusion techniques are good enough to deceive even Nascent Soul cultivators. He shouldn’t be in any real danger…”

Still, they were puzzled. With the short amount of time that had passed, where could Lin Jing have possibly gone? By all logic, he shouldn’t have been able to travel far.

Finally, as their meeting date drew closer, a pair of eyes slowly opened in the Hourglass World.

“I finally got the Three Heads, Six Arms Technique to the entry level after nearly a year… Not easy, but at least it wasn’t as difficult as the Heavenly Demon Physique.”

Unfortunately, the technique’s only real effect at the moment was allowing him to simultaneously control six magical artifacts—but he didn’t have any that suited him.

Right now, his hands were full of whips, hammers, swords, and halberds…

But they were all just loot from the Cavern Heaven battles—at most mid-grade artifacts, and not even ones he was proficient with.

The true strength of this technique lay in wielding multiple artifacts at once…

But if the user lacked high-quality weapons, or if they didn’t match his fighting style, then it was rather awkward and impractical.

“Well, at least I’ve learned it. I can find the right artifacts later. What I really need is a set of six interconnected weapons…”

Lin Jing dismissed his transformed body and glanced around the still-barren Hourglass World, shaking his head.

The place was strangely desolate—despite the Pine Leaf Mouse’s long efforts at reforesting it, not a single plant had successfully taken root…

Still, even with the poor environment, the core effect of the place was incredible. It had taken him what felt like an eternity to grasp the Three Heads, Six Arms Technique, yet hardly any time had passed in the outside world.

Aside from his own gains, Fire Qilin Dragon had finally managed to finish refining its debt of pills and had even cultivated Yangflame after a long effort.

While its yang energy wasn’t as strong as someone with the Primordial Yang Body, it was still a fire dragon—naturally full of yang energy. By investing even more time, it had managed to replicate Ouyang Hao’s exotic flame.

Now, it possessed five exotic flames in total, its power nearing a critical breaking point. Even with its Dragon Emperor Bloodline, its current body was struggling to contain any more strength.

Its next step?

To compress and refine its internal power into an unparalleled Monster Core, triggering a complete transformation—paving the way to an even higher level of strength!

Meanwhile, back at the Nameless Pill Pavilion—

Lin Jing, now concealing his presence, returned from the Hourglass Space and looked around.

One person was slumped over a table. Another was dozing off on a chair.

Seeing the two like this, he fell into deep thought.

He stepped out of the pavilion, then walked back in—this time dispelling his third-layer illusion technique, fully grasping the situation.

“Senior Sister… Xu Qingyao?”

At that moment, the moment Lin Jing removed his invisibility, Xu Qingyao abruptly lifted her head from her slumped posture, her expression unreadable.

“The Holy Son, huh? He’s finally here.”

Lin Jing scratched his cheek. He figured he should probably explain that he had been trapped in an alternate space all along.

“Why aren’t you saying anything?” Xu Qingyao’s eyes swept over the young man dressed in Firecloud Pill Robes, looking genuinely surprised.

She had heard Ouyang Hao say that this Beast Taming Sect Holy Son was an expert in illusions…

But she hadn’t expected his abilities to be so advanced—even her demon senses couldn’t see through his disguised form.

No wonder he had survived as a Longevity Body cultivator.

This guy had some serious skills.

“Junior Brother!”

Just then, Ouyang Hao woke up, his eyes lighting up as he saw Lin Jing.

Damn it. If he had known Lin Jing would cut it so close to the meeting time, he wouldn’t have scheduled it so late!

“Thank you, Senior Sister and Senior Brother, for helping me out…” Lin Jing smiled. “I really appreciate it. That Cavern Heaven situation… was pretty unexpected.”

“So, do you still insist on wandering around alone?” Xu Qingyao stood up and asked,

“Junior Brother, come back with me. My palace isn’t located within the main Demon Court—the only ones nearby are Demon Emperor-level beings. With your illusion skills, no one will be able to see through your disguise.”

“Of course, I know you’re worried about running into Demon Ancestor-level monsters—or even a Demon Emperor himself.”

“But don’t worry. The Demon Emperor has been in seclusion for years, never making an appearance.”

“As for the Demon Ancestors within the Demon Court? Most of them reside within their own clan territories. Naturally, following me means you’ll eventually meet them—but if you have this, it won’t be a problem.”

Xu Qingyao tossed a Monster Core to Lin Jing.

The moment he caught it, his heart shook.

A Demon Emperor’s Core?!!!

Just by touching it, he understood its immense power.

“In the Demon Court, there’s a particular race called the Chameleon Clan. Even before they begin cultivating, they possess the innate ability to blend into their surroundings.”

“As they advance, they become incredibly skilled in illusion techniques. A Demon Emperor-level Chameleon can even deceive Demon Ancestors, slipping past them unnoticed.”

“Their Monster Cores have the ability to enhance illusion arts. This Chameleon Demon Emperor’s Core is something I exchanged from the Demon Court’s treasure vault long ago for my own safety. But I don’t need it anymore, so I’m giving it to you.”

“With your illusion techniques, combined with the enhancement of this Chameleon Monster Core, you won’t have to worry about exposing your true identity before a Demon Ancestor ever again.”

“So? Will you come with me now?”

Lin Jing grinned while holding the Chameleon Demon Emperor’s Core.

“No problem. From now on, wherever Senior Sister goes, I go. Whatever you need me to do, I’ll do it. That Transformation Pill—I’ll definitely refine it for you!”

After wandering aimlessly for so long, did Lin Jing not want a stable place?

Of course not.

He had just never felt safe enough before.

But now?

Senior Sister had given him absolute security.

Even more reliable than Senior Pine!

Xu Qingyao chuckled, appreciating his keen survival instincts.

“What’s your current alias?”

“Haven’t decided yet.” Lin Jing replied.

“You created a fake master named Fire Dragon Pill Sovereign—so from now on, you’ll be Fire Dragon Alchemist. You’ll be my official Pill Dao Guest Elder under the Demon Court. A new recruit.”

“I hear you’re pretty strong… Aside from refining Transformation Pills, I might need you for occasional battles. That a problem?”

“Battles?” Lin Jing blinked.

“The three Holy Maidens of the Demon Court don’t have equal resources, status, or treatment. Our power is determined by three things.”

“First—our individual strength.”

“Second—the progress of our research into Transformation Techniques.”

“Third—the talent and potential of our followers.”

“Since I joined the latest, I have the fewest powerful subordinates. I need to change that. If I can, I’ll get more resources for refining Transformation Pills.”

“Got it. Leave it to me.” Lin Jing nodded.

Even without Pine Leaf Mouse or Ice Soul Gu, just with his illusions and Fire Qilin Dragon, he was already quite strong.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 39: Completing the Five Elements
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 39: Completing the Five Elements

Core Formation Blessed Land…

If such a place exists, that would be even better.

"I'll be counting on you, Senior Sister," Lin Jing said, expressing his gratitude.

As for the situation with the Fire Qilin Dragon… Senior Brother Ouyang must have told Senior Sister about it.

After all, Ouyang, despite being at the Core Formation stage, was chased by a Foundation Establishment Fire Dragon—he must have been traumatized.

Lin Jing chuckled, and Xu Qingyao also smiled faintly.

From the moment she decided to accompany Ouyang Hao here, she had already made up her mind to take Lin Jing with her.

Lin Jing's reluctance to go to the Demon Court was simply because he feared exposure.

Besides, Lin Jing was extremely valuable to her.

Not only did he possess formidable alchemy skills, but his title as the Number One Prodigy of the Ancient Kingdom also spoke volumes about his terrifying strength.

Even if some of his techniques were restricted, Xu Qingyao believed that Lin Jing alone was more than enough to deal with—

[Connection error. Refresh to retry.]

Before leaving with Senior Sister Xu Qingyao, Lin Jing still had one more thing to take care of.

He headed to the Qingming Commerce Guild, and after making a request, he was immediately invited to the upper floor by Elder Ling Quan.

"My young friend, my young friend… why didn't you tell me earlier that your master was the Alchemy Sovereign of the Demon Court?" Ling Quan sighed with a bitter smile.

Had Lin Jing mentioned this sooner, all of this trouble could have been avoided.

He had almost been scared senile by the mere presence of the Demon Emperor’s power.

Qingzhou was nothing like the Ancient Kingdom...

In the Ancient Kingdom, the human race was supreme. There were no demon beast factions—only wild demon beasts meant for human cultivators to hunt.

If the Beast Taming Sect showed even the slightest sign of valuing demonic fortune over human fortune, they would be immediately forced into seclusion for rectification.

All of this was to ensure the prosperity of the human race and to safeguard the human destiny of the Ancient Kingdom.

Qingzhou, however, was different.

Although humans mainly resided in the Thirteen Provinces of Qingzhou, outside of those provinces, demon beast factions thrived.

Together, human and demon forces made up the vast land of Qingzhou.

In fact, from an overall perspective, the demon beast factions were far stronger than the human forces in Qingzhou.

Among human factions, the only one with a cultivator at the Dao-Seeking Stage was Tongtian Tower.

Meanwhile, just in the nearby regions, there were three demon beast factions that had Demon Emperors comparable to a Dao-Seeking cultivator!

As a result, Qingzhou's cultivation sects did not take a hardline approach toward purging powerful demon factions like the Ancient Kingdom did.

Even Tongtian Tower, the most prestigious alchemy force among humans, had many demon beast disciples and collaborated with numerous demon beast clans.

Had Ling Quan known that Lin Jing came from the Demon Court, he would have never agreed to help the Tai Xuan Sect issue a bounty on him.

Now, he was stuck dealing with the aftermath of that mess.

"Senior Ling Quan, there's no need to dwell on it. I'll be leaving with the Holy Maiden soon," Lin Jing reassured him. "Here’s the Gluttonous Pill I owed you."

Lin Jing handed over the repayment with a smile.

Even though nearly a year had passed inside the Hourglass, barely any time had passed in the real world, so there was no accumulated interest.

"Besides, I have another favor to ask of you, Senior Ling Quan."

"Speak freely, my friend," Ling Quan replied.

Lin Jing pulled out several storage bags.

"These belong to Tai Xuan Sect disciples. There are also two from cultivators who perished in the Cavern Heaven… I’ve already taken the Spirit Stones, but the rest—pills, magical artifacts, and various miscellaneous resources—I’d like you to convert them all into Spirit Stones for me."

Ling Quan cleared his throat.

Dealing with stolen goods, huh…

Under normal circumstances, that wouldn’t be an issue.

However, he was very curious about which factions these storage bags originally belonged to.

As he swept through them with his divine sense, his heart suddenly skipped a beat.

"So many specialties from the Eastern Sea… and a Sea Clan trident? Wait, kid—did you kill a Sea Clan prodigy?"

"And these Thunder Pearls… This storage bag is marked with the Lei Clan insignia from Martial Emperor City! You took down a genius from the Lei Clan?!"

"Can you handle it?" Lin Jing asked casually.

Ling Quan took a deep breath.

"It should be fine… The Sea Clan mainly lives on islands across the four great oceans and rarely ventures onto the mainland."

"Martial Emperor City is located west of the Ancient Kingdom, while Qingzhou is to the east. The geographical distance is vast, so the risk of handling this should be minimal."

"You want everything converted into Spirit Stones?"

Although both the Sea Clan and the Lei Clan were formidable, Lin Jing had the Demon Court backing him—and from the looks of it, even the Demon Court Holy Maiden valued him highly.

So Ling Quan wisely chose not to ask too many questions.

"Yes. Convert it all into Spirit Stones, and then use them to help me purchase three specific types of spiritual plants."

Lin Jing had been short on Spirit Stones earlier, but now, after looting three cultivators’ storage bags, he was suddenly flush with wealth.

It seemed that…

Robbery wasn’t much slower than alchemy when it came to making money.

No wonder rogue cultivators loved to kill and loot—even in the heavily regulated Ancient Kingdom, it was impossible to eradicate completely.

"Three Second-Tier Spiritual Plants:

Royal Spirit Ginseng

Withered Immortal Herb

Earth-Devouring Tree

One hundred portions of each, plus seeds."

Ling Quan nodded. "No problem."

Though he agreed readily, he couldn’t help but feel puzzled.

What was Lin Jing planning to do with these plants?

Alchemy?

All three plants had Earth-Wood dual attributes in common.

Royal Spirit Ginseng reinforced an unstable foundation and thickened True Energy, making it a top-tier resource for Earth attribute cultivators.

Withered Immortal Herb was a toxic plant from desert regions, shaped like a human hand and covered in venomous needles. Even a single prick would cause rapid dehydration and death.

Earth-Devouring Tree was a malignant plant that drained nutrients from the soil at an extreme rate, rendering a vast area barren.

However, despite its rapid growth, its wood and fruit were utterly useless—bitter, tough, and devoid of nutrients.

Lin Jing specifically ordered large quantities of these three plants…

Ling Quan couldn’t figure it out.

Apart from Royal Spirit Ginseng, the other two were basically useless for alchemy—he had never heard of any pill requiring Withered Immortal Herb or Earth-Devouring Tree...

One Day Later

Ling Quan swiftly prepared everything for Lin Jing.

Now, as Lin Jing prepared to depart with Senior Sister Xu Qingyao and Senior Brother Ouyang for the Third Holy Maiden’s Palace, his Pine Leaf Mouse was already hard at work in his Beast Taming Space…

It had begun fusing the three Earth-Wood spiritual plants!

"Hey! Hey! Hey—"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 40: The Terrifying Strength of the Little Fire Dragon
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 40: The Terrifying Strength of the Little Fire Dragon

The Royal Spirit Ginseng.

After fusion, its form changed from an ordinary ginseng root to that of a small humanoid ginseng doll—a rather unsettling sight.

[Mutated Royal Spirit Ginseng: If consumed by a pregnant woman, her offspring will be born with an Innate Earth Spirit Body.]

The Withered Immortal Herb.

After fusion, it transformed into a brown cactus, with its sharp spines turning pink.

[Mutated Withered Immortal Herb: If all its spines are inserted into one’s acupoints during cultivation, the body will mummify and transform into a corpse cultivator.]

The Earth-Devouring Tree.

After fusing one hundred small, lush saplings, the result was a single grayish tree seedling.

Inside the Beast Taming Space, the Pine Leaf Mouse sat on the ground, drenched in sweat, panting heavily as it stared blankly at the three mutated plants, deep in thought.

Meanwhile, in the outside world…

Lin Jing was seated cross-legged atop a quasi-Demon Emperor-level giant bird, equally silent.

Holy sh*t...

He could feel that the results of this synthesis were something special.

Although the mutations of the Royal Spirit Ginseng and Withered Immortal Herb were still somewhat niche, the Earth-Devouring Tree’s mutated ability was completely unexpected!

After all, his Glazed Immortal Eyes usually mutated ordinary plants into malignant plants and malignant plants into demonic plants…

Yet this time, a malignant plant had instead mutated into a top-tier spiritual plant!

That was unprecedented!

"Is this what they call…?" Lin Jing struggled to find the right phrase to describe his emotions.

The Royal Spirit Ginseng Doll was obviously not suitable as an organ transplant for the Pine Leaf Mouse.

However, its ability to mass-produce Innate Spirit Bodies was undeniably impressive.

Even though Innate Spirit Bodies were considered one of the more basic special physiques in cultivation, they were still highly valuable.

Any cultivator with an Innate Spirit Body had a 100% chance of reaching Foundation Establishment and a high probability of achieving Core Formation.

Although Nascent Soul breakthrough rates remained low, a sect or family that could consistently produce Core Formation cultivators would grow immensely powerful over time.

One or two Innate Earth Spirit Body cultivators weren’t a big deal.

But if an entire family or sect had hundreds of them, their collective power—especially when using Earth-element formation techniques—would be terrifyingly strong.

As for the Mutated Withered Immortal Herb, that was even less suitable for the Pine Leaf Mouse.

It was essentially a method of creating corpse cultivators.

Lin Jing already had Blood Devil Vines for Blood Path summoning techniques, and his Ice Soul Gu was more than enough for controlling undead creatures.

With those two at his disposal, he had no need for another corpse manipulation method.

But the Mutated Earth-Devouring Tree… now that was something special.

Gold, Wood, Water, Fire, Earth.

The Pine Leaf Mouse was already fully optimized in almost every way:

Gold-Wood attribute: Diamondwood granted it immense defense.

Innate Wood attribute: Boosted its vitality and regeneration.

Water-Wood attribute: Mutated Bloodsucking Vines gave it Blood Path summoning abilities, allowing it to command an army of blood beasts to deplete enemies.

Fire-Wood attribute: Explosive Fruit allowed it to unleash devastating fire-element self-destruction techniques.

With offense, defense, regeneration, and summoning all covered, the only missing piece was speed—but that didn’t matter.

The Pine Leaf Mouse had innate space magic to handle that.

So rather than focusing on combat, giving it the ability to manipulate underground spiritual veins and alter cultivation environments seemed like a valuable strategic addition.

"I wonder… If this tree becomes part of the Pine Leaf Mouse's organs, could it plant entire forests inside the Hourglass dimension?"

Lin Jing decided to stop fusing for now and simply use the plant for alchemy research.

"What are you mumbling about?"

On the back of the giant bird, Xu Qingyao suddenly spoke.

The bird was extremely powerful, but it lacked intelligence—it wasn’t a spirit beast, just a puppet mount.

It seemed like it wasn’t her contracted beast, but merely a slave-controlled ride.

Lin Jing slowly turned to her.

"Nothing. Just chatting with my pet beast, bonding a little."

Xu Qingyao gave him a thoughtful look.

"We’re almost at our destination. Let me ask you something."

"Go ahead, Senior Sister."

"Do your pet beasts have cultivation techniques?"

Lin Jing shook his head.

"Techniques suitable for demon beasts are hard to come by. Only one of my pets, a spirit squirrel, has one. It was passed down from the Guardian Deity of Tiandu Peak in the Beast Taming Sect.

The other two… they have no cultivation techniques."

"I see," Xu Qingyao nodded.

"In the Ancient Kingdom, there are very few cultivation techniques available for demon beasts.

However, in the Demon Court, we have an extensive collection of techniques specifically designed for demon beasts!"

"Since your Little Fire Dragon still doesn’t have a technique, you should pick one for it when we arrive.

It’ll greatly enhance its strength and help it form a stronger Monster Core."

"As for your Ice Soul Gu… The Gu God Sect’s Ten Great Gu have special life origins—they aren’t true demon beasts.

Because of that, the Demon Court doesn’t have cultivation techniques suitable for them.

But they don’t need techniques either.

When the Gu God Sect created them, they were already designed to be immortal."

"In other words, their ability to reincarnate upon death—to reform somewhere else in the world—is their innate technique.

It’s essentially a form of Immortal Rebirth."

"I see," Lin Jing nodded, his eyes lighting up.

"Senior Sister, does that mean you can just freely choose techniques from the Demon Court’s treasure vault?"

Xu Qingyao gave him a calm look.

"As the Holy Maiden, I have immense authority.

Most techniques are available upon request, and I can pass them down to my followers."

"This is exactly why many powerful demon beasts willingly follow the Holy Maidens—to secure powerful techniques for themselves and their descendants.

For example, a Demon Emperor once pledged allegiance to me just so I could use my authority to obtain a top-tier demon beast cultivation technique for its offspring.

To put it simply—if you can offer them benefits, they’ll follow you."

She then gave Lin Jing a knowing glance.

"Speaking of which, since you seem so confident in your abilities, would you be willing to put on a show when we arrive?"

"I’ve stationed Beast Taming Sect cultivators in this place, nurturing their disciples and diverting resources away from other followers.

Many of them are already unhappy.

When you arrive, you’ll be taking even more resources.

Their resentment will only grow.

If you want to secure both cultivation resources and access to techniques, you’ll need to shut them up with overwhelming strength.

What do you think?"

Lin Jing chuckled.

"You just want to see how strong I am, don’t you?"

"No problem. I can take on any opponent below the Nascent Soul Stage."

Xu Qingyao blinked.

"Your Little Fire Dragon hasn’t even reached Core Formation yet, right?"

Lin Jing smirked.

"You forgot about the Solar False Core I gave to Senior Brother Ouyang."

"My Little Fire Dragon trained with it as well—so it’s already at a Core Formation equivalent level."

With five types of Exotic Fire, rare beast abilities, Demon Emperor bloodline, and a top-tier False Core technique—

The Little Fire Dragon was, without a doubt, the strongest of his three pet beasts.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 41: Queyu and Heding
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 41: Queyu and Heding

"Welcome back, Holy Maiden."

Outside the Demon Court's Holy Maiden Palace, two heavily armed Giant Apes guarding the entrance immediately greeted Xu Qingyao as the enormous puppet bird descended.

At the same time, their gazes shifted toward the two figures following behind her—Ouyang Hao and Lin Jing.

A new follower…?

Was this human a newly recruited subordinate?

Was he a pill master or a Beast Taming cultivator?

Humans were actually quite common within the Demon Court.

Unlike other demon beast factions, which prioritized power struggles, the Demon Court's goal was the revival of the demon race.

This had also led to the rise of human fanatics who were obsessed with demon supremacy.

Thus, every Holy Maiden had a number of human followers in her ranks.

The First Holy Maiden preferred collecting male cultivators with special physiques.

The Second Holy Maiden sought out geniuses skilled in innovating techniques and spells.

The Third Holy Maiden, Xu Qingyao, particularly favored alchemists and cultivators of the Beast Taming Art.

If they were an alchemist, she would assign them to study Transformation Pills.

If they were a Beast Taming genius, she would convince them to switch to the Beast Taming Art—complete with a pre-arranged battle beast.

Xu Qingyao turned to one of the Giant Apes and commanded:

"Great Ape, notify all members of Queyu to meet me at Zhongshen Mountain."

After issuing her order, she led Lin Jing and Ouyang Hao into the Holy Maiden Palace.

"Queyu"? What’s that?"

As they walked, Lin Jing turned to Senior Brother Ouyang and asked.

"It’s a group made up of the most talented followers of Senior Sister," Ouyang explained.

"They include both humans and demon beasts, but most of them are descendants of Demon Emperors—the offspring of those powerful demon beasts who serve as Senior Sister’s guardians.

Of course, there are also two humans with high-level demon beast bloodlines.

In short, every single one of them has the potential to reach the Nascent Soul Stage.

Under Senior Sister’s guidance and with the Demon Court’s resources, Queyu will likely become an elite Nascent Soul battle group in the future!"

Lin Jing nodded.

"Sounds impressive… So these are the ones we’re about to beat up, right?"

Ouyang twitched slightly.

At least pretend to respect them, bro.

"Ahem… Aside from Queyu, Senior Sister also has another group called Heding," Ouyang added.

"It consists entirely of alchemy geniuses, both humans and demon beasts, and they specialize in studying Transformation Pills."

Xu Qingyao, who was walking ahead, spoke without turning back:

"And yet, you disciples from the Beast Taming Sect couldn’t even qualify for either group.

Because of that, my decision to take you in has caused quite a bit of controversy."

"Uh…" Ouyang Hao grimaced.

"Senior Sister, the Beast Taming Sect is just a third-rate sect in the Ancient Kingdom… Even if the true disciples from the sect came, they still wouldn’t stand a chance against the monsters in Queyu."

He suddenly grinned and patted Lin Jing on the shoulder.

"But now, it doesn’t matter—because he’s here!"

That’s the guy who took the title of Number One Prodigy of the Ancient Kingdom with his Longevity Body!

Zhongshen Mountain

With the Third Holy Maiden’s summons, figures began emerging from their cave dwellings, descending upon the mountain.

"Strange… Why did the Holy Maiden suddenly call us here?"

Standing atop the mountain peak, a golden-maned young Lion Emperor muttered curiously.

Although only at the early Demon Core Stage, its Emperor-tier bloodline radiated a terrifying presence.

"No idea. Maybe there’s a newcomer joining us?"

Another figure approached—a white-haired youth with scars on his face.

Despite appearing human, he carried a strong wolf-like aura.

"If that’s the case, then our resources are about to be split up again."

He sighed before suddenly grinning perversely.

"I hope it’s a demon beast and not another human.

I wish I could be the Holy Maiden’s pet rather than contracting a beast myself..."

A voice scoffed from above.

"Disgusting. With your measly strength, you think you’re worthy of being the Holy Maiden’s pet?

Even I wouldn’t want you as mine."

A blue phoenix perched atop a tree, speaking in a female voice.

She was a divine bird from the Phoenix Clan, possessing an Emperor-tier bloodline.

"Shut up, stupid bird!" the white-haired youth snapped.

As the bickering continued, a total of four demon beasts and two humans with beast bloodlines gathered on the mountain.

All four demon beasts had Emperor-tier bloodlines—which was absolutely insane.

And the two humans had incredibly high-level demon beast bloodlines as well…

Suddenly—

"SILENCE!"

A voice rang out, and three figures descended in streaks of light—

Xu Qingyao, Lin Jing, and Ouyang Hao.

The Queyu members immediately quieted down and greeted her.

"Holy Maiden."

Xu Qingyao swept her gaze across them.

"Why are we missing one?"

Among the assembled members, there was:

A golden-maned young Lion Emperor

A blue-feathered young Phoenix

A black-haired young Ox Emperor

A red-haired young Ape Emperor

A white-haired wolf-blooded youth

A bear-blooded woman with a massive chest

"That guy probably sneaked out again," the black ox muttered lazily.

Xu Qingyao’s expression remained indifferent.

"He left the palace without reporting to me?

I’ll deal with him later.

For now, you six will suffice.

Let me introduce a newcomer."

She gestured toward Lin Jing.

"He is a Fire Dragon Alchemist and may become a member of Queyu."

The bear-blooded woman, still munching on dried meat, blinked in surprise.

"Huh?"

Her large chest heaved slightly as she stared blankly at Lin Jing.

"This guy… doesn’t even look stronger than the sunlight boy next to him.

And that sunlight boy is way below Queyu’s standards.

Can he really keep up?"

Ouyang Hao’s eyes twitched.

"What the hell did I do to deserve this?!"

"Just you wait! I’ll surpass all of you someday!"

The white-haired wolf youth narrowed his eyes in suspicion.

"Wait… An alchemist?

Shouldn’t he be in Heding?

Why is he joining Queyu?"

The other demon beast descendants also frowned.

"He’s only at mid Foundation Establishment…"

"Is he a Beast Taming cultivator?"

If he was a Beast Taming cultivator, then his personal realm didn’t matter—his battle beasts' strength did.

Xu Qingyao cut them off.

"Ask what’s necessary. Otherwise, shut up."

She turned to Lin Jing.

"Fire Dragon Alchemist, joining Queyu means you’ll have access to the Demon Court’s resources.

However, membership requires passing a test.

You must defeat at least one current Queyu member.

The more you defeat, the better your resources and treatment will be.

If you defeat the strongest member, you will become Queyu’s leader in my absence.

Now—choose your opponent."

Lin Jing glanced at the six formidable figures before him.

Then he asked casually—

"Holy Maiden, who is the current leader of Queyu?"

At his words, five members immediately narrowed their eyes.

This newcomer… sure was bold.

Behind them, a massive black ox, lying on the ground, lazily opened its eyes.

"Human boy, newcomers should act like newcomers.

Your ambition is too high."

Lin Jing smirked.

"Then I choose you."

He paused, studying the ox.

"A Dali Ox Demon… huh?

That’s a rare bloodline."

The Dali Ox Demon slowly stood up, its black eyes locking onto Lin Jing.

"Foolish choice.

But since you were brought by the Holy Maiden, I won’t eat you.

Even though… you do look tasty."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 42: Demonic Arts, Divine Dragon Transformation
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 42: Demonic Arts, Divine Dragon Transformation

"It’s called Niu Di, from the Great Strength Bull Demon Clan, the son of the Bull Demon King. Mid Demon Core Stage cultivation, and currently the strongest member of the Sparrowfeather Squad.

"If you want to challenge it, think carefully." Xu Qingyao spoke.

Lin Jing nodded indifferently. This one would do.

He had already concealed his Longevity Body, yet this big black bull had the audacity to say he looked tasty? Unforgivable.

"Interesting..." The Bull Captain shifted from lying down to standing on all fours, his thunderous voice rumbling, "Kid, I’ll give you three moves first. Don’t say I bullied you."

"The captain’s getting serious..." The other Sparrowfeather members shrugged, thinking this Fire Dragon Alchemist was simply asking for trouble. The Great Strength Bull Demons were born as battle gods—an infant calf’s single horn could shatter mountains. This guy was the only bloodline heir of the Bull Demon King, his power was terrifying beyond compare. Even among them, not a single late-stage Demon Core cultivator was his match.

"Three moves, huh... Then don’t go back on your word. I only just learned this move, so I’m not fully familiar with it yet..." Lin Jing nodded and directly activated the Fusion Secret Art through the Beast Taming Art.

Boom!

During this process, Lin Jing's cultivation realm also underwent a transformation. From mid Foundation Establishment, he instantly surged to a level comparable to Core Formation. A dazzling false core, radiant like the sun, emerged in his dantian.

"Roar..." Lin Jing let out a low growl.

Within the blazing light, his body grew immense and powerful. Every inch of his muscles seemed to have been tempered a thousand times over, brimming with explosive strength. Red scales covered his body, burning like the sun, flashing with a dangerous and mysterious radiance.

"It's the Fusion Secret Art of the Beast Taming Sect! So the Holy Maiden really passed this technique to him!"

Seeing Lin Jing was indeed a Beast Taming cultivator, the other members of Sparrowfeather gasped.

"This guy isn’t simple. His contracted beast is actually from the Royal Blood Dragon Clan! A beast like that actually submitted to a human?"

"A dragon clan beast, so what..." The Bull Captain laughed heartily, not taking it seriously, nor did he interrupt the slow fusion process. Lin Jing’s transformation was indeed impressive, but to him, it was nothing remarkable.

However, in the next second, his laughter suddenly stopped.

A wave of intense heat radiated from Lin Jing’s body.

Sizzle—

In an instant, a terrifying heat scorched his entire body, the scent of roasted meat filling the mountain peak.

It was just a simple "temperature exchange," yet the Bull Captain let out a wretched scream.

"Moo—!"

His body sizzled with oil, and in sheer terror, he stomped backward, leaping over ten meters away. His Demon Core shone brightly as he instinctively activated his core’s divine ability.

Bones and muscles twisted, heaven and earth rumbled—he transformed into a towering minotaur. His physical strength nearly doubled.

Meanwhile, the ground where he had stood, centered around Lin Jing, had already melted into magma.

The searing heatwave threatened to dissolve the entire mountain.

The other Sparrowfeather members, all wide-eyed in shock, retreated hastily, feeling the unbearable heat.

"What kind of flame is this? The temperature is insane!"

"You idiots, it’s the False Core Technique..."

The most shocked of all was Ouyang Hao. He instantly recognized it—this was the same high-temperature technique Lin Jing had passed onto him!

But he never expected Lin Jing to unleash it with such terrifying power.

The Bull Captain had been forced back by heat alone. Without his core’s divine ability to protect him, he would’ve turned into roast beef already!

"Captain, how do you like your meat cooked?"

Dragon-formed Lin Jing teased with a grin, raising his palm.

In the Bull Captain’s horrified gaze, a multicolored flaming handprint descended from the sky.

Its monstrous heat and devastating force—no matter how much the black bull used his Demon Core divine ability, it was impossible to resist. His body felt like it was being dehydrated, his very soul on the verge of being roasted.

"Bro, slow down with your divine ability! This captain... is done for—"

A smaller white bull from the squad shuddered.

Boom!

The flaming hand halted inches above the Bull Captain, never making direct contact.

Yet, just from the residual heat, his body was nearly cooked.

Even Xu Qingyao licked her lips, feeling a sudden craving for roasted beef.

Crushing.

The other Sparrowfeather members widened their eyes.

They were certain—if that flame palm had landed, the Bull Captain would be dead.

Their shock ran even deeper.

Even the personal disciples of the Demon Ancestors themselves... would they have such overwhelming power?

"....."

"Maybe with this Holy Son, we can actually compete against the other two..."

Xu Qingyao’s beautiful eyes gleamed with excitement.

The Sparrowfeather Squad wasn’t weak.

But compared to the two Holy Sons of the Demon Court, who had spent years accumulating power, they were nothing.

The First and Seventh Holy Sons—both had Demon Ancestor inheritance and even past inheritors as followers!

"He actually smells pretty good."

Lin Jing quickly reverted to his normal form.

The Fusion Secret Art consumed a massive amount of energy.

And simultaneously fusing seven different exotic flames to enhance his high-temperature technique took an even greater toll.

Yet when he glanced at the little Bull Prince, seeing a mid Demon Core Stage cultivator with a royal bloodline utterly subdued, he knew—

No matter how high the cost, this move was worth it.

"My blood feels like it’s about to ignite..."

He muttered to himself.

Initially, he had contracted the Dragonblood Carp to balance the Ice Soul Gu’s power.

Now, it seemed like he needed the Ice Soul Physique to cool himself down.

Xu Qingyao spoke, "From now on, Fire Dragon Alchemist will be your captain. If anyone is dissatisfied, challenge him yourself."

No one objected.

None of the Sparrowfeather members wanted to be roasted alive.

This guy wasn’t an alchemist—he was a chef!

After Lin Jing officially became the new captain of Sparrowfeather, the squad departed.

Meanwhile, Senior Brother Ouyang was deeply shaken.

Determined, he resolved to make the Fox Demoness, Hong’er, cultivate the Solar False Core Technique.

Then, he too fused with her—double cores shining brilliantly!

Lin Jing, on the other hand, received a heavy reward from Senior Sister Tian.

The eldest senior sister beamed with joy.

"You really weren’t bluffing. With power like this, you truly are the number one prodigy of the Ancient Celestial Kingdom..."

"When the Holy Maiden Tea Ceremony comes, the followers of the other two Holy Maidens and their Demon Ancestor heirs—I'll leave them to you!"

Lin Jing remained unfazed.

"Senior Sister, just remember to give me the technique. Little Fire Dragon’s power still has room to grow."

Xu Qingyao laughed.

"Of course, I’ve already decided which one is best for it."

"Ouyang mentioned it was originally a Dragonblood Carp from the Beast Taming Sect, right? But now, it’s already successfully leaped over the Dragon Gate.

"Yet just now, beyond its dragon aura, I also sensed Kirin Flame from your beast fire.

"Does it also have Kirin bloodline?"

"That’s right," Lin Jing nodded.

"In that case, compared to ordinary fire-attribute demonic arts, I think Divine Dragon Transformation would suit it better.

"With its flames, none of the Demon Court’s fire arts could compare."

Lin Jing fell silent.

After all, this was a divine flame ability from an Immortal’s inheritance.

"What is Divine Dragon Transformation?"

"A top-tier demonic beast cultivation technique that refines bloodline transformation abilities, perfect for dragon-blooded beasts.

"Most dragon-blooded beasts have complex ancestry with diverse forms.

"By cultivating Divine Dragon Transformation, they can master their ancestral power and possess four beast forms—

(Normal Form): Best balance of attributes, optimal energy control, and fastest cultivation speed.

(Attack Form): Attack power multiplied several times, but defense and speed reduced.

(Defense Form): Physical durability multiplied several times, but attack and speed weakened.

(Speed Form): Speed multiplied several times, but attack and defense lowered.

"By cultivating Divine Dragon Transformation, it can train multiple forms—Carp Form, Divine Dragon Form, Kirin Form—each with extreme attributes, shifting forms to adapt to various battle and non-battle scenarios."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 43: Making Big News
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 43: Making Big News

"I see, Divine Dragon Transformation, huh... It certainly is an interesting technique." Lin Jing nodded after hearing the explanation.

"It does seem to suit it..."

Normal Form: Balanced attributes, strongest control, fastest cultivation speed. Since there was no need for daily combat, it could remain in this form most of the time.

Its daily tasks were alchemy and cultivation, and alchemy required precise control!

When battle was necessary, it could enter Attack Form, greatly enhancing the destructive power of its techniques.

As for Defense Form, it would work best in combination with Fusion, Beast-Weaponization, and other techniques to enhance Lin Jing’s own defenses as a Beast Tamer.

Finally, Speed Form would be the most effective for traveling.

"If there are no objections, I’ll go ahead and exchange for Divine Dragon Transformation," Xu Qingyao said. "Once it cultivates this technique, forming its core later will make it much stronger than naturally reaching Core Formation."

This Demon Court... was pretty great. If only he could bring it under the Beast Taming Sect.

After that, Lin Jing temporarily stayed at the Holy Maiden’s Demon Palace and was given his own cultivation cave.

The cultivation environment here was excellent, even better than the academy’s caves. No wonder the members of Sparrowfeather all chose to cultivate here.

With a stable cultivation environment, Lin Jing first studied the Chameleon Demon Core and became proficient in controlling its enhancement abilities. Then, he began consuming Hidden Dragon Fortune to deduce the formula for Five-Element Treant Pills.

This involved modifying Reflective Diamondwood, Bloodsucking Devil Vine, Exploding Firefruit, and Earth Vein Spirit Tree into organ-like structures for the Pine Leaf Mouse.

The day those major organs were refined into its body would also be the day it reached Core Formation.

However, after starting the deduction of these four Treant Pills, Lin Jing encountered a problem—his Hidden Dragon Fortune was running low.

He had already spent a massive amount on learning illusions, deducing the Solar False Core, cultivating Beast Taming techniques, and Three Heads, Six Arms.

Now, as time passed, he had gone too long without using his "real identity" to stir things up, leading to a slower acquisition of Hidden Dragon Fortune.

At this rate, it might not be enough to complete the four pills, let alone the later Transformation Pill or Heavenly Demon Physique cultivation, both of which would require fortune-based deduction.

"I need a way to gather more Hidden Dragon Fortune..."

Lin Jing fell into deep thought.

"...I got it!"

Suddenly, his eyes lit up with an idea.

"The decline in fame is clearly because I haven't appeared in too long. But my real body is in the Demon Court... As long as I send a clone out to make some waves, people will start talking about me again!"

"Perfect timing—Pine Leaf Mouse has been learning Wood Clone Technique, and with my Chameleon Demon Core-enhanced illusions, I can send an illusionary clone and a wood clone to stir up some chaos."

He started brainstorming what major events were happening in Qingzhou.

Something with as much attention as the Alchemy Conference in a few years—something with maximum exposure!

Since it was just a clone, there was no fear of death.

"If I can’t think of anything, I’ll just ask Senior Sister later. For now, I’ll prepare the clone."

Summoning Pine Forest Jing, he discussed the plan with it. The little green-eyed Pine Leaf Mouse initially nodded, but then furiously shook its head!

Everlasting Emperor’s Longevity Art had reached minor mastery, allowing it to create Wood Clones!

According to Senior Pine, wood clones were much stronger than ordinary clone techniques.

However, they had a major flaw!

This flaw was precisely why Pine Leaf Mouse had never made up its mind to create a bunch of clones to help tend its crops!

"What’s the problem? We have long lifespans anyway. Worst case, I’ll refine an Longevity Pill for you with Longevity Blood later." Lin Jing reassured it.

The Pine Leaf Mouse was hesitant because creating Wood Clones consumed a significant amount of life essence.

Though it didn’t directly reduce lifespan, depleting too much life essence at once could still shorten one’s life.

Of course... the more life essence consumed, the stronger the Wood Clones would be.

"Sigh..." Pine Leaf Mouse hesitated for a moment but eventually sighed and agreed.

After all, this was to accumulate reputation fortune for itself, aiding in the deduction of the Treant Pills.

It decided—it would sacrifice two years of lifespan to create a Wood Clone.

Lifespan was also part of its hoarding obsession... naturally, it didn’t want to waste too much.

"That’s enough." Lin Jing grinned.

Without further delay, Pine Leaf Mouse consumed a massive amount of life essence and spawned a delicate Wood Squirrel and a Wooden Humanoid Clone.

Visually, these wooden clones looked nothing like Pine Leaf Mouse or Lin Jing. Their physical composition was entirely different.

However, compared to other clone techniques, these Wood Clones were significantly superior—not only were they brimming with vitality, making them look more like living beings, but they also inherited most of Pine Leaf Mouse’s spells.

Apart from Glazed Immortal Eyes, abilities like Spatial Flying Leaf Technique were all passed down!

"...Now, let me enhance them further."

Lin Jing formed hand seals, activating the Chameleon Demon Core, imbuing the clones with two illusions strengthened by a royal-grade demon core.

These two illusions, naturally, were the Pine Leaf Mouse Illusion and the Lin Jing Illusion.

Crafting other illusions required effort, but conjuring his own and Pine Leaf Mouse’s was effortless—no one understood them better than themselves.

After receiving souls through Soul Infusion Art, the Wood Squirrel and Wooden Humanoid Clone transformed once again, taking on Lin Jing’s and Pine Leaf Mouse’s true appearances.

Indistinguishable from the real ones.

Their aura was identical to their originals.

"Still not enough."

Lin Jing continued casting.

"Inverse Void Art!"

With a low chant, the illusionary souls of the wood clones turned tangible, exuding an overwhelming presence.

More realistic. More powerful.

"So real... No wonder Elder Zheng from Thousand Illusions Mountain said that illusions can not only transform into beasts but also into beautiful Dao partners!"

Several days later.

Xu Qingyao came to check on Lin Jing and tossed him a demon bone inscribed with runes.

"Divine Dragon Transformation is imprinted within. Crush it to receive the inheritance memory. However, this demon bone has restrictions—after learning Divine Dragon Transformation, you cannot pass it down to others through any form of writing, speech, or transmission. It’s the Demon Court’s way of protecting techniques."

"Understood." Lin Jing nodded.

Most special secret techniques had similar restrictions.

For instance, Inverse Void Art of Thousand Illusions Mountain could only be learned at specific locations within the sect.

The Core Inheritance Techniques of the Beast Taming Sect could only be studied alongside the Beast Taming Art.

The Hidden Dragon Secret Arts of Hidden Dragon Academy could only be transmitted via academy elders using special artifacts.

"Roar!"

Little Fire Dragon excitedly flew out from the pendant on Lin Jing’s meditation staff—it had been waiting for this technique for a long time!

Crack.

It grabbed the demon bone with its claws and crushed it.

In an instant, the inheritance memory of Divine Dragon Transformation flooded its mind.

The complex information made it immediately fly off to the side, closing its eyes in deep absorption.

Seeing this, Lin Jing turned to Senior Sister.

"Are there any major upcoming events in Qingzhou?"

"What for?" Xu Qingyao asked.

"I want to make some big news."

Lin Jing extended his hand, making a tight fist.

Xu Qingyao: ?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 44: Two Great Forms
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 44: Two Great Forms

“What the hll kind of big news is this?”

Xu Qingyao shot an exasperated look at Lin Jing.

Did this guy even understand his own situation? And he still wanted to make big news?

Wasn’t he being extra cautious before? He didn’t even dare to show up at the Demon Court. What the hll changed?

“It’s like this.” Lin Jing explained, “I study alchemy and usually rely on the Hidden Dragon Academy’s Hidden Dragon Secret Arts to deduce formulas. But since I haven’t revealed my true identity for too long, my Hidden Dragon Fortune has almost run out. Now, if I want to research the Transformation Pill, I have to make the name ‘Lin Jing’ spread widely across Qingzhou again.”

“I’ve created two clones to move around Qingzhou in my place, so safety shouldn’t be an issue.”

As he finished speaking, Lin Jing, dressed in his Firecloud Alchemist Robes, summoned two clones—one resembling himself and the other a Pine Leaf Mouse.

The first clone wore a pristine white robe, with a gourd hanging from his waist, looking incredibly handsome—exactly how he had appeared during the Genius Rankings Battle.

“This…” Xu Qingyao looked at the two clones, a little stunned. She couldn’t tell they were clones at all. More than that, she could sense immense power radiating from them—enough to rival an average Core Formation cultivator without any issue.

“Illusions? But the texture feels…” She reached out to touch the Pine Leaf Mouse clone.

“They’re created using Thousand Illusions Mountain’s Soul Infusion Art and Inverse Void Art, enhanced by the Chameleon Demon Core’s illusion ability. So, for a certain period, these clones can fight just as well as we can. Their only drawback is endurance—they can’t sustain long battles. If they take too much damage, they’ll dissipate quickly.”

“I see… Since they’re just clones, there’s no problem. And they’re such flawless illusionary clones at that.” Xu Qingyao relaxed, agreeing with Lin Jing’s plan to send them out to stir things up. After all, this was a necessary step in researching the Transformation Pill. She thought for a moment and said, “Speaking of major events in Qingzhou…”

She pondered before continuing, “What exactly do you plan to do? There are plenty of grand occasions happening. For example, the wedding of the Piao Miao Dao’s celestial maiden from the Misty Immortal Clan. You’re not planning to crash the wedding, are you?”

“Uh… That’d be way too messed up. I’ll pass on that kind of event.” Lin Jing thought for a moment. “Regular incidents won’t generate enough attention. I want something bigger… a one-and-done kind of deal.”

“The Longevity Body—I’m going to reveal my Longevity Body.”

“Huh???” Xu Qingyao froze. “Are you serious? You’re not hiding it anymore?”

Lin Jing nodded. “At this point, I don’t care anymore. The secret’s not going to stay hidden forever. The Ancient Celestial Kingdom, the Yin Corpse Sect… too many people already know about my Longevity Body. It’s only a matter of time before it gets exposed. So instead of waiting, I might as well take the initiative, use it to gain fame and fortune, and strengthen myself ahead of time.”

“With my current situation, even if my identity is exposed, my true self won’t be in any real danger.”

“How do you plan to do it?”

“I’ll publicly announce my Longevity Body and use it as bait to strike back against the two major demonic sects.” Lin Jing smirked. “Since the Yin Corpse Sect can put a bounty on me, I can put a bounty on them too.”

“I’ll offer Longevity Blood as a reward to Qingzhou’s various demonic beast factions—anyone who kills a Yin Corpse Sect or Gu God Sect powerhouse will receive a drop of Longevity Blood. Ten years from now, I’ll distribute the rewards at a designated location.”

“Obviously, this bounty is full of loopholes. No one will actually believe I’ll keep my word or appear in public. But that doesn’t matter. The point is to make waves. A Foundation Establishment cultivator with a Longevity Body placing a bounty on two demonic sects—that’s the kind of thing that’ll spread like wildfire in Qingzhou. Not just among the human factions, but among the demonic beast factions as well. What do you think, Senior Sister?”

Xu Qingyao was stunned. “…As expected of a student from Hidden Dragon Academy. Ruthless.”

Suddenly, she thought of something and spoke up, “If he’s really decided on this, then an event hosted by demonic beasts would be a better fit than one hosted by humans.”

“Does he know about the Sky Lion Clan?”

“The Sky Lion Clan…” The Dragon Carp muttered, “The clan that raises Longevity Body cultivators?”

“Exactly. The Sky Lion Clan holds a grand ancestral worship ceremony every ten years. A major one every century, a minor one every decade. During the festival, they invite various clans to partake in Longevity delicacies.”

“If he insists on going through with this, then the Sky Lion Clan’s centennial ceremony would be the perfect occasion. The timing is just right—it’s less than a year away. The factions that attend will mostly be those interested in Longevity Blood and Flesh, including demonic beast clans and cultivation families that practice beast-blood techniques.”

“If he shows up there, he’s bound to make a name for himself in Qingzhou.”

Lin Jing pondered for a moment. It did seem like a good idea. He asked, “Will the Demon Court send strong representatives?”

“No.” Xu Qingyao shook her head. “The Demon Court and the Sky Lion Clan don’t get along. They walk different paths.”

“Oh…” Lin Jing nodded. The Sky Lion Clan… one of Qingzhou’s top-tier demonic beast clans.

The three greatest demonic beast forces near Qingzhou—Poseidon Palace, the Demon Court, and the Misty Empire—weren’t actually comprised of a single species. They were alliances of various demonic beast races, united under the leadership of a Demon Emperor. But outside of these three, there were independent demonic beast clans that stood on their own.

The Sky Lion Clan was one of them. And the Sky Lion Ancestor was considered the strongest beast ancestor beneath the three Demon Emperors.

“Alright, the Sky Lion Clan it is…” Lin Jing’s eyes gleamed with anticipation. He was curious—just how much would his Hidden Dragon Fortune increase after pulling off such a massive stunt?

At that moment, a familiar cry rang out nearby.

After absorbing the inheritance memories of the Divine Dragon Transformation, the Fire Qilin Dragon had reverted to its Dragon Carp form and was now swimming in confused circles.

“What’s wrong?” Lin Jing blinked and looked over.

“The Divine Dragon Transformation is extremely difficult to cultivate. Even reaching the entry-level is no easy feat. It must’ve run into trouble,” Xu Qingyao commented.

“Bo!!” The Dragon Carp’s big eyes widened as it explained its difficulties.

After some time, it had successfully formed two distinct forms:

Its normal form, the Dragon Carp form.

The Dragon Carp had solidified its most primitive, baseline shape as its standard form.

Additionally, it had created an attack form: the Divine Dragon form.

Dragons were a unique type of demonic beast. While other demonic beasts formed monster cores, dragons formed Dragon Pearls, which possessed immense power and were highly effective for combat—especially since the Dragon Carp planned to cultivate an Emperor Flame Dragon Pearl. Naturally, the dragon form was its best offensive shape.

Tracing its bloodline further, the Dragon Carp identified two more potential forms: one for defense and one for speed.

Its defense form was the Qilin form. The Qilin, also known as the Strange-Scaled Beast, had incredibly tough scales and immense longevity, making it the perfect shield.

As for its speed form, the Dragon Carp traced back to an even more ancient ancestor—an unknown winged, horse-like beast. Though it didn’t recognize this ancestor, its bloodline instincts told it that this form would be the fastest!

However, while it could easily shape its Dragon and Divine Dragon forms, no matter how hard it tried, it couldn’t form the Qilin or Pegasus forms.

“The bloodlines are too ancient—too difficult to awaken. Unlike its dragon heritage, which it has surely reinforced by consuming plenty of dragon-based resources,” Senior Sister said. “There are two options: train slowly over time, or…”

“I can take you to the Qilin Clan.”

“…Did you hear that?” Xu Qingyao turned to Lin Jing. But Lin Jing was lost in thought, his mind racing.
