“…Holy sh*t, so the White Dragon Horse was real? The Yinglong really gave birth to the Jianma, and the Jianma gave birth to the Qilin?!”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 45: Deep Cultivation of the Illusionary Path
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 45: Deep Cultivation of the Illusionary Path

“I'm listening.”

Lin Jing gave up the idea of riding and looked at Senior Sister.

“The Qilin Clan?”

“They are one of the most powerful top-tier demon clans within the Demon Court, not just the follower race of any Holy Maiden. Their heritage is deep, and they can influence the court’s decisions…”

“But if I visit the Qilin Clan as the Holy Maiden, they wouldn’t turn me away either. I mentioned before that I was looking for a Core Formation land for it. The Qilin Clan has many sacred sites that can assist with Core Formation.”

“Among these places, some can even awaken the Qilin bloodline within demon beasts. Training there, your fat fish should be able to cultivate a Qilin form in no time.” Xu Qingyao said.

“I see.” Lin Jing thought for a moment and shook his head. “Senior Sister, let’s hold off on that. Since the Qilin Clan is so powerful and not one of your followers, I’d rather let the Dragon Carp try to excavate its ancestral bloodline on its own first. If it really can’t, then we can go to the Qilin Clan later.”

“I only just obtained the Chameleon Demon Core. I’m not yet confident I can use illusions to deceive beings on the level of a Void Mystical Demon Ancestor.”

“That works too. It’s only just started practicing the Divine Dragon Transformation. Maybe with a bit of time, it’ll get the hang of it on its own.” Xu Qingyao nodded.

Having just settled into the Holy Maiden’s Demon Palace, Lin Jing wasn’t in the mood to rush around. There were still nine years left in the usage cycle of the Hourglass of Time. He planned to let the Dragon Carp train for a few years first. If it still couldn’t grasp the technique on its own, then he’d consider seeking external aid.

By then, the Dragon Carp could just follow Junior Sister to the Qilin Clan—I wouldn’t need to go myself!

After all, training techniques and Core Formation don’t necessarily require a Beast Tamer to be present.

Meanwhile, Peng Shi had to continue refining his illusion techniques. Lin Jing discovered that the Chameleon Demon Core wasn’t just useful for illusions; its wielder could use it to accelerate their mastery of illusions, and it could also break through them. To put it simply, it was an unparalleled artifact for the Illusionary Path.

Give me some time, and I’m confident I can push my illusions to the next level.

By then, I’ll have more confidence in fooling a Void Mystical Demon Ancestor completely.

Right now, there are still ten months until the Sky Lion Clan’s centennial event. I don’t need to rush off looking for trouble—cultivation comes first!

A while later.

Xu Qingyao left, and Huan Yin went back into seclusion, diving straight into the Hourglass of Time.

Before entering, Huan Yin let the Dragon Carp train in the Divine Dragon Transformation, while he called out to Song Linjing.

“Next, you’re going to focus on illusion training. Bring me some Phantom Mushrooms… Hmm… and also some Pre-Synthesis ‘Immortal Vision Mushrooms’.”

“…” Song Linjing froze.

Phantom Mushrooms, a seventh-tier spiritual plant, were a specialty of Thousand Illusions Mountain and a carefully selected ingredient in the sect’s dining hall. They were highly effective for improving Thousand Illusions Codex mastery and aiding illusion cultivation.

Back when Huan Yin infiltrated Thousand Illusions Mountain, he ate quite a few. However, as a special seventh-tier resource, they didn’t benefit him much, so Peng Shi had Song Linjing combine them back then just to see the effects.

Once synthesized, the Phantom Mushroom transformed from a spiritual plant that assisted in illusion training… into a Malignant Plant!

[Immortal Vision Mushroom: Upon consumption, the user will see an illusion of an Immortal imparting Dao teachings. There is a chance of becoming lost in the illusion forever, never awakening. If the user successfully awakens, their illusion mastery will improve drastically.]

Due to its bizarre effects, and given that Huan Yin had been in a dangerous location at the time, he didn’t dare to eat it.

If I eat this and fail to wake up, that’s going to be a huge problem. Even if I wake up, who knows how long I’d be trapped in the illusion? In such a place, it’s far too risky.

But now, in Junior Sister’s domain, with the Hourglass of Time and Chameleon Demon Core, Huan Yin felt it was worth trying out this Malignant Plant he had taken from Thousand Illusions Mountain.

“Junior Sister’s demon core also has illusion-breaking effects. If I’m lost in the illusion for too long, just use that to wake me up. Let’s set a limit of three days.”

“A Demon King-level demon core should be more than enough to break through a seventh-tier Malignant Plant’s illusion.” Huan Yin instructed Song Linjing.

“Got it!” Song Linjing assured him, then handed over a blue mushroom.

Lin Jing took a deep breath, sat cross-legged in the desert, and ate the Immortal Vision Mushroom.

…

When Lin Jing’s consciousness returned, he found himself in an Immortal Palace, surrounded by ten celestial beings, all gazing up at the sky, where a misty Immortal woman’s silhouette loomed above.

Illusions have form, their wonders endless. Between the immortal and the mundane, shadows flicker and fade. Within them lies form, the true spirits of all things. The myriad existences, born of this essence.

“Illusions have form…” From the celestial dome, the female Immortal began imparting Dao teachings. Her voice spread far and wide, and countless celestial beings followed along, chanting. The sound echoed into Lin Jing’s ears, instantly making him dazed—he could feel his Illusionary Path cultivation improving at an astonishing speed!

“Illusions have form…”

Unconsciously, Lin Jing also began chanting along, absorbing the profound Dao principles within the voices.

This must be the illusion.

This Immortal’s teaching… It really feels like it can rapidly enhance my illusion mastery. I won’t rush to leave just yet. Let’s see how long this illusion can last…

He felt confident that this illusion could not trap him.

Time passed.

Lin Jing had become the best performer among the celestial beings, his illusion mastery soaring to terrifying heights.

“Good.” The celestial woman spoke softly, and Lin Jing’s body slowly ascended, summoned before her.

“Your talent is remarkable. You may become my disciple and explore the Grand Dao alongside me.”

Lin Jing continued rising, breaking through the clouds, and gazed upon the celestial woman—only to find that she had the massive head of a squirrel.

Lin Jing: ?

Lin Jing snapped awake, instantly exiting the illusion—only to see that Pine Leaf Mouse was clutching a demon core and smashing it against his forehead, leaving his skin red from the impact.

“Three days already?” Lin Jing took a deep breath, feeling warmth surging through his body. The illusion’s insights weren’t entirely false—he had retained a large portion of the illusionary comprehension, now embedded within him.

“Eek!!” Pine Leaf Mouse nodded, confirming that the three days had passed.

Good, he woke up.

It had been worried that Lin Jing might have slept himself to death.

“Alright.” Lin Jing rubbed the mouse’s head and said, “Let’s go again. This time, I’ll break free immediately. If I don’t wake up, call me.”

Lin Jing ate another Immortal Vision Mushroom, drifting back into the illusionary world.

In the Immortal Palace, he was now surrounded by one hundred thousand carp, their round eyes filled with the desire for enlightenment, all staring upward at the sky—where a celestial squirrel loomed.

“…Damn, something’s wrong.”

Lin Jing’s heart sank.

This mushroom’s aftereffects are insane. Did I eat too many? Why is the Immortal gone… and replaced by a damn squirrel?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 46: The Prototype of the Spell Spirit!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 46: The Prototype of the Spell Spirit!

After a long while, Lin Jing actively broke free from the illusion, realizing that the Immortal Vision Mushroom couldn’t be consumed too frequently. It seemed there needed to be a gap before he could witness the Immortal imparting Dao teachings again.

Seeing Lin Jing emerge so quickly this time, Pine Leaf Mouse poked its head out—Do you want to eat another one?

It spread its paws—Even if you want to, we’re out. I didn’t stockpile many of these.

“Then hurry up and plant more.”

Lin Jing found the Immortal Vision Mushroom incredibly effective. His mastery of the Illusionary Path was already remarkable, yet this mushroom could still improve it significantly. As expected of a treasure synthesized using the Glazed Immortal Eyes.

“!”

Pine Leaf Mouse scurried back into the gourd to plant more mushrooms, while Lin Jing picked up the Chameleon Demon Core to consolidate his newly improved Illusionary Path cultivation.

Thus, as Pine Leaf Mouse continued supplying Immortal Vision Mushrooms, Lin Jing ate mushrooms and comprehended the power of the demon core…

Half a year passed in the outside world.

But inside the Hourglass of Time, Lin Jing had spent five years consuming Immortal Vision Mushrooms.

Having focused solely on illusion cultivation, he had almost lost his sense of time.

Until one day, five years later, Lin Jing's heart suddenly stopped—he realized the Chameleon Demon Core had been completely drained.

The once-mighty demon core crumbled into dust, scattering with the wind.

He couldn’t understand—why did a royal-tier demon core deplete so quickly?

“…Senior Sister isn’t going to scold me for this, right?”

But before he could dwell on it, he rubbed his eyes and noticed a strange sight—a floating, blue-green mushroom had appeared beside him.

He reached out—but couldn’t grab it.

It hovered, untouchable—similar to the Immortal Vision Mushroom, yet subtly different.

“…Wait, what is this?”

The demon core vanished, and this thing appeared in its place.

Lin Jing was certain this wasn’t an Immortal Vision Mushroom—Pine Leaf Mouse’s supply cycle was once a month, and there was still some time before the next batch.

So where did this come from?

“…Damn, did I eat too many mushrooms and end up with mental problems?”

He frowned slightly, but soon, realization dawned—his expression shifted rapidly, turning to delight.

“Could it be…?”

He took a deep breath and immediately activated the Thousand Transformations Technique—and in that instant, his energy resonated with the floating mushroom.

With a single thought—the blue-green mushroom transformed into Pine Leaf Mouse.

With another thought—it became Dragon Carp.

With yet another thought—it took the form of the Ice Soul Gu.

The transformations were so realistic that even Lin Jing, a Beast Tamer and Illusionist himself, struggled to distinguish the real from the fake.

HUM—!

In the next moment—

The blue-green mushroom morphed into a golden-clothed child, appearing no older than ten, an aura of Void Mysticism subtly radiating from its form, sending shockwaves through Lin Jing’s heart.

His eyes flashed—he finally understood what this was.

"A Spell Spirit!!!"

"After five years of training illusions with the Immortal Vision Mushroom and Chameleon Demon Core… I actually cultivated a Spell Spirit using the Thousand Transformations Technique!"

"Of course! The Thousand Transformations Technique was a technique I created through insights gained in Bodhi Heaven. It was the most likely technique to give birth to a Spell Spirit! And now, with so many illusion-based resources assisting my cultivation..."

Lin Jing was ecstatic—he never expected to actually cultivate a prototype Spell Spirit from the Thousand Transformations Technique.

No—wait.

“This isn’t a complete Spell Spirit yet.”

According to records, a true Spell Spirit should possess self-awareness, much like a living entity.

But right now, this blue-green mushroom spirit was still just a shell, linked to Lin Jing but requiring his direct control. It hadn’t yet gained independent consciousness.

“This should be the precursor to a Spell Spirit—the so-called Spirit Prototype. But even so, this means my Illusionary Path mastery is on the verge of perfecting the Thousand Transformations Technique. It’s practically an illusion pet that follows me around!”

No wonder the Chameleon Demon Core was suddenly drained—its energy must have been absorbed during the Spell Spirit’s birth.

Holding the Spell Spirit of the Thousand Transformations Technique—a floating mushroom—Lin Jing pressed his lips together.

He realized that illusions cast through the Spell Spirit were even slightly superior to those enhanced by the Chameleon Demon Core!

And on top of that, the demon core was an external power, while the Spell Spirit was his own—meaning this was a permanent gain.

Seems like I won't have to make excuses to Senior Sister…

But…

Spell Spirits usually took on a form related to their corresponding spell.

It was said that Spirit Dao Sect's True Master Xuanwu cultivated a Spell Spirit from his Xuanwu Shield, which took the shape of a tortoise.

Lin Jing himself had once fought Chen Zhong, a genius of Spirit Dao Sect, who had cultivated a Gravity Spell Spirit—which manifested as a miniature planet.

Yet in his case…

“Why… is my Thousand Transformations Spell Spirit a mushroom?”

“…Did I eat too many mushrooms over the years?”

Lin Jing fell into deep thought.

Logically speaking, the Thousand Transformations Technique was derived from insights into the Mutated Dragonbone Flower. If it had a Spell Spirit, it should look like a Fire Dragon Fruit, not… a mushroom.

Forget it—a mushroom is fine.

At least it’s not an Immortal Vision Fairy.

Summoning a mushroom during battle still looked somewhat respectable.

But if it had turned into a female Immortal, that would’ve been too damn weird.

…

Illusion Training Complete!

Lin Jing spent five years inside the Hourglass of Time, successfully cultivating a Spell Spirit, significantly advancing his Illusionary Path mastery.

With this level of illusion skill, he was confident that unless he faced a Void Mystical Stage or Demon Ancestor-level illusion expert, other Void Mystical or Demon Ancestor-level beings would struggle to see through his disguises!

And if the Spell Spirit eventually gained intelligence, it would be even more terrifying.

“…Without realizing it, half a year passed in the outside world. The Sky Lion Clan’s Centennial Grand Ceremony is getting closer. It’s about time I send out a clone.”

“This Hourglass of Time is ridiculous. Originally, I was the same age as my academy peers. But if I keep training inside it… my age might end up exceeding even Senior Sister and the Sect Master.”

After all—time flowed differently here.

Lin Jing did the math—he was already nearing fifty.

Half a year in the outside world, five years inside…

But even though he aged faster, his gains were enormous.

In just half a year of real-world time, he had successfully formed a Spell Spirit Prototype!

Before deploying his two illusion clones, Lin Jing strengthened his Wood Clone's illusionary soul using the blue-green mushroom…

A few days later—

Two figures, a man and a squirrel, both dressed in Daoist robes, soared toward the Sky Lion Clan’s territory beyond the Beast God Dao.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 47: The Qilin Tea Gathering
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 47: The Qilin Tea Gathering

Lin Jing had just sent off his illusionary form when a figure suddenly teleported in front of them.

"Finally making your move?"

Seeing Lin Jing finally release his clone to stir up trouble within the Sky Lion Clan, Xu Qingyao chuckled, looking forward to the big news that would follow.

"Senior Sister, you sure are carefree. Don’t you need to enter seclusion for cultivation?" Lin Jing glanced at Xu Qingyao.

"Cultivation is a matter of the heart! The further you progress, the less you can rely on bitter seclusion alone." Xu Qingyao spoke up. "You've been in seclusion for half a year. What’s the situation with that carp?"

Lin Jing smiled. "Looks like there's no need to go to the Qilin Clan. It’s progressing at lightning speed. In just half a year, it has traced its bloodline back to ancient times. Attack, defense, and speed—its three major forms have all fully materialized!"

"The demonic technique Divine Dragon Transformation has even reached the third layer. It’s about to use the breakthrough to the fourth layer as an opportunity to form its core!"

"That fast?" Xu Qingyao was surprised.

Reaching the second layer doubled the destructive power of its attack form, but at the cost of reducing the other attributes—defense and speed—to half their original levels.

At the third layer, the destructive power of the attack form tripled…

And the fourth layer would reach a terrifying fourfold increase.

To cultivate to such a level was already frightening. In specific situations, the power it could display compared to before cultivating the technique would be absolutely overwhelming. This was the true strength of a top-tier demonic beast technique.

"Not too bad, I suppose." Lin Jing smiled… though, in reality, it hadn’t been that fast. On the surface, it seemed like half a year, but the truth was five years.

Over five years, the Dragon Carp had cultivated the defense-based Qilin form and the speed-based Pegasus form, bringing its demonic technique to the peak of the third layer. Lin Jing felt that was neither too fast nor too slow—after all, its foundational bloodline was already there.

"But..." Xu Qingyao shook her head and said, "Even though it doesn’t need to visit the Qilin Clan to excavate its Qilin bloodline, you definitely have to go there."

"Hmm?" Lin Jing raised an eyebrow.

"Every so often, the Three Holy Maidens of the Demon Court hold a Holy Maiden Tea Gathering at the territory of a core clan within the Demon Court."

"During this gathering, the Three Holy Maidens present their latest research on the revival of the demon race."

"Additionally, they allow their followers to engage in duels—which is what I mentioned before."

"And it just so happens that this time, the clan hosting the Holy Maiden Tea Gathering is the Qilin Clan. I only recently received this news. They want to see what progress we, the three Holy Maidens, have made on reviving the demon race."

"So, as the current captain of 'Sparrowfeather' and my strongest follower, you have to accompany me to the Qilin Clan and attend the Holy Maiden Tea Gathering!"

"I see." Lin Jing nodded and asked, "No problem. When does this gathering start?"

Previously, Lin Jing had worried that the Qilin Clan’s ancestor might see through his illusion. But now that he had cultivated the Spell Spirit Origin of the Illusionary Path, he wasn’t too concerned anymore.

"The gathering is scheduled a bit after the Sky Lion Clan’s Centennial Grand Ceremony. But we can head there early. Since we’ll be there anyway, I can ask the Qilin Clan’s experts to let your pet beast form its core in their ancestral land. That way, it’ll be like a 'wanderer returning home.' If it demonstrates exceptional talent and potential, the Qilin Clan might even respond favorably." Xu Qingyao suggested.

"Besides..." Xu Qingyao smiled. "How many of those Solar False Cores does he have left?"

"Senior Sister, you need one?" Lin Jing asked.

"During your seclusion, Junior Brother Ouyang helped that fox cultivate False Core Alchemy. I, as a Beast Tamer, haven't cultivated a single Solar False Core. My pet beast, the demon fox, hasn't cultivated one either. With these two Solar False Cores, we can then use the Fusion Secret Art. Golden Core and Demon Core, False Core and False Core—seven cores shining together. Junior Brother Ouyang's strength will increase, and even compared to the previous demon emperors in Sparrowfeather, he won’t be too far behind."

"You plan to take me along to the Qilin Clan for the tea gathering. Ultimately, it’s because the effect of this Solar False Core is too good, right? You want to distribute them to other members of Sparrowfeather as well, so they can cultivate False Core Alchemy and gain an extra trump card?"

Xu Qingyao rubbed her chin. "Those two b*tches have suppressed me for so long. This time, I swear I’m going to shine!"

Lin Jing sighed helplessly. "Alright, fine. I’ll refine some later. But Senior Sister, you might need to provide me with some fire-type demonic beast cores..."

"No problem." Xu Qingyao smirked. "If there’s one thing I have plenty of, it's demonic beast cores."

—

Some time later, the Third Holy Maiden, Xu Qingyao, gathered her forces in Sparrowfeather.

As Xu Qingyao, Ouyang Hao, and Lin Jing approached, the members of Sparrowfeather remained silent.

Seven months ago, the scene of Lin Jing instantly killing the blazing Great Strength Bull Demon was still vivid in their minds.

Although Lin Jing had been very low-key afterward, no one dared to underestimate this Fire Dragon Alchemist. Even Ouyang Hao, whom they had once ignored, had undergone a transformation—his progress had been almost like he was on some kind of drug. He had even mastered techniques similar to Lin Jing's and could now rival them in strength...

"Is everyone here?" Xu Qingyao spoke slowly.

A massive skeletal bird airship emerged from the clouds. Before boarding, Xu Qingyao tossed out several bottles of pills.

"Holy Maiden, what are these?" The members of Sparrowfeather took the pills, momentarily stunned.

"These are Solar False Cores refined by the Fire Dragon Alchemist. The high-temperature techniques Ouyang and he use were cultivated through these pills. Each of you will take one. Since we're heading to the Qilin Clan in advance, try to cultivate False Core Alchemy in the Qilin Clan's Flame Cavern."

The crowd was dumbfounded and turned to look at Lin Jing.

Lin Jing only glanced at them indifferently and said, "Thank the Holy Maiden. If she hadn’t asked me, I wouldn’t have bothered refining these for you."

"Many thanks, Holy Maiden!!!"

Niu Di and the others looked at Xu Qingyao with gratitude. This top-tier False Core Alchemy would greatly enhance their strength before reaching the Nascent Soul stage. Just the thought of obtaining techniques as terrifying as Lin Jing’s made them burn with excitement.

"Many thanks, Fire Dragon Alchemist! Many thanks, Captain!"

They also thanked Lin Jing. He simply waved his hand indifferently, unconcerned.

Soon, everyone boarded the airship, and the demonic vessel took off.

It would take some time to reach the Qilin Clan’s territory. Sitting on the ship, Lin Jing originally wanted to use his Pine Leaf Mouse to sense the situation of his two clones when Ouyang Hao suddenly sneaked over, looking secretive.

"Junior Brother."

"I recently went out and saw our master."

"We’ve been discussing something. Want to give your opinion?"

"What is it?"

"Now that our Beast Taming Sect has secured a foothold under Senior Sister, should we send people to the Ancient Celestial Kingdom, bring back all the Beast Taming Sect members from the Great Wasteland, and stop being a sect of the Ancient Kingdom altogether? Instead, we could fully settle in Qingzhou for generations to come."

Lin Jing: ???

"You’re already planning to run?"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 48: The Ancestral Land!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 48: The Ancestral Land!

"Is this your idea or the Sect Master's?" Lin Jing asked.

Ouyang Hao shook his head. "Neither. It's Senior Sister's idea."

"Thinking about it carefully, the Ancient Celestial Kingdom is built on fate and destiny, which directly conflicts with the development path of our Beast Taming Sect."

"Even if, within the next hundred years, our Beast Taming Sect restructures itself so that human fortune surpasses demonic fortune, what about after a few thousand years? The same situation will arise again sooner or later!"

"That’s also why there are hardly any human cultivators with demonic beast bloodlines in the Ancient Celestial Kingdom, whereas Qingzhou has many. The very foundation of survival is incompatible, making it difficult to thrive."

"Senior Sister believes that if the Beast Taming Sect relocates to the freer lands of Qingzhou, with the Demon Court as a backing, it will undoubtedly develop better and have more opportunities."

Lin Jing fell into deep thought before responding, "The problem is that most of the disciples and elders of the Beast Taming Sect have their families in the Ancient Celestial Kingdom. The sect isn’t their whole world."

"Just like the elders and disciples who came to Qingzhou this time—most of them came alone."

"Yeah." Ouyang Hao sighed. "That’s also one of the reasons the Sect Master thinks it’s a bad idea."

"Do you have any good suggestions, Junior Brother?"

Lin Jing shrugged. "That’s for you, the Young Sect Master, to figure out. You’re the one involved in the sect’s important decisions. When the Ancient Celestial Kingdom is safe, you’re there. When Qingzhou, without the Demon Court’s protection, feels dangerous, you’re here. Personally, it doesn’t really matter to me where I am."

I'm not from this world in the first place. I have no deep attachment to the Ancient Celestial Kingdom, nor do I have any relatives here. I just go with the flow, so I don’t really have a strong opinion on Senior Sister’s idea.

If anything, once I fully refine the Transformation Pill, having the Demon Court as a backer might be far safer than relying on the Ancient Celestial Kingdom.

After all, in the Ancient Celestial Kingdom, even if I have some legal protection as a student of the Academy, that law is just a tool used by the rulers to maintain order. To the powerful figures who make those laws, my Longevity Body could be discarded at any moment. The Beast Taming Sect and I hold no real significance to the Ancient Celestial Kingdom.

But if I can secure a partnership with the Demon Court through the Transformation Pill, then our relationship would be bound by mutual interests. That’s far more reliable than national laws.

"Alright then..." Seeing that Lin Jing had no particular opinion, Ouyang Hao scratched his head.

—

A few days later, Sparrowfeather finally arrived at the Qilin Clan’s ancestral land.

Amidst the towering mountains, a massive spatial vortex led to a hidden realm. Floating above the sky was a Fire Qilin in the Demon Emperor Realm, with red eyes and golden scales.

"Greetings, Holy Son. I am Lin Ye, an elder of the Qilin Clan." The Fire Qilin spoke quickly.

"Elder Lin Ye…" Holy Son Xu Qingyao stepped off the airship. "Thank you for having us. Apologies for the intrusion."

"No need for such courtesy, Holy Son…" The Fire Qilin swept its demonic senses over the people aboard the airship. "Are these the ones who wish to borrow the Qilin Clan’s training grounds?"

"Yes, they are all my followers and have recently been practicing fire-based techniques," Xu Qingyao confirmed.

"Follow me, then."

Upon arriving in the Qilin Clan’s hidden realm, Ouyang Hao and the other members of Sparrowfeather were each assigned a cultivation cave dense with fire-attribute energy by the Qilin Demon Emperor. Only Lin Jing…

"Elder Lin Ye, my follower is a bit different from the others. His contracted pet beast is a Fire Qilin currently on the verge of forming its core. He wishes to borrow the Qilin Clan’s Ancestral Land for its Core Formation. Would that be possible?"

"Hmm?" The Qilin Demon Emperor turned to look at Lin Jing, the only one who had remained behind. "A stray from the Qilin bloodline?"

"Yes, it’s a big fellow with distant Qilin ancestry. My follower nurtured it and unearthed its ancestral bloodline, allowing it to evolve into a Fire Qilin."

"Alright, fine. Since you are under the Holy Maiden’s banner, I’ll allow it into the Ancestral Land. But don’t get your hopes up. Beasts with only a trace of Qilin blood rarely receive the blessing of the ancestors." The Demon Emperor spoke indifferently.

The Qilin Clan’s Ancestral Land.

It was a sacred site where countless ancestors of the Qilin Clan lay buried.

All Qilin beasts broke through there.

If one’s talent was exceptional, there was a chance to awaken a deceased Qilin ancestor’s spirit and receive their blessing on their bloodline!

"Many thanks, Elder Lin Ye." Lin Jing bowed.

The Fire Qilin Demon Emperor didn’t even bother acknowledging him, clearly not taking a human cultivator seriously.

"Release it now. If it manages to awaken an ancestor’s blessing, it may inherit some of the Qilin Clan’s powerful demonic arts." Xu Qingyao suggested.

Hearing this, Lin Jing lightly waved his arm.

A round-eyed Fire Qilin appeared. Though it had a somewhat wolfish, somewhat dog-like appearance, its aura was unmistakable.

At first, the Qilin Demon Emperor didn’t pay much attention. But the moment the Dragon Carp shifted into its defensive form, an overwhelming imperial-level fire bloodline spread through the air. The Qilin Demon Emperor’s pupils shrank instantly.

"Imperial-level bloodline!!!" It exclaimed in shock. "A weak-blooded Qilin with only a trace of ancestry… How could it possibly reach an Imperial-level bloodline?!"

"Chirp~" The Dragon Carp rolled its eyes. Its imperial-level bloodline was the least impressive thing about it.

"If they weren’t exceptional, they wouldn’t be my followers," Xu Qingyao chuckled. "Elder Lin Ye, I’d advise you not to underestimate them."

The Qilin Demon Emperor snapped back to reality. "Apologies. If it truly possesses an imperial-level bloodline, then it might just awaken an ancestor’s blessing upon breaking through. If it chooses to recognize its heritage and return to the Qilin Clan, we would grant it significant resources for cultivation!"

"Then we appreciate Elder Lin Ye’s generosity," Xu Qingyao replied.

Not long after, the Dragon Carp entered the deeper regions of the Qilin Clan’s Ancestral Land to train and break through.

As for Lin Jing, being human, he was not permitted entry.

However, since he was still within the Qilin Clan’s hidden realm, the distance wasn’t far. Thanks to their contract, he could sense the Dragon Carp’s condition as though he were right beside it.

"Train well and condense your core as soon as possible."

With that thought, Lin Jing also entered meditation within the cultivation cave assigned by the Fire Qilin Clan.

Whether human or beast, forming a core was not something that could be accomplished in just a few days. It would take at least one to two months since both Golden Cores and Demonic Cores required time to gestate divine abilities…

As for whether the Dragon Carp would be acknowledged by the Qilin ancestors and receive their demonic arts, Lin Jing didn’t particularly care.

He and the Dragon Carp had no shortage of techniques. The Flame Emperor’s Divine Ability was already more than sufficient.

If not for Senior Sister’s suggestion and the fact that it was conveniently on their route, he wouldn’t even have bothered coming here…

For now, he just hoped the Dragon Carp would complete its breakthrough smoothly and not stir up any trouble in the Qilin Clan’s sacred ancestral land.

"Alright… time to split my focus and check in on my clones."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 49: The Sky Lion Clan
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 49: The Sky Lion Clan

One of the Sixteen Daos of Qingzhou—Beast God Dao.

The Beast God Dao was a unique region within Qingzhou.

Although it was home to many human cultivators, most of them weren’t pureblood humans!

Many carried traces of demonic beast bloodlines, and their special cultivation physiques were often related to beasts—such as Dragon Blood Physique, Barbaric Bull Physique, and Beast King Physique.

Because of this, the cultivation sects and families in the Beast God Dao often walked the fine line between humanity and the beast races.

The Sky Lion Clan.

Centennial Ancestral Tribute Ceremony.

As the grand ceremony approached, not only did numerous beast clans gather to pay homage, but many cultivators also arrived to present tributes.

"We’re finally almost there."

The journey had been long. After three months of travel, the Pine Leaf Mouse and Lin Jing’s clone finally arrived at the Beast God Dao.

Using a plant-based clone formed from vast life force and even consuming a portion of lifespan had its advantages. Despite crossing numerous territories, the connection between the Pine Leaf Mouse and the plant clone remained strong—far more practical than ordinary cloning techniques.

Besides this connection, Lin Jing’s clones also carried self-aware illusionary souls, allowing them to act independently even when separated by great distances. Unlike mindless puppets that only executed pre-set commands, these clones could still follow Lin Jing’s directives.

Other than slightly weaker endurance, they weren’t that different from his true body.

Clip-clop, clip-clop.

As Lin Jing’s illusionary body and the Pine Leaf Mouse walked along a forest path, the sound of hooves suddenly echoed behind them.

A horse-drawn carriage swiftly passed by, but Lin Jing paid it no mind.

With the Sky Lion Clan’s grand ceremony approaching, many cultivators were traveling to present tributes. He had seen quite a few along the way.

However, as this particular carriage continued, it gradually slowed to a stop—making Lin Jing smile.

"A fellow Daoist also heading to the Sky Lion Clan?"

Before long, a chubby Taoist lifted the carriage curtain and called out to Lin Jing.

"Mm." Lin Jing responded briefly.

The chubby Taoist laughed heartily. "I sense that you’re a pureblood human. That must mean, like me, you’re heading there to seek blood, right?"

"Since fate has brought us together, why not hop on? We can travel together!"

Lin Jing’s illusionary body remained calm, gazing at the man.

A Foundation Establishment Perfection-level chubby Taoist, a Qi Refinement-stage horse, and an old coachman hiding his mid-Core Formation cultivation under the guise of a Qi Refinement cultivator.

"Much appreciated." Lin Jing nodded and stepped onto the carriage.

Inside, the chubby Taoist sighed. "Ah, the competition in the Beast God Dao is too intense. Pureblood humans can’t survive here. I’ve given up on being human."

"I hail from Tianyun Temple of Cangyun Mountain. May I ask where fellow Daoist is from?"

"A wandering cultivator," Lin Jing replied.

"A wandering cultivator???" The chubby Taoist was stunned. "Then you must be quite fortunate! What treasure have you obtained to present to the Sky Lion Clan?"

"So that you can decide whether or not to rob me?" Lin Jing chuckled. "This Soul Confusion Incense you’ve filled your carriage with is too weak for me. I’ve been eating poisonous mushrooms for years. Back when I was in Qi Refinement, I treated Soul Confusion Pills like candy."

What the hell?

The chubby Taoist’s expression changed dramatically. Suddenly, he revealed a sinister grin and struck at Lin Jing with his palm.

Boom!

The Pine Leaf Mouse also struck back with a single palm.

As fists and palms collided, the carriage exploded into countless fragments!

The chubby Taoist was sent flying, vomiting blood midair. His body nearly collapsed.

"Diamond Palm…?"

The chubby Taoist muttered those words before losing consciousness.

"And you, old man? Not going to make a move?" Lin Jing turned to the coachman.

"When did you figure it out?" The old man, Ma Tian, stared at Lin Jing.

"Your illusion technique is garbage."

Lin Jing spoke casually. "It’s well known that during every grand ceremony of the Sky Lion Clan, many cultivators from the Beast God Dao bring rare tributes, hoping to receive a drop of Sky Lion blood and transform their bodies into Sky Lion Physiques, becoming outer members of the clan."

"But the Sky Lion Clan has extremely high standards for tributes, so those who qualify must carry valuable treasures."

"Naturally, this gives rise to a lot of bandit cultivators—but unfortunately for you, you just happened to target me."

"Today, I’ll send you both to meet the Buddha."

"A mere Foundation Establishment ant—"

Before the Core Formation old man could finish, Lin Jing’s illusionary body obliterated him.

Instant death.

Lin Jing picked up the storage bags and checked them before sighing.

"Qingzhou really is better than the Ancient Celestial Kingdom. In just three months, I’ve already encountered eight robbery attempts. The harvest has been plentiful!"

"Unfortunately, this clone will probably die soon in the Sky Lion Clan. I’ll organize the loot later and see if I get a chance to retrieve it someday."

Along the journey, whenever he encountered those with ill intentions, Lin Jing’s choices were simple:

—If he could win, he’d counter-rob them.

—If he couldn’t win, he’d enhance his illusions, hide himself, and escape using spatial techniques.

He didn’t maintain complete stealth at all times—it would consume too much energy. After all, he was just a clone, without the true body’s stamina.

However… now that his two clones had finally arrived at the Sky Lion Clan’s territory, he no longer held back.

Lin Jing ignored the official tribute process and instead strengthened his illusions, boldly blending in among the tribute-bearers entering the Sky Lion Clan’s secret realm.

No one noticed a thing.

Now that he had reached his target destination, consuming more energy didn’t matter.

"I wonder if I’ll get to see the ‘Sky Lion Holy Maiden’ at this ceremony."

"You probably will, but forget about receiving the Sky Lion Holy Maiden’s blessing. Unless you're an imperial-level strong clan pledging allegiance, it’s impossible to receive Longevity Blood…"

Lin Jing listened in on a group of beast-blood cultivators as they entered the Sky Lion Secret Realm, discussing the Sky Lion Holy Maiden.

His expression remained calm.

The Sky Lion Holy Maiden—this title referred to nothing more than a captive Longevity Body of the Sky Lion Clan.

It was no secret to the outside world that the Longevity Body was merely a blood sacrifice raised by the Sky Lion Clan.

But the clan had given it a noble title, calling it a divine blessing from the heavens—hence, Holy Maiden.

By leveraging this so-called blessing, the Sky Lion Clan had been able to harvest large amounts of Longevity Blood each year, trading it with powerful clans for benefits.

"According to Senior Sister, the Sky Lion Clan’s captive Longevity Body is already a thousand years old."

"Yet despite a millennium, her cultivation is still only at the early Core Formation Stage."

"This level of control is remarkable. The blood and flesh of a Core Formation Longevity Body aren’t valuable to Demon Emperors, but if she were any weaker, others would fight over her."

"That’s why, even after a thousand years of captivity, nothing unexpected has happened."

Even though I don’t have the power to rescue this unlucky soul who shares my physique…

Since I’m here, I might as well see if I can at least meet her.

Lin Jing gradually distanced himself from the beast-blood cultivators, wandering through the Sky Lion Clan’s secret realm under his illusionary disguise.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 50: Meeting Another Longevity Body
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 50: Meeting Another Longevity Body

"The Sky Lion Clan sure knows how to keep a Longevity Body hidden."

Lin Jing didn't know how long he had been wandering around the Sky Lion Secret Realm, but after a while, he shook his head.

He hadn't found even the slightest trace of the other Longevity Body.

With no other choice, he gave up on his search and followed the crowd, gathering in the central area to await the start of the Ancestral Worship Ceremony.

Causing a disturbance during the ceremony—that was his real reason for coming here.

"The Ancestral Worship Ceremony is about to begin!!!"

Accompanied by the deep, distant chime of a bell, the grand ceremony officially commenced.

Normally, such a ceremony would be an internal affair of a particular race, not something that would invite so many outsiders. However, any faction with a "Demon Ancestor" overseeing it did not follow normal conventions.

"ROAR!!!!"

The chief officiant of the ceremony was a White-Maned Lion Emperor, a peak-stage Nascent Soul Demon Emperor and the current leader of the Sky Lion Clan. As it tore through the void and emerged, countless other Sky Lions stepped out from the spatial rift behind it. Dozens of the Sky Lion Clan’s core members appeared.

From within the rift, a woman in a white dress also stepped forward.

Her face was expressionless, void of any soul. Her eyes were dull and lifeless, like a walking corpse.

Upon seeing her, many of the Demon Kings and Demon Emperors present gulped, their eyes flashing with a greedy, greenish light.

"It's the Longevity Body!"

"The Sky Lion Holy Maiden!!"

"As expected, she appeared."

"I've heard that in every centennial Sky Lion Ceremony, Longevity Blood and Flesh are offered as tribute. I wonder if it's true... Brother Tiger, if the scent of Longevity Blood overwhelms me and I do something foolish, please hold me back. You must hold me back!"

Many demonic beasts suppressed their inner excitement with great effort.

Compared to them, the human cultivators who had come seeking refuge with the Sky Lion Clan displayed more empathy. When they saw the hollow, puppet-like state of the Longevity Body, they could only shake their heads, sighing at her tragic fate.

To be kept in captivity for a thousand years, to have one's blood and flesh constantly harvested—even the most steadfast of cultivators would likely lose their sanity. Let alone a woman.

At this moment, in their eyes, this Longevity Body likely no longer possessed much of her own will.

"Finally..."

Meanwhile, Lin Jing finally laid eyes on the other Longevity Body. He exhaled deeply.

What a miserable sight.

"Good thing Senior Brother Ouyang saved me back then."

Remaining in his state of concealed illusion, Lin Jing slowly stepped out from the crowd of demonic beasts, still unnoticed by anyone.

By now, the Sky Lion Holy Maiden had already taken her place on the ceremonial altar, awaiting her fate as a sacrifice.

At that moment, a figure moved through the core ranks of the Sky Lion Clan, approaching Lin Jing. As they passed, they deliberately cast an illusion over him, ensuring he couldn't see them.

After finishing their task, the figure—another Longevity Body—wanted to speak with Lin Jing, the man who shared the same physique as her.

However, her condition seemed even worse than Lin Jing had imagined.

She merely gazed at him with empty eyes, showing no reaction whatsoever.

"So pitiful."

Lin Jing sighed, raising his hand slightly. A drop of blood radiating a dense Longevity Aura appeared in his palm—it was his own blood.

This time, in order to place a bounty on the Demonic Sect with Longevity Blood, he had brought some of his own to serve as proof of identity.

Only another Longevity Body could perceive its unique aura.

The altar had already been shrouded by Lin Jing’s illusions, and the strongest Sky Lion present—the peak-stage Nascent Soul Demon Emperor officiating the ceremony—was nowhere near capable of seeing through his techniques.

Of course, Lin Jing’s illusions were only capable of small tricks like this. Against a true powerhouse, he wouldn’t be able to do any real harm.

"...."

As Lin Jing revealed the drop of Longevity Blood, the Sky Lion Clan’s captive Longevity Body finally showed a flicker of reaction.

Her vacant eyes slowly regained a trace of light as she stared at Lin Jing.

She opened her mouth slightly, as if she wanted to say something, but no words came out.

Even so, that subtle flicker in her gaze was enough to confirm something for Lin Jing—she hadn’t completely lost herself.

She still had a sliver of consciousness left!

"Hello."

"Longevity Body."

"My name is Lin Jing."

"Aside from you, I am the second Longevity Body in this world."

"It's a shame that our first meeting had to be like this."

Lin Jing walked past her slowly.

The Sky Lion Holy Maiden's gaze once again turned hollow and unfocused, but at that moment, her consciousness was pulled into another world—

A pure white illusionary realm.

The Longevity Body stood blankly in this strange world.

In front of her stood Lin Jing, a small squirrel perched on his shoulder.

"In here, we can communicate freely through our minds and spirits. No need to worry about being overheard."

"You..."

For the first time, the Longevity Body spoke.

But in the next moment—

"Kill me."

"Please, kill me."

She spoke in a hushed voice.

"Is it that painful?"

Lin Jing looked at her.

Within the illusionary space, the Longevity Body murmured:

"I'm begging you... kill me..."

Under the Sky Lion Clan’s control, she couldn’t even commit suicide.

Now that Lin Jing had awakened her consciousness, her first thought was to ask him—the one person who could move freely within the Sky Lion Clan’s domain—to end her suffering.

"Kill you, huh..."

Lin Jing pondered for a moment before shaking his head.

"Unfortunately, this is the Sky Lion Clan’s territory. With the means of the Void Mystical Demon Ancestor and your massive lifespan as a Longevity Body, I have no way to truly end you. Even if I temporarily extinguished your vitality, you'd just be revived through some great divine ability."

The moment he said he couldn't kill her, the hope in the Longevity Body’s eyes gradually turned into despair.

"How many years of lifespan do you have left?"

Lin Jing asked.

"Over twenty thousand years..."

Her voice was devoid of emotion.

"That long?"

Lin Jing raised an eyebrow.

"Did they cultivate you to the small achievement stage?"

The Longevity Body’s aura grew even weaker.

"Mm... To extend my lifespan further, the Sky Lion Ancestor found a longevity cultivation technique that was compatible with my physique..."

Lin Jing understood.

There were indeed specialized techniques that focused solely on extending lifespan.

However, most of these techniques offered little in terms of combat power, and their advancement was slow.

But for a Longevity Body being raised as livestock, none of that mattered.

Shaking his head, he said, "I'm sorry. I can't kill you, and I can't save you either."

"But judging from the situation, they haven't restricted your cultivation, right?"

He stepped forward and gently pressed a finger to her forehead.

In the next instant, an enormous amount of inheritance knowledge flooded into the Longevity Body’s mind.

"This technique is called the Heavenly Demon Physique. It allows you to burn your lifespan in exchange for cultivation breakthroughs. However, mastering it is extremely difficult. I don’t know if you can pull it off... but at least, it gives you something to hope for."

"I'm leaving now. If fate allows, we’ll meet again."

"I have my own matters to attend to."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 51: Shocking Bounty
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 51: Shocking Bounty

Outer World.

All the Sky Lion demons were chanting the sacrificial text in unison. Just as it came time to offer the Longevity Blood and Flesh, countless demons and cultivators at the scene were suddenly shocked to see a young man slowly floating up from the crowd!!

It was Lin Jing, who had just returned to the crowd from the ceremonial platform!

As he rose into the air, he instantly drew all the attention, causing countless Sky Lion demons to glare at him with rage.

"How dare you!!!"

"Brat, you’re seeking death!!"

The demons and cultivators around Lin Jing were also taken aback, their faces filled with shock and fear as they hastily distanced themselves from him.

At this critical moment of the Sky Lion Clan’s grand ceremony, Lin Jing’s actions were nothing short of sabotage. In their eyes, he was already as good as dead.

"Take him down!!" the Sky Lion Clan Leader roared in fury.

However, in the next instant, as Lin Jing slowly raised his palm and repeated his old trick, a drop of blood exuding a mesmerizing aura appeared. The Sky Lions, who were just about to act, froze in place.

"Longevity Blood???"

Their gazes instantly snapped back to the Holy Maiden standing on the ceremonial platform, but seeing that she remained unchanged, their attention returned to Lin Jing.

"No… this isn't the Holy Maiden’s blood. The aura is completely different," one of the ritual elders spoke in astonishment. "Another Longevity Body’s blood?!"

"Brat, you are..."

Just as Lin Jing was about to introduce himself under everyone’s scrutinizing stares, someone in the crowd recognized him first.

"The Suspended Gourd, the Dao-robed Squirrel... he’s that top prodigy of the Ancient Celestial Kingdom, the one who refined the Ten Rare Gu— Lin Jing!!”

Someone gasped in disbelief, unable to comprehend why he had suddenly appeared at the Sky Lion Clan’s ceremony—let alone why he possessed Longevity Blood!

"Lin Jing?" The Sky Lion experts froze. "That human cultivator wanted by the Yin Corpse Sect??"

Suddenly, their expressions shifted. As if realizing something, the truth dawned upon them.

It wasn’t because of the Ten Rare Gu…

"That’s right. I’m the Lin Jing who’s been hunted by the Yin Corpse Sect and targeted by the two great demonic sects." Lin Jing’s illusion smiled faintly and said, "Sorry for interrupting this grand ancestral ceremony."

"I merely heard that the Sky Lion Clan’s ceremony was particularly grand, so I came here specifically to make an announcement."

"Are you all curious why the Yin Corpse Sect is hunting a mere Beast Tamer like me? It’s very simple. It’s not because I have the Ten Rare Gu, nor is it because I have great potential."

"It’s because—

"I possess a Longevity Body."

"I am the second Longevity Body of this era. To the Yin Corpse Sect, I am of immense value. That is why the demonic sects have placed a bounty on me."

"But I always repay my grudges. Since the Yin Corpse Sect is hunting me, I naturally have to retaliate."

"Unfortunately, my strength is limited, so I can only issue a bounty here, entrusting my friends of the demon world to help me eradicate the demonic sects."

"Anyone who hunts down a Nascent Soul-stage demon cultivator of the Yin Corpse Sect can exchange their remnant Nascent Soul with me for a drop of Longevity Blood."

"Anyone who hunts down a Soul Transformation-stage demon cultivator can exchange their Primordial Spirit Remnant with me for 100 drops of Longevity Blood!"

"And anyone who kills a Void Mystical-stage demon cultivator of the Yin Corpse Sect… can claim any one of my Longevity Organs."

"Oh, right, the Gu God Sect counts too."

"This first bounty will be settled in ten years. At that time, I will be outside the Sky Lion Clan’s territory to redeem the rewards."

As Lin Jing spoke, the entire ancestral ceremony fell into complete silence.

Everyone found it utterly absurd—unimaginable.

Never mind the fact that the top Foundation Establishment prodigy of the Ancient Celestial Kingdom turned out to be a Longevity Body—an earth-shattering revelation…

But he actually came to the Sky Lion Clan’s ancestral ceremony just to issue a bounty on the demonic sects?!

What kind of joke was this?!

How did he dare?!

Disrupting the Sky Lion Clan’s sacred ancestral ceremony—did he really think he could leave alive?

This act was so insane that even the gathered demons couldn’t understand it.

Yet when they looked at Lin Jing, they saw that he seemed dead serious, as if he wasn’t joking at all.

"Brat, you disrupted the Sky Lion Clan’s sacred ceremony, and you think you can walk away unscathed?!"

The Sky Lion Clan Leader let out an angry laugh, though to the surrounding demons and cultivators, it didn’t sound like anger at all—it was pure amusement.

If they hadn’t known who Lin Jing was and had only seen the ceremony being disrupted, they would have been furious.

But now that they knew he was a Longevity Body…

For the Sky Lion Clan, the ceremony being ruined no longer mattered.

A living Longevity Body was far more valuable than any ancestral ceremony.

The Sky Lion Clan already had one Longevity Body in their possession. If they could obtain another… their clan’s fortune would be unimaginable.

But most importantly…

To the Sky Lion Clan Leader, capturing Lin Jing meant he could be bred.

Currently, the Sky Lion Holy Maiden had to keep her blood pure and preserve her vital yin due to the nature of her cultivation, but Lin Jing? He had no such restrictions.

While the Longevity Body was notoriously difficult to pass down through bloodlines, that was in the past.

The Medicine Toad Clan had developed a pill—the Tadpole Pill—which, at least during its demonstration at the Alchemy Conference, ensured a 100% inheritance rate for most cultivation-related physiques.

Although the test subjects had only been ordinary spiritual physiques—not divine physiques, let alone a Longevity Body—the Sky Lion Clan Leader was convinced the pill would significantly increase the chances of inheritance.

As long as they obtained the Tadpole Pill and captured Lin Jing…

It would be a heaven-sent blessing for the Sky Lion Clan!

The Clan Leader locked his gaze onto Lin Jing, only to find that the Longevity Body seemed completely unaware of the danger he was in.

"You disrupted the Sky Lion Clan’s ancestral ceremony…"

"So what?" Lin Jing hovered in midair, completely unfazed by the Clan Leader’s threat. "What, does the Sky Lion Clan want to keep me here as well? Make me the 'Sky Lion Holy Son,' drain my blood day and night to feed your clan?"

"Hahahahahaha—"

"Brat, at least you understand!!!"

The Sky Lion Clan Leader’s body began to expand, his voice booming. "Don’t worry! As the 'Sky Lion Holy Son,' the Yin Corpse Sect will never dare touch you again!!"

As he spoke, he reached out with a massive claw to seize Lin Jing.

But neither he nor anyone else noticed…

Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse were mere illusions.

Even Avatar Techniques had telltale signs, but Lin Jing’s illusions were flawless—absolutely undetectable.

Sizzle.

Lin Jing pinched his fingers together, calmly incinerating his own Longevity Blood.

Just as he was about to self-destruct—

BOOM!!!

A terrifying aura erupted.

The Sky Lion Clan Leader, a Soul Transformation peak expert, suddenly had his arm severed by an unknown force!

His expression turned to utter shock as he looked in a certain direction—

There, the Sky Lion Holy Maiden, who had been lifeless as a puppet, was now surging with unimaginable power.

The very space around her twisted and shattered.

She raised her head, a gleam appearing in her once-empty eyes.

Under countless stunned gazes, her cultivation had ascended to the Void Mystical Stage!

"Thank you… for your secret art."

She gazed at Lin Jing, who was frozen midair, dumbfounded.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 52: The Birth of a Demoness
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 52: The Birth of a Demoness

“What just happened…”

The Longevity Body that the Sky Lion Clan had been raising suddenly erupted with Void Mystical energy fluctuations, leaving the Sky Lions and their guests in shock. They couldn’t comprehend what had just occurred.

Even Lin Jing, who had personally taught her the Heavenly Demon Physique, found it hard to believe.

Wait a minute…

How long had it been since he passed the Heavenly Demon Physique to this Longevity Body? Not even half an hour!

He had spent so much time using his Hidden Dragon Fortune to deduce and cultivate the Heavenly Demon Physique, yet he still hadn’t succeeded—but she had mastered it in just moments?!

Lin Jing was baffled. Had he overlooked something? Was there a reason why he still couldn't achieve it?

He had been painstakingly searching for Exotic Flames just to increase his chances of cultivating the Heavenly Demon Physique…

How could the gap between people be this vast?

But there was something else that puzzled him—this Longevity Body was originally at the Core Formation Stage. Even if she burned all her lifespan, reaching Void Mystical still seemed excessive… almost absurd.

“There are too many secrets about the Heavenly Demon Physique… I haven’t mastered it myself, so I don’t fully understand its mechanics. If I get the chance, I need to ask her.”

At this moment, the Sky Lion Holy Maiden, now transformed, unleashed an overwhelming surge of mana, capturing countless Sky Lions and sealing them inside a sphere of power.

At the same time, she rushed toward Lin Jing, grabbed him, and prepared to tear open space to escape the Sky Lion Clan’s secret realm with him.

But this shocking turn of events had finally disturbed the Sky Lion Ancestor, who had been slumbering deep within the secret realm.

A terrifying aura swept through the realm, ripping open the sky.

A colossal lion’s head emerged from a rift in space, its eyes widening in shock at the sight of its Longevity Body’s transformation.

Instinctively, it reached out with a giant claw, attempting to capture the escaping Longevity Body and Lin Jing.

And at that moment, everyone finally understood why the Sky Lion Holy Maiden had captured so many Sky Lions.

"If you move, they all die."

Her icy voice rang out, and the force sealing the Sky Lions inside the power spheres intensified, causing their bodies to nearly distort.

"Ancestor, save us!!"

"Ancestor, help!!!"

"We don’t want to die!!"

These Sky Lions, who had spent their lives devouring Longevity Flesh and drinking Longevity Blood, could never have imagined that the very being they once treated as food now held their lives in her hands.

Having lived among the Sky Lion Clan for so long, the Longevity Body was well aware of their nature.

Although the Sky Lion Ancestor could transform outsiders into Sky Lions, these converted members were never purebloods.

Only a small fraction of the pureblood Sky Lions were considered the true core members of the clan—the only ones allowed to consume Longevity Flesh and Blood.

Every one of these pureblood Sky Lions was the Sky Lion Ancestor’s treasure—after all, breeding pureblood descendants was immensely difficult…

"Release them, and I will let you leave."

The Sky Lion Ancestor spoke in a calm tone, but beneath that deadly calm, a seething fury lurked.

With hostages in her grasp, the Sky Lion Ancestor had no choice but to halt its actions, its gaze turning cruel and ruthless as it glared at the transformed Longevity Body and Lin Jing.

“This is bad…”

At first glance, it seemed like only one outcome was possible.

But the Sky Lions held hostage were still encased in perfectly structured energy spheres, each one pulsating with an unstable power—as if they could explode at any moment.

Sirius, with a furious roar, immediately tried to dissipate the spheres using his demonic energy, attempting to neutralize the threat.

However, before the Longevity Body could even tear open space to escape, the spheres suddenly exploded.

BOOM—!!!

The detonations didn’t physically injure the Sky Lions, but their lifeforce began rapidly draining.

The Sky Lion Ancestor froze for a moment in disbelief, before erupting in an earth-shattering roar of rage.

In the span of a single breath—over 80% of the Sky Lion Clan had perished.

With a furious howl, the Sky Lion Ancestor gave chase.

At this moment, the entire Beast God Dao trembled.

Two colossal Void Mystical auras streaked across the heavens, shaking countless powerful demon beasts and cultivators out of their secluded training.

"That aura… it’s the Sky Lion Clan’s Sky Lion Ancestor…!"

"Isn’t today supposed to be their ancestral ceremony?! What happened?!"

"And the other aura… I don’t recognize it. But it’s at the peak of the Void Mystical Stage! Who is it?! Since when did Qingzhou have such a powerhouse?!"

The entire Beast God Dao was in upheaval.

—Meanwhile—

The Longevity Body, still carrying Lin Jing, suddenly coughed up a mouthful of blood.

She wiped the corner of her lips, but kept flying.

"Hey…" Lin Jing, still being dragged along, was speechless. "You…

"What the hell is going on?! Even if you mastered the Heavenly Demon Physique, this level of power increase is just…!"

"It seems… you taught me the technique, but failed to master it yourself," the Longevity Body said softly.

"The power of the Heavenly Demon Physique… can be freely controlled."

"What do you mean?" Lin Jing frowned.

"I burned nearly all of my lifespan, leaving myself with only ten years to live."

"The strength obtained from burning lifespan… is determined by how long you intend to maintain it."

"If a Core Formation cultivator burns 20,000 years of lifespan and wants the power to last tens of thousands of years, they might only reach the Nascent Soul Stage."

"If they want to maintain it for a thousand years, they might reach the Soul Transformation Stage."

Lin Jing’s eyes widened. "Then you… you’ve boosted your cultivation all the way to the Void Mystical Stage. How long can you sustain it? A hundred years? Ten years?"

This Heavenly Demon Physique—the shorter the duration, the higher the cultivation gain!

"Less than a day."

"I burned 20,000 years of my lifespan to reach the peak of the Void Mystical Stage… but I can only maintain it for less than one day."

The Longevity Body continued, "If we don’t escape the Sky Lion Ancestor within a day… then it’s all over."

Lin Jing froze.

"So in one day… you’ll fall back to Core Formation, with only ten years of life remaining?"

"Yes. But escaping that place, and killing dozens of Sky Lions… it was all worth it."

For the first time in a long while, her eyes were filled with life—a true desire to live.

"Don’t worry. I’ll definitely help you escape… I won’t let you fall into the hands of the Sky Lion Clan and suffer the same fate as me."

After a moment of silence, Lin Jing spoke again.

"Wait a second—do I look like an idiot?"

"You think I’d send my real body to the Sky Lion Clan?"

"This is just one of my clones. If you hadn’t interfered, I was about to self-destruct. Why don’t you just toss me down as bait and make your own escape?"

"Your cultivation may be high now, but without the corresponding spells and experience, you won’t be a match for the Sky Lion Ancestor."

"Sigh…"

The Longevity Body let out a quiet sigh.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 53: Guidance
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 53: Guidance

"Alright, that was a joke. Now, let's talk seriously."

"Spending 20,000 years for just one day—do you really want to waste that day running away?"

"What do you mean?" The Longevity Woman was puzzled.

"Not long from now, you'll only have ten years left to live."

"With your cultivation technique and the Longevity Body’s natural speed, even in a thousand years, you might not even reach mid-Core Formation."

"Which means that even if you escape successfully, you won’t have much time left."

"Unless… you break through to Nascent Soul and gain a massive extension to your lifespan."

"But as I said before, under normal cultivation, it’ll be incredibly difficult for you to reach Nascent Soul."

"It doesn’t matter."

Spending 20,000 years of lifespan—she had no regrets.

If she hadn’t done so, escaping from the Sky Lion Clan’s secret realm would have been nearly impossible.

Just being able to slaughter so many Sky Lions had already satisfied her.

Even if she only had a few more days to live after this, she could accept it.

After all, before this, she had already given up on living entirely.

"But you’re right. Since I don’t need to save you anymore, I might as well fight the Sky Lion Ancestor to the death."

"Even if I can't defeat it like you said… I should still be able to severely injure it."

The Longevity Woman suddenly halted in midair.

Right now, she had immense cultivation and overwhelming mana, but she lacked attack spells and battle techniques.

Even so, with her current Void Mystical peak-level power, even basic martial techniques would have immense destructive force.

"Could it be that the reason I couldn’t cultivate the Heavenly Demon Physique… was because of my mindset?"

She suddenly thought of something.

From the very start, that Longevity Man seemed to completely disregard life and death.

"Maybe." The Longevity Man also understood why he had been so slow in cultivating the Heavenly Demon Physique.

"Is it really worth wasting life on something like this?" The Void Mystical expert continued speaking.

"Does he want to live longer? Of course he does. So listen to what he has to say."

The Void Mystical expert said, "You have no choice. Soon, you will use all your remaining power to cast an illusion on him—a powerful one that temporarily conceals his presence from the Sky Lion Ancestor."

"He will return to the Sky Lion Secret Realm. Without the Sky Lion Ancestor guarding it, that secret realm is completely defenseless against his current power. He will sweep through it in the shortest time possible and plunder all of the Sky Lion Clan’s accumulated treasures."

"As one of the top-tier demon beast clans, the Sky Lion Clan must have an unfathomable hoard of resources. That trove—while it may not be enough to help him reach Nascent Soul within ten years…"

"It will be more than enough to ensure his survival. And eventually, he will have the power to completely wipe out the entire Sky Lion Clan—including the Sky Lion Ancestor."

After the Void Mystical expert finished speaking, the Longevity Man was momentarily stunned.

"Why go this far to help me? Just because we’re both Longevity Bodies?"

"As long as the Sky Lion Clan’s treasure trove is enough to guarantee your Core Formation, the rest will all go to you." The Void Mystical expert replied.

"Fine."

The Longevity Man had not expected this answer.

Lin Jing smirked.

"After all, we’re both Longevity Bodies. My cultivation speed is slow as well."

"Thanks."

"It’s nothing." Lin Jing’s expression turned distant.

A Core Formation cultivator suddenly skyrocketing to Void Mystical peak—an overwhelming level of power.

Even though it would only last for one day, Lin Jing was certain—by tomorrow, all of Qingzhou would be in an uproar.

The Longevity Woman’s actions, in a way, had already helped Lin Jing.

Soon, the entire continent would realize that Longevity Bodies could use secret arts to rapidly boost their power.

And with that knowledge… anyone who wanted to target a Longevity Body would have to seriously reconsider the consequences.

This was exactly the kind of fear and deterrence Lin Jing wanted the Longevity Body to command.

"Time is short. Let’s begin. After everything is done, make sure to pass on all of your insights into cultivating the Heavenly Demon Physique to me."

Lin Jing shook his head, smirking.

"Are you even human? You say you’ll master something, and then you just do it?"

"Compared to you, I’d say you’re the real monster." The Longevity Woman said softly.

A mere Foundation Establishment cultivator… had accomplished all of this?

"After I ransack the Sky Lion Clan’s secret realm, I’ll use an illusion to conceal myself. Soon after, I’ll either personally come find you or send someone in my place."

Lin Jing helped her disguise herself and handed her a tracking pinecone.

At the same time, his clone and the Pine Leaf Mouse had exhausted all their energy, dispersing into the air.

—Meanwhile—

Kirin Clan.

Lin Jing’s true body opened his eyes, stepping out of his cave dwelling to report everything to his Senior Sister.

If the Longevity Woman successfully plundered the Sky Lion Clan’s vast accumulation of resources, she would likely need to rely on the Demon Court to process it all.

"You’re looking for Big Sister?"

Moments later, Lin Jing arrived at his Senior Sister’s temporary cave dwelling, only to find that she wasn’t there.

Only a pet beast was cultivating in the area.

"The First Holy Maiden and Second Holy Maiden have already arrived at the Kirin Secret Realm. The five Kirin Elders are currently discussing preparations for the Holy Maiden Tea Ceremony with them."

The Fire Phoenix inside the cave said.

"I see. Thanks, Phoenix Sister."

Lin Jing nodded, then said, "I’ll wait here for now. It shouldn’t take too long, right?"

—Elsewhere—

Kirin Courtyard.

The five major Kirin Clans—Gold, Wood, Water, Fire, and Earth—had gathered their Great Elders, along with three Holy Maidens of the Demon Court.

The First Holy Maiden, Liu Yan, smiled at Xu Qingyao.

"I can’t believe you brought your followers to the Kirin Clan so early. I heard from Elder Lin Ye that they’ve been training in seclusion, preparing for the upcoming battle?"

"So what?" Xu Qingyao replied indifferently.

Of the other two Holy Maidens, she disliked this one the most.

"With their talent, they’ll never amount to anything… Xu Qingyao, I heard that your Beast Taming Sect in the Ancient Celestial Kingdom produced a genius who contracted all Ten Rare Gu, and is even being hunted by the Yin Corpse Sect."

"If it’s him, his talent is worth cultivating—far more than those weaklings who follow you. Haven’t you tried recruiting him?" The Second Holy Maiden, Shen Xi, asked.

Xu Qingyao glanced at her. "Too bad. That Beast Taming Sect Holy Son was teleported away by the demonic sects. His whereabouts are unknown—I’d say there’s an 80% chance he’s already dead."

The Kirin Clan’s Elder Lin Ye chuckled, interrupting their conversation.

"Since all eight Holy Sons have no objections, let’s proceed with hosting the Tea Ceremony tomorrow."

Just then, a small elder from the Earth Kirin Clan suddenly received an urgent message from a Land Spirit Pearl.

His expression changed slightly.

"Holy Maiden, the Beast Taming Sect cultivator you were just discussing… was his name Lin Jing?"

Xu Qingyao turned to him. "Yes, Lin Jing."

The Earth Kirin Elder took a deep breath.

"We just received intelligence. Lin Jing appeared at the Sky Lion Clan’s centennial ceremony today… and publicly revealed himself to be a Longevity Body.

He has placed a bounty on both the Yin Corpse Sect and the Gu God Sect—using his own Longevity Blood and Flesh as the reward!!"

The room fell silent.

A Longevity Body?!

Xu Qingyao raised an eyebrow. "Oh… so today was the Sky Lion Clan’s ceremony?"

"That’s not even the shocking part!" The Earth Kirin Elder’s voice trembled.

"The moment Lin Jing appeared—

The Sky Lion Clan’s captive Longevity Body suddenly ascended to Void Mystical Peak, slaughtered over half of the Sky Lion Clan on the spot—

—and then escaped with Lin Jing!!!"

Xu Qingyao: "…What?"

Her expression froze.

"The hell did you just say?!"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 54: Core Formation
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 54: Core Formation

“What kind of joke is this?”

First Holy Maiden Liu Yan was stunned, her alluring face showing a look of disbelief.

“Are you sure your intel is correct?”

The other Grand Elders of the Kirin Tribe were also in shock.

The Longevity Body of the Sky Lion Clan had been kept in captivity for a thousand years without any incident, and now you’re saying that his cultivation suddenly surged to the peak of Void Mystical Stage, wiped out most of the Sky Lion Clan, and even killed a significant number of their people?

To put this into perspective, even the five Grand Elders here had only reached the peak of Nascent Soul Transformation!

In the entire clan, only the oldest ancestor, who had been in seclusion for years, had reached the peak of Void Mystical Stage.

And now you’re telling me that a mere blood sacrifice has reached that level of cultivation?

“Third Holy Maiden, do you have any insight? After all, this Lin Jing comes from the Beast Taming Sect.”

“I don’t know.” Xu Qingyao shook her head. “I left the Beast Taming Sect when I was young and never met Lin Jing. Even the Sect Master of the Beast Taming Sect only met him once.”

“Shortly after, the sect was sealed, and the disciples scattered—including him.”

“That’s why I told you that you should have taken him under your wing,” Shen Xi said slowly. “One person toying with and severely damaging the Sky Lion Clan to this extent, even kidnapping the Sky Lion Holy Maiden… I’m really curious. What kind of person is he?”

“But now I understand why Lin Jing didn’t come looking for your protection after arriving in Qingzhou. If he truly is a Longevity Body, then naturally, he wouldn’t dare step into the Demon Court.”

The Kirin Elders nodded in agreement.

Exactly.

A Longevity Body… there wasn’t a single demonic beast race that wouldn’t covet it.

The Sky Lion Clan had managed to keep one in captivity, and countless demon races had envied them for it.

Luckily, the Kirin Tribe was known for their long lifespans and didn’t place too much importance on longevity. Otherwise, they would have been just as envious of the Sky Lion Clan’s fortune.

“Regardless of whether Lin Jing is willing to come to the Demon Court, Third Holy Maiden, his current situation must be dire.”

“He has already offended two major Demonic Sects, and now he’s provoked the Sky Lion Clan. That Sky Lion Holy Maiden won’t be able to protect him for long.”

“We don’t know exactly what happened to her, but I suspect she used some secret art that burns her lifespan. Only such a method could temporarily boost the cultivation of a Longevity Body to that level.”

“And such a method can’t last long. So I’m wondering… should we send a team to find these two Longevity Bodies and bring them back safely?” Grand Elder Lin Ye suggested.

“At this point, only the Demon Court can shelter them.”

There were only two Longevity Bodies in the world. Even if they had no intention of consuming them, they couldn’t allow them to fall into another faction’s hands.

“You’re right.”

Xu Qingyao took a deep breath and said, “I’ll send people to search for them. I also ask all of you Elders to mobilize the Kirin Tribe’s experts to assist in the search.”

“You should help too.” First Holy Son Liu Yan’s mind stirred, and he said, “You’ve even slept with a Longevity Body.”

“A Longevity Body, one of the hardest physiques to cultivate, yet he became the number one prodigy of the Ancient Kingdom…”

Seventh Holy Son Shen Xi looked at Xu Qingyao, for the first time realizing the true wonder of the Beast Taming Art.

Thinking about it carefully, that technique really did seem to be a perfect match for the Longevity Body.

We can’t let Xu Qingyao find Lin Jing first. We must capture him and the other Longevity Body before she does.

The First and Second Holy Maidens shared the same thought, their tea party completely forgotten.

However, just as the three Holy Maidens and the five Kirin Elders were about to take action, a wave of solemn and majestic energy suddenly spread from a certain part of the Kirin Secret Realm.

The air began to ripple with delicate spiritual energy, as if ancient memories were being gently awakened.

Then, an astonishing scene unfolded—those ancient Kirin ancestors who had long slumbered in the realm began to rise from their graves under the light of dusk. Their presence sent tremors through the bloodlines of every Kirin present.

“Hm?”

The unexpected turn of events made the First and Second Holy Maidens, as well as the Kirin Elders, pause. Seeing this, First Holy Maiden Liu Yan immediately congratulated, “I wonder which prodigy of the Kirin Clan is breaking through in the ancestral land?”

“Judging by this phenomenon, someone must have gained the recognition of an ancestor.”

The five Kirin Elders remained silent.

Grand Elder Lin Ye muttered, and the two Holy Maidens were stunned.

“The one currently in the ancestral land isn’t a Kirin Tribe member. It’s the pet beast of Third Holy Maiden’s follower.”

“That little Kirin with an Imperial Bloodline… it really did gain the recognition of the ancestors. But…”

“How is this possible? This aura… it doesn’t feel like just one ancestor, but… many, many ancestors have been awakened by its breakthrough!!!”

The other elders spoke in disbelief.

They had known from Lin Ye that an outsider was undergoing a breakthrough in the ancestral land, but they never expected it to be such a monster.

Eighteen pillars of light shot into the sky.

In the Kirin Ancestral Land, countless azure lights illuminated the entire secret realm.

Not only did it attract the attention of everyone present, but it also awakened countless Kirins in closed-door cultivation.

Even the ancient monsters in the depths of the secret realm—those who had reached the Void Mystical Demon Ancestor Realm—opened their eyes in response to this disturbance.

“Rare Bloodline…”

A deep voice echoed from within the Kirin Clan’s depths.

“The Kirin Tribe… how long has it been since we’ve seen a rare bloodline?”

“Third Holy Maiden, just who is this follower of yours? And what exactly is his pet beast, for it to move our ancestors so deeply?”

Facing the five Kirin Elders’ questioning, Xu Qingyao opened her mouth slightly but didn’t know how to respond.

She really wanted to say that, actually, this person and the Lin Jing they had just been discussing were the same person.

But of course, she couldn’t say that.

“Fire Dragon Alchemist is my secret trump card. Naturally, his and his pet beast’s potential are beyond question.” Xu Qingyao said indifferently, “That’s why I wasn’t particularly interested in the Holy Son of the Beast Taming Sect, Lin Jing. Because I already have a far stronger follower.”

“And now, it seems he has lived up to my expectations.”

The other two Holy Maidens looked at Xu Qingyao, cursing her in their hearts.

As if it wasn’t bad enough that some freak from the Beast Taming Sect was connected to her, now this Fire Dragon Alchemist with a Kirin pet had appeared out of nowhere?

At the same time, Lin Jing, who had been waiting outside the cave for Senior Sister, glanced toward the Kirin Ancestral Land.

“So, he finally completed his Core Formation at this moment.”

After months of cultivation, Dragon Carp had finally condensed a demon core within the Kirin Ancestral Land using five different exotic flames—and successfully nurtured its demon core ability!

However, Lin Jing showed no joy. His expression remained silent.

Because despite Dragon Carp’s Core Formation, his own cultivation was still at mid-Foundation Establishment, with a thin line separating him from late-Foundation Establishment.

And more importantly, Dragon Carp’s demon core ability… was monstrous.

Its Demon Core Ability—Myriad Fire Cauldron could refine living beings directly into pills to enhance cultivation…

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 55: Monster Core Divine Ability
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 55: Monster Core Divine Ability

“This isn’t a demonic technique?”
