Lin Jing stood outside Senior Sister Xu Qingyao’s cave dwelling, looking in the direction of the ancestral land.

Ten Thousand Annihilation Cauldron... Can it directly refine living beings into pills that enhance cultivation?

The divine abilities born from a Golden Core or Monster Core vary endlessly, depending on a cultivator’s techniques and physique. Some are offensive, some defensive, and some auxiliary...

Lin Jing had originally thought that since the Dragon Carp condensed its Monster Core using exotic flames, it would develop an extremely lethal offensive divine ability. However, unexpectedly, it turned out to be an alchemy-based one.

That said, it made sense. After all, the Dragon Carp spent every day refining pills...

As for what type of divine ability Ten Thousand Flames Cauldron was, based on the Dragon Carp’s feedback, Lin Jing judged that it was likely both offensive and auxiliary!

Before Ten Thousand Flames Cauldron, enemies would be as weak and helpless as mere alchemy ingredients, trapped inside the cauldron, slowly refined alive into pills.

Only living beings could be refined into pills within the cauldron. If it was a dead object, it wouldn’t work.

This was why Lin Jing called this divine ability a demonic technique.

And to refine living beings... if the cauldron didn’t possess immense power, it would be nearly impossible.

After all, alchemy was a meticulous art—even refining dead materials required an alchemist to have extreme control to avoid an explosion. Refining a living being that could resist? That was obviously even harder.

“This divine ability... how should I put it…” Lin Jing murmured.

The mainstream alchemy techniques today all refine dead materials. Even when using Monster Cores or organs, they are always extracted post-mortem—very few alchemists directly refine living beings…

Rather than calling the Dragon Carp an alchemist in the future, perhaps Pill Demon would be more fitting.

But to Lin Jing, this Ten Thousand Flames Cauldron had arrived at just the right time. After all, with his Longevity Body, he had an enormous need for pills that could rapidly increase his cultivation.

“Demonic? So be it.” Lin Jing shook his head.

In his past life, he had thrown live shrimp into boiling hotpot and live crabs straight into steamers. If refining some demonic beasts alive could enhance his cultivation, Lin Jing saw no problem with it. The only question was—how high was the conversion rate?

“Core formation was successful. But what’s with these eighteen pillars of light in the Kirin Ancestral Land?”

“Did the Dragon Carp’s innate potential truly gain the recognition of the Kirin Clan’s ancestors?”

With doubts in his mind, Lin Jing temporarily left Senior Sister’s cave dwelling and flew toward the Kirin Ancestral Land. He had a feeling that Xu Qingyao and the others would also be drawn by this disturbance.

And sure enough.

As Lin Jing flew, several streaks of light soared up from another direction, converging with him outside the ancestral land.

“Flame Dragon Alchemist.”

Among them, Xu Qingyao was present. She spoke, “What’s the situation with that Kirin?”

“Holy Maiden, it has successfully broken through to the Monster Core stage. However, due to the anomaly in the ancestral land, it doesn’t know what to do next.” Lin Jing halted midair and reported to Xu Qingyao.

“So, he’s the Flame Dragon Alchemist? The beast tamer of the Kirin currently breaking through inside the ancestral land?”

The First and Second Holy Maidens were also present. They looked at Lin Jing in surprise, trying to discern what was so special about him.

The secret ace cultivated by the Eighth Holy Son.

Although Lin Jing himself was only at mid-Foundation Establishment, the commotion caused by this Kirin’s breakthrough was so astonishing that even the two Holy Sons wouldn’t dare underestimate it.

After all, even among the Kirin Clan’s own prodigies, none had ever triggered such a massive response from all of their ancestral spirits at once!

Just how terrifying was this Kirin’s talent and potential? They couldn’t even begin to fathom it.

“It doesn’t need to do anything!”

One of the five great Kirin Clan elders who had followed along spoke up:

“It only needs to remain where it is. The awakened ancestral spirits will bestow their blessings upon it.”

“Some may pass down spell techniques.”

“Some may strengthen its bloodline.”

“Some may directly enhance its cultivation.”

“This process will take some time. All it needs to do is accept the blessings.”

“Thank you for your guidance, esteemed elders.” Lin Jing cupped his fists slightly in gratitude.

“No, we should be thanking you.”

Elder Lin Ye of the Flame Kirin Clan took a deep breath, looking toward the ancestral land.

A Kirin with such unparalleled potential... If it acknowledges its ancestry and returns to the clan, our Kirin Clan will gain another future prodigy of the heavens!

“You all.”

Just as everyone stood guard outside the ancestral land, the void suddenly split open.

A dark-colored Kirin slowly emerged.

Upon seeing this Kirin, the five great Kirin Clan elders immediately bowed in respect.

“Clan Leader.”

“There is no need for you all to stand guard here. The ancestors of the Kirin Clan are using their power of inheritance to enhance that Kirin’s bloodline. It is not an innate Kirin—its bloodline is flawed. After this bloodline refinement, it should reach the threshold of the ‘Ancestral Bloodline.’ This process will take a long time.”

“Understood.”

The five elders were shocked. Eighteen ancestral spirits were working together to strengthen its bloodline?

Near the Ancestral Bloodline?

Did this mean that when it reached the Demon King or Demon Emperor stage in the future, its bloodline strength could potentially reach the level of an Ancient Ancestral Kirin? Would it then have a stable path to becoming a Void Mystical Demon Ancestor?

“Not good.”

The First and Second Holy Maidens’ expressions shifted slightly.

“Three Holy Maidens, you should also go rest. The ancestral land is not a place for too many people to gather.” The Kirin Clan Leader spoke.

“No problem.”

Only the Third Holy Maiden, Xu Qingyao, was smiling knowingly—she had realized something.

Moments later, the Kirin Clan Leader took a long, deep look at Lin Jing before disappearing back into the void.

“Let’s go. Let it absorb this opportunity on its own.” Xu Qingyao grabbed Lin Jing and prepared to leave, not wanting him to interact too much with the other two Holy Maidens.

At the same time.

Within the void, the Kirin Clan Leader turned to another direction and spoke:

“Elder Ancestor, are you certain that the Kirin breaking through in the ancestral land possesses a Rare Beast Bloodline?”

“Yes. I have lived for countless years. Not only have I encountered Rare Beasts, but I have also dealt with true Immortal Beasts.”

From deep within the void, an ancient voice echoed.

“The Third Holy Maiden, Xu Qingyao, has brought us an extraordinary gift.”

“The birth of a Rare Beast is tied to an Immortal’s fall, their flesh transforming into an astral anomaly. The divine abilities born within its bloodline are essentially the inheritance of Immortal spells—the embryonic form of Immortal Techniques.”

“Once a Rare Beast’s divine abilities evolve sufficiently, they will transform into Immortal Techniques. And ‘Immortal Techniques’ have the power to convert a demon beast’s demonic blood into ‘Immortal Blood.’”

“In other words, Rare Beasts are inherently half-Immortal in nature. Among human cultivators, only those with Immortal Physiques can suppress a Rare Beast’s bloodline.”

“For the development of your Kirin Clan’s inheritance, a Kirin cub without Immortal Potential is of utmost importance. After all, when it fully regresses into an Immortal Beast, it will only be the weakest combat power in the Tianyuan Realm, where ascension is no longer possible.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 56: Reversed Heavenly Gang
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 56: Reversed Heavenly Gang

Lin Jing was dragged back by Senior Sister Xu Qingyao, only to be stared at intensely.

“Aren’t you going to say something?” Xu Qingyao asked.

“I didn’t expect the Dragon Carp to cause such a huge commotion either.” Lin Jing shook his head. “But it’s just that a few more Kirin ancestral spirits were awakened—it should be a good thing, right? At the very least, its bloodline strength will be greatly enhanced.”

“It turned out much better than I expected. I was worried at first that its breakthrough might go wrong and end up blowing up their ancestral land.” Lin Jing chuckled.

“That’s not what I’m talking about—though that’s important too…” Xu Qingyao said. “I don’t know what’s up with that big fat fish of yours, but judging by its displayed talent and potential, we might be able to use it to turn the Kirin Clan from a neutral faction into my supporters.”

“Let’s set that aside for now. What’s going on with the Longevity Body in the Sky Lion Clan?”

“This news you stirred up… isn’t it a little too big?”

Her mouth twitched. No wonder Lin Jing had been worried that the Dragon Carp would blow up the Kirin Ancestral Land.

Lin Jing blinked. “The news spread this fast…?”

“I taught the Longevity Body that was being kept by the Sky Lion Clan a secret art that burns lifespan. She burned twenty thousand years of lifespan to temporarily raise her cultivation to the peak of the Void Mystical Stage…” Lin Jing rubbed his nose.

“You—” Xu Qingyao felt a headache coming on. Sure enough, this guy isn’t easy to deal with.

“I didn’t expect her comprehension to be so high. She mastered the Heavenly Demon Physique so quickly—I haven’t even learned it myself.” Lin Jing shrugged.

“Right now, that Longevity Body is following my suggestion and heading back to the Sky Lion Clan to plunder resources. But her cultivation can only last for a day before it drops back to the Core Formation Stage. Even if she has my illusion techniques to conceal her, being out there at Core Formation is still too dangerous. Besides, my illusions won’t last long. I might need you, Senior Sister, to help take her to a safe place.”

“She burned twenty thousand years of lifespan… Does she even have any time left to live?”

“She’s got about ten years left. I’m hoping that within these ten years, she can raise her cultivation to the Nascent Soul Stage. That way, she can extend her life again.” Lin Jing continued, “A Longevity Body dying just like that would be too much of a waste.”

“Give me the location.”

Xu Qingyao knew that finding her was the most urgent matter. If the Longevity Body truly carried the Sky Lion Clan’s wealth, this was an opportunity to seize an enormous fortune.

Lin Jing handed over the token—a pinecone—to Senior Sister. The next moment, a half-step Nascent Soul white peacock appeared.

“This is my external avatar for sensing the power of heaven and earth. It will go retrieve the other Longevity Body.” Xu Qingyao said.

“A beast-type external avatar?”

Lin Jing was surprised upon seeing the peacock.

Xu Qingyao explained, “Normally, human cultivators can only refine humanoid avatars. But I have half-demon blood, so my external avatar can be a beast. As long as it serves its purpose of sensing the power of heaven and earth, the race doesn’t matter.”

The peacock Xu Qingyao spoke, “If there’s nothing else, I’m leaving now. The other two Holy Maidens and the Kirin Clan have probably already begun searching for the two Longevity Bodies. We can’t let them find them first.”

“Alright.”

Lin Jing nodded, waiting for her to return with good news.

After the peacock avatar left, Xu Qingyao’s main body asked, “Are you planning to use the Sky Lion Clan’s resources to raise her to the Nascent Soul Stage?”

“Yes.” Lin Jing nodded. “Her Longevity Body has fully matured. The lifespan increase isn’t just tenfold like normal—it’s far greater. The five hundred years of lifespan gained at the Nascent Soul Stage should be amplified to at least twenty thousand years.”

“At that point, with the Nascent Soul Stage as her foundation, twenty thousand years of lifespan, and the secret art that burns lifespan, her combat strength will be terrifying. Even in the Demon Court, aside from the Demon Emperor, no one would dare claim they could defeat her.”

“That’s truly monstrous.”

Xu Qingyao was astonished. “And since she’s your benefactor, your bond with her will be special. If anyone wants her, they’ll have to get through you first…”

The Core Formation cultivator sighed. “Hoping for her to burn lifespan again is a luxury. Breaking through from Void Mystical to Nascent Soul was already nearly impossible. But having a Longevity Body advance from Nascent Soul to Nascent Soul Transformation… that’s far too difficult. If she burns too much lifespan again, she might not be able to replenish it.”

“And asking you to switch to the Beast Taming Art…? That seems even less realistic.” Xu Qingyao shook her head.

The issue with switching cultivation methods was that the higher one’s cultivation, the harder it became. Once a cultivator reached Core Formation, attempting to switch meant abolishing their cultivation and starting over—a much riskier process than for Qi Refinement or Foundation Establishment.

Furthermore, most cultivators could quickly recover after re-cultivating due to their body's muscle memory. But for someone with a Longevity Body, whose cultivation was extremely slow, restarting would take an unfathomably long time.

“We’ll see.”

Lin Jing planned to observe the effects of the Ten Thousand Flames Cauldron first. If it worked well, it might be the key to the Longevity Body’s cultivation. In that case, switching techniques could become viable.

Ten Thousand Flames Cauldron…

Once the Dragon Carp reached the Nascent Soul Stage, he would unify the Beast Taming Sect and form a demon-catching squad to hunt beasts for refining into pills. This was where sect advantages outshined rogue cultivators.

“Alright, we’ll talk more after we bring her back.” Xu Qingyao agreed.

“Senior Sister, when is the Holy Maiden Tea Ceremony scheduled?” Lin Jing suddenly asked.

“Seven days from now.”

“That’s bad.”

Lin Jing frowned. “I doubt the Dragon Carp’s situation will be settled within seven days. If it can’t participate in the battle, and I can’t reveal the Pine Leaf Mouse or the Ice Soul Gu, my combat strength will take a major hit.”

Xu Qingyao smiled. “It’s fine. It’s just a Holy Maiden Tea Ceremony. If you lose, you lose.”

“If we can win over the Kirin Clan, it’ll still be worth it.”

“How can it be fine? Didn’t you say the performance in the Holy Maiden Tea Ceremony determines the Demon Court’s resource allocation? That’s not a small amount, is it?”

Lin Jing pondered. “Even without pet beasts, I still have other techniques. There are seven days left. You protect me, and I’ll focus on training.”

Xu Qingyao: ???

Lin Jing coughed. “I have a drop of Zhuyan Demon Ancestor’s blood. If things go well, I might be able to summon a Zhuyan for battle. But my cultivation is still too low. If I try to comprehend it alone, it could be dangerous.”

“No matter what, I have to help you secure your followers. In exchange…”

“What do you want?”

“A large quantity of supreme-grade spiritual herbs that can repair shattered Core Formation Pills and Monster Cores.”

Before the Dragon Carp reached the Demon King Stage, it couldn’t recklessly use its Monster Core Divine Ability. But Lin Jing wanted to test the Ten Thousand Flames Cauldron—so he needed spiritual herbs to repair the Monster Core after using divine abilities.

These herbs were extremely rare, but the Demon Court should have them in storage.

Shortly after the Longevity Body’s rampage in the Sky Lion Clan, news of her returning to plunder the Sky Lion Secret Realm once again shocked Qingzhou.

Three days later.

In a cave, the exhausted Longevity Body sat concealed by illusions, adjusting her state. Just then, a majestic white peacock flew inside.

She opened her eyes.

“You… were sent by Lin Jing to pick me up?”

Xu Qingyao’s avatar: ???

“What do you mean he sent me? He begged me to come!”

“So, you’re the Longevity Body… What’s your name? Come with me—I’m taking you back to the Demon Court.”

Longevity Body: ???

Demon Court?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 57: Late Foundation Establishment!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 57: Late Foundation Establishment!

Qingzhou, Yin Corpse Sect Headquarters, Yin Corpse Secret Realm.

Any force with cultivators above the Void Mystical Stage typically establishes its core base in an alternate dimension attached to the Tianyuan Realm.

These alternate spaces, created by Void Mystical experts, are far safer and more concealed than external lands, making them difficult for enemies to invade.

Among the four great Void Mystical Patriarchs of the Yin Corpse Sect, Nine Infant Demon Lord was infamous for his demonic arts, having stripped eight divine physique prodigies of their Nascent Souls and fused them into himself, thus earning his name.

He was also the mastermind behind the Yin Corpse Sect’s "Immortal Corpse" project...

However, due to interference from the Ancient Celestial Kingdom, that plan was now nearly in ruins.

“Patriarch! The Longevity Body has surfaced!”

A Nascent Soul cultivator, wrapped in corpse-sealing cloth, stood before a graveyard, calling out to Nine Infant Demon Lord.

From within the graveyard, an emotionless voice responded.

The Yin Corpse Sect protector bowed and spoke.

“Reporting to Patriarch, our divination was correct—Lin Jing, the Longevity Body, is indeed in Qingzhou. However… he appeared at the Sky Lion Clan’s Centennial Grand Ceremony.”

“The Sky Lion Clan?”

The graveyard trembled violently as Nine Infant Demon Lord tore through space and emerged.

“What is that Longevity Body doing in the Sky Lion Clan?!”

Protector Yuan trembled as he continued,

“At the grand ceremony, Lin Jing openly revealed his identity as a Longevity Body and placed a bounty on our sect’s cultivators—offering his own Longevity Blood and Flesh as a reward to any beast faction that brought back our heads…”

Nine Infant Demon Lord was stunned for a moment, then laughed in rage.

However, the next sentence wiped the smile from his face.

“Then, by some unknown method, he instantly boosted the Sky Lion Clan’s captive Longevity Body to the peak of the Void Mystical Stage. Together, the two massacred most of the Sky Lion Clan and emptied their treasury before vanishing…”

“I suspect… it’s the legendary Heavenly Demon Physique. After all, Lin Jing has had contact with the Demon-Slaying Bureau. His real goal in going to the Sky Lion Clan may have been to pass on the Heavenly Demon Physique to the other Longevity Body.”

“If that’s the case, it explains how two low-stage Longevity Bodies managed to cause such chaos in the Sky Lion Clan…”

A dead silence followed.

Nine Infant Demon Lord finally spoke in a low, ominous tone.

“Heavenly Demon Physique… Since the destruction of the Heavenly Demon Sect, in all these millennia, only two people in the Ancient Celestial Kingdom have ever cultivated it successfully.

“And you’re telling me… both of these Longevity Bodies have achieved it?!”

“Damn it!!”

BOOM!

The graveyard exploded in a surge of dark energy.

Protector Yuan remained silent.

The True Purpose of the Yin Corpse Sect

Countless years ago, in an ancient ruin within the Ancient Celestial Kingdom, the Yin Corpse Sect discovered a Celestial Beast Corpse.

This corpse was firmly bound to the ruin, making it impossible to move.

Thus, the Yin Corpse Sect transformed the entire ruin into one of their branch strongholds, beginning an endless effort to refine the Celestial Beast into a Corpse Puppet.

However, just as they reached the final stage, the Demon-Slaying Bureau discovered their operations and destroyed the base.

Most of the Yin Corpse Sect cultivators stationed there were wiped out, and even a Void Mystical Patriarch had his physical body obliterated.

Although they eventually managed to transfer the Celestial Beast Corpse, the forced relocation severely damaged it, making it desperately in need of rare resources to repair.

Through research, the Yin Corpse Sect found that the best materials for restoring the Celestial Beast Corpse were the purest sources of Life and Death Energy.

One of these sources resided within Longevity Bodies.

Whether refining the Celestial Corpse or repairing the Corpse Puppet, a Longevity Body was essential.

This was why the Yin Corpse Sect had targeted Lin Jing from the very beginning and orchestrated the Hidden Dragon Academy infiltration plan.

However—

Lin Jing vanished like a rat in the shadows, avoiding detection despite the efforts of Void Mystical divination experts searching for him.

And now, after all this time, the first news they received about Lin Jing was…

He and another Longevity Body had caused absolute devastation in the Sky Lion Clan.

It was beyond terrifying.

“Originally, if we couldn’t find Lin Jing, we planned to simply seize the Sky Lion Clan’s Longevity Body.”

“Even though the Sky Lion Ancestor is extremely difficult to deal with, compared to restoring the Celestial Beast Corpse, offending a Sky Lion Ancestor is nothing.”

“But now, that Longevity Body’s Life Energy has likely been completely drained…”

“Only Lin Jing remains.”

“According to reports, he comes from the Beast Taming Sect, and the sect master’s adoptive daughter is a Demon Court Holy Maiden. If he chooses to join the Demon Court, then all hope of capturing a Longevity Body is lost.”

“Although the chances of a Longevity Body willingly aligning with the Demon Court are slim, still…”

Nine Infant Demon Lord’s voice grew dark and ominous.

“You idiots… You knew about the Longevity Body for so long—why didn’t you capture him immediately?!”

“If we had taken him earlier, we wouldn’t be in this situation now!”

Protector Yuan remained silent.

The truth was that the Yin Corpse Sect barely had any powerful cultivators left in the Ancient Celestial Kingdom. Most were stationed to guard the Celestial Beast Ruin.

At the time, Lin Jing was just a mere Qi Refinement-stage Longevity Body—he wasn’t considered a priority.

By the time he entered the Beast Taming Sect, even sending a Nascent Soul demonic cultivator to capture him would have been difficult.

The sect had a protective grand formation and a Dragon Vein guardian—attacking it outright would have required careful planning to avoid alarming the Demon-Slaying Bureau.

But before they could even discuss their next move, Lin Jing left the Beast Taming Sect and joined Hidden Dragon Academy.

At that point, capturing him became even more difficult.

And then, when the Celestial Beast Corpse was damaged, requiring Life Energy to repair it, no one had anticipated just how important Lin Jing would become.

“Enough.”

Nine Infant Demon Lord exhaled.

“Continue searching for Lin Jing. Do not give up. He may not have fully mastered the Heavenly Demon Physique yet.”

“Before the sect master entered seclusion, he entrusted me with refining the Celestial Corpse. I will not disappoint him…”

A flicker of fear passed through his expression.

The current sect master of the Yin Corpse Sect, the Corpse Emperor, was an ancient Dao-Seeking powerhouse—even older than the first Ancient Emperor.

He was a Corpse Cultivator, but had suffered severe injuries in his battle against the Ancient Emperor, forcing him into seclusion for thousands of years.

The Yin Corpse Sect’s very existence revolved around gathering rare physiques and resources to sustain his recovery.

The Celestial Corpse Refinement was an absolute priority.

“I will personally hunt the Longevity Body.”

Even though his previous incursion into Hidden Dragon Academy had left him cursed and wounded, there was no more time for recovery.

With Lin Jing’s identity exposed, countless factions would soon come after him.

As the Yin Corpse Sect was thrown into chaos, Lin Jing was in seclusion under Xu Qingyao’s protection.

By using his Beast Taming Physique to suppress the Zhuyan Demon Ancestor’s Blood, Lin Jing was gradually comprehending its terrifying ancient power.

The clash between them deepened his understanding of the Zhuyan Demon Ancestor.

After several days of intense cultivation—

Lin Jing broke through to the Late Foundation Establishment Stage!

Xu Qingyao, who had been guarding him, smirked.

“He nearly drained all the spiritual energy in this cave while breaking through.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 58: Disciple of the Demon Ancestor
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 58: Disciple of the Demon Ancestor

"Feels... pretty good." Lin Jing stretched lazily and smiled. "A solid foundation means I needed more spiritual energy to break through... Senior Sister, how long until the tea ceremony?"

"It's today," Xu Qingyao replied. "If you didn’t finish your cultivation soon, I was going to wake you up myself."

"Get ready. I've already brought Gu Qianqiu back to my palace. Let's wrap up this tea ceremony quickly. This Longevity Body girl is like a mute—she won’t talk to my avatar, nor will she tell me how much she looted. Clearly, she still doesn’t trust me enough. You might have to approach her yourself."

Lin Jing was stunned. "That’s understandable, right? After all, you took her to the Demon Court. This place sounds even scarier than the Sky Lion Clan. It’s natural she wouldn’t trust you. By the way, her name is Gu Qianqiu?"

Senior Sister smirked. "You didn’t ask her name? Then what did you call each other? Never mind that—has your big fat fish finished yet? Are you, a Beast Tamer, really going to fight without your pet?"

Sensing for a moment, Lin Jing was surprised to find that the Dragon Carp’s bloodline had reached an incredibly terrifying level. When it first evolved to an emperor-tier bloodline, it was barely hanging on—its innate talent was too weak, and all its strength lay in its rare beast abilities. But now, under the blessing of the Kirin Clan’s ancestor, its bloodline strength had nearly reached the pinnacle of the emperor-tier.

Strength, defense, mana, and vitality had all surged.

"It might take a few more days."

"The Kirin Clan wouldn’t care, but the other two Holy Maidens might not agree. The tea ceremony’s date has been set, and if we postpone it unilaterally, we’ll be making an enemy for no good reason. They wouldn’t agree, since it concerns their own interests."

"What a hassle." Xu Qingyao sighed. But Lin Jing didn’t mind. "It’s fine. Just trust in my strength..."

"Of course I trust you. I was the one guarding you when you suppressed the Zhuyan Bloodline, so I could feel that power firsthand. But among the followers of the other two Holy Maidens, there are ‘Demon Ancestor Disciples.’ Their cultivation is at the peak of the Demon Core Stage—they’re no pushovers. You’re only at late Foundation Establishment. That’s an entire major realm of difference."

Lin Jing chuckled. "Senior Sister, you’re forgetting something important. Do you remember what power I used to suppress the Zhuyan Bloodline?"

His eyes flashed as he looked at Xu Qingyao. In that instant, she felt a familiar suppressive force pressing down on her demon bloodline. But with her cultivation at the peak of Nascent Soul and her human bloodline making up more than half, the suppression wasn’t too obvious.

For a Demon Core Stage cultivator, though—for a pure-blooded demon beast—it would be an entirely different story.

"A power derived from the Beast Taming Art," she murmured.

"I call it the Beast Taming Physique. It’s a power that was born when I broke through to the Extreme Stage of Qi Refinement. It effectively suppresses demon beast bloodlines. With this ability, as long as my opponent isn’t human but a demon beast, their strength won’t be fully unleashed. The gap between us isn’t as big as you think, Senior Sister."

"So that’s how it is." Xu Qingyao felt relieved, but then became frustrated. She had always been gifted—already at Foundation Establishment when she joined the Beast Taming Sect—so she never even considered reaching the Extreme Stage of Qi Refinement. If she had known that it could cause such a transformation in the Beast Taming Art, she would have never rushed her Foundation Establishment.

...

Not long after, Xu Qingyao summoned the entire Sparrowfeather Squad to attend the Holy Maiden Tea Ceremony. When Ouyang Hao and the others arrived, their gazes were still fixed in the direction of the ancestral land...

"This monster..."

They had, of course, noticed the commotion caused by the Dragon Carp. After all, the pet beast had received blessings from eighteen Kirin ancestors at the same time. Just how absurd was this Fire Dragon Alchemist?

"Wait, the Holy Maiden Tea Ceremony is today, isn’t it? Fire Dragon, your pet is still over there—how are you going to fight?" Senior Brother Ouyang asked. The other Sparrowfeather members were concerned too. This Fire Dragon Alchemist was strong, sure, but he practiced the Beast Taming Art—his full strength relied on his pet beast. If his strongest pet was still receiving blessings, how was he going to fight?

"That is a bit of a problem. But who said I only have one pet?" Lin Jing said casually. "Let’s go. Stop worrying about my affairs."

Everyone froze. Right. Who said he could only have one pet?

But in theory, shouldn’t a Beast Tamer focus on cultivating one strongest pet? Weren’t all other pets just auxiliary tools? Could it be that every single one of his pets was ridiculously strong?

Kirin Courtyard

The Eighth Holy Son, Xu Qingyao, and the leader of the Demon Subordinates were the first to arrive. The First Holy Son, Liu Yan, and the Seventh Holy Son, Shen Xi, had yet to show up. Aside from them, the courtyard was filled with many demon beasts and a significant number of Kirin Clan members.

Among them, the most eye-catching figures were a Dragon Turtle and a white-and-black patterned bear.

Both had cultivation at the peak of the Demon Core Stage, and their bloodline strength was terrifying—far beyond even most emperor-tier demon beasts. Most importantly, aside from their bloodline power, they also possessed other fearsome abilities within them.

"That Dragon Turtle and Iron-Eating Beast were taken as honorary disciples by Demon Ancestor-level monsters and received part of their inheritance. They’re extremely difficult to deal with. They’re the strongest generals under the First and Second Holy Maidens." Ouyang Hao whispered to Lin Jing.

As Lin Jing observed them, the gathered demon beasts were also watching him. After all, they had all witnessed the commotion caused by his pet beast in the ancestral land.

But now, they had the same question as the Sparrowfeather Squad.

His pet beast was still receiving blessings, meaning it obviously couldn’t fight in the tea ceremony. Yet why did the Third Holy Maiden look so unbothered? Why hadn’t she requested the Kirin Clan to change the date?

"Since all three Holy Maidens are present, let us now examine the progress each of you has made in the Demon Race Revival Plan over the past decade."

The host of the tea ceremony was the Kirin Clan’s chief, a dark-attribute Mutant Ink Kirin, who had reached the early Void Mystical Stage.

Seeing that everyone was seated, he slowly continued, "But before that, as per tradition, we shall start the tea ceremony with an entertainment segment. The Kirin Clan will present treasures as stakes for the tea ceremony."

"There are three supreme treasures. The competition will follow an arena format—three sides will compete, and the victor will stay while the loser is eliminated. The Holy Maiden whose follower stands on the arena at the end shall receive all three supreme treasures, which she may then bestow upon her most outstanding followers."

"Additionally, the results of this competition will be reported to the various clans of the Demon Court. This concerns the resources each Holy Maiden can request for her followers’ cultivation."

After speaking, the Kirin Clan’s chief presented three treasures.

"The first: a top-tier exotic flame, the Heavenly Thunder Flame Origin Fire—an exotic flame born from the heavens, imbued with the power of lightning."

"The second: a top-tier magical artifact, the Lin Bone Ring, which can summon a peak Demon Emperor-level Bone Kirin to fight on the user’s behalf."

"The third: the Thousand-Year Lifespan Fruit. Consuming it extends life by a thousand years. However, this fruit is extremely potent and cannot be consumed by those nearing the end of their lifespan. Only those in their prime with abundant vitality can absorb its energy."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 59: Prison-Suppressing Dragon Turtle
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 59: Prison-Suppressing Dragon Turtle

"As expected of the Kirin Clan, they’re truly generous."

The three Holy Maidens beamed with joy, and their followers were eager to fight.

A top-tier exotic flame—once refined, it could be used for a lifetime without issue, not to mention that the Heavenly Thunder Flame was among the most terrifyingly destructive of its kind.

With this flame, one could even experience a faint trace of tribulation lightning in advance—undeniably precious. Only an ancient clan like the Kirin, who had birthed multiple Void Mystical Demon Ancestors, could afford such treasures.

As for the Bone Kirin Ring, it was a life-saving artifact gifted by the Kirin Clan to their most talented youths. Extremely rare, it required no mana to activate and could summon a Bone Kirin guardian at no cost.

The Bone Kirin itself was a mutant species of the Kirin Clan, born without flesh—only a skeletal frame—yet possessed terrifying combat prowess. Its bones were as tough as magical artifacts, making it a formidable warrior.

The Kirin summoned by this ring wasn’t its true form, but even as an avatar, its combat power was unrivaled within the Nascent Soul Demon Emperor realm.

As for the Thousand-Year Lifespan Fruit, it was a specialty of the Kirin Clan, nurtured for countless years by their unique longevity aura. Its only drawback was its extreme potency—far less gentle than other longevity elixirs—and its resistance buildup was immense, meaning it could only be consumed once.

For them, the key wasn’t just the added years, but the extension of their "prime cultivation period." With a prolonged golden age, they could train at peak efficiency for a longer time without decline in learning ability due to aging.

"Win it for me." The First Holy Maiden concealed her expression with a fan, her eyes gleaming. "If you secure victory, all three of these treasures will be yours. I’ll even add three more resources of the same level as a reward."

Behind her, both humans and beasts stirred with excitement.

"If you claim the ultimate victory, not only will these three treasures be awarded to the outstanding performers, but I will also personally craft an exclusive demon technique tailored to you." The Second Holy Maiden, Shen Xi, spoke.

Behind her, the gathered beasts took a step forward.

The two Holy Maidens were determined to win. After all, they had dominated every previous Holy Maiden Tea Ceremony.

Although this time, the Third Holy Maiden, Xu Qingyao, had brought along a freak known as the Fire Dragon Alchemist, his pet beast was still receiving the Kirin Ancestors' bloodline blessing and couldn’t participate in battle.

Even if it could, that newborn monster Kirin had only just reached the initial Demon Core Stage—it might not even be able to defeat the Demon Ancestor Disciples at the peak of the Demon Core realm.

"Do your best." When it came to Xu Qingyao's side, she simply said, "How’s that Solar False Core working for you? If you perform well… I’ll have the Fire Dragon Alchemist make you a few more."

Lin Jing: ???

"ROAR!!" Led by the great black bull, the Sparrowfeather Squad was fired up.

"You guys can forget about showing off." Lin Jing wasn’t about to waste his time making pills for them.

One wasn’t enough?

Senior Sister really had no stock left? Why was she making him hand out rewards?

"Alright, as per the rules, First Holy Maiden, please send out your defender first," the Ink Kirin Clan Chief announced.

The First Holy Maiden nodded. From behind her, a massive crimson Dragon Turtle slowly stepped forward and flew onto the arena.

It was none other than her strongest subordinate—a Dragon Turtle with the inheritance of a Demon Ancestor!

At this moment, as the Seventh and Eighth Holy Sons watched, Ouyang Hao once again leaned close to Yan Yi and whispered, "This guy is ridiculously strong."

"The Demon Court has a top-tier technique called Life or Death Scripture. Its recovery and defensive capabilities undergo qualitative leaps with each breakthrough, but it’s incredibly difficult to cultivate. That guy is one of the very few who succeeded. Because of this, he was taken as an honorary disciple by a Demon Ancestor of the Demon Court, who then passed down a top-tier attack and defense technique—Dragon Prison Suppression Art."

"You know about the Dragon Prison Suppression Art, right? The Dragon Prison was an ancient East Sea Dragon Clan prison, rumored to have once held even half-immortals from the Ancient Celestial Palace. The Dragon Prison Suppression Art was cultivated by the Prison-Suppressing Dragon of that era—it transforms the body into an immortal prison, unbreakable and unshakable, capable of suppressing with sheer force. It was thought to be lost when the East Sea Dragon Clan was wiped out, but apparently, a Demon Ancestor of the Demon Court still possesses it—and even passed it down to that guy."

The alchemist nodded—it made sense. After all, the Dragon Clan had once been the leaders of the ancient demon race. It wasn’t surprising that the Demon Court had preserved some of their legacies.

At this moment, the Second Holy Maiden, Shen Xi, spoke. "Sending out your strongest right from the start? Interesting. Aren’t you afraid we’ll just wear it down with repeated attacks?"

"I have no such fears," the First Holy Maiden replied with a slight smile.

"Second Holy Maiden, please send out your challenger," the Kirin Clan Chief continued. The sequence of defenders and attackers was evidently determined by the ranking of the Holy Maidens.

The Second Holy Maiden nodded as well. From behind her, a massive Late-Stage Demon Core Giant Elephant grew even larger as it stepped onto the arena.

The Giant Elephant’s bloodline was overwhelmingly dominant. With a thunderous stomp, it slammed down on the Dragon Turtle. But as prison runes spread across the turtle’s shell, a loud boom sent the elephant flipping backward.

This outcome surprised no one. The power gap was obvious, and the Second Holy Maiden’s true goal was simply to wear the opponent down.

However, the scene that followed made both Shen Xi and Xu Qingyao’s expressions shift.

After two more rounds of attacks, the Dragon Turtle showed no intention of counterattacking—it simply kept defending with the Dragon Prison Suppression Art.

Logically, this should have been a decent strategy, as the Dragon Prison Suppression Art was bound to consume a massive amount of energy. But…

As the battle went on, the Giant Elephant was the one getting exhausted, while the Dragon Turtle’s power was steadily rising. Its energy, instead of depleting, was actually being replenished.

"Mastered Dragon Prison Suppression Art… It has fully mastered the technique?" The Second Holy Maiden frowned.

"Finally noticed?" The First Holy Maiden smirked. "I paid a steep price to send Egu to Poseidon Palace for deep training so he could perfect the Dragon Prison Suppression Art. At full mastery, the more damage he endures, the stronger he becomes. If you think you can beat him through attrition, you’re deluding yourself."

"Your Highness, you don’t need to explain everything so clearly," the Dragon Turtle, Egu, said indifferently, as he sent the Giant Elephant crashing off the arena with a single strike. His crimson eyes swept coldly across the crowd, lingering especially on the Iron-Eating Beast under the Second Holy Maiden.

Now knowing that his formidable opponent had fully mastered his technique, the Iron-Eating Beast’s expression grew serious.

Even Xu Qingyao frowned—she hadn’t expected this at all. She glanced at Lin Jing, only to see him looking utterly unbothered. She let out a sigh of relief.

"What do you think…?"

"The opponent is strong, stronger than I expected. Beating him will take considerable effort—I'll have to go all out," Lin Jing admitted. "But… it’s still within my capability. Ouyang, do you want to go have some fun?"

For the other Sparrowfeather members, performing well for rewards wasn’t a huge deal. But Lin Jing treated Senior Brother Ouyang with respect—if he wanted to step onto the stage for a moment of glory, Lin Jing had no objections.

"Huh? Me? Quit joking," Ouyang Hao froze.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 60: The Monkey King Emerges
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 60: The Monkey King Emerges

"Haven't you cultivated Twin False Cores?" Lin Jing asked. "Doesn’t matter if you can break through or not—just land a hit and come back down."

"Forget it, forget it." Ouyang Hao gave a bitter smile, lacking confidence. "The gap in cultivation is too wide, and my bloodline is much weaker. Most importantly, my opponent has a water attribute, which counters the Solar False Core Technique."

"No point in handing my power over to the enemy. Captain, you go up."

"Yeah, Captain, you should go." The great black bull and the others chimed in. They had no interest in fighting that monster. Even with the Solar False Core Technique, they'd definitely be one-shot!

Let Lin Jing or the Iron-Eating Beast—monsters of the same level—fight the Dragon Turtle instead. If they both took each other down, that would be ideal. Then, the rest of them could finally have their moment to shine.

Lin Jing was speechless. He was trying to hand Ouyang Hao an easy contribution. Even a tiny effort would count. But once he went up, that was it—there’d be no next time.

"If you’re not going, then I’ll do it." Lin Jing shook his head and stepped onto the arena.

Seeing this, the Kirin Clan’s chief and elders, as well as the two other Holy Maidens and their followers, all focused their attention.

The Third Holy Maiden was sending him in this early?

They had expected that the smartest move would be for the Fire Dragon Alchemist to wait… After all, what if his Fire Kirin finished receiving the ancestral blessings and emerged soon?

Going up now wasn’t a wise choice.

"Late Foundation Establishment..." The Dragon Turtle, Egu, glanced at Lin Jing and curled his lips in disdain. "You’re the Beast Tamer under the Third Holy Maiden. I assume this means you have other pet beasts to use?"

"That’s right." Lin Jing chuckled.

"If it’s that Fire Kirin, I might be a little cautious. But anything else..." The Dragon Turtle, like most of its kind, was filled with arrogance.

However, as ripples of spatial energy flickered around them, Lin Jing began summoning his other pet beast—causing Egu’s expression to change drastically.

Boom.

A sound like a war drum echoed as Lin Jing stood on the arena. Beside him, a towering white-haired, red-footed giant ape slowly emerged. The moment it appeared, the entire audience gasped, feeling an overwhelming bloodline pressure.

Dragon Turtle Egu felt it immediately—this giant ape’s bloodline was suppressing his own!

"Ancestral bloodline?! What kind of joke is this?! A Demon Ancestor-level bloodline?!" Egu cried out in shock.

It wasn’t just him—the First and Second Holy Maidens also abruptly stood up, unable to believe that Lin Jing had summoned a beast with an ancestral bloodline.

This was an ancestral bloodline—an entity with an incredibly high likelihood of becoming a Demon Ancestor!

Every demon beast present was in shock. Even the Kirin Clan’s powerhouses were astounded.

They had assumed the Fire Kirin was Lin Jing’s strongest pet. Who would have thought he’d summon something with an ancestral bloodline instead?!

"A remnant of the Primordial Era… Zhuyan." A Kirin Clan expert recognized the creature.

"How could a Zhuyan still exist in this era?" The First and Second Holy Maidens were bewildered.

The Zhuyan radiated the aura of an early-stage Demon Core cultivator. Its ancestral bloodline pulsed with boundless killing intent, making it indistinguishable from a real, living being.

Only Xu Qingyao knew the truth—Qi Rong was an illusion. A manifestation created through illusion techniques and materialized using the Inverse Void Art. However, because the illusion of the Zhuyan was so exaggeratedly realistic, even the Dragon Turtle saw it as genuine.

In the eyes of everyone, what stood before them was a real ancestral beast!

The members of Sparrowfeather stood frozen, pointing at the Zhuyan in disbelief.

"It-it-it-it...!"

Their Holy Maiden had brought back an absolute monster! Even a beast with an ancestral bloodline was within his summoning ability?!

No wonder he looked down on that Longevity Body girl, Lin Jing!

"Smash." Lin Jing commanded.

In the next instant, the Zhuyan swung its massive fist.

BOOM!

The prison runes on the Dragon Turtle's shell shattered, its demon technique broken.

Then—CRACK!—the shell itself began to crack in several places.

Dragon Turtle Egu was stunned, heart pounding in fear.

The arena fell silent. Then, Egu suddenly released a massive shockwave, forcing the mountain-sized Zhuyan backward.

Immediately after, the cracks on his body and shell began to heal at an astonishing speed.

Huff… huff…

Panting heavily, Egu suddenly burst into laughter.

"HAHAHAHAHA!"

He let out a sigh of relief.

The bloodline aura of the Zhuyan had indeed startled him. But now that they had exchanged blows, he realized something.

It could break through his defense, but it couldn’t kill him in one hit or seriously injure him. That meant… victory was still his.

"Your bloodline is indeed formidable, but your cultivation level is too low."

Boom!

The Zhuyan struck again.

But the Dragon Turtle, having cultivated the Life or Death Scripture, had absurd defenses and regenerative power. No matter how hard the Zhuyan pressed, it couldn’t inflict fatal damage.

"Demon Core Divine Ability—!"

Unwilling to stay on the defensive, Egu activated his Demon Core Divine Ability. Although using it would wear down his core, obtaining elixirs to restore it was no issue given his status.

This was a defensive-type divine ability. Once activated, his shell became as impenetrable as the Ninth Heaven itself.

When the Zhuyan’s next punch landed—Egu remained completely unharmed.

Instead, the Zhuyan’s fist let out a crack—the sound of bones breaking.

"Looks like Egu still has the upper hand..."

The Kirin Clan’s experts were still in shock over the existence of the Zhuyan, but the difference in cultivation levels was difficult to overcome.

"Can he really do it…?"

Xu Qingyao was now concerned. After all, the Zhuyan was just an illusion.

If it had been the real thing, perhaps it would stand a chance.

Had Lin Jing miscalculated the difference in power?

She observed him closely—he showed no signs of panic.

"You’ve got some skill." Lin Jing muttered.

The Dragon Turtle was even tougher than he had anticipated.

He shot a reassuring glance at his senior sister, then turned his gaze to Egu, who was beginning to act smug.

"In that case… let’s use a Demon Core Divine Ability too."

The Zhuyan illusion naturally had no such ability. But its form had yet to reach its peak.

By mimicking a divine ability, its illusion could be further strengthened!

Up until now, the illusion beast hadn’t even been enhanced with Spell Spirit Origin!

"Transform. You have my permission to go all out."

"ROAR!!!"

As Lin Jing spoke, the Zhuyan—still reeling from the impact—let out a deafening roar.

A unique fire erupted around its body.

With a mighty bellow, its form shrank in size.

Within its illusionary body, a mushroom-like heart throbbed powerfully.

Lin Jing… began to amplify the illusion using Spell Spirit Origin!

At the same time, his mana consumption skyrocketed—many times more than before.

Fortunately, thanks to his Qi Refinement Thirteenth Stage Foundation Establishment mana reserves, and the additional energy from his other pet beasts…

The Pine Leaf Mouse had no choice but to dump all of its mana into its Beast Tamer.

As the flames dissipated, the Zhuyan’s new form hovered in the air.

No longer a white-haired, red-footed giant ape—it had become an ordinary-looking monkey.

But as the crowd looked on, their expressions grew even more complicated.

Its body had shrunk significantly, yet…

This monkey now wore gleaming golden armor, a golden crown on its head, cloud-walking boots on its feet, and red silk fluttered behind it.

In its hand—it wielded an iron staff.

Though its cultivation and bloodline aura remained the same, it now exuded an even more terrifying presence.

"Is that… still a Zhuyan?" The Kirin Clan’s experts were baffled.

A Ruyi Jingu Bang… a Phoenix-Winged Purple Gold Crown… a Golden Chainmail Armor… Lotus-Woven Cloud-Walking Boots…

Lin Jing stroked his chin. Ever since he had learned illusion techniques, he had been trying to perfect this form, but it had never quite worked. Now, with the ability to flawlessly replicate the Zhuyan, using its image as a foundation and Spell Spirit Origin as the core, had he finally achieved a small success?

This form—this was what he meant when he told Senior Sister he would go all out!

"So this is its Demon Core Divine Ability? Summoning a few magical artifacts? You think that’ll be enough to defeat me?"

The Dragon Turtle felt a faint sense of unease, but still forced itself to fight. Channeling its bloodline power, it condensed an Immortal Prison to suppress its enemy.

"Heh heh!!"

However, as the celestial prison came crashing down, the monkey’s face remained playful and carefree, completely unfazed.

Its Fiery Eyes Golden Gaze flashed.

The Dragon Turtle was struck by a wave of terror, as if it had fallen into an illusion—Layered Illusions!

In its vision, chaos engulfed the ancient East Sea Dragon Palace. The supreme Ancient Celestial Palace trembled.

That iron staff swung down.

The Immortal Prison shattered in an instant…

The Dragon Turtle, its mind shaken by the illusion, lost control of its demon power. Its protective divine ability collapsed.

"Little turtle, big talk for such a small thing. Here, take one, two, three strikes from me!"

The monkey transformed into its Three Heads, Six Arms form. Multiple iron staffs rained down at once.

BOOM!

The Dragon Turtle's shell shattered into pieces, blood and flesh splattering as it was hammered deep into the ground. Its vision blurred.

"You… who… are you…?"

The monkey reverted to its normal form, spun its staff with a flourish, then pointed it at the dumbfounded First and Second Holy Maidens and their followers.

"I barely even used any strength, and it’s already down. Why don’t you all come at me together?" Lin Jing had it taunt them with a smirk.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 61: The Dragon Carp Returns
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 61: The Dragon Carp Returns

Seeing the Dragon Turtle’s shell shattered in just a few strikes, the First Holy Maiden couldn’t accept it. Just who was this one man and one monkey that Xu Qingyao had brought in? E'Gu was, after all, a recognized demonic prodigy by the Demon Ancestors...

"Oh hohohoho…"

While the First and Second Holy Maidens were in shock, only Xu Qingyao burst into laughter, completely abandoning any semblance of a Holy Maiden’s dignity.

Then, she spoke lightly, "Let's take it one at a time."

She wanted to savor the expressions of helplessness on the First and Second Holy Maidens’ faces a little longer.

However, after witnessing the strength of the Great Sage, even the Second Holy Maiden’s strongest follower—an Iron-Eating Beast that had once fought evenly with the Dragon Turtle—had lost all confidence.

"I hate monkeys," it muttered to itself.

Unfortunately, the duel had to continue.

And if someone managed to take one strike, then they’d fall by the second… or the third.

"Cough..."

With a final, deafening blow, the Iron-Eating Beast’s armor was completely shattered, and its entire body was sent flying. The rest of the challengers were left in complete terror.

As for the Sparrowfeather Squad, the silence was absolute.

"This is indeed the pressure of an Ancestral Bloodline."

The Black Kirin Clan Leader took another deep look at Lin Jing, trying to uncover the truth. But unfortunately, even with his Void Mystical Early Stage cultivation, he couldn’t see through anything.

Lin Jing’s Great Sage illusion was stronger than an actual Zhuyan Demon Ancestor not just because he had spent immense mana to conjure an illusionary body adorned with power-enhancing magical treasures, but also because he used Layered Illusions to forge the Great Sage’s Illusionary Mind.

Within the Little Sage’s body, he had layered multiple illusion realms—

The first layer imprinted the grandeur of wreaking havoc in the Dragon Palace and the Heavenly Court, aligning perfectly with the Zhuyan’s natural warlike instincts, allowing for a flawless fusion between beast and illusion.

The seventh layer projected his Beast Taming Physique's full oppressive aura, an illusion that, when gazed upon, would paralyze the enemy with sheer presence alone.

He didn’t even need to cause real damage—so long as their mental state wavered for even a moment, preventing them from smoothly using their Demonic Core Divine Abilities, the battle was as good as over.

This illusion beast, crafted using Spell Spirit Origin, Layered Illusion Technique, Spirit-Piercing Method, and Inverse Void Art, was without a doubt the pinnacle of illusion techniques in the present era.

"I once thought I was simply unlucky to encounter a Kirin cub with rare blood…"

"But now, I see…

"It was because I was too weak—that’s why the rare-blooded Kirin was willing to follow me!"

The Kirin Clan Leader was in awe.

Even Xu Qingyao, at the Foundation Establishment Stage, had never possessed a pet beast with an Ancestral Bloodline.

At this moment, before the Little Sage’s overwhelming might, not a single fighter from the First and Seventh Holy Sons’ factions dared to step forward.

Both Holy Sons were helpless and powerless.

"That damn Xu Qingyao... what kind of absurd luck did she stumble into?"

"Tch, losing a tea gathering duel is nothing."

"Fine. Let’s just end it here."

With that, the Kirin Clan Leader officially announced the start of the next phase of the event—though it was already clear that the match was completely one-sided.

But just as he spoke, a tremor rippled through the ancestral lands of the Kirin Clan.

"ROOOOAAAR!!"

At the same time, a mighty Kirin charged forward.

Even though Lin Jing had been experiencing bloodline suppression this entire time, he could still faintly sense what was happening outside.

He had never truly cared about winning or losing in the Kirin Clan’s gathering.

What mattered to him most was whether or not his Pine Leaf Mouse and Ice Soul Gu were safe.

The only regret he had was that he couldn't fight alongside Lu Jian.

When that moment came, his junior sister would surely reward him handsomely for his glorious performance.

Unfortunately, up until the tea gathering began, the Dragon Carp had yet to complete its bloodline enhancement.

The Dragon Carp was anxious beyond words.

With impatience fueling its potential, it began absorbing blessings at an even faster rate—and at the very last moment, on the day of the tea gathering, it finally completed its bloodline refinement.

BOOM!

It slammed down beside Lin Jing.

First, it glanced at the monkey.

Then, it glanced at Lin Jing.

Finally…

Its eyes locked onto the First and Second Holy Maidens' followers.

"It’s already over."

Lin Jing patted its head.

"Look at you, getting all worked up. You didn’t miss any blessings, did you?"

"Roar???"

The Dragon Carp blinked in confusion.

"Over? Already?"

"Yeah. It's over."

Lin Jing turned to look at the First and Second Holy Maidens.

They both took a deep breath.

A Beast Tamer who wasn’t even in his peak condition had single-handedly crushed their entire faction with just one pet beast.

This Fire Dragon Alchemist—just what kind of monster was he?

If he was allowed to reach Nascent Soul or even Soul Transformation Stage…

Then defeating Xu Qingyao would become impossible.

"But you arrived just in time."

Lin Jing dismissed the Great Sage illusion, then recalled the Dragon Carp.

The moment the Dragon Carp entered the Meditation Staff’s spatial realm, it immediately heard Lin Jing’s voice—

"That battle drained too much energy… I barely have any mana left. Transfer all of yours to me."

Dragon Carp: ???

Meanwhile, outside, the Kirin Clan Leader congratulated Lin Jing for his ultimate victory.

It was also a congratulation for his pet beast successfully receiving the blessings of the Kirin Ancestors.

Three divine treasures floated toward Xu Qingyao.

Of course, she didn’t keep them for herself—this was all Lin Jing’s achievement.

As soon as he returned to her side, she handed them to him without hesitation.

"They're yours," she said with a smile.

"There will be even more rewards when we return."

"Thank you, Senior Sister!"

Lin Jing grinned happily as he collected the three treasures.

The Exotic Flame was perfect for the Dragon Carp—now that it had entered the Demonic Core Stage, its body could withstand even greater destructive forces.

As for the Bone Ring, he decided to keep it as a life-saving tool.

Even though a Soul Transformation Stage Demon Emperor's Bone Kirin wouldn’t be able to withstand too many powerful enemies, it was still a useful trump card.

But the most unexpected and precious treasure was the Thousand-Year Longevity Fruit.

Could it be used to extend the remaining ten years of life that Gu Qianqiu had left?

The fruit was extremely potent—with Gu Qianqiu’s frail body, she might not even be able to handle it.

If she couldn’t take it, then he’d either eat it himself or give it to Pine Leaf Mouse.

If he ate it, he wouldn’t receive the Longevity Body’s benefits, but an extra thousand years of life was still valuable—something he could burn through later without regret, since it was essentially free.

If he gave it to Pine Leaf Mouse, it could sacrifice five hundred years to create an even stronger Wood Clone—one that could stir up even more trouble in his place.

"The entertainment is over. Now, let’s move on to the real matter at hand."

The Kirin Clan Leader turned to the three Holy Maidens.

"Over the past ten years, how has the Demon Race Revival Plan progressed?"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 62: The Ancestral Recognition Pact
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 62: The Ancestral Recognition Pact

"Alright, the grand plan for the revival of the demon race has yet to succeed. The Holy Maidens must continue their efforts," said the Kirin Clan Leader before proceeding to the final segment of the Holy Maiden Tea Ceremony.

The three Holy Maidens would compete against each other to assess their progress in strength.

"It's a done deal, a complete victory this time." Senior Brother Ouyang approached Lin Jing, who was replenishing his true energy, and said, "The followers of Her Highness the Holy Maiden may be weak, but she herself is the strongest among the Holy Maidens!"

"Do you know why?" He transmitted his voice to Lin Jing.

"I can more or less guess." Lin Jing kept his eyes closed, showing little interest in this battle.

His task was already complete.

If Senior Sister ended up getting beaten next, there was nothing he could do to help.

Ouyang Hao chuckled. "The First Holy Maiden has given birth to too many children, causing a decline in her physique and, naturally, in her strength. However, while she isn't powerful now, her future is promising. Once her children grow up, they will become an undeniable force—her true followers in the future."

"But our Holy Maiden is different. Her followers are weak—not because they started out that way, but because her strongest ones were directly contracted as her pet beasts, becoming a part of her own strength. They no longer count as mere followers…"

"So, the weakness of her followers has nothing to do with her being new to the Demon Court. It's entirely self-inflicted…"

"With herself and four pet beasts, it's practically a five-on-one situation, making her the strongest among the Holy Maidens…"

Lin Jing fell silent for a moment. As expected of someone from our Beast Taming Sect...

Just as Senior Brother Ouyang had said, Senior Sister fought against the other two Holy Maidens with no regard for fairness, summoning four pet beasts to gang up on them.

A white crane, a phoenix, a golden roc, and a peacock.

The crane was the first pet beast she had contracted at the Beast Taming Sect, while the other three were chosen from the Demon Court for their outstanding talent and potential.

Both the phoenix and peacock had emperor-tier bloodlines.

The golden roc's bloodline was even more terrifying—it was an ancestral-tier bloodline, akin to the Zhuyan, a true ancient beast.

However, while Lin Jing's Zhuyan ancestor bloodline was merely an illusion, Senior Sister had actually contracted a real ancestral-bloodline pet beast.

Across the entire Tianyuan Continent, there were probably fewer than ten Nascent Soul cultivators who could defeat her. The only one Lin Jing could think of was the current top-ranked prodigy on the Ancient Kingdom’s Celestial Rankings, the Ancient Imperial Prince.

Previously, although Xu Qingyao could suppress the other two Holy Maidens, her followers were completely overpowered. But with Lin Jing’s addition, she achieved an all-around victory over them.

Before long, the tea ceremony concluded. The Third Holy Maiden emerged victorious against the other two and received an invitation from the Kirin Clan Leader.

...

Deep in the Kirin Clan’s Void Realm.

The Dark Kirin Clan Leader walked ahead, with Xu Qingyao following behind.

"Ancestor, I have brought the Third Holy Maiden," the Dark Kirin Clan Leader announced.

As his words fell, a golden light flickered in the depths of the void, outlining the ethereal figure of a Holy Light Kirin. Its aged whiskers swayed as its divine eyes gazed at Xu Qingyao.

"Greetings, elder," Xu Qingyao bowed slightly. This being was the lowest-ranking elder of the Kirin Clan, yet its cultivation had reached the peak of the Void Mystical Stage.

"At the Eight Holy Sons’ Hall, he should already know why you’ve come to meet him…"

"Of course, it's about the Kirin that belongs to the Fire Dragon Alchemist." Xu Qingyao lifted her gaze toward the massive figure. "This should be about its ancestral recognition and return to the clan."

"This cannot be done. It is not an orphan and is already properly placed. However, since it still follows the Fire Dragon Alchemist as his companion, it cannot truly return to the Kirin Clan in the strictest sense. It can only be recognized in name," the Kirin Ancestor stated.

"Naturally, the Kirin Clan is a clan of auspicious beasts. As long as it acknowledges its lineage in name, the clan's fortune will rise. And as its strength grows, the fortune of the clan will rise even further."

"However, as a member of our Kirin Clan, it must one day fully return."

"I hope that once the Beast Tamer dies, it will return to the Kirin Clan entirely, and even in death, its bones and soul shall rest in the Kirin Ancestral Land, protecting the clan for a thousand years."

Uh...

Xu Qingyao’s thoughts turned peculiar. She understood the Kirin Ancestor’s intent—he wanted the Dragon Carp to eventually return to the clan and shift its loyalty from assisting Lin Jing to dedicating itself to the revival of the Kirin Clan.

And the Kirin Ancestor was reasonable about it—he wasn’t demanding an immediate return, just waiting for the Beast Tamer to die!

On the surface, this wasn’t a problem. The Kirin Clan had incredible longevity. Outlasting a human cultivator of the same realm was effortless. By the time the Beast Tamer passed, the Dragon Carp might have only used up a fraction of its lifespan.

This was exactly why powerful-bloodline demonic beasts were willing to become pet beasts. They could use their master's resources to grow stronger while knowing they would outlive them in the end.

But the problem was…

Lin Jing wasn’t like her.

Lin Jing had the Longevity Body. Even if that big fat fish died of old age, Lin Jing might not even grow a single white hair.

Seeing Xu Qingyao hesitate, the Kirin Ancestor added, "Of course, during this time, my Kirin Clan will fully support you, Third Holy Maiden. The entire clan will assist in your research on the Transformation Art."

"Alright, deal. I agree on behalf of the Fire Dragon Alchemist," Xu Qingyao finally said. "Once his natural lifespan is near its end and the Kirin loses its master, it will return to the Kirin Clan!"

The Kirin Ancestor nodded in satisfaction.

...

"This is how it is..."

Afterward, Senior Sister explained the agreement between herself and the Kirin Clan to Lin Jing and the Dragon Carp, leaving Lin Jing speechless.

"..."

The Dragon Carp also lacked confidence in its ability to outlive its Beast Tamer.

"It doesn’t matter. Even if the Kirin Ancestor later realizes you and Lin Jing are the same person, it won't be a problem. As long as we can use the Kirin Clan’s position to request more resources from the Demon Court, that’s all that matters."

"Once the Transformation Pill is successfully developed, political allegiances won’t matter anymore. The Demon Emperor once said that whichever Holy Maiden manages to create a Transformation Art will be nurtured as the next Demon Court heir, personally accepted as his direct disciple." Xu Qingyao pursed her lips.

"Becoming the Demon Emperor’s disciple—that’s the real goal."

"Understood. Once we return, I’ll begin refining the Transformation Pill immediately," Lin Jing replied.

After the Sky Lion Clan incident, the Hidden Dragon Fortune he had obtained was many times greater than even winning first place in the Genius Battle—enough for him to help the Pine Leaf Mouse reach the Core Formation Stage.

"Mm. But before that, follow me to meet Gu Qianqiu and plan her cultivation path. Ten years is too short." Senior Sister shook her head.

"It’s fine. Maybe we can first give her an Ice Soul Lesser Gu to freeze her aging, slowing down the loss of those ten years of lifespan and allowing her to become a Gu Master first…" Lin Jing suggested.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 63: Gu Qianqiu
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 63: Gu Qianqiu

The Holy Maiden Tea Ceremony had ended, and the three factions returned to their respective domains.

With the Kirin Clan choosing its side and committing to fully supporting the Third Holy Maiden in her bid for the Demon Emperor’s direct disciple position, they spared no effort in publicizing Xu Qingyao’s victory and the potential of her top general, the Fire Dragon Alchemist, as they reported the tea ceremony results to other major demon beast clans.

“The Third Holy Maiden, Xu Qingyao, easily defeated the First and Second Holy Maidens... Her combat strength is terrifying.”

“Her follower, the Fire Dragon Alchemist, has contracted a quasi-ancestral-blood Fire Kirin and an ancestral-blood Zhuyan... He single-handedly fought all the followers of the First and Second Holy Maidens and won with ease…”

The various demon beast clans of the Demon Court were stunned.

The Third Holy Maiden had performed flawlessly in this tea ceremony. Apart from the fact that none of the three Holy Maidens had made any progress in the Demon Race Revival Project, Xu Qingyao had achieved an overwhelming victory in every other aspect.

Among them, the Fire Dragon Alchemist, who had contracted a quasi-ancestral-blood Fire Kirin and an ancestral-blood Zhuyan, began to gain recognition across the Demon Court.

Meanwhile, deep within Xu Qingyao’s palace…

Having burned twenty thousand years of her lifespan, she was now in an extremely weakened state, continuously meditating to recuperate.

Seeing her like this, Lin Jing immediately realized that in her current condition, she likely couldn't handle any potent medicinal resources directly.

"You’re here." Deep inside the cave dwelling, Gu Qianqiu slowly opened her eyes and looked at Lin Jing, her face pale.

"With your condition, cultivating normally would be extremely slow. It would be best to spend some time recuperating first." Lin Jing said, then turned to Senior Sister and added, "Senior Sister, send her more nourishing spiritual pills and medicines."

Xu Qingyao rolled her eyes at Lin Jing and said, "I've already sent some… Anyway, Gu Qianqiu, now that this guy is here, you can finally tell us what exactly you managed to loot from the Sky Lion Clan, right?"

Gu Qianqiu finally retrieved a spatial treasure fused within her body. She lifted her hand, and a pearl-shaped artifact floated toward Lin Jing.

"Everything is in here."

"The core treasure vault of the Sky Lion Clan was hidden within an isolated dimension, heavily sealed, accessible only to the Clan Leader and the Ancestor…

"I used a secret technique to temporarily push my cultivation to the peak of the Void Mystical Stage, but I lack advanced spatial mastery and couldn't open it normally. I captured the Sky Lion Clan Leader, but even in death, he refused to unlock the vault. In the end, fearing the Sky Lion Ancestor might return, I brute-forced it, shattering the dimension completely. The ultimate treasures were scattered into the void..."

"In the end, I could only take all the ordinary resources the Sky Lion Clan had placed outside the vault." She sighed with deep regret.

Xu Qingyao also felt a deep sense of loss after hearing this.

"At least none of it was left for the Sky Lion Clan!"

Lin Jing chuckled as he took the spatial treasure and inspected it. "As expected of the Sky Lion Clan. Even their external resources are this vast. Not many magical artifacts, but just the low, mid, and high-grade spirit stones alone could pile into a mountain."

"Beyond that, there are tons of spiritual plants… A large number of monster cores, though unfortunately, none above the fifth grade."

"But it's still more than enough…

"Next, we’ll use these spirit stones to set up a top-tier Spirit Gathering Formation here, entirely dedicated to helping you break through to the Nascent Soul Stage."

"As for the spiritual plants and monster cores, I’ll take them for alchemy. If I refine any suitable pills, I’ll provide them to you—it’ll be far more efficient than you consuming them raw. What do you think?"

Gu Qianqiu nodded slightly and said, "I don't mind. My life was saved by you—I'll do whatever you decide."

"Since that's settled, your main focus moving forward should be to recuperate first, then cultivate as much as possible. Soon, Senior Sister will give you something to slow down your life depletion. You only have about ten years left, but with this, you might last longer."

"Alright…" Gu Qianqiu replied. "My only remaining purpose in life is the complete destruction of the Sky Lion Clan. Once that is done, my life is yours to command."

"Good." Lin Jing had no objections to repaying kindness with kindness.

"When the time comes, I plan to form a ‘Demon Hunting Squad’, tasked with capturing and exterminating these ruthless demon beasts, refining them into pills. You'll be the captain. Ensuring your success in reaching Nascent Soul is not just your goal—it’s your mission. No objections, right?"

Xu Qingyao: ?

"No objections." Gu Qianqiu nodded.

If her future was to hunt down and eradicate brutal demon beasts, then such a future was exactly what she wanted.

A thousand years of resentment and darkness had been suppressed within her heart. Destroying the Sky Lion Clan alone would not be enough.

Though…

Wasn’t this the Demon Court?

Gu Qianqiu remained silent. During her time in the Sky Lion Clan, she had heard that while they were violent and powerful, there were three even stronger and more savage demon beast forces nearby.

The Demon Court was one of them.

"...Say..."

Just as Lin Jing and Xu Qingyao were about to leave, Gu Qianqiu suddenly hesitated.

"Hm? Do you have another request? Speak freely." Lin Jing turned back to look at her.

Gu Qianqiu’s gaze was distant.

"I was born into the Gu Family, a cultivation family in Spring Autumn City, Piao Miao Dao, Qingzhou. After my Longevity Body was exposed, I was imprisoned by the Sky Lion Clan. Now, a thousand years have passed… I don’t know what happened to my parents, or my family…"

"I understand." Lin Jing said. "I’ll have someone investigate your family’s situation."

"However…"

"Mm, I know. I won’t hold out too much hope." Gu Qianqiu lowered her head.

The strongest cultivator in the Gu Family had only reached the Nascent Soul Stage—far too weak to have rescued her from the Sky Lion Clan.

She didn’t blame them.

What she feared, however, was whether her existence as a Longevity Body had dragged her family down.

Although rare physiques were difficult to pass down through bloodlines, a single Longevity Body appearing in a family could mark them as a ‘Longevity Clan’, inviting persecution.

Outside Gu Qianqiu’s chamber…

"The Gu Family issue—I’ll have someone look into it. But… what’s with this ‘Demon Hunting Squad’?"

Senior Sister shot a skeptical look at Lin Jing after they left.

"You, of all people, are from the Beast Taming Sect, which advocates harmony between humans and demon beasts, and you’re in the Demon Court, where demon beasts make up the majority. Now you want to form a Demon Hunting Squad? Are you trying to rebel?"

Lin Jing grinned and said, "This squad will be extremely useful in the future. There are countless demon beasts out there, and not all of them align with the Demon Court or the Beast Taming Sect. We’ll just hunt those ones—like the Sky Lion Clan."

"Speaking of which, Senior Sister, don’t forget the spiritual items needed for repairing monster cores and golden cores, and what exactly was the ‘additional reward’ you promised from the Kirin Clan?"

With the Kirin Clan's allegiance, Xu Qingyao was in a great mood. She casually said, "No problem. You’ll get them soon. As for the additional reward—I’ll lend you my Holy Maiden Token for a while. How about that?"

"Uh?"

"The Holy Maiden Token contains a trace of the Demon Emperor’s will. Since you cultivate the Illusionary Path, you should know exactly what that means. But I think it’s best if you wait until you reach the Golden Core or Nascent Soul Stage before borrowing it."

Lin Jing took a deep breath.

"Senior Sister, you’re too damn generous!!! If there’s ever a deal like this again, please, count me in!"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 64: The Frozen Beauty Cultivator
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 64: The Frozen Beauty Cultivator

Afterward, Lin Jing returned to his cave dwelling.

The extra reward from Senior Sister was indeed tempting, but she was right—given his current strength, trying to experience the Demon Emperor’s power was nothing but wishful thinking. It would be better to wait until his cultivation improved.

"All the spirit stones go to Gu Qianqiu for cultivation. With such an immense quantity of spirit stones, even a pig could cultivate to the Demon Emperor level. But a Longevity Body can’t be measured by normal standards, and time is still too short… I need to supplement this with other methods."

"All the spiritual plants and herbs—I'll use them to help the Pine Leaf Mouse cultivate the Everlasting Emperor’s Longevity Art to perfection."

"As for the remaining monster cores… I'll use them for alchemy! Every last one goes to Gu Qianqiu."

Among the countless types of pills, there were two mainstream categories:

Pills that increase cultivation.

Pills that assist in breakthroughs.

"The problem is, their effects are terrible. A pill refined from a Demon Core Stage monster, after purifying the impurities, might not even be enough to push a Foundation Establishment cultivator up a minor realm."

"For a Longevity Body or a Golden Core cultivator, it's even worse—practically useless."

"Luckily, Gu Qianqiu looted enough monster cores, and their quality is decent."

"But making Dragon Carp refine this much would be too exhausting… I'll have to borrow Senior Sister’s ‘Crane Cauldron Alchemy Division’ to do it for me. I'm such a good Beast Tamer."

"Still, compared to these mediocre cultivation pills, what I truly look forward to is the Fire Dragon Alchemist refining pills with the ‘Myriad Flames Cauldron’. Turning living creatures into pills will undoubtedly be far more effective—perhaps a single one will be worth hundreds of normal cultivation pills!"

The only issue was that Dragon Carp wouldn’t be able to use its Monster Core Divine Ability without restriction until it reached the Nascent Soul Demon King Stage.

"Still, once Senior Sister delivers the spiritual items to repair monster cores, I’ll capture a demon beast and test it out. Otherwise, I’ll always be curious!"

Just before entering the Time Sandglass, Lin Jing had another thought.

"Since the Emerging Lotus Heart Flame has healing properties, could it also repair damaged monster cores?**"

"Ordinarily, it wouldn’t work, but since Dragon Carp wields the** Flame Emperor’s Divine Ability,** it could fuse other exotic flames with the Emerging Lotus Heart Flame, strengthening it immensely. At that point… could it repair monster cores?"

"If so, Dragon Carp might be able to use the Myriad Flames Cauldron to refine pills even before reaching Nascent Soul…"

But before all that, the priority was researching the Treant Pill for the Pine Leaf Mouse, helping it reach Core Formation as soon as possible!

Inside the Time Sandglass…

The remaining time inside was running out. For every ten years spent inside, the external world required a one-year cooldown before it could be used again.

Lin Jing was about to experience his first cooldown period, but before that, he could still make use of the remaining time.

"Wake up."

Lin Jing called out, and deep within his body, a tiny self-sealed, hibernating spider suddenly stirred.

"...Hungry..."

As soon as the Ice Soul Gu awoke, it complained of hunger.

To prevent the Gu God Sect from tracking it, it had been playing dead to remain hidden—until now, when Lin Jing finally decided to wake it up.

"Sorry, while you were asleep, I siphoned a lot of your energy."

Lin Jing scratched his face awkwardly.

Even though the Ice Soul Gu had been hibernating, it hadn't escaped the fate of being used as an emergency power source.

"But don’t worry—I’ve prepared food for you."
