He pulled out several ice-attribute monster cores.

The next moment, countless white silk threads shot out from the Ice Soul Gu, piercing into the monster cores, rapidly absorbing their frost energy.

"When you're full, I need you to produce a sub-Gu for me. A weaker one, with its ice properties toned down. It only needs to slow down the host’s aging—nothing else."

As a reward, Lin Jing took out a few more ice-attribute monster cores.

The Ice Soul Gu crawled out of his body, dazed, and mumbled:

"Where… is this? You didn’t fall in love again, did you? You need a new host? What kind of physique is it this time…?"

"This is a separate space, so even if the Gu God Sect tries to track you, they won’t be able to locate you."

"As for the sub-Gu, it's for another Longevity Body. It will reside within her."

"...Another Longevity Body?!"

The Ice Soul Gu was stunned.

Its icy blue eyes widened in shock.

Wait… so its sub-Gu gets to enjoy the longevity bloodline… but it has to stay here and just sniff the scent of long life without ever tasting it?!

What kind of cruelty was this?

It refused!

No way!

"Your sub-Gu won’t be able to absorb energy from the other Longevity Body. She’s extremely weak right now. If your sub-Gu drained her, she’d die instantly."

"The only job of your sub-Gu is to slow down her lifespan depletion, even if it means sacrificing itself."

"...Oh!"

Hearing that its sub-Gu wasn’t going to be living in luxury but was actually going to suffer, the Ice Soul Gu suddenly felt much better.

"Kekeke, go forth, my sub-Gu! Fulfill your mission! If I can’t feast, then neither can you!"

"...Huh???"

Suddenly, Ice Soul Gu turned its attention to Dragon Carp, who had just sent a greeting through their contract.

"...Wait… why the hell is Dragon Carp already at Core Formation?!"

"啵~~"

Dragon Carp casually responded, "Not just me—the Pine Leaf Mouse is about to reach Core Formation too."

The Ice Soul Gu froze.

…

It couldn’t sleep anymore.

Returning to Gu Qianqiu’s Cave…

By the time Lin Jing returned, Senior Sister had already finished setting up the Spirit Gathering Formation.

Countless spirit stones were buried underground, forming a spiritual vein that sustained the formation.

Stepping inside, Lin Jing almost didn’t want to leave—it was too comfortable.

"This is an Ice Soul Gu sub-Gu. Once you merge it into your body, it should extend your lifespan by ten years."

"At the same time, it’ll compensate for your lack of offensive abilities—so don’t waste time learning other spells. The power of the Ice Soul Gu will be more than enough."

With a flick of his finger, the sub-Gu flew toward Gu Qianqiu.

As soon as she saw the spider approaching, her pupils shrank, and she scrambled backward, pressing against the wall.

"D-Don’t come any closer!"

Lin Jing blinked.

"Wait… you’re afraid of bugs?"

"...So what if I am?!"

She stared at the Ice Soul sub-Gu, swallowing nervously.

"Well, afraid or not, you still have to merge with it."

Ignoring her protests, Lin Jing commanded the Ice Soul sub-Gu to crawl onto her face and into her mouth.

A piercing scream echoed through the cave.

For someone who had once reached the peak of the Void Mystical Stage and had slaughtered countless Sky Lion demons, her fear of bugs was… understandable.

Before long…

A blue-eyed, snow-haired frozen beauty cultivator was born.

…

Meanwhile, deep in the void…

The Divine Pine was cultivating when, suddenly, a pile of treasures flew through a spatial rift and landed in front of it.

It froze.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 65: Old Friends
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 65: Old Friends

After helping Gu Qianqiu freeze her age, Lin Jing no longer disturbed her cultivation.

For Gu Qianqiu, Xu Qingyao’s palace was now the best place to cultivate. Though the place was frequented by demons, her current physical condition meant she no longer attracted them. As for their mutual enemy, the Sky Lion Ancestor, even if it had the audacity to steal someone from the Demon Court Holy Maiden’s territory, it would have to think twice. Killing a Demon Court ancestor might not cause much of a stir, but if it harmed one of the three remaining half-demon Holy Maidens responsible for the Demon Race Revival Plan, it might just receive a slap from the Demon Emperor.

While Gu Qianqiu focused on cultivation, Lin Jing refused to fall behind.

First, he tossed all the spiritual plants he had snatched from the Sky Lion Clan to the Pine Leaf Mouse, letting it pick out the useless ones for practice. Then, he gave the Heavenly Thunder Flame Source to the Dragon Carp, allowing it to refine another top-tier exotic flame. As for the Ice Soul Gu, Lin Jing currently had no special resources for it, so he put it back into slumber—though it was reluctant.

He believed that by the time it woke up again, he would have found the perfect resource to help it form a Core.

With his three pet beasts settled, Lin Jing also started using this time… to deduce the formula for the Treant Pill using Dragon Fortune.

…

Some time later, Senior Sister finally completed the spirit pill for repairing Golden Cores and Monster Cores.

“Thank you, Senior Sister.” Lin Jing took the storage bag from her.

Xu Qingyao asked, “How’s Gu Qianqiu doing?”

“She’s alright. Her body has mostly recovered, and with the help of the Ice Soul Lesser Gu, she’s managed to slow her life force from depleting. She should have around twenty years left to cultivate.”

“I also looked into it—apparently, the Sky Lion Clan fed her a lot of life-extending resources and cultivation techniques in the past, which made her body highly resistant to such methods. It’s unlikely she’ll be able to extend her lifespan any further with external resources.”

“Oh. I just hope she can successfully reach the Nascent Soul stage in the end. Otherwise, all these resources will have been wasted.” Senior Sister shook her head. Even she had never been so extravagant with spirit stones for cultivation.

“By the way, Senior Sister, you came at just the right time. I have a few more favors to ask of you.” Lin Jing pulled out another storage bag. “Inside are the Monster Cores the Sky Lion Clan had stored externally—about ten thousand of them….”

“I want to refine them all into ‘Ascension Pills’ to help Gu Qianqiu cultivate. But I don’t have the time to handle it all myself. Could I borrow your Crane Cauldron and its alchemists?”

Senior Sister cut him off. “They’re still researching the Transformation Pill. They don’t have the time to work on this.”

Lin Jing grinned. “Haven’t they been researching it for ten years now without making any progress? They should take a break, clear their minds. Refining some simple Ascension Pills would be a nice change of pace.”

“Consider it a vacation for them.”

Xu Qingyao’s lips twitched. “Since when did you start researching the Transformation Pill?”

“Soon, soon.” Lin Jing explained, “Once my squirrel reaches Core Formation, I’ll need it to cultivate a new variety of Transformation Grass. I prefer to start with materials first, rather than alchemical theory—that’s always been my approach to pill refinement.”

“Fine, fine. Do as he says. That task will go to the Crane Cauldron for now.” The junior sister accepted the Monster Cores, then turned back. “By the way, there’s something else.”

“Regarding the investigation He Weilan asked us to conduct on the Gu Clan, I used the Demon Court’s intelligence network and found some things.”

“Before the intelligence on Gu Qianqiu’s Longevity Body was exposed a thousand years ago, the entire Gu Clan mysteriously disappeared. Because it happened so long ago and the Gu Clan was a major family, we still haven’t identified which faction was responsible. However, there was one person—Gu Gantong—who survived because he had married into the Misty Immortal Clan of Piao Miao Dao.”

“If we trace the bloodline, this Gu Gantong should be Gu Qianqiu’s half-brother.”

“Misty Immortal Clan?” Lin Jing mused. “The top clan of Piao Miao Dao? Senior Sister, didn’t you mention that a Misty Immortal Clan princess is getting married soon?”

“Yes. The Misty Immortal Clan never allows their women to marry outside the clan. Even those with mediocre talent would still have their husbands marry into their family. Gu Qianqiu’s brother fell in love with an ordinary female clan member and married into the Misty Immortal Clan.”

“But a thousand years have passed. Since their talents were mediocre, they long turned to dust, never breaking through to the Nascent Soul stage. However, one of their descendants showed great potential and has reached Nascent Soul. That person is still alive today.”

Lin Jing nodded. “Got it. Keep investigating. Most likely, some faction saw the potential of the Gu Clan’s bloodline and decided to conduct experiments on them. No need to tell Gu Qianqiu yet. Once we uncover the full truth and she reaches Nascent Soul, we’ll inform her then.”

By then, in addition to the Sky Lion Clan, another major force would be added to their revenge list.

Lin Jing chuckled.

The treasures of the Sky Lion Clan were just too tempting—he wasn’t done plundering them yet.

Coming from a peaceful country in his past life, his favorite saying had always been: A just cause always has justification.

“You…” Xu Qingyao observed him and realized that his violent tendencies might even exceed her own. Was he already scheming how to provoke Gu Qianqiu into seeking revenge for her clan?

But which faction had destroyed the Gu Clan?

“I’m leaving,” Xu Qingyao said, departing.

Lin Jing remained, gazing into the sky before preparing for another trip.

This time, he wasn’t going out to stir trouble—he needed to capture some live demons to test the effects of the Myriad Flames Cauldron.

…

Not long after, the Pine Leaf Mouse, in the middle of cultivating the Longevity Technique, once again had a year of its lifespan drained by Lin Jing to form a Treant clone embryo.

Using this humanoid wood clone as the foundation, Lin Jing applied illusion techniques to mold it into a different figure. However, this time, it wasn’t Lin Jing’s own duplicate, nor the Fire Dragon Pill’s clone, nor the appearance of Huang Yu.

It was a completely new identity.

Tall and imposing, with a strikingly handsome face.

Teeth as white as carved silver, lips red and perfectly shaped.

Dressed in a monk’s robe and holding a meditation staff—he had transformed into a bald monk.

“Let’s see… where are the demons causing havoc among the people?”

Concealing two Myriad Beast Circles within his robes, his illusionary form left the area, heading out to capture demons.

First, he arrived at the Rogue Cultivator Alliance in Shuangxian City, located in Chenghua Dao, one of Qingzhou’s sixteen provinces.

The main hall served as an intelligence hub where rogue cultivators gathered and exchanged information.

Lin Jing intended to capture demons of different cultivation levels—Qi Refinement, Foundation Establishment, Monster Core, and Monster King—to conduct comparative alchemy experiments. To ensure he captured the right ones and didn’t accidentally harm any Demon Court allies, he first gathered intelligence.

As soon as the monk entered, he overheard a few rogue cultivators complaining nearby.

“What’s up with those monsters in Soul-Cleansing Mountain? It’s pitch black, and even Golden Core cultivators are getting sucked dry like mortals.”

“No idea! Where the hell did those mosquito beasts come from?”

“I heard that years ago, some unknown mosquito beasts were spotted in Soul-Cleansing Mountain. Their cultivation was weak, and they didn’t seem valuable, so no one paid them any attention. Who knew that after a few years, they’d multiply into such a terrifying swarm? Harvesting Soul Restoration Grass from the mountain is going to be nearly impossible now…”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 66: Brotherhood in the Myriad Flames Cauldron
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 66: Brotherhood in the Myriad Flames Cauldron

Mosquitoes!

Hearing the conversation between the Foundation Establishment rogue cultivators, Lin Jing’s body stiffened, and faces of old friends flashed before his eyes!

He was certain they were talking about the Cloud Sea Mosquito Beasts.

However, since this place was far from the Cloud Sea Mountains, it was understandable that these rogue cultivators didn’t recognize the Ten Terrors.

Back when the academy underwent its great teleportation event, Lin Jing and the mosquito beasts had been separated, each transported to different locations.

Given their vast numbers, it was highly probable that some had been sent to Qingzhou.

Lin Jing had long prepared himself for the worst, assuming the entire mosquito beast swarm had perished. After all, they were only truly formidable when gathered in a swarm. A lone mosquito beast, even at its peak, could be taken down by any Qi Refinement cultivator.

But now… it seemed some of them had managed to survive the harsh environment and even rebuild a new swarm!

"Also, I’ve found the Qi Refinement-stage demon beasts I needed. They can all become brothers in the Myriad Flames Cauldron together.”

Lin Jing chuckled. With their rapid reproduction rate, mosquito beasts were the perfect alchemy ingredients. Unfortunately, their cultivation was too low—they could never break through to Foundation Establishment. Even if refined into pills that boosted cultivation, Lin Jing himself wouldn’t be able to use them.

However, they would be excellent resources for nurturing young sect disciples.

According to the rogue cultivators, the mosquito beasts were in Soul-Cleansing Mountain…

Soul-Cleansing Mountain

Located on the border between human and demon beast territories, this was one of the sixteen perilous regions of Qingzhou.

Its danger didn’t lie in the strength of its resident demon beasts but in the environment itself.

Soul-Cleansing Mountain emitted a strange toxic miasma that not only caused cultivators to fall ill but also eroded their souls if inhaled too much.

Only certain poison-attribute demon beasts could survive in such an environment.

Despite the dangers, the mountain was rich in rare spiritual herbs. The same miasma that harmed souls also nurtured Soul Restoration Grass, a local specialty known for repairing soul damage.

Many rogue cultivators risked their lives for this precious herb, using defensive spells to shield themselves as they quickly harvested and left.

The key was ensuring that no powerful poison-type beasts attacked during the process.

Lin Jing wasn’t sure how the mosquito beasts had adapted to this toxic environment, but if they were immune to it, then the mountain had become their perfect breeding ground—protecting them from extermination by cultivators.

“If I’m going to Soul-Cleansing Mountain to find the mosquito beasts, I might as well capture a few more demon beasts of other cultivation levels.”

“Foundation Establishment and Monster Core-stage beasts are manageable, but the Monster Kings in the mountain will be a problem. My preparations might go to waste against them.”

Lin Jing had planned to capture a Monster King, but these creatures could freely use divine abilities from their Monster Cores.

Even if he brought the Dragon Carp with him, a direct fight would be difficult.

But since he hadn’t yet settled on a target Monster King, the Ice Soul Gu had made ample preparations.

Against lower-level demon beasts, Lin Jing’s greatest advantage was illusion magic—not because illusions lacked offensive power, but because of their concealment abilities.

Using these abilities, the Ice Soul Gu wouldn’t have to engage in direct combat with weaker demon beasts. Instead… it could use poison.

Among the vast collection of spiritual plants he had stolen from the Sky Lion Clan, Lin Jing had selected several Grade-7 poison plants before leaving.

With the Dragon Carp’s help, he refined them into a batch of Grade-7 Poison Pills.

These pills were potent enough to work on opponents at his level.

The challenge, however, was delivering the poison. Without illusions, this would be much harder.

In addition, he carried a supply of Ice Soul Gu Poison—one of the deadliest toxins, even difficult for Nascent Soul cultivators to purge.

As long as he used illusions to remain hidden and successfully infected a Monster King with the Ice Soul Gu Poison, then catalyzed it with poison pills, even a Nascent Soul-level Monster King would be done for.

At least… that would work against a normal Monster King.

But the ones in Soul-Cleansing Mountain were poison-type creatures.

Even if Lin Jing successfully poisoned them, their natural resistance to toxins would be much stronger than ordinary Monster Kings.

A Grade-4 Poison Pill might even become nourishment for them instead.

While the Ice Soul Gu Poison could suppress their natural poison, it would take years for them to die from it—and Lin Jing didn’t have that kind of time.

A Better Target

Just as he was contemplating this issue, he noticed a wanted poster among the many bounty notices plastered on the walls of the rogue cultivator alliance hall.

WANTED: SCORCHING SUN MONSTER KING

A rogue Monster King from the Qingming Dao region.

It was known for eating humans.

Normally, it would feast on ordinary rogue cultivators, but one day, it accidentally devoured the descendant of a Half-Divine powerhouse from the Rogue Cultivator Alliance.

Since then, it had been relentlessly hunted.

Eventually, it fled into Soul-Cleansing Mountain.

Unlike native poison beasts, this Tiger King wasn’t a venomous creature.

However, its strong yang energy allowed it to protect its soul, enabling it to survive in the toxic mountain environment.

Taking advantage of the natural dangers, it had temporarily evaded capture.

The alliance had refused to give up, however, and had issued a bounty on it.

Lin Jing didn’t care about the bounty itself.

What interested him was that this was a non-poison-type Monster King in Soul-Cleansing Mountain.

“That changes things.”

“If I successfully poison the Scorching Sun Monster King, its yang energy will be depleted as it struggles to resist the Ice Soul Gu Poison.”

“Once its yang energy weakens, the mountain’s miasma will become a deadly weapon against it.”

“On top of that, unlike the poison-type beasts, it won’t be able to completely resist Grade-4 Poison Pills.”

Lin Jing’s eyes gleamed as he left the alliance hall, heading straight for Soul-Cleansing Mountain.

A Monk Walks Among Rogues

“What’s with that bald guy? He just walked in and left.”

“No clue. But why would a Buddhist cultivator even come to a rogue cultivator alliance? Aren’t they all from monasteries?”

The surrounding cultivators whispered among themselves as they watched Lin Jing go.

Into Soul-Cleansing Mountain

Whoosh!

Leaving the alliance, Lin Jing soared through the air, heading deeper into Soul-Cleansing Mountain while scanning for traces of the mosquito beasts.

During this time, his presence inevitably attracted the attention of some venomous creatures.

Of course, this was intentional.

Gliding over the forest canopy, he suddenly felt a sharp projectile whizz toward him—swish!

A venomous arrow shot upward from below.

A late-stage Foundation Establishment Poison Frog had ambushed him.

“One down.”

Lin Jing’s expression remained calm.

Raising his hand, golden Diamond Palm energy radiated with Buddhist light, crushing downward.

The venomous arrow struck the golden palm, but the Buddhist light instantly purified it.

With a sharp croak, the Poison Frog was caught in the golden grip and thrown into the Myriad Beast Circle.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 67: Seeing Mosquitoes, Missing the Tree
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 67: Seeing Mosquitoes, Missing the Tree

"ROAR!!"

As Lin Jing flew through the air, the Buddhist light radiating from his body seemed to disgust all the high-level poisonous creatures in the area.

Along the way, he swatted down numerous lower-tier venomous beasts.

Then, as he passed by a poisonous swamp, a massive Poison Flood Dragon emerged from the depths, spewing a wave of purple Dragon Breath toward him.

"Capture."

Lin Jing dodged the venomous breath, and under the glow of Buddhist light, he unleashed the supreme pressure of the Beast Taming Physique.

The Poison Flood Dragon immediately panicked.

What it had assumed to be a defenseless bald monk turned out to be an absolute nightmare for beasts.

But before it could escape, Lin Jing had already thrown out his treasure—Myriad Beast Circle.

"ROAR!!"

Inside the Myriad Beast Circle, the Poison Flood Dragon thrashed violently, attempting to break free.

Unfortunately for it, Lin Jing had already activated a Grade-4 Bewitching Pill inside the artifact.

It didn’t take long for the dragon to succumb to the hallucinogenic mist, falling into a deep slumber before its consciousness was completely erased by the artifact.

Mosquito Swarm Sighting!

Continuing deeper into the miasma-filled region, Lin Jing suddenly heard a familiar sound—

"Bzzzzzz!"

"Whooooosh!"

"A mosquito swarm in action!"

Excitement surged in Lin Jing’s heart as he accelerated forward.

Before long, several streaks of light brushed past him—rogue cultivators of varying levels from Foundation Establishment to False Core.

"Freeze!"

As they passed Lin Jing, one of the cultivators instinctively threw several immobilization spells in his direction.

"Apologies, Daoist friend! The swarm is vicious. You’ll have to be the bait!"

"Better you than us!"

"A monk should sacrifice himself for others, right?"

Their hearts pounded as they sped away, convinced the monk would be devoured by the incoming swarm.

But then, something utterly terrifying happened.

The massive mosquito horde rushed past Lin Jing… without attacking him.

He stood completely still, eyes cold, while the bloodthirsty swarm didn’t even slow down.

"Faster. Drain them dry."

Lin Jing’s current form was entirely made of wood and illusion energy—he had no blood for the mosquitoes to feast on.

Moreover, the Beast Taming Physique aura he emitted was an overwhelming suppressive force—the very embodiment of their king.

Those rogue cultivators would never have guessed…

This very swarm had descended from the mosquito beasts that had been separated from Lin Jing during the academy teleportation!

Moments later, the mosquito horde caught up to the fleeing cultivators and, in an instant, drained them to husks—not a single drop of blood remained in their bodies.

Lin Jing smirked.

"Had your fill? Good. Now it’s time to come home."

He soared toward the swarm, his heart racing with excitement.

A brief spiritual scan told him that the mosquito swarm had grown to nearly 100,000 in number.

Though most were no stronger than Qi Refinement Stage Six, such a sheer quantity meant that only a Nascent Soul cultivator could handle them without issue.

"How did you all reproduce so fast?"

Lin Jing furrowed his brows.

Even with two Myriad Beast Circles, he couldn’t store them all.

After exerting his Beast Taming Physique aura to subdue the swarm, Lin Jing pondered his next move while the constant buzzing made his head ache.

"Quiet."

He ultimately selected 1,000 of the most elite Qi Refinement Stage Seven and above mosquitoes and stored them in the Myriad Beast Circle.

As for the remaining nearly 100,000 mosquitoes, he already had a plan.

"Releasing them into Qingzhou might turn them into an invasive species. If they reach a population of a million, nearby cities could be wiped out."

"Bringing them all back would be too troublesome. Killing them would be a waste."

"Instead, I’ll use them to hunt down the Monster King for me!"

The mosquito swarm, on its own, couldn’t take down a full-powered Monster King.

But against a poisoned and weakened Monster King?

Their suicidal blood-draining attacks would only accelerate the poisoning process, buying Lin Jing precious time.

But even after securing the mosquitoes, Lin Jing felt like something was… missing.

He stared at the massive swarm, lost in thought.

"...Right. Where’s Senior Pine?"

It had been years since he last saw that ancient Divine Pine Tree.

Given how he and Gu Qianqiu had caused absolute havoc in the Sky Lion Clan, news should have reached the Ancient Kingdom and beyond by now.

Would Senior Pine have heard about it?

Would it come find him?

Lin Jing shook his head and turned his gaze back to the mosquito army.

"Let’s go. Time to hunt a king."

One Month Later in Tianyuan Continent

One Year Later in the Hourglass of Time

Progress had been astonishing.

Lin Jing had completely deduced the formula for the Treant Pill, fusing four powerful plants:

Bloodsucking Vine

Diamondwood

Exploding Firefruit

Earth-Devouring Tree

The refining task was handed to the Dragon Carp.

By now, the Dragon Carp had broken through to the Monster Core Stage and refined another top-tier exotic flame.

Although Lin Jing’s own cultivation level limited its advancement, the Dragon Carp’s alchemy skills had reached the brink of Fifth-Tier Pill Sovereign.

If it succeeded, it would match the legendary Jie Geng Alchemist from Tongtian Tower—a genius who had reached Fifth-Tier Alchemy while still in the Golden Core Stage.

Jie Geng Alchemist was a core talent of the strongest alchemy sect.

But the Dragon Carp?

It had brutally fought its way up, self-taught and battle-hardened.

While the Flame Emperor's divine abilities had certainly played a role, its own determination couldn’t be ignored.

During the Dragon Carp’s refining process, the Pine Leaf Mouse had also fully absorbed all the Sky Lion Clan’s spiritual plants using the Everlasting Emperor’s Longevity Art.

However, the final perfection of the technique proved to be far more difficult than Lin Jing had anticipated.

Was it a problem with the technique itself?

Or was it simply because the Pine Leaf Mouse’s cultivation level was still too low?

Either way, after absorbing all the spiritual plants, the Pine Leaf Mouse finally perfected the Longevity Art.

And the rewards were astonishing.

Though it had only just reached Peak Foundation Establishment, its mastery over plant manipulation—accelerating, controlling, and maturing vegetation—had already reached the level of a Monster King!

At this rate, the rare spiritual plants Lin Jing had stolen from the Beast Taming Sect would soon mature.

More importantly, the perfected Longevity Art had increased the Pine Leaf Mouse’s lifespan by 2,000 years—bringing it to a staggering 4,000 years.

However, this also meant that the Pine Leaf Mouse had reached the absolute limit of what the technique could offer.

This technique had originally been designed for plant spirits.

Senior Pine had already modified it to work for beasts.

But unfortunately… its tier was still too low.

"The Pine Leaf Mouse is at the brink of a breakthrough. Like the Dragon Carp before it, it can no longer suppress its advancement."

"If only Senior Pine were here… maybe it could deduce a higher-tier version of the technique."

At this thought, Lin Jing suddenly felt a deep longing.

Maybe it was because…

Seeing the mosquitoes made him miss the tree.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 68: Testing the Pills
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 68: Testing the Pills

A few more days passed.

During his cultivation, Lin Jing sensed that Dragon Carp had successfully refined the four types of Treant Pills. Meanwhile, the clone he had sent out to capture beasts had also returned with its spoils.

Once the swarm of mosquitoes had settled back in place, the clone set out in search of the Blazing Sun Demon King. After some effort, it finally located the target and successfully poisoned him using illusions and venom.

Everything unfolded just as Lin Jing had planned. Under the combined effects of various poison pills, Ice Soul Gu poison, and countless suicidal mosquito attacks, it took less than a month for the Tiger King's yang energy to weaken significantly, leaving him unable to resist the miasma.

Thus, the game of "mosquitoes hunting a tiger" came to an end.

With the materials from the mosquitoes, poison frogs, poison flood dragon, and the Tiger King now gathered, Lin Jing had everything needed to test the Myriad Flames Cauldron and start refining pills.

.............

"Good work."

Inside the Myriad Beast Circles, one was filled with a swarm of mosquitoes, while the other held three severely weakened demon beasts.

Lin Jing summoned Dragon Carp and placed the four beasts—whose intelligence had been wiped—before it.

"We've got beasts ranging from the Qi Refinement stage to the Demon King realm. Try refining them with the Myriad Flames Cauldron."

"Bo!!!"

Dragon Carp paid no attention to the other three beasts but instead stared at the mosquitoes with excitement, as if reuniting with old friends.

"That's right, I brought them back." Lin Jing chuckled.

"Once this is done, you'll have as many Longevity Blood Mosquitoes as you want. Alright, let's start refining. Afterward, I'll head over to Crane Cauldron to capture a few trial beasts to test the effects of the four Treant Pills..."

Mosquito beasts had a special bloodline that made them unsuitable for testing pills—Lin Jing had tried before. Otherwise, he would have used them directly.

"Also, grab a few normal 'Rising Origin Pills' so we can compare the effects of the two different types of pills on cultivation advancement!"

As soon as Lin Jing finished speaking, Dragon Carp let out a "Bo!" It had been eager to test the Myriad Flames Cauldron’s ability ever since it gained its Monster Core divine ability.

Its fish eyes locked onto the beasts, and as its red monster core spun, a massive cauldron, entirely made of flames, materialized in the void before them. The cauldron spanned over ten meters in length, width, and height, its interior resembling a swirling vortex of fire. The moment Dragon Carp's monster core developed fine cracks, a terrifying suction force erupted from within the cauldron, instantly absorbing all four demon beasts of different cultivation levels.

Even Lin Jing himself felt an immense pull, his body momentarily unsteady.

"Holy sh*t, that suction is intense! Feels like it's about to pull me in too. Dragon Carp, did you do that on purpose, or was it an accident?" Lin Jing, feeling the overwhelming force of the Myriad Flames Cauldron firsthand, quickly activated his protective mana and retreated several steps.

"Bo?"

Dragon Carp was stunned—it had no idea! The suction must have been too strong for it to control properly.

The mosquito beast was the first to be sucked into the cauldron. Before Lin Jing could even examine it closely, a wisp of blue smoke rose from its body, and in the next moment, it had transformed into a golden pill.

Judging by the fluctuations of its quality, it was a first-grade pill.

Not even a minute later, the late Foundation Establishment stage poison frog followed suit. Blue smoke rose from its body with a soft "boop," and it turned into another golden pill—this one seemingly of second grade.

"So, a Qi Refinement beast is refined in an instant, and a Foundation Establishment beast takes less than a minute?"

After about seven minutes, the poison flood dragon, which had just reached the early Demon Core stage, was also refined. The speed was so astonishing that even Dan Yao and Bai Biao would have found it unbelievable.

Refining an eighth-grade pill, even for someone with extremely poor skills, would take at least half a day. An experienced pill master could take several days. Yet the Myriad Flames Cauldron had refined it in mere minutes.

The most heaven-defying feature of the Myriad Flames Cauldron was its ability to refine multiple living creatures simultaneously.

That meant, in theory, one could refine multiple pills at once using the same set of ingredients.

While refining beasts from the Qi Refinement, Foundation Establishment, and Demon Core stages was relatively fast, the process slowed significantly when it reached the Blazing Sun Demon King. The efficiency of the cauldron's refinement increased at that point, but even after half an hour, the Demon King had yet to be fully refined.

Meanwhile, Dragon Carp’s monster core was already approaching its limit, struggling to maintain the Myriad Flames Cauldron. Numerous cracks had formed on its core.

"Take a pill."

At Lin Jing’s reminder, Dragon Carp immediately pulled out the high-grade spiritual pills given by Senior Sister, along with a fourth-rank spiritual plant, Nirvana Blossom, and stuffed it into its mouth to recover.

Finally, after nearly two hours, the Blazing Sun Demon King was fully refined, transformed into a fourth-grade golden pill that could enhance cultivation. Judging by the medicinal fluctuations, both Lin Jing and Dragon Carp estimated that the pills produced by the Myriad Flames Cauldron were several times more effective than their regular counterparts!

"!!!!"

"Bo!!"

As the Myriad Flames Cauldron dissipated, Dragon Carp let out a painful wail. Lin Jing examined the four golden pills of varying grades in his palm and grinned at it.

"Eat some more recovery herbs. Also, test whether the fusion of Lotus Heart Flame has any healing effects on your monster core."

"I'm heading to Crane Cauldron to get the comparison pills. You go rest first, and I'll update you once I have results."

Dragon Carp eagerly nodded before slipping into the meditation staff.

...........

Crane Cauldron.

The alchemy division under the Demon Court’s Third Holy Maiden, Xu Qingyao, specialized in researching Transformation Pills.

However, thanks to Lin Jing’s influence, Crane Cauldron had now been turned into a mass-production facility dedicated to refining "Rising Origin Pills" for Gu Qianqiu.

Disguised as a Fire Dragon Alchemist, Lin Jing flew to the Crane Cauldron’s alchemy chamber, only to be stopped by an old man.

"F-Fire Dragon Alchemist, sir! How may I assist you?"

As Xu Qingyao’s trusted subordinate, Lin Jing not only took over the position of Sparrowfeather Squad’s leader but also became the acting head of Crane Cauldron. With his success in refining special pills, none of the alchemists there dared to disobey him.

"Dan Lao, fetch me a bottle of Rising Origin Pills. Also, grab me five trial pill mice."

"Yes, sir!"

The old man quickly retrieved the requested items, and Lin Jing returned to his cave dwelling.

Inside, he grabbed a Qi Refinement stage trial pill mouse and force-fed it a first-grade cultivation pill refined from a mosquito beast using the Myriad Flames Cauldron.

It wasn’t that Lin Jing doubted Dragon Carp’s abilities—he just knew that "pills are one-third poison." To be safe, he needed to ensure there were no harmful side effects before consuming one himself.

"Squeak..."

The trial pill mouse, originally at the sixth level of Qi Refinement, swallowed the pill. In the next moment, its cultivation surged—seventh level, eighth level...

However, as its cultivation rose, and under the horrified gazes of the other trial pill mice, all of its fur fell off, leaving behind a completely bald mouse.

Lin Jing: ?

"What the hell just happened?"

He immediately called for Dragon Carp.

Dragon Carp narrowed its eyes, exuding an air of wisdom. It speculated that the phenomenon might be similar to molting in certain animals—the old fur was unable to adapt to the stronger body, so it had to shed and regrow new fur to match the increased cultivation.

"A minor issue in comparison to the cultivation boost," it added.

Minor issue? How the hell was he supposed to give this to Gu Qianqiu? Was she supposed to become a monk?!

"Are you sure this isn’t just because your Monster Core divine ability isn't proficient enough... or that you screwed up the refining process?!"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 69: Plant Mouse
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 69: Plant Mouse

Dragon Carp’s guess had some merit.

After further research, Lin Jing discovered that the excessive potency of the cultivation pill was indeed the cause of the hair loss in the trial pill mouse—it simply couldn't adapt to the surge in power immediately.

Fortunately, the hair loss was completely reversible. Dragon Carp refined a Regeneration Pill and, despite it being a bit of overkill, used it on the little white mouse. Instantly, its fur grew back, now a thick, glossy black.

"Black fur doesn’t get dirty as easily. Consider it a free upgrade, no need to thank me." Lin Jing smirked at the formerly white-furred mouse.

"Squeak…" The trial pill mouse stared at its new black coat, utterly dejected.

"So in summary, taking a cultivation pill requires a strong physique and resilient meridians. Otherwise, the overwhelming medicinal energy could throw the body into chaos, resulting in hair loss. But since the effect is completely reversible, it really is just a minor issue. With the help of a Gluttonous Pill to aid digestion, the problem should be significantly reduced."

With his research on the cultivation pill concluded, Lin Jing let out a breath of relief. Others might have trouble handling its potency, but he and his contracted beasts all had incredibly strong foundations, making it impossible for them to suffer from such side effects.

Of course, that confidence didn't necessarily apply if they tried to take a pill refined from a Nascent Soul-level demon king.

Lin Jing rubbed his chin. Oh well, compared to the boost in cultivation, a little "loss" was a fair trade.

"Go rest for now. Next, let's test the Treant Pills."

He waved the black-furred trial pill mouse away and lined up the remaining four white-furred ones neatly in a row.

Handing them each a different pill—Bloodsucking Devil Vine Treant Pill, Reflective Diamondwood Treant Pill, Exploding Firefruit Treant Pill, and Earth Vein Spirit Tree Treant Pill—he watched as the four mice nervously swallowed them.

The effects were immediate.

The first white-furred mouse let out a startled squeak as its tail transformed into a long, red vine.

The second mouse’s soft flesh hardened instantly, its skin now resembling tree bark.

The third mouse appeared unchanged on the surface, but when Lin Jing and Dragon Carp examined it with their divine senses, they found that its heart had turned into a red fruit.

The fourth mouse had the most dramatic transformation—its head sprouted a delicate green sapling.

All four mice exhibited no adverse reactions after taking the Treant Pills.

"Now let's test their abilities."

Lin Jing took out a drop of mosquito beast blood. As expected, the first mouse, which had acquired the Bloodsucking Devil Vine trait, successfully used blood-path summoning magic to manifest a blood mosquito.

"This might be the first time an organism other than the Pine Leaf Mouse has acquired a plant-based ability." Lin Jing was intrigued.

The second mouse's defense had increased significantly, gaining the ability to reflect a portion of damage back to the attacker.

The third mouse… had the worst luck. To test the Exploding Firefruit’s ability, Lin Jing had it sever one of its own ears and detonate it. Fortunately, the explosion was incredibly powerful—enough to threaten a Foundation Establishment cultivator. Feeling pity for it, Lin Jing named it One-Ear and gave it a healing pill.

The fourth mouse was even more impressive. It could absorb earth vein energy and release spiritual power through the sapling on its head like a geyser.

"Squeak squeak squeak…"

Watching its four companions turn into something neither fully mouse nor fully plant, the black-furred mouse let out a sigh of relief. Compared to them, it had gotten off lightly—only a change in fur color.

However, it was now the weakest among the group, having reached Qi Refinement Level 4, just a step away from Foundation Establishment.

Then—

A shiver ran down its spine.

From behind, came the voice of a devil.

"The Treant Pills seem to work well. However, that was just a single dose. I wonder if there would be any rejection effects if multiple pills were taken together? Normally, a creature can only consume one Treant Pill in its lifetime, but my research suggests that after my modifications, taking multiple pills should be possible. Of course, we’ll need to test that further... Just to be sure nothing goes wrong with the Pine Leaf Mouse later."

Lin Jing turned his gaze to the black-furred mouse and beckoned.

"Hey little guy, you’ve got the highest cultivation. Want to become as strong as them? Come on over. Eat these four pills, one by one."

Black-furred mouse: ...

"Squeak—!!!"

Accompanied by a desperate squeal, Lin Jing's cave dwelling became the stage for a horrifying, mouse-mangling body modification experiment.

When the black-furred mouse took the first Treant Pill, nothing seemed out of the ordinary. However, as soon as it swallowed the second, things got rough—it struggled to sit still, its tiny body visibly resisting the transformation.

This process wouldn’t be completed quickly.

"Your turn."

Lin Jing left Dragon Carp in charge of supervising the trial and turned his attention back to refining pills.

He took out a third-grade Rising Origin Pill, as well as second, third, and fourth-grade cultivation pills, and began his own cultivation.

First, he consumed a second-grade cultivation pill—one refined from a Foundation Establishment-stage poison frog. As expected, it had no adverse effects on him.

However, it also didn’t noticeably improve his cultivation.

Next, he tried a third-grade Rising Origin Pill. The effect was barely different from the second-grade cultivation pill—after taking it, his cultivation fluctuated slightly, but the improvement was negligible.

"At this rate, using low-grade pills to advance from late Foundation Establishment to Great Perfection would take an absurd number of them."

Then, he decided to try a third-grade cultivation pill refined from a poison flood dragon.

The moment he swallowed it, a violent surge of spiritual energy rampaged through his body. This time, he finally felt a clear improvement in his cultivation, though the effect still wasn’t too impressive.

"It’s confirmed. The cultivation pills refined by Dragon Carp have the same effect as a pill of a higher grade. A second-grade cultivation pill works like a third-grade Rising Origin Pill. A third-grade cultivation pill is roughly equivalent to a fourth-grade Rising Origin Pill!"

"Not only is the refinement process faster, but the effectiveness is superior. Once Dragon Carp reaches the Nascent Soul stage, this Monster Core divine ability will be incredibly valuable!"

Taking a deep breath, Lin Jing picked up the fourth-grade cultivation pill refined from the Blazing Sun Tiger King. Without hesitation, he swallowed it, using a Gluttonous Pill to aid absorption.

As the raging medicinal power flooded his body, Lin Jing steadied his mind. Though he inevitably lost a few strands of hair, his cultivation soared like a pet beast undergoing breakthrough feedback.

Several days later—

Lin Jing had reached the absolute peak of the Foundation Establishment stage, just one step away from Great Perfection.

Meanwhile, Dragon Carp examined its results with satisfaction.

The Plant Mouse was born—

A mouse with a fruit for a heart, tree bark for skin, a vine for a tail, and a sapling growing from its head.

"Bo—"

Dragon Carp turned to Lin Jing, who had just opened his eyes, and confidently declared that the pills had been tested—they wouldn’t be fatal. The Pine Leaf Mouse could safely consume them.

"That so…?"

Lin Jing smiled in satisfaction.

"Perfect. I’ll use the Pine Leaf Mouse’s Core Formation breakthrough to push myself into Foundation Establishment Great Perfection…"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 70: Five-Element Divine Ability
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 70: Five-Element Divine Ability

With the pill trials complete, Lin Jing looked at the five trial pill mice. These little creatures had survived a brutal ordeal—truly, their luck wasn’t bad.

Thanks to the Treant Pills, their constitutions had undergone a fundamental transformation. While they were still far from possessing the power of demon kings, they were now far stronger than ordinary demon beasts—truly, anomalies among their kind.

"You don’t need to return to Crane Cauldron. Even I wasn’t entirely sure this trial would succeed. Surviving this is your fortune. Cultivation is all about defying the heavens—stay near my cave dwelling and cultivate. Once you reach Foundation Establishment and strengthen your cultivation, I will grant you official status in the Demon Court."

"...!"

The five pill mice bowed deeply to Lin Jing.

Under the black-furred mouse’s lead, they left the cave dwelling, tunneling into the nearby mountains to establish their own nests. Meanwhile, Lin Jing entered the Hourglass of Time to prepare for the Pine Leaf Mouse’s Core Formation breakthrough.

If even Qi Refinement-stage trial mice could handle four Treant Pills, then for the Pine Leaf Mouse, this was going to be a breeze.

Crack.

Inside the Hourglass of Time, the Pine Leaf Mouse swallowed one pill after another.

While the pill mice had taken several days to fully integrate the Treant Pills into their bodies, the Pine Leaf Mouse took just minutes to completely absorb and transform all four pills.

At this moment, the Pine Leaf Mouse was in its true demon form, its Beast King Manifestation, as it consumed the pills. As a result, its appearance subtly changed.

The wooden colossus of a squirrel took on a deeper hue, its body now shimmering with golden light.

Its small pine tree sprouted additional saplings of other tree species, making it resemble an entire mountain ecosystem.

The pinecones on its body also changed—from a natural brown to a blazing red, hinting at explosive properties.

Additionally, vines wrapped around its arms. Compared to the other trial mice, it truly embodied the concept of a plant-mouse hybrid.

But all of this was merely a conduit—a medium for mastering the Five-Elemental Forces!

With these plant organs as channels, the Pine Leaf Mouse could now perceive the circulation of metal, wood, water, fire, and earth energies within its body. The wood element served as the core, capable of converting itself into the other four elements.

At this moment, the Pine Leaf Mouse—having already pushed its cultivation to the peak of Foundation Establishment through the Longevity Technique—began compressing its five-elemental energy, forming a Monster Core!

The Earth Vein Spirit Tree withered.

Its skin shed.

The Flaming Pinecones disintegrated.

The Bloodsucking Devil Vine shriveled.

Red leaves scattered to the ground...

All five elemental forces converged within its body.

Lin Jing stood watch beside it the entire time, but surprisingly, Dragon Carp seemed even more invested in the Pine Leaf Mouse’s breakthrough than he was.

Dragon Carp—currently the undisputed strongest among them—possessed six different mutant flames and multiple bloodlines. It should have no reason to worry. Even if the Pine Leaf Mouse formed a Five-Elemental Core, the pills used for its breakthrough were still refined by Dragon Carp, so logically, it shouldn’t surpass it in strength, right?

"..."

Dragon Carp's eyes flickered, watching the transformation unfold.

Time passed. The colossal demon king manifestation stood towering over the land.

Lin Jing sat cross-legged atop it, carefully observing every slight shift in the Pine Leaf Mouse’s energy.

The addition of four plant organs had elevated its bloodline once again.

Its evolutionary path was now fully oriented toward plant-based life, and before even completing its Core Formation, its bloodline strength had surged, officially reaching the Imperial Tier—joining the ranks of the Ice Soul Gu and Dragon Carp.

Of course, bloodline power was secondary to the Monster Core Divine Ability!

That was the true mark of ascension from Foundation Establishment to Core Formation—the manifestation of all prior cultivation efforts!

In the blink of an eye, a month passed within the Hourglass of Time.

As waves of Five-Elemental Spiritual Qi surged, the Pine Leaf Mouse's energies coalesced into a five-colored Monster Core.

And within that core, a divine ability had been gestating all this time.

Finally—

As the Pine Leaf Mouse firmly stabilized its aura at the early Core Formation stage, Lin Jing also benefited from the breakthrough feedback, reaching the Great Perfection of Foundation Establishment.

At the same time, the Monster Core Divine Ability surfaced in their minds.

"Squeak…."

[Monster Core Divine Ability: Five-Elemental World]

A miniature world exists within the Monster Core. The more Five-Elemental substances stored inside, the stronger the world’s power. The Monster Core itself can be unleashed as a direct attack, using the power of the Five-Elemental World to suppress enemies!

"A spatial attribute divine ability?"

Lin Jing was stunned.

"Squeak!!"

The Pine Leaf Mouse was equally thrilled.

Under normal circumstances, unless a creature possessed a special bloodline or cultivated a unique technique, only those at the Void Mystical Stage or beyond could create an internal spatial dimension.

While Lin Jing had plenty of storage artifacts, he never felt completely safe leaving items inside them—after all, they could be looted or destroyed by enemies.

But now, the Pine Leaf Mouse had a storage space within its Monster Core!

Its resource security had skyrocketed. Even if someone shattered its Monster Core, it could self-destruct, ensuring that no one else could claim its treasures.

"But this divine ability of yours is way more dangerous than Dragon Carp’s."

Having a self-contained world inside its core meant that it could launch its Monster Core as a direct attack.

Just the thought made Lin Jing shudder.

Using Monster Core divine abilities already wore down the core. If it were used as a projectile, the risk of shattering the core entirely became exponentially higher.

"Bo, bo…"

While Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse conversed, Dragon Carp grew impatient.

So? How strong was the Pine Leaf Mouse after its breakthrough?

"No rush. The most important thing right now is… the Glazed Immortal Eyes!"

"Can it now synthesize third-rank spiritual plants? Can it create resources of other attributes?"

Lin Jing called out.

In response, the Pine Leaf Mouse withdrew from its colossal "Demon Emperor Form", reverting to its tiny squirrel size.

Since all Five-Elemental energy had condensed into its Monster Core, its tree-based features had fully retracted.

In its small form, even the red-leaf plant traits had disappeared.

But Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse both knew—those plant abilities were still fully intact.

All it had to do was channel its Monster Core energy, and in an instant, all plant-based transformations would return.

For example, the Spatial Red Leaves—before, the Pine Leaf Mouse needed to rely on ordinary leaves for synthesis.

Now?

It could conjure them at will.

The same applied to Bloodsucking Devil Vine, Explosive Fire Pinecones, and all its other abilities.

"Here."

Lin Jing placed a pile of Transformation Grass—a gift from Senior Sister—on the ground.

"Squeak!!"

The Pine Leaf Mouse patted its chest, completely confident.

Its eyes focused, and it activated the Glazed Immortal Eyes.

Just as expected—now that its cultivation had advanced, its ability to synthesize higher-grade spiritual plants had greatly improved!

However…

Unlike when it synthesized first- and second-rank plants with ease, fusing a third-rank Transformation Grass took a staggering toll.

Its eyes stung with pain, and its mana drained at an alarming rate, converted into ocular power.

Within seconds, the Pine Leaf Mouse had exhausted all of its Core Formation-level mana—and the Transformation Grass wasn’t even fully synthesized yet!

Realizing the problem, Lin Jing quickly absorbed Dragon Carp’s mana while channeling his own into the Pine Leaf Mouse.

With the combined energy of three beings, the synthesis barely succeeded.

The pile of Transformation Grass had become a dark-red variation…

"Wait—what?"

【Mutant Transformation Grass: When consumed by a pre-Core Formation demon beast, it triggers the Transformation Tribulation. If the creature withstands the heavenly punishment, it can assume human form.】

What the hell? It already turned into a mutation— before even refining it into a pill?!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 71: Tribulation of Transformation
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 71: Tribulation of Transformation

"This isn't even alchemy, and it's already done?"

The effects of synthesizing the Transformation Grass left Lin Jing stunned. While this outcome was possible, wasn't it a little too easy?

Had he really just bypassed the rules in one step with his Glazed Immortal Eyes, as if making a wish?

"Wait a minute… there's a catch."

It only works if consumed by a demon beast below the Demon Core Stage... meaning it’s only effective for Qi Refinement and Foundation Establishment-level demon beasts.

That part wasn’t a problem—after all, the grade of Transformation Grass wasn’t high to begin with, just like the Bloodsucking Devil Vine, which could only convert blood of the same tier.

But expecting Qi Refinement and Foundation Establishment-level demon beasts to endure the Tribulation of Transformation... wasn't that a bit much?

Forget tribulation lightning, even natural lightning with slightly greater power was already beyond what Qi Refinement and Foundation Establishment-level demon beasts could withstand.

Though he wasn’t sure of this tribulation’s power yet, based on past synthesis experiences, there was an 80% chance that this Mutated Transformation Grass was another Malignant Plant...

"Let’s experiment."

Lin Jing couldn't wait to test it out. He took Pine Leaf Mouse and Dragon Carp with him, stepped out of the Hourglass of Time, and exited his cave dwelling. From his Myriad Beast Circle, he retrieved a Qi Refinement Ninth Stage Mosquito Beast.

A life-or-death experiment like this... it was best not to use the Dan Mice, who had just barely escaped death. Better to leave it to the Mosquito Beast.

The Mosquito Beast flew over to Lin Jing.

"Hold this herb, fly a bit farther, fly higher, then absorb it."

The Mosquito Beast followed orders immediately, flying a distance away to ensure no nearby life forms were affected. Only then did it consume the Mutated Transformation Grass.

In the next instant—

Boom!

An astonishing surge of demonic energy erupted from the Mosquito Beast, stirring the winds and clouds above. In a breath’s time, black storm clouds spread across the sky, flickering with golden lightning within.

In the Mosquito Beast’s mind, an image surfaced—

A humanoid figure with retained antennae and wings, a voluptuous body, an exotic beauty, and red skin covering her entire frame...

If nothing unexpected happened, this would be its transformed form—the Mosquito Woman.

However—

Before the Mosquito Beast could even begin its transformation and channel the power of the Transformation Grass,

the moment it sensed the tribulation’s energy—

Lin Jing, Pine Leaf Mouse, and Dragon Carp all had their pupils contract sharply.

"You’ve got to be kidding me... this power..."

That tribulation’s aura—forget Qi Refinement and Foundation Establishment demon beasts—even Pine Leaf Mouse and Dragon Carp, who had already reached the Demon Core Early Stage, felt a severe threat of death.

"The strength of this tribulation... while not completely outrageous, is still terrifying. Compared to the heavenly tribulation faced upon ascension, this might only be a fraction of its power. But even so... this lightning is completely on par with a Nascent Soul Stage lightning cultivator’s full-powered attack..."

"This is nowhere near something a Qi Refinement or Foundation Establishment-level demon beast can endure."

"Even if Pine Leaf Mouse or Dragon Carp were the ones attempting it, relying solely on their physical bodies, it’d be absolutely impossible to withstand..."

"The path to transformation remains perilous for demon beasts. To defy the heavens is no easy feat."

BOOM!

The tribulation lightning struck down.

The Mosquito Beast had still been immersed in the stirrings of its bloodline memory, but in the very next second—

It was annihilated.

Not only did it fail to transform, but its entire existence was erased without a trace—not even dust remained.

At the same time—

The moment the tribulation’s energy was released, the powerhouses within the Xu Qingyao Palace all turned their heads toward Lin Jing’s cave dwelling.

Among them, Senior Sister Xu Qingyao was the first to teleport over.

She looked at the silent Lin Jing and his beasts, then at the now-charred mountain peak, and immediately asked, "What happened?"

"Why did I sense tribulation energy?"

Lin Jing grinned at her.

"Senior Sister, you weren’t wrong. That was indeed a tribulation just now. One of my pet beasts was undergoing tribulation."

"...Huh???" Xu Qingyao was dumbfounded.

What kind of joke was this? Since when was tribulation something that could just be "triggered" like that?!

"Where is your pet beast? What did it do to trigger tribulation?"

"The pet beast got struck dead. It sacrificed itself for the sake of researching transformation techniques," Lin Jing said.

Xu Qingyao fell silent for a moment.

Then—she frowned.

"Are you messing with me? Your pet beast was struck dead, and none of you are reacting at all?"

Lin Jing, the Living King of Hell, shook his head.

"It wasn’t a contracted pet beast. Just one of the Mosquito Beasts I raise as food sources. But they do count as pets in a way, since they can be used in battle..."

"Oh... wait, that’s not the point!"

Xu Qingyao suddenly became excited. She grabbed Lin Jing by the shoulders and shook him violently.

"Your research has progressed?! Even though this looks like a failure, the fact that a mere Mosquito Beast could trigger tribulation—that’s huge! Quickly, tell me—how far have you gotten?"

"We’ve already successfully modified the Transformation Grass into a new variant."

Lin Jing explained, "The modified Transformation Grass now possesses the power to invoke the transformation tribulation. If the user can withstand the tribulation’s baptism, they will likely transform into a demon race. But..."

"This Mutated Transformation Grass has a major flaw—it only works for Qi Refinement and Foundation Establishment-level demon beasts. However, the Tribulation of Transformation is so powerful that even an ordinary Demon Core Stage demon beast would be completely obliterated if they tried to endure it..."

"So... for now, the situation is still uncertain."

"I estimate that we’ll need to approach this from two directions:

First, modifying the Transformation Grass so it works on Demon Core Stage and higher demon beasts.

Second, finding a way to either weaken the tribulation’s power or increase the survival rate of those enduring it."

"...Uh..."

Xu Qingyao’s expression shifted.

Making Foundation Establishment-level demon beasts endure tribulation just to transform?

How was that any different from having no progress at all?!

Unlike Lin Jing and Pine Leaf Mouse, she couldn’t see the effects of the Mutated Transformation Grass firsthand. And with no successful case yet, her hope wasn’t exactly high. She mostly saw this as Lin Jing’s speculation.

Still, she patted Lin Jing’s shoulder encouragingly.

"As long as you have confidence, that’s what matters. Let me know if you need any help."

Lin Jing didn’t hold back.

"I do."

"First, we need lots of Transformation Grass—the more, the better. We’ll need a ton of it for upcoming experiments."

Although Pine Leaf Mouse could plant and accelerate growth, synthesizing Transformation Grass drained too much energy. Wasting time on farming would be inefficient.

"Next—Senior Sister, are you familiar with the Ginseng Tribe?"

"The Ginseng Tribe? The plant spirit race?"

"Yeah. I heard that in a past Tongtian Tower Alchemy Conference, the champion was Ginseng Tribe King Ginseng. He created a pill called the Lightning Avoidance Pill, which can make the user nearly immune to lightning-based attacks and natural lightning.

I think... it might pair well with our modified Transformation Grass."

"If we can find a Foundation Establishment Stage demon beast specializing in body refinement and lightning cultivation, maybe... just maybe, we can get one successful case."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 72: Advancement of the Immortal Eyes
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 72: Advancement of the Immortal Eyes

“I see,” Xu Qingyao nodded lightly.

“The Plant Spirit Tribe has an incredibly difficult cultivation path, even harder than that of demon beasts or humans. They are far more likely to attract tribulation, which is why, despite the existence of more than a dozen Dao-Seeking Stage powerhouses among humans and demon beasts, the Plant Spirit Tribe has yet to produce even one.”

“For this reason, they have conducted extensive research on methods to counter tribulation….”

“However, the Ginseng King Tribe has little to do with the Demon Court. They are much closer to Tongtian Tower, essentially acting as one of its vassal forces.”

“Seeking pills from the Ginseng King Tribe may not be easy. After all, Tongtian Tower might not want our Demon Court cultivators to have an easier time overcoming tribulation.”

Hearing this, Lin Jing replied,

“All paths are driven by interests. As long as we offer enough benefits, they won’t refuse. Right now, our priority is to successfully transform a single demon beast. Once we have a successful case, Senior Sister, your status in the Demon Court will skyrocket. After that, anything you want to do will become much easier.”

“Until we get the pill from the Ginseng King Tribe, I’ll also work on researching an alternative high-efficiency tribulation-resistant pill myself.”

Lin Jing nodded.

With that, Senior Sister Xu took off immediately, heading to plan her visit to the Ginseng King Tribe.

As the Holy Maiden of the Demon Court, and now with the support of the Kirin Clan, seeking pills from the Ginseng King Tribe was indeed not impossible.

“Alright, now all I have to do is wait for Senior Sister’s Lightning Avoidance Pill.”

What Lin Jing should be doing right now was gathering spiritual herbs that could effectively increase lightning resistance and enhance physical toughness, then having Pine Leaf Mouse synthesize them into pills to boost the success rate of transformation.

However, with Pine Leaf Mouse’s breakthrough, Lin Jing had too many things he wanted to experiment with. He pushed this task to the back of the queue—too lazy to deal with it. Might as well just wait for Senior Sister’s pill to be ready.

First, he tossed a few high-grade spirit stones to Pine Leaf Mouse to help it recover from the energy drain of synthesizing Transformation Grass.

Then, Lin Jing dug up a pot of soil and placed it in front of Pine Leaf Mouse.

Logically speaking, now that Pine Leaf Mouse had condensed a Five-Element Demon Core, his Glazed Immortal Eyes should be able to synthesize resources of other attributes, right?

This pile of yellow soil was no different from random tree leaves—it wasn’t even a low-grade resource. Lin Jing wondered if Pine Leaf Mouse could synthesize it now.

“Ugh…”

The robe-clad squirrel sat in meditation for a moment, recovered its energy, then looked at the pot of soil prepared by Lin Jing.

With a single sweep of its Glazed Immortal Eyes—

The next instant.

Lin Jing’s expression twisted with joy as he witnessed something incredible.

Pine Leaf Mouse’s attribute shift had indeed triggered an evolution in the Glazed Immortal Eyes—

The entire pot of soil transformed into a small pile of black soil!!!

[Mutated Spirit Soil]

When scattered over land, it accelerates land pollution, making it difficult for nearby spiritual plants to survive.

“Excellent! This familiar feeling is finally back.”

Seeing the major evolution of the Glazed Immortal Eyes, Lin Jing was thrilled.

From now on, he could synthesize resources of all five elements—Gold, Wood, Water, Fire, and Earth.

Although Pine Leaf Mouse might still not match Dragon Carp in battle strength, in strategic value, Lin Jing considered it completely irreplaceable.

Next, Lin Jing took a pot of Spirit Spring Water and had Pine Leaf Mouse synthesize it.

[Mutated Spirit Spring]

If consumed by mortals or beasts, regardless of gender, they will conceive.

“….”

Then, he grabbed some Crimson Fire Gold, a dual-attribute metal and fire resource used as furnace fuel by Crane Cauldron, and had Pine Leaf Mouse synthesize it.

This was an Eighth-Grade resource, more difficult to synthesize than Transformation Grass, but still a hundred times easier than soil or water.

And the synthesized material was absolutely worth the effort.

[Mutated Crimson Fire Gold]

If refined in spirit flames for a thousand years, it has a slight chance of developing sentience, turning stone into spirit.

“Weak,” muttered Ice Soul Gu, exhaling coldly.

In ancient times, no race was more ordinary than demon beasts or Plant Spirits.

It wasn’t just mountains and rivers that could become spirits—even a rock could cultivate into a monster, and a small mountain could become a mountain god.

But among them, this particular race had one of the hardest evolution paths, even harder than Plant Spirits.

Naturally, their numbers were incredibly rare, and during the resistance against the Immortal Palace, they were completely wiped out.

Yet now, Glazed Immortal Eyes could not only revive demon beasts, but even help metals and stones awaken?

“The cultivation path is working….”

“Now, I have three new developmental paths….”

Lin Jing stroked his chin, looking at the exhausted Pine Leaf Mouse, which was scratching its head in frustration, staring at the pile of pills before it.

Even after strengthening its demon core with five-element energy, Pine Leaf Mouse had limitations.

For example:

It still couldn’t perform "secondary synthesis."

Ordinary leaves could become Red Leaves, but Red Leaves couldn’t be further synthesized.

It couldn’t synthesize "complex items."

Pills and artifacts made from multiple materials couldn’t be synthesized.

It couldn’t synthesize "lifeforms."

Even if he gathered a swarm of mosquitoes, it couldn’t merge them into a single larger entity.

The further he went, the crazier the ideas got.

But Lin Jing believed this was the next step in unlocking the Glazed Immortal Eyes’ potential.

If condensing a Five-Element Demon Core had unlocked some hidden features, then…

Was there a way to unlock secondary synthesis?

Or to synthesize pills and artifacts?

Or even… merge lifeforms?

Lin Jing had no concrete ideas yet—he could only figure it out as he went.

“Alright, time for my Core Formation breakthrough.”

Lin Jing had already reached Peak Foundation Establishment, fully prepared to enter Core Formation.

However, after the previous disaster, he decided to prepare a pill for support this time.

He also planned to have Ice Soul Gu undergo Core Formation alongside him, to avoid dragging out the process for another ten years.

There was just one problem—Ice Soul Gu’s Core Formation resources.

Although its natural talent meant it could easily form a core if placed in an Extreme Cold Region, Lin Jing couldn’t treat it worse than the others.

After all—

Dragon Carp had five different exotic flames for core condensation.

Pine Leaf Mouse had the Sky Lion Clan’s countless spiritual plants, along with the Five-Element Treant Pill.

He couldn’t shortchange Ice Soul Gu, since this would directly impact its Demon Core Divine Abilities.

“The best thing for Ice Soul Gu is Longevity Blood, but that only extends lifespan—it’s useless for Core Formation.”

“Other than that… does it want to devour its own kind?”

Gu insects had a special trait—they could enhance their toxicity by consuming each other.

At its level, this wasn’t very efficient, but something was better than nothing.

The problem was—

The Ten Rare Gu were scattered across the land, each in the hands of powerful cultivators.

Even the Gu God Sect had been looted, leaving them with only three left.

“…Not gonna lie, every single Gu owner is at Void Mystical Stage.”

“There’s no way I can steal from them.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 73: Whereabouts of the Ten Rare Gu
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 73: Whereabouts of the Ten Rare Gu

Back at the Academy, Lin Jing had thoroughly investigated the Ten Rare Gu.

These Ten Rare Gu each belonged to a different element: Gold, Wood, Water, Fire, Earth, Wind, Thunder, Yin, Yang, and Ice.

Originally, each of the ten high-ranking members of the Gu God Sect held one of these powerful insects.

However, over time, as these Gu Masters perished, the Ten Rare Gu inevitably scattered across the world.

Due to their rebirth mechanism, these Rare Gu did not die with their masters.

In theory, the Gu God Sect should have been able to retrieve them immediately, since they were the sect’s own creations.

But the problem was—

The Ten Rare Gu were far too powerful.

Even demonic sects had likely fallen victim to these thefts.

In the end, under the calculations and schemes of various factions, the Gu God Sect managed to hold onto only four of the ten:

Yang Gu

Thunder Gu

Wind Gu

Ice Gu

Each of these had their own inheritance line.

For instance, the Ice Gu had always been held by the Gu God Sect’s Ice-Fire Holy Maidens.

However, after the previous Holy Maiden died in an internal sect conflict, the Gu Sect lost control over the Ice Soul Gu.

And ultimately—

The Ice Soul Gu fell into Lin Jing’s hands.

“The Gold-Element Golden Light Gu was sealed inside his own body by a high-ranking monk from the Western Desert… Getting a Rare Gu from the Buddhist sects? Impossible.”

“The Wood-Element Myriad Poison Gu—which contains the most diverse range of poisons among the Ten Rare Gu—is currently being held by Tongtian Tower, used for poison pill research. Even less of a chance of getting that.”

“The Water-Element Deathbane Gu was taken by Poseidon Palace. That sect is a beast clan power that split from the Ancient Dragon Palace, and nearly all oceanic demon beasts are gathered there…”

“The Fire-Element Heavenly Flame Gu is in the hands of a powerhouse from the Demon-Slaying Bureau, and that powerhouse is on the same level as Tongzi Xian…”

“The Earth-Element Stone Man Gu is in the Martial Emperor City’s Lord’s Mansion. They used it to create an entire army of petrified soldiers, like an army of terracotta warriors.”

“The Wind Gu and Thunder Gu are with the Gu God Sect’s Elder Wind and Elder Thunder.”

“The Yang Gu is in the hands of the Gu God Sect’s leader…”

“And the Yin Gu… is with an independent Void Mystical Stage rogue cultivator—Granny Yinshan.”

“No wonder the Gu God Sect is only after the Ice Soul Gu in my possession. It’s the easiest one to retrieve.”

“The other Rare Gu holders are almost all Void Mystical Stage experts, and on top of that, their backing factions are all far stronger than the Gu God Sect.”

Lin Jing reviewed the situation carefully.

If he relied on brute force, the easiest to acquire would likely be Granny Yinshan’s Yin Gu.

The Buddhist Sect monks were protected by a Dao-Seeking Stage Buddha Lord.

Tongtian Tower also had Dao-Seeking Stage powerhouses.

Poseidon Palace was the same.

The Demon-Slaying Bureau and Martial Emperor City? Not even worth considering.

As for the Gu God Sect, while their current sect leader was not Dao-Seeking Stage, their previous leader had attained that level.

However, because that leader had offered himself as sustenance for the Gu God Sect’s Divine Gu, he was always in seclusion and rarely emerged.

After weighing all the options, the only target that wasn’t protected by a Dao-Seeking Stage backer was Granny Yinshan.

But even she was no pushover.

She was at Peak Void Mystical Stage, and in her youth, she had been the Senior Sister of Water Moon Immortal Island, known as the most talented genius in that sect’s ten-thousand-year history.

No one knew why she later betrayed the sect, but she had somehow remained unscathed despite the fallout.

“Water Moon Immortal Island is located in the Eastern Sea. They only accept male disciples and only practice water-based techniques. Every generation, their island lord has reached the Dao-Seeking Stage.

For Granny Yinshan to defy them and still survive… Her power must be terrifying.”

“… That damn Ice Soul Gu, giving me such a headache. You really want to devour your own kind like Senior Pine? Keep dreaming.”

“But… if the Transformation Research goes well, and I earn the Demon Emperor’s favor, maybe—just maybe—I’ll have a chance to acquire another Rare Gu…”

Lin Jing shook his head.

Unless he could get the Demon Emperor to personally step in, there was no way he was getting his hands on another Rare Gu.

“For now, it’s more reliable to find an ice-attribute demon beast with a Demon Emperor bloodline and just make it into a puppet for the Ice Soul Gu.”

Preparations for Tribulation Resistance

In the meantime, Lin Jing sent Pine Leaf Mouse back to synthesizing more Transformation Grass.

Though Pine Leaf Mouse would rather hoard resources inside its Five-Element Demon Core World, stockpiling supplies, they still needed enough Transformation Grass for experiments first.

As for Dragon Carp, Lin Jing assigned it to attempt refining Fifth-Tier Pills, aiming to break through to the Fifth-Tier Pill Sovereign level.

And Lin Jing himself?

He spent his time waiting for Pine Leaf Mouse’s Transformation Grass synthesis, flipping through Crane Cauldron’s countless pill books, and studying the Crane Cauldron alchemists’ research findings.

His goal: refine the synthesized Transformation Grass into pills that work on high-rank demon beasts.

A pill that only lets Qi Refinement and Foundation Establishment demon beasts transform wasn’t enough.

If the Demon Court was to truly revive the demon race, they needed a method that worked on high-level demon beasts too.

Two Months Later—Senior Sister Returns

Xu Qingyao returned from the Ginseng Tribe.

“I didn’t fail the mission,” she said, “I managed to obtain three Lightning Avoidance Pills. It’s a bit few, but… even for the Ginseng Tribe, the materials for these pills are extremely rare.”

“Three is good enough,” Lin Jing nodded. “What was the price?”

Xu Qingyao sighed.

“…A portion of the Transformation Pill’s alchemy notes.”

“In ancient times, not only could demon beasts transform into humans, but so could Plant Spirits.

The Ginseng Tribe is likely interested in transformation methods. Since they know that I’m leading the alchemy research on transformation in the Demon Court, they requested our experimental records in exchange.”

“No big deal. So far, Crane Cauldron has only developed two Transformation Pills—

One is a poison that causes bloodline collapse.

The other is a demonic pill that induces Qi deviation.

Neither are valuable, just failed experiments that help eliminate wrong paths.

Now it’s all up to you, Lin Jing. Don’t disappoint me.”

“I won’t,” Lin Jing promised.

“But the most important thing now is the demon beast that will undergo tribulation.”

“We have the Transformation Grass to trigger the Tribulation of Transformation.

We have the Lightning Avoidance Pills to mitigate the lightning damage.

Now, we just need a Foundation Establishment demon beast with:

A strong physique

Innate lightning resistance

“Senior Sister, does the Demon Court have such a demon beast?”

Xu Qingyao fell into deep thought.

“…Yes, we do.

But even with Lightning Avoidance Pills, making a Foundation Establishment demon beast endure tribulation is still a near-suicidal endeavor.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 74: Three Methods
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 74: Three Methods

"Still, we have to give it a try." Lin Jing spoke.

"Yeah, but it's a pity that the Thunder Qilin lineage of the Qilin Clan has long been extinct. Otherwise, we could just find a genius from the Qilin Clan directly." Xu Qingyao shook her head.

"Among the demonic beasts that follow me, there isn't a suitable candidate. I just thought about it, and the most qualified should be the Thunder Dragon Clan."

"Thunder Dragon Clan?"

"Yes, the Thunder Dragon Clan originated from electric eels transforming into dragons. They belong to the acquired dragon species, but their ancestor, the Thunder Dragon Patriarch, was exceptionally talented and reached the Void Mystical Demon Ancestor stage... If I recall correctly, the current little princess of the Thunder Dragon Clan is only at the Foundation Establishment stage. She has the purest ancestral bloodline of the clan in the past thousand years."

"The dragon race has incredibly strong bodies, and with its lightning-infused bloodline, it should be better suited to withstand the tribulation. Within the Demon Court, under the Demon Core stage, it is probably the best choice."

"Then let’s go with that." Lin Jing nodded and said, "Senior Sister, please ask the Thunder Dragon Clan if they’re willing to try."

Xu Qingyao spoke, "That’s simple. Just like the Qilin Clan, the Thunder Dragon Clan has not assisted any of the Holy Maidens, but as a Holy Maiden, I was once gifted a Dragon Pearl by the Thunder Dragon Clan Leader, which allows me to contact them directly."

In the next instant.

A spectral image of a divine dragon with blue scales and a white body, enveloped in lightning, emerged from the Dragon Pearl—it was none other than the Thunder Dragon Clan Leader’s Dharma body.

"Holy Maiden Xu??" The Thunder Dragon Clan Leader looked surprised. Lately, Holy Maiden Xu Qingyao had been unusually active—not only did she suppress the other two Holy Maidens at the Qilin Tea Ceremony, but she had also gained the support of the Qilin Clan!

"Senior Thunder Dragon." Xu Qingyao greeted, "It's been a while."

"Holy Maiden Xu, what is the reason for contacting me?" The Thunder Dragon Clan Leader asked.

Xu Qingyao replied, "My research on transformation has made new progress—I’ve developed a new variety of the Transformation Grass."

"Hmm??" The Thunder Dragon Clan Leader was momentarily stunned before breaking into a delighted smile. "That is great news! What makes this new variety different from the previous Transformation Grass?"

"The new Transformation Grass can trigger the Tribulation of Transformation. As long as the physical body endures the baptism of this tribulation, it should be able to transform into a demon race..." Xu Qingyao explained.

"Tribulation of Transformation..." The Thunder Dragon Clan Leader frowned. "What level of power does this tribulation possess?"
