"It’s equivalent to a peak Nascent Soul lightning cultivator’s full-strength attack… However, only Qi Refinement and Foundation Establishment stage demonic beasts can consume this Transformation Grass." Xu Qingyao paused. "Of course, I have obtained Lightning-Avoiding Pills from the Ginseng King Tribe, so as long as the Foundation Establishment genius is proficient in lightning techniques and has a strong body, they might be able to withstand the tribulation."

The Thunder Dragon Clan Leader chuckled and immediately understood everything. "So, Holy Maiden Xu, you wish for my daughter to be the test subject for this new Transformation Grass?"

"A Tribulation of Transformation… such a thing has never been heard of! In ancient times, whether one transformed by directly consuming Transformation Grass or by cultivating transformation techniques, there was never any tribulation. Holy Maiden Xu, how can you guarantee that after surviving this near-death tribulation, one will definitely transform into the demon race?"

Xu Qingyao hesitated and looked at Lin Jing—there was no guarantee. This was all based on Lin Jing’s theory, and no one could be certain that surviving the tribulation would result in transformation.

"If I recall correctly, Holy Maiden Xu, you have developed two types of Transformation Pills before, both of which ended in failure. The side effects were severe, and several genius demonic beasts perished in the experiments. Even though it was all for the grand plan of reviving the demon race, this kind of unguaranteed risk—I cannot comply. My daughter is the hope of the Thunder Dragon Clan, and as her father, I cannot let her take such a gamble."

Xu Qingyao wanted to say something, but the Dragon Pearl's glow dimmed, and the projection shook its head before dissipating.

Xu Qingyao sighed, while Meng Jing beside her said, "Senior Sister, don’t be discouraged. It’s understandable—if we consider this from the perspective of the Thunder Dragon Clan Leader, his decision makes sense."

"Yes, the eldest princess of the Thunder Dragon Clan is indeed their hope. Letting her bet everything on such a slim possibility is truly unrealistic. If she succeeds, it would be great, but if she fails, they would not only lose her but also earn the enmity of the Thunder Dragon Clan."

"So, what do we do now?" Senior Sister looked toward the Dragon Pearl. "Can you investigate again and see if there’s another suitable beast?"

"Forget it." The Dragon Pearl projection shook its head again. "I feel like the result will be the same. You should just cultivate a suitable candidate yourselves."

"Cultivate one ourselves??" Xu Qingyao paused, then realized. "That’s right! It only needs to be at the Foundation Establishment stage. With enough resources, we should be able to train one in a few years! And with targeted cultivation, it might yield even better results!"

"Most importantly, we can directly select those who are willing to sacrifice their lives for the revival of the demon race."

"Exactly." Lin Jing spoke slowly. "I have three methods in mind. Senior Sister, would you like to hear them?"

"Go ahead."

"Senior Sister, do you remember the mosquito beast that died during the Transformation Tribulation? That mosquito beast was one of the Ten Terrors of the Cloud Sea, and its species has a unique trait—the more there are, the stronger their individual attributes become."

"A million mosquito beasts can threaten a Nascent Soul cultivator. A hundred million can be nearly invincible below the Nascent Soul Transformation stage. According to records, the theoretical limit of a mosquito swarm is ten billion. A ten-billion-strong mosquito swarm would make even Nascent Soul Transformation cultivators avoid them."

"The mosquito beast that failed to transcend the tribulation earlier died because it was alone. But if we cultivated ten billion mosquito beasts, then let just one of them undergo the tribulation while the others supported it—along with various medicinal aids—perhaps it could succeed. Most importantly, with enough numbers, even if one or two attempts fail, we can keep trying until we succeed."

"Oh? I recall that mosquito beasts reproduce by absorbing blood—the higher the quality of the blood, the faster they reproduce. The Demon Court is overflowing with high-quality blood, including various types of demon ancestor blood. It is indeed possible to raise a ten-billion-strong mosquito army..." Xu Qingyao found this method feasible.

"What about the other two methods?" Seeing Lin Jing nod, Xu Qingyao asked further.

"The second method is the one Senior Sister just mentioned—training a suitable tribulation survivor normally within the Demon Court."

"As for the third... My Ice Soul Gu is currently at the Foundation Establishment stage. If I merge with it, we will be treated as one entity and can face the tribulation together. However, this would put me at great risk, so I personally don’t want to try it. I still prefer the ten-billion mosquito swarm plan."

"Then let's go with that plan." Xu Qingyao agreed. "I’ll be responsible for finding enough demon blood for you."

Lin Jing smiled. "However, there’s still one major issue with this plan—controlling a ten-billion-strong mosquito swarm is beyond our capabilities. It will be difficult to get them to cooperate, and their ferocity will be hard to suppress. So, there’s one more thing I must ask Senior Sister to handle—taming their bloodthirsty nature."

"Just tell me what you need."

"The Myriad Beast Circle from Myriad Beast Sect. We’ll need a large number of them, and ideally, we should also obtain their sect’s treasure—the Beast God Circle."

Xu Qingyao smirked. "Myriad Beast Sect, huh? I’ve been wanting to deal with them for a while now. This is the perfect chance to annex them into the Beast Taming Sect."

Lin Jing: "..."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 75: Annexing Myriad Beast Sect
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 75: Annexing Myriad Beast Sect

Annexing Myriad Beast Sect…

Lin Jing stroked his chin and said, "It’s not impossible."

Something like this was almost unheard of in the Ancient Celestial Kingdom, but in a place like Qingzhou, it was quite common for large sects to absorb smaller ones.

However…

"It won’t be the Demon Court annexing Myriad Beast Sect, but rather the Beast Taming Sect. After the annexation, who will be in charge?" Lin Jing asked.

After all, aside from Guest Elder Shen Song, the Beast Taming Sect still didn’t have a single Nascent Soul stage expert, right?

Even though Myriad Beast Sect wasn’t considered a major sect in Qingzhou, it was still backed by a Nascent Soul sect master. No matter what, they would need to station at least a Nascent Soul cultivator at Myriad Beast Sect after taking over.

"Let’s have Elder Li Hai manage it. Though he’s a bit weak, having just broken through to Nascent Soul recently, as long as he has the support of the Demon Court, and we arrange for a Demon Emperor to assist him in overseeing Myriad Beast Sect for a while, things will gradually stabilize."

Holding onto this belief, he had come to Qingzhou along with the sect master to seek opportunities. And now, it seemed that he had proven his philosophy correct, as he had managed to break through to the Nascent Soul stage before reaching his natural lifespan’s end.

"By the way, when will the sect master come out of seclusion?" Lin Jing asked.

Unlike most Beast Taming Sect elders and disciples who came to Qingzhou to train and take on missions, just like Senior Brother Ouyang had done before, Senior Sister Xu did not allow freeloaders.

But when it came to her adoptive father, Xu Xiaoyao, she made an exception—bestowing him with a vast amount of resources, which had kept him in secluded cultivation, refining treasures. Lin Jing had been here for quite some time but had yet to see the sect master.

"It may take a few more years, or even decades. The resource I found for my father to refine his External Incarnation is 'Celestial Reed.' Celestial Reed contains spatial and sky attributes, making it a top-tier material for refining an External Incarnation. Once successfully refined, it will not only make it easier to comprehend sky energy and ascend to the Nascent Soul Transformation stage, but it will also aid in understanding spatial energy and breaking through to the Void Mystical Stage."

"However, refining it into an External Incarnation is more difficult compared to other resources," Xu Qingyao said slowly. "I just hope my father succeeds..."

"That would indeed take some time..." Lin Jing nodded.

"You should be close to breaking through to Core Formation soon, right? Compared to Gu Qianqiu, your cultivation speed is quite fast."

"Fast? It’s all thanks to the Beast Taming Art..." Lin Jing shook his head.

"It’s still early, but if you truly discover the path to transformation, and the Demon Emperor asks you what reward you want, you might want to consider requesting a treasure for refining an External Incarnation."

"An External Incarnation is incredibly important for future cultivation. Think carefully… about the path you want to take in the future."

"I understand."

After witnessing so much, Lin Jing knew that an External Incarnation was not just about comprehending the power of the heavens and ascending to Nascent Soul Transformation.

Most External Incarnations were refined using plants imbued with powerful natural energies, fused with a Nascent Soul cultivator’s divine sense. Aside from helping to comprehend the Dao of Heaven and Earth, External Incarnations had another unique trait—

Unlike ordinary clones or even the wooden clones of Pine Leaf Mice, an External Incarnation refined via Nascent Soul Possession could cultivate different techniques from the main body.

For instance, Lin Jing’s main body cultivated the Beast Taming Art, but his External Incarnation could cultivate the techniques of Elder Li Hai, and it wouldn’t be a problem.

Many weak Nascent Soul cultivators would have their External Incarnation practice nature-aligned cultivation techniques to enhance their comprehension of the Dao of Heaven and Earth. Some, like Xu Qingyao’s plan for the sect master, even dabbled in the Dao of Space for greater ambition.

Lin Jing was sure that he would refine an External Incarnation, but he wouldn’t make such a choice. While the Incarnation could also cultivate the Art of Beast Taming, this would only allow him to contract more pet beasts. However, the Beast Taming Art, though powerful, was not the ultimate path. In the future, he could have even better options.

Beyond that, for someone with the Longevity Body like Lin Jing, an External Incarnation had an even greater strategic value. If there ever came a day when his main body was devoured for its Longevity Body, he could still survive through the External Incarnation—he would merely lose the Longevity Body.

In other words, a Nascent Soul cultivator with an External Incarnation was practically equivalent to having "two lives," as long as both were not killed at the same time.

For this reason, most cultivators considered the Nascent Soul stage the true beginning of immortality, as they gained access to many powerful methods.

"If I refine an External Incarnation, I won’t have it cultivate the Beast Taming Dao. The Incarnation isn’t a Longevity Body, so its cultivation speed won’t be slow, and it won’t need to rely on Beast Taming Dao’s feedback. After all, the Incarnation’s contracted pet beasts wouldn’t provide cultivation feedback to my main body."

"But things aren’t absolute. If I happen to come across a few rare or even immortal beast cubs in the future, and my main body doesn’t have enough contract slots, then having the Incarnation cultivate the Beast Taming Dao wouldn’t be a bad idea either." Lin Jing fantasized.

After finalizing the plan to annex Myriad Beast Sect, Xu Qingyao and Lin Jing immediately started preparing.

Xu Qingyao began gathering her Demon Emperor protectors. She didn’t summon all of them, but four Nascent Soul Demon Emperors responded to her call—more than enough to deal with Myriad Beast Sect.

Additionally, Elder Li Hai, who had been leading a group of Beast Taming Sect disciples on their training journey in Qingzhou, was also summoned back by Xu Qingyao.

"Greetings, Holy Maiden." Elder Li Hai bowed.

"Elder Li Hai, I have a task for you. Do you have the confidence to handle it?"

"Please instruct me, Holy Maiden."

"I intend to annex Myriad Beast Sect into the Beast Taming Sect and have you temporarily oversee it. What do you think?"

"Ah? Me?" Elder Li Hai was stunned.

Xu Qingyao smiled and said, "Don’t worry, Elder Li Hai. I will personally eliminate all obstacles for you."

Elder Li Hai’s expression grew serious before he finally said, "I will follow the Holy Maiden’s orders."

"By the way, Holy Maiden, I assume you’ve heard about what happened with the Sky Lion Clan? Lin Jing has indeed arrived in Qingzhou. Has the Demon Court found any trace of him?"

"Not yet, but given how that guy operates, he’ll definitely protect himself. Let’s not worry about him for now," Xu Qingyao said casually.

"Alright then..." Elder Li Hai nodded, though he was still worried about Lin Jing. In his eyes, Lin Jing was the Beast Taming Sect’s greatest prodigy, even surpassing Xu Qingyao.

Compared to annexing Myriad Beast Sect, finding Lin Jing was the real priority.

—

A few days later.

Qingzhou, Senluo Path.

Outside Myriad Beast Mountain, a spatial rift was suddenly torn open in midair.

The aura of several Demon Emperors surged as Xu Qingyao walked at the forefront. Behind her, on her left and right, were Elder Fang from the Beast Taming Sect and Lin Jing, disguised as the "Dragon-Slaying Alchemist."

Further behind, hidden in the folds of space, four Nascent Soul Demon Emperors stood ready, awaiting orders to take action.

"Which esteemed cultivator is visiting Myriad Beast Sect?!"

Within Myriad Beast Sect, the Nascent Soul Sect Master, Myriad Beast Lord, instantly broke into a cold sweat. Transforming into a streak of rainbow light, he flew into the sky and looked toward Xu Qingyao.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 76: Seven-Star Beast God Circle
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 76: Seven-Star Beast God Circle

"Beast Taming Sect, Xu Qingyao."

Facing Myriad Beast Lord's inquiry, Xu Qingyao replied indifferently. That simple response instantly made Myriad Beast Lord’s face turn deathly pale.

"So this day has finally come..."

Regret flooded Myriad Beast Lord’s heart. Back when the Beast Taming Sect was sealed by the Ancient Celestial Kingdom, he had sent his vice sect master and two core disciples to the kingdom, hoping to annex the Beast Taming Sect.

Of course, the plan ultimately ended in failure.

If it had stopped there, it wouldn’t have been a big deal.

But then news spread across Qingzhou—Holy Maiden Xu Qingyao was the adopted daughter of the Beast Taming Sect’s sect master.

Worse still, to restore the reputation of the Beast Taming Sect, this very Holy Maiden had wiped out a Nascent Soul-level force.

That was merely his perspective.

From the Holy Maiden’s standpoint, things might look very different.

After all, as the sect master’s daughter, she would likely inherit the Beast Taming Sect one day. That meant the Beast Taming Sect was her power. Attempting to undermine that power? In Qingzhou, that was no different from courting death.

"May I ask what brings the Holy Maiden here today...?" Myriad Beast Lord asked politely.

"Surrender, or be destroyed," Xu Qingyao stated bluntly.

Myriad Beast Lord’s face darkened.

At that moment, the Nascent Soul elders of Myriad Beast Sect arrived. But in the face of the overwhelming presence of the Demon Court, their momentum was completely crushed.

"I will allow Myriad Beast Sect to keep its current inheritance. You only need to submit, and half of your sect’s resources must be handed over every ten years."

"Sect Master!!"

Vice Sect Master Miao Ling turned to Myriad Beast Lord in panic, afraid that he would act rashly.

Sure, Myriad Beast Sect had a guardian treasure. Sure, they had a protective grand formation.

But all of that was utterly meaningless before the Holy Maiden’s resolve.

After all, this group of women had the power to mobilize Void Mystical combat strength.

Everything they did was considered part of the grand plan to revive the demon race, and they had the full backing of the Demon Court.

Frankly speaking, Myriad Beast Sect’s first mistake was provoking the Beast Taming Sect in the first place.

"...Fine. Myriad Beast Sect will submit to the Demon Court and become one of its vassal forces," Myriad Beast Lord sighed, feeling as if he had personally doomed his sect.

A sect focused on the Beast Taming Dao, now reduced to a subsidiary of a demon faction…

"Who said you’d be joining the Demon Court?"

"From today onward, Myriad Beast Sect will be a vassal of the Beast Taming Sect."

"Elder Li Hai, from this day forth, you are the Beast Taming Sect’s envoy to Myriad Beast Sect. Sect Master Myriad Beast, all Nascent Soul-level cultivators of Myriad Beast Sect must now surrender a strand of their Nascent Soul essence."

"If Myriad Beast Sect causes no trouble within the next hundred years, your Nascent Soul essences will be returned."

A Nascent Soul was formed from a cultivator’s soul and spiritual power. Losing a strand of Nascent Soul essence was minor in the short term. However, if that essence was damaged, it would impact the entire Nascent Soul—causing anything from cultivation regression to outright destruction.

"You... sigh..."

The Nascent Soul elders present were unwilling, but as Myriad Beast Lord was the first to hand over his Nascent Soul essence, the rest had no choice but to follow suit.

"Is this enough for the Holy Maiden?"

The Phantom Mountain Lord understood that protecting their sect’s inheritance was now the highest priority.

If the mountain remains, there will always be wood to burn.

"You’re quite the fool," Xu Qingyao laughed playfully. "You’ll regret this decision in the future. Sticking with the Beast Taming Sect will be far better for your sect than struggling on your own!"

She then casually tossed the collected Nascent Soul essences to an astonished Elder Li Hai.

And then…

Xu Qingyao raised her hand.

In the next instant, from deep within Myriad Beast Mountain, a brilliant light shot into the sky.

Seeing Xu Qingyao’s greedy gaze, Myriad Beast Lord could no longer remain silent.

"Holy Maiden, that is our Myriad Beast Sect’s guardian treasure!!!"

"You needn’t worry."

"From today onward, Myriad Beast Sect will enjoy the Demon Court’s protection. Are you afraid that, without your guardian treasure, you won’t be able to compete with Flower Sect and Thousand Illusions Mountain? If those two sects dare lay a hand on Myriad Beast Sect, I’ll personally deal with them."

As she finished speaking, seven Myriad Beast Circles appeared in her hands.

Then, she threw them to Lin Jing.

This was Myriad Beast Sect’s guardian treasure—the Seven-Star Beast God Circle.

The so-called Seven-Star Beast God Circle was a set of seven High-Grade Myriad Beast Circles combined into one artifact.

A single High-Grade treasure was standard equipment for a Nascent Soul cultivator.

However, the Seven-Star Beast God Circle was so powerful that even a single Nascent Soul cultivator couldn’t fully wield it. It required the combined efforts of an entire sect, and its combined might was comparable to a Top-Grade artifact.

"This is it."

Lin Jing held the Seven-Star Beast God Circle in his hands, extremely satisfied.

With this, he could begin refining his ten-billion mosquito swarm.

Of course, he couldn’t yet activate all seven Myriad Beast Circles at once. But the mosquito swarm wouldn’t emerge all at once—it would be nurtured gradually. As long as he refined them one step at a time, it would be fine.

Moreover, Lin Jing had already mastered the Three Heads and Six Arms technique, an ability perfectly suited for wielding multiple artifacts at once.

As long as he pushed Three Heads and Six Arms to its peak, he could possibly unleash even 10-20% of the Seven-Star Beast God Circle’s full power while in a fused state.

"Fell right into my hands… with Three Heads and Six Arms, my artifacts are now complete. One per hand, with one even to spare."

As Lin Jing basked in satisfaction, Myriad Beast Lord seemed to resign himself to his fate.

"You may take the Seven-Star Beast God Circle, but… what about the beast horde sleeping within?"

"The beast horde will be returned shortly." Lin Jing replied.

The Seven-Star Beast God Circle contained countless slumbering demonic beasts, sealed through special methods for generations. This was Myriad Beast Sect’s ultimate trump card—

In times of crisis, the entire sect could awaken and control the beasts to launch a counterattack against invaders.

But against the Demon Court, a force several tiers above them, Myriad Beast Sect didn’t even entertain the thought of resistance.

—

The Demon Court’s arrival at Myriad Beast Sect naturally drew the attention of the other three major forces in Senluo Path—

Senluo Hall, Flower Sect, and Thousand Illusions Mountain!

Each of these forces had their own Nascent Soul experts, who arrived to observe the situation.

The Nascent Soul cultivators from Senluo Hall and Flower Sect could only sigh at the cold reality of the cultivation world as they watched Myriad Beast Sect fall.

As for Thousand Illusions Mountain’s Nascent Soul Grand Elder—who looked like a ten-year-old golden-haired girl—her expression was one of confusion.

"That one in the Firecloud Alchemist’s robes… an illusion?!"

"This person’s skill in Illusionary Dao is nearly as strong as mine… no, even better!"

She couldn’t help but take a closer look.

And in doing so…

She became the first person to see through Lin Jing’s illusions since he had mastered them.

Before her Illusionary Dao-enhanced Nascent Soul vision, Lin Jing’s disguise as the "Dragon-Slaying Alchemist" fell apart, revealing his "Thousand Illusions Mountain Disciple Huang Yu" persona.

And beneath that disguise—

The true face of Lin Jing, the Beast Taming Sect’s Longevity Body prodigy, was laid bare before her eyes.

Illusions undetectable to Void Mystical cultivators couldn’t last even a few minutes under her scrutiny.

"...Huang Yu, the traitorous disciple Phantom Bright wanted to introduce to me???"

"Wait, no—Beast Taming Sect’s Lin Jing?!"

Her mind reeled.

At that moment, Lin Jing also realized someone had pierced his illusion.

He instantly turned to look in a certain direction.

In the next moment—

The Thousand Illusions Mountain Grand Elder turned and ran.

She had just seen something she absolutely should not have seen...

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 77: Return to Thousand Illusions Mountain
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 77: Return to Thousand Illusions Mountain

"What's wrong?"

Senior Sister Xu Qingyao saw Lin Jing's expression suddenly change as he looked in a certain direction and couldn't help but ask.

She followed his gaze but saw nothing.

However, she could sense that from other directions, unknown powerful figures were secretly observing this place.

This wasn’t surprising. With so many Demon Emperors descending upon the Myriad Beast Sect with such a commotion, it was bound to attract the caution of local experts.

"I feel like my illusion has been seen through." Lin Jing transmitted his thoughts to his senior sister.

"Who could possibly see through your illusions?" Xu Qingyao paused. She was well aware of Lin Jing's mastery of illusions, and on top of that, with the Chameleon Demon Core she had given him, even Void Mystical Stage cultivators shouldn't be able to see through it!

"Wait… Thousand Illusions Mountain." Senior Sister suddenly realized something.

Xu Qingyao recalled, "Before planning to take over the Myriad Beast Sect, I investigated the situation in Senluo Path. The Grand Elder of Thousand Illusions Mountain is indeed an illusionary genius. She was born with an Illusion Body, her mana inherently carries illusionary properties, and she possesses Illusion Eyes. Even as a mortal, her gaze had a mesmerizing effect. Once she achieved mastery, no one dared to offend her, because even those one or two realms above her couldn't even find her presence."

"This is exactly why Thousand Illusions Mountain has stood firm for a thousand years. Even Void Mystical Stage forces wouldn't dare underestimate them. After all, facing a true illusion master, even if a Void Mystical Grand Cultivator himself isn’t afraid, without a means of countering her, offending her would be a nightmare for the lower-level cultivators in their sect."

"Senior Sister, please accompany me to Thousand Illusions Mountain once more!" Lin Jing decided to investigate.

Their spiritual exchange was incredibly fast. After entrusting Elder Li Hai with managing the Myriad Beast Sect and ordering the Demon Emperor guardians to stand by and protect the sect, which had lost its Guardian Treasure, the two immediately turned and rushed toward Thousand Illusions Mountain.

This sudden action directly startled the Sect Master of the Flower Sect.

The Demon Court… wouldn’t be planning to swallow Thousand Illusions Mountain right after taking over the Myriad Beast Sect, would they?

Could they be aiming to take over all of Senluo Path?!

...

At Thousand Illusions Mountain, Grand Elder Huan Li had just returned to the sect when she cursed herself for letting curiosity get the better of her and glancing over at them.

Before she could even think of a way to handle the situation, two familiar auras had already descended upon Thousand Illusions Mountain.

Sect Master Huan Gongming was alarmed and was just about to go out to investigate when the voice of Grand Elder Huan Li reached his ears.

"Gongming, step back. Leave this to me."

"Yes, Grand Elder." Huan Gongming withdrew and entered the core area of the Sect Protection Formation, awaiting the outcome of the negotiations.

Outside the mountain, Huan Li gracefully flew into the sky and looked at the descending Xu Qingyao and Lin Jing.

"I wonder, what brings the honorable Third Holy Maiden of the Demon Court to Thousand Illusions Mountain?"

"Senior Sister, it's her." Lin Jing confirmed, "She's clearly over a thousand years old but still looks like a little girl. Most importantly, I can sense that she's using an illusionary form, yet I can't see through it to her true body. Her mastery of illusions is definitely beyond mine."

"Thousand Illusions Venerable, you know too much. It seems that today… wait a minute, what is 'loli'?" Senior Sister Xu Qingyao glared at the Grand Elder of Thousand Illusions Mountain before turning to Lin Jing with a questioning look.

"Uh… it kinda means not a big guy, it's a term from your hometown’s dialect."

"I see." Senior Sister nodded.

"D*mn it, they actually talk it out first." The Senluo Path Grand Elder suddenly cursed under her breath. "You just glanced at them a couple of times—do they really plan to annex your Thousand Illusions Mountain just like they did with the Myriad Beast Sect?"

"Tell them that you, Shulanping, are not as easy to mess with as the Myriad Beast Sect."

"Besides, that brat infiltrated your Senluo Path and secretly learned your sect’s secret techniques—what do you have to say about that?"

Just as Xu Qingyao had said, the Grand Elder of Senluo Path was indeed less confident than the Myriad Beast Sect’s Sect Master.

When faced with the strong, the Myriad Beast Sect had no choice but to submit;
resistance wasn't even an option.

But Thousand Illusions Mountain was different. Grand Elder Huan Li’s illusion arts were at their peak. If she truly wanted to hide or escape, unless a supreme powerhouse made a move, few could actually capture her.

"Grand Elder, there's no need to panic. I have indeed learned Thousand Illusions Mountain’s techniques, but how could that be considered stealing? To this day, I am still a disciple of Thousand Illusions Mountain. Could it be that Grand Elder does not acknowledge me as her disciple?" Lin Jing chuckled.

The corner of the Thousand Illusions Mountain Grand Elder’s mouth twitched—she absolutely couldn’t acknowledge this disciple.

If word got out that Lin Jing had once joined Thousand Illusions Mountain, could the sect ever have peace again?

The Yin Corpse Sect, the Gu God Sect, the Sky Lion Clan—any one of them was not something Thousand Illusions Mountain could afford to offend.

And, of course, the Demon Court… they couldn't afford to offend them either.

After all, if she remembered correctly, there was a clan within the Demon Court known as the Chameleons, who were also extremely proficient in illusions. Though she had absolute confidence in her own illusions, she wouldn’t dare underestimate the illusion masters among the demon beasts.

More importantly, Lin Jing was still so young, yet his mastery of illusions was already close to hers. She couldn’t even begin to imagine the heights his illusionary arts might reach in the future.

"You two should leave. I didn't see anything, I don't know anything, and from this day forth, Thousand Illusions Mountain will be sealed off for five hundred years and remain uninvolved in worldly affairs." The Thousand Illusions Grand Elder waved her hand, dismissing them.

"That won't do." Xu Qingyao pondered for a moment before saying, "Thousand Illusions Venerable, I see that your Thousand Illusions Mountain is already struggling to maintain its legacy. For a thousand years, no disciple has emerged with the potential to reach the Nascent Soul Transformation Stage. Why not continue recognizing him as your disciple? In the future, he can inherit Thousand Illusions Mountain. With his unparalleled talent in the Illusion Dao, he certainly won’t tarnish the name of your sect."

"You’re already at an advanced age. It’s time to let go of mundane affairs and wholeheartedly pursue the Void Mystical Stage. At your current cultivation level, the sect is no longer of much help to you. How about this—let him take over Thousand Illusions Mountain, and you come with me to the Demon Court? Your sect specializes in illusionary beasts, right? When the time comes, I’ll introduce you to the various Demon Ancestors to discuss the Dao together. What do you think?"

"Uh…" Thousand Illusions Grand Elder Huan Li was stunned.

She looked at Lin Jing. Indeed, she had never seen an illusion master this monstrous before. If Thousand Illusions Mountain’s legacy was passed to him, it might actually be a blessing rather than a disaster.

Her greatest concern had always been the sect’s inheritance. If that was secured, she could finally focus on pursuing the Void Mystical Stage.

Xu Qingyao had genuinely tempted her.

"I'm sure the Venerable is a wise woman. You won’t regret this choice. Sticking with us is definitely better than letting Thousand Illusions Mountain struggle alone!" Xu Qingyao grinned boldly.

The Grand Elder of Thousand Illusions Mountain’s face darkened.

D*mn it… was this the same thing they told the Myriad Beast Sect’s Sect Master?!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 78: Mosquito Woman
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 78: Mosquito Woman

The Grand Elder of Thousand Illusions Mountain, Huan Li, remained silent for a long time. She felt that her curiosity had doomed her—by seeing through Lin Jing’s secret, he was bound to retaliate.

After countless considerations, she realized she had no other choice. Just because she had looked at Lin Jing one extra time in the crowd, she had sealed the fate of both Thousand Illusions Mountain and herself.

She was now going to be confined in the Demon Court—though, admittedly, it was a place full of opportunities.

"Guess I’m just unlucky. Let’s do as you say." Huan Li sighed. It was still a better fate than that of the Myriad Beast Sect.

Besides, if she didn’t make this choice, once the Myriad Beast Sect grew under the Demon Court’s backing, the disciples of Thousand Illusions Mountain would likely have an even harder time surviving here.

Xu Qingyao glanced at Lin Jing. Now that this "threat" had been resolved, she smiled and said, "Then it’s decided."

"From today on, we’re on the same side. But, Thousand Illusions Venerable, can you tell me… why do you maintain this appearance?"

The Grand Elder of Thousand Illusions Mountain, Huan Li, looked at Xu Qingyao and said, "What do you mean by 'maintain this appearance'?"

"This appearance is not fake! Thousand Illusions Mountain has the Inverse Void Art. I have already cultivated it to perfection—this form has permanently merged with me. So, this is my true appearance."

"Holy Maiden, you’re still young. When you get older, you’ll come begging me to teach you the secret of eternal youth. So, be polite to me."

Senior Sister shrugged. "Whatever makes you happy."

Fine, stay young forever—but over a thousand years old and still looking like a child? Isn’t that a bit excessive?

Xu Qingyao found the woman a little eccentric.

Lin Jing, standing to the side, stroked his chin and pondered—so now Thousand Illusions Mountain belonged to him?

"From now on, you—Huang Yu—will be the future heir of Thousand Illusions Mountain." Huan Li said seriously.

"Alright." Lin Jing didn’t care much. He could just have Pine Leaf Mouse create another Huang Yu clone and leave it at Thousand Illusions Mountain as a mascot.

"Your illusion arts should have reached the stage of Spell Spirit Origin Prototype, correct?" Huan Li asked.

She chuckled and said, "Not bad, but there are still flaws in your illusions. Once we return to the Demon Court, I’ll help you correct these. That way, your Spell Spirit Origin will be able to develop its own intelligence faster."

Lin Jing naturally had no reason to refuse. "Then I’ll thank you in advance, Grand Elder."

"Don’t call me Grand Elder. I’m only a thousand years older than you—just call me Sister."

"Since I’m entrusting the future of Thousand Illusions Mountain to you, I will teach you everything I know." She looked at Lin Jing and said, "You haven’t switched cultivation methods yet, have you? You’re still cultivating Thousand Illusions Codex? When you create an External Incarnation in the future, why not have your second body specialize in illusion arts?"

"We’ll see, we’ll see…"

"We’ll see?!" The "loli" Grand Elder nearly jumped up to hit him. With his unparalleled illusion talent, he was still hesitating about making his avatar specialize in illusion arts?!

…

Not long after, their trip to Senluo Path concluded.

For reasons unknown to the outside world, both the Myriad Beast Sect and Thousand Illusions Mountain suddenly sealed themselves off for internal restructuring.

And the mastermind behind all of this, Lin Jing’s group, had already returned to Xu Qingyao’s palace.

With the Myriad Beast Circle now in hand, they could begin the Ten-Billion Mosquito Swarm cultivation project.

The Demon Court would provide the blood needed to accelerate the mosquitoes’ reproduction, while Lin Jing, using the Myriad Beast Circle and his Beast Taming Physique, would refine their violent nature through training.

Mosquito swarms were already highly intelligent;
their aggression wasn’t the main issue—control was.

"Although I don’t have Yan Xiangbei’s Weapon Master Physique and can’t control high-grade magical artifacts at the Foundation Establishment level, with my Golden Core cultivation, multiple pet beasts as mana batteries, and the Three Heads, Six Arms technique, I can still manage one or two of them."

With his Three Heads, Six Arms and Dragon Carp fusion form, Lin Jing held a Myriad Beast Circle in each hand, with another hanging from his chest.

In this state, he felt that if he encountered the Nascent Soul-level Blazing Sun Tiger King again, he might not even need poison—he could simply use the Myriad Beast Sect’s Guardian Treasure to suppress it.

And once he gathered all ten billion mosquitoes, his strength would be even more terrifying.

However, at his current level of Three Heads, Six Arms, wielding the Seven-Star Beast God Circle for just a few seconds would drain almost all of his pet beasts’ mana—and that was with no demon beasts inside it. It seemed improving his mastery of Three Heads, Six Arms was a top priority.

…

In the blink of an eye, two years passed.

Well, two years for the outside world—over a decade for Lin Jing.

Thanks to the Hourglass of Time, he had cultivated for many more years.

Within the hourglass world, he dedicated himself to mastering the Three Heads, Six Arms technique. With Senior Sister’s help and the insights recorded in the Demon Court, he finally reached a decent level of mastery, allowing him to better control multiple high-grade Myriad Beast Circles.

Although he still couldn’t unleash the full power of the Seven-Star Beast God Circle, with the ever-growing mosquito swarm, simply keeping their aggression in check was no longer a problem.

"No wonder the Ten Terrors of Cloud Sea are so feared. Their reputation is well deserved…"

Over the past two years, the mosquito swarm’s reproduction speed exceeded all expectations. With no resource constraints or natural predators, and by constantly absorbing the blood of Demon Kings and Demon Emperors, their numbers finally reached the ten-billion mark.

Even in the Cloud Sea Mountain Range, such a mosquito swarm would be extremely rare.

"I’m already over sixty years old now…" Lin Jing sighed.

Although he had spent most of his time in seclusion, making him feel no real change in age, he still thought of himself as a man in his twenties.

With everything in place, Lin Jing and Xu Qingyao were ready to attempt their first Demonic Transformation Plan—

Using the ten-billion mosquito swarm to help a single mosquito beast undergo Tribulation of Transformation!

…

In a secluded valley within the Holy Maiden’s palace, inside a formation, dense mosquito swarms covered the mountains.

Lin Jing stood at the center, maintaining his Three Heads, Six Arms form, resembling a demon god. His three heads and six arms swayed as he manipulated the Beast God Circle to suppress their aggression.

Unfortunately, at his current level, merely keeping the ten-billion mosquitoes in check was his limit—he still couldn’t command them in battle. But for now, this was enough.

At the center of the valley, a carefully selected mosquito beast stood amidst the resonating bloodline of the swarm. Its body pulsed with blood energy, no longer limited to the Qi Refinement Stage.

As Lin Jing fed it Transformation Grass to attract tribulation, Lightning-Avoiding Pill to increase resistance, Gluttonous Pill to aid digestion, and Royal Blood Pill to temporarily enhance its bloodline and physical strength, thunder rumbled above.

"Will it succeed?" Xu Qingyao stood at the edge of the formation, tense.

Lightning flashed across the valley, making the mosquito swarm restless. If not for the Myriad Beast Circle’s suppression, they would have scattered long ago.

As for the mosquito beast undergoing transformation, it remained the center of Lin Jing and Xu Qingyao’s attention.

Compared to that previous failed mosquito beast, this one had a much stronger foundation. Even as its body was torn apart by the sea of lightning, it did not instantly turn to ash.

"Still not enough? Its base cultivation is too weak…"

However, as time passed, Lin Jing and Xu Qingyao’s hearts tensed—the mosquito beast’s aura grew weaker, its injuries worsened, and it seemed its body could no longer withstand the tribulation…

But in the next moment, their tense expressions turned to joy.

Just as the heavenly tribulation faded away, under the radiance of a crimson light, the heavily injured mosquito beast transformed into human form—

A Mosquito Woman with antennae and wings, a fiery figure, a bewitching face, and red-toned skin!

Her blood-red eyes blinked in confusion.

Once merely a bloodthirsty, mindless beast, she now possessed intelligence on par with humans.

As she gazed at the vast mosquito swarm surrounding her, her eyes finally landed on Lin Jing—the human who held overwhelming dominance over her bloodline.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 79: The Fourth Holy Maiden
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 79: The Fourth Holy Maiden

"Did it work?"

"Did it work?...""

Staring at the newly born Mosquito Woman in the center, Lin Jing and Senior Sister Xu Qingyao were both in a daze.

They had already prepared for the possibility of a failed transformation, but unexpectedly, the situation took a dramatic turn for the better.

"Hahahaha! Father, Mother, it worked!" Senior Sister's heartbeat quickened, her face flushed—not because the Mosquito Woman was unclothed, but because, after working so hard to revive the demon race, she had finally made a breakthrough!

Before long, even Mother Crane would be able to take human form.

She was about to approach the Mosquito Woman for a closer look when Lin Jing's expression suddenly changed. He reached out to stop her and said, "Wait. Something's not right."

At that moment, the newly awakened Mosquito Woman was recalling some unpleasant memories.

After evolving into a demon, she finally broke free from the bloodline constraints of the mosquito beasts, advancing her cultivation from the Qi Refinement stage—where mosquito beasts were forever stuck—to the Foundation Establishment stage.

At the same time, she had become something like a queen among mosquitoes, forming an even deeper bloodline connection with countless others of her kind. This sovereign-like connection allowed her to access many ancestral memories.

She saw a terrifying black hand descending from the sky, devouring mosquito flesh and blood...

And the master of that hand...

At that moment, due to Lin Jing being in a fusion state with the Dragon Carp—and given that the Dragon Carp had grown up feeding on mosquito beasts—the highly perceptive Mosquito Woman instantly sensed danger, triggering an intense stress response.

Buzzzzzzzzz...

In an instant, the ten-billion-strong mosquito horde broke free from Lin Jing’s control within the Myriad Beast Circle and began responding to the Mosquito Woman’s command.

Then, they turned toward Lin Jing with hostility.

"He... flesh... blood..." The Mosquito Woman struggled to speak as countless mosquitoes took flight, revealing their sharp mouthparts. Lin Jing instinctively took a step back.

"What the hell..." Senior Sister Xu Qingyao felt her scalp tingle as she watched the swarm rebel against Lin Jing’s control. This ten-billion-strong mosquito army—even she wouldn’t stand a chance against them! They would definitely need the intervention of demon emperors and other powerful protectors.

"I'm innocent here!" Lin Jing released his Three Heads, Six Arms fusion form. Was it just because he had been feeding mosquito beasts to the fish all this time??

He guessed that was likely the case, along with the fact that... his flesh and blood held an extreme allure for the Mosquito Woman.

Now that she had evolved into a true demon, her ability to access bloodline memories and sense blood had reached an unprecedented level.

"Call for backup, stop them!" Lin Jing shouted.

Just as the Mosquito Woman prepared to lead her swarm into action, and just as Xu Qingyao and Lin Jing were about to summon reinforcements—

Suddenly, the entire valley seemed to freeze in time.

The previously restless and aggressive mosquito swarm instantly became motionless, hovering in place as if in a trance.

A pair of deep blue demon eyes slowly opened in the void, gazing down upon the scene.

In an instant, cold sweat drenched the Mosquito Woman's back. Her intelligence was still only at the level of a human child, and most of her actions were driven by bloodline instincts.

If her bloodline instincts had urged her to fear and resist Lin Jing, then before these eyes, all she felt was submission and obedience.

"His Majesty the Demon Emperor!!!" Xu Qingyao gasped, recognizing the owner of those eyes. The Demon Emperor had emerged from seclusion!

And she had appeared at the very moment a new demon had been born.

"The Demon Emperor???" Lin Jing's heart trembled as he looked toward those eyes.

Sure enough, the next moment, a majestic female voice resonated from the void.

"Xu Qingyao, you have done well."

"You overpraise me... It was all..."

"The main credit goes to this Longevity Body cultivator from the Beast Taming Sect, correct..." The voice echoed once again.

"A Longevity Body, contracted with two rare beasts, and the Ice Soul Gu... Indeed. As a fellow inheritor of immortal arts, I should have realized earlier—only immortal arts can break the Heavenly Dao rules set by the ancient Immortal Palace."

"However, rare beasts are few and far between, and not all immortal arts can break these rules. It seems their inheritance must contain an extraordinary divine ability. The one who originally possessed it... must have been at least of the 'Immortal Lord' level."

The Demon Emperor had seen through all of Lin Jing's background in one glance. This didn’t surprise Lin Jing in the slightest. After all, she was one of the strongest beings in the lower realm. However... her referring to herself as an inheritor of immortal arts—that caught him off guard.

He understood what that meant.

Rare beast divine abilities were also called "immortal arts."

So, the Demon Emperor also possessed rare beast bloodline...

It was said that the Demon Emperor came from the Divine Tiger Clan, an ancient demon lineage.

The Divine Tigers were a major demon clan, second in power only to the Dragons, Phoenixes, and Qilins. As a core battle clan of the demon race, they had suffered the most severe losses in the Immortal Palace War.

Other demon clans had only been cursed to never take human form. But the Divine Tigers had it worse—their bloodline itself had nearly died out, reducing a mighty clan to a single surviving lineage.

The current Demon Emperor was the sole remaining bloodline of the Divine Tiger Clan.

Dragons, Phoenixes, and Qilins still had their respective lineages, yet the last Divine Tiger had become the ruler of the Demon Court. There had to be deeper reasons behind this.

The power of rare beast bloodlines was truly terrifying.

"Your Majesty..." Lin Jing gave a slight bow. He wasn’t too worried about his identity being exposed anymore—he had already demonstrated his value.

And that value was his greatest safeguard.

"Would you be willing to give me a sample of the item used to aid her transformation?" the Demon Emperor asked.

"Of course." Lin Jing immediately took out a synthesized Transformation Grass. In the next instant, the Transformation Grass vanished into the void.

The Demon Emperor continued, "The restrictions on this Transformation Grass have been partially lifted, but as a result, its transformation power has weakened significantly. It can now only affect demon beasts below the Demon Core stage and will trigger the Heavenly Dao's punishment... That must be why you chose the mosquito beast as your transformation subject."

"It seems that reviving the demon race is only just beginning."

"Yes, Your Majesty. But give me some time, and I will definitely develop a transformation pill that works for demons of all levels," Lin Jing promised, realizing this was his best opportunity to tie himself to the Demon Court.

"Hmm. From this day forward, I name you the Holy Son of the Demon Court—the one and only Holy Son. Your status will be above the Three Holy Maidens. From now on, they will all follow your command."

The Demon Emperor’s decree left Senior Sister momentarily stunned.

"No. Now, there are Four Holy Maidens. Though her intelligence is newly formed, she is the first demon to be born in this era—a truly significant event."

A divine light shot down from the void, enveloping the Mosquito Woman. Her bloodline strength instantly surged to the royal level.

"The Holy Son is not your enemy. On the contrary, he is your benefactor, your 'creator.' From now on, disobeying the Holy Son is unacceptable. Stay by his side as his attendant."

"Mm... mm..." The Mosquito Woman nodded repeatedly. At that moment, her initial hostility completely dissipated, her instincts suppressed. She looked respectfully at the Demon Emperor and then at the newly appointed Holy Son, Lin Jing.

Seeing that even a mosquito had been granted the status of a Holy Maiden, Xu Qingyao temporarily forgot about her own aspirations of becoming the Demon Emperor’s personal disciple.

"As for the Third Holy Maiden, Xu Qingyao—I will take you as my personal disciple once I fully emerge from seclusion."

"But for now, keep all of this quiet. Once I have fully returned, we shall discuss it further. Holy Son, for finding the path to transformation, do you desire any rewards?"

"I will grant you three wishes, unrelated to your research on transformation. But know this—once you make your requests, from this day forward, you can never betray the Demon Court."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 80: Three Wishes
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 80: Three Wishes

"Your Majesty..." Lin Jing's heart trembled slightly. Three wishes? Then he wouldn’t hold back.

"Your Majesty, as someone with a Longevity Body, I hope to receive your protection and grow smoothly."

In truth, whether he mentioned it or not, Lin Jing understood that with his current value, the Demon Court would inevitably protect him.

However, he still chose to use up a wish to secure a direct promise from the Demon Emperor.

"As the Holy Son of the Demon Court, I will naturally ensure your growth. Additionally, I will assist you once more—eliminating potential enemies and shaking the world."

"Have you ever heard of the Alchemy Conference?"

"The Tongtian Tower Alchemy Conference?" Lin Jing raised an eyebrow. "Of course, I've heard of it."

"It is the greatest event in Qingzhou. Every power in Qingzhou, and even those beyond, pay attention to it."

"At that moment, many forces will take interest in you. I will take action then—eliminating any enemy that comes for you. All battle spoils from this event will belong to you."

"Finally, during the conference, you will publicly declare to all demon beast races that you have discovered the method of transformation."

"From that day forward, you will no longer need to walk the cultivation world in disguise. As the Holy Son of my Demon Court, you will walk in the open—no one will be able to ignore your existence."

"This..." Lin Jing was moved. It seemed that the Demon Emperor’s ambitions were not small. With the revelation of the demon race’s path to revival, she clearly intended to expand the Demon Court’s influence further, attracting more demon beast races into its fold.

Allowing him to showcase the transformation method at the Alchemy Conference served two purposes:

First, it was equivalent to announcing to the world that the demon race had begun its resurgence. The Alchemy Conference as a stage was far more convincing than the Demon Court making the proclamation itself.

Second, the emergence of his Longevity Body would surely attract many powerful figures. At that time, the Demon Emperor could use this as an opportunity to display the strength of the Demon Court—perhaps even making a move against demonic sects like the Yin Corpse Sect.

"Thank you, Your Majesty," Lin Jing said. "However... I originally planned to attend the Alchemy Conference to obtain the Tongtian Tower’s collection—specifically, the exotic flame known as Candle Age Fire. If I participate using the Transformation Pill, it might not be so easy to get the Candle Age Fire anymore..."

"So my second wish is to obtain this time-attribute top-tier exotic flame, Candle Age Fire."

Although he now had the Demon Court’s protection, Lin Jing understood that his own strength was his greatest safeguard.

The Heavenly Demon Physique—he had to master it.

He didn’t have the same natural talent as Gu Qianqiu, so if he wanted to achieve the Heavenly Demon Physique, he absolutely needed Candle Age Fire to assist him.

Even though he wasn’t entirely confident that obtaining Candle Age Fire would guarantee his success in cultivating the Heavenly Demon Physique, he couldn’t afford to miss this opportunity.

"Granted." The Demon Emperor agreed without hesitation.

"As for my third wish, may I keep it in reserve and use it later?"

Initially, Lin Jing had considered asking the Demon Emperor to help him seize a Rare Gu from the Gu God Sect. However, since she had already promised to assist him once—sweeping away strong enemies from the Yin Corpse Sect and the Gu God Sect, with all the spoils belonging to him—he decided to hold off on that request.

If he could lure out someone within the Gu God Sect who possessed a Rare Gu, he might still be able to obtain it through battle, making it unnecessary to waste a wish.

At present, Lin Jing had no particularly urgent requests. If he could save this wish for a critical moment in the future, its value might be even greater.

"As you wish." The Demon Emperor did not refuse. Then, after taking the synthesized Transformation Grass from Lin Jing, she disappeared from the area.

—

Meanwhile, within the Demon Court’s domain, in a pure white space, a snow-white Divine Tiger silently poked at a large blade of grass behind her.

"So, I can transform just by using this grass..." She picked it up and licked it.

"This grass is too high-grade. If only there was a way to further enhance spiritual plants... Then I wouldn’t have to rely on that Longevity Body brat’s research—I could transform first myself."

"I need to find another way..."

"The demon race is about to be revived. My ancestors, I am so close to succeeding! Stop appearing in my dreams—I’m already at the Demon Emperor stage, I really don’t want to dream anymore. I will restore the demon race, I swear..."

—

"Holy Son!"

"Demon Emperor’s disciple!"

"Holy Son!"

"Demon Emperor’s disciple!"

At this moment, having discovered the method of transformation and gained the favor of the Demon Emperor, Lin Jing and Xu Qingyao were still immersed in boundless joy. Holding hands, they celebrated the changes in their status.

"Junior Brother, I always knew you could do it!"

"It’s all thanks to Senior Sister’s unconditional trust and support that we’ve made this progress."

"But we can't celebrate too soon. The current transformation method is still too limited—we must quickly develop a universal method." Xu Qingyao exhaled deeply.

"Speaking of which, the Alchemy Conference is in about two years, right? Though His Majesty the Demon Emperor didn’t specify which session we should reveal the transformation method at, I hope it’s sooner rather than later." Lin Jing was already eager for the Demon Emperor to take action and help him wipe out the two major demonic sects.

Oh, and while she was at it, maybe she could deal with the Sky Lion Clan too—he had already offended them anyway.

"Holy Son..." At this moment, the Mosquito Woman—now officially the Fourth Holy Maiden of the Demon Court—flew over to Lin Jing and Xu Qingyao.

"As per His Majesty’s decree, from now on, I am Holy Son’s attendant. Please provide your orders, Holy Son." The Mosquito Woman led her mosquito army and knelt before Lin Jing.

Lin Jing smiled slightly and said, "For now, you and your swarm will settle in this valley and focus on recovery. The Third Holy Maiden will provide you with food and find suitable cultivation techniques for you. From today onward, your duty is to lead the mosquito army and suppress their violent nature."

"As you command." The Mosquito Woman nodded obediently.

This army was powerful enough to make even Nascent Soul cultivators wary. At present, even with the Seven-Star Beast God Circle, Lin Jing couldn’t completely control them. However, the Mosquito Woman could. The demon race’s innate advantages were undeniable.

Fortunately, she was now his attendant.

After renaming the valley Mosquito Valley, Lin Jing and Xu Qingyao sealed the area with a barrier.

On the way back, Xu Qingyao asked, "So, you're going to start preparing for the Alchemy Conference as per the Demon Emperor’s instructions, right? Now that we have her permission, I can freely access the Demon Court’s treasury. If you need anything, just let me know."

"I’m not sure what I need yet. I’ll let you know later. But... does the Demon Court have any ice-elemental heavenly treasures, corpses of demon beasts with royal-tier bloodlines or higher, or powerful human cultivators who specialized in ice techniques or had ice-attribute physiques? Preferably ones I can use however I want."

"Hmm?" Xu Qingyao tilted her head. "You planning to use them to cultivate that Ice Gu?"

"Yeah, among my three contracted beasts, it has the lowest cultivation. It’s about time to strengthen it."

"Once it reaches Core Formation, you should be able to break through to Core Formation yourself. Have you thought about your fourth contract beast? That Mosquito Woman...?"

Lin Jing was momentarily stunned before shaking his head. "Not suitable. My other contracted beasts have eaten way too many mosquitoes."

"But Senior Sister, you did remind me of something. One of my candidates for the fourth contract beast should be born soon. I don’t know if I can form a contract with it yet... but I’ll need your help finding some materials."

He handed over a list of rare resources required for the Longevity Treasure Turtle—one of the candidates for his fourth contract beast.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 81: The Method of Thunder Refining
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 81: The Method of Thunder Refining

"So many space-attribute materials..." Xu Qingyao browsed through Lin Jing's list.

"Are you planning to cultivate a space-type contract beast?" She put away the list, her gaze falling on Lin Jing. It was understandable—at his level, only a space-attribute beast would be worthy of him.

As for the Mosquito Woman, though she was the first demon race member of this era and could command the mosquito swarm, the ability to mass-produce such beings in the future meant she wouldn’t remain rare. More demon prodigies would emerge in time. But a space-attribute creature was different.

"Yeah, I obtained a blueprint for an intelligent space turtle from Mystic Mechanized Manor in the Ancient Celestial Kingdom. These are the materials needed to build it. But I’m not sure if the Beast Taming Art can form a contract with a mechanized lifeform… I’ll have to try." Lin Jing said.

So, another mass-producible entity…

Xu Qingyao shook her head and said, "I don’t think it will work. Don’t get your hopes up too much. When I arrived in Qingzhou, I tested it myself—Beast Taming Art can only contract demon beasts. It doesn’t work on plant life, let alone mechanized beasts or artifact spirits."

"After all, the Beast Taming Art was originally a cultivation method used by servants in the ancient Immortal Palace to raise demon beasts. Even though our predecessors in the Beast Taming Sect have refined it over time, it hasn’t strayed too far from the original version."

"Its limits probably stop at creatures like the Ice Soul Gu. The Ten Rare Gu have evolved into a unique form of life, but they were originally just insects, which technically makes them demon beasts. However, mechanized creatures have no connection to demon beasts at all."

"After all, you've already developed a transformation method, entered the Bodhi Cavern Heaven, and cultivated the Fusion Dragon Secret Art. Improving a technique shouldn’t be a challenge for you."

"Who knows? Maybe one day, you’ll be able to contract spiritual plants, demon beasts, artifact spirits, corpse beasts, mechanized beasts, and even spell spirits!"

Lin Jing chuckled. "I’ll take that as a blessing from Senior Sister."

The reason he hadn’t fully decided on the mechanized turtle as his next Golden Core contract beast was that he wasn’t sure he could actually contract it. As Xu Qingyao pointed out, it wasn’t a true demon beast.

But even if he couldn’t, it didn’t matter. His desire to contract a mechanized beast wasn’t because of any significant advantage—it was simply because he had an affinity with Mechanized Arts. The mechanized turtle had accompanied him for a long time, and if he could form a contract, it would be an interesting experiment rather than a purely strategic move.

As for modifying the Beast Taming Art, that was indeed an option.

Many cultivators initially practiced lower-tier cultivation methods. But when they reached a higher level, instead of abandoning their foundation and starting over, they chose a different path—modifying their techniques.

Only a handful of individuals could accomplish this, as it required exceptional talent and understanding.

—

After returning to his cave dwelling, Lin Jing began preparations for the Alchemy Conference two years later. Since it was a pill-refining competition, he couldn’t just use Transformation Grass directly.

He had to refine the Transformation Grass into Transformation Pills to make its usage easier!

Over the past period, Lin Jing had already developed some ideas on how to modify the Transformation Grass.

If he wanted it to work on higher-level demon beasts, he had to enhance its quality. There were two possible approaches:

External Enhancement – Transplanting the Mutated Transformation Grass into a special blessed land to allow it to grow again and reach a higher quality.

Alchemy Refinement – Using traditional pill-refining techniques to extract the essence of multiple Mutated Transformation Grass samples, concentrating their effects into a single refined form to enhance their power.

Thunder refining was definitely the better choice.

Currently, under the constraints of the Heavenly Dao, the production of special Transformation Grass was extremely limited. Even if the Demon Court assigned a Demon Emperor to oversee its cultivation, the output would still be low. Relying solely on Senior Sister to supply it might not be enough—he might have to take a share from the other two Holy Sons.

"But improving the grade of the Transformation Pills can wait. In the Alchemy Conference, as long as the pill demonstrates the ability to induce transformation, that will be enough. The level of demon beast it can transform isn’t the immediate concern."

"The real issue is… how can I ensure that higher-level demon beasts survive the tribulation lightning when transforming? Or better yet, how can I negate the Heavenly Tribulation? Only then can the demonstration proceed smoothly…"

"Is there a method that could work?"

As Lin Jing utilized his Hidden Dragon Fortune to simulate possibilities, a new idea emerged.

The method combined his current conditions with remnants of knowledge from his past life.

In his previous life, he had read countless web novels and watched numerous fantasy films. Many works depicted legendary pills so defiant of the heavens that their creation attracted pill tribulations. Only after surviving the tribulation would the pill be considered complete.

But on the Tianyuan Continent, such a phenomenon had never been recorded. There were no known pills that had ever triggered tribulation lightning.

Thus, Lin Jing's final deduction was—Thunder Refining Alchemy.

By employing a special alchemical technique, he could deliberately summon tribulation lightning upon the pill itself.

This would allow the pill to absorb some of the tribulation’s power, transferring part of the burden away from the user.

Through this Heavenly Tribulation Tempering, the pill’s side effects could be reduced.

For example, the biggest drawback of using Transformation Grass was that it triggered a transformation tribulation. But if he used the Thunder Refining Method to refine the Transformation Grass into Transformation Pills—forcing the pills to endure a portion of the tribulation lightning—the burden on the user might be alleviated.

The path to transformation was already a defiance of the heavens. Both creatures and objects were challenging the natural order. There was no reason why only demon beasts should bear the full cost.

However, there was a catch.

If the Transformation Pills failed to withstand the lightning tribulation, they would be destroyed, wasting valuable materials.

By transferring the risk onto the pills, he could ease the difficulty of transformation. Whether this would weaken the effectiveness of transformation was still unknown.

"Luckily, I acquired Heavenly Thunder Flame from the Qilin Clan—a fire born from tribulation lightning. The Dragon Carp has already refined it completely. Perhaps, using Heavenly Thunder Flame, I can draw down weak tribulation lightning to temper the Transformation Pills."

The Thunder Refining Method was Lin Jing’s experimental approach to reducing the side effects of Transformation Grass.

This time, he was taking an unconventional route—not by adding balancing ingredients or altering its medicinal properties, but by refining it through alchemy techniques.

For Lin Jing and the Dragon Carp, this was a major challenge. After all, they only had two years to perfect this technique.

No—eleven years.

Because they had the Hourglass of Time—they could spend one year inside it, equivalent to ten years in real time.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 82: A New Vessel
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 82: A New Vessel

For a while, Lin Jing and the Dragon Carp focused on researching the Thunder Refining Method.

Meanwhile, the Pine Leaf Mouse had to work overtime for the Transformation Pill experiments, requiring a large supply of Mutated Transformation Grass.

After some time, Xu Qingyao returned with a gift for Lin Jing.

Bang!

A massive crab carcass was thrown to the ground by Senior Sister.

"This is an Ice Emperor Crab, at the peak of the Demon King realm. It was the incarnation of a genius from Poseidon Palace, possessing an ice-element emperor-tier bloodline. During its challenge against a Kirin Clan genius on land, it was slain. Due to its powerful ice bloodline and the fact that the Kirin genius hasn't decided how to deal with it yet, the body has been well-preserved."

"I heard about it and specifically requested it. Didn't you need an ice-element vessel to raise your Ice Soul Gu? What do you think?"

Hearing this, Lin Jing's gaze landed on the crab. He immediately sensed the astonishing cold energy radiating from it. It seemed his Ice Soul Gu was in for a real treat—its combat ability was even likely to undergo a transformation.

First, his palm frosted over. Then, a crystalline tiny spider emerged from his palm, awakening.

"I want to cultivate... I want to form my Core... I don’t want to sleep anymore..."

The Ice Soul Gu suddenly jolted awake, shouting loudly, as if it had just woken from countless nightmares of being beaten up by Squirrel Carp.

"Hmm, you won’t have to sleep anymore. Just focus on cultivating. Feel free to leak your aura—it’d be best if you attract all the experts from the Gu God Sect to Qingzhou." Lin Jing smiled.

Bring them all in—let His Majesty the Demon Emperor take them down!

And then, all those Rare Gu as spoils of war... would be his!

"???" The Ice Soul Gu was dumbfounded. Are you crazy, or am I still half-asleep? What the h*ll happened while I was sleeping?

Beside them, Xu Qingyao examined the Ice Soul Gu. "So this is your Rare Gu? You were planning to let it attempt demonization? Given its foundation, it's possible. And even if it gets struck down, it seems like it has two ways to revive..."

"By the way, is this Ice Soul Gu male or female?" Senior Sister asked curiously.

Most living beings had genders, but some demonic beasts were unique—take Mosquito Beasts, for example.

Mosquito Beasts were an all-female species. They didn’t reproduce through mating but rather by absorbing blood, meaning male mosquitoes weren’t needed at all.

So Xu Qingyao was quite curious about this Ice Soul Gu. After all, the Ten Rare Gu were one-of-a-kind species and didn’t seem to require mating to reproduce.

"Rare Gu have no gender," Lin Jing explained.

"But since it has historically followed the Ice and Fire Holy Maiden of the Gu God Sect for generations, it has likely been subtly influenced and leans more toward a female identity."

"...?!"

The Ice Soul Gu suddenly turned to Xu Qingyao, its eyes dazed. "You smell delicious..."

"Huh??" Xu Qingyao blinked, sniffed her own arm. "I don’t use perfume. Maybe it’s because I had roasted meat before coming here?"

"Ignore it. What it means is that you probably taste delicious," Lin Jing firmly gripped the Ice Soul Gu in his hand. "How many times have I told you—stop telling everyone they smell good!"

To be fair, Senior Sister was a half-demon, so she probably did smell nice. And to a Gu worm, she’d make an excellent host.

The Ice Soul Gu struggled, suffering immensely in Lin Jing’s grasp.

As a gluttonous Gu worm, it had only ever eaten ice-element spirit plants and fruits since birth, absorbing natural cold energy. Not once had it been able to feast on the kind of delicacies it actually craved.

But now, its life was finally improving.

"This is what I prepared for you." Lin Jing tossed it onto the Ice Emperor Crab.

The Ice Soul Gu, still drowsy, suddenly realized there was something incredibly delicious nearby.

It immediately burrowed into the Ice Emperor Crab’s corpse, and within moments, the massive, half-human-sized crab seemed to come back to life, emanating an overwhelming chilling aura.

"How is it? Is the parasitism working well?" Lin Jing asked.

"Mm-hmm." The Ice Soul Gu responded happily. Not only was the Ice Emperor Crab extremely powerful, but it was also entirely ice-element, and—most importantly—its body was very similar to the Ice Soul Gu’s own.

"That’s good. For now, just parasitize its corpse and absorb its nutrients. No need to return to my body," Lin Jing instructed.

Glancing at it, he estimated that in this parasitized state, the Ice Soul Gu’s combat ability had already reached the Demon Core Stage. However, as it gradually drained the Ice Emperor Crab’s energy, its ability to control the crab would weaken over time. On the other hand, the Ice Soul Gu’s own strength would slowly grow.

"A spider and a crab... actually a pretty good match." Senior Sister nodded, but she quietly made a mental note to hold a grudge. This damn spider actually wanted to take a bite out of her...

Still, considering Lin Jing’s sake, she let it slide—for now.

Meanwhile...

At the Tongtian Path, atop the Tongtian Tower.

Inside a special pocket dimension at the tower’s peak, an ordinary-looking old man in alchemist robes was in secluded cultivation.

But the next moment, after who knows how many years, the old man suddenly opened his eyes, looking in a particular direction.

"Lord of the Demon Court, what are you doing here?"

His gaze met two giant eyes forming in the sky. A moment later, a sentient Celestial Pill with an eye appeared beside the old man, rippling with spatial energy.

"Old man, he’s obviously here to steal me! I’ll power you up—hurry and drive him away!" the Celestial Pill urged.

"Relax, Celestial Pill Spirit, no need to panic," a voice emanated from the Demon Emperor’s eyes.

But the Celestial Pill Spirit remained on high alert.

Even though the Tongtian Tower Master and the Demon Court Lord were both at the Dao-Seeking Stage, there were levels within that realm.

If it were those four Demon Beasts from the Snow Plains, who had artificially ascended to Demon Emperor using special resources, the Second-Generation Ancient Emperor wouldn’t hesitate to fight them all at once.

But the Demon Court Lord was different.

He was a true combat-type rare beast, and among all Demon Emperors, he ranked in the top three in raw battle power.

Without the Celestial Pill Spirit’s enhancement, the Tongtian Tower Master would stand no chance.

Even with its help, victory was uncertain—unless the Celestial Pill sacrificed itself completely, merging into the Tower Master’s power.

"No need to be nervous." The Tongtian Tower Master spoke slowly, reassuring the Celestial Pill Spirit.

With Celestial Pill Might, the Demon Court Lord wouldn’t dare act recklessly.

"Tongtian Tower Master, I want the Candle Age Flame you’ve been hoarding. Name your price," the Demon Emperor stated.

The Tower Master remained silent for a moment.

So, it’s after the Candle Age Flame...

Slowly, he spoke. "One drop of False Immortal Blood."

During the Ancient Celestial Palace War, the Demon Race fought the Celestial Palace, slaying many celestial beasts and immortals from the upper realm. Naturally, they had collected immortal blood.

However, due to the lower realm’s restrictions, these so-called True Immortals had their power suppressed, reducing them to False Immortals.

"A mere fire seed for False Immortal Blood?" The Demon Emperor scoffed.

The Celestial Pill Spirit retorted, "Then why are you even here trading for it?!"

"Fine, go get the flame," the Demon Emperor relented, not bothering to argue.

After all, he had already found a method to evolve the Mutated Transformation Grass.

Once he fully transformed, the inherited Demon Arts, spells, and treasures exclusive to the Demon Court would further amplify his power.

By then, he wouldn’t just be the Lord of the Demon Court.

He would rule all of Qingzhou.

Even with the Celestial Pill’s support, the Tongtian Tower Master would be no match for him.

The fate of Qingzhou would be decided by the Demon Court.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 83: The Longevity Treasure Turtle
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 83: The Longevity Treasure Turtle

"Better not."

After some thought, Lin Jing decided not to hastily insert the black iron fragment into the Soul Core to activate the Longevity Treasure Turtle's intelligence.

Right now, he hadn’t yet reached the Core Formation Stage and had no available contract slots. Activating it now would be pointless…

Moreover, this Longevity Treasure Turtle was built entirely based on the blueprints given by Fairy Mo. Lin Jing still didn’t fully understand the true nature of the black iron fragment, so creating an autonomous sentient entity and keeping it by his side felt too risky. Caution was necessary. He decided to study more Mechanized Arts before making any decisions.

There were forces in Qingzhou that specialized in Mechanized Arts. While they might not compare to Shenji Manor, they weren’t far behind. Given his current status as the Holy Son of the Demon Court, he could easily make contact with them and even pursue advanced studies—something that would help in upgrading the Treasure Turtle in the future.

"For now, apart from lacking intelligence and Spatial Concealment Abilities, it already possesses all other capabilities of a Longevity Treasure Turtle. This should be enough for now."

Lin Jing summoned his Spell Spirit Origin: Mushroom, and gently placed it onto the palm-sized turtle before him.

The Longevity Treasure Turtle looked plain and ordinary. Its earth-brown shell made it resemble a common land tortoise, simple and unremarkable.

After applying a long-lasting illusion spell on it, Lin Jing issued its first command.

"Alright. Go on a journey."

He wasn’t worried about losing the Longevity Treasure Turtle or it failing to return. Since he had refined its Soul Core, he could sense its presence within a certain range.

And even if it traveled beyond that range to search for treasures, Lin Jing had placed a Wood Clone Seed—condensed by the Pine Leaf Mouse—inside the turtle. Through this, he could always track it down.

Since he wasn’t rushing to contract it, Lin Jing decided to observe its abilities first.

As long as there were no glitches, that was enough.

"Command received... initiating startup..."

"Preparing for treasure-seeking expedition..."

This was the recorded formation voice within the Mechanized Turtle, a pre-set mechanical voice designed to make the mechanized beast seem more lifelike.

If Lin Jing found it annoying, he could erase it. But he had left it in for fun, following the blueprints’ design. However, since the recorded voice library was quite complicated, he had no idea what all the preset messages were.

"Go on, then."

Lin Jing nodded.

The Mechanized Bamboo Turtle turned around and started moving.

"Setting off now! Wishing my master eternal life! Please, live long enough to see me return…"

Lin Jing: ???

…

There was nothing wrong with this blessing, but why did it sound more like a curse?!

Never mind, as long as there are no glitches, it’s fine. At least it left successfully to begin its treasure hunt.

A Moment Later…

The Longevity Treasure Turtle infiltrated Gu Qianqiu’s Cave Dwelling.

It blankly stared at the Longevity Body, unsure how to absorb this life-extending treasure into itself.

"…Why do I feel like I’m being watched?"

At the same time, Gu Qianqiu, who was in the middle of cultivation, suddenly opened his eyes.

He saw nothing—yet he couldn't shake the eerie feeling that someone’s eyes were locked onto him...

"Sh*t. I knew there would be a glitch."

Inside his cave, Lin Jing sensed the Longevity Treasure Turtle’s actions and rubbed his forehead.

Because he had refined the Soul Core, this little guy didn’t recognize Gu Qianqiu as a longevity resource—instead, it saw Lin Jing as its master.

But in the end...

It still ended up fixating on Gu Qianqiu.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 84: Pill Completion
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 84: Pill Completion

Although Lin Jing really wanted to modify the Longevity Treasure Turtle, the Dragon Carp had seemingly succeeded in refining the Candle Age Fire. That meant he had no time to play with the mechanized turtle for now.

Preparing for the Alchemy Conference was the priority!

Manually driving the Longevity Treasure Turtle out of the camp, he sent it off to wander the sixteen paths of Qingzhou freely. After that, he stopped paying attention to it.

As for what the turtle might bring back in the end, Lin Jing didn’t have high expectations—he just hoped it wouldn’t self-destruct.

Although his illusions could ensure it wouldn’t be discovered by powerful beings, if it decided to jump into a poisonous swamp or a volcano, there wasn’t much he could do about it.

Time flew by.

A year and a half later, in the Tianyuan Continent’s time.

Lin Jing, still holding the title of Holy Son, was living in the Third Holy Maiden’s palace.

"After all these years, it's finally almost done."

Although only a year and a half had passed in the outside world, for Lin Jing and the Dragon Carp, it had felt like several years. They had spent every day researching the Thunder Refining Method, to the point where their minds were nearly numb.
