Under the direction of the Three Great Pill Sovereigns, order at the Alchemy Conference was quickly restored.

Everyone’s attention returned to the conference itself.

"The Sun and Moon Pill Sovereign—she’s the only one among the Twelve Pill Sovereigns of the Tongtian Tower who specializes in the Dao of Space-Time. It’s said that her pills, after being tempered by time and the sun and moon’s cycle, have their effects multiplied several times."

"That’s how she refined her Seventh-Tier Pills!"

"I heard her newly accepted disciple, Pill Sovereign Jie Geng, is also competing this time." The crowd turned to a female alchemist at the refining zone. Her gaze, however, remained fixed on Lin Jing, deep in thought—wondering if he was the Fire Dragon Alchemist she had gifted the token to…

"The Divine Cauldron Pill Sovereign—rumor has it that three of the world’s top ten pill cauldrons are in his hands. He regularly uses nine different cauldrons, each with its own artifact spirit. He’s not only a pill master, but also a Sixth-Tier Artifact Refiner who specializes in cauldron forging!"

"And the Creation Pill Sovereign—strongest in combat among the Twelve. He harnesses the power of nature in his alchemy, storing elements of the world in his pills."

With three of the highest-ranking figures in the alchemy world watching, the participating alchemists were visibly tense.

"In Qingzhou, we’ve always prided ourselves on leading the way in the Dao of Alchemy."

"The path of cultivation begins with pill refinement."

"In the ancient realms, the very first sentient beings began their cultivation journey by refining external pills—elixirs that unlocked the path to power."

"Later, those with exceptional talent developed the method of cultivating internal pills. Thus, the path of Core Formation was born."

"No matter the race, those who pursue cultivation refine their internal core. Qi Refinement, Foundation Establishment, Core Formation—be it Golden Core or Monster Core, all life’s cultivation stems from this root."

"Standing on the shoulders of our ancestors, blessed with the finest resources and environment, it is both our duty and legacy to explore the Dao of Alchemy. Today, Qingzhou’s grand alchemical gathering begins. May all of you showcase the pills you’ve poured your hearts and talents into, and may Qingzhou’s alchemical realm forever thrive in vibrant growth… Tongtian Tower hereby declares—the Alchemy Conference is officially open!"

"All alchemists—begin pill refinement immediately!"

As soon as the Sun and Moon Pill Sovereign finished speaking, over a hundred alchemists jumped into action. Each of them was a famed genius in the alchemy world, alchemy masters in their own regions—but now gathered together, they seemed oddly inconspicuous.

That’s because this conference didn’t care about one’s alchemy rank or cultivation level. What it truly tested was creativity, intellect, and innovation.

An alchemist might be able to refine high-tier pills with a great success rate—but if the formula was created by someone else, it held no value at this conference.

And so, the audience was treated to a jaw-dropping sight.

One alchemist after another employed self-created refining methods. Over half of them used pill recipes and spiritual plants the crowd had never seen before.

No one knew what any of them were trying to refine—and that uncertainty was what stirred the hearts of Qingzhou’s cultivators.

Right now… within Tongtian City, at least ninety percent of all attention was focused on Lin Jing.

After all, there were rumors saying that in this conference, Lin Jing might refine a Longevity Pill—using his own flesh and blood as the core ingredient.

"Is that for real…?"

While everyone held their breath, each alchemist began at their own pace. Some used the basic pill platforms provided by Tongtian Tower, while others summoned their own personal cauldrons.

But Lin Jing didn’t summon a cauldron at all. Before the conference began, instead of calling out a cauldron or furnace—he summoned a pet beast.

Zhu Yan!

Pop! Under countless watching eyes, Lin Jing summoned his Dragonblood Carp, now at the Early Core Formation Stage.

"A carp? Looks like it’s fire-elemental? That’s rare..."

The Dragonblood Carp’s appearance didn’t cause much of a stir. After all, other beast tamers also had pet beasts assisting with their alchemy. In the alchemy world, that wasn’t unusual—especially since this year’s competition even included demonic beasts and spirit creatures.

However, Lin Jing’s approach seemed different. Most human alchemists’ pet beasts merely helped by breathing beast fire—the alchemist still remained the main actor.

But Lin Jing? He summoned the alchemy beast, summoned a pile of alchemy materials—then casually stepped aside, pulled out the Heavenly Demon Scroll, and started reading.

He was letting the Dragonblood Carp handle the entire pill refinement process on its own.

"Begin refining," he instructed. After that, his full attention was on the scroll.

Every time he spotted someone who wanted him dead, he smiled.

This Transformation Pill required a Thunder Refining Method and the use of Heavenly Thunder Flame, so it had to be the Dragonblood Carp doing it.

Sure, Lin Jing could fuse with the carp and simulate Heavenly Thunder Flame using illusion techniques—but that was way too much hassle. And he hated hassle.

Besides, this wasn’t cheating. As a Beast Taming Cultivator, his beast was a part of himself. Even if he didn’t personally participate in the process, the resulting pill should still be credited to him.

"He’s seriously letting his pet beast refine the pill?"

The crowd was stunned. And the materials Lin Jing had laid out? Also strange.

Most of them were spiritual plants no one recognized.

There was only one explanation—they were hybrid breeds Lin Jing had cultivated himself, not common pill materials.

Then again, most of the alchemists here had similar custom-grown ingredients.

That was exactly why no one could guess what pill Lin Jing was making. Even the main ingredient—Transformation Grass—was no longer its original color. And since Transformation Grass had been extinct for years and only the Demon Court had the know-how to grow it, most had long forgotten its existence.

"There’s a lot of thunder-element spiritual plants in there… could he be making a thunder-type pill? Doesn’t seem like a Longevity Pill, though..." The audience fell silent—then suddenly, their expressions changed drastically.

“!!!”

All at once, the Dragonblood Carp’s body expanded rapidly. It swallowed every single ingredient from the pill platform in one gulp—then its body flared with fiery red-gold flames laced with lightning!

As that flame spread, the entire arena’s temperature soared. Among the hundred-plus alchemists, anyone using low-grade flames saw their fire instantly extinguished, flickering like candlelight in reverence before a flame emperor.

Even those refining with top-tier Exotic Flames struggled—their flames began flickering uncontrollably, some even slipping out of their control.

"What’s happening?!"

Too many alchemists were stunned, turning to look at the Dragonblood Carp.

"A top-tier exotic flame—Heavenly Thunder Flame? This carp actually refined such a dominant flame at the Core Formation Stage? But even that shouldn’t be enough to suppress this many exotic flames!"

The carp hadn’t even started refining yet. It was just channeling its entire arsenal of exotic flames—using the Flame Emperor Divine Ability—to fuse them into a new, enhanced version of the Heavenly Thunder Flame.

The power of this upgraded flame was now so overwhelming that it disrupted the very existence of other exotic flames in the arena.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 95: Ingested Pill
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3. Chapter 95: Ingested Pill

The Dragonblood Carp's abnormality quickly drew the spiritual senses of the Three Great Pill Sovereigns.

Forget the average audience—even alchemy grandmasters deeply familiar with exotic flames found themselves puzzled.

"Is that... really just the Heavenly Thunder Flame?"

The quality of the flame was terrifying. Maybe it hadn’t reached the level of Immortal Fire yet, but just judging by this Heavenly Thunder Flame alone, they could conclude one thing:

This Dragonblood Carp, in the Core Formation Stage, was absolutely an unstoppable force.

It could incinerate techniques with a single blaze—burning all divine abilities to ash.

"Unless...?" The Creation Pill Sovereign frowned—but before he could finish his thought, Lin Jing spoke slowly, under the furious stares of all the alchemists whose pill refinement had been disrupted.

"Apologies—my pet beast possesses the bloodline of a Flame-Attribute Rare Beast, so its flames are naturally overwhelming..."

His words sent shockwaves through the crowd.

Even more shocking than the appearance of the Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor.

In an instant, countless alchemists turned green with envy.

"What?!"

"That carp... it's a Rare Beast?!"

"And it has a Flame-Type Rare Beast Divine Ability?!"

Rare Beasts were the definition of rare.

For comparison—among human cultivators, only those with Immortal Physiques could rival Rare Beast bloodlines in rarity.

Now, hearing that Lin Jing had actually contracted a flame-attribute Rare Beast, jealousy flooded the faces of countless spectators.

"I knew it..."

The Creation Pill Sovereign took a deep breath—even his eyes flickered with greed.

"With a flame of this caliber… if that carp can control it perfectly, even at Core Formation Stage, it could easily refine Fifth-Tier Pills. And given the potential of a Rare Beast—once it breaks through, its pill-refining skills... might even surpass ours."

Even as he said this, the Creation Pill Sovereign quickly suppressed his desire—because right at that moment, Lin Jing’s Heavenly Demon Scroll lit up again… adding more names.

To most human cultivators, the Longevity Body wasn’t particularly tempting.

But a flame-attribute Rare Beast pet? That was something else entirely.

The Creation Pill Sovereign saw the Dragonblood Carp’s potential—naturally, other powerful figures did too.

They all took a deep breath, well aware: if they could snatch this carp and make it their own pet, it would be equivalent to gaining a potential Seventh-Tier alchemist by their side.

And not just an alchemist—one with high combat power.

Within human cultivator factions, enslaving fellow humans was rare and frowned upon—seen as demonic practice.

But enslaving demonic beasts? There were countless techniques for that. The Beast Taming Art of the Beast Taming Sect, and the techniques of the Myriad Beast Sect, were just the tip of the iceberg.

So now, many powerhouses burned with a new greed—for the Dragonblood Carp.

"I want the Ice Soul Gu, and I want this carp too," said an elder from the Gu God Sect. He now understood—it was the carp that Lin Jing had used to suppress the Ice Soul Gu.

"Bullsh*t," muttered the Four-Infant Demon Lord, frowning.

"I get it now. The Longevity Body’s true trump card... isn't the pill he plans to refine."

"It’s this Dragonblood Carp—this flame-type Rare Beast!"

"Even we can see the carp’s alchemy potential—how could Tongtian Tower not see it?"

"This is a Rare Beast Bloodline... can you really be sure that the Tower Master, that alchemy fanatic, won’t take an interest in this carp? If he accepts it as a named disciple, then as the pet’s Beast Tamer, Lin Jing will almost certainly gain the tower’s protection!"

"In that case..."

"Let’s just hope the Tower Master doesn’t get involved. The Dragonblood Carp can go to Tongtian Tower—but the Longevity Body, our Yin Corpse Sect will claim, no matter what."

Those who wanted to make a move on Lin Jing now realized—this was getting complicated.

"So many more..." Lin Jing looked silently at the Heavenly Demon Scroll.

Even several Tongtian Tower alchemists were now showing interest in his Dragonblood Carp.

But everything the Nine-Infant Demon Lord had considered—these alchemists quickly figured out too.

They realized Lin Jing’s trump card was likely this Dragonblood Carp with a Rare Beast Bloodline—and that he intended to use it in exchange for the protection of Tongtian Tower.

If that was true, then Lin Jing and the carp were effectively one of their own. Once the tower's powerhouses understood this, they immediately reined in their hostility and refocused.

"Focus on alchemy!"

At this point, of the hundred-plus participants, only a dozen or so alchemists could still control their flames.

All of them came from powerful alchemy factions and wielded top-tier Exotic Flames—barely managing to continue refining under the oppressive pressure of the Flame Emperor Divine Ability.

The rest exhaled, preparing to start again—there was still time, after all.

But in the next moment, as the thunderflame aura around the Dragonblood Carp expanded even more, it formed a Thunderflame Cauldron, sealing the carp within—it inevitably drew everyone's attention again.

"It swallowed the pill ingredients... turned its own body into a cauldron... this is an actual ingested pill technique?"

"Did this Longevity Body and his Rare Beast pet invent a new pill-refining method?!"

The Dragonblood Carp’s Thunder Refining Technique was indeed unheard of—even the Three Pill Sovereigns had never witnessed this kind of “ingested pill” technique.

But then, the Dragonblood Carp lived up to every bit of their high expectations. Not long after sealing itself in the Thunderflame Cauldron, it swam within and spat out a red pill with thunderbolt patterns, straight out of the cauldron.

Though judging by its aura, the pill was only Third-Tier, the speed of refinement was staggering.

"So this was Lin Jing’s real goal? To get the Tower Master to accept his pet as a named disciple?" The Academy’s Vice Dean was the last to realize it, cursing silently. If Lin Jing pulled this off, they wouldn’t be able to bring him back to the Ancient Celestial Kingdom.

By now, no one really cared what pill Lin Jing had refined.

Because most believed Lin Jing's true purpose in joining the Alchemy Conference was to showcase his and the Dragonblood Carp’s potential—and exchange that for protection from Tongtian Tower.

Even the Three Pill Sovereigns, at this moment, had to admit—Lin Jing’s plan had a high chance of success.

After all, Flame-Attribute Rare Beasts—there might only be one in the entire world.

The Dragonblood Carp might never surpass the Tower Master in alchemy, but it was fully capable of surpassing the rest of them. If the Tower Master had even a hint of talent-cherishing intent, Lin Jing would be safe.

What pill it was didn’t matter anymore—even if it was just some common Third-Tier pill.

If it was refined that fast, it probably wasn’t anything special...

That’s what everyone thought—

Until—BOOM!

A bolt of lightning tore through the sky.

A dense Tribulation Cloud began to gather—a Heavenly Tribulation was forming in its midst...

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 96: Pill Tribulation
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3. Chapter 96: Pill Tribulation

“Heavenly Tribulation?”

The heavenly tribulation came suddenly, out of nowhere, shocking everyone. They couldn’t understand—how could something like a tribulation appear at the site of the Pill Assembly?

Normally, tribulations only appear when powerful cultivators are making breakthroughs, nearing or exceeding the limits of the world…

“The power’s not that strong, though.”

The moment the tribulation appeared, quite a few Void Mystical Stage elders were startled. But when they realized the tribulation’s power was only around the Core Formation level, they breathed a sigh of relief.

It wasn’t something they triggered…

A tribulation of this level, even if it spread to them, wouldn’t mean much. Its power wouldn’t increase significantly.

That said, just in case, the old monsters still moved a little farther away for safety.

Soon after, Lin Jing from the pill-forging zone slowly spoke again.

“This tribulation is a Pill Tribulation.”

“It was triggered by the pill.”

“Our self-created Thunder Refining Method, after the pill is formed, must undergo the Pill Tribulation to be considered a complete success.”

“So, what comes next is the final step of our pill forging…”

“It typically won’t affect cultivators. But… as for other pills, I can’t say.”

As Lin Jing finished speaking, the entire place was stunned.

Pill Tribulation?

The Three Pill Sovereign Sects fell silent.

This so-called Pill Tribulation was unheard of, but the Thunder Refining Method Lin Jing mentioned was something the three great sects recognized.

They didn’t know exactly what the Thunder Refining Method was, but the fact that it could make a pill attract a tribulation—what kind of boat was this Longevity Body forging?

They were sure that triggering a tribulation wasn’t something that could be done easily. Even if Lin Jing’s exotic flame was a high-grade Heavenly Thunder Flame, it would still be a pipe dream to summon a tribulation.

So there was only one possibility left—whatever pill Lin Jing was refining must also be forbidden.

The Three Pill Sovereign Sects hadn’t expected much from Lin Jing’s pill at first, but now, watching the lightning suddenly strike, they realized this was far from simple.

“What the hll!” Now that they knew it was a Pill Tribulation, the Void Mystical Stage old monsters were relieved. But the other alchemists still working in the pill-forging zone were dumbfounded.

Just like Lin Jing said, the Pill Tribulation didn’t really affect cultivators. But—it did affect pills!

With a loud rumble, the tribulation lightning struck the Transformation Pill that the Dragon Carp had spat out. When the lightning hit, though incredibly intense, the Tier-3 pill was completely unharmed.

But in that instant, the tribulation lightning’s residual energy scattered across the refining zone.

The leftover lightning locked onto the other competitors’ unfinished pills.

Lin Jing had tested this kind of tribulation before—it didn’t target people, only pills. And if there were pills nearby that hadn’t completed their own tribulations, they’d definitely get hit.

So, as soon as the Transformation Pill was formed, with a loud “boom,” the alchemists still working at the scene… their furnaces exploded on the spot!

They had endured the might of the Heavenly Thunder Flame, but they couldn’t withstand the effect of the Pill Tribulation.

At that moment, the competing alchemists were ready to kill someone.

Even the calm and collected Alchemist Jie Geng, who had no ill will toward Lin Jing before, just stared blankly at the pill that had blown up in front of her.

“Dmn it all…”

But right after, seeing Lin Jing holding the Transformation Pill, they all took a deep breath and couldn’t help wanting to know what kind of pill this was.

Why… could it trigger a Pill Tribulation?

The Pill Assembly lasted several days—any pill successfully refined within that time was considered valid. Now that Lin Jing had already finished his, even though others’ furnaces had exploded, they could just start again.

“Finally succeeded.”

At this moment, Lin Jing held the Transformation Pill and smiled.

He looked at the Three Pill Sovereign Sects and said, “Senior of the Sun and Moon Pill Sect, may I test the pill now?”

“You may.” The elder of the Sun and Moon Pill Sect took a deep breath and said, “May I ask, what is the pill you refined? What is its name?”

“To summon a Pill Tribulation—this is unheard of. You and the pill are both remarkable. As expected of a pill beast with the bloodline of a flame-attribute rare beast…”

Lin Jing held the pill and said, “This pill has two names. One of them relates to its effect—I’ll keep that one a secret for now.”

“The second name… is ‘Death.’ It’s a pill that can bring death.”

Death?

Everyone was confused.

“What a load of mystery.” Many of the strong cultivators waiting for the Pill Assembly to end and see Lin Jing’s final outcome scoffed. Although Lin Jing had shown potential that even Tongtian Tower would be tempted by, they didn’t believe the Tower Master would definitely choose him.

The key point was that the Yin Corpse Sect and Gu God Sect experts also seemed to be present. If these two demonic sects had their eyes set on Lin Jing, then even the Tower Master of Tongtian would have to weigh his options.

After all, Lin Jing was from the Ancient Celestial Kingdom, and Tongtian Tower’s relationship with that kingdom wasn’t great.

As for the Yin Corpse Sect and Gu God Sect, their relationship with the kingdom was even worse. Both were major forces with Dao-Seeking Stage experts—enemies of an enemy might not be friends, but there was no need to make them enemies either.

Tongtian Tower didn’t have any deep hatred with the Yin Corpse Sect or Gu God Sect.

So, most people believed the Tower Master would be unlikely to protect Lin Jing.

More likely, they’d just want the Dragon Carp and not care whether Lin Jing lived or died.

“I now need a pill tester.”

Lin Jing spoke. Before he could even say what kind of person he needed, thunder rumbled in the distance, and with a dragon’s roar, a divine dragon bathed in lightning flew over.

It quickly soared into the pill-forging area and landed in front of Lin Jing.

“I’ll do it.”

“Thunder Dragon Princess!”

Most people present naturally recognized the Thunder Dragon Princess. But they were stunned—why would the young princess of the Thunder Dragon Clan show up of her own accord to test a pill for Lin Jing?

Wasn’t there a rumor that Lin Jing had defeated and driven off the Thunder Dragon Princess when she challenged the geniuses of the Ancient Celestial Kingdom?

So why was she now willingly testing a pill for him?

Had she been won over by this Longevity Body’s strength?

“Thunder Dragon, lightning-attribute spirit plant, pill activation… So this really is a lightning-attribute pill.” Some alchemists speculated.

“Alright, you it is.”

Lin Jing directly tossed the Transformation Pill to her. The Thunder Dragon Princess swallowed it like a precious treasure, completely unbothered by the fact that it had come from the Dragon Carp’s mouth.

In the next instant—

The scattered tribulation clouds began to gather again.

“Why the h*ll is it back again?” Some contestants, who needed a long time to refine their pills and had just restarted their fires, broke down inside. They hadn’t expected the tribulation to return.

But this time—it wasn’t a Pill Tribulation!

With a loud boom, a Transformation Tribulation lightning bolt struck down.

This level of tribulation lightning was nothing to a beast genius like the Thunder Dragon Princess.

Bathed in lightning, she let out a low moan. And just as everyone was still wondering about the pill’s effect, their pupils suddenly shrank in shock.

Amidst the brilliant light, the Thunder Dragon Princess’s massive body rapidly shrank in the lightning. And then… a graceful humanoid figure slowly took shape!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 97: A Single Pill Shocks Qingzhou
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 97: A Single Pill Shocks Qingzhou

The shocking transformation of the Thunder Dragon Princess left all the participating alchemists and watching cultivators in utter disbelief.

What was this?

A pill… that changes form?

Under the effect of the Transformation Power, the Thunder Dragon Princess’s body began to shift rapidly. It didn’t take much time before she completely shed her massive dragon form…

In its place stood a youthful, radiant human woman, now revealed before the crowd!

“…Never mind.”

Lin Jing had originally planned to use an illusion spell to help her form clothing. After all, the post-transformation body had no attire. It didn’t matter to beasts, but from a human standpoint, it was better to be dressed…

But now, seeing the Thunder Dragon Princess’s appearance, Lin Jing felt there was no need.

“So this is the Transformation Pill… this is… what it feels like to become a member of the demon race?”

The Thunder Dragon Princess extended her hand, eyes under her long lashes filled with wonder as she examined her body.

Her words once again silenced the entire audience…

At first, everyone was just confused.

That pill… actually turned the Thunder Dragon Princess into a humanoid?

But right after, they realized something wasn’t right.

This wasn’t just a superficial change in shape. It seemed… like she had fundamentally become another species.

The Thunder Dragon Princess’s own words confirmed it.

“Transformation Pill…”

“Demon race?”

Some alchemists murmured. Soon, someone made the connection to the Thunder Dragon Princess’s identity.

The Thunder Dragon Clan—part of the Demon Court.

And the Demon Court was a demon race revival alliance made up of various ancient beast clans that had once been part of the demon race.

The Demon Court’s mission was well-known across many forces and cultivators in Qingzhou. They also understood that, ever since the Immortal Palace had sealed the world’s laws, it had been near impossible for the ancient demon race to revive—almost an unreachable dream.

The fact that the Demon Court had tried for ten thousand years without success proved just how difficult it was.

And now—what were they witnessing…

The Thunder Dragon Princess of the Demon Court, after taking one pill… actually transformed into a demon?!

Lin Jing’s pill… was a Transformation Pill?!!

Everyone was dumbfounded.

And next, the Thunder Dragon Princess’s words plunged Tongtian City into chaos. Even cultivators who were initially composed, including the Eight-Armed Elder, Lin Jing’s fellow old monsters, and the weaker cultivators from the Ancient Kingdom, all dropped their jaws in disbelief.

The just-transformed, high-status Thunder Dragon Princess knelt halfway in her new demon form and respectfully saluted Lin Jing:

“Thank you, Your Highness the Holy Son, for the pill.”

“My Thunder Dragon Clan is willing to go through fire and water for Your Highness the Holy Son, to slay anyone who dares plot against you!!”

“I beg Your Highness… lead us demons to reclaim our ancient glory!!!”

Boom!

Her words were like a massive quake, shattering the minds of countless onlookers. No one was confused by the title “Holy Son.”

Although it was known that Lin Jing was the Holy Son of the Beast Taming Sect, they were now convinced—the Holy Son she referred to was far more than that.

The Transformation Technique that the Demon Court had pursued for ten thousand years but never found…

Had now been successfully refined by Lin Jing, this Longevity Body cultivator…

Then his Holy Son status… could it be…?

At that moment, the Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor who came to rescue Lin Jing, the Vice Dean who had planned to bring Lin Jing back safely, and the Three Pill Sovereign Sects who had hoped to recruit him—every one of them changed expression.

No one expected that Lin Jing’s ultimate trump card had nothing to do with the Ancient Kingdom… or with Tongtian Tower.

But instead… was the very place that should’ve been the deadliest den of dragons and tigers to him—the Demon Court!

“Go!”

“Go!!”

“GO!!!”

In that instant, countless hidden powerhouses realized the truth—this was all a trap. Things had spiraled far beyond their expectations, and now they didn’t want to stay in Tongtian City for even a second longer. A dreadful terror loomed over them.

One after another, Void Mystical old monsters listed in the Heavenly Demon Scroll tried to flee Tongtian City.

But in horror, they discovered—Tongtian City had already been sealed off by an invisible spatial barrier. The one who set it up possessed spatial laws several dimensions beyond their understanding—utterly unfathomable.

“You came for me… now you want to leave? It’s too late.”

At this moment, sensing that some wanted to flee, Lin Jing looked up at the sky and said:

“I request His Majesty the Demon Emperor to strike down my enemies and uphold the dignity of the Demon Court!”

Boom.

As his words fell, a pair of deep-blue demonic eyes slowly opened above the skies of Tongtian City, casting a gaze down over all.

As these eyes appeared, whether it was the Sky Lion Ancestor, the Nine-Infant Demon Lord, or the elder from the Gu God Sect, all turned ashen-faced. It was simply absurd.

“What kind of sick joke is this?!” In the city, a red-haired elder screamed at the sky in madness, for he felt a deathly pressure crashing down on him.

The same Skyfire Ancestor listed in the Heavenly Demon Scroll finally understood the meaning of Lin Jing’s earlier words.

This pill had two names.

One was the Transformation Pill.

The other… was Death.

Indeed, this was a pill that brought death.

And now, the Demon Emperor had descended.

In the Yuan Continent, among the countless demon clans and many demon emperors, the Lord of the Demon Court—one of the top three in strength—had been summoned by Lin Jing into this great city.

Cold sweat poured from countless powerhouses. The Three Pill Sovereign Sects were struck speechless.

The developments were too fast and too terrifying to process.

Just a moment ago, Lin Jing had been a lamb to the slaughter. Now…

He was the Demon Court’s Holy Son, protected by one of the most powerful demons of an entire realm.

At that moment, the might of the Demon Emperor spread not only through Tongtian City but all across the sixteen provinces of Qingzhou. Countless gazes turned, shocked, toward Tongtian City.

Back then, the chase between Gu Qianqiu and the Sky Lion Ancestor had already stirred an entire Dao Domain. But now—the Demon Emperor’s arrival was shaking all of Qingzhou.

The power of a Dao-Seeking Stage—enough to burn heaven and earth.

“Those who oppose the Holy Son of the Demon Court…

“Die.”

The Demon Emperor’s voice rang out. One after another, the Void Mystical old monsters near the city’s edge began to bleed violently.

At the same time, Lin Jing slowly rose into the air and extended his hand. The Dragon Carp flew over and merged into his body. A top-tier exotic flame—Netherworld Ghostfire, which thrived by burning life and soul—slowly emerged in his palm.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 98: The Path of the Ghost God
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 98: The Path of the Ghost God

Netherworld Ghostfire.

Though it was a top-tier exotic flame, what made it special was its growth potential.

Only by burning souls could it drastically increase in quality.

This wisp of Netherworld Ghostfire from the Dragon Carp was still far from top-grade. But now that His Majesty the Demon Emperor had promised to end the lives of all powerful enemies himself, Lin Jing could take this opportunity to practice a few more spells.

The Heavenly Demon Divine Sense Technique was one of them...

And this Netherworld Ghostfire spell that burned souls—naturally another.

Right now, in Tongtian City, with so many powerful enemies listed on the scroll, Lin Jing figured... he’d probably be able to raise the Netherworld Ghostfire to its peak.

“Lord of the Demon Court, this is Tongtian City—territory of Tongtian Tower. You dare strike at us during the Pill Assembly? Are you looking down on Tongtian Tower?!”

When he entered the city, he had been warned—during the Pill Assembly, no disturbances were allowed.

All grudges must wait until the Assembly ended.

The Demon Emperor should have to follow the rules too!

But facing this threat, the Demon Emperor didn’t care at all. Just with pressure alone, he rendered the Skyfire Ancestor motionless. He couldn’t circulate any energy, pinned to the ground like a withered old corpse—utterly miserable.

“Lord of the Demon Court, you…”

Even the three Pill Sovereigns of Tongtian Tower were at a loss.

By duty, they should maintain order during the Pill Assembly. After all, several alchemists participating were currently being suppressed by the Demon Emperor’s might!

They were crushed to the ground, miserable and unable to continue pill forging…

Such a disruption should count as an affront to Tongtian Tower—but the one causing it was someone even the Three Pill Sovereigns couldn’t deal with.

“Tower Master! Tower Master…” They could only anxiously call out to the secluded Tower Master.

Of course, with this much commotion, how could the Tower Master not sense it?

At that very moment, in an alternate dimension, he was facing both the Spirit of the Immortal Pill and the Demon Emperor. Internally, he was utterly shaken.

“What? You plan to stop me?”

The Demon Emperor, in his demon form, looked at the Tower Master and the Immortal Pill Spirit while holding a tiger seal exuding terrifying might. It made the Tower Master extremely cautious—this was a treasure of the ancient Divine Tiger Clan, only usable by those with demon bloodlines.

The Lord of the Demon Court was already terrifying due to his rare beast bloodline. Now that he had transformed and inherited the legacy of the ancient demon race, his power was beyond comprehension.

“No fighting, no fighting!” the Immortal Pill Spirit quickly sent a voice transmission to the Tower Master. “Old man, I just sensed the aura of a ‘Pill Tribulation.’ Isn’t a Pill Tribulation only triggered when a sentient Immortal Pill is born in the lower realms? How can a person summon one?”

“If that tribulation affects me, I’m done for. I’m scared…”

Hearing the Pill Spirit’s hesitation, the Tower Master let out a long breath and said, “Congratulations, fellow Daoist. You’ve found the path of transformation. Standing up for the Demon Court’s Holy Son is understandable. However, I must ask—please don’t harm our Tongtian Tower alchemists.”

“Of course. That Lin Jing from your Tongtian Tower is very sensible. He didn’t land on the Heavenly Demon Scroll. But as for the forces aligned with Tongtian Tower—well, they can blame me for sweeping them away.”

As the Demon Emperor finished speaking, the Tower Master silently accepted it.

He truly didn’t want to fight the power-unknown Lord of the Demon Court.

Seeing the Tower Master’s lack of intent to fight, the Demon Emperor even revealed an amused expression.

“Fine then. Once I’m done here, I’ll go to the Yin Corpse Sect’s headquarters and drag out that Corpse Emperor for a beating. Qingzhou ought to know the strength I had before even transforming.”

That guy… The Tower Master broke into a cold sweat.

Just how powerful had the Lord of the Demon Court become even before transforming?

……

Outside.

The Nine-Infant Demon Lord, the elder from the Gu God Sect—every one of them was suppressed. There was no longer any pride of the Void Mystical Stage on their faces.

Likewise, desperate not to die, they too tried to threaten the Demon Emperor.

“Lord of the Demon Court, our Yin Corpse Sect has no feud with yours. Are you really going to start a war with us?”

“Even if you kill me here, His Majesty the Corpse Emperor can revive me. Then it’ll be an endless war between our sect and the Demon Court!”

Their threats, like those of the Skyfire Ancestor, echoed throughout Tongtian City.

However, neither the Demon Emperor nor Lin Jing paid them any mind at this point.

“I’m a Pill Assembly contestant…”

Even the red-haired woman who had been by Lin Jing’s side, despite being part of the grand event, was burned to ash by a single wave of Netherworld Ghostfire.

And she wasn’t alone—anyone listed on the Heavenly Demon Scroll, regardless of status…

The flames unleashed from Lin Jing surged like ghostly dragons, devouring them one by one.

Watching Lin Jing slaughter fellow contestants and Pill Dao geniuses alike, the Three Pill Sovereigns were drenched in cold sweat.

Hovering high above, they dared not speak—let alone intervene.

The Tower Master of Tongtian Tower had already sent word to the Twelve Pill Sovereigns, allowing the Demon Court’s Holy Son to act freely.

That left the Three Pill Sovereigns of the Pill Assembly feeling extremely uneasy.

“Netherworld Ghostfire… The Demon Emperor’s actually helping him refine his spell.” They hadn’t expected that the Dragon Carp also possessed the Netherworld Hall’s ghostfire technique.

Now, Lin Jing seemed like a ghost god emerging from hell.

Even those one or two realms higher than him trembled with fear just being near.

As he passed by Alchemist Jie Geng, she stared at him in terror. But since she hadn’t been listed on the Heavenly Demon Scroll, Lin Jing didn’t go around killing indiscriminately just to train his ghostfire technique.

“Lin Jing, you…”

Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor looked at the now fully dragonized, slowly rising Lin Jing, her face filled with shock.

At this point, it seemed she no longer had any role to play.

“Lin Jing!”

The Vice Dean of the Academy looked at him—and saw the truth in Lin Jing’s eyes.

This Longevity Body… from now on, he would no longer be a cultivator of the Ancient Celestial Kingdom.

No longer a student of the academy.

No longer a Demon-Slaying Guard of the Bureau.

He had become… the Holy Son of the Demon Court.

The beacon of the demon race’s revival.

This Longevity Body cultivator was now walking a path no one of his kind had ever walked before—

The path of a Ghost God.

The hope of the demon race.

“This is… what the Sect Regulatory Office meant when they said demon fortune outweighs human fortune…” The Vice Dean had never imagined that the office’s assessment could be this accurate.

Right now, Lin Jing didn’t say much.

He simply clasped his hands toward the Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor and the Vice Dean, then soared away from the pill-forging area—no longer concerned with the outcome of the Pill Assembly.

His first target: the elder from the Gu God Sect, still trapped at the edge of the barrier by the Demon Emperor—yet to be killed, still carrying the parasitic Thunder Tribulation Gu inside him!

“So there really is a Gu God Sect cultivator here…”

“But only one? What a shame…”

“Thunder Tribulation Gu… I never thought there’d be a day when my Ice Soul Gu could devour one of the Ten Rare Gu.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 99: Thunder Tribulation Gu
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 99: Thunder Tribulation Gu

“Heavenly Thunder…!!!”

Under the overwhelming might of the Demon Emperor, Lin Jing’s first target, Elder Thunder Gu, was completely engulfed in black lightning.

Inside his body, a black scorpion tail flickered with lightning as it pierced straight into Elder Thunder Gu’s heart.

The venom of the Lightning Gu spread throughout his body. His fusion with the Gu pushed his physical form beyond its limits. The violent lightning barely allowed him to move again under the Demon Emperor’s oppressive aura.

At that moment, Elder Thunder Gu made a quick decision—to summon heavenly lightning, blast open a gap in the sealing barrier, and escape!

But to his despair, even that last sliver of hope was denied by the Demon Emperor...

Heavenly Tribulation? To someone like the Demon Emperor, that was no longer something to fear.

Nascent Soul, Soul Transformation, Void Mystical, Dao-Seeking, Ascension...

However, due to changes in the world's laws, cultivators capable of actually reaching the Immortal Realm no longer exist.

Even after enduring the Ascension Tribulation, one still remains in the Dao-Seeking Stage, unable to gain any true connection to the Immortal Realm. As a result, the Ascension Stage has gradually disappeared from the Tianyuan Continent.

Still, many Dao-Seeking powerhouses continue to challenge the Ascension Tribulation to validate their Dao!

The Demon Emperor is one such powerhouse who attempted to cross the Ascension Tribulation in an era where ascension is no longer possible—one of the top existences in all of Tianyuan Continent. There are less than ten like her.

If not for the constraints of this era, she would've already ascended.

It’s precisely because she had experience enduring the fake Ascension Tribulation that the Demon Emperor dared to directly face the Transformation Tribulation.

Right now, though the Thunder Tribulation Gu was violent beyond compare, it was, after all, only a Void Mystical Gu. To think it could affect a demon emperor who had already crossed a pseudo-Ascension Tribulation and a Transformation Tribulation? That was pure fantasy.

Boom!

In the skies, a massive black scorpion silhouette appeared within the black tribulation clouds. But before the lightning could even form fully, a deep tiger’s roar from the void shattered space across all of Tongtian City. The newly formed thunderclouds instantly scattered.

“So this is the Lord of the Demon Court… I wonder how she compares to the Gu God,” thought Elder Thunder Gu bitterly, realizing that even after pushing the Thunder Gu to its limit, he couldn’t even flee.

Pfft.

Before Elder Thunder Gu could react further, the tiger's roar that shattered the tribulation also crushed his organs and soul. The Thunder Tribulation Gu, fused to his heart, was forcibly expelled.

“You old b*stard… fight your own battles.”

Seeing no hope of escape, the Thunder Tribulation Gu decided to self-destruct, seeking a chance at reincarnation. But instead, it was met with Elder Thunder Gu’s mocking laughter as he knelt, legs shattered and body in shambles.

“There’s no getting out of this. Out of all the enemies here, the Holy Son of the Demon Court came straight for me… I know exactly what he’s after.”

Nearly blind, Elder Thunder Gu looked up at Lin Jing descending from the sky, his body wreathed in the dual forces of ice and fire, and saw a shadow of the Gu God Sect’s former Ice-Fire Holy Maiden.

The Ice-Fire Holy Son of the Gu God Sect had also cultivated both flames and frost, using fire to balance the overwhelming power of the Great Gu.

“Your Majesty the Demon Emperor, please assist in extracting the Gu. That Lin Jing’s Gu could be of great use for researching a Transformation Pill.”

Just before the descent of Ice Soul, Lin Jing’s Gu, who had intended to self-destruct, was horrified to find its body completely out of its control. A dominating demon force was peeling it away from Elder Thunder Gu.

“Longevity Body—!”

The moment the Gu was ripped from his body, Elder Thunder Gu screamed in agony. So did the Lin Jing Gu.

Though it had once reached the Void Mystical Stage, under the Demon Emperor’s power, its lightning force was rapidly being erased.

Its cultivation fell—Void Mystical... Soul Transformation... Nascent Soul... Core Formation... Foundation Establishment... Qi Refinement...

In no time, all its strength was wiped out. A white lightning-shaped scorpion, completely devoid of consciousness, floated behind Ice Soul.

“This is the Thunder Gu’s origin. I’ve already reshaped its core power. You now have two options,” said the Demon Emperor.

“One: find a place rich in lightning energy and re-nurture it. It will be reborn like your Ice Soul Gu and serve you again.”

“Two: feed it to Ice Soul Gu, letting it absorb its strength. But that will place a limit on Ice Soul Gu...”

“The reason Gu God Sect cultivators never let the Ten Rare Gu devour each other is because once they do, the hybrid power prevents them from ever being reborn in their elemental lands again.”

“I choose the second.”

Lin Jing grabbed the Thunder Gu and sealed it inside a wooden box that materialized out of thin air.

He already knew this. The moment a Rare Gu possesses power from another Rare Gu, it loses its rebirth ability.

But Ice Soul Gu didn’t care. After all, even if it were to reincarnate, it would lose all memories—it wouldn’t be the same being.

Rebirth was only a desperate option, a last resort to preserve the bloodline.

If given a choice, no living being would want to start over—they’d rather reach the peak in one lifetime.

To devour the other nine Rare Gu and replace the Gu God—that was a future the other Rare Gu couldn’t see, but Ice Soul Gu could.

How could it ever give that up?

“Two Rare Gu in one host, huh…”

Now that he had lost the Thunder Gu, Elder Thunder Gu had also lost his last hope.

With a faint sigh, he said weakly, “May the heavens allow His Highness the Holy Son to plant a Gu within me and make me your slave… please…”

Before he could finish, a ghostfire ignited, consuming Elder Thunder Gu—whose powers had been completely sealed by the Demon Emperor.

In the flickering light of soul-burning flames, Lin Jing slowly rose into the air again.

“I’m the Holy Son of the Demon Court—what would I want with you?”

Sensing the ghostfire growing in terrifying power as it devoured Elder Thunder Gu’s lingering soul, Lin Jing locked on his next target: the Nine-Infant Demon Lord of the Yin Corpse Sect.

That fiend had nine Nascent Souls. His soul power was immense—perfect to push Ghost Extinguish to its final evolution.

Moments later, Lin Jing descended near the spot where the Nine-Infant Demon Lord lay sprawled.

His nine Nascent Souls had left his body and formed an infant array, struggling miserably.

As Lin Jing appeared, the Nine-Infant Demon Lord’s pupils shrank.

“If I had known… I should’ve just finished you off in the Ancient Kingdom…”

“Sending me to Qingzhou… was the Yin Corpse Sect’s biggest mistake.”

As Lin Jing arrived, the Demon Emperor struck again to suppress the Nine Infants.

If the Yin Corpse Sect had chosen to withstand pressure from the Ancient Kingdom and pursued Lin Jing there, things might not have ended up like this.

But now, they had handed him off to Qingzhou—and the Nine-Infant Demon Lord regretted it deeply.

Watching the ghostfire blaze, he realized… he had created an even more terrifying monster.

“Tell me—why is the Yin Corpse Sect so desperate to find me?”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 100: Celestial Beast Corpse
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 100: Celestial Beast Corpse

As for why the Yin Corpse Sect wanted to capture him, Lin Jing had some vague clues and had investigated it before.

However, he wasn’t clear on the deeper inside story.

Although he now had the protection of the Demon Emperor and it didn’t matter anymore, he still wanted to get to the bottom of it.

“Hahahahahahahaha…” The Nine-Infant Demon Lord laughed madly.

“To fall at the hands of a mere Foundation Establishment brat… what a miscalculation.”

“The Corpse Emperor will avenge me…”

Buzz!

He immediately attempted to self-detonate his Nascent Soul.

Lin Jing, expression calm, waved away the ghostfire and moved on in search of his next target.

“If you won’t talk, so be it…”

“I don’t have time to waste on you.”

In Tongtian City, Lin Jing had become a god of slaughter, a ghost of vengeance.

Everyone listed in the Heavenly Demon Scroll—he didn’t intend to let a single one escape.

In the process, the entire Qingzhou was thrown into complete chaos.

Divine Flame Path.

Heavenly Fire Sect.

Because of the existence of Skyfire Ancestor, the Heavenly Fire Sect stood at the top of the Divine Flame Path.

However, on this day, many elders and disciples were horrified to see a crack appear on the statue of Skyfire Ancestor enshrined deep within the sect.

The incense energy supporting it rapidly dissipated.

Moreover, the life incense of several elders and disciples who had accompanied Skyfire Ancestor to Tongtian City extinguished one after another—all of them perished.

This scene left everyone in the Heavenly Fire Sect in utter terror.

Combined with the terrifying fluctuations from the direction of Tongtian Dao, they couldn’t even imagine what was happening over there.

And then—right at that moment—

A massive rift tore open in the sky above the Heavenly Fire Sect.

Thunder and lightning raged through the air.

Within the lightning, countless shadowy Thunder Dragons roared.

At the head of the pack was a Thunder Dragon radiating Void Mystical Demon Ancestor might, shocking the entire Divine Flame Path.

“Th-Thunder Dragon Patriarch?!”

The appearance and aura of this divine dragon left the Vice Sect Master of the Heavenly Fire Sect horrified, prompting him to instantly activate the sect’s protection formation.

In the face of this formation, the Thunder Dragon Patriarch spoke with disdain:

“The Heavenly Fire Sect’s ancestor harbored ill intent toward the Holy Son of the Demon Court and has been executed. By decree of Her Majesty the Demon Emperor, the Thunder Dragon Clan is here to wipe out the Heavenly Fire Sect.”

“You have one stick of incense’s time. Disband your formation and surrender, and you will not be killed.”

As soon as he finished speaking, the entire Heavenly Fire Sect went pale.

The Ancestor… was dead?!

What kind of joke was this?

The Holy Son of the Demon Court? Since when did the Demon Court have a Holy Son…?

But this was no isolated event.

It was happening in many places at once.

At the very moment the Thunder Dragon Princess achieved transformation, all Void Mystical Demon Ancestors of the Demon Court, along with the First and Second Holy Maidens, were pulled into a pitch-black space.

Within that darkness, a pair of terrifying demon eyes opened, making the Demon Ancestors unable to breathe.

“From this day forward, the Longevity Body Lin Jing is the Holy Son of my Demon Court, his status above all the Holy Maidens.”

“The Third Holy Maiden, Xu Qingyao, will be personally accepted as this emperor’s disciple, inheriting my teachings…”

This decree from the Demon Emperor shocked all the demon ancestors.

“The Transformation Technique has descended. The demon race is about to rise again. All of you—prepare yourselves.”

“From here on, the territories of these forces will be taken over by the Demon Court.”

“Heavenly Lion Clan… Heavenly Fire Sect…”

One by one, the demon ancestors listened in disbelief to the Demon Emperor’s orders.

Even the two Holy Maidens looked stunned.

But soon, the demon ancestors understood—

It was the Third Holy Maiden, Xu Qingyao, together with Lin Jing of the Beast Taming Sect and his Longevity Body, who discovered the Transformation Technique, prompting the Demon Emperor to act on such a large scale.

“Transformation Technique…”

The Kirin Ancestor was utterly stunned.

The Kirin Clan had invested so much in the Third Holy Maiden, and now, she had found the Transformation Technique and become the Demon Emperor’s personal disciple!

Shock gave way to overwhelming joy.

“The Heavenly Lion Clan will be completely wiped out by our Kirin Clan!”

The Kirin Ancestor stepped forward, volunteering to lead his clan in destroying the clan with whom Lin Jing had the deepest grudge.

“The Heavenly Fire Sect will be destroyed by us, the Thunder Dragon Clan,” declared the Thunder Dragon Patriarch, the first to know the truth and choosing a nearby target.

“The Sky-Swallowing Python Clan—leave that to me…”

At that moment, the demon ancestors competed to express loyalty to the Demon Court and take action.

And just like that, all of Qingzhou plunged into total upheaval.

Blood flowed like rivers. Wars erupted nonstop.

And at the center of this bloodshed, igniting it all—was Lin Jing, with each swing of his blade inside Tongtian City.

With every death of a Void Mystical or Soul Transformation expert from a major faction, a Demon Court clan would descend with an army.

In no time, the stench of blood saturated the skies above Qingzhou.

The upheaval in Qingzhou even began to affect other regions.

Ancient Celestial Kingdom.

Sect Regulatory Office.

As a nation of cultivators specializing in Heavenly Fortune, the Sect Regulatory Office mainly oversaw the fluctuations of fortune among sects under the Ancient Kingdom.

There was a great hall within it, dedicated to holding the symbolic tokens of various sects.

Even if a sect was under reform, as long as it hadn’t been officially disqualified, its token remained.

But in this moment, inside the Reform Hall, a wooden crane-shaped sculpture representing the Beast Taming Sect began to shift.

The originally balanced Human and Demonic Fortune forces twisted violently.

Suddenly, the Demonic Fortune exploded outward, shattering the wooden sculpture.

The terrifying wave of Demonic Fortune surged out and suppressed the Human Fortune from other sects—such as the Heavenly Sword Gate and Spirit Dao Sect.

It was like a demonic storm had erupted inside the Sect Regulatory Hall, flipping over the tokens of countless sects.

The scene made the Void Mystical Prefect of the office rush to the hall in shock.

“That’s… the Beast Taming Sect of the Ancient Kingdom…”

“How is this possible?! The Beast Taming Sect’s Demonic Fortune just now… actually reached the point of overturning the Kingdom’s fortune…”

He was dumbfounded.

Though he didn’t know what happened, he immediately erased the Beast Taming Sect’s name from the list of registered sects, barely preventing the spread of that fortune.

Even then, he was left gasping for breath, face deathly pale—this was a first for him.

He turned to look toward Qingzhou.

Clearly, something major had happened to the Beast Taming Sect over there.

“I need an audience with the Emperor!”

The Prefect opened a spatial gate directly to the Ancient Kingdom’s imperial city.

Meanwhile, at the top of the capital’s ancient city walls, the Second Ancient Emperor stood watching the rising dragon-shaped Demonic Fortune from Qingzhou with a grave expression.

“…So, the Lord of the Demon Court has developed ambitions to rule all demon beasts and intends to take over Qingzhou.”

“What exactly happened there…?”

While the beast clans and cultivation families across Qingzhou were suffering annihilation, one of the Three Great Demonic Sects—Yin Corpse Sect—was oddly spared.

In the Yin Corpse Sect headquarters, inside the secret realm, the life tablet of the Nine-Infant Demon Lord shattered.

But no demon beast army came.

Because the Yin Corpse Sect… would be facing the Emperor directly.

The secret realm was forcefully torn open.

The Corpse Emperor’s Daughter, who was deep in seclusion, instantly awoke.

She saw a chubby little girl with a tiger’s head floating above a strange space, quietly studying a celestial beast corpse sealed by the Yin Corpse Sect.

It was the corpse of a bull-shaped demon beast.

Even after who knows how many years of death, it still radiated terrifying pressure.

“So this is what you wanted the Longevity Body for…?”

“From now on, this celestial beast corpse belongs to the Demon Court.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 101: Famous Across the Land
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 101: Famous Across the Land

One month later.

The full story behind the earth-shaking “Demonic Turmoil in Qingzhou” had spread across the continent.

It all began with the Yin Corpse Sect and Gu God Sect scheming to obtain the Longevity Body and the Ice Gu. They used a teleportation treasure from the Ancient Celestial Palace to transfer Lin Jing—a cultivator from the Beast Taming Sect of the Ancient Kingdom—to Qingzhou, ensuring he would be beyond the Ancient Kingdom’s protection.

However, what the Yin Corpse Sect and Gu God Sect never imagined was that in Qingzhou, Lin Jing would develop the Beast Transformation Technique and gain the favor of the Demon Court’s ruler. This supreme being of the demon race personally conferred upon him the title of Holy Son of the Demon Court.

To help the Holy Son sweep away his enemies and establish authority, the Qingzhou Alchemy Assembly became the spark that ignited total chaos.

In Tongtian City, four Void Mystical cultivators and five Void Mystical demon ancestors—all of whom had plotted against Lin Jing—fell in the same period.

Nine Void Mystical experts perished in Tongtian City alone!

As for Soul Transformation, Nascent Soul cultivators and demonic beasts, the death toll was beyond measure.

This act of upending a region’s power structure and disrupting its balance soon drew the attention of two other Demon Emperors near Qingzhou.

One was the Forest Sea Dragon Emperor, residing in the Misty Forest bordering Qingzhou.

The other was the Yin-Yang Whale Emperor, lord of Poseidon Palace in the Eastern Sea.

Originally intending to act and keep things in check, both Demon Emperors went silent after witnessing the Demon Court’s ruler appear at the Yin Corpse Sect’s headquarters and personally engage the Corpse Emperor in a battle so intense it shattered the void.

The Yin Corpse Secret Realm was destroyed, and the Corpse Emperor had no choice but to flee through corpse-rebirth.

While the Corpse Emperor wasn’t among the strongest Dao-Seeking experts—and he was already injured—in battles between cultivators of that level, if one side chose to escape, it was nearly impossible for the other to kill them. Especially if the defender was on their own turf.

Yet the Demon Court’s ruler almost did it.

She stormed the enemy’s stronghold alone.

And though she didn’t fully kill the Corpse Emperor, forcing him into corpse-rebirth was no different from death.

His entire Dao-Seeking cultivation base was reset to zero.

Even if he survived by luck, he would have to start over from the Qi Refinement Stage.

“That once-glorious ancient demon race has returned.”

Within Qingzhou, countless powerful demonic beasts were stirred and began to consider joining the Demon Court.

The demon race was known as the second largest race on the Tianyuan Continent.

But many believed the demons’ innate potential far surpassed that of the human race.

The only reason humans dominated was because the ancient Celestial Palace in the Immortal Realm had been human-led, leaving behind legacies that favored humans.

If one looked toward the Immortal Realm, the demon race had every advantage except reproduction rate.

“The entire continent’s power structure is about to change.”

Far to the north—

In the Snow Plains, a land covered in snow and ice all year, ruled by harsh conditions.

Though human cultivators existed here, the true rulers were the four Demon Emperors of Frost:

Ice Emperor, the Ice Bear.

Snow Emperor, the Nine-Tailed Snow Fox.

Cold Emperor, the Cold Feather Eagle.

Frost Emperor, the Fearsome Frost Wolf.

These four Demon Emperors had once ventured with Lin Jing into a Snow Plains secret realm, where they discovered an ancient immortal item frozen in time.

Relying on that item, they simultaneously broke through to the Dao-Seeking Stage and came to dominate the Snow Plains.

However, in terms of strength, they were considered the weakest of the Demon Emperors—far inferior to Qingzhou’s eight Demon Emperors.

As such, they’d been marked by the Seventh Ancient Emperor.

Now, under the Ancient Kingdom’s assault, the Snow Plains were steadily losing ground.

Upon hearing the upheaval in Qingzhou, the four Demon Emperors of Frost simultaneously had the same idea:

“Can we form an alliance with the Demon Court in Qingzhou?”

“We only reached the Demon Emperor Realm thanks to that immortal item. We lack a powerful inheritance. Meanwhile, the Second Ancient Emperor grows stronger daily due to the First Ancient Emperor’s legacy. If this continues, we’ll be swallowed by the Ancient Kingdom.”

“But if we can obtain the Transformation Technique from the Demon Court, we could inherit ancient demon divine arts and transform ourselves completely.”

“I believe the Demon Court ruler would love to see us and the Ancient Kingdom destroy each other—it would open the door for the demon race to grow stronger.”

“But don’t forget—the Holy Son who holds the Transformation Technique was once a cultivator of the Ancient Kingdom.

Even though he’s now the Holy Son of the Demon Court, he might not help us attack his homeland. After all… he’s human.”

“We won’t know until we try.”

Thus, the Four Frost Emperors sent emissaries to Qingzhou.

It was a long journey—who knew when they’d arrive?

At present, although the events in Qingzhou hadn’t reached the lower-level cultivators outside the region, every Dao-Seeking and Demon Emperor level existence already knew the story.

The core themes:

The Rise of the Demon Race

The Demon Court’s annihilation of the Yin Corpse Sect

Among them, the destruction of the Yin Corpse Sect was something even the First Ancient Emperor had failed to accomplish.

Of course, back then the Ancient Kingdom had been fighting multiple Dao-Seeking forces at once. The First Ancient Emperor had been stretched thin and only managed to heavily injure the Corpse Emperor and drive the Yin Corpse Sect out of the kingdom.

Now, all these monumental events revolved around a single name—

Lin Jing, bearer of the Longevity Body.

So many Void Mystical experts had fallen.

So many factions had been wiped out.

And it all tied back to him.

Thus, Lin Jing—a mere Foundation Establishment cultivator—became a name known to every Dao-Seeking expert.

His reputation exploded.

No longer was he the pitiful Longevity Body on the run.

He now stood shoulder-to-shoulder with the greatest heirs of the era:

The Ancient Imperial Prince

The Heir of the Martial Emperor

The Senior Sister of Water Moon Immortal Island

The Buddhist Son of Sun Temple

The Shaman Son of the Cloud Sea Mountains

He had become one of the highest-ranked second-generation elites of the Tianyuan Realm.

In Tongtian City, now veiled in ghostfire.

After the dust settled, the Tower Master of Tongtian Tower and the Celestial Pill Spirit emerged.

They stood atop the tower, gazing out at the nearly transformed Qingzhou, and shook their heads.

“With ghostfire techniques used to this extent, my Tongtian City… has become no different from hell.”

“What I don’t understand,” the Pill Spirit added, “is this:

The Longevity Body is such a tempting prize. The Yin Corpse Sect and Gu God Sect both sent top experts to seize him.

Why… didn’t anyone from the Netherworld Hall show up?”

“No idea,” the Tower Master said. “But thank the heavens they didn’t. Otherwise, they’d have ended up like the Yin Corpse Sect and Gu God Sect—devastated.

At least the Gu God Sect is far from Qingzhou, so the Demon Court ruler can’t easily strike them.

But the Yin Corpse Sect? They got wiped out. Only a few scattered remnants remain.”

“That Demon Court ruler is terrifying.

Good thing I didn’t let you fight her.

Otherwise, we’d be toast too!”

The Tower Master glanced at the Celestial Pill Spirit and sighed.

In the end, the Pill Spirit was just too timid, unable to unleash its full power.

If it had, as the only true Celestial Pill in the lower realm, even the Demon Emperor would have had to tread carefully.

“For now… let’s see what the Demon Court does next.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 102: Sect Relocation
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 102: Sect Relocation

Just as Lin Jing’s name was spreading across the world…

A group of cultivators had already arrived in Qingzhou from the Ancient Celestial Kingdom.

They were the cultivators the Beast Taming Sect had stationed in the Ancient Kingdom.

After becoming the Holy Son of the Demon Court, Lin Jing knew it would be difficult for him to return to the Ancient Kingdom.

As long as he and Xu Qingyao remained in the Beast Taming Sect—and simultaneously held the titles of Holy Son of the Demon Court and personal disciple of the Demon Emperor—the Beast Taming Sect would never be accepted back by the Ancient Kingdom.

So, the only option was to send people to escort the Beast Taming Sect disciples from the Ancient Kingdom to Qingzhou.

Those willing to come to Qingzhou could make the journey.

Those unwilling to leave their homeland would stay behind in the Ancient Kingdom.

They could continue learning and passing on the Beast Taming Sect’s techniques and even establish a new sect under a different name within the Ancient Kingdom. But from that moment on, they’d be completely severed from the original Beast Taming Sect.

As for whether the Ancient Kingdom would accept such a second-generation Beast Taming Sect… well, that wasn’t Xu Qingyao’s problem to worry about.

“Personal disciple of the Demon Emperor…”

“Holy Son of the Demon Court…”

“What kind of combo is that?”

“Did I hear that right?” muttered Elder Mo, his eyes twitching.

Along with the Black Bear Monster and the others, Elder Mo had just arrived in Qingzhou—and upon hearing all the rumors about Lin Jing and Xu Qingyao, he was completely numb.

Even the old Demon Kings and ancestors were all trembling.

They were sent to Qingzhou to work hard—not to become heirs to the f***ing Demon Court!

Now, no one could even tell whether Lin Jing and Xu Qingyao were still the Beast Taming Sect’s Holy Son and Sect Master’s daughter—or the Demon Court’s Holy Son and the Demon Emperor’s personal disciple.

Which identity took precedence?

So was it the Beast Taming Sect taking over the Demon Court, or had the Beast Taming Sect been absorbed by the Demon Court?

As the Ancient Kingdom’s main forces of the Beast Taming Sect approached, Lin Jing and Xu Qingyao—having already returned to the Demon Court—began planning the next steps.

“When we left Tongtian City, you spoke with the Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor and that Void Mystical cultivator from the Academy. What did you say to them?” she asked.

“Yeah.” Lin Jing nodded. “Since it’s inevitable that the Beast Taming Sect is severing ties with the Ancient Kingdom, I told the two of them that I’d like to use a Great Divine Ability to move the original mountain gate of the Beast Taming Sect to Qingzhou… After all, that mountain range is the real foundation of the Beast Taming Sect, and it’s home to many of the disciples’ pet beasts.”

“Not a bad idea,” Xu Qingyao said, “but the Ancient Kingdom probably won’t agree—especially since there’s still a Undying Demon sealed beneath it.”

Lin Jing chuckled. “They’ll agree. The Demon Emperor’s strength and the prestige of the demon race have already spread across the continent. And the Ancient Kingdom is currently at war with the Four Totemic Demon Emperors of the Snow Plains.”

“The Snow Plains are losing ground fast. If I were one of those Demon Emperors, I’d definitely hop on the demon race’s revival train right now and flip the tables.”

“From the Ancient Kingdom’s perspective… even though its cultivators have always prioritized the kingdom and humanity above all, once cooler heads prevail, they’ll realize the demon race’s rise is unstoppable. At that point, making enemies of the Demon Court wouldn’t be smart.”

“And us—we were once cultivators of the Ancient Kingdom. Though we can’t go back because of the Kingdom’s broader fate, we can still serve as a bridge of peace between the Ancient Kingdom and the Demon Court. This would give the Ancient Kingdom enough time to fully devour the Snow Plains. So really, asking for something this small isn't unreasonable.”

“In fact, there’s a high chance not only will they give us the Beast Taming Sect’s old mountain gate… they might even throw in some extra resources.”

“Like what?” Xu Qingyao asked.

“Like that Undying Demon you mentioned.” Lin Jing looked at her.

“What do you want that thing for? Planning to form a contract with it? Sure, it’s a Rare Beast, but still…” She raised an eyebrow.

Lin Jing said, “The Demon Emperor successfully destroyed the Yin Corpse Sect and brought back a Celestial Beast corpse.”

“According to the Demon Emperor’s analysis, refining this celestial corpse into a puppet requires three unique conditions.”

“One is the Yin Corpse Sect’s Corpse Manipulation Secret Art. The other two are tied to the strength of the manipulator.”

“You need someone who possesses both extreme vitality and extreme death energy to refine the celestial corpse.”

“The Demon Emperor said that the corpse doesn’t serve much purpose for the Demon Court, so she just gave it to me.”

“After all, as a Longevity Body, I already carry the source of extreme vitality within me. I have a natural advantage in refining celestial corpses.”

“That’s also why the Yin Corpse Sect tried to capture me and possess me.”

“And as for death energy, corpse cultivators like Nine Infant Demon Lord and others from the Yin Corpse Sect draw on the power of the Corpse Emperor, so they kind of meet the death energy requirement. If I can merge both extremes, I could control a celestial corpse puppet.”

“You mean…” Xu Qingyao began to understand.

“Yeah. That Undying Demon sealed beneath the Great Wasteland’s earth veins—its Rare Beast divine ability is the essence of death, born from a celestial’s death art.”

“Back then, even when it was just at the Nascent Soul Stage, that death-based divine ability allowed it to survive attacks from even Nascent Soul Transformation and Void Mystical Stage powerhouses.”

“So, if I can extract its power, maybe I can gain both the ‘extreme vitality’ and the ‘extreme death energy’ I need to command the celestial corpse.”

“I see,” Xu Qingyao nodded. “But will the Ancient Kingdom really give it up?”

Lin Jing said, “I heard from a disciple of a Demon-Slaying Clan that the Undying Demon is pretty unique. Its power is just pure death energy, and most people can’t handle it. Keeping it around just shortens your lifespan. And the Ancient Kingdom doesn’t have anyone who cultivates in death energy either. So it really only has one use left.”

“It once massacred countless lives in the Wasteland, devouring a ton of cultivators. Sealing it under the Wasteland instead of capturing it actually helped boost the Wasteland’s fortune, aligning better with the Ancient Kingdom’s development path.”

“But it’s been thousands of years now. Its fortune’s probably been drained dry by the Ancient Kingdom. At this point, it’s basically useless.”

“Then maybe you really can pull it off,” Senior Sister said with envy. “A celestial beast corpse… if you manage to refine that…”

Lin Jing gave a bitter smile. “It’s not that easy. To refine a celestial corpse, I’d need to perfect my Longevity Body—reach the true extreme of vitality. On top of that, I still don’t know how to steal the Undying Demon’s divine ability.”

“The Demon Emperor said the celestial beast is mine, but she won’t help me refine it. She doesn’t have the time. I’ll have to figure it out myself.”

“Unless…”

“Unless what?”

“Unless I use my final wish—then she might help me figure something out.”

“But I’m not gonna use it. That last chance to ask the Demon Emperor for help? No way I’m burning it on something like this. I’ll find my own way.”

Lin Jing’s gaze grew sharp. Now that his name shook the world and his Hidden Dragon Fortune had become a constant passive effect—he was sure he could find a way.

But before that, his next step was to let the Ice Soul Gu devour the Thunder Gu… and push for Core Formation.

Lin Jing was very curious—what kind of poison pill divine ability would emerge from the fusion of these two Rare Gu?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 103: Demon-Hunting Squad
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 103: Demon-Hunting Squad

Not long after, the Beast Taming Sect cultivators from the Ancient Celestial Kingdom arrived at the Demon Court.

During this time, Lin Jing was searching for a new Cave Dwelling of his own. He planned to help the Ice Soul Gu break through once he settled down.

Now that his identity as the Holy Son of the Demon Court was officially public, it obviously wouldn’t do for him to keep freeloading at Senior Sister’s place. It was time to establish his own team.

And for that, he needed a territory that was entirely his.

The Tongtian City battle had brought Lin Jing tremendous gains.

Nine Void Mystical Stage experts, dozens of Nascent Soul Transformation cultivators, and hundreds of Nascent Soul Stage beings had perished in Tongtian City. All the magical artifacts, pills, and treasures they carried had ended up in Lin Jing’s hands.

Even with just a rough glance, Lin Jing could tell: while these weren’t the entirety of those cultivators’ and beasts’ assets, it was more than enough to support one or two talented cultivators to reach the Void Mystical Stage.

For someone like him—whose “talent wasn’t great”—even if he couldn’t reach Void Mystical, making it to Nascent Soul Transformation Stage wouldn’t be too difficult.

Even just a portion of these resources was enough to help Lin Jing raise a team of absolute loyalists.

The Demon-Hunting Squad.

The Dragon Carp’s Monster Core Divine Ability required a steady supply of living creatures for alchemical refinement. Lin Jing himself wouldn’t have the time to catch all those beasts, so a personal force became essential.

Instead of relying on outsiders, it was better to train his own squad—one that would remain loyal even if he turned against the Demon Court.

The first person he picked was Gu Qianqiu, the Longevity Girl.

“Mid Core Formation… not bad.”

He arrived at Gu Qianqiu’s cultivation spot.

“Still too slow,” Gu Qianqiu glanced at Lin Jing and shook her head. “Not sure if I can reach Nascent Soul within the time limit…”

“There’s a lot of chaos outside lately. Did something happen?” she asked.

“Yeah, the Beast Taming Sect disciples from the Ancient Kingdom have come to join us.”

Gu Qianqiu nodded slightly. Lin Jing continued, “I’ll be leaving here soon. Want to come with me?”

“Alright.” Gu Qianqiu was surprised, then added, “Honestly, I haven’t wanted to stay in the Demon Court for a long time.”

Lin Jing cleared his throat awkwardly. “I’m not leaving the Demon Court—just leaving the Third Holy Maiden’s territory. Not long ago, the Demon Emperor named me the Holy Son of the Demon Court, so I’m planning to find a new domain of my own.”

Gu Qianqiu: “…”

“Holy Son of the Demon Court???” She looked baffled. Xu Qingyao had become Holy Maiden because of her half-demon bloodline—so what about Lin Jing?

“…You’re half-demon too?”

She had always thought there was something… inhuman about his potential.

Due to her constant cultivation, Gu Qianqiu hadn’t kept up with external news.

Bang.

Lin Jing pulled the Sky Lion Ancestor’s head from his storage bag and tossed it on the ground.

Gu Qianqiu’s pupils shrank.

“I invented the Beast Transformation Technique, earning the Demon Emperor’s trust and the title of Holy Son. Not long ago, the Demon Emperor personally helped me settle all my past enemies—this one included.”

“Your greatest enemy has fallen. What will you do now?”

Staring at the Sky Lion Ancestor’s head, Gu Qianqiu looked dazed.

“This really happened…”

She had never imagined the Sky Lion Clan, who imprisoned her for a thousand years, would one day fall.

Her expression shifted several times. She wanted to cry—but after a millennium of being treated as blood food, she had already lost the ability to shed tears.

After calming her emotions, Gu Qianqiu spoke slowly.

“I once said, when the Sky Lion Clan was destroyed, my life would be yours…”

“Didn’t you say you wanted to build a Demon-Hunting Squad to refine violent beasts into pills… Let me help you.” She looked calmly at Lin Jing.

Lin Jing smiled slightly. “I was waiting for that. From today on, you’re the captain of the Demon-Hunting Squad. But until you reach Nascent Soul, you won’t be participating in the actual hunts.”

“Someone else will take care of that.”

“Someone else?”

“Yeah. Let’s go. The Holy Son’s new territory will have even better cultivation conditions than this place. Our next stop is to recruit the squad’s vice-captain—the Fourth Holy Maiden of the Demon Court, and the first demon to ever be born in this world.”

Soon after—

Lin Jing took Gu Qianqiu to Mosquito Valley. There, the Mosquito Woman and her army of ten billion Mosquito Beasts were already waiting.

“Greetings, Your Highness the Holy Son.” The Mosquito Woman knelt, respectfully facing Lin Jing. Gu Qianqiu looked at the massive mosquito army and felt a wave of awe ripple inside her.

“I’ll be leaving soon. From now on, all of you will follow me to a new home,” Lin Jing said.

The Mosquito Woman and her swarm—controlled via the Seven-Star Beast God Circle—were the second force he planned to bring under his command.

The Mosquito Woman had originally submitted due to the Demon Emperor’s power, agreeing to serve as Lin Jing’s maid. But now that her spiritual intelligence had awakened, she’d slowly develop independent thoughts.

Submitting to the Demon Emperor was out of fear. But moving forward, Lin Jing needed her loyalty to be genuine.

“In the future, you’ll serve as vice-captain of the Demon-Hunting Squad. Do well, and I’ll reward you.” He tossed her a formation flag. “This is a high-grade artifact that can work with your cultivation method. It’s also a storage-type tool, filled with blood from various Nascent Soul-level Demon Kings for your training.”

“Do well, and there will be more—like Demon Emperor blood, or even Demon Ancestor blood.”

“And this here is the squad’s captain, Gu Qianqiu. When I’m not around, follow her orders.”

The Mosquito Woman accepted the flag with delight.

This was one of Lin Jing’s spoils of war—useless to him, but perfect for her.

She now cultivated a Demon Court technique, originally developed by an ancient demon strategist—one specializing in battle formations. The technique, Demon Formation Diagram , allowed her to enhance the power of her mosquito army. It was a perfect match for her leadership role.

“As you command, Holy Son.” The Mosquito Woman was ecstatic. Inside the flag were countless amounts of Nascent Soul Demon King blood—more than enough for her to break through to Nascent Soul herself.

Using this harvested blood to train the Mosquito Beast Army and the Mosquito Woman was ideal.

But after receiving the reward, she instinctively glanced at Gu Qianqiu, a flicker of dissatisfaction in her eyes. This woman wasn’t stronger than her—other than smelling inexplicably delicious and making her want a taste—so why should she be giving the orders?

“I’ll show him… I’ll catch the most beasts and make the Holy Son name me the captain…”

Right now, all the clans of the Demon Court were scrambling to hunt beasts. Their targets were the powerful demonic beast tribes that had plotted against Lin Jing. But even with the Demon Court’s strength, some always slipped through the cracks—making this the perfect time to hunt.

And the Mosquito Beast Army? Ideal for the job.

After dealing with the Mosquito Woman, Lin Jing returned once more to Senior Sister’s palace, packed up his final belongings…

Including one last thing:

A little boy with four white mice perched on his shoulders.

They were Mouse Children, experimental white mice used for testing the Transformation Pill.

Lin Jing didn’t expect them to catch beasts—their strength was far too low—but their abilities were perfect for planting trees and enriching spiritual energy in his new domain.

“Holy Son…”

“Let’s go.” Lin Jing said.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 104: Tree Ancestor
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 104: Tree Ancestor

"Leaving already???"

"Why didn’t you call your big sis?"

Just as Lin Jing was about to depart, someone else in Senior Sister’s palace wanted to tag along.

The Grand Elder of Thousand Illusions Mountain—Huan Li.
