This Nascent Soul Illusion Cultivator had been confined here ever since she saw through Lin Jing’s identity.

Lin Jing glanced at her.

"Just kidding, oh great Holy Son, take me with you," Huan Li chuckled. "You're the Holy Son of the Demon Court—walking around without a proper powerhouse at your side? That’s not a good look."

Lin Jing replied, "You're free now. My identity is no longer a secret. You should return to Thousand Illusions Mountain."

He took out a drop of Ancestor Blood—the blood of the Sky Lion Ancestor—and tossed it to Huan Li. "This is the blood of the Sky Lion Clan’s ancestor. Use it to enhance your Illusion Beast Techniques."

Huan Li accepted the blood and stored it in her storage bag, eyes welling up as she cried like a weeping pear blossom. "I’m not leaving. I’m not! Your Spell Spirit Origin of the Illusion Dao hasn’t gained sentience yet—how can I feel at ease? I must stay by your side and pass on all of my Illusion Dao wisdom..."

Lin Jing let out a light laugh.

With his mastery of the Heavenly Demon Divine Sense Technique, how could he not see through the Grand Elder's little scheme?

This woman might not bear him ill will, but her true intentions? Clear as day.

“Heard five Demon Ancestors died in Tongtian City, and all I get is a drop of blood? You think I’m some beggar?”

“Besides, with the demon race reviving and the Demon Court rising fast, this kid’s future is limitless as the Demon Court Holy Son.”

“I’ve got to cling to his thigh to ensure Thousand Illusions Mountain stays safe through the chaos in Qingzhou.”

“On top of that, he’s got the Longevity Body—he could outlive the Demon Emperor. If he and Xu Qingyao inherit the court, as someone who taught him the Illusion Dao, I’ll definitely go down in history. I can’t leave. I won’t!”

Huan Li shook her head firmly. She was staying.

"Not leaving?"

"Can’t leave. Now’s when Holy Son truly needs people."

"Your new territory needs a protective mountain formation, right? I can do that. My illusion arrays are the perfect fit."

Lin Jing shook his head as well—but fine, he really did need people right now.

The mosquito swarm might have Nascent Soul-level combat strength, but they weren’t unified—there were plenty of ways to counter them.

In contrast, Huan Li, a mid-Nascent Soul stage cultivator with masterful control of the Illusion Dao, was far more useful—even more so than some peak Nascent Soul cultivators.

Of course, she wasn’t as handy as Senior Pine, who could not only guard the mountain but also help ripen spiritual plants.

"This whole uproar... even cultivators from the Ancient Celestial Kingdom’s Beast Taming Sect are showing up in Qingzhou. Where’s Senior Pine?"

"Don’t tell me he got teleported somewhere dangerous and... died?"

He felt uneasy. Maybe it was time to mobilize the Demon Court’s forces to help him look for Senior Pine.

After taking a group of people with him, Lin Jing parted ways with his Senior Sister.

Before they parted, the two conducted one final experiment. Senior Sister found a Core Disciple from the Beast Taming Sect willing to take the risk and fed his contract pet beast a Transformation Pill.

The result? Although the pet beast survived the Transformation Tribulation, the contract between beast and tamer was violently torn during the process. This proved one thing: the Beast Taming Art was incompatible with the demon race.

If a pet beast wanted to continue its transformation, the contract had to be severed. But if the contract remained, the transformation would fail.

In the end, the disciple chose to preserve the contract over his now-demonic girlfriend. Still, both he and his pet beast were heavily injured and would need a long recovery.

"Your guess was right. It doesn’t work." Xu Qingyao sighed.

The demon race had evolved—they were no longer just demonic beasts. They couldn’t be bound by contracts anymore.

"Actually, I’d say the experiment succeeded. It shows that if Sect Master and Senior Brother Ouyang are willing to break their contracts with Sect Master’s wife and Hong’er, their beasts could successfully transform," Lin Jing said.

"That’s true." Senior Sister nodded thoughtfully. "But I suspect they still hope to complete the transformation without breaking the contract."

Lin Jing had no solution for that. "Hard to have both. I think we’ll have to work on improving the Beast Taming Art. After I leave, I’ll research Transformation Pills suitable for the Core Formation and Nascent Soul stages. Let’s see who finishes first—me or them coming out of seclusion."

"When they do, please let them know—not to rush it."

"Don’t worry, I’ve already left a message outside their cave dwellings. Once they see it, they’ll come out."

With Qingzhou in chaos, the Sect Master and Senior Brother Ouyang were still closed off in seclusion. No one could force them out, so all she could do was leave a message and wait.

…

Beyond the sixteen routes of Qingzhou lay a paradise for demonic beasts.

Each major beast clan ruled by a Demon Ancestor controlled rich and fertile territory.

After the deaths of five Demon Ancestors in Tongtian City, the Demon Court seized their domains.

Among those, Lin Jing had his eyes on the territory of the Wood Dragon Clan.

The old ancestor of the clan was a massive, wood-element dragon—sworn brother to the Sky Lion Ancestor—and rumored to have ties to the Forest Sea Dragon Emperor in the Misty Forest. But none of that mattered;
the Demon Court’s ruler had crushed him regardless.

The clan’s homeland was called Immortal Prison Mountain, located at the border between the sixteen Qingzhou routes and the Eastern Sea. The area was rich in both wood and water attributes, with several top-grade spiritual veins underground linking sea and land—perfect for nurturing plants.

It was also said that the mountain had once been bathed in the blood of a Celestial Dragon during an ancient war, producing Dragon Blood Crystals, a rare resource that enhanced dragon bloodlines. Truly a blessed land.

After the Demon Court wiped out the Wood Dragon Clan, Lin Jing moved in and claimed the land. However, due to the recent war, Immortal Prison Mountain was now in ruins. Apart from the spiritual veins, the place had been plundered clean by other demon beast clans.

Still, the foundation was solid. Give it some time to recover, and it would soon return to its former glory.

Upon Lin Jing’s arrival, ten billion mosquitoes spread across the mountain, forming the first defensive layer. Shu Tong led a few Plant Mice to start reforestation and plant Earth Vein Spirit Trees to enrich the spiritual energy. Meanwhile, Gu Qianqiu was sent deep into the veins to carve out a cave dwelling for cultivation.

As for the Grand Elder of Thousand Illusions Mountain, Lin Jing put her to work setting up an illusion array, forming the second defensive layer. With her drawing on the spiritual veins, even a Void Mystical Stage cultivator would have a hard time breaking through in a short time.

While Lin Jing was busy renovating his new base, in the Tianyuan Rift of the void, a massive Void Mystical fluctuation surged forth. Gigantic tree roots pierced through the spatial rift, absorbing nutrients from the void.

"HAHAHAHA! I knew it—I’m Heaven’s chosen one! Thanks to the random treasures I picked up, I’ve broken through to the Void Mystical Stage ahead of schedule!"

"Hang in there, my little Longevity rascal! Don’t be afraid! With me, the Tree Ancestor of the Void Mystical Realm, here now, nobody’s gonna bully you again! To h*ll with that Nine Infant Demon Lord—I’m coming for you next!"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 105: Return of Senior Pine
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 105: Return of Senior Pine

Several months after the chaos in Qingzhou…

A crack tore through the void in a corner of Qingzhou.

And then… a pinecone popped out of the rift.

"This is it."

Senior Pine took a deep breath after emerging from the void.

Back during the Great Transfer incident, it had gifted Lin Jing a leaf capable of concealing fate.

Besides hiding Lin Jing from Fate &
Destiny, that leaf also had a tracking function—allowing Senior Pine to pinpoint his location.

"This place… is it Qingzhou?"

Qingzhou was nothing like the Ancient Celestial Kingdom. It was overrun with demonic beasts and teeming with demonic cultivators. Lin Jing, a mere Longevity Body at the Foundation Establishment Stage, could easily end up as beast chow if he wasn’t careful.

"He should be in that direction!"

Though it could still sense the position of the leaf it gave Lin Jing, Senior Pine was panicking. The leaf might be intact, but that didn’t guarantee Lin Jing was still alive.

"Please don’t be dead…"

Senior Pine sped off toward the sensed location, flying at incredible speed. Just as it was nearing the Wood Dragon Clan’s territory—

BOOM!

A terrifying spatial force suddenly surged toward it, locking down the area and making Senior Pine tense up.

"F**k! Who the h*ll is this Void Mystical powerhouse?!"

It was scared out of its bark. Qingzhou really was as dangerous as rumored. Barely arrived and already being targeted by a peak Void Mystical Realm Demon Ancestor!

"A tree demon? They say the old Wood Dragon Ancestor and the Forest Sea Dragon Emperor were of the same lineage… So, now that the Holy Son has taken over the Wood Dragon Clan’s land, the Forest Sea Dragon Emperor really did send someone to stir up trouble… Tch, bold move—even knowing the Holy Son’s identity!"

In the void, the Kirin Ancestor, who had come on Xu Qingyao’s advice to seek the Transformation Technique from the Holy Son, let out a cold snort. He prepared to capture this intruder and offer it up as a greeting gift.

He was intimately familiar with all the plant-type Void Mystical spirits in Qingzhou—but this pine tree was a stranger. That left only one possibility—it had to be from the Misty Forest, under the Forest Sea Dragon Emperor.

"Wait, this is a misunderstanding!"

Dodging the Kirin Ancestor’s spatial seal, Senior Pine shouted, not wanting to get dragged into a random fight.

"Misunderstanding? You sneak onto the Holy Son’s land and expect me to believe that? What’s your purpose?"

The Kirin Ancestor found that while this pine tree’s cultivation wasn’t high, its spatial affinity was surprisingly advanced. He manifested his true form—his colossal figure blotting out the sky.

"Demon Court? Ah—then it’s definitely a misunderstanding! I’m one of your own, one of your own!" Senior Pine quickly changed his tone.

"You know someone named Xu Qingyao? Is she part of the Demon Court? I watched that girl grow up!"

Then it hit him—back when he was at the Beast Taming Sect, a few powerful demon beasts had shown up and taken away the Sect Master's adopted daughter.

That group had been members of the Demon Court!

He didn’t know how far Xu Qingyao had risen in the Demon Court, but that was the only connection he could think of right now.

The Kirin Ancestor furrowed his brows. "Who are you, really? Are you a cultivator from the Tianyuan Realm?"

"Oh come on—" Senior Pine was startled again. Why did it feel like everyone could tell he was from the Tianyuan Realm? That shouldn't be so easy to see through.

What he didn’t realize was that Xu Qingyao becoming the personal disciple of the Demon Court’s ruler was hardly a secret to ancient old monsters like this one.

But Senior Pine quickly recovered—clearly the Kirin Ancestor was just suspicious, not certain. "I’ve been in secluded cultivation too long, out of touch with the world… Wait, you’re telling me Xu Qingyao's got that kind of status in the Demon Court now?"

"That’s right. Back then, they said she had traces of demonic bloodline, so they took her away…"

"You really know Her Highness the Holy Maiden?" the Kirin Ancestor asked, skeptical but curious. "Could you be from the Ancient Kingdom?"

"More than that—I’m the Guardian Pine of the Beast Taming Sect! I can't believe that little girl actually became the Holy Maiden of the Demon Court!!" Senior Pine was stunned.

The Demon Court—the top faction among demonic beasts.

That adopted daughter of Xu Xiaoyao really hit the jackpot.

If that was the case, then had the Beast Taming Sect settled in Qingzhou under Xu Qingyao’s banner?

And what about Lin Jing?

Had he also taken refuge under his Senior Sister?

"No—wait—this is bad." Senior Pine’s expression changed. He realized something.

If Lin Jing was desperate, he might’ve really gone to the Demon Court for protection.

But the Demon Court’s internal politics had to be messy as h*ll.

Lin Jing had the Longevity Body—if a high-ranking beast took a liking to that physique, not even Xu Qingyao might be able to protect him.

Just now, this Kirin Ancestor said the place ahead belonged to the Demon Court’s Holy Son.

And in that territory, Senior Pine had sensed the presence of the item he left with Lin Jing.

A terrible thought crossed his mind—what if his “little Longevity rascal” had been captured by the Holy Son… and turned into blood food?

The Demon Court’s Holy Son could definitely rival or even overpower the Holy Maiden.

"Whether you're one of us or not… I’ll have to check with His Highness the Holy Son first." The Kirin Ancestor pulled out a token. A moment later, an image appeared on the token—Lin Jing’s projection.

"Kirin Ancestor?" Lin Jing’s image spoke.

At the time, Lin Jing had been guarding the Ice Soul Gu in seclusion. But then Xu Qingyao messaged him saying the Kirin Clan wanted to quickly obtain the Transformation Technique.

Considering they’d gifted him the crucial Heavenly Thunder Flame, Lin Jing had opened a back door, letting them come retrieve some Transformation Pills in person. He had originally planned to have one of his clones receive them.

He hadn't expected the Kirin Clan to send the Ancestor himself.

Now it felt improper to send just a clone to greet him.

After all, the Kirin Ancestor was practically one hoof away from the Dao-Seeking Stage—a peak Void Mystical Realm demon ancestor.

"Your Highness, this tree here was acting suspiciously near your domain. I found it rather suspect," the Kirin Ancestor reported, glancing over at the stunned Senior Pine.

At that moment, Senior Pine stared blankly at Lin Jing’s projection, who the Kirin Ancestor had respectfully addressed as Holy Son.

Wait—what???

Lin Jing is the Holy Son?!

"Senior Pine?!" Lin Jing also noticed the familiar figure beside the Kirin Ancestor, his face lighting up with surprise and joy.

"You finally made it! I was just about to send someone out to search for you. Wait—hold on!! You—You actually broke through to the Void Mystical Stage?!"

"Why the h*ll are you so shocked?!" Senior Pine barked. "And since when did you become the Holy Son of the Demon Court?! I get it now—it’s a dmn heart tribulation. I must not have fully passed the Void Mystical Tribulation yet!"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 106: Ancient Mystery
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 106: Ancient Mystery

Shen Song felt like he was still dreaming.

But once Lin Jing brought Senior Shen Song into the palace and explained in full how he had ended up in Qingzhou, Shen Song realized he really was dreaming.

"You’re telling me… you taught the Longevity Body demon girl raised by the Sky Lion Clan?"

"And then she smashed the Sky Lion Clan’s treasure vault and let countless treasures spill into the void?"

Shen Song’s face looked strange. Yep, definitely dreaming. No way all this was real—it was too much of a coincidence.

"Senior Shen Song, you're not dreaming."

"I really have become the Holy Son of the Demon Court. That mosquito woman sitting on the mountain peak outside? You must’ve seen her."

"And yes, the Transformation Technique was created through me. The alchemy event in Tongtian City—just ask any cultivator in Qingzhou, and they can rattle off every detail." Lin Jing smiled.

"...."

What Shen Song found hardest to accept was: If all of this is true… doesn’t that mean the batch of resources I stumbled upon—the very ones that let me break through to the Void Mystical Stage early—were all thanks to this kid?

"Give me a moment to process this..."

Seeing Shen Song's overwhelmed expression, Lin Jing laughed. He’d long wanted to see this very look on Senior Shen Song's face.

He was surprised Shen Song had managed to break through to the Void Mystical Stage, sure—but clearly, everything he had done in Qingzhou felt even more fantastical from Shen Song’s perspective.

"Yeah, laugh it up, you little sh*t."

Mo Song, seeing Void Mystical Lin Jing giving off that “seventh-gen immortal” sort of relaxed vibe, wanted nothing more than to pour a bucket of cold water over him.

"His experience really is impressive," Mo Song muttered. "Getting the Demon Emperor’s backing means you can stop worrying for now. But… he’s getting happy too soon."

"He thinks just because he has the Demon Emperor’s protection, everything’s smooth sailing?"

"Hmm?" Lin Jing raised a brow. "Senior Shen Song, what’s he trying to say…?"

Without the Demon Emperor’s support, Lin Jing figured nothing in the Tianyuan Realm could really threaten him anymore. After all, the Demon Emperor’s power was definitely top-tier in the realm right now.

But something about Shen Song’s serious tone didn’t feel like a joke—Lin Jing suspected he was aiming at something deeper.

"You were transported to..."

Shen Song then finished recounting his own story prior to his arrival, not hiding the fact that he was the reincarnation of the Demonic Sky Sect Leader—a being from beyond this realm.

When Lin Jing finished listening, he fell completely silent.

"So… you’re a reincarnated soul, no longer affiliated with the Demonic Sky Sect."

"And from that remnant demon soul you encountered after its failed ascension, you learned a lot—including why people can’t ascend to the Upper Realm right now?!"

"What is it?!" Meng Da hadn’t paid much mind to Shen Song’s background—after all, he too was an outsider.

But the reason behind the blocked ascension path? That piqued his curiosity.

Even though he longed to reach the Immortal Realm, something this major had to be tied to a massive shift in the balance between realms!

"If you know the Demonic Sky Sect Leader was originally from another cultivation world, do you know what his purpose was in coming here?"

Meng Da shook his head.

Shen Song looked to the sky. "Just as the Tianyuan Realm is an affiliated territory beneath the Immortal Palace in the Upper Realm, the Demonic Sky Sect’s headquarters—the Demonic Sky World—is also a subordinate realm tied to a powerful demonic sect in the Immortal Realm."

"Countless years ago, the Demonic Sky Sect in the Immortal Realm gave birth to a terrifying Demon Lord with peerless talent and strength. Through a powerful divine technique, he severed the connections between the Immortal Realm’s factions and the Lower Realm, hoping to fuse the entire Lower Realm into one mega-world. His goal? Refine it into his own Dao simulation and break past his limits."

"But limited by the current heavenly laws, immortals couldn’t descend into the Lower Realm. So that Demon Lord had to rely on his sect’s forces in the Lower Realm to wage war and refine world after world."

"The Demonic Sky World is full of terrifying experts... thanks to the cross-realm arts passed down by the Demon Lord himself, they could easily travel to other worlds. Their sole mission: seize complete control over each target realm."

"Then you, senior..." Lin Jing looked toward Mo Song. "Given all this, your mission here in the Tianyuan Realm was—"

"Correct. I was supposed to conquer this realm as well. But as you can see, my past self was killed by the First Ancient Emperor, and I could only reincarnate into who I am now," said Shen Song.

Lin Jing: "..."

"And according to that demon soul remnant, if the Demonic Sky Sect agents dispatched to a realm fail to conquer it within the designated time, the sect will send even stronger cultivators to take over."

"In other words, when my past self was destroyed, that mission was considered a failure. The Demonic Sky Sect probably already knows, and who knows when they’ll dispatch a more terrifying force to re-invade the Tianyuan Realm."

"When that happens, forget about the Demon Emperor protecting you. She may not even be able to save herself. A Longevity Body like yours? That’s the first type of resource the Demonic Sky Sect collects whenever they invade a new world."

Lin Jing: ???

"Senior, you just got back and you’re already bringing news this awful?" Lin Jing rubbed his temples. He had just latched onto the Demon Emperor’s thick golden thigh!

"A pretty hopeless future, huh?" said Shen Song with a chuckle. "Especially for you ."

"Crossing realms isn’t easy—it takes time. A thousand years, maybe ten thousand. For current cultivators in the Tianyuan Realm, they might die of old age before the next invasion even happens. But you —you’ll still be around when it comes."

Lin Jing exhaled deeply. "I know you’re telling me all this because you already have a plan to deal with it, right?"

"Heh." Shen Song grinned and pulled out a massive golden bell. "I did get some good stuff from that demon soul remnant."

"This here is a realm-tier treasure. Once activated, the cultivation world it’s used in will enter a ‘Golden Era’ for ten thousand years—immortal fortune will flourish, and prodigies will emerge like mushrooms after rain, even Immortal Physiques won’t be rare. But once those ten thousand years pass, the realm will fall into a ‘Post-Spiritual Age,’ where even reaching Nascent Soul will be near impossible. This treasure squeezes the world’s potential dry."

"With this, we might be able to massively boost the Tianyuan Realm’s strength in the near future—to fend off the Demonic Sky World’s invasion."

"But activating it requires at least ten Dao-Seeking Stage cultivators to power it at the same time. Now that you’re the Holy Son of the Demon Court, you should consider offering this to the Demon Emperor—see if she can gather enough people to juice up the Tianyuan Realm."

Lin Jing was stunned, and looked at Senior Shen Song. "But wait—if you’re technically a cultivator from the Demonic Sky World, why are you standing firmly on the side of the Tianyuan Realm and fighting back against the sect?"

Shen Song replied, "Heh. During my breakthrough to the Void Mystical Stage, I saw fragments of my past life. Turns out I wasn’t originally from the Demonic Sky World—I was taken from another realm during an invasion, brainwashed, and trained into a genius."

"But my race is… special. I was never fully brainwashed. And for the whole mission, I just went through the motions. The Demonic Sky Sect conquered my hometown and still expected me to work for them? As if I’d go along with that crap."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 107: Transcendence into Sainthood
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 107: Transcendence into Sainthood

The intelligence brought by Senior Shen Song left Lin Jing silent for a long time.

As expected... there's no such thing as absolute peace in this world.

He sighed. “Senior Shen Song, what happened to the remnant demon soul you encountered in the void?”

“Naturally, it went to reincarnate.”

“In any case, that old fox helped me quite a bit. He gave me the Golden Bell because he hoped that after being reborn into the Tianyuan Realm, he could grow up during its Golden Era.”

“So... you’d better not have any kids anytime soon. Wouldn’t want to accidentally end up giving birth to an ancient monster that’s lived for ten thousand years.”

Lin Jing forced a smile. “What kind of nonsense is that…”

“I get the gist now. Senior Shen Song, you’ve only recently broken through to the Void Mystical Stage—do you need some time to consolidate your realm? I can arrange a cave dwelling for you.”

“My Ice Soul Lesser Gu is undergoing a breakthrough. I need to be there for it.”

“I know you're busy. When two Void Mystical Demon Ancestors visited, you even brushed them off with a clone.”

He instantly saw through it—this Shen Song wasn’t meeting the real Lin Jing, but a wood-element avatar. The real one was likely still in seclusion, guarding the Gu’s advancement.

But really, none of this was urgent. Shen Song hadn’t even planned to tell Lin Jing about any of it this early.

After all, the invasion of demons from outside the realm didn’t have much to do with a Foundation Establishment cultivator like Lin Jing. If the sky fell, taller people would be there to hold it up.

Originally, Shen Song had planned to wait until he reached the Dao-Seeking Stage before setting the Golden Era in motion. With the help of that Dao-Seeking demon, he’d recovered a lot of past-life memories. With those inheritances, he figured he could probably reach the Dao-Seeking Stage within a thousand years.

But now that Lin Jing had become the Holy Son of the Demon Court, Shen Song decided to tell him in advance. After all, if the Golden Era could be activated earlier, his own cultivation speed would increase as well—he might only need a hundred years to reach Dao-Seeking.

“After all, there’s no precedent for a Gu devouring another Gu. I need to see it for myself to feel at ease,” Lin Jing said with a chuckle.

“That spider’s really living the high life, sticking with you.”

Soon after, under Lin Jing’s wood clone’s arrangements, Senior Shen Song settled down on this mountain to consolidate his breakthrough.

As for the Kirin Ancestor, he too had been sent off by another of Lin Jing’s clones—with a few ordinary Transformation Pills as a parting gift.

Just like that, two more months passed.

Deep within a supreme water-type spiritual vein—no, it should be called an ice-type vein now. To make it more suitable for the Ice Soul Lesser Gu’s cultivation, Lin Jing had transformed the water vein using various ice-type spiritual items from heaven and earth.

At this moment, within the supreme ice vein, a cocoon made of spider silk was trembling slightly. Lin Jing sat cross-legged outside the vein, his divine sense constantly monitoring it.

He had initially worried something might go wrong during the process of devouring the Thunder Tribulation Gu. But thankfully, the Demon Emperor had personally erased all its consciousness and power, reverting it to its purest form. This had made things much easier for the Ice Soul Lesser Gu.

“So delicious.”

“Mmhm~ So good.”

“I’ve never had anything this tasty in my life…”

As a bittersweet voice echoed, spiritual power suddenly surged from within the ice vein. Lin Jing stood up, and with a boom , the cocoon exploded into a shower of white silk, resembling a meteor shower made of snow-white threads.

From within that dazzling scene, a small crystalline spider emerged, its body flashing with subtle arcs of lightning.

“You did it,” Ice Soul muttered with a faint smirk as she glanced toward the Dragonblood Carp Gu.

Cultivation Level: Early Core Formation Stage!

And at the same time, a new Monster Core Divine Ability had been born within her!

[Monster Core Divine Ability – Transcendence into Sainthood!]

Lin Jing’s eyes narrowed. This was quite the divine ability.

The Thunder Tribulation Gu had possessed a unique trait: using its electric Gu poison to stimulate the host’s body, forcibly pushing their physical capabilities past their natural limits—unlocking bursts of power, defense, and reflexes beyond their realm.

Back then, Elder Thunder Gu had used this very technique to break free from the Demon Emperor’s suppression.

However, such a method put tremendous strain on the body and often caused it to start deteriorating after combat.

Now, after being devoured by the Ice Soul Lesser Gu, that same method had evolved. By fusing in her extreme frost power, she could balance movement with stillness, soothing the physical overload and easing the side effects.

This new divine ability, Transcendence into Sainthood, was the perfect fusion of motion and stillness—granting Ice Soul a powerful Gu poison that could boost another lifeform’s physical strength with zero side effects!

If the target’s cultivation wasn’t too high, it could even boost them up by a full major realm.

“This divine ability… is really impressive,” Lin Jing couldn’t help but say. Once he finished tempering his body with Thunder Dragon scale blood, combining it with Ice Soul’s Gu poison might actually allow him to physically ascend to sainthood someday!

“Impressive, huh…” Ice Soul felt nothing but sadness inside.

“I still can’t beat those two…”

This divine ability was strong, sure—but it required a host or pet to be effective. On her own, it didn’t boost her combat strength by much.

That meant she was still no match for the Pine Leaf Mouse or the Dragonblood Carp.

Even though the Dragonblood Carp’s core ability was also more of a support skill, in Ice Soul’s eyes, the Carp’s rare beast divine powers were just too overwhelming.

Especially after the Tongtian City battle—Lin Jing and the Carp had used Ghostfire to burn the souls of countless cultivators.

Across the entire Tianyuan Realm, the only person who might surpass the Dragonblood Carp in Ghostfire mastery was likely the Dao-Seeking Hall Master of the Netherworld Hall.

Those ancient Void Mystical elders, with thousands of years under their belts, probably couldn’t produce ghostfire of the same caliber. And once the Carp infused it with the Flame Emperor’s divine arts and other fire types, it had reached the level of quasi-immortal flame . That wasn’t something from the mortal world anymore.

Then there was the Pine Leaf Mouse.

Though the mouse’s rare beast ability wasn’t a battle skill, and its Monster Core Divine Ability wasn’t some invincible Flame Emperor-type technique—its power scaled directly with the number and quality of Five-Element items in its internal space.

The problem was… after the Tongtian City battle, Lin Jing looted every storage bag from his defeated enemies and stuffed the Pine Leaf Mouse’s inner space full of top-tier Five-Element treasures.

Now, the sheer quantity and quality of elemental items in the mouse’s core space had reached a ridiculous level. It could no longer be called an ordinary ability—it was something monstrous.

“Don’t worry about it. I’ll feed you more rare Gu in the future—you’ll catch up to them eventually.” Lin Jing chuckled and also suppressed his own urge to break through.

The feedback from Ice Soul’s advancement had given him the ability to attempt Core Formation, but before going into seclusion, he still had something important to do.

He took out the tiger fang the Demon Emperor had given him—a token that allowed direct contact—and channeled his spiritual power into it.

“Your Majesty the Demon Emperor, bad news—really bad news...”

Demon Court.

The Demon Emperor, deep in research on her clan’s inheritances, furrowed her brow.

“You’re the Holy Son of the Demon Court now. You represent our honor—what kind of disaster could make you panic like this?”

She was well aware of Lin Jing’s background: joined the Beast Taming Sect, it got sealed;
joined the Academy, it shut down…

Now that he’d joined the Demon Court, she was confident she could protect this Longevity Body.

The Demon Court was vast and mighty—one of the top factions in the Greater Yuan Realm. Surely it wouldn’t be shaken just because of a single Longevity Body.

She hoped that as the Holy Son, Lin Jing would develop the proper composure—to stay calm no matter the crisis.

“Tianyuan Realm might be facing total annihilation!” Lin Jing called out.

The Demon Emperor: “...?”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 108: Sea of Falling Stars
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 108: Sea of Falling Stars

The Tianyuan Continent is facing a catastrophic disaster…

Hearing those words come from a mere Foundation Establishment cultivator’s mouth—meant to warn her, the ruler of the Demon Court—left the Demon Emperor with a strange, surreal feeling.

“You’ve gone mad from cultivation.”

Her first instinct wasn’t to believe Lin Jing, but to suspect that he had fallen into qi deviation.

After all, cultivating Ghostfire Techniques and the Heavenly Demon Divine Sense, Lin Jing had killed quite a few people.

The resentment of those powerful cultivators at death would surely turn into heart demons, interfering with his future cultivation.

After the battle in Tongtian City ended, the Demon Emperor had once asked Lin Jing about his mental state.

Lin Jing claimed that with the support of Buddhist teachings, heart demons wouldn’t be able to affect him.

The Demon Emperor thought—maybe she needed to take the Holy Son on a trip to the Western Desert and ask the Buddha of the Sun Temple to purify him.

“Come.”

With a wave of her hand, the Demon Emperor used her spatial divine ability to summon Lin Jing from the edge of Qingzhou directly before her.

“Your Majesty.”

Lin Jing gave a slight bow, and the Demon Emperor examined him carefully before speaking slowly: “You don’t look like there’s anything wrong.”

“Your Majesty, I have something I’d like you to look at.” Lin Jing knew that what he was about to say sounded too far-fetched, so he simply took out a crystal.

“A Memory Crystal?” The Demon Emperor recognized it instantly. It was a materialized memory, allowing others to vividly experience fragments of someone’s past.

At first, as she held the crystal, the Demon Emperor showed no reaction.

Through the crystal, she saw through the eyes of a fox-type beast from a place called the Sea of Falling Stars—a realm under the Immortal Realm’s jurisdiction.

Unlike other cultivation worlds, the Sea of Falling Stars existed in a lawless zone—belonging to no Immortal power, yet closest to the Immortal Realm and most directly connected to it.

But this proximity didn’t bring it prosperity. The Sea of Falling Stars was a harsh, disaster-prone land, unsuitable for cultivation or survival.

In the end, it was repurposed as a dumping ground—used by the Immortal Realm to discard filth, cursed items, and toxic pollutants. The Immortals would toss their trash here and call it disposal.

Some Immortals who had offended powerful factions would even sever their immortality, return to mortal form, and escape to this realm closest to the Immortal Realm.

Thus, it came to be known as the “Trash Heap of the Immortal Realm.”

And yet, despite its filth and danger, many high-level cultivators from lower realms would come here to scavenge for treasures.

Though technically all trash, it was still Immortal Realm trash. Over time, the Sea of Falling Stars became a hub for scavengers.

The owner of this memory was one such beast, born in the Sea of Falling Stars.

After years of hardship, it managed to cultivate to the Dao-Seeking Stage through sheer fortune and perseverance.

There, it encountered countless experts from other cultivation realms—and even witnessed the downfall of entire cultivation worlds.

It also uncovered secrets of the Immortal Realm that even Dao-Seeking-level experts in normal realms could never access.

Including the real reasons many cultivation worlds can no longer ascend—

And the true force behind it all: the ambitions of the Demonic Sky Sect!

However, based on the memory, the Sea of Falling Stars itself didn’t seem to be among the realms that couldn’t ascend…

Due to its special geography, one could smuggle their way into the Immortal Realm from there without passing through a standard Ascension Calamity.

But such a route was far more dangerous than ascension.

The spatial turbulence was nightmarish. Even if one survived by sheer luck, they’d arrive in the Immortal Realm without undergoing any transformation or becoming a true immortal.

They’d still have to cultivate slowly—plus, they’d be a rogue cultivator with no official affiliation or foothold, making it extremely dangerous.

As such, unless in absolute desperation, no one chose to ascend via the Sea of Falling Stars.

This particular fox-beast had been forced into that choice—on the run from powerful enemies.

It was incredibly unlucky… and yet also lucky.

In the end, it survived the chaotic void currents with just a sliver of its soul intact.

Eventually, that soul fragment drifted near the Tianyuan Realm…

And countless years later, during the Great Transfer, it met the Guardian Deity Pine of the Beast Taming Sect, who had accidentally entered the Void.

Until Pine returned and brought back the intelligence he’d received from the beast’s remnant soul.

“The Immortal Realm’s trash heap, the Sea of Falling Stars…

Severing the connection between upper and lower realms… the Demonic Sky Sect’s plot to devour and refine all lower realms…

The path to ascension…” the Demon Emperor murmured.

“No wonder…”

It was the first time she’d heard of this peculiar star realm, but even so, she believed it existed.

Because of the First Ancient Emperor…

Back then, the First Ancient Emperor wasn’t close to the end of his lifespan, yet still chose to fully cultivate the Second Ancient Emperor and hand over the Ancient Kingdom.

Many speculated that he was entering final seclusion to attempt ascension.

Some believed he had already ascended.

But in truth, very few knew—the First Ancient Emperor had forcibly broken through the Tianyuan Realm’s barrier and entered the stars.

Now, the Demon Emperor speculated: perhaps after destroying the Demonic Sky Sect, the First Ancient Emperor obtained intel about the Sea of Falling Stars—and decided to go there, to attempt ascension.

But she didn’t need to think too hard to know that such a journey was nearly suicidal.

Forget actually ascending in the Sea of Falling Stars—even finding it in the vast starry void was like finding a needle in a haystack.

Without precise coordinates, it was practically impossible.

In fact, not just the Sea of Falling Stars—stumbling upon any other cultivation realm out there was astronomically unlikely.

Each cultivation realm had its own World Boundary Wall,

making it hard to exit, even harder to enter.

These barriers also served as protective membranes, shielding cultivation worlds from star beasts roaming the void.

“So the Tianyuan Realm can’t ascend because of the Demonic Sky Sect’s interference.”

“That explains everything,” the Demon Emperor analyzed.

“There are rumors that some mysterious stars in the sky are the remains of fallen immortals—passing on divine abilities.

And the timing of their appearances… coincides with when ascension in the lower realms became impossible.”

“I used to wonder why Immortals would pass on divine techniques that way.

But now I suspect—it might be that some in the Immortal Realm are trying to strengthen the lower realms through these methods.

If nothing else, it would buy time to resist the Demonic Sky Sect’s invasion.

Delay their success—

Until those in the Immortal Realm can resolve the root of the problem: the Demon Lord himself.”

“But there’s still one thing I don’t understand—why can only demonic beasts inherit Immortal divine powers…

Could it be that this method of cross-realm transmission was created by a powerful beast Immortal?”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 3: Chapter 109: The Rise of a Golden Era (End of Book 3)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 3 Chapter 109: The Rise of a Golden Era (End of Volume 3)

The Demon Emperor’s speculation wasn’t without reason.

There had to be a purpose behind the Immortal Realm aiding lower-realm demonic beasts in evolving into Rare Beasts.

If the goal was to help the lower realm better resist the Demonic Sky Sect, then that explanation made sense.

As for why only demonic beasts were able to inherit Immortal divine abilities, the Demon Emperor’s theory was one possibility. Perhaps this plan was spearheaded by a powerful beast cultivator from the Immortal Realm.

Another possibility was that the cost of such inheritances was simply too high. After all, the limited number of Rare Beasts spoke volumes.

And for the human race, they didn’t need these inheritances as desperately as demonic beasts did.

Because humans were already favored by Heavenly Fortune...

The offspring of demonic beasts at most experienced bloodline mutations, evolutions, or reversion to ancestral forms.

Some of the top-tier physiques granted divine abilities that were no weaker than those passed down by Immortals to Rare Beasts.

Once Senior Pine returned with the “Golden Bell” and the Tianyuan Realm entered its Golden Era, although all races would benefit, humanity would clearly gain the most from it.

“You mean the catastrophe facing the Tianyuan Realm is the future return of the Demonic Sky Sect?”

“Exactly. The Tianyuan Realm has already faced the Demonic Sky Sect once before. Although the First Ancient Emperor wiped them out back then, the fact that they severed the Tianyuan Realm’s connection to the Immortal Palace shows how determined they are.”

“They’re not the kind to give up after a single failure.”

“But... when the Demonic Sky Sect will return is uncertain. They’re at war with several cultivation worlds at once—it’s impossible to predict their next move.”

The Demon Emperor fell into slight contemplation.

“That’s what makes it so troublesome...

“If they return now, the Tianyuan Realm wouldn’t even be able to resist.

“But if they come back several thousand years later, the situation might be different.”

“Exactly,” the Ancient Emperor nodded. “The current top powerhouses of the Tianyuan Realm are all survivors from the Immortal Alliance era thousands of years ago. They’ve lived through two eras, possess ten-thousand-year-old cultivation bases, and have witnessed countless upheavals and shifts in power. The fact that they’re still alive speaks volumes of their strength.”

“But a few thousand years from now, those experts will inevitably pass away. Whether the next generation of cultivators can match them is still unknown.”

Just take the Seventh Generation Lin Jing as an example—his strength is already far from that of the First Generation Yan Si.

What about the Eighth Generation Lin Jing—will that strength slip even further?

No one could say.

The First Generation of cultivators like Yan Si were warriors forged during the chaotic end of the Immortal Alliance era.

But those born into peaceful times, without the immense pressure, may not be able to cultivate powerful divine abilities.

The average lifespan during the Dao-Seeking Stage could reach a few thousand years, unless one had the Longevity Body.

Even then, unless you had an extraordinary means of lifespan extension, death still awaited in the end.

If the Demonic Sky Sect invades before those top-tier experts pass away, then the Tianyuan Realm will still be safe.

“You don’t look like you have any good solutions,” the Demon Emperor looked toward the Ancient Emperor.

“Senior Pine brought back a treasure from the Void,” Lin Jing finally brought up the Golden Bell.

He took out the Golden Bell for the Demon Emperor to inspect. Before Lin Jing could explain what it was, the Demon Emperor had already recognized it.

“I’ve seen this in the ancient records of the Immortal Palace—it's a Treasure of Fortune... said to originate from Immortal crafting methods.”

“Never thought something like this would still exist in the Void.”

Lin Jing was speechless. The Void really was full of treasures. After all, countless artifacts flowed into the Void every moment.

Whenever spatially sealed treasure vaults were forcibly broken into, most of the resources inside would get swept into the Void.

The treasury of the Sky Lion Clan was one such case.

This Golden Era Bell was one of the items picked up by a remnant beast soul drifting outside the Void for years.

“So what you’re saying is, we should activate the Tianyuan Realm’s Golden Era. Forget about what happens ten thousand years from now—let’s focus on raising the realm’s power during that time.”

Lin Jing nodded slightly.

“Since the Tianyuan Realm will suffer either way, we might as well make sure the Demonic Sky Sect doesn’t take its origin power and turn the realm into a discarded husk. In that case, even a Post-Spiritual Era would be a decent outcome.”

“After all, the Post-Spiritual Era won’t last forever. As long as the Tianyuan Realm gets enough time to recover, spiritual energy will return, and the realm can regain its former glory.”

“And during these ten thousand years, we can prepare thoroughly—store up enough resources so that even in the Post-Spiritual Era, the Demon Court will have enough to sustain itself and continue cultivating.”

The Demon Emperor fell silent.

Having just revived the demon race, she really didn’t want to start thinking so hard already.

“This whole situation... the one who should be most concerned is the Second Ancient Emperor.”

“After all, the Ancient Kingdom was founded on Heavenly Fortune. If the Tianyuan Realm is invaded, the Ancient Kingdom will be the first to suffer.

“When that happens, both the Second Ancient Emperor and the entire ancient clan’s strength will plummet.

“The very foundation of the Ancient Kingdom might collapse.”

“And if the Golden Era is initiated, the biggest beneficiary will be the Ancient Kingdom. So even if it’s going to happen, it shouldn’t be led by our Demon Court.”

A flash passed through the Demon Emperor’s eyes.

“If we let the Second Ancient Emperor know that the Ancient Kingdom stands to gain the most from this Golden Era, then both the event and this treasure could be shared with him.

“And in return, we can extract a hefty price from the ancient clan.”

“That way, whether it’s during the Golden Era or afterward in the Post-Spiritual Era, the Demon Court will have enough resources to secure its interests.”

With her tiger-like face and stout build, the Demon Emperor folded her arms behind her back and began pacing, pushing her brain to its limits.

Born with Rare Beast divine powers and an Immortal inheritance, protected by the previous Demon Emperor, she’d always solved problems with brute force—smashing everything in her path.

She’d never experienced true setbacks before.

But there was one thing she knew for certain—now that the hope of the demon race’s revival was finally in sight, now that the Demon Court was on the verge of becoming the second great power of the Tianyuan Realm, she absolutely didn’t want it all to crumble in her hands.

So, the Golden Era—she believed it had to be started.

Even if humanity would benefit the most, the demon race wouldn’t be far behind.

After all, demon beasts and humans could intermarry and produce offspring.

These half-demon children could inherit both cultivation physiques from humans and bloodline powers from demonic beasts and demon clans.

From that angle, the Demon Court was also a key beneficiary of the Golden Era.

And since the era could be started by anyone—as long as they had ten Dao-Seekers—she figured selling the Golden Bell to the Ancient Emperor and letting him gather the manpower might be more profitable.

After all, the Ancient Kingdom was filthy rich, and the Demon Emperor found herself very tempted. No way she was letting the Ancient Kingdom profit for free.

“Leave the Golden Bell to me,” she said.

“Looks like it’s time to have a chat with the Second Ancient Emperor.”

“This discovery—you and that pine tree get top credit. Your rewards will come soon.

“If the Golden Era begins smoothly, your path as a Longevity Body cultivator will go even smoother.”

“I’ll send you back for now…”

“How’s the research on the Transformation Pill going?”

Lin Jing replied, “Your Majesty, I plan to break through to the Core Formation stage first. With a higher cultivation base, pill refining will go more smoothly.”

The Demon Emperor nodded.

“I heard it took you ten years to break through from Qi Refinement to Foundation Establishment. You should focus on researching the Transformation Pill first. Once the Golden Era begins, it won’t be too late to break through then. By that time, you might even develop an even stronger Core Formation divine ability, and your closed-door cultivation could be much shorter.”

Lin Jing gave a bitter smile. How did the Demon Emperor even know it took him ten years to reach Foundation Establishment?

Whatever, she had a point.

After reporting everything to the Demon Emperor, Lin Jing didn’t concern himself with the matter anymore.

After all, opening the Golden Era required ten Dao-Seeking Stage cultivators. That kind of matter was best left to the top experts of the Tianyuan Continent.

As for him, he returned to his palace and continued his research on the Transformation Pill.

Until—

Six months later.

Lin Jing vaguely sensed some changes stirring within the Tianyuan Continent.

But what truly astonished him was what appeared in the skies above the Tianyuan Continent: a massive golden list hovering across the firmament.

At the same time, a voice filled with authority—like the voice of the Heavenly Dao itself—echoed throughout the Tianyuan Continent, entering the ears of every cultivator.

The voice seemed to belong to the Second Ancient Emperor.

“This is the Golden Fortune List. It will record the names of the Tianyuan Continent’s top prodigies.

All who make the list shall be favored by the fortune of the realm—gaining help in cultivation, breakthroughs, and enlightenment.

The list ranks the top geniuses of every race by strength.

Defeat someone on the list, and you can seize their fortune and enhance your own.

The Golden Era begins.

All of you—do your best.”

With the appearance of the Golden Fortune List, many cultivators looked shocked, not understanding what was happening.

Some were aware that the Ancient Kingdom had its own Genius Rankings, but this—this list involving the entire continent’s fortune—what exactly was it?

Why had the Ancient Kingdom created something like this?

Soon, more and more names began appearing on the golden list.

The very first name belonged to none other than the widely renowned Ancient Imperial Prince—the Crown Prince of the Ancient Clan—already at the early Nascent Soul Stage.

After him came the top-tier prodigies from other Dao-Seeking-level factions—the most elite of second-generation Immortals.

There were a total of one hundred names on the list.

Lin Jing glanced at it and saw that his own name wasn’t there.

Even the person ranked one hundred had already reached the Nascent Soul Stage—not a single Core Formation cultivator had made it, let alone someone like him who was just at Peak Foundation Establishment.

All those on the list shared one thing in common—their age.

They were all in the prime phase of their cultivation years, still rapidly growing.

The Second Ancient Emperor had used the True Dragon Fortune Secret Art to refine the Golden Bell a second time, creating this list so that prodigies with greater talent could better reap the benefits of the Golden Era.

“It’s not unlike your Hidden Dragon Secret Art,”

A voice rang beside the Ancient Emperor’s ears—it was the Demon Emperor, letting him know that perhaps all the factions had now come to an agreement.

“The Golden Era and the Golden Fortune List, huh…”

Yan Si turned and walked back into her cave dwelling.

She hadn’t paid much attention to the disputes leading up to the Golden Era’s activation, but she had finally concluded her journey toward Core Formation.

“Pine Leaf Mouse, Dragonblood Carp, Ice Soul Gu...

Stand guard for me.”

“I’m entering seclusion—to form my Golden Core!”

End of Volume Three: [Chaos in Qingzhou]

Next Volume: [Heir of the Demonic Sky Sect]

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 1: Emerging from Seclusion
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 1: Emerging from Seclusion

The opening of the Golden Era brought immense benefits to many cultivators.

For some powerful-bloodline demonic beasts, reproducing and producing offspring was extremely difficult.

For high-level cultivators, ensuring that their descendants perfectly inherited their cultivation talents was also a major challenge.

But not long after the Golden Era began, second-generation Royal-Blooded and Reversion-bloodline beasts began popping up across the continent like bamboo shoots after the rain.

With each newborn, all kinds of divine physiques and strange phenomena manifested throughout the Tianyuan Continent.

The Tianyuan Realm had truly entered its prime. And it wasn’t just the new generation—

Even the older generation felt their bottlenecks loosening with the arrival of this Golden Era.

At the number one sword sect in the realm—the Heavenly Sword Gate of the Ancient Kingdom—

Finally, in the third year of the Golden Era, he shattered his bottleneck and became the first cultivator in a millennium to successfully reach the Dao-Seeking Stage.

The reason Heavenly Sword Gate was only the second strongest sect of the Ancient Kingdom, rather than the first, was because the Spirit Dao Sect had a Dao-Seeking ancestor, whereas the Heavenly Sword Gate’s own had long since passed.

Now, with Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor’s breakthrough, the two sects were finally equal in top-tier strength.

His breakthrough also lit a fire under countless old monsters, who, under the influence of the Golden Era, began entering deep seclusion.

Suddenly, very few senior cultivators showed their faces.

With the appearance of the Golden Fortune List, the continent gradually became a playground for geniuses of all races vying for fortune.

Cold Moon Sword Immortal, the personal disciple of Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor, became the only sword cultivator on the continent to inherit a Dao-Seeking sword lineage thanks to the Golden Era.

Though she had only recently broken through to the Nascent Soul Stage, her peerless sharp aura still earned her a place on the Golden Fortune List.

Heirs from all major factions, determined to become the stars of their era, set out across the land—

cultivating, dueling, and stealing fortune from other prodigies.

With the old generation in seclusion and the new generation roaming the world, the battle for fortune became the main theme of this Golden Era.

Under this backdrop, shocking news and legendary feats were born almost every single day.

Five years after the Golden Era began, the protagonist of that once-earthshaking incident in Qingzhou… was no longer the most talked-about figure of the age.

Except… within the Demon Court.

“Your Majesty, has His Highness the Holy Son still not emerged from seclusion?”

“It’s been five years… has something happened to him?”

“The Holy Son possesses the Longevity Body. His cultivation advances slowly. It’s only been five years—what’s the rush?

Your current task is to expand the Demon Court’s territory within Qingzhou.

Once the Holy Son comes out of seclusion, I’ll naturally reward those who have contributed.”

With a casual wave, the Demon Emperor dismissed the elders and ancient races of the Demon Court.

Since the revival of the demon race, more and more beast clans sought to join the Demon Court.

But the court did not accept just anyone.

Most of these clans wished to join in order to transform into demonfolk, but the problem was:

Transformation Grass and Transformation Pills were in severely short supply.

Even within the Demon Court itself, distribution was limited.

So, any beast clan wishing to join had to undergo strict selection.

“He should be almost ready…” the Demon Emperor murmured, glancing in the direction of Lin Jing’s retreat.

This Golden Era had even made her feel the possibility of breaking through to a higher realm.

While it was true the Longevity Body cultivated slowly, it shouldn’t be taking this long.

At the same time — at Immortal Prison Mountain.

By now, this territory under the rule of Jindan had become a prison in every sense of the word—

its underground layers transformed into a massive holding ground for demonic beasts.

For years, Mosquito Man, obeying Jindan’s orders, had led countless mosquito swarms and secretly captured large numbers of demonic beasts—

sealing them on this mountain, awaiting Jindan’s return from seclusion, when they would be refined into pills.

As for Jindan himself, almost no one knew—

he had actually already broken through to the Lin Jing Realm just a year after entering seclusion.

Even Bao Qi hadn’t expected it—

that he’d go from Peak Foundation Establishment to early Lin Jing Stage in just seven years.

After all, it had taken him ten whole years just to go from Qi Refinement to Foundation Establishment.

That’s why he hadn’t dared to attempt his breakthrough inside the Hourglass of Time, which only allowed ten years at most.

If he failed to break through within the time limit and got forcibly ejected, the interruption might ruin the entire attempt.

So instead, he broke through in the outside world—

and to his surprise, it only took four years.

Thinking back, his earlier breakthrough was slow because of lack of preparation.

But now, with both full preparation and the boost from the Golden Era, it made sense.

Even after breaking through to the Core Formation stage, Lin Jing wasn’t satisfied.

Still failing to master the Heavenly Demon Physique, he returned to the Hourglass and cultivated in isolation for ten more years.

With the Partial Hidden Dragon Fortune he’d obtained earlier, he re-tempered every single spell and divine ability he had—whether he’d learned them well or not.

Unfortunately, the one technique he most wanted to master—the Heavenly Demon Physique—still eluded him…

But fortunately, in other areas, those ten years inside the Hourglass brought major gains.

The three biggest gains were:

1. His Physical Body.

Back at the Beast Taming Sect, Lin Jing and Senior Pine had split a few Spiritual Seeds.

These seeds were treasures from the Immortal Palace Cavern Heaven.

Even now, as the Demon Court’s Holy Son, Lin Jing still considered them immensely powerful.

Now that the Pine Leaf Mouse had completed the Everlasting Emperor's Longevity Art, it had matured all of the seeds.

Cloud-Hidden Orchid, a nemesis to heart demons—

Lin Jing consumed it directly, ensuring that even if he killed endlessly or lived for ten thousand years, he would never fall into madness.

Nine-Sun Divine Lingzhi, which massively boosted fire and yang-type cultivation—

He gave this to the Dragonblood Carp, and after Lin Jing reached the Core Formation stage, the carp’s cultivation soared to the peak of the Core Formation realm.

According to Senior Pine’s earlier assessment, this lingzhi was strong enough to elevate the carp directly to the Nascent Soul stage.

But at that time, it had only possessed King-tier bloodline.

Now, it was close to Ancestral Blood, meaning it could handle even stronger mana—

So the breakthrough to Nascent Soul still needed a bit more time.

That said, in terms of strength, the carp could now fight toe-to-toe with Nascent Soul-stage demon kings.

And most importantly, the Nine-Life Exquisite Grass—

which granted incredible regeneration, practically giving Lin Jing a Nine-Life Physique.

He took it himself.

After taking the Nine-Life Exquisite Grass, Lin Jing used Thunder Dragon Scale Blood, the carp’s Heavenly Thunder Flame, and the Ice Soul Gu’s Tribulation Lightning to refine his body.

Under the tempering of thunder and fire, Lin Jing’s physique eventually reached a level comparable to the proudest talents of the Dragon Clan.

Even though his cultivation was only at early Core Formation,

his physical strength alone rivaled those peak Core Formation Thunder Dragons.

In short—

Lin Jing now could take on most Core Formation-level attacks without using any defensive techniques at all.

Even if injured, his regenerative powers allowed him to recover instantly.

And this… wasn’t even his biggest gain.

His second major gain—was a Golden Core divine ability he birthed within himself:

“Divine Dominion Technique” (《神御之术》).

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 2: The Demon Emperor’s Mission
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 2: The Demon Emperor’s Mission

Upon reaching the Core Formation stage, Lin Jing naturally birthed his own Core Formation divine ability.

His divine ability was called Divine Dominion Technique (《神御之术》)—

a fusion of Beast Taming Dao and Longevity Physique.

Although he still hadn’t mastered the Heavenly Demon Physique, this Divine Dominion Technique filled in some of those gaps.

The two shared many similarities.

The core of Divine Dominion Technique was that Lin Jing could expend his lifespan and Core Formation power to command and manipulate things beyond his level.

Right now, using it once would consume about ten years of lifespan at most.

If Lin Jing was willing to pay that price, even High-Grade Magical Artifacts that were normally beyond his control could be temporarily wielded.

In fact, even if his opponent were human, he could try to use Divine Dominion to seize control.

In Lin Jing’s eyes, this was basically a variant form of the Heavenly Demon Physique.

The strength of the Heavenly Demon Physique lay in its explosive power—

Whether he burned ten years, a hundred, or even ten thousand years of lifespan,

once activated, the boost was immediate and overwhelming.

But the Divine Dominion Technique, with his current proficiency, could only burn a thousand years of life at most.

The power increase wasn’t as dramatic as the Heavenly Demon Physique.

However, in terms of finesse, Divine Dominion was far superior.

The Heavenly Demon Physique simply amped up cultivation level without improving spellcasting technique—

essentially brute strength with no control.

But Divine Dominion enhanced Lin Jing’s Beast Taming Level, granting him control over all things.

It was a major upgrade to his already formidable Beast Taming arsenal.

As for Lin Jing’s third major breakthrough…

After ten years, he had finally helped the Spell Spirit Origin: Illusory Mushroom (术法元灵·幻菇) awaken full spiritual intelligence—

His Illusion Dao mastery had reached perfection.

“Master,”

The floating Illusory Mushroom hovered at Lin Jing’s side. As Lin Jing stood up, it asked,

“Shall I stop generating your cultivation illusions?”

“Mhm, disperse them,” Lin Jing replied.

With that, the illusionary forest, ice-and-lightning ruins, and alchemy room within the Hourglass of Time all dissolved instantly.

The rest of his pet beasts inside the hourglass lifted their heads.

“It’s time to leave.”

“Now it’s your turn to take on a new task.”

Looking at the mushroom, Lin Jing suddenly recalled what the Demon Emperor had told him—

Only with the resolve to die can the Heavenly Demon Physique be mastered.

But with his current identity, how could he ever face that kind of desperation?

Under the Demon Emperor’s protection, it was too difficult to encounter a truly life-threatening crisis.

However—

Now that the Illusory Mushroom had gained full awareness, Lin Jing finally saw a glimmer of hope for mastering the Heavenly Demon Physique.

“From now on,” he instructed,

“you’re allowed to create illusionary crises at any moment—so long as it doesn’t endanger my life—

especially when my mental guard is lowest.

Design scenarios where I must feel like death is certain—

and right at the moment I successfully cultivate the Heavenly Demon Physique, but before it activates, wake me up.”

“No limit on the number of attempts.”

Using illusions to cultivate the Heavenly Demon Physique—

it was the only viable method Lin Jing could come up with.

After giving the order, he even sealed his memory of this command, so he wouldn’t be on guard against the Illusory Mushroom.

“...”

The mushroom scratched its head, falling deep into thought.

This was… insane.

How was this not a completely unreasonable request?

It was literally a fragment of Lin Jing’s spell essence—a part of his own psyche.

Basically, Lin Jing had just asked it to hypnotize himself.

Wasn’t that just a recipe for going mad?

Would he really be able to distinguish illusion from reality?

Five years into the Golden Era.

Lin Jing finally emerged from seclusion.

His own cultivation: Early Core Formation Stage

Pine Leaf Mouse: Mid Core Formation Stage

Dragonblood Carp: Perfected Core Formation Stage

Ice Soul Gu: Mid Core Formation Stage

Upon emerging, Lin Jing immediately reported to the Demon Emperor.

At the same time, the Demon Emperor’s voice came through his Tiger Fang:

“Congratulations.”

“That carp’s cultivation is just one step from becoming a demon king? Impressive.”

“Yes. We’ll begin refining a Transformation Pill capable of working on Demon Emperors and even Ancestral Beasts as soon as possible. Please rest assured, Your Majesty.” Lin Jing pledged.

Before his seclusion, they had already improved the Transformation Pill, refining one capable of letting demon kings take human form.

He had handed it over to his senior sister to await the Sect Master and Senior Brother Ouyang’s return.

As for whether they’d choose to break their contracts and transform—that was their choice.

Regardless, the pill was already enough for the Beast Taming Sect.

But for the Demon Court, they needed something that could work on Demon Emperors and Ancestral Beasts—that was the real strategic value.

“No rush,” said the Demon Emperor.

“Since the carp is already at its peak, why not wait until it forms a Nascent Soul before refining again?

At its current level, it might be able to help produce a pill for Demon Emperors—

but a pill for an Ancestral Beast? That’s still a stretch.”

“Understood.”

Lin Jing was about to suggest letting the carp prepare for its breakthrough, but the Demon Emperor had already made arrangements.

“There’s a volcano in the Western Desert, known as Buried Immortal Volcano.

It was formed during the Celestial Palace War, when the Celestial Alliance activated a massive formation and pulled pure solar core flames down to incinerate the fallen immortals of the lower realm.”

“With that carp’s current level, ordinary fire no longer enhances its flame power.

Next, I’ll escort it to the Buried Immortal Volcano,

to absorb and refine the flame essence there.

If successful, and combined with its Rare Beast Divine Ability,

it can fuse all flames into one and master a flame on the level of Immortal Fire.”

“At that point, whether it’s for refining Transformation Pills or helping you contend for Golden Era fortune,

it will become your most powerful battle companion.”

Lin Jing blinked in surprise.

So this… was what it felt like to be a top-tier immortal scion?

“As for you,” the Demon Emperor continued,

“you’ll accompany your pet to the Western Desert—

and while it cultivates, I have a task for you.”

“The Demon Court’s Western Desert branch has sent word...”

“The High Monk of Golden Light Temple—

after failing to break through to the Dao-Seeking Stage during the Golden Era—

is now nearing the end of his lifespan.”

“Sealed within his body is one of the Ten Rare Gu—the Golden Light Gu.

Soon, it will become a masterless treasure.”

“To prevent it from falling into the wrong hands after his death,

the monk used his final spiritual power to purify it back to its original form—

the Thunder Tribulation Gu—

and laid down several trials of inheritance.”

“Any prodigy with decent Buddhist affinity may attempt the trial and earn the right to inherit the Golden Light Gu.”

“You should meet the requirements.”

“So while the carp works on breaking through, you’re to go win the trial and retrieve the Golden Light Gu.”

“I am of the Divine Tiger Clan, aligned with Metal Element.

If you acquire the Golden Light Gu and gain access to its dominant metal power,

I’ll pass on a special Imperial Weapon of my clan to serve as your protection.

Only those with the strongest metal-element force can activate it—

and right now, this is your most efficient path forward.”

Lin Jing gripped the Tiger Fang tightly.

So that monk who sealed the Golden Light Gu…

was dying?

And now the Golden Light Gu was about to change hands?

First the Ice Soul Gu got upgraded by devouring the Thunder Tribulation Gu…

and now this?!

What kind of busted luck was this?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 3: Golden Light Gu
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 3: Golden Light Gu

"Thank you, Your Majesty."

"This westward trip, I will definitely acquire the Golden Light Gu."

Lin Jing had no objections to the Demon Emperor’s arrangements.

Although the Buried Immortal Volcano sounded extremely dangerous, and obtaining the Golden Light Gu would no doubt be full of obstacles, at the very least, with the Demon Emperor’s protection, he didn’t have to worry about his life.

Since the Western Desert trip posed no threat and promised considerable benefits, naturally, he wouldn’t resist.

"Once you're ready, come see me."

The Demon Emperor's final voice transmission came through.

Lin Jing fell into thought on the spot...

"Let’s go check on Senior Shen Song. You all come out too and pay your respects to the old one."

He first headed to where Senior Shen Song cultivated.

Before he could even call out, the massive pine tree glowed with a golden-green light.

"You finally broke through to Core Formation."

"Not bad."

"Looks like senior has also stabilized your realm." Lin Jing smiled faintly.

"Heh, naturally, this wasn’t a difficult thing." Shen Song spoke. "But, how come this little guy is still mid-stage Demon Core??"

The old tree glanced at the pine leaf mouse perched on Lin Jing’s shoulder.

The pine leaf mouse gave a quick greeting, saying realm was just a worldly matter.

Then, Dragon Carp and Ice Soul Gu also emerged to greet him.

In contrast, Dragon Carp was quite smug, as it currently had the highest cultivation.

Plus, it had overheard Lin Jing's conversation with the Demon Emperor...

The opportunity to control an Immortal Flame...

"That one huh..." Lin Jing looked at the pine leaf mouse, shook his head, and said, "Senior, no need to mind its realm. Though it looks like it’s only mid-stage Core Formation, that’s because it hasn’t spent these years cultivating at all—it’s been..."

"Hoarding goods, right." Shen Song sighed.

"Exactly." Lin Jing smiled and nodded. After all, the pine leaf mouse’s hoarding benefits were enormous now. The more it hoarded, the stronger its Core Formation power. It only looked weak, but if it used its demon core divine ability, even Dragon Carp might not gain the upper hand.

"I understand. What about that one?" Shen Song looked at his demonic-path disciple, Ice Soul Gu.

"Didn’t this guy already swallow the Thunder Tribulation Gu? Why is its growth so slow?"

"Squeak squeak squeak…" The little spider covered its face with several legs. It couldn’t help it—there had been no time to cultivate. It had been constantly assigned by Lin Jing to discharge electricity inside his body, helping temper his physique.

But thankfully, Ice Soul Gu had also heard the Demon Emperor's plans and knew it would soon get to swallow the Golden Light Gu.

Tears of exhaustion trickled from its mouth.

Golden Light Gu.

A metal-element rare Gu…

Lin Jing was quite interested in it too.

This Golden Light Gu had two core traits.

First, Gu Poison.

Its poison could cause other beings to “rust.” In this state, cultivators would increasingly lose control over their bodies, becoming unfamiliar with spells and martial skills—as if they had neglected cultivation for hundreds or thousands of years—eventually turning into someone completely useless.

And under “rust” status, the victim’s body parts—bones, teeth, internal organs—all had a chance to be corrupted by metallic toxicity.

So, to be the host of the Golden Light Gu, one’s body must withstand the corrosion of rust. Otherwise, they’d have to cultivate night and day to prevent “rusting,” essentially having no freedom at all.

Second, top-tier metal element mastery.

Just like Ice Soul Gu had supreme frost control, and Thunder Tribulation Gu had top-tier lightning control, the Golden Light Gu could also manipulate and imbue containers with peak-level metal affinity. That was precisely what the Demon Emperor had her eye on.

In her view, having Lin Jing start from scratch learning metal-element techniques was unrealistic. So the Golden Light Gu became his shortcut to wielding metal-element imperial weapons.

Using this power, the Golden Light Gu could also manipulate those infected with its poison, turning them into controllable weapons.

However, Lin Jing hadn’t heard of the head monk of Golden Light Temple ever using that ability. That monk seemed to only use the Gu to enhance his Golden Body.

When the Golden Light Gu merged with its vessel, the body would appear clad in golden armor, radiating light, invincible in offense and defense. Combined with Buddhist Golden Body techniques, it made the monk of Golden Light Temple reputedly second only to one in Buddhist defense.

"I see." Watching the behavior of Lin Jing’s pet beasts, Shen Song understood. "So next, you’re probably going to leave here, to help them break through further, right? You came to bid me farewell."

"Surely not to have me protect your path. You’re now the Holy Son of the Demon Court, protected by the Demon Emperor. What use is this old scallion?"

"Don’t say that, senior... I did indeed come to say goodbye before departure. But we should be back before long. In the meantime, I’ll entrust this Immortal Prison Mountain to your care."

Lin Jing smiled and bowed.

"That’s it? Nothing else?"

Shen Song clearly didn’t buy it.

Lin Jing looked up and grinned: "Since your cultivation has reached the Profound Void stage, surely you can help the pine leaf mouse deduce the next realm of Evergreen Emperor Longevity Art? It’s already perfected the current level, but that only equates to a Nascent Soul-tier technique. It’s clearly not enough anymore."

"After becoming Holy Son, I reviewed many techniques in the Demon Court. I still think this one suits it best. Might you..."

Shen Song rolled his eyes at the eager mouse. "When I was its age, I could already deduce my own cultivation art… but fine, since you’re all so busy, I’ll help. But I estimate that pushing this to the Soul Transformation stage is the limit—my own Longevity Art only goes that far."

"Beyond that, you’ll have to find your own way… With your Hidden Dragon Technique, and your chance to get on the Fortune Gold List, your own comprehension should surpass mine eventually…"

"By the way, you also cultivated that Heavenly Demon Divine Sense technique, right?" The old pine tree suddenly remembered.

Lin Jing nodded.

"It’s already perfected."

"I know. You’ve been using that to scan me all this time. Don’t worry, I bear you no ill will. If I did, the Demon Emperor would’ve crushed me long ago." Shen Song huffed.

Lin Jing laughed. "Of course I know. But as a demonic cultivator, one can’t be too trusting, not even of close ones. Wasn’t that something you taught me? Besides, that divine sense technique was brought to the Tianyuan Realm by you, wasn’t it? It probably doesn’t work on you?"

Shen Song thought for a moment. "If it were the me from my past life, it probably wouldn’t work. I’d definitely have a way to nullify it. But now, I can’t ignore it anymore."

"After you leave, I’ll also head out. I plan to collect the remnants of the Heavenly Demon Sect’s inheritance—those parts the Ancient Kingdom didn’t fully seize when the sect was destroyed.

When the time comes, aside from the Heavenly Demon Divine Sense and Heavenly Demon Physique, I’ll teach you more demonic sect divine abilities."

"Compared to native Tianyuan techniques, these are all far more powerful. Learning them gives you more options."

"Options...?" Lin Jing had a bad feeling.

"Heh heh. Even though the heavens loathe the Heavenly Demon Sect, as a demonic cultivator, who the hell sticks around to perish with one world?

As long as the green hills remain, there’s no fear of having no firewood. If you’re cornered, you can sever your Longevity Physique, assume a new identity as a remnant of the Tianyuan Heavenly Demon Sect, and defect to the Demon Realm.

For someone as gifted as you, the Demon Realm won’t turn you away. Of course… you’ll have to go through some heavy brainwashing and screening. As long as you can hold out and not give yourself away…"

"A demonic cultivator’s revenge… even ten thousand years later, is never too late…"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 4: Westward Quest for the Golden Light Gu
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 4: Westward Quest for the Golden Light Gu

Lin Jing was speechless.

Senior Shen Song's mindset was just too dangerous.

"Senior, if it really comes to that point, it wouldn't be called defecting to the Demon Realm. It would be called a Tianyuan cultivator enduring humiliation as an undercover agent in the Demon Realm, waiting for a chance to take revenge."

After correcting Senior Shen Song’s statement, Lin Jing said, "Hopefully, that day never comes."

"Best case scenario, some great power in the Immortal Realm takes care of the Demon Lord soon, then reopens the ascension path, allowing immortals to descend and wipe out the lower realm’s Heavenly Demon Sect once and for all. That way, the Tianyuan Realm won’t have to live in fear anymore..."

"You think too beautifully." Shen Song envied that kind of optimism.

"Naturally. Optimists don’t die young." Lin Jing hesitated, then said, "Senior, I think there’s one more thing..."

"What now?" Shen Song had already been about to see him off, only to realize Lin Jing really was full of requests.

"A spell spirit. If you’re hoping I’ll praise your talent, then yes, it’s impressive. But why does a phantom-type spell spirit look like a mushroom? Don’t tell me you were influenced by me and now have a fondness for grass and wood spirits?"

"Mememushroom!" The spell spirit gave the rough, huge, and ugly Shen Song a glance, then dove back into Lin Jing’s body.

It was just waiting for an opportunity to hypnotize Lin Jing...

"That’s not it." Lin Jing spoke. "It’s about your neighbor—the other Longevity Physique—Gu Qianqiu."

"Oh, her." Shen Song said. "Seems she’s already at peak Core Formation. Cultivates pretty fast. But this Immortal Prison Mountain’s spiritual energy—out of ten parts, she alone takes up eight... I only let her have it because she doesn’t have many years left to live."

Lin Jing said helplessly, "I’ve prepared some resources to help with her breakthrough to Nascent Soul and some gentle longevity-extending ones. While I’m gone, I’ll have to trouble senior to watch over her. Just make sure she successfully breaks through before her lifespan runs out."

With the arrival of the Golden Era, Gu Qianqiu should, in theory, find it easier to reach the Nascent Soul stage.

Still, Lin Jing wasn’t at ease. He planned to leave behind everything he’d obtained in Tongtian City that could extend life or aid in breakthrough.

As for his own future needs, he could just acquire what he needed from the Demon Court.
