"Fine, fine..." Shen Song muttered. Now that Lin Jing was under the Demon Emperor’s protection, Shen Song wasn’t sure if he’d ever get Lin Jing’s blood again. But now, with a second Longevity Physique appearing, even if he couldn’t get Lin Jing’s, at least he had a backup...

"Leave it to me. Worst case, I’ll use demonic techniques to push her up there. You can count on me."

............

Not long after, Lin Jing reluctantly bid farewell to Senior Shen Song.

Gu Qianqiu was still in seclusion, so Lin Jing didn’t disturb her.

He inspected the dungeon, rewarded Vice Captain Mosquito Girl of the Demon Capture Squad, then contacted his senior sister.

For now, Dragon Carp had yet to reach Nascent Soul, and using its divine powers still posed a strain, so Lin Jing planned to wait until it broke through and returned before refining this batch of demons and monsters.

Besides, in just a few years, the number of captives in the mountain dungeon wasn’t that many yet.

Might as well stockpile more...

With Elder Huan Li of Thousand Illusions Mountain stationed at the Immortal Prison, Lin Jing wasn’t worried anything would happen to the captives.

"Out of seclusion? Congrats!"

Before he could even pull out the communication jade, his senior sister transmitted directly.

"Yeah, and congrats to you too, Senior Sister, for reaching the top ten of the Fortune Gold List..." Lin Jing looked up at the sky.

The Fortune Gold List didn’t show normally. Cultivators had to concentrate their minds to see it.

Among the giant names on the golden list in the sky, Senior Sister Xu Qingyao’s name stood out at number nine.

After years of grooming by the Demon Emperor and massive resource investment, she was now considered the strongest Nascent Soul genius in the world.

After all, she now represented the Demon Court and the Demon Emperor. Her strength couldn’t afford to be weak. With the immense resource support, not only she herself, but all her beast pets were stuffed with resources.

"Stop it. The top eight are all at Soul Transformation already. Give me a few more years, and when I’m fully prepared, I’ll challenge Soul Transformation too. Though... it’ll probably take a while." Xu Qingyao said irritably.

Prodigy.

That word usually implied youth.

Under normal circumstances, anyone who reached Soul Transformation should already be halfway through their lifespan, hardly what you’d call a prodigy.

But there was this small group of freaks—still in their prime relative to their total lifespan—who had already reached the Soul Transformation stage.

Currently, in the Tianyuan Realm, there were eight such people.

Compared to their total lifespan, they were still youths or young adults. But in cultivation, they were already halfway up the mountain.

In the eyes of the world, these eight people had the greatest chance of reaching the Dao Seeking stage.

"Keep it up, Senior Sister. I just wonder when I’ll be able to get on that list..." Lin Jing mused. If he made it, the boost to his cultivation speed would be huge.

Heading to the Buddhist sect to contend for the rare Gu wouldn’t be peaceful. He didn’t know if his current strength would be enough to make the Fortune Gold List.

After all, he’d looked carefully... When the list first appeared, all one hundred were Nascent Soul and above. But five years later, six of the names were now Golden Core cultivators.

That meant these six had already defeated certain Nascent Soul-level prodigies and taken their fortune.

Six wolves, huh...

It wasn’t like defeating some old Nascent Soul who got there by dragging out the years.

It meant that if these six reached Nascent Soul, they’d likely land near the top of the list. Their talent was terrifying!

"You..." Xu Qingyao heard Lin Jing mumbling and asked.

"How long is your current lifespan?"

Before Lin Jing could answer, she continued, "If we go by current age versus total lifespan, we might barely qualify as prodigies. But you? You’re probably still an infant. I represent the Demon Court, and the Demon Emperor—it's normal for me to be anxious. But you don’t need to be. Your life... is long."

Lin Jing chuckled. "Alright. Actually, I just wanted to ask—has the Sect Master and Senior Brother come out of seclusion yet?"

"Mm, they both have."

"After coming out, they were completely stunned by all the changes..."

"And the Transformation Pill?"

"Father and Senior Brother had no patience. They directly terminated the contracts, letting Mother and Hong’er complete their transformations..." Xu Qingyao sighed. "They said who knows how long modifying the Beast Taming Art would take. Instead of waiting forever, better to break the contract now and then figure out how to modify it to allow contracting beastfolk."

Lin Jing blinked. "They’ve transformed already huh..."

"Then, Senior Sister, please tell them for me—I forgot to mention something earlier... During this time, absolutely don’t have children."

He had no idea how that fox demon soul Shen Song met had reincarnated. He just hoped it wouldn’t end up in someone familiar’s belly. That would be really awkward.

Especially Senior Brother Ouyang—very dangerous!

After all, the races were similar!

"Got it." Though Senior Sister didn’t know why, she still nodded and said, "I’ll pass the message."

"And the Beast Taming Sect—everything’s fine for now, right?" Lin Jing asked his final question of concern.

“You mean the Beast Taming Sect’s mountain gate? The Ancient Nation has already given the green light. Now the Beast Taming Sect’s mountain gate has been relocated to the site once occupied by the Sinluo Hall of the Qingzhou forces. After the Tongtian City battle, Sinluo Hall was also wiped out by the Demon Court. Their territory became vacant and was handed over to the Beast Taming Sect. Currently, the Myriad Beasts Gate and Thousand Illusions Mountain have also pledged allegiance to the Demon Court. This region can basically be considered Demon Court territory now.”

“As for that undead demon you’re most concerned about, the Demon-Suppressing Pagoda has also been relocated by Her Majesty the Demon Emperor using a divine ability. It’s still being sealed underground beneath the Beast Taming Sect’s mountain gate. I bet it doesn’t even know it’s been moved.”

“Then it’s all good.” Lin Jing nodded. Looks like after the trade between the Demon Emperor and the Ancient Emperor, the Beast Taming Sect had been properly arranged.

Even if the Ancient Emperor disliked non-humans, he probably couldn’t resist the temptation of the Golden Bell, and wouldn’t care anymore about the fate of the Beast Taming Sect or a small cultivator like Lin Jing.

Lin Jing once again looked toward the Fortune Gold List. After all, out of the current top ten names, three were from the Ancient Nation, and both first and second place were Ancient Clan prodigies! The foundation of the Ancient Clan was terrifying.

This opening of the Golden Era clearly benefited the Ancient Nation—the number one human power—the most.

First, the Ancient Prince...

Second, the Ancient Princess...

Before the Gold List appeared, no one even knew that the Second Generation Ancient Emperor had a daughter. After all, whether it was the Hidden Dragon Secret Art or the True Dragon Secret Art, reputation was everything. That’s why the rumors of the Ancient Prince having triple physiques and being at the thirteenth layer of Qi Refining had spread across the continent.

But this Ancient Princess had no fame whatsoever before, which was highly unusual—unless she hadn’t cultivated the Ancient Clan’s secret techniques...

“Soul Formation, huh...”

After confirming there were no issues with the Beast Taming Sect, Lin Jing made some preparations and headed to the Demon Court headquarters to request an audience with the Demon Emperor.

“I’ll use space magic to take you all to the Western Desert. The journey is long, and traveling across the void will take time.” At the Demon Court headquarters, the Demon Emperor sat on her dragon throne, hands resting on the armrests, eyes fixed on Lin Jing below.

“Okay.” Lin Jing directly took out the “Void-Dizziness Pill” he had prepared in advance and swallowed it.

Then, under the Demon Emperor’s speechless gaze, he asked, “Your Majesty, when I get there, should I enter the Western Desert as the Demon Court’s Holy Son, or disguise myself as a monk to retrieve the Golden Light Gu?”

“Of course as the Demon Court’s Holy Son, what kind of stupid question is that? I’ve said it before—now that you’ve become the Demon Court’s Holy Son, you don’t have to skulk around anymore. No one can touch you.”

“Since you also have Buddhist cultivation attainments, you meet the Golden Light Temple’s conditions. If they don’t play by the rules, I’ll personally go have a word with the Buddhist leaders. At that point, it won’t be following their rules anymore—it’ll be a straight-up snatch! But, if you can’t obtain the Golden Light Gu through their rules, then that means you’re just not as good as their Buddhist prodigies. I won’t help you save face.”

Lin Jing smiled, indicating he understood...

“Also, for this westward trip to fetch the gold, pick any Demon Ancestor in the Demon Court to serve as your escort. If you run into some blind idiot, just have them squash it for you. Remember, you’re the Demon Court’s Holy Son now—start acting like it.” The Demon Emperor clapped the arms of her throne repeatedly with her tiny hands.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 5: Arrival of the Holy Son
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 5: Arrival of the Holy Son

Western Desert.

This desert lies to the west of the Ancient Celestial Kingdom, in the opposite direction of Qingzhou, and is extremely far away.

A few days later.

At the far western edge of the Tianyuan Continent, a spatial vortex condensed, and immediately after, nine divine golden Thunder Dragons pulled a wooden flying boat out from within it.

On top of the flying boat, several figures appeared.

The Lord of the Demon Court dressed in golden dragon robes, Lin Jing in a blue robe, and an old man with yellow hair dressed like a coachman.

"Just ahead is the Immortal-Burying Volcano. This is a forbidden land of immortals and gods, ordinary people cannot get close. Let’s part ways here."

Once the Demon Emperor spoke, Lin Jing immediately summoned the Dragonblood Carp and instructed it, “Go with Her Majesty the Demon Emperor to the Immortal-Burying Volcano to cultivate.”

Looking forward to the birth of the first Nascent Soul pet beast by his side...

"Pop~" The Dragonblood Carp nodded, its sharp eyes gazing into the distance, already sensing the terrifying fiery aura ahead.

Just a mere Immortal-Burying Volcano…

Wait until Emperor Dragon Carp pays it a visit!

With the Demon Emperor sis watching over—it’s not like the flames could kill it!

“Thunder Dragon Patriarch, I leave the safety of the Holy Son to you now. I hope there won’t be a need for this Emperor to step in.”

The Demon Emperor gave a calm glance at the yellow-haired elder—this was the guardian Lin Jing ultimately chose, the Thunder Dragon Clan’s patriarch!

The Thunder Dragon scales and blood gifted by the Thunder Dragon Clan previously had seemed enough to Lin Jing, but turned out to be far from sufficient.

On this western journey, Lin Jing was determined to forge an invincible physique. The current strength of his body still felt far from enough, so keeping the Thunder Dragon Patriarch by his side made it easier to ask about body-tempering methods...

As for being the Holy Son’s guardian, the Thunder Dragon Patriarch accepted joyfully. As long as he kept the Holy Son happy, maybe—just maybe—he could become the first patriarch in the Demon Court to complete transformation!

To show the sincerity of the Thunder Dragon Clan and repay Lin Jing for helping their little princess transform, the patriarch even summoned nine talented collateral juniors of the Thunder Dragon Clan to pull Lin Jing’s flying boat!

Each of them had reached the Demon King stage of Nascent Soul!

If Lin Jing hadn’t refused, he’d even have dragged the Thunder Dragon Princess over to serve Lin Jing personally.

“Your Majesty can rest assured. With me here, no one will lay a finger on the Holy Son. If anyone does, I’ll throw myself into the Immortal-Burying Volcano!” the Thunder Dragon Patriarch vowed.

His yellow-haired coachman appearance was just a transformation, not a true form. It was for convenience in staying close to Lin Jing—after all, his true body spanned a thousand meters and wasn’t suitable for moving around in human territory.

“Mm.” The Demon Emperor nodded, then vanished with the Dragonblood Carp in tow before Lin Jing and the others.

After she left, the Thunder Dragon Patriarch let out a sigh of relief.

“Holy Son, where to next?” he asked.

“Shall we head straight to Golden Light Temple?”

Lin Jing’s old ancestor already knew that Lu Cheng’s mission this time was to obtain the Golden Light Gu.

In the Western Desert, there were eighty-one Buddhist cities, each with a monastery.

Golden Light Temple was one of the stronger ones among the Eighty-One Monasteries of the Buddhist Sect.

The temple's head monk had reached the peak of the Void Mystical Stage and held significant status in the Buddhist world.

In terms of power, the head monk of Solar Temple belonged to the first echelon just below the current leader of the Buddhist Sect.

“No rush. To do a good job, one must first sharpen their tools. Let’s visit the Demon-Slaying Temple first.” Lin Jing spoke after a moment of thought.

“Demon-Slaying Temple?” The Thunder Dragon Patriarch was startled, not understanding why the Holy Son would go there.

Lin Jing smiled and said, “The Beast Taming Sect and the head monk of the Demon-Slaying Temple go way back.”

“If we hadn’t come to the Western Desert, it wouldn’t matter. But since we’re here, we should pay a visit.”

The head monk of the Demon-Slaying Temple was the very monk who had entered the Ancient Kingdom, joined the Demon-Slaying Bureau, and worked alongside the Beast Taming Sect and other Great Wasteland sects to seal the Immortal Demon.

It was also this monk’s Dharma body that helped Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse develop their Buddhist cultivation talent.

This time, coming to the Buddhist Sect to obtain a Gu—if Lin Jing approached purely as the Holy Son of the Demon Court, he’d surely face many obstacles.

But if he could gain some assistance from the head monk of the Demon-Slaying Temple, things would likely go much smoother.

Even with the Demon Emperor backing him, Lin Jing wasn’t foolish enough to pick hard mode for his mission. If there’s an easy way—why not take it?

Find an old acquaintance and build rapport first!

The cultivation world is all about connections!

The Demon-Slaying Temple was located in Demon-Quelling City.

In the Western Desert, there were still a few mortals, but the environment was harsh, with many demons and ghosts, so mortals could only survive by relying on the Buddhist Sect.

Therefore, all the commoners in the eighty-one Buddhist cities of the Western Desert were followers of the Buddhist Sect. If any of their children showed spiritual talent, they’d send them to train in the temples.

But compared to the Ancient Kingdom, the human population in the Western Desert was still too sparse, which was also why the Buddhist Sect wished to spread their teachings in the Ancient Kingdom...

“Abbot, Hidden Spirit Temple requested our help a few days ago. The demon sealed beneath Hidden Spirit City is about to break free. Reinforcing the seal no longer works!”

“Abbot, Awakening Temple also sent a request for aid the day before yesterday. Three of their high monks have been possessed by ghosts!”

“Wandering Temple.....”

In the Demon-Slaying Temple, a white-robed elder monk sat with closed eyes, listening to reports from the disciples below, then slowly said, “The arrival of the Golden Era is both a calamity and an opportunity for our Buddhist Sect.”

“Countless years ago, the Western Desert was a fertile land, but due to the Celestial Palace War, it turned barren and became a dead zone.”

“Later, countless demons emerged. Among the many sects across the continent, only our Buddhist Sect chose to settle in this desolate place for countless years to suppress these chaotic demons…”

“Not just because of our compassion, unwilling to see the world fall into demonic chaos, but also because suppressing demons is a form of cultivation for us—it represents great merit.”

“These demons were born from ancient wars, indescribable, impossible to eliminate. They can only be sealed within the earth’s veins and suppressed by many temples. Originally, this was sustainable, but the advent of the Golden Era has loosened their seals.”

“Still, that’s fine. After tens of thousands of years of growth, our Buddhist Sect is no longer what it once was. These revived monsters will become nourishment for the growth of our disciples.”

“Since several temples have asked for help—any of you willing to go demon-slaying? Truly eradicating these demons would be a deed of great merit.” The white-robed elder looked at the disciples, only to see their faces filled with bitterness.

What he said wasn’t wrong.

But it’s fine to deal with ordinary demonic beasts or evil cultivators.

These demons born of the grudges of fallen immortals and powerful beings, fused with all manner of strange forces, simply couldn’t be judged by common sense…

Even after so many years of development, the Buddhist Sect had grown strong, but the demons recorded in the ancient texts were still terrifying beyond measure…

Just as silence fell over the temple, suddenly, almost simultaneously, several monks broke into cold sweat and looked outside the city in alarm.

“Abbot, many powerful demonic beasts are approaching…”

The white-robed elder furrowed his brows, sensing the aura of a Demon Ancestor. His divine sense extended outward—and saw nine Thunder Dragons pulling a flying boat slowly descending outside Demon-Quelling City. Standing on the boat was a young man in blue robes, with a squirrel perched on his shoulder.

“Holy Son Lin Jing of the Beast Taming Sect, here to visit.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 6: Demon-Slaying Temple
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 6: Demon-Slaying Temple

"That’s… an Ancient Thunder Dragon!"

The many monks of the Demon-Slaying Temple also saw the scene outside the city through their divine sense.

They couldn’t help but be shocked—someone was actually using nine Ancient Thunder Dragons to pull a carriage? Who could this person be?

And as Lin Jing announced his name, their expressions changed again.

As expected!

They didn’t pay much attention to the title of Holy Son of the Beast Taming Sect, but the name Lin Jing was not unfamiliar to these monks present.

Demon Court, Holy Son!

The monks of the Demon-Slaying Temple were instantly on guard, unable to understand why the Holy Son of the Demon Court would appear in this place.

Though they were all second-generation prodigies, there were levels to that. Some prodigies from Dao-Seeking forces might be highly regarded, but that didn’t mean the true experts of those forces would make a move on their behalf. Many Dao-Seeking powerhouses didn’t bother with worldly matters.

But Lin Jing was different. He was the hope of the Demon Court’s revival. The Demon Emperor was willing to personally act to wipe out Lin Jing’s enemies, which already showed his status. Probably only the Ancient Imperial Princes and a select few other second-generation heirs could rival Lin Jing in status.

"Beast Taming Sect..." At this moment, the white-robed old monk was also stunned for a second, but then he spoke to calm the crowd.

"No need to worry. Holy Son Lin has come with no ill intentions."

"You all stay here."

The white-robed old monk looked toward the outside of the city, and in the next instant, appeared atop the clouds beyond the city wall. His form transformed from that of an old monk into a white-robed young monk.

He had heard the subtext in Lin Jing’s words. Lin Jing had not come representing the Demon Court Holy Son, but as someone of the Beast Taming Sect.

Very few people knew that the abbot of the Demon-Slaying Temple had, during his travels in the Ancient Celestial Kingdom, developed a friendship with the second-generation sect master of the Beast Taming Sect.

This time, Lin Jing could be considered a junior of an old friend coming to visit.

Besides that, the white-robed monk also recognized the connection between Lin Jing and himself.

This Holy Son of both the Demon Court and Beast Taming Sect—his Buddhist cultivation talent had been awakened through the monk’s own Buddhist Light!

"Senior, we 'meet' again." Lin Jing looked at the young monk. The monk’s image was identical to the one seen in the suppressed illusion within the Beast Taming Sect’s Demon-Suppressing Pagoda.

"Holy Son..." The young monk smiled and said, "I’m surprised too. That strand of consciousness I left behind and the opportunity it carried… ended up with the Holy Son of the Demon Court."

Lin Jing smiled faintly and said, "I have to thank you for the baptism of Buddhist Light. It helped me a lot. As for the Demon Court—well, that came later."

"That’s why I’ve come to the Western Desert this time, specifically to visit and thank you."

The young monk said, "No need to be so formal. My Buddhist Light only helped awaken a Buddhist cultivation talent. But for you to grow this far—it certainly wasn’t because of my light alone. It’s all your own ability…"

"This isn’t the right place to talk. Would you be willing to come sit in the temple with me?" the white-robed monk invited. Lin Jing nodded, then turned to the Thunder Dragon Ancestor beside him.

"I’ll be back soon."

The Thunder Dragon Ancestor gave a nod. He also looked at the white-robed monk for a moment, and the monk returned the gaze, inwardly sighing at how seriously the Demon Court valued its Holy Son.

Moments later, Lin Jing was seated in the tea courtyard of the Demon-Slaying Temple, personally hosted by the temple’s abbot.

"May I ask what brings Holy Son Lin to the Western Desert this time?"

"Could it be… you’ve come to slay demons too?" the white-robed monk asked.

"Sort of," Lin Jing said. "Recently, there seem to be more demons and devils in the Western Desert. Are there many cultivators coming to slay them as well?"

"Yes. With the opening of the Golden Era, countless ancient demons are awakening. The Western Desert is about to fall into chaos again..."

The white-robed monk thought for a moment and said, "Do you know the Cold Moon Sword Immortal?"

Lin Jing said, "Of course."

"Ever since the Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor reached the Dao-Seeking realm, the Cold Moon Sword Immortal has become his successor. She’s now one of the most famous sword cultivator prodigies on the continent. She’ll be arriving soon to slay demons too."

"When I was younger, I stayed with the Demon-Slaying Bureau in the Ancient Kingdom for some time, so I still have contact with them. A few days ago, the Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor sent me a message, asking me to look after his disciple for a while."

The white-robed monk sighed. "From what the Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor said, the Cold Moon Sword Immortal is from the Feng family of Martial Emperor City. She traveled to the Ancient Kingdom with her parents when she was young. Along the way, they were attacked by demons. Her parents died protecting her. Fortunately, a passing elder from the Heavenly Sword Gate saved her life."

"That’s why she hates demons so much. Upon hearing about the current demon chaos in the Western Desert, she insisted on coming."

"There are probably many prodigies like her who’ve come to slay demons. That is a blessing for the Western Desert."

Lin Jing stayed silent. This was the first time he had heard of the Cold Moon Sword Immortal’s background. So her parents had been brutally killed by demons… No wonder she was so determined to team up with him, even using herself as bait. It seemed she really did want to kill as many demons as possible.

"Holy Son, could it be that you also came due to a personal experience, to slay demons?" The white-robed monk knew that Lin Jing had once been persecuted by the Yin Corpse Sect, so he asked.

"No," Lin Jing said directly. "I heard that the abbot of Golden Light Temple is looking for a successor for the Golden Light Gu. I came for that."

"With the Buddhist cultivation talent awakened by you, I suppose I’m half a Buddhist cultivator too."

"So I should qualify to accept the inheritance."

"I also heard that slaying demons is part of the trial for inheriting the Golden Light Gu at Golden Light Temple. So demon-slaying is naturally something I’ll have to do while I’m here."

The white-robed monk paused for a moment.

"So that’s it… you came for the Golden Light Gu…"

"Even so, you’re still the Holy Son of the Demon Court. Your identity may be hard for many in the Buddhist sects to accept."

Lin Jing smiled faintly, looking at the monk.

"That’s why I’m here to visit you, Senior. You’re one of the top ten Void Mystical stage monks in the Buddhist world, and I’m technically your half-disciple. Why shouldn’t I count as half a Buddhist disciple?"

"You’re just like your second-generation sect master—both slippery fellows. Back in the day, he was the same," the monk was stunned again, then laughed heartily, recalling his younger years.

Back then, his cultivation wasn’t high—just Nascent Soul. He’d relied on a unique Buddhist sealing divine ability to help the Great Wasteland seal an Immortal Demon. The bond formed during that time was truly memorable.

The old monk fell into thought.

"For the sake of that old bond, I’ll help you. But Holy Son, you’ll have to agree to one thing: in this inheritance competition, you mustn’t harm the Buddhist prodigies. Also, help us deal with a demon revived from ancient times," the monk said. "What does Your Highness think?"

"Lightning is a demon’s nemesis," I looked toward Lin Jing’s ancestor within the city.

I felt that if Lin Jing’s ancestor made a move, it could greatly relieve the pressure on the Buddhist sects. Besides that, if someone like Mou Xing is determined to get the Golden Light Gu, it’s best to avoid life-and-death fights… keep it a fair competition, and avoid weakening the Buddhist world’s strength.

Lin Jing nodded and said, "That’s fine. But I heard the trials for the Golden Light Gu require a high level of Buddhist understanding. I’ve only cultivated two Buddhist techniques so far. Could you maybe teach me a few more Great Vehicle Buddhist arts…?"

"Is the Indestructible Golden Body or Tathagata Divine Palm available here?"

White-robed monk: ?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 7: Here for the Gu
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 7: Here for the Gu

“This old monk’s really stingy.”

After some time, Lin Jing left the Demon-Slaying Temple. Outside the city, on the Nine Dragons Carriage, Ancestor Thunder Dragon was waiting for his return. Seeing Lin Jing mumbling to himself, he couldn’t help but ask:

“Has Your Highness achieved your goal?”

Lin Jing sighed, “I guess you could say that.”

As he spoke, he held out his hand, and his palm began to shimmer with seven-colored Buddhist light.

The next moment, Lin Jing’s entire body was wrapped in that same seven-colored light!

In a flash, he vanished from where he stood and reappeared nearly a kilometer away.

“This is…” Ancestor Thunder Dragon exclaimed in shock, “Turning oneself into Buddhist light, a teleportation technique with light-speed movement!!!”

He was utterly stunned. “No wonder His Highness called that monk stingy. Once someone comprehends spatial power, who would still use a technique like this? Our Thunder Dragon Clan has a similar technique that transforms the body into lightning—it’s even better than this! Would Your Highness like to learn it?”

Back aboard the flying vessel, Lin Jing replied, “No need. With the power of the Thunder Tribulation Gu, I can achieve lightning transformation too. This Buddhist Light Technique was created by the Demon-Slaying Temple’s senior Wu Mo. He passed it on to me just to give me a Buddhist identity.”

“But more than this technique, I’d rather learn the Buddhist Sect’s Indestructible Golden Body. Too bad that kind of supreme Buddhist technique isn’t taught to anyone outside the direct lineage. Even the lower-tier version, the Vajra Body, Senior Wu Mo was unwilling to teach me.”

“Looks like being the Second Sect Master’s disciple doesn’t get me very far…”

“Definitely stingy,” Ancestor Thunder Dragon nodded. “Your Highness need not care. These techniques aren’t worth it anyway. With the Thunder Dragon Clan’s scale-blood body tempering, your physical strength won’t lose out to those Buddhist body cultivators.”

Besides… Lin Jing also had the Thunder Tribulation Gu. Ancestor Thunder Dragon couldn’t even imagine how strong Lin Jing’s physical body might eventually become. The Thunder Dragon Clan itself might not even be a match.

“That’s true, but a lot of the scale-blood the clan gave me was taken by Senior Sister Holy Maiden. It’s just not enough,” Lin Jing sighed.

“That’s nothing,” Ancestor Thunder Dragon said obsequiously, “It’s just scale-blood. I have plenty. Your Highness, take as much as you want.”

The old dragon was now completely doting on Lin Jing, all to ensure the Thunder Dragon Clan became the number one clan in the Demon Court.

After all, His Majesty the Demon Emperor was only one individual—not a clan.

“Then I won’t hold back. I’ll leave all the scale-blood needed from Core Formation to Nascent Soul transformation to you, Senior.” Lin Jing nodded.

The dragon coachman was stunned and muttered, “Your Highness… you could’ve at least pretended to be polite. I’m an old dragon… where would I get that much scale-blood…?”

“Can we use the Clan Chief’s and the Crown Prince’s instead??”

..........

The Nine Dragons Carriage rolled on. Having obtained the Buddhist Light Technique, Lin Jing headed toward his real destination—Golden Light Temple.

Ancestor Thunder Dragon acted as the coachman, soaring through the void. Right now, Golden Light Temple was without a doubt the liveliest place in the Western Desert.

Buddhist prodigies from all eighty-one cities of the Western Desert, along with Buddhist cultivators from the Ancient Kingdom, Qingzhou, Martial Emperor City, and elsewhere, all traveled thousands of miles to worship.

The Gu God Sect’s Ten Rare Gu were famous across the world. To earn the recognition of Golden Light Temple’s abbot, the Indestructible Monk, was a one-way ticket to the top.

Besides that, the current resurgence of demons in the Western Desert was also a major reason why so many Buddhist cultivators had returned.

It wasn’t just disciples of the Buddhist Sect either. As the Demon-Slaying Temple’s abbot Wu Mo had mentioned, quite a few non-Buddhist cultivators came to slay demons, some even making it onto the Fortune Gold List, looking to weaken their own fortune.

Golden Bell City—home of Golden Light Temple.

Much like when Thunder Dragon first visited Demon-Suppressing City, with the spatial passage opened, Lin Jing’s four dragons pulled the flying vessel into view, instantly drawing the attention of the entire Golden Bell City.

Within the city, whether they were Buddhist cultivators or Fortune Gold List contenders seeking karmic opportunity, all were stunned by such a flashy entrance.

“Who is that?”

“Too arrogant! This is sacred Buddhist ground—aren’t they afraid Golden Light Temple will throw them out?”

“Exactly… Wait, hold on!”

Suddenly, someone noticed the figure standing atop the vessel, and cold sweat immediately dripped from their forehead.

“That guy looks really familiar… Could it be…?”

Many people recognized Lin Jing’s face, and those who had just called him arrogant quickly shut up, afraid they’d catch the attention of this devil.

Boom!!!

As Lin Jing arrived, a golden light shot into the sky from Golden Light Temple.

Next, a Golden Body Arhat appeared in the clouds, gazing at Lin Jing standing at the front of the flying vessel.

“Young Lord of the Demon Court, halt.” He called out, and Lin Jing didn’t move forward.

The Golden Body Arhat wore a stern expression. If Lin Jing advanced even one more step, it looked like he’d act.

After all, the abbot of Golden Light Temple was currently in terrible condition—this was the temple’s weakest moment.

“No need to be nervous.”

“This Holy Son heard that the Indestructible Monk of Golden Light Temple is looking for a host for the Golden Light Gu, so I came to give it a try.” Lin Jing smiled slightly. His words immediately silenced all the cultivators watching.

The Demon Court’s Holy Son… came for the Rare Gu!

Makes sense. If there’s anything at Golden Light Temple that could draw the Demon Court’s Holy Son, it’d be a Rare Gu.

Rumor already had it that he possessed another Rare Gu—the Ice Soul Gu. Now wanting to claim the Golden Light Gu as well wasn’t far-fetched.

“Absurd.” The Golden Body Arhat frowned. “The abbot’s Golden Light Gu would never be passed to someone from the Demon Court.”

“You should leave. You don’t meet the conditions to inherit the Golden Light Gu!”

Was it going to turn into a fight...?

In Golden Bell City, many visiting prodigies were shaken. The Demon Court’s Holy Son was no pushover, but Golden Light Temple clearly didn’t welcome him. This might turn into a full-blown snatch-and-grab for the Gu!

“Let’s go.” Many visiting cultivators immediately turned to leave the city, wanting no part in what could turn into a massive battle.

Others couldn’t help but stay, eager to see how things would unfold. The drama-loving spirit in them was ablaze.

“Oh? But this Holy Son heard that as long as one is a Buddhist cultivator, they can undergo the inheritance trial.”

“The Buddhist Sect claims that all Buddhist cultivators in the world are part of the sect, spreading the Dharma everywhere. You can’t just deny that when it comes to me.”

“This Holy Son has demonstrated Buddhist techniques multiple times in the Ancient Kingdom—it’s no secret. My Buddhist talent was even discovered by Master Wu Mo during my training in the Ancient Kingdom. That should make me at least half a member of the Demon-Slaying Temple, so I should qualify.”

With a faint smile, Lin Jing continued, “Besides, I’ve got a lot of experience handling Rare Gu. Leaving the Golden Light Gu with me would best ensure it doesn’t wreak havoc on the world in the future.”

The Golden Body Arhat’s face stiffened. Seeing that Lin Jing was determined to obtain the Gu, he couldn’t risk clashing directly.

“You…”

After all, Lin Jing wasn’t wrong. Back when the Buddhist Sect was still weak, in order to grow and spread their teachings, they’d declared that any Buddhist cultivator, no matter where they practiced, was part of the sect.

They grew stronger by casting a wide net and leaving their inheritance everywhere. If they denied that now, it’d look like the sect couldn’t stand by its own words.

Besides… Wu Mo…

One of the nine Void Mystical-level monks of the Buddhist Sect. The Arhat fell into thought—this Demon Court Holy Son’s Buddhist talent had been discovered by Wu Mo himself?

“Very well. But words are just words. Rumors are still rumors. As long as Your Highness can use Buddhist techniques to defeat one of our sect’s prodigies of the same cultivation level, it will prove your claim true.”

“Your Highness, what do you say?”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 8: A Sense of Oppression
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 8: A Sense of Oppression

"After all, even true disciples of the Buddhist sect wouldn’t be allowed to take the inheritance trial without exceptional Buddhist attainments. Of course, this is also for the inheritor's safety. The Golden Light Gu's power is overwhelmingly dominant—without a certain level of Buddhist power, it’s simply impossible to control. Although Your Highness has successfully tamed the Ice Soul Lesser Gu, handling two Rare Gu at once may still be too much.”

The Golden Body Arhat took a step back, planning to persuade this Holy Son of the Demon Court to withdraw, all within the rules.

There was no need to doubt the strength of this Holy Son of the Demon Court. He had once been the number one Foundation Establishment prodigy in the Ancient Kingdom, and now seemed to have successfully reached the Core Formation stage. With a rare beast and a Rare Gu under his command, his power was undoubtedly terrifying.

But if the combat rules were restricted to only allow the use of Buddhist arts, then the Golden Body Arhat was confident—Lin Jing absolutely could not defeat a Buddhist prodigy of the same level.

"Alright." Lin Jing spoke. "Then, which Buddhist disciple do you intend to pit against me?"

Before the Arhat could answer, a voice rang out from within Golden Bell City.

"How about I be your opponent?"

Somewhere, a clean-cut monk in gray robes, holding the leg of a demon and gnawing on it, looked up at the sky and said, “Master Liu Zhi, may I represent the Buddhist sect in a friendly match with His Highness the Holy Son? Coincidentally, I came here for the Golden Light Gu as well.”

Countless divine senses descended.

This little monk, though only at the peak of Core Formation, was... known by nearly everyone.

He was one of the few Core Formation cultivators listed on the Fortune Gold List!

Ranked 99th on the Fortune Gold List, he had defeated a Nascent Soul stage contender while still at the Core Formation stage.

This little monk was considered someone who had joined the Buddhist path midway. Originally a prodigy from a side branch of the City Lord’s Mansion in Martial Emperor City, his most important mission—as with all side-branch heirs, male or female—was to form marital alliances with other major forces. Qin Siyuan was no exception. His family had arranged a marriage that he was extremely unhappy with.

Unable to escape the marriage no matter what he did, he finally joined the Buddhist sect. Who would have thought—he had extraordinary Buddhist talent. His cultivation progressed rapidly, and he was soon taken in as a disciple by a Void Mystical Stage Buddhist cultivator. Today, he was one of the most dazzling prodigies in the Buddhist sect.

“Hmm?” Seeing him still in Golden Bell City, the Arhat fell into thought.

Originally, he had planned to summon a Core Formation stage genius from Golden Light Temple to fight, but who would've thought this guy wanted to step in?

Letting Qin Siyuan fight would be far too unfair to the Holy Son of the Demon Court.

However, this way, it would ensure absolute victory.

It would completely eliminate Lin Jing’s chances of winning.

“What does Your Highness think?” The Arhat looked at Lin Jing, leaving the decision to him.

“Fortune Gold List, huh…” Lin Jing mused. So if he beat this guy, he could get on the list?

Not bad…

Lin Jing said calmly, “He’ll do.”

After speaking, his entire body transformed into Buddhist light, instantly teleporting to the ground in the form of glowing particles. This move left the Golden Body Arhat stunned—wasn’t that the Buddhist Light Art of Master Wu Mo…? Could it be this Holy Son of the Demon Court wasn’t just awakened to Buddhist talent by Wu Mo’s early inheritance, but had also mastered Wu Mo’s original spell?

Even if that were the case… Qin Siyuan's expression turned serious—he hoped the Golden Light Gu wouldn't fall into the Holy Son’s hands.

“Seems like he’s not just some nobody.” The Arhat’s Buddhist light technique had earned Lin Jing’s approval. He tossed aside the demon leg and stepped into the air. The duel between the two instantly caused a stir in Golden Bell City. A Holy Son of the Demon Court clashing with a Buddhist prodigy—such a scene was rare.

"Does he know the Vajra Indestructible Body?"

As the fight began, the Arhat stood with hands behind his back and asked.

“Of course.” Qin Siyuan replied calmly.

“And the Indestructible Golden Body?”

“I’ve mastered it as well.”

“As expected of a Buddhist prodigy.” Lin Jing nodded in recognition. After all, he’d heard that mastering the Indestructible Golden Body was incredibly difficult—even among Nascent Soul Buddhist cultivators, few succeeded.

The technique required more than just physical strength—it demanded absolute control over the body.

“May I, the Holy Son, witness it for myself?” Lin Jing requested.

Qin Siyuan didn’t respond verbally, but his appearance had already transformed—now clad in golden armor, his body had become like a Vajra.

Before many Nascent Soul cultivators could even react, Qin Siyuan had already become a streak of golden light and landed before Lin Jing.

At this moment, his body was towering, nearly twice the height of an average man.

A golden fist blocked Lin Jing’s vision and smashed down with force, answering Lin Jing’s request through action.

BANG!!!

All they heard was a loud bang as Qin Siyuan’s fist landed squarely on Lin Jing. Lin Jing’s protective true energy rippled like a pond struck by a meteor, spraying out violently. His body was blasted away like a cannonball, slamming into the city wall and collapsing it.

“What immense power.” Many Buddhist cultivators watched Qin Siyuan in shock. No wonder he made it onto the Fortune Gold List—he shattered the Holy Son's protective true energy with one punch and sent him flying!

However, at this moment, Qin Siyuan slightly furrowed his brow and looked at his fist... on its surface, the golden body had... developed fine cracks.

The Vajra Indestructible Body—was broken.

And the opponent had seemingly not taken any action—he’d merely relied on his protective true energy and physical body to take the hit.

Other spells had been disabled;
that was fine. But he hadn’t used Buddhist arts either. At this moment, Qin Siyuan felt a bad premonition—this fight might be very difficult.

“Not bad. Your physical strength is almost on par with an average Thunder Dragon.” At the same time, from the rubble of the wall, a relaxed voice rang out.

A figure dusted himself off and walked out of the smoke. Aside from a few tears in his clothes, he looked completely unharmed.

This sight made the Arhat and Qin Siyuan's pupils shrink.

“What kind of joke is this—completely unscathed?” The watching Buddhist cultivators couldn’t comprehend it.

Neither could the Golden Body Arhat. He looked toward the Thunder Dragons—even if Lin Jing had tempered his body with Thunder Dragon Scale Blood, it shouldn’t be strong enough to clash directly with a golden body. He hadn’t been in the Demon Court for long!

Something was going far beyond his expectations.

How could the Holy Son of the Demon Court have such a ridiculous physique? With such a powerful body, even the most basic Buddhist arts would produce terrifying destructive force.

What he didn’t know was that Lin Jing had done more than temper himself with dragon blood. He had spent ten years being struck by lightning and burned by fire in the Hourglass of Time—not ordinary lightning, not ordinary fire—just to achieve an invincible body!

And with the Hourglass, the one thing Lin Jing never lacked was time.

Add in a foundation of the Thirteenth Stage of Qi Refinement, and even the Thunder Dragon Prince and Princess might not have stronger bodies than Lin Jing.

And what Lin Jing said next cast an even darker shadow over the Buddhist prodigy’s heart.

“Judging by the feel of it, if I take another three hundred and seven Golden Body strikes, I might be able to deduce and comprehend the Indestructible Golden Body myself. The only question is—can your mana hold out for that many attacks…”

“Shall we see who falls first—you, knocking me down with your Golden Body, or me, learning the technique from you? My Buddhist nature is quite strong, after all.”

“But your physical strength is lacking, so this Golden Body likely isn’t your trump card. Inside you... it seems there’s a demon sealed by Buddhist arts. Or are you going to use its power directly?”

A monster… At this moment, even those cultivators who weren’t fighting Lin Jing broke out in cold sweat, feeling the oppressive pressure of this so-called Holy Son of the Demon Court.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 9: Asura
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 9: Asura

"You bastard.."

"Aren’t you being a little too arrogant?"

Staring at the calm and composed Lin Jing, Qin Siyuan put his hands together.

After repairing his cracked golden body, his figure and appearance began to change gradually.

"I admit... among the Core Formation cultivators I’ve met, you have the strongest pure physical power."

"But trying to comprehend this supreme Buddhist art just by experiencing Golden Body strikes... isn’t that a bit too little Golden Body for something like the Indestructible Golden Body?"

"Even for me, when I first learned the Indestructible Golden Body, I suffered greatly."

"But, since you want to try, I’ll grant your wish."

This was Qin Siyuan’s Core Formation divine ability: Asura War God!

After transforming, his physical strength multiplied several times, and the power of his martial techniques also greatly increased.

Right then, Qin Siyuan ignored the wear and tear on his Core and activated the divine ability. The scariest part was—after transforming into Asura, he could still maintain full-body golden armor.

The golden, shining Asura giant glared furiously at Lin Jing and threw a punch.

Boom!

Lin Jing also launched a Vajra Fist. As the massive and small fists collided, Lin Jing heard a cracking sound—his hand bones, arm bones began to fracture, and the next moment, he was blasted away by the shockwave.

"Now, my physical body."

Feeling himself crack Lin Jing’s bones with a single punch, Qin Siyuan spoke—though inwardly shocked. In this state, even a Nascent Soul cultivator’s manifestation could be shattered by him, and yet he had only fractured this guy’s bones?

Just how strong was the Holy Son of the Demon Court’s body?

"Smash!!!" No matter how Lin Jing trained, Qin Siyuan had no intention of losing to a guy who defected to the demon race. After blasting Lin Jing away, his figure flashed and landed heavily in the direction Lin Jing flew, clapping his hands together and directly sandwiching Lin Jing between his palms. With a thunderous boom, waves of air rolled out and clouds dispersed.

Qin Siyuan’s attack was extremely fierce. This scene made the Golden Body Arhat breathe a sigh of relief.

It looked like Qin Siyuan was indeed more skilled. He’d seen Lin Jing’s counter earlier—it was a Vajra Fist, indeed a rudimentary Buddhist technique. Though it had terrifying power when paired with that mighty body, it couldn’t compare to Qin Siyuan’s combined divine ability of Asura War God and Indestructible Golden Body, even if it meant damaging his Core.

Still, the fact that he wasn’t blasted into a blood mist under that combination already earned Lin Jing the Golden Body Arhat’s recognition.

But this was the limit. With this blow, Lin Jing’s internal organs must have been shaken. Even for a body cultivator, the insides are always weaker than the outer shell.

And Qin Siyuan’s palm strike—its damage went straight inside!

"Your Highness the Holy Son—" Seeing Lin Jing’s condition, the nine Thunder Dragons watching from outside the city were shocked and quickly looked toward the coachman-like Thunder Dragon Ancestor, deeply worried about Lin Jing’s safety.

"No problem." The Thunder Dragon Ancestor just shook his head. Having studied body tempering for thousands of years, it could see Lin Jing’s physical nature better than anyone.

"No need to worry... His Highness’s body isn’t just a bit strong in offense and defense." Ancestor Huangzhi squinted his eyes.

As he finished speaking, people saw Qin Siyuan’s clasped giant palms suddenly tremble. Under the shocked gazes of the Thunder Dragons and many Buddhist cultivators, those massive palms were forcibly pried open from within by a single man.

That man didn’t show any signs of damaged organs or fractured bones.

"Impossible." Seeing this, Qin Siyuan took a deep breath. He had clearly felt it—he was sure the guy’s bones had cracked, his internal organs had bled.

"Could it be..." This Buddhist prodigy was suddenly stunned. Could it be that in just a few breaths, Lin Jing had already healed all the damage?

That fast?

"Nice strike." Lin Jing pried open the massive hands and offered some praise. Thankfully, he had taken the Tier-Six rare plant, Nine-Life Exquisite Grass, giving him terrifying regenerative ability.

Otherwise, that blow would indeed have broken the body he had spent ten years tempering.

Looks like his body still wasn’t strong enough...

That insane regenerative ability was actually a key reason Lin Jing had achieved his current physical strength. Without the rare plant he found in that Cavern Heaven, he couldn’t have endured the damage from lightning and fire tempering.

"Buddhist Light." Lin Jing turned into a stream of Buddhist light from between the two palms. Now that the opponent could break through his defenses, there was no way he would just sit and take hits. Whether being struck by the Golden Body or striking it himself, both allowed him to experience the power of the technique.

Since no one was willing to teach him, Lin Jing would see for himself—could he steal-learn the Indestructible Golden Body through battle?

After all, the difficult part of the technique was said to be the absolute control over one’s body. And after enduring the harshest of tempering, Lin Jing had already reached the pinnacle of body control.

"Have you ever been kicked by Buddhist Light?" As a beam of light, Lin Jing’s speed completely surpassed Qin Siyuan’s. The beam descended in a flash, his leg wrapped in Buddhist Light, and smashed into the Asura’s head. Boom! Qin Siyuan’s head snapped sideways in Asura form, his entire body staggering in the other direction.

Lin Jing realized that this Buddhist Light technique, paired with physical combat, was quite effective—it could better accelerate himself and thus deliver even more powerful blows.

"D*mn it." Stabilizing himself, Qin Siyuan was extremely unwilling, but the next moment, Lin Jing’s storm of kicks rained down again, toppling his massive body to the ground!

"D*mn... still didn’t break through his defenses?" The situation seemed to be turning awkward. Both of them were scratching each other—neither could hurt the other.

However, this mutual ineffectiveness clearly didn’t satisfy Qin Siyuan.

After all, maintaining this divine ability placed huge pressure on his Core.

Lying on the ground, a strange aura began leaking from his body. Black mist oozed from his eyes, ears, nostrils, and mouth.

Seeing this, Lin Jing knew the other was about to bring out his true trump card. He had sensed long ago that Qin Siyuan had a terrifying monster sealed inside him. From what the guy had shown so far, defeating a regular Nascent Soul cultivator wasn’t hard—but to beat someone who had made the Gold List? Far from enough.

Looks like the guy was ready to use his ultimate move.

"If this still counts as Buddhist art, then I might as well..." Just as Lin Jing was about to unleash the demonic forces sealed in the Buddha-Demon Tree—

Suddenly, a voice rang out from Golden Light Temple.

"That’s enough." The voice echoed through the entire city. In an instant, it halted Qin Siyuan’s actions, and the black energy within him was forced back.

"This is a sacred Buddhist ground. The two of you should not unleash such dark forces in battle. Let’s call this match a draw. Liu Zhi, bring His Highness the Holy Son over. His Buddhist attainments are sufficient to undertake the Golden Light Gu’s inheritance trial."

The voice came from none other than the Indestructible Monk of Golden Light Temple.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 10: The Fourth Pet Beast
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 10: The Fourth Pet Beast

"I haven’t lost yet!"

The battle had been interrupted, and Qin Siyuan clearly wasn’t convinced.

He climbed back up, looking like he still wanted to fight.

Lin Jing looked toward Golden Light Temple—was the abbot finally willing to come out?

As for the interruption, Lin Jing didn’t care. Although he hadn’t gotten more contact with the Indestructible Golden Body, the stealing was already complete.

Of course, that didn’t mean he’d mastered it.

"Mogu." Inside Lin Jing, a small mushroom transformed into Qin Siyuan’s appearance, like a mini Nascent Soul, its whole body exuding the golden body, its illusion nearly materialized.

It was basically a memory card, manifesting the power of the Indestructible Golden Body.

Although it would be more troublesome and probably not replicate much of its essence, at least it was a method.

"Sigh...." At the same time, the Golden Body Arhat let out a helpless sigh.

So they’d alerted the abbot after all...

"I was too careless. According to the abbot’s instructions, this battle shall be considered a draw." The Golden Body Arhat also knew there was a terrifying thing sealed inside Qin Siyuan.

If Qin Siyuan borrowed its power in such a sacred Buddhist ground, the consequences would indeed be poor.

"Take me to your abbot." Lin Jing spoke, and though the Golden Body Arhat was unwilling, he could only lead the way.

At the same time, he shook his head at Qin Siyuan, signaling that it was over.

He was very dissatisfied with this rising Buddhist talent’s performance.

Though Lin Jing’s body was ridiculously powerful, with the difference of several small realms, Lin Jing hadn’t even used his main spell techniques, yet still forced Qin Siyuan to such a state. If this spread, it would surely damage the Buddhist sect’s reputation.

"Since the abbot has spoken, let the two of you finish this during the final Rare Gu competition."

Watching the backs of the Golden Body Arhat and Lin Jing, Qin Siyuan fumed with frustration.

"What are you looking at."

He canceled his divine ability, pulled out a demon leg again, took a vicious bite, and glared at the bystanders.

.............

Compared to Master Wu Mo of the Demon-Slaying Temple, the Indestructible Monk of Golden Light Temple looked far more aged.

Like a frail old man, he sat silently on a golden lotus, only slowly opening his eyes once Lin Jing was brought before him.

"Didn’t expect His Highness to be interested in the Golden Light Gu as well."

"Good things are naturally interesting to everyone. So, can I take the inheritance trial next?" Lin Jing smiled.

"Mm. Since Master Wu Mo passed his original Buddhist Light Technique to you, Your Highness can indeed be considered part of the Buddhist sect."

The Indestructible Monk spoke: "In the coming trials, Golden Light Temple will not make things difficult in the slightest. However, His Highness must also follow the proper rules."

Lin Jing expressed no issue with that.

"You should already know a bit about the Golden Light Gu’s inheritance trial, correct?"

"But let this humble monk explain it again."

"The first trial of the Golden Light Gu is about having strong Buddhist cultivation. In this, His Highness qualifies."

"As for the second trial, it requires mastery of the ‘Buddha-Demon Pearl,’ a Buddhist technique, and cultivating it to the Great Perfection stage. A perfected Buddha-Demon Pearl can seal various evil spirits and demons within the pearl, subduing demons with one’s own power."

"As for this, the humble monk already sensed it earlier. His Highness has indeed mastered the Buddha-Demon Tree, and to a deep degree, thus also qualifies."

"The third trial is to use the Buddha-Demon Pearl to successfully seal a ghost of at least the ‘Nascent Soul level.’"

"And the final trial is a comparison among those who have passed the first three."

"Not a duel, but rather, a comparison of who has sealed the more ferocious and evil ghost within their body—and who can remain conscious while channeling more of the ghostly divine power."

"Finally, this humble monk will determine the inheritor of the Golden Light Gu based on each one’s aptitude."

"Got it." Lin Jing nodded. He had indeed already known all this.

And it saved a lot of trouble—when he studied Buddhism in the Ancient Kingdom, the Buddha-Demon Pearl was part of what he learned.

His mastery of the Buddha-Demon Pearl had unknowingly already reached a high level.

He had first sealed the evil power of the Ice Soul Lesser Gu, then the countless resentments of powerful beings that had clung to him in Tongtian City, which had already brought his Buddha-Demon Tree to Great Perfection.

But there was one feature of the perfected Buddha-Demon Tree he hadn’t tested.

A perfected Buddha-Demon Tree could even seal conscious living beings.

The Ice Soul Lesser Gu was indeed a living being, but it was a pet beast—Lin Jing had no need to seal it. He had only sealed the negative energies it emitted that affected him, so this power had gone unused.

Unexpectedly, it was now exactly what this Golden Light Gu trial called for.

"Does it have to be a ghost?" Lin Jing asked.

The Indestructible Monk nodded. "With the Golden Era now underway, the resurgent monsters in the Western Desert are overwhelmingly ghosts."

"These ghosts are formed from the grudges of ancient immortals, or the obsessions of countless dead experts, or mutated remnant souls. All are great calamities to the world. Compared to tangible monsters and demons, they are far harder to deal with."

"This trial isn’t just to choose a suitable inheritor for the Golden Light Gu, but also to cultivate those who can counter such phantoms."

"Understood."

"A ghost of Nascent Soul level and above, huh."

Lin Jing understood that the difficulty likely didn’t lie in subduing a Nascent Soul ghost.

After all, the trial didn’t require the ghost to be captured personally.

So having a Guardian capture one was totally acceptable.

The real challenge... was how to use the Buddha-Demon Tree to seal a Nascent Soul ghost across realms, and how to seize power from the ghostly being without being backlashed.

Perhaps in the eyes of the Indestructible Monk, only someone who could manage that would be qualified to suppress the fully awakened Golden Light Gu in the future.

"Senior Indestructible Monk, Golden Light Temple should have a map of powerful ghost locations in the Western Desert, right?"

"Could I get a copy? Save me the time of searching for one myself."

"You interrupted my fight with that Buddhist prodigy earlier and cost me a spot on the Fortune Gold List. Consider this compensation." Lin Jing chuckled.

"Of course." The Indestructible Monk chuckled too. "Then I’ll trouble Your Highness to clear out as many ghosts as possible..."

"But this humble monk also has one thing to remind Your Highness."

"Even before the Golden Era began, one of the Three Great Demonic Sects—the Netherworld Hall—seems to have moved its base to the Western Desert. Now that the Golden Era is underway, the Netherworld Hall has become even more active. While our Buddhist sect is working to exterminate the ghosts, the Netherworld Hall seems intent on protecting them. Please, Your Highness, be cautious of these demonic cultivators as well."

"Netherworld Hall..." Lin Jing narrowed his eyes. So that group had come to the Western Desert.

No wonder they didn’t show up during the Demon Court’s great purge.

"Thank you for the warning, Senior."

Compared to the Netherworld Hall, Lin Jing was currently more concerned with what powerful ghosts existed in the Western Desert.

As a Longevity Body cultivator with life-burning Beast Taming Divine Arts, he naturally didn’t fear ghosts. This trial, honestly, just meant asking the Thunder Dragon Ancestor to go help him subdue a ghostly servant, and then trying to tame it himself.

He really had to sigh.

Now that he’d advanced to Core Formation, he had another contract slot.

But all his companions couldn’t be contracted with the Beast Taming Art.

The Mechanized Turtle—can’t be contracted.

The Spell Spirit Origin—can’t be contracted.

Mosquito Woman and other demon clans—can’t be contracted.

Immortal Cow—can’t be contracted.

Elder Pine and other plant spirits—can’t be contracted.

And now, he was being asked to catch ghosts—obviously also uncontractable.

Lin Jing pondered—he really needed to revise the Beast Taming Art when he had the time. At the very least, it had to be more versatile. When that’s done, he could finally consider his fourth pet beast.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 11: Void Mystical Ghost
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 11: Void Mystical Ghost

“Your Highness, Holy Son.”

On the Nine-Dragon Boat, the Thunder Dragon Ancestor stepped forward as soon as he saw Lin Jing return from the Golden Light Temple.

Seeing the scroll in Lin Jing’s hand, he instantly knew that the Holy Son had once again fleeced those bald donkeys.

He just wasn’t sure if this Indestructible Monk would be any more generous than that Wu Mo Abbot.

“Thank you for waiting so long, Senior,” Lin Jing said.

“To serve the Holy Son is no trouble at all,” the Thunder Dragon Ancestor chuckled.

“To have your protection is truly my fortune,” Lin Jing sighed. “I’m afraid I’ll have to trouble you again for the next trial.”

“Hmm?” The Thunder Dragon Ancestor grew alert.

After listening, the Thunder Dragon Ancestor finally understood.

He stroked his dragon whiskers, pondering. “So, Your Highness needs me to suppress a Core Formation-level ghost?”

“I’ll beat it half to death first, then it’ll be easier for you to seal it inside yourself!”

Lin Jing nodded—that was exactly the plan.

“Piece of cake!” The Thunder Dragon Ancestor had thought it would be some impossible task. This?

Next second, the Holy Son added, “But it’s not a Core Formation-level ghost—it’s a Void Mystical-level ghost.”

Thunder Dragon Ancestor: ?

“The final trial is determined by the Indestructible Monk himself, who judges based on the ghost sealed. If I seal a Core Formation-level ghost just like all the other Buddhist prodigies, I won’t have any advantage, and he’ll still be more inclined toward true Buddhist disciples.”

“If there’s no clear gap between us, the right to explain and decide naturally lies with Golden Light and the others—we wouldn’t be able to argue against it anyway.”

“Only by creating a significant gap between me and the other contenders can I firmly take the Golden Light Gu. If even then the Golden Light Temple shows bias, we’ll have grounds to bring our case to His Majesty the Demon Emperor.”

“This...” The Thunder Dragon Ancestor had to admit the logic was sound, but... a Void Mystical-level ghost? Did the Holy Son even realize the kind of bullsh*t he was saying?

That kind of thing—can you just seal it because you say so?

Even the Indestructible Monk himself might not be able to handle it so easily.

He was just a guardian, not some death-seeking warrior!

Guarding Lin Jing had been a relatively easy task, theoretically speaking. After all, most top cultivators feared the Demon Emperor and wouldn’t dare make a move on Lin Jing.

The weaker small fries could be dealt with casually by the guardian.

If a true powerhouse appeared, the Demon Emperor himself would step in.

So all the way through, it was unlikely anything too difficult would happen.

But... actively seeking trouble? That’s a whole different story.

A Void Mystical-level ghost—that’s the trouble.

Even if the Thunder Dragon Ancestor had natural abilities that countered ghosts, he had no desire to actually fight another Void Mystical entity.

And most importantly—he didn’t believe Lin Jing could succeed in sealing a ghost of that level.

Even a Core Formation ghost was pushing it...

It was like asking a Golden Core-stage Beast Tamer to control a Core Formation demonic beast.

“It’s not that I don’t want to help,” the Thunder Dragon Ancestor advised, “I’m just afraid Your Highness won’t be able to handle it.”

“Even if I beat the Void Mystical ghost until it’s barely breathing, with your current cultivation, I fear it’d still be hard to seal. And even if you succeed, the ghost’s power at the Void Mystical level is far more condensed than Core Formation spiritual power—it’d be hard to use and may backfire!”

“True.” Lin Jing understood that too. But without pushing to this level, he might not be able to win the Golden Light Gu. He had no choice.

Besides, losing under the rules meant the Gu would be forever out of reach—going to the Demon Emperor wouldn’t help either.

“There is a sliver of possibility, though,” Lin Jing revealed his advantage.

“Ghosts are mostly formless beings, undead entities. What they fear most is someone full of yang energy and vigorous life force.”

“I have the Longevity Body. My vitality is already on par with Void Mystical-level cultivators. And this is only the peak of minor achievement in the Longevity Body. If I can advance to major achievement, my life force will rival even Dao-Seeking cultivators. Without doubt, I’d be a natural ghost nemesis. With vitality like that, it’d be extremely hard for any ghost to bite back.”

“Moreover, what ghosts fear even more is heavenly thunder. Right now, my Ice Soul Gu has devoured the Thunder Tribulation Gu. When I fuse with it, I can wield heavenly thunder—another ghost-repelling ability.”

“Also, the Ice Soul power of the Ice Soul Gu has extremely strong suppressive effects on these ghost beings. It’s one of the Ten Rare Gu specifically designed to target souls.”

“So, if Senior could just help beat the ghost to a sliver of life, I might just have a shot at sealing it.”

“And I don’t even need to seal it permanently—just keep it under control for a day or even half a day. Once the inheritance is complete, I can immediately extract and destroy it.”

After Lin Jing finished speaking, the Thunder Dragon Ancestor fell into deep thought.

“And, the kind of ghost we choose matters too,” Lin Jing shook the scroll in his hand.

“This is the intel I got from the Golden Light Temple—it contains information on most powerful ghosts in the Western Desert.”

“Ghosts come in all forms—human-shaped, beast-shaped, or strange, untraceable things beyond comprehension.”

“What we want is definitely a beast-shaped ghost.”

“That way, my Beast Taming Art and Beast Taming Physique might actually work. And, what you don’t know, Senior, is that I have a divine ability that can massively boost my Beast Taming level...”

That was the lifespan-burning Divine Control Technique. Lin Jing could temporarily sacrifice decades of lifespan in exchange for a surge in Beast Taming prowess. For the Golden Light Gu, burning a hundred years of life? Totally worth it.

All these stacked advantages were why Lin Jing believed this was worth a try.

“Alright then...” Seeing the Holy Son had indeed thought this through, the Thunder Dragon Ancestor nodded. Besides, His Majesty the Demon Emperor was in the Western Desert too—what could possibly go wrong?

Let’s do it!

He was the Thunder Dragon Ancestor, master of lightning—he wouldn’t be scared of some wandering ghosts!

All for the Transformation Pill!

“Then does Your Highness have a target in mind?” the Thunder Dragon Ancestor asked.

Lin Jing unrolled the scroll.

“Not yet, but it’s not too late to look now.”

The scroll recorded over a hundred tough Nascent Soul-level ghosts that the Buddhist sects had failed to exorcise.

As for Core Formation-level ghosts, there were about a dozen.

And at the Void Mystical level—there were three.

Skipping over the Nascent Soul and Core Formation ghosts, Lin Jing’s eyes went straight to the three great Void Mystical ghosts.

The Buddhist sects had documented these three not because they wanted anyone to deal with them, but as warnings—do not go near these three evil spirits.

“The first one—The Ghost Bride...”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 12: The Ghost Bride
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 12: The Ghost Bride

“The Ghost Bride, suspected in life to be a cultivator with the Yin-Convergence Spirit Body.

This physique possesses cold Yin spiritual power, and upon death, easily transforms into a powerful ghost.

The Ghost Bride appears as a spirit clad in a red wedding dress, wearing a red bridal veil that obscures her face. Because of her physique, after death, she became a vessel for countless ancient resentful souls.”

“After the Golden Era descended, the Ghost Bride first appeared in Qibao City, one of the Eighty-One Buddhist Cities of the Western Desert. Every Buddhist cultivator who tried to purify her was killed by her—even the abbot of Qibao Temple, the Qibao Patriarch whose cultivation was at late Core Formation, was no exception.”

“Countless cultivators and civilians fled Qibao City, which soon became a ghost town.”

“Buddhist experts launched multiple assaults. Even Void Mystical-level monks descended, attempting to reclaim Qibao City, but were all repelled by the Ghost Bride—unquestionably a true Void Mystical combatant...”

“The second, the Shadow Ghost.”

“A formless ghost, with a black shadow for a body. Aside from its possession and body-snatching ability, no other special powers have been discovered.”

“When Buddhist experts gave chase, it had already taken over the body of a lone Demon Ancestor roaming the Western Desert. By then it had already taken shape—hard to deal with.”

“Its power is hard to estimate. Extremely dangerous.”

.......

“The third, the Ghost Mother.”

“Appears as a pink jellyfish-like spirit. Origin unknown. It is a vessel ghost.”

“Possesses the eerie ability to enter magical artifacts and weapons, transforming into an artifact spirit.”

“Once inside, it can sever the connection between the artifact and its owner, unleashing power far beyond what’s normal.”

“Because of the Ghost Mother, the Western Desert has seen multiple artifact devours its master incidents. The highest-ranked known victim was a half-step Void Mystical artifact cultivator from Martial Emperor City, who came to forge Buddhist tools—only to be slaughtered by his own artifacts.”

“Now, the Ghost Mother has become a moving treasure vault, hoarding countless magical items, and still actively searching for more.”

.....

After reading through these three ghost entities officially recognized by the Buddhist sects as Void Mystical-level ghosts, Lin Jing fell into deep thought.

“This Ghost Bride is a humanoid ghost—not very suitable.”

“This Shadow Ghost, although it possessed a Demon Ancestor’s body, that body’s just a shell. Whether the ghost is fundamentally demonic or not is hard to say.”

“As for this vessel ghost—the Ghost Mother—visually, it’s a jellyfish, so it’s a sea beast, that’s true. But... it seems even more bizarre than the other two.”

“The Buddhist sects are really screwed, huh?” muttered the Thunder Dragon Ancestor, who had been sneakily peeking. “And these are just the Void Mystical-level ghosts—not even the tangible demons and monsters.”

“So, Your Highness, which one are we going to catch?”

After a bit of thinking, Lin Jing pointed at the Ghost Mother’s profile. “I’m more interested in this ghost—the vessel ghost that controls artifacts and kills. Based on the current information, it’s the only one that actually manifests in beast form. It might have been a jellyfish in life.”

“Though... I have no idea why a jellyfish would be in the desert.”

The Thunder Dragon Ancestor mused, “Maybe in ancient times, this place wasn’t a desert. And if that ghost’s been around for a long time, it makes more sense. Compared to the other two, that one at least leaves traces when it moves—might be easier to track...”

“We’ll just fish it out.”

Lin Jing summoned the Pine Leaf Mouse. The little Pine Tree climbed onto Lin Jing’s shoulder, and he said, “Since this Ghost Mother is a vessel ghost and likes seizing magical weapons to kill, we’ll just bait it with a big enough pile of weapons.”

“Over the next period, I’ll have to trouble Senior Thunder Dragon to carry as many high-grade magical artifacts as possible to draw the Ghost Mother’s attention here in the Western Desert.”

“As for me, I’ll need to go into seclusion for a while—to push my Longevity Body to major achievement.”

“I’ll provide all the magical artifacts for Senior to use as bait.”

If there’s one thing Lin Jing wasn’t short on, it was magical items. After the battle in Tongtian City, who knew how many artifacts and weapons he had collected—all stored in the Pine Leaf Mouse’s Monster Core.

As for pushing the Longevity Body to major achievement, Lin Jing was already just one step away.

In truth, Gu Qianqiu understood the Longevity Body better than Lin Jing did. In the thousand years she had been reduced to blood food by the Sky Lion Clan, the entire clan had poured their efforts into enhancing Gu Qianqiu’s Longevity Body.

That’s how Gu Qianqiu had achieved the miracle of attaining major achievement in the Longevity Body while still only in early Core Formation.

You have to understand, even with Lin Jing’s exceptional foundation of Qi Refinement Stage Thirteen before Foundation Establishment, by the time he reached early Core Formation, his Longevity Body had only reached the peak of minor achievement.

Of course, that didn’t mean Lin Jing was slow in progress.

Usually, for high-grade physiques, it was extremely rare to reach major achievement even by the Nascent Soul stage. Only low-level physiques like elemental ones might rapidly mature early on through external aid.

Under normal circumstances, for a physique with power on par with divine bodies, one had to reach the Nascent Soul stage to even hope for major achievement or perfection.

If someone could achieve major or even full completion before Nascent Soul, it meant they had the potential to fight across realms just through their physique alone!

The Longevity Body might not be known for raw combat power, but in terms of effectiveness, it was definitely comparable to divine bodies. For Lin Jing to nearly reach major achievement in just a few decades of cultivation—it was already quite impressive.

And now, aiming to push his Longevity Body to major achievement wasn’t that difficult—because even before his Core Formation, he had already obtained all of Gu Qianqiu’s experiences on cultivating the Longevity Body.

It’s just that improving the Longevity Body had never been Lin Jing’s top cultivation goal. He hadn’t actively focused on it, since raising its level only extended lifespan—it didn’t boost his combat power.

In fact, it might attract higher-level beasts instead.

He already had enough lifespan—what use was a few extra thousand years?

Naturally, Lin Jing had prioritized illusion arts, body refinement, and other paths that enhanced his strength.

But now, the situation was different.

He needed the Longevity Body’s vitality to suppress a ghost.

Also, after breaking through to Core Formation, his lifespan had more utility—it could power his divine ability and the Divine Control Technique.

So now, bringing the Longevity Body to major achievement and gaining more lifespan had a much higher return on investment. Lin Jing decided it was time to break through.

“Alright then...” the Thunder Dragon Ancestor sighed. Just hope this Ghost Mother wasn’t too strong. If he couldn’t handle it and had to ask the Demon Emperor for help—what a humiliation that’d be.

...........

While Lin Jing focused on temporarily enhancing his Longevity Body and assigned the Thunder Dragon Ancestor to “fish” for the Ghost Mother...

In Treasure City—

The red-robed Ghost Bride stood beneath crumbling ruins and reached out her hand, murmuring with confusion: “A ripple from one who lives...”

“Found that... perhaps I can recover fewer memories.”

A sheet of paper, shaped like a marriage certificate, condensed in her palm and slowly faded away.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 13: Netherworld Hall
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 13: Netherworld Hall

News of Lin Jing’s arrival in the Western Desert began to spread from the Golden Light Temple.

It didn’t take long before the entire Western Desert knew the Holy Son of the Demon Court had arrived.

Naturally, there weren’t many Buddhist cultivators in possession of Lin Jing’s Longevity Flesh, and the ghosts residing in the Western Desert also generally showed no interest in the Longevity Body.

Because they were already “dead,” existing purely in the form of souls.

This form was already a defiance of heaven—robbing time from the Heavenly Dao itself.

As a result, weak ghosts had extremely short lifespans and could dissipate at any moment.

Stronger ghosts, however, if they found the right method, could persist for terrifyingly long spans—an extreme contrast.

But on the other hand, while not limited by lifespan, ghosts had more weaknesses and their cultivation was not easy.

Aside from Buddhists and ghosts, news of Lin Jing also reached the ears of the Western Desert’s demonic beasts and the demonic sects.

And then came an odd development—few powerful beast clans in the Western Desert tried to capture Lin Jing to devour him.

Most instead tried to use him as a bridge to connect with the Demon Court and become part of the demon race, searching tirelessly for traces of Lin Jing.

As for the Netherworld Hall...

A white-robed Netherworld Saintess, her long black hair covering her face, knelt on one knee before the throne.

Seated upon it was a figure draped in black robes, with no flesh or skin, a human-shaped skeleton whose heart was formed by a flickering nether ghostfire—

A ghost cultivator at the Dao-Seeking Stage... the Hall Master of the Netherworld Hall.

“According to the intel, the Holy Son of the Demon Court is likely here for the Golden Light Gu of the Golden Light Temple, which has nothing to do with our plan.”

“However, it’s said the Holy Son displayed the Netherworld Hall’s ghostfire technique in Tongtian City and, with the Demon Emperor’s help, cultivated it to perfection... I don’t know what his intentions are.”

The Netherworld Saintess finished her report, but the Hall Master remained silent for a long while.

Eventually, he spoke slowly: “Since the Demon Court isn’t here for us, don’t concern yourselves with them.”

“My unfortunate junior wasn’t weak, and even he was forced to disband—best not to provoke this Demon Court Master.”

“Our target is that vessel ghost.”

“Once we obtain control of it, whether it’s the Demon Court or the Ancient Kingdom, we’ll have no reason to fear either.”

A flicker of ghostfire lit up in the Hall Master’s hollow eyes.

The vessel ghost—Ghost Mother.

What even the Buddhist sects didn’t know was—

This ghost wasn’t a conventional one. According to ancient records in the Netherworld Hall, it was actually a war weapon created by the Ancient Celestial Palace—a final-tier artifact spirit.

It was essentially a universal key, capable of activating any magical artifact.

Even immortal-grade artifacts could be partially controlled by it...
