The Hall Master’s bony fingers lightly tapped the armrest.

Immortal artifacts...

Weapons said to only be usable by true immortals.

At present, throughout the entire Tianyuan Continent, while a few immortal artifacts still remained in the hands of major forces,

not even the Ancient Emperors of the Ancient Kingdom could activate them.

Right now, the only one on the continent known to be able to use an immortal artifact—

was the City Lord of Martial Emperor City.

No one knew what method he used,

but precisely because of it, he was able to stand between the Ancient Kingdom and the Western Desert with the power of just one city, answerable to neither side.

To be exact... the entire Martial Emperor City was the immortal artifact.

A city used as a weapon!

Coincidentally, the Netherworld Hall also possessed a legacy immortal artifact.

Unfortunately, they had never found a way to activate it.

An unactivated immortal artifact was no better than scrap metal.

And that final-tier artifact spirit from the Ancient Celestial Palace—the Ghost Mother—was one of the Netherworld Hall’s few hopes of triggering it.

Several years ago, Netherworld Hall cultivators located the seal site of the vessel ghost in the Western Desert. But before they could crack the ruins and retrieve the sealed war spirit,

the Golden Era descended, and the vessel ghost broke free on its own.

Once free, it became uncontrollable. Its whereabouts unpredictable. Even if the artifacts it possessed were destroyed, sealed, or blocked, its true body couldn’t be found.

Now, nearly half the Netherworld Hall’s members were scattered throughout the Western Desert, examining magical artifacts in hopes of tracking down the ghost.

....

“Why hasn’t it shown up yet...”

“It’s been six months already...”

Six months later, above the Western Desert—

The Thunder Dragon Ancestor was flying the Nine-Dragon Boat aimlessly across the region.

Following Lin Jing’s instructions, it was hauling a wagon full of magical artifacts in hopes of attracting the vessel ghost. But after half a year, the Ghost Mother had refused to invade a single item.

Though, the Thunder Dragon Ancestor had heard occasional rumors of the Ghost Mother killing elsewhere, by the time they arrived, the ghost had always vanished without a trace.

“Old Ancestor, could it be that you’re just too strong, and that’s scaring her off?”

One young Thunder Dragon prodigy hauling the cart tried flattering him.

“That’s not it!” the Thunder Dragon Ancestor shook his head. “Not long ago, that vessel ghost even dared to snatch a Buddhist artifact from a temple guarded by a Void Mystical monk. She doesn’t strike me as the timid type.”

“Then maybe it’s that the artifacts we’re using as bait don’t suit her tastes?” another Thunder Dragon youth guessed.

“Maybe one or two items, sure. But we’ve got a whole wagon here, including top-tier magical artifacts. She can’t possibly be that picky,” the Thunder Dragon Ancestor still didn’t get it.

And to bait the Ghost Mother properly, he’d even sealed off his thunder powers and his dragon clan yang energy.

Ten thunder dragons all sighed in unison—first time they’d ever found ghost-catching to be so difficult.

“We’re running out of time. The Golden Light Temple’s inheritance trial ends in just half a year,” the Thunder Dragon Ancestor calculated in his mind.

At the same time, he glanced into the flying boat. Fortunately, the Holy Son had yet to exit seclusion.

They had arrived in the Western Desert rather late. The Golden Light Gu’s inheritance trial, due to the Indestructible Monk’s limited lifespan, would only last three years.

By the time Lin Jing and the others showed up, only one year remained.

By now, the other Buddhist contenders had probably already sealed their ghosts and were waiting for the final contest.

“If this keeps up...” As the Thunder Dragon Ancestor mulled over whether he should use the Thunder Dragon Clan’s treasured artifact to help the Holy Son fish, suddenly, his expression shifted as he looked toward the distant horizon.

“She’s here!!!”

As the sky darkened, the Thunder Dragon Ancestor felt the ghostly might of a Void Mystical-level entity and his eyes lit up.

The nine thunder dragons pulling the boat quickly transferred the Holy Son and the others into the Thunder Dragon Ancestor’s internal space to shield them from any battle fallout.

However—

Just as the Thunder Dragon Ancestor prepared for a showdown with the vessel ghost he’d waited six months for,

the ghost that appeared made the entire dragon freeze in thought.

Staring at the “Red-Robed Ghost Bride” before him, the Thunder Dragon Ancestor couldn’t help tugging at his beard.

Wait a minute—why is it the Ghost Bride, not the vessel ghost???

“Ghost Bride it is, then. If we can’t catch the vessel ghost, we still need something to show for it,” the Thunder Dragon Ancestor gave up thinking in the next instant.

His aura immediately surged, crashing down toward the Ghost Bride.

Crack!!!

The Thunder Dragon Ancestor returned to his true form, massive bolts of lightning coiling around his body. With a roar of thunder, a sphere of lightning the size of a dragon pearl formed in his mouth—

“On your body...”

Faced with the Thunder Dragon Ancestor’s overwhelming power, the Ghost Bride instinctively felt a sense of dread.

But there was something inside the Thunder Dragon Ancestor that made her ignore the danger and confront him head-on.

“On your body, there’s a marriage certificate—that belongs to me.”

“I don’t intend to fight you. I’m only here for that marriage scroll.”

“Huh???” The Thunder Dragon Ancestor froze at the Ghost Bride’s words.

Marriage scroll?

He knew exactly what he had brought with him.

And those nine juniors certainly had nothing to do with this Ghost Bride.

So what was she talking about?

“Senior, let me out. The marriage scroll she’s talking about is with me.”

At that moment, a voice came from within the Thunder Dragon Ancestor’s body, sending him once again into deep thought.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 14: Obsession
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 14: Obsession

On the flying ship, Lin Jing opened his eyes.

From his storage pouch, he took out a marriage certificate imbued with chilling spiritual energy.

In fact, he had already completed the Longevity Body half a month ago.

But after achieving mastery of the Longevity Body, he didn’t rush to leave seclusion. Instead, he further cultivated the Divine Control Technique.

After all, he might need to use Divine Control later, so it was better to get familiar with it in advance.

He spent several years of lifespan and a large number of pills for repairing his core and finally reached basic proficiency in this Core Formation Divine Ability.

The cost wasn’t much. After all, now that he possessed the completed Longevity Body, his lifespan reached twenty thousand years. These few years were nothing but a drop in the bucket.

And what woke Lin Jing from cultivation wasn’t the rough action of the Thunder Dragon Ancestor suddenly stuffing the flying ship into its inner space, but rather the spiritual energy ripples from that dust-covered marriage certificate in his storage pouch.

At the Ancient Celestial Kingdom Academy, Mr. Cao, an expert in the Art of Helping One’s Wife Prosper, once taught a class on treasure-hunting techniques.

And this marriage certificate was one of the ancient treasures that Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse found during that class.

The marriage certificate was something Mr. Cao had come across by chance. Its origins were unknown, but he roughly speculated... that due to the frequent wars in ancient times, the certificate was filled with a cold, resentful energy—possibly left behind by a woman who had set a marriage contract, but before the man could marry her, he died in battle, thus imbuing the certificate with this kind of power.

This useless item, at the time, Mr. Cao had suggested could be used as a hand toy to improve resistance against chilling spiritual power.

Lin Jing found it reasonable, but in reality, after tossing the certificate into a corner of his storage pouch, he completely forgot about it.

But who would have thought... at this very moment, the chilling spiritual energy in the marriage certificate had actually triggered a resonance.

This phenomenon could almost confirm that the person who had left their spiritual energy on the certificate had appeared—and was nearby.

What Lin Jing couldn’t have imagined was that the owner of the certificate turned out to be none other than the Ghost Bride—one of the three major Void Mystical ghosts of the Western Desert?

Even Lin Jing found such a coincidence unbelievable. He couldn’t help but reevaluate Mr. Cao’s skills.

“Wife-prospering” Cao Ziwei really lived up to his name.

With talent like that, Lin Jing even wanted to poach Mr. Cao from the Ancient Kingdom to the Demon Court.

At that moment, Lin Jing stared at the certificate, used his divine sense to observe the clash between the Thunder Dragon Ancestor and the Ghost Bride, and seeing the Ghost Bride’s desire for the certificate, he began to contemplate and came up with an idea.

“How... do you have her marriage certificate?”

Outside, the Thunder Dragon Ancestor was stunned, but under Lin Jing’s strong insistence, it still released him from its inner Void Mystical space.

The Thunder Dragon Ancestor judged that this Ghost Bride wasn’t a match for him.

Compared to the Shadow Ghost and the Ghost Mother, the Ghost Bride was relatively ordinary.

In the Thunder Dragon Ancestor’s opinion, she must’ve been born from unfulfilled obsession before death. Such ghosts existed everywhere and were among the easiest types to deal with.

Compared to artifact-possessing ghosts of unknown origin, this Ghost Bride was someone he was confident he could suppress within a hundred moves.

The next moment, Lin Jing appeared, standing atop the Thunder Dragon Ancestor’s head, staring at the Ghost Bride on the other side and took out the certificate: “Is this what you’re looking for?”

Seeing the ancestor produce the certificate, the Ghost Bride’s stunning face showed a strained expression. She immediately reached out her hand to grab the certificate.

“Bold.”

The ghost hand struck, and the Thunder Dragon Ancestor growled. A sea of thunder and lightning spread out, cutting between the Ghost Bride and Lin Jing.

“Give it back to me.”

The Ghost Bride withdrew her ghost hand, the ominous aura on her body intensifying. Around her, numerous shadows of chaotic laws emerged. Seeing this, the Thunder Dragon Ancestor frowned.

“As expected.”

“Holy Son, this Ghost Bride was definitely formed from a lingering obsession during life.”

“Plus, she possesses the Yin-Gathering Physique. As a result, during her transformation into a ghost, she absorbed many ancient souls filled with resentment, ultimately merging into this Void Mystical state...”

“As it stands, though her current consciousness is dominant, she likely lost the memories and obsessions from her life during the long transformation.”

“So, she’s in a dangerous, highly unstable state—not a complete ghost.”

“In this condition, she could lose control at any time from the lack of purpose, causing all the ghosts within her to revive. She’s a fusion ghost.”

“This marriage certificate was something from her life. If she comes into contact with it, she might regain her memories and gain full control of her body.”

“Thus, whether she becomes a complete ghost or dispels her obsession and fades away, this certificate holds immense meaning for her.”

Lin Jing nodded, understanding as well.

“Your Highness, does this marriage certificate have any use to you?” the Thunder Dragon Ancestor asked.

“None.” Lin Jing gave his consent.

“Ghost Bride, how about we make a deal?” the Thunder Dragon Ancestor looked at the Ghost Bride.

“Wh...what... deal.”

“We’ll return this marriage certificate to you. Afterward, if you regain your memories and let go of your obsession, and no longer wish to linger in this world, then you can disperse, dust to dust, kings to kings.”

“If you still hold onto your obsession and wish to become a ghost cultivator, that’s your choice. But in either case, you’ll need to do one thing for us. It’ll take less than a year of your time.”

“You should understand—with me here, you can’t take the certificate from me by force. This is your only way to obtain it.” the Thunder Dragon Ancestor proposed the deal.

Though the Ghost Bride wasn’t his match, killing her brought no benefit.

If she went all out, he might not walk away unscathed either.

But if they could get her to willingly be sealed by the Holy Son and assist him in obtaining the Golden Light Gu, that would be much more important.

After speaking, the Thunder Dragon Ancestor took out a scroll and said, “This is a Heavenly Dao Contract. The terms are already written. If you agree, we’ll proceed. If you break the contract afterward, you’ll be punished by heavenly lightning.”

“Fine.” Taking the contract, the Ghost Bride agreed to the deal. She was on the verge of losing control of the other spirits within her. After reviewing the contract, she left her soul imprint on it, and Lin Jing handed the certificate back to her.

Whether the return of the certificate would liberate the Ghost Bride or create a more powerful ghost, Lin Jing didn’t know. But he and the Thunder Dragon Ancestor shared the same thought—if they could have a Void Mystical ghost voluntarily follow for a while, it would definitely make obtaining the Golden Light Gu easier.

The appearance of the Ghost Bride made Lin Jing temporarily give up searching for the Artifact Ghost.

The next moment.

Upon receiving the certificate, the Ghost Bride’s thoughts surged. The ancient resentment dominating her body calmed entirely, and complex memories flooded her mind.

The blurred writing on the certificate also became clear in her sight.

“So that’s how it is. I was a girl from a cultivation family, in love since childhood with another. We made a marriage pact, but due to ancient war, she had to go to the battlefield and never returned.... I searched far and wide to find her, arrived at the battlegrounds, but before I could reach her, I died…”

The Ghost Bride tried hard to recall the face of her beloved, but could only remember the back of a sword-wielding female cultivator.

“Before I know the result, I will not fade. Even if it’s been so long, even if she has already died, I must know what became of her.” After retrieving part of her memory, the Ghost Bride’s power actually surged further, fully suppressing the resentment spirits within her.

However, next to her, Lin Jing and the Thunder Dragon Ancestor both had question marks above their heads.

“She? She?” Two female cultivators got engaged? What the h*ll? Ancient cultivators were this wild?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 15: The Dao
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 15: The Dao

Regarding the Ghost Bride’s situation, Lin Jing and the Thunder Dragon Ancestor only paused for a moment before regaining their composure—their minds were especially resilient.

So what if it was a romance between two female cultivators? It wasn’t a big deal.

After all, compared to intermarriages between ancient demon clans and the human race, compared to the Beast Taming Sect’s Sect Master and Senior Brother Ouyang Hao falling in love with a beast-shaped demon beast, at least the Ghost Bride’s was within the same species.

“Thank you for bringing the marriage certificate.”

The Ghost Bride emerged from her river of memories: “According to the contract, I will be sealed by him for one year and lend my power for his use.”

“However, our strength differs greatly—even if I willingly cooperate, with his current abilities, I doubt he can truly control my power.”

“After all, even I couldn’t perfectly control this power at first.”

The reason the Ghost Bride was now so powerful wasn’t because she had been extremely strong in life, but because of her special physique and the fact that she had died on an ancient battlefield. Her ghostly body had absorbed the power of countless war dead over a long period.

Although she had now retrieved some memories through an item from her past life and regained control over the ghost body’s dominant consciousness, fully consolidating that power would still take time.

The Ghost Bride could not guarantee that the power she lent would be something Lin Jing could fully command.

“There is still time.”

The coachman-like Thunder Dragon Ancestor spoke: “There’s still half a year. During this time, let His Highness the Holy Son study your Dao and familiarize himself with your power.”

“My Dao…” The Ghost Bride was confused.

As an old ancestor of a clan, the Thunder Dragon Ancestor had seen much. He began to explain to the Ghost Bride.

“You were born through a twist of fate, so you probably don’t know much about the path of cultivation. In that case, allow this old man to enlighten you!”

“No need to go over the early stages of cultivation—let’s talk about Nascent Soul, Void Mystical, and Dao-Seeking stages…”

“The path of Nascent Soul is the process of becoming a deity of heaven and earth, cultivating the forces of the world. With a single thought, one can overturn the heavens.”

“Progressing from Nascent Soul to Void Mystical means, on this foundation, mastering the power of space and opening an inner space within one’s body.”

“Void Mystical cultivators then refine that inner space with the heavenly and earthly power cultivated during Nascent Soul, shaping it into the image that best suits them—simulating the creation of a world. That is the Void Mystical path.”

“The life-and-death battles of Void Mystical are clashes of these inner world domains, competing to see whose spatial domain is stronger!”

“But that only covers the early and middle stages of Void Mystical. In the later stage, as the domain becomes complete, the cultivator must pursue a higher result—finding their own Dao. That Dao is like the soul or will of a world, a rule granted to their Void Mystical domain.”

“A Void Mystical cultivator’s consciousness becomes the Dao, acting as the Heavenly Dao of their inner world. Only by accomplishing this can one achieve Great Perfection of the Void Mystical stage.”

“This process is also called Merging with the Dao. So the Void Mystical stage can broadly be divided into two phases: Void Refining and Dao Merging.”

“The Void Refining phase is the process of opening an internal space, refining the void, and perfecting the spatial domain.”

“The Dao Merging phase is when the cultivator’s own consciousness transforms into the will of the Dao and integrates into the Void Mystical world, simulating the Heavenly Dao.”

“Your talent is excellent. Merely recovering your memories has already formed the rudiments of your Dao. Just now, you used the Dao of Obsession to suppress those residual consciousnesses within your body that tried to seize control.”

“In searching for your husband… or wife… whoever it is, that obsession allowed you to transform from a human to a ghost, and from an incomplete form to a dominant, full-bodied ghost cultivator. So this Dao is the foundation of everything you are now.”

“And if he succeeds in merging with the Dao someday and completely masters the Dao of Obsession, then as long as that obsession persists, he will continue to exist. Even if only a trace of soul remains, he could return from death through flame.”

“This… is Dao.”

“As for the Dao-Seeking realm, it’s still too early for you. But ultimately, it means using your own Dao to oppose others’ Daos and even the Heavenly Dao. If your Dao does not collapse during that process and defeats the other Daos, then your own power will only grow stronger. The so-called Dao-Seeking is the process of testing whether your Dao is strong enough.”

“When your Dao is tempered to perfection and breaks through the rules of the lower realms, you will reach the so-called Ascension Realm.”

The Ghost Bride mused, “So my Dao is the Dao of Obsession?”

“Exactly, the Dao of Obsession derived from emotion! Some enter the Dao through love, others through severing emotions. Emotion is one of the more common paths to enter the Dao.” The Thunder Dragon Ancestor nodded.

“So, senior, what you mean is that I should also try to learn her Dao. But how do I learn it? Also, the Dao of Obsession might not be compatible with my own Dao. If I study it, will it affect my future cultivation?” Lin Jing voiced his doubt.

The Thunder Dragon Ancestor said: “For every cultivator, the path of cultivation is filled with countless choices. Each choice could become a Dao. There’s no rule that says every cultivator can only have one Dao.”

“For instance, I’ve merged with three different Daos, chosen three different paths. But unfortunately, none of those Daos were strong or suitable enough to let me break through Void Control and enter the Dao-Seeking realm.”

“In the end, my Daos weren’t powerful or compatible enough. I couldn’t even take the first step of Dao-Seeking.”

“So, understanding the Dao of Obsession deeply won’t affect your future. It’s like temporarily learning a spell. Right now, His Highness the Holy Son is still at a low level of cultivation and hasn’t found his own Dao. But once he enters Void Mystical, he will already have countless Daos to choose from. At that time, choosing the one he finds most compatible to merge with is what matters most.”

“After all, choosing the wrong one is like taking the wrong path—it could waste countless years and still lead to failure at Dao-Seeking. Those who fail to break through are mostly the ones who picked a Dao that didn’t suit them.”

“In short, gaining insight into the Dao of Obsession now doesn’t mean you must merge with it in the future. It’s just to better fuse with the Ghost Bride’s power for the time being.”

Lin Jing nodded. Indeed, merging with the Dao at the Void Mystical stage was still far away for him.

He had only cultivated to the Core Formation stage. He didn’t even have a concept of his own Dao yet.

This Dao wasn’t about Sword Dao, Beast Taming Dao, or Illusion Dao—it referred to the cultivator’s original intention, the thing that supported them all the way to the peak.

Lin Jing thought for a moment. At present, he was likely still in the “seeking Dao” phase. He had yet to find a Dao that truly belonged to him. If he reached the Void Mystical stage without finding it, he might forever be stuck at the early or middle stages of that realm, unable to move forward.

“What will my Dao be?”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 16: Entering the Ghost City
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 16: Entering the Ghost City

To better understand the Ghost Bride’s Dao and thus use her power more effectively, the Thunder Dragon Ancestor and Lin Jing went to the Ghost Bride’s stronghold—namely, the Buddhist City she had occupied.

As for the Buddhist sect, after learning the truth about the Ghost Bride’s condition, they gave up on fighting her.

First, because the Ghost Bride had reached the Void Mystical stage, and trying to completely suppress and kill her would come at too great a cost.

Second, and more importantly, the Buddhist sect saw that the Ghost Bride’s state was unstable. In such an unstable condition, after some time, the Ghost Bride might collapse and perish on her own.

Since there was a chance to wait her out without lifting a sword, there was no need to pay a huge price to attack.

However, what the Buddhist sect couldn’t have imagined was that, by coincidence, the marriage certificate Lin Jing had brought from the Ancient Kingdom had stabilized the Ghost Bride’s form and turned her into a ghost with complete dominant awareness.

“Half a year remains, Your Highness. In this half year, as long as you grasp the basics of the Dao of Obsession, you’ll be able to better resonate with the Ghost Bride’s power. Coupled with your own advantages, no one will be able to compete with you in the Golden Light Gu trial.”

The Thunder Dragon Ancestor’s role wasn’t limited to providing blood for body-tempering—his cultivation experience along the way was enough to act as a teacher to both Lin Jing and the Ghost Bride.

Lin Jing looked toward the Ghost Bride walking absentmindedly ahead and asked, “Senior, have you remembered your name?”

“Tang Ling,” said the Ghost Bride.

“Senior, you’re thinking of your fiancée, right? Does she have a name? The more information we have, the easier it’ll be to find her traces.”

“Our Demon Court can help Senior search for her.”

The Ghost Bride shook her head. “I only remember she was a sword cultivator, with great talent. The rest… I can’t recall.”

“Sword cultivator…” Lin Jing and the Thunder Dragon Ancestor exchanged glances—this scope was far too broad.

The Ghost Bride must have lived fifty thousand years ago.

Fifty thousand years ago, even the Demon Court’s ruler and the First Ancient Emperor hadn’t been born yet. To find traces of a sword cultivator from such a chaotic era was just too difficult.

Still, if it existed and happened, then it could be found.

The Tianji Pavilion of the Ancient Kingdom was said to be able to deduce the future—if so, then tracing the past was certainly within the realm of possibility.

And beings with similar mastery of the Dao of Time weren’t exclusive to the Tianji Pavilion. Among beast clans, there were also such experts.

Lin Jing thought for a moment and said, “After we leave the Western Desert, I’ll seek out someone in the Demon Court who’s proficient in the Dao of Time to help you trace back through time and find her trail.”

“Thank you.” The Ghost Bride’s blood-red eyes looked at Lin Jing with a softer gaze, though to Lin Jing, she still looked rather terrifying.

“Next, I’ll help you comprehend the ‘Dao of Obsession.’ In my understanding, the Dao of Obsession means ‘a thought that cannot be shaken, even in death’—even if dead, you’d claw your way back as a ghost, just to accomplish something.”

She stopped, looking at the Thunder Dragon Ancestor and Lin Jing. After hearing the Thunder Dragon Ancestor’s explanation, she had gained a deeper understanding of the Dao of Obsession.

She now understood why the Thunder Dragon Ancestor had said that as long as her obsession remained, she would be hard to truly kill.

But after looking at the Thunder Dragon Ancestor and Lin Jing again, she shook her head.

“You don’t have any strong obsession,” the Ghost Bride first said, commenting on the Thunder Dragon Ancestor.

The Thunder Dragon Ancestor flicked his beard. True, the strongest obsession he’d ever had wasn’t even about seeing the demon race rise again—viewing the demon race’s revival as more important than his own Dao breakthrough was something that belonged to only a few weaker Demon Court members. And with the descent of the Transformation Pill, even that obsession had faded considerably.

“And he also doesn’t have any real obsession. In that state, it’s actually hard to comprehend your Dao. In half a year, I fear there won’t be much progress.”

The Ghost Bride also saw that Lin Jing was carefree by nature. Until now, he had never been consumed by any particular obsession.

With that kind of foundation, he wasn’t suited to learning the Dao of Obsession.

“No obsession?” The Thunder Dragon Ancestor turned to look at Lin Jing. What the h*ll—even if I don’t have one, how can a human, whose mind and emotions are more complex, not have any obsession?

The Thunder Dragon Ancestor asked, “Your Highness, is there really nothing you’re intent on pursuing?”

Lin Jing stood among the ruins of the Ghost City and shook his head. “Power, maybe? But if you mean true obsession, then no, I’ve never been overly attached. Back when I first entered Qingzhou, I was probably desperate to grow stronger for self-preservation, but once I had the Demon Court’s protection, my cultivation mindset relaxed a lot.”

“I’ve never had an overwhelming desire to become number one on the continent, or a burning need to ascend to the upper realm.”

“This is a tough one…” The Thunder Dragon Ancestor sighed. Lin Jing had the Hidden Dragon Arts, and the ancestor had originally thought that with the Ghost Bride’s cooperation, Lin Jing could at least begin to comprehend the Dao of Obsession in half a year.

But now, seeing Lin Jing’s state of mind, it was clear that it didn’t align with the Dao of Obsession at all. The difficulty might be much greater than expected.

However, in the next moment, the Ghost Bride found another potential starting point.

“Although he doesn’t have any obsession, there seem to be… other things inside his body. Those two things carry incredibly strong obsessions.”

“So strong they might reach the level of ‘if unfulfilled, they’ll die with eyes open and rise again as vengeful ghosts.’”

“Hm?” As the Ghost Bride spoke, both Lin Jing and the Thunder Dragon Ancestor were startled.

“Senior Tang Ling, are you referring to…” Lin Jing pulled out the Pine Leaf Mouse and the Ice Soul Gu.

“Eh!!”

“Huh?”

The Pine Leaf Mouse and the Ice Soul Gu, who had been hoarding supplies and digesting the Thunder Tribulation Gu’s power respectively, landed one to each of Lin Jing’s shoulders.

“Yes, those two,” the Ghost Bride confirmed.

“The intensity of their obsession… is terrifying.”

Lin Jing fell into thought, glancing left and right at the Pine Leaf Mouse and the Ice Soul Gu.

As for the Pine Leaf Mouse, he could basically guess—it was probably an obsession with hoarding, right?

Wanting to fill all its storage items, including the Monster Core Space, with supplies. After all, the mouse would even forget to cultivate just to hoard more.

This was a strong compulsion it had long before being contracted.

As for the Ice Soul Gu…

Lin Jing swept it with his Heavenly Demon Divine Sense, peering into the Ice Soul Gu’s mind. Normally he wouldn’t invade a pet’s privacy, but now that the Ghost Bride said the Ice Soul Gu’s obsession rivaled that of the Pine Leaf Mouse, he had to take a look.

Within the Ice Soul Gu’s mind, she appeared as a creature with a crystalline spider body and half a girl’s figure of ice and snow extending from her head.

Her countless legs pinned down the Pine Leaf Mouse and the Dragon Carp.

Within her crystal-like body, all nine of the Rare Gu—including the Thunder Tribulation Gu and the Golden Light Gu—were sealed in ice.

“So this is her obsession?” Lin Jing was speechless.

It seemed her obsession was to devour the other nine Rare Gu, attain absolute power, then defeat the Pine Leaf Mouse and the Dragon Carp—and maybe even transform into a demon race being as part of her pursuit of strength.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 17: Intruder
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 17: Intruder

"Maybe I can have the two of them comprehend the Dao of Obsession, then, when I borrow the senior’s power, I can merge with them. That way, their comprehension would also become mine."

Lin Jing realized that being a Beast Tamer was truly a great profession. Paths that didn’t suit him could be learned by his pet beasts instead.

As long as the beasts learned it, it was practically the same as he himself learning it.

"This is the only option left." Ghost Bride Tang Ling also felt that this was the best choice for now.

"Let’s go." She looked ahead at a Buddha Tower shrouded in a gray, ghostly aura.

Once a sacred Buddhist site, it was now completely overrun by ghost entities…

In fact, apart from the Ghost Bride, countless ghost entities still lurked in the Ghost City, but due to her immense power, they would always hide in silence whenever she appeared, not daring to show themselves.

And now, they too would face their doomsday. To create a peaceful environment for Lin Jing’s cultivation, under the instructions of the Thunder Dragon Ancestor, the Thunder Dragon prodigies pulling the carriage flew directly to all corners of the Ghost City...

Within the Ghost City, the Ghost Bride stood atop the Buddha Tower, eyes closed, continuously releasing her aura, and within that powerful intent, lay the Dao of Obsession she had mastered.

The different levels of the Buddha Tower became the cultivation grounds for Lin Jing and the others. The Dao of Void Mysticism—even just brushing against it at the outermost edge—would cause intense inner tremors in Pine Leaf Mouse and the others.

What this manifested as was the Ghost Bride using her Dao to erase the obsessions of Pine Leaf Mouse and Ice Soul Gu.

"Sigh..."

Seated cross-legged in the Buddha Tower, Pine Leaf Mouse in a Daoist robe looked tense, its expression shifting constantly. Its mind and thoughts were engaged in a fierce internal struggle, feeling like it was slowly losing its obsession with hoarding.

In a sudden moment, it actually felt that stockpiling resources in huge amounts wasn’t all that meaningful, constantly denying the behaviors it had held onto for years.

One of its biggest dreams in cultivation had been to own a massive storage space and fill it with treasures. But now, it suddenly couldn’t understand the purpose behind such a goal.

Ice Soul Gu was in a similar situation, beginning to question whether its competitiveness was just a pointless obsession.

Pine Leaf Mouse and Dragon Carp were both rare beasts that inherited the divine abilities of immortals. Although it was a Rare Gu created by the Gu God Sect, in the end, it was still the crystallization of a mortal Gu cultivator’s intelligence—merely a creation from the lower realm.

Under these circumstances, losing to Pine Leaf Mouse and Dragon Carp was completely normal. Compared to other lifeforms on the Tianyuan Continent, it was already powerful enough. There was no need to keep competing with Pine Leaf Mouse and Dragon Carp. Maintaining its current state was already good enough...

Their obsessions were being destroyed.

However, the Ghost Bride wasn’t doing this just to destroy the obsessions of Pine Leaf Mouse and Ice Soul Gu. Her true intent was to help them strengthen their obsessions.

If the Ghost Bride unleashed her full power, she could easily shatter their Dao Hearts in an instant, making them lose the motivation to cultivate.

But now, she was controlling her power very precisely, keeping her influence just slightly weaker than their existing obsessions.

This way, Pine Leaf Mouse and Ice Soul Gu would remain in a constant state of internal resistance.

As long as they held firm, stayed true to themselves—whether for hoarding or for being the strongest—that obsession would become stronger.

Eventually, that very obsession would influence their future battles and cultivation, producing a positive effect.

"A chance to comprehend the Dao of a Void Mystical cultivator... That time, Dragon Carp probably missed its opportunity. After all, it didn’t have a Demon Emperor accompanying it while cultivating at the Immortal-Burying Volcano. This time, its luck should be the weakest."

"I wonder how it’s doing now."

Pine Leaf Mouse looked toward the direction of the Immortal-Burying Volcano, unable to sense if Dragon Carp had broken through to the Nascent Soul Stage yet.

With that thought, I myself became enveloped in golden light, allowing the Spell Spirit Origin Fungus to project the fluctuations of the Indestructible Golden Body onto me, planning to delve into it here.

Several months ago.

Immortal-Burying Volcano.

At the edge of a volcanic crater, the Demon Emperor sat cross-legged.

Inside the volcano, countless white flames flowed like rivers.

A small carp was rolling and twisting within, letting the flames scorch its body.

The Demon Emperor’s divine sense remained locked onto Dragon Carp. From the outside, she saw that outside its monster core, Dragon Carp had begun forming a blurry image of a tiny carp through the power of its bloodline.

"It’s reached the Phantom Infant Stage."

Phantom Infant Stage—the transition between Core Formation and Nascent Soul. Although the Nascent Soul wasn’t fully formed yet, some of its powers had already emerged.

"If it manages to refine the flames of this Immortal-Burying Volcano, its bloodline strength should ascend to the Ancestor Realm."

Sensing Dragon Carp’s phantom infant power, the Demon Emperor made her judgment.

Human and beast cultivators reached Nascent Soul through two different methods due to racial differences.

For beasts, their monster core didn’t shatter. Instead, relying on their bloodline power, a monster infant shell would form around the core to protect it.

For humans, true essence would condense and shape inside the core, and finally break out from within, forming a Dao Infant.

Each method had its own advantages. The beast-type Nascent Soul had stronger oppressive power. With just a thought, its aura could crush low or even same-level cultivators like a beast king, making others tremble in fear.

Thus, Nascent Soul-level beasts were also called Demon Kings.

But human Nascent Soul cultivators didn’t have that kind of oppressive aura. Instead, they could combine their Dao Infant with true essence to summon a Nascent Soul Manifestation.

This Manifestation looked like a towering divine armored being, which wrapped around the cultivator, greatly enhancing both offense and defense. The cultivator could also channel spells through the Manifestation, amplifying their power!

The Demon Emperor’s eyes burned with intensity. Just as she was pondering when Dragon Carp, with its activated ancestral bloodline, would finally ascend to the true Demon Ancestor Realm and become a great general of the Demon Court...

Suddenly, her brow furrowed. She looked toward the edge of the Tianyuan Realm.

At that very moment, it wasn’t just her—every cultivator on the Tianyuan Continent who had reached the Dao-Seeking Stage sensed something strange, as if the boundary wall of the Grand Origin Realm was under some kind of impact!

"Already... this soon?"

In the direction of the Ancient Kingdom, the Second Ancient Emperor narrowed his eyes, looking beyond the heavens, a ripple rising in his heart.

A few years ago, the Lord of the Demon Court had brought intelligence revealing the ambitions of the Demonic Sky Sect to the Dao-Seeking experts of the Tianyuan Realm.

To prepare for the invasion of a powerful enemy, the Tianyuan Realm initiated a Golden Era, hoping to spend ten thousand, or at least several thousand or hundreds of years to bolster the realm’s foundations.

But who would’ve thought, in just a few short years, the Second Ancient Emperor could already feel someone trying to break through the boundary barrier and descend upon the Tianyuan Realm.

Although it seemed like the attempt had failed, it was still a bad omen.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 18: Origin Realm
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 18: Origin Realm

"Fellow Daoists, I trust you all sensed it too."

In a void-like space, numerous indistinct figures gathered.

The Second Ancient Emperor, relying on the power of the Tianyuan Dragon Vein, had summoned the consciousness of many Dao-Seeking experts here.

"Just now, in that fleeting moment, a being from beyond the realm attempted to cross the boundary wall and enter the Tianyuan Realm."

"This person is extremely powerful, beyond the Dao-Seeking Stage. If this had happened before the start of the Golden Era, he might have already succeeded in breaking through."

"But now that the Golden Era has begun, the boundary wall has been strengthened, which is likely the only reason that unknown being failed to breach it."

"So you're saying... a cultivator from the Demonic Sky Realm has already arrived?" a noble and graceful female cultivator's silhouette slowly spoke.

She was the Island Master of Water Moon Immortal Island, one of the few female Dao-Seeking cultivators.

An old man in alchemist robes said, "Isn’t it also possible that it wasn’t a cultivator from the Demonic Sky Realm, but rather a cultivator from another realm who got lost in the starry sky, or that the boundary wall was accidentally struck by a void beast?"

"The odds are too low. No matter what, we must prepare for war." A burly man’s silhouette spoke up.

The Second Ancient Emperor looked around at everyone.

"Do you all still remember why we initiated the Golden Era?"

"To cultivate Tianyuan Realm’s prodigies, to give those at the end of their lifespan a chance to break through their bottlenecks."

"But as it stands, if we assume the worst-case scenario, we may not have enough time."

"If the Demonic Sky Realm invades, with the Tianyuan prodigies only at the Nascent Soul or Soul Transformation Stage, they’ll not only be ineffective—they’ll become the primary targets for elimination."

Among the figures, the tiger-headed and stocky Demon Emperor had been listening in silence. She finally spoke, "If you have an idea, just say it."

The Second Ancient Emperor turned to her. "After the Golden Era began, many ancient secret realms surfaced. Among them, ruins of the shattered Celestial Palace also began to appear."

"The Ancient Kingdom discovered a certain location through this—have any of you heard of the Origin Realm?"

Origin Realm?

Even among the gathered Dao-Seeking powerhouses, few had heard of this place.

Until a whale-shaped beast’s silhouette spoke, "The Origin Realm… wasn’t the formation diagram for entering it lost during the Celestial Palace War?"

"I bring it up now because we’ve found a way to enter the Origin Realm from the Celestial Palace ruins." said the Second Ancient Emperor.

"So what exactly is the Origin Realm?"

One of the Dao-Seeking cultivators asked, confused—it didn’t sound like just a small secret realm.

"The Origin Realm is also known as the Minor Immortal Realm. It’s a rather special space in the lower realm. The biggest difference between the Immortal Realm and the lower realm lies in the existence of Immortal Sources in the former—they generate immortal energy."

"The Origin Realm is the only place in the lower realm capable of generating Immortal Sources and producing immortal energy. Moreover, the natural energy there is exceptionally abundant—an ideal place for Nascent Soul and Soul Transformation cultivators to train. No place in the lower realm compares."

"Of course, in terms of quantity, it’s nothing compared to the Immortal Realm—it’s like a drop in the ocean. But for the lower realm, it’s still incredibly rare."

"According to records from the Celestial Palace, many Immortal Realm powers once cooperated to transform the Origin Realm into a dedicated training ground for prodigies from the lower realm."

"This Origin Realm can only be entered and occupied by Nascent Soul and Soul Transformation cultivators. Anyone beyond that level will be expelled. Every ten years, a portion of Immortal Source is produced, and this becomes the most coveted treasure for the prodigies inside."

"In ancient times, Celestial Palace prodigies could enter the Origin Realm and compete against other prodigies from different cultivation realms. But after the Celestial Palace shattered, the means to enter the Origin Realm were lost."

The Yin-Yang Whale Emperor, ruler of Poseidon Palace, shared what he knew.

"That’s right. Tianyuan Realm happens to have a limited number of entry slots. We might be able to send some of Tianyuan’s prodigies into the Origin Realm to train. There, the highest level any outsider cultivator could reach would also be Soul Transformation."

"Compared to the Tianyuan Realm, which might face war, the Origin Realm would be much safer."

"Besides, if they manage to obtain Immortal Sources and cultivate there, the benefits are unimaginable."

"When they return, they should all be powerful cultivators at the Void Mystical Stage. At that point, they’d have the qualifications to join the two-realm war—and a decent chance of survival."

After the Second Ancient Emperor finished, the Dao-Seeking experts remained silent. The Origin Realm…

"You Ancient Kingdom people have this opportunity and don’t keep it to yourselves, sending only your ancient clan prodigies in, but instead you're sharing it with us?"

"Spit it out—what’s the catch for entering the Origin Realm?" asked the Witch God of the Cloud Sea Mountains.

The Second Ancient Emperor replied, "Cross-realm teleportation has very high activation requirements. Just like starting the Golden Bell, it needs several Dao-Seeking experts working together to activate the formation diagram."

"Moreover, Tianyuan Realm only has ten slots to enter the Origin Realm. If those ten aren’t the strongest prodigies of the Tianyuan Realm, once inside, they’ll likely become targets of bullying by other realms’ prodigies and fail to obtain any resources."

"Inside the Origin Realm, only with full cooperation from the strongest Tianyuan prodigies can we hope to gain the greatest benefits. Then, regardless of how the war with the Demonic Sky Realm ends, Tianyuan Realm’s future would still hold a glimmer of hope."

"How do we choose these ten?"

The Martial Emperor approved the idea, believing that sending part of their prodigies into the Origin Realm to act as Tianyuan’s 'seed' plan was viable.

Immortal Source energy—he didn’t even need to ask to know how valuable it would be for cultivation. Now that the path to ascension was broken, the Origin Realm might be the only place for cultivators to experience immortal energy.

"We’ll take the top ten from the Fortune Gold List."

"The period—ten years."

"Ten years from now, the top ten ranked on the Fortune Gold List will be sent by us into the Origin Realm for cultivation..."

"If none of you have any objections, I, Gu, will share everything I obtained from the Celestial Palace ruins regarding the Origin Realm with my fellow Daoists."

.............

Much later, Western Desert, Immortal-Burying Volcano.

The Demon Emperor’s consciousness returned. She looked at the Dragon Carp still swimming in the volcano and fell silent.

"The Origin Realm, huh?"

"I wonder if that girl Xu Qingyao can hold her current ranking. As for Lin Jing..."

The Demon Emperor sank into thought. Within ten years—could Lin Jing make it?

As a Longevity Body, the Demon Emperor actually felt that letting Lin Jing enter the Origin Realm might be the best option.

After all, the rules of the Origin Realm were very favorable to Longevity Bodies.

Inside the Origin Realm, the cultivation ceiling was the Soul Transformation Stage. Anyone who broke through to the Void Mystical Stage would be expelled back to their own cultivation realm.

In that case, even if no new Immortal Sources were produced for a while, prodigies from other realms could still remain there indefinitely—after all, Soul Transformation cultivators had incredibly long lifespans.

A few might choose to break through only when their lifespan was nearly depleted, choosing to remain in the Origin Realm during their golden years of cultivation.

With that, even weaker prodigies could stay in the Origin Realm for a long time.

But Lin Jing was different.

As a Longevity Body, he could, if he chose, remain dormant in the Origin Realm for thousands—even ten thousand—years. Through sheer time alone, he could become the overlord of the Origin Realm, monopolizing all Immortal Sources.

That kind of power accumulation would be terrifying. Future Tianyuan Realm prodigies entering the Origin Realm would also benefit from Lin Jing’s protection.

Compared to struggling against Dao-Seeking experts in the Grand Origin Realm, it would be far better.

"Pass the intel to him. Let him decide for himself. But... if he goes, who’s going to refine the Transformation Pill Vessel?"

The Demon Emperor paused.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 19: Leaving One’s Homeland
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 19: Leaving One’s Homeland

"This is the Indestructible Golden Body."

Within the Buddha Tower, Lin Jing stood up, his entire body glowing with golden Buddhist light. After a rudimentary entry into the Indestructible Golden Body, his defense had improved once again.

"Not only is my defense stronger—in this state, I also have greater resistance against all kinds of demonic and evil forces."

The Spell Spirit Origin Fungus, seeing that Lin Jing had finally learned it, wiped off imaginary sweat and flew back into his body.

Seeing this, Lin Jing mocked himself,

"Illusion art and golden body—does this make me a little Oscar statuette?"

Boom!

At that moment, an explosion echoed from far off in the Buddha Tower.

Over the past few months, both pet beasts had gained insights into the Dao of Obsession, and under Lin Jing’s arrangement, had begun applying them in real combat.

The rules: no use of monster core divine abilities.

And if Ice Soul Gu won, it could take ten percent of Pine Leaf Mouse’s hoarded spirit stones for its own cultivation.

With this rule in place, Pine Leaf Mouse’s eyes were bloodshot. Its spiritual energy recovery rate had skyrocketed—after all, without being able to use monster core divine abilities, it didn’t hold much advantage over Ice Soul Gu.

Its hoarded treasures... were at real risk of being lost.

As for Ice Soul Gu, it didn’t care much about that ten percent. It simply believed that, under these rules, it had a real chance of defeating Pine Leaf Mouse.

After being used as a refrigerator by Pine Leaf Mouse so many times, it just wanted to win for once and finally feel proud. This obsession greatly boosted its power, and the battle between the two turned especially brutal.

"This is the Dao of Obsession. Before, they did have obsessions, but it didn’t really enhance their power. Now, with obsession involved, they both gain nearly ten percent in power—that level of enhancement is terrifying."

"In the future, before sending Pine Leaf Mouse into battle, maybe I could set a punishment—if it loses, it has to use up all the resources it’s hoarded... not confiscated, just forced to use them to enhance its strength. But even if it’s for personal use, for a hoarder like it, that’s probably a doomsday scenario, right?" Lin Jing’s head was full of wicked thoughts.

"Act like a person, will you? The Dao of Obsession isn’t for bullying your pets." The Ghost Bride appeared beside him.

She also looked at the city turned to ruins from the fight.

In its Demon King true form, Pine Leaf Mouse stood over a hundred meters tall, like a small mountain, its body covered in all kinds of malignant plants—it looked like a monster.

Ice Soul Gu, meanwhile, had parasitized the corpse of a Crab Demon King, fighting from within.

Perhaps influenced by its youth, Pine Leaf Mouse’s fighting style still leaned heavily toward mechanized arts. On its shoulders were two wooden cannons, modeled after the Water Cannon Turtle, constantly firing explosive pinecones.

Each pinecone had power on par with a self-detonated monster core, forming massive fireballs that rose into mushroom clouds. Facing this terrifying firepower, the ice-based Ice Soul Gu suffered endlessly, having to stimulate the Crab Demon King’s physical body with lightning energy to stay mobile, darting around the city like a bolt of lightning.

Even then, it couldn’t avoid the shockwaves and was injured multiple times.

However, despite its overwhelming firepower, Pine Leaf Mouse’s movement speed in true form was limited, making it a live target for Ice Soul Gu.

Heavenly lightning repeatedly struck Pine Leaf Mouse’s head, leaving black scorch marks and cracks across its body.

Yet both sides had incredible defense. Pine Leaf Mouse’s wooden body was made of Diamondwood. Whether it was defense or regeneration, it was top-tier. Ice Soul Gu’s lightning strikes couldn’t cause critical damage.

As for Su Ying Gu, piloting the body of a peak Demon King from Poseidon Palace, it had thoroughly protected its core. Even when the host body got damaged, it could slowly regenerate using frost power.

All for the sake of not losing... The two had already fought for several days without a victor, and they had burned through an unknown amount of spirit-replenishing pills.

"Stop." Ice Soul Gu called off the battle with Pine Leaf Mouse and Lin Jing.

Beside them, the Ghost Bride also agreed—it was crude, but this could be considered a successful initiation into the Dao of Obsession.

"Sigh..."

Hearing the order to stop, Pine Leaf Mouse sighed in relief and quickly returned to its small, nimble form to rest. Ice Soul Gu remained inside the Ice Crab, somewhat frustrated.

A pure ice-type Demon King corpse simply couldn’t let it unleash its full potential...

As expected, human pets were still more effective. It looked up at the Fortune Gold List in the sky, itching to pick a few from the continent’s top 100 prodigies.

"It’s about time."

The test for inheriting the Golden Light Gu was about to end. Lin Jing had already decided to attempt sealing the Ghost Bride into the Buddha-Demon Pearl.

Even with the Ghost Bride’s cooperation, Lin Jing wasn’t completely confident, so he had to leave some buffer time.

Just as Lin Jing looked at the Ghost Bride, ready to have her fulfill the contract, the Tiger Fang he carried suddenly shook.

In the next moment, a terrifying aura that made the Ghost Bride’s pupils shrink slowly spilled out. Startled, she immediately dove underground. At the same time, the Thunder Dragon Ancestor within the Buddha Tower also snapped open his eyes.

"Your Majesty the Demon Emperor."

"Demon Emperor?" Lin Jing held up the Tiger Fang. A projection of the Little Demon Emperor appeared on it.

"Lin Jing." Then, the projection spoke with a mature voice that didn’t match the figure.

"Your Majesty the Demon Emperor—has it ended on Dragon Carp’s side?" Lin Jing asked respectfully, but he hadn’t sensed that Dragon Carp had successfully refined the Immortal-Burying Fire or reached Nascent Soul.

"There’s just something I need to tell you."

The Demon Emperor’s projection paced mid-air, hands behind her back, wearing a thoughtful expression.

"Did any of you just sense the anomaly in the Tianyuan Realm?"

"Anomaly? No." Lin Jing shook his head.

The Demon Emperor looked at him. "The matter you reported to me... it might be happening soon. Not long ago, all the Dao-Seeking experts of the Tianyuan Continent sensed an external force trying to enter. It’s very likely... the invaders from the Demonic Sky Realm."

Lin Jing’s heart skipped a beat. So soon?

"To prepare for the worst-case scenario, the Second Ancient Emperor has proposed a plan."

The Demon Emperor then explained everything about the Tianyuan Fire Seed plan—to send ten prodigies into the Origin Realm for cultivation—as proposed by the Second Ancient Emperor.

After listening, Lin Jing stood in thought. "Origin Realm... Your Majesty means I should try to compete for one of those spots?"

The Demon Emperor’s eyes gleamed. "If the Demonic Sky Realm invades, you’ll absolutely be one of their primary assassination targets—not only because of your Longevity Body, but because you’ve mastered beast transformation techniques. The revival of the demon race would greatly strengthen both the beast race and Tianyuan as a whole. To prevent that, you are someone they must eliminate."

"So, I still want you to go to the Origin Realm. What do you think? Are you willing to temporarily leave the Tianyuan Realm... and go far away?"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 20: Grand Celestial Gathering
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 20: Grand Celestial Gathering

“Think about it carefully. Going to the Origin Realm is the best choice for you. After all, the strongest being there is only at the Nascent Soul Stage. One day, with your Longevity Body, you could become the strongest in the Origin Realm and monopolize all its resources.”

Lin Jing pondered slightly.

“Ten years... the top ten... the time is a bit tight, but it’s not impossible. After all, it’s only the top ten, not the first place.”

Right now, the Dragon Carp was about to form its Nascent Soul. If it could synthesize Immortal Flame, there was still a good chance.

However......

“But if we leave, what about the Transformation Grass and Transformation Pills?” Lin Jing voiced his concern.

What made him the Holy Son of the Demon Court was entirely due to his skill in producing Transformation Pills.

If he left, the Demon Court’s path to revival would essentially be cut off.

Stockpiling a large quantity of mutated Transformation Grass and leaving behind the Thunder Refining Method was just a temporary fix—it would run out sooner or later.

“I naturally thought of that.”

The Demon Emperor spoke slowly: “Just split yourselves. Half will stay, half will go to the Origin Realm.”

Lin Jing: ???

“External Incarnations,” the Demon Emperor explained.

“The key to refining the Transformation Pill is that Pine Leaf Mouse and Dragon Carp of yours.”

“You’re a Beast Tamer, no doubt. If you’re heading to the Origin Realm, they have to go with you. Otherwise, you won’t be able to compete with other realm’s prodigies.”

“But if they leave, the Transformation Pills can’t be refined.”

“The best solution is for them to each reach the Nascent Soul Stage and then each create an External Incarnation. That way, your true self can leave, and the incarnations stay behind in the Tianyuan Realm. Although this means you’ll only have half your combat power compared to other prodigies who have incarnations, and can’t rely on them to better comprehend the laws of the Origin Realm, at least it leaves a backup for both the Demon Court and yourself.”

“Whether your true body in the Origin Realm dies in battle, or the incarnation left in the Tianyuan Realm is assassinated—as long as not all are killed, there's still hope for a comeback.”

“External Incarnation is different from a regular clone. Regular clones can inherit common spells but not rare beast divine abilities. Incarnations can replicate even those. So, creating incarnations is the only choice.”

An External Incarnation was a powerful divine art that a cultivator could only forge once. Lin Jing was also hearing for the first time that incarnations could inherit rare beast powers… The Demon Emperor was a rare beast, so it must have tested this itself. The possibility was very high.

“In that case, no problem.” Lin Jing felt this method was feasible.

“Good. Within ten years, climb into the top ten of the Fortune Gold List. But you may not need to run around for ten years, constantly challenging others or plundering fortune to climb the ranks.”

“Have you heard of the Grand Celestial Gathering?”

Lin Jing recalled: “The Grand Celestial Gathering is a grand event held by the Ancient Celestial Kingdom once every 60 years. At that time, all sects and families from the Nine Domains of the kingdom participate, and the Ancient Emperors personally attend.”

“During the gathering, prodigies from minor sects compete in duels. Those who perform outstandingly attract the attention of the Fortune True Dragon, increasing their sect's fortune…”

“Exactly. The Ancient Emperors intend to hold a Tianyuan-wide Grand Celestial Gathering ten years from now, modeled after the kingdom’s version. Only the top 100 prodigies below the Fortune Gold List will be allowed to participate in the final selection. So, no matter how hard you work to raise your ranking on the Gold List, if you don’t shine at the Grand Celestial Gathering ten years from now, you’ll still have no chance of breaking into the top ten.”

“Therefore, you must not waste the ten years just cultivating in seclusion, hoping to finalize everything at the gathering.”

“The downside of that would be exposing your divine abilities and trump cards early, making you a target for other prodigies seeking to steal your fortune.”

“Hopefully, both she and Qingyao can break into the last ten spots, allowing your Demon Court to take seven of them. That Immortal Source truly is a dangerous thing. It’s said to be made for those who possess rare beast bloodlines like you two—possessing shattered Immortal Beast bloodlines... degenerated into Immortal Beasts.”

“Thank you for the reminder, Your Majesty. I’ll do my best.” Lin Jing’s heart stirred. Ten years from now... the Grand Celestial Gathering, huh?

So soon, and he’d have to face off against the top second gens of the Tianyuan Realm…

Ten years—Lin Jing could accept that. After all, he had the Demon Court at his back, endless resources, and once the Dragon Carp reached the Nascent Soul Stage, it could mass-produce Golden Cores to help boost his cultivation. Plus, with the Hourglass of Time, ten years could equal over fifty. It was worth a try.

After explaining everything, the Demon Emperor added, “Do your best. My true body has already gone to the Realm Boundary Wall to help the other Dao-Seeking cultivators reinforce it. With luck, it can hold for ten years... Also, since you're going to the Origin Realm and aiming for one of the ten prodigy spots, you absolutely must obtain the Golden Light Gu from Golden Light Temple.”

Lin Jing said, “Rest assured, Your Majesty. I’ve already partnered with the Void Mystical Ghost Bride. The Golden Light Gu is ours. As long as the monk from Golden Light Temple is fair, the Golden Light Gu is already in the Demon Court’s pocket.”

The Demon Emperor chuckled: “If that bald donkey dares backtrack, I’ll personally fetch it. But wait—how did you manage to strike a deal with the Void Mystical Ghost Bride???”

She raised an eyebrow.

“I picked up her marriage certificate back during my Qi Refinement stage.”

“Never mind.” The Demon Emperor gave up thinking. For a prodigy with this much fortune, anything could happen to him.

It turned back to helping the Dragon Carp synthesize the Immortal Flame.

...........

After the conversation with the Demon Emperor ended, the Ghost Bride emerged from underground, and the Thunder Dragon Ancestor descended beside Lin Jing.

“Does His Majesty have any instructions?” the Thunder Dragon Ancestor asked.

“We must obtain the Golden Light Gu. The Tianyuan Realm may soon be turned upside down.” Lin Jing took a deep breath and told both the Ghost Bride and Thunder Dragon Ancestor what the realm might soon face.

“Huh?” The Thunder Dragon Ancestor was equally shocked. It hadn’t even officially become a demon yet, and there was going to be a war already?

“What’s the Demonic Sky Realm?” The Ghost Bride didn’t really care. After all, she was already dead. She just wanted to know her fiancée’s fate. After that, whether her soul scattered or not didn’t matter.

“A bunch of bad people from another realm.” Lin Jing glanced at the Ghost Bride, intending to first secure the Golden Light Gu. Though technically he was also an outsider, he figured he wasn’t one of the bad ones.

Once he had the Golden Light Gu, he’d quickly raise his contract beasts’ cultivation levels. Otherwise, even if he barely made it into Tianyuan Realm’s top ten prodigies, he still couldn’t shine in the Origin Realm.

The Demon Emperor made a good point. That place was way too perfect for him—it was like his dreamland.

A place where an Immortal Source emerged every ten years, and the strongest cultivators were only Nascent Soul.

Lin Jing wished he could settle down there forever and never come out.

And if he ever did come out, it’d have to be after both he and his six contract beasts all broke through to the Void Mystical Stage.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 21: Don’t Underestimate Contract Beasts as Prodigies
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 21: Don’t Underestimate Contract Beasts as Prodigies

At the top of the Buddhist pagoda, Lin Jing looked at the Ghost Bride.

To squeeze into the top ten prodigies of the Tianyuan Realm, the Golden Light Gu was a must-have. After all, the Golden Gu could greatly enhance the power of the Ice Soul Gu.

After finishing his conversation with the Demon Emperor, Lin Jing realized a very serious problem, so he suddenly started paying special attention to the individual strength of his pet beasts.

Earlier, the Demon Emperor had implied that he and his beasts could enter the Origin Realm as one entity…

This was a big issue.

The Demon Emperor thought that way because the various Cavern Heavens and secret realms of the Tianyuan Realm all operated under that logic.

But would the Origin Realm really recognize him and the Pine Leaf Mouse, Dragon Carp, and Ice Soul Gu as a single entity?

The reason Tianyuan Realm’s realms accepted this was because this was the Celestial Palace’s territory, and the Beast Taming Art originated from the Celestial Palace itself.

It was a public area jointly reconstructed, maintained, and managed by many Celestial Realm powers on par with the Celestial Palace, serving as a training ground for prodigies from various realms.

The Origin Realm had rich resources, and cultivators from every realm wanted in. Because of that, the number of entries had to be limited to prevent those resources from drying up.

So there was a high chance that the Origin Realm’s rules wouldn’t recognize the Beast Tamer and their contract beasts as one being. If that were the case, then things would get messy.

Forget about the main body going in while leaving incarnations behind—even both versions might not be allowed entry.

But it wasn’t completely hopeless. If Lin Jing wanted to bring Pine Leaf Mouse, Dragon Carp, and Ice Soul Gu along, there was still a way.

That would be to have Pine Leaf Mouse, Dragon Carp, and Ice Soul Gu each make it into the top ten of the Fortune Gold List on their own. As long as they could defeat other top-ten prodigies individually, then even if the Origin Realm didn’t acknowledge beast contracts, the four of them could use four separate slots to enter.

Who said a pet beast couldn’t be a realm’s prodigy?

Although this might earn him a beating from some great powers in Tianyuan Realm, it wouldn’t violate the entry rules set by the Second Ancient Emperor.

So just to be safe, Pine Leaf Mouse, Dragon Carp, and Ice Soul Gu all needed to have the power to suppress prodigies on their own.

Lin Jing wasn’t too worried about the Pine Leaf Mouse and Dragon Carp—both were rare beasts, near-immortal beings. The challenge was the Ice Soul Gu.

For Gu worms, having or not having a human host was a night-and-day difference in combat power. So he had to boost its individual power as much as possible.

Devouring other rare Gu was a solid strategy.

Then, in battle, even if it couldn’t win by brute force, it could win by elemental advantage. If facing a prodigy from Poseidon Palace, it would use the Thunder Tribulation Gu. If facing a wood-based cultivator, it would use the Golden Light Gu… as long as it had enough elemental types, it could gain a huge edge in fights.

“Senior, we’re ready. Can we begin?” The Ghost Bride before him was the key to obtaining the Golden Light Gu!

“Mm.” Tang Ling nodded.

“You can start. I won’t resist.”

“Then I’ll begin.” Lin Jing extended his hand, summoning the Buddha-Demon Pearl outside his body. The upper half of the pearl was gold, the lower half black—gold radiated rich Buddhist light, while black surged with demonic energy. Holding it gave Lin Jing the feeling of gripping a Poké Ball, about to catch a Pokémon.

“Go.” With focused eyes, Lin Jing activated the Buddha-Demon Pearl to absorb the Ghost Bride. In the next instant, she, having offered no resistance, twisted like a phantom and was sucked into the pearl. But right after that—crack—a flicker of light sparked from the pearl, and it showed signs of cracking.

It seemed the Ghost Bride’s power was too great. Even though she was intentionally holding back, the Buddha-Demon Pearl still couldn’t handle it!

“Seal!!" The Thunder Dragon Ancestor slowly drew the pearl into Lin Jing’s body while simultaneously activating the Immortal Body to suppress the pearl’s Buddhist power. At the same time, it used the terrifying vitality of the young Longevity Body—equivalent to twenty thousand years—to suppress the ghostly divine power flooding from the pearl.

“Senior, can you hear me?”

“Yes,” the Ghost Bride answered.

“Next, I’ll need to extract power from the Buddha-Demon Pearl—borrow some of your ghostly divine force.”

“Go ahead.”

“Good!”

With the Ghost Bride’s cooperation, things became much easier for Lin Jing.

He now had much more confidence in obtaining the Golden Gu.

Next, Lin Jing entered a half-month-long closed-door training session for refinement and synchronization.

Until the time for the Golden Light Gu’s final inheritance approached, the Nine Dragons Carriage once again set sail. Lin Jing stood at the front of the wooden boat, atop the dragon’s head, his complexion quite poor.

Originally, Lin Jing looked full of life and youthful energy.

But after sealing the Ghost Bride for a while and using her ghostly divine power, he now looked gloomy, with visibly accelerated life force loss. In the words of the Thunder Dragon Ancestor, he now resembled a devil cultivator from the Netherworld Hall.

“It’s a powerful force, but it can’t be sealed in the body too long. Once I obtain the Golden Light Gu, I must release the Ghost Bride quickly, or my body won’t be able to handle it. I’ll just keep getting weaker, like being drained of Yang energy by a female ghost.”

The side effects of sealing the Ghost Bride were even worse than raising a rare Gu.

But that was due to the huge gap in cultivation. If they were both in the Void Mystical Stage, the burden of using a rare Gu would definitely be heavier.

“Your Highness, we’re almost at Golden Light Temple.”

“Congratulations, Your Highness. You’re about to obtain a rare Gu. With the Ghost Bride, these Buddhist prodigies won’t be able to compete with you.”

“Our journey through the Western Desert is almost at an end,” the Thunder Dragon Ancestor said with a cheerful smile.

“Mm." Lin Jing caught the hint and said, “You’ve worked hard, Senior. Once we return to Qingzhou, I’ll go into seclusion to refine pills and prepare nine Transformation Pills suitable for Nascent Soul-stage Demon Kings in the Thunder Dragon Clan as thanks for your protection.”

“Thank you, Your…” The flying ship exited the void and descended over Golden Light Temple in Golden Body City. The Thunder Dragon Ancestor’s thanks were caught in his throat as he froze, staring at the ruined city before him.

“Huh? Did we get lost?” The nine Thunder Dragon prodigies pulling the carriage were also completely baffled. They hadn’t taken a wrong turn.

It had only been six months. How had Golden Body City ended up like this?

Lin Jing furrowed his brow. In the next moment, three Void Mystical auras spread out from the city. One of them was very familiar to Lin Jing—it was Master Wu Mo of the Demon-Slaying Temple, the monk in the sun-colored robes.

Another was the Indestructible Monk of Golden Light Temple. But now, he no longer looked frail and lifeless—his vitality was brimming.

The last one was a Void Mystical nun—Master Jingxin of the Buddhist Sect.

As Lin Jing and the Thunder Dragon Ancestor arrived, the three monks stepped out of the ruins of Golden Light Temple together.

“We meet again,” Master Wu Mo smiled.

The Indestructible Monk gave Lin Jing and the Thunder Dragon Ancestor a polite palm-pressing gesture and said, “Hehe, you’ve arrived. A few days ago, demonic cultivators from the Gu God Sect came to steal the Golden Light Gu... and this Buddhist City suffered as a result…”

“The Gu God Sect came? Was it Elder Wind Gu or Sect Leader Yang Qiancheng?” Lin Jing’s eyes flickered.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 22: Racing Against Time
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 22: Racing Against Time

“Your Highness, there's no need to worry. I used the Reignition Buddhist Technique to temporarily restore my peak power and repelled those demonic cultivators.”

“The Golden Light Gu is still sealed intact inside the temple and hasn’t fallen into the hands of the Demonic Sect.”

“Now, all participants in the inheritance trial have arrived and are waiting inside the Inheritance Hall. Your Highness is the last one.…

Now that you're here, we can finally begin the final inheritance trial.”

So it was the Reignition Technique—no wonder the Indestructible Monk looked much better.

But what left Lin Jing speechless was that he wasn’t worried about the Gu God Sect stealing the Golden Light Gu.

He was concerned about who from the Gu God Sect had come.

If the sect leader who wielded the Yang Gu and the elder who held the Wind Gu had both left their headquarters and come to the Western Desert, Lin Jing’s next order might have been to send the Thunder Dragon Ancestor after them to seize the rare Gu.

Since it had only been a few days, they might still be in the Western Desert! But now that the three major Buddhist powerhouses were here, those people definitely wouldn’t dare return.

“You used the Reignition Technique, then doesn’t that mean…” the Thunder Dragon Ancestor looked at the Indestructible Monk. Humans really were fragile. Thankfully, it was a dragon, with a relatively long lifespan.

But while it was still marveling, it didn’t know it was about to become a beast of burden again.

They hadn’t even faced off against a Void Mystical ghost yet, and now the Holy Son was eyeing a Void Mystical Gu cultivator.

“It’s fine. All humans must die eventually. Using the Reignition Technique only accelerates the process. With my current state, I can still host the Golden Gu’s inheritance without issue.” The Indestructible Monk smiled.

At that moment, Master Jingxin and Master Wu Mo were both observing Lin Jing.

They could sense a powerful ghostly divine energy coming from him.

They couldn’t help but look toward the Thunder Dragon Ancestor, curious about what kind of ghost Lin Jing had sealed with its help.

But facing the gazes of two Void Mystical Buddhist cultivators, the Thunder Dragon Ancestor curled its lip... These bald donkeys clearly had no idea what a child of fortune was.

Honestly, even it didn’t have high hopes when Lin Jing said he wanted to seal a Void Mystical ghost. Who would’ve guessed they didn’t even need to fight in the end.

These guys were in for a big surprise.

“In that case, let’s not keep the other prodigies waiting.” Lin Jing was also eager to start the final trial.

.............

Meanwhile, inside the Inheritance Hall of Golden Light Temple.

Several Buddhist prodigies who had passed the earlier tests had gathered here.

Their cultivation levels ranged from Core Formation to Nascent Soul.

Qin Siyuan, the monk from Martial Emperor City who had fought Lin Jing previously, was also here.

In the time they hadn't seen each other, his cultivation had apparently advanced from Core Formation to Nascent Soul—perhaps stimulated by their last battle.

But among the group of prodigies, Qin Siyuan wasn’t the center of attention.

Instead, a monk whose bald head burned with flames drew the most eyes.

He was Dao Hui, the Child of the Sun, born with the Innate Solar Divine Body, and a disciple of the Solar Temple.

“Little Brother Thunder Dragon,” a younger mage looked at him and said, “You were right. Seems the Solar Temple is truly aiming for the Golden Light Gu this time.”

“I just don’t want Buddhist treasures falling into the hands of the Demon Race,” said Dao Hui as he slowly opened his eyes.

“After all, the Demon Court’s Holy Son is also here, trying to lay his hands on our Buddhist inheritance. I heard some Buddhist prodigies on the Gold List were defeated by him. That’s when I knew—I had to come.”

Beside him, Qin Siyuan narrowed his eyes, sensing an insult. He looked at Dao Hui and said, “First of all, I didn’t lose. Second, the Golden Light Gu is a demonic artifact—what Buddhist inheritance are you talking about? If you’re the top Buddhist prodigy, how about getting into the top ten of the Gold List before you act all high and mighty.”

“Calm down, calm down.” The other Buddhist prodigies chuckled bitterly—they knew these two would bicker.

Everyone knew Dao Hui didn’t like Qin Siyuan, who had joined the Buddhist sect halfway through his life. Qin Siyuan had broken precepts multiple times, never really taking the Buddhist path seriously. But his talent was too exceptional for the sect to give up on him.

“My Solar Sutra is now perfected. I’ll be reaching the Nascent Soul Transformation stage soon. Once I do, the Gold List’s top ten will have a place for Buddhist cultivators.” Dao Hui wasn’t fazed by Qin Siyuan’s jab.

“Perfected Solar Sutra?!”

All the Buddhist prodigies were stunned. The Solar Sutra was the core technique of the Solar Temple. Once perfected, one could enter the Immortal-Burying Volcano and undergo Nascent Soul Transformation through the Immortal-Burying Flame. If Dao Hui succeeded, he might really make it into the top ten.

“But it’s not like there aren’t any Nascent Soul cultivators in the top ten. You need to break through just to get in? The Demon Court’s Holy Maiden made it in at Nascent Soul Stage. Don’t embarrass the Buddhist sect. The Demon Court’s Holy Son is already here too. Losing to the Holy Maiden is one thing, but if you also lose to a Core Formation brat, let’s see what excuse you have then.”

Qin Siyuan couldn’t stand how some Buddhist disciples pretended to be righteous all the time. He pulled out a drumstick and started eating it again.

“Just a Core Formation cultivator.” Dao Hui cast a casual glance at Qin Siyuan. At the same time, the one everyone was waiting for—Lin Jing—arrived at the Inheritance Hall, accompanied by the three Buddhist powerhouses.

All the Buddhist prodigies instantly turned their gazes toward Lin Jing.

Sensing the hostile stares, Lin Jing remained unfazed.

He looked at the Indestructible Monk and said, “I remember you said the final trial is a contest of whose sealed ghost is stronger, right?”

“Yes.” The Indestructible Monk nodded.

Lin Jing looked over at his old acquaintance, Qin Siyuan, and smiled, “That Asura—you didn’t fully unseal that thing in your body last time. What stage is it?”

Qin Siyuan frowned and replied, “What’s sealed inside me is a peak Nascent Soul battle ghost. You got lucky last time. If we fought again, things wouldn’t end the same.”

“Peak Nascent Soul, huh…” Lin Jing scanned the room, pausing a few extra seconds on the monk with flames on his head.

“About what I expected. Among everyone here, the strongest sealed ghost seems to be his. That ghost must be at half-step Void Mystical, nearly at the Void Refining Stage.” Lin Jing looked at Dao Hui.

Thanks to the enhancement of the Heavenly Demon Scroll and the nourishment of multiple Void Mystical beings, Lin Jing’s divine sense was now frighteningly powerful—even more so than some of the Void Mystical Buddhist cultivators here.

“What are you implying?” Qin Siyuan frowned.

Lin Jing gave a slight smile and said, “What I’m saying is, if this is the level of my competition, then there’s no point continuing. Senior Indestructible Monk, the ghosts in their bodies are too weak. Looks like the Golden Light Gu really does belong in my hands.”
