“Let’s wrap this up quickly—I’m in a rush. Besides the Golden Light Gu, I still need to go after the Gu God Sect and reclaim the remaining Gu.”

“Your Highness, you…” The Indestructible Monk froze as Lin Jing said this, and in the next moment, everyone’s expression changed.

Even Master Wu Mo and Master Jingxin looked stunned.

“I’ve sealed one of the three Void Mystical ghost entities recorded in Buddhist texts—the Ghost Bride—into the Buddha-Demon Pearl. I don’t think anyone else here has sealed a stronger ghost. So, let’s keep it simple.”

He summoned the Buddha-Demon Pearl. Inside the now-transparent pearl, bound in Buddha chains, the wedding-dressed Ghost Bride came into view.

“This… how is this possible.” The Indestructible Monk was dumbfounded. This defied all logic.

Even if the Demon Court’s Holy Son was a Ghost Tamer instead of a Beast Tamer, there was still no reason he should’ve been able to seal a Void Mystical ghost!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 23: Ghostly Divine Power
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 23: Ghostly Divine Power

"Impossible."

"Absolutely impossible."

After Lin Jing revealed the sealed ghost entity, not a single person in the Buddhist sect could believe it,

from the Buddhist prodigies at the Core Formation Stage, to the Buddhist experts at the Void Mystical Stage.

"Master!" Qin Siyuan, who had once been defeated by Lin Jing, looked utterly shocked as he turned his gaze toward Master Jingxin.

This nun at the Void Mystical Stage also didn’t want to believe it, but her divine sense told her—it was all true.

Inside the Buddha-Demon Pearl, it was indeed that Void-level Ghost Bride the Buddhist sect had temporarily given up trying to deal with.

What on earth is going on...

"Thunder Dragon Ancestor? No... that’s not it..." Master Wu Mo in white looked toward the outside of the hall.

Even with the Thunder Dragon Ancestor’s help, it wouldn’t be impossible for Lin Jing’s group to kill a Void-level ghost entity. However, directly killing one and sealing it inside

the body of a Core Formation Stage cultivator are two completely different things.

The latter is far more difficult.

Even someone like Dao Hui from the Solar Temple, with the Child of the Sun physique—a natural nemesis to ghost entities—absolutely couldn’t seal a Void-level ghost entity inside himself at the Core Formation Stage. Not even at the Nascent Soul Stage would it be possible.

So how did this Holy Son of the Beast Taming Sect manage it?

“Holy Son of the Demon Court, are you forgetting something?”

While the other Buddhist prodigies stared at the Ghost Bride in speechless shock, the monk Dao Hui from the Solar Temple finally spoke up after a moment of silence.

He actually wasn’t interested in the Golden Light Gu. Though it was extremely useful for Buddhist cultivators who refined Golden Bodies, he was cultivating the Solar Sutra—an extreme yang Buddhist art—and didn’t have much need for it. He’d only gotten involved to prevent the Demon Court’s Holy Son from stealing a Buddhist opportunity.

If the Holy Son of the Demon Court truly obtained this inheritance, what face would the Buddhist sect have left?

Right now, Lin Jing’s performance had indeed exceeded his expectations—but that didn’t mean he’d just hand everything over.

"The final test isn’t just about who sealed the higher-level ghost entity. It’s also about who can control more ghostly divine power while remaining conscious!"

"If all you did was seal her but you can’t control her power, then it doesn’t count as success..."

"Maybe it was just a fluke—she might’ve been in a chaotic state, and you took advantage of it."

Dao Hui remembered—the Ghost Bride was a hybrid ghost entity, and her state was extremely unstable. If he guessed correctly, Lin Jing had likely taken advantage of the internal conflict within her consciousness and, with help from a guardian, barely managed to seal her.

But even if the sealing succeeded, Dao Hui absolutely didn’t believe Lin Jing could control the ghostly divine power of a Void-level entity!

It was like a Core Formation cultivator trying to activate a top-grade artifact—he’d collapse from exhaustion or die trying.

Even if the Ghost Bride fully cooperated, there was no way a Core Formation cultivator could handle Void-level ghostly divine power.

Dao Hui was banking on this point. He believed Lin Jing had used some bizarre method to seal the Ghost Bride in his body, but sealing her didn’t mean he could bear her power. As long as Lin Jing couldn’t control her power, it didn’t matter how high-level the sealed ghost was.

That so-called seal would be a joke. If even a tiny bit of the sealed ghost’s power couldn’t be used or suppressed, then the seal would be no different from paper—it could be broken at any time.

And in that case, how could anyone claim Lin Jing had successfully sealed the ghost entity?

Then they’d have a reason to disqualify the Core Formation cultivator from that perspective.

After all, Golden Light Temple was looking for a Buddhist prodigy who could control the power of the Golden Light Gu. Simply sealing it in one’s body would be too wasteful.

Dao Hui’s argument also received the approval of the other Buddhist prodigies. Even if they couldn’t get the Golden Light Gu themselves, they didn’t want an outsider like Lin Jing to get it either!

"True." Many of the prodigies exchanged looks, finding it hard to believe that Lin Jing could control the Ghost Bride’s power. If even a wisp of her power could cause Lin Jing to fall into madness, then the seal would be meaningless.

"Master Dao Hui makes a good point," a group of Buddhist geniuses echoed in agreement.

"Senior Indestructible Monk, what I said makes sense, doesn’t it?" Dao Hui also turned to remind the Indestructible Monk not to rush into any decision.

However, before the monk could reply, Lin Jing simply shook his head.

"Not crying until you see the coffin, huh." He cast a cold glance at the flame-crowned Dao Hui.

Then, the Buddha-Demon Pearl was retracted by Lin Jing, and a chilling, sinister force spread out from his body.

He let out a cold snort. Technically, what Dao Hui said wasn’t wrong. Even a wisp of the Ghost Bride’s power shouldn’t be something a Core Formation cultivator like him could control.

But Dao Hui’s mistake was in ignoring Lin Jing’s identity as a Beast Taming Cultivator.

He wasn’t just a Buddhist cultivator.

Relying on Buddhist methods alone, he really couldn’t control the Ghost Bride’s power.

But... as Lin Jing burned ten years of his lifespan, his Core Formation began to operate, and he activated the divine art—Divine Control Technique. The wisp of ghostly divine power released by the Ghost Bride was instantly subdued!

BOOM!!!

Lin Jing’s long black hair whipped wildly in the air, his entire body glowing red under the intertwining ghostly power. His green robe turned crimson, and his soul’s power surged to terrifying levels.

"What are you trying to do..." Dao Hui’s pupils shrank as he sensed the overwhelming force radiating from Lin Jing. He cursed internally—this guy, could he really control Void-level ghost power?

What kind of joke is this?

And this was just a question—yet the Holy Son of the Demon Court was already showing killing intent toward him?!

Dao Hui broke into a cold sweat.

Feeling Lin Jing’s murderous aura, he quickly attempted to rouse the terrifying ghost entity sealed within his own Buddha-Demon Pearl to fight back. But to his horror, his sealed ghost began to shudder uncontrollably at just a faint trace of the Ghost Bride’s aura—it seemed terrified.

"Stop, Holy Son!!"

Sensing Lin Jing’s intent to kill, the Indestructible Monk was startled and just about to intervene, when a bolt of lightning flashed through his mind:

“Don’t interfere in a junior’s fight. Don’t worry—the Holy Son can control it.”

At this moment, Lin Jing was indeed radiating immense killing intent. But it wasn’t entirely from himself—it was the influence of the Ghost Bride’s power.

The ghost power of a Void-level entity was still too strong. Even the tiniest sliver nearly overtook Lin Jing’s emotions. That killing intent was only a fraction of the ghostly divine power’s negative emotions.

What impacted Lin Jing most in that instant was the Ghost Bride’s Dao of Obsession. Under its influence, his mind was filled with images of the sword-wielding female cultivator from her memories. He almost lost himself to her yearning and was on the verge of being possessed—becoming part of her.

And in her mind, Dao Hui was the culprit responsible for the death of her beloved.

If it had been anyone else, they would’ve already struck Dao Hui down under the influence of ghostly divine power.

Thankfully, Lin Jing had trained specifically for this. In that instant, the Ice Soul Gu merged with him. Drawing upon its recent cultivation in the Dao of Obsession, it swiftly froze the negative emotions, allowing Lin Jing to regain clarity in a flash—and perfectly control the ghostly divine power.

He struck with a single palm. The despairing force swept forth. Dao Hui tried to resist with his own strength, but as a Nascent Soul cultivator, even if his power was naturally suited to countering ghostly force, how could it compare to Lin Jing—who had sacrificed ten years of his lifespan to unleash a portion of Void-level might?

BANG!

Unsurprisingly, Dao Hui spat blood as he was sent flying, the flames above his head extinguished. He crashed down beside Master Jingxin’s disciple, Qin Siyuan, who stared at him in shock.

"This..."

Qin Siyuan was shaken to his core. Dao Hui was a prodigy ranked in the top fifteen on the Fortune Gold List, a Buddhist monster with a good chance of breaking into the top ten after reaching Nascent Soul. Even though he didn’t get along with Dao Hui, he had acknowledged his strength.

Yet this Holy Son of the Demon Court... was terrifying.

He actually used to think he could compete with this monster?!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 24: First Time on the List
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 24: First Time on the List

After blasting Dao Hui away with the power of the Ghost Bride, Lin Jing remained calm.

Although the Divine Control Technique consumed a lot of energy, it couldn’t match the vastness of his twenty-thousand-year lifespan—it was like an endless sea.

“Senior Indestructible, don’t worry. I promised Master Wu Mo that during this inheritance trial, I wouldn’t harm the Buddhist prodigies. This strike was just to show this fellow Daoist whether I could use Ghostly Divine Power.” Lin Jing clasped his hands toward the Indestructible Monk.

With that said, he looked at the rest of the Buddhist prodigies.

“In that case, this Golden Light Gu—I suppose no one else is going to compete with me for it, right?”

“No, no one.” The Indestructible Monk smiled bitterly, glancing at the unconscious Dao Hui monk.

He silently thought to himself—just what kind of monster is this Holy Son of the Demon Court?

Sealing a Void Mystical ghost entity was already impressive enough, but to actually wield her power? And to wield it so effortlessly?

Looks like this Golden Light Gu really had to be handed over to this Holy Son of the Demon Court.

The Indestructible Monk believed that if he tried to go back on his word now—under these circumstances, especially if he favored his own Buddhist disciples—the Demon Court would probably send their experts to demand an explanation from the Western Desert in no time.

Moreover, Lin Jing had indeed contributed to the Western Desert—at least by helping them take care of a Void Mystical ghost entity. With that in mind, giving him the Golden Light Gu wasn’t a loss.

The Indestructible Monk sighed in his heart. This outcome wasn’t entirely unacceptable.

“Many thanks, Senior Indestructible.” Lin Jing respectfully saluted.

“Then, Senior, may I receive the Golden Light Gu now?”

“Mm...”

The Indestructible Monk nodded. Fortunately, this prodigy of the Demon Court didn’t seem to be an evil person either. The Yin Corpse Sect had been destroyed by his hand. Now, he was urgently requesting the Golden Light Gu to go after the Gu God Sect’s expert. If Lin Jing’s obsession with Rare Gu could lead him to destroy the Gu God Sect, then the Indestructible Monk figured—he would have done a good deed after all.

Moments later, Lin Jing took the Golden Light Gu from the Buddhist sect.

After Lin Jing left, Master Wu Mo looked at the Indestructible Monk, who was on the verge of burning out his life, and said, “Not only did you pass on the Golden Light Gu to him, but you even gave him your insights into the Indestructible Golden Body. Aren’t you worried that this Holy Son of the Demon Court might take your legacy and stray from the right path?”

The Indestructible Monk shook his head. “His fortune is unparalleled—even a Void Mystical ghost entity could be tamed by him. Since that’s the case, I’d rather form a karmic bond. Besides, didn’t you also teach him your Buddhist Light Technique?”

“But...” He looked up at the Fortune Gold List in the sky and noticed that although Lin Jing had defeated Dao Hui, he still hadn’t made it onto the list...

“Relied too much on external power? After all, it was Void Mystical-level Ghostly Divine Power. The Fortune Gold List probably didn’t attribute that power to him personally. Maybe he has to defeat other prodigies using his own pet beast to truly seize fortune.” said the Indestructible Monk.

“Perhaps.” Master Wu Mo nodded, not particularly concerned. With Lin Jing’s potential, it was only a matter of time.

After acquiring the Golden Light Gu, Lin Jing didn’t care whether he made it onto the Fortune Gold List. His current priority was pursuing Elder Wind Gu of the Gu God Sect...

According to the Indestructible Monk, Elder Wind Gu had led a few of his disciples to invade Golden Light Temple. Though it was a pity the sect leader hadn’t come, Lin Jing was very eager to find Elder Wind Gu.

Thus, before completing the task given by the Demon Emperor, Dragon Carp remained in the Western Desert, accompanied by the Thunder Dragon Ancestor, searching for traces of Elder Wind Gu, hoping he was still somewhere in the region!

Besides, Dao Hui hadn’t successfully broken through yet—they’d have to wait a while for him anyway.

Half a month ago.

Immortal-Burying Volcano.

The Immortal-Burying Volcano was one of the ten forbidden zones of the Tianyuan Continent, famous for its blazing Solar Essence Fire, strong enough to kill even immortals.

It was an extremely dangerous place. The flames would often erupt spontaneously, and if approached recklessly, even a Nascent Soul cultivator could be incinerated instantly.

But danger didn’t necessarily mean certain death.

For those with special physiques or cultivation techniques, it was also a holy land for training.

For example, for the Solar Temple... the Solar Sutra could resist the harsh environment of the Immortal-Burying Volcano, even converting the terrifying Solar Essence Fire into usable energy.

Anyone who fully mastered the Solar Sutra would earn the basic qualification to enter the volcano’s outer region and experience the power of the sun. If a cultivator reached Nascent Soul Stage while mastering the Solar Sutra, that was even better—they wouldn’t even need to forge an external incarnation, and could use the Solar Sutra alone to sense the world’s power surrounding the Immortal-Burying Volcano and condense a Solar Nascent Soul.

“Lin Jing.” At this moment, a figure was speeding through the skies above the Western Desert. It was none other than Dao Hui, the Solar Temple prodigy defeated by Ghost Bride’s power. His face was dark, his anger over the Golden Light Temple incident burning fiercely.

“Holy Son of the Demon Court, just wait until I reach Nascent Soul Stage.”

Dao Hui couldn’t accept his defeat to the Holy Son—especially these past half-months, with rumors spreading across the Western Desert of Lin Jing’s victory and his acquisition of the Golden Light Gu.

Though clever people could deduce from the list changes that Lin Jing used external power to win, many ignorant fools thought he, a Buddhist prodigy, was inferior to the Holy Son of the Demon Court. For someone like Dao Hui, who valued reputation—especially that of the Buddhist sect—this was unacceptable.

But he didn’t go seeking revenge immediately. He didn’t believe a mere Core Formation cultivator like Lin Jing could keep a Void Mystical ghost sealed for long.

So, he planned to break through to Nascent Soul Stage at the Immortal-Burying Volcano first. At that point, not only Lin Jing, but even the Demon Court Holy Maiden, he’d make her answer for it.

“The solar power at the edge of the Immortal-Burying Volcano... why does it feel so weak?”

Feeling immense pressure, Dao Hui approached the volcano. But as soon as he neared it, he sensed something strange. During this Golden Era, with spiritual energy resurgent, the volcano’s fire should be even more terrifying—so why did it seem so much weaker now?

It didn’t take him long to find the cause.

Suddenly, a burst of terrifying demonic energy surged from a volcano crater—it felt like someone had just formed their Nascent Soul, but the overwhelming energy instantly convinced Dao Hui this was the birth of at least a Demon King with Ancestor-level bloodline!

“Impossible! A Demon King... no, could even a Demon Core Stage entity come here to cultivate? Could it be the Golden Crow Clan?”

He couldn’t believe it. He flew closer to investigate—but what greeted him was a mysterious female voice from who knows where.

“You just broke through and already have a good-looking opponent. Little Carp, go smack him around a bit.”

“What the h*ll is this thing?” Before Dao Hui could react, a carp riding fire waves leapt out of the volcano, its sharp gaze locked onto him.

“Splash!”

Dao Hui’s heart sank. This was insane...

..........

Half a day later, many cultivators noticed a change in the upper ranks of the Fortune Gold List. But the details of that change left everyone baffled.

Fortune Gold List, Rank 12: Dragon Carp, Cultivation: Early Nascent Soul Stage,

Reason for being listed: Defeated the Buddhist Solar Physique, Dao Hui!

“What the h*ll? Who the hll is Dan Xue? Beat someone at Nascent Soul Small Perfection while only at early stage? A demonic beast prodigy? From what faction? Could it be the Demon Emperor’s son?”

“Sounds more like a species name than a real name. A carp with no dragon blood?! What is going on? It's already a Demon King, right? Is it a sentient beast—or just a nameless spirit beast?”

“Dan Xue... wait, could it actually be Dragon Carp, the Holy Son of the Demon Court's rare beast?”

“That’s gotta be it—Lin Jing himself didn’t get listed. How could a pet beast show up solo? Unless... maybe no Beast Taming cultivator was present, and Lin Jing got wrecked... or maybe it’s another carp entirely…”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 25: True Name
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 25: True Name

Western Desert.

Lin Jing and his group were also watching the Fortune Gold List.

After searching for Elder Wind Gu in the Western Desert for a long time with no results, Lin Jing was finally greeted by a pleasant surprise.

Dragon Carp had successfully broken through to the Nascent Soul Stage!

Its bloodline strength had also risen to Ancestor-tier due to refining the Solar Essence Fire!

This meant that as long as it didn’t fall midway, it could almost assuredly ascend unimpeded to the realm of Demon Ancestor, creating a whole new bloodline of its own.

And the benefit this brought to Lin Jing was that his own cultivation, previously at the early Core Formation Stage, was pulled up to the mid Core Formation Stage thanks to the breakthrough feedback.

The sudden surge in cultivation forced Lin Jing to quickly sit down and stabilize it.

“Dao Hui... isn’t that the monk from the Solar Temple? The one with the flaming head.”

“Your Highness Dragon Carp... how did it end up fighting him?”

“And... actually won?”

The Thunder Dragon Ancestor was stunned.

It had actually solo-killed a prodigy from the Solar Temple!

Sure, Dao Hui had suffered a loss to Lin Jing before—but that was with the Ghost Bride helping. Dragon Carp was just a pet under the Holy Son’s command!

In the Thunder Dragon Ancestor’s view, Dao Hui was absolutely no pushover.

He had reached Nascent Soul Perfection, ranked twelfth on the Fortune Gold List, had a Solar Physique that ranked in the top twenty of the Physique Rankings, and had even cultivated the top-tier Buddhist technique, the Solar Sutra... As much as he didn’t want to admit it, their own Thunder Dragon Crown Prince, back in his Nascent Soul days, definitely wouldn’t have been a match for this Dao Hui.

And yet this Buddhist prodigy... was defeated by someone of lower cultivation.

No wonder they called it a semi-immortal physique rare beast...

“He was probably heading to Immortal-Burying Volcano to comprehend the power of the heavens and earth to ascend to Nascent Soul Transformation, and happened to run into Dragon Carp.”

The Holy Son Lin chuckled.

To ascend from Nascent Soul to Nascent Soul Transformation, one must comprehend the power of heaven and earth.

But that power also had tiers.

The power of wind and rain, for example, was relatively weak.

The power of sun and moon, on the other hand, was among the highest tiers.

Since Dao Hui cultivated the Solar Sutra and had a Solar Physique, of course he would come to Immortal-Burying Volcano to comprehend the power of the sun.

With his foundation, advancing to Nascent Soul Transformation should’ve been easy.

But it looked like he was incredibly unlucky—he ran into Dragon Carp. Who knew how he was doing now.

In Lin Jing’s eyes, it wasn’t surprising that Dragon Carp won.

Back in the Battle of Tongtian City, it had used the souls of countless Void Mystical, Nascent Soul, and even Dao-Seeking cultivators as nourishment to perfect its Netherworld Ghostfire Technique, which was likely second only to the Hall Master of the Netherworld Hall.

Its rare beast divine ability also allowed it to fuse various Exotic Flames, taking any flame as its core and increasing its power to an absurd level...

After all, its Netherworld Ghostfire was already the highest quality possible in the lower realm. And once fused with other Exotic Flames through its rare beast divine ability, its flames had already reached the level of a Quasi-Immortal Flame.

This was precisely why it could enter Immortal-Burying Volcano. The Yangflame it controlled was a subordinate flame of the Solar Essence Fire. By using other flames as fuel and synthesizing a Quasi-Immortal Yangflame, it could adapt even better to the Solar Essence.

This was the key that allowed it to enter Immortal-Burying Volcano and attempt to refine the Solar Essence Fire.

More than a refinement—it was essentially upgrading its own Yangflame. Compared to its own foundation, even the Demon Emperor’s assistance was secondary.

Otherwise, any other Core Formation cultivator, even with Dao-Seeking support, would’ve been unable to refine the Solar Essence Fire.

Now it seemed clear—Dragon Carp had perfectly refined the Solar Essence Fire, and all of its own flames, now fused through the Flame Emperor’s Divine Ability, had definitely reached the level of True Immortal Annihilation!

“With Immortal Flame in hand, within the same stage, unless someone borrows overwhelming external power, no one can defeat it.”

“As for Dao Hui, though he had external power within him, Immortal-Burying Volcano is a forbidden zone for ghost entities. His Ghostly Divine Power wouldn’t have surfaced at all, and he could only rely on his Solar Physique and Solar Sutra to fight. As strong as those are, they’re still crushed before Immortal Annihilation.”

Immortal Flame, huh.

Now Dragon Carp was practically invincible.

In all of Tianyuan Continent, among natural forces, probably only the Tribulation of Ascension could match it in quality.

The counterpart to Immortal Flame was Immortal Lightning.

And the symbol of Immortal Lightning was Heavenly Tribulation.

But not all tribulations were truly immortal-grade.

Only the so-called strongest—the Ascension Lightning Tribulation—had perfect strength and grade, and was the final test the Immortal Realm bestowed upon lower-realm cultivators.

As for Void Mystical Tribulation, Dao-Seeking Tribulation, Transformation Tribulation, and those triggered by Thunder Tribulation Gu—they only unleashed a portion of a true tribulation’s power. At best, they were pseudo-immortal, quasi-immortal lightning.

Otherwise, if they were complete Immortal Lightning, not even Void Mystical or Dao-Seeking cultivators would survive.

Now that Dragon Carp possessed true Immortal Flame, even though it was synthesized rather than innate, it gave it the confidence to ignore any tribulation—except the Ascension Tribulation.

“But that’s not the main point... the main point is, how did my pet beast get listed before I did...”

Lin Jing scratched his head.

Still, this clarified the list’s rules for him.

As long as a pet beast separates from the Beast Tamer, and fights without borrowing any power from the tamer, and defeats other prodigies on its own, it can appear on the list independently—the achievement isn’t credited to the tamer.

As for the tamer themselves, they can’t rely solely on their pet to make the list—they need to contribute directly to battle, at least using Beast Taming Secret Arts.

“Well, either way, it’s a good thing.”

“Congratulations, Your Highness the Holy Son, and congratulations to His Highness Dragon Carp as well.”

“But, Holy Son, doesn’t Dragon Carp have a true name bound to its fate? Why does the list show only the species name?”

After a while, the Thunder Dragon Ancestor voiced the doubt in his heart.

“Uh...” Lin Jing fell into deep thought.

“Isn’t there a saying that names given by geomancy masters can boost one’s cultivation fate... I’d always planned to wait until I met a great master before asking for a name that could enhance its fortune.”

“So that’s why! No wonder Your Highness the Holy Son can raise pet beasts so well—you even pay attention to tiny details like that,” flattered the Thunder Dragon Ancestor.

“And then?” he asked.

Lin Jing muttered, “Then I just got used to calling them by their species—Pine Leaf Mouse, Dragon Carp, Ice Soul Gu... and forgot to ask a master for names.”

Thunder Dragon Ancestor: “...”

The old dragon wiped his face and said, “With your current status, Your Highness the Holy Son, finding a fortune master should be easy. There must be plenty in the Western Desert. If you have time, you should ask one to name them. After all, they’ve made it onto the Fortune Gold List—representing the highest talents in the Origin Realm. Not having names is just... weird.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 26: Ran into a Ghost
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 26: Ran into a Ghost

Lin Jing felt that what the Thunder Dragon Ancestor said made sense.

At this point, it wasn’t just some pet-raising game within his sect anymore. These creatures were now beast prodigies recognized across the entire continent—they couldn’t keep going around with childish nicknames. They needed proper, imposing names that truly represented them.

He had just been about to ask the Thunder Dragon Ancestor if there were any powerful fortune masters in the Western Desert, when the Tiger Fang given to him by the Demon Emperor activated.

“It’s His Majesty the Demon Emperor.”

Lin Jing motioned for the Thunder Dragon Ancestor to keep quiet for a moment.

“Did you get the Golden Light Gu?”

After pulling out the Tiger Fang, the Demon Emperor’s voice came through.

“I did not fail you.”

“Mm-hmm. I even saw its achievement.” Lin Jing looked up at the sky.

“...” The Demon Emperor paused for a moment and said, “Immortal Flame really is powerful. That monk didn’t stand a chance. But the backlash from it is equally intense—even a rare beast with Ancestor-tier bloodline couldn’t fully handle it.”

“In any case, come to the Immortal-Burying Volcano. I’m waiting with it outside.”

With that, the Demon Emperor ended the communication.

Lin Jing looked a little weirded out—what happened exactly?

He probed around, and once he confirmed that Dragon Carp was still alive, he relaxed.

With the Demon Emperor nearby, how could anything have gone wrong?

“Looks like it’s time to return to Qingzhou. Let’s go meet up with His Majesty the Demon Emperor.” he said to the Thunder Dragon Ancestor.

“We’re not going after Elder Wind Gu anymore???” the Thunder Dragon Ancestor blinked.

Lin Jing shook his head. “It’s been so long with no sign of him. He was probably driven off and fled the Western Desert. After all, this is Buddhist territory. Failing once means there’s little chance of a second try.”

“Alright then...” The Thunder Dragon Ancestor nodded. Great—first the ghost incident, now this wasted search. He was starting to realize he really wasn’t cut out for tracking people down.

“Once we return, I’ll have the Ice Soul Gu devour the Golden Light Gu...”

“Also, I’ll use the Demon Court’s power to help you investigate ancient matters—please don’t worry.”

Lin Jing looked at the red figure inside the flying ship.

The Ghost Bride.

After leaving Golden Light Temple, Lin Jing couldn’t bear her power sealed anymore, so he released her from the Buddha-Demon Pearl.

Since she was also an incredibly strong combatant, Lin Jing had asked her to stick around temporarily in case she could help fight Gu God Sect elders.

But now that they’d given up searching for Elder Wind Gu and were preparing to leave the Western Desert, it was time to part ways.

“Thank you.” The Ghost Bride nodded, then vanished into the void. They had fulfilled each other’s part of the contract…

“Let’s go.”

As he watched her disappear, Lin Jing sighed softly. He felt he had a strong connection with the Ghost Bride—a connection that surely wouldn’t end here.

Soon after.

Outside the Immortal-Burying Volcano.

This region around the Immortal-Burying Volcano, much like the Misty Forest, was nearly cut off from the outside world. The extreme heat was so intense that even spiritual sense was scorched just by releasing it.

Even though they hadn’t yet reached the volcano itself, Lin Jing already felt an immense pressure—like his whole body was melting.

Thankfully, the Thunder Dragon Ancestor cast spells to shield them from the heat.

Upon arrival, Lin Jing and his group successfully reunited with the Demon Emperor.

“Dragon Carp...”

Immediately afterward, they saw the charred remains of a grilled fish lying by the Demon Emperor’s feet.

“Your Majesty... this...”

Lin Jing, expecting to see a smug, bragging Dragon Carp, one who couldn’t wait to show off to Pine Leaf Mouse and Ice Soul Gu, was instead handed a half-cooked fish straight from the Demon Emperor’s hand.

“It was fighting a monk earlier and fused a flame that reached Immortal Flame level—it severely injured the monk.”

“But the power of the Immortal Flame was too strong. It suffered some backlash itself. It’s not fatal, but it’ll probably need time to recover.”

“I underestimated how terrifying Immortal Flame truly is...” the Demon Emperor said casually.

“Your Majesty, please do something!” Lin Jing clutched his heart.

“Even with your power, you can’t heal Immortal Annihilation Wounds?”

“No, letting it recover on its own is better. That way, it can gradually learn to master the Immortal Flame,” explained the Demon Emperor.

She folded her arms, looking completely unconcerned with Dragon Carp’s condition.

“Well...” If it was for Dragon Carp’s own good, then it had no choice but to suffer a bit longer.

Beside him, in Lin Jing’s body, the Ice Soul Gu secretly breathed a sigh of relief.

Thankfully Dragon Carp was unconscious right now.

Otherwise, given that it had broken through and made it onto the list first, it would definitely have rubbed it in everyone’s face.

“What about that Dao Hui monk?” Lin Jing suddenly asked.

“Don’t tell me he burned to death??”

Lin Jing asked nervously. He’d held back and didn’t kill the guy—if he ended up dying to Dragon Carp, that would be so unlucky.

Facing Immortal Flame, Lin Jing had already suffered backlash himself. Wouldn’t Dao Hui have had it even worse?

“He’s fine. That monk had a life-saving item and was taken away by a bald guy from the Solar Temple,” said the Demon Emperor, signaling Lin Jing not to worry.

“Looks like it’ll still be a while before Dragon Carp can start refining pills,” said Lin Jing.

“No rush. The spiritual energy around Immortal-Burying Volcano is dense. It’ll actually help it recover and grasp the Immortal Flame more easily. For now, stay here with it. Once it has a basic mastery of Immortal Flame, then return to Qingzhou.”

The Demon Emperor added, “Once it’s familiar with Immortal Annihilation, it should be able to refine a Transformation Pill that allows Demon Ancestors to take human form. At its current condition, even if it returns, it wouldn’t be able to refine any high-grade pills.”

“Understood.” Lin Jing nodded, then asked, “What about you, Your Majesty?”

“This avatar of mine will remain in the Western Desert—but I won’t be sticking around with you. That bald guy who rescued the little monk earlier came to me with something—just going to catch up with an old friend,” said the Demon Emperor casually. “Before I leave, I’ll draw a barrier around this area for your group to operate safely.”

Lin Jing was speechless. That bald guy… don’t tell me it’s the head of the Buddhist Sect, the current Solar Buddha?

……

In the following days, Lin Jing stayed by Dragon Carp’s side as it recovered.

It remained unconscious the whole time.

During this period, the most energetic one was Ice Soul Gu. After seeing Dragon Carp make the list, it got hyped up and wanted to go out and solo some prodigies, ready to throw hands—but Lin Jing held it down.

“You’re just a little Core Formation Stage Gu. What prodigy are you gonna fight? Wait till you reach Nascent Soul at least.”

At that moment, Lin Jing had no idea—after leaving him, the Ghost Bride hadn’t returned to Ghost City, but had instead started wandering the Western Desert alone.

Though Lin Jing had offered to help, she still wanted to search for traces of her past herself.

Until—

Up in the sky, the Ghost Bride spotted the silhouette of a female cultivator with a sword, and her gaze suddenly turned hazy.

“That looks like...

“That back looks familiar.”

She descended swiftly.

At the same time, having just slain a demonic beast and arrived from the Ancient Kingdom to exorcise demons, the Cold Moon Sword Immortal Feng Ning noticed a powerful ghost entity suddenly appear. Her expression shifted.

“Quick, use the sword intent Master gave us to save our life!” the Sword Spirit exclaimed nervously, eyes locked on the Ghost Bride. But what the Ghost Bride said next stunned the Sword Spirit.

“Is that you... husband?”

Cold Moon Sword Immortal, Sword Spirit: Who are you talking to?

“Ran into a ghost.”

Cold Moon Sword Immortal’s mind went blank. She felt like she’d literally—and figuratively—just run into a ghost.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 27: The Straggler Fish
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 27: The Straggler Fish

“Void Mystical ghost entity...”

A powerful ghost descended, and Cold Moon Sword Immortal managed to stay calm.

After all, she was now a Dao-Seeking personal disciple, the student of a peak sword cultivator,

and had plenty of life-saving techniques.

Among them was a life-saving sword intent at the Dao-Seeking Stage, gifted by her master, the Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor.

But now, Cold Moon Sword Immortal wasn’t quite sure how to judge this Void Mystical ghost entity.

Had its consciousness become disordered?

It called her husband?

“Hold on!”

“Ghost Bride!?”

Suddenly, Cold Moon Sword Immortal thought of something. Ghost Bride—in this Western Desert, there should be only one Void Mystical Ghost Bride.

A few days ago when she arrived in the Western Desert, she had already heard rumors.

Lin Jing, the Longevity Body, had also come to the Western Desert, seemingly for the Golden Light Gu.

To obtain the Golden Light Gu, Lin Jing used Buddhist techniques to seal a Void Mystical Ghost Bride, defeated countless Buddhist prodigies, and successfully acquired the Gu, making a name for himself across the Western Desert.

Later on, there was even some filthy creature suspected to be Lin Jing’s pet that defeated the monk Dao Hui, stirring up many speculations.

Cold Moon Sword Immortal was no longer surprised by Lin Jing’s antics.

But this Ghost Bride, could she be...

“What did you do to Lin Jing?” she questioned, her first thought being that the Ghost Bride had broken through Lin Jing’s seal and escaped.

If that were the case, wouldn’t Lin Jing be in danger?

“Lin Jing...?”

The Ghost Bride was still sizing up Cold Moon Sword Immortal, feeling out her presence to see if she was the one from her memories.

Who knew, this sword cultivator even knew Lin Jing.

Could she be Lin Jing’s sweetheart?

The Ghost Bride couldn’t accept that—her eyes turned red!

Although from the looks of it, the sword cultivator before her wasn’t the same person she once knew.

But they were far too alike.

A descendant?

A reincarnation?

The Ghost Bride didn’t know, but she wasn’t going to let Lin Jing off easy... why was this guy everywhere?

“Don’t worry, he’s fine.” The Ghost Bride clenched her teeth.

Cold Moon Sword Immortal scrutinized her and spoke: “Didn’t he seal you? Why are you here?”

She quietly sent a message to Lin Jing.

But then discovered that she couldn’t reach him at all.

Two possibilities—either he was out of range of the communication artifact,

or Lin Jing was in some special place that blocked communication.

“He probably went to the Immortal-Burying Volcano. In that place, you won’t be able to contact him.” The Ghost Bride saw through Cold Moon Sword Immortal’s little action and spoke flatly.

“Carrying a spirit-infused sword in the Western Desert is extremely dangerous. There’s a creature nearby that specializes in devouring spirit artifacts. If you don’t mind, let me accompany you for now, and I’ll explain slowly.”

..........

Immortal-Burying Volcano!

At this moment, Lin Jing had no idea that after he left, the Ghost Bride had actually encountered Cold Moon Sword Immortal.

Right now, he was listening to the slightly recovered Dragon Carp excitedly explain how it had subdued the Buddhist Son of the Solar Temple.

The Dragon Carp showed no signs of being injured—only an energetic spirit, eager to rush out and fight again.

What’s the point of training Immortal Flame alone? It wanted to temper it through clashes with the contenders on the Fortune Gold List!

“Calm down, you still can’t properly control Immortal Flame. What if you burn someone to death?”

“Besides, His Majesty the Demon Emperor set up a barrier here. Others can’t come in, and we probably can’t get out either.”

“We can only obediently train here until His Majesty returns.” Lin Jing shook his head.

“Dmn...” the light in Dragon Carp’s eyes dimmed.

Can’t get out, huh...

Now that it had such strong power, the Dragon Carp didn’t feel like doing boring spell training. It wanted to stir things up like it did in Tongtian City. Unfortunately, the current environment didn’t allow that.

“Dmn—” it howled at the sky, venting its feelings, hoping to add a little thrill to this quiet life.

Then—

Boom.

Something strange happened.

Lin Jing froze and looked up at the sky.

Dragon Carp also shot a sharp look upward.

A familiar vortex appeared in the sky. The sight of it made the Thunder Dragon Ancestor guarding the edge of the barrier widen his eyes and halt his instructions to the nine Thunder Dragon prodigies!

“A Cavern Heaven, a Cavern Heaven has appeared—it’s been summoned by His Highness the Holy Son.

“Unfortunately, the Cavern Heaven summoned is at the Core Formation level, so you nine won’t be able to benefit from it!”

The Cavern Heaven arrived extremely fast. At that moment, the Dragon Carp’s once-bored expression turned into joy.

Cavern Heaven!

As the Emperor Dragon Carp, it knew it was blessed with fortune. Boring cultivation at a volcanic crater was no fun—competing for fortune in the Cavern Heaven was what it was meant to do.

With a bang, the Cavern Heaven enveloped Lin Jing. The Dragon Carp also dived back into the Beast Taming Space. No matter how strong the Demon Emperor’s barrier was, it couldn’t override the Cavern Heaven opportunity created by the Immortal Palace.

Lin Jing was surprised during the teleportation. This Cavern Heaven... he seemed to have noticed a pattern.

Every time he arrived in a new place and broke through a new stage, it didn’t take long before a Cavern Heaven appeared. This time... after reaching Core Formation and arriving in the Western Desert, sure enough, a Cavern Heaven was triggered again.

But this time, Lin Jing didn’t need to hold back. He didn’t need to hide his identity, nor worry about retaliation after taking others’ opportunities—he could go all out in the Cavern Heaven.

Crack.

The Thunder Dragon Ancestor and others watched helplessly as Lin Jing was engulfed by the light beam of the Cavern Heaven and directly transported away, all wearing looks of envy.

But just a moment later—“pop!”—something unexpected happened.

From within the white light beam, a red Dragon Carp was bounced out. Dragon Carp stared blankly at the beam, totally confused.

It tried to charge back in with all its might, but went straight through—it didn’t work.

Frozen in place, Dragon Carp had a very bad feeling.

“This...” The Thunder Dragon Ancestor flew over and explained, “Your Highness Dragon Carp, it seems this Cavern Heaven is for the Core Formation stage. You’ve already reached the Nascent Soul stage, so you can’t enter.”

“This hasn’t happened before... When a Core Formation-level Beast Taming Cultivator contracts a Nascent Soul-stage pet beast, they’re usually considered one unit—but apparently, the realm rejection prioritizes the higher stage.”

“Pop??” Dragon Carp looked at him—can’t go in? You’re saying I can’t go in?

The Beast Tamer, Pine Leaf Mouse, Ice Soul Gu—all of them went in. And you’re saying only it can’t go in??

“That seems to be the case. But no need to feel too bad, Your Highness. You have Immortal Flame now—there might not even be anything in the Cavern Heaven at that level worthy of you. It’s not really a big loss.” The Thunder Dragon Ancestor consoled.

Dragon Carp trembled slightly—really??

I’m smart, don’t try to fool me.

“Sigh.” The Thunder Dragon Ancestor looked at the light beam, finding it quite troublesome. His Majesty the Demon Emperor had given the Holy Son a mission—during this time, he was supposed to help Dragon Carp master the Immortal Flame. Now that the Holy Son had been pulled into the Cavern Heaven, relying on Dragon Carp alone to train seemed a bit risky.

“If that’s the case, Your Highness Dragon Carp, let this old man help you train. Weren’t you saying earlier that you wanted to temper the Immortal Flame through battle? Coincidentally, I’ve never dealt with Immortal Flame either—let’s fight!” said the Thunder Dragon Ancestor.

Dragon Carp went silent, looking toward the Cavern Heaven. Deep down, it still felt cheated.

Its Beast Tamer and other little partners went inside to snatch rare opportunities from a bunch of weak Core Formation cultivators, and it had to stay outside and brawl with an old Void Mystical dragon—what the h*ll was that about...

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 28: Projection of the Strong
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 28: Projection of the Strong

“Did Dragon Carp not get transported in?”

After entering the Cavern Heaven, Lin Jing realized the recently advanced-to-Nascent-Soul-stage little carp seemed to have been rejected by the Cavern Heaven.

“This unlucky kid.”

Makes sense—Beast Taming Cultivators are a weird group.

Their own cultivation level and their pet’s stage can sometimes differ by a whole major realm.

Generally speaking, the cultivation world tends to consider the higher of the cultivator’s or the pet’s realm as the true realm of the Beast Tamer.

For example, if the Beast Tamer is at Core Formation and the pet is at Nascent Soul, then the Beast Tamer is considered to be at Nascent Soul stage.

But this Immortal Palace Cavern Heaven didn’t seem to follow that rule.

Lin Jing shook his head. If his combat power had relied solely on one demon king pet, and he, a Core Formation-level Beast Tamer, got selected into a Cavern Heaven that rejected the pet for being too high-level—he’d be screwed.

The pet gets blocked for being too strong, and the Beast Tamer’s whole combat strength is in the pet... good luck competing for any fortune.

At the moment, having entered the Cavern Heaven, Pine Leaf Mouse and Ice Soul Gu were raring to go.

And they held a seven-second moment of silence for Dragon Carp.

“Why’s there no one? What kind of Cavern Heaven is this?”

At first, Lin Jing saw no one—everything around was pitch black.

But moments later, as three figures appeared in midair projections, Lin Jing finally understood what kind of Cavern Heaven this was.

Projection Heaven.

Just like the previous Dragon-Phoenix Cavern Heaven, there was no competition between cultivators—everyone had their own independent challenge system.

Throughout history, many prodigies from the Tianyuan Continent had entered various Cavern Heavens.

When they entered, the strength they displayed was recorded and branded into the Cavern Heaven.

And those imprints were used in this Projection Stage.

Projection Heaven would select three prodigies of the same realm as the entrant from countless others, and the challenger could choose one to battle.

Fail the challenge, and your cultivation drops by a small realm.

Succeed, and you’d be rewarded with a technique or secret art from the Immortal Palace.

The three projections varied in difficulty—the tougher the projection, the better the reward.

“This is actually a pretty fun Cavern Heaven.”

Lin Jing looked at the three prodigies the Cavern Heaven had generated for him. One glance—and he was immediately taken aback.

The first projection was an old acquaintance.

Although the face was a youthful one Lin Jing didn’t recognize, the name and technique certainly were.

Name: Zhang Jiuying

Realm: Late Core Formation

Physique: Innate Nether Spring Physique (when lifespan runs out, can turn into a Corpse Cultivator and live another life.)

Cultivation Technique: Nine Revolutions Core Ascension Art (can cultivate multiple dantians to hold more cores or nascent souls.)

Secret Arts: Core-Snatching Art (seizes other cultivators’ cores and fuses them into the body), Soul-Snatching Art (same, but for Nascent Souls)

....

Spells: Corpse-Nurturing Art, Ghost Household Charm...

Challenge Difficulty: Low

“The Nine-Infant Demon Lord of the Yin Corpse Sect, one of the main reasons I ended up in Qingzhou. He actually entered a Cavern Heaven too.”

“One of the four great Void Mystical cultivators of the Yin Corpse Sect—one of the three great demonic sects. And his Core Formation stage projection is rated the lowest difficulty among the three?”

“This Cavern Heaven... without Void Mystical-level potential, it’s gonna be tough.”

This guy’s techniques and secret arts were undeniably evil, but honestly, incredibly powerful.

Rumor had it, the cores and nascent souls he fused still retained the original owners’ divine abilities.

In other words, this cultivation and secret art combo could allow one to possess multiple divine abilities at once—utterly broken. Even if the match wasn’t perfect, beneath the Void Mystical stage, he was almost unbeatable.

He turned to look at the second projection.

Not an old friend—but still deeply connected.

It was a youth with bugs crawling in his eyes.

Name: Duan Zhong

Realm: Mid Core Formation

Physique: Acquired Gu God Body (can fuse multiple Gu at once and become a Gu God)

Cultivation Technique: Gu God Art (enhances Gu powers, nullifies fusion side effects, and causes host Gu to continually evolve.)

Secret Art: Gu Crafting Method (breeds Gu using various poisonous insects)...

Spell: Insect Curse Art (casts curses using bugs, killing targets from miles away)...

Challenge Difficulty: Medium

“This guy—I remember now, wasn’t he the founder of the Gu God Sect? The current Dao-Seeking Gu God in the sect was trained by him. One Gu passed through generations—he died, the Gu remained.” Lin Jing fell into thought. To have founded a great demonic sect, reached the Dao-Seeking stage, and even had the potential to ascend—this terrifying figure was only rated medium difficulty?

Also—why are they all demonic cultivators?

He looked at the third one.

A female cultivator with her face covered in white veil, her features unreadable.

Name: Luo Miao

Realm: Early Core Formation

Physique: Creation Immortal Physique (can bestow spiritual awareness to mountains, rivers, plants, and trees, turning them into her servants.)

Cultivation Technique: Creation Immortal Art (derived from her physique, allows her to absorb power from the spirits she creates.)

Secret Arts: Tree Realm Array (instantly transforms multiple ancient trees into tree demons, forming a deadly forest array), Dragon Vein Sky-Clashing Art (awakens a dragon vein into a spirit dragon to fight), Great Flooding Art (spiritualizes a small lake and floods the field), Spirit-Sealing Finger (converts nearby spiritual energy into spirits, absorbing it all for herself and leaving enemies with none)...

Challenge Difficulty: High

Lin Jing: ?

“Holy sh*t.” Seeing the last projection, Lin Jing swore—Creation Immortal Physique, one of the Ten Great Immortal Physiques!

Luo Miao...

That name sparked a memory. He remembered—it really was her.

Back in the Beast Taming Sect, Lin Jing had studied the history of the Tianyuan Continent. He knew that the Immortal Palace had once been completely wiped out by the Immortal Alliance—formed by four top sects uniting numerous forces.

At first, he didn’t know much about these top sects. After the Immortal Alliance destroyed the Immortal Palace, that part of history was erased—at least within the Ancient Kingdom, it was hard to find any record of it.

But if you left the Ancient Kingdom—like coming to Qingzhou—you could still dig some up.

Luo Miao was actually the sect master of one of those four Immortal Alliance leaders—an Innate Immortal Physique cultivator.

This woman was unbelievably powerful. She alone made the Immortal Palace deeply wary and nearly shattered its weakened Lower Realm stronghold.

It was said that the Immortal Alliance had to deploy Immortal Artifacts and alter the laws of the Tianyuan Continent, restricting plants, rocks, and trees from developing spirits—just to counter her Creation Immortal Physique. She could freely awaken hundred-thousand-year-old spiritual plants and add them to her army... she alone could form a legion.

Sadly, no matter how skinny the camel, it was still bigger than a horse—the Immortal Palace still had deep reserves. In the end, they killed her.

But some speculate that if she’d survived the war, no matter how weak the Immortal Alliance became afterward, the Ancient Kingdom would never have risen—she alone could’ve left behind a terrifying heavenly army and ruled the Tianyuan Continent for a long time.

Five thousand years ago, during the Ancient Kingdom era, another Immortal Physique emerged in the Tianyuan Continent. Unfortunately, that one vanished without a trace before reaching even the Core Formation stage. Some suspect it was wiped out early by powerful figures fearing its potential.

“Can I even win this?” Lin Jing fell into deep thought. This Creation Immortal Physique was among the strongest of the ten. Without Dragon Carp, his battle power was limited. If he lost, he’d drop back to early Core Formation. But only by choosing her could he get the best reward—and fighting an Immortal Physique was a rare opportunity...

Who to pick?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 29: Freak Physique
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 29: Freak Physique

One was the Void Mystical Demon Lord of the Yin Corpse Sect...

One was the Dao-Seeking Patriarch of the Gu God Sect...

One was the Immortal Physique leader from the Ancient Immortal Alliance...

When it came to the young Nine-Infant Demon Lord, Lin Jing was confident he could win.

Against the young Gu God Sect Patriarch, it would be a bit trickier—after all, one of Lin Jing’s current main battle strengths involved a Rare Gu.

In terms of Gu techniques, he wasn't quite on par with the opponent.

If his Ice Soul Gu were somehow suppressed during the fight, losing a bit of strength was one thing, but empowering the enemy would be far worse. A definite risk.

As for the Creation Immortal Physique... to be honest, Lin Jing couldn’t clearly judge that one’s power.

“Qii~”

Pine Leaf Mouse had already perched itself on Lin Jing’s shoulder, clenching its fists while staring at the Creation Immortal Physique, its robes fluttering in the breeze!

That Immortal Physique… looked like her storage bag was packed with good stuff.

“Even if she’s got treasure, it’s just a projection, not the real deal. You won’t be able to snatch it.” Lin Jing reminded it.

Pine Leaf Mouse reacted like it had just been struck by lightning, clutching its head in its tiny paws, clearly not the brightest.

“Immor…” At the same time, inside Lin Jing’s body, Ice Soul Gu was practically drooling. As much as it wanted to challenge an old ancestor, the rewards for fighting the Immortal Physique were clearly better.

Only the best rewards would give it hope of surpassing the Emperor Dragon Carp.

“Exactly what I was thinking. Alright then, decision made—this is a rare chance. Let’s go up against the Immortal Physique.”

“Whether we win or lose, a battle against a youthful Immortal Physique will be a valuable experience.”

“If we win, our Dao Heart will grow stronger, and the path of cultivation will be that much clearer.”

“If we lose, we’ll learn and bounce back stronger, sharpening our own path.”

Lin Jing fixed his gaze on Luo Miao, the Creation Immortal Physique. With a single thought, the other two projections dissipated, and hers became even more lifelike.

Crunch.

She looked as though she had stepped straight out of the long river of history. Her gaze swept coldly over Lin Jing and his companions. Instantly, she summoned two ancient mountains, worn by time, which rose behind her and propped up the heavens and the earth.

“The Light of Creation…” Under her hand seals, those two mountains were instantly enveloped in a silver glow. With a thunderous rumble, they transformed into two Mountain Spirits. Lin Jing and the others frowned—each one of these spirits radiated power on par with the Nascent Soul Stage, overwhelmingly terrifying.

And they weren’t just typical Nascent Soul humans—they were incarnations of nature itself, their lifeforce origin even closer to the Dao Merging Stage than that of humans.

“She actually moved two million-year-old spirit mountains into her storage space and can transform them mid-fight? And she’s only in the Early Core Formation Stage—lower than me—yet can instantly summon Nascent Soul-level Mountain Spirits…”

“The Creation Immortal Physique... is even more powerful than I imagined.”

BOOM!!!

One of the Mountain Spirits slammed its palm down like a five-fingered mountain. Lin Jing and Pine Leaf Mouse instantly felt crushing pressure. Their bodies froze up—they couldn’t dodge in time and could only stare helplessly at the giant hand descending!

“Get in.”

Facing a full realm difference, Lin Jing’s expression turned serious. His legs even bent slightly under the weight. At that critical moment, Ice Soul Gu activated its Core Formation Divine Ability—Transcendence into Sainthood!

“Stillness and motion united. The body becomes sacred!”

Sizzle.

Violent electric currents surged across Lin Jing’s body, icy energy curling around him.

His body’s strength surpassed its limits, allowing him to shatter the pressure. With a quick motion, he flung Pine Leaf Mouse off his shoulder and into the Beast Taming Space!

Boom!

The giant palm slammed into the ground, forming a huge crater and triggering a massive gust of spiritual energy that whipped into a hurricane of Spiritual Power...

Meanwhile, Lin Jing’s body flickered into an afterimage, hovering mid-air. He looked toward Luo Miao, now perched on the other Mountain Spirit’s shoulder.

“From one Longevity Physique to another—I respectfully request a lesson in the might of the Immortal Physique.”

With a wave of his hand, he summoned Pine Leaf Mouse again. This time, the little creature rode a red leaf and soared off instantly.

Now that Lin Jing had merged with Ice Soul Gu, his own strength was formidable. It was smarter to let Pine Leaf Mouse find its own opportunity.

But against the Creation Immortal Physique, even a numbers advantage meant nothing to a Beast Taming Cultivator.

Casting a side glance at the departing Pine Leaf Mouse, Luo Miao once again formed a hand seal. The spiritual energy hurricane stirred up by the Mountain Spirit’s palm was instantly transformed—molded into a wind demon that resembled a Kama-itachi.

“Ka-ka!!”

The wind demon swept past, flipping Pine Leaf Mouse mid-air. Its robes flailed wildly.

“Another one…” Lin Jing watched as she casually fused storm and spiritual energy into yet another Nascent Soul-level monster. He took a deep breath—he really couldn’t tell where her limit was.

In just ten seconds, she had already used Core Formation cultivation to summon three Nascent Soul-level beasts.

“She can’t be allowed to keep this up.”

In a flash, Lin Jing appeared near her. His golden-glowing fist exploded toward her.

Transcendence into Sainthood!

Indestructible Golden Body!

Diamond Fist!

A strike strong enough to obliterate most Demon Kings, the strongest move in Lin Jing’s body cultivation path, roared forward with unstoppable force, shaking the air with its violent energy.

But Luo Miao clearly had more up her sleeve than just monster creation and regeneration. She simply raised her hand, and a dense wall of wind pressure coiled around Lin Jing, slowing his power and speed. Then, a barrage of rock shields formed from the Mountain Spirits blocked his strike…

The Creation Immortal Art—it allowed her to borrow the power of the creatures she created!

BOOM BOOM BOOM BOOM BOOM BOOM BOOM!

The Diamond Fist shattered layer after layer of stone shields, but they kept repairing themselves just as fast. Soon, Lin Jing was completely engulfed in a giant boulder sphere, sealed in.

However, Luo Miao suddenly turned sharply—what she’d just sealed was only a phantom clone, a real-made illusion of Lin Jing!

The real Lin Jing had already looped around from another direction, launching the same attack at close range.

He was too close—she had no time to cast a spell to defend.

“Perish.” Lin Jing muttered as his fist struck her chest.

But the next moment, his expression shifted in shock.

Her technique wasn’t just about using her creations’ spells—she could inherit their traits too!

The Mountain Spirits’ terrifying defense and mass… it all seemed concentrated within her.

Not just one spirit’s strength—but both combined.

Bang.

Lin Jing was blasted backward. Luo Miao only took a tiny step back, barely harmed. Then she raised her hand again, casting yet another summoning.

An ancient tree, so old it seemed to have survived ten thousand lifetimes, roared as it opened its thousand eyes—transformed into a Tree Demon.

Hovering in the air, Lin Jing finally understood—why even the ancient behemoths descended from the Immortal Realm lost to the Immortal Alliance.

This Creation Immortal Physique... it was practically a cheat code.

And according to his knowledge, the Immortal Alliance had three such physiques back then.

“I may not have brought the Emperor Dragon Carp, but this version of the Creation Immortal Physique isn’t even her strongest form. The Cave Heaven imprint only records her power when she entered—it can’t contain the plant creatures she created in the outside world. She can only use what’s stored in her storage bag, so she’s actually pretty limited here!”

“But even with those limits… she’s still a freaking monster. No wonder she’s ranked fourth strongest physique of all time!”

“Clearly… defeating her without paying a hefty price is out of the question.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 30: Divine Scripture
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 30: Divine Scripture

Lin Jing quickly glanced over at Pine Leaf Mouse’s situation.

Caught between a Mountain Spirit and a Wind Demon, it was in rough shape—right now, all it could do was keep dodging using spatial magic.

Pine Leaf Mouse was only at the Core Formation Stage. Even if it assumed its True Form of the Demon King, it would still struggle against a Mountain Spirit—let alone with a Wind Demon in the mix.

In this kind of situation, the only attack that might still affect both of them was its Monster Core Divine Ability.

Its ability, Five-Element World, became more powerful the more elemental objects it stored inside.

And after the battle in Tongtian City, Pine Leaf Mouse’s Five-Element World had absorbed countless inheritances from Void Mystical and Nascent Soul cultivators, making its power utterly terrifying.

So, if it unleashed its Core Formation Divine Ability, Pine Leaf Mouse was confident it could go toe-to-toe with both opponents.

However, it was still just at the Core Formation Stage. Using an ability of that magnitude would place an enormous burden on it. At best, it could only use it once—then it’d be completely spent.

After all, defeating the Mountain Spirit and Wind Demon wasn’t the goal—taking down the Creation Immortal Physique was.

But using the Monster Core Divine Ability directly against her was also risky.

In cultivation battles, intel was everything.

Lin Jing had already tested the waters for Pine Leaf Mouse: Luo Miao’s defenses were ridiculously high—stronger even than the two Mountain Spirits. A direct hit from the monster core would end poorly.

Even if it broke through her defenses, the violent collision would cause massive damage to Pine Leaf Mouse’s core. That wouldn’t just knock it down a small realm—it might fall all the way back to Foundation Establishment. And that’s assuming it even managed to break her defense in the first place. Whether it could actually kill her was another question entirely.

“According to the intel, she can channel the powers of the creatures she creates through her cultivation technique—”

“—even overlaying their attributes onto herself.”

“But she’s only at Early Core Formation Stage. There has to be a limit to how much she can inherit… But honestly, that doesn’t matter, because even in her current state, we can’t lay a finger on her.”

“To beat her, we have to reduce the durability of her physical body.”

“Her current defense comes from the Mountain Spirits. If we can eliminate those, and prevent her from borrowing their traits, that would be her weakest moment.”

“But without the Emperor Dragon Carp, taking down these creatures with just Pine Leaf Mouse and me is too hard.”

“Even if we kill a batch, if she can summon more, we’re still stuck in a dead end.”

“There’s only one option left—see if I can use the Divine Control Technique to steal her summoned creatures for myself.”

“If I gain control over them and her power weakens, that’s the best chance to strike.”

“If her power doesn’t drop, it’ll still be an improvement to the current situation…”

“The worst-case scenario is if I can’t seize control at all.”

In a flash of thought, Lin Jing made his decision. He’d go all-in to challenge the Creation Immortal Physique.

At this point, there was no winning without sacrifice.

Whirr—!

As the newly summoned Nascent Soul Tree Demon lashed out with countless limbs, Lin Jing became a streak of light, weaving through the gaps in the massive trunk.

Buddhist Light Technique!

As he blinked between the gaps in space, the Core Formation cultivator appeared on the Tree Demon’s shoulder, locking eyes with the projected Creation Immortal Physique.

“Divine Control Technique.” Lin Jing slammed a palm onto the Mountain Spirit and burned through ten years of lifespan to activate his Core Formation Divine Ability. A glowing Core Mark immediately appeared on the creature’s body.

RUMBLE.

Just as the Mountain Spirit was about to swat away the "fly" on its shoulder, its raised arm suddenly froze in place.

It worked!

His Divine Control Technique had succeeded in seizing control of the creature she summoned!

At that moment, Lin Jing caught a rare flicker of emotion across the otherwise unconscious Luo Miao’s face. As his divine sense swept outward, he confirmed it—now that he controlled the Mountain Spirit, her power had actually decreased!

Just as he suspected…

“Crush her.” Lin Jing gave the order. The Mountain Spirit raised its enormous hand and slammed it down at the Creation Immortal Physique.

Luo Miao gave a light humph, leaping off the Mountain Spirit and landing atop the Nascent Soul Tree Demon. But in the next moment, a few red leaves fluttered down, and Lin Jing, holding one of those red leaves, switched places with Pine Leaf Mouse, landing on the second Mountain Spirit.

Again, he burned ten years of lifespan, slapping another Divine Control Mark onto it.

To weaken the Creation Immortal Physique, Lin Jing burned twenty years of his life back-to-back and cast his Divine Ability twice in a row. His golden core was now riddled with cracks.

“I can’t keep going like this. The outcome will be decided here and now.”

Having taken control of both Mountain Spirits, the physical toughness of the Creation Immortal Physique finally dropped to a normal range.

Lin Jing didn’t press further—while he still had lifespan to spare, his golden core was overloaded.

More importantly, he could feel Luo Miao trying to wrest back control of the two Mountain Spirits. If she succeeded, it would all be for nothing.

At this critical moment, Pine Leaf Mouse, who had swapped positions with him, launched its attack at the exact same time.

This was it!

Its eyes glowed bright red. Letting out a battle cry, it summoned its monster core from within, raising it above its head as though it were lifting the sky.

The massive core was even larger than a Mountain Spirit—like a five-colored meteor crashing from the heavens. It came hurtling down toward the Nascent Soul Tree Demon and Luo Miao in one devastating blow.

One Core to crush all.

“Aiii—!!”

As the monster core descended, countless cracks began to form across its surface. The Tree Demon’s branches were pulverized in an instant.

Though Luo Miao had no consciousness, her combat instincts screamed at her: there was no way she could withstand that hit.

BOOM!

Inside the Cave Heaven, all the projections shattered into glimmering light and vanished.

Lin Jing and Pine Leaf Mouse plummeted from the sky, landing heavily on the ground. Enduring the agonizing pain of a fractured golden core, they sat cross-legged to begin healing.

At the same time, the reward projection from the Immortal Palace finally appeared before Lin Jing—it was either a technique or secret art.

And the moment he saw it, his expression darkened even more.

“I already have a main cultivation technique. What the h*ll is this supposed to be?! I sacrificed twenty years of my life, risked my golden core shattering, and all I get is another technique?!”

Pine Leaf Mouse and Ice Soul Gu also froze for a second. What kind of garbage…

Immortal Palace High-Tier Formation Dao Technique: Divine Scripture—uses Dragon Veins as formation patterns, the world as the formation map, and the cultivator as the core. With ritual offerings, it allows one to summon the projection of a deceased cultivator who has become an object of faith and legend…

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 31: Missing Persons
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 31: Missing Persons

Outside the Immortal-Burying Volcano.

Not long after the Cavern Heaven descended, its light gradually faded.

Thunder Dragon Ancestor wasn’t too surprised—

In his opinion, even if the Holy Son hadn’t brought back the Dragon Carp, he was still the strongest among Core Formation cultivators.

Unless an Immortal Physique appeared, no one at the same stage could possibly rival him.

“Hm?”

But precisely because he believed that, he was shocked to see Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse stumbling out, both covered in injuries.

“Your Highness... how did you...”

“Use the Lotus Heart Flame to help us heal.”

“Coming!!” Not far away, the Dragon Carp saw how miserable Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse looked and instantly knew this house would fall apart without it.

It quickly floated over and sprayed healing Lotus Heart Flame, while trying to casually fish for details about what good stuff Lin Jing had picked up this time.

Last time, he’d gotten the Hourglass of Time from the Cavern Heaven—absolutely top-tier, wouldn’t even trade it for a quasi-immortal artifact. So this time had to be just as good, right?

“Don’t ask.” Lin Jing sat cross-legged, clearly a bit depressed.

What he got was the top-tier formation technique hidden by the Immortal Palace: the Divine Scripture.

It’s a powerful cultivation technique. Once mastered, it can transform a cultivator’s physique into a formation core.

When that formation core is located in a place with a Dragon Vein, it can borrow the vein’s power to summon projections of legendary figures from history—similar to the Projection Heaven technique.

But there are conditions and limitations to the summoning.

First, the figure can’t be some nobody. Even long after death, they must still have people worshiping and offering sacrifices to them. Also, the cultivator must possess a personal item the figure left behind.

Pretty strict.

And even if summoned, the projection doesn’t retain its original power. Its cultivation is limited to that of the one who summons it.

For example, if the summoner is at the Core Formation stage, even if the projection was a Void Mystical expert in life, it would still only be Core Formation level now.

Of course, since the projection was once incredibly powerful, even limited, it’d still be formidable. The Scripture notes that summoned projections possess a certain degree of consciousness!

This technique is meant for extremely specific situations within the Immortal Palace.

For instance, during a catastrophe, a Divine Scripture cultivator could summon ancestral projections to resist invaders.

Or if a technique or secret art is lost, a projection of a master who knew it could be summoned to pass it on again.

Functionally, the Divine Scripture definitely lives up to being a top-tier formation technique. Though it requires high talent, harsh conditions, and drains at least half the user’s spiritual energy for a temporary projection—that’s not the worst part.

The real issue is that one can only cultivate one main technique. Lin Jing already has the Beast Taming Art, so he can’t switch.

Jiang Gang had considered one possibility: let Lin Jing’s avatar cultivate it before reaching the Nascent Soul Stage.

But giving something this valuable to an avatar? It just doesn’t feel right for a Core Formation-level choice.

What Lin Jing really coveted was the Creation Immortal Physique, but even he knew that kind of body was purely based on chance. Even the Immortal Palace couldn’t artificially create it. A fully grown Immortal Physique could shake the entire Immortal Palace!

“...”

At this moment, hearing that all Lin Jing got was that, the Dragon Carp also looked disappointed and tried even harder to heal them. What a loss. Without this lucky koi around, things really did go wrong.

Thunder Dragon Grandpa was right—this Cavern Heaven’s opportunities really didn’t match up to its Immortal Flame.

“But this Divine Scripture isn’t exclusive to humans. That means spirit beasts can cultivate it too. So even if I don’t use it, maybe it’d be good for a pet?”

The Divine Scripture was undeniably mystical. Not cultivating it would be a waste.

“But clearly, the Pine Leaf Mouse, Dragon Carp, and Ice Soul Gu aren’t suitable.”

“A fourth pet? My Core Formation contract slot is still vacant. I haven’t finalized my fourth pet yet. If I can find a beast gifted in cultivating the Divine Scripture, it might be worth contracting.”

While soaking in the Lotus Heart Flame, Lin Jing pondered.

A few days later, after fire therapy from the Dragon Carp and ingesting several rare treasures that repaired Core Formation damage, Lin Jing gradually recovered his vitality.

His recovery was relatively quick, since the strain from using his Core Formation divine ability was shared by both the core and his lifespan.

With some lifespan spent, the burden on the core was lighter.

In comparison, the Pine Leaf Mouse had directly unleashed its core—its condition was far worse. Thankfully, its foundation was solid. Otherwise, its cultivation would’ve dropped to Early Core Formation.

“You recovered yet?”

While Lin Jing and the others were still healing, a ripple appeared in the barrier. A little girl in a dragon robe with a round, tigerish face stepped through. She glanced at the seated Lin Jing and casually asked:

“Your Majesty the Demon Emperor? I’ve mostly recovered. Is your side already done?” Lin Jing opened his eyes and stood up.

“Not yet.” The Demon Emperor waved him down. “That bald guy is demanding too much. I sensed an anomaly in the barrier earlier, and when I checked, I realized you’d been sucked into the Cavern Heaven. I just dropped by to check on you.”

Lin Jing stood up again and said, “Thank you for your concern, Your Majesty. I’m fine, no need to worry. Please don’t let me delay your affairs.”

The Demon Emperor pouted and pushed him back down again. “What affairs? Just rest.”

“Right now, Qingzhou is divided between the Tongtian Tower and the Demon Court. The Buddhist Sect wants to preach among the spirit beasts and is hoping to cooperate with us.”

“I’m currently negotiating with the Buddhist leaders. We haven’t reached an agreement yet. Still playing the game.”

“The Buddhist Sect... wants to preach to the spirit beasts?” Lin Jing frowned.

The Demon Emperor nodded. “That’s nothing new. The Buddhist Sect was never purely human. Back in the day, it included beasts, demons, and humans alike. At one point, even a demon led the sect. But over time, it became human-only.”

“Now they want to bring spirit beasts and demons back into the fold. Not surprising. The Golden Era is coming—it’s a prime chance to build a foundation.”

“Of course, whatever their motives, we can’t let them succeed so easily.” She licked her lips. “Gotta bite off a piece of the Buddhist Sect first!”

Lin Jing nodded. Stuff like that... best left for the leaders of the Demon Court and Buddhist Sect to hash out. He was too minor to weigh in anyway...

“Hm?”

Just as the Demon Emperor saw that Lin Jing was in decent shape and was about to leave, she suddenly paused, giving him a strange look.

“Kid, that turtle outside... is it yours? Why’s it been trying to squeeze through the barrier this whole time?”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 32: Fourth Pet Confirmed
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 32: Fourth Pet Confirmed

“Turtle?” Lin Jing froze. What turtle?

“Although this is the outermost area of the Immortal-Burying Volcano, in its current condition, it won’t hold out much longer. If it keeps going, it might get burned to ashes.” The Demon Emperor waved her small hand and pulled a plain-looking brown turtle into the barrier.

Seeing this turtle, Lin Jing nearly choked—wait a second, a Mechanized Turtle?!

How the h*ll did this thing end up in the Western Desert?

Upon seeing its creator, the Mechanized Turtle calmly spoke:

“Master, I got lost.”

“Finally found you.”

Lin Jing fell into deep thought. Got lost? You got lost all the way from Qingzhou to the Western Desert? Seriously?

Before Lin Jing could speak, the Longevity Turtle automatically pulled out a longevity ginseng from its storage space and offered it up:

“It only took ten years, but I successfully found a treasure that extends life by twenty years.”

It lifted its head proudly.

“You’re quite the turtle, aren’t you?”

Seeing this turtle bring Lin Jing—a Longevity Body cultivator—a ginseng that extended life by twenty years, the Demon Emperor chuckled in amazement.

Although to her, the ginseng was utterly useless for Lin Jing, for ordinary cultivator families, having such a Mechanized Turtle was pretty impressive.

“This is a Longevity Turtle designed by the Divine Mechanism Manor of the Ancient Kingdom. It really is remarkable.”

Lin Jing glanced at the Mechanized Turtle, clearly surprised.

Now this was fate!

He had just burned twenty years of lifespan using his Core Formation divine ability in the Cavern Heaven, and this turtle shows up with the exact thing to replenish it?

Back when he exited seclusion and couldn’t find it in Qingzhou, Lin Jing had feared the worst.

But now, it looked like this guy might actually be useful.

Plus, they had great affinity.

It got lost, yet still managed to end up wandering into the Western Desert to find him.

And it just happened to bring the tonic he needed.

If this wasn’t fate, Lin Jing didn’t know what was.

“Hold up.” Staring at the Longevity Turtle, Lin Jing’s eyes narrowed. Could he let it cultivate the Divine Scripture?

He had skimmed the Divine Scripture inside the Cavern Heaven. As a formation-type cultivation method, it was very different from typical techniques.

It required carving intricate formation patterns into the body using spiritual energy, transforming oneself into a formation core.

And Mechanized Beasts—every single one of them—were essentially natural formation cores, since all of their actions were powered by internal formation patterns.

Many mechanists even used Mechanized Beasts as formation cores to stabilize grand formations.

In other words, as long as the Mechanized Turtle had gained consciousness, it had an incredibly high compatibility with this formation-type technique.

Later, he could even tweak it—besides its innate ability to seek out longevity treasures, he could give it a grave-robbing specialty for ancient relics. That way, it could wander around growing stronger, while also having the ability to summon ancient powerhouses for protection.

In fact, the biggest perk of having a Divine Scripture contract beast wasn’t summoning projections to fight.
