It was being able to ask them for cultivation guidance.

Like, if the Dragon Carp wanted to improve its alchemy skills, it could track down an ancient Pill Ancestor’s relic, summon the projection, and learn from it.

Even the Demon Emperor couldn’t teach the Dragon Carp anything about alchemy. The most she could do was help it strengthen its flames to raise its pill-making level.

This just showed how valuable a good teacher was.

So if ancient experts could lead the way, the path of cultivation would definitely be smoother.

But the biggest obstacle right now was that he hadn’t yet upgraded the Beast Taming Art, so he couldn’t form a contract with the Mechanized Turtle.

Plus, the turtle still had a tendency to get lost, showing its current state was unstable.

To contract it, Lin Jing would have to first refine both the Beast Taming Art and his mechanized techniques.

“Your Majesty the Demon Emperor, may I send an avatar out next?”

“My true body will remain here, assisting the Dragon Carp in mastering the Immortal Flame.”

“What for?” asked the Demon Emperor.

“The Buddhist Sect. The Bodhi Tree. I plan to use the technique I obtained in the Cavern Heaven and this Mechanized Turtle to refine my Beast Taming Art.”

“But before I do that, I want to enhance my comprehension to save time.”

“There’s a temple in the Buddhist Sect called Bodhi.”

“That Bodhi Temple has something known as the Bodhi Tree.”

Bodhi Temple in Bodhi City—one of the Eighty-One Temples of the Buddhist Sect—had a grove of rare plants: Bodhi Trees.

Yes, the same Bodhi Trees found in the Bodhi Cavern Heaven Lin Jing once entered.

But within the Bodhi Cavern Heaven, there were three trees—gold, silver, and green—each offering lesser enlightenment.

In Bodhi Temple, they only had green ones, the lowest grade. Even so, it was still one of the top enlightenment treasures in the entire Tianyuan Continent.

Aside from the Spirit Dao Sect’s rare Insight Fish, there was nothing better than the green Bodhi Tree for enlightenment—not even the buffs from Hidden Dragon Secret Arts could match it.

Maybe now there was something better on the Fortune Gold List,

But regardless, even a green Bodhi Tree was something Lin Jing desperately wanted.

And once the Pine Leaf Mouse’s cultivation was high enough, it might be able to try fusing the Bodhi Tree into something new.

Lin Jing cultivated slowly. Anything that could speed up cultivation was worth collecting—more was always better.

“Alright, go ahead.” The Demon Emperor nodded.

She didn’t bother offering to fetch it from the Buddhist Sect for him.

Inside the Demon Court, Lin Jing could request any resources freely.

But for outside resources—if Lin Jing was confident he could get them himself, she’d let him.

Likewise, she never asked what fortuitous encounters Lin Jing got in the Cavern Heaven, nor did she interfere with what pet he contracted.

As the Demon Emperor, her only job was to supervise Lin Jing’s alchemy and ensure he didn’t die young.

Everyone had their own path to cultivate.

And someone at the Dao-Seeking Stage like her knew clearly: if a cultivator gained a high level through endless resources without real experience, it would be difficult to forge their own Dao.

Not long after, the Demon Emperor left again.

Lin Jing, meanwhile, didn’t consume the longevity ginseng the turtle brought back.

For someone with a Longevity Body, twenty years of life was nothing. Once he reached the Nascent Soul Stage, his lifespan would skyrocket—no need to care about that small loss.

He tossed the ginseng to the Pine Leaf Mouse instead. “Use those twenty years of lifespan to make two avatars. From now on, our real body will stay here cultivating, and the avatars will go handle outside affairs.”

“Sigh...” The Pine Leaf Mouse received the ginseng under the gloomy gaze of the Longevity Turtle, feeling like it was being watched by something dirty.

“Hidden Dragon Secret Art is a secret technique, and the Bodhi Tree is a resource. Next, I’ll climb the Fortune Gold List and get the Golden Era bonus to max out my comprehension. I refuse to believe I can’t refine the Beast Taming Art.” Lin Jing called over the Dragon Carp.

“Lil’ Carp, come here. Stop cultivating for a bit. Let me try something—if beating you up lets me glitch my way onto the Gold List, I won’t have to challenge the other prodigies.”

Dragon Carp: ????

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 33: Root of Chaos
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 33: Root of Chaos

Fortune Gold List.

Forged with the supreme treasure Golden Bell by the Second Ancient Emperor, refined a second time with the True Dragon Secret Art and Heavenly Fate Secret Art.

This list automatically senses the battles of prodigies within a realm who are still at the golden age for cultivation, ranking them accordingly—those at the top receiving greater favor from the world’s fortune.

As long as a prodigy engages in battle in observable regions of the Tianyuan Continent, and no overwhelming powerhouse interferes to shield their presence, the Fortune Gold List can usually detect it.

There are rare exceptions—like when a prodigy enters an independent space such as a Cavern Heaven.

Still, the list is ultimately a man-made treasure. Although partially bound to the realm’s fortune, the Second Ancient Emperor didn’t account for every scenario during its forging.

And so, beyond normal operations, the Fortune Gold List contains a dormant Artifact Spirit responsible for adjusting the rankings.

“What’s going on?”

The ranking mechanism malfunctioned, losing its ability to auto-sort.

“Let me see.”

The golden, dragon-shaped artifact spirit scanned the issue and found the problem was with current rank twelve—Dragon Carp, who had defeated the Buddhist Sect’s Holy Son!

“Huh.”

Based on fortune patterns and color readings, Dragon Carp was from the Demon Court and had signed a contract with another, recognized by the realm’s laws as a unified existence.

However, Dragon Carp’s first entry onto the list had been through a solo battle, so the list registered it as the main entity.

Now, the artifact spirit sensed the Beast Tamer—Lin Jing—was using his contract power to suppress Dragon Carp’s strength... then beating it up!

So... the list glitched—

Should it keep Dragon Carp’s spot and also add Lin Jing to the rankings?

Or replace Dragon Carp’s name with Lin Jing’s?

That, the Second Ancient Emperor had never considered.

“I see...” The artifact spirit fell into deep thought.

“If that fish could defeat the Buddhist Sect’s Holy Son on its own, that’s its own capability...”

“My purpose is to nurture Tianyuan’s prodigies. Those with greater power should receive greater fortune and cultivation boosts. If their rankings are merged, only one party benefits—that’s unfair to the other.”

“Alright, let this Beast Tamer take the spot. Dragon Carp drops one rank. Just an exception—it shouldn’t disrupt the list. No need to report to the Ancient Emperor for something this minor.”

After careful thought, the artifact spirit acknowledged Lin Jing’s defeat of Dragon Carp as valid.

It believed that if a prodigy couldn’t even beat a contracted pet fighting without assistance, they didn’t deserve a higher spot. Fortune should go to those with true talent and potential.

Meanwhile, outside the Immortal-Burying Mountain.

Dragon Carp was miserable.

There was no doubt—it was stronger than Lin Jing. After all, it had reached the Nascent Soul Stage, while Lin Jing was still at Core Formation.

But the problem was the Beast Taming Contract. Without it, Lin Jing could never suppress its strength.

In theory, if the Beast Tamer was only one minor stage above, like Foundation vs. Core, he might barely keep up. But Lin Jing was still just Core Formation, yet had built his foundation with eighteen layers of Qi Refinement and even cultivated a Beast Taming Physique. The contract’s suppressive effect far surpassed average tamers.

So Dragon Carp suffered an extremely irregular defeat at the hands of a Core Formation cultivator.

“So... it actually worked—” Lin Jing looked toward the Fortune Gold List and saw no changes.

Beating your own ranked pet... was it not a valid method to enter the list? Not even by overriding their spot?

Just as he was about to give up and attempt proper entry through a traditional challenge, the sky shimmered faintly with golden light—the Fortune Gold List finally shifted.

Across Tianyuan, many cultivators noticed the ripple—

Indicating a change in the rankings.

Countless cultivators focused in, trying to see which prodigy had advanced again.

Then, collective silence.

Fortune Gold List, Rank 12: Lin Jing. Cultivation: Mid Core Formation. Reason for entry: defeated his own contracted pet, Dragon Carp…

Fortune Gold List, Rank 13: Dragon Carp. Cultivation: Early Nascent Soul…

Cultivators across the continent: ???

“Huh??”

Not just the cultivators—Lin Jing himself was stunned. It actually worked? But why did the list respond so slowly this time?

Was its processing power running low?

And now, this scene cleared up all previous doubts—

So this Dragon Carp really was the Demon Court Holy Son’s fire-type rare beast!

The Buddhist Sect’s Holy Son had actually been solo-killed by Lin Jing’s pet!

“What the h*ll is wrong with this list? A Beast Tamer and their pet can be ranked separately? Aren’t they supposed to be treated as one? And doesn’t a Beast Tamer beating their own pet rely on the contract’s suppression? That kind of targeted force counts as strength? If the pet counts as part of their strength, then why can the pet appear on the list independently?”

“What kind of move was that, Demon Court Holy Son?”

Countless prodigies were on the verge of mental collapse.

Nearly all of them had dropped one spot because of Lin Jing’s sudden insertion.

Especially the Core Formation Perfection prodigy who got bumped out of the top 100—he outright exploded in anger.

Ancient Kingdom.

Inside the royal court.

Soon, the Second Ancient Emperor received reports from cultivators questioning whether it was appropriate for a Beast Tamer and their pet to be on the list simultaneously.

“This was the artifact spirit’s decision—nothing wrong with it.”

The emperor made no move to alter the list’s rules, tacitly approving the artifact spirit’s ranking adjustment.

After court, the Second Ancient Emperor shook his head. It wasn’t favoritism toward Lin Jing.

The Fortune Gold List had already been forged and was partially bound to fate. Changing its rules now would be difficult.

Besides, his true body was outside the realm, maintaining the boundary wall. His current avatar’s power was limited. As long as the list was functioning, he saw no need to intervene over such a minor hiccup.

More importantly, if he did interfere, the Demon Court’s tigeress—that Demon Emperor—definitely wouldn’t let it slide.

So, best leave it be.

He looked toward the top of the list—first and second place were a prince and princess of the Ancient Clan. As long as the core rankings remained unaffected, there was no harm.

“A rare beast, huh. To defeat a Solar Physique prodigy of the Buddhist Sect while still at Early Nascent Soul... its innate divine ability must be terrifying. What a pity, what a shame.” The emperor shook his head. Fate had its own plans—this Longevity Body was destined to have no ties to the Ancient Kingdom.

Still, he only felt slightly regretful.

Even if Lin Jing had stayed in the Ancient Kingdom, the emperor wouldn’t have focused all efforts on grooming the Longevity Body or even the rare beast Dragon Carp.

Compared to Lin Jing, he cared more about the Ancient Clan’s prodigies—his own descendants.

He took out a secret technique.

True Dragon Secret Art: Physique Chapter

Requires immense world fortune. Must be cultivated alongside the Boundary-Breaking Bird.

It can shatter the limit of divine physiques, evolving superior bodies into Immortal Physiques.

“With the Golden Era here, the realm’s fortune has surged. The Immortal Physique chapter of the True Dragon Secret Art left by the previous emperor can finally be cultivated. As long as the prince and princess can unite the Ancient Clan’s and Tianyuan’s strength to complete the Acquired Immortal Physique, then neither demons nor rare beasts will be able to shake the foundation of the Ancient Kingdom…”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 34: Bodhi Leaf
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 34: Bodhi Leaf

While the entire cultivation world was still debating the Fortune Gold List and the Beast Taming Path, Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse had already begun their next major project.

Plotting for the Buddhist Sect’s Bodhi Tree!

By then, secret techniques, rare trees, and the Gold List would form a trinity. Whatever Lin Jing studied would be like having divine assistance.

At the moment, although the Pine Leaf Mouse and the Ice Soul Gu also wanted to follow Lin Jing’s example and get on the Fortune Gold List by beating up the Dragon Carp, unfortunately, the two of them couldn’t win against the Dragon Carp at all.

The Dragon Carp may be meek and submissive when facing a Beast Tamer, but against fellow beasts, it definitely wouldn’t hold back its heavy punches.

So, the Pine Leaf Mouse and Ice Soul Gu could only settle for second best. Under Lin Jing’s arrangement, they would first work on improving their strength.

As for the Ice Soul Gu, it was deeply shaken after the battle in the Cavern Heaven and began to doubt its life.

Even when it used its divine ability to support Lin Jing, it couldn’t even break the defenses of the Creation Immortal Physique and needed Lin Jing to burn his lifespan to create an opening...

One could say that many Void Mystical cultivators treat a Gu like this as a treasure, but with Lin Jing, it was getting the short end of the stick everywhere.

Still, you couldn’t really blame the Ice Soul Gu. Historically speaking, the Creation Immortal Physique isn’t just beyond the level of any Rare Gu—it’s probably stronger than the entire Gu God Sect combined...

To strengthen the Ice Soul Gu, having it devour the Golden Light Gu’s essence would be the fastest path. Lin Jing originally planned to wait until they returned to Qingzhou and had a stable cultivation environment before letting it do so. But seeing as they’d have to stay in the Western Desert for a while, Lin Jing just sent it into the Hourglass of Time to start devouring the Golden Light Gu’s essence early.

As for the Pine Leaf Mouse, if it wanted to improve its strength, it didn’t really need to do anything special—leveling up its cultivation realm was the key. As long as it reached the Nascent Soul Stage, the cost-free Core Formation Divine Ability would be its biggest trump card!

And to raise its cultivation level, there were two shortcuts. One was taking the cultivation Core Pills refined by the Dragon Carp—but Lin Jing himself didn’t even have enough of those yet, so there was no spare to share with the mouse.

So the second shortcut, the old method of improving its cultivation through advancing techniques, became the fastest path. After all, within the Demon Court, it had access to unlimited spiritual plants to consume. The only hurdle in this shortcut was that either Senior Pine or the Pine Leaf Mouse itself needed to deduce the next layer of the Everlasting Emperor’s Longevity Art.

No one knew how far Senior Pine had gotten. But even if he succeeded, deducing one more layer would likely be the limit. The rest of the path might have to be walked by the Pine Leaf Mouse alone.

Which made the Bodhi Tree even more crucial—it could assist the Pine Leaf Mouse in deducing follow-up techniques.

Soon after, the Pine Leaf Mouse managed to create the clone Lin Jing asked for.

It consumed twenty years of lifespan to make this plant-based illusionary body, and it was far stronger than the two clones used when heading to the Sky Lion Clan. As long as it didn’t get killed, it should last a long time.

Other than being used to retrieve the Bodhi Tree in place of the real body, this clone could be handy in many future scenarios.

“The clone’s just there to run errands, so it won’t waste the real body’s time and energy. But if we send only the clone, we won’t get the Bodhi Tree,” Lin Jing murmured as he looked at the clone.

A thing like the Bodhi Tree, aside from the Spirit Dao Sect’s rare Insight Fish, was already the most powerful known comprehension resource. And since it was hard to cultivate, it was also extremely precious to the entire Buddhist Sect.

Even the Buddhist prodigies couldn’t say they’d get an entire Bodhi Tree—at most, they’d be granted the chance to cultivate at Bodhi Temple.

In that situation, for someone like the Leaf Mouse to get a Bodhi Tree, it might actually be easier than getting the Golden Light Gu from Golden Light Temple.

But since the Leaf Mouse had taken an interest in the Bodhi Tree, there was, naturally, a plan in place...

“You remember if there’s still some Bodhi Leaves in his Monster Core Space?”

The Leaf Mouse turned toward the utterly pale, sweat-drenched, and panting Lin Jing Mouse who had just finished creating the clone.

Lin Jing Mouse was already exhausted. Hearing what the Leaf Mouse said now nearly gave him a heart attack, his pupils contracting.

“Ugh...” Lin Jing Mouse sighed wearily. You wanna mess with that now?

Bodhi Leaves, leaves from the Bodhi Tree... While the Buddhist Sect wouldn’t give away full Bodhi Trees, naturally fallen leaves were allowed to circulate.

These Bodhi Leaves also had a minor effect of boosting comprehension, but compared to Bodhi Fruit or meditating beneath the tree, the difference was like heaven and earth. Some Academy teachers even said it wasn’t as good as the boost from the Hidden Dragon Secret Technique.

Still, as a rare resource for enlightenment, even with moderate effects, it was quite valuable. Bodhi Leaves were one of the main items the Buddhist Sect traded externally.

Among the spoils from Tongtian City, there happened to be some Bodhi Leaves.

And those Bodhi Leaves were one of the Pine Leaf Mouse’s favorite hidden treasures, stored deep within its space.

“No leaves, no tree.”

The green Bodhi Tree itself was a fifth-tier rare plant. The Bodhi Fruit was fourth-tier, and the leaves were third-tier.

In other words, with the Pine Leaf Mouse’s current Immortal Eyes, it had the means to synthesize the Bodhi Leaves.

“The Buddhist Sect has been researching how to cultivate the green Bodhi Tree into the golden or silver Bodhi Tree of the Bodhi Heaven, but they’ve never succeeded.”

“Your rare beast divine ability, in some ways, can mutate Bodhi Leaves. As long as the mutation effect is good, we can use that as a reason to pretend we’re cultivation masters and negotiate with the Buddhist Sect to help them grow their Bodhi Trees!”

“With the precedent of the Mutated Transformation Grass, plus the Beast Taming Sect possessing an extremely rare Grass-Type Void Mystical Beast, and finally, using the mutated Bodhi Leaves we produce as our bargaining chip—even if we can’t get them to give us a Bodhi Tree outright for research, as long as we sign a contract to share results in the future, borrowing it for a hundred and eighty years shouldn't be a problem.”

That was Lin Jing’s plan to get the Bodhi Tree. Whether it succeeded would largely depend on what kind of mutated Bodhi Leaf the Pine Leaf Mouse could create.

“Ugh...” The Pine Leaf Mouse thought for a moment, then nodded painfully. Fine...

There weren’t many Bodhi Leaves in the space to begin with. After fusion, there’d be even fewer. But if they could get a Bodhi Tree in return, and a terrifying number of leaves, the trade-off was totally worth it—the Pine Leaf Mouse could do that math.

With that, it took out all the Bodhi Leaves, laid them out on the ground, and activated its divine ability!

Then... failure.

The Pine Leaf Mouse collapsed weakly and fell asleep.

“Mm... I just barely recovered from my injuries, then spent lifespan to make a clone... better sleep a bit before trying again...”

The next day.

Just as Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse were hoping to fuse the green Bodhi Leaves into at least second-tier silver Bodhi Leaves like those in the Bodhi Heaven, the many green leaves scanned by the Glazed Immortal Eyes... turned into a single ink-black leaf.

Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse fell into deep thought, a bad premonition rising. Please don’t turn into a Malignant Plant... We’re still hoping to use this to get the Buddhist Sect’s tree...

They looked over.

[Mutated Bodhi Leaf: After ingestion, for one year, comprehension of cultivation techniques and spells not aligned with one’s nature will greatly improve... But for techniques that are aligned with one’s nature, progress will become increasingly difficult—ranging from stagnation to even Qi deviation...]

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 35: Primordial Sword Intent
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 35: Primordial Sword Intent

This mutated Bodhi Leaf...

Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse scratched their heads.

“This mutation result… surprisingly normal?”

The two of them looked at each other in confusion.

“The less compatible the technique, the faster the learning... the more compatible, the slower the comprehension.”

“Though it’s a bit of an odd path, it’s undeniably useful.”

“Sigh!”

The Pine Leaf Mouse nodded heavily.

It was truly an unconventional enlightenment-type rare plant.

“For normal cultivators, it might not be very useful, but in certain situations, this thing is a divine treasure!”

Normally, cultivators wouldn’t train in arts that were incompatible with them.

A fire spirit body cultivator would have to be completely bored to practice water arts.

Even if aided by Bodhi Leaves and could barely master water techniques, as a fire spirit body, using water arts would be pointless. The time would be better spent refining fire techniques.

After all, even with compatible techniques, few people reach mastery. Diverting energy to incompatible arts would be a waste of effort.

But that’s just under normal conditions. Say a fire spirit body cultivator had one Nascent Soul-level fire technique and one Nascent Soul-level water technique. Even if he had unlimited mutated Bodhi Leaves making it effortless to cultivate water arts, he’d still never choose them.

But—what if he stumbled upon an immortal-level water technique? Could he still call it incompatible with his fire spirit body?

That wouldn’t be incompatibility. That would be the fire spirit body being unworthy of the noble immortal art.

He’d absolutely try everything to learn it.

In such cases, even if he had to give up the fire body’s boosts to fire arts, he’d still choose the immortal technique.

And in this context, Bodhi Leaves became incredibly useful.

At its core, the Bodhi Leaf wasn’t made to enhance one’s usual techniques. It existed for cultivators who acquired top-tier techniques but, for one reason or another, couldn’t cultivate them properly.

These techniques—even just reaching entry level—could bring massive benefits to the cultivator.

Lin Jing glanced at the Pine Leaf Mouse and said, “You be the guinea pig and let’s test the effect.”

Pine Leaf Mouse: ???

“Squeak!!!” The Pine Leaf Mouse wobbled on its feet, looking utterly weak and exhausted.

Though this didn’t look like a Malignant Plant, it still didn’t want to be the first to try.

“Fine...” The Pine Leaf Mouse really did look a bit too spent, so Lin Jing shook his head and started looking for a new test subject. Around here… his gaze turned beyond the barrier—only the Thunder Dragon Ancestor and those nine Thunder Dragon prodigies seemed suitable.

Cui Yan pondered for a moment, then vanished in a flash, descending inside the barrier.

“Your Highness, why have you come? Are you heading out?” The Thunder Dragon Ancestor hurried forward, dispersing the group of Thunder Dragon prodigies.

“Senior, is there a particular technique you’ve always admired but found yourself completely untalented in?” Lin Jing asked.

The Thunder Dragon Ancestor paused, not quite sure why His Highness was suddenly asking this. After a moment’s thought, he sighed and said, “There is!”

“Your Highness may not know, but when I traveled the continent in my youth, I befriended a human sword cultivator. His sword intent was incredibly domineering—it could easily pierce my dragon scales. I was once haunted by the shadow of his sword intent.”

“He was a good man and offered to teach me the Way of the Sword, but limited by my race, I couldn’t wield such a human-forged weapon well. Even the most basic sword techniques felt clumsy in my hands. I had no talent for it, so I eventually gave up.”

“Maybe… if I could fully transform into a demon race being, into human form, I’d have a chance.”

Lin Jing verified, “Do you remember your friend’s name?”

As the strongest of the Thunder Dragon Clan, anyone who bested him in his youth couldn’t have been some nobody.

The Thunder Dragon Ancestor smiled. “Your Highness surely wouldn’t know him. He trained deep in the mountains, never stepped into the world. A true sword maniac.”

“He only cared about swordsmanship. He never cared about advancing cultivation or using his sword to seize resources. He was an odd one.”

“Being human, his life was short. He’s been gone for many years now.”

“Sometimes I think—if only he had been a demon. He still wouldn’t have lived forever, but he wouldn’t have turned to dust so soon.”

“But Your Highness probably doesn’t have such worries. With the flow of time, even ten thousand years later, your elegance will remain.” The Thunder Dragon Ancestor didn’t forget to offer a flattering remark.

“Alright, alright.” Lin Jing cut him off.

“Senior, try this.” With that, he handed over the mutated Bodhi Leaf and explained its effects.

The Thunder Dragon Ancestor’s expression grew strange. Such an odd item?

He’d had tea brewed from Bodhi Leaves before, but he never imagined a mutated one could do this.

“I’ll give it a try.” It was just a third-tier Bodhi Leaf. The Thunder Dragon Ancestor didn’t worry about side effects. He absorbed it into his body, then shed his illusory form and returned to his true body, circling in the air. A medium-grade spiritual sword appeared in his massive dragon claw.

“It’s been so long since I took this sword out,” the Thunder Dragon Ancestor said sentimentally. Then he closed his eyes, recalling memories of his youth.

Time flowed like a river, and his mind drifted back to the days shortly after he had formed his demon core. A brief chapter of the past.

[Thunder Dragon, want to try sword techniques? They’re fun.]

[No. I’m a beast. What use have I for swords.]

[What if you someday transform into a demon? Many ancient demons use swords too, don’t they?]

[Then I’ll learn after transforming.]

[Who knows how long that’ll take? I might be dead by then. Just try it. This is the sword intent I created—Primordial Sword Intent. It can merge with one’s Dao and transform into any sword intent…

Primordial Sword Intent…

For all the Thunder Dragon Ancestor’s life, he had never managed to grasp this “Primordial Sword Intent” his human friend left behind. He hadn’t even tried. After the friend died, he picked up the man’s sword, wanting to study it—but in the end, realized he truly lacked the talent.

“If only I had let him teach me more while he was alive.”

But today, as he recalled those events, the memories felt clearer than ever. He could even feel the sting of that sword intent lingering in his body.

Unknowingly, he became fully immersed in that pure relationship.

Meanwhile, the nine Thunder Dragon prodigies nearby suddenly turned, startled by what they saw in the distant sky—a divine sword, formed of golden lightning, breaking through the thunderclouds.

The sharp sword intent mingled with devastating lightning. Just seeing it, their scales were pierced with pain.

“What is that? Ancestor’s spell? Does our Thunder Dragon Clan even have a technique like this?” They were baffled. They had never seen or heard of such a thing.

In a flash, the lightning sword dissolved into an ethereal sword intent and merged with the Thunder Dragon Ancestor’s medium-grade spiritual sword—once just a barely preserved artifact, now radiating with unprecedented brilliance.

“This is… the Primordial Sword Intent?”

The Thunder Dragon Ancestor opened his eyes, stunned. He didn’t even know how he’d just learned it. But now that he had, he truly understood the talent his friend once had. If that man had chosen to enter the world and improve his cultivation, he’d be no worse than himself today.

“Sigh…”

“Thank you, Your Highness, for this leaf.” At this moment, the Thunder Dragon Ancestor was deeply moved. Though the sword intent hadn’t made him stronger yet, mastering it brought closure to that old friendship. He felt a newfound completeness in his heart—something that would surely help him in future Dao-Seeking breakthroughs.

Meanwhile, Lin Jing was watching the ancestor in contemplation, surprised he’d comprehended it so quickly.

“It’s nothing. The leaf was just a trigger. Senior, you must have studied this sword intent a lot. That’s why you had today’s moment of insight.” Lin Jing believed that was the real reason.

The Thunder Dragon Ancestor laughed heartily.

“What a shame. Such a powerful sword intent, and my old friend left no successor. Your Highness, would you be interested in learning this Primordial Sword Intent? Its greatest feature, like a Core Formation Divine Ability, is that it merges with the cultivator’s own strength to become a personalized sword intent!”

“Me?” Lin Jing thought for a moment, then shook his head with a smile. “Forget it. I already have too many techniques to manage. Why not teach it to the Ice Soul Gu? If it learns it, it’s as good as me learning it.”

Thanks to the fusion properties of Gu insects, he could use the Ice Soul Gu’s techniques.

Combining that with the Beast Taming Sect’s Fusion Secret Art, Lin Jing could even bring out over 100% of the Ice Soul Gu’s spell power—an advantage not possible when fusing with the Dragon Carp.

“Huh?” The Thunder Dragon Ancestor was puzzled. Teach it to the Ice Soul Gu? What the h*ll—that thing’s a spider! How’s a spider supposed to comprehend sword techniques?

He imagined a spider floating in mid-air, eight legs each wielding a sword.

Or maybe a tiny spider standing on a surface, countless spiderwebs tying up a dozen swords…

No matter how he imagined it, it didn’t seem compatible.

“If compatibility were a requirement, it couldn’t even cultivate.” Lin Jing muttered. This leaf… it might just win over the Buddhist Sect!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 36: The Fall of the Demon Court
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 36: The Fall of the Demon Court

Not long after, Lin Jing's avatars headed toward Bodhi Temple.

Their departure was unusually grand.

Though not as imposing as the Nine Dragons Carriage, flying on a tree was still incredibly rare.

A pine tree stretched across the clouds, with Lin Jing sitting cross-legged atop it, while the Pine Leaf Mouse on his shoulder controlled everything.

“The Holy Son of the Demon Court...

“He’s actually still in the Western Desert!”

“He’s already obtained the Golden Light Gu. Is there still something he needs here? Where is he going?”

Lin Jing’s appearance drew the attention of most Western Desert cultivators. These days, with the Dragon Carp having soloed the Holy Son of the Solar Temple, Lin Jing’s reputation had once again surged.

“That direction… it’s Bodhi Temple...”

Finally, as he neared his destination, the Western Desert cultivators confirmed Lin Jing’s endpoint.

“I wonder, Your Highness, what business brings you to Bodhi Temple?” Lin Jing had just entered the city when a serene voice echoed in his ears from the void.

Lin Jing smiled and said, “Naturally, I have important matters to discuss.”

“Please.” The abbot of Bodhi Temple extended an invitation.

A spatial portal immediately opened in front of the avatar.

Inside the temple, in a courtyard.

On the other end of the portal, a stone table and tea had already been set. An old monk sat across from Lin Jing and asked, “May I ask, Your Highness, what important matters do you wish to discuss with Bodhi Temple?”

Lin Jing got straight to the point and produced a mutated Bodhi Leaf. “I’ve heard Bodhi Temple has long sought to cultivate the Bodhi Tree into a golden-silver Bodhi Tree within the Bodhi Heaven but has yet to succeed. Though I am unworthy, I’ve been inside the Bodhi Heaven and have studied the Bodhi Tree to some extent. I’ve also performed modifications on the Bodhi Leaf, and the results were excellent.”

“However, lacking a complete Bodhi Tree, the cultivation results weren’t optimal. I’ve come here today to propose cooperation — let the Demon Court assist the Buddhist Sect in modifying and cultivating the Bodhi Tree!”

The old monk nodded and asked, “And how does Your Highness propose we cooperate?”

Lin Jing smiled. “Bodhi Temple would need to provide some Bodhi Trees to be transplanted to Qingzhou for research by the Demon Court.”

The old monk shook his head. “Your Highness, the Bodhi Tree is a sacred treasure of the Buddhist Sect and cannot leave our grounds. However, if you are sincere about cooperation, you may stay in Bodhi Temple for an extended period.”

“No can do. Only Qingzhou’s environment is suitable for plant modification. And I’m not asking the Buddhist Sect to donate anything — at most, in a hundred years, whether successful or not, I’ll return the Bodhi Trees intact.”

“Senior, there’s no need to reject me so quickly. Why not first take a look at my modified Bodhi Leaf results?” Lin Jing offered the Bodhi Leaf and spoke calmly.

The abbot of Bodhi Temple fell into deep thought. “Regardless of the effectiveness of this Bodhi Leaf, the decision concerning the Bodhi Tree is not mine alone. If Your Highness doesn’t mind, you may stay here a few days while I consult with the other Buddhist powerhouses.”

Lin Jing waved his hand. “I’m pressed for time. I don’t believe a high monk of the Void Mystical Stage can’t make a decision about a single tree.”

“You said it yourself — once I’ve seen the results of your Bodhi Leaf modification, I’ll know what decision to make.”

“This Bodhi Leaf here...”

At Bodhi Temple, Thunder Dragon began negotiating with the abbot. When he explained the new effects of the Bodhi Leaf, the old monk indeed revealed a look of disbelief.

What the Buddhist Sect had been able to draw out from the Bodhi Leaf was extremely limited — and just with one leaf, Thunder Dragon could produce such an improved cultivation effect?

How on earth did he do it?

.....

“Looks like things are progressing smoothly on the avatar’s end.”

“Then let’s wait and see what good news comes." While the avatar negotiated at Bodhi Temple, Lin Jing’s main body also held a piece of the mutated Bodhi Leaf.

Since he still hadn’t managed to comprehend the Heavenly Demon Physique after all this time, perhaps he simply wasn’t compatible with this secret art. That being the case, maybe this Bodhi Leaf could help...

The appearance of this leaf was an unexpected blessing, with effects entirely different from the Fortune Gold List, Hidden Dragon Secret Art, or the orthodox Bodhi Tree.

So, the Dragon Carp began refining Immortal Flame.

The Ice Soul Gu devoured the Golden Light Gu.

Lin Jing cultivated the Heavenly Demon Physique.

As for the Pine Leaf Mouse, it obediently synthesized more mutated Bodhi Leaves for Lin Jing’s cultivation.

A large number of Bodhi Leaves were to be purchased from the Buddhist City by the Thunder Dragon prodigies.

However, a few days later, the Thunder Dragon Ancestor suddenly interrupted their cultivation with a loud shout.

“Your Highness, something terrible has happened! Quick, follow me and escape!”

The Thunder Dragon Ancestor arrived in a panic, his face anxious, leaving Lin Jing utterly confused mid-cultivation.

But in the next moment, Lin Jing’s expression also changed — he sensed that the two avatars he had sent out had been destroyed.

“What happened?” Lin Jing asked.

“A powerful being from the Demonic Sky Realm used some unknown method to easily tear through the boundary wall maintained by many Dao-Seeking cultivators of Tianyuan!”

“Forget ten years — it didn’t even hold for one.”

“Her Majesty the Demon Emperor, together with several Dao-Seeking cultivators, fought to repel the invasion. But multiple factions, fearing the rise of the Demon Court, struck Her Majesty from the shadows during the battle. Though the initial wave of invaders was barely repelled, Her Majesty suffered serious injuries and went missing. Before disappearing, she used a secret art to send me a message — to take you and flee the Western Desert, and beware the Buddhist Sect.”

“The Demon Court... may be doomed.”

“Have they all gone insane?” Lin Jing was stunned. With foreign enemies at the gates, these Dao-Seeking cultivators were still worried about the rise of the Demon Race.

“Let’s go!” Lin Jing’s heart tightened. He decided to follow the warning and escape.

But it seemed they were already too late. Just as the Thunder Dragon Ancestor opened a spatial portal, a scorching aura descended upon them. A vast Buddhist light appeared, like a blazing sun, illuminating the Immortal-Burying Volcano even more brightly.

A majestic Buddha’s silhouette emerged, standing tall between heaven and earth. Although his face was serene and benevolent, brows like crescent moons and lips curved in a gentle smile, his presence made both Lin Jing and the Thunder Dragon Ancestor tense up.

“Solar Temple’s Buddha.”

This person’s hands naturally formed a dharma seal. Every movement seemed to illustrate the cycles of karma and reincarnation. He sat calmly upon a blazing sun, gazing down at the Thunder Dragon Ancestor and Lin Jing. “Holy Son of the Demon Court, you’ve managed to refine a Transformation Pill and modify the Bodhi Tree — presumably thanks to those two rare beasts. With the current crisis in Tianyuan Realm, their power is crucial. Hand them over to the Buddhist Sect for cultivation. That would be the best outcome — for them and for Tianyuan Realm.”

As he finished speaking, the surrounding space had already been sealed. The Thunder Dragon Ancestor’s eyes narrowed, and he whispered, “Your Highness, I’ll do everything I can to protect your escape.”

Crack!

The moment his words fell, a terrifying pressure engulfed Lin Jing and the Thunder Dragon Ancestor. The latter instantly exploded into a mist of blood, falling dead before Lin Jing’s eyes.

“Deceitful nonsense.” The Buddha gently raised his hand. Beside Lin Jing, the struggling Pine Leaf Mouse and Dragon Carp were yanked up by an invisible giant hand, completely unable to resist. Lin Jing’s eyes turned bloodshot. In that moment, it was as if something in his body had switched on — flames burst from within him.

“B*ld donkey, you dare?!” Lin Jing felt like his body was about to explode. His lifespan burned like mad. At that moment, he actually comprehended the Heavenly Demon Physique!

Late Core Formation... Core Formation Perfection... Early Nascent Soul...

But the next instant, a tiny mushroom popped out from inside Lin Jing, shouting in horror: “Stop burning! Stop burning! It’s all fake! It’s all an illusion it created! Don’t waste your lifespan!”

Lin Jing: ?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 37: Let It All Burn
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 37: Let It All Burn

As the Spell Spirit Origin Fungus jumped out, everything around vanished—the Buddha Lord of the Solar Temple disappeared, the shattered remains of the Thunder Dragon Ancestor vanished too, leaving only Lin Jing sitting cross-legged on the ground, his body emanating a black-red flame-shaped spiritual energy.

“Goo.....”

The Spell Spirit Origin Fungus helped Lin Jing recall—it was Lin Jing who had asked it earlier to find a chance to create an illusion to assist with learning the Heavenly Demon Physique, not it causing trouble!

It simply thought that while Lin Jing was cultivating undisturbed beneath the volcano, that was a good opportunity...

Besides, Lin Jing had also taken the Mutated Bodhi Leaf. Using both methods together, the success rate might’ve even been higher.

Most importantly, it had just combined Lin Jing’s recent experiences to design a script that, in its opinion, was logically sound... and it actually worked.

However, the Spell Spirit Origin Fungus didn’t expect it to work too well—Lin Jing comprehended too quickly, leaving it no time to stop him from burning his lifespan...

Looking now at Lin Jing, who had already broken through to the “Perfected Nascent Soul Stage,” it figured he must’ve burned a fair bit of his lifespan.

The Illusion Fungus glanced cautiously at Lin Jing, only to see him not focused on that issue at all, but instead looking toward the elder Thunder Dragon flying over, drawn by the disturbance.

“You’re not dead, Senior? That’s good then.”

Thunder Dragon Ancestor: ???

“Huh?” The Thunder Dragon Ancestor was stunned—why would it be dead?

“Your Highness... the Holy Son, you…” It looked at Lin Jing, whose cultivation had reached the Nascent Soul Stage, full of disbelief. “Heavenly Demon Physique?” It thought of something and cried out.

In truth, it didn’t even know if Lin Jing had the Heavenly Demon Physique.

But either way, there weren’t any enemies around right now, so why was the Holy Son burning his lifespan?!

“It’s a long story...”

Lin Jing’s gaze turned distant. Though things had gone a bit off-track, at least he’d successfully comprehended the Heavenly Demon Physique.

He reached out with his senses and found that his plant clone was totally fine and still negotiating with the Buddhist Sect.

So it really was all an illusion. That mushroom... it really had some skill—it even managed to fool him.

“It’s fine, I don’t blame you. In fact, I have to thank you. If it was just the Mutated Bodhi Leaf, I might not have succeeded.” Lin Jing looked at the little mushroom.

“Goo!” The mushroom flew back into his body. As long as it wasn’t being blamed, that was great—it even deserved a thanks!

“Your Highness, what exactly happened?” The Thunder Dragon Ancestor was still confused.

“During the process of cultivating the Heavenly Demon Physique, I accidentally burned some lifespan and raised my cultivation to the Perfected Nascent Soul Stage.”

“As for how long I can maintain it... about a year. So it didn’t take too much lifespan—not the end of the world.”

At the time, Lin Jing had intended to burn 99% of his lifespan to raise his cultivation temporarily to the Dao-Seeking Stage and escape the space blockade from the Buddha Lord and the others.

So during the process of raising his cultivation, he had also been continuously reducing the duration of the boost to keep things balanced.

In the end, when his cultivation reached Perfected Nascent Soul and the duration shrank to one year, the Spell Spirit Origin intervened just in time to stop him, preventing him from going too far and burning even more life.

Otherwise, he might’ve ended up at the Dao-Seeking Stage with only a few hours to live.

So, compared to his own lifespan as a Longevity Physique, the lifespan lost this time wasn’t too big a deal.

Still, it was a loss. Losing a hundred years of lifespan for nothing—Lin Jing still felt the sting. He hadn’t even lived a hundred years yet.

Once the Heavenly Demon Physique activated, it couldn’t be undone. And for the next year, he’d have to maintain this state. He couldn’t even cultivate during that time.

Lin Jing’s heart bled.

“So that’s how it is.” The Thunder Dragon Ancestor said, “As long as you're okay, that’s good.”

Lin Jing nodded, but he didn’t want this new level to go to waste, nor his burned lifespan. He needed to find a way to make the most of his current Nascent Soul Stage cultivation—otherwise, it’d be a total loss.

But this cultivation level wasn’t exactly great either—just Perfected Nascent Soul...

Now that he had the identity of the Holy Son of the Demon Court, with Void Mystical guardians, Nascent Soul was barely different from Core Formation.

“I guess the Fortune Gold List is my only shot.” Lin Jing looked toward the top ten of the list and said, “With my previous cultivation, even with the Dragon Carp assisting me, it wouldn’t have been easy to break into the top ten. But now that I’m at Perfected Nascent Soul, with the Dragon Carp’s help, I might actually have a shot.”

“If I make it, I can enjoy the fortune bonus of the top ten. They say the difference between top ten and the rest is like heaven and earth.”

“If ten years from now I’m heading to the Origin Realm, I won’t be able to enjoy the Tianyuan Realm’s Golden Era benefits anymore. So burning some lifespan now to enjoy more benefits—it might be a good choice.”

“I could even keep burning more lifespan, push my level to the Nascent Soul Transformation Stage, and take the top spots from the Ancient Imperial princes and princesses. That way, I could enjoy the peak bonuses of the Golden Era—even for just ten years, the benefits could be massive.”

“After all, I’d only be losing a bit more lifespan.”

“Besides that...” Lin Jing closed his eyes and sent his divine sense inward, discovering a phantom infant covering his golden core! It looked like a mini version of himself!

Cultivation achieved through special physiques rarely forms a true Nascent Soul—so what he had now was just a phantom infant. Even so, it still possessed Nascent Soul power, just a bit more fragile than a real one.

“Now that I’ve mastered Nascent Soul power, one of the key abilities—aside from body possession and spiritual manifestations—is crafting external incarnations using Nascent Soul energy. Even if my cultivation is temporary, maybe I can still craft one early. If so, over the next ten years, I might be able to develop its techniques and spells to a decent level.”

Soon, Lin Jing decided to embrace the mess, let it all burn. He even considered continuing to burn lifespan to ascend to Nascent Soul Transformation and challenge the princes and princesses of the Ancient Clan for the top ranks.

But before doing that, he had to talk it over with the Demon Emperor. After all, if he knocked the Ancient Clan heirs off their spots and they lost their minds over it, things could get ugly.

And there was one more thing—he didn’t even know the exact strength of the Ancient Clan princes. They were the most famous prodigies of the continent. Unless he reached the Void Mystical Stage, even Nascent Soul Transformation might not be enough to beat them. But the price for that stage was one he couldn’t accept.

So before making his move, he had to understand his enemy’s strength and be sure that Nascent Soul Transformation would be enough to win.

Right now, what he needed most was to find the weakest among the top ten and test his own strength...

“The weakest right now is Senior Sister... that won’t work.” Lin Jing shook his head.

At the same time.

Lin Jing’s clone was still waiting for the Buddhist Sect to finish their discussions, but news of the clone’s appearance had attracted some unexpected visitors.

A young monk nervously arrived at the courtyard where Lin Jing’s clone was staying and said, “Your Highness, there’s someone outside the Bodhi Temple who wishes to see you...”

“Not seeing anyone.” Lin Jing’s clone didn’t even open his eyes.

“He says his name is Gao Sheng, from the Demon-Slaying Bureau of the Ancient Kingdom, and claims to be your friend and classmate.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 38: Definitely Up to No Good
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 38: Definitely Up to No Good

Gao Sheng?

Lin Jing paused slightly after hearing the visitor's name.

What the h*ll is this guy doing in the Western Desert?

He had heard that the Demon-Slaying Temple and the Demon-Slaying Bureau were closely tied, and that many cultivators from the Bureau had come west to eliminate demons, including Cold Moon Sword Immortal, but at least she was a direct disciple of the Dao-Seeking Stage, a Fortune Gold List contender, capable of defending herself. Gao Sheng, though—what was he doing stirring up trouble…

“Take me to him,” Lin Jing shook his head. He was an old friend, after all. Whatever his reasons for coming, he still had to meet with him.

Moments later, outside Bodhi Temple.

Lin Jing saw an old friend he hadn’t met in years.

He was leading a Dragon Horse, waiting in the shade of a tree outside the temple. Judging by his cultivation, he had already reached the Late Core Formation Stage.

“Brother Lin!”

When Gao Sheng saw Lin Jing walk out, his eyes lit up, and he eagerly stepped forward, “It’s been forever. I heard you were nearby, so I rushed over—and you’re really here!”

“Brother Gao, what are you doing all the way out in the Western Desert?” Lin Jing was confused.

Gao Sheng hushed him and grinned, “I sneaked out here—for a major case!”

Lin Jing’s vision went dark. Knew it. Definitely up to no good.

This guy’s already in his thirties, and he’s still giving his family headaches.

“A major case, my *ss. I’ll be asking a Buddhist powerhouse to escort you back to the Ancient Kingdom. Wherever you came from, go back there.”

The Western Desert wasn’t like the Ancient Kingdom. Gao Sheng was, after all, a pretty decent old friend, and Lin Jing didn’t want him dying out here.

“Don’t!” Gao Sheng quickly said, “It really is a major case.”

“This isn’t the place to talk—let’s find somewhere, and I’ll explain everything in detail!”

Lin Jing eyed him suspiciously, but figured it couldn’t hurt to hear him out, so he led Gao Sheng to his private residence.

Once they arrived, Gao Sheng started whispering mysteriously, “Brother Lin, do you know the three great Void Mystical ghost entities of the Western Desert? The Ghost Bride, the Artifact Ghost, and the Shadow Ghost!”

Lin Jing nodded, “Of course I know. Are you saying your so-called major case is related to them?”

Gao Sheng nodded immediately, “Then, Brother Lin, do you know why the Netherworld Hall, one of the three major Demonic Sects, has been so active in the Western Desert these years?”

Lin Jing didn’t know, but judging from Gao Sheng’s expression, he could guess.

“Netherworld Hall used to be active in Qingzhou, and now they’ve come to the Western Desert. Could it be related to the three Void Mystical ghost entities?”

“Bingo. When I was investigating a case on the border of the Ancient Kingdom, I accidentally entered a Celestial Palace ruin, and from there, I obtained a vital piece of intelligence.”

“What kind of intel?”

“The Artifact Ghost, one of the three ghost entities, is suspected to be a war weapon created by an ancient Celestial Palace—an ultimate artifact spirit, capable of acting as a key to activate a Celestial Artifact.

And according to the records in the Demon-Slaying Bureau, Netherworld Hall possesses a Celestial Artifact that’s been passed down to this day. So I reasonably suspect they’ve come to the Western Desert for that Artifact Ghost!”

“If Netherworld Hall succeeds, the consequences will be unimaginable!”

Lin Jing stayed silent.

“And this kind of intel, instead of reporting it to your grandfather or the Bureau, you sneak off on your own—what the h*ll, do you think you alone can take on the Artifact Ghost? Or Netherworld Hall?”

Gao Sheng shook his head, “My grandfather went on a mission to the Cloud Sea Mountains, I couldn’t reach him at all. And the Bureau… honestly, there’s no one who can handle this matter.”

“If my guess is right, only a Dao-Seeking expert in the entire Ancient Kingdom could resolve this case. What do you think, Brother Lin? And there are no Dao-Seeking experts in the Bureau.”

“Then you came alone to the Western Desert…” Lin Jing suddenly thought of something and asked, “Cold Moon Sword Immortal?”

“Exactly. I followed Senior Sister Feng Ning here. She came west to slay demons too…” Gao Sheng whispered, “Don’t forget, her master is also a Dao-Seeking expert now.”

“Also, the Heavenly Sword Gate has a Celestial Sword that was passed down but has never been usable.”

“If I can pass this intel to Ancestor Sky-Splitting Sword, through Senior Sister Feng Ning, and she obtains the Artifact Ghost, her power would rise immensely. Even if not quite at the level of the Martial Emperor, she’d be way more than a mere new Dao-Seeker.”

Lin Jing understood now, “But then why don’t you go directly to court and report it to the Second Ancient Emperor…”

Gao Sheng paused, then muttered, “I don’t really like his policies. Compared to the Ancient Kingdom, he favors the Ancient Clan, his direct bloodline. To him, the Ancient Kingdom is basically just the vessel for his True Dragon Secret Art.”

“By comparison, I respect Ancestor Sky-Splitting Sword more. Back in the day, she trained for hundreds of years in the Demon-Slaying Bureau, her swordsmanship is peerless, and she’s renowned throughout the cultivation world for standing up for justice. Sword cultivators of the Heavenly Sword Gate have an outstanding reputation in the Ancient Kingdom. I think if there’s a chance to activate a Celestial Artifact, better to leave it with them than give it to the Ancient Clan. After all, the Heavenly Sword Gate is still part of the Ancient Kingdom.”

“Unfortunately, it seems like Ancestor Sky-Splitting Sword isn’t at the sect right now. I’m not qualified to contact her directly, so I had to settle for seeking out Senior Sister Feng Ning instead.”

“But the problem now is—before, I could still pick up on her whereabouts, but lately, she’s completely disappeared from the Western Desert. I can’t find her at all!”

Lin Jing finally understood. He looked at Gao Sheng and said seriously, “Brother Gao, don’t you know my current identity? I’m no longer a cultivator of the Ancient Kingdom. I’m now the Holy Son of the Demon Court. A Celestial Artifact like this—it’s not just the Ancient Kingdom, the Heavenly Sword Gate, or Netherworld Hall that has one. The Demon Court has them too.”

“You’re not worried that by telling me such important information, I might report it to the Demon Court and snatch this opportunity?”

Gao Sheng burst out laughing, “I don’t think you would. Back at the battle in Tongtian City, Ancestor Sky-Splitting Sword was the only one from the Ancient Kingdom who came to help you. Maybe she didn’t accomplish much, but I believe, knowing your character, you wouldn’t help the Demon Court compete with her for this chance.”

“If she can wield the Celestial Sword, it’ll be a win for all humankind.”

Lin Jing chuckled and shook his head, “Fine, since you trust me that much—and since this intel is something you discovered—I’ll pretend I never heard it.”

“So what you want now is for me to help you find Cold Moon Sword Immortal in the Western Desert, and then use her to contact Ancestor Sky-Splitting Sword, and work together to stop Netherworld Hall and the Artifact Ghost, right?”

Gao Sheng clapped his fist to his palm, “Exactly! You got it. I’m counting on you, Brother Lin.”

Lin Jing sighed. This guy… what kind of luck does he have. Looks like neither the Ancient Kingdom nor the Buddhist Sect knows the true goal of Netherworld Hall or the situation with the Artifact Ghost—but Gao Sheng found out early.

A war artifact spirit from the Celestial Palace… so that thing he wanted to seal later had this kind of origin.

Sometimes, Lin Jing really didn’t know if Gao Sheng was a genius detective… or just a damn lucky idiot. Still, talking trash about the Ancient Emperor behind his back like that... seriously rebellious, man.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 39: Pet Beast Snatched
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 39: Pet Beast Snatched

"Got news from the clone."

"But it's not progress on the Bodhi Tree matter. Instead, it's an unexpected piece of information."

Burial Immortal Volcano.

Lin Jing, who was familiarizing himself with the power of the Phantom Infant, slowly stood up.

The Artifact Ghost Mother was a universal artifact spirit capable of activating Celestial Artifacts...

This intel, it had to be said, was extremely valuable. If it leaked out, countless top forces would scramble for it.

"Gao Sheng truly deserves to be called a Child of Fortune, his luck is off the charts. But this time, his actions are a bit unworthy of the name his family gave him."

"Reporting to the Ancient Emperor would be the real 'Gao Sheng' (high rise), but he plans to hand the intel over to the Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor instead. He really underestimated him."

Lin Jing took out the communication treasure that Cold Moon Sword Immortal had once gifted him...

If she was still in the Western Desert, he should be able to find Cold Moon Sword Immortal with this.

"Forgot that near the Burial Immortal Volcano, such communication treasures are useless..."

After trying it out, Lin Jing found that the treasure was basically dead. He couldn't help but start flying away from the volcano.

The Thunder Dragon Ancestor immediately followed.

However, even after flying some distance, the communication treasure still didn't work. Lin Jing frowned.

Cold Moon Sword Immortal should have only just arrived at the Western Desert not long ago, no reason to leave so soon.

If he couldn’t reach her now, it meant she was also in a place similar to the Burial Immortal Volcano.

No wonder Gao Sheng couldn’t find her and had to ask him for help.

"Your Highness Holy Son, what happened?" Thunder Dragon Ancestor asked Lin Jing.

"Senior Thunder Dragon, contact all branches of the Demon Court in the Western Desert. Help me find someone with all your might."

"Who?!"

"Disciple of the Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor, Cold Moon Sword Immortal, Feng Ning."

"Feng... the girl from the Feng family, one of the Eight Great Families of Martial Emperor City." Thunder Dragon Ancestor nodded, indicating he understood.

"Please return inside the barrier, Your Highness Holy Son. Issuing the orders to all the beast clans will take some time."

Lin Jing nodded and said, "No need to worry about me..."

Soon after, Lin Jing returned to the barrier near the Burial Immortal Volcano, quietly waiting for the Demon Court to help him find Cold Moon Sword Immortal’s whereabouts.

He sat cross-legged and continued cultivating, inwardly observing the misty Phantom Infant, murmuring, "With a solid foundation, it's not that hard to control Nascent Soul power. Maybe I really can use this temporary Nascent Soul power to forge an external incarnation in advance."

"Normally, Nascent Soul experts forge incarnations to better comprehend the power of heaven and earth and eventually ascend to the Soul Transformation Stage. But in my case, whether staying in the Demon Court during the Golden Era, or going to the Origin Realm rich in natural forces and Immortal fate, I won't have trouble advancing to Soul Transformation. So my external incarnation doesn’t necessarily have to be made from supreme-grade spiritual plants aligned with nature. I can pick another path!"

"The incarnation could follow the Beast Taming Dao to increase the number of my contract beasts, but that's not particularly meaningful."

"Another option is to find some top-tier Immortal Palace techniques, ones that are considered good even in the Immortal Realm, and let the incarnation cultivate them."

"Although such techniques are extremely rare, if I can get the Mechanical Turtle to master the Divine Scripture, I'd have the chance to summon Ancient Powerhouses and inherit their techniques. It’s not impossible."

"The most absurd option is... just as Senior Magic Pine suggested... to become a 'Heir of the Demonic Sky Sect', giving both my true self and incarnation an extra path to survive in this lower world ruled by the Demonic Sky Sect."

Lin Jing took a deep breath. Since he had already mastered the Heavenly Demon Physique and Heavenly Demon Divine Sense, it wouldn't hurt to learn a few more Demonic Sky Sect inheritances.

But what material should he use to forge the external incarnation so that it would best align with the Demonic Sky Sect inheritance...?

As he pondered, Lin Jing’s expression suddenly changed—he sensed an immensely powerful ghost aura.

"Your Highness Holy Son, bad news, hurry, we’ll cover you!"

A Thunder Dragon rushed over, looking anxious.

"The Artifact Ghost Mother we once searched for has suddenly appeared, possessing a treasure on the wooden ship, and is currently snatching all the fishing artifacts. Once she finishes, she’ll likely go on a killing spree!"

"We’ve already tried to recall the Ancestor. Until he returns, please retreat into the depths of the Burial Immortal Volcano, Your Highness. The Ghost Mother, being a death spirit, can only operate near the edges of the volcano. She definitely wouldn't dare go deeper!"

What the hell, the Ghost Mother appears now?!

Lin Jing froze. A year ago, he had tried everything to find the Ghost Mother for the Golden Light Temple’s test, and no matter how he baited, she never showed up.

Now, after he had already passed the test using the Ghost Bride’s power, she popped up?!

And just after he learned the Ghost Mother's true identity!

"Yeah right, what a f***ing convenient plot you’re writing here."

"Mushroom, I’ve already completed the body refinement. You don’t have to deliberately create illusions anymore."

"Illusion, break!"

Lin Jing shouted lowly—but nothing happened.

The Spell Spirit Origin Fungus emerged, looking wronged and puzzled. Illusion? What illusion?

It hadn't created any illusion at all.

Not an illusion?

It's real?!

Lin Jing's face changed again. It really was that coincidental.

"Fear not, Your Highness!!!"

At the critical moment when Lin Jing froze, a thunderous boom rang from the sky. The Thunder Dragon Ancestor broke through the air. Although Lin Jing had sent him to issue a wanted order, he hadn't gone far—he had hidden in the void, using lightning to transmit commands across the Western Desert’s beast clans.

Receiving the Thunder Dragon prodigies' urgent cry for help, he immediately interrupted the command and rushed back to protect Lin Jing.

Terrifying dragon might, thunder power, and sword intent merged into one overwhelming force, enveloping the Ghost Mother who was stealing artifacts on the ship.

But facing the Thunder Dragon Ancestor’s might, the Ghost Mother was no fool. She instantly disappeared, likely possessing one of the artifacts and becoming its artifact spirit.

"Capture alive." Lin Jing gave the order.

The Thunder Dragon Ancestor heavily nodded, but despite sweeping the artifacts on the ship with lightning-like divine sense, he couldn’t detect any trace of the Ghost Mother, causing his brows to furrow.

"Damn, sigh!"

Until—the Dragon Carp and Pine Leaf Mouse suddenly cried out in alarm, drawing the Ancestor’s and Snow Thunder Dragon’s attention to underneath the ship.

They saw the Mechanical Turtle, which had been recharging and resting at the edge of the barrier, suddenly and weirdly moving.

An Artifact Ghost couldn’t amplify the artifact’s power beyond limits without integrating its own force.

The Mechanical Turtle was the only space-type construct here. Though mechanical, the Artifact Ghost seemed able to possess it.

"Bad!"

Thunder Dragon Ancestor and Lin Jing both exclaimed.

Just as they were about to act, the Mechanical Turtle slipped into the void and vanished without a trace.

Even a ghost knew—at this point, fighting over some artifacts and clashing with Lin Jing and the Thunder Dragon Ancestor was unwise. Avoiding conflict was the best move.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 40: Martial Emperor City
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 40: Martial Emperor City

"Your Highness Holy Son, th-this..."

As the Artifact Ghost rode the space-attributed Mechanical Turtle into the void, Thunder Dragon Ancestor's first instinct was to chase after it, but he also worried the enemy might circle back and harm Lin Jing, so he was frozen in place, unable to decide.

"Senior, where’s your external incarnation?" Lin Jing asked.

"It died long ago!" Thunder Dragon Ancestor replied, understanding that Lin Jing had wanted his clone to give chase.

But having such a convenient tool like an incarnation, any cultivator would use it recklessly until it was gone. How could it possibly still be intact?

Those who reached a high cultivation stage and still had an intact incarnation were extremely rare.

Lin Jing: "...Forget it."

"But Your Highness Holy Son’s turtle..."

He had originally planned to simply report the Artifact Ghost’s information to the Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor, but now it seemed that grudge was officially settled.

......

Meanwhile.

Bodhi Temple.

The Abbot of Bodhi Temple had been experimenting with the effects of the Mutated Bodhi Leaf.

According to Lin Jing's description, this Tier-3 Mutated Bodhi Leaf could generally last for a year on cultivators of the corresponding level, but if used by cultivators with higher cultivation, the duration would be reduced.

Although the Abbot wanted to personally test it, he worried the Tier-3 Bodhi Leaf might not last long on him, so he decided to grant this opportunity to his disciple.

"Master, I..."

At this moment, a young monk was cultivating a strange technique under the Abbot’s protection.

"Master, it worked, it worked, I think I’m really pregnant!"

Feeling the movements of the embryo, the little monk showed a look of pure joy.

"Good, good, good."

The Abbot was quite surprised—this Mutated Bodhi Leaf was indeed as useful as the Holy Son of the Demon Court claimed.

"This time, you should be able to sever your worldly attachments."

"Thank you, Master." The little monk bowed deeply. Born an orphan, he had entered the temple as a child and was meant to become a Buddhist prodigy. However, due to his deceased parents' last wishes, he had always harbored a persistent obsession.

His late parents had hoped he would carry on the bloodline of their destroyed clan and rebuild it.

Yet the Buddhist techniques he cultivated forbade intimacy with women.

This obsession had blocked his advancement in the Dharma.

Under such circumstances, the only solution was to reproduce asexually, without male-female union.

Few demon beasts even had this ability.

Among the human race, only some rare talents could barely cultivate the ancient secret art for self-reproduction.

This secret art was easier for women to learn but extremely difficult for men.

Only a few men with extraordinary talent could manage it.

Clearly, the little monk was not among them. Despite years of effort, he had made no progress.

Now, with the help of the Mutated Bodhi Leaf, he had finally conceived a bloodline offspring. His joy was indescribable.

In this way, not only would he fulfill his deceased parents' wishes and revive his clan’s bloodline, but he could also continue to advance in the Dharma without breaking his vows.

"Looks like the Holy Son of the Demon Court spoke the truth," the Abbot muttered as he continuously fiddled with his prayer beads, lost in thought.

"However, since this involves the sacred tree of the Buddhist Sect, I must consult the Buddha Lord..."

............

"So, you're here for the Bodhi Tree."

Under a Bodhi tree, Lin Jing and Gao Sheng drank tea and casually chatted about their recent experiences.

"Truly, the ways of the world are unpredictable," Gao Sheng sighed.

"Even if you had become the Holy Son of the Gu God Sect, I would have thought it more likely than you becoming the Holy Son of the Demon Court."

"Come on, I didn't expect it either. Back then, you were just a second-generation kid who stole a token to go demon-slaying. Now you’ve actually dug up intel capable of shaking the entire Ancient Kingdom," Lin Jing chuckled.

Gao Sheng sighed, "Honestly, I wish I didn’t have this luck. You have no idea how conflicted I am... But forget it, since the decision's made, I can only walk this path to the end. I just hope I chose right."

"If I were you, I would’ve made the same choice. Don't burden yourself too much," Lin Jing comforted him.

"You really get me," Gao Sheng downed his tea. "At first, I didn’t feel it much, but after all these years working in the Demon-Slaying Bureau, after seeing so many cases of 'demon cultivators' being judged, I’m increasingly convinced the Ancient Kingdom has gone wrong."

"The First Ancient Emperor founded the kingdom not for his own cultivation, but because he couldn't bear to see the human race suffer in chaotic times. The fortune of the kingdom came later, not the other way around."

"But now... the cause and effect have reversed. Stability of the kingdom trumps everything. To keep the kingdom’s fortune intact and constantly supply it to Ancient Clan prodigies, sacrificing low-level cultivators and commoners is no longer rare.

Even those whose crimes weren’t severe get labeled as 'demon cultivators' and disposed of. The laws of the Ancient Kingdom have long been corrupted.

I might be considered a 'beneficiary', but this wasn’t why I joined the Demon-Slaying Bureau in the first place."

"The demons I wanted to slay were those who harmed the innocent. The monsters I wanted to kill were those who brought chaos, not the so-called 'demons' defined by the Ancient Kingdom."

"That’s why I didn’t want to hand over the Artifact Ghost intel to the Ancient Kingdom. But now, by giving it to the Heavenly Sword Sect... maybe the situation will change a little."

Gao Sheng suddenly found the tea flavorless and asked Lin Jing if he had any wine.

"I'm still a kid. I can't drink," Lin Jing said slowly. "You shouldn't drink either. Save your worries for later. Once you’ve reached a higher cultivation, it'll be easier to act. No matter how the world is, small characters like us can only survive in the cracks for now."

"Don't worry. I've already sent people to find Cold Moon Sword Immortal..."

"Huh?"

Lin Jing suddenly froze.

"Found her."

"That fast?" Gao Sheng was surprised.

"Strictly speaking, we’ve just gotten her whereabouts," Lin Jing said. "A beast clan on the edge of the Western Desert reported that they saw Cold Moon Sword Immortal heading toward Martial Emperor City.

She should have left the Western Desert, though it’s unclear whether her destination is Martial Emperor City or returning to the Ancient Kingdom."

"Leaving so soon? No wonder I couldn't find her in the Western Desert!" Gao Sheng was speechless.

Martial Emperor City.

Eight great martial families.

The Feng family.

Cold Moon Sword Immortal Feng Ning didn’t really want to return to her family, but she couldn’t withstand the Ghost Bride’s pestering.

The Ghost Bride insisted on seeking portraits or relics of the Feng family's ancestor.

Feng Ning felt helpless—did this Ghost Bride think her ancestor was her fiancée?

Even if so, the person’s been dead for countless years...

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 41: Stone Man Gu
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 41: Stone Man Gu

After learning where Cold Moon Sword Immortal had gone, Lin Jing patted his butt and set off to find the Abbot of Bodhi Temple.

"I came here with sincerity, how long does your Buddhist Sect intend to keep discussing? My time is precious too. If it won't work out, then forget it."

Compared to the Green Bodhi Tree, it was obvious that the key to activating the Celestial Artifact was more important. Lin Jing also didn’t want such a treasure to fall into the hands of the Netherworld Hall.

As far as he knew, the Hall Master of the Netherworld Hall and the Sect Leader of the Yin Corpse Sect were brothers, disciples of the same master.

This was a little-known secret. Lin Jing himself hadn’t known at first, until the Demon Emperor told him after destroying the Yin Corpse Sect.

Well, let's just assume those two brothers have a good relationship...

Since the Netherworld Hall was also a potential enemy, Lin Jing naturally wouldn’t give them the chance to grow stronger.

"Don't be anxious, Holy Son, don't be anxious." Facing Lin Jing who came knocking, the Abbot of Bodhi Temple greeted him with a smile.

"I don't have time to wait. Once you have the answer, just send the Bodhi Tree to this location." Lin Jing threw over a map.

The Buddha Lord’s incarnation was currently negotiating with the Demon Emperor’s incarnation about spreading the Buddhist teachings among the Demon Race. Giving away a Bodhi Tree was just a goodwill gesture. Lin Jing didn't think the Buddha Lord would refuse.

He didn’t plan to wait here any longer, and was ready to head toward Martial Emperor City with Gao Sheng to catch up with Cold Moon Sword Immortal.

"I understand. Once the Buddha Lord agrees, Bodhi Temple will immediately send the Holy Tree." The Abbot of Bodhi Temple pressed his palms together, curious about what urgent matter Lin Jing was so anxious to attend to.

..........

That day, a huge spatial vortex appeared above Bodhi Temple, and nine Thunder Dragons pulled a spirit boat out of it.

In terms of cultivation, the prodigies of the Thunder Dragon Clan might only be at the Nascent Soul Stage, but in terms of speed, even ordinary Nascent Soul Transformation cultivators might not match them.

"Holy sh*t." Even though Gao Sheng was a second-generation immortal, he had never seen such a spectacle. Not just him—even his Nascent Soul stage grandfather wouldn't be qualified to have nine Thunder Dragon prodigies pulling his carriage.

"Thanks, brothers." After boarding the flying boat along with Lin Jing’s clone, Gao Sheng muttered to the Thunder Dragon prodigies.

"Sit tight, we’re taking off." Lin Jing smiled. Immediately, the Thunder Dragons roared, and accompanied by lightning and thunder in the sky, the flying boat shot through the clouds like a streak of immortal light.

With the assistance of the nine Thunder Dragon prodigies, they traveled at an incredible speed, and within just a few days, they reached the edge of the Western Desert, seeing from afar the Martial Emperor City, known as the number one city under heaven.

Martial Emperor City was grand and majestic, surrounded by walls tens of meters high, truly deserving its reputation as a city-shaped Celestial Artifact, far more magnificent than the capital of any Ancient Kingdom.

"We're going down." On the flying boat, Lin Jing said to Gao Sheng.

Inside Martial Emperor City, although entry wasn't as strictly controlled as the Ancient Kingdom's capital, that was only for humans. As for the Thunder Dragon prodigies, Lin Jing had them stay in place. This trip was merely to find Cold Moon Sword Immortal, no need for a high-profile entrance.

He and Gao Sheng traveled together on foot toward the city gates. As they approached, Lin Jing saw Martial Emperor City's iconic Stone Man Army!

On the city walls, dense ranks of stone soldiers stood vigilantly, gazing outward. Each stone soldier exuded the aura of a seasoned general, carrying a bloodthirsty presence.

"Speaking of which, he should have less than eight Rare Gu left under his control now, right?" Seeing the Stone Man Army, Gao Sheng couldn’t help but ask.

"Elder Thunder Gu of the Gu God Sect likely died in Tongtian City. As for my Thunder Tribulation Gu, whether he got it or not..."

Martial Emperor nodded: "Yes, eight left. Don’t know if he’ll have a chance to collect the others..."

"That Thunder Dragon Clan, seems they also didn't get the Stone Man Gu, otherwise they wouldn’t..."

The stone soldiers lining Martial Emperor City's walls were exactly the masterpiece of one of the Ten Rare Gu, the Stone Man Gu.

The core power of the Stone Man Gu lay in its “Petrification Poison,” which could completely turn any infected life form into stone, becoming a slave controlled by the Gu worm.

However, the truly terrifying part wasn’t just that.

The Stone Man Gu was a poison insect that resembled an earthworm and was extremely skilled in earth-related techniques. Thus, its vessel could survive, hide, and cultivate deep underground, making it almost impossible to destroy. This earth-type ability ensured the Stone Man Gu’s vessels could evade most powerful enemies.

Furthermore, its poison could "kill earth veins" swiftly, poisoning spiritual veins, not just affecting people but also directly crippling a sect’s foundation, making it extremely hard to defend against.

"This, actually, is both easy and hard," said Gao Sheng nearby.

"Although the Stone Man Gu is in the hands of Martial Emperor City’s City Lord’s Mansion, Martial Emperor himself surely looks down on such things."

"That's why, after obtaining the Stone Man Gu, it was always passed around different cultivators within the City Lord’s Mansion."

"The current holder of the Stone Man Gu is Martial Emperor’s youngest disciple, Fang Renzong."

"It’s said Fang Renzong is naturally belligerent and loves betting matches. I wonder if he’s daring enough to put the Stone Man Gu up as a wager."

"A few years back, it was still in his senior brother’s hands, but after the Fortune Gold List came out, to strengthen the younger disciple, the senior brother gave him the Stone Man Gu."

Lin Jing smiled, "You're suggesting that I, a Core Formation Stage cultivator, should challenge Fang Renzong, Martial Emperor's successor currently ranked sixth on the Fortune Gold List, and already at Early Nascent Soul Transformation?"

Although Fang Renzong was the youngest among Martial Emperor’s four disciples, his cultivation was already at the Early Nascent Soul Transformation Stage!

Gao Sheng laughed awkwardly and scratched his head, "That’s why I said, it’s not easy."

"But it’s indeed an opportunity. If the Stone Man Gu were still in the hands of those three Void Mystical Stage successors, it would be hard to get. But if it’s with this younger Nascent Soul one, maybe we can lure him into a wager..."

"Huh?" Gao Sheng froze, "You're serious? Core Formation against Nascent Soul? No, wait, Lin Jing's Dragon Carp has already reached Nascent Soul, so Nascent Soul against Nascent Soul?"

Lin Jing gave him a sideways glance and said, "It’s worth a try. If I can't beat him, I’ll just break through to Nascent Soul myself."

Now that his Heavenly Demon Physique was complete, he was planning to forcibly ascend to Nascent Soul anyway to cause trouble for the Ancient Imperial Prince and the Ancient Clan Princess. Testing his strength against Martial Emperor's successor along the way wasn’t a bad idea either, provided Fang Renzong was willing to gamble.

As they walked along, they paid the spirit stones to enter the city successfully. But just after stepping into Martial Emperor City, Lin Jing suddenly remembered something.

He hesitated and said, "I almost forgot. Back when I was in the Cavern Heaven, I killed one of Martial Emperor City’s prodigies. Although I used a fake identity, by now it wouldn’t be hard to link it back to me... I looked into it;
