Just recently, it was rumored that the Ancient Imperial Prince, having reached first place on the Gold List, had obtained an Immortal Technique inheritance from ancient ruins.

And that might just be one of many opportunities the Ancient Imperial Prince had gained lately.

Now, with Lin Jing rising to sixth place, someone else had to be bumped from the top ten.

Before Lin Jing headed to the Western Desert, the Gold List top ten included eight Nascent Soul Transformation cultivators and two Nascent Souls.

Now, with one of the two Nascent Souls having advanced to Nascent Soul Transformation, the top ten consisted of nine Nascent Soul Transformation cultivators and one Nascent Soul.

That remaining Nascent Soul was none other than Xu Qingyao, famed as the strongest Nascent Soul in the Beast Taming Dao. She had originally been ranked ninth, but when the tenth place advanced, she was pushed to tenth.

Now, with Lin Jing's forceful entry, Xu Qingyao was inevitably pushed down to eleventh place...

Xu Qingyao’s drop out of the top ten had initially made many assume it was due to some other prodigy reaching Nascent Soul Transformation. However, when they saw the name, they all paused in surprise.

Inside the Ancient Kingdom.

The Ancient Imperial Prince, the Ancient Clan Princess, the Dao Child of the Spirit Dao Sect...

In the Eastern Sea, the Crown Prince of Poseidon Palace, the Senior Sister of Water Moon Immortal Island...

In the Misty Forest, the Dragon Emperor’s personal disciple...

In the Cloud Sea Mountains, the Witch God Hall’s Holy Son...

In the Snow Plains, the Totem Holy Son...

One after another, the top ten fortune-touched prodigies stared silently at the new name.

“Mid Core Formation Stage.........”

“Why is someone at Mid Core Formation on the list...”

“Wan Renzong, the Martial Emperor’s successor... was defeated by the Holy Son of the Demon Court??”

At the moment, Lin Jing didn’t have time to mourn for Senior Sister. He believed that once she broke through to Nascent Soul Transformation, she would surely return to the top ten.

Right now, Lin Jing still had something important to do—to take his Stone Man Gu.

He condensed a large hand out of his tangible divine sense and dragged the unconscious Martial Emperor’s successor up from deep underground, pinning him to the sky. Then he began extracting the Stone Man Gu from his body!

“Aaahhh—”

A miserable cry rang out. Wan Renzong, though unconscious, couldn’t help but let out a painful wail. Upon seeing this, the three other Void Mystical Martial Emperor successors showed flashes of anger in their eyes.

“Stop!!!” they shouted in unison, trying to halt Lin Jing’s actions. But Lin Jing didn’t stop.

The Thunder Dragon Ancestor roared and appeared near Lin Jing, glaring death at the three Martial Emperor successors.

“You bet, you pay. What, can’t accept the loss?”

“Relax. Our Holy Son won’t take his life. He’s just taking the Gu, that’s all.”

The Stone Man Gu inside Wan Renzong was also at Nascent Soul Transformation Stage, but it had always been sealed.

Wan Renzong hadn’t possessed the Stone Man Gu long enough to properly use its power.

Now, Lin Jing violently tore open the seal. The Stone Man Gu immediately awakened.

This black earthworm-like Gu had just planned to cause chaos and escape, but then it sensed a terrifying divine sense locking onto it completely.

This divine sense made it tremble all over, as if it were facing the Gu God himself.

“H*ll, what’s going on...” thought the Stone Man Gu, shuddering.

“Be good.”

Then, Lin Jing used Divine Control Technique to suppress the Stone Man Gu into his hand.

The three Martial Emperor successors could only watch as Lin Jing pulled the Gu from Wan Renzong’s body.

But just as the Thunder Dragon Ancestor had said—this bet had been set by Wan Renzong and Lin Jing from the beginning.

Plenty of strong cultivators had witnessed it!

Sure, Martial Emperor City could renege, but... even if rumors said the Demon Court’s Demon Emperor was seriously injured and missing, the Martial Emperor himself had also gone missing after being seriously injured.

Even the Artifact Spirit of Martial Emperor City was nowhere to be found.

If they went back on the deal, it would inevitably lead to a direct clash with the Demon Court...

“Ridiculous. This agreement was improper from the start.”

Still, the Stone Man Gu was far too important to Martial Emperor City.

One of the Martial Emperor’s successors narrowed his eyes coldly—this Stone Man Gu had been given to Wan Renzong by him.

He had used it to forge an army of Stone Soldiers.

If Martial Emperor City lost the Stone Man Gu, their years of foundation would be destroyed, and the army would gradually go out of control. He couldn’t allow the Gu to fall into someone else’s hands.

He was just about to disregard the Thunder Dragon Ancestor and forcibly retrieve the Gu when—

a familiar voice suddenly rang in his ear.

“Give it to him.”

This order, under normal circumstances, he would’ve ignored completely.

However, that familiar and weak voice made the Martial Emperor successor’s pupils shrink.

It was the long-missing Martial Emperor!

“Master…”

He was shocked, sensing the frailty in his master’s aura.

“I’m fine, just need some time to recover. That Stone Man Gu—let him have it. Since your junior gave it away himself, let him win it back himself.”

“Yes...”

The Martial Emperor’s chief disciple gave up on reclaiming the Gu.

He understood now—his master wanted to use this Holy Son of the Demon Court to sharpen the younger disciple, to give him motivation.

This battle would undoubtedly spread across the continent.

His junior Wan Renzong would be branded with endless shame.

But perhaps, that very shame would drive him to grow stronger...

“Let’s go.”

Having finished the duel, Lin Jing had no intention of lingering in Martial Emperor City.

He glanced at Feng Ning, Gao Sheng, and the others below, then stood once again atop the Thunder Dragon Ancestor—ready to get back to his true task.

Which was to find the Artifact Ghost.

Moments ago, his Demonic Divine Sense had swept the surroundings and discovered many hidden enemies.

Not just experts from Poseidon Palace and the Misty Forest—even Gu God Sect experts… quite a few had shown up.

But now wasn’t the time to fight.

His current lineup still wasn’t enough to kill those enemies.

Although Cold Moon Sword Immortal Feng Ning now had the power to slay Void Mystical cultivators, that was a power entrusted by her master for use against the Artifact Ghost and the Netherworld Hall.

Lin Jing didn’t feel comfortable asking the Sword Immortal to help kill personal enemies.

Besides, the ones here weren’t even the strongest of their factions.

Even if they were all killed, it wouldn’t solve much—only temporarily inflame tensions.

Of course, if those people didn’t know their place and dared to interfere in their search for the Artifact Ghost, then he wouldn’t hold back...

If that happened, and Cold Moon Sword Immortal borrowed the power of the Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor to slay enemies—then it would be fully justified.

After all, finding the Artifact Ghost was the top priority.

Anyone who got in the way—must be eliminated!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 52: A Twist of Fate
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 52: A Twist of Fate

Western Desert Region.

Not all cultivators were paying attention to the battle at Martial Emperor City,

The ghost cultivators of the Demonic Sect, Netherworld Hall... had focused solely on searching for the Artifact Ghost from beginning to end.

They ignored the battle at Tongtian City.

They still ignored the battle at Martial Emperor City.

Even the Netherworld Saintess didn’t care about her Fortune Gold List ranking, dropping from eleventh to twelfth.

Because after prolonged efforts, the Netherworld Hall had finally found hope in capturing the Artifact Ghost!

The Artifact Ghost was extremely cunning, never clashing with powerful cultivators, making direct capture nearly impossible.

Inside the eerie altar, the Netherworld Hall Master, draped in black robes and made entirely of bones, had ghostfire flickering in his eyes.

The Netherworld Saintess, dressed in white and revealing only her long black hair, looked solemn.

Besides them, there was another person—an emaciated old man’s soul. Though this soul had only Nascent Soul Stage cultivation, at this moment, both the Hall Master and the Saintess had their gazes fixed on him.

This old man, though "not highly cultivated," was the top tomb raider in the Western Desert. His techniques, spells, and experience were all tailored for dismantling various formation seals.

During their hunt for the Artifact Ghost, Netherworld Hall had also been capturing all sorts of talents capable of breaking ancient formations—countless cultivators were enslaved, their souls used for service, but even after catching over a hundred people, nothing had come of it.

Until this tomb raider... successfully cracked the formation sealing the Artifact Ghost.

If this damaged formation could be repaired, the altar would become like an eye of a storm, forcibly drawing the wandering Artifact Ghost back and resealing it!

This altar had been constructed specifically to suppress the Artifact Ghost—even the ghost itself couldn’t completely destroy or resist it.

“All the materials you requested are here. As long as you can successfully reseal the Artifact Ghost here, you’ll become the Vice Hall Master of the Netherworld Hall. Not only will you regain your freedom, we’ll also reshape a new body for you,” said the Netherworld Saintess.

“Thank you, Hall Master, thank you, Saintess... I’ll give it my all. Not to brag, but our family has been tomb raiding since the Celestial Palace Era. My grandfather, my father—they were all Void Mystical Stage tomb raiders. It’s just that, by the time it got to me, there weren’t enough tombs to raid, and the resources simply weren’t enough for me to cultivate to Void Mystical Stage. That’s why I...”

“Enough nonsense.” The Netherworld Hall Master glanced at the shifty-eyed tomb raider. “Get started.”

“Yes, yes...” The tomb raider felt bitter. He had raided tombs his whole life—those of Buddhist monks, royal families from the Ancient Kingdom, even ancient cultivators from the Celestial Palace...

And yet, he hadn’t been caught by the Buddhist Sect, nor by the Ancient Kingdom, nor killed by ancient formations... In the end, he was personally captured by the Hall Master of the Netherworld Hall, one of the Three Major Demon Sects, a Dao-Seeking expert.

None of them were good people, so why make it hard for each other...

What had he done to deserve this? Just raided some tombs—was that worth being shattered into a fragmented soul?

Perhaps this was the karma of generations of tomb raiding... finally catching up to him.

The tomb raider remained sorrowful, only hoping the Netherworld Hall would honor their word.

Looking at the formation, I can tell it originates from the Celestial Palace Era—very profound. I only figured it out thanks to my ancestors’ long-standing dealings with Celestial Palace tombs.

But to be honest, I’m not entirely sure I can repair the altar and re-absorb the Artifact Ghost, Bao Gui. Still, at this point, I have no choice but to try.

Otherwise, what awaits me is soul annihilation.

“It must work...” Under the pressure of the Netherworld Hall Master and the Saintess, the tomb raider began repairing the Bao Gui altar. Who knew how long passed—he instinctively wiped his sweat, only to realize he was just a soul and had no sweat to wipe.

“How’s it going?” asked the Netherworld Saintess.

“I think... I think it worked. As long as we offer a large number of magical artifacts as tribute, the sealing altar should be reactivated. This is a targeted seal—once activated, it’ll unleash a suction force that can pull in the target no matter where it is and seal it!!!”

“However, I estimate that at least a quasi-celestial artifact must be sacrificed to activate it...” the tomb raider said cautiously.

At his words, the Netherworld Hall Master remained expressionless. A quasi-celestial artifact...

Though incredibly rare, it was nothing compared to the hope of controlling a Celestial Artifact.

He extended his hand and handed a quasi-celestial artifact to the tomb raider’s soul...

The tomb raider nodded and nervously began the sacrificial ritual.

Who knew how long had passed before he suddenly exclaimed in shock.

“Success?” asked the Netherworld Saintess.

He was overjoyed: “A few small hiccups, but it should have worked. Under the altar’s power, the Artifact Ghost’s strength will now be heavily suppressed—possibly even knocked unconscious. But because the altar is ancient, its suction might be weaker, unable to break through the void and summon it quickly. Lords, now is the time—you can search around the altar with more force!”

“If luck’s on our side, we should be able to find it soon as it's being pulled in by the altar!”

“Act fast—don’t let others beat us to it.”

“You’ve done well.” The Netherworld Saintess gave him a rare look of approval. He really was a capable one.

At the same time.

Somewhere in the Western Desert.

The Artifact Ghost, currently possessing the Longevity Turtle, suddenly sensed a familiar power.

Its jellyfish-like, phantom form trembled in alarm within the Longevity Turtle.

Sealing power...

That familiar sealing power immediately struck terror into the Artifact Ghost.

Its mind faltered—it sensed the sealing altar was forcibly summoning it. But since the altar was designed specifically to suppress it, the Artifact Ghost had no way to resist. It could only struggle frantically inside the Longevity Turtle, continuously channeling and enhancing the turtle’s power in an attempt to escape.

In the end, however, it couldn’t withstand the power of the sealing altar and soon lost consciousness.

Once the Artifact Ghost fainted, the altar’s suction force began forcibly pulling it in.

But at that very moment, the Longevity Turtle—whose strength had been greatly enhanced by the Artifact Ghost using its ultimate artifact spirit power—began acting unlike any other treasure whose spirit had fallen asleep.

Normally, even a Celestial Artifact, once it lost its artifact spirit, would just lie still, devoid of will.

But mechanized beasts were different. Even without an artifact spirit’s control, they could still move...

“Reboot complete. Commencing treasure hunt.”

The Longevity Turtle, after rebooting, looked blankly ahead. Facing the altar’s suction, it paused briefly—then ran toward something that attracted it more than the altar... the Longevity Physique.

“They say once you make it into the top ten of the Fortune Gold List, luck will follow you, opportunities will flood in—you could just walk outside and pick up rare treasures. They say it’s ten times stronger than my Fortune Physique. I wonder if that’s true. Brother Lin, we’ve been traveling for so long—how come you haven’t found a single treasure?” In the group that had left Martial Emperor City, Gao Sheng glanced at the Gold List, curious.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 53: The Sky-Splitting Sword
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 53: The Sky-Splitting Sword

“How would I know… besides, I only just made it onto the Fortune Gold List, opportunities don’t show up that fast.”

Lin Jing sat cross-legged on the Nine Dragons Carriage, currently experiencing the benefits of being in the top ten of the Gold List.

On either side of him stood Cold Moon Sword Immortal Feng Ning and the famed detective Gao Sheng. Compared to Gao Sheng, who was more interested in the fortune-related blessings from the list, Feng Ning was focused on the comprehension bonuses brought by it.

“Comprehension boost… I really do feel it. In my current state, while not quite on the level of that golden Bodhi Tree I encountered in the Bodhi Cavern Heaven, the effect is still impressive...”

“Before long, I might be able to successfully improve the Beast Taming Sect’s cultivation technique.”

“Geez, why improve a technique?” Gao Sheng asked in confusion. If he had this comprehension boost, the first thing he’d do was cultivate some ultra-difficult secret arts…

Lin Jing shot him a look and said, “Right now, the Beast Taming Art only allows contracting with demonic beasts—no other races. That’s too limiting.”

Gao Sheng was shocked. “What else do you want to contract… humans? Human Taming Art? Wait a sec—we’re heading to meet the Ghost Bride right now. Don’t tell me you’re planning to contract that senior…”

She rubbed her forehead, seriously considering sending this guy back to the Ancient Kingdom to keep him from dragging them down later.

Thrown to the edge of the flying vessel, Gao Sheng finally understood. “Oh, that mechanical turtle. Right, that one... it’s the same one Lin has been using since he entered the academy.”

“Brother Lin, truly worthy of respect. So loyal… what a faithful man...” He laughed and looked over.

“If the Heavenly Demon Physique can get someone into the top ten, then I wonder if I can make it up there by tapping into the power of Sword Nurturing Technique...” Cold Moon Sword Immortal was clearly tempted and started scheming.

“Aren’t you afraid the Sky-Splitting Ancestor will wake up and scold you for abusing her technique? Then go ahead...” Lin Jing chuckled. The next moment, he used his Heavenly Demon Divine Sense as usual to check for anyone trailing them.

Given Lin Jing’s current divine sense, unless someone was extremely skilled at stealth, no one could escape his notice.

And such people… were rare. At least none of the group from Martial Emperor City fit that bill.

Soon, Lin Jing narrowed the range of his divine sense.

This time, he didn’t detect any tails. But just as he was about to resume refining the Beast Taming Art, a spatial ripple suddenly flared up around them, instantly putting Lin Jing on high alert.

“Who’s there…” Even with his Heavenly Demon Divine Sense, he hadn’t sensed someone approaching via the void.

This person’s mastery of spatial concealment had reached an unbelievable level.

“Who!” Not just Lin Jing, but also Thunder Dragon Ancestor, Cold Moon Sword Immortal, and Gao Sheng all instantly turned warily toward the void.

Then… they all fell silent as a majestic, royal-looking turtle emerged from the spatial ripple.

“Huh…” Thunder Dragon Ancestor blinked. Wasn’t that the mechanical beast the Artifact Ghost had taken?

How did it come back?

What shocked Thunder Dragon Ancestor even more was that the turtle still radiated Peak Void Mystical Stage energy—formidable beyond belief. The Artifact Ghost might still be inside!

“Your Highness, be careful!! That’s the Artifact Ghost!!!”

Thunder roared. He was just about to act when Thunder Dragon spoke in confusion: “Wait, it’s the Artifact Ghost, but it’s not the Artifact Ghost.”

“My divine sense is sharper than even Lin Jing’s ancestor’s. I could see it at a glance. Though there’s indeed an artifact spirit within the Longevity Turtle, for some unknown reason, it’s currently unconscious—completely unaware.”

“Now this Longevity Turtle looks like it rebooted and, against its basic system directives, found its way back to me.”

Lin Jing was stunned.

“What the h*ll is going on?” Even he was confused.

Beside him, Feng Ning and Gao Sheng looked completely baffled.

A moment later, Lin Jing pieced things together and fell silent.

“This Gold List... really is something else.” He muttered.

“I can feel the fortune bonus too—truly.”

“Gao Sheng, didn’t you just ask why I haven’t found any treasures yet? Well, look—it’s right here.”

“The Artifact Ghost that stole my mechanized beast got injured and passed out. Then my mechanized beast dragged it back... even got temporarily upgraded by it.”

“What is this situation...”

Even Lin Jing found it absurd after saying it out loud.

Gao Sheng and Feng Ning were once again stunned.

“No way, that actually works???” Gao Sheng was dumbfounded. They had expected to pay a steep price to retrieve the Artifact Ghost, but it just wandered back on its own while unconscious?

He knew Lin Jing’s fortune from the Gold List was insane, but this was next-level insane.

He couldn’t even imagine how easy life must be for Ancient Clan Princes and Princesses.

“So, what do we do with the Artifact Ghost now?” Feng Ning asked. Getting their hands on the Artifact Ghost had been unexpectedly easy—but now there was another issue.

The Artifact Ghost… had been brought back by Lin Jing’s mechanized beast. The Heavenly Sword Gate couldn’t really claim any credit—in fact, they did absolutely nothing. Even though Gao Sheng had shared the intel and was hoping to pass the Artifact Ghost to the Heavenly Sword Gate, Feng Ning felt awkward just taking it.

“Don’t celebrate just yet.”

“What matters now isn’t how to deal with it, but rather… the one who injured the Artifact Ghost might be arriving too.”

“And the one coming... is very strong.”

Lin Jing took a deep breath, his expression suddenly grave. His divine sense was shaking his very body, and his Hidden Dragon Secret Technique issued a warning—something major was about to descend. Sure enough, the moment he spoke, the bright day turned pitch-black.

“Dao-Seeking….”

Beside him, Thunder Dragon Ancestor’s pupils shrank. He spoke with difficulty.

“Could it be...”

“The Hall Master of the Netherworld Hall—‘Emperor!’” he exclaimed.

Sure enough, two figures appeared in the sky, their auras eerie—it was the Netherworld Hall Master and the Netherworld Saintess.

“Still a step too late… the thing fell into troublesome hands.”

A trace of ghostfire flickered in the Netherworld Saintess’s eyes as she looked down at Lin Jing’s group, clearly finding the situation difficult.

It hadn’t gone as smoothly as that tomb raider claimed… while a comatose Artifact Ghost was easier to catch, it also meant others could beat them to it.

And the worst part? The ones who got there first… were two prodigies under the protection of Dao-Seeking experts…

“You two, hand over the item, and the Netherworld Hall will reward you generously.” The Netherworld Saintess spoke. Even for the sake of the ultimate artifact spirit, the Hall didn’t want to directly kill or rob Lin Jing and Feng Ning. In their eyes, those two likely didn’t even know the Artifact Ghost’s true identity.

If they could make a trade, that would be ideal.

After all, even if one could wield a Celestial Artifact, it didn’t make them invincible. The Martial Emperor could wield one, and he still wasn’t a match for the Ancient Kingdom. It was just a ticket to becoming the strongest Dao-Seeker.

One was a Demon Court Holy Maiden, the other a Dao-Seeking sword cultivator—not worth provoking lightly.

“You refuse?” Faced with the pressure of a Dao-Seeker, the Netherworld Saintess frowned. Lin Jing was holding the turtle, gaze intense, clearly not planning to hand it over. And the Cold Moon Sword Immortal was even more extreme—her eyes burning with terrifying battle intent, hair billowing, one hand already on her sword.

“Looks like it’s finally my time to make a move…” Feng Ning laughed coldly, drew her sword, and with a swing, shattered the sky. Its might was terrifying—so much so that the Saintess and Thunder Dragon’s pupils constricted. They hadn’t expected a Nascent Soul sword cultivator to suddenly unleash a Dao-Seeking level sword strike…

Even Lin Jing and Gao Sheng were shocked—because the sword wasn’t aimed at the enemy, but at them!!

“The Sky-Splitting Sword can slash open the void, teleporting cultivators—regardless of distance—straight to the Heavenly Sword Gate’s holy land. Back then, Master intended to use it to get you out of Tongtian City… and now, it’s finally being used. I’ll send you two ahead to the Heavenly Sword Gate. I’ll stay behind to cover the retreat...”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 54: Beneath the Immortal Sword
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 54: Beneath the Immortal Sword

With the swing of the Sky-Splitting Sword, the darkness was torn apart, and dawn returned.

“Dao-Seeking Sword Spirit…” the Bone Emperor and the Netherworld Saintess retreated, and the Bone Emperor immediately realized—this Cold Moon Sword Immortal’s sword held a Dao-Seeking level Sword Spirit!

Moreover, this Sword Spirit’s comprehension of spatial laws surpassed even his own!

This strike contained the ultimate mysteries of space—even though the Bone Emperor tried to stop it, he only managed to graze himself and failed to prevent it.

Every cultivator walks a different path—he specialized in soul power, cultivating without relying on the physical body. As for the Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor, the reason she had yet to reach Dao-Seeking was directly related to her chosen path of power.

The Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor specialized in the Dao of Space—a force incomparably powerful. Back during the Nascent Soul Stage, she left behind battle records. Without even stepping into the Void Mystical Stage, she had used her sword to slash open a rift in the sky. Combined with her Sword Dao, she was once considered the most powerful in offensive force among those at her level.

The night was torn apart… the Bone Emperor looked at the scratch on his arm, then at Lin Jing and the mechanical turtle who had just been teleported away, his expression darkened.

“You deserve to die.”

Thunder Dragon Ancestor realized that this lady had only sent Lin Jing and Gao Sheng away, completely ignoring him and the nine Thunder Dragon prodigies who had been dutifully pulling the carriage, and he almost burst out.

Seriously, girl, do you actually plan to fight a Dao-Seeker? You’re just Nascent Soul Stage!

The nine Thunder Dragon prodigies also broke out in a cold sweat—Who am I? Where am I? What am I supposed to do next?

“Senior Thunder Dragon, please assist me—cover the formation.” Feng Ning spoke up. The Sky-Splitting Sword had cost her too much;
she couldn’t teleport everyone. She could only send the crucial Lin Jing and the Artifact Ghost first.

As long as the Artifact Ghost was safely teleported away, she figured the Bone Emperor wouldn’t push things too far.

“Master, please aid me too.” She gripped the quasi-celestial sword that housed her Master’s slumbering Sword Spirit… And she believed, as long as she could stall for time, Lin Jing would find a way.

…………

At the same time.

Heavenly Sword Gate.

The sky suddenly tore open with an immense sword scar.

In an instant, all elders and disciples of the Heavenly Sword Gate turned their eyes to the sky.

“What is…???”

The Sect Leader of the Heavenly Sword Gate—a Void Mystical Stage sword cultivator—though not as dazzling as the Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor, was still a famed figure across the continent. His gaze burned as he looked at the two figures falling from the sword mark…

“Lin Jing? Gao Sheng???”

As the two figures appeared, the one most shocked was a Core Formation Stage sword cultivator who stood dumbfounded, completely confused as to why his two old classmates were now dropping into the Heavenly Sword Gate!

This person was none other than Yuan Jing, a sword cultivator from the Dragon Palace group of Hidden Dragon Academy.

“The Sky-Splitting Sword… so it had this kind of move…”

Landing in the holy land of the Heavenly Sword Gate, Lin Jing’s mind reeled. This move wasn’t even complete yet.

He could vaguely guess the original design intent of the shattered technique left behind by the Sect Master of the Sky-Splitting Sword Sect. The Thunder Dragon Ancestor probably aimed to create a space-type sword technique that could slash open space and teleport himself or anyone to a distant location.

However, it was still immature. Right now, it could only teleport beings other than the user to a specified destination.

Even so, it was powerful enough. Back then, even the Yin Corpse Sect’s spatial teleportation treasure hadn’t produced a similar effect.

“Gao Sheng of the Gao Clan…”

“Holy Son of the Demon Court?”

No sooner had Lin Jing and Gao Sheng landed than hundreds of sword cultivators surrounded them in a formation. At the forefront stood the Sect Leader of the Heavenly Sword Gate, a handsome middle-aged man in white robes. He scrutinized the two, quickly recognizing them.

“Why have you appeared here? Give me an explanation.”

Clearly, he was unaware of the Sky-Splitting Sword move.

Lin Jing glanced at the Sect Leader and cupped his fists slightly.

“Senior, it’s a special circumstance. From here on, please let this man explain. I must not be disturbed.”

After speaking, he looked toward the silver immortal sword towering into the clouds at the heart of the Heavenly Sword Gate and sat down cross-legged.

That silver sword was the Celestial Sword passed down from ancient times—Heavenly Sword Gate’s Guardian Sword.

It had always stood in the center of the sect, a symbol of their heritage. Though no one had ever managed to wield it, it was undoubtedly the belief of all the sect’s sword cultivators. Every disciple aspired to one day surpass all their predecessors and draw the sword, becoming the strongest swordsman.

Cold Moon Sword Immortal had teleported them here using the Sky-Splitting Sword—Lin Jing naturally couldn’t let her face the enemy alone.

Although she had inherited the full sword realm of the Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor through Sword Nurturing Technique, it was still inherited power. The Bone Emperor, however, was an old monster who had stayed at Dao-Seeking Stage for thousands of years. Her safety wasn’t guaranteed.

Now, with the Second Ancient Emperor, the Demon Emperor, the Martial Emperor, and the Buddha Lord all rumored to be gravely injured, it was likely no one could support her in time. That meant the only one who could aid Cold Moon Sword Immortal… was Lin Jing himself.

“She sent me to the Heavenly Sword Gate, probably so I wouldn’t burn through my lifespan to fight the Bone Emperor…”

“Sure, I can temporarily reach Dao-Seeking, but after that, I’d probably only live a few more minutes.”

“Her real intention must be…”

“To see if I can control the Artifact Ghost, and then wield the Heavenly Sword Gate’s Celestial Sword—using its might to intimidate the Bone Emperor...”

“Smart woman.”

Lin Jing sat down to cultivate, trying to see if he could use the Divine Control Technique to take command of the unconscious Artifact Ghost, and through it, wield the Celestial Sword.

“With a Nascent Soul Stage base, and consuming some lifespan to cast Divine Control… maybe I really can manipulate this weakened Artifact Ghost.”

He held the mechanical turtle, cutting off all external interference. Gao Sheng, on the other hand, had to deal with the surrounding sword cultivators alone. He swallowed nervously.

“No need for so many people, really.”

“Sect Leader Senior, let’s have everyone back off first—I’ll explain things slowly.”

“At the last Grand Celestial Gathering, my old man even served you tea. We’re not bad guys…” he said, glancing at the clearly grim-faced Sect Leader.

…………

Beyond the Heavens.

World Boundary Wall.

After setting up training arcs for their respective prodigies, many Dao-Seeking experts refocused on reinforcing the World Boundary.

Even though the breach by the Demonic Sky Sect had been orchestrated by them, they couldn’t discount the possibility of a real break-in, so they continued strengthening the barrier.

“Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor… is she slacking off? Why’d she only leave an avatar to reinforce the wall?”

“What kind of training arc did she design for her disciple that even her true body had to go oversee…”

Some Dao-Seeking experts were extremely curious. But before long, they all felt powerful battle ripples spreading from the world below.

A Dao-Seeking battle…

The experts were shocked, turning their attention there—and once they saw what was happening, they were stunned.

D*mn it, that Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor… in order to train her disciple, she actually incarnated herself as a Sword Spirit and let her disciple personally fight a Dao-Seeking demonic cultivator!

“She’s lost her mind.” This was overkill—even for one’s own child, this level of dedication was rare!

And that opponent—it was the Bone Emperor. That skeletal freak didn’t seem like the kind to fake a fight… could this be a real duel?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 55: Sword Sect and Dao-Seeking
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 55: Sword Sect and Dao-Seeking

Heavenly Sword Gate.

The Sect Leader of the Heavenly Sword Gate dismissed the unrelated onlookers, ready to hear Gao Sheng’s explanation.

With wild gestures and animated storytelling, Gao Sheng gave a vivid and simple account…

“You brat, why didn’t you say so earlier!” The Sect Leader’s expression changed dramatically.

“Feng Ning, that girl… is wielding the Sword Spirit of our Ancestor… and fighting the Bone Emperor?”

“I’ve already said it as fast as I could.” Gao Sheng was a mess himself now, not knowing what to do.

“Sister Feng Ning—that’s my real sister.” He hadn’t expected her to send Lin Jing and the Artifact Ghost to safety and even bring him along.

Such immense kindness—he couldn’t just stand by and watch something happen to them!

Now, he pinned his hopes on the Sect Leader.

“The Ancient Kingdom and the Western Desert are worlds apart. By the time support from the Heavenly Sword Gate arrives in the Western Desert… the battle will likely be over.”

“Besides… the Heavenly Sword Gate isn’t qualified to interfere in Dao-Seeking battles.”

“If His Majesty the Ancient Emperor were present, there might be a way. But according to reports, the Lord of the Demon Court, the Ancient Emperor, the Buddha Lord, and the Martial Emperor—all Dao-Seeking experts—have been severely injured due to matters beyond the heavens and are now in secluded recovery. Currently, the Ancient Prince is temporarily managing state affairs. Though the Prince is also a peerless talent, he likely cannot intervene either…”

The Sect Leader didn’t want their own Dao-Seeking Ancestor and top prodigy to fall in the Western Desert.

The future of the Heavenly Sword Gate rested on those two.

“Whether we have a way or not, I must enter the capital and try. You, come with me—we’ll request an audience with the Prince.”

At this moment, all he could think of was to seek help from the Ancient Clan.

The Tianyuan Ancient Kingdom had stood for ten thousand years—even if the Second Ancient Emperor couldn’t act, he believed the clan’s deep foundation would still allow them to step into a Dao-Seeking-level conflict.

After all, the Netherworld Hall was an enemy of the Ancient Kingdom, and the Heavenly Sword Gate was a sect under its protection. If the Ancient Clan had the ability, he was certain they would lend a hand.

“Alright, I’ll go with you. But let’s leave Brother Lin here—he might have thought of something. It’s better not to disturb him.” Gao Sheng glanced at Lin Jing, who was meditating beneath the Heavenly Sword. He took a deep breath.

He had only given the Sect Leader a basic explanation and hadn’t mentioned the Artifact Ghost.

Gao Sheng wasn’t stupid—he could roughly guess what Lin Jing wanted to do… and there was no way he’d tell the Sect Leader that.

After all, Lin Jing was about to try controlling the Heavenly Sword Gate’s Celestial Sword—that would be far too impolite.

…………

At the same time.

Lin Jing was fully focused—Hidden Dragon Secret Technique boost, Fortune Gold List boost… and now… the Pine Leaf Mouse emerged from the gourd and brought out a green Bodhi Tree, sitting cross-legged atop it, acting as protector for Lin Jing beneath the tree.

Triple comprehension bonuses elevated Lin Jing’s level of insight to the current peak.

That green Bodhi Tree—before the Heavenly Sword Avatar and Gao Sheng had left Bodhi Temple, the Abbot had ultimately refused a trade and lent a green Bodhi Tree to the Heavenly Sword and the Demon Court, sending it to Immortal-Burying Volcano.

It was thanks to that green Bodhi Tree that the Heavenly Sword had been able, after fighting the Martial Emperor’s successor, to comprehend spiritual sense usage and certain Beast Taming techniques within just a few days.

Now, Lin Jing’s comprehension had surpassed even that time—although the boost wasn’t as strong as from a golden Bodhi Tree, it was no less effective than a silver one.

With all these boosts, Lin Jing’s understanding of the Core Formation Divine Ability, Divine Control Technique, surged. Initially, he could at most consume ten years of lifespan to enhance his Beast Taming prowess.

But now, with improvements across the board, in just a short time, Lin Jing pushed the technique’s lifespan consumption cap to over a century.

“If I really can control the Celestial Sword, not just the Netherworld Hall—Poseidon Palace, Misty Forest—they’ll all pay…”

“When the time comes, I’ll follow Gu Qianqiu’s example and plunder what I can. As long as the resources gained offset the lifespan cost, it’s not a loss!”

“That should be enough!”

Lin Jing suddenly opened his eyes and instantly burned sixty years of lifespan, boosting his Beast Taming comprehension by an entire major realm atop his Nascent Soul foundation.

Though the boost wouldn’t last long, it was enough for this trial.

“Control!”

He gave a low shout, and spiritual energy surged. Though the Sect Leader had already left with Gao Sheng, he’d left behind a group of strong Heavenly Sword Gate cultivators to monitor Lin Jing.

To them, Lin Jing was a hot potato—after all, he was the Holy Son of the Demon Court. Mishandling the situation could easily spark a diplomatic incident.

“He opened his eyes…” The sword cultivators supervising him hadn’t expected Lin Jing to end his meditation so soon. As for his previous use of the Buddhist Sect’s rare plant, the Bodhi Tree, they hadn’t understood that at all.

But what happened next made even less sense.

Lin Jing raised the mechanical turtle in his hands—it slowly floated upward. Then he stretched out a hand and pulled out a soft, jellyfish-like pale purple spirit from inside.

The moment it appeared, the cultivators’ expressions changed. From it, they felt spiritual fluctuations on par with Peak Void Mystical Stage.

“What is that??” A Heavenly Sword Gate elder gasped. What he saw was something resembling a Void Mystical-level soul—being casually controlled by Lin Jing??

“YAAHH!!!”

In the next instant, the unconscious Ghost Mother was forcibly awakened under the power of Divine Control. Her aura exploded with terrifying might. Instantly—clang!—all the sword cultivators were shocked to see their swords rise uncontrollably into the air, pointing at Lin Jing.

“You’ve got to be kidding.” One by one, they tried to retrieve their swords—many of which were blood-bound natal spiritual swords—but they refused to obey, brimming with sword intent and aimed directly at Lin Jing.

“!!!” Everyone was stunned.

They had no intention of hurting the Holy Son of the Demon Court!

Why were their swords turning on him?!

If the Holy Son were cut down here by a swarm of rogue swords… it would be catastrophic.

“Grand Elder, what the h*ll is going on??” Someone turned to a nearby Void Mystical Stage sword cultivator who had been sipping wine. Even he looked horrified now and tossed aside his gourd. “How should I know? My spiritual sword even has your sword spirit grandma inside, and now it won’t listen to me!”

He grabbed for his sword—only to be pushed back by it.

“That thing’s the culprit!” The Void Mystical sword cultivator glared at the Ghost Mother Lin Jing was controlling.

“Behave!”

Meanwhile, Lin Jing’s eyes flashed coldly. “Help me. Those people want to capture you. If you fall into their hands, you won’t end well. But if you listen to me, we can talk about anything afterward.”

Lin Jing’s Divine Control Technique didn’t just enslave—it could also distort will, akin to brainwashing, to fulfill command conditions. Under the intensified force of the technique, the weakened Artifact Ghost was silent for a moment, then chose to comply.

After all, no matter what happened, it would be better than being sealed by that altar again.

The moment he gained control over the Artifact Ghost, Lin Jing took a deep breath. Instantly, he felt his mastery over “artifact-type” control leap to an entirely new level—like comprehending the Dao beneath the sword!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 56: Wielding the Immortal Sword
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 56: Wielding the Immortal Sword

"This feeling... is truly incredible."

Lin Jing was filled with disbelief. His base cultivation was only Core Formation Stage,

But through the Heavenly Demon Physique, his temporary cultivation had reached Nascent Soul Transformation Stage!

The Divine Control Technique, which consumes lifespan to enhance one's mastery over artifacts, stacked with everything else, had successfully allowed him to momentarily control the weakened Ghost Mother, a Void Mystical-level being.

And this Ghost Mother was a special case—though her cultivation wasn't high, she was a war-spirit artifact created by the Celestial Palace using immortal methods, specifically designed to suppress magical artifacts.

High-grade, top-grade, quasi-celestial artifacts… to her, they meant nothing.

Her most terrifying feature—she could even temporarily become the artifact spirit of a Celestial Artifact without one and control it.

All of these stacking factors let Lin Jing, for a brief moment, touch the apex of power—like a Dao-Seeker.

"Go!"

In the next moment, Lin Jing formed a hand seal. Under his control, all the floating swords in the Heavenly Sword Gate returned to their masters.

Then, under the stunned eyes of countless cultivators, the Ghost Mother he was controlling… directly flew into the sect’s Celestial Sword, Wen Tian.

"Borrowing this sword."

"It’ll be returned shortly!"

Lin Jing spoke.

Immediately after, Wen Tian, the Celestial Sword that had lain dormant in the Heavenly Sword Gate for countless years, its spirit lost in the ancient war, suddenly erupted in dazzling brilliance and a resonant sword cry.

At that moment, disciples of all levels and ages gaped in disbelief.

"The Celestial Sword... it's..." one elder after another stared blankly.

The Celestial Sword... was awakening.

And not just within the Heavenly Sword Gate—

Having just reached the Tianyuan Imperial Capital, the Sect Leader of the Heavenly Sword Gate dropped Gao Sheng in shock, turning to look back at the sect—at the sword light soaring into the heavens.

"That’s... the light of the Celestial Sword???"

"What’s going on in the sect?"

“Holy s**t, Brother Lin is incredible.” Gao Sheng, falling from the sky, gasped in awe. He summoned his Flying Dragon Horse, mounted it, and looked back toward the Heavenly Sword Gate. Had he really done it?

At the same moment, the Ancient Prince stepped out of the palace, staring solemnly at the sword light rising from the direction of the Heavenly Sword Gate, puzzled.

"Could it be that someone in the sect has finally drawn the Celestial Sword...? Was it the Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor?" he muttered.

By now, every cultivator at or above the Void Mystical Stage in the Ancient Kingdom had sensed the movement.

The awakening of Wen Tian had sent tremors through the land.

The revival of a Celestial Artifact was always an earthshaking event.

Even beyond the heavens, the Dao-Seekers, who had just been watching the battle between the Bone Emperor and the Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor’s sword spirit, now frowned and turned their gazes toward the Ancient Kingdom.

Though the distance and protective formations over the Heavenly Sword Gate obscured vision, they could still sense that an immense Celestial Sword power had emerged from that region.

"Unbelievable… is that Wen Tian, the Celestial Sword of the Heavenly Sword Sect?"

Du Xing, Lord of the Sword Ancestor City, spoke in surprise.

His ability to control a Celestial Artifact came from the fact that the artifact spirit of the Sword Ancestor had once guided his cultivation. Through the refining methods taught by the spirit, he had also repaired the damaged Sword Ancestor—an incredibly rare, mutual relationship.

But as far as he knew, other than his Sword Ancestor, almost all Celestial Artifacts salvaged from the ancient era had lost their spirits.

Aside from rare cases like Du Xingdong, most required ancient immortal methods to activate—methods largely inaccessible even in the Upper Realm.

So how on earth was that boy from the Heavenly Sword Gate activating a Celestial Artifact...?

“No wonder that girl dared to fight the Bone Emperor with her disciple. She can actually activate a Celestial Sword? That’s insane.” The Whale Emperor of Poseidon Palace said.

“Right? Even the Ancient Clan hasn’t activated their Celestial Artifacts yet,” laughed the Witch God of the Cloud Sea Mountains, glancing toward the Second Ancient Emperor. The implication was clear: the Ancient Kingdom might have just birthed a new, uncontrollable powerhouse.

Until now, the Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor alone wasn’t enough to compete with ancient powers like the Ancient Kingdom—

But if she could wield a Celestial Sword, it changed everything.

She’d qualify to enter the ranks of the strongest Dao-Seekers.

“What’s going on…” muttered the Second Ancient Emperor, his expression shifting, until—

The Wen Tian Sword burst from the Heavenly Sword Gate and flew toward the Western Desert.

Only then did the Dao-Seekers see clearly.

It wasn’t the Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor summoning the sword from afar—

It was someone else, riding the sword, headed for the Western Desert, sword intent erupting.

On the sword stood a youth with long hair fluttering and a Pine Leaf Mouse on his shoulder.

His eyes gleamed with sharpness as he looked to the horizon.

“Lin Jing of the Longevity Physique?”

“Holy Son of the Demon Court?”

“It’s him?”

“Why can he wield the Celestial Sword?!”

This scene left even the Dao-Seekers stunned—none of them had expected this.

And within the Heavenly Sword Gate, elders and disciples alike were speechless in horror.

They watched the fading light of the flying sword in complete silence, collapsing to the ground in disbelief.

“Lord of the Demon Court, give me an explanation.” The Second Ancient Emperor’s glare locked onto the Demon Emperor.

Though the situation was unclear, the image spoke for itself.

A sect-suppressing Celestial Artifact of the Ancient Kingdom had been taken by the Holy Son of the Demon Court!

This, the Second Ancient Emperor could not accept.

“I don’t know either!” The Demon Emperor, tiger-headed and dazed, pressed both hands to her head. She was just as bewildered as the other Dao-Seekers.

“Lin Jing is your Demon Court’s Holy Son—how do you not know what he’s doing?”

“I really don’t!” the Demon Emperor barked in frustration. “I’d like to know what the hll is going on!”

This Saint Son could wield a Celestial Artifact—where did he learn that?

She had been planning to give Lin Jing a quasi-celestial emperor weapon as protection.

Before she even got the chance—he already knew how to wield a Celestial Artifact?!

What kind of joke was this?

She had been peerless her entire life, yet two things had always haunted her:

One, she couldn’t transform her form.

Two, she couldn’t wield the Celestial Artifact left behind by the Demon Court.

Now, the transformation issue had been solved—by Lin Jing.

Next… was she supposed to ask the Holy Son to teach her how to wield a Celestial Artifact?

Absurd.

"The direction of the sword... Martial Emperor City, Western Desert… is he planning to ride the Celestial Sword to support the Sky-Splitting Sword Spirit?" the Martial Emperor’s eyes gleamed.

"Looks like it." The Sun Buddha Lord sighed. "It seems the Demon Court’s Holy Son is the true chosen of this realm’s fortune. From humble origins, he’s come this far—much harder than those born into greatness."

The Bone Emperor, Lord of the Netherworld Hall... also a Dao-Seeking expert.

He, too, could sense what the others sensed.

The Artifact Ghost had escaped, and the furious Bone Emperor had resolved to capture the Sword Spirit of the Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor to compensate.

But before that could happen,

A flash of Celestial Sword power burst from the Ancient Kingdom, and a terrifying sword light crossed from its heart—aimed to pierce him!

“D*mn you, beast-taming brat!!!”

Had he had eyeballs, they’d be bloodshot now.

This opportunity to control a Celestial Artifact should have belonged to the Netherworld Hall!

How did this beast-taming brat deserve it?!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 57: They All Have to Die!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 57: They All Have to Die!

"Master, why are you awake!"

At this moment, Feng Ning was fighting Emperor with intense ferocity.

The feeling of casually wielding her master’s Sword Dao realm left Cold Moon Sword Immortal completely enthralled.

But before she could truly enjoy the battle, a figure emerged from her sword—who else could it be but the Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor?

The moment the Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor appeared, Feng Ning instantly lost her power, her combat strength falling back to its original level.

"I told you, when the Sword Nurturing Technique is activated, my injuries recover very quickly."

Dressed in simple hemp robes, the Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor called her sword back and nodded as she spoke.

"From here on out, leave it to your Master." The reason it was so fast—was because she hadn’t actually been injured at all.

If this continued, it would definitely be disadvantageous to her. But the Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor wasn’t too worried—because once the situation turned bad here, she believed those people outside the heavens would make their move.

After all, everyone’s script included luring out these misfit Dao-Seekers and eliminating them one by one.

The Bone Emperor was one of those misfits.

Its master was an old demonic figure from the Immortal Alliance. That old demon had two most outstanding disciples—one was the Corpse Emperor, the other, the Emperor.

When the Immortal Alliance was destroyed, the Corpse Emperor and the Emperor were still unripe, but not long after, they achieved Dao-Seeking and founded the Yin Corpse Sect and the Netherworld Hall respectively.

The top forces on the Tianyuan Continent today had all more or less participated in the war to destroy the Immortal Alliance. While the main force was the First Ancient Emperor, the other sects had also contributed significantly.

Because of this, those with “pre-dynasty ties” like the Corpse Emperor and the Emperor were never able to fully integrate into today’s mainstream cultivation world.

Unlike places like Qingzhou and Martial Emperor City, which, though still in competition with the Ancient Kingdom, could still engage in trade—buying pills from Qingzhou, magical artifacts from Martial Emperor City...

You could say that even demon beast factions were more accepted by righteous sects than these two demonic sects.

After all, the downfall of the former sects of these two Dao-Seeking demonic cultivators was inextricably linked to many of the current top forces.

The First Ancient Emperor had considered exterminating the Corpse Emperor and the Emperor entirely later on—but at that time, the Demonic Sky Sect emerged and was even more rampant, drawing away most of the focus for the righteous path... plus, the Corpse Emperor and the Emperor had countless life-saving techniques, making it nearly impossible to truly erase them.

In fact, the situation with the Gu God Sect was not much different.

The sect’s founder, Duan Zhong, was originally an ordinary mortal, and it was only by chance that he obtained the legacy of the Immortal Alliance, gradually embarking on the path to immortality.

Back then, the Immortal Alliance led by cultivators like Luo Miao with the Creation Immortal Physique, had indeed destroyed the evil Immortal Palace and saved the continent as the righteous leaders. But with time passing and the sages gone, decline became inevitable.

Many wicked forbidden arts related to the human body, like Gu arts, were developed during that stage by some cultivators of the Immortal Alliance who sought power at any cost.

"If they don’t show up soon, something big’s going to happen..." the Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor muttered to herself, staring at the Bone Emperor.

Feng Ning might not have sensed it, but she did.

The Celestial Sword of the Heavenly Sword Gate had been activated!

And she didn’t need to guess who it was.

The Holy Son of the Demon Court—Lin Jing!

Right now, just two words: Regret it!

Why did she allow her disciple to teleport Lin Jing and the Artifact Ghost to the Heavenly Sword Gate?

At first, she didn’t take it seriously at all, never even considered the possibility that Lin Jing could control the Artifact Ghost or wield the Celestial Sword.

After all, the Netherworld Hall coveted the Artifact Ghost because it was a spiritual entity, and the Hall was a master of soul manipulation.

A Dao-Seeking stage Emperor being able to control a Peak Void Mystical Stage Artifact Ghost and use it to wield a Celestial Artifact—that, the Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor could understand.

That was at least plausible.

But Lin Jing? A Beast Taming Cultivator who only reached the Nascent Soul Transformation Stage by burning his own lifespan—how the h*ll was he controlling a Peak Void Mystical Stage Artifact Ghost?!

Even though she knew Lin Jing probably meant well, trying to use the Celestial Sword to support them, the Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor still felt a faint sadness in her heart. The Celestial Sword... the Celestial Sword she had dreamed of for so long, was now being trampled underfoot by a junior like Lin Jing.

"That brat... surely he wouldn’t not return the Celestial Sword, right?" You couldn’t blame the Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor for thinking this way—the temptation of a Celestial Artifact was just too strong. And Lin Jing could now wield it with ease!

"Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor..."

With the real Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor now present, the Emperor grew even more silent.

Earlier, when her state was unstable, he could still deal with her. But now that the real one had awakened, it was clearly going to be tough.

Sword cultivators... were just too effective against ghost cultivators.

Ghost cultivators weren’t truly powerful to begin with—they were fundamentally incomplete existences, good only at bullying those who didn’t specialize in soul cultivation.

Their only advantage was longevity.

But every powerful sword cultivator had a soul like a Sword Spirit—sharp and fierce, giving ghost cultivators no edge.

What’s more, a powerful Celestial Sword was already on its way from the Ancient Kingdom.

"So fast!"

Suddenly, the fluctuations of the Celestial Sword were growing stronger and stronger. After a moment’s thought, the Emperor chose to retreat directly, vanishing into the void. Seeing this, the Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor didn’t try to stop him.

BOOM!!!

Until the Wen Tian Sword descended from the sky, and Lin Jing atop it looked at her and Feng Ning hovering in midair, then couldn’t help but say: "Senior Sky-Splitting... your injuries... you’ve already fully recovered?"

"Where’s the Bone Emperor??"

"It saw that I had recovered, and with you arriving wielding the Celestial Sword, it ran." said the Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor.

"Senior, can you track where it escaped to? We can’t just let it go like that." said Lin Jing—after all, wielding the Celestial Sword like this, he was literally burning his lifespan!

The Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor shook her head. "It’s not really worth it. Even if the two of us catch up, we can’t kill the Bone Emperor. The reason the three major demon sects have survived till now is because each of them has a completely unsolvable escape technique. The Emperor’s soul-splitting secret art lets it divide its soul into countless fragments and attach them to different vessels. Unless all the fragments are destroyed at once, killing just one is meaningless. It can still resurrect from another bone."

"Back then, the Bone Emperor was killed multiple times by His Majesty the Ancient Emperor, but he could never completely eradicate it... so, the Netherworld Hall should be left to that one."

She hinted at the Buddha Lord of the Solar Temple in the Buddhist Sect—he had long been researching how to completely destroy the Bone Emperor.

The Buddhist secret arts were even more effective against undead than the Sword Dao. Compared to them figuring it out, the Buddha Lord surely had already locked onto the exposed Netherworld Hall.

Lin Jing nodded and didn’t push further. Standing atop the Celestial Sword, he then said something that deeply stabbed at the Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor’s heart.

"Alright then, but Senior, let me borrow the Celestial Sword for half a day. I’ll return it when I’m done—I’m heading to deliver two strikes to the Poseidon Palace and Misty Forest!"

Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor: ...

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 58: Not Playing With You Anymore!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 58: Not Playing With You Anymore!

"Deliver two strikes to the Poseidon Palace and Misty Forest..."

As Lin Jing said this, it wasn’t just the Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor who was worried he might damage the Celestial Sword—even outside the continent, the Yin-Yang Whale Emperor of Poseidon Palace and the Forest Sea Dragon Emperor of Misty Forest were both startled.

"Sh*t, this brat..."

"Is he full and bored or what? Instead of continuing to hunt down the Emperor, why is he coming at us with two sword strikes?"

"What did we do to provoke him?"

Granted, they were both Dao-Seeking Demon Emperors, but nobody wanted to get slashed by a Celestial Sword for no reason.

The Martial Emperor himself wasn’t top-tier in terms of cultivation or combat strength, but with the Celestial Artifact that was Martial Emperor City, he instantly became one of the continent’s peak Dao-Seekers.

And that city was a defensive-type Celestial Artifact.

So the Yin-Yang Whale Emperor and the Forest Sea Dragon Emperor wanted nothing to do with Lin Jing, this mad dog.

It wasn’t out of fear—it was just unnecessary. They had no grudge with him.

Even if they could take two strikes, what was the point? For a Dao-Seeking Demon Emperor to spar with a mere Core Formation brat, no matter the outcome, they’d end up looking ridiculous.

Not to mention, the Demon Emperor of the Demon Court was present—there was no way they could actually harm the Holy Son of the Demon Court.

And if they didn’t show up, then inside the Poseidon Palace and Misty Forest, there wouldn’t be any demon beasts capable of surviving a strike from a Celestial Artifact.

Even a Void Mystical Demon Ancestor would die.

"Demon Court Lord, this is your fault for coming up with this stupid idea—getting us to go along with your act. Now look what’s happening. You’d better fix this."

"Yeah, tell your Holy Son to stop showing off. Wielding a Celestial Artifact like that must cost him dearly—he might drop dead young at this rate."

"This is pointless. We’re not playing with you anymore." The Yin-Yang Whale Emperor and the Forest Sea Dragon Emperor urged the Demon Emperor of the Demon Court, making it clear they didn’t want to continue with this so-called training scenario.

D*mn it—other Dao-Seekers design their trial scenarios to pressure their own prodigies. But you? You're putting pressure on us.

"Ah, this... this..." At this moment, the Lord of the Demon Court also realized she might’ve screwed up. From the beginning, nothing had gone according to her script.

She had intended for Lin Jing to awaken the Heavenly Demon Physique under pressure... but after investigating, it seemed that he had already awakened it before the script even started.

She figured they could just muddle through the trial to fire up some competition between Lin Jing and Xu Qingyao—but who knew that in the blink of an eye, Lin Jing would be riding a Celestial Artifact, threatening to strike the heartlands of two Dao-Seeking forces and scaring the hell out of everyone.

"This brat, why is he so hard to manage? Can’t he just follow the script for once!" The Demon Emperor gritted her fangs, then let out a long sigh.

Meanwhile.

Lin Jing, ignoring the Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor’s attempt to stop him, continued riding the Celestial Sword toward Qingzhou—he was in a hurry.

This trip to Qingzhou wasn’t just to intimidate the two Demon Emperor factions—he also had to find the Demon Court’s own Celestial Artifact and take control of it.

That way, before the Demon Emperor fully recovered, he—who could burn lifespan to activate a Celestial Artifact—would still have the means to keep suppressing his enemies.

............

Qingzhou—territory of the Demon Court Holy Son, at Immortal Prison Mountain.

To pressure the Demon Court and force them to hand over the Transformation Technique, Lin Jing’s base had naturally become the prime target for Poseidon Palace and Misty Forest to encircle.

Two Void Mystical Demon Ancestors were stationed around Immortal Prison Mountain, unleashing pressure.

Though they hadn’t launched an attack, their presence still heavily impacted Lin Jing’s faction.

For instance, the mosquito beast army led by the Mosquito Woman hadn’t gone out hunting in a long time—trapped inside Immortal Prison Mountain and unable to move.

But these two Void Mystical Demon Ancestors also didn’t intend to escalate further—firstly, because Lin Jing was in the Western Desert and not present here, and secondly, because a Void Mystical-level demon power fluctuation was coming from within the mountain...

Since the main target wasn’t around, there was no way the Ancestors from Poseidon Palace and Misty Forest would randomly start a fight with another Void Mystical entity inside—not worth it. There weren’t even any treasures here.

Inside Immortal Prison Mountain.

The Grand Elder Huan Li of Thousand Illusions Mountain was living in constant anxiety. Thankfully, the Beast Taming Sect’s Void Mystical Tree Ancestor was here—otherwise, she wouldn’t know how to help Lin Jing protect his home.

"Captain, if this keeps up... we might not reach the minimum capture quota set by His Highness the Holy Son..." The Mosquito Woman was also deeply worried and turned to Gu Qianqiu, captain of the Demon-Hunting Squad.

"Senior Tree Ancestor, maybe the two of us should just eliminate those two Demon Ancestors outside?" Gu Qianqiu looked up at the towering tree atop the mountain.

"Thanks to your help, I’ve now broken through to the Nascent Soul Stage and gained a much longer lifespan. Combined with the Heavenly Demon Physique, stepping into the Void Mystical realm is much easier... with your power, we might not lose to those two outside."

"Nope, not fighting." Senior Mo Pine replied with an annoyed tone, "What’s the point? They haven’t even attacked yet."

"Sigh, I was hoping to go out for a bit. What the hell is this mess..."

"Relax, those two out there have no intention of fighting. Since that’s the case, just wait—there’ll be a turning point."

"I don’t buy it. With such a huge threat from the Demonic Sky Realm, and these guys still have time for internal strife? Are they nuts? As long as the Dao-Seekers of the Tianyuan Realm aren’t idiots, uniting against a common enemy and leveraging the Golden Era to develop peacefully is the only sane strategy!!!"

"Senior, what do you mean?" Gu Qianqiu didn’t get it.

Mo Pine spoke slowly and wisely, "From my experience, these people just love stirring up trouble when they’ve got nothing better to do. This whole situation could very well be orchestrated by those Dao-Seekers—just to put some pressure on the next generation and give them a chance to gain experience.

If I were in their shoes, I’d probably do the same thing!"

"You saw the Fortune Gold List—Lin Jing is already up to rank six. A Core Formation cultivator fighting Nascent Souls—it must be because he’s awakened the Heavenly Demon Physique.

And it probably happened under pressure from this exact situation."

"The Demon Emperor really is a damn genius. If I were her, I’d train Lin Jing the same way."

"Is that so..." Gu Qianqiu muttered, still worried about Lin Jing, who was far off in the Western Desert. After all, this was just Senior Mo Pine’s guess.

"Don’t worry. It’s probably exactly that. And even if it’s not, you don’t need to worry about that brat. He’s too important to the Demon Court. Even if the Demon Court Lord were seriously injured, she’d still drag herself up, half-dead if need be, just to keep him safe."

"But I still trust my gut. As a demonic cultivator, never trust just what your eyes see or your ears hear—use your own experience to understand the essence of things."

"Sure, Lin Jing’s situation in the Western Desert might look rough right now—probably running from strong enemies all over the place—but that kind of state is exactly what’ll push him even further..." Mo Pine hadn’t even finished speaking when he felt the wave of the Celestial Sword arrive in Qingzhou. He stared in silence as Lin Jing, riding the Celestial Sword and cutting across the sea by the shortest route, descended into Qingzhou.

And then he froze—

Wait a second, what the h*ll is going on?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 59: Imperial Weapon, Celestial Artifact
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 59: Imperial Weapon, Celestial Artifact

“Lord of the Demon Court, stop him, he’s reached the Eastern Sea!”

The Celestial Sword flew through the air, arriving at the edge of the Eastern Sea. The Yin-Yang Whale Emperor urged anxiously,

If this goes on… the consequences will be unimaginable!

“Don’t rush me, I’m organizing my words,” muttered the Demon Emperor.

At that moment, on the edge of the Eastern Sea, within the Immortal Prison Mountain.

Lin Jing’s entrance truly shocked Mo Pine.

Isn’t that the Celestial Sword from the Heavenly Sword Gate?

Why is that thing under Lin Jing’s feet… for a moment, he lost the ability to think.

Their eyes were vacant—living for half their lives, they’d never seen anything like this!

A sword larger than a city...

“What are you standing there for? Run! You want to get slashed by the Celestial Sword?”

The Demon Emperor’s voice echoed in their ears. Though the two Void Mystical Demon Ancestors had no idea what just happened, they ran faster than anyone!

They even gained new insights into the Dao of Space from the experience.

The Celestial Sword’s power was far too terrifying. Even from a great distance, they could feel the threat of death.

At the same time, Lin Jing sensed two Demon Ancestors fleeing from his territory.

He didn’t even have to think—he knew it was the ones from Poseidon Palace and the Misty Forest, coming to his turf to cause trouble.

A cold glint flashed in Lin Jing’s eyes.

His main goal in returning was actually to contact the Demon Court’s various Demon Ancestors to locate where the Court’s Celestial Artifact was hidden.

But now that he’d seen the enemies—how could he not strike?

“Since you’re here, stay.” Atop the Celestial Sword, Lin Jing was about to command the swordlight to slash at the two Demon Ancestors when suddenly, the Tiger Fang he carried finally awakened and spoke.

“Spare the demons.”

“Huh, Your Majesty the Demon Emperor??”

The Demon Emperor had revived. Lin Jing was surprised, but he didn’t stop his hand—if he delayed any longer, the enemies would escape.

“Don’t kill, don’t kill,” the Demon Emperor said.

“The moment you successfully wielded the Celestial Sword, I awakened. The Whale Emperor and Dragon Emperor have sensed everything.”

“They’ve already admitted fault and promised compensation to the Demon Court and to you.”

“The lifespan you expended will all be compensated tenfold in various resources.”

“Given the current state of the Tianyuan Realm and the Demon Court, it’s not the time to trigger a war. Let’s call it off.”

“Huh?” Lin Jing was surprised. They’re just admitting fault this easily?

He thought he’d have to fully unleash the power of a Celestial Artifact to shock these Demon Emperors into submission…

But…

“Your Majesty the Demon Emperor, I’ve already burned my lifespan. How can I just let it go?” Lin Jing felt the pain of lost years.

“What do you want then?”

“Can I use the Celestial Artifact to rob the remaining Rare Gu from the Gu God Sect? Also, among the resources compensated by Poseidon Palace, the water-type Deathbane Gu must be included.”

“Fine, I support that. Unleash your pent-up anger on the Gu God Sect! Go without worry, don’t fear the Gu God—if he dares to retaliate, I and the Yin-Yang Whale Emperor, and the Forest Sea Dragon Emperor will all back you up.” The Demon Emperor nodded, both hands raised in approval.

Lin Jing: ???

At the same time, the Demon Emperor looked toward the Yin-Yang Whale Emperor in the heavens, signaling that the Deathbane Gu now belonged to the Demon Court.

“Fine, fine.” The Yin-Yang Whale Emperor, a beast stronger than the Gu God, casually gave the Rare Gu to the Demon Court, eager to end the farce quickly.

Rare Gu might be valuable to those at or below the Void Mystical Stage, but for Dao-Seeking experts, they weren’t all that significant.

“You Misty Forest folks have to contribute something too, like a large number of rare herbs and woods,” the Demon Emperor said, looking toward the Forest Sea Dragon Emperor.

The Forest Sea Dragon Emperor was silent for a moment, then waved a claw—fine, fine, he also wanted the farce to end.

With promises from both the Yin-Yang Whale Emperor and the Forest Sea Dragon Emperor, the Demon Court’s Lord was more than satisfied.

Still, the Demon Emperor understood—they agreed so quickly because they were clearly eyeing the Transformation Technique and wanted to cozy up to the Demon Court.

“Now then, are you only able to control this Celestial Sword, or can you wield any Celestial Artifact?” the Demon Emperor asked Lin Jing.

“All of them,” Lin Jing replied from atop the Celestial Sword.

“Good, then return the Celestial Sword of the Heavenly Sword Gate. Use the Demon Court’s Celestial Artifact instead.”

Refocusing on Lin Jing, the Demon Emperor, under the Second Ancient Emperor’s gaze, intended for Lin Jing to return the Celestial Sword.

The Demon Court didn’t lack Celestial Artifacts—no need to use someone else’s!

She didn’t believe the Court’s Celestial Artifact was any worse than the Heavenly Sword Gate’s broken sword.

At that moment, somewhere in Qingzhou, the sky split open. The Demon Emperor’s palace appeared, releasing a blinding light.

Two artifacts shot out from it, turning into beams of treasure light, soaring toward Lin Jing.

In a flash, one was an imperial weapon of the Divine Tiger Clan used by successive Demon Emperors—near-Celestial Artifact grade. The other was a Celestial Artifact passed down in the Demon Court for generations. Both now appeared before Lin Jing.

The imperial weapon was the gold-element artifact the Demon Emperor previously promised Lin Jing, which originally required the Golden Light Gu to barely activate.

But now that Lin Jing could control Celestial Artifacts, this imperial weapon posed no issue.

“A bell…” The golden imperial weapon was a golden bell. With a sweep of his Divine Sense, Lin Jing immediately understood it was a dual-purpose weapon for attack and defense.

When rung, the golden-elemental soundwaves could destroy everything, unleashing tiger-like roars.

Against strong enemies, it could form a protective shield to defend its user.

For some reason, holding this thing, Lin Jing imagined the Demon Emperor wearing a bell around her neck…

He stashed the golden bell for now and looked toward the ancient Celestial Artifact preserved by the Demon Court… Though called a Celestial Artifact, it was actually an ink painting.

On the painting were the Azure Dragon, Vermilion Bird, White Tiger, and Black Tortoise—four sacred beasts—brimming with profound meaning.

This was the Demon Court’s Celestial Dao treasure: the Four Saints Diagram. It was once seized from the Immortal Palace. In those days, the palace used it to summon four Dao-Seeking sacred beasts in battle. But after the artifact spirit dissipated, it lost its spiritual power.

“Try and see if you can activate the Four Saints Diagram and unleash its true power.”

Under the Demon Emperor’s expectant tone, Lin Jing nodded, guiding the artifact ghost out of the Celestial Sword and into the Four Saints Diagram… In the next moment, the “spiritless” Celestial Artifact in the Demon Emperor’s words began to tremble, its aura surging. Inside, the four ink-painted beasts seemed to come alive, each eye flashing with light, roaring as they soared from the painting, shaking the heavens!

“Roar!!!” The four ink beasts bellowed, their presence far surpassing ordinary Demon Ancestors.

“Not bad, not bad.” Seeing this, the Demon Emperor’s eyes lit up. Though the four ink beasts were a bit weak—barely reaching Dao-Seeking stage—their strength lay in numbers.

Records say these beasts could also use a joint attack technique.

“Not bad.” Lin Jing, sensing that he could control the four ink beasts just like the Celestial Sword, nodded. Flying on swords wasn’t his thing, but riding fierce beasts—now that was his specialty.

“In that case… Gu God Sect, here I come.”

“No, wait, there’s a Void Mystical rogue cultivator in Qingzhou who has the Yin Spirit Gu. Better grab her on the way before heading to the Gu God Sect. There’s still time.”

Lin Jing came fast and left faster. In the blink of an eye, he was standing atop the Black Tortoise ink beast, heading for Granny Yinshan’s turf.

Watching Lin Jing’s swift departure, Senior Mo Pine remained silent for a long time.

“Senior, what… what’s going on now?” Gu Qianqiu asked.

Mo Pine: “Don’t ask.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 60: Seizing the Gu
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 60: Seizing the Gu

Granny Yinshan.

A rogue cultivator at the Peak Void Mystical Stage.

Also a renegade cultivator from the Dao-Seeking force, Water Moon Immortal Island.

She had been in closed-door cultivation for years in Qingzhou’s Tianyin Mountain, with a few female disciples serving her.

As the strongest rogue cultivator closest to Dao-Seeking, very few in Qingzhou dared provoke Granny Yinshan. However, she never imagined that without doing anything at all, a harbinger of doom would descend right upon her lair.

“One… two… three… four…”

“Four Dao-Seeking-level Demon Emperors’ auras…”

Within Tianyin Mountain, as Lin Jing arrived, the four ink beasts easily crushed the mountain’s protective barrier. The female disciples serving Granny Yinshan stared in horror at the massive beastly silhouettes looming in the sky, their expressions frozen in fear.

Granny Yinshan immediately emerged from seclusion. Though called “Granny,” she retained the appearance of a beautiful woman in her early thirties—whatever appearance-preserving method she used, Lin Jing could tell it wasn’t illusion, but her actual skin.

“Granny Yinshan, that’s you.”

Standing atop the ink tortoise, flanked by the other three ink beasts, Lin Jing’s arrival made Granny Yinshan’s pupils contract instantly.

The Holy Son of the Demon Court…?

She never would have guessed it was the Holy Son of the Demon Court descending upon her lair with four Dao-Seeking-level beasts!

What the hell… since when did the Demon Court have these four monsters…

Wait a second—when did she have any beef with this Demon Court Holy Son?

“So it’s the Demon Court Holy Son. I wonder what business Your Highness has at my Tianyin Mountain…” Granny Yinshan greeted politely.

Lin Jing smiled lightly: “Granny, this Holy Son has been collecting the Ten Rare Gu. I was on my way to destroy the Gu God Sect for a good harvest, but suddenly remembered that you have a Yin Gu in your hands. So I figured I’d stop by first to make a deal before heading to the Cloud Sea.”

“Would you be willing to part with it…?”

Granny Yinshan’s mouth twitched. Does this look like a normal deal to you?

You’re off to destroy the Gu God Sect? You even talk about wiping out a Dao-Seeking demonic sect—me, a Void Mystical rogue cultivator, how can I possibly resist the Demon Court’s grip?

“The Yin Spirit Gu… I can give it to Your Highness, but on one condition. If you agree, I’ll hand it over willingly.”

Granny Yinshan took a deep breath, forced to accept the situation.

“Please, state your condition.”

“Your Highness likely knows that I’m a renegade from Water Moon Immortal Island. But do you know why I left?”

Lin Jing shook his head.

“Water Moon Immortal Island has always accepted only female disciples and remains isolated from the world. No men are allowed on the island. I was deeply enamored with the current Island Master, so I changed my gender and infiltrated the island in hopes of getting close to her. But in the end… I was exposed.”

Lin Jing: “So that’s… wait, huh??”

“He’s a guy?” The Yin Spirit Gu froze up and said, “Wait a sec, wasn’t he the lowest-talent disciple in Water Moon Immortal Island in the past ten thousand years?”

“If I hadn’t damaged my foundation by changing gender, maybe I would’ve reached Dao-Seeking by now,” Granny Yinshan said.

Lin Jing didn’t know what to say to that, but he was in a hurry and didn’t want to get involved in personal drama: “Alright, I’m not interested in your backstory. Just tell me your condition.”

“The current Senior Sister of Water Moon Immortal Island—the Island Master’s personal disciple—is ranked in the top ten of the Fortune Gold List. If nothing goes wrong, she should be able to enter the Origin Realm. Your Highness will also enter the Origin Realm, yes? I hope to trade the Yin Spirit Gu for one promise: protect her life in the Origin Realm.”

“That girl… she’s quite good, just a bit careless,” Granny Yinshan added.

Lin Jing fell silent. Though she called herself a renegade, it seemed Granny Yinshan still had complicated ties to Water Moon Immortal Island.

Which made sense. She was still an extremely powerful cultivator. Even if the island couldn’t officially take her in due to ancestral rules, they didn’t have to sever ties completely.

Wait… could that current Senior Sister be the lovechild of her and the Island Master…?

“Fine. If we both enter the Origin Realm, I’ll keep her safe inside,” Lin Jing agreed.

Now that he had the Heavenly Demon Physique, Lin Jing was confident facing any geniuses from other realms.

............

With a promise exchanged for the Yin Spirit Gu, Lin Jing left Tianyin Mountain. Once he extracted the Yin Gu’s origin, Granny Yinshan’s body aged significantly, and her cultivation level dropped a tier. It was clear she had been deeply bonded to the Gu.
