Lin Jing didn’t get it because of the promise—he got it because he displayed power that made Granny Yinshan wary.

Having secured the Yin Gu, Lin Jing had no time to examine it. He rushed straight toward the Cloud Sea Mountains.

Coincidentally, as Lin Jing’s group left Martial Emperor City, the two Void Mystical cultivators of the Gu God Sect still hadn’t given up. They were still coveting the Rare Gu on Lin Jing.

However, as Lin Jing flew west from the Ancient Kingdom on the Celestial Sword, the two Gu God Sect cultivators were scared out of their wits and dove straight into the Cloud Sea Mountains, racing toward the Gu God Sect headquarters.

This gave Lin Jing the perfect chance to capture them all in one go.

“Go.”

“Excuse me, seniors—where are you headed?”

On the outskirts of the Cloud Sea Mountains, Lin Jing’s Divine Sense detected their movements and intercepted them directly.

He smiled at the Sect Leader and Elder Wind Gu of the Gu God Sect—his smile dazzling.

“Demon Court Holy Son.” The moment they saw Lin Jing, the two Void Mystical Gu cultivators didn’t look too happy.

And when they saw Lin Jing had swapped the Celestial Sword for four Dao-Seeking ink beasts… their scalps tingled.

“No need for nonsense. Be smart—hand over the Wind Gu and Yang Gu origins.”

“Holy Son of the Demon Court, don’t push us too far.”

“Our Gu God Sect has two Dao-Seeking Ancestors!!!”

The Sect Leader growled, threatening Lin Jing: “One of our ancestors is currently in closed-door cultivation within the Gu God Secret Realm… you better—”

Before he could finish, Lin Jing waved his hand. The Azure Dragon ink beast gathered a ball of black light in its mouth and blasted it in one direction. “Boom”—the sky shattered. A secret realm hidden in the Cloud Sea Mountains shook violently, with countless space fragments pouring into the void.

“Your Gu God Sect—the Gu God might be Dao-Seeking, but the Gu Insect Clan can’t fully exert their power alone. The former Sect Leader may have been Dao-Seeking, but he was nothing more than a vessel forcibly elevated by the Gu God to channel power. Together, they can unleash full Dao-Seeking might. So don’t try to fool this Holy Son like a child.”

“That strike should be enough to wake your Gu God up.”

The Sect Leader and Elder Wind Gu’s pupils shrank. Was this lunatic seriously going to start a full war with the Gu God Sect…?

They hadn’t even fully processed it when two ink beasts slapped them into a bloody mist—instant death. The Rare Gu that had been residing in their bodies emerged in various forms, staring in terror at the monstrous group that was Lin Jing’s entourage...

Meanwhile, outside the world,

Seeing that Lin Jing was really planning to crush the Gu God Sect, several Dao-Seeking experts sighed in awe—this was their first time seeing such a fierce prodigy.

“Come on, come on! He’s starting a war—who’s coming with me?” The Demon Emperor was excited, no longer avoiding conflict after her transformation;
she was now addicted to combat.

“That Gu God guy… you can’t kill him so easily…” said a Dao-Seeker from the Witch God Hall.

The Witch God Hall, located in the Cloud Sea Mountains, was a Dao-Seeking faction formed by several cultivator tribes. They’d long dealt with the Gu God Sect and understood them well. Like the Corpse Emperor and Bone Emperor, the Gu God couldn’t be fully killed through brute force alone.

“Even if we can’t kill him, we can at least severely injure him like the Corpse Emperor—make sure he can’t cause trouble for a thousand years!” Just as the Demon Emperor grew eager for battle, a voice suddenly reached her ears.

“Lord of the Demon Court, let’s make a deal. Don’t let your Holy Son take the top two spots on the Fortune Gold List. My royal heir is cultivating a secret art that requires immense fortune. In exchange, I can compensate you and that Longevity Physique in other ways.”

The Demon Emperor paused and looked toward the Second Ancient Emperor in the distance with her big round eyes. Ha! That guy finally spoke up too!

“What kind of secret art is so hard to cultivate? Tell me—I’m curious,” she licked her lips.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 61: Rare Gu Gathering
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 61: Rare Gu Gathering

The Cloud Sea Mountains were now shaking continuously.

The danger level here was no less than that of the Misty Forest. Fearsome creatures such as Mosquito Beasts still inhabited the area, and only the Witch Tribe, who had lived here for generations, could barely survive in this place.

But today, even those beasts known as the Ten Terrors of the Cloud Sea were all crouching low in fear, staring at the sky in terror.

Among the mountains, groups of oddly dressed cultivators were all kneeling in unison, bowing in a certain direction, praying for the gods' protection. This was a local custom—whenever an incomprehensible disaster occurred, they would pray to the gods for peace and safety.

Lin Jing forcefully shattered the Gu God's secret realm and executed two Void Mystical Stage Gu cultivators by activating the Celestial Artifact Four Saints Diagram, directly turning the Cloud Sea Mountains upside down...

“You little brat, courting death.” A furious roar rang out from the void.

A monster-like elder appeared. Though humanoid, he had almost no human traits left.

Above the elder's head, white strands floated like insect antennae. His purple skin was covered in irregular white spots, and he even had a non-human spiked tail...

The moment he appeared, he swept his tail across. The blood-red wind blade cut through space itself. The Vermilion Bird Ink Beast instantly spread its wings to shield Lin Jing from the strike.

At the same time, several Ink Beasts surrounded the monster.

The Gu God... the fused form of the Gu God Sect's old ancestor, looked coldly at the four Ink Beasts around him. Facing four Dao-Seeking-level Ink Beasts, he showed not the slightest worry. Instead, his icy gaze turned toward the Wind Gu and Yang Gu captured by the Azure Dragon Ink Beast and White Tiger Ink Beast.

“Useless trash...”

Seeing how the Gu God Sect declined year after year, the Gu God sighed. After the first Sect Leader died, it had helped the Gu God Sect create the Ten Rare Gu, partly to nurture powerful cultivators to serve it, and partly so that the sect could still flourish for ten thousand years even without it.

But now look what had happened.

The Rare Gu were being stolen one after another. Losing them was bad enough.

But now, someone even dared to snatch them right at their doorstep.

“You brat, do you really think you can challenge me just because of these four things?”

The Gu God glanced dismissively at the four Ink Beasts around him. It could sense their essence—though they looked strong on the surface, their roots... were still bound to the young cultivator before him.

In other words, how long these Ink Beasts could fight depended entirely on this young cultivator.

In fact, as long as it could get past these beasts and kill the young man, the fight would be over.

With that, there was nothing to be afraid of.

“If this is all you’ve got, then prepare to die....”

Just as the Gu God finished speaking, Lin Jing snorted coldly and shouted, “Your Majesty the Demon Emperor, help! The Gu God is going to kill me!!”

Then, several spatial rifts opened nearby.

A black-and-white whale, large enough to cover the sky, swam through the clouds like it ruled the heavens.

A giant green dragon with wings, resembling a massive lizard, slowly emerged from the void. As it appeared, the vegetation of the Cloud Sea Mountains reacted as if facing an emperor, offering their spiritual energy.

At the forefront, a boy wearing golden dragon robes stepped out, smiling kindly at the Gu God...

“Isn’t he the one bullying the Holy Son of the Demon Court just because he’s older?”

Gu God: ?

The Gu God bared its teeth.

The Demon Emperor pointed at it and said, “Cut the cr*p! Die for me!”

As soon as the Demon Emperor finished speaking, the three Dao-Seeking Demon Emperors surged forward. Lin Jing wanted to help, but didn’t even get the chance.

All he could do in the meantime was command the Ink Beasts to keep pounding the Wind Gu and Yang Gu half to death.

The three Dao-Seeking Demon Emperors joined forces, and the Gu God was no match. Before long, it severed one of its own arms and fled its lair.

Seeing this, the Yin-Yang Whale Emperor and Forest Sea Dragon Emperor shook their heads and gave chase.

As for the Demon Emperor, she floated in mid-air, letting out a deep breath before turning to Lin Jing.

“See? The Yin-Yang Whale Emperor and Forest Sea Dragon Emperor are quite sincere. To make up for their bad judgment, they even helped us wipe out the Gu God Sect!”

Lin Jing hesitated to speak, wanting badly to say: Your Majesty the Demon Emperor, do you really take me for a fool?

But he was tactful and replied, “It’s mainly thanks to Your Majesty’s overwhelming demon might. If not for your swift recovery and breakthrough, the consequences would’ve been unimaginable...”

The Demon Emperor nodded, “Makes sense. Earlier, the previous Gu God Sect ancestor was already killed by us. With the Gu God losing its vessel, its combat power has dropped greatly. But the Gu God itself... isn’t that easy to kill.”

“You possess the Ten Rare Gu—you should know... even the Ten Rare Gu under the Gu God have the ability to not die and be reborn. The Gu God that granted them such powers is an even more unfathomable existence.”

“Next, the heavily injured Gu God will likely reincarnate and cultivate a new vessel to slowly regain its strength. Though it’s hard to kill it completely, this does mean it’ll be in a long period of weakness... The best outcome is to find and erase it over and over during its weak phases,” the Demon Emperor said, stroking her chin.

“In any case, you don’t need to worry about that. Just take your Wind Gu and Yang Gu and go home in peace.”

Lin Jing gave a bitter smile and nodded. Now that the Ice Soul Gu would awaken, it’d probably feel like celebrating the New Year.

Right now, it was still digesting the Golden Light Gu.

And while that happened, Lin Jing had also obtained the Stone Man Gu, Yin Spirit Gu, Wind Plague Gu, and Yang Origin Gu one after another...

Among them, the Wind Plague Gu, Yin Spirit Gu, and Yang Origin Gu each had incredibly powerful effects. If the Ice Soul Gu could also devour them in turn, his strength would see a huge leap.

Wind Plague Gu: its poison is colorless and odorless, airborne, and anyone it touches becomes a toxic puppet controlled by it.

Yin Spirit Gu: its poison is the poison of inner demons. Anyone who meets the gaze of its host will be plagued by heart demons, eventually enslaved in spirit.

Yang Origin Gu: can kill through bloodline. To poison a Void Mystical Stage ancestor, you only need to capture one of his descendants—no need for a direct fight at all.

Besides their poisons, once fused with a Gu cultivator, the three Rare Gu also bring various benefits.

Wind Plague Gu grants top-tier wind control, comparable to having the Wind Spirit Physique.

Yin Spirit Gu enhances the Nascent Soul to the extreme, greatly boosting mental and soul power.

Yang Origin Gu is the most unique—it strengthens the vessel’s bloodline and reproductive capacity, allowing its host to bear many offspring with inherited talents. Upon birth, each child is automatically implanted with a Sub-Gu, ensuring lifelong loyalty and preventing any betrayal...

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 62: Negotiating Terms
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 62: Negotiating Terms

Precisely because of the unique ability of the Yang Origin Gu, the Sect Leader of the Gu God Sect had many talented children.

The oldest were already over a thousand years old.

The youngest were still chasing grasshoppers.

These descendants held control over nearly all important positions in the Gu God Sect—Holy Sons, Holy Maidens, and others.

This, undoubtedly, strengthened the Sect Leader’s control over the Gu God Sect.

However, nothing is ever perfect.

The Witch God Hall, which had long been at odds with the Gu God Sect, once developed a White Witchcraft spell that could neutralize the Yang Origin Gu’s poison. When the Sect Leader lost control over those children, the Gu God Sect plunged into civil strife!

“What are you daydreaming about?”

Lin Jing returned to his senses and said, “I was just thinking, since the White Witchcraft of the Witch God Hall is so effective against Gu poison, why did the Gu God Sect still choose to base themselves in their territory instead of places like Qingzhou?”

“Obviously because the Cloud Sea Mountains have enough poisonous insects and venomous creatures. It’s more suitable for the Gu God Sect to grow,” said the Demon Emperor.

“And another reason—competition breeds progress. The Witch God Hall isn’t as strong as the Ancient Kingdom and can't truly damage the foundation of the Gu God Sect, but still poses a threat. That’s...” The Demon Emperor paused, unable to find the right words.

“Adversity leads to growth, comfort brings decay?” Lin Jing offered.

“Yes, yes, yes.” The Demon Emperor nodded, then frowned, “Why are you worrying about all that? Anyway, there’s something important I need to tell you.”

She grinned and said, “Those life-burning spells of yours seem to have scared the Second Ancient Emperor!”

“Guess what? He actually came to this emperor to make a deal—asking you not to challenge the Ancient Imperial Prince and the Ancient Clan Princess.”

“I remember not long ago, he was still confident, thinking that even if you cultivated the Heavenly Demon Physique, you still wouldn’t be a match for them.”

“But now that he’s seen you can even activate a Celestial Artifact, he seems unwilling to gamble anymore!”

Lin Jing was stunned and said, “The Ancient Emperor sure dreams big!”

“I’ve burned so much of my lifespan to break through to the Nascent Soul Transformation Stage just to seize first place on the Fortune Gold List. If I don’t take it, wouldn’t that be a massive loss?”

“Exactly.” The Demon Emperor nodded. “So, the Second Ancient Emperor offered some pretty good terms.”

Lin Jing hesitated. “What kind of benefits?”

The Ancient Kingdom was vast and powerful—if the benefits outweighed the fortune bonus from the Gold List, then maybe it was worth considering. After all, Lin Jing wasn’t someone who fought merely for fame or glory.

“The main benefit... first, let’s talk about why the Second Ancient Emperor doesn’t want you to take first or second place.”

“Within the True Dragon Secret Art of the Ancient Clan, there’s a secret technique that can, with a vast amount of fortune power, break the limits of one’s physique and evolve it into an Immortal Physique.”

“The First Ancient Emperor back then wasn’t born with an Immortal Physique. He destroyed the Immortal Alliance by monopolizing most of the world’s fortune to evolve his True Dragon Divine Body into a True Dragon Immortal Physique. Originally, the True Dragon Immortal Physique wasn’t even one of the top ten Immortal Physiques.”

“But ever since then, the Tianyuan Realm has lacked the conditions needed to cultivate Immortal Physique secret techniques—until... the Golden Era began, and world fortune surged. The Second Ancient Emperor, having missed the prime age for cultivation during the Golden Era, could no longer practice the Immortal Physique technique himself, so he placed all his hopes on his two descendants. That’s why... the Ancient Imperial Prince and the Ancient Clan Princess are desperate for the fortune power from the Fortune Gold List.”

“Compared to achieving the Immortal Physique, even a one-percent chance of defeat is unacceptable to someone like his seventh-generation descendant.”

“True Dragon Secret Art, Immortal Physique...” Lin Jing raised an eyebrow. So they actually had a secret technique like that. No wonder...

Looking at the Demon Emperor, Lin Jing asked, “Then the Second Ancient Emperor’s condition is...?”

“That’s right. The Second Ancient Emperor said that as long as you don’t compete for the Gold List rankings against the Ancient Clan within ten years—that is, before entering the Origin Realm—he’ll pass the Immortal Physique secret art to you. Although, by then, without the fortune bonus from the Golden Era and the Gold List, the Origin Realm’s fortune is even more abundant—no worse than the Tianyuan Realm. Of course, it’s also harder to obtain, but with your Heavenly Demon Physique, you won’t be bullied even in the Origin Realm. There’s still a chance you’ll catch the Origin Realm’s favor and successfully cultivate an Immortal Physique.”

“Besides the Immortal Physique chapter of the True Dragon Secret Art, during these ten years, the Ancient Kingdom will also allocate a top-grade cultivation Dragon Vein for you to train in and make up for your losses.”

“And finally, when you return from the Origin Realm, the Second Ancient Emperor promises to let the Ancient Kingdom’s rare beast—the Boundary-Breaking Bird—assist you in cultivating once.”

Lin Jing curled his lips. “Aside from the Dragon Vein, everything else sounds like empty promises.”

“Mm, very accurate.”

“Your Majesty the Demon Emperor, I don’t think the Second Ancient Emperor’s sincerity is enough.”

“Indeed. And even if your Longevity Physique evolves into a Longevity Immortal Physique, it doesn’t seem to fundamentally change anything,” the Demon Emperor said. “At the very least, it should be the full True Dragon Secret Art! I’ll go renegotiate the terms!”

“Once the Second Ancient Emperor’s side is settled, I’ll talk to the old man from the Spirit Dao Sect next!”

“Wait, what’s this about the Spirit Dao Sect?” Lin Jing was taken aback.

The Demon Emperor said, “While the Second Ancient Emperor was privately messaging me, the old Daoist from the Spirit Dao Sect messaged me too.”

“He hopes you won’t challenge the current third-ranked Spirit Dao Sect Holy Son on the Gold List!”

“In exchange, the Spirit Dao Sect will let you cultivate beside their Enlightenment Pool, using the rare beast—the Insight Fish—for one year! The effect won’t be any worse than the fortune bonus from the third-ranked Gold List spot—maybe even better.”

Lin Jing: “...”

“Apparently, that Holy Son from the Spirit Dao Sect drank too much fish soup from the Insight Fish and is now immune to its rare beast divine ability. But the Gold List bonus still works well for him, so he doesn’t want to lose his ranking.”

“If the Ancient Clan deal falls through, the Spirit Dao Sect deal probably won’t happen either—after all, their Holy Son will drop to fourth place.”

“The Ancient Kingdom really is deep-rooted,” Lin Jing twitched at the corner of his mouth. The top three on the Gold List were all dominated by prodigies from the Ancient Kingdom. Now the big shots behind these prodigies were even willing to spend money to buy the rankings. Shady, truly shady.

Luckily, he was on the bribed side.

“Isn’t one year a bit too short? Since the Insight Fish is no longer useful to the Spirit Dao Sect’s Holy Son, why not make it ten years? If they let me use it for ten years, I’d definitely consider their request,” Lin Jing calculated. Since it’s all about enlightenment bonuses anyway, having the Insight Fish isn’t much different. More importantly, the Insight Fish could be used by his pet beasts too—Gold List first place only benefited himself!

“Ten years! That might be difficult. I’ve heard that the Spirit Dao Sect’s Insight Fish was ‘over-used’ in earlier years—the rare beast divine ability was tapped too often, and now it’s weakened.”

“Your Majesty, this problem can be handed off to the Second Ancient Emperor. If he wants me not to challenge the Ancient Imperial Prince and Ancient Clan Princess, he should convince the Spirit Dao Sect. As the Spirit Dao Sect’s superior, he should have a way to persuade them.” Lin Jing finished speaking, but even he was drained. The celestial diagram floating behind him collapsed, and the Ink Beasts dissipated one by one...

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 63: Belonging
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 63: Belonging

The time limit for the Divine Control Technique ended.

Lin Jing no longer had the strength to support the Artifact Ghost.

The Artifact Ghost floated dazedly out of the Four Saints Diagram, but before it could react, the Demon Emperor swept in like a gust of wind and snatched it into her hand while simultaneously retrieving the Celestial Artifact...

“So the source of your ability to control the Celestial Artifact is this jellyfish? I see.” The Demon Emperor drooled slightly.

“Only at the Void Mystical Stage, and you’re already able to control a Celestial Artifact—this thing is somewhat like the ultimate Artifact Spirit created by the Ancient Immortal Palace... Could it be?” The Demon Emperor, well-versed and worldly, probably didn’t slack on her homework under the last Demon Emperor’s watch.

After stuffing himself with a large pile of supplements, Lin Jing’s aura remained unstable as he replied, “That’s right.”

“Excellent. Welcome to the Demon Court.” The Demon Emperor extended her arm, locking eyes with the Artifact Ghost in her hand.

“From now on, our Demon Court has gained another Dao-Seeking combat force!”

It struggled furiously.

Lin Jing hesitated to speak.

Alright then, looks like no one else should even think about coveting the Artifact Ghost anymore.

“Speaking of which, Your Majesty the Demon Emperor, shouldn't we now proceed to claim the Gu God Sect’s inheritance?” Lin Jing looked toward the vast Cloud Sea Mountains.

In the Cloud Sea Mountains, there seemed to be many bases belonging to the Gu God Sect.

Now that the Gu God had been driven off and the Sect Leader of the Gu God Sect had been slain, all of that was now unclaimed property.

.............

A few days later.

Lin Jing returned to the Western Desert and reunited with Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor, Cold Moon Sword Immortal, and the Ghost Bride.

“You’re finally back.” The Ghost Bride let out a sigh of relief upon seeing Lin Jing return.

“Mm. But the thing I promised you might still take a while.” Lin Jing nodded.

“That’s fine. After all, I wasn’t much help.” The Ghost Bride was speechless. Heaven knew she only went out to search for the Artifact Ghost, not expecting everything that followed.

First the Mechanical Turtle brought the Artifact Ghost to Lin Jing, then Lin Jing controlled the Celestial Sword and roamed the continent.

When she first sensed the Celestial Sword’s activity in the Western Desert, she never imagined Lin Jing was behind it.

Lin Jing then looked toward the Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor and Cold Moon Sword Immortal.

“No need to say more, Your Highness the Holy Son. Since it was you who found the Artifact Ghost and you can command it, this old woman naturally won’t fight you over it.” The Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor waved her hand and promptly recalled the Celestial Sword from Lin Jing’s hand.

Being able to reclaim the Celestial Sword—she was already satisfied.

And she understood that Lin Jing had no real say in the matter. The Demon Emperor of the Demon Court would never let go of something as valuable as the Artifact Ghost.

At least the Demon Court hadn’t confiscated the Celestial Sword, so that wasn’t greedy.

Cold Moon Sword Immortal also gave Lin Jing a nod. Although Gao Sheng had originally intended for the Heavenly Sword Gate to gain the Artifact Ghost, given all the unexpected developments, the Artifact Ghost had become almost impossible to claim for the sect.

“Now that the Celestial Sword is recovered, this old woman won’t stay longer. Your Highness the Holy Son, you’re always welcome to visit the Heavenly Sword Gate. Since you’ve had experience commanding a Celestial Sword, your talent in the Sword Dao must’ve awakened. It wouldn’t hurt to delve into sword cultivation in the future!”

With that, the Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor sliced open space and departed.

As for Cold Moon Sword Immortal, after bidding farewell to her master, she asked, “Are you doing okay?”

“I’m fine, no big deal.” Lin Jing smiled.

“Then we’ll wait for you in Martial Emperor City. Once your pet beast masters the Divine Scripture, we’ll summon the Feng Family Ancestor together.” Feng Ning spoke up.

“For now, I’ll be cultivating in Martial Emperor City for a while, digesting the Sword Dao inheritance from my master. Senior Tang Ling is also planning to guide my Sword Spirit a bit.”

“I’ll aim to reach Nascent Soul Transformation within ten years and try for a spot in the top ten of the Fortune Gold List.” She looked up toward the sky.

If it had been before, she’d have never dared think it.

But after experiencing her master’s Dao-Seeking Sword Realm, Feng Ning understood her master’s deep intentions.

Lin Jing nodded. “I may need to return to the Ancient Kingdom for a bit. If all goes well, I should be back at Martial Emperor City soon to find you all.”

Ancient Kingdom, Spirit Dao Sect, Rare Beast, Insight Fish...

With so many enlightenment bonuses, Lin Jing believed he could quickly improve and perfect the Beast Taming Art.

...........

“It’s all over now, right?”

After bidding farewell to the Ghost Bride and the others, the Demon Emperor was already waiting to escort Lin Jing to the Ancient Kingdom for enlightenment.

“Mm.”

“Good. The Ancient Emperor has agreed to our terms.”

“Besides that, I also casually asked the Dragon Emperor and Whale Emperor—they’re both willing to pay a heavy price to protect their own prodigies’ rankings. This time, we really hit the jackpot.”

The Demon Emperor licked her lips.

First, Second, and Third on the Gold List had been locked down by the Ancient Kingdom.

Fourth and Fifth were held by the Crown Prince of Poseidon Palace and the Dragon Emperor’s disciple and companion.

Now that Lin Jing wouldn’t be contesting the top three spots, the Demon Emperor figured—thanks to the Insight Fish’s enlightenment bonus—rankings four and five also didn’t matter that much.

So why not make deals with Poseidon Palace and the Misty Forest, just like she had with the Ancient Kingdom?

And the results greatly exceeded her expectations.

“That Dragon Emperor was willing to offer a quasi-immortal-grade spiritual herb.”

“As for the Whale Emperor, they were willing to hand over an ancient relic.”

“The spiritual herb is called Immortal Water Lotus. In the lower realm, it’s a top-tier material for refining an external incarnation. Incarnations made from Immortal Water Lotus don’t resonate with natural world power—they don’t help with Nascent Soul breakthroughs, nor are they attuned to space power, so they don’t assist with Void Mystical progress...”

“But such incarnations are uniquely attuned to Immortal Realm energy, much like demonic beasts with rare bloodlines—they absorb immortal power with incredible efficiency!”

“In the Tianyuan Realm, the incarnation would be of little use. But in the Origin Realm, if there’s a chance to obtain Immortal Source, then this incarnation would be the ideal body.”

“Additionally, incarnations refined from Immortal Water Lotus naturally possess a Flawless Divine Body...”

“As for the Whale Emperor’s treasure—it’s Dragon Saliva Wine, a relic from the Ancient Dragon Palace, capable of restoring lifespan. It doesn’t increase or extend lifespan, but can repair lifespan lost due to injury or secret techniques. The effect varies by individual, but it can at least restore a thousand years.”

“What do you think?” The Demon Emperor finished, and Lin Jing was stunned.

“Then I’ll just settle at sixth place.” Lin Jing was extremely tempted—especially by that Dragon Saliva Wine.

That was a real treasure.

His whole purpose in fighting for a higher rank had been to compensate for the lifespan he’d burned.

Now, if he could restore some of it, plus all these extra resources—he felt it was completely worth it.

“But the Dragon Emperor and Whale Emperor being so generous—surely it’s not just about protecting their prodigies’ rankings?” Lin Jing asked.

“Of course not. They’re also eyeing the Transformation Technique! Given the current state of the cultivation world and the Demon Court’s strength, they’ve lost any hope of acquiring it by force. That leaves only one path—allying with the Demon Court.”

“These things... actually, they’re all negotiable.” She once again pulled out the Artifact Ghost.

“The real headache is this thing. Why is its intelligence lower than a three-year-old tiger cub? It only reacts on instinct—no way to hold a proper conversation. It’s driving me crazy.”

The Ghost Mother twisted into various forms in her hand. Seeing this, Lin Jing was speechless.

This Artifact Ghost was a war weapon. What use did it have for intelligence? As long as it could occupy and devour Magical Artifacts, that was enough.

What mattered was the one controlling it.

This way, the Celestial Palace could ensure that even if this war weapon were lost, few would be able to use it.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 64: Rare Beast Father-in-law
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 64: Rare Beast Father-in-law

Even though she was a Demon Emperor with immense power, the Artifact Ghost didn’t obey her at all.

Lin Jing had only barely managed to control it because he had the advantage of the Beast Taming Path, and the Artifact Ghost had been weakened by a restraining formation at the time.

Now, seeing that she couldn’t successfully cooperate with the Artifact Ghost, the Demon Emperor was growing anxious.

After all, this was a top-tier ghost entity capable of activating a Celestial Artifact. Even she wanted to try wielding a Celestial Artifact.

But despite using all her strength, she still failed.

This gave the Demon Emperor the distinct feeling that even if she erased the Artifact Ghost, it still wouldn’t cooperate with her in wielding the Celestial Artifact.

Lin Jing shook his head at the sight, thinking that even if the Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor obtained the Artifact Ghost, he’d probably end up just like the Demon Emperor. Perhaps only the Bone Emperor, who had mastered the Soul Path, would truly have a way to control it.

"Forget it. I’m done. Here, take it back."

After multiple failed attempts, the Demon Emperor threw the Ghost Mother back to Lin Jing, startling him.

After all, in his current state, he hadn’t activated the Divine Control Technique and couldn’t control the Ghost Mother.

However, maybe because the Demon Emperor was nearby, or perhaps because Lin Jing had previously commanded the Ghost Mother, it obediently curled up in Lin Jing’s arms without resisting.

"I actually..." Lin Jing really wanted to say that unless he kept burning his lifespan, there was no way he could suppress this thing.

And if he had to keep doing that—well, how much life did he even have to burn...

"You’re at the Nascent Soul Transformation Stage now. If you modify the Beast Taming Art, can you directly form a contract with it?" the Demon Emperor asked.

Lin Jing shook his head. "I don’t think so. Even though I temporarily boosted my cultivation to the Nascent Soul Transformation Stage, my number of contract slots didn’t increase."

"Right now, I still only have one slot at the Mid Core Formation Stage."

"And I can also sense that this drop of contract blood can only bind a beast that matches the level of my original cultivation stage."

"Meaning the upper limit is a pet at the Early Nascent Soul Stage. This one’s already at the Void Mystical Stage, so no matter what, it’s extremely hard to contract it."

"But..."

Lin Jing paused. In theory, that was true.

But now, with the bonus from the Hidden Dragon Secret Technique, the bonus from the Fortune Gold List, the bonus from the Bodhi Tree, and even the future bonus from the Insight Fish, he himself was becoming a theory breaker.

Is it possible that by modifying the Beast Taming Art, he could break through these theoretical limits?

The Demon Emperor said he should try to contract the Artifact Ghost. If he succeeded, his strength would definitely take a qualitative leap.

Even if he couldn’t use 100% of the Artifact Ghost’s power, just being able to utilize 10% or 20% would be a massive improvement.

Forget Celestial Artifacts—if he could freely use a top-grade artifact like the Beast God’s Seven-Star Circle without pressure, his combat strength would skyrocket!

"If I can create a sealing method that suppresses its power and cultivation level, and in that sealed state form a contract with it, and its level gradually unlocks as my own cultivation improves... then maybe I can pull it off."

Lin Jing murmured.

"But I was originally saving the fourth contract slot for the Mechanical Turtle... wait a minute, the Mechanical Turtle doesn’t have intelligence. If I fuse the Artifact Ghost with the Mechanical Turtle and make it the turtle’s intellect—problem solved!"

"What problem did you solve exactly..." the Demon Emperor asked. "That Artifact Ghost’s intelligence is like a two-year-old human child. Whether it’s there or not, what difference does it make?"

Lin Jing paused, then said, "There’s still a difference. Having some intelligence is better than none. The Beast Taming Sect has secret techniques that help contract beasts develop intellect. As long as there’s some intelligence, it has the potential to grow. Sooner or later, it can mature."

"Since you’ve already decided, that’s good. No matter what, you must contract it. Don’t let it fall into the hands of the Ancient Kingdom or Netherworld Hall." the Demon Emperor reminded him.

With that, without another word, she swept Lin Jing into the void, heading straight for the Spirit Dao Sect.

..........

Outside the Spirit Dao Sect.

The Spirit Dao Sect’s Dao-Seeking elder and the Insight Fish had been waiting for the Demon Emperor and Lin Jing for quite some time.

"Greetings, Senior Ling Daozi." Lin Jing gave a slight bow.

The Spirit Dao Sect’s Grand Elder, Ling Daozi, was the second most powerful person in the Ancient Kingdom after the Ancient Emperor. He had mastered dozens of spell techniques with Spell Spirit Origins and Secret Arts. His strength was immense.

"No need for formality." Ling Daozi, with his fake sage-like demeanor, didn’t act like a high-level cultivator at all. Laughing heartily, he said, "We should thank Your Highness the Holy Son for showing mercy. Otherwise, our Spirit Dao Sect’s Daozi wouldn’t have gotten to enjoy the benefits of placing top three on the Gold List."

"You just had to sacrifice me!"

Beside him, the Insight Fish, with a lump on its head that looked like a brain and a face like a goldfish, muttered grumpily.

Its eyes looked past Lin Jing and Ling Daozi toward the small-framed, arrogant-looking Demon Emperor.

"Sister Demon Emperor, considering how hard I worked, could you give me a Transformation Pill?"

"Depends on your performance." the Demon Emperor replied. "Take good care of the Holy Son from the Demon Court, and we’ll talk. This emperor won’t treat you unfairly."

"...” the Insight Fish muttered.

Its rare beast divine ability was called the Enlightenment River.

After activation, it could transform the surrounding water into Enlightenment Water that enhanced comprehension.

But Enlightenment Water had its limits. Once the Insight Fish left the water source, the water lost its effect.

So whether soaking in it or drinking it directly, the Insight Fish had to be present.

More importantly, the effect of Enlightenment Water varied among cultivators.

Some became immune after one or two uses, while others found it effective even after dozens.

Because of this, the Spirit Dao Sect used it as a secondary way to evaluate disciples’ talent.

And to climb the ranks, the sect’s Daozi—who responded exceptionally well to Enlightenment Water—drank so much of it that he became immune. That’s how desperate he was.

"Enough talking," the Demon Emperor said.

The Insight Fish flicked its tail. "Can’t stop talking yet. Let’s lay down some ground rules: no hugging and biting. Look at your Holy Son—his eyes are practically glowing green when he looks at me."

The Demon Emperor looked at Lin Jing.

Lin Jing rubbed his chin and said, "That’s not what I meant."

He didn’t like eating fish. Too many bones—too much hassle.

If he liked fish, he would’ve eaten the Dragon Carp by now.

"It’s just... Senior Insight Fish, judging from your voice... are you female?"

"What of it?" The Insight Fish was instantly on guard.

"Do you have a partner? My Dragon Carp is also a rare beast—and also a fish. Would you consider... combining your bloodlines..."

"The offspring would definitely be of Celestial Beast potential."

Ling Daozi, the Demon Emperor, and the Insight Fish all went silent.

Lin Jing was thinking: if the marriage worked out, he’d be the Insight Fish’s father-in-law.

Wouldn’t he get to drink Enlightenment Fish Soup forever?

Though... his Dragon Carp’s EQ wasn’t very high. Probably couldn’t pull it off.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 65: Imaginary Enemy
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 65: Imaginary Enemy

Spirit Dao Sect, Enlightenment Pool.

This was one of the resting places of the Insight Fish.

Typically, Spirit Dao Sect disciples qualified to engage with the Insight Fish would cultivate here.

But today, the Enlightenment Pool welcomed a cultivator who wasn’t from the Spirit Dao Sect.

Lin Jing finished planting the Bodhi Tree beside the pool, changed into his self-made big pants, and stepped straight into the water along the edge.

The Insight Fish looked up and said, “No need to take it off, no need to take off your clothes!”

Lin Jing glanced at his big pants. “I didn’t take anything off.”

The Insight Fish explained, “You can just come in wearing your Dao robe!”

“You don’t even know a basic spell like Water Repelling Technique?” The Insight Fish was in disbelief.

“Never learned it. But my Dragon Carp seems to know Crystal Flame. That might repel water! I’ll try borrowing its technique…”

“Don’t mention that thing again. This fish is older than it by centuries! Whatever, just wear that.” The Insight Fish gave in and began activating its Rare Beast Divine Ability.

During the process, it rested within a treasure jade, quietly nourishing the pool water.

Lin Jing shook his head, giving up on the marriage idea.

After entering the Enlightenment Pool, Lin Jing once again regained that feeling—

That state under the Golden Bodhi Tree where creating new techniques felt almost effortless.

“This... is what true cultivation should be.” Lin Jing took a deep breath, closed his eyes, and began merging the Buddha-Demon Pearl, Sacred Wood Seal, and other sealing techniques he had mastered with the numerous Mechanized Arts he had scavenged. He entered a state of enlightenment and technique creation.

He soaked near the poolside, with the ground around him covered in mechanical components.

The Mechanical Turtle was among them.

..........

At the same time.

The Spirit Dao Sect’s Daozi had learned from the Sect Master that the sect had traded away his opportunity for enlightenment to ensure he wouldn’t be challenged by the Holy Son of the Demon Court.

“Sect Master, I don’t need that!” The Daozi of the Spirit Dao Sect was a chubby Daoist who, like the sect’s Grand Elder, had no appearance of an immortal sage.

But he truly was the disciple with the highest comprehension in the Spirit Dao Sect. Back when Lin Jing fought Chen Zhong, Chen had already shocked people by mastering a gravity spell with a Spell Spirit Origin during the Foundation Establishment Stage. But this Daozi? He did it during the Qi Refinement Stage.

“Defeating Wan Renzong doesn’t mean he can defeat me. Back when our founder assisted the First Ancient Emperor in wiping out the Demonic Sky Sect, he left behind many spells to counter their cultivators. I’ve studied them all. That Holy Son from the Demon Court only made the Gold List at the Core Formation Stage because he learned the Demonic Sky Sect’s techniques.”

“I knew you wouldn’t accept the Grand Elder’s decision… but that Demon Court Holy Son is truly strange.” The Sect Master hesitated, then shook his head. “But since it’s the Grand Elder’s will, so be it.”

“If you’re unwilling, then in ten years, during the Grand Celestial Gathering, challenge him. Defeat him. Prove to the Grand Elder that you’re the true one. For now, just follow orders.”

The Daozi of the Spirit Dao Sect clenched his teeth.

The same happened with the Ancient Imperial Prince, Ancient Clan Princess, Poseidon Palace Crown Prince, and Dragon Blood Willow, a descendant of the Dragon Emperor.

None of them could understand why their father emperor or master was so worried about the Demon Court Holy Son.

“Even if that Lin Jing guy has Demonic Sky techniques and can use some weird method to activate a Celestial Artifact, he’s not necessarily my match.” The Ancient Imperial Prince, in particular, couldn’t make sense of it.

Now, all the powerful figures knew that Lin Jing’s ability to use a Celestial Artifact wasn’t his own strength—it was due to a fluke where he had managed to control the Ghost Mother, who had been heavily injured by the Netherworld Hall.

If that counted as one’s own strength, then the Ancient Imperial Prince could also draw on his nation’s fortune and Dragon Vein to fight Lin Jing. That wasn’t normal Nascent Soul power either—he could use half a kingdom’s strength. Why fear a Celestial Artifact?

Besides, Dragon Veins were part of nature’s forces;
using them brought no pressure.

But a Void Mystical Artifact Ghost like that wouldn’t be recognized by the Fortune Gold List, so he had no idea what his father was even worried about.

Top-ranked prodigies were all proud. None of them could accept being “protected” by their elders in this manner.

But what they didn’t know was that this was also a form of cultivation arranged by the Dao-Seeking elders.

“This kind of action is highly insulting. With their personalities, they won’t take it well. It’s even harder to accept than losing in a direct duel.”

“In this way, we plant a fire in their hearts, making them work hard for our recognition—to prove we were wrong.”

“More importantly, this Demon Court Holy Son cultivates many Demonic Sky Sect Secret Arts. He’s practically a successor of the Demonic Sky Sect. He may even continue to seek out their techniques. In the future structure of the Tianyuan Realm, he is undoubtedly the best imaginary enemy.”

Outside, Ling Daozi stroked his beard. In this way, even without his guidance, the Spirit Dao Sect’s Daozi would surely dedicate himself to studying techniques that restrain Demonic Sky cultivators.

Perhaps one of these prodigies would even develop a spell, formation, or artifact that neutralizes the Heavenly Demon Physique.

In short, regardless of the outcome of Lin Jing’s future battles with these prodigies, it would enrich their experience in dealing with Demonic cultivators...

..........

At present, Lin Jing still didn’t know that beneath the surface of being “acknowledged” by various Dao-Seeking seniors, he had actually been set up by them to become a training opponent—the imaginary enemy—for their own prodigies to prepare against the Demonic Sky Sect army.

After all, in the Tianyuan Realm, if anyone was closest to a Demonic Sky Sect cultivator, it was undoubtedly Lin Jing.

If he found out what these Dao-Seeking old foxes were up to, he’d definitely curse them in his heart.

Still, as long as the promised resources were delivered, he wouldn’t be too bothered by being used this way.

Who knew how long had passed...

Under Lin Jing’s skilled hands, the Longevity Turtle had undergone complex modifications.

The biggest change was a sealing formation and a fusion array inscribed specifically by Lin Jing for the Ghost Mother.

Throughout the process, the Artifact Ghost remained obedient inside the Longevity Turtle’s body, letting Lin Jing run all his experiments.

This convinced Lin Jing that after using the Divine Control Technique on it, the Artifact Ghost had grown somewhat attached to him.

As a war weapon with limited intelligence, the Artifact Ghost responded very differently to someone who had used it versus a stranger.

About a month after Lin Jing entered the Enlightenment Pool, the Artifact Ghost inside the turtle shell began lowering its cultivation level.

Using a special sealing formation, it voluntarily sealed its realm, successfully reducing its power to the Early Nascent Soul Stage.

At the same time, thanks to the fusion array, the Longevity Turtle’s body became special to the Artifact Ghost. The Mechanical Turtle was becoming like a natal vessel to it.

The turtle’s abilities also began subtly affecting the Artifact Ghost’s personality.

Just like how an environment shapes a person, the Mechanical Turtle’s presence would begin influencing the Artifact Ghost’s development, leading to their gradual assimilation...

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 66: Reinventing the Technique
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 66: Reinventing the Technique

Over the past month, aside from Lin Jing, the pet beasts hadn’t been idle either.

The Pine Leaf Mouse sat atop the Bodhi Tree, guarding Lin Jing’s cultivation while burning through resources to raise its cultivation level.

It had now reached the Late Core Formation Stage.

Before, the Pine Leaf Mouse might not have cared much about levels and only focused on hoarding goods, but things were different now.

After the battle at the Gu God Sect, the Pine Leaf Mouse suffered a major blow.

During the battle in Tongtian City, Lin Jing had shoved all the loot into the Pine Leaf Mouse’s Five-Element Monster Core, making its Monster Core Divine Ability incredibly overbearing—capable even of injuring an equal-level Immortal Physique.

And after the Gu God Sect battle, Lin Jing once again gained tons of loot.

Although the Void Mystical Stage experts of the Gu God Sect weren’t as numerous as those in Tongtian City, this time they were raiding the enemy’s headquarters, their base of operations, so the resources obtained were on another level entirely.

After all, strong cultivators may not carry much with them,

But a sect’s foundation always contains a massive hoard of resources!

Especially since the Gu God Sect nurtured and refined Gu, which required large quantities of Five-Element items!

However, what should have been a wildly satisfying haul for the Pine Leaf Mouse ran into a problem.

Its Monster Core space hit its limit. No matter how it tried to pack it in, there was simply no room left.

Who knew that the Monster Core space even had a limit!

That broke the Pine Leaf Mouse completely—it began working hard on cultivation, because this way, the Monster Core could be strengthened.

“Ugh ugh ugh....”

Also, after seeing how Lin Jing used the Fortune Gold List ranking to extort so many resources, the Pine Leaf Mouse was filled with envy.

If it could get into the top ten of the Gold List too, wouldn’t it also be able to auction off its ranking like Lin Jing?

Even if it didn’t auction, with the attribute boost from the Gold List, it could improve its comprehension of the Spatial Dao and avoid a situation like this again.

Although the top ten on the Gold List were all Nascent Soul Transformation experts, the Pine Leaf Mouse was full of confidence.

After all, once it reached the Nascent Soul Stage, the burden of using Core Formation Divine Abilities wouldn’t be as heavy.

By then, it could shove the Gu God Sect’s legacy inside too.

With one Monster Core slam, even a Nascent Soul genius might not survive it.

After all, this would be an impact backed by the foundation of a Dao-Seeking demonic sect!!!

If that’s still not enough, it could always go to the Demon Court for another big load.

Compared to the Pine Leaf Mouse working hard on cultivation, the Dragon Carp was much more relaxed, refining Gluttonous Pills inside the Hourglass.

Its cultivation was now at the Early Nascent Soul Stage, limited only because Lin Jing’s original realm wasn’t high enough—it couldn’t go further yet.

Following the principle of the strong leading the weak, it was now fully acting in service of the Ice Soul Gu.

The Ice Soul Gu had happily devoured the Golden Light Gu and was just about to show off its power, only to open its eyes and find itself surrounded by the origins of Stone Man Gu, Wind Plague Gu, Yin Spirit Gu, Yang Origin Gu, and Deathbane Gu.

It looked at its round spider belly in confusion for a moment, then could only continue devouring.

To keep it from dying of overstuffing, the Dragon Carp, under Lin Jing’s orders, kept refining “Digestive Pills.”

“Once the Pine Leaf Mouse breaks through to Nascent Soul and with the resources from these two battles, its power will be terrifying.”

“When the Dragon Carp fully masters its Immortal Flame, that will be its trump card for crossing realms.”

“As for the Ice Soul Gu, after swallowing so many Rare Gu origins, who knows what level it’ll reach. If I can get the Ten Thousand Poison Gu from the Tongtian Tower and the Heavenly Flame Gu from the Demon-Slaying Bureau, then with ten Gu combined—it’ll be perfect.”

“Hopefully by then, they’ll all be strong enough to enter the top ten of the Gold List. Even though I won’t stir up trouble on the Gold List anymore, there’s no rule saying pet beasts can’t compete. If they knock those top five guys off their spots, they can’t blame me.” Lin Jing murmured.

Of course, the difficulty of that was massive. After all, those monsters were also geniuses favored by the Dao.

“What’s urgent now is to keep comprehending the Dao and improve the Beast Taming Art—figure out a way to contract it!” Lin Jing looked at the Mechanical Turtle.

In cultivation, time flies.

Lin Jing felt like a long time had passed.

But in truth, not much time had gone by at all.

Under multiple comprehension boosts, he felt as though a hundred years had passed, yet he had only taken a month to refine the Beast Taming Art by combining it with Mechanized Control Arts—making it a technique that could contract mechanized beasts.

And with this rare opportunity, Lin Jing didn’t stop after that.

He continued further refining the Beast Taming Art, gradually removing its contract limitations.

From being able to contract only demonic beasts, now even plant spirits and corpse-type demons could be contracted!

“Based on the Divine Control Technique’s principles applied to the Beast Taming Art, in the current state...”

Lin Jing circulated the technique several times and felt that now, he could even contract fellow humans!

In other words, even if a human genius stood next to him—so long as they were in the Early Nascent Soul Stage—and he had spare Contract Blood, he could contract them just like a pet beast!

And that person’s cultivation breakthroughs would still provide feedback to him.

“Dmn it, what the hll am I even doing.”

By the time Lin Jing came back to his senses, he had completely overhauled the Beast Taming Art.

In this epiphany state, he’d gotten so immersed in it that he couldn’t pull himself out.

Originally, he only meant to refine the Beast Taming Art into a Beast Control Art that could contract shape-shifted demons.

Who would’ve thought he’d go so overboard by accident.

“Let’s hope I’ll never actually need to use this ability.”

Lin Jing shook his head but suddenly had a thought.

Since with his refinements, the Beast Taming Art could now even contract humans,

Then theoretically—could he contract his own External Incarnation?

If so, the link between his main body and his avatar would be even deeper.

And unlike other cultivators, he wouldn’t need to fear losing his incarnation.

Because with the Beast Taming secret techniques, the External Incarnation could be resurrected through Immortal Rebirth, just like a pet beast.

“I’ll wait until I’ve refined an incarnation and test it out then.”

“But yeah, half a year just went by like that. Improving techniques really eats up time.”

Lin Jing sensed his surroundings—half a year had already passed since he entered the Spirit Dao Sect.

With all those boosts, and it still took half a year—honestly, it’s because he got a bit greedy.

Because during the process of refining the Beast Taming Art, he even tried to see if he could produce two drops of Contract Blood in one realm, like when he reached the limit during the Qi Refinement Stage.

Unfortunately, even after successfully making the technique compatible with contracting all beings, the idea of producing two drops of Contract Blood in a single realm still failed.

At the moment, with other tasks on hand, Lin Jing stopped fussing over it and looked toward the Mechanical Turtle still cultivating nearby.

“Finished cultivating?”

By now, Lin Jing had already passed the Divine Scripture onto the Mechanical Turtle for cultivation—it had officially become his fourth pet beast.

Judging by the timeline, it should be around the point of minor success with its talent.

“Already finished!”

“When can I go treasure hunting again?”

“It’s been six months since I went out—I’m gonna die here.”

“Yet another day of failing to find any longevity items...

“How about starting a war to relieve some boredom? I still have lots of weapons here.”

The Longevity Turtle sluggishly crawled over, now weird beyond words after all of Lin Jing’s ‘adjustments.’

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Vol. 4 Chapter 67: Divine Scripture Target
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol. 4 Chapter 67: Divine Scripture Target

“Mm... Lin Jing has now already formed a contract with the Mechanical Turtle. The Artifact Ghost and the Mechanical Turtle have fused well, and its intelligence is steadily growing, but this has also brought a problem. That is, after gaining awareness, the Longevity Turtle really doesn’t like staying in one place for too long and often asks Lin Jing to let it go out treasure hunting. But now that they're at the Spirit Dao Sect, Lin Jing definitely needs to keep an eye on it. Otherwise, all those life-extending treasures and magical artifacts in the Spirit Dao Sect would end up in its claws. Moreover, after taking the Artifact Ghost—this War Weapon—as its artifact spirit, the Longevity Turtle’s personality has become extremely aggressive, like a war maniac.”

“Got it.” Lin Jing replied perfunctorily.

“Next, I’ll take you on a mission. After completing this task, I’ll let you go travel.”

He scratched his ear. Among the four pet beasts, the Longevity Turtle was the one that needed the least cultivation.

Just by relying on its ability to wield artifacts beyond its level, it ranked first in combat power among the four pet beasts.

Although its Cultivation Realm is currently sealed at the Early Nascent Soul Stage, with the capabilities of the Artifact Ghost, it can still easily control top-grade magical artifacts—and top-grade artifacts are normally only manageable for Peak Void Mystical Stage cultivators.

Therefore, as long as there are enough artifacts, even at the Nascent Soul Stage, it wouldn't be at a serious disadvantage against a regular Nascent Soul Transformation Stage cultivator.

“Keep your word...”

“You’re leaving??”

Sensing the movement, the Insight Fish also woke from its slumber.

“Just stepping out to take care of some things. I still have ten years of usage rights over you, so I’ll definitely use them up. I’ll be back soon.” Lin Jing smiled and said goodbye.

But Lin Jing himself estimated that he wouldn’t even need the full ten years.

Because the effect of the Enlightenment Pool gradually diminishes, after another year or two, he’d likely have to rely on drinking the Insight Fish’s algae-washing water just to barely maintain the benefits.

Therefore, Lin Jing planned that once the pool’s effect was no longer obvious for him, he’d allocate the remaining time for each pet beast to use...

Let them take turns, so nothing goes to waste.

“Then I’ll go back to sleep.” The Insight Fish rolled its eyes and tiredly dozed off again, utterly dispirited.

..........

At the same time, Lin Jing’s figure flickered and disappeared, Pine Leaf Mouse took Lin Jing and, with one spatial teleport, they left the Spirit Dao Sect.

Now that the Mechanical Turtle had partially mastered the Divine Scripture, he naturally had to fulfill his promise—to help the Ghost Bride revive the Feng Family’s ancestor and do some archaeology.

However, even though the Mechanical Turtle’s technique was partially mastered, it hadn’t truly been used yet. To avoid any issues at a critical moment, Lin Jing decided to delay his trip to Martial Emperor City and test it out first.

“The activation condition for the Divine Scripture—to revive a powerful figure’s projection—requires that even after their death, there are still people continuously worshipping and offering to them.”

“And, there must be a personal belonging of theirs to serve as the offering.”

“In addition, a Dragon Vein must be used for the formation pattern... it consumes a huge amount of Dragon Vein power. The conditions are extremely harsh.”

“F***, hasn’t the top-grade Dragon Vein promised by the Ancient Kingdom been extracted yet? If that promise still isn’t fulfilled when I return to the Ancient Kingdom, I’ll tear up the deal.” Lin Jing grumbled.

After muttering, he began searching his storage bag for a suitable relic.

The Beast Taming Sect does have relics left behind by the first through fourth Sect Leaders, and the sect has continued to offer to them, but they’re too weak—summoning their projections wouldn’t be cost-effective.

He had also looted a relic left behind by the Gu God Sect’s first Sect Leader Duan Zhong, but the Gu God is a b*****d—it didn’t let the sect continue honoring their founder. They all turned to worship it instead, so that doesn’t meet the summoning conditions...

“Don’t know if the First Ancient Emperor died out in the stars. If he did, I wonder if I could summon the Second Ancient Emperor’s father.” Lin Jing looked toward the skies.

Although he didn’t have any relics of the First Ancient Emperor, relics of such a major figure might actually be easier to obtain.

After all, as the strongest person of the previous era and the founder of a nation, who knows how many good things he left behind or bestowed.

Like the Hidden Dragon Academy where Lin Jing had once stayed—it had a personal magical artifact gifted by the First Ancient Emperor stored within....

And during the founding of the nation, many families and sects received similar gifts.

So, if Lin Jing wanted to get a relic of the First Ancient Emperor, he might not even need to deal directly with the Ancient Clan.

Of course, all this hinges on whether the First Ancient Emperor actually died in the starry skies—if he’s still alive, then naturally the Divine Scripture cannot summon his projection.

.............

With the idea forming, Lin Jing locked in his target.

He recalled some things, then continued fleeing toward the borders of the Ancient Kingdom.

He knew exactly where to go to get the Ancient Emperor’s relic.

At this point, the advice of the Beast Taming Sect elder to read more books really paid off.

“Cultivation Family, the Lü Family!”

Lin Jing’s eyes lit up.

The Lü Family Ancestor was a great general who followed the First Ancient Emperor in his conquests during the founding of the Ancient Kingdom—a powerful cultivator.

In theory, the Lü Family today should be like the Zhao Family—a prominent cultivation family in the Tianyuan Ancient Kingdom.

However, after the Second Ancient Emperor ascended, due to a particular incident, the Lü Family was exiled to the borderlands, far removed from politics ever since.

Now, under pressure from political rivals, the family doesn’t even have a single Void Mystical Stage cultivator left—only one Nascent Soul Transformation Stage elder remains, barely hanging on.

“When the Second Ancient Emperor took the throne, he immediately made many changes to the Ancient Kingdom’s existing policies!”

“Though a new emperor, the Second Ancient Emperor was still a Dao-Seeking expert—no matter how radical his reforms, the old officials didn’t dare object.”

“But there was one exception—a court cultivator from the Lü Family.”

“Unofficial histories say that within a month of his coronation, the Second Ancient Emperor issued seventeen reforms, and every single one of them was publicly criticized by that Lü cultivator—so much so that the emperor nearly blew a lung.”

“Shortly after, the Lü Family was accused of some fault and exiled to the border.”

“But everyone knows, it was all because of that stubborn Lü official...”

“To offend the Second Ancient Emperor like that and still only be exiled from Tianyuan’s capital to the borders... it’s all thanks to the ‘death immunity magical artifact’ granted by the former emperor.”

“After founding the nation, the First Ancient Emperor granted twenty-four founding powers an immunity artifact—these were all personal magical artifacts he used himself during the nation’s founding battles. As long as the Lü Family doesn’t rebel, even the Second Ancient Emperor can’t touch them.”

“Today, among all the holders of the ‘death immunity magical artifact,’ the Lü Family is probably the most fallen. Let’s go see if we can ‘borrow’ that immunity artifact from them.” Lin Jing sped up, eager to try reviving the First Ancient Emperor.

If this works, it’ll cause more shock than activating any Celestial Artifact.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 68: Mass-Producing Divine Physiques
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 68: Mass-Producing Divine Physiques

Lin Jing headed west like a flash of lightning. Although his breakthrough to the Nascent Soul Transformation Stage came only through erupting the Heavenly Demon Physique, even without grasping the power of heaven and earth, the vast mana base of the Nascent Soul Transformation Stage alone allowed him to push the Buddhist Light Technique to its limit,

“Lü Family...”

At the borderlands, in Wangxian City, though the Lü Family had declined, it still held dominion over this region.

Before arriving in Wangxian City, Lin Jing concealed his presence. His Divine Sense swept over the city and quickly locked onto the strongest cultivator within.

It was a mid-Nascent Soul elder, who, judging by appearance, did not have many years left to live. In his current state, not to mention the Void Mystical Stage—even the Late Nascent Soul Transformation Stage was likely forever out of reach.

In one step, Lin Jing arrived in the elder’s secluded cultivation chamber and stood behind him. A breath later, the cross-legged elder trembled all over, suddenly teleporting up and turning to face Lin Jing, who had been standing behind him for quite a while.

Cold sweat poured from his head as he stared at the young man in front of him, completely unaware of how this person had entered his cave dwelling, which was layered with defensive formations.

“Who are you!” The Lü Family Ancestor was as if facing a great enemy, mana surging from his entire body, but moments later, he gave up resisting. The pressure from the youth before him was simply too immense—he had no desire to fight.

Lin Jing was not using his true appearance at the moment, so the Lü Family Ancestor didn’t recognize him. Lin Jing chuckled and said, “Fellow Daoist, I’ve heard your Lü Family possesses a personal magical artifact bestowed by the First Ancient Emperor—is that true?”

The Lü Family Ancestor’s expression changed. This person… had come for the “death immunity artifact”?

“What do you intend to do?”

“I want to borrow the token.” Lin Jing said.

“Impossible!” The Lü Family Ancestor frowned. “I advise you to give up on this idea. Don’t do anything rash.”

“That artifact may have been the First Emperor’s personal item during his campaigns, but it’s only a high-grade magical artifact. For someone of your strength, it’s not particularly useful.”

“The artifact’s value lies in its symbolic significance. Families that received such bestowed artifacts from the First Emperor are under the Ancient Kingdom’s highest level of protection. Even if such a family commits a heinous crime, they can be spared severe punishment.”

“So, to my Lü Family, this artifact is of extraordinary significance. But in your hands, it would just be a regular high-grade artifact. Moreover, if the Ancient Kingdom finds out you took an imperial gift, you will definitely be hunted by stronger forces from the kingdom!”

“This hot potato may not be as great as you imagine.”

“Please leave. I’ll pretend I never saw you.”

Lin Jing shook his head and said, “Given your Lü Family’s current state, even with the bestowed artifact, it’s difficult to regain past glory. Why don’t we make a deal—you give me the artifact, and I’ll offer you a great opportunity... a chance for your Lü Family to rise again.”

“But...” The Lü Family Ancestor was just about to scoff, when Lin Jing’s next words made his pupils contract.

“I can help your Lü Family produce ten Divine Physique-level descendants. Ten Divine Physiques—do you understand what that means?” Lin Jing looked at him meaningfully.

The Lü Family Ancestor’s mouth opened slightly, doubting whether he had heard correctly.

Ten Divine Physiques?

Was it the Divine Physique he knew?

Divine Physiques, even among rare cases, were almost guaranteed to achieve Nascent Soul Transformation.

The Lü Family Ancestor extended his hand, revealing a terrifying brown ginseng doll in his palm.

“This is a Mutated Earth Spirit Ginseng. If consumed before pregnancy, it will produce a child with an Innate Earth Spirit Body.”

“You even have that thing?!” The Void Mystical elder was stunned. But an Innate Earth Spirit Body... that’s far from a true Divine Physique!

The Lü Family Ancestor then took out a vial of blood.

“This is the blood essence of a mid-Void Mystical Stage Earth-element Demon Ancestor.”

And he wasn’t done yet—he pulled out a cultivation manual.

“‘Scripture of the Earth God,’ a Void Mystical-level Earth-element cultivation technique, containing a top-tier Foundation Establishment method. With the Demon Ancestor’s blood, an Innate Earth Spirit Body can be cultivated into an Earth Divine Physique.”

“With this much blood essence, and this technique, it’s enough for your Lü Family to produce ten Acquired Divine Physiques. This opportunity—do you want it or not?” Lin Jing spoke calmly.

The Mutated Earth Spirit Ginseng had been synthesized by the Pine Leaf Mouse from standard Earth Spirit Ginseng.

The Demon Ancestor’s blood was seized by Lin Jing from the Gu God Sect.

As for the ‘Scripture of the Earth God,’ it was looted from Tongtian City.

Lin Jing had many such miscellaneous items. Taken alone, their value might be limited, but combined—they could create astonishing results.

Like now: the ability to mass-produce Acquired Divine Physiques!

Lin Jing gave a gentle push, sliding the Mutated Earth Spirit Ginseng, the ‘Scripture of the Earth God,’ and the Demon Ancestor blood toward the Lü Family Ancestor for inspection. The elder took a deep breath, quickly flipping through the scripture and sensing the blood, realizing Lin Jing wasn’t lying.

These were indeed Void Mystical-level techniques and Demon Ancestor blood...

“You actually...” He didn’t expect Lin Jing to possess such rare treasures. But more baffling was why Lin Jing would trade items that would make even Void Mystical Ancients drool—for an imperial artifact that was useless to him.

“Well, are you trading or not?”

Lin Jing asked.

“If you don’t, I’ll just go to another family.”

“The Ancient Kingdom doesn’t belong solely to the Lü Family.”

The Lü Family Ancestor gritted his teeth.

A fierce internal battle raged in his mind.

“I... will trade.”

He suddenly made a decision that went against his ancestors’ will, breathing heavily—the temptation of ten Divine Physiques was simply too great.

Among those ten, even if only one cultivated the ‘Scripture of the Earth God’ to the Void Mystical Stage, then as Lin Jing said, the Lü Family would return to its peak.

“I like dealing with decisive people—saves me trouble.”

Lin Jing nodded in satisfaction and tossed over another plant.

“This is a Mutated Withered Immortal Herb. If you stab all its needles into your acupuncture points, you can transform into a corpse cultivator. I see your lifespan is running out—consider it. Though your cultivation will drop a lot, at least you’ll live longer and witness the Lü Family’s rise.”

Lin Jing casually tossed over one rare treasure after another—items the Lü Family Ancestor had never seen before.

But these treasures... made his eyelids twitch.

Fellow Daoist, this stuff of yours... doesn’t seem like what a normal cultivator should have.

After that, Lin Jing handed over nine more Mutated Earth Spirit Ginsengs to the Lü Family Ancestor.

“The bestowed artifact’s power lies, as you said, in its symbolism. Our trade is just between us—no third party will know. As long as I don’t talk, and you don’t talk, the artifact’s deterrent force will remain intact. The Lü Family will still be protected, as long as you don’t commit treason.”

“Many thanks... fellow Daoist.” The Lü Family Ancestor gave a respectful bow. At this moment, this was indeed an overwhelming opportunity.

Although the Earth Divine Physique ranked only around 90 among common Divine Physiques, if the Lü Family could suddenly produce ten, it would still be terrifying.

“I ask fellow Daoist to wait a few days. The bestowed artifact is hidden in an extremely secure place. Give me some time, and I’ll retrieve it for you!”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 69: Resurrecting the First Ancient Emperor?
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 . Chapter 69: Resurrecting the First Ancient Emperor?

Lin Jing nodded and chose to wait. He wasn’t worried about being tricked—someone of the other party’s strength wasn’t even qualified to try.

Fortunately, this Ancestor of the Lü Family was a sensible person. After secretly testing the effects of the Mutated Earth Spirit Ginseng and confirming that a fetus within a pregnant Lü woman—previously an ordinary child—had transformed into an Earth Spirit Body, he couldn’t help exclaiming in wonder.

An Earth Spirit Body might not be extraordinary, with only average talent and potential, but it wasn’t something one could just create. Even if two people with Earth Spirit Bodies came together, it wouldn’t guarantee a child with the same physique—the randomness was incredibly high.

The Lü Family didn’t even specialize in Earth-type cultivation, so it was nearly impossible for them to produce multiple offspring with Earth Spirit Bodies.

Maybe this Ancestor could easily recruit a dozen disciples with Earth Spirit Bodies, but they wouldn’t carry the Lü bloodline—there’d be no point in raising them.

“Fellow Daoist, this is the ‘Imperial Bestowed Artifact.’”

Lin Jing didn’t have to wait long before the Lü Family Ancestor brought out a black-gold token.

“Array token.” Lin Jing recognized the artifact’s nature at a glance.

“I can definitely feel a trace of True Dragon fortune from it.” Lin Jing put the token away with a smirk, certain that this was indeed an artifact refined and carried by the First Ancient Emperor for a long time.

“Well then, Fellow Daoist, until we meet again.” Now that he had what he came for, Lin Jing had no intention of lingering. He was already itching to try out the Divine Scripture.

But as Lin Jing’s departing figure faded, the Lü Family Ancestor looked down at the ginseng baby in his hands, heart inexplicably trembling.

It felt scorching hot.

“This strange... Fellow Daoist, why did he insist on acquiring something bestowed by the late emperor?”

“What is his goal?”

“Logically speaking, that token is just a useless formation component...”

Now that the sacrificial item was secured, Lin Jing still needed a Dragon Vein. These were abundant in the Ancient Kingdom, but every single one was strictly controlled—very inconvenient to use.

So, Lin Jing simply flew out of Ancient Kingdom territory and headed to a domain of his own!

This stretch of mountains near Martial Emperor City originally belonged to a rogue cultivator of the demon race.

That demon ancestor was no weakling. Being able to claim territory on the border of Martial Emperor City and the Ancient Kingdom meant they were quite formidable. Several demon clans with Demon Emperors in charge had invited this ancestor to join them, but all offers were declined.

However, after the revival of the demon race and the rise of the Demon Court, this demon ancestor was quick to pledge allegiance, waiting her turn for the Transformation Technique...

Her territory happened to be established atop a rather high-quality spiritual Dragon Vein!

Hovering over the mountains, Lin Jing released a faint aura and swept the area with his divine sense, searching for the demon ancestor.

“Where the h*ll did this brat come from, barging into my turf and spying on me bathing?”

A moment ago, feeling the ancestor’s divine sense, the furious demon burst out. Space tore open, and a monstrous creature descended—feathers covered in rock-like spikes, vaguely resembling a phoenix.

The demon ancestor, Stone Chicken Empress, was a spirit beast that had evolved from a mountain chicken. She’d once swallowed a phoenix fossil shard, reaching her current state through cultivation.

She didn’t just gain rebirth abilities similar to a phoenix, but could even produce a rare refining ore called “Phoenix Essence Ore.”

Thanks to that ability, she and Tang Pinghui had always maintained a smooth and correct partnership.

“Stone Chicken Empress, a pleasure to meet you.”

“No, this isn’t our first meeting. Back during my battle with Martial Emperor’s successor, Wan Renzong, in Martial Emperor City—you were watching in secret, weren’t you?”

“Y-Your Highness the Holy Son?!”

Stone Chicken Empress’s eyes widened. “Your Highness! What brings you to my humble home?”

“That time... my main body was still in seclusion. It was only my avatar attending the battle...”

“To greet Your Highness with an avatar was most disrespectful. I had planned to visit once my true body exited seclusion, but you left too soon…”

Lin Jing smiled. “It’s fine. I’m passing through and hoping to borrow the Dragon Vein beneath your lair. I won’t take it for free.”

He pulled out something he had no use for.

“This is a Mutated Crimson Fire Gold. If you temper it in spiritual flame for a hundred years, it should develop spiritual consciousness and transform into a sentient stone.”

Stone Chicken Empress’s eyes lit up. Such a treasure???

Currently, there were no rock spirits on the Tianyuan Continent—this Mutated Crimson Fire Gold was especially precious.

Even better, it was highly compatible with Stone Chicken Empress herself.

“Thank you, Your Highness! I will definitely try to hatch it and raise it like my own child!” she said.

“It’s just a Dragon Vein. Please, feel free to use it as you wish, Your Highness.”

“Thank you. Once we produce higher-grade Transformation Pills, the Demon Court will prioritize you.”

Under Stone Chicken Empress’s expectant gaze, Lin Jing descended deep into the earth. At the same time, he summoned the Longevity Turtle carrying the Divine Scripture onto his shoulder.

“With the Dragon Vein as the array pattern, the cavern as the array map, you as the formation core, and the Imperial Bestowed Artifact as the sacrificial offering—all conditions to activate the Divine Scripture Array are met. The rest is up to you.”

Lin Jing addressed the Longevity Turtle.

“And we’ll finally get to verify—whether the First Ancient Emperor is still alive!”

He had learned from the Demon Emperor that after training the Second Ancient Emperor, the First left the Tianyuan Realm and ventured into the stars, seeking an opportunity to ascend—and never returned.

It was a journey full of peril, and even now, the Demon Emperor didn’t know what had become of him.

Now, perhaps, through this top-tier technique from the Immortal Realm, they could find out. If the First Ancient Emperor wasn’t dead, the Divine Scripture couldn’t be activated. But if he was, then in theory, the scripture would summon his projection!

“His Highness really is so charming...” Outside, in the sky, Stone Chicken Empress was brooding over the Crimson Fire Gold while fantasizing about Lin Jing’s handsome demeanor.

“Once I transform, maybe I’ll try pursuing His Highness. If I could become his consort, my life would be complete.”

“Though I do wonder... what’s His Highness using my Dragon Vein for? Cultivation?”

As her protection duty extended for several days—

One day, she suddenly screamed.

“D*mn it, what’s the Holy Son doing?! My Dragon Vein—it’s been drained dry!!! That’s the core of my cultivation foundation!” Her vision went black.

At the same time, deep within the earth’s vein cave, Lin Jing and the Longevity Turtle stared at the now-powdered Imperial Token and the depleted Dragon Vein. Their pupils constricted as their minds trembled.

Lin Jing’s mind was a bit of a mess.

“Did it work?”

“So the First Ancient Emperor really did die out there in the stars.”

“And now... we’ve summoned his projection.”

“But just how powerful was the First Ancient Emperor? We sacrificed a high-grade artifact and drained a top-tier Dragon Vein, and even then, the projection doesn’t seem like it’ll last long.”

Lin Jing stared at the towering figure slowly taking form in front of him...

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 70: Extreme Reversal
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 70: Extreme Reversal

The figure before him wore black ceremonial robes, his expression stern. As his form slowly solidified, he opened his eyes—filled with confusion.

“I thought I was already…”

He looked forward and saw Lin Jing in a blue robe standing opposite him, with an awkward-looking turtle perched on his head, staring at him in astonishment.

“You summoned me in this form?” the man in black asked.

“That’s right…”

“Junior greets His Majesty, the Ancient Emperor,” Lin Jing offered a slight bow with his fists. “Indeed, it was this junior who took the liberty of summoning your projection. If I’ve offended in any way, I ask for your forgiveness.”

“So that’s how it is. This is the Divine Scripture, the legendary formation-based technique from the Immortal Palace.” He sighed. “No wonder I retain only a fraction of my power—barely at the Nascent Soul Stage… And the energy is fading fast. I fear I won’t last much longer before dispersing again.”

The First Ancient Emperor was full of emotion. “To think, after being dead for so many years, I’d have a chance to return to the Tianyuan Realm like this…”

Ascension... truly unimaginably difficult.

“This junior once heard that Your Majesty journeyed into the stars in pursuit of the ascension path. Now that several thousand years have passed, I was curious about your fate.
