That’s why I dared to use the Divine Scripture. May I ask... what led to your death? Is there really hope of ascension out in the stars?”

The First Ancient Emperor looked at Lin Jing and shook his head. “I don’t know…”

“I was headed to a place called the ‘Sea of Meteors,’ but before I even arrived, I was struck by a flying meteorite and killed instantly—body and Dao destroyed.”

Lin Jing: ?

“Huh?” Lin Jing was dumbfounded. The First Ancient Emperor—once the most famous name in the Tianyuan Realm, the legend who ended the Immortal Alliance’s reign—didn’t die during ascension or in battle with some stronger being from another world. He... got hit by a meteorite? That’s it?

The death felt absurdly anticlimactic.

The First Ancient Emperor chuckled. “I know it’s hard to say out loud, but that’s the truth. In the Tianyuan Realm, I was blessed by the world’s fortune, incredibly lucky. I cultivated the Dao of Fortune to its peak. But once I left for the stars, it was as if fate reversed—my fortune turned cursed. I don’t know why, but even an ordinary person might’ve had better luck than me. Everything became a struggle.”

Lin Jing’s expression stiffened slightly.

“You summoning me now might also be destiny’s doing. The ‘True Dragon Secret Art’ I created, wondrous though it is, only works properly within the Tianyuan Realm. Once outside, its power drastically weakens. I see that you also practice the ‘Hidden Dragon Secret Technique’—are you from Hidden Dragon Academy?”

“Just like that technique, if it leaves the Tianyuan Realm, it becomes ineffective. No matter how famous it gets across realms, it can’t gather the same fortune energy.”

“May I ask you to arrange a meeting with the Second Ancient Emperor? I wish to pass on an improved version of the ‘True Dragon Secret Art’ to the Ancient Clan. Even if it can’t match its original power outside the Tianyuan Realm, at least it’ll prevent future cultivators from ending up like me—hounded by misfortune, struck down by random meteorites.”

“There’s actually such a restriction?” Lin Jing was surprised. “This junior is a disciple of the Beast Taming Sect from the Wasteland Domain of the Ancient Kingdom, a student at Hidden Dragon Academy, and a member of the Demon-Slaying Bureau.”

“Your Majesty’s request—I can certainly honor it.”

“Given the Ancient Clan’s current situation, this information is indeed extremely important.”

“The past can be terrifying,” the First Ancient Emperor muttered. “To think that such a person could be born within the Ancient Kingdom. Although that body doesn’t seem capable of using any real strength, all the cultivation techniques, secret arts, and spells you've mastered seem to remain in your memory. Perhaps you can guide him in cultivation for a time.”

The Divine Scripture hasn’t been lost—so Wu Rui was right in her prediction. Wu Rui must’ve obtained it from a Cavern Heaven. For Lin Jing to obtain a technique of this level from a Cavern Heaven… his talent is likely rare in all of existence! I’d be glad to teach such a prodigy and strengthen the Ancient Kingdom!

“But what did he mean by the Ancient Kingdom’s current situation?”

The First Ancient Emperor looked at Lin Jing, puzzled.

“In ten years, the Ancient Kingdom plans to send a group of prodigies to the ‘Origin Realm.’ If the True Dragon Secret Art is limited outside the Tianyuan Realm, the Ancient Clan’s prodigies could face danger when they arrive there.”

“Origin Realm…” the First Ancient Emperor mused.

“Has anything else significant happened in the Tianyuan Realm while I was gone?”

Lin Jing nodded. “If we’re talking major events, there’ve been quite a few in recent decades. First, a peerless prodigy appeared in Qingzhou’s Demon Court. He developed a Transformation Technique that allowed demonkind to return to the world.”

The First Ancient Emperor nodded. “That is indeed a major event.”

“Second, this Demon Court prodigy uncovered intelligence that posed a massive threat to the Tianyuan Realm. The realm has since entered a state of military preparedness. All major factions have joined forces. Even sending a group of prodigies to the Origin Realm is part of preparing for this looming crisis.”

Before the First Ancient Emperor could ask further, Lin Jing explained the origins of the Demonic Sky Sect.

After hearing it, the First Ancient Emperor’s face turned extremely grim. “I suspected the Demonic Sky Sect had a mysterious background… So they’re guided by the Immortal Realm’s Demonic Sect, aiming to devour one lower realm after another…”

“Back then, extinguishing the Heavenly Flame Demonic Sect was incredibly difficult. Even to the end, I wasn’t sure if their Sect Leader had truly been destroyed…”

Lin Jing remained silent. That’s because... his reincarnation is now my guardian...

“In that light, leaving the Tianyuan Realm back then truly was irresponsible.” The First Ancient Emperor sighed. “Had I stayed, maybe the Tianyuan Realm wouldn’t be in such a dire state today.”

“Little brother, in that case, I must ask you again—please take me back to the Ancient Clan.”

He looked at Lin Jing with sincerity. “As thanks, whatever you ask—I will grant it!!”

“Whether it’s making the Beast Taming Sect the state religion of the Tianyuan Ancient Kingdom, or marrying a princess of the Ancient Clan and learning the True Dragon Secret Art—I’ll give you anything!”

Lin Jing was deeply moved, but replied, “Your Majesty, that won’t be necessary. I’ve already led the Beast Taming Sect to join the Demon Court. I no longer reside in the Ancient Kingdom. Besides, I find my current cultivation art, the Beast Taming Art, quite suitable—I don’t wish to switch to the True Dragon Secret Art…”

“I’ll help Your Majesty fulfill your wish, but let’s keep things fair. A clear exchange—one favor, one return.”

After speaking, the First Ancient Emperor was stunned.

What the h*ll?

“Allow me to reintroduce myself. Beyond my former affiliations, I am now the Holy Son of the Demon Court in Qingzhou. I’m no longer a cultivator of the Ancient Kingdom.”

“The main reason is that I possess the Longevity Physique, which is hard to nurture. Only the Demon Court was willing to give me full protection—that’s why I had no choice but to make this decision.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 71: Traveling in Disguise
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 71: Traveling in Disguise

The First Ancient Emperor’s face turned dark.

D*mn it, so this kid is that so-called peerless genius of the Demon Court—Lin Jing!

Earlier, sensing that Lin Jing cultivated the Hidden Dragon Secret Technique, he had assumed the boy was a prodigy of the Ancient Kingdom. But now…

Now it turned out he wasn’t even from the Ancient Kingdom—he wasn’t even from the human race’s forces—but the Holy Son of the demon race’s forces!

What made it worse was that Lin Jing had apparently defected from the Ancient Kingdom to the Demon Court!

“Say no more.” Seeing that Lin Jing wanted to explain further, the First Ancient Emperor interrupted, “I get the general idea.”

“You possess the Longevity Physique, a constitution that is notoriously hard to nurture and protect. During your time in the Ancient Kingdom, they didn’t shelter you adequately…”

“But because you developed the Transformation Technique for the demon race, the Demon Court views you as the key to their revival and offered you greater protection. That’s why you chose to join them.”

“That useless fool.” The First Ancient Emperor cursed aloud, swearing a few times at the Second Ancient Emperor. If he remembered right, the True Dragon Secret Art should contain a secret technique for observing a person’s latent fortune and talent.

A prodigy like Lin Jing—one who could obtain the Divine Scripture from a Cavern Heaven and even develop a technique that revived the demon race—was obviously a child of destiny. He couldn’t fathom how the Second Ancient Emperor failed to recognize and nurture such talent.

Only one explanation made sense: the Second Ancient Emperor never cared about cultivators from the lower classes.

“Wait a minute…” The First Ancient Emperor suddenly asked,

“After I left, did the Second Ancient Emperor implement a bunch of new policies?”

Lin Jing nodded. “Yes, quite a few.”

The First Ancient Emperor paced around. Even without knowing the specifics, he could already tell that the Ancient Kingdom now was vastly different from the one during his reign.

He asked cautiously,

“Does the Ancient Kingdom still offer free spirit root testing to every commoner?”

Lin Jing shook his head.

“And are the fortune Dragon Veins still evenly distributed across every region?”

Another shake of the head.

The First Ancient Emperor’s face darkened again. “I knew it. That brat never listened to a word I said.”

“Your name’s Lin Jing, right? I’ve decided—I’m not going back to the Ancient Clan just yet.”

“If you have time, take me on a tour around the Ancient Kingdom. I want to do a little ‘traveling in disguise’ to see what’s become of the country I left behind for thousands of years.”

“Haha.” Lin Jing replied, “I’ve got no problem with that. But my time is extremely valuable, and summoning Your Majesty’s projection already cost me a lot of effort. As long as you offer enough in return, forget traveling in disguise—even if you wanted to sit on the Demon Emperor’s throne, I’d figure something out for you.”

Legend said the First Ancient Emperor rose from humble beginnings—a commoner who stumbled upon a fateful encounter during chaotic times and began cultivating. That’s why he valued mortals more than those born into cultivation families or sects.

Now, suspecting the Seventh Ancient Emperor hadn’t followed his original intentions for the kingdom, he was especially displeased.

“You really don’t fear death, huh.” The First Ancient Emperor snorted.

“You’re not quite dead anyway—just got killed by a meteorite,” Lin Jing corrected.

“And now I’m alive again.” The First Ancient Emperor had no choice but to rely on Lin Jing. Though he felt that, with a few methods, he could avoid being fully controlled by the turtle, he also knew he couldn’t separate himself and exist independently.

In other words, if he wanted to remain active in the Tianyuan Realm, he had to rely on Lin Jing and the mechanical turtle.

“Well then, Your Majesty, for the time being, how about you travel with me in the guise of an old servant? Let’s observe the current state of the Tianyuan Realm together.”

“And if you know any powerful techniques, don’t hold back—teach me everything.”

“I’ll do my best to fulfill your wishes,” Lin Jing added with a grin.

The First Ancient Emperor, a carefree sort himself, simply waved it off and didn’t fuss about rank or title.

Before Lin Jing’s eyes, he quickly transformed into a dusty, world-weary old servant, looking like he’d been mistreated for years.

“Let’s go, Your Highness,” he said.

“Alright, but before returning to the Ancient Kingdom, I need to visit Martial Emperor City—there’s someone else I need to summon.”

The First Ancient Emperor asked, “Is it the Martial Emperor? Did he die too?”

“No, he’s still alive and well.”

“Then who are you resurrecting?”

“The Feng Family Ancestor.”

“Who?”

“An artifact refiner from the Void Mystical Stage.”

“Why bring back that guy? A Void Mystical artifact refiner… If you’ve got questions about refining, just ask me. I might not be able to forge Celestial Artifacts, but I’ve created plenty of near-immortal-grade pieces.”

“It’s not about refining… it’s about unresolved feelings between people and ghosts.”

“…?”

As Lin Jing and the now-servant-disguised First Ancient Emperor emerged from the empty Dragon Vein cave, Stone Chicken Empress rushed over the moment she saw Lin Jing.

“Your Highness, my Dragon Vein… sob sob sob… my Dragon Vein…”

Lin Jing glanced at the dried-up vein—drained to summon the First Ancient Emperor—and fell silent. A bit embarrassed, he said, “There was a bit of an accident. I’ll compensate you later for this spirit vein.”

“How about this—don’t cultivate here anymore. Go to Qingzhou, to the Demon Court. Wait for me there. Within ten years, I’ll get you a Transformation Pill and arrange a new cultivation cave.”

“Really?! Great!” Stone Chicken Empress immediately brightened up.

“By the way… who’s this?” She couldn’t help asking when she noticed that Lin Jing had gone in alone but emerged with an old man carrying a turtle.

The turtle could be Lin Jing’s pet, but what about this old man? Where did he come from?

“This is Old Gu. He’s my personal cook—amazing chef,” Lin Jing said.

“I can cook too! How about I stay with Your Highness as well!” Stone Chicken Empress protested, jealous that even some scruffy Nascent Soul old man got to follow him.

Where’d this wild hen come from… The First Ancient Emperor glanced at her, unimpressed.

“Your Highness, we should get moving. I don’t have much time left,” he urged.

“Alright,” Lin Jing declined Stone Chicken Empress’s offer and, with the First Ancient Emperor in tow, set off for Martial Emperor City.

Watching their backs fade into the distance, Stone Chicken Empress muttered,

“Forget it… that old guy won’t last long anyway. Once he’s gone, I’ll become the Holy Son’s personal chef instead. My specialty—stir-fried river stones—is unbeatable.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 72: Smuggling
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 72: Smuggling

Martial Emperor City, Feng Family.

Half a year after their last meeting, Lin Jing stepped into this place once again.

Unlike before, when he was accompanied by the Thunder Dragon Ancestor as his Guardian,

this time, it was the First Ancient Emperor transformed into an old servant who followed him.

Though the cultivation level of his companion had dropped, his identity had risen sharply.

“I wonder how Senior Thunder Dragon is doing.”

Standing in front of the Feng residence, Lin Jing was curious.

Back then, when facing the Emperor, Cold Moon Sword Immortal had teleported him and Gao Sheng away, leaving the Thunder Dragon Ancestor behind to fight...

The Emperor didn’t stop him, as its main target then was the Dao-Seeking Sword Spirit.

However, after the Thunder Dragon Ancestor dropped the Thunder Dragon prodigies off at the Solar Temple, before he could return, the Bone Emperor had already been repelled by Lin Jing wielding the Celestial Sword,

leaving only the Thunder Dragon Ancestor, who returned to the scene in confusion afterward.

“Senior Thunder Dragon should be back in Qingzhou by now. After comprehending the Primordial Sword Intent, he wanted to focus on studying it, but had no free time. Now that he’s back, he should finally be able to cultivate properly.”

Lin Jing took out a sword-shaped dragon scale.

Sword intent fluctuations rippled across the dragon scale.

Back then, the Thunder Dragon Ancestor had said he’d pass the Primordial Sword Intent to Lin Jing, but Lin Jing didn’t have the energy to study it himself, so he decided to have his pet beast learn it instead.

This dragon scale was the vessel carrying the Primordial Sword Intent.

“This sword intent’s not bad.” Beside him, the First Ancient Emperor nodded in approval.

“So, he plans to turn that dragon scale into a sword?”

“Not exactly.” Lin Jing put the dragon scale away. “It’s just a cultivation tool. Before returning to the Ancient Kingdom, I’ll hand it over to my pet beast.”

“Your pet beast is going to learn sword intent??” The First Ancient Emperor was stunned.

“No problem.” Lin Jing nodded. With not just a Mutated Bodhi Leaf but also Enlightenment Pool water helping, the Ice Soul Gu will definitely be able to learn it.

Once the Ice Soul Gu learns it, it’s basically like I’ve learned it too.

As the two chatted, eight figures slowly appeared within the Feng Family grounds.

They were the Feng Family’s current Peak Void Mystical Stage Thunder Dragon, Feng Yu.

Cold Moon Sword Immortal Feng Ning.

And the Ghost Bride Tang Ling.

“Didn’t expect him to return so soon.” Seeing Lin Jing, Feng Ning was pleasantly surprised. It had only been half a year since they last parted. Had the ancestor already trained the pet beast to master the Divine Scripture?

That was fast.

“Your Highness has finally arrived!!”

Ancestor Feng Yu looked eager, since they were about to resurrect their clan’s progenitor.

“Mhm.” Tang Ling gave Lin Jing a nod. Everyone’s eyes shifted to the turtle perched on the First Ancient Emperor’s head, knowing that this little guy would be key to what came next.

“But this person is...” Ancestor Feng Yu glanced at the First Ancient Emperor, frowning. Although the old man only had the early Nascent Soul Stage cultivation, he gave off an unsettling vibe.

“He goes by Turtle Immortal, has some strange abilities. He follows me around doing odd chores. Don’t worry about him,” Lin Jing brushed it off.

At that moment, “Turtle Immortal,” the First Ancient Emperor, was sneakily eyeing Tang Ling...

Where’d this wild ghost come from.

So the “unresolved love between human and ghost” Lin Jing mentioned was about this female ghost, huh?

Could it be... the Feng Family Ancestor was her old flame?

The First Ancient Emperor’s mind raced. The Feng Family—yes, they were an artifact refining clan from Martial Emperor City...

This Holy Son of the Demon Court, not cultivating properly, yet meddling in these matters of love—unexpected.

“She’s not bad though.”

But in the next moment, the First Ancient Emperor was distracted by Cold Moon Sword Immortal Feng Ning. He was skilled in talent scouting. Back then, with his keen eye, he’d recruited many gifted cultivators to his cause.

You could say that many of the Ancient Kingdom’s founding families were not descendants of immortal sects, but grassroots cultivators like himself.

Now, with one glance, he could tell—this female sword cultivator had the potential for Dao-Seeking. Her talent might not surpass this “Holy Son of the Demon Court,” but she was still among the top elites of the continent.

“Are you a Feng Family cultivator, or from the Heavenly Sword Gate?” he asked after noticing the Heavenly Sword token at Feng Ning’s waist.

“Hm?” Feng Ning glanced at the First Ancient Emperor. “I’m a cultivator from the Heavenly Sword Gate.”

“Good, good, I knew it. No way a small temple like the Feng Family could produce such a prodigy. Good to know you’re from the Ancient Kingdom,” the First Ancient Emperor laughed heartily, causing Ancestor Feng Yu’s face to darken and black lines to crawl across his forehead.

This Nascent Soul brat really knows how to hit a nerve.

If it weren’t for him following the Holy Son of the Demon Court, Feng Yu would’ve slapped him flying already.

But what shocked Feng Yu was that despite this old man’s disrespect, the Holy Son didn’t react at all, which made him suspicious—who exactly was this old man?

He might not be what he seems.

“By the way, is the Dragon Vein ready?” Lin Jing asked. “The Feng Family shares a massive Dragon Vein with Martial Emperor City, right? We definitely can’t use that one for the Divine Scripture—it’d attract opposition from the City Lord’s Mansion and other families.”

Ancestor Feng Yu grinned. “Rest assured, Your Highness. I’ve already prepared everything.”

“Dragon Veins are rare and mostly monopolized by the Ancient Kingdom. Even the most basic ones aren’t easy to obtain...”

“But a few days ago, I asked a friend in the Ancient Kingdom to smuggle two Dragon Veins out. Should be enough!”

“What the h*ll.” The First Ancient Emperor blurted out, “Smuggling Dragon Veins? That’s a capital offense!”

Spiritual veins only provide spiritual energy.

But spiritual energy Dragon Veins provide not only energy, but fortune!

They’re naturally rare and precious.

“This sort of thing happens all the time. There’s already a secret trade network, it almost never gets exposed!”

“Even if it does, the Ancient Kingdom can’t do anything to us. They’ll only punish the Ancient Kingdom cultivators who facilitated the trade. All the risk is on their side! Of course, we still paid a price—several times higher than the value of the Dragon Veins themselves!”

“I see, I see.” The First Ancient Emperor said with biting sarcasm, “Senior, I too would love to procure a Dragon Vein for my family, just a basic one. Could you kindly tell me which Ancient Kingdom cultivator I need to approach to make a purchase?”

Ancestor Feng Yu looked at Lin Jing.

Lin Jing looked up at the sky.

“This Holy Son would also like to buy a few. Do tell us.”

“It’s the Sect Regulatory Office. What we bought were basically branch Dragon Veins from sects that had been sealed by the Ancient Kingdom... if Your Highness is interested, I’ll give you the contact details of that official.”

“Just pass it to Turtle Immortal,” Lin Jing nodded. The Sect Regulatory Office, huh—may they rest in peace.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 73: Dissipated Obsession
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 73: Dissipated Obsession

Soon after, Lin Jing and the others found a place within the Feng residence and used two Dragon Veins to set up the array patterns.

These two Dragon Veins were of very ordinary quality—enough to perhaps allow a Foundation Establishment clan to produce a single Nascent Soul cultivator at best, far inferior to the one the Stone Chicken Empress had.

However, compared to reviving the projection of the First Ancient Emperor, reviving a Peak Void Mystical Stage artifact refiner didn’t require such high-grade Dragon Veins.

“So tired... turtle's exhausted...”

The Mechanical Turtle, acting as the formation core, laid on the ground as if the earth was its bed and the sky its blanket, mentally drained—having performed the Divine Scripture twice in a row was truly exhausting.

But for the sake of Lin Jing’s promise—that once it completed its task, it could travel freely—no matter how tired it was, it endured.

Throughout the process of reviving the Feng Family Ancestor, the First Ancient Emperor watched with childlike curiosity, eager to see how he himself had once been resurrected.

Before long, under the watchful eyes of Lin Jing and the others, the projection of a blue-robed elder appeared above the dried-up Dragon Vein.

Feng Ling, the Feng Family Ancestor, opened his eyes in shock, raising his arm, astonished as to why he, long deceased, had reappeared in the world.

“Ancestor!!”

At that moment, Ancestor Feng Yu’s eyes were red as he looked at Feng Ling and said, “It’s me. I’m Yu’er.”

“Who are you?” Feng Ling looked at him.

“Do I know you?”

“You two... aren’t even from the same era, are you?” Feng Ning couldn’t help but interject.

“Oh.” Ancestor Feng Yu paused, then recalled—Feng Ling had passed away before even his grandfather was born. He’d tried the emotional approach way too early.

He quickly said, “This junior is a cultivator of the later generations of the Feng Family. I was the one who asked this great one to summon the projection of our ancestor.”

Feng Ling’s gaze swept over Lin Jing, Feng Ning, the First Ancient Emperor, Feng Yu, and Tang Ling one by one—and from Lin Jing and the Mechanical Turtle, he could indeed feel a powerful suppressive force.

“I ask you,” now finally seeing Feng Ling, the Ghost Bride took a deep breath and said, “Do you know the name Jiang Feng?”

As soon as she spoke, Feng Ling’s expression changed. “Who are you, and where did you hear that name?”

“So you do know it.” At those words, Tang Ling’s eyes sparkled. After searching for so long, she had finally found some answers.

“Wait!” Suddenly, the more Feng Ling looked at the Ghost Bride, the more familiar she seemed.

A chill ran down his spine.

“You’re... Tang... Tang…”

“It’s me.” The Ghost Bride looked at him and smiled. “You really are her child. More accurately, a descendant of the two of us. Feng Ling, she never forgot her promise to me.”

Having received the answer she sought, the Ghost Bride Tang Ling’s smile spread. Meanwhile, Ancestor Feng Yu stood dumbfounded—wait, this Ghost Bride... was really the Feng Family’s progenitor?!

The founder of the Feng Family, Feng Ling, was actually a descendant of this Ghost Bride??

Judging from Feng Ling’s reaction, he clearly recognized her!

Oh no.

I’m in deep trouble, Ancestor Feng Yu thought, glancing at Lin Jing. Did this mean... he now had to call that Ghost Bride Ancestor too...?

“Senior Tang Ling, do you want to look for Senior Jiang Feng’s relics and resurrect her projection so the two of you can reunite?” Lin Jing asked, a weight lifted from his heart. This was one more promise fulfilled—he had helped the Ghost Bride fulfill her final wish.

The Ghost Bride Tang Ling just gazed silently at the stunned Feng Family Ancestor for a long while, then shook her head.

“No... my obsession is already fading. Let the past drift away with the wind. Knowing the truth is enough to bring peace to my heart.”

“To meet again... might not actually be better.”

“The Divine Scripture... I’ve never used it, but it gives me an eerie feeling. You should be cautious with it. Some extremely powerful spells, when cast too often, may eventually backfire on the user...” She offered this advice out of concern for Lin Jing.

The First Ancient Emperor stared at the female ghost in surprise—such insight!

That was exactly how the True Dragon Secret Art was.

Unmatched within the Tianyuan Realm, but once outside... pure misfortune. The karmic backlash from fortune had once plagued him badly, and he had no desire to experience it again.

In the First Ancient Emperor’s eyes, the Divine Scripture was also extremely heaven-defying. But what would be the eventual cost of summoning projections of the dead like this...?

Most likely, when a certain trigger is reached, the user would be plagued with great misfortune.

“Senior...” Feng Ning and Ancestor Feng Yu looked at the Ghost Bride, whose expression had grown vacant, and felt a foreboding sense.

“Do you have a sword? Just a normal sword,” the Ghost Bride turned to Feng Ning.

“I do,” Feng Ning replied in confusion, taking out a spiritual sword she had forged herself.

The Ghost Bride nodded and said, “Now that my obsession has faded, I have two paths ahead. One, defy fate and continue down the path of a ghost cultivator. Two, go with the flow and dissipate along with my obsession.”

“Neither path appeals to me. I don’t want to remain in this world alone, nor do I want to vanish entirely.”

“Today, I will reshape myself, becoming a mindless Sword Spirit, bound to your sword. This way, I can do one last thing for the bloodline she left behind.”

“Senior!!” Feng Ning gasped, but before she could react, the Ghost Bride had already ignited herself, merging into Feng Ning’s sword.

At the same time, a voice echoed in Feng Ning’s heart.

“You remind me so much of her. That’s why I’ve always treated you like my own descendant.”

“I know you don’t get along with the Feng Family, but I still hope that if the Feng Family ever faces a crisis, you’ll lend a hand—within your ability, of course...”

“And aside from that... Lin Jing...”

Outside.

Seeing the Ghost Bride burn herself away and merge into Feng Ning’s sword as a Sword Spirit, both Feng Ling and Feng Yu were horrified. That meant she was now truly... completely gone.

“Mother!” Feng Ling cried out. Though it was his first time seeing the Ghost Bride, he was certain—this was the other mother that Jiang’s mother had often spoken of.

And Ancestor Feng Yu was now filled with guilt for how he had treated her.

He’d thought the Ghost Bride harbored ill intent. Who would’ve guessed she was actually the Feng Family’s progenitor? He regretted it deeply.

“Sigh.” The First Ancient Emperor shook his head.

Lin Jing also stood silently watching, not stopping it.

The Ghost Bride had been born from obsession. Now that it had faded, she chose to become a Sword Spirit to protect the Feng Family’s descendant. That, too, was a new kind of longing.

“Senior...” Even Cold Moon Sword Immortal, as firm-hearted as she was, couldn’t help the stir in her heart.

Lin Jing had always hated these sorrowful scenes.

He sighed.

“Seeing how much you all miss Senior Tang Ling, and now that she’s ‘truly dead,’ she should meet the Divine Scripture’s criteria. So how about this—let’s smuggle two more Dragon Veins and summon her projection to chat a bit?” He hadn’t listened to a single word of Tang Ling’s advice.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 74: External Incarnation
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 74: External Incarnation

It’s just a cultivation technique, what kind of side effects could it have.

Even if there are side effects, it’s the Mechanical Turtle using the Divine Scripture, what does that have to do with Lin Jing,

Lin Jing’s philosophy is to not worry about things that haven’t happened yet.

By the time side effects show up, he might already be an Immortal Emperor, and could fix it with a flick of his hand.

Better to enjoy life in the moment!

The word “optimism” defined Lin Jing’s entire life.

“Let’s not bother Senior Tang Ling after all.”

Feng Ning said helplessly, “I also think summoning a dead person whose obsession has already dispersed might bring some bad consequences.”

Since he came back, he hadn’t been happy once.

“Alright then.” Lin Jing nodded.

“At this point, we’ll stay in Martial Emperor City for a few days, but we won’t get involved in the Feng Family’s matters.”

He looked at the summoned projection of the Feng Family Ancestor.

As for how this Feng Family Ancestor would communicate with the Feng Family, he didn’t care.

“Thank you.” The Feng Family Ancestor, though he didn’t know Lin Jing, still cupped his fists toward the young man, grateful he had summoned his projection.

“Your Highness the Holy Son, I’ve prepared a residence for you. You can rest at the Feng Family these next few days.” Ancestor Feng Yu said.

Lin Jing waved his hand and said, “It’s alright, no need.”

“I have other things to do in Martial Emperor City, so I won’t stay any longer.”

.............

Lin Jing didn’t stay long at the Feng Family.

It’s not like he had anything urgent to do…

It was simply because the First Ancient Emperor had sent him a secret voice transmission, saying he wanted to look around Martial Emperor City.

Back then, he had really wanted to bring Martial Emperor City into the Ancient Kingdom, but sadly couldn’t overcome the combination of the Celestial Artifact and the Martial Emperor.

Now, he wanted to first see how Martial Emperor City had developed, and then compare it with the Ancient Kingdom.

If the Ancient Kingdom hadn’t developed as well as Martial Emperor City over these past few thousand years, he planned to take off his shoes and properly educate the Second Ancient Emperor’s butt and cheeks.

“Your Majesty.”

“What is it.”

As Lin Jing and the First Ancient Emperor walked through Martial Emperor City, Lin Jing suddenly asked, “You’re probably the person who understands the Demonic Sky Sect best in this era, right?”

Senior Shen Song… isn’t human.

“I want to refine an External Incarnation, solely to cultivate the techniques of the Demonic Sky Sect. I wonder if Your Majesty has any advice.”

“I already knew you were cultivating the Heavenly Demon Physique… Longevity Physique and Heavenly Demon Physique, truly the best combination. You should know, even among the Demon-Slaying Bureau, not many can cultivate the Heavenly Demon Physique. Your talent really is excellent.” The First Ancient Emperor muttered.

He still regretted that a talent like this wasn’t retained by the Ancient Kingdom.

“Please instruct me, Senior.”

“Listen carefully then. The Demonic Sky Sect has countless techniques, but in my view, only five are truly worth cultivating.”

“First, the cultivation technique: Heavenly Demon Canon. Its trait is the ability to cultivate a formless body, lacking any physical form, giving a one-hundred percent success rate for body possession, and allowing multiple possessions. Worst of all, it can devour fortune to speed up cultivation, and it enhances all other Demonic Sky Sect techniques.”

“To destroy the Demonic Sky Sect back then, the Ancient Kingdom paid a huge price…”

“Second, the physique: Heavenly Demon Physique, which you already cultivate, so you should understand its effect.”

“Third, the divine sense technique: Heavenly Demon Divine Sense, which you also already cultivate.”

“Fourth, the core technique: Heavenly Demon Finger. A single finger can shatter stars. It’s an incredibly destructive technique—among all I’ve seen, it ranks in the top three. But cultivating it requires devouring the heart demons of others, and it’s highly prone to backlash.”

“Fifth, the core secret art: Heavenly Demon Crossing. A spatial transference technique, the most powerful void transference method I’ve ever seen. It allows your physical body to easily travel through the void, cross any spatial barriers—no matter how vast the world, you can come and go freely.”

“In my opinion, the other techniques of the Demonic Sky Sect aren’t worth it, but these five are the foundation of their power!”

“Even the Sect Leader of the Demonic Sky Sect back then didn’t master all five. Since your main body has already learned the Heavenly Demon Physique and Heavenly Demon Divine Sense, now you just need to consider the Heavenly Demon Canon, Heavenly Demon Finger, and Heavenly Demon Crossing.”

“To better cultivate the Heavenly Demon Canon, I recommend using a fortune dragon vein as the material for the External Incarnation.”

“To better cultivate the Heavenly Demon Finger, I recommend using Heartless Stone from Tianshan as the material.”

“And for Heavenly Demon Crossing, it’s best to use a Void Stone that has drifted through chaotic void currents for many years.”

“But then again, refining an External Incarnation is extremely difficult. Normally, cultivators only use one material to refine it. Every extra material increases the risk of failure. You’d better think it through.”

Lin Jing took a deep breath—this was the most troublesome part. Theoretically, using just one material was best, but to make the incarnation flawless, multiple materials were needed…

Previously, the Demon Emperor had even helped him extort a quasi-immortal grade Immortal Water Lotus from the Misty Forest, which was also well-suited for refining an incarnation.

With so many materials, he couldn’t refine the External Incarnation properly at all.

“So troublesome…”

“Take your time to think. Of the Five Heavenly Demon Techniques, aside from the treasure needed to cultivate Heavenly Demon Divine Sense, which the Ancient Kingdom no longer has, the Demon-Slaying Bureau has the other four, and I still remember them all. If you want to learn, I’ll teach you.”

Lin Jing fell into deep thought.

But after just a moment, he stopped hesitating.

“Your Majesty, you and Turtle can go wander Martial Emperor City on your own, that shouldn’t be a problem, right?”

“What about you?” The First Ancient Emperor was surprised.

“I’m going to take some time to refine an External Incarnation. I’ll be back soon. After we leave Martial Emperor City, my main body has to go cultivate at Spirit Dao Sect. I might only be able to send the incarnation to accompany you on this ‘secret tour.’”

“And just in time, I’ll trouble you to guide my incarnation a bit.”

The First Ancient Emperor was surprised: “The materials I just mentioned for refining an External Incarnation—you have them all?”

Lin Jing shook his head. “I thought it over. It’s still best for the incarnation to focus on Heavenly Demon techniques—they really are powerful. But there’s no need to make the materials perfect. Using multiple materials is too troublesome. It might take decades or even a hundred years. I don’t have that kind of time anymore.”

A Longevity Physique saying he doesn’t have time… The First Ancient Emperor couldn’t help but roast him in his mind.

But he understood—what Lin Jing meant by “no time” was that he also had to enter the Origin Realm, and it was opening in less than ten years.

If Lin Jing went into seclusion to refine the incarnation using just one material, it might still be possible.

But using three or four? The difficulty was unimaginably high. Even ignoring the time to gather the materials, ten years wouldn’t be enough.

And refining it after entering the Origin Realm... would be even worse. Too many enemies there, no peaceful cultivation environment.

“So what do you plan to use?” the First Ancient Emperor asked.

“Immortal Water Lotus.” Lin Jing chose the simple route—just one material.

“A plant?” The First Ancient Emperor was stunned again. “You have such a top-grade External Incarnation material, and you’re still asking around? Just use that.”

“Using that thing to cultivate demonic arts isn’t too bad either.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 75: A Real Promotion
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 75: A Real Promotion

Immortal Water Lotus, a plant that harmonizes with immortal energy, comes with a Flawless Divine Body.

The Flawless Divine Body, simply put, is immune to all contaminants that could affect it.

Like the First Ancient Emperor just mentioned, to cultivate the Heavenly Demon Finger, a cultivator must devour another person’s heart demon power.

Heart demon power—most cultivators avoid it like the plague, let alone actively touch it.

Heart demons pose the greatest threat to a cultivator, and must be overcome by the cultivator themselves. Because heart demons are contagious, any attempt to help might infect others, leading to mental instability.

So the process of cultivating Heavenly Demon Finger is extremely dangerous.

That’s precisely why the First Ancient Emperor recommended using Heartless Stone to refine the External Incarnation—to follow the Path of Heartlessness. That way, one can avoid the invasion of heart demons to the greatest extent.

Heartlessness is the best weapon against heart demons.

Someone with a Flawless Divine Body cultivating a demonic art with side effects—combined with the Heavenly Demon Physique and the Longevity Physique—is a perfect match.

“I was just weighing my options, seeing if there was anything better than Immortal Water Lotus.”

“There’s not. In the Tianyuan Realm, Immortal Water Lotus is one of the best materials for refining an External Incarnation.”

“Unless some immortal item appears, but compared to the gentle Immortal Water Lotus, immortal items aren’t something the average cultivator can refine.”

“Where’d you even get such a great item?”

Lin Jing smiled. The Demon Emperor had said the same thing—there was nothing in the Demon Court better than the Immortal Water Lotus.

He had suggested that if Lin Jing wanted to refine an External Incarnation, he should prioritize this.

“Misty Forest,” Lin Jing said.

“No wonder...” The First Ancient Emperor nodded. “That Forest Sea Dragon Emperor guy took the Wood Path to its extreme. He has the power to evolve plants to quasi-immortal grade. If it’s him, maturing an Immortal Water Lotus isn’t strange at all.”

“But to be given such a treasure—it means you’re really something. It’s probably because of the Transformation Technique, right?”

“Maybe.” Lin Jing answered slowly.

After all, reaching the level of the Forest Sea Dragon Emperor, Yin-Yang Whale Emperor, or the Lord of the Demon Court, progressing normally becomes nearly impossible.

Transformation is, without doubt, a rare path forward.

“In any case, if you use Immortal Water Lotus to refine the External Incarnation, it’ll still help with cultivating Heavenly Demon Finger.”

“It just won’t help with Heavenly Demon Canon or Heavenly Demon Crossing.”

“It’s not a big deal... I’ll figure it out later.” Lin Jing wasn’t planning to dwell on it anymore. Using Immortal Water Lotus to refine the External Incarnation had another benefit: once in the Origin Realm, he could rely on the incarnation to better absorb immortal essence and grow stronger.

Moreover, the incarnation would be even more compatible with immortal techniques that require immortal energy.

Lin Jing might not have immortal techniques yet, but with the Divine Scripture, he could summon projections of ancient immortals and have them teach him. That would be quite convenient.

..........

Some time ago, his heart demon entered the Hourglass of Time, ending the process of refining the External Incarnation.

As for the First Ancient Emperor, he wandered around Martial Emperor City out of boredom.

Before long, he lit up at the sight of a talent.

“Acquired Fortune Physique?”

Fortune Physique, wasn’t that the physique I created myself to boost fortune? But it’s incredibly hard to cultivate—rare even among the Ancient Clan...

“This kid must be from the Ancient Kingdom, right? There’s no way someone smuggled a secret art I created all the way to Martial Emperor City!”

So, the First Ancient Emperor intercepted a young man riding a white horse who had just entered the city.

“Old man, what do you want?”

Gao Sheng frowned and looked at the annoying old man who blocked his way.

This was the same Gao Sheng who had been sent to the Heavenly Sword Gate by Cold Moon Sword Immortal via the Sky-Splitting Sword, and later dragged to the capital by the sect master.

Of course, because Lin Jing had suddenly taken control of the Celestial Sword, Gao Sheng and the sect master hadn’t even had time to meet the Ancient Imperial Prince before the sect master dragged him back to the Heavenly Sword Gate.

At the Heavenly Sword Gate, Gao Sheng lived in constant fear, worried the Second Ancient Emperor would come after him—after all, he had a big role in the recent chaos.

Fortunately, the Sky-Splitting Sword Ancestor recognized that Gao Sheng had delivered vital information to the Heavenly Sword Gate instead of the Ancient Clan, and pointed him toward a promising path.

“Go find my disciple again.”

“She’s still in the Western Desert.”

“Your fortune is sky-high. Being by her side won’t harm her. I’ll also give you a life-saving sword intent to ensure your safety on the journey.”

And so, Gao Sheng fled the Ancient Kingdom again, coming to find the Cold Moon Sword Immortal to form another demon-slaying team with her.

Now he’d arrived in Martial Emperor City looking for Feng Ning—but before he could find her, he ran into a beggar-like old man.

“Young man, I see you have unique bones and astounding fortune. Tell me, are you from the Eastern Ancient Kingdom?” the First Ancient Emperor asked with a cheerful smile.

“You’ve got a sharp eye, old man.” Gao Sheng replied, “How’d you know I came from the Ancient Kingdom?”

“If you’ve got something to say, say it quick—I’m in a rush.”

The First Ancient Emperor chuckled, “Acquired Fortune Physique—that’s a Foundation Establishment secret art. If I recall, only the Ancient Kingdom has it. Even most Ancient Kingdom cultivators can’t access this Foundation Establishment technique. Only nobles and high officials...”

“Sh*t.” Gao Sheng was shocked. The Fortune Physique wasn’t something obvious like Fire or Water Physique—it was deeply hidden. Yet this old man saw through it at a glance...

“Senior, you have sharp eyes. May I ask your name?” Gao Sheng instantly became respectful, realizing this old man was no ordinary figure.

Cultivation... probably at least Nascent Soul Transformation, right?

“Just call me Turtle Immortal...” The First Ancient Emperor stroked his beard. That turtle didn’t want to stick with him and had gone off to explore Martial Emperor City on its own.

“So, Senior, why did you stop me?” Gao Sheng asked.

The First Ancient Emperor said, “Oh, nothing much. I’m from the Ancient Kingdom too, though I haven’t been back in a long time. I miss life over there.”

“I was just curious how much the Ancient Kingdom has changed lately. Judging by you, you must be from a noble family. Would you mind walking and chatting with me for a bit?”

“Gladly.” Gao Sheng wasn’t in a hurry. “I’ll treat you. Let’s head to the best tavern in Martial Emperor City and have a drink!”

“You’re generous, little brother. But I’m curious—at just the Core Formation Stage, you traveled all the way to Martial Emperor City. What for?”

“Well, the great era is upon us, ghosts and demons rising in the west. I came here to slay demons. This may not be the Ancient Kingdom, but there are still many commoners who need protection. The Buddhist sect’s manpower is limited. I may not be powerful, but I can still do something.” Gao Sheng spoke with righteous vigor, revealing his Demon-Slaying Bureau badge as he talked.

“Ah? So you’re from the Demon-Slaying Bureau! The cultivation world needs people like you. Actually, I have some old friends in high positions back in the Ancient Kingdom. I’ll have them promote you!”

“Promote me?” Gao Sheng chuckled. “I’m not interested in that.”

The First Ancient Emperor narrowed his eyes, picking up on a trace of dissatisfaction.

“Indeed, a talent like you shouldn’t step into that dirty pond. Under the Second Ancient Emperor’s leadership, even the Ancient Kingdom is getting worse by the day!” said the First Ancient Emperor.

“Yeah...” Gao Sheng blurted out instinctively, then suddenly covered his mouth and snapped, “What are you talking about, old man? Under the Second Ancient Emperor’s leadership, the people of the Ancient Kingdom are living happy lives!”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 76: Acting Separately
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 76: Acting Separately

Hourglass of Time.

Lin Jing sat cross-legged on the ground, the quasi-immortal item “Immortal Water Lotus” floating before him.

This Immortal Water Lotus looked like a fairy among flowers—Lin Jing even suspected that if he used it to refine his incarnation, he might turn into some pretty boy.

He wasn’t the only one.

Dragon Carp was also going to refine an External Incarnation alongside Lin Jing.

Lin Jing was refining his incarnation to enhance combat power—they’d be going to the Origin Realm together.

As for Dragon Carp, its incarnation would stay behind in the Tianyuan Realm, guarding the Demon Court and focusing on refining Transformation Pills.

Besides Dragon Carp, Pine Leaf Mouse would also leave its incarnation in the Tianyuan Realm, but since it hadn’t formed its Nascent Soul yet, it wasn’t the right time to refine one.

“Use this thing to refine your incarnation.”

Lin Jing took out a piece of quasi-immortal grade Lightning-Struck Wood.

The Demon Emperor had given it to him, saying it had been born accidentally when the former emperor of the Demon Court was undergoing tribulation.

Not exactly a top-tier material for refining an incarnation—far inferior to Immortal Water Lotus—but the best option currently available in the Tianyuan Realm for Dragon Carp.

Normally, cultivators—whether human or beast—refined incarnations to better comprehend natural forces.

But someone like Dragon Carp, who had already formed Immortal Flame and possessed Ancestral-tier Bloodline, didn’t need an incarnation for that.

So for it, the incarnation—like Lin Jing’s—would focus more on function.

Obviously, since Dragon Carp’s incarnation was staying behind to refine pills, it had to emphasize fire and wood attributes.

Dragon Carp itself was fire-attribute… using its Nascent Soul to refine the incarnation would naturally make it fire-attribute, so the material only needed to cover the wood aspect.

Moreover, the Transformation Pill’s refining method differed from others, requiring the Thunder Refining Method—so the incarnation also needed to carry lightning properties.

Lightning-Struck Wood perfectly matched that requirement. Dragon Carp’s incarnation was basically born for refining Transformation Pills.

“Rrrip~~” Dragon Carp sighed dramatically. Poor incarnation—it was about to inherit its alchemy legacy.

While mourning its incarnation in silence, Dragon Carp was secretly delighted—it finally didn’t have to refine pills anymore...

“Alright, let’s begin.”

Lin Jing spoke softly. Together with Dragon Carp, he released their Nascent Souls, sending them respectively into the Immortal Water Lotus and the Lightning-Struck Wood.

The method for refining an External Incarnation was simple—just like body possession. As long as the Nascent Soul entered the material, the rest involved shaping it using the External Incarnation secret art.

The creator of the External Incarnation technique was long lost to history, but this method—carving and merging natural treasures with one’s Nascent Soul—had become essential for all cultivators at or above the Nascent Soul stage.

The only challenge and drawback lay in the reliance on mastery of Nascent Soul power. Refining an incarnation drained mental energy. If it failed, you’d never get another chance. External Incarnation was a one-time-use secret art—each cultivator could use it only once.

Some had tried to improve the technique, but no one had succeeded.

Hourglass of Time—three months later.

Outside world, Ninth Heaven.

Four figures emerged.

Both Lin Jing and Dragon Carp had refined their incarnations quickly—only took a few months.

Mainly because their Nascent Souls were incredibly strong: one refined it with Nascent Soul Transformation cultivation, the other with Ancestral-tier bloodline. Plus, both had cut corners in sculpting the incarnations—skipping steps, sacrificing completeness—which sped things up.

Two identical figures stepped out from the Hourglass of Time. Lin Jing’s incarnation gave a small nod.

“You two head out first—I’ll go pick up the First Ancient Emperor and the Mechanical Turtle.”

“Take care.” Lin Jing’s main body cupped his fists toward the incarnation, then left Martial Emperor City with the two Dragon Carps.

Lin Jing’s next move was to have the incarnation stay with the First Ancient Emperor to gain some benefits from him,

While the main body and the two Dragon Carps returned to Qingzhou.

After all this time, Lin Jing figured his demon-hunting team had probably caught a lot of demonic beasts. Last time he returned to Qingzhou, it was in a rush—he hadn’t stayed long.

This time, he wanted the two Dragon Carps to refine a batch of Core Formation Pills and convert all the beasts, letting Pine Leaf Mouse and Ice Soul Gu aim for the Nascent Soul stage.

Then, he’d leave Dragon Carp’s incarnation in the Qingzhou Demon Court to focus on making Transformation Pills.

Having received so many benefits from the Demon Court, Lin Jing figured he should contribute something while training.

After leaving Dragon Carp and Pine Leaf Mouse’s incarnations in the Qingzhou Demon Court, Lin Jing’s main bodies would finally have time to take turns drinking Insight Fish soup, without worrying about pill refinement.

By then, they could fully focus on preparing for the Origin Realm expedition…

As the main body headed to Qingzhou, Lin Jing’s incarnation glanced at a restaurant in Martial Emperor City and frowned.

“That turtle… why is it separated from the First Ancient Emperor again...”

Lin Jing sighed and recalled the Mechanical Turtle first. Before long, it came back with huge bundles, looking very excited.

“What’s all this?”

“Don’t tell me you stole it from Martial Emperor City?”

Lin Jing scanned the Mechanical Turtle’s internal space. The turtle replied, “No no no!”

“Following Master’s orders, I absolutely didn’t steal or rob—these are all failed artifacts discarded by major refining guilds. I picked them up from the trash.”

Lin Jing: ?

“Why’d you pick up a pile of junk? Weren’t you supposed to accompany the First Ancient Emperor?”

The Mechanical Turtle stood up, took out a half-ruined medium-grade flying sword, gave it a gentle lick—and with a flash of treasure light and formation patterns, it turned into a top-quality flying sword.

“Still usable,” it said.

“Alright, alright.” Lin Jing scratched his head. “If you wanna pick stuff, then go ahead. I’m off to find the First Ancient Emperor.”

Lin Jing sensed that the First Ancient Emperor was currently drinking at a restaurant in Martial Emperor City. How strange—can projections even drink?

He blinked out of sight, stepping into the restaurant’s second floor—and was greeted with a scene he’d never forget.

There sat his old classmate Gao Sheng, face flushed red after who knows how many days and nights of drinking, arm in arm with the First Ancient Emperor, loudly saying:

“That new policy after the Golden Era began—don’t even get me started. On one hand, they encourage commoners and low-level cultivators to have more kids, but on the other hand, they don’t provide any support...”

“They want more kids just because the Golden Era raises the chances of giving birth to special cultivation physiques—but what about the ones born without cultivation talent? Most get abandoned. I’ve seen too many orphans and resentful ghost infants during my travels. Tell me—is that what people should be doing? I bet the Ancient Kingdom ran the numbers. As long as more kids with cultivation physiques are born, even if the others suffer horribly, it won’t affect the nation’s fate much.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 77: Ancient Emperor Takes a Disciple
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 77: Ancient Emperor Takes a Disciple

"Cough, cough."

Lin Jing walked into the private room.

The First Ancient Emperor's eyes lit up when he saw Lin Jing had returned. He said, "You’re finally back. I thought I’d never see you again before I died."

"Let me introduce you to this young brother I just met..."

Lin Jing fell silent for a moment, then coughed again, the sound sobering Gao Sheng up from his drunken state.

Once a bit clearer, Gao Sheng suddenly rubbed his head, his face flushing purple, then pale, before bursting out, "D*mn it, old man, you tricked me into talking..."

"Huh—wait a sec—Brother Lin, why are you here?!" Gao Sheng suddenly noticed Lin Jing standing at the door, completely shocked.

"I was just about to ask why you’re here."

"Even if I was tricked, if this gets out, my whole clan is done for."

Gao Sheng looked terrified. Who knew what kind of nonsense he'd been spouting over the past few days—

From imperial harem gossip to the kingdom’s future, anything he could comment on, he probably did.

Lin Jing was speechless... If you didn’t already have those thoughts, would you have spilled your guts just from being drunk?

"What’s with you, kid? You were the one hugging me, begging me to listen. Now you're saying it's my fault?" The First Ancient Emperor complained, "Wait—hold on, you two know each other?"

"Don’t worry." Lin Jing reassured Gao Sheng, saying, "This guy’s one of us."

"I wasn’t around earlier, and he was wandering around Martial Emperor City alone. Didn’t expect you two to actually run into each other."

"One of us??" Gao Sheng froze.

He looked at the First Ancient Emperor. "Don’t tell me... an elder from the Demon Court in disguise?"

"Hell, if word gets out I’ve been hanging around with a demon from the Demon Court sabotaging the Ancient Kingdom, I’m done for!"

Lin Jing waved his hand. "Relax. He’s a projection of an ancient person I summoned using the Divine Scripture, not a demon."

"A projection!" Gao Sheng wiped his sweat and said, "Well, that’s a relief. He’s dead then."

Suddenly remembering something, he turned to the old man. "Wait, could you be Ancestor Feng Ling of the Feng Family?"

"Nope," Lin Jing replied.

"I’ve already summoned the Feng Family Ancestor too, but he has a different identity."

"What?" Gao Sheng asked.

"Tianyuan Ancient Kingdom. The First Ancient Emperor..." Lin Jing looked helplessly at Gao Sheng.

Gao Sheng: ".....?"

The next second, Gao Sheng passed out cold.

"Huh?" The First Ancient Emperor scratched his chin. "This kid’s Dao Heart is really that weak? And you actually told him who I am. Are you two that close?"

Lin Jing nodded. "Pretty close. He might’ve said a bunch of rebellious stuff, but Your Majesty the Ancient Emperor, no need to mind it. Young people like to criticize the government. Totally normal. If you really told him to rebel, he still wouldn’t do it."

The First Ancient Emperor laughed. "Actually, I heard quite a few interesting opinions from him. Even though he’s a second-generation cultivator, he seems to care about the common people. That was rare even in my time."

"Probably a genetic mutation. Brother Gao’s never been normal," Lin Jing said.

"Anyway..."

The Ancient Emperor looked Lin Jing up and down and said, "Innate Flawless Divine Body, Peak Foundation Establishment Stage. But this External Incarnation’s realm—don’t you think it’s a bit low? Normally, once an External Incarnation is successfully refined, the cultivation level should be at least Nascent Soul. Even the underdeveloped ones should be Early Nascent Soul. Yours is like, mildly disabled."

"Couldn’t help it. To save time, I rushed the refinement method. I didn’t pay much attention to its realm. I’ll upgrade it later," Lin Jing replied.

He had worried that refining an External Incarnation would take too long, so he hadn’t fully utilized the power of the Immortal Water Lotus. As a result, the incarnation's realm ended up far lower than it should have.

Otherwise, given the quality of the Immortal Water Lotus, it could’ve reached Perfected Nascent Soul Stage when formed, even surpassing the original body’s cultivation.

But that could’ve taken ten years, or even centuries. The more advanced it got, the more time and patience it would need. Lin Jing didn’t have the patience. Realm? Whatever. He had a Cultivation Core Pill anyway. He could just upgrade using the Core refined from the Dragon Carp later.

"Fair enough. You’ve got the Heavenly Demon Physique, so your realm doesn’t matter anyway," the First Ancient Emperor said, picking his nose. "What about your main body?"

"My real body had other business. Already left."

"For now, this incarnation will accompany you," Lin Jing chuckled.

"How about this—your main body stays as Holy Son of the Demon Court, and this incarnation becomes my disciple and stays in the Ancient Kingdom?" the Ancient Emperor suddenly suggested.

"I’ll get my Second Generation son to pass a new law recognizing 'dual citizenship.' It’s not like we’re lacking outrageous policies already—one more won’t hurt."

Lin Jing fell into deep thought, his gaze drifting.

"D*mn it, you’re actually hesitating? Do you realize what a massive opportunity this is?!"

"If you’re not going to do it, I’ll just make this kid my disciple!" The First Ancient Emperor kicked the unconscious Gao Sheng lying next to them.

"Since I’ve managed to come back for a while, I figured I might as well leave something behind."

"Now that the Second Generation guy has funneled all recent resources into the Ancient Clans, I’ll just pass some other stuff to cultivators outside the clans."

Lin Jing raised a brow and said, "Take him as your disciple?? You sure?"

You sure your legacy won’t be ruined by this guy...?

...

Not long after.

Qingzhou, Immortal Prison Mountain.

After many hardships, Lin Jing’s true body and party finally returned.

As soon as Lin Jing approached Immortal Prison Mountain, the Mosquito Woman led a swarm of mosquitoes to welcome him. The Grand Elder Huan Li of the Thousand Illusions Sect and the Longevity Body Girl Gu Qianqiu each stood atop a pine branch, welcoming back the master of this place.

"You brat, care to explain what happened six months ago?"

Senior Pine, who had escorted someone out before, immediately asked upon seeing Lin Jing. Dmn it, last time the kid had just flown back on a Celestial Sword, then left on the Wen Dao Ink Beast. What the hll was that about?

Lin Jing gave a bitter smile. "Just a small matter, not worth mentioning. I accidentally discovered a method to activate Celestial Artifacts in the Western Desert. So I used two of them to shake up some potential enemies and took out the Gu God Sect as a warning to others... Nothing worth mentioning, really."

"This time, I’m back to refine some pills for breaking into the Nascent Soul Stage. I’ll be staying for a while."

After Lin Jing finished speaking, even the mindless Mosquito Beasts wanted to cuss him out.

Activate Celestial Artifacts. Annihilate the Gu God Sect. Break into Nascent Soul Stage.

All these phrases together—wasn’t there something seriously wrong with this picture?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 78: Wanting to Sap Pine''s Life
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 78: Wanting to Sap Pine's Life

"You all worked hard during this time, so I brought gifts for everyone."

Lin Jing smiled as he looked at the group, then took out a drop of violet blood and tossed it to the Mosquito Woman.

"Demon Emperor’s blood?"

Holding the blood, the Mosquito Woman looked mesmerized, her whole body trembling.

"I used Divine Sense to inspect the Immortal Prison. I’m very satisfied with the number and quality of beasts inside. This is your well-deserved reward."

"What kind of reward is this? Just capturing some beasts no stronger than the Nascent Soul Transformation Stage, and she gets Demon Emperor’s blood?"

Beside them, Mo Pine couldn’t help but complain. "The benefits of working under you are insane."

It was even tempted to join the Demon-Hunting Squad.

Back then, although the Demon Emperor and the others failed to capture the Gu God, they did severely wound it. Among them, the Demon Emperor collected a good amount of the blood that spilled from its wounds, which was then purified and given to Lin Jing.

This blood was excellent for enhancing the Ice Soul Gu. Lin Jing had a whole bottle, so giving one drop to the Mosquito Woman was nothing.

"Senior Huan Li, this is..."

"A Grotesque Bug corpse?!" The Thousand Illusions Mountain Grand Elder’s eyes lit up as she recognized the small insect Lin Jing had thrown out.

"Mm, one of the Ten Terrors of the Cloud Sea Mountains. It’s said these bugs can manifest illusions so bizarre and indistinguishable they can even illusion-kill Nascent Soul cultivators."

"While clearing out the Gu God Sect, I found that they had successfully refined this kind of deadly insect and named it the Illusion-Kill Gu. But like all Rare Gu, Illusion Gu are extremely hard to control—only an Illusionary Path master can tame them."

"This here is also the Illusion Gu refinement method from the Gu God Sect. A token of thanks for guarding the Immortal Prison, Senior."

Huan Li happily accepted it and said with a grin, "Your Highness the Holy Son is too kind. I don’t dare call myself senior anymore. I’m just the one watching the mountain gate for His Highness now. You really don’t need to be polite."

"I love this gift."

"I’ll pass," Gu Qianqiu said when she saw Lin Jing looking her way, clearly meaning to give her something too. She shook her head.

"I didn’t do anything—just cultivated."

"Alright then," Lin Jing said.

Gu Qianqiu: …

Skipping her, Lin Jing turned to the eager Mo Pine. As an elder, it shouldn’t be so expectant about a junior’s gift—but Lin Jing, that brat, really did have some good stuff.

That battle in Tongtian City might not have seemed like much—

At best, he blew up some Void Mystical cultivators’ resources.

But if Lin Jing truly wiped out the Gu God Sect, now that was something.

That sect had a ten-thousand-year legacy, on the same level as the Tianyuan Ancient Kingdom. One could imagine how many treasures it had stashed.

"What the h*ll is this?"

Seeing Lin Jing pull out a separate storage pouch and toss it over, Mo Pine’s face darkened.

"Don’t tell me this sht is literally sht?!"

Lin Jing quickly clarified, "Not just any sh*t. According to the Gu God Sect’s records, this is the excrement of an ancient Celestial Beast—Celestial Dung."

"You’re saying this is still sh*t?!" Mo Pine cursed.

"It’s different. The Gu God Sect used this as fertilizer, and it works incredibly well for cultivating spiritual plants. They even used it to raise near-Immortal-Grade poisonous plants."

Next to them, Gu Qianqiu felt relieved she hadn’t accepted any gift.

But in the next second, Lin Jing suddenly said, "Actually, I did prepare something for you too. Congrats on reaching Nascent Soul Stage. Your gift might take a little longer to deliver, though."

"This pouch of Celestial Beast remains with Senior Pine is the key."

"In the Western Desert, I also snatched a Bodhi Tree from Bodhi Temple. Next, I plan to work with Senior Pine to figure out how to propagate more Bodhi Trees."

"If we succeed, you can use the Bodhi Tree as material to refine an External Incarnation. The flaw of the Longevity Physique is that cultivating techniques and spells is too slow. But with an External Incarnation made from Bodhi Tree, that can be fixed!"

"As for an incarnation meant for understanding natural forces—no need. The Demon Court has many places ideal for that kind of enlightenment. They’ll make arrangements for you."

Gu Qianqiu was speechless. She had thought she dodged a bullet—and now she had to refine an External Incarnation using a tree grown from Celestial Beast dung? Help...

"What the h*ll," Mo Pine muttered. So it had to work again?

Still, after closely sensing the Celestial Beast remains, Mo Pine fell silent. It could tell—this stuff really did help spiritual plants grow.

Apparently, it came from a wood-attribute Celestial Beast that had consumed immortal plants.

At the Celestial Beast level, excretion was almost unheard of. So if it did excrete, that meant it had eaten something even its celestial body couldn’t fully digest.

...

A while later, only Lin Jing and Senior Pine were left.

Though the pouch of Celestial Beast remains was indeed valuable, Mo Pine still felt a bit salty.

"After all the effort I put into advancing your ‘Everlasting Emperor’s Longevity Art’ to the next layer, this is how you repay me?!"

"You succeeded, Senior?!" Lin Jing was surprised—and delighted. He had thought the Pine Leaf Mouse would have to use Insight Fish Soup to push through, but now that Senior Pine had done it, that saved them a lot of trouble.

"You shouldn’t judge by appearance, Senior. This Celestial Beast relic really is the rarest, most suitable thing I found for you among the Gu God Sect’s treasures. When I swing by the Ancient Kingdom’s treasury later, I’ll definitely bring back something even better for you."

"Fine! Seeing as you’re being so filial, I’ll let it slide that you gave me crap."

Putting the image of the gift aside, Mo Pine did understand it was genuinely good stuff.

"Right, I had one more favor to ask, Senior."

"I know, you want help propagating the Bodhi Tree, right?" Mo Pine said. "Rumor has it only the Buddhist Sect knows how to do that—but that’s just because the plant-spirit races have fallen into decline."

"But with my Void Mystical Stage cultivation, plus this Celestial Beast dung, I should be able to grow a whole Bodhi forest for you."

"That’s not the favor." Lin Jing rubbed his hands together.

"You see, the Pine Leaf Mouse is about to reach Nascent Soul Stage too. That means it’s time to refine an External Incarnation."

"I do have some good materials for it—but none of them really match it perfectly."

"Sometimes, grade isn’t the key—compatibility is everything..."

"So I was wondering... as the strongest plant-based lifeform on the Tianyuan Continent and the Pine Leaf Mouse’s master, do you have anything you’d like to pass on to it???"

That’s right.

The material Lin Jing had in mind for the Pine Leaf Mouse’s External Incarnation—was Mo Pine himself.

Only, Mo Pine had just found out.

"Whatever conditions you want, I’ll try to fulfill them. Look, want me to give you a sip of Longevity Blood first to taste?"

"Get lost!!!"

From within Immortal Prison Mountain came Mo Pine’s furious roar:

"D*mn it! I knew it—back when you were in Foundation Establishment, you were already eyeing me. Now that it’s time to refine an External Incarnation, you’re still not gonna let me go!!"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 79: All at Nascent Soul
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 79: All at Nascent Soul

Immortal Prison Mountain, inside the Immortal Prison.

Screams rang out continuously.

Two identical Dragon Carp kept unleashing their Rare Beast Divine Abilities, refining wave after wave of demons and monsters.

One after another, Cultivation Core Pills were refined from the lives of these creatures.

“Holy Son of the Demon Court, even as a ghost, I won’t let you go!!”

“D*mn you, Longevity Physique! If there’s a next life, I’ll eat you alive!!”

Curses echoed throughout the Immortal Prison, and both Dragon Carp wiped sweat off their foreheads—already sick of hearing it.

Right now, the real Dragon Carp felt like crying. Refining Cultivation Core Pills was way more exhausting than making regular pills.

With one premium pill after another fed to it, it didn’t take long for the Pine Leaf Mouse to break through to Perfected Core Formation Stage.

Paired with the newly advanced layer of the Everlasting Emperor’s Longevity Art derived by Senior Mo Pine, in just one month of external time inside the Hourglass of Time, the Pine Leaf Mouse broke through from Core Formation to Early Nascent Soul Stage.

At the same time, the upgraded Everlasting Emperor’s Longevity Art was cultivated to perfection by the Pine Leaf Mouse with ease.

The perfected form of the Everlasting Emperor’s Longevity Art represented not just an increase of thousands of years in lifespan, but also signified the transformation into a Nascent Soul Demon Emperor-level grass-elemental spirit.

Under normal circumstances, achieving perfection in this technique was nearly impossible—it would usually take a thousand years of effort.

But Lin Jing’s side had an overwhelming surplus of wood-element treasures. With no restrictions on absorption, the Pine Leaf Mouse’s cultivation rose like drinking water.

“It’s said that the Forest Sea Dragon Emperor’s disciple is a sentient willow tree at Early Nascent Soul. I wonder, with the Pine Leaf Mouse’s current strength, how evenly matched they’d be?”

Lin Jing noted the Pine Leaf Mouse’s condition and started guessing.

The Everlasting Emperor’s Longevity Art was extremely effective against plant spirits. After all, it was a hidden ace of Mo Pine, the "traitor" among plant life.

Even if the Pine Leaf Mouse’s realm was one full stage lower, with the technique's countering properties and its absurdly powerful Monster Core Divine Abilities, it might still hold its own.

Though training the technique had consumed nearly half of its wood-element resources,

The Pine Leaf Mouse’s Monster Core Space still held a vast store of Gold, Wood, Water, Fire, and Earth treasures.

Wood-elemental items were more abundant than the others. Even after spending half, the Five-Element World inside it remained balanced and powerful.

“Won’t be long now before I drop back to Core Formation.”

While the Pine Leaf Mouse stabilized its breakthrough, Lin Jing was calculating the remaining duration of his Heavenly Demon Physique.

When he fought the Martial Emperor’s successor, he didn’t burn too much lifespan and had no intention of staying at Nascent Soul Transformation for ten years straight. He had limited it to just six months.

His foundation was too low. Maintaining Nascent Soul Transformation for years would be painfully costly.

Now, once his realm fell back to Core Formation, he could use the Dragon Carp’s Cultivation Core Pills to ascend to Nascent Soul.

Then, by using the Heavenly Demon Physique again to return to Nascent Soul Transformation, the toll would be much lighter.

That was also why he had hurried back to his own territory—before the drop happened.

By the time he headed to the Origin Realm, he could combine this with the Poseidon Palace’s lifespan-recovery resources.

All in all, he would lose very little lifespan but gain enormous returns.

“.....”

While Guardian Mouse was stabilizing its realm, and Lin Jing was waiting for the Heavenly Demon Physique to expire,

Over on the Green Emperor Gu’s side, it had finally finished devouring all the remaining Rare Gu origins.

The Gu’s core provided it with a modest amount of nourishment. There was no need for extra Cultivation Core Pills.

By the time it woke up, accompanied by immense pressure, its cultivation was also at Early Nascent Soul Stage.

It was unclear whether this was the limit after devouring multiple Rare Gu, or just a restriction based on its Beast Tamer’s realm—Ice Soul.

“With this, Pine Leaf Mouse, Ice Soul Gu, Dragon Carp, and Mechanical Turtle—all four Contract Beasts have reached Nascent Soul Stage....”

Lin Jing was quite pleased. Now only he was left. Once he broke through—everyone would be at Nascent Soul.

“How are you feeling?” Lin Jing dragged the just-awakened Ice Soul Gu out of the Hourglass of Time.

Still a bit dazed, Ice Soul Gu shook her head and mumbled, “So full... so bloated... kinda wanna throw up...”

“That so? I was thinking of stopping by Tongtian Tower to fetch the Wood Gu for you to devour too.”

“Squeee!!” Ice Soul Gu backed up a step in panic. “Can’t eat anymore—not even one more!”

Lin Jing laughed. “Weren’t you the one so ambitious about swallowing all the Rare Gu and replacing the Gu God?”

“Oh right—this bottle of Gu God’s blood, drink it when you get a chance. Her Majesty the Demon Emperor personally collected and purified it for you.”

“Once you drink it, you’ll be one step closer to the Gu God.”

Hearing that, Ice Soul Gu’s legs turned to jelly. She admitted—yeah, she might’ve been showing off a little before.

She had lived this long but never thought she’d be scared just from having too many treasures to digest.

She didn’t know what kind of lives the other Rare Gu lived with their Gu Masters, but her days… might be a little too good.

“I’d better go stabilize my power first before drinking anything else,” Ice Soul Gu muttered fearfully.

“Fair.” Lin Jing nodded. “While you’re at it, produce more sub-Gu from the Yin Spirit Gu.”

“My External Incarnation needs to devour Heart Demons to cultivate Heavenly Demon Finger, but powerful Heart Demon cultivators aren’t easy to find.”

“So, we’ll manually create Heart Demons in others using the Yin Spirit Gu, then devour and ‘heal’ them. Genius, right?”

Lin Jing poked Ice Soul Gu’s belly. That was the perk of devouring multiple Rare Gu—so many new functions.

Hearing her Beast Tamer's latest wicked idea, Ice Soul Gu nodded and showed an evil grin. So twisted—but she liked it...

Meanwhile—

Lin Jing’s External Incarnation had returned with the First Ancient Emperor to the Tianyuan Ancient Kingdom.

Besides Lin Jing and the First Emperor, Gao Sheng, now favored by the Emperor, originally wanted to go demon-slaying with Cold Moon Sword Immortal.

But instead, he was forcibly drafted into the Emperor’s undercover inspection team…

“Guide me well. Do it right, and I’ll take you as my disciple.”

Gao Sheng, stunned by the Emperor’s promise, wasn’t sure if he was dreaming.

Meanwhile, Lin Jing’s Incarnation had already learned Heavenly Demon Canon, Heavenly Demon Finger, and Heavenly Demon Crossing from the First Ancient Emperor.

Happy to act as a background prop, he cultivated while casually watching the drama unfold.

His main body also got those techniques from Senior Mo Pine.

After confirming both versions matched, he felt safe to begin practicing.

But right now, one issue troubled Lin Jing—

Though Senior Mo Pine had agreed to pass some of his power to the Pine Leaf Mouse, there were conditions.

“I can split half my essence for its Incarnation, but the energy drain is massive.”

“Spiritual power can’t be replenished by Celestial Beast dung or Longevity Blood. But—if you can get me a whiff of ‘Celestial Pill Aura’—just a speck of dust from the Celestial Pill Spirit in Tongtian Tower—then I’ll go all in.”

“But I heard… that Celestial Pill is skittish. Not easy to deal with.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 80: The Transaction
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 80: The Transaction

"I'm going to give it a try."

For the sake of the Pine Leaf Mouse's incarnation, Lin Jing decided to accept Senior Mo Pine’s request and see if he could obtain a wisp of Celestial Pill aura.

He also planned to try and retrieve the Wood-type Ten Thousand Poison Gu from the Tongtian Tower.

"Good, good, then go on."

"As long as you can manage it, I’ll assist that little guy in refining the incarnation!"

Senior Mo Pine continued planting trees while ushering Lin Jing away. After all, it was impossible for him to get the item himself—

But Lin Jing... was another story.

After all, Lin Jing’s identity and potential were undeniable.

Seeing how many Bodhi Trees Senior Mo Pine had successfully propagated, Lin Jing couldn’t help but look forward to the results.

Once the numbers grew a bit more, the Pine Leaf Mouse could try synthesizing with them.

Just the mutated Bodhi Leaf alone had such great effects—he couldn’t even imagine what a full Bodhi Tree could produce when used for synthesis.

"Then I’m off."

Lin Jing left Immortal Prison Mountain and headed straight toward Tongtian City. Since both places were in Qingzhou, it didn’t take him long to arrive...

"Senior Celestial Pill of Tongtian Tower, are you there?"

Upon arrival, Lin Jing used divine sense to send a voice transmission. Only high-level cultivators could hear such a thing.

He stood calmly above the cloud layer outside the city, gazing at the sky-piercing tower.

Before long, several powerful alchemists descended. Their identities were clear—Twelve Pill Sovereigns of Tongtian Tower.

Among them were three familiar faces Lin Jing had seen before—Sun and Moon Pill Sovereign, Divine Cauldron Pill Sovereign, and Creation Pill Sovereign—the hosts of the previous alchemy conference.

“Holy Son of the Demon Court!!!”

The moment the Tongtian Tower leaders saw Lin Jing, their expressions changed instantly.

Everyone knew the current relationship between Tongtian Tower and the Demon Court was anything but harmonious.

The human territories of Qingzhou had been increasingly encroached upon by the Demon Court lately, damaging Tongtian Tower’s interests.

What’s more, Lin Jing had once unleashed a massacre in Tongtian City—it was impossible for the cultivators of the tower to have a good impression of him.

“Holy Son of the Demon Court, Senior Celestial Pill never receives visitors. Please leave.”

Sun and Moon Pill Sovereign took a step forward and directly turned him away.

“I came with sincerity. Is Senior Celestial Pill really not willing to see me?” Lin Jing chuckled, his personal aura overwhelming that of several others.

The Pill Sovereigns hesitated with dread. Rumors said this Holy Son of the Demon Court had discovered a method to activate a Celestial Artifact...

At first, Misty Forest and Poseidon Palace had their doubts about the Demon Court, but ever since the Holy Son triggered a Celestial Artifact and shocked the world, both forces had immediately changed their stance.
