Not only did the two Demon Emperors stop targeting the Demon Court, they even allied with the Demon Court's Lord to help the Holy Son destroy his former enemy—the Gu God Sect.

Two out of the three major Demonic Sects were annihilated because of Lin Jing.

So, while these Pill Sovereigns didn’t like him, they didn’t dare fully offend him either.

“Let him in.”

Just as the Pill Sovereigns hesitated, an aged voice came from within Tongtian Tower. Upon hearing it, their expressions shifted slightly, and they immediately cupped their fists toward the tower peak. “Tower Master.”

"Hm?"

Lin Jing looked up at the tower in surprise. Wasn’t the Tower Master reinforcing the Boundary Wall with the Demon Emperor and Ancient Emperors?

Or perhaps, the current voice was only the incarnation left behind to guard Tongtian Tower.

Lin Jing flew past the Pill Sovereigns directly toward the tower's summit.

At the top, an old man stood gazing into the heavens.

Beside him, a sentient pill with eyes, a nose, and a mouth trembled timidly, hiding in his clothes.

“Young one, may I ask what brings you to my Tongtian Tower?”

Indeed, this Tower Master was merely an incarnation. His gaze was deep as he looked at Lin Jing and asked.

“Junior greets Senior,” Lin Jing said straightforwardly. “I’ve come to request a wisp of aura from Senior Celestial Pill.”

“Ahhh, I knew he was here to scheme on my body! You damn Longevity Physique! As someone with such a body, can’t you try to empathize with me?” Celestial Pill cried in fear.

“It’s just a wisp of aura...” Lin Jing said.

Celestial Pill snapped, “There’s no method in the Lower Realm to restore my damage! Even a speck of pill dust will greatly harm my vitality!”

“I’m willing to trade, a fair exchange.” Lin Jing looked to the Tower Master and asked, “Would you be interested in the Blood of the Gu God?”

The Tower Master shook his head. “Though Gu God Blood is precious, it still doesn’t match the value of a wisp of Celestial Pill aura...”

“Then how about a wisp of Immortal Annihilation Flame?”

The Tower Master again shook his head.

Just as Lin Jing was about to continue bargaining, the Tower Master spoke.

“I can allow the Celestial Pill Spirit to give you a wisp of aura, provided you agree to two conditions.”

“What conditions?” Lin Jing asked.

“Old man, stop casually agreeing on my behalf!!” Celestial Pill Spirit immediately detached itself.

“Hush,” the Tower Master said. “Don’t be so agitated. I wouldn’t let you suffer a loss.”

He turned to Lin Jing. “The first condition is: once Your Highness the Holy Son returns from the Origin Realm, you must bring back ten wisps of Celestial Energy for our Tongtian Tower.”

“As you know, the cultivators of Tongtian Tower are not skilled in combat. Even our strongest prodigy only ranks eighteenth on the Fortune Gold List, making entry into the Origin Realm impossible. That’s why we need your help to retrieve these rare resources for us.”

Lin Jing fell into brief thought. “And the second condition?”

Indeed, Tongtian Tower was one of the rare Dao-Seeking powers whose own prodigies didn’t make it into the top ten.

Pathetic...

“The second condition is also simple. If I’m not mistaken, one of your pet beasts possesses a divine ability to mutate spiritual plants, correct?”

“The second condition is that you provide one hundred rare spiritual plants that have never appeared on the Tianyuan Continent.”

“As long as you agree to both conditions, you will receive a wisp of Celestial Pill aura!”

Lin Jing furrowed his brows. These conditions weren’t hard to fulfill, but still...

“What if I die in the Origin Realm and can’t bring back the Celestial Energy for you?”

The Tower Master laughed heartily. “With your strength and foundation, unless you become the public enemy of the Origin Realm and are besieged by several Immortal Physiques or dozens of top-tier geniuses—

How could you possibly die there?”

Lin Jing nodded. “Alright. I accept both conditions. But in return, I also want the Ten Thousand Poison Gu stored in Tongtian City!”

“Agreed. But I want a drop of Gu God Blood in return,” the Tower Master stroked his beard and bargained.

At the side, Celestial Pill Spirit fumed. How exactly was this not a loss for it?!

“Celestial energy will help you survive longer. Even if you are a Celestial Pill, without being bathed in celestial aura for too long in this Lower Realm, you will eventually decay,” the Tower Master warned.

“The prodigies of Tongtian Tower are disappointing. At this point, we can only rely on those from other factions.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 81: The Immortal Demon Serpent
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 81: The Immortal Demon Serpent

In the end, Lin Jing successfully obtained a wisp of Pill Dust and the Ten Thousand Poison Gu from Tongtian Tower—much more smoothly than he had expected.

However, he also had to bear the karmic burden of retrieving several wisps of Celestial Energy for Tongtian Tower in return...

"If the situation in the Origin Realm is truly as recorded in ancient texts, it shouldn’t be too difficult for me..."

Ten Thousand Poison Gu—among all Rare Gu—is the most venomous and complex. It’s the only poison among the Ten Rare Gu capable of threatening even cultivators in the Dao-Seeking Stage.

Of course, it could only pose a threat—it couldn’t actually inflict fatal harm on such cultivators.

Still, once it’s consumed by the Ice Soul Gu, its own toxicity would certainly be greatly enhanced.

As for the Pill Dust, this was the crucial item in Lin Jing’s quest—a life-saving miracle medicine.

Even if one was gravely wounded, left with half a Nascent Soul, body destroyed, and soul fractured, it was said that the aura of a Celestial Pill could instantly restore them to peak condition.

“No rush to head back just yet.”

“Senior Divine Pine is still busy planting trees, and the Pine Leaf Mouse is still consolidating its realm. A realm elevated through pills and treasures always requires extra time to stabilize.”

Lin Jing looked toward the edge of Qingzhou.

Now, even Void Mystical Stage cultivators were wary of Lin Jing.

Mostly because Lin Jing had once shown the ability to activate a Celestial Artifact.

However, he had now lost that ability. Neither the Celestial Sword nor the Four Saints Diagram belonged to him.

Even the generous Demon Emperor hadn’t planned to gift the Demon Court’s sole Celestial Artifact to Lin Jing—he only had temporary usage rights under special circumstances.

Furthermore, the Artifact Ghost had been sealed from Void Mystical Stage down to Nascent Soul, making it unable to activate the artifact.

Thus, Lin Jing urgently needed to reinforce his trump cards.

On the path of cultivation, regardless of one’s situation, it’s always best to have several hidden killing moves prepared for the unexpected.

“Back when the Demon Emperor helped me annihilate the Yin Corpse Sect, I obtained a Celestial Beast corpse from them.”

“If I can control that Celestial Beast corpse, it would be no different from wielding a Celestial Artifact. Even if it’s slightly inferior, it wouldn’t be by much.”

“To do that, I’ll need to use the Yin Corpse Sect’s Corpse Manipulation Secret Art. I already have the method, just haven’t learned it yet. I can use Insight Fish Soup to practice it later.”

“In addition, I need both extreme vitality and death energy. My Longevity Physique is the source of vitality. That leaves only death energy to refine.”

“The only source of death energy that can rival my Longevity Physique... is the rare beast suppressed beneath the Beast Taming Sect, one that possesses the Immortality Divine Ability—the Immortal Demon Serpent!”

“When we first met, I was just in the Qi Refinement Stage. It’s been decades. I wonder what condition it’s in now. With my current strength, maybe I can try to refine it!!”

Lin Jing’s eyes lit up—he had found his next destination: the new mountain gate of the Beast Taming Sect!

Currently, the Immortal Demon Serpent remained sealed beneath the Beast Taming Sect, as per a deal between the Demon Court and the Ancient Kingdom.

Qingzhou, Beast Taming Sect.

Lin Jing returned to the sect unnoticed. He went straight into the depths of the sect’s spiritual veins, reaching the underground space connected to the Demon-Suppressing Pagoda.

He stepped into the underground pool. The Immortal Demon Serpent stirred—its eyes sweeping left and right.

Before, when the seal was reinforced, it sensed that Lin Jing was still sealed and never regained consciousness...

“Break.”

With a soft word, Lin Jing easily unraveled the seal on the Immortal Demon Serpent.

Back at the Spirit Dao Sect, he had deeply studied sealing techniques while researching how to seal the Void Mystical Artifact Ghost.

Though not yet at the level of creating Spell Spirit Origins, unraveling the old seal left by the White-Robed Monk wasn’t difficult at all.

Drip...

As Lin Jing began unlocking the seal, a deathly aura surged up from the Demon-Suppressing Pagoda.

With a maniacal laugh, golden chains shot out from the dark pool.

A massive serpent bound in golden chains rose up, its savage gaze scanning everything around it.

“Finally! I’ve waited so long for this seal to decay!!!”

“It didn’t decay—I released you.”

The serpent froze mid-laugh and looked down at the small figure—Lin Jing. Its eyes flared with fury.

“It’s you!!!”

In an instant, the Immortal Demon Serpent remembered—it was this very Beast Taming Sect disciple who once used Illusion Techniques to mess with it, forcing it into humiliating submission.

“You’ve actually reached Early Nascent Soul Transformation Stage?!”

It was stunned.

How long had it been?

Even sealed, its body could still sense the passage of time.

Not even a hundred years had passed, and the Qi Refinement brat from back then was now in Nascent Soul...

For reference, the serpent itself was only at the Peak Nascent Soul Stage.

“So what if you've reached Nascent Soul? I’m going to kill you today! I’ve been starving for ages—you’ll make a fine meal!”

The Immortal Demon Serpent’s eyes radiated killing intent. As a rare beast with the Immortality Divine Ability, it didn’t care about its opponent’s cultivation level.

Its only fear was being sealed again.

Being imprisoned for thousands—or even tens of thousands—of years was its greatest nightmare.

If the enemy only knew how to fight, even a Nascent Soul cultivator wouldn’t scare it!

“Still groggy from sleep?” Lin Jing said coolly, stretching out his hand.

A Buddha-Demon Pearl appeared.

“If I could easily undo your seal, that means I’ve mastered powerful sealing arts. Sealing you again would be effortless.”

The serpent paused. “...Oh, right.”

The next moment, Lin Jing activated the Buddha-Demon Pearl.

The weakened Immortal Demon Serpent was instantly sucked in, transforming into a small clay serpent as it got pulled into a swirling vortex of Buddhist light.

Enraged, it screamed, “You brat! Just wait! Even if you seal me, I won’t submit! I’m an Immortal Rare Beast, with an Undying Body!

Even your Longevity Physique can’t outlast me! One day I’ll break this seal and destroy your whole damn fam—”

Before it could finish, its expression changed drastically.

It felt a terrifying force erasing its consciousness, forcibly drawing out its Death Essence to be absorbed by the Buddha-Demon Pearl.

“Divine Control Technique.”

Lin Jing burned his own lifespan and activated the Divine Control Technique, manipulating the Immortal Demon Serpent sealed inside the pearl.

He had never expected cooperation. His true goal was to refine it entirely using his cross-stage Beast Taming ability.

“No need for your cooperation. I prefer brute force.”

The Holy Son spoke calmly.

In the next instant, the Immortal Demon Serpent was completely terrified.

This was the first time in its life it had encountered someone with Void Mystical-level Beast Taming power.

This terrifying force felt even more soul-crushing than sealing techniques.

It couldn’t understand—how had that human brat become so terrifying in such a short time?!

“Ay... Grandpa, Grandpa, I was just joking! Spare my little life! I’ll do whatever you say! I’ll be your pet! I’ll be your dog! I’ll serve you till the end!

Please, please stop! Abusing a rare beast like this—are you even a real Beast Tamer?!”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 82: Rare Gu
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 82: Rare Gu

If it were an ordinary Beast Tamer, the first thought upon encountering a Rare Beast would indeed be to form a contract with it and turn it into an ally.

Even if the Immortal Demon Serpent’s power source was too sinister and would bring many negative effects, most Beast Tamers wouldn’t easily give up. They’d try every possible way to overcome those obstacles.

But Lin Jing was different.

He had encountered too many Rare Beasts—from the Lord of the Demon Court to the Dragon Carp—he had become desensitized to their allure.

No matter how talented or full of potential the Immortal Demon Serpent was, it didn’t appeal to Lin Jing at all. He had no intention of nurturing or contracting it.

He only wanted to squeeze out every bit of value it had right now, regardless of the consequences.

He directly used his Control Dao Ability to forcibly make it strip its own source. In the process, the Immortal Demon Serpent’s foundation would be gravely damaged. Unless some Celestial Item repaired it, its cultivation realm would hardly progress again.

What Lin Jing was doing was essentially abolishing its Rare Beast bloodline—and in a very low-cost-efficiency way at that. After all, the extracted source would also lose its growth potential.

“No wonder it’s the source of a Rare Beast bloodline. This intense death energy—I’ve never seen anything like it.” As Lin Jing extracted the bloodline source, he couldn’t help but marvel.

The death energy contained in this mere Nascent Soul Stage beast was even deeper and purer than that of a Void Mystical ghost entity. It was more intense and potent than the Netherworld Ghostfire cultivated to the extreme by the Dragon Carp.

Even when the Buddha-Demon Pearl absorbed negative power from Rare Gu, it could still maintain balance. Even when sealing the Ghost Bride, it wasn’t affected. But now, it had only sealed part of the Immortal Demon Serpent’s bloodline source, and it had completely turned into a Demon Pearl.

Its Buddhist nature could no longer suppress the demonic nature.

This death energy had directly transformed the Buddha-Demon Pearl into a Death God Pearl filled with death power.

“Seal!” Lin Jing called out in a low voice. Spiritual power inside him turned into branches that tightly bound the Death God Pearl.

If he hadn’t used other techniques to re-seal the Buddha-Demon Pearl—already a sealing artifact—the power of this bloodline source would surely have gone berserk and affected his vitality.

“But this bloodline source is this stubborn? Seems it won’t be easily rooted out.”

After stripping away ninety percent of the Immortal Demon Serpent’s bloodline, even with his Nascent Soul Transformation cultivation as a foundation and lifespan-burning use of the Divine Control Technique—equivalent to Void Mystical level Control Dao Ability—he still couldn’t force it to release the last core source.

No matter how hard he tried, Lin Jing couldn’t affect it at all.

“So be it.”

Lin Jing pulled out the now-drained Immortal Demon Serpent.

It was on its last breath. With a palm strike from Lin Jing, the Immortal Demon Serpent “died” completely.

But with a sweep of his Divine Sense, Lin Jing could still sense that its source could not be entirely destroyed.

Given time, it would eventually revive.

Of course, before, it might have revived quickly. But now that more than half its bloodline source had been extracted, it was basically sealed away.

It might take thousands of years to gather enough strength to revive again.

“Sacred Wood, seal!”

Lin Jing took out several saplings and formed a hand seal. Instantly, countless sacred trees wrapped around the entire Demon-Suppressing Pagoda.

Regardless of when the Immortal Demon Serpent would revive, Lin Jing directly sealed the entire pagoda to prevent any chance of escape.

After finishing everything, Lin Jing turned his attention inward.

The Death God Pearl, entangled in sacred wood, radiated overwhelming death energy. Around it flowed the vibrant Longevity Blood.

The ultimate forces of life and death were now present.

All he needed now was to master the Corpse Manipulation Secret Art from the Yin Corpse Sect, and he could try to control the corpse of the Celestial Cow.

“Compared to the regenerative, endless Longevity Bloodline, this stripped deathly bloodline source isn’t my own power. Not only can it not grow, but it also can’t regenerate. I need to find a way to transform it into a growing, regenerating force. Only then can I control the Celestial Beast corpse for the long term—not just two or three times.”

Lin Jing quickly thought of a solution.

“I can refine this deathly bloodline source into an External Incarnation—one that’s filled with powerful death energy. That way, it’ll have regenerative and growth potential.

Though it can’t match the Immortal Demon Serpent or perfectly inherit its Rare Beast bloodline, it’d still be like a man-made ‘budget version’ of an Immortal Rare Beast.”

“Ice Soul Gu... Mechanical Turtle—among the team, they’re the only ones who are Rare Beasts.”

“This death source… might be perfect for use.”

“Have the Ice Soul Gu use this death bloodline source to refine an External Incarnation.

Then, it can merge into the host body and assist you in using the Corpse Manipulation Secret Art.”

The serpent made a decision—since the Ice Soul Gu had bad luck and even mutated due to its contract, they might as well artificially grant it part of a Rare Beast bloodline!

As for why it wasn’t the Artifact Ghost—it was purely because the Artifact Ghost was even more bizarre than Gu worms.

Gu worms at least reached Core Formation or Nascent Soul stages and had things like “Monster Core” or “Nascent Soul.”

But the combination of Artifact Ghost and Mechanical Beast was simply power-level-wise at the Nascent Soul stage.

They didn’t even have a Core Formation level, let alone Divine Abilities.

One was an artificial artifact spirit, the other an artificial mechanism—completely non-living entities.

The serpent didn’t know how to refine them into External Incarnations via Nascent Soul methods.

If he hadn’t worked so hard to modify the Beast Taming Art, he wouldn’t even be able to form contracts with them.

“Let’s go.”

Before making any more decisions, the serpent left the Demon-Suppressing Pagoda.

What he had done inside was bound to have caused a stir and drawn attention from the Beast Taming Sect.

Right now, many cultivators of the Beast Taming Sect had already gathered around the Demon-Suppressing Pagoda to observe the situation.

Just as they were deciding whether or not to enter and investigate, Lin Jing’s figure flashed out of the pagoda with a burst of spiritual light.

“Lin Jing?!”

So that’s why there was a disturbance in the Demon-Suppressing Pagoda—it was you!

Senior Brother Ouyang was among them and looked at Lin Jing in surprise and joy.

Aside from him, Elder Mo, the Black Bear Spirit, and other old acquaintances had all reached the Nascent Soul Stage.

As for the Elders who were already at Nascent Soul like the Demon King Elders and Sect Leader Xu Xiaoyao, their cultivation had now reached the peak of the Nascent Soul Stage—just shy of Nascent Soul Transformation.

The overall strength of the Beast Taming Sect still wasn’t particularly powerful, but under the protection of the Demon Court, it was thriving.

“It’s me. Greetings, Sect Leader, Grand Elders, and Elders,” Lin Jing said with a faint smile.

A bunch of Demon King Grand Elders waved their hands repeatedly.

“No need for formalities, no need at all.”

They wiped their sweat nervously—how could they accept Lin Jing’s bow?

This divine disciple of the Beast Taming Sect had already slain several Void Mystical Demon Ancestors. They couldn't bear the courtesy.

“Sorry to disturb you all. I returned this time not only to check on the Immortal Demon but also to discuss an important matter with the Sect Leader.”

Lin Jing turned to look at Xu Daoyao.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 83: Blood Bodhi
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 83: Blood Bodhi

“What? You actually succeeded in improving the Beast Taming Art?”

Inside the Assembly Hall of the Beast Taming Sect, only Sect Leader Xu Xiaoyao, Senior Brother Ouyang, and Lin Jing were present.

Both were stunned by Lin Jing’s sudden declaration.

They had long been working to improve the Beast Taming Art so that “Dao Companions” who transformed into members of the demon race could still retain their contract bonds.

But modifying a cultivation technique—especially one handed down from the Immortal Palace—was no easy feat.

The two had already resigned themselves to dedicating their entire lives to the task. Who would’ve thought Lin Jing would bring such a bombshell?

“Yes, and it doesn’t even require abolishing one’s cultivation and starting over. As long as you continue cultivating on top of your current foundation, it will work. I’ll record the improved Beast Taming Art shortly. From now on, the technique won’t just let you contract beasts—it’ll also allow contracts with shape-shifted demon race, and plant spirits!”

Lin Jing smiled faintly. Of course, the version he was leaving with the Beast Taming Sect wasn’t the complete version he now possessed.

For the Beast Taming Sect, just having a version that could contract with demonkind and plant spirits was already a huge improvement.

“Junior Brother... that’s just like you...” Senior Brother Ouyang was full of excitement and couldn’t wait to try it out. Sect Leader Xu Xiaoyao looked as if he had something to say but paused. Having someone like Lin Jing in the sect felt like a blessing from the heavens.

“No need to be so polite. Actually, it’s all thanks to the Fortune Gold List,” Lin Jing chuckled. “Senior Sister seems to have brought back quite a few good things for the sect—I figured I’d contribute something too.”

“Improving the Beast Taming Art is just one of them.”

“Next, I’ll have the Dragon Carp leave behind a wisp of its ancestral blood that possesses Dragon Scales Fire, a Celestial Flame. With this flame, any Dragon Carp in the sect with sufficient potential can bathe in it to mutate into a Fire Carp, gaining potential rivaling that of a Demon King’s bloodline.”

“In addition, I’ll have the Pine Leaf Mouse, using its Nascent Soul Demon Emperor-level mastery over plants, condense a space-attribute pine tree for its kin in the sect to comprehend the Dao of Space.”

“Lastly, I’ll have the Ice Soul Gu condense ten Ice Soul Sub-Gu, which will be sealed within the Beast Taming Sect. If any talented disciple has an affinity for the Gu path, they may choose to contract one.”

After Lin Jing finished speaking, Sect Leader Xu Xiaoyao repeatedly exclaimed, “Good! Good! Good!”

“From today onward, the Fire Carp, the Pine Leaf Mouse that masters space power, and the Ice Soul Sub-Gu shall be the three great totems of our Beast Taming Sect—only the most exceptional disciples may form contracts with them!”

He laughed heartily, feeling as though the future of the Beast Taming Sect had suddenly brightened.

If Senior Sister Xu Qingyao had contributed mainly cultivation resources, then what Lin Jing brought was true heritage.

“This is probably what those three guys wanted as well,” Lin Jing muttered to himself. After all, the Pine Leaf Mouse and Dragon Carp were more “authentically sect-born” than him. They were born in the Beast Taming Sect and would never be stingy in giving their kin opportunities.

As for the Ice Soul Gu... that one wished it could release a hundred Sub-Gu and sow chaos across the land while it waited for its harvest.

...

In the days that followed, to further fortify the sect, Lin Jing stayed at the Beast Taming Sect for several days.

During that time, the Pine Leaf Mouse successfully emerged from closed-door training and excitedly showed off its new power.

Meanwhile, Lin Jing’s Heavenly Demon Physique effect wore off, and his realm dropped back to the Core Formation Stage.

However, due to the breakthroughs of several of his contracted beasts during this time, his original cultivation was pulled directly to Core Formation Perfection—he wasn’t as far from the Nascent Soul Stage as he’d imagined.

After wrapping everything up and visiting old friends in the sect, Lin Jing returned to Immortal Prison Mountain.

At this moment, the descendants of Senior Pine had already successfully propagated the initial Bodhi Tree. A whole grove of Bodhi trees had become a new scenic feature of the area.

“You’re back?”

“Yes, I’ve returned—not failing your expectations. I managed to retrieve the Celestial Pill Pine Leaf…”

“I didn’t expect anything! Would’ve been better if you didn’t get it!” Senior Pine muttered. Even if the item was useful, the suffering would be his alone.

That damn powder—he had basically transplanted a tree heart into the Pine Leaf Mouse. Though the Pill Dust could help heal and restore it later, the pain of transplanting a tree heart had to be endured by the old guy himself.

“Give me the Pill Dust. I need to go rest in seclusion. That tree planting drained me.”

Senior Pine took the Pill Dust from Lin Jing and said no more, heading off to make preparations.

After respectfully seeing Senior Pine off, Lin Jing turned his gaze toward the entire grove of green Bodhi Trees with a look of anticipation.

“Pine Leaf Mouse, let’s synthesize this thing next.”

“Ehhh!!” The Pine Leaf Mouse was summoned by Lin Jing and climbed onto his shoulder, hesitating as it looked at the mountains of Bodhi Trees, swallowing nervously.

Synthesizing this many Bodhi Trees into one… what a waste… If the resulting item turned out useless like most synthesized objects, wouldn’t that be a huge loss?

After all, regular Bodhi Trees already had a great effect—enhancing enlightenment for all cultivators below the Void Mystical Stage.

“It’s fine. With Senior Pine here, even if the synthesis fails, we can just plant another batch.”

The Pine Leaf Mouse, reassured, gritted its teeth and began manipulating the whole mountain of Bodhi Trees to move toward it.

In the next moment, the entire Bodhi Grove was enveloped in Glazed Immortal Light and vanished instantly.

But at the same time, using this Rare Beast Divine Ability consumed an unimaginable amount of energy.

The Pine Leaf Mouse’s power was rapidly drained. Lin Jing quickly transferred spiritual power to it, but even he was being sucked dry.

The Dragon Carp and Ice Soul Gu inside the Hourglass of Time had no idea what was going on, but under Lin Jing’s call, their power was drained too.

One tree drained the entire team’s spiritual energy, knocking them out cold on the spot.

Much later, Lin Jing woke up with his head resting on the Mosquito Woman’s lap.

“Your Highness the Holy Son, you’re awake.”

The Mosquito Woman looked at him with concern. “A patrolling Mosquito Beast saw you fainted on the mountain, so I came to check. Don’t worry, it was just spiritual power exhaustion. I’ve already transferred power to you.”

“Thank you.” Lin Jing rubbed his head.

“Did Your Highness pass out because of that tree?”

The Mosquito Woman frowned and looked toward a nearby Bodhi Tree emitting a blood-red glow. A strange aura emanated from it.

“This isn’t silver, nor is it gold.”

Lin Jing popped a few pills and woke up the still-dazed Pine Leaf Mouse.

“Quick, take a look—what effect does this tree have?”

“...”

The Pine Leaf Mouse weakly glanced over—and immediately perked up.

[Mutated Bodhi Tree: Great enlightenment between life and death. The closer you are to death while meditating under this tree, the greater the boost to comprehension.]

“Wha—wha—what the hell...”

The Pine Leaf Mouse jumped up, pointing frantically.

It was one thing to risk your life in battle, but now you had to risk your life just to cultivate?!

It looked at Lin Jing and strongly recommended ditching this twisted thing.

“What if it turns out amazing? Let’s have the Dragon Carp try it first,” Lin Jing mused.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 84: Heaven-Defying Bonus
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 84: Heaven-Defying Bonus

"Dragon Carp, there's something good—try it first."

Lin Jing shook the Dragon Carp awake again. The Dragon Carp blinked blearily at the Blood Bodhi in front of it.

"The more severe your injuries, the closer you are to death, the greater the Comprehension Boost."

"Sounds powerful. If I half-kill myself, the effect might rival the Golden Bodhi Tree! Even if it doesn't, it should at least compare to the Silver Bodhi Tree, right?"

"You’ve used the Pain Pill a lot before and have experience cultivating in extreme injury states. You’re probably the best one to stay conscious while training in that condition. This is a rare opportunity—let’s see if you can push the Divine Dragon Transformation to a new level."

Lin Jing’s devilish words drilled into its ears, and the Dragon Carp sobered up. Its big, copper bell eyes stared at the Blood Bodhi… What the h*ll is this!

"Pop!!!!" The Dragon Carp wailed. How could that be the same?!

The Pain Pill just hurts;
it doesn't actually injure!

Getting injured would make it extremely fragile and helpless. If the Pine Leaf Mouse’s creation had any issue, it would be the fish on the chopping block.

"Ay!!" The Pine Leaf Mouse jumped out, pointing at the cowardly Dragon Carp, declaring that its creation was absolutely reliable.

This kind of golden opportunity—other carp wouldn’t even dream of it.

"Pop pop!!" The Dragon Carp spun around and made a suggestion.

Since Senior Mo Pine had put so much effort into cultivating all these Bodhi Trees, why not give this great chance to him?

Anyway, in a moment, Senior Mo Pine would have to dig out his tree heart to provide External Incarnation materials.

If he didn’t use Celestial Pill Dust to treat the wounds during that process, wouldn’t that count as a life-threatening injury?

That would perfectly activate the Blood Bodhi.

They might even be able to save the Celestial Pill Dust that was about to be used.

“Hmm.” Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse froze, staring at the incredibly filial Dragon Carp, lost in thought.

“You little carp… your brain’s getting sharper by the day.” Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse pointed at it, commenting.

…

“Senior? Senior?”

God Pine had just taken root into the Earth Vein, intending to rest, when Lin Jing came over again and noisily woke it.

“If you’ve got something, spit it out…” It glanced at Lin Jing carrying the Blood Bodhi and paused, surprised.

“When you cut your own origin and dig out your tree heart later, maybe give this a try,” Lin Jing explained.

God Pine’s face turned dark.

This good, green Bodhi Tree—what kind of mess had they synthesized from it?

“The owner of this Glazed Immortal Eyes spell definitely wasn’t some proper celestial,” God Pine grumbled.

“Heh… heh…” Lin Jing flashed an awkward but polite smile. He thought the same thing.

Compared to the divine ability Glazed Immortal Eyes, the Dragon Carp’s Flame Emperor’s Divine Ability seemed way more dependable.

“Look at this.” God Pine gazed at the Blood Bodhi, his face suspicious. Compared to the Leaf Mouse, he was even more timid—mainly because his cultivation level was lower and he was worried that big tree might affect him too much.

“You want to use me as a guinea pig, huh.”

As expected of an old devil, Senior God Pine saw through Lin Jing and the others’ intentions at a glance. Despite their vigorous head-shaking, it snorted.

“I get it. Get lost already. I’ll let you know the results afterward.”

“Alrighty.” Lin Jing immediately dragged the Pine Leaf Mouse and Dragon Carp away to give God Pine space.

While waiting, Lin Jing took out a batch of Cultivation Core Pills refined by the Dragon Carp.

Besides that, he also pulled out a spiritual fruit.

“A specialty of the Cloud Sea Mountains, a Tier-5 spiritual fruit—the Celestial Surge Fruit.”

“If nothing goes wrong on Senior Mo Pine’s side, I might try cultivating and breaking through beneath the Blood Bodhi myself.”

“The Celestial Surge Fruit is a spiritual fruit used to break through cultivation bottlenecks quickly. Normally, for a cultivator to break into a new major realm, they would have to undergo closed-door cultivation for decades or even centuries to stabilize their foundation and solidify their realm. Once in seclusion, time becomes unknowable.”

“I’m about to break through to Nascent Soul, and I can’t afford to do it slowly anymore. There’s not much time left—I need alternatives. I originally planned to use other means to save time, but now, maybe I should give this Celestial Surge Fruit a shot.”

“After consuming the Celestial Surge Fruit, the fruit transforms into a gentle yet forceful spiritual energy, forcibly pushing the cultivator past their bottleneck. It can speed up a breakthrough dozens of times or more, often allowing a breakthrough in a very short span.”

“But the price is high—it damages a cultivator’s meridians and foundation, making the breakthrough extremely risky. Typically, only those in dire straits and desperate for power are willing to risk it.”

“After all, one slight misstep and they could perish during the breakthrough.”

“Even if they succeed, the damage to their meridians and foundation isn’t something the average person can handle.”

“But for me right now, time is more important. First, I’ve got a solid foundation from cultivating all the way to the Thirteenth Layer of Qi Refinement, so even if I take damage, it won’t be devastating. Second, the Dragon Carp can not only form Celestial Annihilation with healing properties to repair that damage, but I also have plenty of healing divine medicines from previous spoils.”

“Of course, most importantly, while my meridians and foundation are injured, I’ll also be able to fully experience the Blood Bodhi’s effects.”

Compared to my earlier plan to mimic the Hourglass of Time by using a time-accelerating formation based on Dragon Vein flow, this current method suits the situation better.

As Lin Jing prepared his resources for charging into Nascent Soul, over on God Pine’s side—after absorbing a wave of Earth Vein energy—he had already begun cutting away his origin to provide materials for the Pine Leaf Mouse’s External Incarnation.

“No loss, no loss.”

While hacking away at himself, God Pine kept muttering, comforting himself. After all, the Pine Leaf Mouse’s external incarnation would remain in the Demon Court of the Tianyuan Realm to synthesize Transformation Grass.

In other words, the Pine Leaf Mouse’s incarnation would always stay by God Pine’s side!

“This Glazed Immortal Eyes is truly an unbelievable divine ability. The stronger the incarnation, the more powerful the ability becomes. Later on, when I have this little Pine Mouse’s clone synthesize resources, it’ll be much more convenient.”

God Pine stabbed a branch into his own body. From inside, a golden-green tree heart—like a Core Formation, Nascent Soul, and True Spirit all in one—was forced out.

He dug out two-thirds of his essence. With a scream of agony, his aura plummeted.

At the same time, what had once been a sacred body rapidly split open with cracks and wounds.

“F***! This is the first time in my life I’ve ever dug out my own tree heart... hurts like h*ll.”

“Hm? Wait a second?”

The intense pain and dizziness made God Pine want to immediately use Celestial Pill Dust to heal his Nascent Soul injuries.

But right then, a ripple from the nearby Blood Bodhi made his expression freeze in disbelief.

“I haven’t even started cultivating—why is my spell realm rising on its own? Aren’t only spells at the Spell Spirit Origin level capable of self-cultivation?”

He stared at the Blood Bodhi in astonishment.

This thing… is it really that powerful???

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 85: Time for the Hunt
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 85: Time for the Hunt

Originally, God Pine didn’t have high hopes for the Blood Bodhi. After all, he was already at the Void Mystical Stage, but the effect of the Blood Bodhi exceeded his expectations.

It could actually make spells train themselves!

That was terrifying.

Of course, God Pine could sense that the speed of automatic training for his spells wasn’t fast—it was significantly slower than if he cultivated actively.

But that wasn’t the point of this tree. Its true value lay in enabling cultivators to keep cultivating even while severely injured—without lifting a finger.

Just imagine—a Core Formation cultivator on the brink of death after repeatedly using Core Formation Divine Abilities, their core shattered and vitality nearly extinguished.

If they wanted to heal, who knows how long it would take—decades, maybe centuries.

And during that time, in order to heal, they would waste countless precious years. Their cultivation would completely stagnate.

Normally, someone seriously injured wouldn’t have the willpower to focus on cultivation. That’s why God Pine hadn’t been impressed at first.

But if the tree could let spells cultivate like they had spiritual intelligence, that changed everything.

“Although the speed is slow, I’m not even actively cultivating right now. If I actively train while using it, wouldn’t that make it even faster?”

“This is a real treasure.”

God Pine’s eyes gleamed as he began cultivating the Heavenly Demon Crossing, a secret technique from his past life.

He found that the technique, which had always been hard to use with his pine tree body, now flowed much more smoothly.

“Screw healing—let’s cultivate first!”

...

A few days later.

God Pine handed over a sapling-sized tree heart to Lin Jing and transmitted a message: “Here’s the material. The Blood Bodhi works great. You guys go make another one yourselves. There are still many immature Bodhi saplings on the mountain—find resources to ripen one yourselves... This one, I’m keeping.”

Holy Son Hall.

Lin Jing caught the tree heart source that descended from the sky and paused slightly.

Looks like the Blood Bodhi really was impressive, or else Senior Mo Pine wouldn’t have kept it for himself.

“Well, can’t complain. Senior Mo Pine still left me this—judging by the energy, it’s at least two-thirds of his origin essence.”

Lin Jing handed the External Incarnation material to the Pine Leaf Mouse, then looked at the now-barren Bodhi Forest with a sigh.

He took out a Sixth-Tier Spirit Spring he had “borrowed” from the Gu God Sect, intending to grow another Blood Bodhi for himself.

...

One month later in the outside world.

Inside the Hourglass of Time.

Beneath a crimson Blood Bodhi tree.

With a crisp crack, accompanied by the sound of Dao, Lin Jing’s main body’s aura broke through from Peak Core Formation to the Nascent Soul Realm like a flood bursting through a dam.

His Core shattered completely.

From within, a tiny infant emerged, sitting cross-legged atop the broken Core, radiating powerful waves of energy.

The Nascent Soul bared its teeth as it opened its eyes.

Its entire body was in agony as though it had been torn apart. The once-resilient meridians were already cracking in multiple places, oozing blood inside the body.

Using the Celestial Surge Fruit to brute-force his breakthrough left Lin Jing’s Nascent Soul with none of the clarity or joy normally expected.

He just felt like he was about to die.

But once the breakthrough was completed, Lin Jing’s strength underwent a massive transformation.

Meanwhile, the Pinewood Lin Jing who was still refining his External Incarnation had yet to stir, still in the early stage of Nascent Soul, but...

Thanks to the lifted realm cap, the Dragon Carp’s cultivation directly shot to Mid Core Formation Stage.

It seemed the Ancestral-tier bloodline gave it a massive boost, drastically increasing its breakthrough speed.

But the Dragon Carp was nothing compared to the Ice Soul Gu.

With the Beast Tamer’s realm cap removed, its advancement was flat-out absurd.

Not long ago, it had just broken into Early Nascent Soul, but as Lin Jing broke through, it shot up to Perfected Nascent Soul Stage.

The combined power of the Eight Rare Gu had been previously limited by Lin Jing’s own cultivation, preventing the Ice Soul Gu from fully digesting them.

There were still many nutrients in its bloodline left unabsorbed.

Now that Lin Jing had unlocked the seal, the Ice Soul Gu—having consumed seven kinds of Rare Gu essences—was now only a step away from the Nascent Soul Transformation Stage!

Moreover, as it completely digested the Rare Gu essences, its bloodline level also rose to Ancestral-tier, on par with the Dragon Carp.

It might not yet match the Dragon Carp’s Celestial Flame or Rare Beast Bloodline, but Lin Jing believed—

Once the Ice Soul Gu consumed the last two Rare Gu, absorbed the Gu God’s blood, and refined the Immortal Demon Serpent’s origin, it might not quite reach an Emperor-tier bloodline, but it wouldn’t be far off.

Of course, compared to the changes in Lin Jing’s main body, the biggest transformation was in the Mechanical Turtle following his External Incarnation.

“Uhh...?”

Its realm had been stuck at Early Nascent Soul, limited by Lin Jing’s own cultivation and sealed.

But now, the seal shattered—

It immediately leapt to the peak realm a Beast Taming Cultivator could sustain—Early Nascent Soul Transformation Stage!

Just like that, it became the highest-level pet in Lin Jing’s team.

“Huh.” The First Ancient Emperor glanced at the little turtle on the Incarnation’s head. “So your main body broke through to Nascent Soul, huh.”

“Mm. I’ll bring the main body to the Ancient Kingdom in a while... but for now, let this one go stretch its legs.”

The External Incarnation of Lin Jing grinned as he looked at the eager Mechanical Turtle.

“I can finally go travel on my own???” The turtle had waited way too long for this day.

“Yup. But you’ve gotta return before His Majesty the Ancient Emperor fades away to extend his life.”

“And I’ll choose your travel destination too.”

The Mechanical Turtle was bitter, but nodded.

Before, it was too weak for Lin Jing to let it roam freely.

But now, as a Nascent Soul Transformation Stage mechanical beast, it could fully wield quasi-Immortal Artifacts, and its strength gave Lin Jing full confidence to let it go.

Lin Jing’s Incarnation looked at the Fortune Gold List.

Ancient Imperial Prince, Ancient Clan Princess, Daozi of the Spirit Dao Sect, Crown Prince of Poseidon Palace, Young Master Dragon Willow, Holy Son of the Demon Court, Martial Emperor’s Successor Wan Renzong, Senior Sister of Water Moon Immortal Island, Divine Son of the Witch God Hall, and Holy Son of the Snow Plains Totem...

Those were the top ten.

Lin Jing pointed at the last four. “These four haven’t paid their protection fees—Martial Emperor City, Water Moon Immortal Island, Cloud Sea Mountains, Snow Plains.

You can choose any of them to travel to. Your mission: defeat any one of these four.

Not kill—just defeat.”

“Hmm... The Martial Emperor’s Successor dropped a Stone Man Gu, and Water Moon Immortal Island dropped a Yin Spirit Gu. Let’s prioritize those two.”

“Since your essence is a mechanical beast, I’m not sure if you can appear on the Gold List...

By artifact spirit age, you’re an old ghost.

But by mechanical component age, you’re a newborn.

And as a mechanical creation, you don’t even have a set ‘golden cultivation age’... so who knows how the list judges it.”

“Either way, give it a try. Even if you can’t get on the Gold List, treat this as scouting for the Pine Leaf Mouse, Dragon Carp, and Ice Soul Gu.

Once my main body finishes up, I’ll send them out to hunt these Gold List prodigies one by one...”

Beside him, the First Ancient Emperor mourned silently for those geniuses and urged, “Alright, let’s go rescue Gao Sheng!”

The First Ancient Emperor wanted to test the reporting mechanism in the Ancient Kingdom.

He had just gotten Gao Sheng to disguise himself as a rogue cultivator and report a corrupt official using a regulatory artifact from above—

Only to have the local Demon-Slaying Bureau capture him on the spot as a suspected demon cultivator.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 86: The Most Bizarre Challenger
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 86: The Most Bizarre Challenger

Treasure hunting—ideally for something that extends lifespan.

Warfare—seizing weapons and equipment during battles.

A new mechanical tortoise born from the fusion of a Longevity Turtle and a War Weapon Spirit, with both obsessions locked into its body and soul.

After being released by Lin Jing, this little tortoise chose to travel in the direction of the Yunhai Mountain Range.

As for this travel route, the Mechanical Tortoise was quite pleased.

After all, a place like Yunhai Mountain Range, with frequent deaths and countless unclaimed storage pouches lying around desolate corners, was ideal for it to shine...

Still, the Mechanical Tortoise hadn’t forgotten Lin Jing’s main task for it—attempt to challenge the Holy Son of the Wu God Hall, who was currently ranked ninth on the Destiny Gold List.

This journey wasn’t exactly short. Most cultivators at the Nascent Soul Transformation Stage would take a long time to make the trip. The Weapon Spirit, however, was an exception—it could control all sorts of supreme flying swords, top-tier vehicles, and even space-based magical treasures to get there.

Using supreme-grade magical treasures wasn’t impossible either, but it required extra effort based on the quality of the treasure, making them unsuitable for prolonged combat.

As for quasi-immortal artifacts, unless one had special cultivation techniques or constitutions, Nascent Soul cultivators simply couldn’t activate them. Even if they could unleash a single blow, they’d be completely drained—and even then, the artifact might not unleash even a tenth of its full power...

But as a weapon born of war, the Weapon Spirit was different. It had an innate advantage. Now that its Nascent Soul cultivation had been unlocked, it could fully activate quasi-immortal artifacts, and the effort required was no more than a typical Nascent Soul cultivator using a high-grade magical treasure—perhaps even less.

This overpowered ability, combined with Lin Jing handing over several quasi-immortal artifacts from past loot, meant that even Lin Jing had no idea what kind of explosive power the Mechanical Tortoise could unleash. But it was safe to say—it would definitely be the strongest in the current team...

...

Wu God Hall was a cultivation sect made up of ancient Wu tribes who believed in gods.

And the Holy Son of Wu God Hall was one of those most capable of communicating with the gods, able to easily channel the power of ancient deities.

"Ma no ku sa, da mu sha ye, ka ma bei..."

Atop a mountain peak in the Yunhai Mountain Range, standing on a dragon vein and surrounded by chaotic celestial phenomena, a young man with strange markings on his face sat cross-legged, chanting unknown words.

Drizzle and snow blended together, drifting down from the sky. Occasionally, lightning would strike near him. Amidst this chaos, he remained motionless, seemingly merged with the heavens and earth.

While the young man was cultivating, a ripple in space suddenly flickered—and a tortoise appeared behind a rocky outcrop, quietly spying on him.

Before the Mechanical Tortoise could continue spying for long, the young man opened his eyes and said, “Come out. You've been watching for quite a while.”

The Mechanical Tortoise slowly stepped forward. The Holy Son of Wu God Hall frowned, confused as he looked at this “mechanical creation.”

"Nascent Soul Transformation Stage... a mechanical beast?"

Although Wu God Hall was a semi-isolated sect, as its top genius and a powerhouse among Dao-Seeking factions, the Holy Son was well-informed.

Seeing that it wasn’t a remnant from the Gu God Sect, nor did it seem like someone from the Hall had come looking for him, the Holy Son showed a hint of curiosity.

“From the Ancient Kingdom’s Shenji Manor, perhaps?” Based on what he knew, that was the most logical conclusion.

But the next second, the Mechanical Tortoise shook its head and made a strange sound—part machine, part soul—and said, “Yes yes, from Shenji Manor…”

“Just happened to be traveling here. Want to have a fight with you.”

“Gained sentience… yet still an unintelligent mechanical beast…” The Holy Son of Wu God Hall looked surprised. “Could it be from some minor ancient ruin?”

Since the Golden Small World had opened, countless relics had appeared. Cultivators had unearthed all sorts of bizarre things. He’d never heard of an ancient ruin that didn’t have some mechanical puppet crafted by Mechanism Arts guarding its entrance and blocking intruders.

BOOM!!!

The next instant, the Holy Son chanted a spell with extreme speed, and a lightning spear descended from the sky, piercing down toward the Mechanical Tortoise by the rock. Since it said it wanted to fight, he asked no more.

Facing the lightning summoning technique, the Mechanical Tortoise’s eyes gleamed. It didn’t dodge in the slightest. Instead, it spit out a supreme magical treasure—an Evasion Pearl—and held it in its mouth.

Immediately after, before the descending lightning spear could fully strike the tortoise, it began to dissolve in midair. This was one of the spoils Lin Jing had taken from the Gu God Sect, rumored to be capable of avoiding heavenly tribulation. It wasn’t much use to Lin Jing, who already had the Ice Soul Lightning Tribulation Gu, so he gave it to the Mechanical Tortoise.

“? A supreme magical treasure?”

Seeing the tortoise carry such a treasure, the Holy Son of Wu God Hall was stunned.

That lightning strike was just a basic move, no more than a casual flick of the hand. But as one of Tianyuan’s top ten prodigies, even a casual attack wasn’t something a normal Nascent Soul cultivator could resist. It was meant to test the tortoise—and its response surprised him.

“Tortoise Flying Sword.”

The Mechanical Tortoise muttered in a mechanical tone, retrieved the Evasion Pearl, and then pulled out another supreme magical treasure—a flying sword, one of Lin Jing’s trophies from Tongtian City. Under the tortoise’s control, the sword shot out with a "whoosh", turning into a white streak aimed at the Holy Son’s neck.

Screech—

The sword’s power tore through space, leaving a long black rift in its wake.

“It can spit out flying swords… and it’s another supreme magical treasure.” The Holy Son of Wu God Hall was shocked. He couldn’t understand what was going on with this tortoise. Two supreme magical treasures already?

In the outside world, even a single supreme magical treasure could become the foundation of a Nascent Soul-level sect, requiring the strength of the entire sect to maintain it.

“You’re not the only one with treasures!” The Holy Son also pulled out a supreme magical treasure. As the top genius of a Dao-Seeking sect, of course his standard gear wouldn’t be mere high-grade treasures.

He summoned a green flag, which once planted midair, released a phoenix-shaped artifact spirit illusion. It stirred a storm and let out a phoenix cry, blowing away the flying sword.

What he didn’t know was—the Mechanical Tortoise loved it when others used magical treasures to fight it.

Rather than using its own, it was even better at snatching the treasures of others.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 87: Fierce Battle in the Sea of Clouds
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 87: Fierce Battle in the Sea of Clouds

“What the h*ll.”

After taking out the Wind Phoenix Banner, the Holy Son of Wu God Hall had effectively countered the Weapon Spirit’s flying sword attack. But what left him dumbfounded was that, in the very next instant, his connection with the banner was severed. The entire banner flew toward the tortoise along with the wind, showing not even a shred of loyalty to him.

Meanwhile, the flying sword from earlier turned against the wind, curved midair, and once again turned into a streak of rainbow light rushing straight at him.

“Wind Phoenix Banner, return!”

“Return!”

“Come back to me!”

Completely ignoring the incoming flying sword, the Holy Son of Wu God Hall just wanted to know—why the hell his banner wasn’t listening to him anymore.

That banner had been gifted by Lord Wu God...

Then he saw it— the tortoise’s shell actually opened like a pot lid, swallowing the Wind Phoenix Banner right into its body. The Holy Son of Wu God Hall finally lost it.

“What the hell did you just do?” His eyes narrowed as he extended his arm, and with a bang, grabbed the flying sword coming at him at the speed of light. The moment he grasped the supreme flying sword’s blade, its sword light burst apart like a meteor shower. The sword light that hit the ground easily carved out countless cracks in the earth. But when it landed on the Holy Son, it only left shallow red scratches across his body, each one instantly healed by a flash of white light.

Chomp, chomp.

The flying sword trembled, trying to escape his grip. But the Holy Son’s hand was like a mountain, pinning it down completely.

Seeing this, the Mechanical Tortoise was genuinely surprised. So this Holy Son of Wu God Hall had some skills—he could casually grab a supreme flying sword and ignore its blade damage with just his flesh?

Clearly, relying solely on supreme magical treasures wasn’t enough to beat this guy.

The Mechanical Tortoise grew serious… The Beast Tamer had really given it a tough mission.

Still, both were at the early stage of Nascent Soul Transformation, and the Mechanical Tortoise didn’t believe it would lose to this guy.

“Give me back my Wind Phoenix Banner, or don’t even think about getting your flying sword back,” the Holy Son barked, clenching the struggling sword tightly.

But his threat didn’t faze the Mechanical Tortoise at all. Trading a soulless flying sword for a supreme banner with a spirit? Totally worth it!

Besides, the Mechanical Tortoise didn’t believe the opponent could snatch its treasure anyway.

“Screee!”

The next instant, to test the Holy Son’s limits, the Mechanical Tortoise skipped past supreme treasures and pulled out a genuine quasi-immortal artifact—a long whip made from the tail of some unknown creature, covered in venomous barbs.

The Gu God Sect had no true immortal artifacts, but they did have quite a few quasi-immortal ones. This whip, the Poison God Whip, was one of them. Now it had become one of Lin Jing’s trophies.

Under the Mechanical Tortoise’s control, the whip released a terrifying aura that made the Holy Son’s expression shift dramatically.

“Gu God Sect’s treasure… the Poison God Whip…”

Before he could think further, the whip lashed out through the air. With a flash of violet light, the Holy Son was sent flying, his beast-hide robe torn open, a deep gash blooming on his chest, red blood bubbling with purple venom boiling out like magma.

“Ugh.” The Holy Son spat out a mouthful of poisonous blood, clutching his chest. He immediately cast White Witchcraft to detoxify himself while locking eyes on the Mechanical Tortoise, which had already retracted the Poison God Whip…

“A remnant of the Gu God Sect… or could it be…”

In the next moment, the Poison God Whip lashed out madly—eighteen consecutive strikes from the air. The Holy Son’s eyes widened. Even he could barely activate a quasi-immortal artifact. Maybe once he reached the Void Mystical Stage, he could do it. But that Nascent Soul Mechanical Tortoise? It was swinging that thing around like a blade of grass. How?!

“Is it the Demon Court Holy Son’s sword pet??”

Using substitution witchcraft, the Holy Son scrambled to avoid the whip, turning into a stone figure, then a scarecrow, then various beasts—appearing in different locations one after another. While dodging, he came to a realization.

“I’ve never heard of the Gu God Sect using mechanical beasts… then you must be Lin Jing’s pet. He’s the one who dared to attack the Gu God Sect with that strange ability to activate immortal artifacts across realms.”

“With help from several Dao-Seeking Demon Emperors, he really did wipe out the Gu God Sect.”

“This Poison God Whip—besides showing up in the hands of Gu God Sect survivors—would most likely end up with Lin Jing.”

“According to the Destiny Gold List, one of Lin Jing’s pets has already made it onto the list. So you’re here trying to defeat me on your own and claim a spot independently from your master?”

After deducing the Mechanical Beast’s origins and intent, the Holy Son snorted coldly. The next instant, the ground behind the Mechanical Tortoise exploded. A youth identical to the Holy Son—except for his gray hair—leapt out from the earth vein.

His gray hair and eyes gave him a grim look, and the runes on his face were more complex. His fierce gaze locked onto the Mechanical Tortoise.

“You’ve been bullying my clone long enough. Now it’s my turn.” In a single thought, the Holy Son fused his clone back into his true body.

With a roar, the real Holy Son cast a divine image technique, growing to a hundred meters tall. This wasn’t a normal projection—his massive form resembled a god descending from the heavens. His face transformed into that of a green-faced titan, and divine runes covered his body.

Divine Spirit Projection!

BOOM!

A giant divine hand slammed toward the Mechanical Tortoise.

The Poison God Whip lashed out again, leaving blood trails on the divine hand, but the poison had no effect.

This projection completely nullified toxins—it was a direct counter to the Poison God Whip!

But what stunned the Holy Son was—the damn turtle, seeing its Poison God Whip countered, actually summoned a second quasi-immortal artifact!

And this time, it activated both simultaneously!

Another treasure from the Gu God Sect—this one was a black bottle, a sealing tool used by the sect to trap deadly poison bugs.

“Seal!!!” the Mechanical Tortoise raised its head and bellowed.

“You d*mn turtle…” the Holy Son couldn’t believe it. Just activating one quasi-immortal artifact was ridiculous enough—and now this thing was using two, without a single drop in power?

With a loud boom, the divine might of the Holy Son’s projection was still no match for the power of a quasi-immortal artifact. His true body was sucked right into the bottle. But as the Mechanical Tortoise carried the trembling bottle on its back, it realized the Holy Son had used some kind of witchcraft—the bottle hadn’t sealed away his power, only temporarily captured him.

Right now, the Holy Son was struggling inside, ready to burst out at any moment.

Seeing this, the Mechanical Tortoise was just about to add a little “special sauce” into the bottle—when the surrounding void shattered, and an old man with bird feathers sticking out of his head stormed out, furious.

“Fear not, Holy Son! I’m here to save you!!”

Who the hell are you!?

The Mechanical Tortoise looked blankly at the Void Mystical Stage elder bursting through space, annoyed that this old guy was interfering in its duel.

“Go away.”

Not wanting the outcome of the fight affected by outsiders, the Mechanical Tortoise instantly activated its third quasi-immortal artifact!

A treasure shaped like a compass.

It was the very same treasure that had caused the great academy relocation back then—an immortal palace teleportation artifact. Whether it was the same one or a copy was unknown.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 88: Defeat
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 88: Defeat

The power of this Immortal Palace Teleportation Treasure lay in its unpatterned spatial teleportation—so chaotic that even a Void Mystical cultivator couldn't stop it, and even the one activating it couldn’t control the result.

That was how the artifact was designed, so the Weapon Spirit had no way of precisely directing the teleport—only random transmission was possible.

Still, being the final artifact spirit created by the Immortal Palace, it had its own unique insights into controlling such treasures, far superior to any human cultivator.

Even though it couldn’t determine the final location of the compass teleportation, the Mechanical Tortoise could still take others along with it—and make sure they landed at the same location together.

“Not good!”

The Grand Elder of Wu God Hall saw the strange tortoise about to use spatial magic to take the Holy Son away and instantly pushed his power to the limit!

Anxious and desperate to block the teleportation, he tried everything, but the power of the Immortal Palace treasure was simply too absurd. Even a Void Mystical Stage powerhouse couldn’t do a thing. All he could do was watch helplessly, eyes bloodshot, as his Holy Son was abducted by a tortoise.

“Lord Wu God, something terrible has happened!”

With no other choice, the Grand Elder of Wu God Hall took out the emergency contact treasure gifted by the Wu God. Meanwhile, Wu God himself, who had been reinforcing the world barrier, immediately stopped his work.

“Why the panic? He has my life-saving Witch Soul on him. His life isn’t in danger... Let me see what’s going on.”

Thanks to the Witch Soul, Wu God could accurately pinpoint his location. Otherwise, even with Dao-Seeking power, finding someone in the vast world of cultivation would be like finding a needle in a haystack.

After some time, Wu God located them in the Qingzhou region. There he found a Nascent Soul Mechanical Tortoise shaking a quasi-immortal sealing treasure and continuously stuffing it with all kinds of poisoned magical items to exhaust the Holy Son’s power...

“This turtle...”

“This treasure...”

Wu God’s face darkened as he turned to the Demon Court Lord, who was still earnestly reinforcing the world barrier, and said, “Your Holy Son… what’s his meaning behind this?”

“Huh?”

The Demon Court Lord looked at him, puzzled. “What’s wrong? I don’t know anything. Is he in danger?”

She quickly used the “Imperial Weapon” and “Tiger Fang” to lock onto Lin Jing’s true location—both of which bore her personal mark.

“Isn’t he quietly and obediently cultivating?” Sensing that Lin Jing was currently in the Immortal Prison Mountain of Qingzhou, the Demon Empress was confused.

“Still pretending! That turtle pet of his ran off to Yunhai Mountain Range and captured the Holy Son of Wu God Hall with a quasi-immortal artifact!” Wu God exploded.

Having his Holy Son taken down solo by a single pet of the Demon Court Holy Son—if it were up to him, Wu God really didn’t want to confront the Demon Empress.

But not knowing Lin Jing’s intentions and out of concern for the Holy Son’s safety, he had to at least communicate with her.

After all, if he—an elite of Dao-Seeking Realm—stepped in while the Holy Son wasn’t in real mortal danger, it wouldn’t look good.

Ultimately, this was still a clash between the younger generation.

Dmn it. Wu God had thought that now that the Demon Court Holy Son had reached sixth place, he wouldn’t be eyeing ninth. Who knew that bstard wanted his pet to climb into the top ten on its own… Wu God was fuming.

“Oh, I see.” The Demon Empress put on a sorrowful expression. “So he got captured by a pet... Well then...”

“Maybe your Holy Son wanted to voluntarily step down from the rankings. Don’t blame me. I didn’t interfere. And if there's any real danger to the Holy Son’s life...”

“Well, that’s when us elders get involved. For now, let’s just keep reinforcing the barrier. We’re almost done with the upgrades...”

Wu God’s face got darker and darker.

The real issue now wasn’t the Holy Son’s safety.

It was that if the Destiny Gold List really showed the Holy Son of Wu God Hall getting solo-defeated by the Demon Court Holy Son’s pet, Wu God would never live it down.

“Relax. Don’t be so petty. This emperor helped you guys wipe out the Gu God Sect, didn’t I? And even if he gets kicked out of the top ten, wouldn’t it just be the Totem Holy Son of the Snow Plains replacing him? It’s just going from ninth to tenth. Not a big deal,” said the Demon Empress with a wave of her hand.

Wu God looked toward a certain location in the outer void, where four Snow Plains Demon Emperors were also busy reinforcing the world barrier.

Those four Demon Emperors had once been at odds with the Ancient Kingdom. Before the coming of the Golden Era, they were even in open conflict.

But in the end, the Snow Plains chose to ally with the Demon Court, entering into a “Pact of Alliance,” and paid a massive protection fee. That stopped the war between the Ancient Kingdom and the Snow Plains.

Of course, the most critical factor was that the Second Generation Ancient Emperor had no interest in war with the Snow Plains anymore.

Compared to the impact of the Golden Era and the looming threat of the Heavenly Demon Realm, territorial expansion of the Ancient Kingdom was now trivial.

Temporarily setting aside old grudges with the Snow Plains and getting their help to strengthen the world barrier was actually a win for the Ancient Kingdom. After all, if any foreign enemies breached the world, the Tianyuan Ancient Kingdom would be the first to suffer.

As for the cultivators and civilians who died due to his wars? The Second Generation Ancient Emperor believed that was inevitable. There are no eternal enemies—only eternal interests. Sacrifices must be made for the glory of the Ancient Kingdom.

Qingzhou.

Perhaps due to spatial inertia, the Mechanical Tortoise and the Holy Son of Wu God Hall were randomly teleported here.

Once it realized it had landed in Qingzhou, the Mechanical Tortoise chose to continue tormenting the Holy Son inside the bottle while also going to find Lin Jing’s true body.

Before long, it noticed that the Holy Son had already lost consciousness inside the bottle. So it let him out, poked him a few times, and confirmed that he had indeed been defeated.

Then it focused its spiritual power into its eyes and stared blankly up at the Destiny Gold List in the sky.

The Holy Son of Wu God Hall was still sitting in the ninth position.

It had defeated him, yet didn’t make it onto the list.

“#￥%#￥%” Seeing this, the Mechanical Tortoise cursed loudly at the Destiny Gold List, recalling what Lin Jing had once said:

“Because your life essence is that of a mechanical beast, it’s unclear whether you can appear on the Gold List... If we go by artifact spirit age, you’re an old ghost. But by material freshness, you’re still a baby. And as a mechanical construct, you don’t have a ‘Golden Cultivation Age.’ Who knows how the list will judge you.”

Now the Mechanical Tortoise understood—the Destiny Gold List never planned to include “mechanical lifeforms” as a valid category.

Enraged, the Mechanical Tortoise stuffed the Holy Son back into the treasure bottle and headed straight for Immortal Prison Mountain...

Since it couldn’t get on the list itself, it might as well drag the Holy Son back and let the Pineleaf Mouse, Dragon Carp, or Ice Soul Gu defeat him instead... At least that way he’d be useful.

The only question was—could any of these “senior fellows” beat him as easily as it had?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 89: Powerful Foundation
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 89: Powerful Foundation

Xian Prison Mountain.

Now at the early Nascent Soul Transformation Stage, Lin Jing gently stroked the corpse of the Immortal Ox in front of him, his gaze intense.

At this point, the injuries to his foundation caused by advancing to the Nascent Soul Transformation Stage had completely healed. At the same time, during his recovery, he had gained an unexpected reward—he had fully mastered the Corpse-Driving Secret Technique of the Yin Corpse Sect.

Originally, Lin Jing thought he would need the Enlightenment Fish Soup to swiftly master the technique, but the effect of the Blood Bodhi had truly surprised him.

He had a theory: in a near-death state, the enhancement from the Blood Bodhi might not be inferior to the Enlightenment Fish Soup.

Of course, it’s extremely difficult for ordinary cultivators to focus on cultivation while severely wounded. Lin Jing had managed it only because he merged with the Ice Soul Gu, using its power to freeze his pain and seal off the worsening injuries without actually healing them. This allowed him to fully absorb the benefits of the Blood Bodhi.

“Also probably has something to do with the Corpse-Driving Secret Technique being half a Control Dao technique… it was relatively easy to cultivate.” Lin Jing murmured.

Using the purest source of life as a power source, he tried to control the Immortal Beast corpse in front of him. When he found it could only twitch slightly, he chuckled and shook his head.

“But right now, the Death God Pearl is already given to the Ice Soul Gu to refine its external avatar. Once it’s done, I’ll have it try again.”

“Besides, the mana of the Nascent Soul stage is too weak. To control it perfectly, I’d definitely have to burn life force to return to the Nascent Soul Transformation Stage, then keep burning lifespan to perform Divine Control Technique, raise my Control Dao level to match the Corpse-Driving Secret Technique—only then could I control this Immortal Corpse properly.”

He put the Immortal Ox corpse back into his storage bag. Then, suddenly sensing something, he turned toward a direction—he could feel the Mechanized Turtle approaching, and couldn’t help but mutter:

“Looks like... it didn’t succeed, huh…”

...

Not long after, the Mechanized Turtle returned carrying the Holy Son of the Wu God Hall, successfully regrouping with Lin Jing.

Without needing a report, Lin Jing already understood everything the Mechanized Turtle had experienced during this time through their contract link.

“So that’s how it went. What a pity—mechanical lifeforms can’t make it onto the rankings like humans and demon beasts.” Lin Jing sighed.

This meant the Mechanized Turtle couldn’t enjoy the Dao comprehension boost from the Destiny Golden Ranking... but it wasn’t a big deal.

After all, its core strength lay in its ability to control treasures beyond its level. This ability was already the pinnacle in the lower realm and couldn’t be enhanced further. As long as it had that, the Mechanized Turtle could be almost invincible in the lower realm. Compared to that, the golden ranking’s Dao benefits were far less important—for it, the benefits meant more for Song Leaf Mouse, Dragon Carp, and Ice Soul Gu.

“Well done, and you even brought back the Holy Son of the Wu God Hall.” Lin Jing smiled at the Mechanized Turtle.

“Mission complete! Can I finally travel freely now? Freedom, freedom, and more freedom!!!” The Mechanized Turtle was more eager for freedom than anything else.

“Ah... well.” Lin Jing fell into thought as he looked toward the golden ranking, then grinned.

“One last mission. Absolutely the last one.”

Mechanized Turtle: ???

“Look, the Wu God Hall’s Holy Son is ranked ninth. That means if we keep him detained on Xian Prison Mountain and continuously defeat him, Song Leaf Mouse, Dragon Carp, and Ice Soul Gu can at least occupy the ninth and tenth positions.”

“But of course, all three of them have to make it into the top ten.”

“Now check this out. Yuzhejian Island is pretty far from your location. You could follow the same method and snatch the Junior Sister of Water Moon Immortal Island too. That way, the fourth spot would be ours.” Ice Soul Gu stroked its chin, thinking it was totally feasible.

Although Granny Yinshan had used a Yin Spirit Gu as trade to ask me to protect that Junior Sister in the Source Realm, from another angle—if I don’t let her return to the Source Realm, wouldn’t that be the best kind of protection? As long as she doesn’t go back, she’ll never run into danger there…

Water Moon Immortal Island is a place with deep restrictions and heavy concealment—extremely hard to find. Any other pet might get lost under the sea. Only the Mechanized Turtle has hope of successfully reaching the island and capturing the Junior Sister.

Though I can’t exactly bring my pets to challenge their door, that entire island is full of handsome male cultivators. Ice Soul Gu was worried that once it got there, it wouldn’t want to come back.

“Abba abba abba… Mechanized Turtle suspects this really isn’t... the last mission. You want me to kidnap Water Moon Immortal Island’s Senior Sister now?”

The turtle was confused. Wasn’t that Senior Sister ranked eighth? Song Leaf Mouse and the others haven’t even reached the Nascent Soul Transformation Stage—could they even beat her solo??

“Relax,” Lin Jing said. “The techniques and spells of Water Moon Immortal Island only reach their full power in ocean environments. That’s why the island is located at sea and rarely lands.”

“At sea, they’re nearly invincible. But once on land, their strength drops by thirty percent. That’s exactly why I want you to bring her here...”

“Go ahead. On the other side of the ocean lies freedom.”

“Hmm? Looks like Ice Soul Gu is done. Perfect timing—leave the Wu God Hall’s Holy Son to me. I’ll have Ice Soul Gu give it a shot.”

...

Inside the Hourglass of Time.

Ice Soul Gu looked at the black spider avatar wrapped in death energy, now at the Half-Step Nascent Soul Transformation Stage, and silently acknowledged it.

This avatar was refined using the blood of the Gu God along with the death origin of an Undying Rare Beast. It gave Ice Soul Gu the feeling that it was even stronger than its main body.

Even though its main body had also reached Half-Step Nascent Soul Transformation by devouring the Fang Poison Gu—achieving Nine Gu Unity!

Fortunately, an avatar is still an avatar. With a single thought from Ice Soul Gu, the avatar fused back into its main body. The merging of the two powers left Ice Soul Gu feeling utterly refreshed—it even had the urge to go challenge that fish’s immortal fire right now.

“How does it feel?” Lin Jing asked via telepathy. Ice Soul Gu nodded excitedly—wonderful, absolutely wonderful.

“Good. Next, I’ve got a Nascent Soul Transformation Stage opponent for you to practice with. But he’s not your average Nascent Soul cultivator. Even though you’re only a step away from breaking through, Gu insects aren’t made for solo combat—you might not be able to beat him.”

“Leave it to me. I’ll crush him!” Ice Soul Gu didn’t believe that with its current foundation, it couldn’t take down a mere early-stage Nascent Soul.

“Don’t get cocky.”

“He’s the Holy Son of the Wu God Hall—a top-tier genius of the Tianyuan Realm. He’s experienced in countering Gu insects. Better not take him lightly. To boost your chances, I’ll let you use one of the Void Mystical Stage Demon Ancestor corpses we killed in Tongtian City. With your current power and mastery of death energy, parasitizing a demon ancestor should be no problem. With that vessel, you’ll be able to unleash your full power.”

Lin Jing made the arrangements. The Ghost Puppet had a quasi-immortal artifact, and Ice Soul Gu now had a demon ancestor corpse!

...

No one knew how long had passed, but the Holy Son of Wu God Hall woke up in an unknown mountain range.

In agonizing pain, he looked toward the ice spider crouched not far from him, now at Half-Step Nascent Soul Transformation Stage.

“What the h*ll... that’s... Lin Jing’s Ice Soul Gu!!?”

Just as he recognized the situation, the Ice Soul Gu flicked out a pile of top-grade spirit herbs with its silk.

“Hurry up and eat them. After that, let’s have a fight!” Ice Soul Gu urged.

The Holy Son nearly blacked out. D*mn it... that twisted Holy Son of the Demon Court… just you wait.

A freaky mechanical beast with a quasi-immortal artifact I couldn’t beat. And now even this lowly Gu bug that’s barely at Nascent Soul wants to humiliate me?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 90: The Collapsing Holy Son
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 90: The Collapsing Holy Son

“Impossible, absolutely impossible…”

Several hours later.

Slightly recovered, the Holy Son of the Wu God Hall began to fiercely battle the Ice Soul Gu.

The Holy Son prided himself on his wealth of experience fighting Gu insects, but the Ice Soul Gu’s power was far beyond what he had anticipated.

He had never seen a Gu that, without even reaching the Nascent Soul Transformation Stage, could parasitize and control the corpse of a demon ancestor—especially one at the peak of the Void Mystical Stage!

“Raaawr~~” In the end, Ice Soul Gu chose to control the corpse of the Heavenly Lion Ancestor, a peak-tier demon ancestor from Qingzhou under the Dao-Seeking Stage.

Normally, even with its current Nine Gu Unity, the Ice Soul Gu wouldn’t be able to perfectly control the corpse of a Void Mystical peak beast. But its bloodline was no longer just that of a Rare Gu.

Its avatar had been refined using the bloodline of an Undying Rare Beast fused with the blood of the Dao-Seeking Gu God. With the avatar’s power supporting it, and the Ice Soul Gu now possessing a portion of death origin, it handled the corpse with surprising ease.

Though the Heavenly Lion Ancestor was dead, a starved camel is still bigger than a horse. Beasts were known for their physical strength—dead or alive, their sheer body power could still crush an early-stage Nascent Soul cultivator into despair.

Beautiful!

The massive lion slammed its paw down, and the Holy Son’s dharma body was sent flying several kilometers like a rolling mountain.

“Raaawr~~!” Ice Soul Gu pressed the attack.

Though it could control the corpse of a Void Mystical demon ancestor, the rate at which its strength was draining was frightening. In this state, it could only maintain control for a few minutes—within that time, it had to settle the match with the Holy Son!

“Alright then…”

Meanwhile, atop a mountain peak, seeing that the Ice Soul Gu was suppressing the Holy Son as well, the Mechanized Turtle finally understood.

As expected—every one of Lin Jing’s pets was a total freak.

...

Not long after.

The Destiny Golden Ranking shifted.

This time, it was a change in the top ten. Every cultivator sensed it immediately.

When they focused mana into their eyes to gaze upon it, one by one, expressions of disbelief appeared across many faces.

On the Destiny Golden Ranking, rank nine: Ice Soul Gu. Cultivation: Half-Step Nascent Soul Transformation. Reason for listing: defeated the Holy Son of the Wu God Hall, Ta Wu.

“Ridiculous. What the h*ll is going on?”

In a snow-covered land, deep within a totem shrine, the burly Totem Holy Son stepped into the blizzard, staring in disbelief at his fall from the top ten.
