“That guy Ta Wu... was defeated by... the Demon Court Holy Son’s pet—a mere Rare Gu??”

What he couldn’t accept was that because of the other’s defeat, he himself was knocked out of the top ten!

Ice Soul Gu making it onto the list alone caused an uproar across Tianyuan Continent.

The Wu God Hall and Gu God Sect had clashed for nearly ten thousand years. No one ever expected the Holy Son of Wu God Hall to be solo-killed by a single Rare Gu.

“...”

In Sea God Palace, Misty Forest, some top talents who’d previously resented their factions paying Lin Jing protection fees to secure ranking spots now fell silent, staring thoughtfully at the shifting Golden Ranking.

What a f***ing joke. If Ice Soul Gu can enter the top ten by itself, after Lin Jing entered with his Heavenly Demon Physique by burning life force, then... what about us?

They heard that a Gu insect needed to merge with a vessel to fully exert its strength. Now, these elites couldn’t help but imagine—how terrifying would Chen Xing be if he fused with his Chen Xing Gu?

Back then, Lin Jing had supposedly defeated the Martial Emperor’s heir purely with his own strength—without using his pets much at all.

“This Demon Court Holy Son…”

The moment it entered the ranking, Ice Soul Gu immediately felt the Dao comprehension boost and couldn’t help but be amazed.

Most of Ice Soul Gu’s powers were inscribed into its bloodline—already refined to the extreme through countless reincarnations. All it needed was to steadily enhance its bloodline, and its abilities would automatically improve.

Very few Rare Gu would bother cultivating other powers. But now, Ice Soul Gu felt that its innate abilities were no longer enough.

“Sword Dao... Sword Dao…” Ice Soul Gu decided: from today, it would begin cultivating the “Primal Sword Dao” that Lin Jing wanted it to learn.

With the Primal Sword Intent gifted by Thunder Dragon Ancestor, it believed it could master it soon.

Aside from that, Lin Jing also carried a life-saving Sword Intent at the Dao-Seeking level, gifted by Feng Ning. That too would be a good reference.

Not long after, Ta Wu—the Holy Son who had been defeated by Ice Soul Gu—recovered from his despairing injuries once more.

He had lost.

Lost terribly.

Just as he was about to return to the Wu God Hall in the Cloud Sea Mountains, carrying his shame, another carp blocked his way.

“Blorp...”

Dragon Carp stared at the Holy Son with a dazed look. The meaning was clear—after Ice Soul Gu, it was now the carp’s turn to challenge him.

“Lin Jing’s Flame Rare Beast, the Dragon Carp...” The Holy Son’s expression changed.

What the hell—after the turtle, and the spider, now even a carp wanted to fight him?

“Late-stage Nascent Soul? You think you’re worthy?” The Holy Son snapped.

A turtle that could control a quasi-immortal artifact was bad enough.

A Rare Gu that could command a peak Void Mystical demon ancestor’s corpse was worse.

And now, even a late-stage Nascent Soul carp wanted to beat him?

Originally just at mid-stage Nascent Soul, Dragon Carp had now broken through to late-stage by devouring a ton of cultivation pills.

Unfortunately, time was still a bit short—otherwise, it would have preferred to reach peak Nascent Soul before challenging the Holy Son.

Still, late-stage was good enough.

After all, it had spent a long time mastering control over its immortal flame.

“Crack...”

Facing the Holy Son’s contempt, Dragon Carp narrowed its eyes.

The pressure of its ancestral-level bloodline began to spread—just that alone already rivaled the Holy Son.

After all, even if the Holy Son kept progressing, Void Mystical peak was likely his ceiling. His chances of reaching Dao-Seeking were slim.

But ancestral-level bloodlines were destined to become Void Mystical demon ancestors…

And that was just the beginning.

From that bloodline emerged a top-tier beast flame—Dragon Scale Flame.

Then came Youmo, a top-tier ghost flame birthed through the slaughter of many Void Mystical, Nascent Soul Transformation, and countless Nascent Soul beings.

Then Zhushui Flame, a time-attribute beast flame, and Heavenly Thunder Flame, a lightning-based special flame.

Even scarier—the quasi-immortal fire from Burial Immortal Volcano, Solar Essence Flame, appeared.

And more powerful flames like Regenerative Lotus Heart Flame and Crystal Blaze appeared one after another, forming fireballs behind Dragon Carp.

The Holy Son’s expression instantly changed.

At this moment, any one of those flames alone could pose a threat to him.

Especially that quasi-immortal Solar Essence Flame, successfully refined with the help of the Demon Emperor—seeing it, the Holy Son was certain:

This carp really could challenge him across levels.

But what made him mentally collapse was that all those terrifying flames…

Were now merging.

Into one.

Releasing a wave of power that sent chills down his spine—this power was far more terrifying and pure than even the tribulation he had once seen the Wu God Hall’s Grand Elder undergo.

“An immortal-grade flame?”

In that instant, he finally understood why this carp, even at early-stage Nascent Soul, had once defeated the Sun Temple’s top disciple at full-stage Nascent Soul.

“Monster!” he cursed under his breath, turning and fleeing.

He’d rather be smashed by a quasi-immortal artifact or flattened by a demon ancestor’s corpse than be burned by an immortal-grade flame that should never have existed in the lower realm!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 91: The Collapsing Artifact Spirit
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 91: The Collapsing Artifact Spirit

Destiny Golden Ranking, ninth place: Dragon Carp.

Cultivation: Late-stage Nascent Soul.

Listing reason: Defeated the Holy Son of Wu God Hall, Ta Wu.

Not long after, Dragon Carp’s name successfully appeared on the Destiny Golden Ranking.

Seeing that both it and Ice Soul Gu defeated the same person, but its ranking was higher, Dragon Carp grinned so wide its mouth nearly split.

Meanwhile, Ice Soul Gu looked at the rankings like the sky had collapsed—no matter what, it wanted a solo rematch with Dragon Carp.

Lin Jing guessed that although they both defeated the same person, Dragon Carp had relied entirely on its own power without external help. Plus, with lower cultivation and pure Rare Beast bloodline, its destiny potential was greater.

Thus, it was ranked above Ice Soul Gu.

You had to understand—just recently, the Holy Son of Wu God Hall had been defeated by Ice Soul Gu.

And now, in a short time, he’d been soloed by Dragon Carp too?

And both defeats were cross-realm victories!

“No way…”

All the top talents on the continent were stunned. These were people ranked in the top ten of the Golden Ranking—how could they still be defeated by someone of lower cultivation?

Just what kind of monster was this Demon Court Holy Son?

In the Western Desert, at the Buried Immortal Volcano—

After Lin Jing and the others left, the Buddha Child of the Sun Temple had finally seized the right to cultivate there. He had just broken through to the early Nascent Soul Transformation Stage and was staring at the Golden Ranking, eyes blazing.

After his breakthrough, he had originally wanted to seek revenge on Dragon Carp. But now that Dragon Carp had even defeated the Holy Son of Wu God Hall, the Buddha Child took a deep breath—his confidence suddenly wavered.

After all, that immortal flame’s power was ridiculously insane.

“Better to challenge the Holy Son of Wu God Hall first. If I can beat him easily, then I’ll go after that carp.”

He made his decision.

Meanwhile, in Qingzhou, at the Demon Court—

Just out of seclusion, Xu Qingyao looked at the Golden Ranking and fell silent.

Three of the top ten spots were now taken by Lin Jing’s side.

Her own ranking had been pushed far down.

“That brat... pushing me out of the top ten was bad enough. I go into seclusion, and now I’m almost pushed out of the top fifteen?”

“And he even has so many pets making it onto the ranking on their own. That squirrel of his must be next!”

“At this rate, I might not even get into the Source Realm!”

Xu Qingyao had also successfully broken through to the Nascent Soul Transformation Stage.

This time, she had secluded herself for quite a while, because not only had she broken through, but each of her pets had also reached the Nascent Soul Transformation Stage at the same time.

Now, she alone equaled the combined power of several Nascent Soul experts.

Xu Qingyao was confident she could break into the Golden Ranking top ten.

However, seeing Lin Jing’s stunning performance, she couldn’t help turning to scold her own pets.

“Look at that! Other people’s pets can rank on their own. Why don’t you guys give it a try?”

Her pets all pulled bitter faces.

Even after reaching the Nascent Soul Transformation Stage, they still lacked confidence.

What a joke—none of them were Rare Beasts or had ancestral bloodlines.

Even at this stage, challenging someone like the Holy Son of Wu God Hall or the Totem Holy Son of the Snow Plains would only lead to disaster.

Those maniacs could shred ordinary peak demon lords with their bare hands…

“Master, let’s just forget it. If you want, better we all go in together.”

A pet gave a helpless suggestion.

...

“So... will I really get b*nished for this?”

Somewhere else, Cold Moon Sword Immortal had been making slow progress by comprehending the Dao-Seeking Sword Intent left behind by her master.

Her cultivation hadn’t yet reached full-stage Nascent Soul, nor had she finished condensing her Nascent Soul to compete for a spot in the Source Realm.

She looked at the Golden Ranking, practically ruined by Lin Jing, and smiled bitterly.

Knowing the second-generation Ancient Emperor well, she could already guess—if this continued, he was definitely going to step in.

Now that Dragon Carp had pushed the Holy Son out of the top ten, the Wu God could no longer sit still.

He had been hoping that the Holy Son would become the next Dao-Seeking Wu God after him!

And now he couldn’t even make it into the top ten of the ranking.

If he couldn’t enter the Source Realm either, what hope was there of reaching Dao-Seeking?

“Fine, fine, you wanna play like this?”

“Ancient Emperor, you tell me—how is it fair for ‘one person’ to occupy three spots?”

The Wu God questioned the second-generation Ancient Emperor.

At this moment, the Ancient Emperor looked at the Golden Ranking in silence, head aching.

Back then… why did he ever agree to let artifact spirits qualify, allowing pets to enter the ranking?

He’d never imagined Lin Jing’s pets would actually make it into the top ten solo.

If it were only top 20, that would’ve been fine. But now, with so many pets in the top ten, other Dao-Seeking factions whose interests were infringed would never let it slide.

“What’s unfair? The Destiny Golden Ranking is made from our Demon Court’s materials. The competition rules are right there—if you’re weak, just admit it,”

The Demon Emperor muttered when he saw the Wu God starting to lose his cool.

“Look at that old man from Tongtian Tower—none of his people made it into the top ten, and he’s not complaining.”

“Second-Gen, don’t you dare try to redistribute rankings based on tantrums. We agreed the strong would take the spots…”

Seeing the Wu God and Demon Emperor arguing, the other Dao-Seeking cultivators maintaining the realm barrier couldn’t help but chuckle—except the Tower Master of Tongtian Tower.

Seeing this, the Wu God turned his glare to one breathtaking female cultivator, dragging her into the mess.

“What the hell are you laughing at? That turtle that kidnapped my Holy Son is now heading straight for your Water Moon Immortal Island. You’ll be laughing when your top talent gets taken too!”

The Island Lord of Water Moon Immortal Island’s face changed.

She quickly cast her divine sense—and sure enough, found a turtle sneaking around their island’s perimeter, clearly trying to find a way in.

“Demon Court Lord…” she looked toward the Demon Emperor.

She remembered that Granny Yinshan had once sent a message saying that the Demon Court Holy Son had promised: if her disciple from Water Moon Immortal Island entered the Source Realm, the Demon Court would protect her safety there.

But she’d never imagined her disciple might not even qualify for the Source Realm now.

This was bad.

As a Dao-Seeking expert, she wasn’t naïve enough to believe the Demon Court would still uphold that promise once her disciple fell off the list.

The Demon Court wasn’t a charity babysitter.

All she could hope for now was that her disciple wouldn’t be solo-killed by a pet…

“Let the youngsters fight if they want to…”

“I remember the Demon Court Holy Son still has a squirrel pet, right?”

The Wu God gave a sly glance at the Island Lord.

“Enough.”

At this moment, even the second-generation Ancient Emperor realized the balance of the Golden Ranking had been tipped too far.

But if he tried to change the rules now, the Demon Court would definitely cause a scene.

“What did our Holy Son do wrong?”

“Maybe he just didn’t want to leave his pets behind if they couldn’t count as one entity in the Source Realm.”

“Look how hard he’s working. I’m touched!” the Demon Emperor sighed emotionally.

The Ancient Emperor rubbed his temples.

“Back then, I really didn’t consider whether beastmasters and their pets should count as a single entity for entering the Source Realm…”

“Since that’s the case, why don’t we let them test it at the Source Realm teleportation array?”

“We’ll see whether, when inside an inactive formation, they count as one entity or multiple. That way, we won’t be guessing anymore.”

“My guess is—it probably counts as a single entity.”

The Ancient Emperor was clearly exhausted.

Lin Jing was still in the Nascent Soul stage, yet with the bonus slot from his extreme path, he already had five contracted pets.

Counting himself, that was six potential slots.

If they all made it in, before the Demon Cult even invaded, the cultivation world of Tianyuan would erupt into civil war!

And the worst part—under the current rules, the Demon Court had done nothing wrong.

The Golden Ranking itself had been created from the Demon Court’s Golden Bell.

[Artifact Spirit, look what you've done. Think of a solution. Make a perfect rule that balances everyone.]

The second-generation Ancient Emperor sent this message to the sleeping artifact spirit—startling it awake in confusion.

“Uh... how am I supposed to do that? There’s no such thing as perfect fairness…”

The artifact spirit began frantically calculating, realizing it had planted the seed of this disaster the moment it allowed that exception long ago.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 92: Ancient Teleportation Array
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 92: Ancient Teleportation Array

“Lin Jing.”

In Qingzhou, at Immortal Prison Mountain.

Lin Jing was waiting for the Mechanized Turtle to return when he suddenly heard the Demon Emperor calling.

“Come do—”

“Is something the matter?” he tilted his head and asked.

“What do you think?” the Demon Emperor replied grumpily.

“Your actions have already been reported to this Emperor.”

“The second-generation Ancient Emperor wants to change the rules of the Golden Ranking…”

“Of course, he can change it however he wants, but our Demon Court isn’t about to take a loss,” the Demon Emperor said. “You’ve always worried that your pet beasts can’t be counted as a single entity with you when entering the Origin Realm, right?”

“The second-generation Ancient Emperor plans to take you to an inactive 'Ancient Origin Realm Teleportation Array' to test it out—see if you count as one quota or multiple. That way, they can adjust the rankings later.”

“Oh?” Lin Jing looked at the Tiger Fang in his hand, nodded, and said, “Sure.”

He couldn’t be at ease unless this issue was clarified.

...

Before long, Lin Jing and the others were sent through space by the Demon Emperor using a great divine power. Even the Mechanized Turtle, who had just found a chance to enter the Water Moon Immortal Island and was preparing to follow his master senior sister, was dumbfounded as it got sucked into a spatial vortex.

When Lin Jing and the others opened their eyes again, they were already in the ruins of an immortal palace. Ten sky-piercing pillars, each topped with a glowing orb, enclosed a ten-pointed star array.

The Demon Emperor and the second-generation Ancient Emperor each stood atop a pillar, gazing down at Lin Jing and the others who had just been teleported there.

“Your Majesty Demon Emperor, Your Majesty Ancient Emperor,” Lin Jing gave a slight bow, and the Pine Leaf Mouse, Dragon Carp, Ice Spirit Gu, and the Mechanized Turtle also mimicked a formal salute.

“So you are Lin Jing,” the second-generation Ancient Emperor scrutinized him.

“All right, let’s get on with the test,” the Demon Emperor urged.

The second-generation Ancient Emperor withdrew his gaze and gestured for Lin Jing and his pet beasts to enter the array.

Lin Jing looked toward the ancient teleportation array of the Origin Realm and stepped into it. The next moment, one of the ten orbs atop the sky-piercing pillars lit up.

“These ten Immortal Light Orbs represent the ten entry slots that the Tianyuan Realm can send into the Origin Realm. When the Origin Realm was first formed, Immortal Realm powerhouses set limits on how many entrants each lower realm could send. Weaker subordinate cultivation realms might get only two or three slots, while stronger ones might get over ten.”

“The Immortal Palace that once ruled Tianyuan wasn’t a top-tier force in the Immortal Realm, but it was first-class. That’s why Tianyuan got as many as ten slots.”

After Lin Jing stepped in, the Pine Leaf Mouse instantly teleported onto his shoulder.

At that moment, everyone looked at the Immortal Light Orbs and saw that only one orb remained lit—it hadn’t counted Lin Jing and the Pine Leaf Mouse as separate entities!

“Just as expected!” The second-generation Ancient Emperor let out a breath of relief, and so did the Demon Emperor.

Next, the Dragon Carp and the Ice Spirit Gu entered the array one after another, yet no new orbs lit up. Clearly, like the Pine Leaf Mouse, they were counted as part of Lin Jing.

Until...

The Mechanized Turtle cheerfully climbed onto the array—only to be blasted out by a flash of light, rolling a great distance away.

Lin Jing and the others looked at the Mechanized Turtle: “...”

“Huh?” The Mechanized Turtle rolled over and stood up, glaring at the Origin Realm teleportation array.

The second-generation Ancient Emperor spoke: “Looks like the Origin Realm’s rules set by the Immortal Realm recognize that cultivators who practice the 'Beast Taming Technique' and their pet beasts are a single entity. However, this turtle—an artifact spirit that was sealed from the Void Mystical Stage down to the Nascent Soul Transformation Stage for the contract—was still seen through by the Origin Realm’s rules.”

“In the Origin Realm, only lifeforms from the Nascent Soul to Divine Transformation stages are allowed in. Even though its cultivation was perfectly sealed to deceive technique-based restrictions, it still couldn’t fool the Origin Realm’s rules.”

“What a pity.” Lin Jing looked at the Mechanized Turtle. Indeed, its core cultivation was Void Mystical Stage, making it ineligible for entry.

So, only he and the Pine Leaf Mouse, Dragon Carp, and Ice Spirit Gu could enter the Origin Realm. The Mechanized Turtle would have to remain in Tianyuan?

That would definitely weaken his battle power significantly.

Among all his quasi-immortal weapons, only the bell gifted by the Demon Emperor could barely be activated through the Golden Light Gu and the Divine Tiger Clan’s special secret arts. The others would be unusable.

“If the little turtle can’t go, then so be it. It’ll stay with me to guard the Demon Court,” the Demon Emperor said with a smile. Having Lin Jing and his pets count as one was already a good outcome.

As for this turtle that could fully power an immortal weapon, the Demon Emperor didn’t want to let it go anyway.

“Guard the Demon Court? I want to travel,” the Mechanized Turtle muttered. Since it couldn’t go to the Origin Realm, it might as well tour the Tianyuan Realm... Without Lin Jing to restrain it, the freedom was unimaginable.

“Lord of the Demon Court, Holy Son of the Demon Court,” the second-generation Ancient Emperor suddenly said.

“Since beast tamers and their pet beasts can be counted as one, I want to revise the Golden Ranking rules: Beast Tamers and their pets will share a single ranking and won’t be listed separately… Additionally, pet beasts will enjoy the same fortune bonuses as their Beast Tamer.”

“Are you satisfied with that? This way, the Demon Court doesn’t lose out. In fact, the pets will benefit even more from the Tamer’s highest ranking bonuses.”

“Is that possible?” The Demon Court Lord’s eyes lit up.

“If we manage to hold onto the sixth spot in the Golden Ranking, it’s doable,” said the second-generation Ancient Emperor. “The current fortune ranking system uses all mobilizable worldly fortune of the Tianyuan Realm to the fullest. But I can push it even further—at the cost of shortening the Golden Era by a few dozen to a hundred years.”

“Fine, let’s do that then. I’m not blind to your struggles,” said the Demon Emperor with a generous wave of his hand.

Then, he looked at Lin Jing and asked, “What do you think about this change?”

“The key point is that activating the Origin Realm teleportation array requires several Seeking the Way-level experts working together. If the Ancient Kingdom and Demon Court hog all ten spots, none of the other Seeking the Way forces will be willing to help you nurture talents. Even if you make it to the top ten of the Golden Ranking, you won’t get into the Origin Realm.”

“That’s likely the second-generation Ancient Emperor’s concern.”

Lin Jing nodded and cupped his fists. “I’ll follow Your Majesty’s arrangements.”

He didn’t really mind, as long as he wasn’t on the losing end.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 93: The Fifth Pet Beast
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 93: The Fifth Pet Beast

After discussion, the second-generation Ancient Emperor decided to use great power to revise the rules of the Fortune Golden Ranking.

Just as the Demon Court Lord was about to temporarily send Lin Jing away, Lin Jing hesitated and quickly sent a voice transmission to the Demon Emperor,

“Your Majesty, Your Majesty, the top-grade cultivation dragon vein that the second-generation Ancient Emperor promised me—he hasn’t given it yet.”

“Hasn’t given it yet?” the Demon Emperor questioned the second-generation Ancient Emperor.

After a brief silence, the second-generation Ancient Emperor said, “Extracting a top-grade cultivation dragon vein deals considerable damage to national fortune. It requires extremely cautious handling. I promise to deliver the dragon vein within half a year.”

“As for the True Dragon Secret Arts’ Immortal Body manual, that must wait until the ten-year agreement is up—only then, before he enters the Origin Realm, can it be handed over.”

The second-generation Ancient Emperor worried that if Lin Jing got the Immortal Body manual early, the Demon Court might flip the script and compete with the Ancient Emperor’s prince and princess for the world’s fortune by cultivating this immortal body.

But if the prince and princess of the Ancient Clan had already completed the Immortal Body and entered the Origin Realm, it wouldn’t matter if Lin Jing got the manual then.

However, what the second-generation Ancient Emperor didn’t expect was that Lin Jing suddenly shook his head and said, “Your Majesty Ancient Emperor, I’ve suddenly lost interest in the True Dragon Secret Arts’ Immortal Body manual. May I trade this deal for something else instead?”

“What do you want instead?” the second-generation Ancient Emperor’s expression didn’t change.

Truthfully, Lin Jing no longer wanted the Immortal Body manual—what a joke. The actual creator of that manual was right next to his avatar;
why bother trading with the second-generation Ancient Emperor?

All he needed to do was speak a few ill words about the second-generation Ancient Emperor in front of the first-generation Ancient Emperor, curry a bit of favor, and getting the complete True Dragon Secret Arts wouldn’t be out of the question.

After all, the first-generation Ancient Emperor didn’t seem to hold much regard for the concept of “Ancient Clans” and didn’t think only Ancient Clan cultivators could cultivate the True Dragon Secret Arts.

Of course, considering the various backlash issues when taking the arts beyond the Tianyuan Realm, Lin Jing wasn’t necessarily interested in cultivating it at all.

“One of the Demon Extermination Division’s leaders, Liu Gongzhuo’s Heavenly Flame Gu.”

“And, I want all high-security prisoners above the Nascent Soul Transformation Stage held by the Ancient Kingdom.”

The second-generation Ancient Emperor didn’t find Lin Jing’s request excessive. He knew Lin Jing was collecting rare Gu. Even if Lin Jing collected all ten Great Rare Gu, he didn’t think much of it.

The fusion of the ten Rare Gu was merely additive;
it wouldn’t cause any qualitative leap—at most, it would boost the individual Gu from standard Void Mystical Stage potential to half-step Seeking the Way level.

To the Ancient Emperor, that was nothing. Even the arrival of the Gu God wouldn’t impress him.

As for Liu Gongzhuo, it wouldn’t be difficult to obtain the Heavenly Flame Gu—just compensate with equivalent or superior resources.

However, the second-generation Ancient Emperor couldn’t understand why Lin Jing wanted so many high-security prisoners.

Was he cultivating demonic arts?

“Fine.” In the end, the second-generation Ancient Emperor still agreed to the new trade. Compared to the Immortal Body manual, these requests were insignificant. In fact, if possible, the second-generation Ancient Emperor would prefer Lin Jing never get the chance to cultivate the Immortal Body at all, even if the chance was slim.

After all, no one was more suited for the True Dragon Secret Arts than the Ancient Clan. Even if Lin Jing cultivated it, the progress wouldn’t be great.

As for why Lin Jing wanted the Ancient Kingdom’s prisoners—it was mainly because those held by the Demon Court had nearly all been refined by the Dragon Carp.

The Golden Core cultivators were nearly depleted.

Yet his own and his pet beasts’ cultivation still had much room for growth.

So when he obtained a new batch of criminals, he’d sweep through them with his Heavenly Demon Divine Sense—those who were truly vile and unforgivable would be refined on the spot.

As for those falsely accused or whose crimes weren’t severe enough to warrant death, he’d recruit them into the demon-catching squad to serve him and collect more pill materials as repayment for their rescue.

Originally, Lin Jing didn’t think many would qualify—but after what his avatar saw with Gao Sheng and the first-generation side, he’d changed his mind.

Some local officials, eager to close cases quickly, would use large-scale memory-altering spells to rewrite rogue cultivators’ memories and coerce them into confessing to crimes, branding them as demonic cultivators.

Although cases above the Nascent Soul Transformation level should be handled directly by the Demon Extermination Division headquarters and shouldn’t be that sloppy—who could really say? Lin Jing trusted his Heavenly Demon Divine Sense more, which could peer into the depths of a person’s mind.

Soon after, Lin Jing was sent back to Qingzhou by the Demon Emperor. At the same time, the rankings on the Fortune Golden Ranking changed slightly.

The names of the Dragon Carp and Ice Spirit Gu disappeared from the top ten—vanished from the rankings. The two who had been bumped out before, the Totem Holy Son of the Snowfield and the Holy Son of the Wu God Hall, returned to the top ten.

“Good, good, good...” The Wu God Hall’s Holy Son, with a snot-covered face, wiped his nose as he rushed through the Cloud Sea Mountain Range. This was the one time he truly supported the second-generation Ancient Emperor. Looks like that shameless move by the Demon Court’s Holy Son had finally been shut down.

Many cultivators believed it was the second-generation Ancient Emperor who acted, banning the practice of listing pet beasts and beast tamers separately, thereby punishing Lin Jing. But in reality, Lin Jing had gained far more.

Though they were removed from the list, the Dragon Carp and Ice Spirit Gu now received even greater Enlightenment bonuses—almost identical to the sixth place on the Golden Ranking, which was Lin Jing’s position.

Even the Pine Leaf Mouse, who hadn’t appeared on the ranking, now enjoyed the sixth-place bonus.

And even the Mechanized Turtle—being an artificial lifeform and ineligible for the ranking—received the fortune bonus thanks to its contract with Lin Jing.

“In other words, all my contract beasts now get the sixth-place bonus, regardless of race or stage. Not bad at all,” Lin Jing licked his lips.

“In that case, the most cost-effective move now is to quickly contract a ‘fifth’ contract beast.”

He had just broken through to the Nascent Soul stage and gained another contract slot.

If he used it quickly, the new pet beast could enjoy nearly ten years of Enlightenment fortune bonus—and could travel to the Origin Realm with him.

Originally, Lin Jing had thought about waiting until he reached the Origin Realm to see if any special pet beasts were worth contracting. But now, he decided it’d be better to use the slot while still in the Tianyuan Realm.

He began thinking carefully—what beast would be suitable...

“A Celestial Beast corpse? If I could contract one, it’d definitely make commanding it easier in the future.”

“No good. Even if it’s a corpse, it’s still a Celestial Beast—too high in rank to contract. And it’s dead;
it can’t gain Enlightenment. A waste of a slot.”

“My external avatar? I could contract that!”

“But that also feels like a waste. After all, an external avatar can already enjoy my fortune bonus and enter the Origin Realm with me without taking up a contract slot. Even if I did contract it, now’s not the time.”

“A public auction? There must be Seeking the Way forces who can’t enter the Origin Realm—like Tongtian Tower. If they sent someone to contract with me, they could easily gain entry. But Tongtian Tower only has a single Celestial Pill worth contracting...”

“Wait, if I contract my senior sister Xu Qingyao, then have her cultivate an improved Beast Control Technique and contract someone from the Beast Master Sect... and repeat the process—wouldn’t I be able to bring the entire Beast Master Sect into the Origin Realm?”

But Lin Jing suspected that if he tried that, the will of the Origin Realm might blacklist him—and he’d be forced to wander the stars with the whole Beast Master Sect.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 94: Taking a Disciple?
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 94: Taking a Disciple?

Lin Jing felt this kind of nesting contract scheme was truly terrifying!

But the key problem was—though Senior Sister was a half-demon, she most likely wouldn’t allow him to contract her. Besides, he also found the idea rather weird.

However, once the thought emerged, Lin Jing became obsessed with trying it out.

Let one cultivator practicing the Beast Control Technique contract another who also practiced the Beast Control Technique, then let the contracted one go on to contract another... and repeat the process.

In this way, based on the feedback effect of Beast Control cultivation, as long as the last person in the chain made a breakthrough, all those “upstream” from them—possibly dozens—would likely receive feedback as well. The efficiency would be insane.

“Since I already shared the ‘beast control’ portion of the revised technique with the Beast Master Sect, I might as well share the ‘human control’ part too.”

“Create a ‘Human Master Sect,’ let it develop quietly, and observe the results and potential issues. If everything checks out, I can reserve a future contract slot specifically for this kind of operation.”

After deciding, Lin Jing was ready to act.

Passing on a technique like this was no different from accepting disciples—so he had to be very selective about the candidate.

“Find the First Emperor...”

“His Qi-Watching and Talent-Reading Technique is unmatched. If Mr. Cao of the Academy saw it, he’d kneel down and call him ancestor.”

On Lin Jing’s avatar side:

After a period of cultivation, the avatar—who focused on the Heavenly Demon path—had broken through to the early Golden Core stage.

At the moment, the avatar, the First Emperor, and Gao Sheng were walking along a country road toward the next city. Lin Jing suddenly asked, “Senior, could you teach me your Qi-Watching and Talent-Reading Technique?”

“Huh?” The First Generation Ancient Emperor blinked, looking at Lin Jing with suspicion. “I could teach you other things, but this one—I definitely can’t.”

“This is the very foundation upon which the Tianyuan Ancient Kingdom was built. If your Demon Court learns it, won’t all the talents of the world end up in your pocket?”

Lin Jing pouted and said, “But the second-generation Ancient Emperor didn’t inherit your talent-recognition technique, and the Ancient Kingdom is still doing pretty well, isn’t it?”

The First Emperor’s nose twitched. Lin Jing wasn’t wrong. Although he had seen many outrageous things along the way, the Ancient Kingdom’s strength was steadily growing—even more prosperous than during his own reign.

“That’s only because I laid down an indestructible foundation. If it were all squandered in a few thousand years, now that would be outrageous.”

“Look, the Ancient Kingdom is still thriving for now, but keep this up for a few more millennia and the cracks will start to show. By then, it’ll be too late to fix anything.”

“In any case, that secret technique is off-limits. But, if you tell me your goal—what kind of talent you’re looking for—just one or two people, I can help you find them and point you in the right direction.”

The First Emperor grumbled on. Even if Lin Jing wanted to learn the Heavenly Demon techniques, he wouldn’t care. After all, that didn’t affect the Ancient Kingdom’s interests.

But his talent-recognition technique? Absolutely not.

Back then, he had used that exact technique to unearth a bunch of grassroots geniuses—who later cultivated to the Void Mystical Stage. They became his most trusted followers in establishing the Ancient Kingdom and overthrowing the Immortal Alliance.

“Fine, forget about the recognition technique. How about this instead—Your Majesty gives me the True Dragon Secret Arts’ Immortal Body manual?”

“That one’s got problems.” The First Emperor grinned, showing his teeth. “Besides, looks like you’re about to vanish. When my descendant summoned your projection last time, you should’ve taught him then—only if he can keep you stable in the physical world for at least six months.”

“When that time comes, this Emperor will go find that rascal descendant of mine and settle the score.”

“Got it.” Lin Jing nodded.

“What’s this Immortal Body thing? Master, you want to learn that too?” Gao Sheng, who had been standing by, suddenly lit up with interest.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 95: Nascent Soul Cave Heaven
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 95: Nascent Soul Cave Heaven

“Senior, this isn’t what you just said would happen.”

Lin Jing’s mouth twitched. If it were just the Eternal Life Constitution, the First Ancient Emperor wouldn’t have reacted like that. There must be something more in the bloodline the other party detected—something hidden beyond just the Eternal Life Constitution.

“Ahaha, kid, you know how fast someone with the Eternal Life Constitution can cultivate. Forget it, maybe my powers have weakened too much and I made a mistake in observation. When I awaken again, I’ll do another reading.”

“When that time comes, I’ll definitely find you a disciple with talent rivaling an immortal body and potential for Seeking the Way.”

After he finished speaking, the image of the First Ancient Emperor vanished due to the strain of the observation, leaving Lin Jing and Gao Sheng looking at each other.

The First Ancient Emperor’s nervous expression was written all over his face.

What exactly did he see?

“Brother Lin, Master just...”

“Once you’ve got the dragon veins, you can come find me at Ling Dao Sect. By then, I should be cultivating there using the power of the Dao-Comprehending Fish.”

“Alright… but are you planning to look for the person Master observed just now?” Gao Sheng asked.

“Whatever made the First Ancient Emperor react that way, refusing to let me take that person as a disciple, really got my curiosity going. I don’t know the exact details or location, but just knowing they have the Eternal Life Constitution is probably enough.”

“When it comes to detecting cultivation physiques in this world, aside from the First Ancient Emperor, the Yin Household Sect was unrivaled. When it was destroyed, the Demon Court seized a huge amount of their secret arts. Next, I’ll cultivate those Yin Household techniques and use my blood to search for cultivators with similar constitutions.”

“Even if I don’t take them as a disciple, leaving someone with the Eternal Life Constitution wandering outside is too dangerous. I’d better bring them back to the Demon Court and protect them properly,” Lin Jing said. Gao Sheng nodded—he really didn’t think the First Ancient Emperor needed to be that anxious.

Who could it possibly be, that even Lin Jing wasn’t allowed to take them as a disciple? That’s a bit much.

...

After Lin Jing’s avatar parted ways with Gao Sheng, he headed toward Ling Dao Sect, while his real body also set off on the path from Qingzhou back to the ancient kingdom’s Ling Dao Sect, planning to reunite with his avatar.

Regarding the matter of the fifth contracted beast, after seeing the First Ancient Emperor’s abilities, Lin Jing finally made his decision: he would still rely on the elder’s power, have him observe the fate of the world, and see what sort of beast would be best suited for him.

However, before Lin Jing’s real body even left the boundaries of Qingzhou, something that was both expected and unexpected came looking for him again.

While riding the Mechanized Turtle and flashing through the void, Lin Jing suddenly looked up mid-teleport—only to see a massive pillar of light descending from the sky.

“It’s arriving now?”

“A cave heaven…”

It was indeed a cave heaven. As expected, one descended right after Lin Jing broke through to a new realm. Lin Jing smiled. He didn’t exactly like these unplanned events, but since the Immortal Camp’s cave heaven insisted on gifting him an opportunity, he couldn’t refuse.

“I wonder if you…” Lin Jing had just started to ask if the Mechanized Turtle could enter, when before he could finish his sentence, he and the other contracted beasts were pulled into the cave heaven. Meanwhile, the Mechanized Turtle was directly bounced off by the cave heaven’s pillar of light, looking utterly confused.

“D*mn…” After being flung away, the Mechanized Turtle vaguely heard the laughter of a koi fish, as if it were saying: finally, someone else missed out on a lucky chance.

That koi fish—Koi Little Emperor—had finally found a “predecessor” who wasn’t a turtle.

At the same time, across the Tianyuan Minor Land, seven Nascent Soul-stage prodigies were also chosen by the cave heaven.

In the ancient kingdom, inside the Heavenly Sword Sect, just as Cold Moon Sword Immortal Feng Ning returned to the sect to prepare for her breakthrough into Divine Transformation, she suddenly looked up and was sucked into the cave entrance.

“I thought I wouldn’t encounter a cave heaven opportunity until after the Nascent Soul stage. So it’s finally here.”

In the ancient domain, Tianyuan Ancient City, at the Eighth Prince’s residence, the prince—at half a step into Divine Transformation—also looked up and responded to the cave heaven’s summons, voluntarily stepping into it.

“A cave heaven, a fortune of the Immortal Palace… I wonder if this time, I can change my fate and make Father look at me instead of just my older brother.”

Elsewhere in Qingzhou, at the Tongtian Tower, the registered disciple of the Tower Master—also the most skilled prodigy of the tower, Du Zao—looked up in surprise at the light from the cave heaven.

“This is... Maybe I can use this opportunity to gain even greater strength! Breaking through to Divine Transformation isn’t hard for me, but surpassing those freaks at the same level—that’s what’s really difficult!”

The last one was also from the Tianyuan Ancient Kingdom.

In the greater domain, a white-clothed girl walking among the clouds saw the light of the cave heaven and rubbed her eyes.

“Whoa, can’t believe it! I, Bai Yuran, just broke through to Nascent Soul and already get a chance to enter a Nascent Soul-level cave heaven…”

Five beams of cave heaven light fell upon the ancient kingdom and Qingzhou, sweeping up five prodigies from these two great fortune-rich regions.

A moment later.

At one side of a massive arena, the figures of Lin Jing and the other four slowly materialized. Inside this cave heaven, aside from the arena, there was nothing else.

At that moment, the five of them looked at each other—Lin Jing was surprised, Cold Moon Sword Immortal was shocked, the Third Prince and Du Zao both had dark expressions, and the young junior sister from the Heavenly Secrets Pavilion, Bai Yuran, was simply stunned.

Lin Jing and Cold Moon Sword Immortal were surprised to see each other here. The Third Prince and Du Zao were annoyed that they’d been matched with Lin Jing. Only Bai Yuran, although shocked to see these people here, was even more shocked by the cave heaven itself...

Before the cave heaven could even introduce its rules, Bai Yuran exclaimed in surprise, “The Twin-Realm Cave Heaven?! This cave heaven hasn’t appeared in over ten thousand years…”

“But it’s fine… since it’s you guys, we’re in good hands.”

“Everyone, don’t worry! This cave heaven doesn’t require us to compete against each other!”

“According to the records from the Heavenly Secrets Pavilion, in the Immortal Palace’s cave heavens, there is one particularly unique cave heaven that connects two realms!”

“As everyone knows, Tianyuan Realm is a subordinate cultivation realm under the Immortal Palace, but very few people are aware that the Immortal Palace actually governs two subordinate realms. There’s another one called the Tianming Realm, also under the Immortal Palace’s control.”

“It’s said that during the Immortal Palace era, the two cultivation realms used to communicate often. But after the Immortal Camp era ended, they lost all contact. The only opportunity for interaction now—is through this cave heaven. Among all the cave heavens, this one connects both realms, creating a two-sided contest where each realm becomes a team. The prodigies of both realms compete, and the victorious side earns all the opportunities within the cave heaven! The losing side gets punished.”

“But this cross-realm cave heaven rarely appears. According to the records, the last time it showed up was tens of thousands of years ago. I really didn’t expect… wow…”

“Twin-Realm Cave Heaven?” Everyone else was shocked too. At that moment, on the other side of the arena, the silhouettes of five Nascent Soul-stage prodigies appeared.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 96: Servant’s Cultivation Technique
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 96: Servant’s Cultivation Technique

“...This cave heaven—it hasn’t appeared in a long time, has it?”

“According to the records, ever since something happened in the Immortal Realm, communication between the two realms’ Immortal Palaces was completely severed.”

“Yeah. Since we’re matched with the Tianyuan Realm, we should take this chance to exchange some intel. But unfortunately, the Twin-Realm Cave Heaven comes with a punishment system for the losing side—each person loses one minor realm. Otherwise, with such a rare opportunity to make contact, the outcome wouldn’t matter as much.”

On the Tianming Realm side, five cultivators—three men and two women—also appeared.

What stood out was that they were all dressed uniformly in high-grade Dao robes of azure and white, embroidered with patterns of the sun, moon, and stars.

As the two sides saw each other, a voice from the Immortal Palace echoed in their ears.

【Rules of the Twin-Realm Cave Heaven Battle: There will be a total of five matches. Each realm must send out one person per match. No one may fight twice.】

【Each match grants points. Matches 1 to 3 award 2 points each, the 4th match awards 3 points, and the final match awards 4 points. The realm with the highest total points after all five matches wins.】

【The Divine Physique Pill is refined by Immortal Palace alchemists using the origin blood of various races. Upon consumption, it grants a non-conflicting acquired divine physique.】

【The five members of the losing realm will each drop one minor realm in cultivation.】

【The first match will begin in three minutes!】

“A point system!”

Although both sides wanted to talk, the cave heaven battle naturally had to take priority.

The point system meant the realms couldn’t rely on a single powerful genius to sweep the match.

Every participant had to contribute.

“Two, two, two, three, four—that’s a total of thirteen points. We need at least seven points to win.”

“The three- and four-point matches are the most crucial. Who’s taking those?”

From the Tianyuan Realm side, Bai Yuran spoke up: “Holy Son, Sword Immortal Leng Yue, Your Highness the Third Prince—it’s great that you’re all here. You know I’m not good at fighting. I’ll take one of the two-point matches. I’ll leave the more important ones to you!”

“I just broke through to Nascent Soul. Whether or not I get a reward, I really don’t want to drop a realm…”

“Hey, hey, hey.” Alchemist Du Zao felt completely ignored and couldn’t help but assert his presence.

“This shouldn’t be up for debate. Let him take the four-point match—that is, the final one. He should be able to secure it for the Tianyuan Realm,” Cold Moon Sword Immortal looked at Lin Jing. No one at the scene objected to her suggestion.

Bai Yuran glanced at Lin Jing and pouted. That guy had promised to team up with her before leaving the Heavenly Secrets City, but by the time she came out of seclusion, he had already become the Holy Son of the Demon Court. The gap between them—human and demon—felt vast now. Still, no one on the Tianyuan Continent would doubt Lin Jing’s strength.

Although he appeared to be only at the early Nascent Soul stage, with his Heavenly Demon Physique and Beast Taming Path, even Divine Transformation prodigies on the continent would be shaken by his true power.

Whether Lin Jing could handle a seven-point match—both the Eighth Prince and Du Zao believed that among the seven present, he was definitely the one who could.

“Let Brother Lin take the seven-point match then. What do you think, Brother Lin? Or… do you plan to use some tactics and forfeit the highest value match? After all, we don’t know what the Tianming Realm’s strength looks like.”

The Eighth Prince furrowed his brow. “Their robes are those worn by ancient Immortal Palace disciples. That implies that before losing contact with the Immortal Realm, the Tianming Realm was still fully under the Upper Realm’s Immortal Palace control. That Immortal Palace likely wasn’t overthrown, meaning their various secret techniques and inheritances should be very well preserved. I expect... they’ll be extremely tough to deal with.”

“You sure about this? If you take the seven-point match, and they lose the rest, it could all come down to you,” Lin Jing said hesitantly.

He wasn’t sure if his spirit beasts could substitute in for the Tongtian Tower’s alchemist.

A reward like an acquired divine physique was already very good. Lin Jing didn’t currently lack a divine physique, but honestly, who would turn down another one?

Of course, the main thing was—he didn’t want to drop a realm either.

“No need to worry.” Cold Moon Sword Immortal gave Lin Jing a glance. “Don’t you trust me? You take the four-point match, I’ll take the three-point one. With the two of us, those seven points are secured. We don’t need to worry about the others.”

“Even if they lose, it won’t matter.”

“…That said, His Highness the Third Prince should still be quite strong, right?” Bai Yuran chuckled.

The Third Prince of the ancient kingdom might not be as famous or naturally talented as the Crown Prince or the Ancient Emperor’s heir, but among the younger generation born after the decision to produce descendants, he was considered a standout.

Plus, the Ancient Emperor hadn’t been stingy with resources for the royal clan.

“I’ll win too,” the Third Prince said calmly.

“Can you guys stop ignoring me? Just because I’m an alchemist doesn’t mean I’m weak, alright? I’m ranked 19th on the Golden Ranking!” Du Zao said, exasperated.

He was the final champion of a certain world’s alchemy conference and was taken in as a registered disciple of the Tower Master for his talent in the Dao of Alchemy… but in terms of regular combat, it was clear no one here trusted him.

“I’ll trust you guys this once.” Lin Jing looked at Bai Yuran. “It’s been a while. Do you think this setup will work? Can you foresee the final outcome?”

Bai Yuran smiled bitterly. “Don’t make it hard for me. Inside this cave heaven, my abilities are greatly limited. But I think... it should be fine.”

“I’ll take the first match. I can’t guarantee a win, but I’ll do my best to gather intel for the rest of you.”

Everyone exchanged glances and then nodded.

“We’re counting on you, Master Bai,” the Third Prince smiled gently. He already sensed that the junior sister from the Heavenly Secrets Pavilion might be planning to use that secret technique.

Soon, the three-minute countdown ended.

From the Tianyuan side, Bai Yuran of the Heavenly Secrets Pavilion stepped forward. She was at the early Nascent Soul stage.

From the Tianming side, a cute and innocent-looking female cultivator stepped forward as well—mid Nascent Soul stage.

“Hello there, cultivator of the Tianyuan Realm.” The Tianming girl smiled and spoke. “How should I address you? I’m Yue E, a cultivator from the Immortal Palace of the Tianming Realm.”

“Bai Yuran, cultivator of the Heavenly Secrets Pavilion, Tianyuan Ancient Kingdom.” The junior sister from the Heavenly Secrets Pavilion smiled slightly in return. After her words, faint doubts stirred among the Tianming side.

Could it be that the Tianyuan Realm’s Immortal Palace suffered some kind of mishap and lost its authority? They even allowed their cultivators to form factions based on national identity...

Among the five of them, it seemed only one was cultivating an Immortal Palace technique—that young man in green. He must be practicing the Immortal Palace’s Human Control Technique. As for the others, they didn’t seem to be from the Immortal Camp at all.

Among the other four Tianming cultivators, a refined, scholarly youth spoke up.

“Human Control Technique? Isn’t that the technique used by servant disciples who raise spiritual beasts?” a short youth muttered.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 97: Tianyuan vs. Tianming
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 97: Tianyuan vs. Tianming

“It's rare for cultivators from both realms to have a chance to meet. Fellow Daoist, how about exchanging some intel before we start the match?”

Yue E from the Tianming Realm continued to smile as she looked at Bai Yuran. “There’s no time limit for the match anyway, so why not make the most of it?”

Bai Yuran gently shook her head. “If you truly wanted to exchange intel and information, that would be fine. But if I’m not mistaken, you cultivate the sound-based technique ‘Nine Arts of Immortal Melody’, right? One of the arts states that as long as the enemy hears your voice, they’re already ensnared. Even ordinary conversation plants a ‘sound curse’ in my body. Once you choose to trigger it, it can cause Immortal Melody shockwaves inside me, pushing me into qi deviation.”

“The more I hear your voice, the stronger the backlash will be…”

“Under those circumstances, I really don’t dare chat with you too much.”

The female cultivator Yue E from the Tianming Realm showed a flash of surprise—she hadn’t expected this unassuming girl from the Tianyuan Realm to see through her technique.

“No, that’s impossible. ‘Nine Arts of Immortal Melody’ was created only after the Tianming Realm lost contact with the Immortal Realm. It was first developed here in the Tianming Realm. How could you know it so well…”

Bai Yuran smiled faintly. “Even the secrets of heaven can be revealed—what’s a little secret like yours in comparison? Your strength hasn’t reached the level where I can’t see through you.”

“So what?” Yue E sneered. Even if the girl from Tianyuan had seen through her cultivation method, it was too late now. In the next moment, waves of Immortal Melody exploded within Bai Yuran’s body, causing her expression to twist as she coughed up blood. Her spiritual power surged out of control, and it looked like she might explode at any moment.

But at the same time, Yue E’s face also changed—her gaze turned slightly unfocused.

As Bai Yuran endured the torment from the Immortal Melody within her, she spoke: “What a pity. The moment you looked into my eyes, I also activated a Heavenly Secrets Technique. Not only did I uncover all your cultivation methods, arts, and techniques, but if I want, I can also force you to reveal other secrets you know.”

“Tell me the intel on the other four members of the Tianming Realm.”

Neither side had expected the first match to evolve like this.

Another female cultivator from the Tianming Realm frowned deeply. “From the situation, it looks like Tianyuan lost this match… but it seems that wasn’t their goal to begin with.”

All the Tianming cultivators furrowed their brows—they realized something was off.

“What is this?” Du Zao from Tongtian Tower looked shocked.

The Third Prince took a deep breath and explained, “It’s one of the Heavenly Secrets Techniques from the Heavenly Secrets Pavilion. It can quietly probe another cultivator’s techniques and arts. It can also, through a huge expenditure of spiritual power, force the opponent to reveal confidential information. But it has its flaws—it consumes a lot of energy and can’t actually inflict physical damage.”

“Junior Sister Bai probably knew she couldn’t beat her opponent, so she used this method to make her loss more valuable. If we can get intel on the other four Tianming cultivators, then our chances of winning… increase significantly.”

As the Third Prince explained, Lin Jing looked over at Bai Yuran in surprise. She’s improved a lot. But he figured her Heavenly Secrets technique wouldn’t work on him.

After all, he had two layers of solid defense: the first was his Heavenly Demon Divine Sense, which constantly enveloped his surroundings, blocking all mental and spiritual intrusions.

The second was the Spell Spirit: Illusion Mushroom fused into his body. With it, Lin Jing’s form was in constant flux—ordinary means couldn’t reveal his true self.

Unless, of course, it was something he didn’t mind others seeing.

Like earlier, when Hong Qing tried to sense his spiritual energy. When Lin Jing noticed the probing, he didn’t bother resisting, letting the other party “detect” his energy as Beast Taming True Essence.

To Hong Qing, that was a vital piece of intel.

But something like the Eternal Life Constitution—an identity he had always carefully hidden—couldn’t be uncovered by simple detection methods. Before entering the Origin Realm, Lin Jing had gone to great lengths to ensure that he wouldn’t become an easy target for the demon clans of other realms.

“…Understood.”

At that moment, under the compulsion of the Heavenly Secrets technique, Yue E spoke with a trance-like gaze and revealed the information about the other seven members from her side.

“Besides you, the other seven are also cultivators of the Immortal Palace. The man named Tian Lian, mid Nascent Soul stage, is a core disciple of the Immortal Palace. He has the Heaven’s Mercy Physique, which is loved by the heavens and will never suffer heavenly tribulation. His technique is Heaven’s Extinction Divine Art, which allows him to temporarily raise his cultivation to mid Divine Transformation. However, the ability can only be used under punishment from the heavens, and he can’t use any shortcuts or boosts—his combat strength is quite weak.”

“The refined-looking one is named Yuan Liu. Early Nascent Soul stage, Immortal Palace core disciple, has the Innate Void Divine Physique, which renders all mana-based attacks ineffective—he can only be harmed by pure physical damage. He cultivates the Minor Void Technique, a space-attribute technique that lets him master teleportation, spatial concealment, and spatial strikes.”

“The short, stout one is named Zhuo Mu. He’s a rare beast in human form and a core disciple of the Immortal Palace. Peak Nascent Soul cultivation, emperor-rank peak bloodline, capable of assuming the form of a True Beast Emperor. His bloodline rivals ancestral-level beasts. His beast technique Heaven Peck is highly lethal and causes unhealable, worsening wounds that even high-level healing spells can’t treat.”

“The one meditating with his eyes closed… is the top Nascent Soul cultivator of the Tianming Immortal Palace. He possesses dual innate physiques: the Innate Dao Physique and the Innate Lunar Physique, and his true form is actually a Solar Physique. He cultivates the top-tier immortal art Four Yin Four Yang, which allows him to manifest two Spell Spirits and control two top-grade magical treasures simultaneously.”

“His strength has been evaluated by the Immortal Palace’s Vice Palace Lord as being unrivaled among those of the same realm—even immortal physiques can’t match him.”

With each piece of intel Bai Yuran extracted, her expression grew more serious.

Every single one of the Tianming Realm’s chosen five—besides the one she faced—was incredibly strong.

“This is going to be a problem…” Someone on the Tianming side sighed, shaking their head. All this intel—just spilled like that. They had underestimated the Tianyuan Realm.

Still, they weren’t overly worried. Luckily, Yue E didn’t know their deepest secrets—just the surface-level info…

“Tianyuan Realm must have lost the Immortal Realm’s inheritance. Otherwise, how could only one of their five people be cultivating an Immortal Camp technique—and even then, just a servant’s technique? A realm that’s lost its connection to the Immortal Realm must have fallen significantly.

“Even though Bai Yuran’s technique is strange, it doesn’t seem to have caused Yue E any substantial harm. Once she wakes up, this match’s outcome is already decided—our realm holds the advantage.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 98: The Cave Heaven’s Loophole
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 98: The Cave Heaven’s Loophole

“Why the h*ll are all these guys so strong!?”

As Bai Yuran used her Heavenly Secrets Technique to extract intel on the enemy, Du Zao and the Third Prince’s expressions both shifted slightly.

None of the five from the Tianming Realm were weak!

“This is bad.”

Du Zao’s face darkened. Among the remaining four enemies, he didn’t feel confident beating any of them.

A guy who could temporarily reach mid Divine Transformation… there was no way he could win.

If it were just a Nascent Soul cultivator who’d forcibly reached early Divine Transformation by burning potential, he might manage. But someone at peak condition, an elite at that stage? Absolutely no chance.

If he had that kind of ability, the Tongtian Tower would’ve already bet their future on him.

Another one of them had a Void Physique that nullified mana-based attacks, meaning only physical attacks could hurt them. Du Zao’s strongest weapon was his exotic flame—completely useless. No way he could win in close combat either;
he wasn’t a martial cultivator from Martial Emperor City.

And even if someone from Martial Emperor City fought the guy, they might not win. After all, the Void Physique user also mastered space techniques, which were a nightmare for body cultivators. That guy was basically invincible.

As for the transformed rare beast—that was just absurd. One little scratch from it could cause wounds that couldn’t be healed and would rapidly worsen. That meant even a minor slip in combat could escalate into a fatal injury. Unless someone could absolutely crush it in strength, how could anyone escape unscathed?

And then there was that monster known as the top Nascent Soul cultivator of the Tianming Immortal Palace. Dual spell spirits, dual innate physiques. That guy was practically their number one. No doubt stronger than all the rest. Du Zao suddenly felt an enormous amount of pressure.

He wasn’t the only one. The Third Prince’s expression grew solemn too. He’d thought that, having cultivated the True Dragon Secret Art, he’d at least be able to hold the two-point ground. But now, he was hesitating.

My strongest technique forms a fortune dragon using my own destiny. If I were in the Tianyuan Realm, that dragon would benefit from our nation’s fate. As long as I don’t face that dual solar-lunar guy, I could fight. But in this cave heaven… The Third Prince gritted his teeth.

Now, only Cold Moon Sword Immortal and Lin Jing remained calm after hearing the intel.

“Pfft…”

Bai Yuran had wanted to dig for more info, but the backlash from Nine Arts of Immortal Melody, combined with the cost of using her Heavenly Secrets Technique, pushed her past her limit. She coughed up a mouthful of essence blood and was immediately teleported out of the arena, falling unconscious.

At the same time, two translucent sigils formed by mysterious energy manifested on the Tianming side—indicating they had won the first round and earned two points.

“You dmn btch!!”

Now fully conscious, Yue E’s expression was twisted with fury as she glared at Bai Yuran. The friendly smile from before was long gone.

Even though she won, exposing her team’s intel had been a huge mistake. If Tianming lost the overall match because of her, she didn’t even want to imagine what kind of punishment she’d face from the Immortal Palace.

“Heh…”

On the Tianyuan side, Alchemist Du Zao had just fed Bai Yuran a recovery pill. She soon woke up and grinned mockingly at the Tianming girl.

“So, who’s next?” Bai Yuran now assumed the role of strategist and asked casually.

Cold Moon Sword Immortal glanced toward Du Zao and the Eighth Prince.

Obviously, she was planning to guard the three-point ground, and Lin Jing the four-point one. As long as the two of them secured those seven points, even if the other three lost, Tianyuan would still win.

“Looking at those two, they clearly aren’t confident,” Lin Jing commented.

“The intel she got ended up shaking our own side more than the enemy,” he added, glancing at Bai Yuran.

She rolled her eyes. “Their cultivators are strong, sure. The Tianming Immortal Palace has had stable inheritance with no major wars. Of course they’d be ahead.”

“In the Tianyuan Realm, wars between the Immortal Palace and Immortal Alliance caused the loss of tons of legacy techniques. Plus, the Immortal Palace modified world laws to restrict various races and cultivation paths to punish native cultivators.”

“And the Immortal Alliance? Didn’t even last long before the Ancient Kingdom overthrew it. Two wars back-to-back severely damaged the foundation of the Tianyuan Realm. Sure, there were plenty of top-tier experts born through blood and fire, but in terms of inheritance… we’re clearly behind the Tianming Realm.”

“Well then, should I go next?” Lin Jing asked. “In case both of you lose, and Feng Ning loses too, we’d be in real trouble.”

Feng Ning (Cold Moon Sword Immortal) looked at him.

“You think I’ll lose?” she said, clearly displeased. Lin Jing knew better than anyone how strong she was among same-realm geniuses.

He’d seen the opportunities she’d gained with his own eyes.

“I’m just saying, what if.” Lin Jing shrugged. “Don’t you guys remember? Cave heavens have loopholes. For example, when a cave heaven descends, if cultivators of the same realm are nearby and timing works out, they can enter as well.”

“In other words, while each side can only field five in battle, the number of available participants isn’t necessarily limited to five! If someone accidentally followed us in, then technically, we have six people to choose from.”

“…You mean—” Bai Yuran’s eyes lit up.

“You want to do the same thing you did with the Golden Ranking. Let your contracted beast act independently and replace Du Zao or the Third Prince in battle? Wait—can that even work!?”

“Uh…” Du Zao blinked.

We can do that?

So now he might not even have to fight, and instead rely on one of the Demon Court Holy Son’s spirit beasts to fight in his place?

Granted, Lin Jing’s spirit beasts were insanely strong.

But still, wouldn’t that make him look useless? Just riding the win with no effort?

“It’s just a guess. I don’t know if it’ll work. But the cave heaven should have a will of its own, right? Like a preset program on a mechanized beast. Let’s ask and see.”

Lin Jing raised a hand and summoned the Pine Leaf Mouse. It scampered up his arm and perched on his shoulder.

A moment later, Lin Jing smiled. The cave heaven had responded.

【Spirit beasts may enter battle independently of their Beast Tamer master. However, once their master enters a match, that particular spirit beast can no longer be summoned and may only fight once.】

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 99: This Squirrel’s Not Right
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 99: This Squirrel’s Not Right

"Sure." Lin Jing nodded. Since that’s the case, then it’s even more solid!

He smiled, though he had his own little scheme.

The domain rules were: [Each of the five winning cultivators may select one type of *Divine Physique Pill from the Immortal Palace treasury as a reward. The more outstanding the performance, the broader the selection range, and the higher the quality of the pill.]

If the Pine Leaf Mouse fought in place of Alchemist Du Zao, then wouldn’t that mean the reward would go to the Pine Leaf Mouse?

In that case, Lin Jing would receive two Divine Physique Pills as a reward!

"Fellow Daoist Du, what do you think about letting my pet take your place in the match? You might miss out on the Divine Physique Pill reward, but at least this ensures that the Tianyuan Realm will ultimately secure victory."

"I won’t let you suffer a loss either. Once we return to Tianyuan, I’ll gift you a wisp of Immortal-grade Lotus Heart Flame. Though it’s not a source flame and you won’t be able to refine it, just like the Immortal Pill Spirit in your Tongtian Tower, this flame can heal almost any injury in the lower realms—even foundation damage or origin wounds!"

"Of course, as one of the gentlest exotic flames, you should understand what an Immortal-grade Lotus Heart Flame means for an alchemist. Its value is in no way inferior to a Divine Physique Pill."

Du Zao’s pupils contracted. Lin Jing actually had an Immortal-grade flame. Was it from that Dragon Carp?

So that’s why—now he understood how that Dragon Carp could use Late Nascent Soul cultivation to defeat a Nascent Soul Transformation-stage Holy Son of the Wu God Sect.

"If you put it that way, I’ve got no objections." Du Zao agreed immediately, afraid Lin Jing would change his mind. That was an Immortal-grade flame, after all.

Even the Tower Master of the Tongtian Tower—his master—didn't possess an Immortal-grade flame. At most, he had a few quasi-Immortal flames.

It was said that only with the help of the Immortal Pill Spirit could flames temporarily be upgraded to Immortal-level.

A Late Nascent Soul Dragon Carp actually... so this is what a Rare Beast is?

Just like Lin Jing said—even if it’s not a source flame he can refine, a mere wisp of Lotus Heart Flame still held immense value. Especially for someone like him—who might not need the healing attribute—but could use it to cultivate in an Immortal-grade flame, making all fire-related techniques and spells twice as effective, maybe even developing new effects.

Even in the Tongtian Tower’s special training environments, the best he could get was cultivating in a quasi-Immortal-level gentle flame. But an actual Immortal flame... he doubted even his Tower Master had experienced that more than a few times.

Sure, the Immortal-level effect came from a Nascent Soul-level Rare Beast, but his own cultivation wasn’t high either—this was more than enough!

Du Zao even concluded that a gentle Immortal flame probably had limited combat value for Lin Jing, since he wasn’t a fire-path cultivator. But for Du Zao? He could easily make ten times better use of it.

Compared to a Divine Physique Pill he wasn’t sure suited him, he’d rather not have it at all.

“Since you’ve agreed—Pine Leaf Mouse, you’ll handle the next match.” As Lin Jing finished speaking, he glanced at the arena. The Pine Leaf Mouse yawned and instantly teleported up onto the stage.

So much time had passed;
its spatial manipulation had long since evolved. No need to use the red leaf as a medium anymore—it could teleport anywhere within the range of its divine sense. That was also because it had stashed tons of space-related collectibles in its beast core space…

“Wait, not the Dragon Carp or the Ice Gu?” As Lin Jing let the squirrel go, Du Zao blinked. If he remembered correctly, this squirrel hadn’t even made it onto the Golden Ranking, right?

Unlike the other two—they were both in the top ten!

“Close enough. It just didn’t have time to get on the list, not that it lacked the strength.” Lin Jing casually dropped a line that made Du Zao, the Third Prince, and Bai Yuran all feel a sharp pang in their guts.

…

At that moment—

The Tianming Realm side was stunned to see that the Tianyuan Realm didn’t wait for the “three-minute prep time” to finish and instead sent someone up early.

Everyone entered the stage at the same time, so no one knew who their opponent would be. But going up early like this…

This move basically exposed their fighter in advance, making the Tianming Realm feel as though the Tianyuan side was looking down on them.

And what’s more, the fighter wasn’t one of the original five—it was a... mid-stage Nascent Soul squirrel spirit that seemingly came out of nowhere!

“Huh? What the h*ll is this thing?” A Rare Beast in the form of a woodpecker scratched its head, looking totally baffled. “Where’d this squirrel come from?”

“It’s that beast tamer’s pet.” The cultivator with the Heaven’s Mercy Physique spoke. “Looks like the Tianyuan Realm intends to let this pet fight independently from the Beast Master, meaning they think this pet alone is stronger than at least one of their original fighters—making their odds better this way.”

“But as a cost, once the beast tamer enters a later match, this pet can’t participate again.”

“Even so, this is pretty arrogant. Just charging up like that…” The woodpecker muttered, “This squirrel’s clearly wood-elemental. Its bloodline is pretty strong—close to mine. Probably peak imperial level. But the main thing is, it used spatial teleportation to enter the arena—it just revealed that it knows space spells.”

“A mid-Nascent Soul, wood-element squirrel spirit that can use space techniques? Its physical strength doesn’t look like much. Want me to handle it?” Yuan Liu, who had the Void Divine Physique, spoke up.

Everyone nodded. A squirrel spirit’s spatial skills couldn’t possibly surpass Yuan Liu, who cultivated the Minor Void Technique. He not only had the Innate Void Divine Physique, but also an acquired Void physique. His spatial mastery was formidable, and the squirrel likely relied on mana, not brute force. Against someone with the Void physique, it would be helpless.

This fight looked like an easy win.

When time was up, Yuan Liu, now in the Late Nascent Soul stage, also teleported into the arena and smiled at the Pine Leaf Mouse.

He even wondered if the other side had sent this squirrel spirit up just to hand over some free points.

…

Once the opponent appeared, the Pine Leaf Mouse stared blankly at his… storage pouch.

In the next moment, under Yuan Liu’s curious gaze, the Pine Leaf Mouse summoned a red leaf and flew high into the sky using it.

“Hey, little squirrel, what are you up to?” Yuan Liu looked up, not rushing to attack.

He wanted to see if he could extract some useful information from this squirrel. However...

At that moment, the Pine Leaf Mouse was recalling the information Lin Jing had provided before the match.

[Use physical attacks on him. Don’t mix in mana. Hmm… but with your destructive power, even if you do mix some in, it shouldn’t matter. The opponent’s just Late Nascent Soul. He’s immune to Nascent Soul-level spell attacks—but surely he can’t completely block ones strong enough to insta-kill a typical Divine Transformation-stage enemy, right?]

Physical attacks.

The Pine Leaf Mouse’s eyes sharpened. It raised both claws overhead, then offered up its beast core.

“Huh?” Yuan Liu froze, then his face changed dramatically. That harmless little squirrel just now... had savagely ripped out its beast core. And it was growing—madly—until it blanketed the arena spanning thousands of meters in every direction.

“That’s a beast core from a Nascent Soul beast???” He was stunned. Then he watched as the Pine Leaf Mouse casually tossed the enormous beast core like a ball—hurling it violently at the arena.

“You’re not afraid of damaging your own beast core?” he exclaimed.

“Sigh... Beast Core Divine Art: Five Elements World!”

At that moment, not just Yuan Liu—every Tianming Realm cultivator froze. From that beast core, they felt an overwhelming pressure. It was like the Divine Soul of a peak Divine Transformation-stage Beast Emperor had appeared in the void.

Even the one known as Tianming’s strongest Nascent Soul cultivator—the possessor of the Lunar Solar Physique—frowned slightly, unable to understand how a beast core could be this overpowering… completely unprecedented.

“This—!” The entire arena became a target zone. Yuan Liu’s face changed drastically. He had no choice but to hide in an alternate space. But to his horror, even in that space, he could clearly perceive—under the crushing descent of the beast core—that the space itself was beginning to collapse.

The power of that attack exceeded his imagination.

It was as if he were being slammed by a miniature world!

“Void!” In desperation, Yuan Liu could only rely on the Void Divine Physique, hoping to negate most of the mana damage. But when that beast core—like a star—slammed down on him, he realized...

That beast core had no external mana. It was purely… physical mass and density at an unimaginable level.

Crack.

The impact struck the arena, crushing the opponent beneath the core. As cracks spread across the platform, the Pine Leaf Mouse, standing atop its beast core, scratched its head—feeling like it had forgotten something.

“This squirrel’s not right!”

At the same time, as Yuan Liu’s completely disfigured body was transported back to the Tianming Realm’s side, all the cultivators held their breath, staring silently at the squirrel on stage—like they were looking at a monster.

This squirrel… what the h*ll?! Not only did it smash someone with its beast core—it hit that hard?

Is that a beast core or a top-tier artifact!?

On the Dayuan Realm’s side, the others also went silent, finally understanding what Lin Jing meant by: It just didn’t have time to get on the rankings.

“Heh, maybe I’ll let my pet handle the next match too. I brought three of them today, after all.” Lin Jing looked at the Third Prince and added, “Don’t worry, there’s compensation. For example—I’ll introduce you to a senior even more powerful than your father, before you even meet the Crown Prince.”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 100: Another One?
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 100: Another One?

“What senior...?”

Noticing that Lin Jing wanted to claim his battle spot too, the Third Prince grew wary.

He wasn’t like Du Zao.

Du Zao was just an alchemist with limited strength, and a cultivator from the Tongtian Tower. Giving up his spot for the sake of Tianyuan Realm’s victory wouldn’t cause much of a stir.

But his own status was more sensitive. As the Third Prince of an ancient kingdom, while not as influential as the Ancient Crown Prince, he still represented the honor of the ancient clan to some extent.

If he showed cowardice and gave up his spot to Lin Jing, the Holy Son of the Demon Court, he would absolutely be attacked by his imperial brothers.

If the ancient clan then chose to cut off his cultivation resources, the loss would outweigh the gain.

Most importantly, this domain event would’ve been for nothing. All the opportunities would have nothing to do with him.

After all, what Lin Jing promised Du Zao just now was an Immortal-grade flame—something even a second-generation Ancient Emperor couldn’t obtain!

If it were a visible, tangible resource, the Third Prince thought, he might consider it. After all, he didn’t want to be the reason everyone lost a small realm either.

But what exactly did Lin Jing mean by the opportunity he’d give him as compensation?

Introduce him to a senior even more powerful than his father?

What the h*ll—was he talking about the ruler of the Demon Court? The Third Prince was baffled.

That sounded way less useful than an Immortal flame. Even if he met the Demon Court’s ruler, what could he do with it?

“Your Highness, what do you think?” Lin Jing looked calmly at the Third Prince of the ancient kingdom. Facing those otherworldly, bewitching eyes, the Third Prince narrowed his own and took a deep breath.

“Interesting.”

“Judging from Brother Lin’s promise of an Immortal flame to Fellow Daoist Du, Brother Lin is clearly not one to be stingy. Now I’m very curious—what kind of senior could Brother Lin be referring to, that you’d say he’s stronger than my royal father?”

“If Brother Lin is confident, then it’s no issue for me to yield this slot.”

“Much appreciated.” Lin Jing gave a slight smile. Now things were locked in.

As long as they won this next point too, that would make four points on Tianyuan Realm’s side.

That way, even if Feng Ning lost her match, as long as he secured the final two points, the total of eight would guarantee victory.
