“You won’t regret this.” Being able to enter the same domain was already a sign of fate. And with this Third Prince so tactful, arranging for the First Ancient Emperor to meet him first wouldn’t be a difficult task for Lin Jing.

“The other side’s gonna be dumbfounded.” Seeing Lin Jing make another deal, Du Zao, Bai Yuran, and the others couldn’t help but smile wryly.

What kind of situation was this?

Everyone in Tianyuan Realm already knew how ridiculous Lin Jing’s other pets—the Dragon Carp and the Ice Gu—were.

They didn’t even have to guess: the third round was probably going to be another one-sided crush... Might as well offer early condolences to Tianming Realm.

This was a Nascent Soul-level domain, yet they’d encountered a monster who could wreak havoc even among Divine Transformation geniuses.

Meanwhile—

On the other side of the arena, the Tianming Realm camp was wearing dark expressions. Even Chang Wangshu, known as the number one Nascent Soul cultivator in Tianming, looked puzzled.

That squirrel just now… he’d vaguely felt threatened by it!

“That beast tamer... seems way stronger than our own.” A few people exchanged glances.

In Tianming’s Immortal Palace, most beast tamers came from decent backgrounds but had poor cultivation potential, so they were assigned as caretakers for the palace’s spiritual beasts.

These spiritual beasts often had more talent than the disciples themselves. In this way, these caretakers could cultivate by raising young spirit beasts and benefiting from their growth.

Even so, not everyone was willing to become a beast tamer. The core of the Beast Taming Path was investing resources into pets. Even if these pets were strongly bonded, in the end, they were still “external.”

With limited resources, most cultivators would rather invest in themselves than in a pet with its own thoughts.

Some cultivators didn’t even fully trust their own family members—let alone a beast. Compared to that, buying a magic treasure or refining a puppet was far more in line with most cultivators’ values.

So even among the Immortal Palace’s caretakers, beast taming was never the first choice.

Plus, there had never been a known case of someone in the lower realms successfully ascending after cultivating the Human Control Technique, so in Tianming Realm, the Beast Taming Path was always considered a minor one.

“The main drawback of the Human Control Technique is that you can’t ascend alone—every contracted beast must survive their own ascension tribulation for the tamer to ascend. That’s just way too hard. And the tribulations for this technique are among the most terrifying.”

“But now that the path to ascension is cut off… that drawback kinda doesn’t matter anymore.”

For the first time, the Tianming geniuses were seeing a beast tamer with contracted pets this powerful. At this point, they felt the Pine Leaf Mouse’s peak imperial bloodline was the least impressive part—almost like bloodline fraud.

Was that supposed to be just a Divine Transformation peak-potential beast? Even direct descendants of a Beast Emperor wouldn’t necessarily be that strong!

They’d seen descendants of Beast Emperors before, but even they wouldn’t be so absurd as to use their beast core as a weapon to crush someone and shatter space!

“The two realms really have gone in totally different directions. Someone actually cultivated the Human Control Technique to this level.”

“No matter—it was just a loss. I’ll take the next match and secure the next two points.” Rare Beast Zhuo Mu waved dismissively. “That squirrel might have a Rare Beast bloodline too—that’s the only possibility. After all, we don’t have their intelligence.”

“In that case, we’ll leave it to you,” said the Heaven’s Mercy Physique, glancing at Zhuo Mu. “Be careful.”

“Relax.”

As soon as the time limit ended, Rare Beast Zhuo Mu stepped onto the arena.

At the same moment, Tianyuan Realm’s next fighter entered the stage. Seeing the opponent, Zhuo Mu froze.

It wasn’t one of the remaining four fighters—it was another unfamiliar face.

A big-eyed, slightly clueless-looking... red carp floating in the air.

“What’s going on.” Tianming Realm’s female cultivator Yue E stared blankly at the two beasts in the arena.

“A fish demon at the Nascent Soul stage?? Wait, don’t tell me... is this also that beast tamer’s pet? Another one going up solo? Just how confident is he in his pets? Don’t tell me every single one is as monstrous as the last one…”

Even disciples of a Dao-Seeking powerhouse shouldn’t be this over the top.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 101: Dao Heart Shattered
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 101: Dao Heart Shattered

The Dragon Carp stared at the transformed Rare Beast, Zhuo Mu, while recalling the Pine Leaf Mouse’s reminder.

It was only after the match that the Pine Leaf Mouse finally remembered what it had forgotten.

It had originally planned to “swipe” the opponent’s storage pouch during battle, but who would've thought the opponent was so fragile? One Beast Core Divine Art dropped, and the guy instantly triggered the domain’s protection mechanism.

In the arena matches of the dual-domain, any side that lost combat ability would be forcibly teleported off the stage, which indirectly increased the survival rate of defeated cultivators.

As a result, the Pine Leaf Mouse hadn’t even had time to reach for the pouch before the other guy vanished!

With that experience in mind, the Pine Leaf Mouse had seriously warned the Dragon Carp: Whatever you do, don’t finish them off in one hit—help me get that pouch!

“...” The Dragon Carp scratched its head, thinking this was way too much trouble. Honestly, it didn’t like stealing storage pouches.

It glanced at the bird-feathered young man across from it but couldn’t even tell where his pouch was hidden. Compared to the Pine Leaf Mouse with its space mastery, the Dragon Carp really wasn’t good at this kind of thing.

“I don’t know why it’s another pet beast, but tough luck. Tianming Realm can’t afford to lose again.”

“For the sake of Tianming Realm’s victory, this battle... you’ll have to be the sacrifice.”

Zhuo Mu’s eyes were full of killing intent as he licked his lips. He had no personal grudge with the Dragon Carp, but killing it would greatly benefit the Tianming Realm.

Even though the Dragon Carp was fighting alone, it and Lin Jing were essentially one.

If he could kill this carp, then even though Lin Jing wasn’t personally in the match, the backlash from the pet’s death and the severed contract would severely affect him.

The Tianming side had already realized Lin Jing was no ordinary opponent. Besides the squirrel and the carp, there might be other powerful beasts. If they could cripple Lin Jing now by taking out the Dragon Carp, it would increase their odds in the following matches.

“Looks like Zhuo Mu’s aiming to kill the carp.”

“Whatever, kill it then. Tianming and Tianyuan are already cut off—there won’t be any interaction in the future anyway. Victory and domain opportunities matter more.” The sound cultivator from Tianming Realm spoke coldly.

The rest silently agreed with Zhuo Mu’s decision.

At the same time, the Tianyuan side also sensed the killing intent aimed at the Dragon Carp.

But seeing Lin Jing’s calm expression, they relaxed.

“Die—!”

Just like the Pine Leaf Mouse, Zhuo Mu immediately used his strongest move: the Rare Beast Divine Art, Heaven Peck, intending to end the fight fast.

He soared into the air, and his human head transformed into a bird’s, his wedge-shaped beak glowing with a radiant blue light.

Buzz! A lance-like beam of light shot toward the Dragon Carp. Seeing this, the carp narrowed its eyes, those big round eyes closing slightly.

It had planned to dodge—but realized the surrounding space had been sealed.

“Trying to run?” Zhuo Mu grinned, revealing a set of spatial formation flags—clearly part of a coordinated battle formation.

“Roooar!!!”

With its path blocked, the Dragon Carp growled. Seeing that the opponent didn’t intend for it to dodge, it couldn’t be bothered either. Flames in the shape of dragon scales erupted from its body as it transformed into a blazing Flame Qilin, choosing to tank the Rare Beast Divine Art head-on.

Boom!!!

As its technique activated and it took on the Flame Qilin form, its aura multiplied several times. Its true bloodline fluctuations burst forth, and the oppressive power of ancestral blood filled the arena, making the Tianming cultivators’ pupils constrict.

“What’s going on? This carp’s bloodline… it’s actually peak ancestral level! A whole level higher than Zhuo Mu!”

The carp form wasn’t even its true battle form. Only in transformation did its real bloodline strength reveal itself. The terrifying pressure of its ancestral lineage stunned Zhuo Mu, but he immediately sneered:

“So what if it’s ancestral blood? It’s nothing compared to a Rare Beast bloodline!”

Crash!

The Heaven Peck Divine Art smashed directly onto the Qilin Dragon Carp’s scale armor—and to the Tianming cultivators’ disbelief, that terrifyingly destructive Rare Beast attack only left a faint white mark. Not even a single scale was broken.

“That’s enough. A scratch is still a wound.”

Though shocked by the carp’s defense, Zhuo Mu sneered mockingly, thinking the carp had underestimated his Divine Art.

The next second—

The Rare Beast power activated. Crack, crack, crack... A series of shattering sounds echoed, causing the Tianming side to breathe easier.

This ancestral-blooded Qilin’s physical resilience might be monstrous, but the Rare Beast Divine Art was an immortal inheritance—completely unreasonable.

Though it had only left a “scratch,” the Heaven Peck Divine Art had an unhealable, rapidly worsening curse-like effect. The situation was tipping.

The Dragon Carp’s injury began rapidly deteriorating, turning from a small wound into something more serious.

“Will it be okay...?” The Third Prince frowned with concern.

“Just watch.” Lin Jing replied simply.

His gaze swept toward Zhuo Mu, who had taken out a defensive high-grade artifact, clearly intending to stall for time.

So this was the Divine Art of the Rare Beast, huh?

Not bad.

But what a pity.

According to Bai Yuran’s intel, this Divine Art wasn’t invincible. As long as one possessed immortal-grade healing resources or techniques, it could be undone.

Roooar... And as it happened, the Dragon Carp had just that.

It ignored the woodpecker, focusing on experiencing the Divine Art. The dragon-scale flames around its body slowly morphed into a healing variant: the Life Lotus Heart Flame.

“Pointless…” Zhuo Mu sneered, thinking the carp was trying to heal its rapidly cracking scales.

But the next moment, his face changed drastically.

The healing flame on the Qilin Dragon Carp suddenly radiated a wave of immortal-grade purity. Its quality shot up to an unthinkable level.

And in a blink, the wounds… were completely healed.

The Tianming cultivators, who had been awaiting victory, all froze.

“Wait—is that… Immortal Flame?”

They were stunned.

This beast actually possessed a healing Immortal Flame?!

“That’s it???” On the Tianyuan side, Alchemist Du Zao’s face lit up in excitement.

As expected—Lin Jing hadn’t lied!

ROOOOAR!!!

Once its wounds were healed, the Dragon Carp once again turned its gaze toward the horrified woodpecker.

Its body shifted once more. A long dragon’s form emerged from the rising Lotus Heart Flame, and it spat a fireball—like a dragon pearl—straight at Zhuo Mu.

But this fireball carried the essence of ghostly hellfire—an immortal-grade nether flame.

“What the h*ll is that!!!” Zhuo Mu panicked.

As soon as the flame burst out, it ignited ghostfire deep in his soul.

Before he could react, the dual flames merged into an enormous pill furnace, trapping him inside!

“H-help—I su…” Zhuo Mu tried to surrender.

He realized the opponent’s control over fire was terrifying—maintaining the flame at just the right level: not enough to knock him unconscious, but enough to keep him in unbearable agony.

This sheer helplessness struck pure fear into him.

But before the domain could react—Poof!—with a wisp of blue smoke, the Rare Beast was completely vaporized.

No trace of life remained.

In its place… a single pill floated in the air.

The sight was so eerie that several cultivators from the Tianming side shuddered, feeling their Dao Hearts begin to crack.

What kind of sick joke was this?

At the same time, the Dragon Carp glanced calmly at the newly refined cultivation pill.

Since the guy had wanted to kill it, well—this Emperor Carp wasn’t one to be messed with.

A pill refined from a Rare Beast should be extremely nourishing. Maybe it would even have special effects...

It turned around and looked at its Beast Tamer, ready to show off—

“I don’t eat pills made from humanoid beasts.” Lin Jing coldly shut it down.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 102: The Devious Cultivator
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 102: The Devious Cultivator

“Zhuo Mu... is dead?”

“He was actually refined alive into a pill…”

Several cultivators from the Tianming Realm turned pale.

This outcome was completely unexpected.

Zhuo Mu not only failed to kill his opponent, but instead died himself!

Everyone knew that when a cultivator in the Pocket Heaven loses their strength, a teleportation protection mechanism would activate.

Zhuo Mu had been confident in killing his opponent because his Rare Beast Divine Art caused wounds to worsen continuously and become incurable. Even if the carp had been teleported back while on the verge of death, as long as the cultivators of the Tianyuan Realm had no healing methods, it would still die quickly beneath the arena.

The Pocket Heaven only handled teleportation, not healing.

That’s why his plan wasn’t to finish off the opponent on the arena.

Yet the result stunned everyone on the Tianming side—his opponent not only possessed healing Immortal Flame but also managed to kill Zhuo Mu on the arena itself, despite the Pocket Heaven's protection mechanism. This scene was truly chilling.

Even those on their own side were visibly shaken by Long Li’s actions.

Especially Alchemist Du Zao—his mouth suddenly went dry. Live refining… that was a textbook demonic path alchemy method.

And to think he could refine a Rare Beast at the Nascent Soul stage so quickly—not only due to the power of the Immortal Flame but also because of Long Li’s own mastery over fire control.

He looked toward Lin Jing in shock.

He was the first to understand how Long Li managed to kill his opponent in the arena.

Because that fire-transmuting cauldron divine ability wasn’t aimed at instantly killing.

Inside the pill furnace, the beast’s condition hadn’t even reached a critical or dying state.

It was like “boiling a frog in warm water”—the moment the pill was complete, everything happened in an instant. The alchemy technique suddenly exploded with immense power, instantly killing the opponent and leaving the Pocket Heaven’s protection mechanism too slow to react.

“Zhuo Mu… really is dead…”

Even now, several cultivators from the Tianming Realm couldn’t accept it.

No matter what, Zhuo Mu was one of the top-ranked Nascent Soul disciples of the Immortal Palace.

He was also a "pet" favored by a major figure.

And just like that, he died in the Pocket Heaven battle…

Killed by one of a Beast Master’s pets, without even a chance to resist.

“There’s no time to mourn Zhuo Mu. Tianyuan Realm has already earned four points. If they get three more, we’re finished.”

“The next match is critical. Senior Sister Tian Lian, Senior Brother Lü, what should we do?”

The sound-path female cultivator looked anxious—she definitely didn’t want her cultivation level to drop.

“Tianyuan Realm…” The Void Divine Physique cultivator Yuan Liu, still lying on the ground recovering, was filled with dread. This Tianyuan Realm was much stronger than expected. This was bad.

“We have no choice.”

Senior Brother Lü, who had the Lunar Dao Physique, looked toward the arena and said, “Forget the planned order. You’ll go next.”

“Only by winning this match does the Tianming Realm still have hope of turning the tide.”

“If we lose this one, even if I win the last four-point match, it will be meaningless.”

“But…” Yue E said, “Even if Senior Brother Lü gets three points, it’ll only be 4:5. What if, what if we can’t win that final four-point match… Senior Sister Tian Lian…”

Lü Wangshu looked toward the pensive Tian Lian and said, “Don’t worry. The intelligence you exposed to the enemy earlier was only surface-level.”

“If she’s pushed to the edge, even I wouldn’t want to face her.”

“Junior Sister Tian Lian, if I secure these three points, can you guarantee that—regardless of how strong the opponent is—you’ll take care of the four-point match? Even if it means using that move.”

“After all, if we let you fight this round, with your personality, you wouldn’t be willing to pin our final victory hopes on me. You’d rather pay a huge price and use that move, wouldn’t you?”

Tian Lian said helplessly, “Indeed. I’m not someone who likes leaving the outcome to others. But if I become the final decider, if it really comes to that, I’ll stop at nothing to win.”

“Then that’s good.” Lü Wangshu said, “What she exposed earlier was only part of her abilities. In truth, she can forcibly channel her energy and push the power of the Heaven’s Extinction Divine Art to the peak of Divine Transformation. But at that point, even with her Heaven’s Mercy Physique, she can’t completely avoid tribulation.”

“Heavenly tribulation will still descend. But due to her special physique, the tribulation will instinctively avoid her, instead causing indiscriminate destruction around her. Even she can’t control the heavenly upheaval at that moment. That’s her strongest trump card.”

“Indiscriminate heavenly lightning capable of killing Divine Transformation cultivators, combined with temporary peak Divine Transformation power—there’s no way an ordinary Nascent Soul can match that.”

“But if she forcibly activates it, she’ll suffer backlash from the technique, and her realm will definitely fall. The consequences are nearly the same as losing for the Tianming Realm—or even worse.”

“Yeah.” Tian Lian nodded. “So unless it’s a critical moment, I won’t go that far. After all, if I use that method to win three points, and Senior Brother Lü loses the four-point match, my situation would just be even more miserable. Although… I really can’t imagine anyone within the Nascent Soul stage defeating Senior Brother Lü.”

“Then you’ll decide the final outcome. Don’t let me down. If it really comes to the point where you need to forcefully use your power and suffer a drop in cultivation, I’ll make up for that loss—as long as the Tianming Realm wins in the end.”

Lü Wangshu, possessing the Lunar and Solar Dao Physiques, stepped onto the arena.

Time was up.

He was now very curious who the next cultivator from the Tianyuan Realm would be.

Would it be that Beast Master?

According to the Human Control Technique, a Nascent Soul-stage Beast Master could contract four pet beasts.

Earlier, the opponent had already sent out two, who could no longer fight again.

If the opponent had two more beasts just as strong, then this would be a real battle.

Or… had the Beast Master’s strength been exhausted after those two pets, making them unable to appear again, forcing another cultivator to step onto the arena?

As he speculated, the Tianyuan Realm side also made their decision.

“Lin, want to take my slot?”

Cold Moon Sword Immortal stroked her restless Spirit Sword, motioning for it to calm down. “Even though I’m confident I can win against one of the two remaining opponents… I have no confidence in winning easily. Even if I win, I’d likely pay a heavy price.”

“And if I recall correctly, the rules of the Pocket Heaven say that the better your performance in battle, the higher quality Divine Physique Pill you can choose from, right?”

Feng Ning smiled at Lin Jing and said, “Can you still crush them? If not, want to sub in for me? That way, you can get better rewards too.”

“When the time comes, if there’s a Divine Physique Pill suitable for me, I’ll pay the price and buy it. If there isn’t one, I’ll just give it to you.”

Lin Jing was stunned.

You really are the best at scheming.

You’re not a sword cultivator—you’re a devious cultivator.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 103: Shadows
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 103: Shadows

“You can do that?”

Several cultivators from the Tianyuan Realm were stunned when they saw Cold Moon Sword Immortal also not going up.

Five rounds in the Pocket Heaven—are they really going to let the Holy Son of the Demon Court fight four of them?!

“If I had known, I wouldn’t have bothered gathering intelligence… I lost so badly. Even if Tianyuan Realm wins in the end, with how I performed, the Divine Physique Pills I’ll be allowed to choose from probably won’t be high-quality…” Bai Yuran sobbed.

According to the Tianyuan Continent’s Divine Physique rankings, the difference between those ranked 11–20 and those 91–100 was massive.

At the same cultivation level, a Solar Physique cultivator could easily beat down a Fire Physique cultivator.

Bai Yuran was on the verge of tears. It was the first time she’d ever felt the Heavenly Divination Art was useless.

The intel she gathered seemed totally worthless—because no matter who the opponent was, that Holy Son of Longevity still kept one-shotting everyone on sight.

For the Tianming Realm’s geniuses to match Lin Jing, they’d probably have to bring in someone from the Divine Transformation Stage.

“You don’t need to regret it. I don’t have that many pet beasts. Five rounds aren’t enough for me anyway.” Lin Jing offered a bit of comfort as he sent out his final pet beast.

“This is the last one I’ve got.”

As he spoke, the Ice Soul Gu he summoned had already stepped onto the arena.

“A spider?”

When Lü Wangshu and the Tianming Realm saw that the opponent was a spider, they all froze for a second.

“Another pet beast?”

Lü Wangshu felt suffocated regardless of the outcome of this match.

He had assumed the opponent would take this three-point round extremely seriously—and it’s just another beast?

“A Beast Master, huh. First time seeing a Beast Master like this. Well, this farce ends now.” He was just about to circulate his power when—suddenly—his expression changed.

He had been poisoned without even realizing it!

The toxin surged wildly, attempting to invade his body. Lü Wangshu quickly used the intense Yang energy granted by his Solar Physique to suppress it, finally managing to catch his breath.

It was Wind Plague Gu—colorless, odorless, airborne. Virtually impossible to guard against.

“What’s happening?” Seeing Lü Wangshu go on high alert, the other Tianming cultivators realized something was wrong.

At the same moment, just as Lü Wangshu suppressed the toxin from Wind Plague Gu that had entered through his skin, his mind began to spiral.

What if I lose?

In an instant, the consequences of losing the two-realm Pocket Heaven battle flooded his thoughts. He couldn’t stop imagining.

Falling in realm, returning in disgrace to the Tianming Realm, losing his title as top Nascent Soul genius, getting disappointed stares from Immortal Palace elders and the palace master, being removed from their list of favored disciples…

In that moment, the poison of the Yin Spirit Gu had gripped Lü Wangshu’s heart.

“That spider… it’s the culprit.”

Luckily, as someone with the Innate Dao Physique and a clear Dao heart, he quickly suppressed the Yin Spirit Gu’s effects.

The poison had been planted the moment Ice Soul Gu locked eyes with Lü Wangshu.

Facing such an eerie spider, Lü Wangshu felt completely outmatched.

Even with his so-called toxin-proof Solar Physique, he could only suppress the poison—he couldn’t neutralize it.

“I heard you’re pretty tough?” Ice Soul Gu looked at its opponent, muttering in a childlike voice.

Inside its crystalline body, shimmering motes of light in all colors sparkled—that was the power of its various Gu.

“…It’s a special kind of spider. A toxic Gu?”

“And its venomous bloodline—definitely Ancestral grade.”

Sweating heavily, Lü Wangshu backed away, and black and white Yin-Yang fish emerged from his eyes.

“Distract it.”

He summoned two spell spirit forms, then retreated further to focus on suppressing the toxins.

But to his horror, even after suppressing both the Wind Plague Gu and Yin Spirit Gu toxins, the poisons started changing form—as if alive—vowing to destroy him.

That was because Ice Soul Gu didn’t just use those two poisons. It was a compound venom, strengthened by Ten Thousand Poisons Gu and the Doom Gu!

Ten Thousand Poisons Gu gave the toxins the ability to mutate constantly.

Doom Gu gave the poison vitality—like living venom insects, multiplying and evolving inside the enemy’s body.

These two powers made the toxins incredibly hard to cure or suppress.

Although Ice Soul Gu could also control corpses, its specialty was poison—especially now that it had fused nine Gu into one.

It was devastatingly effective against peers at the same level.

“No—I have to destroy it!!!”

After a while, Lü Wangshu realized that not even his Solar Physique, supposedly the bane of all evils, could hold off the poison. Panic crept in.

Are you kidding me? In this situation, I’d rather face that squirrel or the carp!

“D*mn it! My acquired Solar Physique still can’t compare to an innate one. And my Divine Physique isn’t even perfected yet…” He clenched his teeth. There was no time to think.

All he could do was hope his two spell spirits could take down the Ice Soul Gu.

After all, spell spirits were made entirely of mana—there’s no way they could be poisoned, right?

But he was dead wrong.

Those two spirit forms, evolved from the basic Sunfire and Shadow arts into sentient Yin-Yang fish, got played like fools right before Lü Wangshu’s eyes.

Ice Soul Gu didn’t even intend to fight head-on.

It activated the Stone Man Gu’s power and burrowed underground.

The two Yin-Yang fish, having lost their target, hesitated for a second and then dove underground in pursuit.

“La-la-la~”

Ice Soul Gu refused to confront them directly.

“Without your master’s divine sense to assist, you’ll never track me down underground. Hurry up—if you don’t, your master’s gonna die from the poison.”

Ice Soul Gu rambled on while the two Yin-Yang fish started sweating profusely.

But compared to them, it was Lü Wangshu on the arena who was in the most despair.

He frantically searched for antidotes—but none worked.

And trying to project his divine sense to assist the spirit forms would only make the poison worse!

“It’s over.”

At that moment, even the slowest cultivators from the Tianming side could see what was happening.

Their top Nascent Soul genius was being toyed with by a toxic spider—completely unable to fight back?!

What kind of joke was this?

This couldn’t even be called a battle.

“Unbelievable!”

Moments later, the poison in Lü Wangshu’s body erupted.

His life force plummeted rapidly.

Even though the spirit forms were still chasing Ice Soul Gu underground, he was deemed defeated and teleported away by the Pocket Heaven.

“Senior Brother Lü!”

Lü Wangshu being teleported meant the Tianming Realm had officially lost, even though one round remained.

“That Beast Master on the other side… is a monster.”

“Tian Lian, just forfeit the final match. I can’t hold the poison back anymore… I need to get help from a Dao-Seeking elder at the Immortal Palace…”

Lü Wangshu had lost all his earlier confidence. A shadow had taken root in his heart.

Watching others fight those pet beasts hadn’t seemed that bad.

But after experiencing it himself—he felt nothing but despair.

“I… I understand.”

Tian Lian took a deep breath, scalp tingling. She looked at the spider that had disappeared from the arena and felt an overwhelming dread toward the Beast Master who had never even fought.

Who was that guy?

“Concede.”

Soon, Tian Lian stepped onto the arena.

Since the Tianming Realm had already lost, the only way to keep Senior Brother Lü from dying was to end this immediately—by conceding.

Even if she won, it was meaningless now.

She never imagined things would end like this.

Tianyuan Realm…

Forget the Tianming Realm—even Tianyuan cultivators themselves were shocked at how absurd Lin Jing’s power was.

The opponents were definitely left with a deep psychological scar.

One day, Lin Jing might become their inner demon.

From how the Tianming cultivators performed, they could already picture Lin Jing entering the Origin Realm and facing geniuses from all realms.

“The final round.”

Lin Jing stepped onto the arena himself. He had no more pet beasts left—but that didn’t matter.

“I concede—!”

Seeing that the final round was against that bizarre Beast Master himself, the Heaven’s Mercy Physique conceded instantly.

But—

Seconds after conceding, she realized with horror—

The Pocket Heaven did nothing.

“You chose to concede, huh…” Lin Jing stood calmly on the arena, holding out his hand. A chubby little mushroom danced on his fingertip.

“I concede!!!”

The Heaven’s Mercy Physique’s face changed—but no matter how loudly she shouted, the Pocket Heaven showed no reaction.

Moments later, she finally realized what was wrong. She looked at Lin Jing in shock.

An illusion!

“This… is a spell spirit of the Illusion Dao?” Her eyes locked on the mushroom.

“Smart. You figured it out.” Lin Jing smiled. “You’re only shouting ‘I concede’ inside the illusion. In reality, you’re just standing there silently. Which means, the Pocket Heaven doesn’t recognize your surrender.”

“You…” The Heaven’s Mercy Physique was in despair.

What kind of freak was this guy?

“You’re a Beast Master… and you’ve also cultivated Illusion Dao to the level of forming a spell spirit?!”

Monster.

“Relax. I know you want to concede quickly to keep that guy’s poison from worsening.”

“In this illusion, time flows differently. Outside, maybe only a second has passed.” Lin Jing stroked his chin.

“But I could just as easily make it so that hours go by outside while just moments pass in here. Which means—whether that Tianming genius lives or dies is up to me.”

“Fellow cultivator, you don’t want him to die, right?”

“If you want to save him, and rush back to ask an Immortal Palace expert to detoxify him—there may still be time.”

“And to do that, all you need to do is answer a few questions.

After all, wasn’t our original goal… to exchange intelligence?”

Lin Jing’s voice was low and calm.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 104: Pangu Continent
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 104: Pangu Continent

Lin Jing's request—Tian Lian had already guessed it.

The two realms had finally come into contact. It would be a huge loss if they didn’t at least exchange some information.

Except… this wasn’t mutual exchange anymore.

It was the other side using sheer strength to force them into one-sided intel disclosure.

Tian Lian had no choice. “Ask your questions.”

“Mm. I want to know the historical development of the Tianming Realm since the Immortal Palace there lost contact with the Immortal Realm’s palace.”

Lin Jing extended his Heavenly Demon Spiritual Sense to read her inner thoughts, ensuring she couldn't lie in front of him.

“After Tianming Realm’s Immortal Palace lost contact with the Immortal Realm, it continued developing under the leadership of the lower-realm palace.”

Tian Lian hesitated for a moment.

“Until… ten thousand years ago, when another cultivation realm—the Heavenly Demon Realm—invaded Tianming. That marked the largest immortal-demon war since we lost contact with the Immortal Realm.”

“Go on.” Lin Jing’s expression remained calm.

“At the beginning of that war, the Tianming Realm was at a disadvantage. But not long after, another army followed closely behind the Heavenly Demon Realm’s forces and entered the Tianming Realm.”

“They claimed to be cultivators from the Cultivation Alliance of the Pangu Continent!”

“With the help of the Cultivation Alliance, the Tianming Realm successfully drove out the invaders from the Heavenly Demon Realm.”

“After that, the Tianming Realm learned the truth from the alliance—about why countless cultivation realms across the lower worlds had lost contact with the Immortal Realm.”

“It turns out that in the Heavenly Demon Sect—the strongest demonic sect of the Immortal Realm—a supreme Demon Lord at the Immortal Emperor level had emerged. Using a Heaven-defying treasure, he severed the connection between all forces of the Immortal Realm and the lower realms. His goal was to fuse all the lower realms into one massive world under his control and refine it to simulate the Heavenly Dao—so that he could break through to an even higher level.”

“However, because of the current Heavenly Dao restrictions, immortals cannot descend to the lower realms freely. The Demon Lord himself also couldn't descend personally. So he had to train his own forces within the lower realms—to invade one cultivation realm after another on his behalf.”

“The cultivators of the Heavenly Demon Realm were the result of that training. They used inter-realm divine techniques passed down from the Demon Lord to move between worlds. Their sole mission was to completely conquer each world, extract its source essence, and wait for the Demon Lord to come and claim it.”

“As for the Pangu Continent—it was originally an abandoned cultivation star. But it gradually became a gathering place for countless wandering cultivators who had lost their homes to the Heavenly Demon Realm’s invasion. These exiles eventually founded the Cultivation Alliance to help other worlds that had not yet been invaded, inviting them to join forces and form a united front against the Heavenly Demon Sect.”

“And through their invitation, the Tianming Immortal Palace also became a member of the Cultivation Alliance.”

With Tian Lian’s words, the current political and military landscape of the lower realms slowly unfolded.

Lin Jing understood now—faced with the rampant invasion of the Heavenly Demon Realm, the cultivation worlds of the lower realm weren’t just lying down.

The Cultivation Alliance of the Pangu Continent—this was the stronghold formed by the realms resisting the Heavenly Demon Realm!

“So, the Tianming Realm not only discovered the truth behind the severed connection with the Immortal Realm earlier than the Tianyuan Realm, but also contacted the Cultivation Alliance sooner, gaining the means to resist the Heavenly Demon Realm…”

“That means, if the Tianyuan Realm also makes contact with this Cultivation Alliance and joins them, then someday, if the Heavenly Demon Realm invades Tianyuan, we too can gain the alliance’s support—boosting our chances of survival. But…”

Lin Jing had not yet had any contact with the Cultivation Alliance himself, so he couldn’t fully trust them.

Still, there was no denying that the Tianming Realm had successfully repelled the Heavenly Demon Sect thanks to the Cultivation Alliance.

That alone made this intelligence valuable enough.

The rest—could be left to the Dao-Seeking experts of the Tianyuan Realm to decide.

“Did she lie to you?”

Lin Jing kept watching Tian Lian. “Then tell me—do you know how to contact the Cultivation Alliance?”

The biggest issue right now was that the Tianyuan Realm had no method of traversing the stars.

Even someone as powerful as the First Ancient Emperor had perished when attempting solo star travel.

Without a precise coordinate and a stable method of travel, it would be nearly impossible for the Tianyuan Realm to join the Cultivation Alliance.

At best, they could hope the Alliance would discover them first.

Randomly roaming the stars in search of them would only lead to the same fate as the First Ancient Emperor.

The Cultivation Alliance had once left a star map in the Tianming Realm, and they had also established an inter-realm teleportation array to the Pangu Continent.

However...

Tian Lian said, “Whether it's the star map or the teleportation array—I’m just a Nascent Soul cultivator. I have no access to either.”

“There’s an inter-realm teleportation array too?” Lin Jing was surprised. It seemed the Cultivation Alliance had developed remarkably well under the threat of the Heavenly Demon Sect.

Crossing two cultivation realms used to be possible only with leftover Immortal Realm technology. Most of those relied on Immortal Realm energy.

After losing contact with the Immortal Realm, such methods had become incredibly rare—like the Origin Realm teleportation array.

“Then do you have the star coordinates for the Tianming Realm?” Lin Jing asked.

Though both were lower realms under the Immortal Palace, Tianyuan and Tianming weren’t close in proximity.

Their positions were still vague and undefined relative to one another.

Tian Lian gave a bitter smile.

It was obvious now—the Tianyuan Realm hadn’t been invaded yet, nor had it connected with the Cultivation Alliance.

She found it hard to imagine how a “closed-off” cultivation world could produce a freakish Beast Master like Lin Jing.

“Fellow cultivator, please don’t make things difficult for me. How could I possibly know that… But I can give you a piece of intel.”

“Go ahead.”

“The Origin Realm—you’re aware of it, yes? That special location created by the Immortal Realm to cultivate geniuses from the lower realms.”

Lin Jing’s heart stirred—he realized something.

“Today, the Origin Realm has become a battleground for the Heavenly Demon Realm and the Cultivation Alliance, as they compete for resources.”

“The Heavenly Demon Realm uses teleportation arrays from conquered realms to send their young elites to the Origin Realm, where they fight over Immortal Essence.”

“And the Cultivation Alliance’s geniuses are also in the Origin Realm, clashing with the Heavenly Demon Sect to stop them from nurturing new power.”

“Those cultivators from the Alliance inside the Origin Realm likely have ways to contact the Cultivation Alliance directly. As for the Tianyuan Realm… I suspect you’ve even lost the means to enter the Origin Realm, right?”

“But—if you can find a way to locate those teleportation arrays and enter the Origin Realm, you should be able to make contact with members of the Cultivation Alliance!”

She wasn’t wrong.

The Tianyuan Realm had indeed lost access to the Origin Realm.

But now, the Second Ancient Emperor had recovered the method.

That made her suggestion highly feasible!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 105: Divine Physique Pills
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 105: Divine Physique Pills

"One last question," Lin Jing said.

"In the Tianming Realm, some prodigies must've already gone to the Origin Realm, right?"

"In the Origin Realm, are the rules still the same as the records—only cultivators below the Void Mystical Stage can exist?"

Tian Lian nodded and replied, "Yes. Whether it's the Heavenly Demon Realm, the Cultivation Alliance, or countless scattered cultivation realms, they can still only send Nascent Soul and Nascent Soul Transformation Stage prodigies in."

"The power of rules in the Origin Realm is extremely strong, established jointly by several Immortal Emperors and Immortal Kings. The Heavenly Demon Sect does have a secret art called the Heavenly Demon Physique, which burns lifespan to raise one's cultivation, but even this physique can't fully function in the Origin Realm."

"The prodigies of the Heavenly Demon Sect, within the Origin Realm, can at most elevate their cultivation to the peak of the Nascent Soul Transformation Stage using the Heavenly Demon Physique. Once they exceed that stage and reach the Void Mystical Stage, they’ll be deemed in violation and forcibly teleported out of the Origin Realm."

"Other than that, any artifact, pill, or secret art that can raise cultivation beyond the Void Mystical Stage also can’t be used within the Origin Realm."

"This is one of the few places even the Heavenly Demon Realm can’t fully control."

Though the Origin Realm might not be very peaceful right now, Lin Jing still felt he needed to go.

As long as no one had Void Mystical-level combat strength, there was nothing to be afraid of.

"It's about time. Forget everything I just asked you," Lin Jing activated an illusion technique again, and the Heaven’s Mercy Physique’s gaze went blank.

"I concede."

On the outside platform, the Heaven’s Mercy Physique shouted out a panicked surrender. The next moment, she was teleported away.

At the same time, the Tianyuan Realm secured final victory. Before Lin Jing, the Pine Leaf Mouse, Dragon Carp, and Ice Soul Gu appeared three pill bottles, from which they could choose one. Just by looking at them, they could see the information on them.

What Ice Soul Gu saw was:

Sixth-grade Divine Physique Pill: Poison God Physique (When consumed by a human cultivator, grants the acquired Poison God Physique, capable of controlling all poisons. Only humans can use.)

Sixth-grade Divine Physique Pill: Heaven’s Calamity Physique (Brings disaster to those nearby;
each time a disaster occurs, the user gains fortune in return. Only humans can use.)

Seventh-grade Divine Physique Pill: Lunar Physique (Only humans can use.)

What Dragon Carp saw was:

Sixth-grade Divine Physique Pill: Dragon God Physique (Contains Dragon God bloodline, grants the Dragon Blood Divine Physique. Usable by humans and demons.)

Sixth-grade Divine Physique Pill: Qilin Physique (Refined using the bloodline of a half-immortal Qilin. Grants the Qilin Physique. Usable by humans and demons.)

Seventh-grade Divine Physique Pill: Medicine God Physique (Refined from the essence of countless divine herbs. Once attained, every inch of one’s flesh and blood becomes the best alchemical ingredient, highly nourishing. Usable by humans, demons, demonic beasts, and plant spirits.)

What the Pine Leaf Mouse saw was:

Sixth-grade Divine Physique Pill: Azure Dragon Physique (Contains the Azure Dragon bloodline that commands plants. Usable by humans and demons.)

Sixth-grade Divine Physique Pill: Void Physique (Grants control over space. Only humans can use.)

Seventh-grade Divine Physique Pill: Longevity Body (Extends lifespan. Only humans can use.)

As for what Lin Jing saw:

Eighth-grade Divine Physique Pill: Illusion God Physique (Only humans can use.)

Eighth-grade Divine Physique Pill: Demon God Physique (Fuses multiple demonic beast bloodlines, turning the user into a half-demon species. Only humans can use.)

Seventh-grade Divine Physique Pill: Devil God Physique (Refined from demonic race bloodline. At full mastery, it produces demonic energy nearly equal to Immortal Power. Only humans can use.)

With the Beast Taming contracts and shared consciousness, the dazzling array of twelve divine physiques overwhelmed Lin Jing and his pet beasts.

"We’ve struck gold this time," Lin Jing took a deep breath.

There were seventh-grade pills here!!!

Not a single physique here was trash like the Fire God Physique or Wood God Physique.

If the Five Elements attribute physiques were considered the weakest divine physiques, and a perfected divine physique corresponded to the Nascent Soul Transformation Stage…

Then the physiques granted by these sixth-grade pills were at least on par with the Void Mystical Stage.

Take the Void Physique available to the Pine Leaf Mouse, for example.

Though an acquired physique developed through techniques, pills, or spiritual treasures might lag behind innate ones in power and potential, for a cultivator, it was still a massive boost.

Especially since the available Divine Physique Pills weren’t capped at sixth-grade—there were even seventh-grade ones.

These physiques were nearly the closest tier to Immortal Physiques.

Even if acquired, their power was still incredible.

For example, the Lunar Physique option for Ice Soul Gu.

"Longevity Body… There’s actually a pill that grants the acquired Longevity Body… In the eyes of ancient Immortal Palace alchemists, is it really that impressive? Right now, on the Tianyuan Continent’s Divine Physique rankings, the Longevity Body doesn’t even crack the top 100. It’s even ranked lower than the elemental attribute physiques."

Lin Jing was surprised. Meanwhile, the pet beasts gave Lin Jing, their Beast Tamer, full authority to choose their pills.

"As for mine… Illusion God Physique, Demon God Physique, Devil God Physique… what the h*ll." Lin Jing definitely prioritized the seventh-grade Devil God Physique. He already had enough illusion-based powers, and had outgrown the need for illusion-based protection. He didn’t have the energy to delve deeper into illusion arts.

Demon God Physique… Lin Jing really wasn’t interested in becoming half-demon.

The only real choice was the Devil God Physique, made from demonic race bloodlines… Lin Jing didn’t know what this "demonic race" was, but a perfected Devil God Physique could channel demonic energy nearly equal to Immortal Power—that was definitely tempting.

Demonic race… probably some special race in a cultivation realm or the Immortal Realm.

"This is it. I’ll let my avatar take it. A Heavenly Demon successor with a Devil God Physique—fitting."

After choosing his own pill, Lin Jing began choosing for the pet beasts. The frustrating part was that most of the pills were limited by race, and almost none could be used by demonic beasts.

But it made sense. After all, the Immortal Palace was a human faction, and alchemists would naturally prioritize pills for humans first, then for humanoid demons. As for demonic beasts? They were just roadside strays.

"Azure Dragon Physique, Void Physique, Longevity Body… Azure Dragon’s out. Void Physique is good, but since it’s acquired, it doesn’t mean much—we can always understand spatial Dao later through expensive resources. Longevity Body… let’s pick that. I wonder if consuming this pill will perfect my own Longevity Body, or if letting the avatar take it will grant the same effect, increasing lifespan and allowing unrestricted use of the Heavenly Demon Physique."

For the Dragon Carp, there was no hesitation—it picked the Medicine God Physique. This pill actually had a wide range of compatibility, but like the Longevity Body, it was basically a Tang Seng body. No demonic beast would want it.

For Ice Soul Gu, it would definitely choose the Lunar Physique. However, the pill could only be used by humans. Lin Jing wasn’t very interested in it. According to records, without the Solar Physique or a supreme yang item to counterbalance, male cultivators with the innate Lunar Physique would become increasingly feminine. This physique was better suited to female cultivators.

Recalling his agreement with Cold Moon Sword Immortal, he decided to give it to her.

Her main cultivation path was the ice-element sword Dao, and the Lunar Physique also contained ice power, so it should be a decent match—likely boosting her strength significantly.

Lin Jing didn’t intend to trade like she’d suggested. He just hoped Cold Moon Sword Immortal could enter the Origin Realm successfully and earn a spot.

In the Origin Realm, without protection from the Demon Emperor and unfamiliar surroundings, it would be wiser to have more trustworthy and powerful companions. Senior Sister and Cold Moon Sword Immortal were reliable. As for folks like the Ancient Prince, Lin Jing couldn’t trust them just yet. If danger struck, the ones who could truly help would still be the familiar ones.

"Besides these Divine Physique Pills, there's one more harvest."

The Dragon Carp had received a cultivation golden core refined using the bloodline of a Rare Beast from the Tianming Realm!

Ordinary cultivation golden cores only boosted cultivation. But this one—even the Dragon Carp could tell it was different. It likely had special effects. After all, it was refined from the life force of a Rare Beast bloodline. As a half-immortal beast, its life potential far exceeded that of ordinary demonic beasts!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 106: Tianming Immortal Palace
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 106: Tianming Immortal Palace

Tianming Cultivation Realm.

Immortal Palace.

As the ancient ruling upper realm power of the Tianming Cultivation Realm, all cultivation sects in this realm were essentially just affiliates of the Immortal Palace.

All the prodigies within those other sects aimed to be chosen by the Immortal Palace and enter it to cultivate.

And the cultivation progress and condition of these prodigies were constantly monitored by many powerhouses within the Immortal Palace.

Being chosen by a Small World was a big deal, even in an Immortal Palace full of elite geniuses.

Especially this time—it was a Two-World Small World, one that hadn’t appeared in tens of thousands of years!

“Grand Elder!”

“Still haven’t come out yet?”

The Grand Elder looked at the robed elders before him and asked.

“Not yet, but it should be soon,” said an early-stage Seeking the Way elder who stepped forward.

“Elder Fu, which disciples were chosen by this Two-World Small World?” the Grand Elder asked.

Elder Fu replied, “All five are core disciples. Among them is Lü Wangshu, the one you’ve favored, Grand Elder! And also that Rare Beast I picked up from outside is among them!”

“Is that so?” The Grand Elder stroked his beard and said, “That Lü Wangshu—he reached Nascent Soul Great Perfection in both his true body and avatar within a hundred years, and completed both the Lunar and Solar Physiques. He’s indeed an excellent seedling.”

“If nothing goes wrong, he should be able to earn one of the spots to enter the Origin Realm in the next batch from the Tianming Realm.”

“If he achieves something in the Origin Realm, then one of us may even take him as a disciple and pave his path to Seeking the Way.”

“Indeed,” Elder Fu said emotionally, “With him, we should have secured victory in this Two-World Small World. I just wonder how much intelligence we managed to gather on the Tianyuan Realm. It also bears the legacy of the Immortal Palace, yet to this day hasn’t been found by the Cultivation Alliance... Could it already have been taken by the Heavenly Demon Sect…”

The Seeking the Way elders all nodded slightly. The two realms’ Immortal Palaces didn’t share completely overlapping inheritances—they each had their specializations.

At present, the Tianming Cultivation Realm wasn’t considered particularly powerful within the Cultivation Alliance.

If they could establish contact with the Tianyuan Realm, and the two Immortal Palace legacies joined forces, their influence in the Cultivation Alliance would surely rise...

Soon, five beams of Small World light vanished at five mountain peaks within the Immortal Palace range.

Each peak housed a core disciple. As the beams dispersed, the Seeking the Way elders quickly extended their spiritual sense.

A moment later, Elder Fu’s expression changed—he discovered that the Rare Beast, Zhuo Mu, hadn’t returned!

That could only mean one thing: it had died in the Small World!

“What’s this!” The Grand Elder's face also changed—he saw that Lü Wangshu, the top Nascent Soul disciple of the Immortal Palace, had returned barely conscious, his entire body afflicted with poison!

“Come!” With a wave of his hand from the clouds, Lü Wangshu was lifted into the air and brought before the Seeking the Way elders.

“It’s Gu poison,” one of the junior elders said, surprised. “Extremely violent Gu poison—hard to suppress without Void Mystical power, and only Seeking the Way power can neutralize it.”

As he spoke, he waved his sleeve, and Lü Wangshu’s complexion visibly improved. At the same time, the other Tianming Immortal Palace prodigies who returned from the Small World were also pulled up by the Seeking the Way elders.

These Nascent Soul prodigies had never seen such a scene. Usually, when someone returned from a Small World, only a Void Mystical Stage elder might come to ask a few questions. But this Two-World Small World had drawn in all these Seeking the Way elders!

“You lost?”

Judging by the disciples’ state, the elders could tell—the Tianming side had likely lost in the Two-World Small World battle!

Their faces darkened. What a bunch of useless trash.

“Grand Elder,” Lü Wangshu said anxiously after waking, clearly panicked that the Grand Elder had personally treated his poison.

“Recover well,” the Grand Elder said disappointedly as he glanced at Lü Wangshu. No matter how talented he was, a loss was still a loss. In the Immortal Palace, there was no such thing as “do better next time.” With so many geniuses from an entire realm gathered here, if you failed, you would be replaced, and the resources would go to someone else.

“We beg the elders’ forgiveness for our incompetence. We lost the Small World battle,” the disciples knelt and confessed their failure.

“Sigh.” A few elders who had been full of confidence could only shake their heads. This batch of disciples was indeed disappointing—far inferior to the last one.

Elder Fu looked at them coldly and asked, “So Zhuo Mu died in the Small World? Did the Tianyuan Realm produce an Immortal Physique or something? A Nascent Soul youth with an Immortal Physique?”

“You’re Tian Lian, right? You tell us.”

Elder Fu looked at the Heaven’s Mercy Physique, who was in the best condition. Tian Lian immediately responded:

“...Elder, it wasn’t an Immortal Physique. It was a Beast Taming cultivator who trained in the Human Control Technique… an absolutely ridiculous Beast Tamer.”

“A Beast Tamer?” Even the Grand Elder looked baffled. A Beast Taming cultivator was able to crush elite prodigies hand-picked by the Immortal Palace from various sects???

The Human Control Technique—wasn’t that a low-ambition technique favored by pampered second-generation cultivators with too much money and no drive?

“Yes, a Beast Tamer… Senior Brother Lü’s poison injury was caused by a poisonous spider sent out by him.”

“Junior Brother Zhuo Mu was also killed by a koi spirit dispatched by him—he was refined alive into a pill.”

“And Junior Brother Yuan Liu was injured by a squirrel spirit that threw a demon core at him…”

“Wait—are you saying all of you were defeated by the same Beast Tamer?” Elder Fu thought he might’ve misheard.

Tian Lian gave a bitter smile. “Each of his pet beasts had the power to crush us. He didn’t even need to act himself—just dispatching them one by one was enough to overwhelm us.”

“What kind of joke is this?” The Grand Elder frowned. This was even more absurd than facing a few Immortal Physiques.

Since when could a Beast Tamer reach this level?

Or rather, if someone had the talent to command such monstrous beasts, why waste it on practicing the Human Control Technique?

Next, the Seeking the Way elders dug deeper into the details of the Small World battle—and the more they listened, the angrier they got.

It seemed the Tianyuan Realm’s Immortal Palace legacy had been broken, and even in that state, their disciples still suffered such a crushing defeat???

Not only did they lose, but they didn’t even bring back any useful intelligence.

“You are the worst group of core disciples I’ve ever seen,” the Grand Elder said calmly. “Your Senior Brother Shangguan is already in the Origin Realm and has refined an acquired Immortal Physique through Immortal Source. Several other senior brothers have also gained significant rewards and are now competing with Heavenly Demon Realm prodigies for more Immortal Source to strengthen Tianming!

“Given your current state, how can the Immortal Palace feel secure sending you to the Origin Realm to contend with prodigies from across the realms?”

“Starting today, the four of you will go to Soul-Burial Valley for closed-door cultivation. If you can’t find your own way out using your own strength, your qualification to compete for the Origin Realm slots will be revoked!”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 107: Reunion
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 107: Reunion

Upon hearing this, the Tianming Realm prodigies' expressions changed. “Soul-Burial Valley” was one of Tianming’s forbidden zones— even a cultivator at the Nascent Soul Transformation Stage entering it faced near-certain death.

“If you can’t even conquer the Soul-Burial Valley, then forget about entering the Origin Realm.”

“According to reports from disciples who returned from the Origin Realm, even Shangguan Ao, who attained an acquired Immortal Physique, didn’t gain much of an edge against the Heavenly Demon Sect cultivators!” The Immortal Palace Grand Elder spoke coldly. “If you go, you’ll just end up as nourishment for those demonic cultivators!”

“Even Immortal Physiques…” Yue E blurted out instinctively in shock. After all, in their understanding, an Immortal Physique was virtually invincible in the lower realms.

Senior Brother Shangguan may have had only an acquired Immortal Physique, but still—surely no ordinary divine physique could compare…

“Immortal Physiques are not invincible.”

“The true core members of the Heavenly Demon Sect receive ‘Demon Blood’ from the upper realm Heavenly Demon Sect, transforming them into ‘Demonic Race’. The Demonic Race is a major race in the Immortal Realm. Their leaders are powerful elders known as Demon Lords. With Demon Race bloodlines, cultivating Heavenly Demon techniques becomes far easier. When the bloodline is pure enough, its power can rival even Immortal Physiques.”

“If we could still connect with the Immortal Palace of the Immortal Realm, you might even get a chance to encounter the power of these upper-realm races. But for now...”

——

Tianyuan Realm.

Lin Jing and the others also stepped out of the Small World.

Tongtian Tower.

As Du Zao exited, he immediately saw the Tower Master’s avatar and the Spirit of the Immortal Pill waiting outside the light pillar. He never expected his master to personally wait for his return!

“That was the Two-World Small World?” the Tongtian Tower Master looked at Du Zao.

“You don’t even have a scratch on you. Don’t tell me you surrendered right away?” the Pill Spirit mocked with disdain. “Even if pill refiners aren’t great at fighting, giving up without even trying? You’ve embarrassed the Tianyuan Realm and the Tongtian Tower!”

“Disciple... disciple... I really don’t know how to explain this…” Du Zao looked helpless.

“The Holy Son of the Demon Court was also chosen to enter the Small World. He borrowed my battle slot for his beast.”

Tongtian Tower Master, Pill Spirit: …

It’s him again.

——

Tianyuan Ancient City, Third Prince’s Mansion.

Seeing his elder brother—the current top ranker on the Genius Ranking, the Ancient Crown Prince—Third Prince felt a guilty chill.

“Two-World Small World…” the Crown Prince stared at the Third Prince. “Don’t tell me you lost. There’s no way to explain that to Father.”

“Don’t worry, big brother. I didn’t lose…” The Third Prince scratched his head awkwardly. “But I didn’t win either. I ran into the Demon Court’s crown prince. He… kind of singlehandedly took out all the Tianming Realm’s prodigies. Gotta admit, he’s pretty strong. But compared to you, big brother, he’s still a step behind.”

“You’re talking about Lin Jing??” The Crown Prince’s brows furrowed—he’d heard a name he really didn’t want to hear. His gaze turned sharper as he stared at the Third Prince.

“Spill everything.”

——

Heaven's Secrets Pavilion, Heavenly Sword Sect...

In Qingzhou and beyond, Lin Jing’s Small World light pillar had also attracted crowds of powerhouses. But when the light faded, Lin Jing was nowhere to be found.

He had used illusion techniques to hide himself and slipped away leisurely. Once he was far from the crowds, he summoned the Dragon Carp.

“Use the Nether Immortal Flame to produce a clone flame and send it to that alchemist in Tongtian Tower.”

“Croak~~” The Dragon Carp nodded and quickly condensed one, shaping it into a lotus flower…

The fiery lotus seemed to have a will of its own, flying off in the direction of the Tongtian Tower.

Aside from placing the Demon Court Holy Son’s token inside, there were no other escort measures.

A celestial flame sent by the Demon Court’s Holy Son—no cultivator in the Tianyuan Realm would dare intercept or steal it.

“Croak…” After laboriously condensing another celestial flame, the Dragon Carp panted and handed over the golden core refined from Zhuo Mu. It asked: how do you use this thing???

It hadn’t used much effort to heal injuries during the fight. It was nearly drained instead from refining its opponent.

“This thing, huh?” Lin Jing took the Rare Beast Golden Core and said, “This pill will definitely grant a huge boost in cultivation, but…”

He probed it with his spiritual sense and sensed a trace of Rare Beast Origin sealed within the pill!

Just like the time he extracted the Undying Serpent’s Rare Beast Origin—during Zhuo Mu’s refining process, a sliver of his origin should’ve been locked inside this golden core.

If Lin Jing and the Dragon Carp were right, consuming this pill wouldn’t just greatly enhance one’s cultivation—it might also grant a portion of the Rare Beast’s Divine Art.

Just like Ice Soul Gu, who, after absorbing the Undying Serpent’s death origin, gained about 10–20% of its undying power. Though it couldn’t compare to the original Rare Beast’s might, its value was no less than most Divine Physique Pills.

“Croak~” The Dragon Carp looked on eagerly.

Since it refined this thing… and Lin Jing had no interest in eating pills made from humanoid demon beasts… then…

“Let the avatar have it. His cultivation is still low. This should help him catch up. Plus, that irreparable, rapidly worsening power—fits well with Heavenly Demon energy.”

Dragon Carp: ?

“Wait… I thought you didn’t eat pills refined from demon races?” The Dragon Carp was so mad it spoke human.

Lin Jing nodded, “I don’t. My avatar does.”

Dragon Carp: ???

What kind of logic is that!?

“But thinking about it, this hard-to-heal, rapidly deteriorating power might also be perfect for Ice Soul Gu’s poison.”

“Gu poison is already hard to treat—combine it with the Rare Beast Golden Core, and even Seeking the Way cultivators might have trouble dealing with it!” Lin Jing’s eyes lit up.

Hearing this, the Dragon Carp panicked.

Give it to Ice Soul Gu? That thing just absorbed the Undying Beast’s origin to refine an avatar!

Maybe just let the Heavenly Demon avatar have it.

It was worried that Ice Soul Gu might suffer spiritual backlash or bloodline instability from absorbing two Rare Beast powers at once.

“Good point.” Lin Jing put away the Rare Beast Golden Core.

Then he looked toward the Tianyuan Ancient Kingdom, preparing to rendezvous with his avatar.

———

Lingdao Sect.

When Lin Jing’s main body arrived at the foot of Lingdao Sect’s mountain, his avatar, already waiting there, came up to greet him.

But before the two bodies could joyfully reunite—

The sky swirled and a dragon’s roar echoed through the clouds.

A gallant youth descended, seated atop a flying dragon made of illusory fortune energy, landing outside Lingdao Sect. He stared at Lin Jing.

“Demon Court Holy Son?”

“Crown Prince of the Ancient Kingdom?” Lin Jing looked up, unsure why this guy had come to find him. Of course, if Second Prince Ancient had not paid a ‘protection fee,’ Lin Jing would’ve already gone looking for this guy.

“I’m simply curious. What kind of demonic creature did you use to grant such fortune to that worthless brother of mine that you dare compare yourself to Father the Emperor? Do you have any respect for the Human Emperor at all?”

The Crown Prince’s eyes narrowed. “But this visit isn’t about that…”

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 108: A Hundred Years Is Not Too Late
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 108: A Hundred Years Is Not Too Late

Hearing the Ancient Prince call the First Generation Ancient Emperor a “monster,” Lin Jing instantly knew this kid had taken a dead-end path.

If circumstances had allowed, Lin Jing honestly wanted to teach him a little hometown tune.

♪ What’s your daddy’s daddy’s name? ♪

“Heh.” There would come a day when this Ancient Prince would kneel and call him grandpa. Lin Jing looked at him calmly and said, “If you’ve got something to say, just say it.”

“My third brother described your battle with the Tianming Realm cultivators to me. Especially the final fight with that girl possessing the Heaven’s Mercy Physique. I doubt it was as simple as them giving up voluntarily like they said.” The Ancient Prince’s eyes were sharp.

“This last fight was the Tianyuan Realm’s final chance to gather intelligence on the Tianming Realm. I don’t think you would’ve let that slip.”

“Back when you wanted to conceal the Longevity Body, you were already practicing powerful illusions at the Qi Refining Stage, weren’t you? Outside Qianlong Academy, when you fought Fang Shilin from Evergreen Valley, you showed quite a bit of illusion skill… If I’m not wrong, before the Tianming cultivator surrendered, you already used illusion or other tricks to get the intel you wanted.”

Lin Jing was slightly surprised—this Ancient Prince wasn’t just insanely talented;
he had brains too...

“So what if I did.” Lin Jing spoke up. “You want intelligence on the Tianming Realm? Fine. Bring me something worthwhile in exchange.”

Lin Jing had originally planned to report the Pangu Continent intelligence to the Demon Emperor, and then let the Tianyuan Pathseekers decide together whether to make contact with the Cultivation Alliance. But since the Ancient Prince was so desperate for it now, Lin Jing didn’t mind making things difficult for him.

“You name it.” The Ancient Prince’s gaze flickered—he was prepared for Lin Jing to ask for the moon. Intel on the Tianming Realm and other cultivation realms was indeed extremely important to him.

“Then I will.” Lin Jing said, “Before I stepped onto the path of cultivation, I accidentally entered a demonic cultivator base called Qing Niu Dao Guan. Inside, a Daoist named Qing Niu sold me to the Yin Corpse Sect. He disappeared after that. You help me capture this Qing Niu Daoist—alive if possible. If he’s dead, you’ll find a way to revive him and hand him over to me.”

Lin Jing suddenly remembered this unresolved grudge and said it casually.

Ancient Prince: ?

“Qing Niu Daoist?” The Ancient Prince hadn’t expected this at all. That was such an old incident—how many years ago was that? The Yin Corpse Sect was already wiped out, and Lin Jing still remembered some nobody like that?

“Alright...” The Ancient Prince’s mouth twitched. This request was both simple and difficult. Qing Niu Daoist was, at best, a Foundation Establishment demonic cultivator—he was probably long dead. And yet Lin Jing wanted to see him alive, even if he was dead. That was a harsh demand.

“You’ve got a year to find him. That’s doable, right?” Lin Jing said. “No need to promise me. If you can’t pull it off by then, I’ll come for your number one spot on the Golden Ranking.”

After speaking, Lin Jing met the Ancient Prince’s eyes. In the next instant, he pulled him into an illusion space.

From a first-person view, the Ancient Prince was now back at the scene of the Small World contest, standing before Tian Lian.

Everything Tian Lian had said to Lin Jing back then now replayed before the Ancient Prince…

The war between the Heavenly Demon Realm and the Tianming Realm, the Pangu Continent, the Cultivation Alliance, the Origin Realm... all sorts of information made the Ancient Prince take a deep breath—he knew his visit had not been in vain!

“Grandfather… left for the stars in search of the path to ascension and hasn’t returned. Could he have also joined the Cultivation Alliance?”

“No matter what, through this Cultivation Alliance, perhaps I can find traces of Grandfather out in the stars.” That was the thought in the Ancient Prince’s heart.

After getting all the intel, I nodded toward Cultivation and said, “You’ll get to work on what you promised him, right away.”

“Whatever.” Cultivation yawned. He couldn’t wait for the Ancient Prince to catch that stinky old man and drag him back.

...

“You all... didn’t forget something, did you?”

Just before leaving, the Ancient Prince glanced toward his personal shortcut that led deep into the Ling Dao Sect’s mountain. He fell into thought.

Thanks to Lin Jing’s reminder, the pet beasts in the Beast Taming Space suddenly remembered something too.

Right, something was missing.

The mechanical turtle that didn’t make it into the Small World—it was nowhere to be seen when they came back out.

At the time, Lin Jing had been busy checking his spoils and didn’t really care where that guy had gone...

“Looks like it got bored and ran off on its own after we got separated. Whatever, leave it be.” Lin Jing was a bit annoyed. Sure, forgetting the mechanical turtle was a bit much, but that guy didn’t even wait a short while and just ran off? That was just bad attitude.

As expected, strong beings always came with some quirks.

Lin Jing sighed, unaware that the mechanical turtle had already entered the territory of the Ancient Kingdom.

“Where exactly is that Longevity Body the old geezer talked about... where... is it….”

The mechanical turtle traveled while admiring the majestic mountains and rivers of the Ancient Kingdom, all while searching for the Longevity Body mentioned by the First Generation Ancient Emperor.

Even though the Longevity Body hadn’t awakened yet, the mechanical turtle believed that with its skills, it could definitely find it.

But if you asked why it was so determined to find the Longevity Body itself, the mechanical turtle wouldn’t be able to answer. It was simply a fixation built into its character—obsessed with finding anything related to eternal life.

“You’re... back so soon? And just now—was that the Ancient Prince?”

When Lin Jing returned to the Comprehension Pool, the Enlightenment Fish asked with clear reluctance.

“Yeah, no need to worry, Senior. We’re ready to resume our cultivation.” Lin Jing’s figure split in two again, making the Enlightenment Fish roll its eyes.

What the h*ll? Isn’t it enough that you’re training by yourself? Now you bring a clone too???

“Please go back to sleep, Senior!” Lin Jing clasped his hands toward the fish. The fish instantly reacted like it had died a hundred times, pulled its head back into the water, turned into a lifeless stone, and sank to the bottom. As it did, it activated its Divine Art, enhancing the spiritual power of the pool.

Seeing that the pool’s water quality had already changed, Lin Jing nodded and turned to the clone. The Heavenly Demon clone, currently at the Golden Core stage, immediately stepped into the pool and caught the God Body Pill Lin Jing tossed over.

The Demonic God Body Pill, a seventh-grade pill, was theoretically meant for the Seeking the Way Stage. However, after testing, the Dragon Carp had confirmed its uniqueness. Even mortals could take it safely, as long as their existing physique wasn’t incompatible with what the pill would grant.

“My main body already has too many special physiques. Cultivating a new one would definitely cause inner conflict—there’s no room left for new Divine Powers.”

“But the clone only has two physiques. Might as well give it a try.”

Currently, the clone possessed two major physiques: the Innate Spotless Divine Physique and the Acquired Heavenly Demon Physique. The Spotless Physique came from the quasi-immortal item Lotus of Immortal Water used to create the clone. The Heavenly Demon Physique came from the main body’s understanding of Heavenly Demon techniques—essentially, the clone re-cultivated a new Heavenly Demon Body.

Whatever the main body had learned, the clone found much easier to train.

Unfortunately, the clone couldn’t inherit anything innate, such as the Longevity Body, which was an inborn physique.

But anything acquired later—bloodlines, physiques, techniques—the clone could pick up with ease.

Now, since there was no conflict between the Heavenly Demon Physique and the Spotless Physique, this unknown demonic bloodline in the pill was likely to be compatible too.

Lin Jing figured—the clone should have no problem adapting!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 109: Complete Heavenly Demon
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 109: Complete Heavenly Demon

At the Comprehension Pool, Lin Jing first let his clone enjoy its use.

In addition, he brought out a mutated Blood Bodhi tree and placed it beside the pool.

Although their minds were connected, Lin Jing still explained to the clone, “Just take the Demonic God Body Pill without worry. If there’s a conflict between the Demonic God Body, Spotless Divine Body, and Heavenly Demon Body, prioritize survival but force the physiques to coexist. After all, it’s a rare seventh-grade God Body Pill…”

“Don’t worry about getting injured from physique conflict. This Blood Bodhi was born for that exact purpose. If you get hurt, rely on the Comprehension Pool and the Blood Bodhi to comprehend a way for the physiques to harmonize.”

“The Dragon Carp… will also help you heal nearby using the Nether Immortal Flame.”

“As expected of myself.” The clone was very satisfied with the original’s arrangements—truly meticulous.

After speaking, the Heavenly Demon clone, who was still at the Golden Core Stage, immediately swallowed the God Body Pill. In the next moment, the pill began altering the clone’s bloodline, making his expression change drastically.

“This is…” The clone’s pupils shrank. This Demonic God Body was immensely overbearing. Using the Heavenly Demon Technique, he had already transformed his heart into a black Heavenly Demon Heart, which was the foundation of the Heavenly Demon Body.

“This demonic bloodline Demonic God Body actually enhances the Heavenly Demon Body.” Lin Jing was surprised, and what came next exceeded even his expectations.

Currently, the clone was cultivating the Heavenly Demon Tome, Heavenly Demon Finger, and Heavenly Demon Crossing!

Heavenly Demon Body and Heavenly Demon Spiritual Sense were inherited from the main body’s cultivation experience, so they didn’t require deliberate training and could progress smoothly. The other three, however, had to be cultivated independently by the clone.

This was one of the clone’s purposes—to cultivate second-tier techniques the main body couldn’t, and to train spells the main body had no time for.

Initially, the clone’s progress in these Heavenly Demon secret arts was average at best. The requirements were harsh, but thanks to sharing the sixth place on the Golden Ranking, things had been progressing steadily.

But now, after consuming the God Body Pill, the clone underwent a strange transformation. Lin Jing noticed that the Heavenly Demon Tome, which had been stuck at the third layer, suddenly broke through to the fourth. Spiritual energy surged wildly into the clone, and his cultivation soared like he had taken steroids, charging from Golden Core to Nascent Soul without the slightest resistance.

Not only did the Heavenly Demon Body perfectly match the demonic bloodline, but even the Heavenly Demon Tome fit it flawlessly.

It was like a cultivator with the Innate Fire God Physique practicing fire-type techniques—perfect synergy, astonishing progress. Lin Jing’s original self, with his Longevity Body cultivating the Human Control Technique, had never experienced this level of harmony.

Moreover, the clone had only been at the entry stage of Heavenly Demon Finger. Since it required the consumption of others’ “inner demons” to advance, it hadn’t progressed much. To boost its power, inner demon force was essential. But now, Lin Jing noticed that even without actively seeking it out, faint, imperceptible negative energies from the world were being passively absorbed by the clone, enhancing the power of Heavenly Demon Finger...

These negative fluctuations weren’t as powerful as inner demon force, but they were far easier to acquire...

【That Elder Zhao is such a two-faced scumbag. He hogged all the credit for the technique I improved. I swear I’ll kill that old dog someday.】

【That b*tch Yan Shuangping! We were engaged, and she still went off with that steward for a few spirit stones. I’ll kill their whole family once I form my Nascent Soul!】

【That bastard Cao Yunfei from the inner sect—just a commoner physique, yet he placed eighth in the tournament. He must have some hidden cheat...】

All sorts of hard-to-detect negative thoughts poured into the clone’s body, loosening the bottleneck of Heavenly Demon Finger.

And then there was Heavenly Demon Crossing, which had only been viewed once and hadn’t even reached the introductory stage—it suddenly matured in an instant.

At that moment, the clone felt as if he could physically touch the ripples in the void, like brushing against water’s surface. His body could almost retreat fully into it, crossing the void. That mysterious sensation was unlike anything he had ever experienced.

“The Demonic God Body greatly amplifies all Heavenly Demon Sect secret arts.”

“Incredible. These secret arts were supposedly created by the Heavenly Demon Sect in the Immortal Realm and passed down from the upper realms.”

“If that’s the case, and the demonic bloodline from the God Body Pill enhances these techniques... then doesn’t that mean the bloodline is directly tied to the Heavenly Demon Sect of the Immortal Realm?”

“Could it be… that cultivators of the Heavenly Demon Sect in the Immortal Realm aren’t entirely human or demonic beast hybrids—but are the so-called ‘Demonic Race’?!”

“These Heavenly Demon secret arts can only reach their full potential in the hands of cultivators with the demonic bloodline…”

“That might really be possible. After all, the Immortal Palace isn’t a faction of the Immortal Realm. Back when it still maintained contact with the upper realms, its alchemists could refine all sorts of powerful items using rare bloodlines sent down from above. This God Body Pill probably has a long history.”

“The Demonic Race… I should ask Senior Mo Pine about this. Maybe he knows something he hasn’t told me yet!”

A complete surprise—but it now seemed this Heavenly Demon clone’s future achievements might far exceed his original expectations.

……

At the same time.

In Qingzhou.

Just as Lin Jing’s clone acquired the demonic bloodline, far away in the retirement cultivation site on Immortal Prison Mountain, Mo Pine suddenly trembled, his expression drastically changing.

A sense of dread from the depths of his soul sent chills through his old pine body.

“This is bad. This is really bad.”

Gu Qianqiu felt the tremor in Senior Mo Pine and immediately rushed over.

“Senior? Senior?!”

She called out a few times before Mo Pine finally recovered. He looked at Gu Qianqiu with great seriousness. “This is bad. Someone from the Heavenly Demon Realm has arrived!!”

Gu Qianqiu was shocked. “What? They’re already here? The Pathseekers...”
