She looked toward the sky—it was too peaceful. There was no trace of battle outside.

“Senior, are you sure? Is this real??”

“It seems the Heavenly Demon Realm’s infiltration methods have evolved. I’m absolutely certain—no mistake. And the cultivator who descended this time isn’t someone like me, plucked from other realms to be used and discarded. This is a core member of the Heavenly Demon Sect, someone granted true demonic blood by the Immortal Realm’s Demon Sect! If they had sent elites like this from the start, the First Generation Ancient Emperor wouldn’t have gotten off so easily!”

“To keep me under control, they planted restrictions deep within my soul that make me innately fearful of and sensitive to demonic blood. I absolutely did not sense wrong—there is a demonic cultivator from the Demonic Race in the Tianyuan Realm now. What the h*ll are those Pathseekers doing?” Mo Pine was terrified—scared that they’d come after him and regain control over his bloodline.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 110: Don’t Panic
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 110: Don’t Panic

“Cannon fodder…”

“Demonic sect cultivator…”

When she saw that Senior Mo Pine had only referred to himself as cannon fodder in the Heavenly Demon Sect, and that he was genuinely afraid of the elite Demonic Bloodline cultivators from the sect, Gu Qianqiu realized something was wrong.

If even someone like Senior Mo Pine—who was just "cannon fodder"—had once caused the Ancient Kingdom and the first-generation Ancient Emperor so much trouble, then how terrifying must the true elites of the Heavenly Demon Sect be?

“Should we contact the Demon Court Lord and inform the Seekers of the Way?” Gu Qianqiu asked.

“We can’t get in touch with the Demon Court Lord ourselves. Find Lin Jing—he can contact the Demon Emperor,” Mo Pine said immediately.

His past identity was a bit sensitive. Besides the two Longevity Body cultivators he had personally cultivated and an out-of-world demon whose reincarnation whereabouts were unknown, not even the Demon Emperor knew about him.

In a case like this, he could only give Lin Jing a heads-up and let him deal with the Seekers of the Way.

Mo Pine directly summoned the Pine Leaf Mouse and Dragon Carp incarnations that were cultivating, farming, and refining pills in Immortal Prison Mountain.

The incarnations were tightly connected. Although it was normally difficult for mere Nascent Soul incarnations to sense each other across the vast distance between Qingzhou and the Ancient Kingdom, Mo Pine had a special formation to enhance that connection, so this wasn’t a problem.

Meanwhile—

Back Mountain of Lingdao Sect, Enlightenment Pool.

Having fully absorbed the Demonic Bloodline, Lin Jing’s Heavenly Demon Incarnation had reached the Illusory Nascent stage—commonly referred to as a "half-step Nascent Soul," just one step away from true Nascent Soul.

The reason he hadn’t broken through yet was because cultivators who trained in the Heavenly Demon Tome could devour a Fortune Dragon Vein during breakthrough to build a stronger foundation. At the moment, it wasn’t a good idea to let the incarnation devour Lingdao Sect’s dragon vein directly. So he would wait for the top-grade cultivation dragon vein promised by the second-generation Ancient Emperor to arrive before having the incarnation break through.

At that time, he could also feed it a cultivation Golden Core refined by Rare Beasts.

“So strong…” Lin Jing was amazed as he sensed the incarnation’s power. The fleshly strength and mana density of the Demonic God Body were simply overwhelming.

In terms of physique, even Lin Jing’s true body—who had trained indestructible body-forging arts to perfection at the Golden Core stage, and activated his bodily limits using the Ice Soul Gu’s Divine Art—was still somewhat inferior to this incarnation.

As for mana, Lin Jing’s true self had reached the thirteenth layer of Qi Refining and completed Foundation Establishment, with a rock-solid foundation. Yet even he felt that the incarnation’s “demonic god mana” was unfathomable.

This Demonic God Body was absolutely one of the closest physiques to a true Immortal Body—utterly domineering.

Of course, this wasn’t solely due to the Demonic Bloodline. After all, Lin Jing’s incarnation had been created using his top-grade Nascent Soul and a quasi-immortal item, the Immortal Water Hibiscus, so its foundation was already extremely powerful.

It was the fusion of these two factors that gave rise to such an invincible Heavenly Demon Incarnation.

“Bwop~”

Just as Lin Jing was marveling at his incarnation’s might, the Pine Leaf Mouse and Dragon Carp suddenly cried out. He paused for a moment, then brought them over and placed his hands on their heads.

In the next instant, it was as if his spirit was linked to a distant shore. A familiar voice traveled across this mental connection and rang in his ears.

“Kid, we’ve got a problem. Cultivators from the Heavenly Demon Sect have invaded the Tianyuan Continent, and the Seekers of the Way don’t seem to have noticed at all!!”

Lin Jing’s expression changed, but then an odd look crossed his face.

Because… he’d already been duped by “Heavenly Demon Sect invasion” alerts several times now. Once, twice… eight times?

This time, Lin Jing wasn’t falling for it again.

“Stop kidding, old man. The realm wall has been reinforced multiple layers by the Seekers of the Way. Even if cultivators from the Heavenly Demon Realm have mastered the ‘Heavenly Demon Crossing,’ there’s no way they’d enter Tianyuan without anyone noticing.”

Mo Pine continued, “This time’s different. Very different.”

“There are three types of cultivators in the Heavenly Demon Realm. First are the young seedlings from other cultivation realms—those with decent potential and still young—who are captured during invasions. These are the lowest-ranking members of the Heavenly Demon Sect, usually assigned to remote cultivation planets to serve as scouts. Their job is to probe whether they can occupy a realm with minimal cost—or else act as cannon fodder in war.”

“I was one of them.”

“Don’t be fooled by the title of Heavenly Demon Sect Master I had in the Tianyuan Continent. In the Heavenly Demon Realm, I was just cannon fodder.”

“The second type are native-born Heavenly Demon Realm cultivators. They were the earliest to be trained directly by the Immortal Realm’s Heavenly Demon Sect and usually hold key positions. The resources and treatment they enjoy are worlds apart from ours.”

“The third type—those are the elite among elites. So exceptional that the Immortal Realm’s Heavenly Demon Sect would grant them Demon Blood, transforming them into members of the Demonic Race.”

“They are the real core of the sect. Each one has power on par with, or even greater than, the first-generation Ancient Emperor.”

“You probably don’t understand what the Demonic Race really is... In fact, I’ve been hiding something from you. Cultivators like me, snatched from other realms to be trained by the Heavenly Demon Sect, are all branded with seals that induce deep fear of the Demonic Race. You normally can’t tell, but once we meet a demonic cultivator, our strength is suppressed to less than ten percent.”

“So, if the ones who descended are demonic cultivators, that would explain everything. The Demonic Race doesn’t exist in the lower realms—they’re a major clan of the Immortal Realm. The Demon Lord himself has this bloodline. Even the weakest among them rivals top-tier divine physiques from the lower realms.”

“Right now, even the Demon Court Lord who’s succeeded in demon transformation still falls short of the first-generation Ancient Emperor. So if these demonic cultivators really have descended, it’s no surprise no one noticed.”

It was rare for Senior Mo Pine to ramble on like this. Clearly, he was genuinely afraid of the demonic cultivators. This was the first time Lin Jing had ever seen the old demon so rattled, and his expression grew even weirder.

Demonic Bloodline… Demonic cultivators… That reminded Lin Jing of the God Body Pill he had just fed to his incarnation.

“Senior, let’s say—just hypothetically—that the demonic cultivators who descended to Tianyuan are only at the Golden Core or Nascent Soul stage. Would they pose a threat to you?” Lin Jing asked.

“Hm?” Mo Pine didn’t understand the question. “Nascent Soul cultivators, even with the Demonic Bloodline, how could they affect me? But kid, don’t forget—anyone who’s good enough to be granted Demon Blood from the upper realm would have mastered every secret technique of the Heavenly Demon Sect, including the Heavenly Demon Physique. Even if they’re just Nascent Soul, they could easily reach Divine Transformation. In that case... if the suppression kicks in, even Heaven might not help me. If they come for me, I can’t guarantee what’ll happen.”

“In any case, contact the Demon Court Lord right away. Inform the Seekers of the Way and get them to track the intruder down. You don’t want your beloved guardian Mo Pine to be turned into some d*mn Heavenly Demon’s pet, right?!”

Lin Jing comforted him, “Don’t panic, Senior. There’s probably no demon invasion. I accidentally obtained a Demonic Bloodline from a small world and infused it into my incarnation. What you sensed... was probably just my incarnation. So... does that mean you can’t beat my incarnation now? Seriously?”

Mo Pine: ?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 111: The Undercover Plan
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 111: The Undercover Plan

“F**k.” Mo Pine was stunned.

What he had sensed—was Lin Jing?

Lin Jing’s incarnation had fused with Demonic Bloodline?

“B*llshit! How could something that ridiculous happen?!” Mo Pine exploded with curses. “You brat, you’re not pulling my leg, are you?”

A small world…

D*mn it. That Immortal Palace organization really had the audacity to extract the blood of Immortal Realm’s Demonic Race and give it as a reward to lower-realm prodigies?

But, thinking again… that wasn’t entirely impossible. Mo Pine’s expression shifted rapidly.

Before the Demon Lord broke through to Immortal Emperor, the Demonic Race was indeed one of the top clans in the Immortal Realm—but not so high as to be untouchable. An Immortal Palace with an Immortal King backing them could very well have had the ability to capture members of the Demonic Race for pill refining.

“Why would I lie to you?” Lin Jing laughed. “The odds of Heavenly Demon Sect cultivators slipping past a crowd of Seekers of the Way unnoticed and invading this realm are clearly way lower than what I just said. Don’t worry, senior—I swear I won’t let my incarnation get within ten thousand miles of you!”

Mo Pine snorted, “Screw you! Just a mere demonic cultivator—what’s there to be afraid of? I was only exaggerating the threat to make you take it seriously. Otherwise, if I really met one, I’d handle it myself.”

“Sure, sure.” Lin Jing played along to save Mo Pine some face. But Mo Pine was still a bit shocked. “You brat actually got your hands on Demonic Bloodline…”

“You really do have some luck on your side. In my view, with how things stand in the lower realms right now, having Demonic Bloodline is even more meaningful than having an Immortal Body.”

“You know what this means?”

“What?”

“Items from the Immortal Realm aren’t easy to transmit to the lower realms. Which means, demonic cultivators down here are extremely rare.”

“If your incarnation goes to any cultivation continent already taken over by the Heavenly Demon Sect, but without a demonic cultivator stationed there—so long as your disguise holds—you’ll be treated like an honored guest. The other Heavenly Demon Sect members will consider you a big shot!”

“The sect’s influence spreads throughout the lower realms. As long as you don’t stir up any major drama, no one will bother investigating your origins... After all, Demonic Bloodline is nearly impossible to fake. Who would've thought the ancient Immortal Palace was crazy enough to extract blood from Immortal Realm clans just to ‘enhance’ some genius.”

“Is that so…” Lin Jing hesitated, then said, “According to intel, there are quite a few Heavenly Demon Sect cultivators in the Origin Realm now. If my incarnation enters with a demonic identity… maybe I could blend in?”

“My real body enters the Cultivation Alliance. The incarnation joins the Heavenly Demon Sect. Inside-out coordination—maybe we can get our hands on more Origin Realm resources that way!”

“Exactly!!” Mo Pine cackled with glee, completely back to his normal self. “The Heavenly Demon Sect members that entered the Origin Realm are basically cut off from external communication. Under such circumstances, you could fabricate any identity and integrate easily.”

“Actually, you don’t even have to make it up from scratch. I’ve still got scraps of info in my memory—tons of Heavenly Demon Sect intelligence I can give you.”

“For example, there was once a demonic cultivator named Heavenly Hatred Demon Empress. She vanished during her return to the ‘Heavenly Demon Realm’ after accidentally falling into a star rift. Presumed dead, maybe erased from existence.”

“Once you verify the details, you could claim she’s not dead—just recovering somewhere—and that you’re her temporary disciple. That would instantly elevate you to a top-tier second-gen demon!”

“And that’s just one story. I’ve got plenty more. You can pick whichever fits best.”

“Of course, there will still be some holes in the story, but it’ll be enough to let you command a faction of Heavenly Demon Sect elites for a while—especially in the Origin Realm, where barely any Heavenly Demon Sect elites have Demonic Bloodline.”

“So basically, once your incarnation reaches the Origin Realm, there’s a good chance you’ll be one of the most noble-blooded members in the local Heavenly Demon Sect!”

“Sooo, kid—you’re seriously lucky… Wait a sec. Your real body’s joining the Lin Jing Alliance? What’s that?” Mo Pine suddenly asked.

A cultivator nearby explained, “The Lin Jing Alliance is a group formed over the past ten thousand years by exiled cultivators from the Tangwen Region, who fled before the Heavenly Demon Sect took over. It’s currently made up of refugees from various Tangwen continents. Though still developing, it’s already strong enough to oppose the Heavenly Demon Realm in some areas. When the junior first arrived in the Tianyuan Continent, the Lin Jing Alliance hadn’t yet taken shape.”

“Brilliant. Your real body joins the Cultivation Alliance to gather resources, your incarnation taps into the Heavenly Demon Sect’s stockpile. In no time, you’ll be ready for Seeking the Way!”

Lin Jing gave a wry smile. “Let’s hope so…”

Because if something went wrong and he got exposed, the backlash would be brutal.

After wrapping up his discussion with Mo Pine, Lin Jing turned to managing access to the Enlightenment Pool, planning to let each of his pet beasts take turns using it.

Even though, according to Mo Pine, he had a huge advantage going into the Origin Realm, Lin Jing still didn’t want to get complacent. He had to keep strengthening his base power just in case.

First up was the Ice Soul Gu. Lin Jing tossed it into the Enlightenment Pool and had it start studying disciplines beyond its usual Blood Gu poison arts—

Like swordsmanship.

With a mutated Bodhi Leaf in its belly, and Hunyuan Sword Intent and Guardian Sword Intent of the Seekers of the Way as its enlightenment subjects, plus a range of external assistance, the Ice Soul Gu would definitely achieve swift mastery in sword arts.

After glancing at the Ice Soul Gu—currently controlling dozens of icy swords with spider-silk-like threads in the pool—Lin Jing’s real body threw out a flying sword toward the Heavenly Sword Sect. Then he transmitted intel about the Two-World Small World to the Demon Emperor before preparing to claim his promised rewards.

The top-tier dragon vein promised by the second-generation Ancient Emperor was due within half a year—no use rushing it.

But what Lin Jing did need urgently were the Heavenly Flame Gu, the final one of the Ten Great Rare Gu, in the hands of Liu Gongzhuo (a head of the Demon Extermination Division), and the high-tier demonic criminals (above Nascent Soul level) imprisoned by the Division—key to leveling up his pet beasts.

Not long after, at the headquarters of the Demon Extermination Division—

Just as Lin Jing arrived, ready to speak with Liu Gongzhuo, he witnessed something thoroughly frustrating:

An elder with a vaguely familiar face came flying in, shouting at the top of his lungs, “I’m Gao Zhao! Which shameless b*stard took my grandson? I just got back from a mission—I’d love to see who gave me such a huge ‘surprise’! Let go of my boy, now!”

“…Lin Jing.”

A voice emerged from within the Demon Extermination Division headquarters, calm and firm: “Brother Gao, calm down. Your grandson violated the Ancient Kingdom’s law by trying to trade a Fortune Dragon Vein. Even if he’s your grandson, he must be tried through legal procedures…”

Gao Sheng’s grandfather’s face twisted, clearly confused—he couldn’t figure out who the Gao Family or Gao Sheng had offended to end up being framed like this.

Meanwhile, Lin Jing, who had quietly arrived, fell into deep thought.

Seriously?

Gao Sheng had the Fortune Physique, but it only worked half the time?

You’re the descendant of a demon-hunting aristocrat and a second-gen cultivator—how did you get caught stealing a dragon vein to resurrect the first Ancient Emperor, right under your own family's nose???

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 112: Internal Strife
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 112: Internal Strife

From within the Demon Extermination Division, a man with a pale and sinister face stepped out. He was a mid-stage Divine Transformation cultivator. He looked at the furious Gao Zhao and said, “The Crown Prince is currently handling state affairs, and one of his directives is a full investigation into recent dragon vein smuggling cases. This is a crucial time—Brother Gao, please don’t make things difficult for me. I’m just following orders.”

Gao Sheng’s grandfather, Gao Zhao, narrowed his eyes. “Don’t try to pass off a feather as an imperial decree. If you’ve got something to say, say it to my face. Framing a junior? Shame on you.”

He snorted coldly, already forming a theory in his mind. The Gao Family was under one of the five Division Heads of the Demon Extermination Division—Tongzi Xian.

After Lin Jing revealed his Heavenly Demon Physique, Tongzi Xian’s political enemies used it as a pretext to attack him, and his faction’s power took a massive hit.

Still, while his adoptive father’s influence had waned, fate had smiled on him. Tongzi Xian had used this golden era to unexpectedly break through to half-step Void Mystical Stage. If he managed to fully step into the Void Mystical Stage, then regardless of outside pressure, the Gao Family could once again thrive for a thousand years.

Gao Zhao figured that Tongzi Xian’s weakened authority, and his own advancement being just one step away, was exactly why he had been sent on a high-risk mission recently. It was a near-death assignment, and fortunately he had survived. But the moment he returned, he found that his beloved grandson had been accused of smuggling dragon veins and was now imprisoned—how could he tolerate that?

Gao Zhao was certain that the person who had sent him on that suicidal mission and had framed his grandson was none other than Gai You, one of Tongzi Xian’s strongest rivals among the five Division Heads of the Demon Extermination Division.

The five Division Heads were: Tongzi Xian, Liu Gongzhuo, Gai You, E Gui, and Sikong Jianluo.

Liu Gongzhuo came from a commoner background and lacked powerful connections to pull strings. On top of that, he carried unstable Gu worms within him, making advancement difficult.

Only Tongzi Xian and Gai You had any real shot at climbing higher. And now, Tongzi Xian had made a critical mistake—he had taught the secret technique of the Heavenly Demon Physique to Lin Jing, leading to the rise of a terrifying Holy Son of the Demon Court. That alone gave Gai You ample ammunition to attack.

Still, Gao Zhao never expected him to go this far. It wasn’t enough to target Tongzi Xian and himself—he even went after his grandson…

“Brother Gao, the evidence is solid. I understand how you feel, but please don’t cause a scene,” the man said.

“Cut the crap. If you’re gonna slap a charge on someone, at least make it believable! What the hell would my grandson want with a dragon vein? He doesn’t even like cultivating!” Gao Zhao roared, his voice echoing throughout the Demon Extermination Division.

At that moment, two ripples of Void Mystical energy surged from inside the building.

One of them belonged to Division Head Gai You, who was accompanied by a young man bound in mana chains—Gao Sheng.

The other was a red-haired man—Division Head Liu Gongzhuo.

“Gao Sheng!” Upon seeing his grandson, Gao Zhao’s expression shifted, but he still greeted them with respect. “Greetings, Division Heads Gai and Liu. Surely there’s been some kind of misunderstanding?”

“Heh, a misunderstanding?” Gai You’s tone was icy. “Gao Zhao, are you suggesting I’m framing your grandson? Or that the Sect Administration Office is? Your grandson tried to trade dragon veins through the Office, and the report landed on my desk. There’s eyewitness testimony and physical evidence. Division Head Liu can vouch for it too. What else do you have to say?”

“As one of the Deputy Division Heads of the Demon Extermination Division—is this how you raise your descendants?”

“I’ve also heard your grandson has a Fortune Physique. Could it be that your whole plan to acquire a dragon vein was related to his constitution?”

Gao Sheng tried to speak but couldn’t—his mouth was still bound. He was furious.

D*mn it! Why was it that cultivators from the Feng Family of Martial Emperor City could buy dragon veins from the Sect Administration Office, but when he spent just as much, this happened to him?

There was collusion! That old ghost Gai must have something shady going on with the Office!

“According to the laws of the Ancient Kingdom, illegal trading and smuggling of dragon veins is punishable by death!” Gai You declared.

Hearing this, Gao Zhao’s eyes turned red. He wanted to protest, but at the moment, only Gai You was speaking. While he doubted Gao Sheng would be stupid enough to do such a thing, with the Sect Administration Office involved, Gao Zhao had to tread carefully.

Even Tongzi Xian himself held the character of Hao Yuncai, the Office’s head, in high regard. Without backing from a top-tier family, she wouldn’t have risen to such a position. It was highly unlikely that either Tongzi Xian or Gai You could manipulate her.

“Gao Sheng’s identity isn’t ordinary,” Liu Gongzhuo said. “That’s why we didn’t torture a confession out of him. We wanted him to confess willingly. Perhaps he can get a lighter sentence that way.”

As he spoke, Liu Gongzhuo released the restriction on Gao Sheng’s voice.

Gao Zhao glared at his grandson. “Is what the Division Heads said true?!”

Gao Sheng spoke with difficulty, “Grandfather… it’s true…”

“You little sh*t!” Gao Zhao exploded in anger. He had thought his grandson was being framed—but it was true?

The Gao Family was already walking a fine line politically. And now this idiot had to stir up more trouble?

“Why the hell did you buy that thing? Did someone trick you? Threaten you?” Gao Zhao still couldn’t understand what this dumb kid wanted a dragon vein for. As far as he knew, cultivating with a Fortune Physique didn’t require one. And even if it did—was this something to buy on the black market?

In the Ancient Kingdom, all dragon veins were “national assets.” All sects and cultivator clans were only granted usage rights—subject to government approval. What Gao Sheng did was outright illegal.

“I…” Gao Sheng was at a loss for words. What was he supposed to say—that he bought the dragon veins to resurrect the first-generation Ancient Emperor?

Honestly, even he didn’t think anyone would believe that.

“Just tell the truth,” came a new voice—causing Liu Gongzhuo and Gai You’s expressions to change. They hadn’t even noticed the newcomer’s arrival.

“Who?!”

Both Void Mystical cultivators scanned the area, only to see a figure step out of a ripple in space—Lin Jing.

Even Lin Jing had to admit—Heavenly Demon Crossing was crazy strong. It wasn’t just a top-tier teleportation technique. When paired with a space-concealing artifact, even Void Mystical cultivators struggled to detect it.

“The Holy Son of the Demon Court?!” everyone gasped.

Lin Jing looked at the two Division Heads and said, “I’m the one who asked Gao Sheng to find a Fortune Dragon Vein.”

The moment he spoke, Gao Zhao’s face turned pale.

Liu Gongzhuo and Gai You’s expressions shifted as well.

With just a single sentence, what was originally a case of a Demon Extermination aristocrat smuggling dragon veins had now escalated—to collusion with the demon race, threatening the national fate of the Ancient Kingdom!

“Brother Lin, hurry and bring out Master!” Gao Sheng was dying inside. If they didn’t bring out his original master—the First Generation Ancient Emperor—he’d be executed and his whole clan purged.

Lin Jing gave a helpless nod. At this point, there was no other option. He would have to summon the First Generation Ancient Emperor here and let him clean up the mess Gao Sheng had made.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 113: The Rise of the Ancient Kingdom
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 113: The Rise of the Ancient Kingdom

For a moment, the few of them stood frozen in a deadlock.

Lin Jing looked into the distance, then tugged at his contract with the Mechanical Tortoise. A little more force, and the contract would be broken.

This was the only method Lin Jing could think of to ensure the other party would instantly sense him, no matter where the tortoise was.

Sure enough—

As soon as Lin Jing tore a crack in the contract, the Mechanical Tortoise, who was busy spying on a little girl somewhere in the Ancient Kingdom, felt a sharp pain in its soul.

“Got it.” The Mechanical Tortoise reluctantly followed the ripple of the contract’s aura. As the distance between it and Lin Jing closed, it soon heard the message transmitted via the bond:

Come immediately!

At the same time, as Lin Jing suddenly fell silent, Liu Gongzhuo and Gai You broke out in a cold sweat. Even though their cultivation far surpassed Lin Jing’s, the vicious record of this Holy Son of the Demon Court left them wary.

Swish.

Gai You secretly contacted the Ancient Prince, who now handled state affairs, briefly reporting the situation and waiting for instructions.

The Holy Son of the Demon Court… if not handled carefully, this could spiral into a serious diplomatic crisis.

Meanwhile, in the Celestial Domain, within the Tower of Heavenly Secrets—

To fulfill his agreement with Lin Jing, the Ancient Prince was currently asking the tower master to help locate “Daoist Qing Niu.”

“How is this possible? At most, the guy is a Foundation Establishment demonic cultivator—how could we find no trace of his existence?!” The Ancient Prince couldn’t believe the tower master’s words.

“It’s true. Though we only know of his past locations, alias, and some of his experiences—if he were truly just a Foundation Establishment cultivator, it should be easy to find him or confirm his life or death. Yet now, not even a shred of presence can be detected. There’s only one explanation...”

“This ‘Daoist Qing Niu’ Your Highness speaks of—the one who sold the Holy Son of the Demon Court to the Yin Corpse Sect—is clearly far more than just a Foundation Establishment demonic cultivator. His true cultivation or mastery of the Divination Path may be beyond imagination,” the tower master said.

“But that just raises more questions. If this Qing Niu Daoist is so powerful, he wouldn't be someone who casually sells a Longevity Body—a body so rare and precious—to some minor outpost of the Yin Corpse Sect. Someone who would do that ought to be a short-sighted Foundation rogue cultivator, not some great expert.”

Unbelievable.

The Ancient Prince had thought the biggest hurdle in this deal with Lin Jing might be that Qing Niu Daoist was already dead, and that he’d have to use forbidden arts to drag out his soul and deliver him to the Holy Son. He never expected Qing Niu Daoist to be nowhere near what he appeared to be.

Just then, the Ancient Prince noticed Gai You’s urgent message from the Demon Extermination Division.

But, still deep in puzzlement, the Ancient Prince couldn’t be bothered to deal with such trivial matters. He simply ignored the message and continued pressing the tower master for more details.

After all, the Holy Son of the Demon Court had made it clear—if there were no results within a year, he would consider the deal broken and challenge the Ancient Prince for the top spot on the Golden Ranking.

“Could it be the Holy Son already found him, and used an immortal artifact to erase all traces of his existence—just to mess with me?” the Ancient Prince wondered.

“Is there truly no other way?” he asked. At this point, the only hope he had of finding Qing Niu Daoist was the tower master.

“Try awakening the Starry Universe Turtle again. Then revisit the incident site in the Ancient Region and investigate further,” the tower master sighed.

The Ancient Prince nodded.

There was nothing else to do.

But just then—

“Stop investigating. Stop. I’m Qing Niu Daoist.”

At the sight of this man, the Ancient Prince’s face changed, and the tower master’s eyes widened in disbelief.

“Master?!”

“The previous master of the Tower of Heavenly Secrets,” the Ancient Prince said, astonished.

“You—You’re not...?” The tower master stared at Qing Niu Daoist in shock.

He clearly remembered his master had already “ascended in meditation.”

“Didn’t die. Didn’t die. After the first generation left, I didn’t want to work for the second gen, so I faked my death and wandered the continent,” Qing Niu Daoist said while picking his nose, leaving the Ancient Prince momentarily stunned.

“Don’t look at me like that. Even while traveling, I still helped dig up some geniuses for the Ancient Kingdom!” Qing Niu Daoist turned to the Ancient Prince.

“Master, are you saying… you were the one who sold the Holy Son of the Demon Court to that Yin Corpse Sect outpost?” the tower master asked in disbelief.

Qing Niu Daoist looked bitter. “When I first met the kid, he was still a mortal. I gave him a quick divination and found his destiny was enormous—he could revive the Ancient Kingdom. But it required him to walk the Beast Taming Path and not be interfered with too much during his growth… So I calculated that tossing him into a Yin Corpse Sect outpost, where I knew a Beast Master Sect spy was hiding, would eventually push him toward the Beast Master Sect. I sold him for a few spirit stones. At first, things were going great...”

The current tower master and Ancient Prince: ???

Revive the Ancient Kingdom?

Are you sure it’s not the Demon Court that’s being revived?

So this is the kind of “genius” you’ve been digging up while traveling the continent?

“Master… that Lin Jing is now the Holy Son of the Demon Court. He’s even discovered a way to revive the demon race. He’s completely broken ties with the Ancient Kingdom. Forget about reviving the kingdom—if the kingdom doesn’t get destroyed by the rising demon race, that would be a win.” The tower master fell silent.

Qing Niu Daoist looked just as glum. When he turned his attention back to Lin Jing, the kid was already the Holy Son of the Demon Court!

That totally didn’t match his original divination!

“I don’t get it either. My skill at reading people was taught by the First Generation Ancient Emperor himself. On top of that, I’ve reached the Seeking the Way stage—how could I be so off when reading the fate of a mere mortal…?”

“Where exactly did things go wrong? It’s a pity—I can no longer see his fate clearly…”

“This guy is such a pain.” Even though they’d found Qing Niu Daoist, the Ancient Prince didn’t feel relieved at all. How could things have turned out so ridiculous? Qing Niu Daoist turned out to be the previous master of the Tower of Heavenly Secrets.

So now how was he supposed to “hand over” this “Qing Niu Daoist” to Lin Jing?

What pissed him off even more was—this former tower master was saying that Lin Jing’s destiny was to revive the Ancient Kingdom…

“What is it now?” Gai You’s constant emergency messages pushed the Ancient Prince to his limit. The guy had pinged him over a dozen times in just a short span. Finally, the Ancient Prince responded, slightly annoyed.

“…What the hell is all this?” Upon reading that Gai You was accusing Lin Jing of colluding with Gao Family’s Gao Sheng to smuggle dragon veins, the Ancient Prince had a massive headache.

The Ancient Kingdom still owed the Holy Son a top-grade cultivation dragon vein! If Lin Jing wanted one, he could’ve just negotiated openly with the Prince and asked for more terms.

Why the hell would he need to use some minor cultivator from a demon-hunting family to smuggle it?!

And this was what Gai You spammed him about?!

‘Your Highness, they say the dragon vein was meant to summon the First Generation Ancient Emperor! To bring back the old emperor and revive the kingdom! This Holy Son of the Demon Court is spreading nonsense and even wants to use the Demon Extermination Division’s dragon veins! I suggest...’

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 114: The Fortune Dragon Emperor
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 114: The Fortune Dragon Emperor

"Absurd!"

"Utterly absurd!"

"This Holy Son of the Demon Court truly believes our Ancient Kingdom can't deal with him?" The Ancient Imperial Prince's face darkened.

Lin Jing bringing the Beast Master Sect under the Demon Court's banner was understandable—after all, the Ancient Kingdom had failed to protect his Longevity Body, and their policies had alienated the sect.

But now that Lin Jing had the Demon Court's backing, did he really think he could act recklessly in the Ancient Kingdom? The prince would never allow it.

First came the disrespectful words toward the second-generation emperor, and now he was using the name of the founding emperor to forcibly seize the Demon Extermination Division's dragon vein...

"Chief Gai, what was your suggestion again?"

Even if Chief Gai proposed arresting this Holy Son of the Demon Court on the spot, the prince would agree. Otherwise, this arrogant fellow would truly treat the Ancient Kingdom as his personal playground.

His face showed utter shock, mouth hanging open as he stared at Lin Jing holding a turtle.

"Having done this once before makes the second summoning much easier. The Divine Scripture already has the imprint." With the mechanical turtle in place and borrowing the Demon Extermination Division's dragon vein energy, Lin Jing had it summon the founding emperor once more.

As the figure from memory materialized before them, Chief Gai stood dumbfounded.

He'd glimpsed this person once in his youth while accompanying his ancestor.

"F-Founding..." The words escaped his lips unconsciously. Nearby, Chief Liu and Gao Zhao were equally wide-eyed, unable to believe what they were seeing.

The founding emperor?!

"W-wait a minute!"

Chief Gai suddenly noticed this "founding emperor" only had Nascent Soul Transformation early-stage cultivation, and his form differed significantly from a living person.

A fake!

Instantly, he understood—this Holy Son must be using some evil illusion technique to defile the founding emperor's image.

Fury surged through him, mixed with cold amusement. Getting involved with this Demon Court scoundrel meant the Gao family and their backer Tongzi Xian would never recover.

The position of Demon Extermination Division Chief that Gao Sheng had risen to? It would undoubtedly be his now—if he handled this situation properly.

"Hmm...?" Meanwhile, the founding emperor awoke again to the surrounding commotion and sighed.

What had happened now? Why had that unlucky fellow Gao Sheng been captured by the Demon Extermination Division again?

"Master!!!" Seeing the founding emperor, Gao Sheng became more emotional than if meeting his own grandfather, tears streaming as he cried out: "Save me! I just wanted to gather more dragon veins to revive you, and they arrested me!"

"These Administration Bureau bastards—when people from Martial Emperor City come to smuggle dragon veins, nothing happens! But when I try to get some for proper use, they immediately report me to Chief Gai here! What kind of justice is this..."

"Internal strife runs deep while colluding with external forces—how can our kingdom prosper like this?"

Lin Jing hadn't expected Gao Sheng to be so eloquent. The founding emperor also grew furious upon hearing this.

He naturally knew about the Administration Bureau's dragon vein smuggling—the Feng Family Ancestor had personally mentioned it.

Now Gao Sheng brought it up again, reigniting his anger. He understood all organizations had some corruption, but this Administration Bureau went too far.

If the illegal dragon vein trade stayed within the kingdom, it wouldn't be so bad—the enhanced power would still benefit some domestic faction. But exporting dragon veins? The Bureau's leaders truly deserved death.

"You're quite close with the Administration Bureau, aren't you?"

The founding emperor looked at Chief Gai. "If I recall correctly, even if cultivators attempted to obtain dragon veins from them, that wouldn't fall under the Demon Extermination Division's jurisdiction, would it?"

"Or did you plan to use my disciple here for some scheme?"

Gai You frowned—how dare this imposter keep pretending!

"Chief Liu, assist me in capturing this fraud! If he's the founding emperor, then I'm the Immortal Alliance Leader and Celestial Palace Master!"

With that, Gai You reached out to arrest the "founding emperor," a magical hand seal materializing in the air. Lin Jing made no move to help.

Though currently limited by the mechanical turtle's realm to only Nascent Soul Transformation early-stage cultivation—making victory against a Void Mystical Stage expert impossible—Lin Jing didn't believe the man who once ruled the kingdom would lack means to restrain its officials!

Sure enough.

"Hmph." As the founding emperor snorted coldly, Gai You's expression changed drastically as terrifying pressure enveloped him.

"Impossible!" he gasped. This was official authority—a unique pressure only effective within the Ancient Kingdom's immortal official system. Higher ranks exerted stronger pressure, but only against fellow officials—a method for superiors to control subordinates.

As a third-rank Demon Extermination Chief, how could this old man pressure him? Though briefly, it disrupted his movements.

However, overcoming rank differences through pressure alone was nearly impossible. Moments later, sweat-drenched Chief Gai broke free—but his hand remained suspended mid-air, unwilling to continue the attack. The previous moment had been too bizarre.

If this old man didn't outrank him, that pressure made no sense. This also left the hesitant Chief Liu uncertain.

Who was this opponent?

"Old friend, wake up and vouch for me."

At that moment, the founding emperor's call reached the Ancient Kingdom's territory. In the First Mountain Under Heaven, at the core of Tianyuan Continent, the mightiest dragon vein slowly opened its eyes.

"True Dragon Summoning... The Ancient Emperor has finally returned..."

Legend said Tianyuan Ancient Kingdom's founder used secret dragon techniques to enlighten the continent's central mountain range, which rose to become a divine dragon sworn to protect the kingdom.

This colossal dragon only awakened to suppress disasters the kingdom couldn't withstand. At Seeking the Way late-stage cultivation, even the second-generation emperor couldn't fully command it—it only obeyed its pact with the founder.

The kingdom's traditional Dragon Ascension Festival commemorated this mighty dragon.

Now, after millennia of slumber, the Fortune Dragon Emperor awakened at last. Transforming into golden light, it surged from the earth's veins into the clouds, shaking the entire continent.

Beyond the heavens, the Seeking the Way experts maintaining the dimensional barriers all jolted at this draconic might, their divine senses sweeping downward while glancing at the second-generation emperor.

"The Fortune Dragon Emperor? Why..." Even the second-generation emperor was astonished. This dragon following the founder shouldn't awaken unless the kingdom faced extinction—only then would this embodiment of the kingdom's core fortune rise to battle.

Celestial Domain, Heavenly Secrets Tower.

The Ancient Imperial Prince and two generations of Tower Lords stared in shock at the distant golden radiance.

"The Fortune Dragon Emperor?!" The previous Tower Lord looked bewildered. With no imminent disaster threatening the kingdom, why would it awaken? Without existential crisis, only one possibility remained—someone had summoned it.

While uncertain if the second-generation emperor possessed such ability, one person definitely could...

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 115: Changing Skies
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 115: Changing Skies

"ROAR!!!"

The Fortune Dragon Emperor's draconic roar echoed across the ancient kingdom as it instantly materialized above the Demon Extermination Division headquarters. Wherever dragon veins existed, it could teleport freely. Now, the dragon emperor's blazing eyes fixed upon the half-human, half-ghost form of the First Ancient Emperor below. After sensing carefully, it confirmed - this was undoubtedly its master!!

"Your Majesty!!! How have you become like this!!"

Even the Fortune Dragon Emperor was shocked by the First Ancient Emperor's current state as a Divine Scripture projection, unable to comprehend what had happened to him.

Meanwhile, Gao Zhao, Chief Liu, and Chief Gai stood frozen nearby, nearly losing all capacity for thought as they stared in awe at the symbol of their kingdom's fortune - the Fortune Dragon Emperor who had slumbered for thousands of years!

The overwhelming pressure from the Fortune Dragon Emperor was unmistakable. Its peak Seeking the Way aura made even Void Mystical Stage cultivators gasp for breath.

Could they have heard wrong...?

This Fortune Dragon Emperor... actually addressed the old man summoned by Lin Jing as "Ancient Emperor Your Majesty"?

But how could this be? How could the First Ancient Emperor only be at the early Nascent Soul Transformation Stage?

Yet the Fortune Dragon Emperor had been enlightened by the First Ancient Emperor himself, having fought side by side with him. It would never mistake its own master.

"Truly worthy of being the First Ancient Emperor..." Lin Jing licked his lips. The old emperor still had tricks up his sleeve.

When summoning projections through the Divine Scripture, the scripture's owner could normally impose certain restrictions.

But with the First Ancient Emperor's profound foundations, neither Lin Jing nor the mechanical turtle could forcibly control him. The emperor could dissipate back to nothingness anytime he wished.

This alone wasn't particularly concerning - it simply meant both sides could refuse the other's demands.

But seeing the First Ancient Emperor's projection actually summon the kingdom's Fortune Dragon Emperor, equivalent to a Seeking the Way combatant, truly made Lin Jing want to applaud his brilliance.

In other words, if some weak schemer tried controlling the First Ancient Emperor through the Divine Scripture, not only could the emperor ignore them and dissipate at will, he could also summon the Fortune Dragon Emperor to annihilate them.

How terrifying.

Good thing I was polite.

This wasn't some early Nascent Soul Transformation cultivator - this was an early-stage dragon tamer bound to a Seeking the Way dragon emperor!

Lin Jing stroked his chin, glancing at the terrified Gai You nearby.

"It's a long story," the First Ancient Emperor sighed as he looked at the Fortune Dragon Emperor. "Being able to return in this form is already fortunate enough!"

"Do me a favor - replenish the energy of the dragon veins here. This kid really abused them recklessly." The First Ancient Emperor's words referred to how Lin Jing had drained over half of the Demon Extermination Division's dragon veins. These veins also served to suppress demons and monsters - what if the imprisoned creatures escaped?

Within moments of the Fortune Dragon Emperor's arrival, three more Seeking the Way auras descended:

The Second Ancient Emperor.

Ling Daozi, founding ancestor of the Spirit Path Sect.

The former Heavenly Secrets Tower master, accompanied by the current master and the Ancient Imperial Prince.

Their gazes upon the First Ancient Emperor each contained different shades of astonishment.

"Everyone's here," the First Emperor murmured, clicking his tongue.

"Father?!" The Seventh Ancient Emperor looked horrified, becoming the seventh person after the Fortune Dragon Emperor to confirm this was no imposter.

Yet what he struggled to accept was sensing that his father had already passed away, returning through some other means.

"G-grandfather?" The Ancient Imperial Prince was equally shocked, even dumbfounded. So those absurd claims Chief Gai relayed about the Holy Son of the Demon Court were actually true?

"Your Majesty..." Ling Daozi and the Azure Ox Taoist also noticed something amiss with the First Emperor's condition, unable to comprehend his circumstances.

"It seems you've all been doing well," the First Ancient Emperor said. "Your senses aren't wrong - this emperor has died. While searching for the path to ascension among the stars, I encountered a near-immortal level Heavenly Demon in the ascension channel and perished together with it."

"A pity - I almost succeeded in ascension."

Lin Jing, Wood-attribute Pine Leaf Mouse, Dragon Carp, and Ice Soul Gu: ????

Weren't you killed by a meteor? Where did this Heavenly Demon come from?

"Fortunately, this boy used the Divine Scripture's immortal palace secret method to recall traces of me, making the best of misfortune. Though he's the Demon Court's Holy Son, considering he was once a cultivator of our ancient kingdom and willingly helped this emperor return, he's earned some merit. Second Emperor - do I have any granddaughters unmarried?" The First Emperor turned to the Second Ancient Emperor.

"No need." Lin Jing's expression turned cold nearby. I kindly brought you back to the ancient kingdom, and now you want me as a grandson?

"Father..." The Second Ancient Emperor struggled to express his emotions, having never expected to see his father again in this lifetime.

As someone who deeply valued familial bonds and succession, the Second Emperor naturally held immense respect for his father. One could say his achievement of becoming Tianyuan Continent's youngest Seeking the Way cultivator was inseparable from the First Emperor's meticulous guidance.

Now seeing his father again after millennia, the Second Emperor's heart churned with emotion. Even his gaze toward Lin Jing changed - where he'd previously felt annoyed by this Longevity Body cultivator, now he viewed him somewhat favorably.

After all, without Lin Jing, he and the First Emperor would have been forever separated by life and death.

"Father, please return to the Ancient Realm with me to talk!" The Second Emperor felt this wasn't the proper place for discussion.

"Return my ass." The First Emperor showed no appreciation, instead glaring at him with exasperation.

"Handle these matters first, then I'll return with you." The First Emperor gave no face to the Second Emperor, instead turning to Gao Sheng. Understanding immediately, Gao Sheng tremblingly presented the results of his months of "incognito inspections."

After scanning Gao Sheng's records, the Second Emperor's expression darkened.

"Father, let me explain—"

"I'm not listening. I've handled what I could. What remains is for you to deal with! Now first resolve this boy's purpose here, then we'll have plenty to discuss!" The First Emperor pointed at Lin Jing.

Taking a deep breath, the Second Emperor felt a headache coming on. He turned to Liu Gongzhuo: "Liu Gongzhuo, refine your Heavenly Flame Gu back to its origin state and give it to Lin Jing. Additionally, I assign you one final task from the Demon Extermination Division - conduct a handover of all demons above Nascent Soul Transformation Stage imprisoned here, entrusting them to Lin Jing's disposal... After completing this, you'll no longer serve as division chief, but instead assume the role of Imperial Protector in the royal city."

"As you command!" Liu Gongzhuo responded in shock, immediately bowing in gratitude. Though the position of Demon Extermination Division chief carried great prestige and authority... compared to becoming protector of the imperial family's princes and princesses, it was like comparing heaven and earth. Though ordered to surrender his Heavenly Flame Gu, the Second Emperor's compensation meant he could now access the kingdom's true power center. If he recalled correctly, imperial protectors were even equipped with quasi-immortal artifacts.

Normally, this position went to Void Mystical Stage cultivators from the imperial clan or those married into it. What qualifications did he have?

As Chief Liu received his promotion, Chief Gai You nearby stared blankly at the First Emperor's "beloved disciple" Gao Sheng, feeling as if he'd fallen into an icy abyss... Was this some joke... Earlier... had he actually tried arresting the First Ancient Emperor?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 116: Ancient Clan''s Longevity Body
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 116: Ancient Clan's Longevity Body

The appearance of the First Ancient Emperor sent shockwaves through all factions.

Beyond the heavens.

A group of Wayseekers who hadn't descended to the ancient kingdom with the Second Ancient Emperor still forcibly turned their gazes toward it.

Previously, the ancient kingdom strictly prohibited these external Wayseekers from peering into its affairs, but the bizarre emergence of the Fortune Dragon Emperor had piqued their curiosity. Now that they were "collectively spying," what could the Second Ancient Emperor possibly do about it?

Yet the moment they looked, these Wayseekers immediately regretted it, wishing they could unsee what they'd witnessed.

"The First Ancient Emperor has actually returned."

These Wayseekers all harbored some fear toward the First Ancient Emperor—after all, either they themselves had been beaten by him, or their masters, parents, or ancestors had suffered his fists.

"Ah, the ancient kingdom's foundations remain terrifying. Not just the First Ancient Emperor, but that old man... the former Tower Master of Heavenly Secrets Tower, Old Man Heavenly Secrets, is actually still alive. I knew that old coot wouldn't die so easily."

That Old Man Heavenly Secrets had faked his death gave other Wayseeker factions the impression that the ancient kingdom might still be hiding a bunch of undead old monsters.

After all, around the time of the Tianyuan Ancient Kingdom's founding, the number of Wayseekers far exceeded the current count.

Many had perished in battles against the Immortal Alliance, its remnants, or various demonic sects, or had died when their lifespans expired. But seeing Old Man Heavenly Secrets' false death, they had to reevaluate the ancient kingdom's hidden depths.

"Fortunately, the First Ancient Emperor isn't in his *complete form*. It seems he died while exploring the Ascension Path. To sustain the First Ancient Emperor's existence consumes the kingdom's fortune, national destiny, and faith power. But the question is—why was it the Holy Son of the Demon Court who resurrected him?"

Numerous Wayseekers turned their gazes toward the Demon Emperor of the Demon Court, their eyes seemingly asking: Does your Holy Son still have his heart set on the ancient kingdom?

"Heh." The Demon Emperor curled her lips. "Didn't expect this, did you? The Immortal Palace's top-secret Divine Scripture is now in the hands of my Demon Court's Holy Son. The First Ancient Emperor was just an experiment—next, all my Demon Court's previous emperors can be revived too!"

Outwardly, she remained expressionless, but inwardly she was cursing up a storm.

After learning that the Mechanized Turtle couldn't go to the Origin Realm, Lin Jing had planned to entrust it to the Demon Emperor's care.

He'd explained that if the Demon Court faced annihilation during this period, the Mechanized Turtle could forcibly break its contract, restore its cultivation, and wield immortal artifacts in battle.

It could even use the Divine Scripture to summon previous Demon Emperors to protect the court.

The Demon Emperor adored this turtle Lin Jing had left behind—this turtle... had heaven-defying capabilities.

However, she'd only just learned that Lin Jing had already applied the Divine Scripture's power to the First Ancient Emperor!

Damn it all—why resurrect this old man of all people? Even in his current transformed state, the Demon Emperor lacked confidence to face him directly.

But from how things looked now, the outcome wasn't entirely bad. That Second Ancient Emperor hadn't been so rebellious as to disregard the First Ancient Emperor in this form. The Demon Emperor licked her lips—as long as the ancient kingdom wanted this "previous emperor" to endure, they'd have no choice but to rely on the Demon Court's Holy Son.

While the various Wayseekers focused their attention on the First Ancient Emperor and the Azure Ox Taoist, Lin Jing naturally noticed this additional elderly Wayseeker.

Recognizing the man's face, Lin Jing slowly spoke, "It's you..."

He immediately identified him—this old man was the very immortal trafficker who'd tested his qualifications back then before selling him to the Yin Household Sect.

"This matter is complicated—I'll definitely give the Holy Son a satisfactory explanation later." Old Man Heavenly Secrets also realized his prophecy hadn't been wrong after all. That Longevity Body had indeed brought prosperity to the ancient kingdom, managing to bring back the deceased First Ancient Emperor. For the kingdom, this was undoubtedly an auspicious sign.

But now a new challenge emerged—how to placate the Demon Court's Holy Son and make him overlook that human trafficking incident.

"Fine. I'll await your answer." Lin Jing narrowed his eyes. How interesting—he'd never imagined his experiences bore traces of Wayseeker planning.

"Seems there's nothing more for me here now?"

"In that case, I won't interfere further in the ancient kingdom's family matters. Farewell."

Currently unable to immediately take charge of the Heavenly Flame Gu and criminal demons above the Nascent Soul Transformation stage, Lin Jing grabbed the Mechanized Turtle and prepared to leave.

No one stopped Lin Jing's departure. Gao Sheng brushed dust off his robes, glanced at his awestruck grandfather, and coughed dryly. "Brother Lin, don't leave so quickly..."

Now having become the First Ancient Emperor's disciple, Gao Sheng's confidence soared—even the Ancient Imperial Prince had to address him as senior. His gaze locked onto Chief Gai of the Demon Extermination Division. "Chief Gai, next, please cooperate with the Administration Bureau's investigation."

Sweat poured down Gai You's forehead as he dared not meet the eyes of either Ancient Emperor or even the prince. Not that these figures paid him any attention—only Gao Sheng still nursed a quiet grudge.

"..." The current Chief Gai sought only to preserve his life. With a thud, he knelt before the First Ancient Emperor.

"Shoo, shoo, scram." The First Ancient Emperor still ignored him, instead scratching his head while watching Lin Jing's departing back. He felt like he'd forgotten something—why was that kid in such a hurry?

......

Lin Jing moved swiftly because the Mechanized Turtle had informed him of the third Longevity Body's location.

Now he had to take advantage of the First Ancient Emperor still being occupied with domestic affairs to kidnap that Longevity Body before the emperor realized.

At the same time, he burned with curiosity—why didn't the First Ancient Emperor want to personally take this third Longevity Body as a disciple?

Mystic Domain, Mystic Wonder Sect—a second-rate cultivation faction with only one Nascent Soul Transformation expert guarding it.

The sect divided into inner, outer, and servant divisions. The inner and outer divisions naturally housed proper disciples, while the servant division mainly consisted of offspring born between outer disciples, thrown into menial labor upon growing older to temper willpower and physique before beginning cultivation.

"What was that light just now?"

In the Mystic Wonder Sect's servant quarters, many servants had been drawn by the golden light from the Fortune Dragon Emperor's emergence. Among them, a little girl on a rooftop repainting rain-faded spirit-gathering array patterns with ink had paused her work for quite some time.

While others were briefly distracted, none were as captivated as this girl. She sensed an inexplicable fluctuation within that golden radiance that made her bloodline tremble.

Bam!

Lost in thought too long, a gust nearly blew her off the roof. After tumbling several times, she barely caught herself. Heart pounding, she looked up to see an expressionless overseer staring down.

"Focus on your work! With zero concentration, how do you expect to cultivate? As an orphan, you need to work ten—no, a hundred times harder than other disciples! If you don't want this servant position, pack your bags and get off this mountain—plenty would kill for your spot!"

"S-sorry." The girl scrambled back to her ink bucket, carefully resuming her task and not daring to glance at the fading light again.

Amidst the clouds, Lin Jing—with the Mechanized Turtle atop his head—gazed at the plainly dressed servant girl. "So it's her? I see now."

"This girl actually has ancient clan bloodline—no wonder the First Ancient Emperor reacted that way!"

"She might even carry imperial blood."

Having dabbled in the Yin Household Sect's secret arts, Lin Jing immediately detected that faint yet familiar bloodline aura from her.

He'd sensed this same aura from the Ancient Imperial Prince, the Third Prince, and even both Ancient Emperors.

Lin Jing lightly landed on the rooftop, looking down at the servant girl. As he descended, she raised her head—their eyes met.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 117: The Rebellious Imperial Princess
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 117: The Rebellious Imperial Princess

"Se-Senior?" The young servant girl looked nervously at Lin Jing, not understanding why he was stepping on the formation pattern she was about to trace.

"Look around," Lin Jing reminded simply. The servant girl looked and was shocked to discover that everyone - from the steward who had just scolded her to the other menial disciples nearby - had frozen in place.

Of course, they weren't truly frozen. Lin Jing had simply used illusion magic to isolate himself and this servant girl from outside interference.

"Do you know who your parents are?" Lin Jing asked directly. The servant girl froze, realizing she was facing a supreme expert, and didn't dare show any disrespect.

She immediately dropped to her knees with a thud, not daring to look directly at Lin Jing's face.

"This junior's mother was an outer sect disciple of the Mystic Wonder Sect."

"As for my father... I've never met him. I only heard others say he was a rogue cultivator my mother met during her travels..."

A rogue cultivator... Lin Jing frowned.

"She... died in childbirth," the girl said, lowering her head.

Lin Jing looked toward the Ancient Domain and roughly understood. This "rogue cultivator" was likely some Ancient Imperial Prince.

This prince must have had a fleeting romance with this Mystic Wonder Sect disciple while traveling.

After growing bored, the prince simply left without a second thought, never realizing the female disciple had become pregnant.

Lin Jing had seen this plot countless times in dramas.

Most likely, the Mystic Wonder Sect disciple never knew her lover's true identity.

"Which damned prince was it?" Lin Jing pondered. "As a prince, he could have any woman he wanted. Why prey on some low-level female cultivator?" His gaze returned to the still-bowed girl.

The girl was quite delicate-looking. Her mother must have been beautiful too.

Perhaps even possessed some special physique...

With her birth mother already deceased, this complicated matters. The girl was only about ten years old - too young for Lin Jing to comfortably consider kidnapping her to teach the Human Domination Devil Arts under Mo Pine's tutelage.

At this age, she needed parental love more than anything. Having already suffered ten years as an orphaned servant, would he really be human if he subjected her to demonic cultivation training?

If she truly had no living relatives, he might not hesitate. But knowing the First Ancient Emperor's character, Lin Jing believed the old man would acknowledge this descendant and provide compensation. She might have better options.

"Forget it," Lin Jing sighed. Better to reunite her with family. There were plenty of candidates for the Human Domination Devil Arts - no need to ruin this girl's life.

"What's your name?"

"Yu Xun'er," the servant girl answered.

Yu Xun'er... Yu was likely her mother's surname. As for her father's surname... Lin Jing's expression turned strange. Why did this feel familiar? Did he have a friend with this name in his past life?

"Would you like to meet your birth father? Your other relatives?" Lin Jing asked. "I could take you to them."

Just as Lin Jing was marveling at what a villain he was, the previously timid servant girl suddenly widened her eyes and raised her voice sharply: "No!"

"I refuse!"

"I can live on my own. If he truly cared about Mother and me, he wouldn't have abandoned us."

Huh... Lin Jing looked at her in surprise. That was genuine resentment.

Though anyone in her position would likely feel the same.

That prince's separation from the Mystic Wonder Sect disciple probably wasn't due to some accident. If he'd truly loved her, bringing her to the Ancient Domain wouldn't have been difficult.

Yet he left without a trace.

Definitely some irresponsible prince sowing wild oats.

"Are you certain?" Lin Jing asked calmly. "What if your father holds a far higher status than you imagine? Returning to him could end your hardships."

"Still no! One day I'll cultivate until I'm strong enough to confront him myself and demand why he abandoned us!"

"But even a Nascent Soul Transformation sect master of Mystic Wonder Sect would be insignificant before your father," Lin Jing pointed out.

Yu Xun'er froze. Before Lin Jing spoke, she'd never considered her father might be someone important.

But this only angered her more - if he was truly such a powerful figure, how could he let them fall to such circumstances?

"No matter who he is!" she declared resolutely.

"What if he's the Ancient Imperial Prince - current leader of the Destiny Rankings, crown prince of the Ancient Clan?" Lin Jing tested.

The servant girl gaped.

"My father is...?"

"Ah, no, just an example," Lin Jing scratched his ear. The Ancient Imperial Prince didn't seem the irresponsible type - probably one of the other star children.

The Second Ancient Emperor's first few children were quite outstanding. Rumor said the later ones... not so much. Probably used up all their luck on the first batch.

"Ancient Imperial Prince..."

"Even if my father were the Holy Son of the Demon Court, I'd never forgive him!" Yu Xun'er clenched her fists, her demeanor completely transformed from before.

"...You know about the Holy Son of the Demon Court?" Lin Jing was surprised.

Good. At least in her eyes, the Holy Son ranked above the Ancient Imperial Prince.

"So even if you carry the Ancient Imperial bloodline, you refuse to let me return you?" Lin Jing asked.

"No need, Senior. My life in Mystic Wonder Sect is fine. This is my home," Yu Xun'er met Lin Jing's gaze firmly.

Lin Jing shook his head. "You'll never reach your desired cultivation level here. I'll give you three choices: First, remain here. Second, I return you to your family. Third, follow me to cultivate. Choose."

"Oh, and earlier wasn't a lie. Your true bloodline is Ancient Imperial royalty. You're the abandoned descendant of some Ancient Imperial Prince..."

"Confronting him won't be easy..."

Yu Xun'er was stunned. Wait... she truly was... Ancient Imperial bloodline?

"Think carefully. Even as an illegitimate child, returning would make you revered by thousands. Even Mystic Wonder Sect's current sect master would treat you with respect."

"Then Senior is...?" Yu Xun'er suddenly grew suspicious of Lin Jing's identity. His willingness to return her suggested he was connected to the Ancient Kingdom or even her father. Yet he also offered to take her as a disciple, making his motives unclear.

"Me? I'm the Holy Son of the Demon Court you mentioned," Lin Jing's eyes gleamed strangely. If she refused to return to the Ancient Clan, he'd keep her temporarily and let the First Ancient Emperor handle it...

Meanwhile, Yu Xun'er's mind blanked. The man before her was actually that legendary figure even more renowned than the Ancient Imperial Prince? That monstrous talent who shook the Great Yuan Continent within a century of cultivation, said to rival even celestial physiques...?

He shouldn't even be affiliated with the Ancient Kingdom... Why would he seek out lost imperial blood like her? Though only ten, her harsh life in the servant division had matured her beyond her years as she absorbed knowledge preparing for outer sect admission.

Holy Son of the Demon Court... Could he be planning to nurture her imperial bloodline to control her as a puppet against the Ancient Kingdom?!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 118: Snatching Talent
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 118: Snatching Talent

"Overturning an ancient kingdom...

"Could I... really become an empress?" Yu Xun'er murmured to herself.

The first female emperor of Tianyuan Ancient Kingdom.

This grand narrative, even if obtained through questionable means, instantly made her pulse race regarding the imperial bloodline she had previously dismissed.

Returning to the ancient clan would only mean remaining a lowly bloodline with no backing.

She was merely the product of some prince's dalliance with a low-ranking female cultivator, her status far inferior to those noble bloodlines born from proper imperial-family alliances.

But following the Holy Son of the Demon Court, even as a tool, seemed to reveal a different path...

"Hold on," Lin Jing interrupted.

After scanning her with his spiritual sense, Lin Jing's face darkened.

Wasn't her internal monologue a bit too dramatic?

Where had her initial nervous, easily-bullied servant girl persona gone?

Then again, one couldn't expect too much from an orphan who grew up alone.

Without some cunning, survival would've been difficult.

Parentless children with no backing naturally became targets.

But that wasn't the issue - since when did he, the Holy Son of the Demon Court, have nothing better to do than overthrow kingdoms?

He didn't even care about ascending to the immortal realm - why would a mere nation matter?

If Lin Jing's previous obsession with strength was to avoid being eaten as the living "Tang Seng meat," now that his safety was assured, he'd rather immerse himself in researching various cultivation techniques and secret arts.

Ever since creating techniques in Bodhi Heaven, Lin Jing found unprecedented satisfaction in developing and improving spells with unique effects.

Breakthroughs in cultivation, battle victories, resource gains - none excited him as much as creating or refining a new spell.

"Enough with those unsightly thoughts..." Lin Jing cut off Yu Xun'er's fantasies. "I sought you out not because of your ancient clan bloodline."

"Rather, like me, you possess one of the world's rare Longevity Bodies. You're simply too young, untrained, so your physique hasn't manifested yet."

Realizing Lin Jing had seen through her completely, Yu Xun'er paled.

But the next moment, she looked up in shock.

Longevity Body?

She had a Longevity Body?

"Even an untrained Longevity Body enjoys ten times the lifespan of same-level cultivators. When the physique reaches minor completion, major completion, then perfection, this multiplier becomes truly terrifying."

"The tradeoff? Cultivation speed becomes ten, even dozens of times slower than others. If you cultivate alone in Mystic Wonder Sect..." Lin Jing chuckled darkly. "You'd never form a golden core. Most likely, you'd end up demon food."

Yu Xun'er turned even paler.

Being eaten by demons didn't sound appealing.

"I'll go with you, senior."

"Coming with me..." Lin Jing pondered briefly. "Then I'll first take you from Mystic Wonder Sect to Qingzhou."

Better to settle her quickly and avoid complications. A small delay mattered little.

Lin Jing extended his hand, summoning magic that lifted Yu Xun'er onto a cloud. Under his control, they soared toward Immortal Prison Mountain in Qingzhou.

But before leaving the kingdom's borders, Lin Jing tensed.

A wizened figure astride an illusory dragon descended before them.

Seeing the girl beside Lin Jing, the old man's nose wrinkled in displeasure.

None other than the First Ancient Emperor.

"I knew I'd forgotten something. You bastard moved fast," the emperor grumbled, eyeing the servant girl hiding behind Lin Jing.

"Shouldn't Your Majesty be handling domestic affairs instead of stalking me?" Lin Jing sighed.

"You've discovered her bloodline, haven't you?"

"Indeed. Quite the surprise."

"Then how is taking her away appropriate?" The emperor massaged his temples. An ordinary Longevity Body, even a kingdom citizen or clan branch member, he might have conceded to Lin Jing.

But this servant girl was direct imperial lineage - born qualified to practice true dragon secret arts with perfect compatibility!

And a Longevity Body!

Such combined heritage loose in the world would destabilize the kingdom's future.

"Why not? She doesn't want to return." Lin Jing smiled. "This isn't abduction - I respect her choice."

"Spare me. Name your price, but she's not going to the Demon Court," the emperor insisted.

Lin Jing considered quietly.

"Child, return with me," the emperor addressed Yu Xun'er. "I'm your great-grandfather, the Tianyuan Ancient Kingdom's founder, the First Ancient Emperor! Let me guide your cultivation."

Yu Xun'er's eyes widened in disbelief at the roadblocker's identity.

"Does Your Majesty lack students? If Gao Sheng isn't enough, might I recommend the Third Prince? He has immortal emperor potential," Lin Jing interjected.

"What nonsense is this? Immortal emperor potential?" The emperor blinked - surpassing even the crown prince?

Lin Jing had inadvertently fulfilled another promise by recommending the Third Prince.

"See for yourself upon returning."

"Enough digressions. A compromise then." Powerless against Lin Jing, the emperor conceded: "You seek to pass on your techniques, yes? You know cultivating a Longevity Body requires insane resources. With two already in the Demon Court, can it support a third?"

"If you don't take her there, the ancient clan will fund all her cultivation - provided she remains in the kingdom, contacting no Demon Court beasts except you."

"After you depart for the Origin Realm, I'll personally guide her. Don't trust the clan? Trust me. Besides, my existence remains bound by your turtle."

"Hmm..." Lin Jing found the terms acceptable. He'd considered this approach himself, trusting the emperor's integrity.

"Very well. She'll stay with me temporarily, then be handed over before I leave for the Origin Realm." Lin Jing planned to first take her to Spirit Dao Sect for guidance.

Ages ten to twenty shaped one's worldview. If teaching her, he couldn't abandon her afterward - that'd be raising a rival.

"Fine, fine." The emperor remained confident he could correct even demonic cultivation influences. Blood ties outweighed all.

Meanwhile, Yu Xun'er stared at Lin Jing in awe. Was that old man truly the First Ancient Emperor? The legendary Holy Son conversing as equals with him - unbelievable!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 119: Star Beasts
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 119: Star Beasts

It must be said that the First Ancient Emperor arrived just in time. Otherwise, after Lin Jing completed his cultivation inheritance, Yu Xun'er might not have been trained by him, but rather by his arch-nemesis—the reincarnated Mo Pine, leader of the Heavenly Demon Cult.

With this matter resolved, a weight lifted from the First Ancient Emperor's heart. However, Lin Jing soon made another request.

"Your Majesty, could you divine the most suitable location for my next contracted beast?"

The First Ancient Emperor felt a headache coming on. Last time Lin Jing had asked this of him, the divination had led to a Longevity Body with imperial bloodline. Now, being asked again, he was genuinely apprehensive.

"Fine... I'll attempt a brief reading." The First Ancient Emperor sighed and activated the True Dragon Qi Observation Technique.

With the assistance of the Fortune Dragon Emperor's power, he wouldn't exhaust himself to the point of dissipation. Moments later, the First Ancient Emperor suddenly looked toward the heavens and gasped.

"Did you find something?" Lin Jing was surprised by the speed—this was the First Ancient Emperor with the Fortune Dragon Emperor's aid, after all.

"Yes and no!"

The First Ancient Emperor frowned. "I was observing where on the Tianyuan Continent might hold your most compatible next beast. Strangely, the result pointed outward."

"Outward? Not the Tianyuan Continent?" Lin Jing was taken aback.

"Indeed, outward." The First Ancient Emperor fell into deep thought. "The fortune points toward the starry void."

"It doesn't indicate a specific individual, but rather an entire species."

"In the starry void, there exist many Star Beasts that feed on starlight and meteors to cultivate. During my travels there, I encountered many—some powerful enough to rival Demon Emperors."

"The implication is that contracting a Star Beast would be most beneficial for you!"

Lin Jing also gazed at the starry sky, not doubting the accuracy of the First Ancient Emperor's divination.

Star Beasts, huh? But the problem was that the Tianyuan's Seekers of the Way had recently reinforced the world's barriers, making entry and exit exceedingly difficult. Where was he supposed to find a Star Beast to contract?

"Forget it." Lin Jing wasn't in urgent need of his next beast. Since he now knew Star Beasts suited him, he could wait for another opportunity.

Four-legged Dragon Carps were hard to find, but Star Beasts were practically everywhere.

"Off I go." Having pointed Lin Jing in the right direction, the First Ancient Emperor departed to attend to his own affairs.

Meanwhile, Lin Jing's eyes glinted before he changed direction and headed straight for the Heavenly Secrets Tower.

At the tower, the former leader—Old Man Heavenly Secrets, also known as the Azure Ox Taoist—awaited Lin Jing's arrival.

"Young Saint..." The old man greeted him with a smile, offering an explanation for his actions in selling Lin Jing to the Yin Household Sect.

"I kept watch over you throughout your imprisonment in the Yin Corpse Sect. Only after the Beast Master Sect took you away—confirming your safety—did I leave."

"That explanation doesn't erase the fear I felt at the time." Facing this Seeker of the Way, Lin Jing showed none of the courtesy he extended to others. No matter the reasons, the fact remained that he'd been sold to a demonic sect.

The excuse of "for the ancient kingdom's prosperity" didn't cut it—the kingdom wasn't his family's, so its rise or fall had nothing to do with him back then.

"This old one admits fault in arbitrarily deciding another's fate." Old Man Heavenly Secrets bowed in apology. "As compensation, how about I provide you with crucial intelligence?"

"Speak."

"Young Saint, you possess two Rare Beasts that mutated during their contract with you, correct?" The old man chuckled.

"When ordinary Demonic Beasts form contracts via the 《Beast Taming Art》 with cultivators of special physiques, there's a chance they mutate into Rare Beasts. Does this theory sound familiar?"

Lin Jing nodded. This was precisely the theory Elder Mo had obtained from a Heavenly Secrets Tower cultivator—though to date, he remained the only successful case.

"That theory actually came from me—disguised as a tower member—to the Beast Master Sect." The old man stroked his beard.

"So it was you." You're the one who turned Elder Mo into a gambling addict.

"Indeed. However, the theory was incomplete at the time." Old Man Heavenly Secrets continued, "I'd been researching Rare Beasts, seeking methods to improve mutation rates for ordinary Demonic Beasts."

"During my travels, I discovered the Beast Master Sect—a unique sect. Their 《Beast Taming Art》, derived from immortal origins, combined with a special physique, creates ideal conditions for mutation."

"I've even taught the original 《Beast Taming Art》 to other special-physique cultivators, guiding their contracts. Yet apart from you, none succeeded out of hundreds."

"I initially thought my deductions were flawed. But now, I've developed a more complete theory. Would the Young Saint be interested? For a beast tamer like yourself, this intelligence should hold significant value."

"Value can only be determined after hearing it."

"Very well." The old man nodded. "Through decades of research, I've found that all Demonic Beasts bathed in starlight harbor dormant Rare Beast bloodlines. The lower their cultivation—ideally none—the more this bloodline accumulates. Upon reaching Foundation Establishment, it dissipates faster than it accumulates."

"The 《Beast Taming Art》 and special physique contracts can awaken this bloodline, but not all physiques work. The stronger the physique's origin power, the higher the success rate. A divine physique cultivator has far greater chances than ordinary spiritual physiques. Additionally, the physique must be innate—acquired ones won't do."
