"Most crucially, the cultivator's and beast's cultivation levels mustn't differ greatly, or it hinders awakening. Thus, a qi-training beast tamer with a divine physique contracting a qi-training cub has the highest success rate!"

"Of course, failure remains possible."

"This summarizes my century of research on Rare Beasts, the 《Beast Taming Art》, and special physiques' influence. Well, Young Saint? Is this intelligence sufficient? It might elevate the Beast Master Sect to become the continent's premier sect."

Lin Jing's eyes gleamed. This old man...

"How do I verify this intelligence's accuracy?"

Old Man Heavenly Secrets smiled. "It's about ninety percent reliable. Compare it with your own case—the Longevity Body, though unranked among divine physiques, possesses origin power surpassing most, nearly rivaling immortal physiques."

"Your two beasts—the squirrel and carp—were contracted before Foundation Establishment."

"As for the Ice Soul Gu, its unique lineage and multiple reincarnations place it outside this discussion." He stroked his beard.

"...Is that so? If this method fails to produce Rare Beasts, I'll take your tower's Rare Beasts for observation." Lin Jing pondered. But the attempt's cost seemed excessive. Innate divine physiques—even in this golden age—were exceedingly rare. These prodigies could excel in any path—who would gamble on Rare Beasts? Unless success was guaranteed, the risk seemed unjustifiable.

Moreover, if the gamble failed, dissolving the 《Beast Taming Art》 would harm the cultivator more than other techniques—not only requiring energy dispersion but also enduring contract dissolution's backlash.

Yet no matter the cost, producing more Rare Beasts would be worth it. Lin Jing resolved to bring this intelligence back to the Beast Master Sect.

As for his next Star Beast, the criteria became clearer—it must be a cub before Foundation Establishment. Better yet, one that had already awakened its Rare Beast bloodline, since his own circumstances no longer fit the old man's "perfect combination."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 120: Secluded Cultivation
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 120: Secluded Cultivation

"Haha! If the Holy Son fails to nurture a Rare Beast within a hundred years according to this method, I'll personally deliver the Starry Void Turtle to the Beast Master Sect!"

The Starry Void Turtle inside the Heavenly Secrets Tower: ?

"Deal."

Considering the valuable intelligence about Rare Beasts and the fact he hadn't actually suffered harm from the Yin Household Sect, Lin Jing temporarily set aside his grievances against Old Man Heavenly Secrets' schemes.

From another perspective, if Old Man Heavenly Secrets hadn't discovered and guided him to the Beast Master Sect, he might have already been devoured by wild Demonic Beasts.

"We'll meet again in a hundred years."

As Lin Jing departed, the Starry Void Turtle materialized beside Old Man Heavenly Secrets, watching the retreating figure. "Is this appropriate?" it asked.

Old Man Heavenly Secrets turned his head. "Rest assured. I've repeatedly divined this using the Heavenly Secrets method—there won't be any mistakes. You won't actually have to go to the Beast Master Sect."

"Rare Beasts possess even greater potential than Divine Physiques."

"Could it be you also believe the Ancient Kingdom is doomed? Planning to find a new home for the Heavenly Secrets Tower in advance? Preparing to relocate to Qingzhou?"

"Nonsense!" Old Man Heavenly Secrets rapped the turtle's shell. "This was decided through discussions with the First Ancient Emperor. I exchanged views with His Majesty at the Demon Extermination Division..."

"This method of evolving Rare Beasts isn't suitable for widespread experimentation within the Ancient Kingdom. Those who can manifest Divine Physiques here mostly belong to noble families and sects with their own traditions—they wouldn't casually attempt beast-taming techniques. The kingdom's policies also prioritize human destiny energy—this can't be changed overnight or decided hastily. Concentrating resources to nurture Rare Beasts would be extremely difficult."

"But Qingzhou and the Beast Master Sect there are different. The sect is backed by the Demon Court and dominates Qingzhou completely. Recruiting Divine Physique holders comes easily to them, and they possess an ideal environment for beast cultivation. In that royal territory, Rare Beasts can be better nurtured."

"As for concerns about whether a certain number of Rare Beasts might threaten the Ancient Kingdom... The First Ancient Emperor stated that our current priority is ensuring the Tianyuan Continent survives the impending Heavenly Demon invasion. During this critical period facing external threats, whether Demonic Beasts or demonic tribes—all are potential allies."

"The stronger our forces resisting the Heavenly Demon invasion, the lesser the losses our Ancient Kingdom will suffer! If other factions lose territory, they can simply relocate—it won't have significant impact. But if our kingdom loses territory and our national destiny dissipates... that would be truly irrecoverable."

..........

En route to the Spirit Dao Sect, Lin Jing transmitted the acquired intelligence back to the Beast Master Sect for Elder Mo to conduct experiments.

Acquiring innate Divine Physique holders wasn't easy—it required time to develop.

After sending this information, Lin Jing temporarily set it aside, awaiting experimental results while returning to the Spirit Dao Sect to continue cultivating using the Enlightenment Pool.

Besides Lin Jing, the ancient clan's Longevity Body—who had fallen asleep exhausted during the journey—was also brought along.

Upon landing, Yu Xun'er was awakened by Lin Jing. Though initially bewildered by the unfamiliar surroundings, she quickly received her assignments.

"Build a wooden house on that nearby mountain to live in. I'll assign you tasks—complete them all, and I'll impart cultivation techniques to start your training."

"Yes, Senior..." Yu Xun'er nodded, intensely curious about these tasks.

Lin Jing then summoned a small mushroom—the long-neglected Spell Elemental Mushroom.

"For the next few years, this will be your teacher."

The Spell Elemental Mushroom could independently create "illusions" without relying on the Ancient Emperor—perfect for tempering one's dao heart through these simulated environments.

Watching the large mushroom materialize, Yu Xun'er gulped—it looked... delicious.

"Mushroom, first construct an illusionary realm titled 'The Holy Son's Legend,' letting her observe my legendary life from a third-person perspective to broaden her horizons. Show me the finished product for approval," Lin Jing transmitted mentally.

After the First Ancient Emperor's return, the Ancient Kingdom experienced earthshaking changes. Incomplete statistics showed over a thousand officials executed within a month—including descendants of founding hero families.

While equally severe in punishing corrupt officials, the Second Ancient Emperor strongly opposed some of his predecessor's other demands—especially reducing cultivation resources for ancient clan members. Most national treasury resources must benefit the ancient clans... not nurture foreign talents.

As the Second Emperor declared: "This kingdom belongs to the ancient clans. Without their sacrifices millennia ago, there would be no Tianyuan Ancient Kingdom today. Only through the ancient clans' strength can the kingdom prosper."

"This isn't the ancient era where immortals oppressed mortals. At our founding, those saved mortals and low-level cultivators might have genuinely wished for the kingdom's prosperity, willing to contribute. But after millennia of peace, priorities have shifted—personal development now outweighs national interests. Outsiders don't deserve substantial investment—minor favors suffice."

On this matter, even facing his own father, the Second Emperor refused to yield. Their prolonged dispute plunged Tianyuan Ancient City and ancient clan members into deep anxiety, fearing collateral damage from the conflict.

These policy debates didn't affect Lin Jing's secluded cultivation at the Spirit Dao Sect.

At least the promised top-tier dragon vein cultivation site, Heavenly Flame Gu, and numerous Nascent Soul Transformation-level demonic prisoners were delivered as agreed—Lin Jing felt perfectly content, unconcerned with external chaos.

"Dragon vein."

"Demon Locking Tower."

"Heavenly Flame Gu."

"Excellent." Beside the Enlightenment Pool, Lin Jing examined the three floating objects with satisfaction.

The dragon vein was stored in a dragon-shaped spatial ring. Nearby floated a black miniature tower—a supreme-grade magical artifact imprisoning numerous demons above Nascent Soul Transformation level.

This supreme dragon vein would facilitate his avatar's breakthrough and years of cultivation. The demons in the Demon Locking Tower could serve purposes beyond alchemical refinement.

By having the Ice Gu employ Yin Spirit Gu's power to inflict inner demons on them, his avatar could then devour these demonic influences to cultivate the Heavenly Demon Finger technique!

If successful, the Heavenly Demon Finger might reach comparable mastery to the Heavenly Demon Divine Sense technique.

After fully exploiting these demons' value, refining them into pills would still be possible.

With the Heavenly Flame Gu's essence, all Ten Great Rare Gu were now assembled—the Ice Gu's existence could be considered complete...

"Now begins the secluded cultivation—converting all these resources into... my own strength."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 121: Intentional or Accidental?
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 121: Intentional or Accidental?

Time flows like a river.

In the blink of an eye, ten years had passed since various Dao-seeking experts began planning their expedition to the Origin Realm.

During this decade, the continent remained stable without major conflicts. The Heavenly Demon Sect that many factions feared still hadn't arrived.

Only within the Ancient Kingdom was there some turmoil, but these were merely internal affairs of the Ancient Clan. The retired First Ancient Emperor ultimately failed to change the Second Ancient Emperor's decisions... The cultivators throughout the Ancient Clan were unwilling to surrender their interests to unrelated outsiders.

Even though the First Ancient Emperor was the progenitor of the Ancient Clan, he now existed only as a remnant form, still constrained by outsiders. Within the clan, while everyone revered and feared him, none were willing to defy the Second Ancient Emperor's decrees for his sake... Not even the Third Prince whom Lin Jing had recommended to the First Ancient Emperor.

Furious, the First Ancient Emperor abandoned matters of the kingdom. Following Old Man Heavenly Secrets' example, he took Gao Sheng and the Mechanized Turtle to travel across the Ancient Kingdom, intending to discover and nurture talents himself.

During this period, Lin Jing had been cultivating in the back mountains of the Spirit Dao Sect, paying little attention to external affairs—after all, focus is paramount in cultivation.

Ten years... no, more than ten years. Occasionally entering the Hourglass of Time, they had all reaped bountiful gains over several decades.

Lin Jing's true form and avatar, along with the Wood-attribute Pine Leaf Mouse, Dragon Carp, and Ice Soul Gu, had all reached the Half-Step Nascent Soul Transformation stage—the Semi-Divine Realm. They were just one step away from transforming their Nascent Souls into "Heaven-Earth Primordial Spirits," needing only to comprehend and fuse with the power of heaven and earth to temper their Nascent Souls with natural forces.

It wasn't that they couldn't break through, but rather they were suppressing their realms. For Lin Jing who now possessed the Heavenly Demon Physique, the difference between Nascent Soul and Nascent Soul Transformation stages held little significance.

Only by laying a stronger foundation would it benefit their future advancements.

Moreover, breaking through from Nascent Soul to Nascent Soul Transformation in the Origin Realm was actually preferable. The density of natural energy there was conservatively estimated to be ten times that of other cultivation worlds, so Primordial Spirits tempered there tended to be far more powerful.

Additionally, they weren't rushing to break through because currently they had access to supreme resources like the Destiny Rankings, Enlightenment Pool, and Bodhi Tree. Spending time comprehending the Dao offered better returns.

Thus, over the years, Lin Jing and his companions' greatest gains weren't in base realm advancement, but rather the dramatic improvements in their mastery of various techniques.

First was the Dragon Carp. At Lin Jing's request, it devoted all its efforts to advancing its alchemy skills. Now leveraging its advantage with immortal flames, it had a certain probability of refining "Seventh-grade Pills"—the highest quality pills in the Tianyuan Continent, second only to the immortal pill in the Tongtian Tower.

While its success rate remained concerning, none could deny that with its immortal flames, the Dragon Carp's alchemy skills now placed it in the continent's second tier, separated from the top tier only by its own foundational realm.

This advancement in alchemy also reflected the Dragon Carp's improved fire control. The immortal flames obeyed its will more readily, and the Myriad Flame Cauldron's efficiency and power in refining cultivation golden cores had multiplied.

Next was the Ice Soul Gu. Over these decades, it focused mainly on sword Dao—an unconventional path. This was primarily because the abilities of the Ten Great Mystic Gu had already been honed to perfection through their cycles of reincarnation. Normal growth sufficed for perfect application, so further effort on gu techniques would yield diminishing returns.

Instead, the Primordial Chaos Sword Intent, the Skyrend Sword Ancestor's protective sword intent, along with various comprehension-enhancing resources allowed the Ice Soul Gu to forge a path utterly distinct from other gu.

The Primordial Chaos Sword Intent could combine with one's own Dao to transform into any sword intent. For the Ice Soul Gu, this Dao was undoubtedly poison. The power of the Ten Great Mystic Gu, converted through the Primordial Chaos Sword Intent, became the Ice Soul Gu's "Poison Sword Intent."

Unlike directly spreading gu poison, imbuing sword intent with gu poison greatly enhanced its invasive and destructive capabilities, allowing it to better penetrate defenses like magical treasures, secret techniques, and physical constitutions.

While poisoning was fearsome, top-tier cultivators had far more complete toxin resistance methods than lower-level practitioners. If poisoning had severe consequences, they simply avoided being poisoned.

But sword intent—sword cultivators—were renowned for their offensive prowess at every cultivation stage. As the Ice Soul Gu understood it, sword cultivators weren't just about raw power, nor were poison specialists merely about tricky mechanics. When power and mechanics combined, the Ice Soul Gu's poison became truly unstoppable.

As for the Dao-seeking level protective sword intent from the Skyrend Sword Ancestor, it wasn't purely for the Ice Soul Gu to comprehend sword Dao principles. Through continuous study—until this Dao-seeking protective sword intent was completely worn away—the Ice Soul Gu's sword Dao attainment now rivaled peak Nascent Soul Transformation sword cultivators.

This might not seem impressive, but remember—it was just a gu insect, a spider that started learning sword Dao from scratch.

Most importantly, when the Ice Soul Gu fused with Lin Jing, Lin Jing would gain all its sword Dao mastery. Even without personal sword skills, he could instantly become a top-tier sword master with attainments matching peak Nascent Soul Transformation sword cultivators—an excellent additional skill.

Finally, the Wood-attribute Pine Leaf Mouse had gained the most among the three spirit beasts. First, it pushed the "Green Emperor Longevity Art" one layer further, raising its ceiling to the Void Mystical Stage. Then it devoted all its efforts to studying spatial Dao.

It even successfully learned top-tier spatial secret techniques like "Heavenly Demon Crossing," using them quite adeptly.

However, the Pine Leaf Mouse studied spatial Dao not for escape or travel, but to create larger storage spaces. Through relentless effort, it finally realized the grand vision Lin Jing had painted for it in childhood:

"One flower, one world; one leaf, one space."

Now, despite only being at the Nascent Soul Great Perfection stage, the Pine Leaf Mouse could create independent spaces within objects like Void Mystical Stage cultivators. A single carefully condensed leaf contained a space comparable to a large storage ring.

That alone wouldn't be remarkable, but crucially, the Pine Leaf Mouse could detonate these miniature spaces at will, creating violent spatial turbulence. While the damage couldn't compare to golden core divine abilities, spatial attribute attacks now ranked as its second most destructive method.

Most importantly... it could stockpile these spatial leaves!

One or two might pale against golden core divine abilities, but a hundred million? A billion?

Yet no matter how impressive the spirit beasts' gains, they paled beside their beast tamer Lin Jing's harvest.

Regarding his true form: besides advancing to Nascent Soul Great Perfection, Lin Jing had mastered the Yin Household Sect's corpse-driving technique. Through his deductions, once both he and the Ice Soul Gu reached the Nascent Soul Transformation stage and fused, by burning lifespan with "Divine Control Art" to elevate his beast taming realm and utilizing life-death origin power, he could preliminarily control that Celestial Beast corpse—forming formidable combat strength.

That Celestial Beast corpse, if controllable, would undoubtedly become a supreme weapon in the Origin Realm.

As for his avatar—this Heavenly Demon avatar now showed preliminary might, having mastered the Heavenly Demon Finger, the destructive centerpiece of Heavenly Demon secret arts.

The demonic bloodline had also reached great completion during this cultivation, just one step from the so-called perfection stage that could rival immortal physiques.

The great completion Heavenly Demon Finger required the cultivator to sever an arm, continuously refining it into a treasure. Only by using this arm could one unleash the technique's full

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 122: Changes in the Destiny Rankings
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 122: Changes in the Destiny Rankings

"What do you mean?"

Lin Jing stood up. The Tianyuan Grand Assembly had already been going on for several days?

Why had no one informed him.

Why had no one invited him.

Didn't all top hundred talents on the Destiny Rankings have the right to participate?

As the Holy Son of the Demon Court and ranked sixth on the Destiny Rankings, could he really be excluded?

"Senior need not worry, you're still ranked sixth on the Destiny Rankings, with no changes..." Yu Xun'er said.

Lin Jing looked up at the sky.

First place: Crown Prince of the Ancient Clan, Gu Xinghe.

Second place: Princess of the Ancient Clan, Gu Youran.

Third place: Holy Maiden of the Demon Court, Xu Qingyao.

Fourth place: Dao Child of the Spirit Dao Sect, Lu Qi.

Fifth place: Buddhist Child of the Sun Temple, Dao Hui.

Sixth place: Holy Son of the Demon Court, Lin Jing.

Seventh place: Successor of the Martial Emperor, Wan Renzong.

Eighth place: Senior Sister of Water Moon Immortal Isle, Shui Lingsu.

Ninth place: Divine Son of the Wu God Hall, Ta Wu.

Tenth place: Young Sect Master of the Heavenly Sword Sect, Feng Ning.

"This Tianyuan Grand Assembly has caused such dramatic shifts in the rankings," Lin Jing murmured.

"The three Demon Emperor factions - Sea God Palace, Misty Forests, and Snow Plains - actually have no one on the list?"

"Were they all pushed off? No... their names don't appear even after the top ten."

"That's not the case," Yu Xun'er beside him said. "The Totem Holy Son of Snow Plains... reportedly perished."

"Apparently after being pushed out of the top ten by Buddhist Child Dao Hui who broke through to Nascent Soul Transformation, he recklessly practiced forbidden arts and died from qi deviation."

"I see." Lin Jing nodded. Probably pressured by the Totem Demon Emperor of Snow Plains into taking desperate measures that failed.

"What about the Crown Prince of Sea God Palace and Young Master Long Liu? Did they perish too?" Lin Jing found this hard to believe.

These two had been in the top five when he last checked the Destiny Rankings, and had even paid him "protection fees."

"Didn't Senior know?" Yu Xun'er said. "Just before the Tianyuan Grand Assembly began, several demonic beast factions including Sea God Palace, Misty Forests, and Snow Plains all announced they were joining the Demon Court."

"Among them, the Crown Prince of Sea God Palace and Young Master Long Liu, disciple of the Dragon Emperor of the Vast Forest, served as envoys to sign a contract with Holy Maiden Xu Qingyao... It's with their support that Xu Qingyao rose to third place on the Destiny Rankings this time."

"Unfortunately, Princess Gu Youran of the Ancient Clan cultivated an Immortal Physique. Even with Holy Maiden Xu and several Nascent Soul Transformation-level demonic beasts joining forces, they were still suppressed by the Immortal Physique..."

"No wonder." Lin Jing finally understood why his senior sister was so formidable. Over these years, through the Demon Emperor's efforts, they'd nearly succeeded in recruiting all the top demonic beast factions of Tianyuan Continent.

The Crown Prince of Sea God Palace and Young Master Long Liu were essentially "hostages."

But it wasn't a bad deal, since this way they could all enter the Origin Realm together and combine resources.

"Wait." Lin Jing remembered his senior sister only had one contract slot left. How could she contract two? Did she cultivate an additional contract slot like him?

"Never mind." Everyone has their own opportunities, Lin Jing stopped pondering. But was the Ancient Clan's princess really that strong?

If the princess had cultivated a Postnatal Immortal Physique, then the Ancient Imperial Prince must have done the same.

The Second Ancient Emperor's goal had apparently been achieved.

Lin Jing could imagine how much more confident the Second Ancient Emperor must feel facing the First Ancient Emperor now, more convinced than ever that concentrating resources on nurturing Ancient Clan bloodlines was the correct choice.

"Feng Ning successfully broke through to Nascent Soul Transformation too, making top ten. That Lunar Divine Physique Pill wasn't wasted." Looking at the rankings, Lin Jing figured this was likely the final list for Tianyuan's representatives to the Origin Realm.

Yet he still wanted to know why no one had notified him to participate.

He took out the tiger fang that could contact the Demon Emperor. Moments later, the Demon Emperor's voice came through:

"You're out of seclusion."

"Your Majesty, the Tianyuan Grand Assembly started... why wasn't I notified?"

"I did call you. You didn't respond."

"Huh? Did you?" Lin Jing fell into thought. When was this?

"Figured you were in seclusion, so I didn't keep calling. Your spot was guaranteed anyway, almost impossible for others to take. Participating or not didn't matter much," the Demon Emperor said dismissively.

"Alright..." Lin Jing scratched his head. So during his cultivation inside the Hourglass of Time, he'd missed the Demon Emperor's calls. But for something this important, shouldn't they have sent someone to Spirit Dao Sect to notify him? Just casually calling with a remote treasure... who answers every single call?

And Spirit Dao Sect wasn't being considerate either. Knowing he was cultivating there, with their own Dao Child attending, they just left him behind?

Lin Jing glanced at Yu Xun'er. This girl had probably been monitoring news from the Grand Assembly at Spirit Dao Sect the whole time, yet never interrupted his seclusion to inform him?

"Your Majesty, are you at the Grand Assembly now? Should I still go?" Lin Jing asked.

"Since today's the last day and the ten spots are basically decided, maybe I shouldn't bother."

"But has the date for activating the Origin Realm teleportation array been set?"

"Come! Why wouldn't you come? Get over here now!" the Demon Emperor cursed irritably.

"That Second Ancient Emperor is too arrogant. Gu Xinghe and Gu Youran have both cultivated Postnatal Immortal Physiques. Even with Xu Qingyao and several others teaming up, they're no match at all."

"Do you know the Demon Court's current situation?"

"Now... the Demon Court stands as a major power comparable to the Ancient Nation - one representing humanity, the other demonic beasts."

"Xu Qingyao representing the Demon Court, allied with the Crown Prince of Sea God Palace and the Dragon Emperor's successor, plus Xu Qingyao's various pets, couldn't even defeat Gu Youran alone. Infuriating!"

"Immortal Physiques think they're so special? Boy, come here and beat up these two Immortal Physique cultivators for me."

Lin Jing smiled wryly. "There's no real meaning to it. Once we enter the Origin Realm, the Destiny Rankings become irrelevant. Taking first or second place now wouldn't matter much."

"How is it meaningless? Establishing the Demon Court's prestige over the Ancient Nation is meaning enough. Hurry up."

"Fine." Lin Jing shrugged and looked at Yu Xun'er. "I'm going to catch the tail end of the Grand Assembly. Want to come?"

"Wouldn't my bloodline be discovered?" Yu Xun'er hesitated.

"Probably would."

"Then I won't go." Yu Xun'er said. "It's not time to interact with the Ancient Clan yet. You go ahead, Senior."

"Alright." Lin Jing looked toward the Ancient Domain. During this period, the First Ancient Emperor had uncovered Yu Xun'er's true identity - she was the seventh prince's daughter. This seventh prince was indeed just a philandering royal known for his romantic escapades.

His talent among the many princes was mediocre, and the Second Ancient Emperor held no high hopes for him.

The ambitious Yu Xun'er naturally had no desire to acknowledge such a useless father, feeling no urge to reunite with him. She only wished to one day eliminate such parasites who brought no value to the Ancient Nation.

She couldn't understand why the Second Ancient Emperor would cultivate geniuses like Gu Xinghe and Gu Youran - that made sense - but waste resources on trash like the seventh prince rather than distribute them to other talented individuals outside the royal bloodline. Too fixated on lineage.

"Blood ties are indeed powerful connections," Lin Jing sighed. From the perspective of the Ancient Clan's patriarch, the Second Ancient Emperor's actions were understandable. But he had another identity - ruler of the Ancient Nation, emperor of humanity.

Compared to his role as clan patriarch, the First Ancient Emperor clearly prioritized his position as nation's ruler more.

"Then wait here. I'll be back soon. Let me see how powerful your aunt and uncle have become under the Second Ancient Emperor's tutelage."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 123: The Arrival
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 123: The Arrival

The Tianyuan Grand Event in the Ancient Domain was nearing its conclusion. At this stage, the top-ranked prodigies on the Golden Ranking had ceased challenging each other. Everyone's capabilities were already well understood. Moreover, the top ten were about to enter the Origin Realm and would soon lose the benefits of the Golden Ranking. The exact ranking positions no longer mattered—securing a top ten spot to guarantee entry into the Origin Realm was the only goal.

Currently, the competition was mainly between the 11th to 100th ranked prodigies. Since the top ten would leave Tianyuan, these competitors would become the new leaders among Tianyuan Continent's prodigies, and they were now desperately showcasing their abilities.

Apart from the competing prodigies, many prominent figures from the Tianyuan Continent had gathered in the Ancient Domain of the Ancient Nation, including the Tianyuan Seekers of the Way who had been maintaining the dimensional barriers—they too had set aside their tasks.

"It seems this is the end," remarked one Seeker of the Way, preparing to leave as the top ten rankings remained unchanged and no lower-ranked prodigies seemed inclined to challenge them.

"What a spectacular event, though it's a shame that person didn't participate in the Tianyuan Grand Event!" sighed another powerful figure.

"Indeed. That monstrous genius who mastered the Heavenly Demon Secret Arts and beast taming, capable of ranking on the Golden Ranking with just his tamed beasts alone—his absence is truly regrettable."

"Yet despite not participating in a single match, the Golden Ranking still places him firmly at sixth position, proving his terrifying strength remains undeniable."

"His foundation is undeniable! I speculate Lin Jing's current true strength rivals—if not surpasses—that of the Demon Court's Holy Maiden. The Holy Maiden, who contracted the Crown Prince of the Sea God Palace and Young Master Long Liu, stands at the pinnacle of Nascent Soul Transformation in the continent. Yet even she was defeated by Princess Youran, who has cultivated an Immortal Physique. Though the Ancient Imperial Prince hasn't made a move, his position above Princess Youran suggests he too has achieved an Immortal Physique."

"The Heavenly Demon Physique likely stands no chance either. Even if the Holy Son forcibly elevated his realm to Void Mystical Stage, an Immortal Physique at Nascent Soul Transformation could potentially overcome a Void Mystical Stage opponent. Let's not forget—the Heavenly Demon Sect... was annihilated by the First Ancient Emperor."

At the Tianyuan Grand Event, experts from all factions expressed regret over that one person's absence—the most monstrous genius of the past decade, who could have tested his might against Princess Youran and the Crown Prince of the Ancient Clan.

Years ago, though Lin Jing was only ranked sixth on the Golden Ranking, his reputation far surpassed that of the top-ranked Ancient Imperial Prince and second-ranked Princess Youran. Many believed Lin Jing refrained from challenging them simply to avoid wasting his lifespan—even with his Longevity Body, the debate over whether Golden Ranking benefits or lifespan was more important remained unresolved. The prevailing opinion was that with his Heavenly Demon Physique fully unleashed and supported by his Rare Beasts, Lin Jing could undoubtedly defeat the Crown Prince of the Ancient Clan and Princess Youran.

It wasn't that Lin Jing couldn't win—the Holy Son of the Demon Court simply saw no value in such a victory. But now, with Princess Youran displaying her Immortal Physique by defeating Xu Qingyao, public opinion had completely reversed.

This was an Immortal Physique. No matter how strong the Heavenly Demon Physique might be, it could never overcome an Immortal Physique. Moreover, Princess Youran had reached mid-stage Nascent Soul Transformation, while the Crown Prince of the Ancient Clan had achieved late-stage Nascent Soul Transformation. According to the Golden Ranking, Lin Jing's normal cultivation level remained only at the peak of Nascent Soul.

The gap was too vast. Even the Seekers of the Way saw no point in Lin Jing competing against the two Ancient Clan prodigies. The Origin Realm was a battlefield for those above Nascent Soul Transformation—even if Lin Jing paid a tremendous price to borrow power beyond his realm to defeat them, it would be meaningless, as such power would be unusable in the Origin Realm, nothing more than an illusion.

The experts also knew about other forces of similar nature, including the Ghost Mother of the Heavenly Demon. The secret of Lin Jing controlling immortal artifacts had been uncovered by various factions through investigations of the Netherworld Hall. The true identity of the Ghost Mother of the Heavenly Demon as a war weapon of the Immortal Palace was no longer a secret.

What was powerful was the Immortal Palace's war weapon—the Ghost Mother of the Heavenly Demon herself—not the Holy Son of the Demon Court. He was merely fortunate enough to know the Ghost Mother and borrow her power, which clearly had no connection to his own abilities and would be inaccessible in the Origin Realm.

The Second Ancient Emperor, though aware that Lin Jing had sealed the Ghost Mother's cultivation and contracted her, also knew the Ghost Mother was barred from entering the Origin Realm. Moreover, the sealed Ghost Mother could no longer control immortal artifacts, only quasi-immortal artifacts at most. Nevertheless, the Ghost Mother's existence had undeniably strengthened the Demon Court's foundation, becoming a key factor in other demonic beast factions submitting to them.

"Has he arrived?" Most people no longer held high expectations for Lin Jing—except for one: the ruler of the Demon Court. The Demon Emperor, after learning that Lin Jing had resurrected the First Ancient Emperor using the Divine Scripture and discovering that Lin Jing had once defeated a Creation Immortal Physique in a pocket realm, held unwavering confidence in him. If Lin Jing could defeat a peer with an Immortal Physique before contracting the Ghost Mother, he would only be stronger now. Though his base cultivation was slightly lower, his Heavenly Demon Physique could bridge the gap—he might not even need to reach Void Mystical Stage to contend with an Immortal Physique.

"It's up to you now." As the Demon Emperor noticed Lin Jing's arrival, other cultivators soon detected a figure standing in the sky. When they looked up, everyone—regardless of strength, age, or gender—felt their pupils constrict at the sight. The figure's aura was too bizarre, and what he rode was even more terrifying.

Above the clouds floated a massive black arm resembling that of a giant. A figure clad in black stood on the back of the enormous hand, while the palm's grotesque flesh-face peered down unnervingly—clearly not a technique of the righteous path. The Holy Son of the Demon Court, Lin Jing, stood nonchalantly atop the Heavenly Demon's arm, observing everything below.

Only when the Seekers of the Way noticed him did he slowly descend, controlling the Heavenly Demon's arm as one would a flying sword, until he landed on the ground.

"My apologies... for being late," Lin Jing said, his gaze sweeping across the Seekers of the Way before finally settling on the Ancient Imperial Prince and Princess Youran. He was the first prodigy bold enough to act so arrogantly before a gathering of Seekers of the Way.

"The Holy Son of the Demon Court!" Many inwardly exclaimed upon realizing his identity—especially those Tianyuan prodigies who had only heard of Lin Jing but never seen him in person.

"Naturally..." The Second Ancient Emperor, seated at the highest position, narrowed his eyes at the latecomer. "Is that... a perfected Heavenly Demon Finger?"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 124: True Dragon Immortal Physique
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 124: True Dragon Immortal Physique

Heavenly Demon Finger!

Many Seeking the Way experts instinctively reacted when they sensed Lin Jing wielding this technique. The Holy Son of the Demon Court had actually mastered this forbidden art to perfection.

First the Heavenly Demon Physique, then Heavenly Demon Divine Sense, and now the Heavenly Demon Finger...

"It seems we can evaluate the results now," remarked Ling Daozi of the Spirit Dao Sect, one of those who had paid Lin Jing's protection fee, finding the situation intriguing.

They were well aware that numerous Heavenly Demon Sect geniuses currently resided in the Origin Realm. Just as they worried their own disciples might be unfamiliar with the sect's techniques, Lin Jing arrived wielding the Heavenly Demon Finger - perfect timing in their eyes.

"I wonder how far his research into countering Heavenly Demon techniques has progressed," Ling Daozi glanced at Lu Qi. Initially, the Spirit Dao Sect's Dao Child had been quite displeased about paying protection fees to this Demon Court Holy Son.

Although Lu Qi had now climbed to fourth place, surpassing Lin Jing, Ling Daozi suspected Lu Qi still wanted to settle their rivalry and prove himself. This "Heavenly Demon whetstone" would still serve its purpose.

The Tianyuan Grand Event clearly needed to continue. If their disciples could experience Heavenly Demon techniques through Lin Jing, it would undoubtedly benefit their upcoming journey to the Origin Realm.

"Or perhaps... why don't the top five come at me together? After all, I have multiple beast companions to assist..."

"Arrogant bastard!"

Lin Jing's words immediately drew curses from numerous geniuses like Lu Qi and Dao Hui - especially Dao Hui, whose eyes burned with vengeance for past humiliations.

Even the Seeking the Way experts were taken aback by the Demon Court Holy Son's boundless audacity. Did he truly understand the terror of an Immortal Physique?

"This guy..." Xu Qingyao, standing beside the Demon Emperor, took a deep breath. Her junior brother hadn't come for revenge - he came to pick a fight!

Having personally exchanged blows with Princess Youran, Xu Qingyao understood the dreadful power of an Immortal Physique. If Gu Youran was already monstrously strong, the Ancient Imperial Prince would be even more formidable.

She urgently sent a mental message advising Lin Jing not to overestimate himself - others had progressed too.

Lin Jing responded with a reassuring look. Only he knew his current full capabilities.

"Heh..." The Ancient Imperial Prince chuckled lightly. "Challenging five opponents violates the rules."

"But I admire your courage. Since you're so confident, and I've yet to fight in this event, let us duel." The prince gazed down at Lin Jing from the elevated platform.

"Truthfully, I've long wanted to teach this Demon Court Holy Son a lesson. But Father Emperor prioritized the bigger picture, insisting I first cultivate my Immortal Physique. Now that I've achieved the same constitution as the First Dragon Immortal Physique, I fear no one."

"My son, go." The Seven Dragon Immortal Physique wielder decided it was time to settle their long-standing conflict with the Demon Court. "As the Golden Ranking's top genius and the continent's foremost talent, you may proceed."

As the prince slowly walked forward on air, Lin Jing's severed demonic arm floated up again. Standing atop it with hands behind his back, Lin Jing faced his opponent midair as the crowd erupted.

Holy Son of the Demon Court versus Crown Prince of the Ancient Clan!

What an exciting development.

Who would have imagined such a spectacle on the Tianyuan Grand Event's final day?

Though not one against five, this would suffice.

"Who will win?"

This question burned in everyone's minds.

"He's done for," the First Ancient Emperor secretly observing the event shook his head upon seeing Lin Jing's arrival. During the Heavenly Demon Sect's destruction, he'd created numerous techniques specifically countering their secret arts - extremely difficult and taxing to master.

These techniques were derived from his True Dragon Immortal Physique. Regardless of his opinions about the Second Ancient Emperor's policies, he had to admit his grandson Gu Xinghe had been well-trained - the second in Ancient Clan history to cultivate the Postnatal True Dragon Immortal Physique.

If his judgment was correct, Gu Xinghe must have mastered many Heavenly Demon-countering techniques for navigating the Origin Realm. With Lin Jing already at a cultivation disadvantage, encountering those techniques could prove disastrous.

Yet the First Ancient Emperor offered no warning. Blood was thicker than water - he wouldn't help Lin Jing against his own descendant. Besides, with the match imminent, it was too late anyway.

"Good luck," the Demon Emperor quietly cheered for Lin Jing.

Dao Hui, Wan Renzong, and Ta Wu - all previously defeated by Lin Jing - watched intently. Though eager to measure their current strength against him, they didn't mind spectating this battle first.

"Nascent Soul Transformation late stage," Lin Jing assessed cautiously. Within the same realm, he feared no Immortal Physique. But facing someone nearly a major realm higher, he needed to bridge the gap first.

"One incense stick's time should suffice," Lin Jing calculated. Temporarily boosting to Nascent Soul Transformation peak for such a short duration would consume minimal lifespan. Previously impossible, his deepened understanding of the Heavenly Demon Physique after acquiring demonic blood made this precision possible.

BOOM!

As their gazes locked, the crowd saw dark flames flicker around Lin Jing as his aura suddenly surged, his cultivation rocketing to Nascent Soul Transformation initial stage in an instant.

"Heavenly Demon Physique!"

The audience buzzed with anticipation as Lin Jing employed his signature technique to level the playing field. But when the Ancient Imperial Prince chuckled, Lin Jing's cultivation didn't rise further - instead plummeting back to Nascent Soul great perfection!

Simultaneously, an invisible dragon of fortune materialized beneath the floating prince's feet. While all Ancient Clan practitioners of the True Dragon Secret Arts could manifest such dragons, the crowd gasped as this one displayed intelligence identical to the First Ancient Emperor's legendary Fortune Dragon Emperor - completely unlike ordinary fortune dragons!

"Your Highness... I shall assist. I've reversed this space's time flow, causing his technique's effects to regress..." the fortune dragon spoke slowly.

Gu Xinghe addressed Lin Jing: "The Heavenly Demon Physique? How naive. As the defeated foe of our ancient kingdom, do you truly consider our clan's secret arts so insignificant that you'd use Heavenly Demon Sect techniques against me?"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 125: A Different Heavenly Demon
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 125: A Different Heavenly Demon

"The Heavenly Demon Physique essentially burns lifespan to temporarily boost one's cultivation realm. But by reversing the flow of time on your magical effects using dragon vein energy, your Heavenly Demon Physique cannot function normally," said the Ancient Imperial Prince with his hands behind his back, watching Lin Jing like one would watch a clown.

Reverse... time?

As the prince finished speaking, many cultivators stood up in shock. From the audience seats, Bai Yuran of the Heavenly Secrets Tower murmured, "Can the True Dragon Secret Arts really achieve such feats? Lin Jing is in trouble now."

"Which side are you on?" asked the Heavenly Secrets Tower Master.

Bai Yuran stuck out her tongue playfully. "The middle. Both the Ancient Imperial Prince and the Holy Son of the Demon Court are my wings."

Time manipulation! While the Heavenly Secrets Tower specialized in divining the future—already defying heaven's will—the discovery that the prince could reverse time on Lin Jing's magic was even more inconceivable.

Was this the power of a True Dragon Immortal Physique?

"Checkmate."

Now, Lin Jing at Nascent Soul Great Perfection posed absolutely no threat to the Ancient Imperial Prince at Nascent Soul Transformation late stage.

With his Heavenly Demon Physique disabled, what cards did Lin Jing have left to play?

"It seems the Ancient Imperial Prince holds the advantage."

"Naturally. One was personally trained by the Ancient Emperor, while the other... making it this far already speaks volumes about his incredible fortune."

"How interesting..." Even Lin Jing himself was surprised. His Heavenly Demon Physique failed, his realm dropped, yet the burned lifespan didn't reverse. This technique perfectly countered the Heavenly Demon Physique—no wonder his opponent was so confident.

But with things having come to this, Lin Jing had no choice but to defeat Gu Xinghe—it concerned the Demon Emperor's dignity. He spoke calmly, "Surely this isn't your full capability?"

"Still refusing to accept defeat?" The prince's eyes flashed.

"It's not about acceptance. Rather, you seem to misunderstand Heavenly Demon magic," Lin Jing smiled.

"While pairing the Longevity Body with the Heavenly Demon Physique certainly optimizes its performance..."

"The Longevity Body actually provides zero enhancement to the Heavenly Demon Physique. Meaning, for my true self, this physique is merely an ordinary technique without any bonuses..."

At first, the crowd didn't comprehend Lin Jing's words.

Wasn't the Longevity Body perfectly compatible with the Heavenly Demon Physique?

Could there be a stronger combination?

But in the next moment, everyone present—especially the prince—abruptly changed expressions.

BOOM!!!

As Lin Jing raised his right arm, a "hum" resonated as vast dark energy coiled around his limb. An abyssal power instantly devoured his arm before transforming into raging flames that enveloped his entire body. In that instant, Lin Jing's right arm completely vanished, replaced by an overwhelmingly terrifying aura.

Gone was the enticing scent of the Longevity Body—instead, his presence now closely resembled the Heavenly Demon arm beneath his feet.

Simultaneously, his neatly cropped short hair grew wildly like dancing black dragons standing on end, while his dark eyes instantly gained crimson streaks.

Lin Jing's entire demeanor had transformed—no longer human-like. Emotions faded from his gaze, his voice grew more ethereal. Until now, Lin Jing had been walking the world in his true form, thus using the Heavenly Demon Physique's realm boost without concern—after all, his original body possessed longevity;
burning some lifespan meant little.

But discovering his true form's Heavenly Demon Physique was completely countered, Lin Jing realized he might need to employ his avatar's power.

While his true form's bloodline and techniques provided zero enhancement to Heavenly Demon arts, the Heavenly Demon avatar was different.

Both the cultivation method "Heavenly Demon Codex" and his demonic bloodline greatly empowered Heavenly Demon magic. As Lin Jing manifested the avatar power concealed within his true form, letting it take dominance, his perfected Heavenly Demon Physique instantly wrought unparalleled changes to his appearance.

Though Lin Jing's cultivation remained at Nascent Soul Great Perfection, in just an instant—not only the prince but even the Fortune Dragon—all sensed a lethal threat.

"Madness! Who provoked this kid?!" Far away in Qingzhou, Mo Pine sensed this power and was utterly shocked.

Moreover, across the entire Tian Continent, every cultivator practicing Heavenly Demon Cult techniques—whether present or not—suddenly felt their hearts gripped, instinctively turning toward a certain direction.

At this moment, the sky turned black in their eyes—a terrifying sensation of a demon god descending upon the mortal world.

On-site, Demon Extermination Division experts proficient in Heavenly Demon magic were most sensitive to Lin Jing's transformation. They instantly fell silent, expressions filled with disbelief.

"This is..."

Lin Jing's metamorphosis caused an uproar. Then, he calmly continued activating his Heavenly Demon Physique. Now, enhanced by the Heavenly Demon Codex and his demonic bloodline's power, the lifespan-burning heart flame blazed at least ten times more violently. Instantly, Lin Jing's realm skyrocketed to Nascent Soul Transformation peak—yet this forcibly elevated magic power was several times more condensed than normal Nascent Soul Transformation energy.

Some Nascent Soul Transformation experts—even some Void Mystical Stage powerhouses—felt inferior when sensing Lin Jing's "demonic energy."

At this moment, the Fortune Dragon's expression drastically changed, exclaiming in horror: "Time reversal... has no effect on his magic now..."

The Ancient Imperial Prince's face darkened.

"Using dragon veins to reverse time has its limits. As long as my spell's power surpasses your dragon vein-enhanced True Dragon Secret Arts, your technique cannot erase my effects."

Lin Jing rose higher. The Heavenly Demon arm beneath his feet now floated to his right side—no longer serving as a stepping platform. At Lin Jing's mental command, its index finger lightly pointed toward the prince.

Beautiful.

Like a black hole devouring the prince and Fortune Dragon, in barely an instant—amidst the prince's incredulous cry and the dragon's horrified roar—the fortune-condensed true dragon was crushed into scattered light by the Heavenly Demon Codex's countering energy.

Simultaneously, the prince's protective true essence shattered. He vomited blood violently, pupils whitening as the Heavenly Demon Finger's strike sent him flying, grievously wounded. Now, every Ancient Clan cultivator present—except the Second Ancient Emperor—stood up in shock at this tournament scene.

Not just Ancient Clan members... experts and prodigies alike all wore expressions of utter disbelief as they observed the Immortal Physique prince being sent flying by a single finger, then turned their gazes toward Lin Jing standing once more upon the demonic arm.

"What kind of joke is this..." Even the First Ancient Emperor muttered to himself, equally baffled. How could the True Dragon Immortal Physique he developed be defeated by the Heavenly Demon Finger? Impossible! He'd fought against the Heavenly Demon Finger before—even at its perfected stage, he'd never witnessed... such might.

It seemed this Heavenly Demon Lin Jing was... somewhat different from the Heavenly Demon Cult cultivators of Tianyuan.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 4: Chapter 126: The Might of the Heavenly Demon (End of Book 4)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 4 Chapter 126: The Might of the Heavenly Demon (End of Volume 4)

"Impossible! Absolutely impossible!!!"

Only one person at the scene remained seemingly composed—the Second Ancient Emperor. His calm was merely a facade, masking his utter disbelief at what had transpired. Beneath the surface, his mind was in turmoil like stormy waves.

Beside him, Gu Youran's eyes widened in shock. Though she hadn't cultivated the True Dragon Secret Arts herself, having practiced other techniques and immortal physiques instead, she had secretly sparred with her elder brother before. Her immortal physique stood no chance against his True Dragon Immortal Physique.

Like most present, she had assumed this battle would be one-sided. Yet now, Lin Jing's single finger strike had shattered the conviction of all Ancient Clan cultivators!

Even allies were stunned—the Demon Emperor of the Demon Court himself blinked repeatedly, utterly bewildered as to when Lin Jing had acquired such terrifying power.

What was that finger technique?

This wasn't something a Nascent Soul Transformation cultivator should possess—even those newly advanced to the Void Mystical Stage would retreat from such an attack.

When... how?

"Cough..." The Ancient Imperial Prince only lost consciousness briefly. Moments later, he hovered in mid-air, staring in shock at his quasi-immortal armor. Without this quasi-immortal artifact's protection, even his immortal physique might have been obliterated by that single strike.

As he numbly observed the massive cracks in his armor, Lin Jing's divine sense casually swept over him like an afterthought.

Boom!

Empowered by the Heavenly Demon Codex and his demonic bloodline, Lin Jing's spiritual sense had transformed into something monstrous—a nearly tangible black tsunami that smashed the prince into the ground a second time.

"Quasi-immortal armor truly is impressive," Lin Jing remarked, surprised the prince remained conscious after two attacks. He prepared to unleash a second finger strike. The colossal Heavenly Demon arm descending from the heavens made all observing geniuses gasp in terror.

Those who had considered challenging Lin Jing moments ago now felt suffocated by the overwhelming pressure of that apocalyptic finger technique.

"Is this... the Heavenly Demon Sect's magic?"

Shui Lingsu, the senior sister of Water Moon Immortal Isle, possessed the keenest perception among the gathered geniuses. Analyzing the Heavenly Demon Finger technique, she instantly understood her fate if facing it directly—death.

The stark realization nearly shattered her Dao heart even from a distance. Blood trickled from her lips as inner demons took root.

Consider this: Lin Jing, merely at the Great Perfection stage of Nascent Soul, had completely overwhelmed an immortal physique cultivator a full major realm above him, relying solely on his Heavenly Demon Physique and two demonic techniques...

A preposterous thought struck all geniuses preparing to enter the Origin Realm—if Heavenly Demon Sect disciples in the Origin Realm were like this... if the Heavenly Demon cultivators preparing to invade Tianyuan World were like this...

They couldn't envision any scenario where Tianyuan cultivators might prevail.

By the time the crowd regained their senses, the Second Ancient Emperor had already appeared at the battlefield, standing beside his son who remained pinned to the ground by Lin Jing's demonic spiritual pressure, personally intercepting the next attack.

The Tianyuan Grand Event arena instantly became a confrontation ground between the Second Ancient Emperor and Lin Jing.

"Your Majesty?" Lin Jing regarded the emperor. His intervention meant the prince had conceded defeat.

Yet as a Dao-seeker, was physical intervention truly necessary to stop the attack?

Unless... the emperor intended to retaliate against Lin Jing for humiliating his son—to crush this outrageously powerful upstart using his superior cultivation.

But even the Second Ancient Emperor wouldn't stoop to such pettiness. Analyzing the residual energy from the Heavenly Demon Finger, he realized this technique contained an extraordinary power beyond normal cultivation—something undoubtedly connected to the Heavenly Demon Sect's deepest secrets.

No records in the Ancient Clan's archives mentioned such power.

He studied Lin Jing intently. "What is this power?"

Lin Jing met his gaze calmly. "The true strength possessed by the Heavenly Demon Sect's core disciples and elites."

"A power that even the Heavenly Demon Sect Leader of Tianyuan World—merely a peripheral member—fears and cannot hope to attain."

"Feeling afraid now?"

His gaze swept across the dumbfounded Tianyuan powerhouses and pale-faced geniuses alike.

"I haven't even fully developed this power yet. A true Heavenly Demon elite would demonstrate far more terrifying might."

"Displaying it here serves as a warning—Heavenly Demon cultivators are far stronger than you imagine. Without proper preparation... Tianyuan World's fate..."

"How do you possess this power?!" The Second Ancient Emperor nearly lost composure, unable to accept that his carefully nurtured immortal physique successor—meant to counter the Heavenly Demon Sect—had been so thoroughly humiliated.

For the first time, he abandoned imperial dignity, eyes flashing with panic as if suddenly losing faith in Tianyuan World's future—though he quickly regained control.

Turning away without another glance at his son, he declared evenly:

"You've won this battle."

"It seems you, not my son, deserve the title of Tianyuan's top genius."

"Everyone has their own destiny. I won't pry into how you obtained this power. However, before your group enters the Origin Realm, I hope you'll spar with each Tianyuan genius—let them all experience true Heavenly Demon might."

"Lest all Tianyuan geniuses perish there, leaving you the sole survivor."

"That would be our world's greatest loss."

As the emperor finished, Lin Jing noticed the Demon Emperor's excited expression—his performance had clearly pleased his faction leader. This trip had been worthwhile.

Though surprised by the emperor's request, Lin Jing readily agreed. Having spent considerable time in Tianyuan World, though not native to it, he didn't wish to see it destroyed.

"Very well." Lin Jing addressed the gathered geniuses, including the senior sister and Cold Moon Sword Immortal. "Following His Majesty's suggestion, before our departure, I'll help you all experience true Heavenly Demon might!"

"For your own good... I won't hold back."

The geniuses paled. What madness was this? They glanced at the pitiful state of the Ancient Imperial Prince—wasn't this supposed to be the Beast Master Sect's Holy Son? The Demon Court's Holy Son? Now he fought like some Heavenly Demon Saint—no, even more ferocious than their previous sect leader!

The upcoming Origin Realm expedition... once anticipated with confidence, now filled them with dread. The thought of encountering dozens of "Lin Jings" there made the future seem bleak indeed.

...

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 5: Chapter 1: Origin Realm
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 5 Chapter 1: Origin Realm

The first Tianyuan Grand Event gathering talents from across the continent concluded in a manner that defied all expectations of the cultivation world. First, the Demon Court unified most demonic beast factions, empowering their Holy Maiden Xu Qingyao to such an extent that she seemed poised to dominate all young talents through beast taming. Then, the two prodigies from the Ancient Clan cultivated Postnatal Immortal Physiques, decisively defeating the previously unstoppable Xu Qingyao.

Just when everyone assumed the event would conclude with these immortal physiques reigning supreme, a Heavenly Demon descended unexpectedly. The Holy Son of the Demon Court, wielding secret demonic arts, suppressed the immortal physiques and demonstrated the terrifying might of the Heavenly Demons to the world. This revelation forced both supreme experts and young geniuses across the continent to completely reevaluate their understanding of Heavenly Demon cultivators.

To prepare the talents for confronting Heavenly Demon practitioners in the Origin Realm, Lin Jing subsequently allowed each participant to repeatedly experience the demonic techniques until they could barely withstand some of the Heavenly Demon's power. "This should suffice," Lin Jing mused. "According to Elder Mo Pine, true Heavenly Demon practitioners with demonic bloodlines are rare. Now that they've all developed countermeasures against demonic arts, the Origin Realm expedition should proceed safely." During this period, Lin Jing had actually been using battles with these top talents to temper his Heavenly Demon avatar.

Since these geniuses were all at the Nascent Soul Transformation Stage, the fights remained balanced as long as Lin Jing's avatar didn't break through to that realm using the Heavenly Demon Physique. Ironically, this restraint made Lin Jing increasingly aware of the overwhelming dominance of demonic bloodlines. He recalled the Two Realms Cavern's description of the Demon God Physique—a perfected form could rival immortal energy with its demonic power.

Immortal energy—a force only obtainable after transcending tribulations and ascending. Even immortal physiques didn't inherently possess it, merely guaranteeing eventual ascension. This realization fueled Lin Jing's anticipation for perfecting his demonic bloodline. While the Demon God Physique lacked the exotic abilities of other immortal physiques, its sheer energy quality seemed capable of overwhelming most of them.

One year after the Tianyuan Grand Event concluded, the gathered talents prepared to depart for the Origin Realm. During this interval, Lin Jing's avatar continued sparring while his true body focused on absorbing the Dragon's Saliva Wine gifted by the Sea God Palace to recover expended lifespan. Remarkably, by the time of their departure, not only had Lin Jing fully restored all consumed lifespan, but his vitality actually surpassed its previous levels.

Unexpectedly, the wine's aftereffects proved formidable. While his avatar only consumed a small amount, his main body frequently battled drowsiness—an effect that might require decades of sleep to fully dissipate. With no time for such prolonged rest, Lin Jing relied on sobering pills crafted by the Dragon Carp to maintain alertness daily. Though slightly inconvenient, recovering his burned lifespan made this side effect acceptable. Barring accidents, the pills would gradually neutralize the lingering influence.

"It's finally beginning," Lin Jing declared, standing atop a mountain peak in the Ancient Nation. Having settled all Tianyuan Continent affairs, his sole focus now was substantially improving his strength in the Origin Realm, breaking through to the Void Mystical Stage, then being expelled back to Tianyuan. Only by reaching that realm could he truly establish a foothold in this lower world—no matter how chaotic the situation became, Void Mystical cultivation would ensure his safety.

Lin Jing had shown no mercy against these talents, unleashing demonic energy like an emotionless machine during their encounters. How much they comprehended depended entirely on their own capabilities. However, for familiar faces like Gu Xinghe and Gu Youran, Lin Jing patiently explained the intricacies of demonic arts—a privilege few enjoyed.

Soon the remaining eight talents arrived as scheduled, ultimately accompanied by the Second Ancient Emperor personally escorting Gu Xinghe and Gu Youran. These two were the only ones Lin Jing's Heavenly Demon avatar couldn't confront while maintaining its Core Formation realm, forcing it to temporarily ascend to Nascent Soul during their sparring sessions—with the Second Ancient Emperor compensating the resulting losses.

"The Origin Realm teleportation array will activate shortly," the Second Ancient Emperor announced upon arrival. "The Pathseekers must also take their positions. Before your departure, I shall reiterate the current situation in the Origin Realm!"

"In ancient times, cultivators from each realm operated independently, divided into countless factions competing for immortal energy-producing Origin Sources within the realm—even practitioners from the same world might not cooperate. Every cultivator pursued a single goal: obtaining Origin Sources by any means necessary."

"However, after the Heavenly Demon Sect disrupted the lower realms and the Cultivation Alliance formed, the Origin Realm's landscape changed significantly. Most realm cultivators now gather collectively, though some independent practitioners remain. Essentially, the realm's forces divide between the Heavenly Demon Sect and the Cultivation Alliance's talents."

"Upon arrival, your primary mission involves contacting and joining the Cultivation Alliance—with one exception: Lin Jing. His avatar will infiltrate the Heavenly Demon Sect as a spy. Therefore, none of you nine may ever mention his demonic avatar within the Origin Realm." Producing a scroll, the emperor continued, "This Pathseeker Scroll contains powerful restrictions crafted by multiple Pathseekers. By signing, any revelation about the Demon Court Holy Son's demonic avatar will trigger contractual backlash!"

The Second Ancient Emperor understood perfectly—having Lin Jing, versed in demonic arts, infiltrate their enemy's ranks to gather intelligence represented the most advantageous strategy for Tianyuan Realm. The talents nodded unanimously;
an embedded spy within enemy lines naturally provided greater security. After all signed the contract, the emperor delivered his final warning: "What follows is most critical. Historical records indicate all realm teleportations arrive randomly in the Origin Realm. None can predict your initial circumstances, so remain extremely vigilant. However, you can rest assured—no threats exceeding the Void Mystical Stage should emerge there."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 5: Chapter 2: The Mountain of Lewd Sacrifices
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 5 Chapter 2: The Mountain of Lewd Sacrifices

They would randomly arrive somewhere in the Origin Realm! This was likely the first crisis one might encounter upon entering the Origin Realm. However, it wasn't as terrifying as imagined. Even if one landed right beside a Heavenly Demon Sect prodigy, it wouldn't necessarily be a terrible outcome.

After all, Heavenly Demon Sect cultivators only had two core missions: in the outside world, it was invading various cultivation realms;
in the Origin Realm, it was seizing Origin Sources by any means necessary. Therefore, without sufficient benefits, Heavenly Demon Sect cultivators wouldn't fight to the death upon seeing someone. Killing a newly arrived cultivator wouldn't yield any Origin Sources... Not knowing the opponent's strength, if both sides suffered heavy losses and missed the chance to compete for Origin Sources, the loss would outweigh the gain.

In the lower realms, there were scarcely any treasures more valuable than Origin Sources.

After everyone indicated their understanding, the Second Ancient Emperor nodded again. Simultaneously, the Seekers of the Way at various locations turned their gazes toward the teleportation array.

"Then let us begin," declared the many Seekers of the Way in unison.

............

"What are you looking at?"

Qingzhou, Immortal Prison Mountain.

"Mmm—" Beside her, Mosquito Woman also hovered in midair, looking in the same direction.

"So what?" Mo Pine grumbled. "You two have Longevity Bodies anyway. But given that kid's circumstances, staying in the Origin Realm long enough to monopolize all Origin Sources would indeed be optimal. Though doing so would likely make him a public enemy of all realms. Even the Cultivation Alliance would probably try to locate Tianyuan Realm and threaten him with its destruction."

After all, among all the prodigies in the Origin Realm, who could outlast someone with a Longevity Body? Even if Lin Jing encountered an immortal physique he couldn't defeat, he could simply wait until they left.

"Enough about him. If he really camps in the Origin Realm and refuses to come out, once Tianyuan Realm's Origin Realm teleportation array regains energy, you can go find him there," Mo Pine said.

"Forget it," Gu Qianqiu shook her head. "I still have tasks he left me... That turtle..." She searched around, then facepalmed—the turtle had indeed escaped again.

The mechanized turtle that couldn't accompany Lin Jing to the Origin Realm had been left behind in Tianyuan. But judging by the situation, neither Gu Qianqiu nor Mo Pine seemed capable of keeping an eye on it.

............

"Ancestor... where should we go next?" Meanwhile, at the border of the ancient kingdom, an elderly man and a young maiden walked along a forest path.

Yu Xun'er looked at her ancestor—the legendary First Ancient Emperor—and gulped. Though her memories were somewhat fuzzy, she vaguely recalled an agreement between Lin Jing and the First Ancient Emperor: once Lin Jing went to the Origin Realm, she would train under the First Ancient Emperor.

"First let's leave the ancient kingdom," the First Ancient Emperor said. "Are you really determined to keep cultivating this so-called 'Human Control Technique'??? Why won't you practice the perfectly good 'True Dragon Secret Arts'???"

"The flaws in 'True Dragon Secret Arts' have been mostly repaired these past few years. That Lin Jing fellow shouldn't have slandered the technique."

"Ancestor, I promised Senior I would master the 'Beast Taming Art' properly. It's not called 'Human Control Technique'..."

"Whether it's called that or not, it can contract beasts but can only contract people. What I taught him wasn't the castrated version... Whatever. From now on, your ancestor will help you select worthy 'capable subordinates' to contract. Your ancestor's judgment of character isn't bad. Once you've cultivated a proper group of subordinates, you'll go compete for the throne against your grandfather's sons!!" The First Ancient Emperor grumbled.

"Okay!"

"Eh..." The First Ancient Emperor looked at Yu Xun'er strangely. Why was she agreeing so readily? Was this girl really so calm about competing for the throne? What exactly had Lin Jing instilled in her?

"The first person I'm taking you to meet is somewhat extraordinary. You should mentally prepare yourself. The first person you'll contract is—"

"Who...?" Yu Xun'er suddenly grew nervous.

"Hehe, the descendant of a general who once expanded our ancient kingdom's borders—the offspring of a half-step Seeker of the Way. Their bloodline has stayed far from court politics for many years. From my observations, their lineage has recently produced another Innate Divine Body, currently at qi training level. Perfect for some training to become your right-hand man..."

"Is that all..." Yu Xun'er pondered.

"Actually... Senior already selected someone for me. He wanted you to take me to find them."

"Huh?" The First Ancient Emperor was stunned. "Who?"

"The reincarnation of the Yin Household Sect's Emperor Hu—Hu Jie. If it's a reincarnation, the memories shouldn't awaken too early. I also think contracting Emperor Hu's reincarnation first as my initial human pet would have more growth potential." Yu Xun'er licked her red lips. "Only then would we have a real chance to overthrow that uncle of mine."

First Ancient Emperor: ???

..........

"Go with peace of mind. I'll take good care of your turtle and the avatars of your two spirit beasts."

Among the many Seekers of the Way activating the teleportation array at the Origin Realm's ancient formation was the Demon Court's Demon Emperor. Her gaze remained calm as she looked at Lin Jing, still brimming with confidence in him. She believed Lin Jing would surely bring back satisfactory results from the Origin Realm.

As the starlight on the ten towering pillars dissipated, under the watchful eyes of numerous Seekers of the Way, these ten Tianyuan prodigies completely vanished from Tianyuan Realm in a flash of light. Their names simultaneously disappeared from the Destiny Rankings, marking the dawn of a new era of prodigies for Tianyuan Realm.

Simultaneously, every prodigy entering the Origin Realm experienced a dizzying, disorienting sensation. What disgusted Lin Jing was how the lingering effects of his longevity wine combined with the spatial turbulence of cross-realm teleportation nearly made him faint immediately—he almost vomited.

"Where is this...?"

Were it not for Ice Soul Gu freezing Lin Jing at the critical moment, he might have entered the Origin Realm while asleep.

After an indeterminate amount of time, Lin Jing appeared on an unknown cliff. He immediately surveyed his surroundings, expanding his immense spiritual sense outward. Soon, he heard a light chuckle.

"Dao friend has excellent luck, arriving directly at the 'Mountain of Lewd Sacrifices' upon entering the Origin Realm."

Whoosh!

Lin Jing's gaze locked onto the source of the voice—a disheveled, burly youth sat cross-legged in a cave dwelling. At Nascent Soul Transformation peak cultivation, his strength was unfathomable.

"Mountain of Lewd Sacrifices? What kind of place is this?" Lin Jing transmitted his voice across the distance.

"In the Origin Realm, the power of heaven and earth is exceptionally concentrated. When under the Immortal Realm's jurisdiction it was manageable, but after the Immortal Realm lost control, all mountains and rivers in the Origin Realm transformed into spirits with extraordinary strength. This Mountain of Lewd Sacrifices is the territory of a mountain god. Fortunately, restricted by Origin Realm's rules, these spirits generally won't kill outsiders. However, out of curiosity about the outside world, they'll sometimes make deals with outsider cultivators..."

"For instance, this Mountain of Lewd Sacrifices—if you offer sufficient tribute, you may enter its territory to evade pursuers."

"Easy to enter, hard to leave. Exiting requires ten to a hundred times the resources needed for entry. If you can't pay, you're trapped here forever. The only other way out is breaking through to the Void Mystical Stage to directly leave the Origin Realm."

"So for dao friend to be teleported directly to the Mountain of Lewd Sacrifices upon arrival... hehehe, what excellent fortune."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Book 5: Chapter 3: Robbery
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vol 5 Chapter 3: Robbery

Lin Jing furrowed his brows.

He suspected that during the teleportation process, the sibling duo Gu Xinghe and Gu Youran had somehow sucked away his luck, landing him in such a place.

“Mountain of Lewd Sacrifices... Could the mountain god here have reached the Void Mystical Stage or even the Seeking the Way realm?” he asked.

If that were the case, it would indeed be very difficult to get out.

“Not at all.” The disheveled young man replied, “It’s only equivalent to the peak of Nascent Soul Transformation, but...”

“But the mountains and rivers in the Origin Realm are rather unique, gathering the power of heaven and earth within them, with an almost infinite lifespan. Not only can it perfectly mobilize the world force of the Origin Realm, but it has also lingered in this realm for tens of thousands of years. Although its cultivation level matches that of a Nascent Soul cultivator, in terms of strength... even a Nascent Soul cultivator with an ‘Immortal Physique’ can’t do much against them.”

“Some native monsters here, who don’t fear the Origin Realm’s heavenly dao rules, even team up with outside cultivators to snatch immortal energy... This mountain god is relatively reasonable compared to them.”

Lin Jing understood. It seemed the intelligence gained from the Tianming Realm was incomplete.

Although seemingly limited by heavenly dao rules and unable to breakthrough to the Void Mystical Stage, as native lifeforms of the Origin Realm, this place was undoubtedly their home turf!

“Then, fellow cultivator, did you also enter this Mountain of Lewd Sacrifices to escape from being hunted?” Lin Jing inquired.

The disheveled youth said, “That’s right. Currently, besides you and me, there are three others in this mountain... We all offended powerful enemies and had no choice but to flee here, seeking the mountain god’s protection.”

“But as I just said, it’s easy to enter but hard to leave. Only if I offer enough resources will it allow me to leave the mountain. Otherwise, I can only leave the Origin Realm by forcefully breaking through the Void Mystical boundary.”

“I don’t want to leave the Origin Realm yet, but I don’t have enough resources to offer as tribute to the mountain god. So for now, I can only constantly use spiritual power to condense piece after piece of top-grade spirit stones, accumulating resources to exchange for the chance to leave.”

“Only, I don’t know how long that will take... Besides that, there is another way, which is to rob resources from other cultivators who also enter the Mountain of Lewd Sacrifices, consolidate them, and offer them to the mountain god.” He chuckled, “The mountain god only protects those inside the mountain from outsiders, but if insiders hurt each other, the mountain god won’t interfere.”

“The reason outsiders don’t want to enter the mountain is because it’s easy to get in but hard to get out;
the price to pay outweighs the gains from killing enemies.”

“So, fellow cultivator, after telling you all this, shouldn’t you make a gesture? You just entered the Origin Realm and must have brought a lot of resources from outside. Hand them over. You are only half a step into Nascent Soul Transformation. Even if you are a prodigy of your realm, you definitely can’t survive a single move from me. We have no grudge or hatred;
I won’t kill you. Give me your resources, and I’ll find you a treasure spot in the mountain. You can cultivate there properly and strive to leave sooner... It’s good for both of us.”

As the cultivator with the surname Fang finished speaking, his figure instantly appeared before Lin Jing, calmly staring at him. The power of Nascent Soul Transformation radiated without concealment, causing a massive vortex in the clouds above.

Lin Jing stood calmly on the mountaintop, also looking at him without surprise. He had long noticed this man bore some hostility toward him.

“You’ve come to the wrong person. But considering your intelligence, I’ll give you three breaths to vanish from my divine sense’s range. I won’t hold a grudge.” Lin Jing said calmly.

“Bold—” Fang squinted his eyes.

To put it nicely, he was half a step into Nascent Soul Transformation;
to put it harshly, just a Nascent Soul novice.

Yet he dared to oppose someone at the peak of Nascent Soul Transformation. He really thought the word “fellow cultivator” would stop him.

Boom!

Tired of talking nonsense, Lin Jing directly extended his hand and condensed a small true essence hand seal with spiritual power.

Against a cultivator a small realm above him, he didn’t even bother using spells.

True, it was the most basic attack, but in the hands of a Nascent Soul cultivator, it was still quite formidable. A hand seal as vast as the sky appeared with his movement, and when his palm descended, it felt as if the sky itself was collapsing.

“Someone not even at that level dares to rob?”

“In terms of true essence quality, he ranks even behind the last ten in your realm’s Nascent Soul prodigies.”

Lin Jing slowly raised his arm, using his finger as a sword. As his power fluctuations reached Nascent Soul level in an instant, a sword energy pierced through the large hand seal, severing five fingers. The spiritual energy ripple roared, sweeping the heavens and earth.

Of course, Lin Jing was not using the Heavenly Demon Physique. Dealing with a cultivator hunted to hide here was not worth wasting his lifespan. He used a short-term power boost fused with the Ice Soul Gu.

“Sword cultivator? The sword intent within the sword energy rivals the peak of Nascent Soul Transformation!” Seeing Lin Jing had a secret method to boost his realm and wield such a powerful sword cultivator attack, Fang was momentarily suspicious but still took no real notice.

Unable to easily defeat Lin Jing, he thought, spending a little more effort was no big deal.

“Wind God Bow, now!”

He took out an exquisite spirit bow wrapped in storm energy from his storage pouch. However, before he could shoot the first arrow, his expression changed drastically. He suddenly lost control of his spiritual power as countless poisonous insects seemed to appear out of nowhere in his meridians.

“Poison! When?!”

He abruptly looked toward Lin Jing, who had lowered his arm and was sitting cross-legged calmly in the air, quietly observing him.
